Z5ochgsEdh8O82g3lUfk0A: Great food! The chicken kebab plate was made to order and really tasty! I love places like this! Family operated and owned!! Will be back soon!!!

Came in from traveling & long meetings, didn't expect much, but was starving...

gmimhqv4vrG7_LYadxFYZA: As a Mexican that lived  in Los Angeles CA for 35 years & my wife & I recently moved to Nashville, I was looking for an authentic Mexican place that serves great food @ great prices.  We found it in Carniceria & Taqueria Don Juan.  The drive through may take a while so plan ahead (it makes sense to me since food is authentic) & don't be surprised with the appearance of the place!  This is one of the best places if not the best I ever had. Better than most Mexican places in LA.  MUST try carne asada burritos & tacos & Mexican coke to go with!

4.5 stars!  The chicken here is on point.  Great marinate and taste excellent.  Not a big fan of their carne asada.  The shredded pork is great as well.  I got the chicken quesadilla, 1 chicken taco, 1 pork taco, and 1 asada taco.  I have to say this is so far the best authentic Mexican fast food joint I found so far.   Also, the food is not very greasy either.

Ok, so you've had tacos before?  Nope, not unless you've had one of these or from another taco stand.  Meat, cilantro, onions.  Lime if you want it (you do) and green sauce (a must!). Get the carnitas.  You won't want anything else.  

I've been here probably a dozen times and the staff is always very pleasant, serve your food promptly, and will give you free samples from time to time.  Eat them.  On Wednesdays they do a special on whole chickens: two for $15 with rice, beans, tortillas, onions, and poblanos.  This is delicious, falling-off-the-bone smoked chicken that is some of the best chicken I've ever had.  I recommend going with friends, preferably 4, if you're going for the chicken.  The only way this place could be better is if they also served beer.

05/13/2017, Noon, First Time Visit(based upon "The Scene"/media reviews).

soFyg4gZqg0YzrDCLk6Jxw: My new favorite ice cream spot! The Mexican Twinky is AMAZING! This will not be my last visit... lk9IwjZXqUMqqOhM774DtQ: Free parking garage.  Eat in grocery store or deli type restaurant....everything is by the pound.

mu2yd4Z5n5NLCaLC_KlqLQ: Delicious fresh chips and guacamole. I had the sopa with sausage, tostada with spicy chicken, and a burrito with carnitas. All were excellent. The only complaint is that the paper lining the plate ended up in my food.
The food here is fresh and delicious. The home made salsa gives Taqueria Azteca a unique flavor. They have many options for meat combinations. I need to come back and try the linqua.

Hands down the best Chorizo and Carne Asada Tacos in town. Occasionally, the chef makes mole sauce and if available, then you're in for a real treat. By far, Taqueria Azteca is the most authentic Mexican food in Nashville. Provecho!
N0IleW0j5d_jYP0v2nVMXg:
I had not heard that Nashville had a Hot Chicken truck until just a few days ago.  My boyfriend is a huge hot chicken fan.  So naturally, this weekend when we saw that The Hot Spot was going to be at the Wanderland Nash Food Truck Frenzy at Elmington Park, we headed on over.

For Nashville food trucks, the prices are average with most meals around the $7 range.  Where as most of the other food trucks had their bottled water and drinks for $1, The Hot Spot had theirs at $1.50.  All their drinks were $1.50 whether it was a 16.9 ounce bottled water or a 12 ounce can of pop.  Probably best to check other food trucks for drinks.

On to the food, I have a very weak palate for spicy.  So my boyfriend ordered the 2 piece "Twisted Chicken" with one with Sriracha on it and one without.  And we also ordered some hand cut fries.  It took a little bit for the ladies in the truck who were taking the orders/payments to get everything completed with our transaction, but by the time we got that taken care of, our food was done.

The seasoning on the chicken is totally bomb!  It is spicy by itself without their sriracha.  The inside of my lip tingled.  It was perfectly spicy for me.  And my boyfriend liked it as well.  He actually said he liked it better without the sriracha on it.  The chicken was fried perfectly.  The skin was crispy without being "over breaded" and was incredibly juicy, tender, and temperature hot...as well as spicy hot!  I kinda wished that my boyfriend and I had not split the 2 piece order!

The hand cut fries were pretty tasty delishus as well!

We didn't get a dessert or try any of their breakfast items, but they do serve the breakfast all day which I love places that serve breakfast all day!

I'm a definite fan.  And if I come across a line of food trucks and The Hot Spot is there, I will probably head straight to The Hot Spot...at least for the food!

So far, I have not been too impressed with the food trucks in Nashville. So, with no real expectations in mind I went over to The Hot Spot trailer while they were parked at Turtle Anarchy. I had never heard of them before this so with the 'deer in the headlights' look I approached cautiously. The person behind the counter offered some suggestions. Their Twisted Chicken, with Jamaican jerk spice and Asian hot sauce, it's Sriracha. I don't know why they don't mention it by name, it's like calling Guinness 'Irish stout from the St. James brewery.' I don't think I've met someone who doesn't love Sriracha so no need for euphemisms.

Anyway, it was $7 for two big pieces of their chicken, spiraled with Sriracha and a separate container containing their fennel slaw. I've been similarly unimpressed with hot chicken but once I sat back down at the bar and opened the container it was a flaky, spicy, but still tasty piece of chicken. The hot chicken I've gotten was just heat but with very little flavor. The chicken, while sweat pouringly, mouth burningly spicy was still really tasty. I kept biting into it thinking 'oh man this is good' while wiping my face on my sleeves, careful to avoid rubbing my eyes with any heat. The random smoothness of the Sriracha with the spice in their batter was awesome. Their fennel slaw, was also really tasty with plenty of fennel seed in the mix and it was basically the only thing that could keep the heat down-too bad that there was so little of it.

My friend came later and I got her the patty melt as a belated bday dinner. She really seemed to like it and it's probably the only thing on the menu that doesn't have spice on it. He was even nice enough to walk it into the brewery when it was ready. 

He eventually left because of the lightning that came in "me in a little metal box with lightning around, I'm leaving" and as my friend and I were thinking of eating again we told him how sad it was that he was wrapped up to go. He gave us what he had left, a huge basket of fries and three big pieces of chicken for free. He's changed my mind about food trucks and hot chicken, but more slaw please!

Yum!  I've visited The Hot Spot a few times now over the weekend at the Elmington Park gathering & boy do they have some tasty, spicy food!

If you can handle it, I recommend the hot burger.  They're a little smaller than the average burger, so 2 may be in order.  Just make sure you have some milk nearby.  The burger comes covered in jalapeños,  cheese, and more.  The fries are pretty tasty as well if you want something that won't set your mouth alight.  The dessert list seems to vary, but they're always quite tasty - that is if you have taste buds left.

(This is a food truck, remember, so you'll have to look up online where they'll be parked for the day)
6USj3cqHAf2HkjBNomafRA: Came back to try the kimbap, it was full of flavour and packed full. Also a great value, you get a lot for the price. Friendly staff as well.

This place is AMAZING! The people who work here are so friendly and nice. And the food is absolutely delicious! I'm from California and am ecstatic I found an authentic korean restaurant. It's delicious! The bulgogi kimchi fried rice is my favorite. Must try!

My co-workers and I always eat at Farmer's Market t on Friday afternoons. I pretty much ALWAYS get Leo's sushi. You can always rely on them for some good, basic sushi. I have tried some of their samplers and my favorite is their salmon, tuna and shrimp rolls. 

I also really like their miso soup but on warm days, you can ask them to substitute it out for their pot stickers. This is one of the only places at Farmer's Market that also carries Coke Zero which is a plus for me. Finally, they always offer delicious samples such as kimchi sushi, yum yum roll, etc. Of course, their sweet smiles always add the perfect touch to this great family owned restaurant. 

The only reason I take off a start is because I feel like their prices are a tad bit more than usual for some simple sushi rolls. 

Visit them and try some great sushi! :)
iTEqQMrPa88swEV-ZR85XA: Best pho and Vietnamese sandwiches in town, hands down. The place may not look welcoming at first glance, but give the place a try, you will not be disappointed.
Food is good and really cheap, but the super sticky floor makes me concerned about the cleanliness of the food preparation area that I can't see. Hence the 4 stars.

I lived in Hanoi for a couple years and this place certainly brought back some memories.  The Bun Rieu was okay, but not great.  The Bun Bo used a poor quality of meat and while the broth was flavorful there was an enormous amount of MSG in it. I had a tofu and vegetable sandwich that I liked a lot even though I'm not sure what the sauce on it was made from. The shop is running it's own bakery (or so it seemed). Local patrons were dropping in to pick up fresh egg custards, fresh pork pies ( Indochine style) and bags of banh mi. The refrigerator in the eating area is packed with wondrous things one would find in Vietnam.  I've long since lost my skill in Vietnamese so I couldn't fully decipher the signs or speak to the staff.  I'm certain this place can do lots of off menu stuff if you know what you want. However, in comparative terms, I would say there are better pho places in Nashville.  Yet, I have never seen another place as homey (in Vietnamese terms) as this one. I wouldn't go back for the soups, but the baked goods and that mysterious refrigerator might get me back in the door. It's definitely worth a visit. Wait staff struggled with English, but I was struggling with Vietnamese so everything worked out eventually.
sway6xsRT_CUC6peK_Pyww: Went  to the Bobby spa... they were excellent great relaxation massage and deep-tissue the ladies were superb and professional

FkoKdgcEPcL2z102J6yEAg: Fresh Dough, yummy 3 cheese blend, and plentiful toppings! Baked wings are delicious too!

CBfW6HPqKP-46zadC1ktVw: Small Store about 1.8 miles from my home. Prices are really good. Meat very affordable and staff is friendly. Nice change from big chain stores. I recommend it!

Small little grocery store not even an 1/8th of the size of most Kroger's or Publix stores. It is very crowded. It is over stocked and it is a little dark and dingy...but the prices are very good!!!

ZLW8u1n776S7maN4KhN6jg: This place is not only affordable but delicious and fresh. I had the bob Marley wrap and it was amazing! Hands down one of the best wraps I have ever had. The hand cut fries on the side were cooked to perfection. This little gem is in an office building and am a little jealous of everyone that works near by.

Ah, so glad that my favorite food truck now has a permanent location. I just wish that it wasn't on the east side of town and only open for breakfast and lunch!

I've visited several times and usually get lunch for myself and take something home for my husband to have for dinner, such as the chicken and dumplings soup or some kind of pork dish. He'll eat anything these folks make.

The menu changes daily, though there are some things that are always on there (usually a burger and specialty tacos, e.g. Korean bbq tacos). There's almost always a vegetarian salad, sandwich, and/or soup, but if there's not, they will happily whip something up, which is nice. In the summer, the squash fritters are fantastic; when it's colder, the tomato bisque with a grilled cheese is wonderful. The bread is all made in-house, so it's no ordinary grilled cheese.

When you go, be sure to save room for a pastry or dessert. Like the bread, they're all made in-house and they are all fantastic. My favorite is a chocolate cookie with a dollop of dulce de leche and also the apple galette. But you can't go wrong with any of them (AND they're cheap, too when compared to what you'd pay at a coffee joint).

It is important to note the operating hours; they're there mostly to service the office workers. But you can get their food at other times on the Riffs food truck.

Gave it try for the first time on Monday. 

I had the Korean BBQ Pork Taco's... they were a home run.  There were so so yummy! 

My only complaint, Guys go buy a couple more table and chairs.... you have room in there. 

Will be back!!
8SqvtaRu9likNDAfAU-HBQ: It was awesome! If you like Americanized Chinese food you will like this. Nothing I had was bad. kPLnhyH2JtpTGQLYEOewiA:
Two Goats provided the most delicious brunch for my daughter's bachelorette weekend. Alicia and Philip were so friendly and went above and beyond to deliver the absolute perfect meal. Our brunch consisted of chicken and waffles, quiche with bacon and spinach vegetable frittata, homemade biscuits, fresh fruit, etc. and everything was absolutely fantastic. In addition to preparing the delicious food, they also cleaned up and left the kitchen spotless. 
I can't stress enough what a fantastic experience it was working with them.

I can't believe we found Two Goats food truck when the Subway across the street was closed - the short rib banh mi that I had was a better sandwich than Subway could make in ten million years.  The short ribs so juicy, the Vietnamese flavors coming together with a rabbit pate, fresh herbs, pickled veggies, and a jalapeno sauce - wowser! These guys know food!

Super fresh and delicious. It was really hard to chose from their menu options because everything sounded great. They seem to have a lot of South American inspiration going on in their dishes, which I really enjoy. Eventually I decided on the corvina ceviche with tostones, and it was fantastic! The ceviche was super refreshing & paired really nicely with the fried plantains. It was a perfect lunch!
jrTMMx95kfFN5fHziUNtpQ: I hesitate to write this review because my husband and I love this place so much, it's like our secret.  Two for one Mexican draft beer and the buffet is fantastic.  There are plenty of gluten-free options, veggie toppings and homemade style sauces not to mention that you can drizzle the whole thing with queso if you want to.  The menu is also great but slightly more expensive than the lunch buffet which can't be beat for $5, ice-cream included!  We also like the fact that the place seems family-run.

FREE 2 FOR 1s ALL THE TIME!  Need I say more?  Seriously...where else in Nashville (or the world, for that matter) can you get two for one drafts, bottles and margaritas ALWAYS?

But aside from the all-day-everyday drink specials (as if I'd need more of an incentive), El Jaliciense has some pretty delicious and authentic Mexican cuisine.  A pretty large portion of the staff at this place speaks fragmented English if any at all, but they're still sweet as pie and pretty fast.  They have the lunch and dinner specials typical of any Tex-Mex restaurant you'll come across, and prices are pretty typical.

El Jaliciense is tucked away in a strip mall on Charlotte, and had it not been recommended to me a couple months back, I never would have given it a chance.  It's a relatively small place with a couple of TVs and about a dozen booths, but it's a great place to dine with your crew.  I just had a few midday beers with some friends there.

I got the number one special (two enchiladas, a taco and rice) with four beers (for the price of two), and I only paid about $12 before tip.  It just doesn't get any better than that.  I would have recommended this place sooner, but I couldn't find anything about it online and had to track it down again to determine the address.  More details to follow!

I was told about this place.  I'm sure if you see alot of the places I review are Mexican Restauraunts.  I love trying different ones out.  But Taqueria El Jaliciense is a buffet!!!  All I could see is unlimited everything and well it is and it is definitly authentic.  There were some things I did'nt know what the heck they were up on the buffet. To answer Greg T's question, YES! I am totally into bowling alleys that haven't been renovated in 30 years. Visited juice bar for a quick smoothie while shopping in the Hillsboro area and had a great experience! The place is clean and bright, they have a variety of smoothies, juices, and açaí bowls, and the servers are friendly and very helpful! They are connected to a poke bowl place that looks pretty good and have a cute covered outdoor area to sit and enjoy your bowl!

Friendly, knowledgeable service.  I asked for something for my allergies and was recommended three different power shots.  I chose the Monster Shot, which opened me right up.  It was a larger sized peppery peppermint essential oils shot which was very good.  I also had the Ginger Greens juice which was amazing.  I can't wait to try the other options.

Jenna is awesome.  Nice meeting you today!
EfJ-1C8PH9TpGTskFqUvhQ: What a great idea - attaching a craft cocktail bar to a music venue.  You can walk through from Marathon Music Works, grab a drink , go back hear some music and glide back again when that craft manhattan runs out.  Really nice, bar and high tops and a little patio..  Smashing!  nice friendly unpretentious bartending.

This is my favorite bar in Nashville, hands down (and I've been to nearly every bar there is, from dive bars and honky tonks all the way to Patterson House). From drinks to vibe, they own it. 

Let me list what I love most: 

1. I've only been on the nights Marathon is not having show, and on those nights, the place is quiet and intimate. Some might say too quiet at times, but if you bring your own group or if you're on a date, it's the perfect space. 

2. The bartenders are amazingly talented! Mark, the manager makes the best drinks in town, hands down, and Zak is new and eager to share his love of the art of mixology! Both love trying out new drinks on friendly and open customers (I love being a bartender's guinea pig). 

3. I love the decor; it's really chic and open and beautiful! 

4. Once we started to become regulars, the staff was beyond amazing. I hosted my boyfriend's surprise party there and they changed the menu so that all his favorite drinks included his name! 

Come check it out and make it your regular place. If you like great cocktails, friendly and creative bartenders, and a lovely space, you will absolutely love it.
I loved this place.  Cool neighborhood, very cool building (looks like a converted mechanic shop).  The cocktails were excellent.
9u2jPq16W7yf_4mQiAhSyQ: I was so happy with my service today that I decided to write a review! The girl who helped me at the drive through window was so nice and super accommodating. According to my receipt Qudriyah helped me. I would recommend this location for sure. My favorite thing to get at Popeyes is the red beans and rice!

Super impressed.  Okay, I had missed the lunch hour rush.  It was now 2:30.  Place was super clean and staff friendly.  My two piece chicken meal was good.  Great chicken albeit could be spicer.  The beans and rice were awesome.

Everyone's got their addictions..... Crack, Heroin, Booze, Gambling...  Mine is Popeye's chicken.  

Alright.  I gave you 5 stars.  I know Cool Springs is whitebred as fuck, but can you fucking open up a location down there already so I dont have to drive across town for my fix?
PfKuhGC46pQjnKgQnts-mA: Surprise of the year! Big time diamond in the rough. Had the special meatball sub and fries with their special sauce, then we played ping pong and drank. Great time, music was loud bring ear plugs. Employees super cool

Great music, food and all around vibe.  I play music there on the regular.  As far as that goes it's a treat.  Very comfortable room, not too big, not too small.  Great spot to go hear local talent, which in Nashville is world class talent.
Great space to squad up. They have karaoke and it's always a blast. Pingpong and pool
4iRzR7OaS-QaSXuvYxEGKA:
What most people call dives whether it be a bar, hotdog stand or cafe, I call home.   This place is actually at 1160 Gallatin Pike S at the intersection of Due West rd and Gallatin Pike and might not come up on your gps if you type in the address listed above.  The owner serves up hot dogs with a variety of toppings in regular and super sizes, don't get the super if you are not up for a 1/4 pound dog!  I noticed a variety of sandwiches, drinks and chips ,You can even have a beer with your meal, this might be hot dog heaven!  I realize the prices are displayed on the board but when I ordered a tea, superdog loaded and chips the owner looked everything over and said "lets call that $5".  I'm pretty sure I made out on that deal!  Some might complain about the decor but it is working class comfortable with odd and funny signs, a pinball machine and what some may say is an "antique" video game.   If you are in Madison and in the mood for a dog, swing on buy and enjoy.  The joint is not in the strip mall but in the lower level of the 5 story office building next to the Nail salon.

Vegwise, I did not see a vegetarian option on the menu!

This place has since moved to Madison Square Shopping Center just down the road from the old location.  They are now located on the left back corner across from The National College of Business and Technology.  Same great food with great prices to match.  I did notice that they now accept credit cards!

This 30+ year-old purveyor of hot dogs, etc., is located in a shopping center / strip mall next to a nail salon. It has no light box sign, but it does have a nice hand-painted sign on its front window. Inside, it's 70s-era wood paneling is packed with the obligatory humorous signs and curios. The owner serves up a very good classic boiled small ($1.70-1.90) or "Super" (1/4 lb, approx. $2.50) wiener in a variety of styles, including regular, saucy (with mustard, sauerkraut and dog sauce [gravy] on it), cheesy, chili-cheesy, slaw, or "special," which combines mustard, kraut, meat sauce, and onions. Other offerings include bags of chips and, for non-hot-dog-lovers, various sandwiches like hamburgers, BLT, beef & cheese, and hot beef. There are some breakfast items to boot, and a pot of coffee on the burner. I like their extra small fountain drink priced at 60 cents (coke, diet coke, sprite, dr. pepper). They also serve the classic Bud, etc. beer selection for 2.00 (small) or 2.25 (large), or 2.25 for a can. There is a few older arcade games, including an early Arkanoid and a vintage pinball machine.
cMBBEj2kbAnndru5Xndg1g: Excellent pizza, especially if you are not in the mood for chain pizza.  Great hole in the wall place.  Delivery is quick and friendly.  A bit pricey, but well worth it.  Best pizza I've had since moving here from CA.  Highly recommend.

This pizza place is located in a gasoline station or to the back side of it actually, BUT OMG..it's the bomb..You can order a large pepperoni pizza with an additional topping for pickup for $7.99 plus taxes...they are QUICK...and Very Very Good!!!
We have ordered from here twice..once delivery and once pick up and both times SPOT ON..
I love Pizza and I love Good Pizza and this is GREAT Pizza..
I would definitely eat here again and again!!!
So I gave them a $10 for my pickup...that was taxes and tip..and Yes I tip for a pickup...someone has to make it for me right????

Can't wait for some additional TUMA pizza, next time I might do some PASTA...looks great on the menu!!!

I've ordered from here often and the pizza never disappoints! The service is VERY fast and friendly. Their BBQ chicken pizza is usually my go to and is so much better than chain pizza places. I've had several other items off the menu and can't complain about any of them. The prices may be a little higher than the chains around, but the quality is worth it. They have specials and coupons attached to their menu and online as well.
ByTZJsTDDhvOYKCj7uoy4w: Truly outstanding in every way.  Staff knows everything about their products.  Helpful, unpretentious, and good wine pairing food.

Very nice wine selection plus eclectic & helpful staff who won't run up a bunch of calls on your cell phone if you happen to leave it behind after having only one glass of the very fine Joseph Drouhin Vero Pinot Noir 2003. They were kind enough to bring us two samples out to try before we made our selection. Aside from the Pinot, the 
Shooting Star Zinfandel 2003 was also very smooth, with a hidden kick at the finish. This place & area reminded me of the Virginia Highlands in Atlanta the way it is just a small house that has been converted into a commercial establishment. Residential neighborhoods are just across the street ... you may even tour through them if your Garmin GPS is feeling like you need to see some sights. They have a nice sized outdoor patio seating area where you can enjoy your wine and people watch the Vandy Candy or whomever else happens to be there when you are.

I went to Rumours on Saturday night for a later dinner with my girlfriend. I was using a half off gift certificate that I had purshased online. Everyone was very friendly and courteous from the get go. We noticed quite a few people dining outside but we decided to eat inside since my girlfriend is a little cold natured. The maitre d really helped out with a wine choice and he seemed to a have a good knowledge of a good number of the wines. A little chalkboard was brought out with the specials was brought out.
  
 We got a 1/2 order crab cake appetizer and a mahi mahi entree which we split.  The crab cake tasted so fresh and the crab in it was of a very mouth pleasing texture.There were some greens also served with the crab cake with a pleasant vinaigrette splashed over them.  The mahi mahi was seperated on two plates for us which was convenient.  It was served over polenta with spinach mixed in. The entree was as good as the entree and all the ingredients seemed to complement each other well.
     
We finished dinner and took a little time to finish our glasses of wine.  I especially liked my girlfriend's wine, which had a bit of a smoky undertone mixed in with it, making it a pretty unique flavor on the palette.  The cook even came out to make sure that we liked our meal and everything was up to par.
     
I was not used to the maitre d tip being seperate on the bill but I didn't mind at all.  Overall it was a very pleasant dining experience with some nice beatles music playing in the background creating a nice ambience.
AeAXSy-FI2rJM451lmoJGw:
This is something tantamount to a spiritual experience and I am shocked that I'm the first to be writing a review.  I've been visiting Izzie's all summer either at their main location at the corner of Riverside and Rosebank or at the East Nashville Farmer's market on Wednesday's.    They freshly make the ices each day with fresh fruit and make them in wonderful combinations that only true genius could conger.  A child's portion is a reasonable $2 and an adult portion is $3.  They tweet out their flavors and satellite locations each day on twitter @izziesice.  

I have to recommend trying the classic lemon at least once or do it in combination with another flavor.  They have the lemon every day and OMG it is this sweet, sour, refreshing experience that  that melts the summer heat away. It reminds me of Polar Cup for anyone who is from Florida and remembers those amazing ices that I used to beg my mother for every day.   So thanks izzie's Ice for settling in so close to my house and thank you Izzie's for giving me a wonderful part of my childhood back that I get to now enjoy with my kids.

Yum! I have been hearing about this place and wanting to try it for awhile. Finally made my way to East Nash to find the truck behind Lipstick Lounge. We got to taste the flavors we were interested in, then got our cups of Plum, Mango and Lemon ice. So smooth and so fresh. I love the bits of plum in my ice. You could tell the sweetness was from the fruit- there wasn't too much added sugar. And it just melts in your mouth. Izzie's and Las Paletas rival for best cold treat on a hot day. We sat out by the truck on the little stumps for chairs and enjoyed our ice outside. Love it!

Izzie's Ice is close to iced perfection in my opinion.  It is lighter than typical ice cream and is absolutely perfect for cooling off on a hot day.

Izzies is a food stand that serves Italian style ices.  The focus is on fresh fruit and the menu changes depending on what is in season.  Some of my favorite flavors are the Watermelon, Raspberry Lemonade, Orange, and the straight Lemon.  They are all incredibly fresh and actually taste like fruit instead of just sugar.  This is probably because they are made from actual fresh fruit (novel idea, I know).  Izzies is one of those rare treats that you don't have to feel guilty about.  I would be lying if I said that I had not counted it as a fruit serving for myself and the kiddo on occasion.      

They are currently set up in the parking lot behind the Lipstick Lounge at the corner of 14th and Woodland.  They also have a smaller cart that travels to events and can be found at the East Nashville farmers market on  Weds. in the summer.  They have recently added hot dogs, black bean salsa, and homemade ice cream sandwiches to the menu.  I usually stick to the ice, but they all look very appealing and it is nice to know that one could make a whole lunch out of a stop.  

If you have not had Izzies Ice, give it a try.  I have a hard time imagining the hardened soul that would not walk away with a huge smile.
14u0W0rX1e_eTkTETrCX8w: My wife and I love the different burrito places around and moes is one of our favorites. I would usually give it a 5 but this location seems to have a tendency to put ingredients in piles instead of spreading them out so you bite into your burrito and get everything g in layers.. If they took the time to spread it instead they would be our favorite instead of blue coast
Great staff, good nachos, has a pokestop, and nice place to hang out and rest at before a movie or trip around the mall.

Very clean, got just what we wanted, fast, friendly. Not sure why the reviews have been so bad, but our experience was great. I don't particularly care for the fact it's inside the mall, but that's just me.
RyIfYlK_Hoa4JeMOMFhZJQ:
Not bad! I'm not going to lie, I always hated the idea of eating here...

Well the sliders with jalapeño cheese are the jam!

My only complaint is the bottom of the buns got soggy before I could get to them - no worries - ill eat much faster next time

i know.  you're thinking "4 stars??  for white castle??"

allow me to explain.  

there is something about the combination of  steamed soggy bread and re-hydrated onions that is addictive.  especially when you find yourself living in a place where this specific flavor is nonexistent.  just as taco bell isn't mexican food - it's taco bell - so white castle isn't a burger and fries.  it's white castle.  it's my guilty pleasure to beat all guilty pleasures.  it is completely satisfying.

I hate to do this. I LOVE Krystal, especially being a Southerner. White Castle is better. It just is. I'm not sure what it is about those things, but they are just better. My wife and I actually did a little taste test. We visited both establishments and we both did a little blind taste test. Unfortunately, we both chose the White Castle burger over the Krystal. Id love to say that it went the other way, but it did not. WC is it. *Fortunately* Krystal still holds the monopoly on most of the southeast and we don't really have a choice. So, most dont even know there is something better.
88CBVHYANgrKdP6Ns9ILlw: The only reason they don't get 5 stars is because they were experiencing staffing issues during my lunch visit. Naz was an AMAZING server. The service was impeccable and food was really good. I love authentic Jamaican food and the spices tasted like a piece of Jamaica in my mouth. Great service, excellent location and really good food.

An unknown treasure hidden away in Green Hills. The food is excellent and the staff are attentive and friendly. I highly recommend this place. I ordered the curried goat (spicy) and it was perfectly spiced, flavorful, and served with the standard rice, cabbage and carrots, and plantains. I cannot wait to return.

We were just looking for a lunch spot after church and found this Jamaican café on Yelp. Reviews looked good, and we've never tried Jamaican before. They have live jazz at night but it was very quiet at Sunday noon. Only one other table being served when we arrived. Very lovely décor, candle light setting. 

We skipped the appetizer and ordered our entrees. Wife got a waffle with fried chicken tenders on top. She said the chicken was very good. I went more Caribbean, ordered fish tacos, which came with a mango, avocado, queso fresco, and additional seasonings, also a very savory sauce--some combination of mayo and tomato base--they were fantastic! 

While we were waiting, they brought us a freshly baked big roll and a pesto sauce that was incredible! Had some pesto left over and our waitress, who is also cook and partner in the business, Erica, put the leftover pesto in a little styrofoam cup to bring home along with the leftover chicken from the missus' meal. I asked and she told me she makes the pesto. 

They do not have a liquor license, but Erica told me customers can bring their own beer or wine and they will serve it. They do have some distinctly Caribbean drink offerings such as licuados, Jamaican sorrell (hibiscus petals steeped like a tea, with ginger added in), and a number of flavored fruit teas such as mango or pomegranate. I went for the pomegranate. It was super sweet--I normally drink unsweet or barely sweetened tea, but this was really great. 

We finished lunch off with a bourbon vanilla bean bread pudding. We had to wait a bit longer than expected for it to come to the table, because it was just coming out of the oven. Each pudding is baked in its own personal sized iron skillet, so it arrived sizzling! A couple small scoops of ice cream accompany the pudding. I dug out a hole in the center of my skillet and just plopped the ice cream on top to form a small white lake. Seriously, this pudding is to die for! We will most certainly be back again.
53A7Yne_S_KizF3i-sDzgg: Heres the deal.  this place is great.  Menu is fine . serivce is fine and the experience is fine will repeat often

I'm a little torn on this one and think it probably warrants 3.5 stars. We made reservations for our 14th anniversary, and noted it on The reservation. When we arrived, we were welcomed and congratulated. Reservations weren't really needed, possibly because it was a Tuesday night, maybe because memorial day was the day before.

Our server brought some complementary champagne and bread. The bread was piping hot and delicious. We ordered the Asian BBQ shrimp, which was good but very spicy hot, more than expected. 

I ordered a simply grilled trout, with butter caper sauce and green beans. The trout was nicely cooked and the sauce complemented it well. Green beans were fine.

For desert I had the key lime pie. Filling was homeade and good, crust was boxed, so overall a little disappointing.

In general, I have to agree with most posters that fish & co is a little pricey for what it is, that the atmosphere is a little underwhelming, and while good, it's missing something. Our experience was good and the staff treated us well, I think was worth the extra star.

I've been meaning to come here for ages and my wife and I finally made it.

The food was fantastic, the service was amazing and the atmosphere was great.

The only negative aspect is that it is just a little bit over priced. Not too bad, though. Maybe 10% higher than they should be...which is negligible I know.
5EGhvgDt_E-JPl4EhQkOtg: Cute little spot in Lennox Village.  Great atmosphere, they even sell books and music.  I love the log cabin feel.  They have ample seating, and a fireplace to boot. Its a great spot.  I'll be back. Oh yeah, and my coffee was AWESOME!  I also had the everything bagel with peanut butter (I had just finished my weekly Saturday run) Yum!

What makes this place great is the service.  Don't get me wrong, the coffee is good too, they just have exceptional people working here.  If you're a frequent visitor, definitely spend the $20 on the ceramic mug.  Refills are $0.75 for life.

Just move to the Lenox area and wanted a close go-to coffee shop other than Starbucks... I just found it! 

The service was terrific. The barista helped me figure out exactly what I wanted and was very personable. I got a large cup of the house blend and was impressed by the quality taste of the brew alone with no cream or sugar. Very well-priced too! I liked the fact that Edgehill used iSquare so I can now use my iPhone to pay for my coffee; so convenient. 

Great, simple atmosphere with large tables, comfortable chairs, and cool art work. The music selection was excellent to provide a relaxing, yet productive environment. Free wi-fi was a must-have for me and Edgehill delivered. 

All in all, this place had everything I hoped for and more considering how close it is to my home. I will definitely be coming back in the near future.
J7Oo9rhwX7O3ak54pu_Mhg:
I don't normally eat fast food, but I used to know someone that was the CEO of both Captain D's and Shoney's....  I used to ask him if he ever ate the food and he said, "Yes, and it's good."

Today, I stopped at one place, and it was closed on Mondays...  silly restaurant.......
So I stopped a few doors down at Captain D's, at noon..  I was adamant. I won't lie..  fast food is the death of humans....
But, they've been advertising flounder.
Today, I decided to try it.
I went in and ordered the blackened flounder with a skewer of shrimp, coleslaw and green beans...  I also ordered the fried oysters and butterfly shrimp.  Total was $12.
I went and sat down and a young lady brought me my meal on a heavy plastic plate.  Not paper.
The food wasn't hot. It was warm.  Palatable.
The amount of food was surprising.  I didn't bother with the thing that looked like a fried submarine, or the rice..  but I ate all the protein and vegetables... and I was full!  Really full..  (also could be from the gluten on the fried shrimp & oysters.. Gluten does blow your belly up to make you look like your about 7 months pregnant)

The meal was good. In a quick moment, it was a very good variety of food.. and it had taste.  I wouldn't do this often... but in a bind, this is as good as a Waffle House as far as fresh food and inventory moving quickly..  

And this particular restaurant was clean and the employees were very nice.  I even got some smiles from everyone.

Good choice for quick food.  This restaurant in particular was a good choice..
Glad Nadine's was closed today.... Otherwise, I never would have stopped here..

Try the flounder..  delicious.
This is one of the nicest, cleanest Capt. D's I've seen.  Food and service are always good, too.

My husband and I normally come here to order take-out, and each time we have been here the service is always fantastic. The employees are always nice and polite, and always give great customer service. Even though we don't eat here a lot, it's always nice to see the restaurant looking good and clean. The food is always cooked to our liking and tastes delicious.

I will definitely continue to come here! :)
FuU2rBUeztCZp8SUNtHcNA: Best food came from miami and realy good Dominican food wao I recommend this place 100%

The place was absolutely amazing all around. The food was well seasoned, and definitely worth a try. They even catered to the English speaking customers by providing an English version of the menu. Very friendly staff.

4.5

I have a good number of Dominican (and, Puerto Rican) friends. A good chunk of them look like me (features, complexion) so when we would go out - usually to a Dominican or Spanish-speaking place - the server(s) would assume that we were all Dominican (Puerto Rican) and cater to us thusly. I say all that to point out that a.) I have been eating Dominican food for years b.) my Spanish is more than serviceable (granted, my accent comes and goes) and c.) I know what I like and how things should taste. La Cocina Dominicana is good stuff.

It should come as no surprise that the restaurant is in Paragon Mills. I didn't know this part of town had a name but as the city gets bigger and more defined, this section has come more into its own. It's a very diverse (not using that word simply to mean 'non-white'; there are people from all over the world in this neighborhood and it shows in the stores and food) area and you can find most any sort of Latin, Caribbean or Arabic food choice (for African go down Murfreesboro and Asian you'll find down Charlotte). There's a small parking lot in front but bypass that. Park in the rear. The restaurant may have started as a gas station but it's a full-on restaurant these days. With a large, bright dining room, there's plenty of room to spread out and seat yourself (I'm hoping that changes; LCD deserves your patronage). When I was there, there were a few latino customers (mostly Dominican but not all) and us. Our waitress was young, personable and fully bilingual. I wanted to dust off my Spanish but thought doing so to an English speaking was beyond-the-pale obnoxious. Don't do this. The menus are in Spanish but some have English translations. If you need one of these menus, ask for it.

The day we ventured in was a bit chilly, so I was looking for something warm and comforting. Unfortunately for me, sancocho was a Saturday item only (I was there on a Friday). I had a taste for bacalao but the waitress couldn't vouch for it. She said the mofongo was bomb so I opted for it. I was a little surprised that the menu offered mofongo and not mangu but that's neither here nor there. They're similar dishes but I would have imagined a Dominican spot would be all about the mangu over mofongo. No worries. I like them both. If you've never had the dish before know this: it takes some time to make. Relax. When it came to the table, it came with a very dramatic presentation. Take it in, take the picture and then set the plantains on the board and start eating. The plantains were fried up nice and the cracklins were delicious. I ordered the mofongo with pork and the meat came cubed and lightly fried rather than shredded. It made it a little hard to mash together with the base but the taste was sensational. Where the dish fell apart for me highlights the fact that I am not Dominican.

Rather than being served with broth, this mofongo was served with salsa rosada -- a lot of it. I've never had salsa rosada with mofongo. Ever. I wasn't expecting it and it bummed me out. Why? I HATE salsa rosada. The isht is nasty and I will never be convinced otherwise. This is how you can tell I have nary a drop of Dominican, boricua or South American blood in me. Salsa rosada (ketchup and mayonnaise) is crazy popular and found in so many inappropriate places. So, for someone else, the slather of salsa rosada on and under the dish would be a welcome surprise, I used many paper towels to try and sop/blot it up. It took away (for me) the otherwise five-star quality of the food. To drink, I got the pineapple juice and it was delicious. It was fresh, not too sweet and not too pulpy. I'd order it again. In fact, I will order it again... on my next visit... where I'll get the bacalao and make sure there's no salsa rosada in sight.

This location needs to be reopened great service and team members please reconsider reopening yhis location
nD-cMgRg5gekeB9qbuEkeA: Decided to give it a shot before heading downtown for New Years Eve. It wasn't very busy, which had us a little nervous. The service was very pleasant, but slow. The food was excellent! Marinaded chicken and Fred's Pasta are fantastic! I would go back for sure :)
Great food!  It is never crowded and you can see some stars there on the right day or night.

I had an AWESOME time today at the bar at Omaha Steakhouse. They had on all the NFL games and even creative names for their NFL theme drink specials!!!! The drinks were the bomb.com especially the Houston Texans Tea Loved it. Also the bartender Nattaly was great we talked and laughed and she made my first time there great!!!
