riBECtbfYcUzDB26Z2pUtw: Been here before but yesterday was extra good!They had pasta with garlic olive oil and scallops.It was amazing it wasn't a overly large portion of food but it filled me up!My husband got an item that was on the menu and it was also good.Service was great but I forgot the young lady's name.

Nice service. We tried the fried calamari and two types of pastas and everything was well prepared. There are two areas to the place (one more casual), allowing for more flexibility for the setting you're in the mood for. Definitely a good addition to the restaurant scene in this part of town.

We ordered take out the first week they were open and it was excellent. I called prior to their opening and spoke with the manager who let me put the order in and pick-up still before the restaurant opened. Food was excellent and the staff could not have been more accommodating. Looking forward to dining in house.
iWE3yZIfQunl1nXevPZrhA: I always try new places and since El Camino is close I thought I would check it out. Very friendly service and I like the set up. If its busy I imagine the table by the front door would not be a great place to sit but maybe they will change it to a lounge. Other than that it is a good place to go. Margaritas and food where good. It is like Taco Mamacita so if you like that place then El Camino's is a nice change of pace. Its definitely a place I will come back to.

We walked up the sidewalk tonight from our building planning to eat at South. We were surprised to see El Camino's open for business. This was only their third night open and if tonight was any indication, this is going to be a good addition to the neighborhood. We started out with a couple of Golden Camino margaritas, pretty decent. The second round was dialed in just right. The chips were made fresh and the queso was ok, would have been much better if it were a little thinner and more jalapeño (then you get 5 stars). 

Now, let's talk tacos!  We had the El Hefe, the Titan, the Mucho Torcho and the Day at the Beach. We were a little surprised to see them all crunchy style but were more surprised at how good they were. For the third night in business...the kitchen is dialed in.  Look out Taco Mamacita...there's some competition coming. 

Thanks to Jaquilin for the great service. 

Welcome to The Row El Camino!
tW-hptQp2G8ShB_In68InQ: Best Chinese food I've ever had! The lunch buffet was just perfect for me , good price and great food! I love the hot chicken with mushrooms, spicy sweet and crispy. Always never enough. Friendly staff and great service!

As has been said, China Spring serves it's purpose.  Sometimes it's a little off, but for the most part, you get exactly what you pay for.  I'm glad it's there and thankful.  The staff is nice.  

Short of the occasional cops in there eating, (cops generally make me nervous, even when not guilty,) and the a-hole customers who think they are special and park in the fire lane, it is a good place!
Delicious food. I eat here regularly at the lunch buffet. The buffet items are definitely better/fresher during the early rather than late buffet hours, but still good. Nice people, too.
DZdE09qqCcX7Atf3Df7Yqg: Greatest version of an egg mcmuffin ever. My daughter ordered it with a runny fried egg and Cheddar cheese plus crispy cherry smoked bacon. We happened in at 8am after dropping a car for repairs two blocks up the street. Wonderful staff, lots of healthy options but I could still get coffee!  The prices are a bit high but the portions are very generous.

A great juice spot in an up and coming neighborhood here in Nashville. Lots of options from juices, smoothies, acai bowls, and protein bowls. Very healthy options. Great for vegans and vegetarians. All of the staff was very knowledgeable and kind. It's a calm environment, so if you want to come and read, study, or just converse with friends, this is the place. Also a good spot for the post workout refueling needs. Definitely will return!

Amazing! Had the Bee Green Smoothie w/ Bee Pollen. 
Outstanding. Will definitely try again when back in Nashville!
HKbRmIZDRqeC6obLG0k_DQ:
Brought back memories, Haven't had JiB in over 30 years. Back in the good old Bronx N.Y.
Gotta say it was GREAT. Too bad we don't have any up in my Neighborhood in Orange County Ny. Had t he Tacos, Egg Rolls, Jumbo Jacks, onion rings, Bacon swiss Buttery Jack. Hell! had it all... Great service and attentive staff at the Drive thru

First time to a Jack In Box restaurant, on 8/11/2012 since I was a little kid in the 70's. The service was excellent and the food was terrific. Anytime we are back in the area ( from Michigan ) we will be stopping by.

Thought I would try something new we don't have this place around my home. I decided on the 1 combo burger and fries it also comes with a drink. There are many options on menu from regular fries to curly. Few different shakes. This is a Military friendly location.
g2l_SUXnpATBiRBQC1NB1A: Nashville actually has several options for Ethiopian restaurants, but this one is unique: very low prices (many $6-7 entrees), large servings, and very delicious food.  It's just too bad that it's hidden next to Music City Thrift where it can't even be seen from Nolensville.  A true diamond in the rough for those in the know.

Yep.  It's all that.  I'm usually a restaurant hopper, never eating in the same place twice.  And if I do, never eating the same dish twice.  But since there are not very many Ethiopian restaurants it's not hard to narrow down a favorite because Abay has the best tasting hands down.

As so far I have only compared this to Gojo, I'd say for the Nolensville area, I enjoy this more.  You definitely get more food for the money, its less crowded, and the sauces seem to have a bit more complexity (especially the beef sauces).  Lots of vegeterian options as well.  Definitely something to check out.  My girlfriend and I usually just split one of the sampler dishes as its enough for two.
ctu_fZxCRoQKRj4kUtOitg: Best Chinese take out in Antioch. I have a great experience every time I go in. Food is fast and correct.
Just good Chinese take out. Always easy to order and always ready when they say it will be.
My wife and I love the food. They are willing to make it the way you request if possible. Staff is very friendly. We go here about 2 times a month. We are never disappointed.
RdwDvJQBFZIoLexSu--OHw: I have had take out from here a few times and it has all been very good!  Have never had the delivery service though.  The pizza was exactly what was ordered and was hot, fresh, and tasted very good.  They were very quick with the order too!  I was in the area and called it in and they had it done in less than 15 mins as they promised on the phone as I walked in the door in exactly that time the first time I ordered.  They have been friendly each time I have been there too.  The only down side to anything I found was the first order I placed they didn't get the name on it but the order was correct and delicious.  I encourage you to give them a try and they do have some very good tasting pizza and are not stingy with the toppings!

I know its a chain but so what!? They make great pizza here. I love the meatball supremo and the 'turbo' crust is a nice touch (basically butter and romano cheese is spread over the edges). Each time I've been here the staff is really friendly and to me that's surprising since the majority of people there are young high school or just out of high school kids who typically juat aren't the friendliest. The salads are fresh and huge for the price. I appreciate the easy online ordering.

This pizza is the best I have ever eaten. 
I get the supreme deep dish. Absolutely the best.
EBKLpGREqUFj-fpIB85pPw:
When I moved to Lenox Village last year, it had almost everything I desired within walking distance. There were all the casual restaurant types I needed - Mexican, Pizza, burgers, dessert. There was a decently-priced gas station a block away. There was a pet grooming shop, a dry cleaner, and a small doctor's office. There was a neighborhood pub with karaoke on Saturdays.

The one thing it did not have was a coffee shop, and boy did it need one. Other than a couple Starbucks that required a drive of several miles in Nolensville/Old Hickory traffic, this area was a veritable coffee desert. 

That's why I was incredibly excited to see Grounds 4 Inspiration open its doors late last year. From my first visit in November, the staff has been friendly, the lattes have been quality for the price, and the location cannot be beat. 

The coffee drinks are standard variety - Lattes, Cappuccinos, Mochas, etc. - but they are well-made with Vienna Roasters beans and capable baristas. The baked goods are tasty, and while I haven't yet tried their lunch offerings, word on the street is that they hold the line well.

My one wish with this shop is that they might somehow create a space for live acoustic music on Friday and/or Saturday evenings. The location and layout of the room would be perfect for a singer with an acoustic guitar to play. There's a ton of musicians in Lenox Village that might welcome an opportunity to play close to home (me included). Just a thought.

Love this place. I go everyday coffee is always great and their breakfast sandwiches are awesome. Michelle is awesome and takes the time to greet and learn everyone's name. Cannot wait till they bring back the lunch menu. Also wish they were open on Sunday : /
The chicken salad  is great - so tasty!  Very friendly atmosphere and definitely a nice gathering place! I'll definitely be back to try more items from the menu.
UjxQP7wWh8P3mblDM4lrTg:
I have a thing for great pretzels. I like them soft on the inside, a nice texture with a bite on the outside, and I like them to have that certain pretzel flavor. Auntie Anne never lets me down. Ever. 

The manager encouraged me to try the pretzel dough wrapped hot dog. Are you kidding? No way. Yes way. I tried it. It was fresh from the oven, freshly baked awesome dough...it made the hot dog perfectly cooked without being overdone. All I can say is...I want another one.
sdUQjlWN9y6yowVcvqomnw: First time getting food from them.  I went with them originally because they deliver. I ordered the spaghetti with meatballs dinner and cannoli for desert.  The dinner wasn't the best I ever had, but it was pretty good.  The cannoli was even better.  I will order from them again.

Just noticed this spot so checked it out for some hangover recovery.  Not much as far as a sit down and eat place,  pretty small.  However,  knowing that they deliver I'll be getting this more often.  I had the Cajun chicken Alfredo and it was really good.  Not what you'd expect from a little hole in the wall place off of Nolensville Rd.  Fried mushroom were decent.  I'd say for what it is... I'll be back terminator style to merk some marinara.

I was nervous at first. I'm not a huge Italian food fan but I am a girl who likes to eat and once my pj pants are on I'm not going anywhere. They had me at we deliver. To date this is what I've had:

Cheese Ravioli - awesome! Tons of melty cheese and stuffed with ricotta.
Meat Ravioli - wasn't a fan of the filling but also covered in cheese and the sauce was good.
But my favorite.. I'm about to order it again today!..
The Italian wedge sandwich. I wasn't sure what these are but its like a pocket sandwich made from some kind of thin crispy yummy bread. It's so good. I'm not sure why people haven't scored them higher all 3 deliveries were quick and they were super nice. Order was always right. What more could you expect ?
wO0NI7NkDdbT46h-TI2BtA: Food and beer selection is extensive. Food is great for brewery! Staff was super nice too. :) Glad they have eat Nash location now. Wish they'd bring back the watermelon beer though!!

I decided to purchase a Living Social pizza and beer voucher as an excuse to give this new east side location a try.  We stopped in on a rainy Saturday night and the place was packed.  The brewery is in a new building, next to a nail salon and dentist and is a more intimate setting than their other locations. First thing I noticed was how bright the lights were.  Upon entering you queue up at the side of the bar to order where a large blackboard hangs up high listing the many beer selections.  Being the first day of fall the pumpkin beer game was strong with at least five varieties.  It was a little awkward having the board up so high and trying to see the selections before reaching the bartender to order.  We tried the large veggie pizza along with a nitro peanut butter milk stout and a maple pumpkin porter.  We enjoyed both beers, their beer menu changes seasonally so there will always be something new to try.  The pizza was good too, but I'm not sure it was $24 good. (Seems high for a 16" veggie pizza.)  The taproom has a long bar with several TVs behind it and several more around the room.  Definitely playing up the sports bar aspect as there was football on every tv when we visited.  I'm glad to see this new addition to the neighborhood so I'm being generous on my rating, they're new to the 'hood and still getting their footing. Tailgate beer lovers will be happy to have a home now on the east side. I'd love to see the lights toned down so it isn't so glaringly bright.  And we felt the food costs were high for what we got.  If there's some way to get the beer menu board lowered to a height that's more eye level it would help too.  Glad to have them here and hope with a few changes they'll be even better.

With the craft beer scene in Nashville growing at such a fast pace, it's nice to see TailGate expanding to East Nashville. The taproom is smaller than their brewery/taproom location in West Nashville, but I was pleased to see so many tap handles. The lunch special is a great one, especially if you can swing a beer at lunchtime - a pizza & a pint for $10. Make sure you pay attention to parking - their lot is next to the building, not behind it.
FYIL5duVgc9eLyYw6hobmw: Stopped in for a light dinner.  Most of the item on the menu looked too large.  I reluctantly ordered the shrimp and grits because it appeared to be one of the lighter items.  After having shrimp & grits in Charleston SC, I've found no one to ever get close.  To my surprise this was one of the best, if not the best S&G's I've ever had.  Service was great and prices are equal to the quality.  Actually the prices are very reasonable.  I would recommend.

This restaurant was great. Service was outstanding and the food was very good. My husband had a pork chop that we agreed was the best either one of us has ever had. I had the trout which was also very good and flavorful. Atmosphere also gets 5 stars because of the great outdoor rooftop seating. We will be going back.

Matt and Kelly gave such great service. Steak was cooked to perfection. Strong cocktail knowledge. Amazing soup. Only gripe is the oysters needed to be shucked a little cleaner, but they were good oysters.
cdPjDZubPvok0VHv654Tvw: A late night in lab brought me to dine at Subway on 21st. Honestly, it was a great sandwich. I got the Veggie Delite on 9 grain honey oat bread. Honestly delicious! And after 5 p.m they have a special of buy two footlongs for $5 a piece. Excellent deal if you bring a friend. Staff was super efficient and friendly!

I really enjoy taking a stroll during lunch or on a weekend while I'm at work to visit Subway to get my nosh on. On the surface of the onion this Subway is not unlike the others in the area when it comes to cuisine but I must say that interior and the efficiency of the staff is what I most enjoy.

They recently made some upgrades making the space larger and more open. But you get the garden variety of people watching especially around lunch time so it is often worth dining in!

Subway with a drive-thru? Sure, why not?

I have been on the run quite a bit lately and good ol' Subway has been an all too convenient option for this broad.

I stick with the veggie patty on toasted wheat bread loaded up with all the veggies and doused with the Sweet Onion Sauce and I'm pretty set.
GvRA736fSqNZPe_OJRCt-w:
Ohmigod I'm about to channel my inner Rachel Ray, but all I can say is:  Yum-o!

Ok, considering how much I  hate RR and her EVOO, this is high praise.  I love the food here - there's always some freshly prepared salads if I'm in a hurry (try the "Pranzo" salad with grilled chicken - raspberry/citrus-y deliciousness), hand-prepared sandwiches made when I order them (shrimp salad on multi-grain - a culinary siren I can't resist), or the specials of the day, served piping hot and plentiful.

Also, they have their own custom blend of coffee shipped in from the roaster (on the Jersey shore, natch), and a plentiful assortment of baked goods that killed any last low carb leaning I had.

And the best part - they don't gouge you at the register.  The food is fabulous, fresh, and fairly priced.  Try them - they are totally worth it!
Used to be an overpriced chain here. Now a warm cafe with great coffee and an overwhelmingly inexpensive Italian lunch and breakfast menu. Lasagna if the day? Come on!

Love, love, love this place.  You never know what's going to be on the menu, but the pastas are simply amazing.  It's one of the most unique lunch spots in Midtown.  The staff is honestly one of the kindest, sweetest groups of people you'll ever meet.  I never can remember the name of this place, but I certainly know how to get there!
2Zhkl-UenNSc7XkdeG112g: Had their spinach stromboli which was very good but after I ate it I wondered if I should've ate it. Why? It was very garlicky indeed, delicious yes, but not advised if you're sitting next to someone on a plane for an hour or more as you will have what I call dragon breath. I ate a pack of mentos afterwards but it didn't phase the constant taste of garlic in my mouth at all. The second downfall is, you get spinach wedged in your teeth so be advised, you'll need tooth picks, a tooth brush, and mouthwash after eating one of these. But again, it was very good.
d6nR5JXQTx105DrEt_ZixQ: Visited them for the first time today and I must say they have the best creole food in town. The staff is so friendly helpful with the menu.We were allowed to sample several tasty items before settling on the drunken chicken and red beans and rice with a side of corn stew. The portions are large and delicious. We will definitely be back soon!

It's great value and taste. The Drunken Chicken was a nice blend of spices and heat and the chicken was moist and tender. The Shrimp Etouffee was very good and the shrimp itself was not over cooked and very plentiful.

This place is great! Owner was super nice. They let you sample 1 or 2 dishes before you order if you want. I tasted the drunken chicken and voodoo chicken. Both were delicious. I went with the drunken chicken and it was sooo good. Great price for a big bowl of food. Food comes out super fast too. Looks like all the sauces are just stewing in pots in the kitchen and they just put whatever you order over a bed of rice. I'll be back! This might be a weekly visit for me. If you like Cajun food you gotta try this place. I was born and raised in Baton Rouge LA and I give this place a .
8F6OsZYrl3LbR17teMJWJQ: After leaving work around 9 pm, I was hungry and wanted something quick.  Sonic to the rescue.  I ordered a cheese burger wirh mayo and mustard.  What I got was completely different.  But a jacked up sonic burger is bettet than NO sonic burger.  Always fast always good.

Who goes to the Juice + Yelp Community Manager Yelp Event and guzzles delicious and nutritious bottles of kale/, carrots, apples, and beet only to hit up the Sonic  drive-thru later that evening to appease a tater-tot craving?

Yup.
Me.

I am vegan and avoid fast food like the plague but when I need some salty, crispy potato goodness, I hit up Sonic for a large tater tot ($2 with tax) and reserve the guilt for the next day. 

Nothing worse than a tater hangover.... everybody has their vices and this happens to be one of mine!

Every time I've driven by this place is open. I assume its 24 hours but I can't confirm that. This is my late night, coming home from downtown, stop to get some grub. It has a drive through, which is good for those grab-n-gos, but they also have the drive-in as well. 

I love their Frito Chili Wrap up thing, the popcorn chicken, chicken toaster club and, of course, the Sonic Blasts. Never had a problem at this one, whether I'm there for the Route 44 Cherry Limeade or a meal.
VaPj2Mpu0IpsUoisyTCAUg: I LOVE everything about That Awesome Taco Truck. The food is fresh, flavorful and just so good! The street corn is a perfect starter or side. If the tamales are available -- get one! The tacos are satisfying and worth every penny. Definitely recommend.

I only tried one taco here, so my review is not a complete picture of this taco truck by any means. I visited during Tomato Fest, and had dinner elsewhere before checking this spot out. Given the fantastic reviews, the small menu, and the adorable truck, I just had to try something from the taco truck. I went with the sweet potato taco, and tried their chipotle salsa on the side. The taco was really tasty, although incredibly pricey- $7 is a lot for just one taco. It was good, don't get me wrong, but I don't think I would pay the $7 for another one of the sweet potato tacos. I would, however, try a different taco or a quesadilla next time. The salsa was delicious and I would recommend trying it- not too spicy, but packed with that smoky chipotle flavor.

Courteous crew and food served in the time they say (5 mins = 5 mins).  Lobster roll was very good (approved by the New Englander in our group) and if you get the Chicken/Carrot Taco, you MUST add the egg.  Messy but delicious.
u1ELaa77voUfj-2GCOIUmQ: Great food!  Very affordable for a big family. I highly recommend this place!  Check it out!

Not as good as a proper sit-down restaurant, but they're not trying to be.  What it IS is a lot better than standard fast food.  Most of the menu items are vicious calorie bombs, but they're tasty and fresh.
Such friendly service and very good food. Good variety too. And they really keep the breadsticks coming!  A great bonus was that I saw their health code and it was a 99.
fRdJbbQk05UctAAX8v4uFA:
Open till 3 Am ! 
1/2 pound burgers,
 Jumbo wings ! 
Love their Philly Steak and chicken Philly sandwiches!
 
Good for delivery or carry out !
This is a amazing restaurant ! I have been coming here for years ! The food is amazing and you get a diverse variety. I recommend the calzone, you will not be disappointed !!!
Great pizza and wings :) ordered the food through Grub Hub.... got the mild wings and pesto chicken pizza delivered right to my  door
Y4ekZBFgSauDaVOqI52vPQ: I have long been a fan of the fish tacos from the Berry Hill location. This new taqueria next to Vanderbilt serves the same kind of food at the same affordable prices as their other locations. Also worth noting, they offer catering. Minus one star because the service is not as fast or efficient as Baja Burrito, though I'll give them the benefit of the doubt for now since they just opened.

I basically wrote a review on my check-in. If I can figure out how to copy and paste it, I will. We love this place and are excited about the new location! Excellent menu (love the seasonal soup, baja fish tacos, and kids' meals). Price is right and they have great parking, too!
Really is the best burrito of all the burrito places that are similar... If have tried Moes, Chipotle, And Freebirds you probably will agree.
LFw9ptRD4_R8RXcZhZYmRA:
3.5 stars
Not the fanciest place for dining in. But their great for cheap eats. Basic menu you would typically find at a food truck spot. Business hours they stay open pretty late. To go orders you can drive up to the curb and one of the ladies will approach your car for orders. And the food will be brought out to your car. 
They have the cantaloupe drink here that comes in a extra large cup for $3. Definitely a favorite that has us coming here just for the drinks.
great food, opened late. service is a bit slow, but the food makes up for it. best place for tortas!

Great little gem on Nolensville, meats are grilled outdoors on wood fire, had carne asada and pastor tacos with rice and beans....very tasty....friendly service, delivered food to car after I ordered at window
JhWdq1s_kN-5N98rl-fc6A: Sylvan Park, WHERE HAVE YOU BEEN ALL MY LIFE?!! well at least where have you been since Ive lived here!? You ever had food so good that it made you make a life decision on if you should go back for another plate and look like a fat bastard or just settle with the lingering thought for the rest of the night on whether you should have went back for seconds? Well this place had me fiending like a crack head for another fried fish plate! My wife and I ordered fried fish, macaroni, two buttered rolls, green beans, some greens and potato salad for Sunday dinner.....LET ME TELL YOU THAT EVERYTHING WAS DELICIOUS SONNN!!  Only problem was we waited to late to order another meal cause they close at 2pm Sundays!!! LAWD LAWD LAWD!!!! i was devastated!!!! But it was ok cause we ordered from them the following day!..lol I don't know about any of the other Sylvan Park restaurants but this one here....this one here....THIS ONE RIGHT HERE SONNN IS OFF THE CHAIN!  Now i haven't sat down and ate inside this establishment so I can't comment on the customer service, so maybe later i will update on that issue, but if your looking for some down south GOOD food this is the Joint you need to venture out and try!!

This is pretty much your standard meat and 3 prices are cheap under 10 plus a drink. The menu varies but it your 3 sides and choice of meat such as meatloaf or fried chicken. I had the meatloaf it was moist not too dry and the sides of mashed potato and cream corn and greens were ok. The best part was their desserts a chocolate meringue that was yummy.
3XoqdiwUGX81F3rsRNkNbA: Absolutely wonderful.    This place is gonna be a big hit.  I don't know what else to say but thanks for coming to Green Hills.

We just had lunch and food was great! Lunch specials include 2 sides for $13.00. It was plenty of food and service was great as well.  Looking forward to coming back for dinner.  BTW.... Michael was our server.
Wow! First time at Char for our Anniversary. Very impressed! Food, service, ambiance were all great. Nice but not fancy. Reasonably priced. Highly recommend.
zS2T7gQNhfUDBi2Y2_fbWw: Food was actually great and the service was also great. An oddly pleasant experience!

Didn't expect much walking in but was immediately sat by the manager and he ended up waiting on us also. The wait-staff was all very nice, friendly and helpful with minor requests. The catfish was much better than expected. It was properly cooked and not overly seasoned and the salads were fresh and freshly washed. The restaurant was clean, the prices were great. We were impressed and will stop there again of we're in the area.
e3UoWkjs3RuHSntfGK9R1Q: Tried the fish tacos today. Best fish tacos I've ever had to date anywhere!!!! Been to Jalisco about 10 times now and have never had anything not excellent.

Definitely wouldn't have imagine this place being super good by the look of it. Place had about 6 tables, pretty small place. No other customers were there till we left because it was around 3pm. We drive by this place everyday since my boyfriend lives near by, I'm glad we finally stopped though. Menu wasn't big but that's okay, it's better to not have so many options. Menu on the board must have been a special because there was a dinner menu they give you when you're seated. Their kitchen was the food truck outside of the building. We got steak tacos and fish tacos. They were so fresh, not over seasoned to the point of salty, and both were cooked perfectly. Will definitely come back and work my way through the menu.

Good traditional Mexican food....the atmosphere is clean and the service was great...I got a simple chicken taco, fish taco with a side of rice and beans. The fish taco for some reason was a little soggy so I didn't like that but would eat there again...
pAVL6huhX8C9tAgaOpNPzQ: We sampled the tamales and pineapple drink during the TIRRC food crawl and they were wonderful!
Autentica comida Mexicana. We had the menudo and the caldo de pollo. The tortillas are home made. Everything was excellent.

Small cafe serving Latin food.  Tortas, tacos, tamales, pupusas, quesadillas, sopitos, and more. Chicken, pork, tongue, beef meat available. They sell bottled sodas, cane sugar instead of corn syrup.  The food is fresh, made to order.  The servings are big.  You get three different sauces and a vinegar cole slaw with your food.  I had to take half my food to go.  I had sopitos, one with beef and one with pork.  The beef was my fave.  I also had a bean and cheese pupusa that i ate later, cold, and it was still good.  It's incredibly inexpensive.  There is a pastry case. The service was really good. A lunch time win.
NvJg4ysrB7J0hrsb2A0gUg: Yes, this is fast food but, the employees were all nice and helpful and eager to please. The food was good and the fries were hot!! No complaints here :)

I love Jack in the Box! I am absolutely positive it is because I only get to eat at one when I am on
vacation. The food is just OK, the service is meh, but I love the big round white headed clown guy!
4Rz9e2RmP7ErILpcls-n2w:
Delicious and innovative food at reasonable prices (especially at lunch).

Chef Brian Hainley prepares the corned beef himself in house and uses it in one of the most delicious Reuben sandwiches ever.

If you're looking for something with more of a Southern flair, try the ridiculously rich Carolina Shrimp and Grits.

The flourless chocolate cake is surprisingly light with a mousse-like consistency. The cake is served with ice cream made in-house. I think Pistachio is the default flavor, but there are sometimes other choices like Butter Pecan.

The atmosphere and service are comfortable and genteel without being fussy or pretentious.

All in all, highly recommended. Nashville is lucky to have a lunch restaurant of this caliber.

Still a great place for lunch, but Belle has gone more casual.

In a way, this is good (more affordable).

But it's sad to see that Chef Brian has removed some of the more ambitious items from the menu.

The burger stuffed with short ribs? Just a memory now.

The amazing fried chicken? Heartbroken to report that it has disappeared.

Apparently Belle does dinner once a month on Thursdays and some of these old favorites are resurrected.

Until then I'll just have to try to visit on Saturday (fried chicken tender and waffle day)!

Service is still lovely. The fruit tea is still spectacular.

I have been to Belle twice for brunch now with my mom who is a serious NYC foodie, i.e., finding restaurants outside of NYC that meet her expectations is extremely difficult. But to my surprise, my mom liked Belle enough that she requested to go back there with my dad and fiance during her stay in Nashville this time.

The Fried Green Tomatoes are reliably delicious and the Belle Benedict is made with two perfectly poached eggs and a side of potato hash-cheese-cream goodness. The MaBelle's Meatloaf--just like mama makes it...but better. The Carolina Shrimp & Grits are amazingly creamy (but might be just a tad more amazing if they used jumbo shrimp). The vegetable panini and bread pudding were perhaps a little disappointing, relative to the rest of our meal. But, by the way, the mimosas are made with fresh squeezed orange juice.

Overall, this is a great place if you're looking for a place that is a little nicer than your usual brunch/lunch spot and you need to impress your hard-to-please out-of-town visitors.
soGLEIcXxTmg7vZ-1C58SQ:
J. Gumbo's has now opened at Peabody Corner. It is terrific! I especially love the Drunken Chicken. It's got a kick, but you won't be sweating the whole time you're eating it. I love how you can taste test things before ordering. Great food, prices and service! 

Nuvo Burrito looks to be moving in soon too! A great addition to this side of downtown.

Quiznos and Gumbo place are awesome! Not a fan at all of Nuvo burrito. It's gluten free, wheat free bullcrap. Never tried the chicken place but from what I hear it's awesome but the wait time takes a while.

This little market is pretty unique I would say. You don't see many food courts outside of a mall. I work nearby and it's very convenient. It gets really busy around lunch time due to all the construction in the area. Haven't tried all the restaurants in there yet but the Mexican place is pretty great. Definitely worth checking out.
uy_x3_mSkzcjod2fM1ZdRw:
As avid fans of The Bachelor franchise, we knew we had to stop in on our trip to Nashville. This place is the CUTEST!!!! 

The first thing I noticed when I walked in is the decor. You can tell they put a lot of thought in to the design of the space and it shows! So cute and very instagram-able for us millennials. ;) 

They have a great breakfast menu ranging from crepes to breakfast tacos and everything in between. My sister and I had the breakfast tacos and our friend had the breakfast crepe. We all loved our food and the coffee was on point too! The prices were also very reasonable.

5/5 would recommend!!
They have the best patio in the Nations, a small but very good beer list, and absolutely the nicest, greatest staff around. Great spot for coffee or a beer after work.

I was very excited to hear that a RB was opening up in the Nations, only a short drive away from my home, and my excitement was justified. First off, I love how spacious the building is. There is a small porch patio, a large dining area inside, and even better, a large dog patio with chairs and fake grass to boot! There are plenty of outdoor seating and a small covered cabana. While it's a bit too hot now (and too many flies around) for outside seating to be enjoyable for more than 20 minutes, I'm excited to visit again when the weather gets a bit cooler. 

The Food: Over my last 2 visits, I ordered the caprese crepe (they have Gluten Free batter, hallelujah!) and a cold brew. Both were good, although the crepe was a little on the drier side. The balsamic glaze was perfect, however. My favorite thing so far though, was their GF peanut butter and jelly oat bar. I wasn't expecting much but holy moly, it was SO DELICIOUS. The bar was sweet but had the right amount of tartness from the jelly and it just crumbled in your mouth. I believe it might sell out quite quickly, as it was already sold out on my second visit. GF or not, try it!
tc-jxeGNjIo4DsYW0wj5Dw: Always friendly - can recommend the veggie burger and catfish sandwich. Both taste great with generous portions.Food can take a while but it's always fresh and worth the wait.

Very nice people in charge of this establishment and good service, if a little slow. Spicy fries, cheese sticks, chicken strips... They have some pretty good choices and the milkshakes are amazing. Really the only place near Brick Church and Trinity Lane worth eating. Some of the best fast food that Nashville has to offer.
Very delicious homemade hamburger. Taste so champions as heaven! I recommend u go there.
f3umcbFgGLVUx1R7iz9Qnw: I stumbled upon this small cluster of business, I absolutely LOVE the little patio area, with the concrete checker board tables- I plan on making it a regular stop on Wed afternoons for my favorite Latte, or lunch or both.  I will start bringing my Chess pieces in case anyone wants to enjoy a casual afternoon game of chess / or backgammon.  Informal at this point but look for me there!  (the one with the bag of chess pieces, Wednesday afternoons)

Riverside Village is an amazing example of the potential to take an old dilapidated corner building and restore it in to a vibrant storefront of community owned businesses.   One of the most sustainable things we can do as a culture is repurpose and when I see it executed  to the extent that the surrounding neighborhood experiences the benefits of said restoration, it's a thrilling thing.

Located at the corner Riverside Drive and McGavock Pike, it currently houses Watanabe (sushi), Mitchells Deli, Sip Coffee and ice cream shop, Bagel Face Bagels, Olive and Sinclaire chocolates, Castrillos pizza, East Side Music and a salon (can't remember name.)   There is a wonderful patio area for the community with art and the like that often plays hosts to a variety of patrons from the various businesses. Because of the success of the area, the entire intersection has began to flourish with the addition of my favorite pub, Village Pub and Beer Garden and an awesome store dedicated to creativity repurposing items,  OMG (old made good).

I am really proud to have Riverside Village literally a hop, skip, and a jump away from my home. 

Mitchell's Deli is my personal anchor in this revitalized area that also includes Watanabe, and Sip Cafe too. With Village Pub, Old Made Good, Castrillo's Pizza along with a few other recently owned independent businesses, Riverside & McGavock has come a long way since I moved here in 2010.
rKRNzDQk1WsP30wVu1H0FQ: I love this place, most authentic Mexican food at a very reasonable price. Me and my partner love going here for quesadillas. I love their suadero quesadilla that is like a Mexican brisket quesadilla. If you are feeling adventurous you should order a quesadilla de Huitlacoche or flor de calabaza. Their aguas frescas are great too and they even have raspados which are a Mexican version of scones but the syrup is made with real fruit. Did I mention that their tortillas are hand made?!

Great tacos at a great price, went here on a whim as we were stopping on a road trip at the local aquarium store. The tacos were very authentic and the horchata was good. The interior was a little lacking but was made up for by the food. The waitress was very kind and attentive as well. If I am ever in the area again I would make a stop here!

This is a great Taqueria in a part of town where there are many that I consider average. The meat quality here is really high and prices are affordable. I keep coming back because the staff is super friendly and everything is fresh. Would highly recommend you check it out.
RVqj00jeAxTtopbgRSbsmQ:
A tasty breakfast can be had here.
During our stay at the Sheraton Music City Hotel we had breakfast at the Old Hickory Grill. The service was excellent and the food very tasty. I got eggs, sausage and wheat toast and my plate left nothing lacking. I enjoyed every bite.
I would have tried it for dinner if I hadn't had another engagement and I saw crabwitch on the menu which looked like a winner but then there's next time. I'll keep this place in mind.
Great friendly service. Amazing food. The drink menu offers unique tiwsts on classics. Food menu has something for everyone. Salmon was top tier.
Excellent service. Had the black and blue burger which was great. Ann the manager really treated us well. Mena the waiter was exceptionally good.
ybKS2qB9MIST0Hl_7ZEDkQ:
I can't wait to go back here!! Thanks for your reviews on when this is (day/time) that was really helpful!

I love this idea of farm to table; you can even see pictures of the farm at the counter where your food is from. I ordered the chorizo/white cheddar crepe with tomato relish on top and a side of potatoes. YUM! It was really delicious. I do recommend getting a side if you get the crepe because it is on the small side, but it is right up there in quality as Marche (and I do love their crepes). I also ordered the honey biscuit with homemade whipped creme and nutella. WOW. That's all I can say. Definitely order this, you will not be disappointed. This gave the Biscuit Love truck a run for their money (you guys are great too!). 

Overall I'll definitely be retuning to a Sunday meal at the Farmer's Market. I love it there and hey you can pick up a few other items after eating. It was a great Sunday Morning. 

Ps. Downside (if any) no coffee. Butter Cake Baby (I think that's the name?) has coffee but bring cash.

I had the bacon and white cheddar omelet- amazing!  I had never heard of the place before- just popped in the food court today seeking a cure for my hangover.  I always approach a trip to the farmers market as an exercise in patience- be ready to fight it out for a parking spot, wait in line, wait for your food, and bus your own table.  But the high quality meal, the friendly staff and chef, and reasonable prices definitely make up for the wait.

As soon as we heard about Speckled Hen, we knew we had to give it a shot. Because we'd heard that the lines get long, we made sure to arrive five minutes before 10 and were second in line.

The first thing I liked about Speckled Hen was the service. Even though everyone was busy getting things in order, they had a smile and a hello ready--and a warning that my chorizo'd biscuits would take a while because they thought they had plugged something they hadn't. No biggie; we were happy to wait and see what they were up to.

We had the "Nutella" toast, biscuits and chorizo gravy, omelette, and iced tea. It didn't matter that the servings were small because the prices were also small to match. We felt like we got a lot of good bites of yummy food. For the price point, you feel like you're getting exactly what you paid for.

My only issue would be the fact that my slice of bread for the Nutella had more holes than dough! But that just meant less carb consumption--and I was willing to look at the glass half full considering how tasty everything was.

Overall, Speckled Hen is a fun experience you should check off the list if you live in Nashville.
2bKliGwbKrEyXNf61vPxmw: I enjoyed the tackiness of this establishment. With a classy name like copa cabana, how could you really go wrong? From the flashing tabletop beer tower to the child who tried to sell us fundraiser candy bars at our table (quickly ushered out by management FYI) to the bad 00s R&B playing overhead, Copa Cabana was delightfully cheesy and a good time was had by all.

This place is not what you would expect. But still great! Just a chill establishment with good beer and the best quesadillas you've ever had!!! Great place just to kick back, drink beer, play pool or darts, meet people while eating killer quesadillas. Do it.

This place is the best dive bar. I'm in town for work and the bartenders shake your hand and introduce themselves. Drink specials are big and cheap and the food is really good on the bar food side. Also if you go for poker it's really fun.
lxCZPleW-CZ4_fJaNQMUCQ:
Amazing assortment of food for lunch. Quante atmosphere and easy to locate. I will definitely come back here. 
Great place to have a lunch date, we had a Valentine's date today...

Yay! Vandy finally has a vendor for Asian food! They sell Pho soup and Bahn mi, which is limited but its a great start!!! And if you're a student, this counts as a meal on the commodore card or if you're visiting, they take credit cards but not cash. It's located on the Vanderbilt campus in Alumni Hall. And they now have bubble tea :)

First of all, if you're a vanderbilt student like me, this place is the absolute best.

I kinda wish their hours were longer, but then again, vandy dining is really bad at that.
So, for all my vegetarians, this is probably the only place you can get vegetarian pho. Apparently, veggie broth in pho doesn't exist, so..... that sucks. BUT, over here it does!

I love their pho and boba! I think it took me some time to get used to the boba here (because it honestly doesn't taste like milk tea boba ), but the taste grew on me and I can't stop eating here. I come here every single day!

So glad this option exists for me and the other students. If you're on the meal plan, I'm sure you have checked this place out. It's fantastic.
NpIHuh0tMIZ-6EDmmwHvZA: More of the top tier of fast food burgers. They don't mess up my orders anywhere near as often as the other "guys" near by. The food doesn't feel as mass produced as the other places.
Customer service is great. Parents messed up. I went back for the order to be fixed. They really made up for it and totally understood. I will continue to frequent this location. Thanks guys

I hate I can't remember her name but the night time supervisor is AWESOME!! I love when she's there. She's always nice and friendly and makes some great recommendations. I tried the new frosty because of her and it was so good! This place always has great service no matter the time and that's why I choose them over everyone else on that street.
tMkwHmWFUEXrC9ZduonpTg: I've been for both lunch and dinner and The Green Pheasant was exceptional both times. The interior decor of the restaurant is really well done with a unique, modern vibe. The food was excellent. Loved the udon noodles with pork and an egg for lunch as well as the mushrooms and the salmon for dinner!

Positives: Different menu, not southern or american food, great atmosphere, good happy hour special, a few good dishes, great sake

Negatives: Average service, high prices, food is not consistent

I work in the neighborhood and have had the opportunity to stop by the Green Pheasant 3x for dinner and 2x for lunch since it opened a few weeks ago.  These guys are hitting on all cylinders right out of the gate.  I had the Tomago (Japanese egg salad, yes the eggs are FedEx'd from Japan) for lunch one day and Hambuga (hamburger) the next.  These would both appeal to someone who has never had Japanese food to the most serious Japanese food fan.  One of my co-workers had the karate (fried chicken) and it too was incredible.

Dinner, the Waloo, A5 Waygu, and King Salmon are my favorites so far. 

I will be back again and again and again.
OSV4mS8wLuRAu5d1nkmaQA: Yes yes yes! Coffee, atmosphere, the people... can't beat a perfect oatmilk cap, tons of plants, good vibes and feminist mood. You'll find me here close to every day from now on.

We stopped by Kettner for a coffee yesterday on our way to brunch. The space is minimalist and modern and cool. The coffee (Americanos this time) was good. The service was good. The prices were what I expected. And it has a door that leads from it into The Bookshop (a lovely, tiny bookstore that I like to frequent). Had a great visit; glad to support a locally owned coffee shop!

Beautiful new addition to the block, convenient to all the new condos and shops on this stretch of W Eastland. They have a main room - high ceilings and beautiful mural on the wall along with a smaller cozy nook around the corner. Simple yet concise coffee menu as well as soft serve and pastry offerings. Really friendly staff with no "coffeehouse attitude".  I tried the Firepot chai, served up in a wide mouth mug perfect for hugging on this cold winter day. Judging by the steady stream of folks coming in, I think people have been waiting for a welcoming coffee spot to call their own.
rxA_8iDMDZk50p6gJNeXqQ: I caught this truck at the Grove tonight in the Boro. I did not have any idea what to ask for, so I asked for what you recommend with some Thai tea. I got a spicy chicken over white rice that was great(chicken curry with jasmine rice?). Steaming hot plate to take home was just what I needed. I can't wait to try it again.

It's been awhile since I've made it to NFM, so we decided to venture out there today for lunch since I needed to restock on my TruBee honey from Batch. Also saw that Laovin Food truck will be there! Since I am Lao, I feel like it's my duty to try to all of the Lao restaurants. They were setup at the pop-up location. The menu was limited, which is expected since they are food-truck based. I ordered the chicken basil fried rice, but instead I was served the chicken sticky wings with rice. The wait was about 15 mins. However, almost all of the restaurants at the popup location always take a bit longer since it's not their standard setup. And I was too hungry at that point to ask for the fried rice so I took the wings. I secretly wanted both anyway. The wings were crispy on the outside and nice and juicy inside. The sauce was sweet and sticky with a slight hint of spice. Pretty good wings. Overall, happy to see a Lao food truck around in Nashville. Love to see the variety of foods and how open Nashvillians are to try it. We've come a long way.

Came in for lunch on a snowy day. We ordered the king noodle soup, which was so comforting with the weather and packed full of flavor! We also ordered the papaya salad and spring rolls. The papaya salad was not that spicy as I wanted, but it was still a great asset to our lunch. I do not recommend ordering the spring rolls. It was not worth it.
ZJC0ONdxmIlwidXd8E0fXg: My New Favorite Place to watch a Game in Bellevue! They have so many TVs you're Guaranteed to have a Good View no matter where you Sit. I prefer the Bar where the have a good selection of Beers on tap. I have been here a few times have been working my way through the menu. The Nonna is my Pizza so far. The Crust is what makes a Good Pizza and Boombozz does not Disappoint. I also Enjoyed the Classic Breakfast Pizza for Brunch. I'm Excited to go Back and try their Waffles.  They Sound Amazing!
Great pizza with garden fresh toppings. Excellent dry rub wings (recommend a side of the mango habanero sauce) as well as large craft brew selection. The gargantuan TV's are a very nice touch!

I beat the rush on grand opening. $8.99 combo 2 topping pizza and salad . Caesar salad and hot sausage basil 8" pizza. Lots of craft beer as advertised.  Large bar . Tables peripherally .  Staff pleasant on grand opening.  Lunch was nice . Can't wait to bring my girls here for pizza dinner . Lots of tv's if preds are on is a plus
MYVWCT18urcDDPl7nJ5cFg: This was the best experience. I am babysitting so I decided to go in to eat instead of going to the drive through and I am so glad I did. The staff was so nice to me. Every last one of them. One sweet lady has been there fifteen years. She works Thur through Sunday mornings. She says her boss won't let her quit. Made my heart so happy and is a great start to my Friday.

So I'm a fatty... But that's okay. The service here is always quick, duh it's McDonald's. But more importantly they're friendly and they get the order correct. I hate going to fast food places that give me the wrong order and send me on my way. The young man who gave me my delicious chocolate shake with Oreos last night said "have a very blessed day!" And he smiled so much that I forgot I was at McDonald's. Needless to say, the shake was fantastic!
This is the absolute best McDonald's I've been to in years they are always friendly and have my order correct and you get in and out quickly.
v_AbyvMdKUi_ac4A8nkG-A: Excellent food and atmosphere.  We had their Southern Nachos which were very good.  Slightly different Han the western or Mexican nachos you get in the Southwest.  Definitely a place to go check out.

Stopped in on a Friday night to have dinner and some drinks with friends, and the dinner did not disappoint.  I got the Bison Burgers (Sliders), and they were hands down some of the best Sliders I have had.  Our waitress was great even with how busy it was on Friday night, and the live music/venue was a lot of fun. Be sure to check out the rooftop bar!
Best spot on Broadway! Great music, great vibe, great service. Ask for Schuyler as your server! Must go to rooftop!!
srBsGpHMxuu8KSQcw7Pf8w:
When it comes to "bang for your buck" it's a kaboom.  
They got a deal on Tuesdays for a leg and thigh for 99cents.  Then you can add a side and a drink for another 99cents.  Dinner for two under $5.  That's a record... for the cheapest date ever (not to be taken as advice or a dating tip).

Now how does it compare to the competitors in taste?  It's inferior to both Popeye's and KFC... but not by much.

The batter is dense; the spicy isn't really noticeable; all the sides are mushy.  But what they do well is that they cut wings to be bigger, so you don't have a puny wing and a huge breast piece.  

Again, for the money you pay... this place rocks.
Quick and easy. Growing up poor and eating a lot of Tuesday specials.... I'm a fan of this place.
2hzXcWn8bOpzGTI4BfnMYw: The interior of this place is very clean and filled with a plethora of seating. It's kind of like a Starbucks where you can sit and have a casual conversation or just come in to study. I prefer it way more than Starbucks though because less people come in and it's less loud. I've tried their French Vietnamese which was really good. They also have a point system where if you collect up to 80 points, you can get a free drink any size.
I had been waiting for this place to open!  I tried the Thai tea for the grand opening. Friendly service and very clean with a wide variety of coffees and teas to try out

This coffee shop is the best! Whenever I'm in town, I go for breakfast, reading my book and getting the "dragon eye" drink. The service is great and I love the staff. A definite go-to if your in the area!
G6EkKOLWiOw_8G7olgvalg:
Great addition to the Village! It seems like there hasn't been a good place to eat on Belcourt in... maybe ever? So it's great for those of us who live in the neighborhood to have two really solid options now (Nicoletto's and the poorly named but delicious Meet Noodles). 

Anyhow, if you're looking for some actual info: this is a casual Italian place. Order at the counter setup. Handmade pasta, lots of varieties and lots of sauce options. Delicious sandwiches, too. I had the meatball sub the other day and it was the bomb dot com. 

Great place overall.
Got the pink sauce, SOOO GOOD!!! I really loved the food all around EXCEPT the brownie (get everything but that-trust me)
Without a doubt authentic Italian ala denta pasta dishes with a great consistency of sauce.
4xcsIgbYhG54418KkAVWow: These are seriously AMAZING! They are gluten free (and free of a lot of other allergens, but I can't remember) and they are so moist and delicious. Seriously, the best gluten free cupcakes I've ever had.

It would be totally unfair to say that these cupcakes are "good for being gluten free!" or "so tasty you can't even tell that there's no eggs or dairy!" because they're just plain good.  

I don't have food allergies and I constantly find myself picking them over other options at Atmology or Whole Foods. I love any sort of spice or fruit cake situation and Joanna always has some great choices like carrot cake and 'nana bread with just the right amount of rich creamy icing. (Wayyyyyyy better than a certain other popular place in town with piles of sickening, gritty, dare I say flavorless icing on top.  Seriously, I know they have their fans but I will fight you tooth and nail on this, those are just not good.)  

This isn't a matter of a great alternative for those with an unfortunate allergy. Get out there and try them! Bring them to your office and don't tell anyone that they're 8-free! Trick your spouse or your kids! It's fun!

A-MAY-ZING.
Ordered a gorgeous and elegant chocolate cake with vanilla frosting for my little who has many allergies.
Highly recommend, most and not too sweet. Will become our go-to cake vendor!!
MOhvznYP1n1iuwtnsnEzEw:
If I lived in Nashville, I would eat here all the time. I have difficulty finding Thai restaurants that do Thai food to my tastes (I used to work in a west coast Thai restaurant that was really good). Here it is. I have to drive from Louisville to Nashville every time I want Thai food.

The outside of the building looks unassuming, but inside looks like a date night kind of place - cool and contemporary. I ate here with four colleagues. 

I had the drunken noodles with tofu, spicy. Great. Really great. My colleagues had pad voon sen, the paneang, and the cashew supreme. They enjoyed it. One was completely new to Thai food, and I recommended she try the cashew supreme, which she had with low spice, and that turned out well.

Service was nice, although slower when it got busy.

I've been eager to check out Thai Esane since several of my friends said it was their favorite Thai restaurant in the area. I had read about this place in some local blogs as well, so I was excited to see if it lived up to my expectations when a girl friend and I both ate here for the first time last night.

I was overall very impressed. For a Friday night, they were busy but there was not a wait and we were promptly seated. The restaurant is very modern/trendy on the inside, but casual enough that were weren't given funny looks for coming to dine straight from our work out!

I love that the menu has pictures and descriptions of all of the dishes. I ordered the Thai Tea, Egg Rolls, and Pad See Ew. All were delicious. The Pad See Ew normally come mild, but they were glad to spice it up for me. I ordered mine with tofu and it was very good.

My friend ordered the Pad Kra Pao and enjoyed her dish as well.

The service was excellent. The prices were very reasonable. My Thai Tea, eggrolls, and entree came up to only $17 after taxes.

I was taking a customer to lunch today, otherwise I would have never selected Thai Esane, which would have been my loss. When you first approach, the outside of the restaurant doesn't pull you in, but after stepping inside you're immediately treated to the surprise of clean, modern interior. We arrived shortly before the lunch rush, and were seated immediately. The wait staff was pleasant, and knowing the lunch crowed was arriving, quick. The menu was large, and adding to the experience for someone inexperienced with Thai food were pictures to help guide your decision. I enjoyed the cashew supreme with chicken, medium spice, and it was wonderful. Next time I may amp up the spice just a bit, although I'm told their 'native' dishes are extremely spicy. I enjoyed my experience, and would definitely recommend this restaurant.
95P1mG-AyDA7rjOmttL84w:
Let's talk about the customer service. These folks were more than accommodating for a large party and completely amazing with explaining the menu. 

Second, the food. Oh man. There was enough of us to warrant ordering one of everything. The beef tartar was on point and either of the pasta dishes would take your taste buds over the top. 

If you're looking for a cool, laid back place with amazing food, I highly recommend this place. I'd give it 10 stars if I could.

Absolutely positive experience.  Each dish was prepared with attention to detail. Flavorful and quite delicious.  The wine was enjoyable also.  We loved the music and ambiance.  A nice touch to play vinyl records.  We will definitely dine Setsun again.
Had a nice little socially-distanced date night here a couple weeks ago.  Spacious-enough outdoor patio is tucked away past the bar and all the flavors in the dishes were outstanding!
3UNVLnSl5TixH1vaddpiTQ: Food and service are excellent!!  The staff is attentive, the food was great everything was fresh. Most mexican restaurants have a lot of stuff all over the place.. it's not like that here and I like that!!
Great food and awesome service. Love these people and my new neighborhood hangout in Antioch.
Very clean and well taken care of!  The staff were very attentive and kind.  The guac was super fresh and yummy!
RxuTWAWF6gXribtmpb7Fqw: My wife and I have been to a lot of Mexican restaurants in the area. and this one is our favorite. They always have good salsa, and the food is always good. The service is sometimes slow, and sometimes they don't charge the same price, but overall this is our favorite restaurant out of all the ones in Hermitage, Donelson, and Antioch.

The food was excellent, the service was great.

They have a salsa bar and always someone behind the salsa bar making home made tortillas, both corn and flour.

If you are in the market for some great Mexican food La Terraza is an excellent choice.

The mariachis were playing music. The bassist had a large visible sticker on his bass that read, "TIPS."

They got off stage and started interrupting couples who were dining apparently asking them if they had any special requests.
They asked me in Spanish so naturally I asked if any of the 3 amigos spoke English. 

The guitar player told me that they charge 20.00 per song. Naturally I lol...I have been blessed in my life with many concerts and when you break it down per song it is well worth the ticket.

20.00 per song? They must have thought that they were performing at the Madison Square Garden.
When I worked at my last job they took us here every Friday. I always got tamales and rice. I still eat this and I still love it. This is my favorite mexican restaurant period!!!
tDZbh9a6gGcHEBpIrSkfmw: I hit up strike out wings food truck and got the family some dinner.  I got a catfish dinner some tenders for the kids and some wings. The catfish had nice size and  had  great spice and breading to it. The catfish and fries went great together. The kids love old the tender and fries. The I hot was sweet and sassy and strikeout sauce. The sweet sassy sauce was great with the wings. The sweet and heat went great together with some ranch dipping sauce. I also got their hottest sauce called the strikeout sauce. It had great heat and taste. I will be getting that sauce again.

So I have been wanting to try them for a while and today I went on Grubhub and got it No contact delivered. I got traditional wings and let me tell you what. SOOOO GOOD!!! I am really picky when it comes to wings and for price and size I am so happy to have found my new joint. The garlic Parmesan taste homemade sauce and the sweet and sassy. Fries are super good and crispy. Totally bang for your buck and can't wait to try more in the future

We have eaten at Strikeout a half dozen+ times and have had a great experience - the food is delicious, sauces are flavorful, and the owners are just delightful, friendly people. Once the pandemic hit we tried ordering once through Grubhub and had a less than stellar experience. I called Strikeout directly and they were super responsive. One of the owners drove over to our house to make it right. From now on, we call directly and go pick up. We know the order will be just right and one of our neighborhood faves gets our $$ (instead of Grubhub and their pesky fees!)
CFHGvmBp8CwYOCUDjwYo2A: I was having issues with my online order & my free coupon code for cheese sticks so I decided to just come into the restaurant and talk to them. I spoke with the manager, Stephanie, and she was a huge help! She honored my free online order code and discounted my deal box also since the app hasn't been working correctly. I also completed changed my mind and wasn't going to eat Pizza Hut anymore but Stephanie made everything right.
Looking for a sit down Pizza Hut innFranklin-Cool Springs-Brentwood area. IS THERE ANY?
_Qq76wygutgcUER6QN1Ffw: This place is incredibly good. Great service. Food comes out hot & fresh. HIGHLY RECOMMENDED!!
Obsessed with this place. The food was amazing. We didn't get anything special, burgers and fries, and they were perfection. Quick made to order.

To all of my friends from Dallas where I grew up "forgive me father for I have sinned" 

I can separate this review in a few parts. Let me start out with the "TL:DR" version.

Hugh Baby's is what most, if not every, regional fast food chain strives to be. Simple, fanatic, you think you're having a stroke it's so consistent service.

"Is it that good?" Yeah... "Are you sure? " why would I post this... "is it consistent?" Enough so that even during the pandemic I've been startled 3 different times when the curbside pickup was at my window so fast I think I've checked my pants 2 times.

Regional fast food places try to serve a lot of needs. I think Texas has a fist full of them, including the specialty version of Dairy Queen that doesn't exist here for reasons I don't understand.

I can, and probably write a Masters level dissertation on regional fast food places and why so many of them don't work at scale (In-N-Out I'm look at you with a lot of dissappointment). 

Hugh Baby's is phenominal, phenomenally simple, fast food.

The staff isn't necessarily 100% flawless (grading on the white kids smiling and being enthusiastic about everything from Chikfila scale, yet they are 97% there). 

It's solid, consistent, fresh, fast food. During these crazy times when Dominos seems to struggle with getting pizza sauce on dough, I'll take it.
Uuzewy2CLg_XCekRhA0PIg: Cool place very inviting and unique in terms of how they flip the room for the comedy shows.  Great pizza from Chicago.  Love the stage in the middle very intimate.  I will go back whenever I'm in Nashville. great job!
Pumped to see The Comedy Bar open a new location in Nashville! A nice change of pace from all the honky tonks and bars. And the only place in town with amazing comedy. You won't be disappointed!

Went last night with a couple friends because they got free tickets from their co-workers at the Omni. They have a Chicago location too but this spot is smaller. Cool little spot inside Gino's East pizza that also recently opened like a month or two ago in downtown nashville. The location is nice because it's not right off broadway, so as locals we didn't have to deal with the tourism and crowds that normally pollute downtown. The show started at 10pm which seems a little late, but I think it's because the venue is essentially the restaurant's main dining room. It was still set up really well and there isn't a bad seat in the house! Intimate space with maybe 75-100 seats. We saw 5 comedians in one show! Some local and a headliner from Atlanta who was hilarious! They also let us order off Gino's menu during the show which was great. Their thin crust is better than anything I've had in nashville so far and they sell it by the slice, so no need to commit to a full pizza if the rest of your group isn't hungry. Overall, really great little comedy club with fun atmosphere and great service.
Bu5sh0AVuv5qTpH7pm7wuA: The service was quick and friendly. The catfish was flavorful and juicy and the restaurant is super chill. Would have loved to have sit upstairs. The muffin cornbread hit in a certain type of way. I feel like the sides are meh but the meat makes up for it.

I've had their breakfast, and for what you get, the prices are great.  I got the two eggs, sausage, and a biscuit, and the cost was $4 tax included.  That's as good as McDonald's and not nearly as manufactured.

I've never had the meat and three, and I probably never will, but the people there were very friendly, the food was inexpensive and tasty, and the environs was simple and attractive.  My only gripe is that everyone, including people dining in, get to-go containers.  Kind of wasteful and inconvenient.  That's my only real complaint.

If you're downtown need a quick and tasty breakfast at a reasonable price, I cannot say enough good things about this place.
Best chicken and fish ever. The flavor is on point chicken is juicy not dry. Love the collared greens. Place is a 5  to me.
nohJqY1rHip5gXrVGOMlIA: Absolutely incredible. The best service I've ever had. Martinis are a must. Tequila old fashion. We had risotto, veal, white bolognese and cacio e Pepe. ALL AMAZING. will be back.

Hands down my  dining  experience at YOLAN was the best I've had in several years here in Nashville.  We actually dined here in September 2020 but I'm just now getting to this review.   Service was FLAWLESS and the food delicious.  My favorite items on the menu were the Gamberoni and the Gnocchi.  Tony Mantuana, the chef, came by the table to see how our food was which was fun!   Must try !

Yolan is amazing! The ambience, service and the food were the finest I've experienced. Every detail was executed to perfection. If you are looking for a memorable dining experience, I highly recommend!
94Kq5YyU6C8cwgdh4_ozjw: Food was great and the service was attentive and friendly without making us feel rushed at all. There is a lively atmosphere and the live music was enjoyable. They've changed the setup and it's no longer cafeteria style which I really liked. Don't forget to look around while you're there. The memorabilia is awesome.

This place is awesome and has a great vibe! Cool, cafeteria style service, and their menu is fantastic. I chose the fried chicken, sweet potatoes, greens, corn cake, and blackberry cobbler. The food was prepared to perfection. Seriously, one of the best meals I have ever had, and certainly one of the most enjoyable. The ambiance is beautiful, warm, and welcoming-it felt like home. Great attention to every detail. The upstairs bar/lounge section is spectacular. We will be returning for another meal before we leave Nashville and head home. What a great tribute to Johnny Cash.

This place has a wonderful vibe. Stop in and stay downstairs but I would head up the stairs. To enjoy the music upstairs, grab a seat in a rocking chair or near the stage and listen surrounded by stained glass and a bit of art. It's a great place to enjoy.
mnb466bWdbXVz5Oa3XnFsA: This is my new favorite brewery in town. The patio outside is inviting and spacious with umbrellas for when the sun is too hot! Great location for pups, kids, and group hangouts. They had lots of board games! The beer is great too. We had the Pale Ale that was on special and were not disappointed. The owners are attentive and friendly which gave it a small town feel. The food options were limited but absolutely delicious so we didn't mind. I got the pork sandwich and IT WAS SO MARVELOUS. I would definitely recommend!

So glad to see two local guys set up shop in the underserved area of Elm Hill Pike, near Donelson.  I stopped in yesterday for my first time and was surprised to see 9 beers already on tap.  I got a flight of 7, which were all fantastic!  I had to go ahead and try the Double IPA and Sour Wheat, which are not normally my cup of tea.  They were both amazing.  I will come back soon to try the food.  Service was as good as it gets and the facilities were comfy and clean.  You definitely want to include them on your brewery tour.  They are not far from downtown or the airport.

Stopped in to try the beers and ordered ribs and potato salad - OH MY Heavens! For this BBQ Ribs fan, I have to say these were in the top 3 of ribs I have experienced. And that is from Memphis and Kansas City. Fall off the bone goodness with great rub, and bark from cherrywood smoke. 
Paired with a potato salad that had sweet Yukon potatoes and and dill - YUM!

The beers were refreshing, balanced and just right. 
So, 5 stars to the crew of Various Artists - well done. Very Well done!!

If you find yourself in the area and you don't stop by, I shed a tear
CxQ5GonUhGjgfX8REEFjdw: The pizza is delicious!! What an amazing addition to the neighborhood. It's a great place to take the family for ice cream! The ice cream is delicious. There have been new flavors every time we go in. Yogi and his staff are friendly and welcoming.

It's the first pizza in Nashville that we can both agree on!  Got the all meat and I normally can't stand Italian sausage b/c it's too sweet or too fennel-y. This is good mildly spicy sausage that plays great w the other meats!  Sauce is light and not heartburn inducing or full of sugar like the chains. Staff is friendly and pizza is quick.
Best pizza in this neighborhood! We order takeout many times, and it is never greasy! Very well cooked and good prices!
xB3MxRBF6l8rd9xxJVnLhw: Really enjoyed the food.  Had chicken tenders and grits.  Really liked it.  Chicken had a lot of flavor and good portions.  Grits were really good.

You can't visit Nashville without trying hot chicken and this place is the top on most lists. Luckily it was very close to where we were staying so it was a no brainer

We stopped in on our way back to the room one evening near closing time. The line was not as bad as we had seen but was still long. 

It was worth the wait. The chickens sandwich at there hot level was awesome. We had to try the hottest and got a wing of that. Shut the cluck up was insanely hot but moist and I could still taste some flavor 

Definitely worth the stop.

The line was thirty minutes long to get in but we waited because he heard the food was worth it.
We were not disappointed.
The Nashville Chicken Sandwich would've been worth waiting an hour outside for.
The pimento Mac n cheese was something I haven't tried before but I actually liked it even more than the fries.
The crinkle fries were a tad overdone but I really liked the seasoning on them--so much that we came back the next day for the fries, again.
The prices are more reasonable that other places on the strip and the food is totally worth it.
Dp_yOU1VrnWDtntp2Kq1Kw: My boyfriend &  I grabbed brunch here on Sunday before we could check into our hotel. The live music was great. The food is delicious. I've never had Smokey bacon like that before. John gave us some good recommendations & made awesome drinks. Highly recommend.

Loved, loved, loved it!  The food and service was great. The atmosphere fantastic. The music put it over the top. Greenwood Rye!  What great teamwork.  Very interesting sound. Thanks for the good time. The Bluegrass Brunch was fantastic.  Will be back next time I'm in town.

Bluegrass and Brunch - Yasssssss Please! Exceeded expectations. Great homemade bloody. I got the short rib Benny. It was beautiful. It was unique yet perfection. My friend had the homemade cauliflower rosemary soup and standard bfast. I would order it just for the potatoes. Great hidden gem on 3rd!
IkLp51a_x_0CPlg0qMEqNg:
Great pizza and pasta - highly recommended!

They went above and beyond on our first visit, and we will definitely be back soon. Happy to have a great fast-casual Italian spot in the neighborhood.

Outstanding. That's really the only way to describe this place is just, outstanding. We got the pinsa pazza and it was like no pizza we have ever had. The crust was crunchy but chewy, and you could tell how high quality the ingredients were. The sauce and fresh mozzarella both had such an incredible flavor. The pesto was present but not overpowering, and the burrata was a great touch on top. This place is legit. We will be back many many times to try their other pizzas and dishes.

Okay the Food here is wonderful.  I'm telling you the fresh pasta and the sauces are delicious. I've had everything from the pasta to the the pinsa and it's all so good. The Pinsa Porcini is so unique and flavorful it is mushroom heaven. Let's talk about the Garlic Confit that is in their Campanelle Pazze. The flavors are insanely good. I'm talking about fresh pasta that tastes good and doesn't leave you feeling heavy. This is a must go to in Nashville, way better than any Italian food I have had in this town. I recommend any of their dishes, but some favorites of mine are the Lasagna, the Campanelle Pazze, the Amatriciana, the Ragu and Funghi. I also love the Pinsa Maleducata, and the Pinsa Porcini :D
lTqAGjcQHeY3eTXBTIcvkA: Simply amazing. I really appreciated the decor and the overall vibe, very hipster and laid back. The cold brew is strong, and our food came out fast and hot. Totally recommend the chorizo gravy with the biscuit.
