We were visiting from out of town & did curbside pick up & brought it back to our airbnb. The food was incredible!! So fresh & full of flavor. Highly recommend!!!
0msaBRr1q6IV0jGOnamlfA:
My husband and I came to Nashville for a quick trip to celebrate my birthday. As a huge Miranda fan, I knew we had to visit Casa Rosa. 

We arrived a little after 5:00 p.m. on Saturday evening and were greeted by the friendly hostess, who told us the wait would be about an hour and a half. Not unexpected for Broadway on a Saturday night. While we waited for our table, we went upstairs to the bar. The live music was awesome. They played a wide range of cover songs and even took requests from the audience. There was a large crowd, but the bartenders served us quickly.

After only 45 minutes, I received a text that our table was ready. We went back downstairs and were seated in one of the comfy booths under the giant disco ball saddle--amazing! Our waitress was very sweet and patient with us as we debated what to order. 

We started with drinks and chips and salsa for an appetizer. The blueberry margarita was to die for. Honestly, one of the best drinks I've ever had. Luckily they sell the mix in the gift shop on the first floor! The salsa was delicious and hotter than expected--in a good way! We almost ate the entire basket of chips just between the two of us. 

For dinner, I chose the Nashville hot chicken tacos, and my husband ordered the Nashville hot chicken burrito. Because the restaurant was so packed, I expected a bit of a wait for food. I wish I had timed it because, I kid you not, our meals were delivered in just a few minutes--the fastest service I have ever experienced. We practically devoured our food, it was that good. I didn't even get a chance to take a picture of my meal. 

We had an amazing time, and we are already making plans to go back. Incredible food, drinks, music, and energy. What more could a girl want?!

My family and I decided to stop here to grab a couple of drinks and a snack while walking around downtown Nashville. 

Decor: SO awesome! Very chic and great photo opp (second floor). Very clean and loved the fact that Miranda Lambert's outfits were framed around the place. 

Service: the staff was SO nice! Our server was great and did an excellent job answering our questions. 

Drinks: so our drinks came out after our appetizer. The bar only had maybe 5 people at it? The bartenders should be doing less meandering and more mixing. The margaritas were excellent! I also LOVE the tequila selection. I'm a huge tequila fan and I think I died and went to heaven. 

Food: We only ordered the dip trio. The green tomatillo sauce was AMAZING!!! I loved that it had a kick to it. The red salsa was good and a bit thicker which made it easy to get on your chips. The guacamole was also great! My 8 year old basically housed it so the two bites I had were good. My only complaint? The chips were a bit greasier than I'd like. However, the portion was HUGE! I mean for $13 you can't beat it! It was like a whole meal in itself. 

Prices: not bad. It was $56 for 3 margaritas and the dip trio. I mean it's to be expected on Broadway. 

I really enjoyed it and would go back in a minute. I also didn't get to checkout the other two floors, but the first two were cool! They had live music as well.

Let's be clear, this is a tourist stop. It is not a place for a nice, quite meal. If you are a Miranda Lambert fan, you will love it. You have to go in knowing that 1. It's expensive 2.  The wait will be long. We went on a Friday at 2pm and was told the wait was 1.5 hours. This gave us time to start at the bottom floor and work our way up to the 3rd floor and take it all in. Suggestion - if you want to eat. Head straight to the 2nd  floor a put your # down. They will text you. A tip is the bar on the 2nd floor is first come, first serve and they do serve food. We managed to grab two seats after waiting for 45 mins and kept checking to see if anything had opened up. The food and drink were all very good. The street corn is AMAZING!!  All drinks were also very good.
QuZedyMTfhVwAYONCvtCVg: I pretty much stopped going to Subway because I felt the quality of the sandwich had really gone down because they were keeping their prices low and it seemed like if you asked for more than lettuce and tomato, any extra dressing, etc it was a real imposition to them. Today I wanted a sandwich I didn't have to drive out of the way for, so I stopped at the Subway near my home. Delightful experience! The prices have gone up but the bread was really fresh as was the other toppers and the Hispanic lady that waited on me actually acted like she was pleased to help me and that just made the experience for me ! I will be back again!
This is one of my favorite Subway stores. The employees are always friendly, the service is quick, and the food is always good. I highly recommend this store.

Bt1kg3Whr5EG6nsp1oC4Pw: Amazing food! We have been 3 times and live in Sylvan Park. We have been needing a healthy, yet filling spot like this for quite some time!

This place is pretty Rad!! Plenty of parking to stop and grab a bite. There's an outdoor patio you can sit at if you don't want to sit inside just yet too. Upon entry there's a hand sanitizer station which is nice considering the times. We had our order taken from the counter almost immediately and it was ready by the time we paid at the other end! I had the Chicken Caesar Wrap with the house greens and it was sooo good! The only thing was that it came with a TON of radishes in it. I wish the wrap was warmed up a little more (for pliability) but other than that it was awesome! I also wish it came with a side of hummus or a serving of fruit or something. The wrap is good on its own though. the chicken is warmed so that's a nice flavorful touch too. The inside is neat and orderly and there's not a soda fountain to be seen! The water dispenser is a little tricky/frustrating though. It's motion sensor activated and comes out in little spurts very quickly. It will splash you if you're not careful. Other than that, I'm happy to support local and not give my money to Panera! Keep it Rad!
Great food! I get the grain bowl with falafel and it is very good. Lots of food too!!

I'm so happy to be a new customer and able to support a really great BLACK OWNED BUSINESS  please support them you'll be mad you waited so long  wish I could do 20 stars

Ordered shrimp and veggies on uber eats and I was surprisingly pleased with the flavor. The food arrived hot and it was delicious. Even if it didn't, it was seasoned well enough to eat cold. I love the food A LOT! A whole lot! Problem I'm having is being able to confirm when they're open.  Like today (Saturday) for instance. G_nOnJ17sTeyfco2oyG25w: Stopped in with a buddy for lunch after some friends recommended this place.   Couldn't have been a better first experience with Somalian food. 5jXiZ23pb-xf457uNM2yZg: Absolutely amazing! Peter the sushi chef knocked it out of the park. Best sushi I have ever had with gorgeous presentation. We will be back!

We had an excellent experience at LJ! We highly recommend it.

Super good service. The rolls were excellent. Good fried dumplings and egg rolls. Beautiful presentation. Highly recommended!
42OM20QndpmsOSY6ccudBA: Old school 50s 60s decor and classic upscale vibe without feeling ritzy. Everything that we ate was amazing. The meal and service was outstanding.   Highly recommend the French Toast and the fruit was fresh. They have in house homemade bread and pastries as well.

Awesome spot to chill, before a game.  Rest from walking the strip. And most of all, grub.  We tried a number of spots. CNW9zFn1nwVDOMvpEK1tbQ: Not only was the lamb wrap really good, I must say Emad have outstanding service. At this airport a lot of the people are rude however Emad changed my whole mood with his great customer service I wanna give you a big San francisco thank you!

I will never understand why every airport restaurant gets eviscerated by the Yelp community. The food is usually perfectly acceptable but people love to get on their high horse, chasing a power trip from crushing some poor small airport food counter into the ground.

This place was JUST. FINE. Was it the best chicken platter I've ever eaten? Hell no. But it did exactly what I expected from it. Fast, cheap, and edible bordering on decent. 

I thought my meal deserved three stars, but I'm giving it four to counteract everyone else on Yelp who absolutely lay into airport restaurants for no good reason. We don't give kids play-doh and judge their creations against Michelangelo's David. We don't give people crayons and expect Van Gogh's Starry Night. And we sure as hell shouldn't go to an airport and expect Michelin star food quality.

Best airport food I've ever had! I got the chicken Greek platter and it came with a huge salad. Chicken was grilled right in front of me with onions and peppers. So fresh! 
The line was long but totally worth the wait. You wait in one line to put your order in and pay and then you get in another line to give the cook your order and then he makes it. Make sure you have enough time to eat here. 
My fiancé's gyro was delicious. He got the gyro platter and it came with rice and salad. The gyro meat was outstanding. They also offer toppings such as feta and taziki , yum!
K7rsFcHcO_LYrgWvTAik2w: We've been wishing for bagels in East Nashville for awhile now, so we were thrilled to see Shep's open in the former (beloved) Marche space. We stopped by this morning for takeout. The space is beautiful and clean. We were a bit disappointed to find out that bagels were limited to 4 per order - we were planning to take home a dozen -- and it seemed as though not all menu items were available. We're hoping this is attributable to the recent opening and getting established. All that said, the bagels, chocolate and raspberry rugelach, and schmears were all delicious. My husband, a native New Yorker, said the lox schmear was the best he's ever had! We're definitely planning to be back to try more of the deli items, and we hope they consider expanding bagel takeout options, and possibly opening at 7am on the weekends for neighborhood locals like us!
Finally a classic NY deli that lives up to the hype! Food was incredible it was so good I went back the next day. They also have a great drink menu for those looking to do a boozy brunch.
Excellent deli! Delicious food and wonderful service! Corn beef is outstanding. Portions are large! As good as many delis in New York! Bakery cookies are wonderful!
25g42U7hBsDPF5RTpZ24mw: What a great new restaurant and the staff was amazing too !! We enjoyed drinks and dinner at the bar, and the chicken Milanese with fettuccine Alfredo was simply amazing !! Can't wait for our next visit !!

We have been hearing great reviews on Slim and Husky's (even saw them featured on Travel Channel's Food Paradise) so now that they are open across from Bridgestone we thought we would give them a try before the Preds game.
We decided on a half "The Smokin Herb with Chicken" and half "Nothin But a V Thang". First I LOVE that you are able to do a half/half even with 2 different sauces!!!
For me The Smokin Herb stole the show! I am a sucker for a good white sauce pizza and this might be the best white sauce I've ever had. 
My husband loved the Nothin But a V Thang which had vegan pepperoni and vegan sausage. Typically it comes with vegan cheese but we asked for regular cheese. We do not eat red meat (due to a medical condition I have) so we really appreciated the variety Slim and Husky's offers to fit many dietary needs.  Also they keep the vegan items separately from the traditional which was great to see.  The vegan pepperoni was AMAZING!!! Next time I would actually try the Smokin Herb with the vegan pepperoni.
The crust is very thin, almost cracker like. They make it in line in front of you (sub shop style). They have drizzle options, which are sauces that they give you on the side.
My only downside is that they did not have garlic knots while we were there. They told us they were out...I LOVE garlic knots so I am looking forward to going back to try them.
This is seriously the perfect eats before walking across the street to Bridgestone for the games

I was really excited to try this place and was not disappointed. The flavors were amazing. I got a slim which was more than enough to satisfy my hunger. The pizza was so good that next time I'll probably order a Husky and save some for later. This was a great meal after going to the National African American Music Museum.

Soooo glad I finally got to taste! 
Slim & Husky's has been on my mind since I first saw the long lines while visiting Memphis TN.

The tots and grilled cheese were top notch. 10/10 would eat again. I will also say tables were 10/10, excellent size for both beer die and beer pong.
3yqN_naC1GZ-61Vldg369g: Nestled inside of assembly food hall towards the back of floor 2, you do not want to pass this up! One of the best Poke bowls i've ever had. You can make your own bowl or there were three options already to choose from, I got the already made option with white rice as the base, marinated tuna (to die for!), crab, edamame, crunch flakes etc the options are plentiful. If raw isn't your thing they also have cooked shrimp and chicken bowls as well. Portions are plentiful and pricing isn't bad considering what you get. Give this place a try you will not be disappointed !
Had Oke poke and it was amazing . The tuna tasted just like what I've had in Hawaii , the place is clean and staff was awesome !

Oke Poke' is a perfect place to try something different, but not too far "out there." My wife got the shrimp tempura poke' bowl. She gets poke' bowls from time to time at various locations, so she has a good idea of what makes a good offering. She really enjoyed the bowl and noted she'd keep it on "her list" should we come back and eat at 5th and Broadway again. I tired some of her bowl and was happy with each bite I took. Oke Poke' is easily on par with all the nice options at 5th and Broadway. A must try!
1IIht_H_qWSTG-kMSvKS6A: amazing wings! possibly the best ive ever had! pizza was great as well but the wings are to die for!!

Our first time at this location was Tuesday June 8 on trivia night. We ordered the beer pretzel and the USA! pizza and everything was served quick and hot and was fantastic! The pizza crust is chewy but crisp and the toppings were fresh. The servers were attentive to the large crowd and we never had an empty glass. 10/10 recommend!

Great experience! I came with a group during Burger Week and loved the Camp Fire burger. It was cooked just right and better than I'd expect from a brewery. Seasoned fries came with it and I asked for those loaded with cheese, bacon, and jalapenos. 

I really love pineapple and tried their "Deez Coconuts" pint. I'll definitely get that again. The biggest surprise to me came in the dessert area. They have beignets that are perfect if you want to end the night with something sweet. Somehow they are the closest that you'll get to New Orleans style in Nashville if you ignore their shape. 

Good stuff!
1dSKEitDDgIkaApe6UNMSA: Omg so goooood!!! I can't even put in words. The lemon pepper wings *chefs kiss*. The oxtail so tender and rich. The sides were amazing. Everything tasted fresh homemade and absolutely delicious! Highly highly recommend!!!

After seeing them on Instagram, I had to give Pepper Pott a try, and boy was I impressed! I love Caribbean food, and with not many options in the 615 I was excited to have another option!

I called ahead to pick up, and they came out to my car and rang me up. I don't know the mans name, but he was extremely kind - they even threw in some free veggie lo Mein as they were testing the menu. 

In total, I got a order of Jerk Chicken, Oxtail, Baked Mac & Cheese, a Caribbean lemonade, and the free lo mein. The verdict? Absolutely amazing!!! The flavors in the oxtail and the sauce was to die for, it fell right off the bone. The jerk chicken was flavorful and moist, and the Mac and cheese was a perfect side. 

My one recommendation is be aware of the sizes, I got a large which I didn't realize was meant for 2-3 people - completely on me for not asking but we ate it all since we had a big group anyways! 

I will definitely be coming back and am excited to see them expand their menu even more!
The beef patties are HOMEMADE! EVERYTHING is delicious. Strongly recommend the ox tail!
s9tbO_-dZ8EqfL5EguAAbQ:
I barely remember eating here. Yelp keeps bothering me to review it. 
Y so needy yelp?

Walk up/ to go and catering / delivery orders only. There is no dining room or out door seating available - always the case physically does not exist-. Food is fresh and location is clean. Have had many food items from events they have catered and recently had my first pick up experience. Great quality food, fair price and welcoming staff. For what it is no complaints.

So happy that a Chick-Fil-A moved into the tower. For the opening day the staff has been friendly and offer fast service. The food is always fresh. I love the app to order and my food is ready when I get there.
bBDDEgkFA1Otx9Lfe7BZUQ: I've hated on this sonic location before, but to be fair, I actually had quick and very friendly service this time. Maybe they it's a fluke, or maybe they have changed things for the better. Good job
0fYRCLVFWte0uo-1W7gJGQ: This whole dining area / experience is cool. You can order from any of the restaurants in here and sit wherever you want. We got sushi from this Horu Sushi Kitchen and it did not disappoint! Highly recommend. They were a little busy, as most good places are, but this one is worth the wait!
Fun atmosphere and good food! Tuna tower wasn't as satisfying as there was not enough tuna. Rolls were food though!

Wasn't sure how the sushi would be with such a small set up that they have for the sushi counter in the Assembly Food Hall but they absolutely delivered. I ordered the Hurricane Shrimp Roll and subbed Crab for the Spicy Tuna and it was delicious. Super fresh and the Spicy Mayo is on point (has a good kick, but not super hot). Will absolutely be back.
1AMdlp3799xhKsEvce9_aA: I messed up the first time and ordered HELLA hot. I am so used to wings never being hot enough. So, definitely don't underestimate the hotness. Otherwise, great food at a great price. The people working there are also super fun.
Delicious! Smelled and tasted great! Got the mild and it had a great kick! Food was delivered piping hot!

This little restaurant makes some biggo chicken. I had the spicy chicken sandwich  and it was so good.
It blows Chick-fil-A and Popeyes out of the water. Got go back to try their cat fish
hmzx6ymJcM_kErCHCGhxxg: I am here more often than I would care to admit. The food is always fresh prepared...and the whole staff is great....very friendly & welcoming ALWAYS!! :-) :-) Thank You, it IS appreciated!!
1Qy-lUPKqHxOV7eukTiaOw: Oh my... this place is amazing, especially for those with gluten sensitivities. The food is very flavorful and great serving sizes. Even our two year old enjoyed her food, which is rare these days. Would highly recommend.

Very flavorful BBQ!  We walked from Vanderbilt Hospital in search of something better than hospital food and this place didn't disappoint at all!  Half order of BBQ Nachos was more than enough food for one and the pricing wasn't bad at all. Staff was very knowledgeable about the menu and very friendly. Would definitely recommend!!!
Best Memphis barbecue, if you are visiting from out of town this the place to go. I can't even give a recommendation becuase everything is amazing. Great food.
k7oZJB7BSx37e-masOmiLQ:
How can you not like this ice cream? 

Creamy, rich and tons of fun flavors. We came here with a few friends and they opted for the "Unicorn Milk Shake" which looked amazing and tasted even better. 

The unicorn horn on this milkshake is a small waffle cone and I believe they add your choice of ice cream in the middle of the shake. This colorful treat is topped with whipped cream and lucky charm marshmallows for some color. My son, on the other hand, just wanted plain ice cream with sprinkles. No joke, might be the best vanilla ice cream I've ever tasted. 

This Hattie Jane's location is in the Assembly Food Hall next to a sushi bar inside Fifth and Broadway. Grab a cone, sit outside and enjoy a view of the Ryman auditorium.

Yummy!

Honey comb
- superb crowd pleaser flavor, definitely would get again

Sweet corn and blackberry jam
- not corn nor blackberry forward,  mild and likeable just for the fact that it's a good ice cream

Roasted peach bourbon
- exactly what it tastes like, you'll know what I mean when you taste it

Overall a win,  worth trying!

Delicious ice cream and unique flavors. I tried the nilla wafer flavor, which is a flavor you do not typically find at ice cream spots, and it hit the spot. My one critique would be, that the scoop is on the smaller size for what you pay.
qp7cJWwx5-j45fLGLYsn_Q: I really enjoyed my meal at Pokeworks. I was visiting a friend that lives around the corner and we popped in to a very warm welcome and fresh food.  I'm used to poke places in California and this setup had far more choices for toppings and sauces than I've ever seen.  It's nice that they hand mix your protein choice with the sauces before you get your toppings  so it's easier to eat.  When I'm near this location I'll definitely be back.

So good! Everything was very fresh - my eyes were burning a bit, so I assume the onions were freshly cut. Easy to make a bowl if you're vegetarian, vegan, or gluten free. I got just a little scoop of the hot peppers in my bowl and it was very hot, so skip those if you're weak when it comes to spice. They have containers of sesame oil, sriracha, and soy sauce to add if you want. I came hungry and the bowl filled me up. The employees were friendly, too! I really enjoyed my meal and will be back!

First time in tonight and really enjoyed the whole experience! The place is bright, well-decorated and they have friendly and attentive staff. They have a great variety of bowls on the menu and they even offered to add additional toppings as they filled your bowl right in front of you. The spicy tuna and yuzu ponzu salmon bowls were delicious--made with super fresh ingredients and very filling! It was awesome to have so many different choices and we especially enjoyed the unique spam/rice snack wrapped in seaweed (wish I could remember the name) and the tasty pineapple ice cream (seriously, it rivals Disney's dole whip!) for dessert! I can't wait to go back and try more!
eBcOmqHyd0kSzZCg0z-HVA: This awesome new Mexican Restaurant is right next to China Spring. We just ate here tonight for the first time and it was delicious !  The best Mexican Restaurant in the area for sure. I'm from So Cal, so I am very picky about my Mexican food, and this place is awesome. I have my fave taco trucks in town, but this is a great brick and mortar spot. My husband got the carne asada burrito with mushrooms and onions inside with queso on top, and it was so good. The kids plates are under $5 for a good portion of food, we ordered a shredded chicken burrito a la cart after trying my daughters delicious kids chicken enchilada. The chicken was so flavorful and juicy. We got some good churros with caramel inside for dessert, which were only $2.50! They had a DJ tonight who played music and you could karaoke if you wanted to, which was a fun touch as well. My only issue was that the music was louder than I would have liked, but I will just ask them to turn it down a bit next time. This place will definitely be a new regular spot for us. We're so glad we finally have a good Mexican place in the area.

We ate dinner here for the first time tonight. We loved both the hot and mild salsa. The Chile Rellano was large and good, I liked that fact that the pepper still had some crispness! My husband had the Cazuelon Ojo Ciliebte and it looked and tasted great! The beans and rice were fair. We plan to go back! There was a miss up with my order but they quickly fixed it! 
The family who runs this restaurant were very kind! I hope our community can help make this restaurant a success!

I was excited to try this new restaurant because it is in the area that I live in. Let me be the first to tell you that this place is everything and some more. From the customer service down to the wonderful food I love everything about this place. I was even able to speak with the new owners of the restaurant and they absolutely have a heart for the community which is also expressed through their delightful food. If I was you I don't waste another second reading this...head there now and just experienced the greatness of this amazing restaurant. I will be a regular and I hope to see this place packed out every time I go.
wXmgk4I18DRFarkqHulYFg: The only place that delivers late night and has a massive delivery zone. Great and timely service. I've only had one issue with them and they gave me my next whole order for free to make up for it. I'd suggest the Lombardi calzone and the fried mushrooms!
Great food and fast delivery! They brought right to our hotel room. Would definitely recommend to anybody staying in Nashville!
XfvJ2iEq0XmciWIJvelZZA:
Café 29 is the upscale title of Cheekwood's coffee shop/café nestled into the old stables at the north end of the mansion. It's a nice little niche serving good coffee and espresso drinks, soft drinks, adult beverages, and various pastries, sandwiches, and snacks. It's also the perfect place to duck in and take a load off after traversing the ups and downs of the estate. The atmosphere suits the ambience of the estate's grand stables/garage - casual with a gaze slightly elevated toward serving general needs of casual folk. Visitors may also be interested in some of the touristy offerings for picnicking on the grounds.

My coffee and pastry were excellent. Your mileage may vary, but I'll certainly be back on a future visit.

The place was next to Cheekwood mansion. I was happy finding it because I was hungry. 
The place was empty but cozy. The interior was casual and simple. I believe in warmer seasons the place could be packed. 
The cafe definitely can improve on the menu bc it had poor choices- just couple salads and snacks. 
We ordered two salads and crackers for the child.
The salads were great! I ordered winter salad with the dressing on the side- it was yummy! Simple but good. 
I liked the posters on the wall that said the history of Cheek family and Maxwell coffee. 
If you visit Cheekwood gardens and mansion, stop to get some food and drinks! 
However, the cafe needs to improve the menu!:)

Not sure why all the hate for Cafe 29. This place is perfect for taking break from the heat of the day and getting a quick snack and a cool beverage. 

Is it overpriced? Yeah of course, it's a cafe inside of tourist attraction, but the food I had was still pretty good. We all order salads and I opted for the Mediterranean salad with grilled chicken. The salad itself was small, but it had great flavor and was refreshing in the heat. We also ordered fries for the table and they were sooo good! 

They serve alcohol here. Ciders, beers, and canned mixed drinks. I had to partake in a local beer from Hap Harry's. Cold beer, botanical gardens, and a lego exhibit... a pretty nice little Saturday if I do say so myself!
_rLAo3IiokiOV2WB_-DyWQ: Ordered the White Oak Barbecue Burger and that shyt was delicious son!! Fries were ok, service was good no delays and that was on a Saturday night so a lot of people at Fifth & Broadway! I have no complaints except on the fries I wish maybe try a different style.

Came here for the first time a few weeks and had a burger and fries with a drink. Both the burger and fries were good. Recently opened near in the heart of Broadway, near the Bridgestone Arena, Broadway and the National African American Music Museum. The service was nice and quick.
It was fabulous, the service was great and quick. Everyone in our party was happy with their meal!
pJv2Xu4yfFeUBiAGY95Feg: Ordered through Door Dash and immediately regretted my decision after looking at the Yelp reviews. Like everyone else, our order took 45 minutes longer than the original expected delivery time AND they messed up our order. In the four back of baos I was suppose to get a veggie, spicy sausage, chicken teriyaki & cinnamon apple but got two spicy sausages instead of the chicken teriyaki. The baos were alright. Lukewarm and the paper really stuck to the bao. The potstickers were very crispy but also very oily.

This was super good - I got some ginger chicken gyoza, ginger chicken dumplings, a teriyaki chicken bao, and a spicy sausage and egg bao. I got it delivered and it still arrived hot and delicious. Big fan! I ate it too quick to take a picture, unfortunately. Great prices too!
1JxSgUivApwcRYFnXw23cQ:
On a busy weekend in Nashville where the coffee shop on the corner near our hotel was teeming with tourists, we happened this newly opened, sparsely populated, and elegant little spot on 4th Ave. The cafe is in the Dream Hotel but it fronts on 4th Ave and anyone can wander in. They have cozy corner seating or a bar overlooking the street which we enjoyed. The latte was beautifully adorned and delicious (and expensive). But we were happy to find a quiet, sophisticated place to have our morning coffee.
The menu offered breakfast dishes and the pastries at the counter looked great. We just had lattes, but I would recommend this spot.

We tried out Natura Cafe today and it was a perfect lunch spot! It's a small cafe tucked in the Dream hotel. The cashier and barista were friendly and competent. The cashier answered all my questions about the hotel, the restaurants and the 70's dive bar in the basement. Such a fun place! 

I ordered the Maverick Club and it was hearty and delicious. Definitely enough to feed two. We ordered some fries with cilantro sauce for the table. I love good french fries and these were perfect! I also ordered a fancy coffee and the name fails me, but it came with a toasted marshmallow and was so good!

The pastries and cookies looked yummy too. This is a great spot to meet up for a coffee or quick lunch! There's also valet and it's only $5!
Ki2k9zcMxrvZC_fJRNZVCA:
Disclaimer: This review is based on the bar area & the appetizer menu. I haven't eaten in the actual restaurant.

A friend and I wandered in on a random Friday night. It doesn't look like much on the outside, and the inside is a little dated, but the place was packed! Luckily, we found a little table in the bar area. The live music was fantastic and our waitress was surprisingly attentive given how busy it was. The drinks aren't overly expensive, and taste great! I can tell I'm getting my money's worth. I recommend the duck nachos for an appetizer. (As I've mentioned in previous review, I'm not a nacho girl) but these aren't heavy and have a variety of flavors. The truffle french fries smelled amazing!

Given how small the bar area is and how busy it was, it doesn't seem like the greatest location to sit down and hang out for hours -- but it's still fun!

My wife and I ate at F. Scott's this weekend. We thoroughly enjoyed ourselves. Fir the appetizer we had the market flatbread which was good. It was topped with chicken and some type of sauce (i can't recall what type of sauce) either way it was good and large enough to share. Fir the entree I had the New York Strip with sweet potato fries. She had the Wagyu beef cheeks with sweet potato fries.  The New York Strip was a club cut one that I'm not used to. It looked like a large fillet but the waiter confirmed the cut for me. It was cooked perfectly tender ave well seasoned. 
The sweet potato fries were ok I thought the texture was a little of but they were still good. My wife's dish the Wagyu beef cheeks was different but good. I know Wagyu is supposed to be super but this was too much. When I tasted her dish the beef dissolved within seconds. I've come to refer conclusion that I like to chew my steak. It wad good though. For dessert we had the sweet potato cheese cake. We were going to split it it was barely enough for one. It was different but good. I am taking one star off for portion size and price and yeah maybe I'm being nit picky but I really do five stars. But all and all I really enjoyed myself. The atmosphere here was great there was a live jazz band in the lounge that you could hear throughout the restaurant. Our waiter was good though sometimes it took him a while to check in on us. Price was expensive. With the tip or Bill was $170. DEFIANTLY a social occasion spot but all in all its with going to just be ready for the bill.
What a great find!  Thank you, Yelp contributors, for leading us to this fine restaurant.  We absolutely loved the food and wine;  the service was even better.
8xcWmiwVCLKT5opCuQV2Qg: I was too lazy to walk over today for the taco salad, and I went somewhere else, and while the folks at the somewhere else place was nice -- they could. It match the quality of Oscar's!
I tried the California burrito and it was amazing. Staff was very friendly and helpful. Meal came up quickly.
Yum!! Visiting from North Carolina and wanted a quick bit for breakfast. So glad we stopped here. Delicious!
8x7oJzbcOndkG7Mlo96eXA: Absolutely loved this place.  Went in for happy hour drinks and had some food.  The food was some of the best I have had in Nashville. We had two different types of tacos, the brisket, the corn succotash, and their sourdough bread with an amazing vegetable butter of fermented Collie flower and olive oil. The flavors were out of this world. I highly recommend this restaurant to everyone.  The ambience is casual which I loved.

This place is pretty cool. The atmosphere is amazing. We had some really unique cocktails, and some delicious nibbles. But it's catering to a pretty slim audience so I wonder about its viability. I went with work colleagues that I was friendly with and looking to impress. It did the job. We walked across the street for dinner at Sea salt.

This classy bar is the new "must try" restaurant in Nashville!!!

The newest social club concept bar, The Black Rabbit, has it all! Delicious food, sophisticated libations, and the ambience of a 1920's speakeasy. This is the perfect place to grab a cocktail after work or start off the evening for a fun bachelor/bachelorette party weekend! Black Rabbit is not your average bar or restaurant.

If you want the ordinary, visit any bar on Broadway. If you want the extraordinary, visit the historic Printer's Alley, do yourself a favor, and visit the Black Rabbit!
xlMQBBt9wrtahdqiRDcVSg: Great mason jar drinks, food was delicious and the vibe was perfect for lunch, people watching.

Yummy food, cool atmosphere. The Mac and cheese is good. Would return. Service was alright but they were also super busy. First place I went to in Nashville and didn't disappoint. Mason jar cocktails are a must have
Great food and atmosphere at very reasonable prices.  Try the Brussels Sprout pizza it's really good.
tIvfmgT1qMeAEQf8CI5fPQ: If you are into adventurous eating, this is the place to go. I think it would be great for a 1st date!! Who wouldn't want to eat boar sausage or elk? :) loved it! The atmosphere is entertaining with an indoor little pond. It's friendly and reasonably priced.

If you leave here hungry, it's your own fault! Extremely generous servings including the complimentary basket of corn fritters. I had the catfish/ribs combo and enjoyed every bite. My spouse had a house salad with chicken; the chicken was too seasoned for her taste and the lettuce was somewhat wilted, but our server Noble made it right and comped half the cost of her meal. Definitely worth trying at least once...I'll come back next time I'm in the area.

We came here on a Wednesday night, party of three. We did not wait very long but out server Susan was absolutely amazing, best server ever. I had the "Southern Surf and Turf", which were ribs and catfish. The ribs were fairly tasty but they seemed par-boiled...that was very disappointing, I had bigger expectations. The catfish was good and the flesh did not taste gritty (but I am not an expert on catfish). We were also given corn fritters at the beginning and were a very pleasant surprise. 

I tasted my wife's pulled pork and it was by far one of the BEST pulled pork I had ever tasted. Cooked to a perfect temperature and full of smoky, juicy flavor --no sauce required,  even though they give you some anyways.

All and all, I think the pricing was fair for the food you received. The decor was slightly dated but I would definitely say that this is a nice casual restaurant suitable for families or some close friends.
sXm5nP7B4QsSJrCSKBMCTg: Cute hipster place. TONS of gluten free options - ravioli, pizza, cheese bread and chocolate cake. Normal stuff too. Italian and Greek food.

There are quite a few places to get pizza in east Nashville and this has to be one of the best local pizzerias around! check it out. Large Carry out under 10 with tax can't beat it for a tasty pizza!!

Love grabbing pizza here! Whether I'm ordering in or carrying out, I've received great service and extensive explanation about menu items I was curious about! Oh, and the pizza is delicious... let's not forget that! I enjoy a meatball and ricotta pizza myself!
rCOIoZ3WuA6pkgRltfEN1g:
Three. Cheese. Tortellini. That's all I've got to say. 

Consistently excellent food & service. The tiramisu is amazing.

Great Early Bird/Happy Hour!  The crab cake is delicious and just $5... can't beat that!  They also offer some great wines for only $4 until 6:30, unlike most happy hours that only have crappy ones.  Also, what makes it even better is that the people there are all so friendly.  Our bartender, Jacob was so wonderful and attentive which definitely made our time there even nicer.  We try lots of happy hours and this is by far the best one in Nashville!  Love it!

My second visit to Amerigo's sealed the deal! I am fully convinced that Amerigo provides stellar service and outstanding food. I can assure all readers of my review that management will go above and beyond the norm to make sure their customers have the very best dining experience possible. This is shown in the treatment by the wait staff. Did I mention the food. Listen folks this is not your run of the mill Italian restaurant. So if you are used Olive Garden then the food and service might not be to your liking because this extraordinary service, awesome food at affordable prices! Need I say more!
RqduySV3Znrn3DCEKNeV6A:
The Lobster pizza rocks!  Really, go get you some!

I went with a group of girls for a bday celebration.  We went early evening on a Saturday night and were seated right away, but it was packed when we left.  The atmosphere is casual and family friendly.  

The lobster pizza was one of the best pizzas I have ever had (and its huge!!I had leftovers for breakfast the next day). A friend had ordered the brussel sprouts and let me have one, they were so good!  

I plan on taking my husband soon!

Always delicious and great service.  Love to support a local restaurant, especially one where the quality is better than most Nashville restaurants (chain or otherwise).   Right when I thought I'd tried everything on the menu, they add new items that are even better than the originals!
good service but recommend the bar. Earl is great. wish the menu changed more often. great value and quality for price.
hqqBYeQQ_c2FMvgGKrr59A:
My wife and I stopped here for our first dinner in Nashville. We were staying nearby and had heard about the restaurant from some friends.We are here from Charleston, SC. Good food is important to us and I am easily disappointed. Cock of the Walk is some good food and great service. 
My wife and I shared the 1/2 and 1/2 Keelboat special. We both enjoyed it all  even though neither generally eat catfish. We usually have seafood and shrimp. I don't remember ever having catfish before. It was good and this may be a spot we return to before we leave.
The meal was great. I don't know how one person could eat it all. The breading was crisp and flavorful. The sides were all good. And our waitress, Stephanie, was as nice as could be, helpful, and friendly. We couldn't have done any better for our first meal in Nashville.

This place was so interesting for us. Being from California, this was a great experience!  The food was great. The pickled onions which I was hesitant to eat at first were surprisingly good. The catfish and shrimp were awesome as was the coleslaw. The service was also pretty good. Just the fact that we ate on tin plates and cups was really cool. All in all the was a great place to experience while in Nashville!!

The food was great. The fish was well cooked and seasoned well.  Service was ok.  The meal was somewhat pricy due to the overload of fries on the pan.  Cornbread was also good, although the hush puppies were sweet.
qZa7ClLmG89oI09E6QUnyQ: Best cafe in Nashville! J & J has an awesome inventory of craft beers, tobacco and imported snacks. The cafe are and WiFi are a plus, too!

I must confess. I have never heard of J&J's (people in my circle of programmers call it JJs) until a friend of mine told me. I drove by there on my way to Vanderbilt, but never stopped.

That will change.

Although mixing alcohol and computers may not be the best idea in the world. It is the  one of the few places that serve alcohol in the market part and drink it in the cafe part. 

If you have never been to J&J's, the cafe is straight ahead as you enter the store. The wifi is free and fast. Also, if you work in the morning, it is practically empty so you have plenty of room to spread out.

Two tips: 1. Parking. There are 2-hour parking meters surrounding the store. There is a limited number of parking devoted to J&Js. They are always full. 2. During my visit, there was no bathroom for customers, so plan accordingly.

JJ's is the perfect hipster coffeehouse in Nashville, and a (thank god) great alternative to (shudder) Starbucks. They play classical music in the mornings and afternoons, serve pretty much the best coffees and looseleaf teas I've had since I moved to Nashville, and there's plenty of tables for you to sit and read, chat with friends, or study (which, as a student, is pretty much all I do there).

Also, be sure to try their dirty chai. It's amazing.
AVf4RO3bh2AAoZeCiPTQNg:
I heard this was a Nashville must so of course we had to come by and try it. There are multiple locations and I'm sure they're all great!

The wait was hours and the building was TOO packed. But, a tip for all you yelpers...order online! This will cut your wait time from an hour to 25 minutes. You can get it to go and actually take it to go or find an open seat and eat in house!

The chicken was AMAZING. I got no heat and it was perfectly spiced and juicy. It came with 2 sides and I would recommend getting the macaroni.

Portions are pretty large that you could even share with someone.

So overall, this definitely is a Nashville must and worth a wait. But remember to ORDER ONLINE!

This spot is amazingly good. Everything we had was awesome from the chicken to the greens was mouth watering. They have different spice levels of chicken and we had the medium and hot they were both juicy, crispy, and made you just want to dig in. The medium and the hot had a noticeable heat difference, personally I enjoyed both. The sides were nice and refreshing compared to the spicy chicken it pairs wonderfully with the potato salad and greens which help to cool the mouth down in between bites of spice. I could see myself coming here every time I visit Nashville.

All Nashville locations of Hattie B's are truly outstanding, and they all offer top-notch service. There are a lot of really good hot chicken joints here in Nashville but this is the best hot chicken place in town for sure, in my opinion. I love this new 8th Avenue location, too, because it's so close to my house.
XnbuS6ieFxsDSH9go88juw: This review is for their donut! They specialize in brioche donuts. The guy taking my money said the dough takes 24 hours to prep. Well, I don't care how you make it, as long as it tastes good and you don't charge me an arm and a leg for a donut, I'm good. I tried the original and my husband tried the one with salt. Both were very good and substantial in size. I like that they were not overly sweet or covered in exuberant amount of icing. They are on the pricier side but not as expensive as some other places in town. I would definitely stop by once in a while for a donut if I'm craving one.

As soon as you go up to the counter, you're greeted by awesome staff who are eager to explain the concept to anyone new. The donuts are brioche, which ties into the buns used for their hotdogs. 

They have some exciting options flavor-wise for both, though I only had a hotdog. The Mexican-food inspired hotdog was amazing, with corn and cotija cheese over a chorizo hot dog. The seasoned fries were super thing and crispy with just the right amount of spice.
Best atmosphere. Best hot dogs. Best beer. Best donuts. The donut + dog is our favorite spot in Nashville. Hands down.
5QsipB9-N4RMj94c-CvwUw: Fantastic dinner at this restaurant.  Food is off the charts.  I had the Salmon and grits and it was VERY good!  The 3 others in my party LOVED their dish as well.  A must go to!  Cant wait to go back.  We have been to all of the new, great places in Nashville and this might be my favorite.

This place is underrated in my opinion. I came with two others for brunch on a Sunday and pleased to find there was no wait, unlike every other restaurant in the vicinity. We sat at the bar and enjoyed great service from Christopher. I appreciated his honest drink recommendations and ability to put his own twist on some of our drinks to better fit our preferences (he made me a version of the cucumber gin fizz replacing the simple syrup with muddled cucumber to cut out some of the sweetness and keep it fresh tasting). You have to get the deviled eggs! The tater tots that came with my green eggs n ham omelette were also amazing.

Love, love the feel/vibe of this place.  Rustic charm with ridiculously great food and service...doesn't get much better than that!  We met up with friends for a light bite and drinks - sat in a bench style table which was cozy and comfortable.  Great beer selection and great apps.  We did not order entrees as we had dinner plans elsewhere but based on the apps we shared (hot fries, mushrooms, brussel sprouts) the entrees have to be amazing.  The service was spot on - not too pushy and not at all annoyed that we were occupying a table for essentially apps/drinks.  Bar was super cute with low lit lighting.  Can't wait to go back!
8QeaCReOO-ryojndLvkBNg: This is a highly underrated restaurant.  The Lamb Keema Biryani is the best you will find anywhere.  Most buffet restaurant have biryani but no where near this quality.    The majestic chicken is outstanding.   My wife says it's probably her favorite thing in the world to eat.    The Chicken and Vegetable Kormas are very flavorful and thick.  The samosa chat is to die for!  I eat a lot of things here I don't find or eat at other Indian restaurants.  This is a good solid restaurant even if it is a chain.

Ordered two lunch items that came with drinks and utensils. 

Pros
Awesome boneless chicken biryani
Quick service
Not a long wait time at all
Everyday is a special 
Really pleasant employee at the counter 
Variety of items 
Very clean indoors
Outdoor seating available 

Cons
A buffet would really bring this place to the front page 


Definitely visiting again!

A jewel of a find in Nashville! This tiny Indian restaurant was a pleasant respite after traveling all day. I ordered the Monday special, the chicken biryani and was not disappointed. I could only eat half of the generous portion yet could not take it with me since I was leaving the next day. It's a family run operation and the authentic food will not disappoint.
GOYnaGqdeI8nzg5EnPOd4w:
This place is hands down the greatest BBQ in Nashville. Not only does provide you with a perfect home style taste, it makes you feel like you're at home. The food, the service and people are amazing. Chris is an excellent up and coming chef who I am counting to make it big!

They take care of me each and every time I step foot in there.

Tips? Amazing. Shoulder? Tender juicy deliciousnous . Chicken? The best in town. Get there now. It makes the others look like amateur hour. 

Happily addicted to the sweet heat,

Adam

On our return family trip from AZ to Boston we were looking for some down home real deal BBQ in Nashville and found G'z on Yelp and it definitely lived up to the hype!..The owners and staff were awesome and the BBQ was tremendous!..As the logo says, at G'z..."A Pig Don't Stand A Chance!!...Highly Recommended!!

https://www.gzsbbq.com/
Some of the best BBQ, people and vibe. If your looking for real BBQ this is the spot!
_yQknBb12RLQA1FhZsJPSA: Oh my goodness!!!!!!!!!!!!!!  I was visiting Nashville for the day and was so excited to get breakfast here!! I wish I had a dedicated GF restaurant in my city!  I had the eggs benedict- which was delicious!  Though, slightly bland on the sauce itself.  I also got to have some of my friend's French toast- heaven!! Absolutely heaven!  The area is adorable too!  We sat outside and it was lovely.  Check out the little shops around there too!  There's a little sewing/knitting place that was cute and a antique shop that we loved!  I will definitely eat here again when I come into town.
Worth the wait!!! The best gluten free food I have ever had!!!! Can't wait for the cookbook!!!

I wanted some good patio weather and a good salad and AMOT was recommended for me. Everything I had- the seasonal vegetables, spinach salad, and some of my bf's sweet potato fries tasted great. However I added chicken to my salad which was covered in pesto. I cannot eat pesto, so I was upset by this, and it would have been okay had the server come back in time to get me a new one, but alas that was not the case. Next time I shall know. Everything else was great!
Gi4ex2Tq7ggRlpN0S7k5Xg: I'm an ice cream girl at heart.  I would eat it every day at least once if my metabolism would allow it.  All I know is, it's a good thing I don't live in close proximity to Jeni's or I would be in big trouble and in need of bigger pants! This ice cream is amazing.  Hands down, the best I've ever had.

I'm a sucker for ice cream, but Jeni's takes the cake (or maybe cone) for my addiction. This place is very modern and had a fun atmosphere about it. Every time I drive by, there seems to me a line out the door. 

I stopped in with a friend to check it out, finally and I'm so glad I did. There was a line when we arrived, but we didn't wait long.

We were greeted by the friendly scooper (not sure the technical term - but it sounds right?) who encouraged me to try a few different types so I got the ice cream I truly loved! 

I had a few samples and chose on the Pistacho and Honey. It was very hard to chose from all the fun flavors they had available. 

I can't wait to head back and try all the other flavors!

I knew going in that we were in for the smallest $9 sundaes in town, yet this place did a brisk business on every January evening we passed by. At $14.20 for that and another small item, we were satisfied, but I don't see how it can be a regular indulgence.
uEe6LCrh8vcPY9-T-CGvGQ: Awesome fried fish and wings.  Had the fried whiting and extra spicy wings.  both were Worth the drive from Midtown Nashhville.  Perfectly fried and seasoned.

The spice is amazing! Go mild unless you wanna burn ever so good 
The beans were the best I have ever had in my entire life

This place is the jam!! 

The sides are all amazing, ordered my chicken hot, it was delicious and I was in tears.. Probably just order medium or mild next time.. Fair warning, it's really hot.
hojeOgKi-dRfd4da6gPdJg:
I love wine bars and it has been a little difficult to find one since moving to Nashville. I think I found my wine bar home here. 

The thing I liked most about this place was how knowledgeable and thorough the bartender was (I think was also the same gentleman who chooses the wines and designs the cocktails). He does explain everything, which I loved, but my friend didn't love so much. 

The wine "flytes" were clearly chosen thoughtfully and every wine I tasted was such good quality. The popcorn is so different...definitely a must try snack while you wait for everything else to arrive. The deviled egg flyte has such unique flavors. The charcuterie board was very full and satisfying. The duck flyte is a MUST. I savored every bite...so much so we ordered the duck entree after that. 

There really is nothing to complain about. They were friendly and, since I'm new to Nashville, they recommended a ton of places to check out. If I could afford to go there twice a week, I would. 

If anyone wants to take me on a date here, I wouldn't be mad about it.

I've been once for a full-course dinner and three times for happy hour for drinks & food.  I've never been disappointed.  Great food and good service.  Their elk burger on their happy hour / lounge menu is delicious.  I really think Flyte is consistently one of the better $$$ restaurants in Nashville, especially with a new restaurant popping up every day that typically turn out to be mediocre.  If you haven't tried it yet, go check it out.

The atmosphere at Flyte is great for a nice evening out. The service here is incredible-quick and frequent yet never in your face. My water was filled and dishes were taken away without me even noticing. 

The wine selection was a little overwhelming, but the waiter was more than happy to help me choose from the large selection. The food was amazing and better than I had even expected. I got scallops for an appetizer, filet for my entree and some sort of apple dish for dessert. They were all very well made with quality ingredients which left me quite pleased with all three of my choices. The only reason this place gets 4 stars from me is because I left without being full-and honestly it doesn't take much food to fill me up. 

I'm sure I'll be returning here again sometime in the near future. This place is now on the top of my favorites list.
9ik4zu2353HJu9QMrVzOiQ: Normally I wouldn't bother reviewing any Mc Donalds, as they're pretty much all the same, but this one was really outstanding.The manager was warm and friendly, the staff were genuinely polite, the service was speedy, the breakfast was hot and the oj was ice cold.Overall a very well organized operation.

So today was a normal Friday, I expected the same mediocre service as every other day at this McDonald's. So I cruise up to the window and order a strawberry milkshake breakfast of champions for $3. I roll up to the window get handed a bag and drive away. Low and behold this bag contains 10 chicken biscuit, 10 hash browns and 10 apple pies. Best McDonalds ever all that for $3, y'all done mcfucked up. But thanks!!!

My AM and afternoon McDonald's.  Sometimes you just need a McDonald's Diet Coke I don't know what they put in it but it somehow tastes better than any other diet coke option available near downtown.  This is only four stars because sometimes they mess up and give you regular coke (maybe 1 in 50 times) so make sure to taste it before you drive away.  

The food is fine it is up to the usual McDonald's standards.  Unsweet tea is good as well but make sure you ask for lemon.  The employees are also always very nice.
BAIETJ6VZyjuVcog_kQVFw:
Yummy food, quick service, and modern atmosphere. Taziki's is located in the busy gulch area and yet they've found a way to not be overly crowded and keep the line moving.

I had the grilled veggie plate and, I don't know what seasoning they use, but my life has been changed for the better. The red peppers and asparagus were mouth-watering. I'll be back. A lot.

Service was awesome here! Same dude who took our order also served the food and took our trash once we were done. They seem to get regulars here too as the staff was overly friendly with certain customers.

It was around 1:30 when I came with my family and it wasn't even packed at all. I saw two or three other groups here finishing their lunches.

My dad and I both got the chicken gyro. I got fruit as a side while he got basmati rice. It was good but could've been more flavorful. I've had better. My mom and sister got the lamb gyro with basmati rice. My sister thought it was just okay and that the rice had too much lemon. My older sister got the mezedes platter which came with hummus, tzaziki sauce, pita, and grape leaves with rice in it. The grape leaves weren't wrapped well. She said it was just okay.

It was clean though! And service was a selling point.

Although it is a franchise, it's really good food. I'm hooked on the salmon greek salad. For the money, its a great lunch option. This one is good and I really like the West End location, too.

Our office is on West End and we often order out lunch for the office on Mondays. As far as healthy food options, this one is really popular in our group.
dBNfyR0RRX-w1SMuYYF3-A:
The Jam is so cute! I was running late to work yesterday and didn't have time to make my usual coffee at home, so I stopped in for an iced coffee on my way.

They were out of iced coffee (Sad) but made me an iced Americano (happy!). It was delicious.  The shop reminds me of time travel into a cooking show from the 1950s because the hip ladies behind the counter are doing the coffeemaking out in the open for everyone's educational benefit. It's pretty adorbs with the aprons and the furniture but so futuristic with the wifi! Yet the milk is kept in like actual glass jars in a fridge and they take it out just for you! 

I came here about a year ago when they were running a scoutmob deal for a breakfast sammy (and remember liking it!) so I'm not sure why I haven't been back until now. Ample parking, quick service, friendly ladies. Just minus 1 star for not having my beloved fave iced coffee (but I'll be back to check to see when they do and to try some food again).

My wife and I stopped here before she had to run to work. I had the "Dirty Chai" and the "Breakfast Sandwich" both were exceptional. Really nice group of people working there. I will say though, if you're looking to get food at this location, give yourself a whole lot of extra time. They are pretty slow when it comes to making their sandwiches and whatnot.

I prefer to do my freelance work in coffee shops, so I've spent plenty of time perusing coffee shops in Nashville over the past 8 years. The Jam is my current favorite. Its warm and comfortable retro atmosphere, friendly and fun staff, delicious food and high-quality coffee keep me coming back at least once a week. The breakfast tacos are a fantastic gluten free breakfast option, and the coffee and smoothies are really good. Wifi consistently works and there are plenty of electrical outlets to charge up.
LF8UjsBRaQ4Aqwu3ARxnBg: Service was amazing and the food was absolutely delicious. We did not wait lonbg at all for a table and shortly after we were seated the appetizers arrived at our table. The staff is attentive and had the table overflowing with huge variety of meats.

My sweet carnivorous husband loves Brazilian Steakhouses.  We've been to the ones iin Memphis and Atlanta and were excited to see that Nashville now has one.  I'm not a huge meat eater, so I had only the salad bar, but it was really good.  My hubby had the full experience and gave it two thumbs up.  Service was superb.
After a nice meat overload meal, they have a great gluten free chocolate cake. Yam yam, I am in heaven!
JWqbjm0wtbbciu_Gb6Eqyw: The food was amazing and the server was awesome. The food came out fast and did not give you the feel that it was setting under a heat lamp... the corned beef was cooked perfectly and the horseradish sauce appeared to be home made. Highly recommended this place...

We ate here for lunch and had a wonderful meal.  The Scotch eggs were tasty and devoured quickly.  The fish and chips were good, but the steak and Guinness pie was the true winner for me.  And the Black & Tan battered onion rings - you don't need ketchup or malt vinegar for these bad boys.  I did dip them in the leftover Scotch egg sauce just for fun and WOW, that was good!  The Guinness is served at the perfect temperature so it goes down smooth and the flavor just right.  I highly recommend this little Irish gem deep in the land of Country Music.  Wish I lived closer because I'd make this as my destination point for St. Patrick's day!

Honestly, this is the best pub I've ever been to in America, and I've been to a few.
I can only surmise that McNamara's employs certified Guiness pourers, as the pints I got were absolutely perfect; temperature was correct (no one ever gets that right, its always too cold everywhere else), they were allowed to rest on the bar for the correct period of time before being served, and the head was amazing and creamy. That's worth the drive from Atlanta alone, in my opinion. 
As if that weren't enough, the food is amazing. Scotch eggs and Black and Tan battered onion rings make for great starters, and the fish and chips are authentic - though don't look for them to be served on newspaper, because honestly, that's just not very sanitary. Steak and Guiness and Shepherd's pies are mouth-wateringly great.
Our waitress Diane was amazing, skilled, and perfect - a feat unto itself. Its so hard to find a great server, and McNamara's seems to be full of them. I tipped 35% and was really happy to do so. to let her know how much we appreciated her.
The band was entertaining and not so loud that we couldn't hear ourselves talk and make moaning noises because the food was so good. Very talented folks.
I've been here twice, and will go every time I get to Nashville. McNamara's is on par with some of the best pubs I've been to in the UK, and better than many there.
Just go.
YOU'RE WELCOME.
G6woICjwtJIbYHc6xatqfw: Best Waffle House! We ordered food to-go and everything was hot and fresh. The order was correct and service was great. Thanks to the great people at Donelson Waffle House for an awesome late night snack!

This was the best Waffle Houses I've visited.  The entire facility (inside and out) is so well-kept I had to ask if the facility was recently renovated (the parking lot was redone recently but the remainder has not).  I found out that this restaurant is a "training" facility where the extremely talented permanent staff trains newbies before sending them off to other WH's.  Looks like they do a lot of training on how to clean!

The staff here definitely knew how to treat their customers and the food was just great, especially for the price.  I thought our local WH was good but this team has set a new standard!

I always get Hash Browns ALL THE WAY and they were excellent!
Love eating here. They can be busy at times so it may take a few minutes for your order to be called. However, the employees are very nice and food is what you'd expect.....delicious!!
2709SJIFgW-8kS54Pnvc9g: Indian/Italian fusion? Yes, please. The food is amazing, the service (while a little uneven) is friendly and very accommodating. My favorite is the curry pasta, the fried gnocchi, and especially the panna cotta dessert...I can't get enough of it. This is rapidly becoming my favorite restaurant--I'm going back again tonight.

I was skeptical at first due to the confusing sign and wasn't sure how to pronounce the name- Then some friends recommended it and I'm so glad I gave it a try! Service and food was excellent and the Indian/Italian fusion is amazing! It was slow when we were there so I'm hoping more people will give this place a try. It's truly unique and delicious and not just a bunch of hype like many new nashville spots. Only suggestion is to improve the sign/branding so it is more clear.
Excellent food and service. Unique concept and will be back again next time we're in Nashville.
CGNccuDSFO9ZtqhQ46msOA: Great food and service. Justin the bartender was very helpful and knowledgeable. Surprisingly good whiskey and tap beer. Food was fresh and very good. We will be back.

The food was really really good, I got the lobster Mac and cheese and cheesecake for dessert. Our server was very attentive. The only downside was the actual service was slow, in terms of waiting for food but I think that might be what some are looking for in this atmosphere. Would definitely recommend the place to friends!

What a great surprise. I had been traveling over holidays (C'mas 2010) and was on the way to NC. I stayed at the Nashville Springhill Suites Airport Center Dr. Dec. 30, 2010. Sorry I forgot one of the lady's name at hotel front desk but I had asked her to recommend an upscale restaurant close by. She quickly recommended Ellendale's. I called to make sure of attire. Upon entering the front door I felt welcomed and knew I was in for a treat. The owner, Julie met us and told us about the history of her restaurant. This place is a gorgeous restored dairy. Ambiance is warm and wonderful. Food was incredible. waitress was knowlegable and had no trouble recommending items when asked. After driving over 7 hours I was needing a treat and Ellendale's was no disappointment. If you are staying (or live) in the Nashville Airport area it is well worth a drive. Only one disappointment, they were out of rabbit but my fish was outstanding. Prob one of the best Tirimisu dessertsI I have ever had.
0ByzGehS0T5muzZ2qYGq_Q: Took me forever to try this place..thanks to my neighbor who went...I'm addicted.  Wednesday $.60 wings...the bomb!  I think they know who I am over the phone!!!
I ordered the house platter and it was great the catfish was the best I've ever had. Love it!!!!!

This little place packs a flavorful wallop! My family enjoys the Asian Heat, Parmesan. I have not tried the sides yet, but they are worth the small upcharge for ordering on Super Bowl Sunday. Remember tasty things are worth the wait!
7WaVMXsqN2nNc3vTNCOOjw: Absolutely the most awesome homemade Italian food south of the Dixie line.  If you ever drive thru downtown Nashville you must eat at this location.

I really enjoyed this meal, and the place! My boyfriend had the gnocchi, and I never enjoyed gnocchi until I ate it last night.. It was SO GOOD!! We told the waiter, and he said apparently they have a unique way of making it there- I'm not exactly clear what they do, but whatever it is, it's awesome. I had the lasagna- also super enjoyable. The only thing was the dessert was a little bland- the pot de creme.. The NY style cheesecake was awesome though. It was prob a 3.5 star experience- what brought it up to 4 for me though, was our waiter. He was just super engaging, kind, funny and genuine. The sort of waiter that makes you want to go back to a place.. I wish I remembered his name!!

The food was excellent only to be topped by the outstanding service.  They were very accommodating because we didn't have a reservation.  The little touches to everything they did made the experience purely enjoyable.  Biscotti with the coffee, staff that worked like a fine oiled machine making sure you were always attended to. So happy we found this place... Thanks yelp!
MXTV7gLn1GcgBIskfIXwMg: We went on Thursday night. Amazing lobster dinner special. Great drink specials. $13 for half lobster stuffed with crab and two sides. Side choices are great veggies! We will be back!

Wow!  What a magnificent place!  I can't describe how good this place is. We started with the crab stuffed mushrooms and it just kept getting better from there. 
The menu suits everyone!  There are the finer things in life, like a phenomenal filet, to a prime rib sandwich that will keep your mouth watering for days, or something as simple as grilled chicken. Their sides also range the entire spectrum and the grilled vegetables, asparagus, and sautéed spinach are incredible!
Add all of the that to the fact that it's easy to get to and they have valet parking.....what else do you need?!?!
You definitely need to try this place out. I'll be utilizing this place for business dinners and out of town visitors from here on out!

I like this establishment. It feels like a good replacement for Blackstone. Loads of beers to try. I'm guessing about 50 on tap, and then hundreds of bottles. I like sweets and sours and stouts. Ran across a nitro raspberry fruit beer that was delicious. Have never seen anything like it before. We were there for lunch. I tried the tacos and they were delicious. I feel like the location in trendy Germantown might not be the exact right spot. Something closer to a major thoroughfare like West End would probably be better for this establishment. Still, looking forward to going back.
gG8opRMkztK0GKiSWbfVlw:
The craft coffee at Garage Coffee Co. is LEGIT! Housed in an old manufacturing building (or so it seems), the shop itself is decked out in industrial furniture to work with its surrounding environment. The gentleman taking our order was helpful in helping my wife make up her mind about a coffee. I went with the Salted Caramel Mocha. It had a beautiful balance of sweet and salt on the caramel. I enjoyed every sip. Acelyn B's was more delicate with lavender. Both coffees were made well and delivered great satisfaction to the tastebud. Notably, they serve their hot coffee at drinking temperature! No waiting for it to cool itself to begin drinking. 

Alongside our coffee, we went with the Turkey Apple Brie on Croissant. The sandwich was warmed up nicely. The croissant was flaky and buttery. The brie was nice and savory with the light sweet of the apple. I almost wish I didn't have to share. I'm pretty sure she feels the same.
Great coffee and great vibes happening in the Marathon building. Lotsa parking. Keep an eye out for Rob the owner....he'll make you feel right at home.
Slingshot and Cuban for the win!!! We absolutely love this place. Making it a morning must have!
6bpM6wCmdp6qZGTQbiR3-A: We had great service,  probably the best we have experienced in a while.  The food was also excellent and very reasonably priced.   We would highly recommend this place.

Super fun place to go to! They have a lot of traditional foods, but also some fun quirks. Peanuts to much on at the table and you can throw the shells on the floor. Itty bitty bucket sundaes that are delicious!!! The rolls are pretty amazing unto themselves. 

Good drink selection, servers were beyond friendly. Just a good joint all around. Check it out, you won't be disappointed.
Always get the ribeye and never disappointed. Salad is fresh and of course could fill up on buttered rolls alone.
r63rFmywD8j_XdXibn4-Bg:
They have really great food. I love their quesadillas and their salsa. It can be pretty busy (like most restaurants in the area) around lunch and dinner. They're pretty quick about rolling people in and out, though. 

They have some of the best beans and rice, too.

I have stopped by here several time in the last few weeks and I like the friendly, consistent service.  The chips are always plentiful and warm.  The bathroom is updated and has always been clean.  The staff are nice and eager to please.  

I have some crazy, self-imposed, dietary restrictions so I usually only eat the Carne Asasda at Mexican restaurants.  El Sombrero's version is called the Guiso Mexicano and it is very good.  A generous portion of grilled steak with peppers and onions, beans and rice.  This dish also comes with tortillas for those who like to wrap!

This meal is hard to beat for $6.99 on the lunch special.  They also offer discounts to police officers.

El Sombrero is a solid place to eat in Lenox Village. The margaritas are flavorful, the service is excellent, and the food is yummy. I went for lunch on a Saturday and had a Mango Margarita on the rocks for under $5. Needless to say, I took a nap shortly afterwards. I have had some overly salty food at times, but other than that I have enjoyed everything I have ordered. I like that they have a vegetarian menu, a seafood menu, and items not typically offered at a Mexican restaurant. They are in the Camino Real restaurant group of which I am NOT a fan, but El Sombrero tastes better. Great for to-go.
dxz6XU1S0BlC420_IiZwYg: All the staff was very nice. The restaurant was super clean including the bathroom. I told them it was my son's 20th birthday and they made sure to make him feel special!

Love the experience and service of TdB, couldn't be happier! Remember to eat light all day and come hungry! I made a reservation via Open Table. While there wasn't a wait when I arrived, it was nice to know they offer reservations! 

Our server explained everything and made sure our drinks were full and checked on us just enough.

If you've never been to a Brazilian steakhouse, this place is a meat-lovers paradise. The food is amazing. They tell you up-front that the meat is cooked medium rare. Otherwise, they'll also tell you if it's cooked any differently. 

The variety of meats will be sure to hit your taste buds perfectly! Chicken, sausage, pork, lamb and chicken circulate from table to table. They provide tongs for you to grab the meat as they cut it on to your plate. Included with your meal is a salad bar which has a variety of foods.They also give you mashed potatoes and cooked bananas. Be sure to try the pineapple from the salad bar! I also couldn't stay away from the sushi! It was just a California Roll, but the best California Roll I've ever had. Those were my two favorite items from the salad bar, I prefer Rodizio's salad bar 10x more. 

Overall, it was a great experience with delicious foods.

I want to preface this review with the fact that I'm not a huge red meat eater. I've never went out & ordered a steak at a restaurant, I'm not sure why, I just enjoy chicken & seafood more. Why would I come to a Brazilian steakhouse that has men walking around with meat on skewers? Well I was invited to dine here with a restaurant reviewer, & I thought it would be something different to try. 

The restaurant is very nice. It's upscale but I don't feel like it's putting on airs about it. All the staff I encountered were very friendly. Our server was nice, but almost robotic in his description of the food & the concept. Speaking of the concept, you sit down & first are able to pick what you'd like from the salad bar. Then once your done you flip a red card over to green, this indicates to the meat men that you're in the market for some meat. I had a "Brazilian Allure" cocktail that had vodka but tasted mainly like cotton candy. It was super sweet. Too sweet for me. It was $12. 

Salad bar, I would call it more of a cold bar, they had lettuce yes, & dressing but other than that there isn't really anything that I'd put on a salad. They have artisan salami & cheeses, olives & even some hot rice & white wine sautéed mushrooms that were great. 

Onto the meat. I ate probably more than I should at the salad bar because I could've left then & been fine, but the meat was a calling. I could smell it. It's not hard to notice it because at any given point there isn't about 4-5 guys walking around with the stuff. This isn't a place to bring a vegan or vegetarian because the meat smell is everywhere. Along with the meat, your server brings you mashed potatoes & fried banana to "cleanse your palate" as he told us. I tried the flank steak, garlic beef, Parmesan pork chop, Brazilian sausage & another meat that I cannot remember. Everything was hot and tasted fresh. If I was a red meat lover I would've been in heaven, but I enjoyed trying it all. 

After you've stuffed yourself with meat, the server brings by a tray of the different cakes & pies they have. The dessert is the only thing you're allowed to take home with you. I sampled the carrot cake and the bananas foster pie. Both were good but I wasn't blown away by them. I suspect they aren't made in house. 

The price. For salad bar only its 24.95, & if you want the meat & the salad bar it's 44.99. I think it's pricy, granted it's all you can eat, but again, I'm not a huge red meat eater.
e3i7sIhjMorKjQZTfEoLgA:
If you are going to have pizza, have Jet's! I generally like thin crust but their thick pizza is very good. No diet here. After the pizza is cooked, they will squirt additional oil on top of the pizza. 

Prices are fair ($10 for 1 large topping with coupon) and you can always find a coupon somewhere (check the back of your receipt from your local grocery store). There is some seating inside and is good for a party up to 6. 

There are only several parking spots, but I have not had a problem getting one.  
I wish they would have happy hour and that would make everything perfect.

Had Jets deep dish tonight. First time having in Nashville. Used to get in Charleston SC. It was delivered super fresh and piping hot. Friendly delivery guy and super fast! Delicious pizza during a home renovation!

For chain, quick-serve pizza, this is unusually tasty. Thee deep-dish versions are the best, because they come with all that delicious, chewy, crunchy crust. You can't go wrong with a classic pepperoni, but they've so got plenty of toppings to let you get adventurous.
TSKCK51GymAsxiA-kx-RiA:
We held our rehearsal dinner in the private space outside two weeks ago. Worked with Graham who was super helpful and made the evening go off perfectly. 

We had family style dinner for 50 people (the brisket was amazing)! And then opened up the party later in the evening for all guests in town. Having the outdoor space and flexibility was a nice way to include everyone and also have our own space. Also found that the price we paid was one of the better quotes that we received. Highly recommend for anyone having a birthday, engagement party or just a get together with a larger crew!

We just had the best experience with server Risa! My fiancé and I have been trying to stick to a keto diet lately and she went out of her way to help us navigate the menu. We ordered the brisket without tortillas and guacamole with veggies instead of the chips. Even with the dietary restrictions the food was so, so good. Just wanted to say thank you again for your patience and willingness to accommodate us!

I was staying at a VRBO across the street so we ended up going here for drinks 3 days in a row. Each time was great from the tasty drinks to the outstanding service. I tried the Buck and the Paloma and they were both great. We also got the nachos which were super tasty. I loved the open kitchen layout and the rustic design of the restaurant too. Next time I visit Nashville I'll definitely be back!
maOMGu_2dSHKydBgZdTsvQ: It is a nice place, if you like country music. The night I went they were having a line dancing contest. it was okay, I really went for the Beer and the free Pool. That was a huge plus...if you can score a table. Get there early if you want to play pool. The drink prices are moderate and the ambiance is nice. It is a cool place to checkout if you are in the area. The bars on the top 2 levels were closed. I had to go down a level to get drinks...That kinda sucked. Other than that it was okay.

Ain't no doubt about it, this one is for the tourists looking to "experience Nashville"--which I was, so take that with a grain of salt.

That said, my two friends and I had an unforgettable time here.  We came for the line dancing and stayed for the fun.
