At first the lesson echoed my middle school gym class lessons--everyone kind of nervous and embarrassed--so I was a bit skeptical, but once the band started going, the energy picked up and the experience completely changed.

Our instructor was really nice and helpful; she choreographed a whole bunch of routines after the initial lesson ended, and on many occasions got the whole crowd on its feet.  My friends had learned several dances in Kentucky, ones that she wasn't familiar with, but she showed them some variations (one that was "super cardio," ha!) so they were able to find a new way to enjoy that particular song.

We ended up staying until the very end, and could have stayed even longer!  We will definitely be back.

Great fun! Good prices. The band was fun, free pool and foosball tables while waiting for band and/ or table. I had the stampede sampler, and I would not recommend it. The ribs were good, the brisket was WAY too fatty, and the pulled pork was okay but too dry. I tried some of my moms monte cristo and it was phenomenal! I would recommend that all day. Overall great place, if I'm ever in Nashville, I will return here.
crgS4ogZEt2rYGwYVfeK2A:
Food is great. Ordering is odd. Menus are a wrinkled piece of paper and what the daily specials are put on a chalkboard that you can't read. 

Also the restaurant layout is odd. There is no flow and off to the side it looks like there would be a hostess stand but there isn't a hostess so it's confusing.

Vegan burger is very tasty - I always go with the lettuce bun - strongly recommend.  Sweet potato hush puppies are a must-try - they taste like a dessert, almost like a fried donut hole - they are addicting.

I've only sat at the restaurant once (I usually order for delivery) but management was very hospitable when I came in - was impressed with the care they showed me - they took care of me when I messed up my order.  Have nothing but good things to say about this place.  It's definitely worth a try

We had a fantastic experience here tonight!  The service was excellent the minute we walked in the door! The food was outstanding!  We had the No. 1 and the No. 5!  The chow chow on the no. 5 was delightfully wonderful!!
42tur3_5JgqOU-5KoY-zHQ: Had the fried baloney and beer battered onion rings. Food was simple and delish. They have a server issue. While my server was awesome but she handled the whole bar and resturant. She did it with a smile and was the best but they need to give her help.

Cool place. Came by for lunch and some music. Good singer and a really nice group of servers and bartender. Good prices and better music. Wings were ok. I hoped they wood have more smoke flavor. I'd come back for the music and cold beer.

I was staying at the Opryland resort for a conference and a Lyft driver recommended this place if I got tired of the resort. It did not disappoint. First of all, the food was well priced compared to the outrageous restaurants at the hotel. I had a chicken and a biscuit for $8.50 which is very reasonable for Nashville. The other great thing about this place is there is live music every night. I went on a Thursday and the band playing was very country- I mean from the 1920s and 1950s country. There was a set local crowed that goes these for this particular genere of music. During the few hours I was there I witnessed a self described drifter go on stage and play a guitar with the band and an 80+ year old Elvis impersonator get on stage to sing while the band members were collecting donations. I sat at the bar and immediately struck up conversation with a couple over their 80s who came their weekly for the music. The local crowd was very friendly and embraced me like I was one of their own. This is a Greta experience if you want to see a side of Tennessee/ the South we don't get to see in the Northeast.
Tmj0Xu3w0c-osJVqKwEBZw:
The waffles are the best I've ever had, and I love a Mimosa with my brunch! Such a great menu, only a handful of options, but I can't wait to go back to try the others. I had the omelette, which was delicious. My boyfriend had the waffle with apricots and the sauce that comes on the side is just too good!!
The servers were friendly and attentive. It really seemed like a team effort between everyone there. I am certain we will be returning next week!
Nice pop up style brunch with bar and a few traditional selections with style. I look forward to trying something new on the next trip.

Dang, this place was great.  A whole hell of a lot better than Steve's.  I typically hate omelets, but I got one here because I'm doing the low carb thing.  And It was the first omelet that didn't make me want to vomit.  That sounds bad, it's not.  It was awesome.  The salad that came with it was fresh and gave a nice contrast to the cheesy eggs. 

The lady got the huevos benedictos which was also amazing, some crazy flavors going on.  If I had to say something negative it'd be that it got a little noisy for a few moments, but there was booze.  So all good.  

Definitely worth checking out.
3_WGRjRrnZX2b4ZVpjmL6w: It's good and very fair priced but I have a hard time with all the 5 star reviews. I had the deep fried catfish, some veggies and we shared a chocolate pie and pecan pie. It is a great lunch spot and it gets very busy which can mean lines at peak times. It's well worth a try.

I came on a Monday when roast beef was one of the meats. It was a tad salty for my taste, but I was near he front of the line and got pieces closest to the end. My partner's fried chicken was really good and juicy. The seasoning came through really well even on pieces without crust. 

For sides I had Mac and cheese and green beans. These were fine-nothing spectacular. My husband had creamed corn and apples--those were amazing. I'm not sure what they do to their apples, but I would love to cuddle up with them on my couch. They were warm and sweet, but still had a tangy bite to them. Just amazing. 

Sweet tea was good, but I haven't found too many places in TN where the sweet tea isn't good :)

Oh my...died and gone to greasy delicious fried food heaven!  Meat and three was so so good...I got the fried pork chop, with mashed potatoes, green beans and mac and cheese.  So much butter and tastiness just all over the plate and I made sure to eat it all.  I have a weakness for comfort food and this definitely hit the spot.  Try to schedule a nap after the meal.
wGbvFI9JBkLB0H0ir_fhRA:
The restaurant is in the lobby of Loew's hotel where we stayed so we had several meals there.

Breakfast was good. The "Tennesse Benedict" was quite good. The oatmeal brûlée was really a brûléed banana on oatmeal. It was very good oatmeal garnished with very good berries. The crispy potato hash was not crispy and had a peculiar, somewhat metallic taste. 

Lunch was excellent. The truffle salt on the French fries is a very nice touch, as is the watercress on the burger. The chicken salad sandwich was pretty basic but very well done. The choice and quality of fruit on the side in place of fries was excellent. 

Dinner was outstanding! The deviled eggs were a wonderful, imaginative twist on the classic. The crab corndogs are something every crab lover should experience. The shrimp and grits is an extraordinary twist on this southern staple. The goat cheese pannacotta was also excellent. 

The prices are high but reasonable. The service was pleasant and appropriately attentive.

Overall a good bar at the Loews hotel across from Vanderbilt.  The Nashville hot sliders are just amazing. Two perfectly cooked hot chicken sliders on sweet buns with slaw and pickles.  

Only issue I had was with the old fashioned I ordered. TOO SWEET!!  I tried their house OF which was sweet and then a classic OF which was even sweeter.  I didn't see them make it, so they must use simple syrup and way too much of it. 

Will be back there on another trip to Nashville.

Hotel restaurants tend to get a bad rap in the food world, but if you find yourself staying at the Lowes Vanderbilt Hotel, you will be in for a treat.

I decided to dine here for a birthday celebration due to the good press Mason's consistently recieves in the greater Nashville area.

If you are not staying at the hotel, you can valet your car.  Do not forget to ask your server for a vocher.  This is a big bonus in my book because it is always gracious of a hotel to pick up the valet tab for their customers.  

The menu and decor both celebrates traditional Southern cuisine, however it didn't come off as upscale to me.  The menu and decor comes off as thoughtful, well planned with a side of kitsch.  Mason jars make up a majority of the decor.  The rich browns and clean lines make for a relaxing dinning room.  There is a private dining area on the side for larger groups (so keep that in mind yelpers if you are hosting a fun event).  I loved the bar menu, it was a leather bound booklet with a very beautiful picture of a rabbit on it.

I started with the Peach Old Fashioned.  They infuse makers mark with peaches.  The house made hop bitters cut through the sweetness of the peaches making for a well balanced drink.  My friend tried the House Made tonic and gin.  The tonic had a bit of an earthy taste to it.  Once the cocktail was completed, the biggest win was it was Wine Wednesday.  Half price bottles of wine (yes please).

The appetizer selection was good.  They were out of oysters, but I lived!  The crab corndogs are so good and the jars were a great complement.  I loved the pimento cheese and pepper jelly.  For dinner, I had the Chicken Gallatine with spicy collard greens and a turnip green puree.  All the flavors blended together beautifully and the chicken was very tender.  The Halibut and the Mason's burger were all enjoyed at the table.

Lastly, the Elivs dessert was the best.  I am of firm belief that dinner should be chocolate, upon more chocolate with chocolate and chocolate, plus some ofther stuff.  So that being said, dark chocolate mousse, with a bacon white chocolate brittle, peanut butter and bananna jam could have been my 1st, 2nd and 3rd course.
KBG3GV4WHU-iLLSWiapbJw: One has to respect this establishment. The food is always affordable, the service is on point and it tastes delicious. I've had almost everything on the menu and it is all tasty. It's really hard to go wrong with this place.
Excellent and cheap food. The jerk chicken was delicious.   The sause had just the right amount of spice.  Prices are very reasonable.   Highly recommended!

Calypso is the rock of East Nashville restaurants.  The food and service are alway great and the price is great.  It's great for kids without being a kids' place.  Parking is ample and there is never a wait.  I like the half chicken all white with jerk sauce, greens, and bean and corn salad.  The veggie plate won't let you down either though.
qvhz-oTyKjpFsYUBy0Uz0Q: We had lunch on a Sunday prior to leaving hotel. Food was very good. Other comments mentioned the price but it no more than I expected inside the hotel. Atmosphere was nice and very family friendly. I would recommend it if you want to eat in the resort.

Great service and an ok place for a bite. Massive portions, the kids had the burgers and those things were gigantic. Just the right size for teenagers but if you are an average person you have dinner and lunch in one order. The smoked trout dip was excellent, sushi was ok. The crab cakes at the Old Hickory were worlds better compared to the Cascades.

Went for a seafood dinner and had sushi and the pan seared Trout. The Trout was cooked very nice over quinoa, pistachio and glazed baby carrots. It was very tasty and plenty of food to satisfy me..our waitress P J was very friendly and a pleasure to talk to.. She described each dish and even took an intertest in us personally. The cost was higher than most restaurants nearby but the quality and atmosphere were well worth it..
Ns8vJxhsc5ygiX7Vff1bDQ: Stopped by after the Titans game. Fantastic atmosphere on the second floor. Open air seating. Bathroom was shit, but that's to be expected after game day. Pulled pork sandwich was solid. Music was fine, covers of Garth Brooks, etc. Compared to Honky Tonk's, I think Rippy's is the winner.

Good ribs, great pulled pork plate with 2 Yazoo Hefeweizen. Pretty good country band, despite the young look ! Good experience, if you don't mind plastic forks, knives and table cloths! You will not go hungry !
LOVE this place.  Always has great service and fantastic music!
nGhSGqhYkEnFOl_4LvqIIA: Great meal!!! The chicken taco salad was delicious. Better than most. Will come back soon. Accommodated a huge party of kids and parents really well. Efficient, fast, accurate, hospitable. Overall a terrific experience.

Our fave Mexican place in town! The chicken quesadilla is my fave, and the lunch combo is super cheap but huge!! The service is always fast and friendly. Their chips and salsa are very flavorful too. Highly recommend--we're here every month!
I picked up a Burrito Mexicano from there, and though the food was not top-of-the-line, it was totally good Mexican food. I can see it being a staple for a quick pick-up.
MFX3Gs26jatY--HB0YdFYw: I went to Vui's for lunch yesterday, and I was impressed by their light, healthy, and simple take on Vietnamese. My mom (who is vegan) got the vegan pho, which she loved. I got the rice bowl with tofu, which was quite delicious. They have a lot of options (including for vegetarians and vegans) - most of their dishes are made with your choice of beef, pork, chicken, shrimp, or tofu. Vui's is quite small, so it can get busy (and don't even get me started on the parking!), so we went at off hours (even at almost 3:00 in the afternoon on a weekday, there were many tables taken inside. Luckily, it was a beautiful day, and they have a charming back patio (as well as a front patio with one or two tables), that is covered and has 5 or 6 tables. We ordered at the counter and sat out on the back patio, which was relaxing and so quiet. I'm sure I'll be back and maybe next time I'll try the shrimp!
*** Just what I needed on this drear evening. Friendly, fresh & fast. Great for a grab & go meal!

Wow I would say get the Pho with beef and make sure you breathe between shovelful, it is so good you can get caught up just shoving it in your mouth. There are so many flavors to be enjoyed, slow down is my advice and enjoy the fresh flavors.
kLG2-BOk3YHDXS8PCxekZA: Pretty neat dive bar. If you don't like people smoking you will want to avoid this place. The food and service was great. Just remember this is a dive bar.

There's a subtle charm about the Gold Rush that keeps me coming back. Maybe it's that it's not pretentious, and a good place to get a no-nonsense cocktail or beer before a show or just to hang out with friends. They have an excellent selection of vodka, and it's been a LONG time since I've had the food here. On my most recent visit I eyed the spinach dip which looked quite delicious. 

I enjoy the Gold Rush. It's a dive bar where you need it most. I'll be back again so I can review the food!

NOTE: They allow smoking, so be prepared!

Classic dive. Food is cheap but you get what you pay for.  Canned ingredients pretty simple. Know that's what you're getting!  PBR and Jamison special is legit.  Too smokey for me but over all you don't go to a dive expecting anything else....
oB19CmdYq9EzVsYf-dbrXQ: Loved this place!! Service was great. Food even better!! Will be back before we leave Nashville.
Very good food. Tacos a win. I liked the Adobe chicken.  Nachos are decent.  The frozen Jack and cokes are good but expensive.
We had the private party room. The queso is fantastic! Loved the hot chicken tacos! The drinks are way too strong. I couldn't finish my mad dog margarita.
Khv2xprJmMO69kjhr6g5Eg: Long line but it moves quickly and everyone is in a good mood so it's kind of fun.  The service is polite and friendly.  We went all out and got into double trouble....a cheeseburger with a hot fudge sundae...yeah, baby!  Whilist waiting we got to see a lot of other delicious dishes go by and the sweet potato fries, corn dogs and onion rings looked especially good.  There is a list of specialty milkshakes that made for some entertaining reading and I think I might have to go back for it.  Rarely get out that way but I think it would be worth the special trip.

I ordered a Double Dipped cone, and it was delicious, and cheap! But I don't see much of a difference between this and any other ice cream joint. But definitely a good dessert to cool off after some Hattie B's so the location is great!

Bobbie's just opened for the season (Spring/Summer) and unfortunately I won't be able to partake in the eating frenzy this year due to relocation. Bobbie's has some of the best burgers, fries and ice cream in Nashville. Their burgers are large and taste like your typical backyard burger. The fries are fresh and good as well. But the Ice Cream is where its at. They dip your soft-serve ice cream into chocolate, butterscotch, and several other flavors of goodness. The shakes are also tasty....
0RxW0oN1s4zgNh6uiWVPZQ:
Eating Environment: meh. Service: meh. Food: outstanding. 

This place serves up tasty dishes, especially if you're looking to eat on the healthy side. Their food is made from very pure and simple ingredients.  The stars that I give are for the food. 

Their wait staff is just so-so, but you can always walk up to a take-out counter. 

The eating environment just seems old/sticky. 

I will be back, but only for the food. :-)

This is my new go-to lunch spot. I knew as soon as I walked in I was going to like this place, because it smelled amazing. I ended up with a tuna salad, which was affordable for a large plate of food, and a fruit tea, which was recommended as one of their best beverages! This is true - get the fruit tea! I will definitely be back to try their veggie burger and black bean nachos! My partner got the Lucayan salad and finished his whole plate, which means he more-than-liked it. Will definitely be back soon!

I entered the place with it's lotus flower emblum thinking, "Everything is going to be made from tofu." I saw the sweet faces of the people working, and the pretty hippie dreds I sometimes envy, and thought, "Yup, tofurky it is." Then we got the menu. Wow. There's chicken! The place has quite a few healthy options, and some not so healthy, so I'd say it's great for your average omnivore.

I really enjoy this place for it's ample parking, nice people, and roomy interior. Sometimes you're elbow to elbow with people at Baja and Sam and Zoe's down the street, so having room to stretch out like Al Bundy is kind of nice, if not rude on my part. The food is great, and at a fair price, so we were able to afford a dessert to go. I can't remember what it was called, it was some type of brownie thing, but it was good.
K2oygjf0jX7TeA__JptQZw: I really love the oyster po' boy here. This place gets fresh oysters delivered to them and you can tell. This sandwich never disappoints. I also love the Two-Step, which is a combination of the Crawfish étouffée and Jambalaya, the BBQ Shrimp, Catfish fingers and Bistreaux Gumbo. I have no complaints about the things I have tried and it's the kind of casual laid-back atmosphere I desire in a restaurant.
Food was good, but my crawfish étouffée was a little cooler than I'd liked. Great front patio.
Had dinner & apps there a few weeks ago. It was delicious & I'm from SW Louisiana, so I know good Cajun food when I taste it. Owners are super nice as well.
APD2VSVODMPpmO1_y-dt3Q: Very good service their country fried fish is very good along with their green beans and not to forget the hush puppies are the Bomb
Always a good lunch experience. Fresh, cooked to order and delicious every time. Managers work very hard and staff is friendly.  Always very clean and welcoming also!
Great Customer Service from Jaila, she was very professional and sweet! Food was fresh and fast.
kRh2647DS9MNnqQs89Ttgg: I haven't tried many dishes here because I found one I LOVE and have never had anywhere else--ENCHILADAS RANCHERAS. They are CRAZY good. It's layer of cheese enchiladas topped with slow cooked pork with tomatoes, onions and bell peppers. I can't recommend it highly enough.

In my experience, it been hard to find a good taco in east Nashville. I really enjoyed this place! Food was good, service was good, prices were good as well. I ordered the chicken tacos but I recommend the fish tacos -- they were delicious! And also, do yourself a favor, and start with the pitcher of margaritas. There were lots of larger groups that were having a blast. It's a great "let's get drunk and eat some good food." Go here over Rosepeppers if you want good mexican.

Our family loves this place.  We are there at least once a week.  Not only do we love the food, but it's the only time my kids will eat rice or beans (seriously, why won't they eat it when I make it at home? but I digress...).

They are very nice and friendly and always acknowledge and say hi to our kids.  It's rare to find a restaurant so accepting of small children! Love this place!
QSn_5UAMTErtS8HpevjHfw: This place has the single best pork shoulder sandwich I have ever eaten. Ever. The okra is phenomenal. And, the fried pies!  My husband got the sweet potato and I got the apple. Holy. Crap. So good. Definitely recommend this place.

This place is awesome! Ohhhh weeeee!!!
Driving through Nashville, it's a must!
The people and food are great :)
Good cookin' the good old fashioned way. They have large portions and they offer student discounts which is great if your a student and on a budget.
VTx3qBeLMNFmBpcUFVNlQg:
Cross your fingers and pray you get Jeff as your server. He's kind of the coolest and gave us some tips on where we should go in Nashville. Along with fantastic service, obviously. 

We pancaked and ate to our hearts' content. Cool concept for a restaurant. Just... go. You'll be happy afterwards. Seriously. Stop looking for places to eat on Yelp and go.

I've been here several times. Most of the time I do the pancakes and potato pancakes but I have also ordered from the menu! They have some awesome sammies and soups!

I love the the organic pancake batter!!! So good with nutella, strawberries and bananas!

It's a fun experience with friends to make your pancakes and not have to clean up after yourself!  

Go on a weekday morning if possible. The weekends are CRAZY with families and young children.

The Pfunky Griddle was such a delight! My husband And I stopped in to see an old friend and enjoy some pancakes. I had mentioned to my Hubbs I was curious about this "cook your own"concept and had been eager to try it for a few years. As soon as we got there we were seated within 10 minutes and were escorted to the back dining room where there are all around windows. It was a rainy day so we were able to enjoy that while eating our breakfast. This place was an old home converted, so this back porch-converted into dining space-was charming with hand painted walls that tell the story of this sweet restaurant. Our waiter Bruce was excellent. He was energetic, friendly, and knowledgeable. He managed multiple tables and fully explained how the concept works. We enjoyed fresh blueberry pancakes, fresh coffee, and were able to customize and cook our eggs to preference. It was so much fun! Thank you for such an enjoyable experience Matt & Bruce.
h3MIdoJFAbmkQaUdqoCTtg:
So I officially started my vacation today (yay!), but had to spend the first afternoon of it at the dentist (boo). I figured to treat myself as compensation I would go have a long lunch a couple beers on a patio. I found myself at Joe's Place for the first time, and am very glad I did.

The service was excellent, even with just the bartender running the whole place. My $5 lunch special pizza was far from gourmet, but it was large enough for two and there were lots of topping options. The side salad was enormous, and more than just a pile of iceberg.  

The patio was completely shaded, and off the street - ideal for a too sunny afternoon. The part that put it over the top was the customer service and welcoming atmosphere. Besides the bartender/server I mentioned, the guy at the smoker (Bill?) when he found out I was spending the first hour at my vacation at Joe's, bought me my next beer. How surprisingly nice!

All in all, a great experience, and I will definitely be back. I recommend you bring a friend, split lunch and a side or app, and both of you can get out of the place with a filling meal and even with a beer and tip for under $10 a piece!

Oh, I almost forgot, the music selection was a major plus: old blues and and country. Loved it!

Smallish place, decent beer specials, haven't tried the food.  You can smoke here, and there is a patio out back.  Bartenders are very friendly and often adorable. Wifi is fantastic.  Free, and I got 18MB down, and 5MB up. ++

Sprint's 4G service is great here too (with MiFi unit on window ledge). I measured the highest rates I have anywhere in Nashville so far, 11MB down, and 1MB up. ++

Great place to grab a beer and finish up some work.

Joe's place is OUR place! It rocks. WE love it and have made it our place to go with our group of friends. They have awesome Karaoke! We used to come on Saturday nights and sing all night long! It's spacious...they always have sports on the tv's, and it's a great atmosphere to just hang out, sing, and drink! Not pretentious at all! Like some places in Green Hills can be... We LOVE Joe's Place!!
cP81x13w3Mg5PhoaSXdV8A: We keep going back after 4+ years -- consistently good and classic Chinese food. Friendly owners and easy to order. Always fast and reliable. Our order has never been wrong.

Let's be honest- it is really hard to find good and somewhat authentic Chinese food in Nashville... but hands down, Hong Kong might be the best we've found so far. 

The food is your standard Chinese take--out. I also appreciate that some of the chicken dishes are already made with white meat.  

The dinner specials are pretty good. It's a ton of food for under $10 (which includes an entree and bowl of soup). 

I've never had a  bad experience. We usually just carry-out so I can't speak to the delivery service, but I know the people are always pleasant when we call! Give them a try!

Solid Chinese Delivery. It's nothing fancy but they are always nice, the food is good and they deliver pretty quickly. There are only one or two tables there and when I've picked up orders, I've never seen anyone eating in the "dining room."
wa9tG3QDiR5Qx8P_5oIwdQ:
One of the best meals we have ever had. The service was sensational- Milana was a dream. 

We had the steak kabobs, crab cakes, and salmon Caesar (the salmon dip appetizer is AMAZING) 

It's the perfect location for a date night as you can dress it up or down and either will work. The entire staff was on point even tho it was a busy Friday night. We will be back!

I liked this J Alexanders way better than the other ones I have gone to. At least here the bartender will respond when you wave the down, or shoot a flair gun in the air. The other ones I have been to they are too busy socializing with each other to take care of their customers.
J Alexander's is a classy/slightly more upscale version of Applebee's. The food is good.. but definitely has the taste and ambience of a chain-restaurant.
8qhwpErz2F-DkPGv5yIoWg: Average fast food service with a blank stare, good food with fast service. Served its purpose B+

OMG, this place is amazing. They literally give you burgers, chicken and hot dogs that taste like they just came off a charcoal grill. It's soooo good. Add that to their price point and you have a home Run here. 

I will be going back to this new hot spot. We loved it.

What I like about this place?  Fast service, value for the money and the HUGE tea.  Yeah it's fast food but the price/quality/quantity ratio are right on.  Get there early, the place is busy with working folk like myself getting a meal for their hard earned buck.
QXWDkZW29q7hUB0maO5EbQ:
So. Good. 

My friends and I have been here three times now, and it's been great every time. There's always been a wait, and sometimes it's really long, but I think it's worth it. Especially since they have such a cool little lounge area in the back. 

The margaritas are delicious, and we've never eaten anything that was less than excellent. It's a little pricier than your regular ho-hum Mexican restaurant, but it's waaaaaaaaay better in my humble opinion.
Great food. Queso is yummy and the service was outstanding! Our waitress was so attentive!

Thoroughly enjoyed this cantina for a late lunch. They make their own chorizo so we opted for the queso fundido as an appetizer. It was very good.  The Naco Taco rocked. The pork melted in your mouth. All of the ingredients were fresh. Very reasonably priced. Server was helpful and friendly. Would definitely go back again when I am in Nashville.
_yZDoWwqkb4BusqhRluNSg:
I ordered through the YELP app and it was flawless.
I found the pizza really good and the service excellent.

Prices were extremely reasonable.

I got the steak and cheese hoagie, and hands down the best one I've had around. The delivery man was also very nice and pleasant and thanked me for my business. I was at work at the airport and wasn't sure if he could find parking so I told him I could meet him out there, but he came to my office instead. I will continue to give them my business

First time trying pizza here. We ordered the "big daddy" with extra cheese and pepperoni, an extra large sausage and tomato and 12 garlic parm wings. Fair pricing. I laughed when I saw the big daddy because it really is BIG.   The pizzas were both really great. Nice flavor. Perfectly cooked. Lots of cheese! Yum! And the wings were wonderful.  Right amount of seasoning. Very good. We would go back here for sure. I'm interested in trying their cheesecake. They have combo deals that are really reasonable. Girl working front is very nice.
LVHlhmgZPv8MlmRLfV_JfA:
Excellent donuts. Fresh and perfect frosting. Not way over sweet just perfect!
The Cinnamon Bund are deLish! I could eat those every day. Good thing I live in another state. Lol
The sprinkle Donuts are my fav like the Cinnamon Buns. Light flakey goodness. 
Great anytime of the day! 
Very friendly staff. 
Highly recommended.
You may bust a button so bring extra buttons, thread and a needle. 
YuMMy!
The donuts are always fresh and delicious, and the staff is always so friendly. Service is always quick, but get here early if you want certain donuts, because they run out pretty quickly!

Great service and selection. The custard donuts aren't too sweet and they offer stuffed croissants, too. They have pretty much everything from cake donuts and donut holes to items like cinnamon rolls and pigs in a blanket.
IJEivHHf5A8LzXXOn0o0Nw:
We enjoyed our dinner very much.
Service was spot on and drinks were very good. My wife had the prime rib and I ordered the scallops. Both were excellent.
Nice 3 piece band as we sat near the stage.
Not too loud for us which was great during dinner. This place fills up quickly so make a reservation
Delicious food and cocktails, friendly and attentive staff, great music! Love this place!

We were referred to go here from a friend in California!!  We had family from Boston, Connecticut & us, New to TN from CA. This is a place NO ONE SHOULD MISS OUT ON when in Nashville.  The Food is beyond amazing, let your taste buds go wild! The service was tops!!!  They have their famous Lobster Bisque that you would be crazy not to try!!  Every Drink, Every meal for all 5 of us was more than we could ask for.....Oh & my Escargot put me in heaven...LOL.   The Owners have an awesome place & we can not wait to return!!
sXotkH2c1vUi6LdPJan3Pg: While we enjoyed our night at Tayst, I wanted to give it a 3 star but the bartender Adrian was so great he deserves a start all by himself.  There was nothing "bad" about Tayst, I'd say it was good but not mind blowing great. Hubby & I had a Living Social coupon and made a night of it by spending a few hours at the Tayst bar (definitely the place to sit if you go).  Instead of ordering just 2 entrees we decided to order several of the small plates.  Some were really good some were just ok but all were definitely small.  In my opinion the cheese plate is small for the price you're paying so skip it and maybe order the seared burrata which was fantastic.  The belly & belly that Adrian suggested was amazing.  Definitely order that if you can.  The three meat ravioli was also really good.  Overall I would still say if you're thinking about going, do it but maybe have a snack before you go.

The food is fantastic. The menu is a little out there with things you don't normally see, but just trust the chefs there, because they know what they're doing!  Our service was great as well. The owner even came up and chatted with us for a bit, very personable. I highly recommend trying Tayst or even revisiting if you have been before. Oh and I'm not sure if they still serve it or not, but if they do, you have got to try the KRISPY KREME BREAD PUDDING!  TO DIE FOR!

This is actually a great restaurant.  I was driving around Nashville looking for a restaurant near Vanderbilt.  This looked like a decent place.  The food was actually incredible.  I think I got something like a pumpkin pasta.  The dessert was great too.

The crowd was a lot more casual than I thought it would be.

I'm just glad that I don't live in Nashville (boring).
2-5VDzx9G7KlF8-nUngphg: The taco salad is great! Nothing fancy, but good food is what counts!
Still the best Mexican restaurant in Nashville!   Good food, good BIG beers, and great prices.  Patio is a great place to hang out with amigos.
They are the best. Antonio remembers us after 6 months. They truly do care about their own. The best place in Nashville. Absolutely love this place!
OwAX9v-cn1kXptUmt2oIaw: Local, fresh, unique pizza. How can you go wrong this? Their wood fire oven is made to get your personal pizza out in minutes and their daily specials are out of this world. The owners are the nicest and enthusiastic about what they do. This review may be short, but that's because I don't need to build them up. One visit and you'll love them, too.

Really good pizza and I'm a sucker for friendly people behind the counter.  Chatted for a few minutes with the couple who opened the place up and they couldn't be any nicer and grateful for their opportunity.  They care about the food and it shows.  I had the hummus pizza and my lady friend had the margherita.  Both were very good.  The farmers market is one of my favorite places to grab a Saturday or Sunday lunch and Bella just made it a harder choice of where to eat.

I love this place. A LOT. Every time my honey bear plays a game in Bicentennial Mall, I head over to Bella Nashville. Side note, he's a vintage base ball player on the Nashville Maroons, and if you haven't already, you need to check out a game.

I always get an egg on my pizza. I tried it once, and can't go back. One because now it's a breakfast pizza, and two because it's so delicious to dip your slice into the yolk. The eggs are FRESH! Do it, try it, you won't regret it. 

Hands down, Bella Nashville is my favorite vendor in the Farmers' Market, and might be my favorite pizza in Nashville. It's definitely in my top two.
JR0G8hPaEUTUUxmDX8v3gA:
I have actually not had the cheese here, I have only had the paninis.  The paninis I have had have been uniformly excellent.  The 10k panini is everything good about Italian food pressed between two pieces of bread.  

Hopefully I will be going back soon to work my way through the cheese selection so I can upgrade this to a 5 star review.

I usually just run in here for the cheese selection. I had the Paninis once and they were pretty good..A friend who knows me well gave me 3 cheeses from here for my birthday. Make sure and get some award winning Bonnie Blue Farm goat cheese. It's made here in Middle TN by yet another California transplant..keep on moving in guys!!

Great spot for lunch, get half of one of their delicious paninis (which are, to any reasonable person, the size of a large whole sandwich from anyplace else).  If you're in a huge rush, be forewarned it takes about 10 minutes to press the sandwiches.

I recently picked up a housemade frozen meat lasagne and popped it in the oven a few nights later.  it was AMAZING.  I think it cost $8 and was enough for 3-4 people.  Delicious.  Service isn't the most congenial, but the prices are so good, who cares?
5WXHGOYSi979NOi1oj2atA:
Loved the mixologists (Liquor Chefs)
The food was outstanding and we will be back.  A little bit of fancy for those times you just need fancy...

The food here is absolutely excellent. RJ Cooper is the chef and he has designed a wonderful menu with a seemingly endless amount of delicious food. We tried a bit of everything it seemed...

The reason I'm writing the review is the short ribs. They are incredible. The definition of 'melt in your mouth' food. Do not miss out.

I'm taking one star away for service. They are nice, attentive, and pleasant, but there seems to be a lot of confusion. Our table was served by five or six different people and they brought us menu items we didn't ask for three times in one visit. I'm sure they'll figure this out as the restaurant is very new, so I fully expect to upgrade this review to five stars when I come back.

But if you can put that aside in the name of good food, come here immediately.
Wow. This place is amazing. Everything is elaborately done and done well. Great small plates. Drinks are wonderful. Try cheese and charcuterie. Amazing desserts.
Aj166F_lBuDkgqWP0asMvA: We loved this waffle house - very clean and from the moment you step in and are greeted by hostess "momma" you know this is going to be good.  We have been to a few other WH around Nashville where you get the feeling the folks working there are all having a bad day but not here - everyone was smiling, loud in saying hello and looked like they were having fun.  I am not sure if it translates into the cooking and food but we really enjoyed our meals and this will be our go to Waffle House in Nashville!!

I haven't eaten at a Waffle House in years but my friends and I were done with the bar and in need of a greasy recharge before sleep and thus hopped a ride to the Waffle House closest to us (actually by way of the Jack in the Box across the street which, while open, refused to take our order because they were busy--just an odd side-note). The service for a late Sunday night was incredibly fast and friendly and the food hit the spot. We loaded up on all of the breakfast standards while I added a patty melt to the mix and everything was cooked to perfection and tasty (and super cheap!).
Looks like its ready to reopen. Its not permanently closed but remodeled. Looks nice.
wueXNaxuNKjr8sq-AyWCog:
I was recommended to this place by a friend...so took my mom and my daughter on a Friday night.  I had read the reviews on here before going, and was happy to hear this wasn't another typical Mexican taco bell type of place.  I'm not a basic chicken, rice, and beans sort of person, so this joint caught my interest by the fresh ingredients and innovative creations.

Loved it.  Best salsa ever.  The 90210 quesadilla is incredible!!  To top off the yummy food, they have a kick butt children's menu with extremely reasonable prices.  They even had "real" crayons and coloring paper which I wasn't expecting...so yeah...very kid and family friendly.  
The owners were very accomodating and very nice to talk to...had an authentic interest in their customers and their opinions.  The decor was semi-mod (I'm not an art major) with a personal touch everywhere you looked.  I love that they rocked the 80's music videos on the TV's.  They mentioned also having a Glee night...

So overall, the prices were reasonable for the amount of food you get, the ingredients are very fresh with a touch of different.  The choices weren't absurdly out there, very creative and hard to pick from!!  I'll definitely be coming back for that mean salsa...drool...

Love this place.  My boyfriend has NEVER had anything but the Tricky Ricardo, not because nothing else looks good, but because he tried it the first time we went and it is so freaking good that he's scared to be let down if he tries anything else.  He craves it once a week.  I usually get the plymouth, since it's delicious but more healthy.  Definitely understand the comments about it not being super seasoned (the meats etc.) but you can kick up the heat/add flavor to your liking with any of there hot sauces or there great salsa or gwaco.  It's NOT a normal burrito joint.  But it's really really good!

These guys are super stars and the service is outstanding.  The burritos are huge and I recommend splitting one or grab a take-out box!  Flavors can be unconventional and over-the-top... the 90210 had scrumptious pieces of just-cooked chicken, artichoke, goat cheese, peppers, pesto, delicious.  My partner had a more traditional pork rice and bean selection which was excellent with a little hot sauce.  The guac is good, too.  Great energy all around.  I'll be back!
W-TAJwlfKb2DQ68m-fwqqw: Love this place.  My wife and I come here all  the time as she loves sushi and it has plenty to offer a non sushi lover like myself.  I typically get the steak, which is cooked well and always delicious.  My wife never complains about the sushi (she is a bit of a sushi snob as well).  Service is always stellar!

Good, generous portions of Hibachi food, but then who pays the higher prices for a Hibachi meal just for the food? The culinary show happening right before you and the freshness of the food being prepared in front of you...that's what increases the prices for a Hibachi meal, right? I know getting the food right is the most important part of any restaurant and I think Tokyo does fine in this regard. They have a very tasty salad, good portion sizes of meat or fish, and a very yummy fried rice. But, their chefs really need to engage more with the customers while preparing their food. Our chef did a 5 second volcano thing, but that was about all the tricks he had up his sleeve. Also, service was a tad too slow in my view, but the staff's friendliness almost made up for it. I'm a big fan of udon noodles and katsu preparations, plan to try those next time. All in all, a very decent Japanese place.
Real and very fun entertainment on the hibachi side..So if you guys wanna have fun and have good food go to Tokyo Green hills so you can experience it..
IhrTLL6az3q192ik4P05IQ: Easily one of my best dining experiences in Nashville so far: the food was fantastic; the cocktails were original (and delicious); and the service was excellent.  Things to know: there are no individual tables (bars and community tables); there is a bar around the kitchen if you like that sort of thing; they have complimentary valet service (don't forget to tip).  Great place for a group of friends!

LOVED my dinner and experience!  Had the 4 course experience from LivingSocial and I will DEFINITELY be back- and soon!  Not only did we have specialty meals prepared for us without having to decide what I wanted, it was EXACTLY what my taste buds were craving!  And I loved getting to watch and interact with the chef as it was being prepared for me.  My fiance and I loved everything about the Chef and I and we will definitely dine there again soon.  The food was unique, gorgeous presentation and fun!

My husband and i got married in November, and our wedding was catered by The Chef and I. They are an amazing team that got to know us very well during the planning process. They took the time to listen to what we wanted and asked the right questions. We were concerned at first because we were on a tight budget, but they were able to work well with us.

We, along with the guests at our wedding, were so blown away with the food that they made for our reception. It was a menu of fish, steak, and chicken, along with some awesome sides to go well with it. I can describe it in one word...AWESOME! A lot of our guests came to us, raving about how delicious the food was and how we made a great choice in choosing The Chef and I as our caterers. We are foodies, after all! Even my dad was convinced to try the fish...as he doesn't like a lot of foods, I didn't think he would like it, but he enjoyed it!

Chef Chris and Erica are fantastic people, and everybody should go to their restaurant to try their food! It's so worth it!
NeFqfOSvIdye13QGhDQ_Hw: We eat here a few times a year when we're in the area.   The food is always fresh and plentiful.   We think they have the best gyros and falafel around and the tabouleh is super fresh and flavorful.   The fresh lemonade and fruit teas are great too.

Great place.  Ordered the Gyro plate and was pleasantly surprised at the large portions given.  Staff was friendly and the restaurant (a converted Burger King) was clean.  It looks like they may offer a buffet style meal, possibly for dinner but not for lunch.  I will definitely go back and try the chicken next.

The food is very good I came based on another person's advise I received 4 big pieces of chicken Butter rice a salad that included carrots it was fresh and good they do charge .55 per glass of fruit tea ..they must sell alot of it
MaTHSI-lt2xS8CHs8up7NQ: If you are looking for an authentic Japanese food then you have come to the right place. You do have to watch out for parking and I think it's rude that people do not take in consideration other drivers but that is what I am finding out in Nashville. I took my cousins who had a sushi restaurant in NJ and everything came out great for them. The ginger pork, the sushi came out with so much food. You can easily get a great meal and drink for 20.00. I am a regular here for being half Japanese we need our fill of Japanese food

This is a great place to get sushi. The parking lot and building are not much to look at. The inside is a bit old but the food is great. Ken is very nice and the staff is great. They have a lot of food I have never tried or seen at other places. We will be back for more and I recommend you give this place a try.

I love Ken's. We go all of the time, the service is always awesome. Great environment and everyone that works there is so nice. Red dragon roll is delicious. Haven't had a roll there I didn't like. Also lunch deals are so cheap.
FtY4uxKAXcZDDFUhfOp-lA: This place is decent but not a place I would frequent often. It used to be called something else a few years ago and under different management I believe and the food was much better. I ordered the falafel plate, lentil soup and baklava. The falafel was fried a little too hard but the lentil soup is puréed but has a good flavor. The baklava was tasty as well. There is adequate seating and somewhat limited parking due to it being near Belmont college.

This replaced Shawarma near Belmont. So glad it did. Food is amazing 

For the price point, quality and quantity you get this is a hidden gem. Owners are super nice and they make the food right after you order it. Haven't been to a restaurant in Nashville that tasted this fresh. 

Definitely worth checking out for any kind of dinner, decadent for a college budget and solid Mediterranean food for everyone else.

Ordered shawarma and my husband ordered falafel. Everything was cooked fresh amd tasted delicious. Prices are very reasonable.The place has a cute aesthetic vibe.  It also has dessert and coffee. Definitely a go to place for quick Mediterranean food in nashville.
LKEbnQHMp-Z6fYg5Wr_yxw:
Dear Calypso, 
I love you and I miss you.  I'm sorry I moved so far away from you, but you know I visit you every time I can.  

There are no other restaurants like you, so fast and cheap and tasty and healthy.  I miss your rotisserie roasted kosher chicken,  fresh leaf-lettuce salads and out of this world home-made dressings, martinique callaloo, mouth-watering sweet potatoes topped with coconut and almond flakes, black bean salads, black bean nachos, chocolate mousse, fruit tea, cinnamon hinted coffee, fresh "barbecue" sauce, curry sauce....I could go on forever, my love.  

Many a nights I have dreamed of sinking my teeth into your orgasmic food.  I ate your food at least three times a week for two years.  Tell Brandi and Robert I said hi, and I miss them.  

-Sandy

My go to place in Nashville for a fresh, fun and filling salad. I'm not sure how healthy all the salads are with beans, nachos, and cheese thrown in, but they'll definitely help you hit your daily greens goal and they are soooo good. Plus, the place is incredibly cute and the homey and the prices are right, too.

There is no better place in Nashville for cheap, delicious food.  I once got through giving up meat for Lent specifically by coming here.  With a very simple, yet informative, menu with some fantastic options for the picker eater in all of us, you will find what you want here without feeling like crap when you leave afterwards.

The chicken is rotisserie-style, fall-off-the-bone-melt-in-your-mouth good.  And you can get it on just about everything.  Sandwiches, salads, on its own with a side sauce (of note, every single sauce or dressing is amazing)...all delicious.  I am a fan of the lucayan salad, which is chicken with almonds, mandarin oranges, and a house made dressing (that I buy to put on anything at home).  I add onions to this salad personally.  I love their black beans with onions and sour cream as well.  They have fruit in the summer months and amazing soups on the colder months.  They are right next to Centennial park, so I recommend getting lunch here and taking it outside when its nice.

The service is fast and friendly, and the servers themselves are always helpful.  

Also, get their fruit tea and coffee (it has cinammon in it, and its so good).  

This is a great place for a quick bite or if you want to relax.  Its how all fast food should be: healthy, easy, fast, and fun...without breaking the bank or your belt.
LmRyuir7orBNN43fyp8buQ: Pizza is really good. Quality ingredients. A medium is the same size as a dominos large pizza. Haven't had anything other than their pizza but it is definitely worth it. If you are a ranch with your pizza guy/gal I highly recommend asking for 2 sides of ranch. It is so darn good. Staff seems to be mostly high schoolers on break and hipsters but it's a tight place with good pizza. 5 stars

Soulshine was found by accident when looking for food Saturday night but was so so good!  I had the gluten free Godfather and it was delicious!  Add a side a ranch and it's so good  plus there was live music which is so Nashville and I loved it!

We had a large bachelor party stop by for some beers and pizza. The service was the best I've had in a long time. To be able to keep 20 drunk males going with only one server is always impressive. She went above and beyond to help us. 

Kitchen sink pizza was good! Great wings too!
CoiSfZwEN0KZwZas6846tQ: Had the chance to stop by on a Monday when they are normally closed. Friendly staff opened up for us and soon had us sampling their craft. Good variety of choc. And Brittles inc. Bourbon barrel yum! A must try for real chocolate lovers!

I haven't been to the actual factory (yet) but let me tell you, this chocolate = YUM! The Mexican chocolate is my favorite with a hint of cinnamon and chili spices (I wish it had a little more spice, but I'm crazy like that). The duck fat caramel are DANGEROUS...I would eat 100 of them if I could! The white chocolate with salt and pepper was just ok. The brittle was good too.

Excellent, top-notch, stellar, superb, best chocolate and candies ever!

The tour was very educational and while it's not as tasty as the finished product, I enjoyed tasting chocolate at every step. The antique melanges that stone grind the chocolate are stunning and I could stare at them for hours. I'm down right impressed at the volume and quality of confections that come out of this small operation. 

My favorites are the white chocolate, duck fat caramels, and we had the choc-cuterie that was smores themed!

Toured about 2 weeks ago and got the wrong size t-shirt as a gift to a friend-- hope they'll let me exchange it by the time I get around to coming back.
BnibjYoTYefJXQ_ZVXo_mQ: This steak house is amazing.  We shared a bone in ribeye and a add on small lobster tail!  The stark melted in your mouth. It was cooked to perfection!  The lobster was one of the best I have had. Sweet and delicious.  The potatoes that are pan fried with onions and gravy are quite amazing as well.  I am a baked potato person but these were better!  Ok enough about the food.....lets talk about the service.  We had Marcos as our waiter and he was point on!  Very helpful with us picking the right selection. He knew the menu well and guided us to perfection.  You won't be disappointed in eating here!  One of the best steakhouses I have ever eaten at.

This is our second time here and they do not disappoint. We had shrimp cocktail, crab cake, lobster tail, filet, and sides of garlic mashed and carrot. The carrot was one of the best things even though everything else was excellent! Creme brûlée was the best I've ever had!!! Service is very personable and top notch. We had Laura this visit and Carlos last time and both were so good at taking great care of us and creating a memorable experience! We travel quite a bit and enjoy great restaurants all over and this is one of our top favorites. Much better than the palm restaurant if you are comparing.

Excellent presentation and taste. If your looking for an upscale mellow but classy dinner night out, this is the place for you! Just perfectly designed and the steak is probably one of the best in Nashville.
9N-VuWIyXdpLtbKmbCNVwg: Vui's kitchen is delicious! I licked my bowl clean (I got the noodle bowl with a fried egg). It's super casual- you order at the counter, get a number then grab a table. Our food came out super fast and everyone was super happy with their meal. They have a few good vegetarian/vegan options too:)

We used to go to the Vui's Kitchen in Berry Hill quite a bit and only recently tried the new Germantown location a few days ago. The menu is exactly the same, but this location has much more seating and space with an added outdoor patio area! There is a small paid lot in front that gives you an hour free and is shared with Juice Bar and Little Donkey. If the lot is full, there is plenty of street parking nearby as well. 

As a pescatarian, I fully appreciate that they have vegan options as anything on the menu can be substituted for tofu. I've tried the vegan pho many times, and thought it was pretty average compared to other vegetarian options available in the Nashville area. The usual side plate of veggies, like basil and bean sprouts, are already tossed in when your pho is served, but they still have the very necessary sriracha and hoison sauce! 
I personally like to get the banh mi and bun bao with tofu instead of meat. All the ingredients were incredibly fresh and the tofu was well seasoned and flavorful! 

The staff is always friendly and the service quick- I've gotten my food in less than 10 minutes each time I do take out. I love that there is a fast and casual option in the area, and I'll continue to be back! Especially for the bun bao in all its squishy bun glory

Great place!!  Great food!  Seating outside on a nice afternoon!  Recommended!  

The Vui salad with the beef and a fried egg on top is to die for!!
yGsmSJR1GYQsFNVbCdLolg:
My bestie and I stumbled upon this little gem while being obnoxious tourists in Nashville. We wandered in from across the street when we realized that the "Johnny Cash Museum" is really just a store (so far). So we decided it was time to start drinkin, and they had a sign out front advertising their Happy Hour.

Maybe I loved this place because of the myriad house-infused vodkas and the corresponding amazing cocktails. Maybe I loved it because it was the first time in my life that I've been told by a bartender "I was warned about you two." Or maybe I loved it because we ran into a friend of the bartender's and her amigas, and they then let us tag along bar-hopping for a very fun, trashed evening. The events of the rest of the evening are not to be discussed in polite company...

In any event, I wish there was a little place like this near me. And FYI, the habanero vodka is hot. Like really hot. And my friend claims her pee smelled like habaneros the next day. So I dare you.

We stumbled upon this restaurant after finding our that the italian restaurant we wanted to go to was closed on mondays. We were originally attracted to the atmosphere and the variety in the menu.
Our server was very attentive and the food was just what we were looking for! Both the interesting cocktails and dinner was very delicious!
Great escape from Broadway, great bison burgers, creative cocktails and awesome bloody Mary. This place have variety if infused vodkas from watermelon to olive.
DMYWMY96WF1ueecIpOA4ZA:
We stopped in for lunch Saturday 3/7/15.  We like to sit in the bar if possible as we are usually wanting to watch sports.  We sat at the bar and the bartender was a nice gal.  

I ordered the simply grilled chicken sandwich and opted for bottomless salad w/ salsa and their rather tasty bbq sauce for my sandwich.  Yum!  The diet pop was flat... :(

I like Red Robin and wish there was one closer to where I live b/c there are so many other options when dining in Nashville.  My boyfriend had some sort of deal so that's why we stopped- we probably won't revisit until I get my free bday burger next mth.

We had a great experience here! Our waiter was so sweet and helpful, the beer was good, it's affordable, and in case I hadn't mentioned.... They serve liquor!!!! I had the Jack Daniel's burger with onion rings and my boyfriend had the bacon cheeseburger. I'm no burger aficionado, but I thought both were delicious! They have a good selection in every area, not just burgers. Next time I'd like to try their desserts!

I was promptly seated,  and the waitress took my drink order quickly.  I ordered the bunless burger with a side salad.   The burger was cooked exactly as I had specified and was yummy!  The food was on my table quickly and the waitress checked back often to insure my needs were met.  Excellent service.
U9mkR8xBAAhwwtTVjWY2_g: I love this Wendy's, the staff is always friendly and the store is always nice and clean. There food is always spot on and even in the drive thru they are fast and never get my order wrong. The restrooms are always clean and well stocked. I go out of my way a bit to eat here...it's in a run down part of town but worth it!

Love their Chili on a cold day and always get a side order...a baked potato with sour cream and chives..they go perfect together..cheap, good..and more than enough to get a full belly..Love love love me some Wendy's chili...

Every time I visit this location.. I get friendly fast service, and good hot fresh food. I drive out of my way just to go to this location because I know they will always make the trip worth while. The reps are always pleasant and welcoming. I have been going to this location for years and everyone has been a good experience. Thank you so much!!
NhdOYwSgspPUVcYakaN3XQ: Great fish sandwiches but a little over priced for the small pieces you receive. Deliciously flavored and cooked to perfection. The staff is always very friendly.

Best BBQ I have had in a really long time. I have lived in the area for about a year, past it all the time and alway meant to stop. The building is not much to look at, but my experience with barbecue is that the best barbecue comes from the most unexpected places. Staff was friendly and nice, ton of food for the price. I've tried the barbecue place down the street, Bones blows it out of the water. So good. Definitely will be having a whole lot more of Bones.

Me and my roommate decided to finally try his place today. We will be back. I ordered 12 Honey Hot wings, and she ordered the BBQ chicken sandwich. Both were delicious, hot, and seasoned right. The lady at the window "Sharice" sorry if I misspelled, was very nice. We will be back for sure. A+
DE1_9uUINrPNh_mhlZFfwg:
Love this adorable bakery. They use only high quality ingredients, and their staff are always really kind, welcoming and knowledgeable. 
My favorite is the cookie sandwiches with icing. My kids love the chocolate cupcakes and double chocolate donuts. We highly recommend this place!
I normally don't eat gluten free and vegan, but I have to admit that the chocolate muffins here were absolutely delicious. So are the triple chocolate brownies! I'll be back!

OMG the almond muffins and the lemon blueberry are by far the best I have ever had. 
It is worth a trip from Atlanta!
AyuNz2WyV4LOTORTdMGuFQ: I love going to this place! The atmosphere is always great and so is the food! I absolutely love their cheese curds and fried pickles! Man they are SO good, I could eat them everyday! Sometimes the service is slow but they always take care of you and will make it right if something doesn't come out the way you expected!
Good mimosas! Great brunch. Both the chicken biscuit and the chorizo burrito were excellent. The hashbrowns that came with it were good as well. Lots of TVs to catch a game. Cool layout as well.

Great beers on draft. And family friendly. Buffalo chicken wrap was great. Catfish fillets were huge. Great service. Manager checked in multiple times. Our server was first night and did great. Will go back again.
vw5C1FO1aD4nc5Jvb1n-Qg: Lunch special $13 for pizza, salad and a beer....delicious! Fun environment, dog friendly patio and awesome beer. If you come at lunch the paid parking lot around the corner is free from 11am-1pm. Will definitely be back soon!
Best pizza i have ever had! Great beer great service 100% amazing! Got the lasagna pizza i cannot recomend this place enough!

I'm trying to figure out what the catch is here. 50 beers on tap, seasonals, saisons, sours, beer for all tastes. $9 normal 64oz growler fills, $13 high gravity (which most breweries can't even fill). I originally went over there just to get a fill of the Sweettart Sour (which I had at a coffee shop), tasted the Coconut IPA, and just had to have a pint of it. Haven't tried the food yet but I will definitely be back to try some more of their beers. Definitely worth the trip if you're a beer lover or just visiting the city for the weekend
VFaIfVHLAJduhiBeZ3TlUw: Excellent! Had the Pro Bowl, which was very very good. Inviting, casual atmosphere with pleasant staff and music. Wish I lived here so I could become a regular!

I just randomly wondered into this store and found my juicing heaven. Yes, there are lots of juice stores, but what makes this difference is the sheer variety of mixes that have (some super creative) that you won't find your run of the mill jamba juice. Oh and yes, my prepper was super fun and helpful in helping me decide the perfect drink (I went with Purple Rain). Oh and yes, they were juicing with plants that were freshly potted - doesn't get better than that!

Be warned though, you can only access this place by foot. Good luck parking.

Really delicious smoothies and friendly service.  I tried the ginger smoothie, DH had a chia smoothie, and my kid had a "Sure Thing" smoothie. We all enjoyed them. Not cheap for the amount of smoothie you get, but very tasty.
7tE9fLBPwXvoxkykGJPbEw:
I tried this place with my girlfriend (see Kala's review) while out running errands. The atmosphere and feel reminds me a lot of Panera Bread and Sweet Tomato's. However, what surprised me the most were the prices. Given that this place is located in Green Hills, on the same block as the mall, you would expect it to be moderately to expensively (not sure if that is a word) priced; yet, instead, despite its location the prices are a bargain for what you get. I mean honestly, $7.85 for a regular size bowl of pasta and a soup or salad... who cares if it does not include a drink, I'll drink water, as that is an awesome deal!

Moreover, in an age where american society expects large portions, for once there is a restaurant that realizes that quality, not quantity, makes a better meal. I had the mac n cheese with meatballs and a side Caesar salad, and that was just enough for me, and it was all delicious. I also tried some of my girlfriend's butter noodles with seared chicken, and that too was just awesome! Now if american style fair is not you're thing, then they also have both Asian style dishes such as pad thai and udon, as well as Mediterranean dishes like spaghetti. Not only that; but, along with chicken and meatballs, one can also have shrimp, beef or tofu as a "meat" option on your pasta dish. Vegetarian or carnivore, this is a great place for those wanting a quick bite or on a small budget.

Funny thing is though, growing up my dad's mom always told me that if I keep eating noodles all the time, I would turn into a noodle. While not true, when craving noodles there is no place better than Noodles and Company.

Great customer service. My family went on a trip to Nashville, TN, and while we were there, we ordered online from a Noodles & Co. there; however, when we went to pick it up, they only have us 4 out of the 6 bowls we'd ordered, which we only found out after getting to back to where we were staying. So, we called them a day or two later when we were back home from Nashville, but they said that since we hadn't called the day we ordered, they couldn't refund us. So, the manager from the Noodles in Nashville called the manager for the Noodles where I live, and the manager gave us 3 free bowls, drinks, and a cookie. Big mess-up in Nashville, but great resolution of the problem, and both managers were really cool about the whole thing!

When you are craving a hearty pasta dish and you want to be in and out of somewhere quick, Noodles and Co. in Greenhills is where you should consider. After enjoying a wonderful manicure next door at Venetian Nail Spa we stopped in for a quick bite before heading over to the mall. I've been on a diet and reallllllly enjoyed that they had the calories listed on the menu, tofu option for vegetarians, and the option for 'buff bowl' (noodles are swapped for spinach and they double up on the veggies). It was not hard for me to decide quickly and know confidently I was getting something healthy.

The staff: very friendly but a little too quick about things, seemed rushed when we got our food and clean up. You could also tell when they are busy bc the floors were a mess, but hey that means good business. Maybe work on the floor cleaning ;)

 If you haven't been to Noodle and Co...go!
GGFmwJLQrwbI9Ytr7IrLvg:
Love the camp fire mix grill ( beef) when they bring it to the table it's still in the tin foil and it tastes as if they cooked it on a camp fire.

Wife would love to have liver and onions but

Enjoyed the atmosphere here! There aren't many vegetarian options at all, and even the ones that look vegetarian are often cooked in a chicken broth. I was concerned, though, with the healthiness of the meal--I don't know if my heart and arteries will forgive me for eating there...

Overall it was a good place to eat and I enjoyed my time. Good prices, too.

Mmmmm...Nothing outta the ordinary for this area but I am from CA and for me, this place reminds me of the last time I was in the south...delicious and heart deadly....The other cracker barrels in the southwest are no good but the ones in TN meet my approval
miPBTqY_QGr4ouNIjia8fQ: So, so, so good. The best Indian food I've ever eaten. I never would have expected this from a non discript little place. The food is exquisite and the delivery service is quick and the food is hot. Delicious in ever respect. Don't miss it!

Cool little Indian "hole in the wall" joint right near everything in Green Hills. It's behind the building, so a bit hard to find. Wife and I stopped in this weekend after seeing some reviews on Yelp.

I see that some of the bad reviews seem to have a problem with the fact that this place is next to a pizza/pasta place (and they clearly share a kitchen, some staff, etc. - with a curtain in the door separating the two).  I am not sure why this matters? We did not smell pizza while we were there, nor did we taste anything besides amaze-balls Indian flavor. I am guessing that was just all in their head... but I suppose if you are bothered by that thing, then at least you've been warned?

I had the Paneer Tikka Masala (with some plain naan for dipping, etc), and my wife had the Vegetable Biryani.  They were both terrific (although I think we both liked mine best).

Seasoning was perfect (gotta love it when you aren't even tempted to add salt or pepper) - we both got level "3" on the spicy scale and that was perfect, too. Good level of heat, but not enough to distract from the flavor.

This place is really small (three 4-seater tables and three 2-seater ones, I think), and there was only one other patron in there - but our service was great, food came out hot and timely. We actually liked the atmosphere.

I will definitely be back, and if I lived close enough, I'd certainly try their delivery service.  Our 2 meals plus one side of plain naan and a garlic cheese naan appetizer (with only water to drink) was right at $30 before tax and tip.

The food was definitely worth it, but maybe a tad too spendy for all the time eating for me. If you wanna do it on a budget, I'd say split a meal and double up on the inexpensive bread/appetizers.

Overall, if you are into Indian (and especially if you are vegetarian), I highly recommend!
Very small and obviously a start up business, but the food was very tasty!  Channa saag, vegetable korma and chicken tiki masala were all very good.
xipdxExzjqukW1HvGHLDVQ: This building has housed several types of restaurants over its lifetime, but the current version has got to be the best use of the space yet.  The layout is great, the atmosphere intimate and romantic.  The only criticism, and it's a small one, is the lighting which was low enough to make reading the menu a bit of a challenge.  Once past that small detail, the rest of the experience was a delight. The service was friendly, and food was outstanding and appropriate for the price.  The presentation was creative but not overdone.  All in all, this is a place I can recommend and to which I intend to return often.

Wow just wow really great food. Nice sweet staff that know there stuff. I had salmon roles in cod  and both were done perfect . We were in a group of 6 . Everyone's food was awesome fresh cooked bread was perfect best I have had in years if they stay this good it will be hard to find a seat.  5 for food 5 for staff 5 for price.

5 stars is a minimum! The brunch was perfection!!! I truly tasted Europe in every bite! The smoked salmon was so fresh and the pears in the salad was heavenly! This is one of the highlights of my birthday! I can't wait to come back for dinner!! The dessert trio was just enough and 'bowl lickable'! :-) The waiter was so attentive and knew the menu very well and even concocted a pineapple bellini for me!! One of Nashville's  best restaurants!!!
zmLhgTlXQn3ZP09MIFEc4A: One of the best restaurants in Nashville! Definitely order the bread (it was unreal, and came with funky butter flavors), and the mushroom appetizer - so unique and rich! We also got a vegetable plate and a pork chop, both were delicious, and we would've gotten dessert if there were any room left in our stomachs. The atmosphere was relaxed/classy, the service was top notch, and the food was out of this world delicious. We'll be back next time we visit.
We've been twice and both experiences were worthy of a 5 star rating.  The chef is masterfully creative and the service  is exceptional.

With so many great restaurants in Nashville expectations are high when you hit a new one. ETC blew away my high expectations. Our dining experience ranks in the top 5 in Nashville over 21 years. The Brie and Berry salad burst with fresh flavors and large enough to share. My wife had the Big Night Pie for her vegetarian entree which was an incredible mix of roast pepper and cheese stuffed phyllo. I had the Lamb and coconut potato cakes. Wow! I did not need my knife to slice my delightful lamb. The plate was surrounded by several savory sauces. Overall a phenomenal meal !
NfyMmdhkwSPjhFrckLbj-w: 3.75 if i could. This restaurant is in the Marriott. I stayed here for several nights and ate here a few times. Happy hour wings  were good and so were the sweet potato fries with BBQ mayo. Strip steak was good. I'd order all that again. Stuff I wouldn't order again: Cajun chicken on pasta special - chicken was really dry. The brisket and cornbread had more sauce than I liked and they can't cook a burger to medium doneness. Still better than decent food though and good to great service most of the time.

Little pork sliders, buffalo wings and fried potato chips are cheaper eats at this lobby bar. Plus points for this cheaper food at normally expensive Marriott. There are also better eating selections like caesar salad, fruit salad and spinach salad. It costs $10 to park there but you can always park far and walk. A precious softplaying muzak keyboard player from TSU did his best to entertain for tips, but he didn't seem to get a lot of love. I almost expected him to break out in some Perry Homo. Occasional elevator music may continue, appropriate since this open lobby bar is next to the three elevators.

Great ambiance, the live music was nice and there were just the right amount of choices on the menu. Service was fast and friendly and the food was between a 3 and 4. Price pushed me into the 4 as they were very reasonable and the portions nice.
Y989Hsw30AYUMcyNjBz9iQ:
Nice, relaxed atmoshphere. Went in for lunch. Since it was raining the place was not too hectic, as my dining partner warned.

Had the mushroom risotto, which was full of flavor and cooked to perfection.

Would definitely come back.

French bistro in Nashville?  Not exactly the first cuisine you associate with Nashville but I think Table 3 incorporates the theme well in their menu.  (They do serve some non-French bistro items)

The restaurant sources all of their ingredients locally EXCEPT the seafood which I'm glad to hear.  Didn't think the Cumberland River produced the best oysters or fish.  Table 3 also has Benton's bacon which I recommend you get on your salads, burgers, etc. Simply delicious bacon.

The croque madame is quite good as is the spicy chicken salad.  The pomme frites are great so I highly recommend those as well. The only disappointment was the French onion soup.  Thought it was on the salty side and the cheese didn't have the slightly burnt crunchy top.  A good experience overall so would be coming back again.

Consistently, reliably good quality classic French bistro fare. A somewhat limited menu, but in my experience everything they do is done well. They usually have a nice selection of fresh oysters. The service is a high point--really excellent. Good wine list. The noise level is attractive--there's a pleasing hum, but one is able to carry on a conversation there. Valet parking. Conveniently or inconveniently located adjacent to the Green Hills Mall and a movie theater megacomplex. Great place to stop into before or after catching a movie.
DN_aM5zIdU4e-m-B9tewvQ: This place makes a solid, delicious gyro. I would call them a little more "Americanized" than some other gyros I've had - they offer fillings like shredded cheddar cheese and ranch dressing instead of tzatziki sauce, but all of that is totally fine with me because it was ridiculously tasty. They also use pocket pitas rather than just having a flat pita folded in half taco-style, which helps make the eating experience a little less messy. And they really stuff the pocket full. I had the combo, which had the gyro, seasoned fries, and a large drink. Definitely recommend this place if you love gryos.

Great spot for a delicious gyro with the friendliest service. Fast, efficient, and a hidden gem of a quick eat for those heading to/from the airport. I wish central Nashville had more international cuisine as tasty as this!
Place was not the cleanest but the food is to die for the service is great. But it just wasn't the cleanest place..
thSOykC8JAPruItPO-c0XQ:
So after a killer hike yesterday, I was riddled with famine. I had the instant craving for a black bean burger and saw via Yelp that Cheeseburger Charley's offered one.

I decided to walk on the wild side and give theirs a try since I was passing it anyways on the ride home from Radnor. This was probably one of the better decisions I made that afternoon. haha. It was insanely delicious! 

I ordered the black bean burger sans cheese on wheat. I dressed it myself (which is a def perk of CC's) with tomatoes, leafy greens, sprouts and pickles. It was THE perfect texture (I like mine a tad crunchy on the edges), full of flavor and for under 8 dollars it came with Baked Lays and a drink. 

I will def be returning for that meal... lunch or dinner! Yum.

When in the 100 Oaks area I like to stop at Cheeseburger Charley's.  Sometimes I order a Cheeseburger, sometimes I order a Hot Dog.  I have never been disappointed in the taste or quality of my meals - including the fries which are always the way Iike them.  The Condiment section is wonderful - they even have several different Mustards to choose from.  I love that I can pile up my burgers with as many onions as I want and as much mustard as I want too.

The Restaurant is always clean.  The burgers are a little more expensive than other places - but they are good.  That is why I always go back.

Nice staff, quick service, good food. Two different veggie burger options, beefalo, turkey burgers, variety of buns (white, wheat, Texas toast, etc), and different sides, including sweet potato fries and baked potatoes. Different from what you'll get a lot of places, but not so much so that it's not a burger joint. Nice outside patio.
841xWZNO5NcSCltjGEtW1w:
Yes, yes, yes! After reading the glowing reviews and trying it myself, I can proudly report that Ruby Ann's is everything Yelpers said it would be! I'm sensing a new craze emerging in Nashville. We've become known worldwide for our Hot Chicken, and now, the Loaded Biscuit is popping up at exponentially growing biscuit houses!

McFerrin Avenue, you have done it again! In 2010, it was Mas Tacos! In 2011, it was The Pharmacy! And now, Ruby Ann's opens across the street from both, three of the most amazing Nashville eateries on one street corner! Although, Ruby Ann's physically sits more on Cleveland Street just west of the intersection.

The Big Rube chicken and biscuit features crispy, tender chicken on a buttery, soft biscuit. The goat cheese offers the perfect balance of flavor with the chicken and pepper jelly. At $5, this is one of the best bargains in Music City! But I highly recommend the addition of bacon for $0.95 so your chicken and biscuit can go from fabulous to astronomical!!! Thank heavens it's not that crunchy, hard, overcooked bacon. It's soft and tender and flavorful the way bacon should be. Yum, yum, yum!!!

The twisted french fries are seasoned with coarse salt and pepper. Very delicious! And the Heath Toffee Snowstorm contains huge, generous chunks of Heath bar and has the consistency of that homemade ice cream your parents used to crank on the back porch! So, so good! Thank you Ruby Ann!
