Grabbed a quick biscuit here after crashing at a friend's place in East Nash. Hash brown was A+. Super crispy and a little salty. 

I got the Dave's Fave. Delicious fluffy biscuit with egg, avocado and veggie sausage. Tasty as hell.

Food is always yummy. Its so good for a hole in a wall. The best friends burger is filling and spicy. The cronuts are tasty but far from Dominique angels. Breakfast sandwiches are good and filling. But my favorite would be the cuts of their French fries. They are to die for!
piJbLNl4cY2akE2oTgJTmQ: This place is amazing! I got the talapia with mango salsa special and 100% fell in love. This is good old fashioned home cookin' like I haven't had in a long time and I must say it was a breath of fresh air. I will certainly be back!

Had lunch friday with my wife.  They have hired additional staff and are putting in a bar.  New staff was friendly and efficient.  Food was great as usual.  I had the blackened talapia over cheese grits with green beans and white beans.  Wife had seafood salad.  The food was great and the service was great.

4 stars for overall pleasurable dining experience.  Clean bright decor, booths and tables, friendly efficient service.  Hot wings $7 and bacon chedder grits $3 for me.
Blackened tilapia with two fried eggs, grits and potatoes for Pablo. $12.  Very nicely presented and tasty.  Pablos dish in particular was really good.  Wings were fried with hot sauce on them, not quite as clever as I like my wings but OK.
Beer available and a choice of desserts plus its across from Flyte so we were able to go from dinner to Happy hour, Lovely, Oh and the fish and grits is open 24hrs on Saturday
Mv0BYm3gQlFM7M7W6ovmqg: Pretty damn good. The lunch buffet is to the point and the rest of the menu is pretty good too. I would give it a 5 star, but the dinner plates are 75% rice, which is not ideal. If you have a hand in the business, please consider giving more salad or meat or something like that and less rice. Still, I come back here often and I would suggest checking it out.

First task: find a parking spot - there are very few street spaces available, so best bet is to pull around to to the small private lot in back, then come in through the back door (if it's before 6:00 pm. OR ... if it's after 6:00, go around to the front and enter, where an unassuming and sparsely decorated interior with glass-top covered tables greets you. Now things get simpler.

A member of their very polite and welcoming staff most likely will ask you to sit wherever you like.

Second task: order from a picture menu. Simple and straightforward. Well, actually, picking what you want to try is not necessarily that easy. So, maybe take Andrew Zimmern's advice, "If it looks good, eat it." (Everything on the menu looks tantalizing.) 

Third task: enjoy aromatic, flavorful, Halal, Middle Eastern food.

When looking at the picture menu, you'll find that nearly all dishes have some common elements - white rice and saffron rice. Presentation of the plates is rather mundane with the two rices each covering half of the plate and the chosen protein(s) arranged around the outside edge. For most dishes a small bland salad is also provided. 

My companions and I started with one of the three soups available. The lentil soup was a beautiful yellow color, dotted with herbs or spices, but very, very thin. The white bean was slightly spicy and appeared reddish - but not to worry, it was still delicious.

I have read some comments that the food here is "average." But I vehemently beg to differ. The bright and glistening chicken is moist, with a tender texture, exotically flavored with Middle Eastern mystery. I simply wasn't able to discern what spices were used to create this delectable offering.

The Tikka Beef (likely marinated in yogurt and spices, then dry roasted - almost too dry, but saved by the seasonings) turned out to be a real star for me. I was simply mesmerized and intrigued (yes, those are the descriptors I want to use) by the wonderful depth of flavor. The meat had a hard-to-describe, delicate earthiness to it. My best guess is that the following herbs or spices may have been involved in creating this unique profile:

--Mysore Cardamom This has been an elusive spice for me to detect with its woodsy, flora and aromatic notes, but it well might have been involved in this culinary mystery.
--Fenugreek - another spice that can add a wonderful earthiness
--Coriander - a tiny touch of its aromatic citrus brings some brightness
--plus a hint of the more mundane duo of Garlic and Ginger

Regardless of the recipe or preparation, both meats provided subtle yet distinctly delicious flavor profiles. So, to call the food average would be a disservice and distortion. Simple and delicious would better describe the quality of the food served here.

One odd note: One of my eight dining companions ordered Lamb Shank; pictured and listed listed on the menu. It wasn't until he had eaten part of it that he discovered it was not a shank, but rather, neck with vertebra distinctly visible. (Still delicious ... but what happened here? He wasn't offended and didn't ask.) 

A final and rather unique aspect of this restaurant is that a Musallah, a dedicated prayer room is available for Muslim daily prayers.

Four solid stars!

I really like their kabob. I tried it If you times. The taste wasn't really the same but then again I heard that a new owner took over. Their rice is really good. I did however feel like the chicken was a little dry but it was still okay. They also have very good customer service.
c5TgDR7ROgkwPMXxtGDiag: Like Panera's consistency in ordering, service, and food regardless of location or state. Always dependable.
The quality of food is getting better and better. They are among a few chain restaurants serving antibiotics-free meat!

It's a Panera just like all the others, but the service here really sets is apart from the others. We were not rushed through a feeding line, instead, the meals (we were there for breakfast) were explained by polite and knowledgable staff. Food was spot on and the coffee was better than anything at Starbucks.
4nxjxYYN3hFDLNC67erEFQ: The food is hot, fresh, and fast. Diner style and plenty of food for the money. The staff were super friendly.

OK. The Hermitage Cafe is where you need to "end" up after a night on Broadway. Period. After two consecutive weekends singing karaoke and having a fun night on the town, I found myself coming back on two very similar evenings. Roll in late at night and order the biscuits and gravy. JUST DO IT. They come smothered in a delicious white gravy with lots of black pepper. YES. I also tried the grilled cheese and grilled chicken sandwich, and both were fantastic. Prices are very cheap ($4.00 for a sandwich and potatoes) and the place is just an experience overall. Check it out for some awesome drunk food. The only annoyance is that they are cash only!

it is with compromised sobriety, greasy fingers and a satiated gut that i muse on the hermitage cafe. i'll be honest with you, i've gone here WAY more times than i want to disclose over the past month, as i now live shamefully close to pat's hermitage cafe. i've gotten food here drunk and sober, and i stick with ol' trusty:

a cheeseburger with fries (per my admission in my review of burger up)

the first time i came here i was sober and figured i wouldn't be impressed. i mean... come the fuck on... sure it's pretty much across the street from the newest residential project in my fair city (rolling hill mill). sure, you could say it's in sobro. but in all actuality, one could leave the hermitage cafe and be at the projects on murfreesboro road right where it turns into lafayette in a matter of ten minutes or so. the mix of people will never be boring. every time i've gone there's been a police officer and some punk rock kids. i've seen tourists, people in construction gear, people in construction gear, people in so condition to be seen in public and people visibly uncomfortable to be amongst people that aren't like them. 

when i got my first burger, i looked at it and didn't think it was anything special. the patties looked like any patty you could get in the grocery store, perfectly flat, perfectly round, and perfectly formed; it immediately made me think "sysco". thankfully i was very, very wrong. i can't quite place my finger on why i liked that burger so much, but it's been consistently goooood every time i've gone and under varying circumstances. also, the amount of fries i've gotten has never been consistent, lol. sometimes i can't finish them, and sometimes i'm tempted to call them back and ask "what the fuck is going on here???", but it's ok. somehow, it kind of adds to the experience.

aside from the inverted hours (10pm to 1.30pm, yes... pm to pm. what type of shit is that??), there is nothing that really... stands out about this place, though that sounds MUCH harsher than it really is. you do get the impression, however, that the hermitage cafe seems to be one of those places where you can just feel the ghosts of business and record deals agreed to on a handshake and basket of fries. the consistent deliciousness, inconsistent portioning, genuine service, and weirdo crowd make this place very charming to me and earns each of this quintet of stars.

eric b
QNj0zyvRY1CDlwnzg9gtgQ: I love this place! Great meats at fair prices and they cook a great lunch on the grill out front. It has the feel of an old country shop, and none of it is contrived. The folks who work here are also very nice and care about the quality of their work. Go get some bacon!

Great meats, great price always friendly and helpful!  Prepared food is good and fresh, craft beer selection is good for the size of the store.  Only thing I can complain about is that it's tucked away in East, with the drive though!

I LOVE living up the street from Roy's. The beer section is so nice for the space, they have so many nice every day needed things (veggies, eggs, milk, juice), and the staff is always so so helpful and kind. I don't eat meat myself but I always go up there to get meat for my dog and they packaged meat brand new and individually for me to freeze. I will continue to come back again and again!
i9TdM513qBCnSDpPttoKOQ:
If you are looking for some comfort food, this is the place to go! First off, the atmosphere is very nice- very open with outdoor seating. We were a party of 6 and ordered all kinds of dishes from salads to pizza. 
I was skeptical about ordering pizza since I'm from NY and it's hard to find good pizza anywhere else, but this pizza was different then your normal- we had the cheese steak pizza and it was excellent! Also ordered the Mac and cheese with fried chicken, which is a must. A few of my friends ordered salads and said they were delicious as well.

Beautiful casual location, with amazing food! We tried a good sampling of the menu and everything was delicious and beat our expectations! Lots of TVs for watching sports.  Plenty of seating inside or outside.  Friendly and attentive service!!! Lots bigger than downtown location! Huge stage for live music too!
The pizza is incredible. Everyone is nice. Order the Philly cheesesteak pizza. I would definitely return.
8W9xvpUWOGEe6tdETiWmJQ:
Good-bye to plain ole ice cream sandwiches!
Hello to chewy cookies with creamy flavorful ice cream!

I had the Gingersnap with Lemon Cream Ice cream and if I hadn't left I would've had a few more!

It doesn't matter if it's cold outside.  You will send your sweet tooth over the moon with happiness if you take the time to enjoy a treat from The Sugar Wagon!
I am on the BOD of a local non-profit and bought boxes of assorted cookies for Thanksgiving gifts.  I have been receiving emails all morning about how "to die for" the cookies are!

New food truck/wagon in town. They stopped by my office and sold us their delicious ice cream sandwiches. I was blown away! The salted caramel ice cream with chocolate chip cookie was to die for. Perfect blend of sweet and salty. They have also been riding along with other local trucks. Follow them on Twitter @sugarwagon to catch up with them. You won't be disappointed!
HfUHovtSl2Mf6qj74J11-w: Good Southern cooking.  Sit and be sever style.   The only menu item not to my liking are the greens.  A bit too much vinegar.   Props to the fried chicken and chicken fried steak in white gravy

I had met my server the night before at the restaurant I work at. Her and her friend recommended I come down and try their food. Ashley told me to get the salmon crochet and turnip greens and her friend said the white beans would hit the spot. They were right. It reminds me of my nanas cooking which is hard to compare to, but they did a fabulous job of suggesting the meal. Very impressed.

I give this  4 stars cause it is exactly what it is supposed to be:  no frills, basic good southern style food.  I was there peak of lunch hour.  They were busy, but I was quickly seated and served.  Service was very fast, and friendly.  Tried my basic gauge for this kind of place:  meatloaf, mashed potatoes, and green beans, with a cornbread muffin.  It was good, nice portions.  The dish could have used some gravy, but it was ok without it.  I'll return, try breakfast.
d_dFD_shmPpDhY4wMfEIRQ:
This was a great find. 

Located ~15-20 minutes outside of the city, it can be challenging to find but well worth the effort. 

Small dining room with ~6-8 tables serving amazing brunch dishes made with ingredients made on the farm. Standouts food-wise were the Pork Belly Grits and the Sweet Potato Hash with Chorizo. 

Also have a great selection of local taps and creative cocktails. Bloody Mary featured house-pickled veggies and an Herb-salt rim. 

After the meal - take a stroll around the farm and check out the chickens, vegetables, etc. 

They are looking to expand their kitchen and open a larger bar with a music stage. Also adding a barn for weddings

Overall a great spot - bright future if they can get people to make the slight trek from downtown and add infrastructure to support more tables.

Cut to the chase-I may have just had the best meal so far in over a year in Nashville.  Now back to the beginning.  The Old School Farm is so much more than a restaurant, make it a destination and plan on relaxing, unwinding and sitting a spell.  After all, you didn't take the drive out in the country to hurry things, now did you?  We arrived early on a Friday, and made our way into the bar for a flight of ciders.  They have several local beers on tap as well as Guinness, along with the above mentioned ciders.  The wine list looked extensive and they're crafting some very inventive house made cocktails.  We sampled the Bloody Mary with house made tomato juice bursting with the flavor of the garden. They have a beautiful large bar room with stage for live music and tables to dine here besides the main dining room.  Since they weren't yet busy we could engage with the staff and learn more about the wonderful program the Old School Farm supports as well as their bee keeping endeavors.  The menu changes weekly depending on what's fresh from the garden and the local farms.  We decided on three plates to share.  A roasted eggplant dip with grilled flatbread, roasted baby beets over yogurt drizzled with their local honey, and the shining star- sweet corn ricotta tortellini with pea shoots and soft boiled quail egg with chili oil.  One bite of the tortellini and we both declared it the best pasta we can remember having.  We finished our meal with a dessert of deconstructed grasshopper pie.  Every plate was a winner. While we were dining the jazz quartet added to the ambience.  We took time to walk the property, admiring their gardens, chicken coops and bee hives.  It looks like they have an open air building that would be perfect for weddings or events.  A visit here is so much more than just a meal, it's an experience worth taking your time to explore.

We had the Corn Cavatelli and the Loaded Potatoes Dauphine. They were both amazing! 

The staff was friendly, and extremely knowledgeable about dishes and their availability. Each dish was prepared from scratch. 

Corn Cavatelli: Each bite is a white knuckle thrill ride on a flavor packed culinary roller coaster. Literally changed my life. 

Potatoes Dauphine: The appearance suggested that my tastebuds would be on a pork infused journey of ecstasy. I was wrong. My taste buds left my mouth, walked across the restuarant, danced on the table on the other side of the room, and then took a nap on the plate. 

Great job!!!!
RB5lcQlhSMbGVmWHklfq-A:
If you're going to Krystal's, you're probably either:

A.) broke
B.) drunk
C.) sober and have bad taste in food.

Whichever you reason may be, this is the friendliest Krystal's around.
I forgot to add that there is a Krystals here in Dallas!!!! It's on Trinity Mills!!! Definitely go there to get you some party burgers as I call em!!!

I'm not a big fan of Krystal's Burgers but I love the bacon or sausage toaster sandwich! My only problem is the fact that they don't have a ready supply of grape jelly which is the only kind i eat,..so it get 4 stars. Now as for the staff well they are always friendly no matter what time I go and my food is always hot so no complaints here! Just stay in stock of the grape jelly and we won't have no problems...:)
C5lUI7qJ8NnmawAFVPZg8A: China dragon is a no frills place . The food tastes good and the portions are large , when you are looking for some good take out I believe this is go to place for me . I always order through the app and it's easy .

- Small portions but the food is top-notch
- Quick turn around, we were seated immediately at 6pm on a Tuesday night
- The owners hospitality made the whole experience worth it!

In a city of mediocre take-out, China Dragon stands above the competition. Offering generous portions and a wide selection of Far East eats, this place is miles ahead of other Chinese restaurants in the city. 

Never once have I had a dish that was less than piping hot and packed full of flavor. A personal favorite is the Kung Pao Chicken, which is overflowing with peanuts, carrots, and celery that rounds out the basic protein. Service is always fast and the staff personable, but parking seems to be an issue during lunch time.

Speaking of lunch, the $5-$6 combo platters are delicious and include an entree, fried rice, and an egg roll. A great price for the amount of food and flavor that you're getting. 

If you're looking for no nonsense, delicious take-out, look no further than China Dragon on West End Avenue.
gOLcG-FlUYYlr-_MrgnEpA: Great food & great service. First time last night. Definitely will go again. Filet mignon & blackened tuna were very good.

Our family has eaten here 4 times in the last 2 months.  The rib rolls are great and you can sub out the chips for any other side.  I've also had the burger(3/5 stars) and one of their brunch wraps, the Chorizo,  twice (5/5 for me).  The service has always been good and we have felt well taken care of.  We will return.

Had out of town clients and wanted an easy and relatively quick lunch in Green Hills.
We were fortunate to be seated outside on a beautiful,non-humid afternoon.
Cameron was our server,and he was great! Very attentive,and answered our questions. He made a few recommendations,so we ordered 2 Hot Chicken sandwiches and a Cobb salad.
We enjoyed everything,but the Hot Chicken was anything but "hot"-no zing at all. It was delicious,but not what we expected.Overall a good experience.
j9pufSFodfNWv5RsiBEfpw:
Overall: Delicious way to spend your lunch hour.

Food: I'm not from Philly, but I have been there and eaten a few of their cheesesteak sandwiches and I gotta say - LOL's version is pretty darn close! My only complaint is there's no cheese wiz, but not every cheesesteak restaurant in Philly has it either so I'll let it slide. The bread is perfect (straight from philadelphia, i think) and the meat is tender and flavorful. I got mine with american cheese & mushrooms and I'd order it again! I got the large size and it is big (but totally worth it) though I am kind of disgusted with myself for eating it all in one sitting - but I couldn't stop!

Atmosphere: Nashville is just now getting on the food truck bandwagon and I'm pretty excited about it. There are only a few options but I'm glad LOL is one of them. Follow them on Twitter (@LOLFoods) to get the truck's daily location.

Cost: about $7 for a sandwich, 50 cents extra for mushrooms and/or peppers

I have eaten twice (already) at the Labor Of Love mobile food truck.  They service one dish only and it is the mighty Philly Cheesesteak sandwich.  The only reason it's 4 and not 5 stars is that they don't have cheese whiz available - only american or provolone cheese.  Order a large the regular way and you will be a happy camper.  Great rolls, delicious meat.  If it's not broke, don't mess with it!  Oh and they also have Italian Water Ice!  I haven't tried that yet though.  I am proud to be the first reviewer of this new local business.  EAT LOCAL!  You can find out more by following them on Twitter at - http://twitter.com/lolfoods.

Love love love, my first real cheesesteak since moving to the south (yes, from Philly, so the bar is obnoxiously high). My only mistake was splitting the generously sized mushroom provolone masterpiece with my husband. The truck owners are friendly and down to earth, and I love the idea of selling spare Amoroso's and Tastykakes to let that fix of home last after you've pulled away from the little yellow truck, kissing your fingertips and licking your paper plate. Will be back whenever we can catch them!!
sIXYKC1FBcknBMHGIu-Hcw: Wow! I am pleasantly surprised by this place! I am in town for work and staying across the street. The yelp reviews were great so I thought I'd give it a try - glad I did! The sub is huge and very fresh. There are so many sandwich options, salads, etc. Very clean inside and the staff was very nice. Too bad I couldn't finish it all... The sandwich is big enough for two people!!

Packed so you know it's good! I got myself the super Reuben, with thousand island instead of spicy mayo. Holy cow, it was the best Reuben I've had in a long time! The 8" was perfect size for me because they give you plenty of meat and sauerkraut. I would highly recommend this place!
Simply the best subs!!!  I ordered a chicken salad with bacon OMG!!  It was the best and the bread was outstanding, too many choices to chose from but I did great.
XMBPQLOxRMHKvjO93njjLg: Great food and beer. Gets kind of loud though, like to the point that even yelling is fruitless.

Twin Kegs is a great neighborhood bar with good food and reasonably priced drinks. They just added some bench seating to their back patio which is really nice. Dog friendly patio. Solid burger. Docking one star because I always leave this place smelling like smoke but that is part of the dive bar experience I suppose. In the warmer months I like to sit outside so the smoke is not an issue. Would recommend, definitely happy this is in my neighborhood.

I'd heard about this place for years - - finally went yesterday.  It was everything I hoped it would be:  a dive bar that serves one helluva burger & has good cold beer on tap.  Do yourself a favor & go have lunch there when you're in the area---you won't regret it.
hAJRNzNudWuUTOUwXurovg: I walked in to kill some time while rush hour was going on and I was welcomed by Colin the bartender. I had no idea that this place was here and I was blown away by the fact that this was one of 3 places right next door to each other. A very cool spot for drinks and to gather for a good time. I would come back the wife and spend more time investigating the other spots that are right next door. I had a quick drink and I wish luck to the this cool concept that I would recommend you try just for the fact you didn't know is it was event here in GTown.

Awesome trolley concept in the middle building, which is where I usually spend my time. Pretty good burger and Reuben, great pretzel. Expansive beer collection on tap at the shogun style bar. Very cool outdoor seating area makes for an authentic neighborhood vibe. Collin the bartender is always friendly and attentive, even when the place is slammed. Easily my favorite bar in Germantown!
Great location and ambiance in this bar.  The trolley inside the bar gives a lot of character and adds to the enjoyment of the overall experience.
RrmvIZlHhtXOA-ZGAyLWHA: I love the Sunday brunch, so sad to see them closer location in downtown Nashville. Franklin location was a little unique, and a nice addition to that part of town. Fortunately I've been told the closest location as well as they're focusing more on the catering business

Do you have a friend that you talk to on the phone and no matter what topic you two start off talking about you always end up on the same subject conversation after conversation?  Well, I have a friend like that and we always end up talking about food and places to eat. That's how I found out about Copper Kettle.

Usually, I take my friend's recommendations with a grain of salt (no pun intended).  But she raved so much about Copper Kettle, I had no choice but to drive there and see if she knew what she was talking about. Boy did she hit the nail on the head!

Copper Kettle serves up some of the best Southern food I've had here in Nashville.  The food was so good I couldn't get the fork up to my mouth fast enough.  Like many southern restaurants in Nashville each day CK offers a different entree.  I went on Tuesday, which was meatloaf & pecan chicken day. I'm not a meatloaf fan but I was willing to try.  I'm glad I did.  I got it with three sides and everything ended up being delicious.  The mac & cheese was exceptional.  As I type this up, I'm about to start working on the slice of red velvet cake I got for dessert.  The portions were a good amount and the price was reasonable.  

No matter how good the food is... if it doesn't come with good service then it's not worth stopping in for. Copper Kettle was worth the drive. It has good food and good service.  I'm giving it 4 outta 5 stars!

Can only speak to the Sunday brunch buffet as I have not ordered off the menu for brunch or been here for lunch, but the brunch buffet is awesome!  Have been several times and it never disappoints!  They usually have a good selection of fruit and colder items, but who am I kidding?  My plate usually ends up filled with their tasty hash brown casserole, biscuits and gravy and bacon and sausage.  They also have this excellent banana custard pudding dish for dessert on the buffet that is so scrumptious!  

As many have noted the views of downtown  from their patio are awesome but this place fills up so I would recommend going early or sometime After 12:30 or so to ensure that you don't have to wait too long to be seated. One of my favorite Nashville brunch spots!
acxFUAHX_t1ESgo_42b4fw:
Keep this place going. 

Smoked chicken quarters are under a buck 4 days a week. The fruit tea is notable.
The best BBQ I have ever had. So wonderful. I did not even need to add sauce for the ribs. The beans were perfectly balanced. Can't wait to enjoy it again.

Small but nice eat in or take out. The staff was very friendly.  But the real kicker is the barbecue sauce! Wow! It has a little heat to it,  just enough to make the flavor pop. I had the brisket sandwich,  it was great! My spouse had the ribs, homemade potato salad and baked beans with meat in them (yum yum) after two bites he had already made up his mind he'd be returning!! Gave four stars because it is a new establishment, they had run out of ribs and I had to come back. But it was sooo worth the trip!
x6oj723phubu9vb7Xq6KhQ: Had some really good spicy wings and thighs with cole slaw. While the Cole slaw was passable and the wings pretty good, the thighs took the trophy. They were a good consistency and moist with enough spice to get your attention without being overbearing. Impressive display for a food truck. Recommended.

We want to give a shout out to the owner of Thunderbird- this guy is living the life. We called ahead to make sure the food truck was still open. He stayed open until we got there and even added on some freebies to our order. 

(The truck is located at the store Craft Brewed, by the way.)

We ended up having the wings, thunder thighs, slaw and smoked mac and cheese. Everything hit the spot. The chicken was smokey and peppery (finally what we expected out of Tennessee smoked meats). Please order the sides, or else you will miss out! Come on the weekend as well, as that's when he serves his mac and cheese.  

This place is modest yet confident in what it does. Do yourself a favor and go straight to Thunderbird.

SO GOOD. Tried the chicken salad (which was smokey whole shredded chicken and was excellent) and the wings. I appreciate spicy food, and these wings are definitely spicy. Here is a a quick break down reviewing each level:

Beginner- more peppery than spicy. Leaves a nice flavorful after taste 
Intermediate- a nice kick at first, very spicy aftertaste
Advanced- *didn't try, but watched my friend sweat for 20 minutes*

Ps. The wings have a lot of substance (meat) as well!
w2c2pV1uh0QS-oIigi-y7g:
If you've never had Peruvian food, it's a must. It's fresh food, fresh ingredients and delicious. Anytime I come to big city's, I look to see if there are any Peruvian restaurants and I never leave unhappy. The plates taste as they look, amazing. I had pollo saltado with yucca which is always good. 

If you like seafood, ceviche is the Peruvian trademark and I always see a ton of people eating it. I don't like seafood so I stick with any Peruvian chicken or steak dishes and I'm never unhappy.
Casual atmosphere, good food, friendly service. Mine was made to order but they have some things set up cafeteria style so would be perfect for a quick lunch.
Easily the best ceviche anywhere near Nashville. Rotisserie chicken & sauces are also on point.
RIJx0KUUxpOvkChwyTk0sQ:
The Market at Table 3 offers great pastries, homemade fresh bread and *Popsicles*, among a wide variety of other baked goods and French fare.  There is also a little refrigerated section that sells your favorite condiments and garnishes that Table 3 provides with their fine dining, such as Rougié (duck fat) or Aïoli!

Open early, this is a nice spot for a quick bite, a fast breakfast and it won't break the bank.  Call ahead and they'll run your order out to you: Curbside Pick-up!

I love Table 3 as a spot for a great dinner meal, so I was excited to try their deli-inspired dining space, The Market.  Artisan sandwiches, coffees, and dessert offerings make up this sunny little space near the cinema at the Green Hills mall.  If you want the quality of Table 3 with a less formal feel to it this is a great place to sit and get a more café-style experience.  Their turkey sandwich with bacon and avocado was absolutely delicious.  It's a fantastic alternative to the always-busy-and-insane Cheesecake Factory when out shopping.  Its more relaxed but with all the yumminess that you'd expect from Table 3.  I will absolutely be trying more of their offerings from here from now on when at the Mall.  They also offer cookware and other miscellaneous items for purchase, and like the food everything is reasonably priced.  I like this much better than the other places you can dine at at the Green Hills Mall, mostly just for the ambience.  With the weekends always so packed out there, this offers a nice refuge to the craziness.
love it here....awesome variety, decent prices, service was good (the young man had a charming and cute personality) wonderful little place to grab a snack before or after shopping....
Xv4PIVA_sQwb9q-uzVq9GQ: This place is somewhat expensive for what you get, but the food really is delicious. Bread was okay but the salad was wonderful-particularly the Caesar salad. We had an Alfredo dish with shrimp that was so good and a Baileys dessert that was noteworthy as well.  My husband thinks that this is the quality of food that would make it in a trendier part of town. They do have a split plate fee so be aware of that. We would eat here again and probably order our own meals.

In my opinion the food is consistently awesome. Your experience will depend upon who your server is. The bartender is extremely rude so don't spend time there! Riz is an awesome server and will make your visit special!!!!  I can't say enough good things about him. Bruce is awesome as well!  Andy on the other hand won't speak to you. However he will come and take your oil from the table and roll his eyes at you when you call him out in it. Please always ask to be seated with Riz or Bruce!
Antonio's is amazing. It's the best restaurant in this area if not the city. Impeccable service and delectable dishes. They never disappoint and that happy hour menu is incredible!
m4ya6j7lF3vKh_0wWpVGFg: Which Wich is an awesome. The featured sandwich "The Wicked" is unbelievable. Recently I have been getting Turkey & Ham, which is also great as is every sandwich. Only thing negative is that despite the set up it is not really a "fast food" place and sometimes takes a while.

I love Which Wich! The fact that you get to design your own sandwich is pretty friggen' awesome. Food always tastes fresh & everyone who works here is friendly. Unfortunately, finding a parking spot can be a bit tricky in this part of town but I still think it's worth grabbing a sandwich from here. Love coming for lunch & dinner!

Want a sandwich just the way you want it? Okay this is your place. You have total control over what you can put on it- i'm talking more than just options that Subway has...we're talking cheese whiz, crispy onion straws and more. 

You start by picking a sandwich i.e. meat...then you choose cheese,dressing, and toppings. You mark all this stuff on a cool brown paper bag. Then they zip it down the line to prepare! They have great shakes too. 

All sandwiches are baked. I always add on a dill pickle.  My favorite is the Club. 

Often busy at peak times and this location has TERRIBLE PARKING. I'd either walk or have someone drop you off and pick you up in 10 minutes. Be careful on west end there are tons of cars turning all sorts of ways on that busy street. Make sure you're not stuffing your face with a sandwich while you're driving. 

Sign up for the loyalty club and get a free sandwich a purchase of a drink.
D7SxS4qCle_Nsv_ataoM1g: GREAT WIFI. I know, may not be the most important facet, but seriously, I needed to get work done and the WIFI speeds were FANTASTIC. Particularly, uploading was FAST. Sometimes when you travel, you just need the internet to work.  Not to mention a comfy spot to hang out and have a drink! I had the spiked coffee which was wonderful! Along with the black bean soup - very tasty.

Best cocktails in Nashville, free from country music but still very good music, played quite silently so talk. Friendly. Nice staff and good service, the snacks and plates are good as well. Will definitely come back. Pitty though they have not updated they website. They are open until 1 am weekdays and weekends.

I've been to the original Steadfast in Germantown many times but today I went to Steadfast Commons for the first time.  As is always true with the original. Quality customer service-truly the best staff, and the coffee soda is always a win.  
Everything about this place is quality and I always walk out happier than I walked in.
3VqPrx-h1B4TPNCcE_xilw:
It's hard to be slim when I like my pizza husky. This is your typical build your own pizza, but it has a special twist with the hip and laid back environment. I might be a little biased because of 90s music and the biggie quotes, but slim and husky definitely lives up to the 5 stars. 

You can either choose from one of their creations or create your own. There are only two sizes, slim (small) or husky (large). If you choose to create your own pizza, then you have a variety of sauces to choose from. I have yet to try the "white sauce is slammin" because the red sauce is just too good to not get. After choosing your sauce, you have a ton of toppings to add to your pizza, which can be harder than creating your brackets for march madness. I don't like adding too much toppings because I don't want them to make my pizza too soggy or weighed down. Once you pick your toppings, all you have to do is kick back and wait for your delicious creation to finish baking! 

Slim and husky is perfect for first dates or anyone who loves pizza aka EVERYONE SHOULD COME AND TRY SLIM AND HUSKY!!!

One of the best pizzas in town. I always get the California Love- a pesto based pie. The personal pizza, any-toppings, one flat rate is hard to beat. My only complaint is the limited space and seating. Have seen the line out the door multiple times and walked across the street to RollOut, their to-go spot. Heads up, service at the RollOut is sluggish at best. Still great pizza though.

We went into the restaurant about noon and Sunday the food and atmosphere was fantastic. The employees were all super helpful and looked like they were happy to be there. Defiantly check this place out if your in Nashville.
f_GIv3cHcyXGxEPXRJ2eCQ: Awesome dim sum. It s great that Nashville has an oppurtunity to enjoy this now

If you know your way around a Chinese menu, beeyond what they'd have at any gooey fats take-out place or (jeesh) steam table buffet horror, you can do pretty well here,. There's a  second "Chinese" menu (bilingual) in addititon to the larger one they generally hand out, and it has some more sophisticated dishes than the norm aorund here.

For example: the whole steamed fish..more vegetavle chocies, or or the chicken with black bean sauce; the latter's with real beans, not soy sauce, fermented,  tangy, even a little properly bitter.  Things seeme to be a little tight there nowdays, and the srevice has slowed.  But the chef can cook if you ask for something that takes cooking...

I think it is hilarious that all these naaaasty chinese places in Nashville that serve bright pink chicken and MSG soup get 5 stars while the best Chinese Nashville has to offer gets much lower. It is because the people who are reviewing this restaurant are West Coast types and really Lucky Bamboo doesn't compare to what you'd find in SF or LA, but it's really a million times better than typical Americanized Chinese slop (I'm looking at you Corner Chinese). 

I enjoyed the dim sum, but the key is to only order things as they come out. They'll try to pass off the cold shrimp rolls on you--DON'T DO IT. (I don't need to say this, but it isn't as good as what you'd find on the West Coast, but it is not bad.)

Also, if you have a Chinese friend, they'll know how to order something better than you will. If you're stuck going with non-Chinese friends, know that Chinese food is usually supposed to be family style so go with a group, order a few dishes and share. Maybe you'll get lucky.
XFWuEv2FMcO5Ifco0tq6Ew: Went here for the very first time to meet a friend and the food was delicious. The omelet I had was the best thing I ever had. I would definitely come back here again . This place is a little expensive but I would definitely come back again. The biscuits they have are amazing and you could eat a million of them things . So if you're looking for a place to eat at go here you will LOVE IT!!

My wife and I decided to grab a quick bite before our mansion tour. So glad we did! The tomato bisque soup was creamy and delicious and the freshly made chicken fingers basically melted in my mouth. A great place to eat if you're visiting the Plantation!

We came here for Mother's Day brunch and was our first visit. The biscuits and caramel rolls were awesome starters. My Irish coffee was outstanding and smooth. My entree was the salmon eggs Benedict. Again, outstanding. So this place is a hat trick for you hockey fans. Highly recommend this for ambience and food.
N7MyXRAC47vldNJQZyWDtw: Great sushi.  No extra-ordinary combinations, just the usual. But nonetheless it was fresh and delcious!  Great prices too! $4-12 a roll...I'm used to Cincinnati $6-16 a roll.

This is one of my regular circuit lunch places in downtown Nashville.  Today, I ate the lunch special- Tempura Shrimp and Teryaki shrimp and chicken served with soup and salad and a drink for $10.95 out the door. The food is always good and the sushi is hard to beat anywhere else.  Good food, good service, good ambiance and price.  I go back time and again.

Miso - Just like you would expect
Eel roll - Perfect
Hook roll - Very fresh
Hot tea - Hot

This is a good place to go for lunch while working downtown. I paid just over $14. Not bad. Service was very friendly and fast. I really like this place and will be back very very soon. 

Seating is rally limited to about 4 people to a table. Not a large place. But, the food is really good for Nashville sushi.
AkNuIbco7mdsuOWLPyZXCg:
Great alternative to all of the fast (or fast-ish) food in the area.  

It took me a few trips to get my order down to something that I know I'll like every time, but now I'm good.  Tinga chicken burrito, no rice, black beans, and various other toppings.  They have a good selection of toppings and then there's also the salsa bar.  I also really like that they have different sized burritos - sometimes, I'm just not that hungry.

Recommend.

When they're on, it's amazing. Staff can be hit or miss as far as how they prepare your meal. Ingredients are good and salsas are great. Eaten here probably 6 times and 5/6 experiences have been worth it.

I love this place! The fish tacos are awesome! We also tried the taco salad with shredded pork - yum! It's quick and easy - reminds me of a Qdoba but with more flair. Their salad dressing is great and their salsas are made everyday!
d2SZeHA8FWNZC-9cENmzVA: First time going to this place!!!!!! It's 1 of the best burgers I've ever had hands down
Great place to get a burger.  Their milkshakes have gotten me through many a long afternoon at the office.  Plus, they use real Hershey's syrup when making their chocolate shakes.

I used to frequent this Cheeseburger Charley's location when I worked at the mall.  The seating area is arranged a little oddly, but otherwise it's pretty much your standard Cheeseburger Charley's location.

As with all locations of this local chain there's an elaborate topping bar and lots of veggie/pesca burger options on the menu.  If you'd like to opt out of the fries the side salad is surprisingly gourmet.  I was pleasantly surprised to see it came with blue cheese and mandarin oranges and, of course, you can still add-on from the toppings bar.
syWnBBs6EN2w1Hd0YFXOdA:
Went to Nashville for a weekend getaway and absolutely loved Radius 10.  If this place was in Atlanta, it would be packed every night!

Loved the atmosphere---an industrial building rehabbed right near the railroad tracks.  In a few months it seems this part of Nashville will be completely changed with all of the building that's happening.  So, get to this gem while you can still get in!

My b/f had the shrimp & grits, which our waitress suggested as her favorite and she didn't lead him astray.  He said this meal was one of the top 5 of his life!

I had the porchop over cheese grits with some kind of molassas and bourbon sauce.  Sooo yummy.  Seriously the grits were killer.

I also had their signature fresh pineapple mojito, which came stirred with a stick of sugar cane.  So delicious!

This is a place that we would definitely seek out again the next time we're in Nashville.

So I decided to give this place another chance after one of my girlfriends went on and on about the shrimp & grits.  I have always left this place hungry before.  Boy am I glad I gave it another try!

My gf and I went there last night for my birthday dinner and got sat immediately at a great table outside.  This atmosphere is really good.  The weather & the sunset helped - but it was so perfect out there!

Our waitress, Rochelle/Rochand?, was spot-on.  I had the pineapple mojito (oh yeah!) and my girlfriend at the pepper margarita.  We were then brought an amuse bouch that reminded me of the sauce you put on a hot brown (for those of you not from Kentucky, it's a pungent cheese sauce with bacon).  DEEElish!

We opened a nice bottle of chardonnay (they are half price on Mondays) and split the heirloom tomato appetizer.  I counted 3 types of tomatoes and it had a savoy pesto sauce and cheese.  I love summer!

My gf was SO right about the Low Country shrimp & scallops.  The grits were so tasty I dipped the shrimp tails in it and ate them (does anyone know if you should eat the shrimp tails, because I've heard both?)   My gf, who just moved to Atlanta, said that her ahi tuna was so good that she may make trips back here JUST for that meal.  We were both in heaven!!

For dessert we had a bday brownie ala mode - I don't know if it is regularly on the menu, but it was a perfect ending to a perfect night.  I love southern, modern cooking.  I have now added Radius 10 to my top 5 restaurants in Nashville (it's still behind Red Pony in Franklin, though!)

For my first time in Nashville, I had a great time. I was there on a business trip, and we took some people out for cocktails and app's here.
They had excellent cocktails, super fresh, and the bartender was awesome.
I love the patio, and the space is very modern and open.
The food was good as well - lobster pizza, hummos, spinach dip.
They also have happy hour specials.  Works for me!  I'll try it for dinner my next time there!
HP03UxUpfwic04tBuqsqPw: I love this restaurant! Every time I go there the servers are super nice and they oblige my vegan requests/substitutions. The food is awesome and the best I've had in Nashville yet! Try this place and you won't be disappointed.

Royal Thai is a solid choice for lunch. They offer an express lunch menu for $9. I had the Pad Thai along with 2 spring rolls. It was authentic and tasted fresh. The inside is a little divey, but nothing out of the ordinary. It was rather slow when I dined in and there was plenty of seating.
Best Thai food I've had in Nashville. Previously we lived in New Haven, CT, which has multiple Thai restaurants -- it took us a while to try out this restaurant but would definitely recommend it!
eIFQYQXu0Rv5A9X95nkUcw: Went here with my two younger cousins after the zoo. The owner was working and was very friendly and knowledgable of the menu. He catered to the kids and let us watch the Disney channel while we ate which was so thoughtful. The service was quick and the quality of the pizza was excellent and affordable. They have games and even a kid karaoke night.Would definitely recommend this place for families or not.
The pizza was delicious - crispy thin crust with generous toppings. The owner was friendly and extremely helpful.

We ate here for the first time tonight and I thought it was great! The staff was very friendly and the pizza was delicious! The have other things on their menu like wings and sandwiches as well. We'll definitely be back soon!!
e5VUh58V61_nD-0HhBXCGw: My favorite spot that is close by. Everything is cook to order if you don't mind waiting 5-10 mins but definitely worth the wait. Place is nice and clean and staff is cool.

Stopped in for a quick lunch after running a few errands. I was throughly impressed during my visit. The location was very clean and had plenty of space. I ordered the gyro/chicken sandwich combo....it was a great price. The fries we also seasoned which was good. I will definitely be returning next time I'm in the area.
Excellent place for great food their is plenty of free parking and the staff really takes care of you it reminds me of the restaurants in Chicago
W6WPIjkrt6M2hqpxf9PRIA: 23 mins for two large pizza and breadsticks. 8 employees and two managers. Place was hoppin. Pizza was good. A little saucier and sweeter than others but it had real meat. Other than the wait. Next time ill call ahead.

Ordered a chicken parm sandwich and a cheese pizza, they were both delicious. 

Since then, we have ordered from them many times - pizza and sandwiches. It's no Joey's House of Pizza, but for a late night delivery option I'd go with these guys over Papa Johns all day. 

There are some bad reviews on here, but the owners must have changed since then because I have never had a bad experience and have been ordering from them for about 3 months.

These people left their cards in the lobby of my dorm. YES. FINALLY somewhere I can get food delivered literally whenever I want. THANK GOD. They will be hearing much more from me and my friends. My only request--more gluten-free options like a gf pizza or nacho appetizer...but then I'd probably order every meal and be broke.
eN1NbRBlOt2gem0fhOjIPQ:
Dan and the crew just catered our wedding.  We couldn't have been happier.  The menu was progressive but familiar enough that the "chicken nuggets and pizza" crowd could still enjoy their meal.  The chicken and waffle on a stick appetizer caused a feeding frenzy.  

I lost count of the number of people that came up to me to say "this is the best food I've ever had at a wedding."  The organization, service, and presentation was outstanding.  

The Inglewood Lounge (where Actual Food resides) is dope too.  

You can't go wrong with these guys.

Dan (owner of Actual Food) put out a phenomenal meal for our group of 18 at his newly and beautifully renovated space on Gallatin. Everyone of us has been raving about this meal, so I gotta thank Dan for making my decision to come here look so good! 

The white bean dip that started us off was absolutely addictive. The lamb & pork kubideh skewers were perfectly seasoned and delicious. The onion & potato gratin, which can oft turn into a bland mess, was anything but -- instead, it was rich and complex. But the true champion of champion dishes was the pork belly/sweet potato/beet dish. One of the most well-balanced dishes I've come across, I could not stop piling more and more of it onto my plate.

Truly a great meal in a great space. Whether you are using Actual Food for catering, or using Dan's space for an event, you cannot go wrong here. Highly recommend.

Actual Food was soo great to work with for our wedding and the food was excellent.  They were always quick to respond the emails, the pricing was very reasonable for the strong quality and they truly delivered the day of the event.  The team worked to overcome a few obstacles last minute - going above and beyone and provided wonderful service to our guests.  We had many compliments on the food and they made adjustments to the offerings based on our vision.  Could not have asked for a better caterer / bar service.  We were very happy with our selection and love what they stand for as a company.
a3_Eci6wmiSkWtP0CjRQvA: Hanging out waiting to get some window glass replaced on a road trip (nothing like a broken back window to add a little overcoming adversity to a honeymoon!). We got to nashville mid afternoon without having eaten, and spun around the block near the glass place to find some food. We were in luck. Two sandwiches, sides, and a drink were eleven bucks, and they hit the nail on the head. Crispy tasty fish sandwich, when the time comes, you just can't beat it, and the captain came through.

Everytine I come to this location I have been greeted in a friendly manner. And I have to say that compared to the Dickerson Rd location this is one of the cleanest nice smelling Captain D's. I will dine there again.
This place i clean people could be a lil nicer but dis not have to wait long. Great fish, make sure you use your captian ds discount card.
Y1tJ_DUEuKQ41dqbMwRDZQ:
Two sauce covered thumbs up here!
I love finding unique fast casual tasty dining experiences in Nashville and Otter's is exactly that. First off, the counter service was friendly and I was greeted with a smile. None of that too-cool-for-school hipster snobery here. Just friendly dudes who seemed genuinely happy to help me with my small order. All those compliments also apply to the gentleman at the pick-up window. The space doesn't have that cookie-cutter feel to it that a lot of fast casual joints tend to have... Otter's has a bit more of a cozy kinda mellow atmosphere. Right on.
Now, on to the grub. I got the #3 which I will easily get lunch and dinner out of today. The chicken tenders were WAY bigger than I expected. I got 3 grilled and 2 fried tenders and even the grilled ones were HUGE. For some reason I expected those to be much smaller. What a great option btw, grilled instead of fried. Brilliant! The tenders were cooked to perfection and although I wanted to keep eating, I was seriously full after eating just 3 of them. Of course I also had Fries, coleslaw, a slice of Texas toast & TWO sauce choices as part of the huge basket. I got the Otter's signature sauce and the spicy selection. The Otters sauce was my favorite and the spicy sauce was also good. Both were far more interesting than the obligatory honey mustard or ranch found elsewhere. Otter's also has those and I think they make their own so they're prolly pretty good. ;-) The fries were good and I like the slaw too. All in all, prolly my favorite fast casual dining experience in Nashville to date!
Keep up the good work Otter's!
Oh, I almost forgot, they are on Scoutmob!

In the land of chicken fingers, good sauce is King. 

All hail King Creole Sauce.

This place is an efficient lunch spot, and the chicken fingers are very good, but in all seriousness; try that creole sauce.

If you like chicken this place isn't hard to beat. Everything is based around what kidn of chicken you want; grilled, fried or buffalo. I personally like the fried but usually go for the grilled. Once you choose how your cooking your chicken then you decide if you want straight tenders, a sandwich, a wrap (my fav) or a salad. The tenders come in a variety of meals with fries, etc. I personally don't care for their fries, but that's just my preference. It's usually busy during weekday lunches but not too crowded. I really love their fried pickles with ranch too but they only serve them at dinner. Parking is usually a pain, as it is always is on Demonbruen. Ooh, and they have a great student discount.
s4kvGChiHll124Rq04QoQg: Chicken was Delicious and Fresh... Staff was Polite and Helpful!! I plan on stopping by every chance I get!!!

Good drive thru experience.  The food order was correct and tasted very fresh.  The staff was friendly and prompt when we got through the drive thru.  The biscuits were made from scratch and fluffy, perfect for an on the go breakfast!

As a North Carolinian, Bojangles has been sorely missing in my life in the pat few years. I don't know how much salt and butter they put in the biscuits but all I know is that my taste buds break out in a chorus of hallelujah when these biscuits enter my mouth. Pure bliss
WwxsLX2Z-SUwiTUs8rsOKg: First off, Diane and Matt are both great bartenders! Always get taken care of there very well. They have the coolest options of different things of which are ALL phenomenal. There on tap seasonal pumpkin beer is mind blowing and like no other. There appetizers beef jerky is the bomb! You can bring your pups and sit outside. I love this place! It's my new fav! They are over priced either in my opinion. I typically get straight chilled shots of well vodka. But either way. This place is chill and awesome! Highly recommend.

Great happy hour place with very good drinks menu. I went there with a big group and they gave us a total section of the bar. We had a great time. There is a parking lot just behind which is an added bonus.

Such a beautiful spot, they lose a star because all the parking around them is $2, which just seems like a way to irritate someone before they step in the door.  The space itself is lovely, woody and lots of plants to give the space an earthy feel.  There's a long bar and a few high tables as well as booths.  There's even two swings in the back of the room near the gorgeous flower mural.  They also have a small patio in front for warm days. Along with beer and wine they have several cocktails on tap; the option for a custom made drink is always available if none of the ones on tap appeal.  I tried the Beets by Dr. Dre, a very flavorful concoction with fresh beet juice.  They also let me try their old fashion which was very unique with the addition of maple syrup.  The tap cocktails are made in small batches so seemed fresh to us.  We tried a smoked cheese flatbread appetizer.  A pita spread with hummus and the cheeses melted over, it was really flavorful. We really appreciated that the music was low so we didn't have to shout and could carry on a conversation.  Really a great addition to the neighborhood.
XdTrPmRcx2NG4btkBclEkA: Good...fast ...needed a little more sauce on the pizza.next time I will order extra sauce and garlic. Will order again!

Exactly what you expect. A small shop with old chairs and not the shiniest paint on the walls, an older balding guy with a big mustache calling the shots and a tired looking dude working the register, and the best damn pizza this side of the Atlantic. Veggie Garden Tomato, Hawaiian, Italian meats, you name it, it's good. Eat here if you love food, don't if you're too busy looking fancy.

While visiting Nashville for a few days we decided to order in Friday night to our hotel. Browsed internet for nearby restaurants and found Roma Pizza. We were taking a chance not being familiar but were pleasantly surprised. We ordered just an hour before store closing but delivery was very quick. Pizza was hot and delicious and Greek salad and dressing was great also.
O5LnTroDDouDbjdjufXJsg: Best Mexican food in this area that I've had. Steak tip burrito - great! Service has always been nice. Always get seated right away and chips are down quickly. I love Mexican restaurants cause you can get a huge Pacifico for CHEAP. We always have a great experience here and enjoy it's so close and convenient.

I'm hard to please when it comes to Mexican food.  We have been searching Nashville far and wide. FINALLY found it here in our own backyard in Bellevue.   

The carne asada was on point, and you can't beat two for one margaritas.

I recently moved back to Nashville and the first restaurant we visited was El Agavero. I was pleasantly surprised at their newly expanded facility. They doubled their seating capacity and their staff and added a bar. They had the same great menu and the same commitment to serve the customer's desires.  I ordered something that was not on the menu and they complied gladly.
l1LgSU6bOX_mjCKgQr7gkw:
Not too bad for fast food! Of course it's not real Carribean food, that would be like expecting Taco Bell to be real Mexican food! But if you're looking for a change from the average fast food burger or sub this is a fantastic alternative. This is one of the only fast food places I'd willingingly go in and eat inside, like an "actual" meal. The grilled chicken sandwich is honestly probably the best I've had. I usually hate them at places because they're always dry and blah, but here they give you a decent-sized, juicy, tender piece of grilled chicken. The lettuce and tomatoes have always tasted like actual lettuce and tomatoes and not the soggy crap you usually get out. 

I imagine it does depend on when you go- of course late at night the last of the food will be still sitting out and during lunch rushes things are always crazy. But the service is quick, the prices are good, and you don't have to wait in Chick-Fil-A like lines.

Been to the Smyrna location twice.  My food allergy son can eat at this place.  Disappointed they plans for Hendersonville location fell through but glad it's still close in Rivergate.

Pros:  Chicken and mojo pork were juicy, moist, and flavorful; sweet plantains; yellow rice with vegetables-different amount of veggies in each bowls...kids loved the rice but skipped the big pieces of broccoli/stem.  Service: This place has not been open a week so the lines were long.  My son wanted more rice but were reluctant to order because of the long wait.  Mentioned it to the wait staff and they brought him a bowl of rice.  Tried to pay for it but he said it was no charge!

Cons:  Can't decide which sauce was my favorite.  Is that really a bad thing?  Cilantro Garlic and guava bbq were tops.  Pineapple rum is too sweet.  Spicy pogo poyo was alright. Nothing special about the fresh salsa.  But the curry mustard was a pleasant surprise. Ok...the real con...saw a wait staff widely wiping tables spreading crumbs all over the floor and seats/benches.  Of course he didn't wipe those down.  Ohhh the roll...looks and feel kinda toasted but like a previous reviewer stated, it was not warm.  Chicken wings: nothing special.

Will try the ribs next time...looks like a new item.  Believe they had riblets before.

I would have given it 5 stars but the drive thru service was awful. I'm am giving the benefit of the doubt to the young girl that was probably newly hired.  She forgot three of the 5 items we ordered... The food is not like any fast food I've had and if you've had Cuban, Puerto Rican or Spanish food, this is what you get.  Not like Mexican food as it is 
not hot, but more refined.  The guava cheesecake is delish.  Have not had the flan there. Will be back!
bki_5P8lQ51wWTd55wikpA: The food was amazing. Service A+. Kitchen had problems with computer system so good was slow coming out.  Manager came to apologize and ended up giving us our meal for free for the delay

AWESOME lunch visit.  We went to watch the WVU basketball game between speakers at the ABCA convention.  The service was top notch and really took care of our needs at the table.  The food was cooked to perfection and tasted great.  Great ambiance and atmosphere.

Back in December 2016 we had such a nice family lunch at this Logan's location! We had come from out of town, just to see the Christmas lights, and decided to grab lunch somewhere. We were glad we chose Logan's! Food was great and service was even better. Our friendly server provided directions to help us on our way as we left. We will definitely stop again when in the area.
cr1BA7byDDj_fkiP7rtNjA: We have been to several stores in the same strip mall as China King, but never had eaten there.  We decided to order take out from there, as the place closer to us has not been very good the last two times we ordered.  We were extremely pleased!   The food was ready as promised, packaging for takeout was excellent and our meals were great!  We have a new favorite!!!  Highly recommend!!!

Food looks great. Egg roll was delicious. Nice and crispy, hot throughout. Beef was very tender but almost tasted like pork.... Fried rice was not a fav- broccoli was perfect. Will try the white rice and chicken next time.

This is the third Chinese place that I have tried out since I moved here from Knoxville, as previously stated the place is small as are most Chinese resturaunts are so no big surprise there. I ordered Hunan beef, my fiance tried out Mongolian beef, and we both got eggrolls. mine was a little bland but still good and my fiances Mongolian beef was awesome. the eggrolls were also pretty good, we will be going back as it is better than the other two places we have tried out.
smSoAF1VqCChVB-WZhHvYw: Staying at the best western on division and needed something easy and quick.  Definitely got the easy and quick and the bonus was delicious.  Excellent pizza, reasonably priced and delivered in about 40 minutes.  Nothing bad to say except I ate the whole thing

Ordered from here through Uber rEast during my stay in Nashville. Ordered the house salad; I am always curious how big salads are especially when ordered to go, but I was very impressed with the size. Then I ordered the buffalo chicken pizza and it was so good! The most important part of a pizza to me is crust and this one passed my test. I would definitely order from here again!

I have ordered dinner from here on several occasions and it was promptly delivered and the delivery person was quite courteous. The food wasn't bad either. I ordered a steak, potato, and salad cooked to perfection. It was cheaper than going to Logan's down the road.
qKz3Sbu_7M3c1u1u8fFdXA: I was in Nashville on business and saw Roma as an option in the hotel guest book. I was dead tired and figured I'd try a local pizza place. I was highly surprised. I got hot wings, a house salad, and white pizza (cheese and spices, no peppers or meat - Philly style). Everything was above average and I was very satisfied. Much better than I expected.

I've only ordered from here once, but as one of the only late-night delivery services at midnight on a Sunday... I wasn't expecting much. I was wrong!!! I ordered wings, chicken parmesan, and garlic bread and every single thing was amazing!!! Not to mention it was delivered within 20-25 minutes. Yum!!!

Pretty good place. I had a stromboli it was pretty good, my friend had the buffalo chicken sub and she said it was good.  This is coming from a guy who is used to chicago pizza. Id eat here again. Very friendly people that worked there too.
hqzObWUta09EEw4fyOGe3A: Oh, the lamb chops!  These are relative recent rotation onto the menu and mercy, they were incredible.  The potato gnocci were simply perfect in texture and flavor.  What an luscious meal.   Looking forward to more return visits.
Nice and cozy little restaurant. Ambiance was decent and good was good- nothing unusually great but still a good experience. My mixed drink could've used some help.

Excellent. We went here on a Friday with four couples. This is not fine dining folks; it's a bistro designed to serve good food while you converse with friends over a glass of vino in a very casual setting. Porter House does not disappoint. It's on a residential street corner with no parking exactly how it should be.  

The server was new but did an excellent job nonetheless. The chef owner came out of the kitchen and asked if we were satisfied.  Nothing like meeting the guy who cooks your food. It obvious he has been 'around' and knows his craft. 

One of the better more thought out menus in Nashville. Why? Too many Nashville menus are repetitive and safe. Tasty but boring. There were multiple items at Porter I could have easily ordered; a decision had to be made soooooooooo we're coming back! 

Decent affordable wine menu and full bar. The bread is the best I've had in Nashville by far.   I had the escargot which was soaked in butter and delish; wife had a salad that was fresh and the right temp. Not sure how anyone can say the prices are high??? Six New Zealand lamb chops for $22? They were amazing. Desserts are very good and not over filling including homemade chocolates.
UesAtrCdqntO6MvejjGDCg: Such a good selection of different sandwiches and wraps, and they are really flexible on customizations. If it's on the menu as a panini, you can get it as a wrap instead. The turkey avocado is SO good! We will definitely be coming back.
Delicious food and smoothie.  Fresh and friendly!  Worth a try if you're in the area, hidden gem.
Delicious coffee, great service, and awesome food! Breakfast is served all day. There is a wide variety of drinks from coffee to fruit juices. Highly recommend!
1iq3rdfVkcmaIIrq91jUbQ: Great service and great food. It was a little tricky to find as you have to go into the hotel. We initially walked past it. It's a large space, but somehow still cozy. We had the eggplant sticks as an appetizer which tasted like eggplant parmigiana without cheese. My sister and I also split the super food salad, which was very good as well. Would highly recommend. Wish we could have tried the breakfast brunch. Maybe next trip.

This review is solely for the drinks/live music.

My friend and I stayed in this hotel thinking it was a good deal out of all the other downtown locations.  We figured we would walk someplace to experience Nashville and get our live music fix.

After a long day of driving through Kentucky, stopping to visit friends, caves, and dinner along the way, we were tired.  The rainy evening wasn't enticing us out onto the streets either, so we figured we would just go down stairs instead.

And I'm glad we did.

We sat at a booth in the back and enjoyed drinks and the live music.  It was quiet and low-key - the prefect end to a long evening.  

Our waiter was great, and the bartenders did a fantastic job with the drinks. The feel of this place is relaxed and yet upscale at the same time.  I felt very comfortable there and I'm not typically a go-out-on-the-town kind of girl, so it was good for me.  And I didn't have to worry about walking back to the hotel late at night after a few drinks from some other place further away.

I would definitely come back!

Wow! What a great find in downtown Nashville. I happened upon this place by accident, and I'm glad I did! The District has a fabulous bartending staff! They are attentive and friendly, but most importantly they make BALLER drinks. The District had a great wine selection and when I was there, I heard some awesome musicians. Anyway, I think I spent like $65 or something on 4 or 5 drinks and an app. It wasn't bad, and I thought it was worth the price.
HKjB6JWmaKSho9CUjjYIYA: Just opened! They have a delicious, extensive menu and the service advisory to go with it. Prices are very fair and several ready made to go items all fresh and fair priced. Good product, service, vibe, and a cool little place to hang out and hide out from the office for a healthy shot!

The people here are so polite and helpful! They have great little healthy snack (breakfast sandwiches all day, wraps, chia pudding, smoothie bowls), awesome smoothies with bee pollen, and great juice! Definitely my hang out spot while in Nashville.

One of my favorite juice shops. All their drinks are high quality and have a consistently smooth texture. Staff is always friendly and it doesn't take long for them to whip up your drink. The main issue is that the drinks are a little too pricy.
RhOmfg3_ZDdL6gpKZSZmvg: Love Zaxby's! We eat here often and in many cases order our office staff lunch from here. Perfect for a quick lunch pick up or a weekend dinner pick up. Just love this place!!!

This was the first time I have been to Zaxby's. The food was really good! I got the Big Zax Snack. The chicken was really tender and the seasoned fries were awesome! If you are hungry for chicken and have given up on that other "traditional" fried chicken place, you should try Zaxby's. I highly recommend it. The restaurant was really nice inside and the service was great. There is plenty of parking and drive-thru service.

Talk about customer service.  I got a boneless wing that wasn't cooked in the middle.  They remade my entire meal (I ate most of it already),  apologized profusely and refunded our entire ticket.  Great example of turning a negative experience into an extremely positive one.  Thanks Oliver!
Q_fvlDrxL4lOeGnDIZOxJA: The man who owns this place is AMAZING!! So friendly and great customer service. The prices are good - less than $10 for most anything. Food is good and fresh - "build your own" style similar to Chipotle. They give you a punch card where 10th meal is free! They have a TV, plenty of tables and booths, and bomb sweet tea!

While heading out to eat last night and as i was pulling into a parking space across the road i noticed Guac acrose the road. My friend and i decided to try it out. For a first time visitor I was impressed by the fresh selections and excellent prices for the portions. It is all made the way you like it and if you order a platter it comes with fresh chips and an excellent cheese dip, plus a drink. I would say if you want something different  give them a try. I don't think you'll be disappointed.
I don't know how this is not a five-star restaurant. Absolutely amazing real text mex the staff is very polite the food is out of bounds I will definitely be back often
VifJl9gMQBbF9ZuNrOoRIQ: Generous portions and friendly service. The place could use a bit of cleaning but other than that, it was great food at a heck of a value. I had the lunch (all day) special and my wife had a chicken platter with rice, veggies, ect... Neither of us could finish ours. If you're in the mood for a good Gyro, don't let the building scare you off. One of the better ones I've had recently. I'll be back again.

Build your own pitas!

Aside from the  meat sitting in a pot and not freshly shaven off, the dryness outweighed the extra tsasiki sauce!  :-(  The cucumbers were a good addition and the pita pocket was stuffed with meat!

The fries reheated well too, the next day! Ha!  Put those bad boys in foil, and in the oven, preheated on 400° for about 5 - success!  

Will return because of flavor and ask for even more cucumber sauce!

In my opinion this place is an unknown gem on Murfreesboro Rd. The quality of the food is excellent, and very well priced. If you like Arab food and are in the area, you can't go wrong with this no-frills little gem.
c3Z0Hm-N2FCwpfLtVVzyLg: Was in the neighborHood yesterday and saw the review so figured I'd give it a try. Place is attached to a Oriental Market and it is a scary looking place, I wasn't quite sure it was open cause it has an abandoned building look. Went in and it was empty, no wait staff or anything, but there was a tv playing and looked open. Waitress came out and offered menu, was alone so I saw there were a few magazines and newspapers hanging around, grabbed the first magazine (maxim) wow, not your normal lunch time reading but, what the hell. Ordered the chicken bulgogi (?) and waited with apprehension. Maybe 20 minutes later, after hearing a good amount of pot clanking in the kitchen out came a wonderful tray of  korean goodness. The bulgogi was served with a assortment of delicious chimchi (7) in all and was fresh and delicious. The place is a little more than run down but the food was fresh and delicious and i didnt get sick, will try again.

I was surprised to see this place come out ahead of Seoul Garden, so I had to give it a try. Previous reviews are right - it looks like a dump before you walk in, and then no one is there. On top of that, there's no music playing. It was the quietest dinner I've ever had.

But who cares?! Korean food is the best thing that could ever happen to you!

Let's talk about the things this restaurant does well- 
1) Service - no one is there, so you get all of the attention 
2) Banchun - delicious, perfectly crisp, with some creative side dishes that I hadn't seen before
3) Dolsot Bibimbap- mixed mixed rice! My husband really enjoyed his serving of this traditional dish.
4) Kimchi Stew - This is the perfect winter soup. Spicy, flavorful, delicious. I predict some takeout orders of this soup in the future.

What doesn't go on the good list is the bulgogi. It wasn't bad- the marinade is delicious, but nothing can be the fresh grilled taste that comes from grilling at your table at Seoul Garden. If you're looking for Korean BBQ, go there, but if you just need a quiet night with some traditional korean food and delicious side dishes, save yourself the hassle and spend a nice time at Manna.

fw*88efkjsd3jwt5uj3owejdskjklj this place is goooooood.

I've only had one thing on the menu and it's one of the dishes  that I judge Korean restaurants by, kimchi jigae  It's basically a semi-spicy stew with kimchi, meat, and soft tofu.  The version here is quite possibly the best of the best in Nashville.  It's not overly spicy like other versions in town, but still has a kick.  The stew is chalk full of soft tofu, and it has a very faint taste of sesame oil.  What makes it is the thicker broth and the INCREDIBLY STINKY kimchi . Yeah, I said stinky.  If it's not stinky and funky, it's not good kimchi. 

The side dishes here are pretty solid.  We were served with a total of 6 sides.  The standout, of course, is the kimchi.  We also had what appeared to be seaweed or some sort of green veggie swimming in a very fishy sauce, it  tasted like sea water.  There was also sweet and sour squash, a very lightly dressed bok choy, bean sprouts, and something else that escapes me at the moment.

Don't except much from the place, you're going here for good Korean food, not for the ambiance and decor.  Don't be scared by the exterior, and definitely don't be scared by the nice old lady that serves and probably cooks the food also.  She speaks English and is super nice.  The inside is simple, the walls are bare, and there's not much else to look at.  

I'm told the kalbi (Korean short rib) is some of the best in town.  I'm sure I'll be back soon to work my way through the menu.  Stay tuned...
znrgu7fSTbFa9LVm__CV7A: I always feel at home at Twin Kegs. The bartenders are friendly and you can drink to your heart's content on a strict budget. Shuffleboard is a great way to pass the time, and it's the perfect place to sit and watch a game without being crowded by a million midtown-ers. My favorite dive-bar in town!

Just came in to try their cheeseburger & fries because I'd heard good things and live nearby. Nothing fancy, or creative, no eggs on top or anything, just a good burger. This is a smoky bar, so if you don't smoke you might want to get it to-go as I did. But the best thing I've had here is the chicken wings. Not buffalo, just barbecued wings that are quite tasty.

My mom always told me not to judge a book by its cover. And in the case of Twin Kegs, I'm so, so glad I didn't.

Nestled in between a Shoney's and another large building, it's very easy to blink and miss my beloved dive bar when you're heading east on Thompson and nearing the Nolensville intersection.

PRO: Cheap beer (seriously, $1-2), DELICIOUS hamburgers, grade A people watching, best karaoke in town.

CON: You'll have to do laundry and take a shower when you get home to get the smoke smell out.

RANDOM TIPS: Cover your drink with a coaster; the regulars like to throw pieces of paper.

Also, check their Facebook page. They post daily specials all the time!
UZ2pUkOSF-DVWHjeovMFoQ:
It is interesting that my perception of the Fiesta Azteca is radically different from the other yelpers on here. 

I had an awesome meal at Fiesta! Not only is this the only authentic Mexican restaurant this side of Nolensville Road but if you go for dinner and drinks after work you'll have no trouble parking in the parking lot behind the restaurant or getting a table right away. No doubt before a concert at Exit/In Fiesta Azteca is packed like a can of sardines.

The chips, oh! The chips. They strike an intimate balance between homemade corn chips and store bought. I'm not really sure which but who really cares as long as they are warm and can scoop up that tantalizing salsa. I ordered the vegetarian fajitas. Filled with broccoli, zucchini, onions, peppers, and tomatoes this steaming iron skillet was placed in front of me deliciously sizzling. The fiesta continued for two more meals after we left! We checked out for under $20 for two for dinner.
This place has a friendly staff and serves up good mexican / texmex at reasonable prices.

so good.

The best mexican I've had in over 5 years.

I pulled up to exit in and saw the line for the Butch Walker / Locksley show was out of control.  So I decided to pop in next door and have a bite.  I usually don't like anything on the menu cause I have to customize, BUT Azteca has the 'customer special' which is exactly what I order.  Meat beans cheese and rice.  lol
