HUGE daiquiri (strawberry to be exact).  And with tip it all came out to $22.  I couldnt even eat 1/4th of it all there was just so much damn food!! 

I really love this place, and am glad that I took a chance.  Perfect before a show :)
ND4gZUqNUWEuk8uZfjvGmg:
I wasn't sure about this food truck initially. Nothing on their menu jumped out at me, but I was hungry and didn't bring lunch today. I decided to try the veggie sandwich and a side of pasta salad. I'm so glad I did - both were fabulous!
I will definitely eat from this food truck again.

I love the food trucks of Nashville and I have seen steaming goat before but they're always surrounded by so many good smells and their truck is kind of contained...

Well today, they were surrounded by the smell of neighbor Electric Slider and I was not in a burger mood...actually I was feeling chilly and veggie-needing and Steaming Goat delivered.

Their menu sounded great but looked a little overwhelming - the portions coming out looked fantastic and the steam coming from the containers was enticing on a very cold November day. I eventually realized their side options were incredible and ordered two sides as a "main" - the Spinach Stuffing balls and the Harvest Hash - both boasting a "spinach aioli" which sounded odd but turned out to be this delicious gravy.

The wait time is not long at all, they take credit / debit per norm and feed superhero baby goats with their tips (it's adorable ;)). Where my lunch made me really happy was in pure flavor. Everything was so wonderfully hot and packed with flavor that I force-fed my partner in crime to the experience of yumminess.

Overall, a good value truck (two filling sides for $6!) with a friendly group running it, cute props on the counter, delicious food you really want to just nom down on and then share the joy of your food truck choice over the others and a super-clever menu that I suspect will update seasonally. Yum. I love it.
First time eating at this food truck - that I found by accident on my way in to work.  AMAZING Is the only way to describe it.
Ui4Kz-qTsPPDykv2cyDSwA:
Very decent for a Chinese restaurant.  Atmosphere is about what you would expect--but it's clean and generally quiet.  If you go for lunch (and you should) it's a fine place to take casual business associates.  

Service is good, and food is above average for this sort of place.  My favorite--and almost the only thing I get--is the cashew chicken.  Hot and sour soup is tasty too--and I'm really picky about my hot and sour.  

I will agree with Peter M--I always feel too full and a bit sick when I leave, but that's not unlike other Chinese restaurants.  And probably my own fault--my eyes are always bigger than my stomach.  You can get out of here for just about $10 or less, so price is right.

If you are in the mood for good Chinese food, August Moon might be your best shot. With the usual spread found at Chinese restaurants, I can tell you from experience that their food is much better than any other place I have tried around these parts. I don't think it's fantastic (again coming from California I have high standards), but when compared to some of the other atrocities that I have found in Nashville, August Moon is really is miles ahead of its competition. I usually head there for take-out but have also eaten in and it has been a great experience. The place is tidy, the service is fast, and best of all the food is really reasonable priced. My to-go orders are always ready on time, steaming hot, and quite tasty. If I had to pick my favorite dish, I would have to go with the General's Chicken and some good ol' steamed rice.
I love August Moon! The food is consistently good and the staff is so pleasant.
Fs9b3fjdASUJxe0tjVdBcw: Another well organized event by Matt Leff and company. It was fun to try several German beers on the lawn of the Parthenon and stroll around people-watching and gawking at rare old Volkswagens... Evocative of the grand Munich beer halls of Octoberfest, the main tent had plenty of seating and a surprisingly charming epically proportioned chandelier.  Gerst Hause provided the food stuffs, especially enjoyed the pretzel rolls.  There was a good band playing when we arrived, but we were a little disappointed when they packed up around five and no band replaced them - we were hoping to hear a polka!  Would love to see some traditional mulled wine - gluhwein - at next years event!

We headed to Nashville for the Blues/Predators game and being Yelpers, of course we checked the Nashville threads to see what was happening while there.  The Volksfest was one of the top events listed and since we were going to Centennial Park for the Parthenon, we wanted to visit this festival.

The area was set up with beer tents, other promotions and lots of volkswagons.  We had the option of buying tokens and steins right at the beginning of walking in.  We started at one end of the tents and tasted our way down while we heard music playing in the background.

We of course spotted the Yelp tent and stopped to talk to the super nice lady sitting behind it.  We talked a bit, checked in and got some great swag.

Proof that Yelp is awesome - thanks for the great suggestion of stopping by the festival!

Tony and I attended the Volksfest today at Centennial Park and we had a great time..I took a ton of photos with my cell phone, but somehow lost them during the download process to my cell phone but thank goodness I got a few on my camera before my batteries died..

We got there about 12:30..We parked and walked across the park enjoying the weather..not too cold and not too hot...just like good beer, just right!!!
We checked in with the ladies and showed our id to get our bracelets..went to the token tent to buy our beer mugs and tokens..$30 worth...we got one stein to share...and that way we had 
plenty of tokens to drink and chow on..

We went to the beer tents in the back..first the dark beer...darker the better..I lived in Germany and it is a required taste for Americans...who are not used to ...you might not appreciate a real dark robust stout of ale...but Tony was game...
Then we found a seat in the bier tent...to listen to the German Polka band...great music...like  she said the more you drink the better they sounded...and they were drinking right along with us..

So I went to the Essen area to get some grub...a Pretzel and soda for one token ($5) great deal...so I thought.. but to my disappointment..pretzelish like bread with mustard..not a true German Stoneground Mustard and not a Bavarian Pretzel...ugh...oh well..lets drink some more..this time...a lighter bier...a pilsner please...Vas..???? no pilsner??? She gave me a blondie...as she called it but it was Pilsner (light)...and it was good...cold...and bier...

Then we thought we would get a brat with sauerkraut on a German brochen...but NIX on that...the sausage was like a Johnsonville...the kraut was sort of a sweet and sourish mix...and the roll ....a normal bun...oh well...I guess I really need to go back to Germany for a real volksfest....
but we had fun....

We got a few more biers and left about 2:45-3..back to Nashville...leaving faux Germany behind until next year!!!
Great event....
mGPBv7-zGpGGIChYVhHwtQ: Really impressed and surprised. Fam and I are finishing dinner before heading over to the Predators game.

Just stayed at the Hilton Downtown and ate/drank frequently at the Sportsgrille.  Me and my colleague enjoyed it! We ate for lunch, dinner and late night. Had a big spread of the menu and loved it. Watch out for the nacho appetizer..it is HUGE for just the 1/2 order!! Delicious too. The dinner meal was excellent, fish taco's and the whole line up! Very pleasant staff that was very attentive and generous. I recommend it. Way better than the standard Hilton lobby fare! Big time fans of Nashville sports too! Go Sportsgrille Staff!!

Nachos are the best I've ever had, also half price with a ticket from game the titans game! Plenty of TVs and cool autographs everywhere. Also, they have a mural of the music city miracle on the wall!!!!
4stdd8Fw3P5NjYtKE1EJMg: Wings were awesome, definitely try the hot honey sauce. I loved them. I'll definitely be going back to get some more. Next time I'll get the jumbo wings because I accidentally ordered the smaller size not knowing how small they'd actually be.

I've been here multiple times now and it's always so delicious. I get the "Squirt burger combo with wings and fries" for $10.95. It's so delicious and filling. I've never had any problems with my food not being tasty or hot. 

The fries by themselves aren't the best thing, but if you're coming here for plain fries, you're missing out.

Also, curly makes everything fresh in house. Which is freakin awesome if you ask me.
Sometimes it takes a bit longer to get your food because of this, but I always find it worth it.
Just order ahead for that reason!

1st, they offer GREAT customer service, which is hard to come by these days (unfortunately)!
2nd, their wing flavors are homemade. And the few we had tried are delicious! 
3rd and lastly, I just finished THE BEST Red Velvet "Cake Shake" EVER!!
We are happy to have found a new spot in Antioch!
IVFnW_Cdy6nUfnkhSF89yw: First time going....sub was excellent! Delicious and filling....I had a regular sized Kahuna, no jalapeños for me, but it was so good. The transaction was smooth and easy and the guy that took my order was pleasant. It was a really great first time experience! We will be returning!
Great place, friendly staff that are extremely helpful. Pretty clean too for a sub shop as well..

Subs made right in front of you! I love subs and I appreciate all the different types of subs out there. I never tried this place before but I got addicted to Firehouse, but this place is definitely delicious as well. Great sizes and fresh toppings. You can even order online for ease and fast pick up.
Awgu2qjOq17hIcHMMdYxCg: Wait was longer than I would have liked. But I was thoroughly impressed with the Sirloin Steak option.

Cheap, tasty and delivered on time.  Easy online ordering via grubhub.  $10 got me steak tips (a little extra for grilled onions) a baked potato, salad and bread sticks.  The steak tips were actually very tasty and cooked to a perfect medium rare.  Was it the best meal ever?  No.  But it was well worth the price and I'd order it again.

One of my favorites!! I've loved their pizza and calzones for years! We just ordered their hot spinach dip, and were so sad when we realized we had eaten the last bite. Ordering out or dining in, these fellas come through.
TplFWsl1xV9PYU38N7_ZDQ: What a clean modern white space with colorful graffiti art that smacks of Japanamation! So cool ... As much as I love barn wood, we have a lot if that decor in Nashville .  The decor is only to be outdone by the delicious Otaku South ramen.... And appetizers ... And sake.  But in all seriousness, it's nice to have a bowl of ramen that doesn't rely on salt as the main seasoning, but rather days of boiling the bones to get a rich, savory stock.  For me, that makes the difference between this ramen place and any other Nashville ramen place.  (PS ... They have vegetarian ramen  and gluten free noodles!) And when Otaku South isn't serving ramen, Pop (the space) will be hosting other pop up restaurants and events...   So, cheers to some delicious variety coming to Nashville!

After posting the review, pop owners put me in touch with the folks who presented the dinner. They have sinned opened 51st Kitchen. Even though much time  passed they stayed in touch on their own and offered us a tasting. The experience was lovely so I'm updating review based on this awesome service.

Popup dinners are all the rage, and POP Nashville is a great space for just such a thing.  I had the privilege of attending a dinner here recently and was very impressed with the whole setup.  It's off in East Nashville, and admittedly I drove right by it because its in a small strip mall on the top floor...with brightly colored lines leading you to the entrance (nice touch!).  The inside is very simple...white walls, wooden tables and chairs, some art here and there but not much.  I would be very easily decorated to whatever the event would require.  It's small, simple, but perfectly set up for a large dinner.  The staff we had were wonderful, and the food delicious.  I would happily come to this space again for any popup meal offered.
YyibusgZiK47Gr33c0yipw:
Casa is a favorite when we're in town visiting friends. Order the fajitas. The meat and veggies are thinly sliced and cooked to perfection. The portions are hearty and you get a separate plate filled with all of the toppings. I didn't like fajitas until i was introduced to the casa version. Mmmm...

The service is friendly. It's southern hospitality with affordable, delicious food.

Just finished a half marathon so probably anything tastes amazing right now. But this Mexican place was recommended by my hotel (La Quinta). The people are friendly. The service is great and the food is smoking hot. Id definitely recommend to friends and return to eat here if I run that race again.

After Seafood I would have to say that Mexican Foods comes in 2nd.  I've eaten at the Elm Hill Pike location but decided it is best for my area to eat local.  My number 1 test for a Mexican Restaurant is how good their Chili Rellenos are.  This location passed with one minor glick (and I find this at most of the others) the Poblano Pepper had not been seeded.  The main problem with that is the seeds are so hot and I am unable to enjoy the entire Chili Releno.  Of course this is something that I'm used to.

From the road this place looks busy day and night so it must be a favorite of many.  I definitely go back and check out their Guacamole and Enchiladas.
B1uf_53BMDEjhCCyjjmaKg: Every time I get a slice here I'm tempted to make drastic statements about Pizza Perfect's greatness.  But with something like pizza, it's hard to commit...it's difficult to use words like "favorite" or "best ever".  While I may get railed by some pizza lovers out there, what makes great pizza is a combination of science and subjectivity.  We all have our preferred styles and cooking methods.  If I can take such a leap, I can't say I've had better pies anywhere else.  Maybe it's because Pizza Perfect reminds me of the pies I used to eat at a local joint growing up, or maybe I've been won over by the 2 for 1 slices on Wednesday with $5 dollar pitchers and 2 for 1 bottles of Sierra Nevada (2 bottles for 3.75.  THREE SEVENTY FIVE!!!!).  While those things are surely wonderful, I still say it's the tasty foldable slices that keep me coming back.  It starts with the dough, and if you like you can purchase fresh dough balls to make your own pies at home.  So good.  So why no 5 stars?  I can't commit.  But maybe if they had more TVs and NFL Sunday Ticket.  Maybe then.
Standard pizza place. I got a anti pasta salad and cheese sticks as takeout. Both were good. Appears to be a popular place in the area that is kid friendly. Lots of families.

I am a pizza fanatic and this is The. Best. Pizza. Ever. The sauce is the perfect combination of salt and sweet. If you like a zestier sauce I recommend trying a side of the sub and calzone sauce to dunk your crust in. YUM. They also got these new larger pepperonis which are the bomb. With that being said, I have tried most of the popular joints around Nashville and have stayed loyal to this one. It's authentic and family owned. Love you Pizza Perfect!
1fuOT8Q1c4jZ1ywNAGPq5w:
Your basic corner store. They have all the essentials. Nothing fancy, imported or organic but much more convient and relaxed than Kroger. 
They have a wide selection frozen and canned foods, an adequate dairy cooler and meats and produce for any southern style dish. 
If you live near to five points its a great spot to get basic pantry and cleaning items and paired with a stop at The Turnip Truck (and woodland wine lol) I can get all I need without even getting in my car!

Bill Martin's is a basic, mediocre neighborhood grocery store - no veggie burgers or red bell peppers here, just the essentials. The produce section is especially limited, but you can get some really good deals ($0.99/lb roma tomatoes and green peppers for about half what you'd pay at Harris Teeter), and the items I bought were all perfectly fine.  

If there are two doors, you usually use the one on your right-hand side, so the people coming out can exit to your left, right?  Well it's the reverse here, and the staff got a good laugh out of watching me go in and out of the "wrong" door.  But at least they were way friendlier than the robotic and/or hostile service I've come to expect at corporate supermarkets.  

Although Bill Martin's smelled a little funny, it looked pretty clean and seemed perfectly safe on the afternoon I stopped in - middle schoolers buying soda, moms picking up a few things on their way home, etc.

Locally owned and operated place, if your in a pickle and you forgot something last minute in the way of food, toiletries, or cleaners in a pinch you are covered. The store is a institution that stands as a paradox to the big box stores and grocery chains. A free standing grocery store in the middle of a increasingly growing hipster populous of my neighborhood. Bill Martin's very existence to the local folk who have relied on this store for decades is paramount to the older community of East Nashville. I find it quit amazing that regardless of the price of property around this institution it stays to form and refuses to buckle to the outside investors who are waiting to pounce on this landmark of what East Nashville is and what it was not so long ago. I think its great that this owner has thrown to finger to the unscrupulous developers who would love to throw up skinny houses and trick folks out of their modest homes to turn a buck. Respect the building, the jobs it creates, and the service it provides to original real East Nashville Folks as well as celebrate the ole southern culture.
zfsteieoV3_R6ZbIm6XYUA:
First time eating here so can't give it 5 stars yet....

Had fish burrito and like my fellow yelper Jason stated IT WAS PHENOMENAL! no problems with freshness of any of the ingredients i requested and customer service was really good! Clean location with outside seating...I'm somewhat of a fan and kinda sad that i just now gave this place a chance..

will return!

The husbandry unit and I have stopped here a few times on Nolensville jaunts. This location is not our first for Blue Coast, but definitely a better experience overall. 

There are always a few diners, but we never have to wait. From the moment we walk in the door, the staff is SUPER friendly. I'm a picky eater, and they have always been more than patient with my indecisive burrito moments.

For the Baja Burrito fans, this place is eerily similar, without the crowds or the wait. (Don't misread me here, I love Baja as much as the next guy. Just sayin'...)

If the weather permits, we like to sit on the front patio, and on occasion, a staff member will walk outside to ask if we need a drink refill, etc. 

My only true gripe is the parking. It's amazing how this area looks so quiet, but there are few places to park. I assume it's because of all the residential units nearby. With other businesses slated to open up in this strip, it will be interesting to see where parking goes from here.
Great quick and casual spot. Better local than the chipotle, etc. service was great. A little skimpy on the meat but ill be back.
QTBdaWGptIqkIvh_LfVxnw:
This Arby's runs like a Swiss watch at lunchtime! Great service, can-do attitude of managers & team. Food is Arby's menu, done well, served quickly but to order. Clean, super friendly. 

Not sure at other times, but lunchtime M-F, this place is the best Arby's. Period!  Best Fast Food place near airport, if you like their menu.
100% real beef! This Arby's is fantastic and I love America! Vote Ben Carson in 2016!

Great place when you are on your lunch break! They are fast and super friendly. They never seemed to be overwhelmed. They are always sure to great and speak with each customer. Also it's clean inside as well.
uEs_RuMTIVRMc-tAaXqk-A:
The minute we walked in the door, we were warmly greeted by the owner who makes the pizzas. He explained the menu to us and the different options we had. We ordered the Hawaiian pizza and he made it within 5 minutes. Upon check out, we signed up for the free Mama Dough Club (I think is what it's called) and we got a free 2 liter! With the club, for every 6 visits, you get a free dessert: chocolate chip cookie dough:)
And the best part...the pizza was delicious!! It took about 13 minutes to cook a large. I personally liked the crust a lot. Even the original crust (instead of the thin) is pretty thin but satisfying! 
We will DEFINITELY be going back!! Exceptional customer service and very tasty pizza!!!

I live nearby, and am so happy to see this come into my area. Since we stopped by the first time, it's become a weekly dinner choice for us. It's easy, delicious, and the man who owns the place is so sweet. We will definitely continue to support them..and I hope others do as well. A great new local business to have in our community!

This pizza is great! The toppings are fresh and the crust is awesome. We've been here about four times in the last few months and have always been extremely happy with our finished pie. This is perfect place to stop by for a quick dinner option that will not disappoint. The owner is also super friendly!
RuGDc85N6CmuH6yUJbL-fA: We went back to Topanaris again based on some of the updated reviews.  Since we'd been last time, they've completely revamped their business model, offering full breakfast service (brunch on Sundays) as well as a weekday lunch buffet.  As iterated in my previous review, the food is excellent with a fair selection of items available.  Our only disappointment was that there was only curry chicken available, with no jerk chicken (we love Jamaicaway's jerk chicken) available.  Since this was our first try at their lunch buffet, I don't know how much the selection changes, but they had a wonderful sampling of veggie dishes with the tofu callaloo, sweet potatoes with pineapple, collard greens, red beans with peas, and green beans.  The buffet is interesting in that it is currently $9.95 for everything you can fit on 1 plate.  Any subsequent plate is $5.  We managed to eat quite our fill on a single plate, so no complaints there.  All in all, we got great food (just like last time) at a much more reasonable price.  Fantastic!

Love this place! I'm an organic gardener and West Indian girl from Florida and this spot is a dream come true. The jerk burger is phenomenal and I am so happy there's finally a reasonably priced place committed to serving organic/wild caught/grass fed/local healthy food near Vandy! Lunch buffet is only $10 and delish.

Organic, healthy! Delicious! Great vegetarian options! 
The lunch buffet had good option for everyone only for $11.95
It's hidden on Hayes street but right around the corner off West End! 
Will recommend this place to everyone.
YjK2fJPXKNo2KPbfJkwEXA:
Small "hole in the wall" authentic Mexican restaurant. My boyfriend and I decided to eat here after getting a certificate for food from our mobile carrier. We were sketched out by the area but after going inside, we were greeted by cheerful staff and even had a gentleman playing his guitar and singing to us. The place is very small. So small they are not permitted to have a liquor license, but can serve beer. Menus are in Spanish, but they also have English versions on hand if you ask.

We started dinner off with the homemade tortilla chips with green salsa. I had chicken enchiladas suizas, while my boyfriend had the lamb flautas. We finished dinner with churros for dessert which were also really good.

The food was OUTSTANDING. Very, very, authentic which means it was a little bit spicier than I am used to but it wasn't anything some extra sour cream didn't take care of. I am totally into supporting small businesses, so I knew I had to leave them a good review! Don't let the outside or the area scare you into eating here, it really is delicious!

Good prices and food, limited menu. We went on a Thursday, and they didn't have any horchata, flan, or queso. What we did have was brought out fast, hot, and was delicious. No, the staff doesn't speak any English, but are very nice.

Hidden gem on nolensville rd. looks a little questionable on the outside and not the best part of town but once you decide to try it you won't regret it. Green Salsa is awesome! I tried the caldo de res and my girlfriend tried te enchiladas estilo Guanajuato which were awesome! Come check this ma and pa place out!
Xz97EH7xunUoRX0Vp3iKug:
Before reading this review please understand that me and a solid group dined here every Friday night for almost two year's at the end of my college experience.  I therefore can admit that my opinion of the restaurant is inflated.

The only two items I can truly gush over are the frozen margaritas and #41.  The margaritas are tasty, powerful, and reasonably priced.  The #41 is the fajita quesadilla and is pure cheesy goodness.

Other than those two items the reason for my 4 stars is the atmosphere and service.  Very friendly and welcoming, they love to make one feel like part of the family.

Mmm mmmm! Their chips and salsa food is delicious. Can't wait to go back! Def recommend this place if your out on the town in Nashville and your craving some Mexican food. The prices are pretty affordable and the serving sizes are great!

All I can say is location, location, location.  I went here with a group of friends on a Friday night for some margaritas and mexican food.  Anticipating that the place would be too packed for 10 of us just to walk in, we made a reservation.  We were running late and called the restaurant to let them know that we wouldn't be on time and they said it would be no problem that they would hold the table for us.  When we arrived we realized that there our reservations were really unnecessary because there was more than one empty table.  The food was your typical Mexican fare.  I ordered a quesadilla with spinach which was seriously ONLY spinach no queso (i don't know if this is normal as I expected cheese with some spinach, like a chicken quesadilla) which for me was just wayyy too much spinach.  However, I sent it  back asking for a plain cheese quesadilla and they switched it out without charging me for a new  order.  The salsa was adequate but I can rave about their margaritas.  The pitchers come cheap and the tequila is added liberally.  A perfect place for a group wanting for a good place to start out the night before heading downtown.
ZNMLzDtpAr-EBqc5hITIaQ: Love this place! We visit the original NYC location and this new Nashville location taste the same. My son was super excited as he loves the rice and chicken. Can't wait to go back.
this place is awesome! amazing service and KILLER falafel. took extra special care of a couple of vegans. perfect lunch!

Probably my favorite new place in Bellevue for a quick bite. Wraps are great but I always opt for the platters which are a great value, the meat and vegetables are always fresh and well prepared. Friendly employees and ownership and immaculately clean. Great quick stop before or after a movie.
b_jg4hexYmhJoo8c4P6MoA: Pretty good food. Service wasn't the best but we had a large party. Sangrias there are the best ever! Looks like a hole in the wall place is the bad aide of town but was pleasantly surprised and will go back
Traveling and found this Hole in a Wall.  Great food and service.  Friendly staff and authentic feel. Sweet tea was BOOM!!!

Came in a Saturday about 8 pm. Seated quickly for a party of 6 and immediately got chips and salsa. Drinks and order was quick. Waiter was very sweet and tentative. Food came out got and lots of it! Have lunch for the next day. Rice was moist and beans had tons of flavor. Carnitas was so good! Definitely would return if in Nashville again.
XKj07H-SUl1_fuvAv3ncHQ: I ordered the Alexandra plate: took over an hour and was a little cold when it arrived, but WOW is this good fresh food! Will order again!

This is my new favorite place in Nashville. When the chef (who also happens to be the owner) told me the ingredients were fresh, he wasn't kidding. The spices were just right, giving that awesome kick to a chicken gyro that I've been craving for months, and he was detailed in our order making sure that it was absolutely perfect from start to finish. Totally going back, and you should go too!

The food was pretty good. I had the shawarma plate (shawarma on a bed of rice with salad and a whole pita bread on the side). Everything tasted fresh, especially the salad. That was my biggest takeaway; wasn't expecting such fresh ingredients from a gas station food truck. Looks can be deceiving. Rice was very tasty. The shawarma was good, but just a little greasy for me. The guys who run the place are very nice and seem to care a lot about making good quality food. It was prepared quick, only took about ten minutes. They have a lot of options on their menu, so I'm excited to try the other stuff on it. Overall, good food with good service. Go try it!
D55f0Ahi5i3P_UUBWXWnFA: This place is literally a life saver. Fresh veggie and fruit juice cold pressed and wonderful reasonably priced healthy meal and snack items as well.  Did I mention that the people who work there are all helpful and friendly...and it's so clean . I am literally in love with this place. When  feeling like I need a super healthy meal to reset my body this is my place. Favorite items. The orange ya glad juice is amazing as are the lettuce wraps with house made peanut sauce .

It's really pretty inside and the employees are super nice. However, my smoothie tasted a tad bit sugary and powdery. Also, no outdoor seating area, which is a bummer. Still, this is a good spot for post-Percy Warner victuals.

I love going to the Juice Bar in BelleMeade. I've tried several smoothies and they are all good but my favorite is the Green one. I also love their soups! I go out of my way to get there because I like it so much. Service is excellent.
luOnXyYlRJUIPEC4PnaO9w: I don't know what others are talking about when they say they weren't pleased with their experience.  I had lunch here today, and it was fabulous!  I had the half rotisserie chicken, greens, and slaw.  Everything was awesome!  The chicken was tender and juicy with a yummy crunchy skin.  You could even see the real herbs used to season it.  I am suprised that no one has commented on the fact that Stone House is a gluten free friendly restaurant.  Even their cornbread is gluten free!  Since they make all thier sides home made, there is no risk of additives and crap that you get when going to a "chain".  The service was great, and they were more than happy to answer any of my questions about ingredients.  In fact, pretty much everything except the sandwiches and mac-n-cheese is gluten free.  My new favorite BBQ place!

This is going to be my go-to bbq place from here on out. I was so pleased to find this place! I have been getting my bbq fix at Hog Heaven, Whitt's and the like. This place has it all though- great meat, variety of sides and indoor/outdoor seating. It's a clean, pleasant place to come bring friends and family. The baby back ribs and the beef brisket were my absolute favorites. Ribs were full of fall off the bone meat and the brisket was perfectly cooked. The pulled pork was alright, but not as good as the other two meats. Of the sides, the potato salad was terrific (sour cream more than mayo), the slaw was really fresh, the mac and cheese creamy. The beans were too sweet for me, though. The sweet potato mashed was good- not too heavy. My husband finished off the cornbread in an instant, so it must have been good. The sweet tea topped it all off. Loved the service, environment and the food!

Full disclosure: I do have a slight professional connection to this restaurant, but that has nothing to do with my opinion on the food. I also grew up in a very small Tennessee town that has zero Walmarts and McDonald's but three hometown barbecue joints, so I feel like I'm a BBQ expert, and until now I had been very unimpressed by Nashville's offering in comparison to its small-town counterparts. Stone House Q still isn't quite as, um, rustic, but they do have to lure in the city folk.

The butts, as they say, are righteous. I enjoyed the juicy pulled pork with the red sauce, which was the perfect combo of sweet and tangy, though I'm still a little unsure of the chicken with white sauce that I sampled (I prefer my BBQ sauce tomato-based, no vinegar, mayo or this Alabamian white stuff). The potato salad was incredible (that's where the mayo belongs), and the mac and cheese was unbelievable... creamy and thick, eggy noodles. Delicious. The cornbread -- gluten-free for you celiacs out there -- tasted amazing, very crumbly and really good to sop up extra sauce.

My only complaint is the beans... they were a little too thick and hearty for my palate, more like a baked bean casserole as opposed to the soupy beans I'm used to in my little hometown. The only other complaint was the dessert... the apple crisp was hands-down delicious, not too sweet at all, but the crust (crisp?) was too hard to cut with the plastic forks -- which makes it a little awkward when sharing a dessert with a friend.

The atmosphere is nice, though it was a little hard to find if you're one of those people like me who rarely ventures THAT far west... but I absolutely loved the Hatch Show art on the walls. Good food, good ambience, good restaurant. But the ultimate verdict will be determined by what my dad (a Texan-turned-Tennessean) has to say about this 'cue.
ProvWPskpyrPOryqVT64Gg:
I will definitely be back. The food was really good - flavorful, good texture... There was a nice, full menu. And the staff was very friendly (though a bit hard to understand - a pretty thick accent, which makes me like this place even more!). 
I didn't give 5 stars because I didn't leave thinking "I must tell all my friends about this", but it's a great option for Greek food in Nashville!

I've seen the sign from the road and finally stopped in. I should have stopped sooner! The food was excellent. They asked if I wanted peppers and onions in my rice. I did. I must say it was the best rice I've gotten around town. Portions were great. Stop in and support this wonderful addition to Bellevue

Love this fast casual Bellevue location. All the usual suspects on the menu (gyros made with lamb or chicken, rice, pita) but the flavor, freshness and friendliness are exceptional. I've been four times and the quality has always been on point. This spot is in my regular rotation.
jnTnR_eN7hGc7GGzGdUt9Q:
Best food truck in Nashville!! 
The chicken & waffles are absolutely delicious! 
You can definitely taste that it's made from scratch and with a lot of love!
I try to get to The Waffle Boss  at least once a week!

We were having a bad day in Nashville.  The sky had opened up and and attacked us with water.  Drenched, we hurried to meet up with the comedy tour bus we had picked as our day's prime activity.  The bus did not show at the appointed hour.  We waited and waited only to eventually learn that the tour bus we were waiting for had collided with a motorcycle parade [true story] and that our tour was cancelled.  Distraught, we wandered around, wondering what we could do with our day.  We ended up taking a short and uninteresting horse drawn carriage ride with a driver who immediately copped an attitude with us as soon as we said we were from California and who, as an added bonus, did manage to convince us that we were about to spend the rest of our vacation with food poisoning due to the hot dog we bought off the street while we were hurrying to meet the tour we didn't get to take.  Disappointed and dejected (and waiting to become powerfully ill) we set out to see the Parthenon.  

In the vicinity of Parthenon, we stumbled on a gathering of food trucks.  Having lived in LA in the not too distant past, where the food truck scene is prime, we pulled over and went to investigate.  We sampled a couple of food trucks, most of which were forgettable and forgotten, and then we tried the chicken and waffles from The Waffle Boss.  We were instantly tickled by the variety of waffles and the guy taking our order was so friendly.  We became cautiously optimistic.  My boyfriend ordered the chicken and regular waffle while I ordered the chicken and red velvet waffle.  We had to wait 20 minutes or so, but man, it was so worth it.  The chicken (which came in strip form) was perfectly cooked and juicy with a crunchy delicious coating seasoned in such a way as to give a little flavor punch and perfectly offset the flavor of the waffle.  The red velvet waffle was a beautiful crimson color, so enticing to the eye that even if I was not a fan of chicken and waffles I would have been thrilled to eat it. [I confess to snapping a couple of pictures so I could elicit the proper amount of envy when I returned to Cali].   It was fluffy, dense (but not overly so) and flavorful.  Sweet, but not too sweet.  Combined, the chicken and red velvet waffle were genius.  Not since Roscoe's in LA had we enjoyed chicken and waffles this much (and that is saying something).   Our day was instantly saved.  For a moment we kind of wished we lived in Nashville. 

Amazing work Waffle Boss!  Cook on!

Can I give 6 stars? I LOVE this food truck. Chicken and waffles have become a great trend lately but I swear, nobody does it the way Waffle Boss does. Living up to their name, WB delivers made-to-order original, birthday cake, red velvet, and other seasonal waffles. The chicken is hot, fresh, cooked with the perfect balance of crunch and tenderness, and immensely flavorful. It was served with a cream cheese frosting and syrup. I seriously finished every bite.

I can't wait to go again!!
1hjKZ2GhYVg4mwPirC_Myw:
What can I say about LaPaz?  I love Dennis and his family.  The fact that they carry Lotus Energy Drink is a MAJOR plus!  Love Chris - Beverage Manager.  That guy can make any drink tastes good!

Best fish taco in town. Love the guacamole.  I like salt, so I have to admit that I have to add salt to my food.  I have to be honest, every time I come here, I order the Fish taco, so other than that and the drinks - I may not be the best person to ask about the rest of the food.

However, the atmosphere and the decor is really nice.  It's definitely a different type of Mexican restaurant vibe.  More on the Modern American / Mexican side.

Give it a try, price is great.  Service is great.  Location is great.  Drinks are great and the Fish Tacos are awesome!

I know this is a chain in the Southeast, but it's the only one in Tennessee, so I consider it a local favorite.  It's one of my favorite low-key restaurants anyway, and has a fantastic patio (open only in the spring and summer).  

They have the best green salsa of any restaurant I've ever been to--hands down!  Their red salsa is good too--spicy!  You get them both for free along with unlimited baskets of house made chips.  I generally fill up on those well before my meal gets to the table!  Oh, and you must order a bowl of the queso.  I have never been there without it!

As for other food on the menu, I enjoy their quesadillas--if you are not super hungry, ask for the half order.  The Arizona is delightfully different--chicken, sun-dried tomatoes, and spinach with cheeses of course.  A lot of my friends love the fajita plates, which I have never ordered, but smell amazing.  

Great place for groups, and good for lunch or dinner.  Plenty of room at the full-service bar as well.

I love, love, love this place. What a great place to sit out on the patio and enjoy a nice cold drink while laughing with friends! I don't think anyone can beat their quesadilla's! I'm looking forward to the next working women's Wednesday!
B0Eb6aEgVIOdo7Jt-d0omA:
Reviewing for the amazing experience I had with your employee, Tracy. She was so kind and friendly. Definitely made my morning!

Thanks, Tracy!!

Omgoodness, went into this location not expecting much more than a sammage. But not that evening. My seed and I was greated by a lady with a smile on her face and seemed just as hapi to be @ work. LaShonda answered a few questions didn't make us feel rushed accommodated our needs and made the sandwiches to perfection. Hmmm is she working today?????
VeQDoCQmur6VG8TMvyzYUQ:
I absolutely love this gym.  The staff is friendly, knowledgeable, and a true joy to work with & be around (from the initial tour, to billing, to everyday questions, you name it.)  They offer a wide array of group classes with fun instructors, to which none of the classes are ever 100% full, which I love because I get my own space and don't feel "stared at."  The cardio area is large with a nice selection of equipment; I've never had to wait on a machine either, which is a huge plus for me.  I'm not one to really socialize while working out unless I specifically come with someone to workout with... that said, on the floor, everyone really respects the space of those around them but doesn't make the environment feel cold or unwelcoming.

I absolutely love how on Monday evenings and Saturday mornings a local farmers market sets up in the lobby with luscious greens, farm fresh eggs and fresh seasonal fruit/veggies.  Talk about encouragement and a friendly reminder to be healthy from within, too!  They also have a health-conscious chef delivering homemade soups, fresh juices and other meals in their cafe to take with you (which comes in handy those nights I'm working out late or I'm on the go with no time to cook.)  I have to give them huge props in this area on standing out as their own entity in the vicinity.

Overall, the environment is great, the facility is clean and I would highly recommend this gym over any other one in our area.  My only suggestion to the club would be for the child care: to segregate the children by age groups (i.e. separating the older children from the younger toddlers/babies.)  Oh, and let me add... the pool is salt water :)

Boost FitClub is the total package! Their trainers are very knowledgeable in every aspect when it comes to exercise, nutrition, and getting fit. Every staff member is super friendly. I have made Boost part of my 'lifestyle' over the past 7 years and couldn't be happier with the transformation I have seen and felt in myself. The best part of my workout is the end-when I reward myself with a delicious protein packed smoothie from the cafe. No matter what kind of a day I have had, I leave Boost feeling awesome! Thank you Boost FitClub for contributing to my awesomeness:)

The old facility was nice and the location was GREAT.  Personally, I don't care much for socializing at the gym.  Everyone was very good about respecting my privacy, but I also got the impression that it was a very warm and friendly environment.  I like this over the aggressive tones that I sometimes get from other gyms (maybe I'm paranoid).  In the fall of 2013, Boost announced a move and MASSIVE facility upgrade.  I saw the plans and new that this was going to be very nice.  I immediately renewed my contract for the next 2 years to lock in my rate.  I moved to the new facility and have been enjoying watching everything come together as I work out.

Sadly, I was informed in early March 2014 that my job would be sending me out of town for the next 10-12 months.  I remembered checking out the Boost reviews and DREADED trying to close my contract.  Technically, I am not changing addresses (I'll be living out of hotels) and I know that it is not permanent.  I read the agreement and I did not feel like I had good argument other than it was a special situation.  I started the cancellation process online and tried to make sure that my situation was understood. I wanted to make sure that I was polite and understanding in the fairness of the situation for both parties.

The GOOD:  Joey and Jon took great care of me.  As soon as I got them all of the facts and had a face-to-face, the problem was solved.  I am not happy to be leaving MY GYM, but I am happy that they made the situation much easier.

The BAD:  It was a bit difficult to get ahold of Joey to start (I did not start by putting in my 30day online notice).  Obviously, it was during their huge renovation AND during the spring membership/renewal rush.  The day that I had my face-to-face, I watched him take back-to-back appointments for a good 90 minutes during my workout before I approached him. It is a small business going through a busy time, no big deal.

Conclusion:  This place is great and the other reviews support that (unless you plan on dropping your contract).  I feel like I would have been writing a very different review if I hadn't been polite.  I had a contract with Boost that I had to break.  I pleaded my case and asked for understanding.  Because of that, I am leaving on good terms and can't wait to tour the finished facility when I come back!
tKRWRAeEI-epyxAixU2vHg:
With any chain restaurant, it seems a bit redundant to review the food and such, but I will say though that the food was delicious, especially considering we came in at an off time. The service and everything can be incredibly hit or miss coming in when it's totally dead like that, but they did not disappoint.

We had Sara, who was incredibly friendly and provided awesome service. The food was hot and fresh, and everything came out pretty quickly. She was also great in checking back just enough, but didn't hover or bug us. Awesome.

I really like this BWW! The food is exactly what you would expect at any other BWW and I've never had any issues with my food coming to me incorrectly or cold. The staff is extremely kind and they really try to go above and beyond to make your dining experience pleasant. 

If you're in the area and craving wings, be sure to stop by!
Eh, the food is good and it's clean. The service has been slow the last few times I've been there, but the quality and upkeep has been consistent. I'll keep going back
clp4nxz206DWqtxf8HtbZw:
The ultimate in greasy spoons, Waffle Houses are the perfect place to wait out a hangover the morning after a wild night out, or just eat some amazingly yummy, bad-for-you food.  If you go in expecting this, you will never be disappointed.  Our waitresses were friendly and very understanding when I walked in with two small children and bags under eyes.  We had just rolled it town the day before and I had been up most of the night, trying to convince them to sleep in strange beds.  I don't go to Waffle Houses often, but when I do I appreciate their niche.

The only thing I would warn is that their cheese is always slices of American.  If you don't like that, dot get cheese on anything.  Everything else is exactly what you think it is.

Fantastic!!! Traveled down from Buffalo and this was close to our hotel. The food taste like it came right out of your grandma's kitchen, and the service is as sweet as their sweet ice tea. Hashbrown bowls we're to die for and the bacon is fresh and crispy.

Busy busy night!!!! Amanda was amazing! It was a hectic night at the Waffle House tonight. We left another location because of issues there, and we were thankful to get Amanda to help us. She was thoughtful, knowledgeable, and a great personality!
0B6JY3Ky8iB52-rPooB2Tw: Wheezy and Victoria were so so great. We came in to watch a game and they were so so nice and accommodating. They kept us updated and were able to just us a table quickly. Great place to watch a game with awesome food and wide beer selection. Highly recommend! Thanks neighbors!! We'll be back

Our new favorite neighborhood bar with great food and drinks. We had the barbecue, potato salad, and a club sandwich. The bushwackers are the best in town! The servers were all really friendly and attentive and we enjoyed getting to know the owners while we were there too. We will definitely be back!

The dining room is clean, sleek, and has plenty of TVs and cool memorabilia. The dry rub wings are excellent and my burger and fries were delicious. The reason why I give 4 instead of 5 stars is the slow pace of service. Since this place is sort of new, I understand, but let's hope this isn't a trend going forward. What a nice place to grab a beer and easy, tasty bar fare after a long day.
yI-yhgK0BiplyyJ2me83nA: me and the old lady enjoyed this place very much. the decor,service and  food was great. we found this little place by accident.prices are reasonable.we ate slow and enjoyed the atmosphere.  i wish other dealerships would incorporate grills/cafes  into their establishment.

I've eaten at Boswell's plenty of times and have not had a bad experience yet.  The burger(sportster custom) is served with plenty of lettuce, tomato and onion with cheese an is flavorful and delicious. I like the fries also but if you get them to go, they will probably be soggy by the time you get to your destination as they serve to go orders in Styrofoam.  I've also gotten the onion rings and you get a great portion but like above, if to go, will be soggy and limp.  I would score the philly the lowest on their menu but it is a biker cafe, not a cheese steak joint.  The Vrod is one of my favorites, a bowl of chili and a grilled cheese sandwich.  The chili is a full bowl and really a quite good bowl of chili and then you get the grilled cheese with it which you can have on either wheat or sour dough, I prefer sour dough.  If you go by on ANY day at lunch, be prepared to wait and if you call in, be prepared to wait at least 30 min.  All in all great food at a reasonable price.

Sometimes you can just be surprised by a place:  well I was surprised by a Harley Davidson shop having an all star lunch.  Little did I know this location had a rockin place to eat inside.

My coworker took me in here for a little break yesterday and I was more than happy with my meal.  They had a good selection of sandwiches, burgers, and salads to make everyone pretty happy.  I opted for just a salad and a potato, but as a partial veg head, let me tell you, CRISP lettuce makes the salad.  I was very happy with the quality of the salad.  My coworker had a delicious looking salmon sammy with these too die for onion rings.

I wish I had room for dessert because the case and a pretty good looking mango mousse cake and a bread pudding, but I was stuffed.

Keep this place in mind if you are hungry, because you can feed your stomach and your wildest motorcycle driving fantasies!  (Yes, I did look at a Harley or two in there as well!)
laJ4SjVAGgmUDfw62eFC_w: Really great foodie dive. The Abyssinia special is your best bet. It is essentially a sampler platter with plenty for 2. We still filled a to go box. Add on 2 spiced teas and your whole bill with tip is only $20. They have all your favorite Ethiopian homestyle dishes served ip with fresh injera.

This is my first visit, but it won't be the last. I just had the Abyssinian Sampler with a friend. There are other familiar Ethiopian dishes for future visits. The place is small, informal, and friendly. The cook knows how to make a good platter.

As a first foray into Ethiopian cuisine we were pleased overall with our experience at Abyssinia. Our hostess/waitress was cordial and informative and made a recommendation for an entree for myself and my girlfriend. The #13 was a wonderful medley of meats and veggies and a great flavor sampling of the native cuisine. Service was sufficient, our meal came out fresh, in a timely manner and the portion size was enough to feed us both. Highly recommended!
g1ilXJRmG8GPQvm9_-izsw:
Took the Missus out for dinner and a movie and decided to check out this reasonable place by the theater.  Apparently they have a newish Executive Chef, as his picture was still on a banner hanging outside.

The atmosphere was nice, warm and modern with cool lamps and white-painted twigs.  We were seated at a table in what felt like a pathway between dining areas, so I asked for a booth and they moved us without complaint.

Our server, Wes, came across like a pro.  He struck just the right balance between taking great care of us and letting us enjoy our date, sometimes checking in, and other times breezing by without a word leaving a fresh club soda with lime in his wake.

We tried an appetizer of thick-cut house-cooked potato chips covered with melted bleu cheese, bacon, green onions, and bleu cheese crumbles.  It was like nachos and loaded baked potato got together and produced this interesting and yummy baby.

The Missus got the steak tip appetizer for her meal and was very pleased with what she got, the tips were tender and flavorful, and she liked the tangy gravy they came in.  She was thrilled with the zucchini & red onion vegetable side.  I got the Mahi Mahi tacos and really liked them, though I was surprised that they came on flour tortillas instead of corn.  The spice was just right, the fish was perfect, and the cabbage was shredded so thin and even, I was thrilled and amazed.

/Digression Alert/ Those of you who know me know I'm a bit crazy about cabbage, and I use it in at least one dish/week at home if not two.  So I had to ask Wes how they shredded the cabbage so nicely - did they use a really sharp knife, a slicer?  Well, this question drew out the Chef who wanted clarification then admitted that they did, in fact use a slicer to get such perfect cabbage shreds.  Since I can't afford my own slicer, he recommended a mandolin, which I'd heard of but never used, as a finger-saving method of getting even and super-thin cabbage shreds.  So, new addition to the Christmas list - a nice mandolin. /End digression/

We were too full for desert (and Harry Potter was waiting) so we paid the very reasonable check (though after reading a couple of reviews here I wished I'd checked Groupon before we went) and headed out satisfied by a really good meal and bonus cooking tips from the chef to boot.

I went to Yolos after seeing Horrible Bosses last night.  Because, I was still full from water and a pretzel (see my Green Hills Regal 16 review), I really just wanted a drink and an appetizer.  The bar was completely full so my friend and I waited for a table. 

When we spoke to the hostess, she told us it would be a 15-20 minute wait. Surprisingly, it didn't take that long.  I think we may have waited only 10 minutes.

When we got to the table, a server told us that our designated server was busy but she would take our drink order.  My friend got her drink relatively fast, but mine took awhile-- I ordered a Cosmo.  Our designated server brought my drink and profusely apologized.

I had the chicken and strawberry salad which was good.  It was full of almonds, apples, strawberries and chicken.  My friend had the chicken tenders, baked potato and salad.

One downside is they don't have give you anything to nibble on.  But, with me trying to lose weight, is that really a downside?

I was kind of surprised to this get low ratings, cause I really liked it. The decor was well done, and gives it the fill of a place you want to sit for awhile with a large group of friends, which we did. There are also TVs playing music videos in the restroom, so I enjoyed part of a Zooey Deschanel video while I was in there. 
We got an order of red pepper hummus to start, which had pico and a balsamic glaze on top of it. Quite tasty. I ordered the margherita pizza, something I order from several places, and it was very good. A buddy of mine got a burger stuffed with bacon, and I believe, brie, which looked awesome. I'll definitely go back.
aTUEeVhePTsPtzMyIqX0vw:
I expected the name "White Trash Cafe" to be an obviously tongue-in-cheek joke, but apparently I don't get it.  This place really is the definition of white trash, and is proud of it: Christmas lights are strung on the walls, shamelessly fake flowers sit on every table, and a random array of stuffed animals, disco balls, and mobiles hang from the ceiling, not to mention the countless figurines, flip flops, and other junk on the shallow shelf that runs the length of the restaurant's walls.

This definitely seems like the kind of place where "everybody knows your name," so if you're not a regular, you kind of have to figure out the protocol for yourself.  Here're a few hints:  you seat yourself, and one of the waitresses brings you a menu and takes your order.  When you're ready to go, walk up to the male server and tell him what you had.  He'll tell you what you owe, and give you your change.  It may or may not be cash only.

My waitress was casually friendly, and brought me my food pretty quickly.  Since I'm a vegetarian and the White Trash Cafe is a meat & three, I just ordered apple cobbler ($2.50), which was really doughy and rich.  I would've preferred a higher ratio of apples to crust, but it was still pretty good.  They do offer a vegetarian plate, too.  

I'm not going to become a regular here, but I'd recommend trying the White Trash Cafe at least once just for the, um, unique experience.  Besides, the prices are very reasonable, and the food is pretty good for what it is.

I'm mildly confused by Justine's review. I don't have a clue what "Starbucks service" is. Anyhow, White Trash cafe is yet another meat and three. If you don't know what those are, well, you'll find plenty of them here in Nashville. It's a huge noon meal, and it usually puts me to sleep.

The decor here is definitely over the top and tongue in cheek. Whenever I've been in, the servers have been friendly. The food has been fine. I'm not sure there's much nuance between one meat and three and another. So, if you are in the mood for fried chicken, macaroni and cheese, fried okra, cornbread, sweet tea, this is a good place. 

I would say it is in a weird location, but I used to work down the street in Berry Hill so it was easy to get to. It does seem to get packed at lunch so be forewarned. Out of town guests of mine love to come here. Lots of people are just amazed by the name.

This is a meat and three with a twist.  In kind of the same genre of Nash Trash tours they are making light of our less genetically endowed fans of country music.  Anyhow if you love tacky and you want to eat in it than this is your place.

Now that being said, part of the fun is that the waitresses, typically dressed like Flo from Mel's diner,  love to snap at you and act irritated at your stupidity.  So expect to be yelled at for taking to long to find a table or be told to hurry up with your order.  It's part of their schtick.  They are really friendly for the most part so just go with it.  

Prices are good and the food is nice and salty -  taste before you sprinkle.  This is a fun place to take your visiting family and I think it's best not to warn them in advance that this whole place is a joke and act seriously in to it.   This is how I take the in laws down a notch if they need a little disarming..
9bnCKFYknD5MaWd3VXhuSw:
It's a Papa Johns. You should know what the deal is; it's delicious, hot, 'merican pizza. 

What I will be reviewing is the ability of this location to provide exemplary product, in the Papa Johns fashion, in a timely and courteous manner.

They do this. Consistent and courteous.

So I previously gave this place a negative review. Since then, the customer service and overall quality has been amazing. I had a hiccup with one order where they forgot my extra cheese cups and ranch BUT the store manager reached out to me and not only gave me the sides for free but a large pizza and wings just for the inconvenience. It was so nice knowing that my experience was heard and fixed. Kudos to you all!

Ordered delivery from here  in a moment of hunger/desperation. The wait time was long, and I was expecting the pizza to be subpar. 

When the delivery guy arrived, he was so kind and apologetic for the delay. But most importantly, the pizza was hot, fresh and amazing. The best Papa Johns I've had in a long time. He even gave me extra sauces. Just had to give props where props are due.
_aITm6sZmcPzlAsaMZUQew:
I was here for breakfast nothing fancy, fast service, hot coffee, good service, lots of seniors with their coffee cups.
As for a fast food joint, it's a winner.

I stop at this McDonalds drive through every Monday through Thursday. There not perfect, they are better than perfect when it comes to customer service. I leave there with a smile on my face. For examples; there system were down and they were only taking cash purchases. I had cash and when I pulled to the window, the male manager couldn't get the register to work. He smiled gave me my order and told me to have a nice day. I told him I could dig up the correct change and he told me not to worry about it. The next day I tried to pay for the meal I received the day before and the meal manager said don't worry about it, today is a new day with a smile. 

Another day that I stopped in I order a big breakfast and when I opened it I dropped it out of my lap onto the floor of my truck. My breakfast was ruined. I threw it away and went back and ordered the same thing. When I got to the window, I told manager McDonalds should have the same guarantee as Dominos. He asked me what did I mean?  I replied that Dominos says they guarantee that you make it home with your pizza or they would remake it. The manager laughed about me dropping my breakfast with me. He had already charged me. It took my receipt and wrote a free breakfast on it and handed to me. 

Today, I ordered. Diet Coke with my breakfast and meant to order a regular Coke. When I got to the window, the lady that always smiles and polite remade the Coke and made another Diet Coke for me at no charge. 
Sometimes , rarely my order might not be up to par, but this McDonalds knows how to take care of their customers and make their customers feel valuable. I know if they treat me that way, they treat everyone that way. Please stop by and then write your experience about the way they treated you.
I dont care what they make their food from or if they hug their chickens each day before they slaughter them. Some things at mc donalds are as good as it gets. And bring back the creepy clown!
j4UO55f979mmMfZbeDtygA: Friendliest service I've had in a looooooong time!  Thank you for the wonderful lunch today!

This is a Pizza Hut, so for the most part you know what you're getting in regard to the food. Like it or leave it. This review focuses more so on this Pizza Hut location's customer service which has always been exceptional, for me at least. I typically carry out and am always promptly greeted (or given a polite "be right there!" from the kitchen). Even when you can see that the kitchen is incredibly busy, whoever comes to the counter always brings a smile. They also treat their customers well -- any time my order is delayed or wrong (which rarely happens), they are quick to offer a sincere apology and sometimes offer an extra item or discount. I've been coming to this location for a few months now -- between the convenience, decent quality of food and efficient/polite staff, I'll keep giving them my business.
cSjeRwkVvBtrcYF5VDgrog: The outdoor area at US Border is really awesome.  The patio is the primary reason I go to US Border because you can smoke and just booze it all day long.  The margaritas are really good and the offer up a decent selection of tequila.  As far as the food goes, I would recommend the cheese dip and the steak fajitas.  They are piping hot and the steak that US Border uses is seasoned really well.  Definitely a good spot for a late Friday afternoon fester session.

I know it's not the fanciest or the most authentic Mexican option, but I really enjoy US Border Cantina.  The service is pretty reliable, and the prices are low.  If you want something satisfyingly cheesy that doesn't demand too much of your palette you have come to the right place. I think the salsa is pretty great, which for me makes a big difference in the over-all experience.  

US Border Cantina is a bit of a party Mexican place.  The decor include colorful streamers and inflatable limes with beer ads hanging from the ceiling.  They serve comically oversized margaritas and beer.  The patio feels more secluded than you would expect being so close to a busy part of West End so I definitely recommend sitting outside if weather permits.

I'm in love, no really, I am. I'm the snootiest person when it comes to Mexican food, but I have found my favorite. I went here for lunch and not only were they able to seat us right away, the service was top notch. 

The immediately gave us chips (multi-colored) and salsa (it came in a container which we got to pour into individual cups for dipping). The salsa had more flavors to it than any other I've had in Nashville. It had some kick to it, but nothing over the top. My drink was never empty either, they continually filled it. 

The queso came pretty quickly and was very creamy. It was white cheese and thick as can be. I liked it though. I got the chimi changa and was surprised by how flavorful the chicken was. It came with rice, beans, lettuce and sour cream. Also, it was covered in a yellow cheese sauce. My only qualm was no guacamole. I actually didn't have any here at all, which I regret. Oh well, just another reason to go back. 

My friend ordered the tacos, to which she said "they have better meat than Las Palmas. Put that on Yelp." Lastly, the bathrooms were SUPER clean. I was extremely impressed. Again, gotta say it, I loved the waitstaff. They acted almost like they were on a tip share. And, when we finished our meal and realized we had 15 minutes left in our lunch break, they didn't have a problem with giving us more chips and salsa to munch on while we sat there and chatted. 

I had a fantastic time and will go back regularly. Oh, and as we left John Rich walked in.
Y5mRODZ73trgPd1C_Aa30w: Great beer. I've tried all the local breweries here in Nashville, and I was really impressed with the lineup. My brother and I really enjoyed the rye IPA and oatmeal stout. They have a great happy hour and a unique vibe where you can check out some local artisan goods. Friendly staff too!

I planned a large group happy hour here 2 weeks ago and it was great (they take reservations!). Not a huge bar area, though, so I'm glad I made a reservation. The happy hour specials are really great and the draft beers were good (4 selections). Also the bartender was super friendly. But they could improve the food (seems like they are using Sam's kitchen?). Every option was a refrigerated dip with chips (except for the off-menu mini chili dog special). There's a pizza oven in the back (and it still kind of smells like pizza from the building's Bosco's days), they should put it to use! 

As for the marketplace, cute but expensive. The concept is a little strange. I can't see myself buying much there. I wish they had turned the building into a small farmer's market -- that's what hillsboro village needs!

It seems most of the reviews here talk about just the bar or the shop area when it first opened and there wasn't much going on. 

As of now, the shop area has a wide variety of vendors. Spices, clothing, art, Nashville made snacks and foodie products, beauty products, jewelry... There's a little bit of everything. There are things which are obviously for tourists, but plenty of normal boutiquey type items natives would want to buy as well. Prices are all over the spectrum. There are $2 items and $1000 items. Not everything is costly and most of it seems on par with other shops in the village. 

While some people may find the concept weird, it seems plenty of others enjoy it. There were people walking around with beers while shopping. Kind of cool. 

I'm not a drinker, so I can't comment too much on the bar itself. They have six beers on tap, brewed in house. (You can see the brewery from the sales floor. So that's cool too.) the people sitting at the bar seem plenty happy to be there. Some food would be good, but as it stands now, the shop itself seems like a solid space. Obviously, people who have bad experiences are more likely to post reviews.


Edit- July 11, 2015- they have pizza now at the bar! Wood fire pizza and a few other similar menu items. YUM.
Y7zKoD1-cajoHVKGNYYqxA: Great Harvest Bread leaves its Green Hills neighbor - Panera Bread behind as far as variety and quality of breads offered.  Its breads and muffins are heavy with rich ingredients.  Several of the breads use whole unground wheat or other unground grains as part of their recipe.  Eatie gourmets like it for sandwiches and more. Best is samples they offer for you to enjoy, so you will know how it tastes before you buy. Most customers take baked goods with them; there is limited cafe-style seating.
Best multi-grain bread on the market and the raisin is excellent too. Friendly staff.
Get the Honey Whole Wheat loaf!  I love everything in here.  They are super generous with samples, which inevitably causes you to buy something else because the quality is just that good!
8u7J1_3LxXeQ0fKflFXVsg: Ordered the baked chicken, Mac n cheese, cabbage, and dressing. Food was fantastic and the service was outstanding. Very welcoming environment! Definitely recommend!

As we were leaving Nashville, my husband and I wanted soul food (sorry Nashville, Meat and 2) and couldn't confirm if Arnold's was open on MLK. day. With that said, we took a chance on Ella Jean's Cafe. They are typically closed on Monday's but due to MLK day there was an event for Vanderbilt students. 

The owner seen us outside and warmly greeted us with what was going on. She gladly escorted us through the event to order our food. I had Fried Chicken, Cabbage, Kale mix and Yams with a roll. Let me tell YOU, I may NEVER go back to Arnold's. This place was it. Nice, friendly comfortable setting. The staff (lady fixing the plate, cook and cashier) were all great.

My husband had the meatloaf, yams and macaroni and he too stated Arnold's is hindsight.

Thank you Ella Jean's for a GREAT experience. My only regret is that we didn't take advantage of this place the 4 years we lived in Nashville. A MUST visit on our next trip!!!
First time at Ella jeans Cafe, had a really, really awesome experience. Very, very friendly people soon as we came into the door, and the food was great as well. We will definitely be coming back!
c_YbtTRac_wv5jpzLQElsQ:
I wound up here originally.because the sandwich shop next door has great food but incredibly bad staff issues. Today is my third visit to this location...the staff has been super friendly, food crisp and fresh. 
It seems the staff actually enjoy what they are doing, which.is a MASSIVE plus! :-) :-) :-) Thank You all for doing your thing in a joyful manner!!! :-)

they have 6.99 sandwich+side+beverage combo. I had a ham classic sandwich which was indeed tasty. But they forgot to change the croissant to wholegrain bread. Never mind, cause the mandarin salad is sooo yummy! I love it!

Must give credit where credit is due. This store ALWAYS has the best customer service. The employees are so friendly, helpful, and always have a smile (even during holiday craziness!) Not to mention, the food is EXCEPTIONAL.  My family has been getting turkeys and hams here for over a decade. Just started incorporating their side dishes into family gatherings as well. The sweet potato souffle is to die for! I can't say enough about this establishment...quality food, clean environment, knowledgeable and eager employees. Way to go Honey Baked Ham!!!
MbVLinePgICrhu9vgFJAcw: Love it!!!  My favorite place in Nashville for Ethiopian food.  I drive 25 minutes to get there, but it's worth the drive.  They recently changed locations, but still the same owner and great food.  The portions are large and the people there are very kind.  I highly recommend!

Yess so good. I go here whenever I need to be in the area for work their food is amazing. I love their raw kitfo and the ayib is different then any I have had before but it is to die for. I must go if nearby.

Delicious Ethiopian food at very affordable prices. I've been here twice now and both times I left for happy and content. The place itself is a cute house that's had been converted into a nice restaurant right off of Murfreesboro. The true mark of excellence at goha is that there are always actual Ethiopians who are always there eating and having out. Highly recommended!
s-fFF-5v6kwuN3YZ0kS-Xw: For Memorial Day weekend, I was staying at the M-Star Motel right across the street and ended up here because I was too lazy to drive downtown and find free parking (good luck). I also wanted some sort of healthy salad to offset all the BBQ and beer I consumed earlier. Walked in about 9:30pm and was sat at a cocktail table by the bar. 10 minutes passed and no server. I asked the hostess (middle-aged woman with a British accent) who my server was. She asked around and discovered that they went on break before I was sat. The hostess was extremely apologetic and got me my drink order. While trying to find someone else to wait on me, I perused the menu and saw this amazing-looking New Strawberry-Avocado-Grilled Chicken salad. I was like, "Hot dayum!" A different waiter came out and got my order going. The salad came out and was PHENOMENAL! I was shocked to be blown away by anything from an Applebee's. The manager came out and apologized for the earlier mix up as well. I'm giving this Applebee's 4 Stars for acting like they actually care and for this amazing new salad of theirs. Good job!

We must have caught this place on a good day.  Food was good.  Service was good and price was good.  After reading some of the reviews I expected bad news.  Not so much.  Salmon was excellent.  I went with a party of 11 which is why we ended up at Applebee's. I wanted Pho or Indian but I was with a group that made that impossible.  You typically know what you get at a chain like this and at least for us, it delivered that experience.  There are better choices but if you find yourself here and get the server we had you won't walk out disappointed.  If you have expectations that are within the margin.
Gregory was a great waiter and the food was good as always but the restrooms does need better attention
XJ68pvsPUTMpDEW0PXIyvg: I've been at most of the hot dog places un Nashville but I have to say that these are the best, Specially the Italian Beef sandwich is Amazing!!!
Happened to be in the Nashville area recently and stumbled upon Jays Chicago.  All I can say is Jay cooks up some awesome food!  Friendly dude too.
Well Done excellent hot beef good portion a excellent value. Friendly people who know good food!
nCwyBkZFkVPJu6zGK64IDg: I had the whiting fish sandwich, it was very flavorful and fresh. The owner was very nice and service was great. I will be back.
What a gem! I was super surprised and impressed with how good the chicken & waffles were. Def would eat here again!
raXCiQLUGbryuLoZistiKw: Very nice, with a menu that is different from a lot of Mexican restaurants, but with just enough that's familiar. Excellent quality and good service.

This was really good.

First, the menu is different from the other Mexican restaurants in Bellevue. Sure, they have the usual suspects, but they also have creative new dishes.

My wife had something with chicken and bacon that was excellent. I don't see it on the online menu, so I can't tell you the name. Sorry.

I tried the Dinner Combo, which is so big it comes on two plates. The menu isn't clear that nearly all of the Combo items are beef. I'd substitute chicken in the enchilada or burrito next time, but that's just my taste. The food was excellent.

Nice dining room.

We'll definitely return.
I really enjoyed the starting of the chips and their red sauce. Service was wonderful, enchiladas were delicious, atmosphere was good.
20wCRJ4MEupm5mUq7i2BmQ:
Peter Chinn's, in what was recently Cheeseburger Charley's, was a surprising delight. The sparse surroundings are a stark contrast to the warm inviting open arms of the corn wrapped goodness these digs churn out.  Seating is a little awkward, but who cares, you are there for the food, right? 

Right. So I'll talk about the food. In the few times I've consumed at this eatery I haven't gotten past the kogi, spicy pork,  and bulgogi tacos. If you are scared of spice or a noob, I'd start with the bulgogi. The beef in the bulgogi was fresh, tender, and cooked to my liking (medium rare to medium). I found it to be a little more bland than I prefer, but i'm a spicy food guy. I was not at all turned off by it, but less interested than my other two meaty endeavors.  2nd in line is the kogi. Being unfamiliar with kogi in general i didn't know what to expect. The cilantro lime flavor, fresh cucumber, and other unidentifiable Korean flavors play a cool and savory note. The pork was tender and reasonably seasoned.  My personal favorite so far is the spicy pork. An almost fruity but hot barbecue sauce sets this morsel off. Hot enough to make your nose run, but not enough to make the bathroom unpleasant. The sweet and spice of the sauce are intertwined nicely, and don't overpower each other.

 Don't get me wrong, you may not have a religious experience during your first trip. I can't say the food is mindblowing or has changed my life, but for some reason I keep wanting to go back again and again. I couldn't give it a  5 star rating, simply because the atmosphere is almost food factoryesque and uninviting. That being said, I think our political leaders should look to food for diplomatic inspiration. We should start dropping tortillas on North Korea any time they act up. This might be the solution to the very angry and ronery Kim.Jong- Il. If only Hans Brix would have stumbled upon the taco trucks of California then maybe the DPRK could take a lesson from LA. At least with the food. The marriage of Mexico, Korea, and Los Angeles does indeed make a happy reception in my mouth. (btw don't tell anyone that South Korea has access to these, or WWIII might break out)


Oh, and try the onion rings.


EDIT: I miss this place. Wish it would reopen.

Awesome replacement to Cheeseburger Charley's and here to give the neighborhood some serious competition.

Had the Bulgogi tacos where the meat was tender. They were awesome!

Not a big fan of the russet fries in general but these weren't too bad. 2 Bulgogi tacos & make it a combo (fries & drink) = $8.70. Still cheaper than Panera's sandwich, drink, cookie.

Went in today as I have been itching to try the tacos.  Ordered a Kogi and a Kimchi Bulgogi taco and fries.    Bulgogi taco was really really good and will be going back just for a couple of these.  The Kogi taco was good as well but not as good athe Bulgogi.  As for the fries... while tasty the whole combination of tacos and french fries just didn't work for me.  Would rather they had tortilla chips or some kind of bean salad.  All in all thought I will be back for the tacos.
5wo7nNHOvfzt2c-PG0BUKQ: Clean, fresh, quiet and good folks working here. Nashville has so much construction that we've had to backtrack all over the place when walking. We were so grateful to find this place. Ask for a bathroom key. And eat, drink, rest and refresh before your continued journey, wherever it may be.

What can I say about subway that has not already been said by Jared?
