There is something truly great about any place where you can get a 12 inch turkey sub loaded with fresh veggies for only five bucks and 630 calories.  The guys behind the counter are super nice and really make an effort to get you out of the deli fast!

Convenient location on the corner of 6th ave and Church Street.

Probably one of the cleanest Subway's that I have been in.
F_K0-xzzEe-nc41OZ43XXA: Friendly staff, lovely natural lighting, fresh herbs, and delicious salads and sandwiches made to order at reasonable prices. I'm a fan. Oh, they also have espresso drinks. Only con I could complain about is the menu is not posted online. I like that this little gem is rather undiscovered (first floor of an office bldg) because it is a nice, quiet place to eat and never has much of a line. :)

One of the only places around Music Row to grab a breakfast quickly with comfortable seating under the AC. The guys here are super nice! I was in town for about a week and had breakfast here every morning before heading to my client's office. Walking distance from all the hotels and businesses. Happy Eats!

I love Benton's!!! I usually order something from breakfast or lunch on a regular basis. They have a wide variety and it's good. My favorite part of going, is talking with Benton and his sidekick/employee (So sorry, I can't remeber his name). They are great guys and they are fast with dispersing orders.
aTWIhy04A5TA0WTckxu8KQ:
The Cocoa Bean is located inside the Gaylord Opryland Hotel - it's right around the corner from the main lobby check in area.

I hit The Coffee Bean twice during my stay at the Gaylord Opryland - both times I ordered iced lattes - first time was a triple shot iced latte with whole milk; the second time a double shot iced latte with whole milk.

Both drinks were strong and tasty and did the trick! Fed my brain the caffeine it oh so desperately craved and needed!

There are a number of tables off to the side where you can sit and enjoy your beverage or work on your notebook computer or mobile device.

Service was good!
_WApK8fcto87CcaU7WbiUw: Even though this store is in a bad part of town, it doesn't affect their cleanliness or service. They do not mind cooking to order nor do they mind custom orders. I have food allergies and I know I can get exactly what I want to eat AND drink at this BK. They have the coke machine at this location that has over a 100 different drink combinations, and it's wonderful that you can get a Diet Caffeine Free Coke in 6 different flavors. I get coupons in the mail almost every month and they will take then with no problem. The banter from the employees is always welcomed after a long day as well... Go get some fresh and kra-zy dinner. I dare ya.

I must say... I got more of a seared bite on the impossible whopper than the actual whopper-no no no no no jo (I AM IN DENIAL!!!)..... no no no no no no no no.. ladies and gentlement, im not gonna trip, i wanted to come in a 100% hater... but imma just flat out say it-- your impossible whopper, BK, is BETTER than your actual whopper, BK !!!!!!!
vAn4rcwbhWDaAzO3OsbCIw:
We went there last night to celebrate my husband's birthday.  I think this was the best meal I've had in the five years we've been in Nashville.  I dined on the Shrimp & Grits (which was amazing) and my husband had the Scallop entree.

Everything about our experience was amazing.  From the service to the ambiance, to the food.  

A must do!

The ambience in this place is a little bland and hotel bar-ish, but the fish definitely makes up for it. I ordered the seafood chowder...this was good, but nothing compared to my boyfriend's meal. He ordered the salmon with the duck fat potatoes, and it was RIDICULOUS. Absolutely the best salmon I have ever tasted. 

The only other negative I would share is that the wine list was unimpressive and everything was $2-$3 a glass more than the local average. 

However, if you're looking for a great cut of fish expertly prepared, this is it.

I visited FIsh & Co the other night for the bourbon tasting event they had for Four Roses Bourbon. The event didnt start until almost 7, but my friend and I got there at about 5. Since I wasn't there for a full dinner, I can't comment fully, but what I saw and tried, I loved.
First of off, the decor of the place is truly gorgeous and luxurious. As upscale a feel as any place in the city. We sat at the bar, which was set out with homemade potato chips and a cheese dip tray, both of which were a substantial upgrade from run of the mill bar snacks.
The bar was also elegant and exceedingly well stocked. Their special was a Black Tie - a drink made with Crown Royal Black and priced at an incredibly reasonably $5. My buddy ordered the half dozen fresh oysters for $6 bucks, which he raved about (being a Kosher head, I passed).
The bartenders were excellent mixologists and came up with a new drink based on the Four Roses especially for the occasion (Brown Sugar Simple Syrup, Apple Cider, and a special Old Fashioned Bitters (I forgot the name) that lent a wonderful cinnamon flavor) that was like Thanksgiving in a glass.

The bourbon tasting was upstairs - a great place for parties or groups, I would surmise. The pass-around's again were off my menu, but the shrimp skewers, and bacon wraps were eagerly gobbled up.

At the end of the event, the restaurant offered anyone who had attended a 10% discount off their dinner, which many, if not most people took them up on.

Unfortunately I had promised my wife I would be home an hour earlier than the event ended, so I didnt get to enjoy.
But its definitely a place I plan to return to very soon
t2B_HVgPg--eUVprWQX_Eg:
Walked in around 1:30 pm on a Saturday and the place was dead.  A worker held the door for us, and the guys were all super friendly - even complimenting our t-shirts and starting conversation.

Our sandwiches were great, and the place was clean and well kept.  Can't ask for much more from the experience - these guys are good!

I've been here twice, both times the subs were exceptionally tasty and well-prepared. The first time was during lunch rush so it (obviously) took quite a while, but these sandwiches are time consuming to make (more so than Subway) so I will not fault them as long as the food is exceptional, and thus far it has been. The service is also great, even in rush hour. Smiles, polite, and patient. Never going to Subway again. The chipotle cheesesteak is amazing, so is the Big Kahuna!
I've always liked Jersey Mike's and this location was no different. Quick service and great subs. 1st choice on my lunch break.
1R5SGpvBMzNfGiVfI5BPnA: I ordered delivery through their website (the cheaper option as delivery apps add their own fees) and have a very happy belly now! My pizza arrived on schedule (expected delivery was between 30-45 minutes and it came in about 35) and exactly as I'd ordered it. Most importantly, it was delicious: fresh and flavorful ingredients on a perfect crust (soft with just the right crunch). I've yet to visit in person, but I'm grateful to have a local option for pizza delivery (rather than a chain) and I love their late hours...5am!
ydfYGGjIZ-WaXC5Z0kzOXg: My favorite subway. Franchiese owner is a nice Indian guy.  The sandwich makers are fast, especially the skinny new guy. This special Melrose Berry Hill area Subway also puts out todays newspapers and mags to make you want to eat and sit a spell.

Probably one of the tastier Subways.  I know they're a chain and all, but it seems like they sprinkle a little bit of crack into the bread at this one, luckily they have a scale next to the soda to remind you that just because it's theoretically healthier, that you cant put 15 meatballs on a sub and call it diet health food.

The main thing I despise about this location is the parking and subsequent exiting during any sort of busy time... you end up blocked in in a worse case by gridlock, and in a best case you're stuck waiting forever for a traffic light to turn if you need to go left.  These, however, are not things that I think Subway can do much about unless they complain to someone loudly.

Place has always been clean when I've been, and I get the sense from people leaving car keys, wallets, phones, etc laying about that there's either some sort of trust in the employees and people there, or it's an elaborate sting operation being conducted by Metro PD to bust sub shop thieves.

For a while this was one of the places you could get a foot long veggie max for $5, sadly this is not to be any more...

I dig it

This is unlike any Subway you have every been to. It is more like a New York deli.  It gets packed at lunch time and the five people behind the counter are each characters with great individual personalities. I love being able to read one of the many magazines they have available.  There is enough seating for over 60 people, which sounds crazy, but I actually saw it totaly full one time. There is a reason so many people come to this subway, and it is the great people who work there. 
I love hearing "next order please" yelled to me when I am still six people away from the counter, because that high-strung skinny guy just has to be as many sandwiches ahead as possible. The middle three people on the line work well together to move orders along at a rapid pace. The mellow young guy at the register rounds out the group, and asks me how I'm doing in a way that makes me feel like he actually cares. 
I recommend coming here at the busiest time possible to get the real feel for the place, otherwise you may walk away thinking it was just another Subway.
8Og1yvdixy6jo1OATAwUQQ: My fav. I am not a fan of pork at all. But they have a turkey sandwich that is amazing. The prices are great and while it is all drive-thru. You can get dinner in a hurry. LOL
ESPdkRZVHOI5r_GxNT9P0Q:
Great customer service!  Great food!

Stayed open after closing to serve us. Friendly staff both at the counter and at the grill.
Great food!! Management really good!! Fixes problems if any really fast. Newly remodeled.
uHfHKZj_RsNwP03ar_vwQg: We stayed nearby at JW Marriott and saw this place so decided to try it one of the nights we were in town. We liked it a lot!! HUGE tv, good food and service and a somewhat fun atmosphere. We had the loaded spoon fries thing, meatloaf and chicken friend chicken. Everything was really good. I'm not a southern food expert so don't know how it compares but we enjoyed it. The meatloaf was mushy - is that a southern thing? Good comfort food regardless. Our appy came out same time as meal so the Manager proactively offered to comp it we never said a word. Nice touch and good meal. There were times I server was hard to track down though.

Enormous physical space right near our hotel, and came highly recommended from multiple people. On our last night in town, we swung by Broadway just to see the hustle and bustle, and then walked to NashHouse. What a feast! The service was friendly and quick despite the fact that they were pretty busy. We loved the live music - a boyfriend and girlfriend guitar and singing combo (@lynavenue). Big fans! Beer list was impressive, food was delicious. Fried green tomatoes, country-fried steak...wow, everything was so good! Would happily return on our next time in town. Awesome spot, thanks for a great time!

We stayed in the Cambria (hotel above this restaurant). It was so convenient for us to head downstairs, have some DELICIOUS southern/ American food, and enjoy some AMAZING TALENT. Their hot chicken, Mac n cheese, pickle flights, desserts, and drinks were fabulous. We ate here 5 times during our stay. The staff is incredible, very attentive, friendly and accommodating. Would recommend 10/10. Would return 1000 times over.
tJk4Bv5Mi_7LiXcJBy21OQ: Located near Tennessee State University, Harper's is the best  southern cafeteria style restaurant around Nashville.  The fried chicken is really good an super crispy, much like Publix fried chicken.  They have all sorts of stuff like mashed potatoes, turnip greens( that is really a southern dish), and banana pudding.  While Harper's is not as heavy on the butter as some other southern style restaurants in Nashville, I would definitely consider it superb comfort food.  The thought of cafeteria style dinning usually conjures up thoughts of gross dim lit buffets with subpar food.  Harper's is the opposite, the food is super fresh and the folks who work there are really friendly.

Been here several times and always had good food and a welcoming atmosphere. Prices are pretty reasonable. The fruit tea is good. Always gotta go with greens as a side at a place like this, and cornbread. I think my favorite entree thus far is the meatloaf, but they don't always have it. Highly recommended! Not sure if I'd go after dark due to the area. But otherwise great :)
Great southern menu! Ribs falling off the bone goodness, and affordable. The staff very friendly and lots of TSU pictures to take in.
yJJ2H1UeYA9AGnOtHODWmQ: Walked in ten minutes before close and they were happy to see us and very kind. They made sure to make the food exactly as we liked and asked about certain things we wanted and how much. My boyfriend had the gyro with fries and I had the falafel plate and they were both great. We eat a lot of gyros in the area and have been meaning to try this place since it opened recently, and it's currently our vote for best gyros around. The inside  is very clean and nice as well.

Really good place for a gyro! The staff was amazingly friendly, the place was really clean, and the food was delicious! I got the chicken gyro and my husband got the lamb. Both were really good, and you get to choose toppings (which is cool). French fries were made to order and they had the choice of seasoned or regular. They both were really good, but I enjoyed the seasoned ones for sure! It was a good place.

I love a FRESH Gyro and it's hard to find a great Gyro, but this was one of the best I have found in a long time. Fries cooked to order. 

They also have a nice size selection of other Greek food as well as some American favorites. 

Although they did double charge my CC. They were  happy to refund me the second one. 

I will be back the next time I am in Nashville.
Xn5NRxcnoG1DbWnvkEGbFg:
If you see the SmoQe truck out, stop and get you some. It doesn't disappoint. Shon is a GREAT guy who is really passionate about providing a delicious meal to his customers and community. There was a time when I lived right around the corner from him and I watched him change this physical location into what it is today. There were times at midnight, you could walk outside and know Shon was prepping for the next day because the neighboorhood would be smokey and you can taste the smoke in the food. 

I had the wings, one friend had the jackfruit BBQ, and another had the pulled chicken BBQ. Simply put....DELICIOUS, juicy, flavorful, we didn't speak. We just ate and kept nodding. He is one man doing the best he can. He's pretty good about keeping his schedule up to date but if he's not there when the hours are posted, feel free to reach out to him or find him on any of the social platforms. #SBB

I indulged in some late night eats at Smoque Signals because it was outside of the "The Set" event. The Brisket sandwich was DELICIOUS! The flavor was perfect and the bbq sauce topped it off just right. This food truck was on point, and I will eat here again!

This is my 1st visit to Smoque Signals. The BBQ was pretty good. They were out of the items I really wanted to try so I'll be making a follow up visit after they gotten settled into their permanent location (2914 Felicia Street). The prices are reasonable and the portions will fill you. I look forward to seeing this business grow at it's new home.

This business originally started as a food truck and has recently acquired a permanent location. According to the owner, the food truck will still make pop ups at events around town, but now you know where to always find them!
ct-moUWiNkO9a8wjge2CkQ: Fresh fruit .good customer service . Good selection .friendly . I love the atmosphere , shop while u are lessening to musical .
Great store verry clean friendly employees great selection of mexican produce  great selection of fruit
Great grocery store with fresh produce & a friendly staff. Talbot's Corner Fresh Produce.
KlhTXr1S73X7xr-qQP8-Ww: I'm glad this Subway opened up. I was, at first, nervous that it would be crawling with college kids...but, luckily, I haven't run into that problem yet. It's on my way home from both of my jobs, so it's an absolutely perfect location. The staff are friendly and patient with high-maintenance requests (not that I've had any, but I have witnessed some).
OwUWlP7J8W8PuIWkRuPj4A:
I was in the mood for a Chinese buffet today so I ventured out to try something new. There is another buffet much closer to my house that I have never enjoyed. I had driven by this restaurant many times but had never been inside...and today, I was very impressed. I didn't go until about 2pm so normally I would expect old, somewhat dried out Chinese food. Yes, I know, and I still went in. This place, however, did not meet my expectations. They happily exceeded them!  First of all, the buffet was only $7.95 and they had a huge assortment of items. Everything looked and tasted very fresh. The service was also very good. 

I own a restaurant here in Nashville so I tend to be very picky when eating out, and I found my new favorite neighborhood  Chinese buffet!  Give 'em a try!
BYGe23aID565LWQSH2oXLA: Higher end than most Mexican restaurants. A great addition to the neighborhood. Great, thick house made chips. Spicy salsa. Margarita is a good size for $6 but a bit too sweet. Small tacos but tasty and fresh. Nice quality ingredients. Love the lime rice.

Habanero Grill is hands down the best Mexican restaurant around. Wonderful, fast service, strong margaritas, and amazing food. The cucumber habanero margarita is absolutely amazing-we order it every time we stop by. Not to mention, their happy hour special is such a deal. Be sure to make this friendly, Donelson grill your regular night out spot.

This is a great new addition to authentic Mexican food in Donelson TN.  It is a small but quaint and tastefully decorated restaurant on Lebanon Pike with fantastic service. We were there early on a Monday night, so it was not crowded, but guests were arriving as we were leaving. Limited parking in front of the restaurant, but there is additional parking in the rear of the building.

Their chips are good and their salsa is definitely home made.  A little heat for some pallets, but very good.  If you don't like salsa with some heat ask them for the mild version. 

My wife and I both had their Habanero Margarita and it is one of the best margarita's I have every had. For dinner we shared 2 appetizers, the Habanero Combo which includes a small selection of quesadillas, chicken flautas, nachos, chorizo taquitos, guacamole and, pico de gallo
and the Besos de Camaron which is four medium shrimp stuffed with pepper jack cheese wrapped with bacon.  Both of these appetizers were offered authentic Mexican taste and we should know as we go to Puerto Vallarata Mexico about every other year.  We also shared the Pan de Elote for dessert.  Absolutely fabulous.
 
The owner came out and talked to us briefly and it is apparent that he knows fresh Mexican cuisine and we enjoyed our conversation, as he is quite pleasant and very personable.

This is, in our opinion, the most authentic Mexican restaurant in Donelson and possibly all of Nashville TN for great authentic Mexican food and great service. 

Highly recommended and you can bet we will be back, since we are locals.
vbzn9jiw5DCbHdzT_jPWSA: Went last week for lunch.  Not very crowded but I don't know why!  They're tucked away in a little shopping area so you'll need to watch for Pita House.  Service was really great; the people are so nice I'd probably go back even if the food weren't great.  It WAS great though!  Had the gyro plate with lamb and a piece of homemade baklava for dessert.  Was full - very.  The lamb was very good, fresh tasting, cooked perfectly and not greasy.  The rice was excellent and the salad literally tasted like it was just made, that fresh!  I will definitely go back and try some other items.  I love gyros but am not an expert on a lot of middle eastern food so will need to ask for some guidance - which I'm sure they will provide.  Highly recommend!

I found this place searching for spots with vegan options while in the area for a photoshoot. The reviews were great and the food looked so authentic so I figured I'd convince the crew to give it a shot. Oh my gosh! We were all so impressed! Incredible quality and amazing flavor! I'm obsessed with the red hot sauce they include with all the hummus dishes! This has quickly become my go to for ubereats! Something quick, healthy and delicious that I know I can feel good about eating. My favorite dishes are the falafel wrap, veggie entree, and fatoush salad! Also, I love that they are small and family owned. I've heard the owner and his son were so inviting and gracious when a friend went by to pick up our to go order. I can't say enough good things! I just wish they were on my side of town in the east!
This food is amazing! Family owned restaurant and you can tell how much they care about the quality of the food.
nsnehrg4TxUbO-6XrcZNFA: The Sushi Rolls are delicious!! They are rolled to perfection and better than any Sushi Rolls we've ever had the pleasure of eating. We ordered 3 Specialty Rolls, Shrimp Fried Rice, and vegetables. The proportions of each roll are generous! The staff was pleasant and did an amazing job getting our order correct and got us out of the door in a timely manner! We got carry out, but can't wait to go dine in! The Shrimp Fried rice was delicious! The only thing that could be better was an increase in the amount of vegetables. Everything was spectacular. Thank you Rock N Roll Sushi for a fantastic meal!

I talked with Steve, he was very nice, and accommodating, as I came in late, a half hour before they closed. So, even though it was 8:30, Steve was kind enough to let me dine in, as Sushi and Hibachi items are of the moment, and best when eaten just after being served. Their Miso is the best I have had in all  the years I have eaten Sushi, since I was introduced to it,in 1991. The Miso had such a great flavor, and the seaweed was not anywhere near as salty as all of the other Miso soup so I have had in Nashville. Next,the ginger dressing on their salad was so wonderful I had to call the waitress over and remark on how good it was, and she told me that they make the dressing there, I love ginger, but most of the ginger dressings are available at Japanese restaurants and supply stores, and are very much the same. Their dressing made me eat every single piece of that lettuce on the plate. I told the waitress that I would've licked the plate if there weren't people in there! And a since there was not the time to really peruse their wonderful menu in depth, and I really enjoyed reading all of the rock sushi rolls that they make, and they are very different from a lot of the rolls you get anywhere else. And knowing that it takes a little while to roll them,I decided to give the chef a break, and just order from the hibachi grill,and also got an order of those great chicken eggrolls, which were delicious with the chili dipping sauce. In retrospect, I perhaps should've ordered several of the rock designated rolls, because they were among the most creative I have seen! And that gives me a great reason to come back! And not to mention the decor is great, the music is great, with the monitor screens of the videos, it was exactly my genre when I was playing alternative rock in Nashville,, back in the day! And the starburst light fixtures are awesome, and I collect 50s furniture and fixtures, much like those starburst lamps, so I was really liking the decor!
Back to the entree', the veggies were great, and still crisp, but just that perfect stir-fry to take the edge off the raw carrots, and not overdo the broccoli, a perfect balance, and the fried rice was great and seemed to have a smoky flavor more than just soy, that made it different from other fried rice I've had in Nashville. And the steak bites were tender, delicious, and just like I ordered them, medium rare! And I tried to hurry, to not keep up past the closing time, but Steve said, take your time, and enjoy it! And I really did! Everything about their restaurant is totally awesome, from the creative rolls on album cover menus, the food, impeccable service and very congenial and accommodating staff, the decor, music, what a perfect experience! I will most definitely tell people about the great sushi restaurant they have got going on!

We thought for the money it was a very good value. We had 4 (simple) rolls for about $24. It was more than 2 of us could eat. 3 would have been plenty. Tuna was fresh... soft shell crab in spider roll was light and not fishy. The rice was done correctly and not gummy. It was good as was the service. Again and most importantly, it tasted fresh. We will be back. Have paid a lot more for much less quality. Happy we went there.
e4S4IjVt9fxnArhX6cyAHA: Great food and cocktails! The vegetarian options were great--my wife and I shared drunken noodles and the kimchi fried rice (both with tofu). An excellent option in the up and coming North Gulch area!

This was one of the best dining experiences I have ever had with Asian cuisine. I went with 3 other friends and they all said the exact same thing. We ordered 3 or 4 different appetizers- all amazing and each got different entrees. (Also were all amazing). The atmosphere was incredible, as well as the service. 5/5 and will be back many more times.

Great atmosphere. Perfect for date night or a girls lunch/dinner. Get the calamari for sure-it will change your idea of calamari! Drunken noodles are also-phenomenal. Service was ok.....seemed like the waiter was more concerned about complaining to a peer about all of the new store openings and how messy it was; that was unfortunate. There was also a total of 4 Menus given to us: drink menu, dinner menu, happy hour menu, and lunch menu. That was a bit odd but I guess it's bc of the time we got to the restaurant. They were a dirty considering they are just paper. The bathrooms are also really dark....like really dark haha-but beautiful and clean. But other than that it was really nice experience and I'd def go back!
HOD0jOOUWNIK3-qBJA2SpA:
This is where I have spent most of my time when in Nashville to visit. In recent years it has gotten much trendier and fancier. I don't love that, but I don't hate it either. It is just different.

Cool restaurants, bars, coffee shops and hotels have popped up. 
My guess is it is also bustling during night-life hours. If you are interested in living it up in Nashville while also avoiding the Broadway scene, this seems like a good choice to explore.

Great day in Nashville.  I read an earlier review and they spoke terrible of this part of Nashville.  I am so glad I did not listen. There was fun shopping and fabulous restaurants and great bars.  I received nothing but hospitality and enjoyed this part of Nashville.

A little touristy but if you are into shopping and food this maybe the place for you. This is where the iconic wings wall is: there was a literally line for hours while we stopped but we hit up the other wall across the street instead. 

Free parking for 1 hour. Get the app, it's a life saver!
931Xzzxb0zOMj_6jgPJerQ:
I will be singing BBB's praises for weeks to come!
The food was great- I ordered the Yard Work skillet. Delicious, fresh, and overall a hearty meal. I also ordered a side of spicy bacon- SOOOOO good. For me, the real shining star was their homemade hot sauce. It was so flavorful with just the right amount of heat. It may be difficult to get a table here- but I promise it's worth it. Our service was impeccable- especially since we had 11 people in our party! BRAVO BBB!

Incredible food and service. I ordered the creole omelette with potato cakes and biscuit and gravy. The portions are great and the omelette was filled with shrimp and a creole sauce. Absolutely delicious food and great service. I would highly recommend this place for anyone who values high quality breakfast food!
Best breakfast restaurant ever. It's big, it's bad. I liked the blt omelet. The workers are very friendly too. I would recommend this restaurant to anyone!!
vHAO6KAlcvt1dpIWVkFdCA: The best roasters in Nashville are at it again! Atmosphere is amazing, new location is clean and sleek. Perfect place to grab my Red Eye on the way out of town!

We stopped in here for a coffee on our way out of Nashville. The place is spacious and has a modern vibe. We walked up to the counter and let the girl know that we hadn't been there before and she offered some suggestions. I ended up with the cold brew which was pretty good. We didn't stay long, but they had several different types of seating areas from what I could see. The noise echoes a bit and it was a little loud, but if I lived in the area I think I'd hang out there.

Best coffee roasters in Nashville! The head roaster himself took the time to explain how they ethically source their beans and cultivate relationships with farmers in Guatemala and other parts of the world. 

-Genuinely friendly and knowledgeable baristas. 
-House made pastries that are incredible. 
-Fantastic space and vibe.

Happy to have them in the neighborhood!
Eq2Myp_b9jG5SERaAmcTSA: Open only a few weeks... best Gyoza ever!!!! Also had hibachi chicken, which was great. Great service as well. Excited to have them in the neighborhood!
BASED. Very delicious. Cute place. Just opened. Food is great. Love the crab Rangoon here. Sushi is great too. Prices are unbelievably low.
Best Japanese food we have found in the area! The food and service were all great. We'll definitely be back!
yKe6c61BxTY6z7YR6zFFFA: Soup of the day for the win!!!!!! On a rainy day running into this place and having a hot delicious lentil soup was just what I needed. I also tried the Avocado Toast(which was really good as well) and got the spinach hummus wrap to go. Nice spot to get some food or coffee. They also have some good looking acaí bowls and smoothies I will have to check out another time. Cashiers were friendly and helpful.

Went to visit my daughter and got smoothie bowls and pre made food here, it was a little pricey but was really good.  If anyone know me I have a very high standard when it comes to food and health and they reached that.  Very cute aesthetics too

After three days of eating mostly heavy, fried (but very good!) food in Nashville, I was dying for something a little lighter and more healthy!   E+Rose Wellness was right near our hotel so we gave it a try!

We both ordered toast: Avocado and Banana toasts.   Both very very good, but a little heavy as far as the bread went.  Everything was fresh and we felt much healthier eating this food than some of the other food we ate over the last few days!   The only really disappointing thing was that they were out of fresh sports for my avocado toast, but they added spinach to it as a replacement.   

They had a lot of neat grab-and-go items for sale.   If we had more time, I would have liked to come back to try one of their "superfood bowls" which looked very intriguing (and yummy!).  

Friendly employees and clean establishment!
SD3NVGGI_qBk0YDyk8mf0w: Nice place for a quick brunch. Cafe environment, order at the door, not full service and no adult drinks. The food was fresh and delicious. Wish there were refills on freshly squeezed oj, it was super. The million dollar bacon was superb. Would definitely recommend.
Great club sandwich! The sourdough bread is just amazing! All the food that passed our table looked delicious! Will be back!

Not sure what all the hate is about on here. I was a Bread & Company weekly regular. I will say, i was disappointed to find out that it was closed but immediately excited once I realized it is mostly the same - just with a new and updated design. I love the food! Always fresh. And the customer service has never been an issue. I'll be back to Sun and Fork very soon!
knMtp8FC6IJhh3X7ms_BCA: I had a pretty good smoothie from here. It didn't take long for them to make it and I didn't have to wait in a super long line. I had the strawberry smoothie.

Quick and friendly service which is nice when you're in a hurry to catch a flight. I went with the large kale smoothie. The fruits were fresh but I didn't see what was used for the kale and spinach. The taste was ok. I would have opted for an all fruit smoothie but this was my lunch and the kale smoothie said it has half a serving of veggies, which I needed. I wish I had paid attention to the sizes because the large was HUGE!! I definitely wasn't able to drink it all by myself. But for the price, I think it was a good value.

I had terrible acid reflux on the plane so no more coffee. I was looking around what to get and ran int this smoothie place. There's one lady working taking orders one by one so you might have to wait a bit. I love Nashville prices! Only half of what you pay elsewhere. My peach of the beach was good but very sweet so I think sugar is added. Regular is pretty large for me so perfect size.
HCHHrf21UAgbxAi8T4Q4Iw: This place was recommended to me by a couple coworkers. It did not disappoint. The prices are your typical downtown range but the portions are way bigger than the other taco joints in town. They seem to have a taste for everyone. I tried the Fish, Gyro and Buffalo Chicken today. I have to say the fish taco was amazeballs!  The gyro was nice and fresh with the veggies and feta. The buffalo chicken was good but could have been a bit spicier. We had chips and queso as well. It was good. The chips are seasoned well and thicker than the typical tortilla style. This spot is a must try.

Delicious tacos. So many options. I loved that there were options for vegetarians, vegas and carnivores alike. My black bean taco was delicious and we went back and got more the last day of our trip. Everything was fresh and delicious and I wish we had a location our where I live in Arizona. Fun environment as well

While drinking at the Yee-Haw Brewery, my boyfriend and I had become famished because we hadn't eaten dinner yet. After deciding we didn't want Prince's after having it twice that week (thou't shall not judge), we opted for the tacos. 

MY boyfriend wanted the Pork Belly and also the Lump Crab, but they were sold out (it was around 9pm, so understandable). 

We ordered queso (DELICIOUS), and the following tacos: Ceviche Shrimp, Buffalo Chicken, Lamb Gyro, Thai Peanut Chicken, and the Duck w/ Mole Sauce. 

All of them were REALLY delicious.... the shrimp ceviche was traditional and served cold inside the tortilla and it was probably my favorite. 

I liked having the option to eat something besides bar food, and I would honestly go here even just to eat if I'm not in the mood to drink! Totally visit it if you're downtown and craving some unique and flavorful tacos.
vpYzoIP0JKb2i-jEfmnkDA:
I've kept my pizza expectations very low in Nashville -- no offense to the city, it's just not New York. But, just the photos I saw of Midnight Oil got me excited. It looked like the NYC slices of my dreams, and I appreciated the fact that other local food reviewers were the ones sharing the photos and rave reviews, not the restaurant itself. Its own social media is vague and humorous -- again, reminiscent of a good pizza place in the Northeast.

I got the Sweet Heat pie -- capicola, jalapeños and honey -- and an order of buffalo wings. Both exceeded my already high expectations. I usually don't get jalapeños on any food I order out because measurements are usually way off what I'd prefer -- either way too spicy or two jalapeño slices on the whole thing. This was the absolute perfect amount. And despite the pizza being huge (and not too expensive for the size at $22), the crust maintained that perfect thinness without being too crispy or floppy.

The wings that were wings, not drums, were great, but that's always a preference thing for me and my own fault for not asking for all wings. The buffalo sauce was also the perfect amount of heat to still be able to taste the pizza in the next bite. I'll have to try the highly-favored centennial wings next time -- whatever that sauce is. I'm in.

Five stars for friendly service.  4 stars for pizza (New York-y but not moreso than Five Points or Joey's as other reviews suggest).  Three stars for the chicken skewers which were hot all the way thru but a little too soft inside.

Excellent pizza, not that I should be surprised that the owners of Centennial deliver great food here too.  Expect a lot of pizza...delicious pizza and their wings are always best with centennial sauce.  Enjoy!
sfnB6QVAZAkXijRWs7adUA: Food is delicious, staff is very friendly. Be prepared to hang out for a bit before being seated, but grab a drink and head into the little alcove while you wait. Adding this place to our local rotation!
Yumm!! Great service, the chief delivered our food personally. No reservations... crowded and crazy busy but loved it!

Exceptional dining experience.  Very fun and authentic atmosphere of a cozy, chic French bistro.  Real French family run, where the co-owner and chef check on every table to ask about the meal.  My wife had the steak frites and I had the duck, both of which were fantastic and we shared an onion soup.  Just a very pleasant and fun dining experience and surprisingly affordable as well.  Will definitely be back soon, toughest part is getting a table with a no reservation policy.
meah5bJYnP24TH6b-8r_Eg: Loved this restaurant and can't wait to return! I had the lamb dip sandwich and corn fritters. It was a great vibe with outdoor seating. I also love that it is a dog friendly place!
Fantastic food and staff! Affordable, creative, and delicious dishes. Definitely going to be a regular spot for me.

Found on yelp. What a hidden gem. Great food atmosphere. Prices. Wow. Ordered the lamb chops it was delicious came with rice and peppers and onions. I also ordered the falafel and coffee. No complaints at all. Everything was perfect. Coffee was good. Gretchen served us and was super nice. She even had heavy cream for the coffee, my pet peeve. Coffee and cream people not coffee and half and half. Super affordable super delicious. I am sure that Peachtree drive in will become a staple for this awesome town. Thank for the fine work.  It shows. Much needed for this day and time
IWRClXjIBarIbY7WUWV-ug: Hands down one of Nashville's unique treasures!! It's crazy to get such specialty food, from a top notch chef with premium ingredients at an affordable price. The interior is beautiful & every dish is a work of art. Run there! 5 stars

Today was our first time at Locust, and if I could give more than 5 stars, I would. My wife and I had fast and friendly service, and the food was fantastic. We ordered some wing skewers, the cucumber salad, crispy dumplings, and the numby noodles. The unique flavors here are a must-try if you're in the 12 South area.
Amazing! We had everything on the menu and it was so good. The steamed dumplings are the best I've had since Din Tai Fung, so tender! Great service, too. Make reservations.
uaX77w8_uIhu9EwxpdwOZQ: I was driving through town and was hankering for a sandwich. This is an excellent joint with fast and friendly service. It's Vietnamese good with a twist. Most importantly, Everyone there were wearing face coverings. Always get the cucumber limeade, it's refreshing good.

This spot is a new favorite for me. The produce is really fresh, the in house shallot Mayo is on point, proteins are tasty and tender.... all of that is secondary to the Bread. The bread is special and some of the best I've had in Nashville.

This has become my favorite Vietnamese spot in town! The amount of fresh herbs used and quality of ingredients reminds me of LA's Little Sister. Highly recommend and they deliver on Postmates.

Have tried the pork bahn mi, shrimp and pork spring rolls and the impossible meatball vermicelli. All excellent! Welcome addition to the more traditional spots in town.
iM0M83HT7qmLfZF8xjDHxw:
This place literally just opened and it is hot! Loved it! Everything was just so pink, so cute, and the decor was great! 

We didn't have reservations but the hostess was able to squeeze us in at a table but told us there was another table being seated in an hour. We took it anyways because we wanted to try it out. 

The cocktails were amazing! The oysters were super fresh and tasty! The mussels, I've never had them the way they were served, but those were great too. 

Grwat

We got a reservation for dinner at 3:45 pm on a Wednesday about 2 weeks prior to our visit.  Reservations are definitely recommended!  The inside decor of this restaurant that sits on top of the Graduate Hotel can be described with the word pink.  

For dinner, I had the mussels and fries.  The Nashville Hot butter was definitely the winner of this dish.  I also had the Sweet Tea Meringue with Peach ice cream dessert.  This one I would pass on if I was to return. 

All in all- White Limozeen is an experience.  Be ready to go all out and take advantage of the Instagram photo-ops outside!
Zack took care of us at a high top near the bar and we had such excellent and attentive service. Nice job team White Limozeen! See you guys soon!
tZCa7lmhBDvyObpxBwI69Q:
Stop reading and just go- you'll love it. 

The staff is extremely friendly and welcoming to any questions you might have on the menu and even helped recommend a few boba options catered towards my taste preferences.

The BBQ Pork Banh was so good I had to message my friends to come try this place immediately after the first bite. The Wild Tiger Boba was not only outwardly aesthetic but also tasted heavenly. If you're familiar with the original Tiger Sugar, this is way better, hands down. 

Can't wait to try the whole menu!

Coming from NYC I was shocked that this place could be so darn good. Aside from having a lovely staff and their cute little bar area. They have amazing food. The boba tea is so good. The wild tiger drink is so yummy and sweet and the boba is warm and delicious. Their tea's are so good the lemongrass is grown by them! I highly recommend having the bbq pork Banh mi or the bbq pork in the vermicelli noodle bowl, but honestly you can't go wrong with anything there. Even the prawn chips are delicious. I dare say it. Best Banh Mi place in Nashville and best boba.
This is a hidden gem. Literally everything is delicious. A banh mi and some Bobba Tea at this place is on point
4gYnn4o8Pnf_8205TObRTQ:
This is my first time here. Found them on Yelp and decided to take an Uber over. I had #39 which is the Parrilla Mexicana. These are fajitas with choice angus beef, chicken, carnitas, chorizo, shrimp grilled with onions, peppers and tomatoes. 

This was excellent. Great flavor and seasoned perfectly. There was a perfect ratio of meat and veggies. Most places give more veggies and are cheap on the meat, not here!  

The chips are warm and crispy. The salsa is perfectly flavored. I got a side of the spicy salsa. Beware, it'll take you breath away but I like to mix spicy  with regular  for the added kick. 

I also had margaritas which are petty much perfect anywhere. 

The staff are extremely friendly. I couldn't have asked for better service, better food or better margs.

We ordered for the first time on May 5, 2020, on Cinco De Mayo. This apparently was a very busy evening for the restaurant. It took a while to get to speak to someone at the restaurant to take our call as they were taking orders from other customers. However, the wait was well worth it. The food was fresh and plentiful! We ordered chicken fajitas, a chimichanga, Pollo fundido, cheese dip and guacamole.  Everyone was very complementary of the taste. This is not the closest of the nine locations but the one that was the close had a long line outside and we were never able to get through on the phone. Staff was great at the East Nashville restaurant. We will definitely order again and visit this restaurant when it is safe to return to eating inside. Until that time, we will travel the extra miles and order takeout.

Thankful that this wonderful establishment was open to fill my voracious appetite tonight. Ordered fajitas to go, and they had them steaming hot and ready to go when I got there 15 min later. I gorged myself and still had about half my meal left, plus a giant, complimentary bag of chips and salsa. This place knows how to treat a customer!!
a7XBfJd0aEo-UpPXetH2JA:
DC (During COVID) from what I can see the lady working is wearing masks. My only concern is there are no gloves. I saw her took payments and got back to cooking without washing or sanitizing first. 

I was craving something nice and warm and I discovered this food truck. Man, that birria ramen and taco is so good! Even though its Tapatío instant ramen I wasn't mad. The ramen was full of meat and packed with flavor from the broth. This food truck is now on my go-to places for comfort food. I can't wait to try the tacos next!

Swing by Tacqueria El Gum for some amazing tacos  with some of the lowest prices in town! I highly reccomend their Birria Tacos - some of the best I've ever had! One of the most slept on food trucks on in Nashville! 

Pictured: Carne Asada Tacos, Birria Tacos, Carne Asada Fries, and a Quesadilla

This comes as a shock to no one, but I found out about Taqueria El Gym via Yelp Nashville's Instagram account when they posted a mouthwatering photo of their food a few months ago. I bookmarked the business, and was in the area today which was a perfect opportunity to sample this food truck gem. This is a stationary food truck located in the parking lot of a gas station, which in taco truck world means it's legit and will very likely be delicious. My BF and I ordered a pretty large meal... steak and chicken burrito, tacos (chorizo and al pastor) their Mexican ramen dish and a tamarind aqua fresca. For a little over $20 (CASH ONLY) we had a feast of authentic Mexican food heaven. The ramen was rich and flavorful with a lot of shredded meat in it; I think shredded beef. The burrito was huge and everything was freshly made and delicious. Plus I ended up with some kind of fried taco/quesadilla combo that was out of this world and a bonus that I didn't even order! The employee gave me a nice amount of red and green sauce which is my jam and amazing. I'll be back to try more of their food. It's so good!
a1ubUzKI23pzTZNvMXU92w: It's really good food down there I from Texas Austin I just come visit Nashville Tennessee and it's really good  you should be trying and they have beautiful Peter bread

I had been hesitant about going because of a Yelp review that said it was recommended women be accompanied by a man. Hogwash, on my 2nd trip, I went with a female friend plus there were two young Middle Eastern women there unaccompanied (although with scarves on). And the fear of being thrown out because of my dress was entirely unwarranted since I also saw Middle Eastern women in western clothes (no scarves) on my first trip. 

First trip: I opted to go with a male friend. For $11 I got - my choice of 3 styles of rice (plain, with saffron or with meat or veggies), 2 dishes (an eggplant stew & a spicy beef dish), roasted potatoes & carrots, my choice 4 soups, Iraqi flat bread that looked very similar to naan), a small salad and a drink. It was enough to feed two people. They had bottles of sriracha on every table which I thought was funny. Obviously, the Thai people stumbled onto something amazing and universal.

Second trip: Awesome lunch with a female friend, we got there just as the food was coming out. One gentleman let us take a picture of his family's breakfast made fresh consisting of a delicious looking Iraqi omelette, veggies with chicken livers. I personally don't like liver but the omelette looked so good I may have to order it. For the same $11, I got the "melt in your mouth" curried beef, chicken with peppers and roasted eggplant & tomatoes, pita bread, soup & salad. My friend took home half her meal plus two lamb shanks cooked in two different styles of sauce ($4 each) for lunch during the week.

I would highly recommend this place. The food is cafeteria style. You should buss your own tables, but that's a small price to pay for the flavor and quality.... I have been there a couple times since but won't bore you with the details. I'm going back soon.

Went here on international food crawl and it was fantastic. The dolmades, the falafel, the beef - everything was delicious. The family who owns it were there and were very friendly. I can't wait to go back
F2We667rrIlAN2NBFpqRtg: New place on the West End side of downtown. Delicious and fresh! We tried the Mango Passion and Detox juice. Both were very fresh and plentiful. It was early so it wasn't busy. It was fully staffed and everyone was friendly and helpful. I will certainly be back.
We had an awesome breakfast at Grain and Berry! The smoothies were perfect! It was so delicious we returned a second time the following day.

Had a delicious smoothie at Grain and Berry (the Health Nut) - I will be back to try the açaí bowls and other things on the menu. The location is a bit hard to see, on the side of the Moxy hotel--but worth finding!
V1ZN0CuQ0xfGzmHZLJ_v8w:
Copper Branch is a newer spot, so it actually didn't come up in my initial research of vegan restaurants in Nashville. I expect the return to office will bring them some good business. I think it may be a Canadian chain, but I didn't mind that. I really appreciated the menu selection. Not too extensive, but enough to offer some variety.

I landed on the Aztec bowl, which was super tasty. It was a great mix of flavors. I only wish I would have been able to have more guac and tofu. I did that to myself, though, because I only ordered the mini. Nevertheless, I do think it was still a good portion, though I could have easily downed the full size.

I was really interested in trying a number of the sides, like the poutine, nachos, or keftedes, but ended up trying the wings. I certainly regret that decision. I thought the flavor and texture were both just awful, and I didn't even finish them. I considered taking my rating down by a star because of that, but I didn't want to discredit the potential goodness of the other options. The aioli was actually really good, though.
This place is great, and the bowls are such a great deal. You get so much nutrition for a reasonable price.

Don't knock this place just because they're plant based! You seriously wouldn't know based on how they cook and season their tofu and tempeh!

The Portabello mushroom burger was fantastic as was the TLT -tempeh bacon, lettuce and tomato sandwich. We also got a bowl and boy was it filling! Filled with quinoa, tofu and all the veggies. Can't wait to come back!

If you're gluten free - they have a lot of gluten free options as well!
vLzNvHifIWgPCqDqv98C2Q: Great food and great service! Everything was fresh and delicious. There were some first week hiccups, but the manager went above and beyond to make it right! We will be back and it's nice that they are so close and I don't have to go to Brentwood.

BBQ Sandwiches are great, ordered through Uber Eats and got a side of their fries and mac and cheese. Their serving for the Mac and cheese is a bit small for the price ($5) and the only thing that ruined my experience is that my sandwhich did not come with BBQ on the side when I requested BBQ sauce on the side instead of on the sandwich. So I received a BBQ sandwhich with no sauce. Aside from that, the taste is good.

I was glad to see the Corkey's location in Nashville. Great service at the drive thru window by Kenshena. I love the potato salad; however, it is little pricey for a quart, $10.99. I hope this location will get corn muffins like they have in Brentwood. I was told they only have rolls at the Nashville location.
SlvVcacPWf6qMLFH6lPQiA:
What a wonderful and authentic place.  Loved the food.  Very nice selection and everything was very fresh.
The lady did not speak much English but was very friendly and accommodating great service

We stopped in for lunch at 1:30 on a Wednesday afternoon.  There were a handful of table's available.  The restaurant was very clean, including the restrooms.  We ordered a couple entrees and soup.  Everything was fresh. The meats were all juicy and tender with great flavor.  Helpful staff.

I'm Colombian and I'm very picky about Colombian food. This was recommended by another Colombian friend. I went there and I was surprised to find out that it's at a gas station. At first I thought they probably only had some empanadas and simple stuff but omg.... they had everything! And the service was amazing! Super friendly people! A true gem! ¡El mejor restaurante de comida colombiana en Nashville!
_d34cqarF3WWEP7m3EUp-g: Absolutely loved our dining experience last night at the Continental. Sean Brock's attention to detail in his creations are unmatched. True perfection. The Prime Rib was OUTSTANDING. Not to mention all the other dishes too. The oyster was also perfect. The staff was also wonderful. We will be back!

Living locally we'd heard the buzz, and seen that reservations are tough to come by. I happened to be on Resy early one morning and a 6pm slot at The Continental was open that evening. I snatched it. The ambiance is lovely, the food is as expected, overall very good (the fish a bit overdone). Yet the service could improve. The Prix Fixe meal comes at an acceptable pace but the desire to clear doesn't. Three times we had to decline removal. Once I had a forkful in my hand, obviously not complete. We also had a bottle of wine with our meal, that we didn't complete. When asked if he'd cork it to go our server replied, of course but there's only a tiny bit left. There was 1/3 of a bottle left; no idea where that was headed. We will go back, hopeful the service gets better.

Had a wonderful experience at @thecontinentalnashville by Chef @hseanbrock. Pâté, potato pave, Continental roast, asparagus chartreuse, house rolls, and some ice cream as dessert. I am stuffed. Definitely want to go back. Swipe to see the shots from the meal!
0ziXZC3-WTI6Ynrpoc50wQ:
My company had a private dining event here in their private room and everything was phenomenal - the drinks, service, food, and atmosphere! Parker was our server and he was the best. Great service and he gave the best recommendations!

Seafood tower: phenomenal seafood and very fresh variety - lobster, crab salad, oysters, poke 

Low country bbq shrimp: shrimp with toasted focaccia dipped in a creamy sauce. This was my favorite dish. The sauce was so flavorful and went so well with the shrimp and bread. 

Catch of the day: sea bass was cooked well with the pork belly and fingerling potatoes and the sauce was very flavorful 


Buttermilk cake: one of the highlight of the evening - butter milk cake with fig, mascarpone, earl grey fluff. Light and not too sweet. Great end to the night. 

Drinks were phenomenal - flavorful Craft cocktails. 
Flying High: my favorite. This is for the gin lovers. It's a nice purple color and light, kind of a floral lavender taste. 
Viper : very refreshing, strong cucumber taste with a hint of chili sprinkled on top but not too overbearing
Moment of clarity: great tequila /peach drink with a hint of eucalyptus.

We're huge fans of 5Church Charleston and 5Church Charlotte so we were excited to see 5 Street Group bring a location to Nashville. It's definitely a moody, trendy vibe inside which is unique to see in Nashville but needed! Despite being new, our waitress and service overall was attentive and prompt. The cocktail, the viper, I wouldn't recommend unless you love cucumber (it's like being at a spa, way too much cucumber, not enough of the other ingredients). Our entrees, the fried chicken and poke bowl were really good and to top it off we got the Baked in Tennessee dessert which my husband said was one of his favorite desserts ever. Also, love the kitchen initiative! Highly recommend!
have come a couple times since they opened a few weeks ago! Very accommodating for creating vegan dishes! A good selection of gin and whiskey. Hope the expand the menu and create more options!
-XXLRkZFz4DFGCyqTVIPqA: My husband and I got the party plater for two and it was amazing! The quality of the sushi was fresh and sooo tasty. Our waiter Ryan was amazing and so informative about the menu! We will definitely come back again.

I finally got to stop in the other day for a late lunch and was SO happy I did!  The food was superb, our server (Ryan) was incredibly helpful in recommending a sharable platter and the atmosphere was spot on. 

I am literally going back tonight. 10/10 recommend.

The menu was limited that particular day, yet there were more than enough tasty option. Their prices are fair and the food is fabulous! John gave great service and if I were back in the area, I'd order a roll or two or ten.
ctSUBPw0Y7KDiZmBhKmiww: Awesome new spot in East! I ordered a bacon, egg and cheese on an English muffin and it was incredible! You could tell the English muffin was homemade--way more delicious than an ordinary one. Their bacon also was  excited to have them in town!

Stopped here yesterday on my way out of town after a visit. Great friendly service and the salmon and spinach egg bite and biscuit sandwich were spectacular! Hope to be back next time I'm in town. Support local!

Our group ordered english muffin breakfast sandwiches with bacon, biscuits, and reubens. Everything was fantastic. That Russian dressing is tangy and sweet and savory. A+ addition to the neighborhood.
oU2dCy6Vag6HuF_SpsseZg: Great food . Sat ourselves. Food came really fast . Wasn't a busy day , Recommend the Birria

I was in the area for a class and found this cute little taqueria! It has a decent sized parking lot. It is pretty small on the inside with maybe only 6-8 booths and tables. There are a few outdoor tables where you can sit and they will come out and bring your food to you. That is what I opted to do since it was a nice sunny day.
I ordered the 3 taco special that comes with one side item. I decided on the discada, the grilled mahi mahi and the carnitas tacos. For my side I got fried plantains. It didn't take too long to get my food after I ordered and when it came I realized my eyes may have been bigger than my stomach! The taco sizes and the plantains were huge! The plantains were delicious. They served them with a sweet creamy sauce that was so delicious. The tacos were absolutely amazing and close to being the best I've had in a long time. They were loaded full with the proteins and had just the right amount of toppings. They were in soft double shells and they tasted really good also. 
If you want some authentic Mexican cuisine you definitely need to give this place a try! If I had been around longer I would've loved to try more things off the menu!
This place rules. I always get the 3 tacos and yuca fries combo. I'm never disappointed.
4_IhZZcWHlIoRd681053Eg: This place is great!!! It's always open when it sassy (unlike the Hillsboro McDonalds). It's clean, fast, and friendly!
I always love this Wendy's. The people are nice and its usually very quick service. Have yet to have a bad experience here.

If you are dying of cancer, you should visit this location. Time slows to a crawl. Seconds become minutes. Minutes become hours. Hours become years. You can bring your dying loved ones here, and get more time with them. It could be a haven for the sick.  I walked in here at 27 and left at 88 but it had only been 25 minutes in the real world - a true rift in the time-space continuum.
SxHcct-HAqUR22bVyYq-0Q: Loved it! It was a Monday night and nobody was in there but us. It's small inside. The tacos were good! Veggie taco was surprisingly really good too! I wasn't sure what i wanted to drink cause i don't like sugary drinks and the bartender whipped up something so good! The music was on point! I think they had it set to Outkast radio. The staff was nice too!

The wings here were tasty. I got the lemon pepper and it was flavorful. The ambiance is casual. I went with my friend during the mid afternoon time and it was not crowded, which was great. We were able to have a conversation but also listen to the music.

Ok so I'm back! They have a DJ!! It's liveeeee! I really don't like the food though. I got chicken and waffles and honestly, hotel breakfast is better. My man Djing here now though so I'm still giving them a lot of stars but the food needs to be better. This place has a lot of potential and I'm excited to see it flourish! I love the free birthday booths 11-3 I think that's lit  they need more servers for brunch though. It's two but they moving a lil slow, for it to be just two they are doing it!
PJFhkLN8Bt6K0nAXESe2bw:
My husband and I stayed at the Capitol Hotel in Nashville, and Till Five Pizza was connected to the property. For being just a tiny "hole in the wall" type place, the food was really good! I ordered the cheese ravioli with white sauce. I've got to say, it was really good! The white sauce was super creamy, cheesey, and flavorful. The ravioli was stuffed really well with cheese and herbs.
My husband ordered the seafood fettuccine alfredo. It had a ton of crab, shrimp, peppers, and tomatoes. The sauce was really good. He very much enjoyed it.
Both meals came with handmade bread sticks. They were a bit dense for me but, he loved the handmade, yeasty flavor.
Overall, I'd say it was a win!

Stopped in around 2 AM, got a club sandwich, was warm and got it within 5 minutes. I've read other reviews, I have no idea what they were referencing. Our group was satisfied and I'm glad I was able to get a quick bite as this is only place in downtown Nashville to get food this late. I'm gonna reference a past review, some jerk left, the Muslim lady was very nice and polite to me, thank you very much!
Really delicious pies. I dont know what these negative reviews are coming from. Definitely try the meat lovers
c7dC_fiXquGaWXyLcENyaw: Super fun vibe. I recommend the "outdoor" seating. They allow pets on the patio, but it's heated and basically feels indoors. The pizza is one of the best in town!

Food was fantastic.  We tried an assortment of small plates and split a pizza among the group.  The tenderloin was amazingly juicy, asparagus were tiny, tender spears and the flavor was great throughout. Our server, Angela, was extremely knowledgeable about the menu.  Service was excellent and we will definitely be back.
The food and cocktails were great with phenomenal recommendations and service from our waiter Max; Definitely will be back!
8crJAfMgFRYwglnPquLZQA: LOVE Jojo's!! Very friendly owners, delicious Cuban sandwich and bonus that they have Jupina! Delicious Cuban coffee as well!  Do your tastebuds a favor and go check them out!

Ate here on opening day.  Got the Cuban sandwich and Yuca.  Very tasty.  I am definitely coming back to try other dishes. One small complaint, my sandwich had way too much mustard on it,  literally dripping from the sandwich.  I'm giving them a break as this was the first day and I'm sure they are still dialing things in.  The yuca came with a mojo sauce that was really good and topped with bacon bits,  which was a welcome surprise.

I hesitate to write this because I'm afraid if word gets out there will be a line around the block. 

That being said, this is the best Cuban sandwich I have ever had in my life. The meat is deliciously seasoned, it's loaded with Swiss, and the bread is authentic.

I would recommend ordering online before you get there or patiently waiting. It can take a bit, but I promise it is worth it.
jf2eOxyT1tlA1lyqKavPXA: I was visiting Nashville from Tampa, and to be honest, only stopped in because it was close to my hotel. I am so glad I did!!!  Delicious and fresh food. Was relatively healthy by Mexican restaurant standards. Honestly, the best Mexican food I have had in a long term. Apparently, the place is relatively new and I would highly recommend a visit. Service was outstanding and Lisa, our server was great and clearly proud of the restaurant. I wish there were Mexican establishments like this in Tampa tbh

Must say, this is probably the best service I've had in over a year. Eladio was our server, he took time to know our names and was very attentive without being overly intrusive. The chips and guacamole were fantastic and fresh. The carnitas tacos were okay, but I'd probably try something different next time. 

The margaritas were tasty. I'd recommend the spicy margarita if you don't mind a bit of a kick.

To start Eladio is fantastic...you couldn't ask for better service anywhere! 
Came here for brunch....I had the best mother Effin huevos rancheros I've ever had in my life...pretty sure if I'm ever on death row this would be on my last meal request list! 
The spicy margarita is amazing and a must have! 
If your in the area and unsure where to eat....stop looking and eat here! You will NOT be disappointed!
D4t9JnvIzJCmiL2PJn9zgg:
We came here on a rainy January morning and were blown away. You walk in the entrance and boom you see a bunch of croissants and delicious desserts to choose from. The atmosphere is super modern and warm feeling and fits 12 South great.

The food: We ordered two bacon squared cube croissants, a babka croissant, and a caramel pecan. 
The cubes were so good with the mix of gruyere and jalapeño jam not being too spicy, but hitting a great sweet/savory spot. The babka croissant was caramelized and filled with dark chocolate. Lastly, my personal favorite was the caramel pecan which was a salted caramel mousse with pecans on top and had a surprise dark chocolate mousse in the very center.

Overall, We loved the atmosphere and the food even more-so. We cant wait to come back and try more foods!

Cool vibe with great plants and welcoming staff!

Had coffee and pastries. All were top notch. The cinnamon twist with cream cheese icing was amazing.
The place is amazing, a diabetic dream!!!!   I think this is the sixth, more talented daughter's restaurant.  Just saying.  Try this place.  It is like a classy persons Willy Wonka!!
iGTrw3hno7Yf6e6B98VZMA:
I'd never been to a Sonic before and was so impressed! We got there after 11 pm, so the "order in the parking lot" option was closed. I was bummed, but then I remembered I've eaten in my car at a fast food parking lot before. 

We ordered a Footlong Chili Cheese Hot Dog (7/10), a Breakfast Burrito (3/10, bland and boring) and a burger with melted cheese on it (10/10, life changing).

Came out quickly. Super inexpensive. Staff was really friendly. Absolutely my new favorite post-bar snack spot.
wB9Toai1rQqeJtxsuNE-zQ: Just ate a hot catfish sandwich , greens and coke slaw. All were delicious, the staff was very nice and WOW , if you order the "hot" you better be ready!
Amazing food!! Sooooo good! It was also featured on Anthony Bourdain's show on Nashville and definitely did not disappoint.

Yay, online ordering! We picked up lunch from here and placed the order online. I think this is the only location that has online orders, since the website says "Online Ordering Antioch." You must pay in-store, though. 

I like to order other proteins besides chicken if the restaurant offers it; it feels more special to me. I ordered the fried pork chop with sides, and based on my last "medium" experience with Bolton's, I remember their spice level being quite strong. This time I ordered mild (the site said lite mild, mild, then hot), but I should have stuck with my guns and ordered hot. The mild had a light dusting of spice, but it definitely still had a kick for being mild! Hot next time, though. The chop was nice and crunchy while still juicy inside, and sides were delicious, too. One thing I noticed is that they pack the bread on top of the proteins instead of underneath. Nothing crazy, but I thought it was interesting. 

The location is easy to get to with its own parking lot. I love that they opened another location on this side of town! I'm ready for an Antioch revival. ;)
_XlYymFKwww0bGIgwvNbcA: Open Mike night Mondays 6:30-9:30 and it's free!  You only get to play one song, but it's cool because of the intimate seating.  #cramped

Came here on a Monday night on our first night in Nashville the Music City, and watched musicians try out at the Open Mic night.  Nothing can beat the experience as a way to start out enjoying the Nashville music scene.  The place was jam-packed with guitar-toting musicians and a few tourists like us.  The crowd was so respectful, attentive, and hushed during every performance, which ran like clockwork.  I thoroughly enjoyed 19 out of the 20 performers we heard, two of whom I though has voices of gold!  Definitely a treat to see this music scene, and see real life, how far musicians go (literally as some came from as far as Australia), to get a chance to make it big!

We had a lovely time here.  You have to queue up electronically to get tickets for a table and we were successful.  this let us walk right in.  It was a great evening of music, good food and drinks.  It really gave us a feel for Nashville.
EIeDtIG5WoD9Fv2ppF3Y-A:
I had a late night haircut in the area & we wanted to get dinner that didn't cost $25 a plate. I feel silly because I'd never heard of this place. Come to find out they've got 5 in the Nashville area & a TON of them in the US. Oh well, you learn something new every day. We stopped in around 6:30 on a Friday night. 

There was little confusion on which side of the counter we needed to go to order. We went the the "enter here to order" spot, then a woman told us to move down to the opposite side where it said "phone orders pick up here". The cashier was friendly & helpful. I ordered the Friday-Saturday daily special which is Taziki's pasta salad, which  is 'a bed of mixed lettuces with penne pasta and grilled chicken, tossed in our homemade balsamic vinaigrette, topped with tomatoes, feta, and fresh basil' for $8.99. We also ordered the hummus for $4.99 that was served with soft and baked pita chips.

We picked a table and popped our card on the table that tells them who ordered what. It didn't take but a minute for us to get our hummus. I enjoyed the hummus. Next time, I think we will just order baked pita chips to eat with it. Our food came out about 5 minutes later. The portion for the pasta salad was pretty big. I was surprised with the amount of ziti noodles. I was picturing more lettuce in the salad, but it's more like a few pieces of lettuce and a lot of noodles and chicken cubes on top. The salad has basil, feta and tomatoes on top, which make it taste really fresh. If you don't like balsamic, don't get this. I personally really enjoy balsamic so I liked it. The noodles and the chicken are covered in a LOT of the dressing/sauce. I think it would be just as good if they drizzled the dressing on the noodles and chicken as opposed to tossing it. Phillip had the lamb gyro and he liked it, but said the lamb was a little chewy. 

Overall, I really enjoyed this place. I will have to keep it on my radar now when I'm wanting something that is fresh and fast.

Wonderful meal at Taziki's Green Hills location! Fresh and flavorful and delivered with exceptional service. All the food is freshly prepared and you can taste the difference. The basmati rice was new to me and I will be back for more! A light, lemony butter flavor that perfectly complimented the turkey club ciabatta that I enjoyed for lunch. Also comes with chips and we tried the hummus and the chocolate cake as well. Both were SO good! Will be back as often as we can get there.

I got the salmon special and it was amazing! Very fresh and light. I also got a slice of chocolate cake. The frosting was a wet glaze that made the cake a little moist and didn't have too much sugar. Yum!!!
T2JzbrPH2GkY6XpyzNe7ow: Excellent food. We both had cheese grits, scrambled eggs (best i've ever had), and bacon, with shared half order of french toast (delish!). Good coffee too. Only negative is that it is very loud. However, we'd still dine here again.

There's a reason this place has so many positive reviews. It's consistantly delicious. They always have a great menu and I appreciate that it's small and changes often. It's a crowded little place if you go at the wrong time, but a mid-week lunch is a good bet for grabbing a table quickly. The steak ceasar is great, if it's offered, and you can't go wrong with any of the breakfast offerings. Really one of the best places in Nashville.

I went there for the first time a few weeks back. I ordered the Trout Hash and it melted in my mouth! It was amazing. They also serve Drew's Brew coffee which is so smooth and delicious. I can't wait to go back here again.
XrikSy0DQ_fhVCaRRBJI-g: Well deserving of its great reputation.Great food and service. Simply a restaurant that does not disappoint.

Excellent steak in a cool looking restaurant.

I think this must be the place to have steak in Nashville.  It was the only restaurant we couldn't get reservations for.  We ended up showing up at 5:30pm and begging for a table after promising to eat quickly.

We started with the cheese plate.  I loved the goat cheese and cow cheese but found the sheep's cheese to be a little waxy.  

I had the Wagyu filet and a side of the risotto hush puppies.  The hush puppies were filler and forgettable.  But the filet was perfectly cooked and so flavorful.  We gilded the lily and got the truffle bernaise, and it was awesome.

Service was spot on, and the cocktail list was fun.

Best steakhouse in the south, best steakhouse in Nashville and one of the best steakhouses in the U.S. Must haves on the menu:

1. Popcorn buttered lobster (what goes better with melted butter than popcorn and lobster?)

2. House made bacon with maple cotton candy (sounds crazy but it is one of the best appetizers in Nashville...hands down)

3. Vanilla Old Fashioned with Uncle Nearest Whiskey (bartender Luke makes this incredibly)

4. Red Wagyu (if you see this on the special menu..get it!...best steak around)

5. Duck tacos (one of my go to's)

6. Wagyu Filet (my wife's go to)

7. Cream Corn Brulee (best side on the menu)

8. Black Kale Salad (beat salad you've ever tried at a steakhouse

Sadly, I've probably had the entire menu as my family comes here at least a couple weekends a month. Highly, highly, highly recommend.
yafQdtH4wvyyD7lqjbzMhg:
This place is so cute, and I love popping by during the summer. While the Italian ice machine or something was broken last fall, I'm not sure if they're getting it back for summer, but my suspicion is yes. The ice is superb and a bargain compared to the price of a smoothie or ice cream at other nearby places.

The food is also quite good. The sandwiches are definitely big enough to share for a lite lunch, or you can share one and get a few cookies to top it off. A lot of the pasta dishes have a sort of surprise spicy edge, but they're all good. Also, most of the cookies are pretty good. Some can be a little dry, but go with your instinct and you'll find one that's divine. Also, the cannolis are raved about, but I wouldn't know, not being a cannoli person...

Service is almost always slow at this mom and pop, so allot enough time to eat there if you're trying to catch a bite before some Village function.

Savarino's is something of a hole in the wall. The place seems to be barely held together by the proprietor and one employee, working at a leisurely pace while a tiny radio blares. The service isn't stellar, but the atmosphere partway makes up for it, and the food does the rest. Savarino is a genius especially as a pastry chef, and his cannolis are the best in town. If you can't visit for a meal, drop by on your way out of Hillsboro Village, especially if you just had lunch next door at McDougal's or the Dog.

Saw this restuarant on the Food Network on "Dives, Diners & Drive-Ins". It is a very good casual eating experience.  I had the spaghetti with meatballs. The homemade noodles are one of the best I ever had.  The sauce is very good too - nice and tangy. My wife had the baked ziti with meat sauce.  She made a "this is too delicious" face and was reluctant to share it with me.  She liked that the sauces were different for each dish. We also had the gelato and italian ice, which was very good.  They have a lot of good desserts that will temp you.  Overall, a great experience and worth the drive.
_4LtVmrCdz40l1DnptAmgQ:
Where everybody knows your name.

Went here opening day for lunch as at the time there was nothing else in walking distance of my job on Music Row.  Jason, the owner, modestly came to our table thanking us for coming.  Wonder if he knew his little place, the first one to open at that point on the Demonbreun strip, would be such a huge success.  There are now Tin Roofs in several Southern hot spots!

Live music is almost nightly, often featuring successful songwriters, for free.  The weekend bands are always entertaining and pack the house.  

The food is much better than most bar food.  Sandwiches are large and served on delicious bread, or you can request it in a wrap like I do.  I love the tuna melt.  Chicken fingers are some of the best, and wings aren't bad either.  The quesadillas are a popular choice.  I like the spicy corn and I have them add guacamole, the BBQ chicken which sounds weird on a quesadilla but it's great, and the Sunny, also containing avocado.  The salads are HUGE.  I get the Mexi salad and add beans, jack cheese, and buffalo sauce as suggested by one of the bartenders.  One time it fed me, two of my friends, and my boyfriend.  Told you they're huge.  I'm not a huge chips and queso person, but their queso dip is excellent and addictive.  I like it with extra jalapenos.

The drinks, well, they're not for lightweights.  Bartenders here pour very generously.  You definitely get your money's worth and happy hour is every day until 7.  

The best thing about this place are the people who work here.  Many of them have been here since opening day.  They're sweet, fun, talented, give you a hard time, make good drinks.  What else could you ask for?  

My typical time to come here is lunch or happy hour.  Later at night, though I'm sure is tons of fun for the Vandy students that help pack it, is in general a younger, more college-y crowd.  There are still those professionals who just go to hear the bands and dance later in the night.  

I love businesses that give back and though I don't have any stats for this place, I have seen them contribute to and hold a number of benefits here over the years.  Also, if you have your own charity, talk to Jason about having a Tin Roof night with your friends and co-workers with a percentage of the proceeds going to your cause.

This was one of my favorite bars while I was in graduate school in Nashville. Its a little annoying that they make you pay a cover, but they always have live local bands playing and the tunes are always great. Its nice to find a place where you can dance, but also where there are places to sit and chat and just hang out with good friends. Recommend highly.
Great service,  excellent guacamole!  Very casual laid back atmosphere.   Live music and pretty good food.
