ReX09lhufLTAx19krkltDA: Nice little cafe right by Vanderbilt.  I got the Vegi Scramble plate and it was delicious!  They do this cute thing with the coffee that's classy.  Good service.

I really enjoyed my Tuesday evening at Fido's. Having never been outside of downtown Nashville (Ive only been here once before) I wanted to try out a new part of town. The neighborhood that Fido's is in seems to be full of an eclectic bunch of people. There were a lot of students (Vanderbilt, I assume) working on homework and groups of friends getting together for a quick cup. 

I had a turkey and brie sandwich on focaccia with a bottled pomegranate cranberry rooibee red tea (rooibos is my favorite so this was a great treat!). The food was great and I savored every bit of the sandwich. 

Plusses:
Free wi-fi
Quick service
Lots of seating
Good-looking menu
Breakfast served ALL day

Minuses:
Loud music
Drafty (i had to wear my coat because of the cold air. Middle of Oct and Rainy)
Pricey for a coffee shop (sandwich and bottled tea was about $12)

***I will go back!

Fido's one of the best, if not the best, coffeeshop that Nashville has. I know that those frou frou drinks can take a while, but if you don't have 10 minutes to go wait for a drink, just grab a cup of the divine coffee (they will make you love black coffee again where Starbucks made you hate it) and go. The food is also quite good. One of the best bargains on the menu is the Spuds McCheesy, which is a (normally) generous serving of hash browns loaded with cheese, tomato, and onion. It really is inexplicably delicious. The cheese grits are also the best of their kind. For lunch, all the sandwiches and salads are a good bet, and the sweet potato fries are quite yummy.

Fido gets busy, but if you don't mind the normal urban bustle, you won't mind this. The people are generally pretty pleasant, and the atmosphere is very nice once you get used to it.  Beware: If you and your friends/co-workers/classmates frequent the area, get ready to run into everyone you've ever known.
j5siJykDq7GWzgSdpOjhdw: We've eaten a lot of burgers in Nashville! This is our fav new spot. Service and atmosphere is casual but jjust one great burger! Check it out!

First time here. I had the Jalapeño Popper Burger and my husband had the The Elvis. Probably the best burger I've had. It was a manageable size that you could actually eat like a burger was meant to be eaten. Not some ridiculous monstrosity. Our server, Kelsey was super helpful and sweet and made great recommendations. We will definitely be back.

Wow. I mean just wow. I was here visiting and tried Jack Browns late night and it was one of the best burgers I have had. Went back for seconds the day after, it was that good. I got the Double Cheeseburger with bacon and Jack Browns sauce. What a treat. Will definitely be back.
NB5XTBuLVCn7c9Lu5SUqKg: I met for dinner with a group of moms adopting from India. We were connecting for the first time and the hosts were very helpful in getting us all sat together and introduced. They genuinely seemed excited for us and sent an appetizer to split. We shared almost all the happy hour appetizers and they were all delicious! I also went here for my birthday a few years back and I remember the staff also making the occasion special! :)

My go to Indian restaurant in Nashville! 

Warning: the spicy ghost chicken appetizer is HOT! The flavors are fantastic but not for the faint of heart. 

The chicken tikka over French fries is fantastic and the flavors are perfect! One of our go to dishes. 

The create your own with tikka masala sauce and shrimp is our personal favorite. I wish there was an option to add additional shrimp because the 6 that come with the order are eaten quickly but the sauce is just incredible. 

The garlic naan is another go to. Perfect flavors!

Totally enjoyed this place which I visited based on my Uber driver's recommendation. Loved the kale pakoras, though I would have recommended that the heat level be upped a tad, but still ... the pakoras were well executed. The Malabar Cioppino was well renditioned too.. my only recommendation to the chef to increase the authenticity of the dish to remain true to the Malabar coast is to consider substituting green mangoes for the lemon which really did not add the touch of tang that the native dish requires. But overall I was impressed and will most likely be back on my next visit for work to beautiful Nashville.
63wsqoXuGgehnuGrTW4y7g: I had been looking for a hot new place to host a party of 11 foodies in Nashville. They source food from local farms.  Found them on eater38 as well.  I broke my gluten free diet to indulge in the cornbread at this place.  I also enjoyed what they refered to as ribs, which ended up being some kind of delishiously stringy rump roast-an all time favorite of mine since childhood.  Not too keen on the blah exterior, but once you get inside, it does indeed feel more like a post-modern farm house.

This was one of the BEST meals I have had in a long time! Every dish we ordered was simply outstanding. The Barbacoa and Pecan Pie to name just a few. Cocktails were also very well-crafted. I highly recommend this spot to anyone looking to get away from the mediocre food served along Broadway. While there's no live music here, it's a foodie's heaven. My only complaint and reason for 4 instead of 5 stars: I wish the food had been coursed; instead, it was all sort of delivered quickly and without much thought for what might go well together or giving us time to enjoy each dish rather than feeling overwhelmed by everything! The server was friendly and things were quick, but sometimes you want to slow down and enjoy everything, especially when it's so good! I'd say if the service could be just a bit more polished, this would easily be a 5-star spot!

Holy yum, this place is good! I got the "giardiniera" (minus the shaved ham) and it was bursting with flavor, yet super simplistic with REAL ingredients. The puffed chickpeas added the perfect crunch. I'll certainly be back, maybe even this week for a second round. Two thumbs way up!
XeogL9kqQxKEO42ZEqMbvg:
The. Best. Chicken. Ever. That is all. 

PSA if you can't handle spices at all, do not order medium heat level

I'm not an expert on hot chicken but it seems these guys do it right. Chicken is HOT but not in a way that lingers. Love the fried grilled cheese. 

Customer service is awesome especially considering it's just a walk up window where all of the interaction takes place. 

The only real con is the fact there is very limited parking. 

Online ordering is super convenient and they send a text when your order is ready. The only time I tried to call in an order I got a voicemail. Not sure if that's typical or not.

If I had my way, I would've tried every single hot chicken place while visiting Nashville. Unfortunately due to limited time and other places we wanted to try, it wasn't possible... this time. :)

We stopped in to Pepperfire after deciding we should branch out from Hattie B's. The girl at the register asked if we'd had Nashville hot chicken before and we said yes, and she warned us that Pepperfire is 2x hotter than Hattie B's. So I ordered mild. BIG MISTAKE. I need to just go with my instinct and not listen to anyone when it comes to spice because the mild was literally no spice, just a lot of black pepper flavor. I'm Korean, black pepper isn't spicy to me. I don't want to burn my face off and not enjoy eating but I would like a bit of a kick if I'm eating something called HOT chicken. Next time I'm getting Hot. Anyway, I got 3 tenders, my boyfriend got the wing plate, and we were both happy with the food (even if it wasn't hot enough). The chicken was still juicy and delicious, so I can't be too upset that I was misled on the spice level. Pepperfire is a little different than the traditional cayenne pepper hot chicken spice blend; as I said, it's mostly black pepper. I didn't mind it though. You can call ahead and do take out or order from Uber Eats too! They have a small parking lot. Not in a touristy area but worth it for a different spin on this staple.
DG_7A-jMSFFYYqmldgGqNg:
The food is legitimately great and the coffee is superb. It is really unpleasantly loud inside. 

I'm not going to say it's terribly creative: Salt and Vine is basically the white box, tapas menu and solid yum of Little Octopus but with wine. I can't speak for the wine. It looks good but it's a bit pricey...and getting people drunk in a concrete "open space" box is a bit mean to the hearing range, but that's really my only complaint. 

The menu is great, there is a reason we are ALL ordering buratta: it's refreshing, delightful, creamy, light on the salt. The chicken thighs fell off the bone: divine, crispy on the outside with a heavy use of oils in the farro to punch in some refined flavor. The farro was overdone to my liking but I may just usually go al dente. The sour cream chips were a bit small for $7 but in no way diappointin and charmingly fresh with a smoked sour cream that was awesome and curious. Salt unevenly spread. The mussels were cooked fabulously, green burry broth was simple and packed with flavor. 

Absolutely solid, great staff, generally appropriate portions to spend, creative delicious, obnoxiously loud, and kind of a stolen but still cool look to it.
Thusly the best restaurant in Nashville. The service is impeccable, the drinks are beyond fabulous, and the food is incredible. Beyond wonderful!

Overall, good experience. Went on a Wednesday night, and the dinner crowd was pretty thin. Our waiter was knowledgable about the wine menu, and from his descriptions, I chose a glass of L'O du Joncier (Grenache), a solid Rhone red that had some intense plum notes and a lingering spiciness. I really enjoyed it, and although I didn't buy one from S&V's "wine shop", I would seek out a bottle in the future. 

For eats, we ordered the crispy chicken thighs, which, looking at past menus, seem like they may be a Salt & Vine staple, with the dressings they're served with dictated by season. Ours was fall oriented - farro and mushrooms. The chicken was perfectly cooked, and the serving was generous. The steak we ordered was also flawlessly cooked, served with a spicy chimichurri. 

At $12 for the wine, $16 for the chicken and $18 for the steak, this newer Charlotte Ave joint is priced fairly competitively with the new casual restaurants of its kind popping up nearby. I enjoyed my evening, and would come again - maybe even for just a glass of wine, or to try a mid-day coffee.
qdNRNugi-XWYFJ4N3DnInw:
As the new duchess I guess i should write a review : )  This is where I live, literally.  I'll run you through last Friday just so you can get an idea of what there is to offer.  After a brief stopover in Riverside Village we dropped off the car and walked up to 3 Crow (now with their new fancy-shmancy outdoor bar, loves it!)  I ordered my usual bushwhacker grenade and posted up at a table inside.  Yes we actually got one on a Friday, it was a-mazing!  Then we cut out around midnight and walked straight through Five Points to 308.  They just redid their menu (more on that later.)  Karoake anyone?  Lipstick Lounge has got you crooning!  Art?  The Building!  Patio?  Red Door East! Bustin' a move? 5 Spot! Family fun? Grassy Knoll movie night! 

When it's not nighttime you can usually find me at the East Side Turnip Truck, Green Wagon, Hip Zipper, periodically running with East Nasty or walking through the hood for some exercise.  Also now that the new artist co-op is up you know I'll be at Alegria and Goodbye Girls. Manscaping?  Wax has got you covered, or bare, (you can have whatever you liiikkkkeee......) Need a new do or spa treatment we have countless salons and the Magnolia spa.  Tattoos?  Yes we have that too.  Do you need centering?  Yoga your butt off at Kali Yuga Yoga.  Once you are prepped for the night feel free to eat at Marche, Drifters, Far East, Nuvo Burrito, Boston Batter'd, Mad Donnas, Rumours East and the newest edition, Five Points Pizza!  Tried your stuff at the tomato festival, sauce is on, but the crust was a little too thick to be NY Pizza.  Close though, closest in the South.  I'm looking forward to trying your stuff for real!  Dessert?  Pied Piper of course! No one starves in 5 Points! 

Oh and finally, how could I forget, Woodland Wine Store!  Probably my favorite part of Five Points.  5 stars every time I walk in there.  Best service in the city.   So there is my Five Points, walkable, likable, livable neighborhood that I call home.

Five Points is one of East Nashville's most condensed neighborhoods and also holds a large percentage of its nightlife.  The main section or "Greater 5 points" area is situated where several streets (including Woodland St and Main St) come together, uniquely forming a pentagon.  One can only conclude this is where the neighborhood derived its moniker.  All of the businesses found here are locally owned and represent a wide variety of Nashville talent and flavor.

There is a small collection of stores that can be found here including a bicycle shop, an organic market (Turnip Truck), a wine store (Woodland Wine Merchant) and a record store (The Groove).  There are several antique stores and an art gallery/community art center.  There's even a small tattoo parlor and a recording studio here.  

5 Points is home to many popular local restaurants that span a variety of cuisines.  You can eat Boston style seafood and sushi at Battered N Fried, have some fancy cafe style food at Marche, or enjoy a romantic evening at award winning Margot.  If you're interested in lighter fare you can hit up Z's Deli for a sandwich, eat a pizza at Pizza Real or grab a hot dog from I Dream of Weenie.

There are plenty of bars here if you're looking for a place to grab a drink or hang out with friends.  You can drink a wide variety of beer at the ever popular 3 Crow Bar, enjoy the sunset on Red Door East's patio, and watch the game or play some at Beyond the Edge. 

If bars aren't your thing, you can enjoy a glass of wine at Rumor's Wine Bar, grab a cup of coffee at Bongo Java or eat some delicious ice cream at Pied Piper Creamery.  5 Points also hosts the popular Tomato Arts Festival in the summer.  If you get tired of visiting these places, don't worry, there are plenty of other establishments nearby (including Mad Donna's, the Lipstick Lounge, Rosepepper Cantina, Sweet 16th Bakery and Portland Brew).

The surrounding neighborhood is quite expansive and encompasses several schools and parks.  The majority of the houses in the area are older Victorian style single family homes, but there are a number of apartments to choose from as well.
5 Points by the slice... Nom nom nom. Crunchy crust. Delicious sauce. Good all around.
zRrxC7NDkgoDKN55Blcong: Strikers and this fig with blue cheese devil on a horseback is really good. Good beer choices too. Plus some darts to kick my wife and coworkers butt. Well deserved rating based on walking in and half of the people wanting to leave. I think they were scared.

My new favorite spot in town. Where else can you get authentic fish and chips, play darts, drink a boddingtons on tap, and watch soccer with a Manchester United flag hanging on the wall? 

The staff is stupid friendly, and the food is above average bar food. As I mentioned, fish and chips are delicious. My wife got the curry (I know, curry at an Brit joint) and it was really, really good. It had fresh beets, cauliflower, carrots, and other fall veggies tossed in a really tasty curry sauce. 

Their beer selection is fab as well. You can get a selection of Brit beers on tap that you can't get anywhere else in the city. 

The only problem with Fleet St is the parking situation. Although, I pay $4 to park at the library and walk 2 blocks. It's totally worth it.
Love this place. Love the atmosphere! Kicked some tail at darts.
4lT3LIMRWCDrpxsjHWscIA: Confession: I went here TWICE during my weekend trip to Nashville.  I couldn't help it!  They made SUCH an amazing veggie burger and I effing love tater tots.  TOTS!  Also, the kitchy trailer park theme of the bar totally works for me.  Come here for the food, stay here for the cheap booze.  Bonus - you can get food late!  Perfect for after your night hanging at the honky tonks.

I'm surprised that I'm giving Paradise Park 4 stars, because I went there one time during the day and it was absolutely disgusting!

at night, however, it's tons of fun!  Very relaxed atmosphere, good band playing great tunes, and cheap beer!  $8 for a pitcher on a Thursday night...I'll take it!

Great place.  No cover charge, cheap beer, TV, and live music!  Decor lives up to the name but an awesome place.  Did not eat here but the menu looks good and apparently they have great burgers.  Will try the food next time.
zwGzwkVeYXE-tRisb8if7A:
We went here for a professional lunch a while back. Smokin Thighs delivers on one specialty, and it does it extremely well--bbq chicken any way you like it. We actually had a toy dump truck full of wings for each table. That's right: not a bowl, not a tray, not a plate but a dump truck. The wings were absolutely delectable and fittingly served as the seasonings were a bit of a grab-bag combo: jalapeños, onions, tomatos, cheese sauce, bbq sauce, and more. They were rich, just a bit spicy, and finger-lickin' good. Of the other chicken items that were ordered, I really enjoyed the buffalo chicken dip. Just the right amount of heat followed by a sour tang which marries well with tortilla chips. All of the offerings were delicious, but I imagine ordering just one would satisfying my bbq chicken cravings for at least a week. Heavy, honest-to-goodness bbq.

The bartenders are attentive, kind, and easily some of the best in town. I had absolutely no negative experiences with any of the staff here, and they were all so accommodating to our large party taking up nearly half of their restaurant. Drinks and food were delivered in a timely matter, and no one complained of any thirst or hunger for the hours we frequented the location. 

My only complaint would be parking. Try to avoid rush hours to this location as the lot is a bit small. You can always park across the busy street, but it's difficult to find a crosswalk nearby to make your way back to the restaurant. 

Indulge in the delicious chicken and order a drink from the lovely staff. Smokin' Thighs puts the happy back into hour.

Good food, close to the fairgrounds, two for one beer great place to grab lunch. The smoked chicken and nachos are amazing! Decently priced if you're not trying to spend $40 on lunch for 2. I will be coming back every time I'm around!
Delicious and not terribly hard to find. I loved the corn salad on the tacos and the mac and cheese is crave worthy
XbXoL8r97I_f5t0hv4BzIw: Came from out of town and stopped in from a recommendation.  Good food, nice staff, good mood and music.

One of my new favorite places! Kaz and his crew serve the drinks strong, the food delicious, and the prices reasonable. I do believe their Friday Happy Hour goes until 9 pm - that's a reason to visit all by itself. 

I've had dinner here a couple of times and brunch once. The Teriyaki burger was a highlight, as was the nutty apple salad. Actually, two of us split these between us and it was a truly perfect meal. As I write this, I am salivating and thinking of returning this week. Seriously. 

The place has been pretty quiet when I've been there. Not sure if it picks up later in the night or just has not been discovered yet. One time, though, some people came in and were setting up for a party of some kind. This would be a great venue for that! It has a fun lounge-style interior.

I'll leave it at that, for now. Just, you know, go to Myridia and see for yourself!

This is a new place, so it's still quite most nights of the week, but don't let that deceive you. It's a great space, with an amazing variety of good food. True "lounge" atmosphere. You an enjoy an entire night here with friends.
CPDaHi2bSPYWLBOSDGY7qw:
The people providing ratings for this place need to give it a break. I had a layover in the Nashville airport for a few hours and spent my first 30 minutes trying to figure out which restaurant wouldn't be the worst. I picked here despite the reviews and left quite happy. 

It's fairly priced and for airport food, it wasn't all that bad. The club sandwich was good and the potato soup was super cheesy. Broccoli was fresh and they had a great selection of sides. For $10, you can't get much better. If you are stuck in Nashville, give it a try. There are definitely plenty of worse options out there.

One last thing; in my experience, airport food workers are some of the most miserable people on the planet. Here though, everyone was incredibly friendly and was even offered a water to go. That type of service goes a long way.

Breakfast was good, and quick. Service was very attentive. Seems like a good place to grab a faster bite when you're waiting for a flight. Prices were similar to other quick service sit down restaurants outside of the airport.

It is foolish to have high expectations for an airport restaurant, but once every few years you encounter an exception. 
My wife chose the O'Charley's at BNA because it was quiet and the game we cared about was on the tv. She impulsively ordered the potato skins. I chose the Black n' Blue Caesar salad featuring sirloin steak and bacon. It was superb, and I was stunned. Even though the potato skins were an indulgence, they were very good. But the best part was the service. There were lots of staff checking on us frequently, and all were very hospitable. 
I hope the management remains the same for awhile.
_nqrmx5xKLQyrbmjhwiiuA:
I understand why people are writing such long posts about this place. It deserves every word of praise. 

The backdrop: The Catbird Seat newly opened late 2011, with Josh Habiger and Erik Anderson at the helm, who have worked under Thomas Keller (The French Laundry & Per Se) and Grant Achatz (Alinea & Next). GQ named it one of the Top 10 Best New Restaurants. My friends in Nashville told me about all the hype. A few days ago, I miraculously scored reservations here. I drove up from Atlanta to eat here. 

The Catbird Seat is marked by a singular sign over a brown door. You open the door, someone is already expecting you, knows your name, and escorts you down a short hallway, up an elevator, and to the second floor. A bright pink hallway opens up to a small white-washed room. $100 per person. Extra for wine pairings.  32 seats, majority of which are bar-style around the center kitchen. No menu. Give your dietary restrictions and they take care of the rest. Right in front of you.  

Each and every dish was spectacular. Stellar through and through. Pleasing to every sense. You savored each dish. But each subsequent dish makes you forget about its predecessor. The portions were just the right size; when you finished, you were yearning for one more bite ... 

Porcini mushroom and parmesan spread mini-Oreos. A triad of snacks: Island Creek oyster with cucumber, a bacon-infused cornbread, and Hot Chicken with dill pickle and Wonder Bread puree (an awesome acknowledgement of Prince's in Nashville). Tasmanian sea trout with pork lardo, clover, French pickles, and egg. Braised pork belly with celery, chartreuse, and beech mushrooms immersed in shrimp and jalapeno puree (heavenly). Alaskan cod with dollops of chamomile, lemon, cauliflower puree, and spring herbs. Roasted pigeon with greens, yogurt, and pigeon liver mousse (decadent). Beef wagyu slow-cooked with juniper sauce, chives, spinach, and trumpets. Brillat-Savarin cheese with rhubarb syrup and puffed wild rice. Cucumber and lime cream with toasted rice. Maple custard dessert with bacon garnish served in an intact egg shell (so unbelievable I started laughing uncontrollably). Pineapple gelee with oak ice cream, cherry crisp, and bourbon balls (beautiful and technically mind-blowing). Toffee and cream mini-Oreos.  This paragraph transpired over 4 glorious hours.  
 
When you take a bathroom break, pay attention to the walls. Framed menus from places such as The French Laundry, Per Se, Alinea, El Bulli ... 

The best meal of my life was at Le Bernadin. This is right up there. This was art. This was joyous. But here are the differences. 

Here, your dish is served by the chef himself, with an explanation and a story. You can ask questions and they will answer you. Hell, I asked them how they made those bourbon balls! When you're done consuming a dish, you look around the place and see dishes you haven't had (you become eager) or you see dishes you've just consumed (you start yearning). You stare straight ahead and watch the meticulous work behind each dish. If they're making that dish for someone else, you can see how your dish was constructed. You're persistently bombarded with visual cues, always thinking about what you had and what you're about to have, that you will salivate to new levels. All the while, you make friends with your neighbors, as you all share in the wondrous disbelief.

The ability to interact with the chefs was so inspiring. You see how hard these guys work. No runners. They're the ones who deliver the dishes. At the end of my meal, it was a pleasure to actually express my gratitude and appreciate in person. And the fact that they were willing to give me a list of personal suggestions for an upcoming trip to Minneapolis is even cooler. 

Josh and Erik go by their first names there. Josh told me he never felt comfortable in the traditional formal white-cloth restaurants. Hence why The Catbird Seat was born. 

This place is a success on so many levels. One of the greatest experiences I've ever had.

Really fun way to spend 4 hours if you like creative food and drink/wine flights
You chat with and watch the chef's prepare the nine course meal.
Not for steak potato people since you have no idea what you will be served.
Expensive but in New York City would be twice as much or more

Chef Ryan Poli has only been the chef at The Catbird Seat for a short time, but has created some amazing dishes that live up to the reputation of the restaurant. Since opening, The Catbird Seat has been #1 on my Nashville bucket list! I finally got to go on January 30 and it was phenomenal!!! It was worth every penny!! The service is stellar and the food is unique and delicious! Definitely splurge for the wine pairing!!! Overall, the best food experience of my life!!!
mV12ZKWE2WuYy0Mq3irzYA:
Amazing. Amazing. Amazing.

The Lamb meatballs is fantastic (reminds me of the flavors at my home), absolutely perfectly cooked, the sauce has the right amount of spices ( Guy Fieri ate this on food channel here,  he does not look like a guy to trust but he was right on spot here)

Even the humble gyro has a touch of perfection here, the quality of beef and more than that, the way it is cooked speaks volumes about how well Mediterranean people cook meat. They are the experts at cooking meat  and Athens proves that.

This is the Best of the Best of Nashville!

One of the best places I have ever been to The appetizers on absolute delight the gyro make me feel like I'm back in newborn and the lamb burger changed the way that I look on burgers . The service is high quality and I never have an empty glass I will be going again

I love this place. There is nothing on the menu that is bad. What is best is they have long hours, so after that late night at the office or studying, you can still grab some delicious food that tastes like home cooking.
rx4fcNjuqoaEugMPq5PBfw:
Yes, it lives up to the Hype! Pretty juicy burgers, soft buns and the beef quality seems top class. All the wait in long hot sun was worth it. There a simple rule to judging a good quality burger, the first bite must make you scream "GODDAMIT" and when you do that you are at the right place.

 Definitely a Nashville gem, a city devoid of good quality burgers.

I suggest order "Gabby" for a double patty or "seamus" for a single patty on your first visit.

A place can only be so good without management really getting their own hands involved.  There's an invisible ceiling when those in charge no longer deign to communicate with the people who come in and buy the product.

However, when you go to Gabby's, the owner, Doug, is not only there, but he's part of the whole process of making, serving, and ringing up the food.  He is very knowledgeable and friendly.  That goes a long way, since people know they'll get a good experience.

I can't speak about the burgers, but I do know a little about their hot dogs: they're delicious.  Fried on the griddle to perfection, you know it'll be a good experience.

I have side experiences that brought this place from four stars to five, making it a truly special experience.  The first was when I missed closing  by less then ten minutes.  The door was locked, but Doug himself came out, shook my hand, apologized that they already started to clean the griddle, and asked me to come back.  How could I blame them if no one had said anything.  The signs were clearly marked, and I was late.  That's customer service.  

The second experience was when I went in, and ordered just the hot dog, not ordering fries, with the excuse that I really am trying to watch my weight.  He suggested the coleslaw: I demurred, since I hate mayonnaise.  Not only did he insist that his slaw did not have mayo, when gave me my order, he included the slaw at no cost.  And you know what, it was pretty decent.  But that's how much he believes in his product.

I've had the fries, and they're great.  If I were a sweet potato man, I'd try them (and I know they're popular).  When I get the chance, I'll try a burger, but so far, Gabby's is an exceptional experience.  If you're anywhere near the baseball stadium, brave the construction and get down there: it may be the best lunch experience in Nashville.
Gabby's burgers are delicious but try the CeeCee Hot dog, from the "secret menu"!
Y96ZZf_fRyRbQtkcQ2Mjkg:
Review based on pulled pork sandwich.

My favorite BBQ is pulled pork and baby back ribs.  So I base BBQ on these two items.  When her during the week and had the pulled pork.  It happened to be on special.  $1.65 a sandwich.

The deal is outstanding and the sandwich is very good.  I think the pulled pork in TN is the outstanding!  I will have to try the ribs sometime.  Had ribs all over the country and the Memphis style is by far the in the country.

The service was friendly and fast.  Always a plus.

So for a real good pulled pork for a good value try Whitt's.
Great BBQ, convenient and a drive-thru! I prefer their pulled pork to some of the trendiest BBQ places in Nashville right now. Only thing that I don't recommend is their potato salad.

I'm going to have to echo Greg C on this one. Whitt's does not disappoint. From the ice in their sweet tea to the vinegar slaw, this is what I'm talking about when I talk about southern bbq. Pulled pork stuff baked potato? Yes please. Side of slaw and beans? Don't mind if I do. It's good, it's cheap. go there now.
yj9lGwTLo_R1rMxJoEiR8g: After braving Trader Joe's on a Sunday afternoon, we decided to reward ourselves with a donut from Donut Den. This was my first time here. I want to say I've had donuts from Donut Den before, but I've never been inside this location. Parking is a little rare in front of the store, we parked more near Parnassus bookstore. We came in around 2pm on Sunday. Two, high school girls were behind the counter. We looked over the cases, they still had a large selection for it being in the afternoon. We picked a regular cake donut and one with sprinkles and icing. Both together was about $2.25. Not bad at all. The cake donut was great. I liked the sprinkles and icing one, but I'm glad I split it because it's very rich.

I and my family enjoy the long-standing Fox's Donut Den. 

Our favorite? The Chocolate Butterfly, only found here. What is it? Think a chocolate version of a cinnamon roll.  They are huge! Approx $3 a piece. However..... They do not regularly make these. You must CALL at least the day before and order!

Will take credit cards (AmEx, V, MC).

You love donuts? Who the hell doesn't love donuts. I stop into Donut Den after my weekly Kroger run. Sure, the guys working there always seem to be daydreaming about some enchanted donut forest, but when you walk in they greet you with a great big smile. The quality of the donuts are superb: the frosting is flavorful and proportioned well, and the dough is fresh, soft, and prepared with delicacy. If you are looking to stop and grab a donut to go, Donut Den is the place if you are in Hillsboro village. If you are looking for an early morning date, or a late night snack with a pal, then Donut Den has the right vibe for that.
tmq5EHdmWm5GG0KkUw3nQg: Best Hot Dog i've ever had. Brought my Dad and Mom here just cause I could. My dad kept telling me how good their slaw was, even went back to buy more in a cup lol.. We ordered the Fried Pickle Wedges just cause I was curious as to how they'd turn out when pickle chips end up comin out all soggy.. Wasn't dissapointed. Came out crisp and tasted wonderful. As for the hot dogs.. I personally got 2 Texas hot dogs (Chili, onion, and cheese).. Had trouble finishing them both just cause of how filling they are so I ended up taking one home for dinner. The people that run the place are super friendly which just makes this place even better. So, in summary if you like hot dogs do yourself a favor and check this place out!

The more I visited...

the less I liked the dogs,
the more I likes the staff (great people here)

...and then I found the Brat.  Perfect brat with kraut.  Ask for spicy brown mustard, and ask for lots of it.

Outstanding. Outside of Smith's hot dogs in Erie, PA, these are the best hot dogs I've ever tasted. And they have the full condiment experience to boot. I can recommend both the Chicago dog (very authentic - mustard, neon green relish, tomato and a pickle slice) and the Italian sausage (get it all the way, with pickled carrots celery and onion, hot and sweet peppers and mustard). The fries are fairly thick, crispy and salty. 

Nice outdoor seating area in the back for a nice day. Otherwise, it's mostly a takeout place because there's not much room to sit inside.
6-5hbU746kMNgim0YOOg6w: Really great location and easy parking!  Have only visited a few times now since I live couple hours away.  Everything so far has been fantastic- Very tasty and healthy food and you can leave feeling like you had a great "cheat"  meal and no regrets of eating bad - Love the local craft beers (especially the Old Shed). Favorites are pulled pork on garden salad and Lime Marinated Chicken- Also the family packs are great for taking some UC home to enjoy - they pack very well and do a good job of keeping the hot and cold separate - Can't wait to enjoy again -

They had only been open 2 weeks when we visited. The food was great! I had the BALT sandwich, and my husband had the ham and pineapple rolls. We were slightly confused on how to order and get the food and drinks,  but they are still new.  Would definitely recommend for a lunch!
Great, fresh food! Even better service. We were treated to complimentary dessert on our visit there with my bachelorette party. I can't say enough about the service and the staff! Excellent.
Q1wTWAhOaoOZQRiGeL6N7g: There is absolutely nothing better than treating yourself to great Chinese takeout after a long week, and their number is in my phone contacts! They even have a dandy way you can order online, so you can order takeout from your Smartphone from the East side, and by the time you drive there its ready. PERFECT! Their soups are classic and delicious, and I have never had a bad experience there. Sitting down dining is always a hoot, the older ladies there are sweet and funny and always attentive. I tip well every time, no matter whether its dine-in or take out. You may even luck out with the occasional free eggroll if they're in a great mood!

Best Chinese restaurant in Nashville period!

My favorite is the spicy braised tofu and scallion pancakes for an appetizer. I have yet to have any meal here that was not amazingly good!

The portions are generally large enough that there is enough to take home for another meal later.

The staff is extremely helpful and attentive and the food usually arrives very quickly.

Highly recommended!

Extremely friendly staff! I placed an order to go, and they made me feel right at home, offering me complementary hot tea with pleasant smiles. The chicken lo mein was pretty good! This is my first time, but it will definitely not be my last!
C1rcsEknUcuA-qTQagdTBw: Amazing space honoring the history of the building, worth going based on that alone; but then there is the food. We had six people and everyone got different entrees but everyone loved their meals. The filet was unbelievable with delicious sides and the fried chicken and the short ribs were our groups favorite. Can't wait to go back and I'm excited to check out lunch and breakfast too.

Not only does the place a great music and food, it is a really cool atmosphere. A must stop. I had the potroast with mashed potatoes. Awesome. My wife had corn cakes, eggs and jalepeno bacon. So good. My son and I sampled the hot sauces. Eachwas unique and very good. Service was top notch.

Tom Morales restaurants always do well in Nashville. I think they are the most popular ones. I always like the atmospheres, and generally like the food. The same can be said here. I love what they've done trying to preserve the historicalness of the site with photos from sit-ins and reconstruction of the old diner feel. Good food too. But nothing unique enough to grab a fifth star.

I would go back. This will be a good place to bring Nashville visitors for lunch, or brunch. A space where children are welcome, but you can also grab a decent cocktail.
dNFGbezaxeBR71BdpspKqQ: Absolutely great bagels and a large selection of mixed cream cheeses. Coffee not very good, for some reason they make coffee weak in the south. Coffee is a 3 and a half.

My new happy place! First of all, the space is absolutely beautiful. It's a freestanding house-sized building on Belmont, close to Bongo Java. There is a patio, an indoor area that is like a patio (floor to ceiling windows all around), and then an inside area with a few tables. You order at the counter, where a plethora of beautiful cream cheeses, sides, deli meats, and smoked fish are on display. I wanted everything. 

I chose an everything bagel with gouda cheese, scrambled egg, bacon, and spicy maple ketchup. It came with a choice of sides, I went with the glazed sweet potatoes. The bagel sandwich was perfect! The bagel itself was the right combination of firm outside and soft on the inside. The sweet potatoes were really tasty; they were chopped into small chunks, soft, and you could really taste the glaze. 

I also had the rosewater yogurt granola parfait, that came topped with perfectly ripe blueberries and strawberries, and was drizzled with lavender honey. This is my love language- anything rosewater, berries, and lavender-honey will make me swoon. I refused to finish it so I could bring it back to my office to make the afternoon a little sweeter! It wasn't cheap ($7), but it was worth it to me as a special treat. 

I also tried the chocolate-dipped coconut macaroons. It was probably the best macaroon I've ever had. It was so moist, flavorful, sweet without being overly so... it was perfection. 

In short, I will definitely be back. While it's more expensive than your average bagel place, the food is superior and special. This is a wonderful addition to Nashville's food scene!!!
Excellent bagels, but chaotic ordering. They got order wrong twice and fixed it. However, the bagel was so good, I don't care.
kIEPaEAFR0QoFDqsE0elkQ:
This place was incredible! We went there tonight, I think it was opening day, and it was such a fun atmosphere with great service!

Many rare brews on tap, growler fills, pints and half pints. My husband and I both ordered hot dogs, the Italian and build your own, all served on pretzel buns! They were incredible!

Oh and my arm is now sore from playing too many dart games. Oh AND they have a classic juke box with some great classic selections. This place is a must-experience.

The Hop Stop is the best bar in East Nashville. They have the great servers and the most knowledgeable bartenders, and really great food. The black bean and avocado salad is my jam. I love that there's not only have amazing hot dogs and French fries, but a lot of lighter options that might be even tastier. Huge selection of beer, probably the largest in the area. But it's not pretentious! I don't feel like I have to be a beer aficionado to fit in or be worthy of the bartenders time. I always just say "surprise me" and they always come through with a new unique beer to try. This is my favorite place to have a drink and grab a bite to eat in Nashville. Keep it up Hop Stop!

LOVE!!!!

You need to visit Hop Stop in East Nashville, amazing craft tap selection, solid food, don't skip it when you check out the best area of Nashville, East Nashville!
YXaecOFzGVPMQIwx2mq7uQ:
Located in the Gaylord complex and pretty vanilla as far as sports bars are concerned. The wait staff were attentive and genuinely happy to be there.  Four different people engaged with me even though I was at the bar with a drink.  That sign of good work culture is better than a badge for management. 

I ordered the cheese steak sandwich and my server made it a point to get fresh fries.  Fry freshness is the foundation for my reviews in this sort of place.  The sandwich was perfectly sloppy with a white American cheese liquified by the slathering of sliced prime rib (I think) and grilled fixings.  She included utensils, enough napkins, and ketchup.  Priced a little heavy, but not by much.  It was what i expected with solid service at a price to be expected in a resort like Gaylord's. 
Thank you.  I would go back.

Ok so this place is very interesting. We had the best server in the world. His name was Daryl and he was very entertaining. I honestly think he should become a comedian. The food was great, I had ordered the cheesesteak. Now Daryl is an older man and he isn't afraid to admit it too. He told us a little story about his background of where he lived and how he grew up. He is very funny and good at what he does. He said, in good humor, that when he has a full on heart attack, he will become a security guard at the hotel. I highly recommend this place and please ask for Daryl when you come. I promise you won't regret it. FUN FACT by Daryl: this restaurant has 111 TVs and they all can go on a different channel.

This was my first visit to Fuse. My friend and I chose to eat supper here during our visit to the Gaylord Opryland Resort for Imaging USA 2015. We got there around 8:30 PM and waited 15 min for a table. Service was okay; our waitress looked miserable the entire time (She looked 8 months pregnant so understandable, just not enjoyable for our experience.) After waiting about 30 min for our food it finally arrived. I ordered the Thai Chicken Skewers and Hummus. Both were quite delicious. Overall I was satisfied with our visit, but there was definitely room for improvement.
inpHHalmEPkTMBg9yOWfLg:
We ate sushi on the 6th floor we order the spicy tuna rolls, tempura rolls, veggie rolls
All so delicious. I was left wanting more our table neighbors were kind enough to share with the rolls of the day. And it was amazing as well.

Not sure why this has such bad reviews, I came here today and loved it!! It's right by my apartment and my boyfriend and I did not have one complaint. The hot chicken and waffles are very hot but very delicious!

Finally found a regular breakfast place in Nashville there on our last day there!  We didn't even realize it was right up the street from our hotel.  All I wanted was eggs, home fries, bacon, and toast and that was what I got!  Also had some pineapple juice and coffee.  I asked for well done scrambled, got well done scrambled but still fluffy and tasty.  Lot's of bacon (as opposed to Butchers and Bee where we got two slices for $5, and not steakhouse slices).  The home fries weren't the flat kind but cubed potatoes with some peppers underneath ( I really have to remember to take pictures).  The toast was multi grain, thick and tasteful.  I think the bill for the two of us was about $30.  Maybe a little more than a typical diner but it was pretty much in the middle of downtown Nashville, so no problem.  Plus it was good and we were satisfied.  Oh, lastly, they put ice in the pineapple juice.  It's usually served room temperature, but that was refreshing.
3iSRhLUZfZSlLaQw-BzloQ:
The decor and atmosphere were awesome!! I would love to sit outside when it gets warmer. 

The wine and sake selection were great. The server was knowledgeable and brought a sample before we had to commit to a bottle. 

The food was good. Some things were better than others. We loved The Bomb sushi roll and the chicken wings. The sweet potatoes were bland and the White Dragon roll was boring. 

The blondie and coffee were delicious. Would definitely go again!

This place rocks! A delightfully varied menu, with many Asian-fusion dishes and many others on either side of the East/West divide. Terrific cocktails and wine list. Smart, cheerful, hip service. They have a lot of booths, which optimizes conversation. I will definitely head back there soon!!

"The Bomb". That is all.

....just kidding. "The Bomb" is a menu item at lovely Virago that I almost never fail to get. Virago isn't my go to sushi place but I can't say i've ever not enjoyed it - old location or new location.
6POxtHV_ZIpCJKBd3u_efA: Went with a group and ordered about 8 rolls. All were fantastic! Probably the second or third best sushi I've had in Nashville, and best in the Bellevue/bellemeade area. The presentation was above and beyond and the service was attentive, knowledgable, and very happy to serve. We went during lunch, with only a few people there, feels like an undiscovered joint for sure. We will all be coming back for more!

My son and I got excellent service there tonight. All the food was extremely fresh and tasted great. When I found out I was allergic to one of the ingredients, they went out of the way to get me a new dish that I wasn't allergic to. That (Beef and Udon soup) was excellent. They are very kind and attentive there. Will visit again!

They've earned this five stars. A group of 10 of us ordered sushi, yakisoba, rice, tempura shrimp and vegetables, and not a bad tasting dish was on the table. We shared sushi around the table so there's no telling what I ate, but all of it was good. Chicken yakisoba was definitely my favorite dish and the Dwayne roll was way better than I thought it would be. I'll definitely be going back for their lunch specials. Our meal was a little over $250 for everyone; drinks included, and it was worth it. And the crazy thing is that they weren't busy...at all! I've gone back once already and will be returning again very shortly.
XeeTJDWyvj6JSDxGgG4A6A: Uniquely fresh pasta with amazing flavor. Better than any I have had in Nashville!

Our first time eating here was tonight and we couldn't be more pleased! I tried the spinach fettuccine Alfredo and my husband had the porcini fettuccine Alfredo with Italian sausage- both were super fresh, cooked perfectly, and were pretty large portions. We got there a little after 7:30 and waited probably 20 minutes for our order, but it definitely seemed like the rush hour and the line had died down completely by 8. We will definitely be back!

This hidden gem was delicious! Before I started a night of drinking at the bar next door, I needed to get some food in my system and this hit the spot. I only got to try the meatball sub but it tasted amazing. I need to come back to try more of the menu!
0macJWiPlgnGl2SkTJCYSw:
The best fried fish I have had in years!  The batter was light, fish was moist and the tartar sauce was perfect accompaniment. My friend had the pickled chicken which was a unique and very tasty preparation.  
The restaurant itself is casual and friendly atmosphere.  In these nice warmer spring days, the patio is a great option for enjoying the meal.

i dined with two other friends August 3rd.  We were there for lunch.  I can recommend this restaurant as a go to place if you want to have a great lunch with favors that are out of this world.  We all had a different dish along with appetizers.  I had the shrimp tacos.  The way Chef B, I asked to meet him, had blended spices in these tacos was great.  My other friends had salad with shrimp, another win in the quality and the third friend had the tuna poke bowl. She described it as the Korean inspired  flavors on Hawaiian classic was  a new experience that she loved! It was vibrant with edamame with cucumbers!  

Chef B came out so we could tell him how much we enjoyed each dish and gave us a background on his reasons for each dish he created.  A quiet professional man that clearly was passionate about the food he creates!

Thank you Chef B!  A final note two of us had the lemon basil smasher!  That was simply DIVINE!

Service was prompt and staff was very attentive!
Absolutely wonderful atmosphere and fabulous food! Happy to find a new place to eat in and around Nashville!
6oWaOMzGPCe5fjZvINe6YQ: One of my favorite sushi restaurants in Nashville! They serve a large assortment of Japanese cuisine that includes quite a few other entrees and dishes besides your standard sushi. The Chirashi bowl is BEAUTIFUL and super fresh. Their rolls are all freshly prepared. Service is friendly but can be slow if they are busy. Overall, I haven't ever had a bad meal here and would recommend this place to any sushi lover!

Have been wanting to try this Japanese place for a while! The sushi was so fresh and very decently priced! Also the sushi chefs were Japanese snd truly knew what they were doing. Everything was fresh & tasty. Also they have a tatami room for group events!!! If you know, you know. 

I also went into the grocery and while it is quite small they have good things as well! Japanese drinks including cc lemon!

This would be two stars on the Coast, but definitely worth four stars in Nashville. 

Fish are fresh enough to be very tasty. The menu has both the familiar staples and some interesting twists. I just wish that fewer rolls had cream cheese in them. I just don't like it. 

The staff is nice, the food is good, the tea is passable. No complaints. This is certainly one of the better places to get sushi in Nashville. 

It gets a bonus for having an awesome little Asian Grocery Store attached. Any place I can get good sushi and then take home a bottle of Sriracha is OK by me. 

BTW, if you don't know what Sriracha is...you haven't lived. It tastes good on EVERYTHING!
6d25hRt6Hz4SPc9Ih327gw:
What I like:
1. Not only my favorite pizza in Nashville, but my favorite pizza hands down (coming from someone who has lived in Chicago and spent significant time in NYC)! 
2. Terrific Service- we hosted my bachelorette party here and our waiter was engaging and attentive- even treating us to stories about his own marriage and previous single life! 
3. Very accommodating to big parties- the back party room was the perfect spot for 10+ girls as they kicked off a night on the town
4.  The other (non-pizza) food is great too- try the garlic bread and apps for a yummy start! 

What I don't like:
1. Nothing!  Note, though, that it does get very crowded especially on Tuesday night slice night! 

Overall verdict: A must visit!
This restaurant has a great vibe-- good for dinner + drinks. Their pizza is among the few pies I will eat in Nashville. The fried ravioli are great, too!

There's things I really like about MAFIAoZAs and a few things I really don't. Overall, do it. I can't help it, I love most of their dishes.

Compliment sandwich time!
a) Food is awesome. Seriously, my neanderthal tastes what you to know that their pies, their pasta, their salads - all excellent in the mouth. Presentation is plenty good as well. I eat this stuff on purpose.
b) Sit at the bar where you can watch them make the food, if you dare. This is where the appeal fizzles. Almost nothing at MAFIAoZA's is made fresh to order. The pizzas are half-prepared before orders are placed. Individual slice orders are pre-cooked and just recheese'd and topped with your requests. I dont know if there's a great way to do this, but it still didn't rock my world. YMMV, I still gave you 4/5 stars. No hate.
c) Alcohol selection is rather good, prices are rather fair, particularly for the area, and the service is overall decent.


Other notes:
The parking situation is terrible. It's loud in there, no matter when you go. I'm not sure how they make pre-prepared food so good, but god blessem' for getting it done.
24eGG105XL98jPPKOgCRxg: My favorite bar in town. It feels like Cheers except there's usually loud music playing and no Ted Danson. I promise you that these are good things.

This sandwich is LEGIT! My husband and I came here on a Friday after leaving the main strip downtown. This was the best late night sandwich I have had in a minute. I highly recommend trying. It is worth the wait:)

Duke's has quickly become my favorite spot to frequent in East Nashville. You must do yourself a favor and stop by for one of their deli sandwiches. Every sandwich I have had at Duke's has been great, but the 2:17 special in particular is absolutely nuts. If you're not hungry, it's also just a great place to grab a drink and chat. Their beer selection doesn't change too often, but the selection they do have is good. The drink deal for a shot and beer for $6 is also an awesome way to start the night. They put on regular DJ shows as well, and it's an overall great place to spend some time!
dzAV7NCPW7PYWLYqgPE_GQ: Don't go for the bottomless $15 mimosas, it's straight Tropicana and no champagne. The food and atmosphere however was AMAZING!

Their brunch is so good! They were very flexible with my friends and I as far as our allergy restrictions went, and you can't beat the bottomless mimosas. The live music was awesome! The wait was not terrible either since I had a group of 12 on a Sunday at prime brunch time, and none of us cared since we had access to the bar while we waited! Haha. Highly recommend this place and I'll be back!
Great place to go to if you want a live band certain nights. Food is good. The setting is great as they have a upstairs and downstairs bar. Check the downstairs out.
C9K3579SJgLPp0oAOM29wg: We stopped in Nashville specifically to check this place out.  Fabulous!!  It was super busy but the food was amazing and the owner is a hoot.  We can't stop talking about it and can't wait for our next trip through town.

Me and my friends are in TN for roadtrip and we heard a lot of great things about this place. 

I love their DRY ribs!!! It is very good to eat it dry or you can always ask for BBQ to add more sauce on top of it. For the sides, we ordered the pork rinds, green beans and casserole. Everything is delicious. If I ever get to come back to TN, I will definitely come visit again!

I only get to Nashville once or twice a year but every trip includes dinner at the Peg Leg. Simply the best BBQ in town and as good as I have ever had. Try the ribs without sauce with the dry rub, the sauce is amazing but the rub is even better.
PAKZ3CLhtKQj8I8NvdfTtw: The food was delicious but our waiter led us to believe we each needed to order 4 courses which was misleading. Order your own main and share the appetizers and sides and save yourself a few bucks.

Tried this place after wanting to go there for months! It did not disappoint!

We went early on Saturday night and it was pretty empty, but we had a reservation nonetheless. We were seated right away. 

We chose Little Octopus because we were drawn in by the healthy menu. We ordered the mushrooms, the beet salad, the tomato salad, the chicken, the steak, and the Maduro Cornbread and ice cream. It was all spectacular! The portions were not huge, but they were just enough for two people to share each dish. 

Our server was really attentive and she always made sure we had plenty of water and anything else we may have needed. There did seem to be alot of waiters/staff hovering around without much to do, which was interesting, but I think effective to the overall well-run restaurant. 

Overall, it was a really great experience and I am excited to take my parents next time they are in town. The reason I give the restaurant 4 instead of 5 stars is because I felt that it was slightly overpriced. Yes, the food was good and yes, it is a high-quality and original place, but this is Nashville and not NYC. That being said, I would not hesitate to recommend this place to others, but I would let them know that it seemed a bit expensive.
Loved the idea of a shareable menu. Every dish we got was delicious and the atmosphere was beautiful.
GzOKM6vX_f7eG4jHMdkeDw: This place is good . The staff is always great polite and helpful. The food is always nice and fresh and one of the pluses is that the often change what they put on the buffet so it's not always the same thing!!!
Very nice atmosphere, food is good for a buffet. Never had an issue in all the years I have eaten here
The honey chicken here is some of the best I've ever had. I have also had the Garlic chicken which wasnt very garlicy. They do not offer brown rice. Reasonable delivery time.
bm5qHk46T4uGBxfaNejkyw: The customer service is the best in Nashville coffee shops! The basil mocha is different but really good!

You should definitely visit Zollicoffee. Even if your not a coffee fan, you will find something to love about this place. Less than a mile from my son's school, I find myself here quite a bit. They have interesting seasonal syrup flavors - and Zolli makes all the specialty syrups himself. Lavender, blueberry, strawberry, mocha cayenne, chocolate basil, etc. They have comfy couches, as well as regular tables. Lots of board games and some books to read. Outdoor seating w a giant Jenga game on a table. WONDERFUL employees. Always delightful. My favorite food item is the yogurt with granola and fruit. My son's favorite is the homemade Chex mix. My daughter is a huge cane of the fruity pebbles rice crispie treats. Oh! The rice crispie treats!!!! TONS of awesome flavors. I try not to eat sweets too much, so I usually only try a little free tidbit near the cash register. HIGHLY recommend this place.
Brent is the man! Really though, friendliest barista on the planet. And he has a cool samurai bun. Drinks were awesome! Glad we stopped in before a show at the Cannery.
VYlwU6ftaIF1WLTQcX-F3A: This is the best hot chicken in Nashville, at my favorite brewery!! Can't get any better honestly. Their hot chicken is incredible and you can even get a flight of every heat level. It's super crispy but tender, and the hot chicken spice is smoky, sweet, hot, and perfect. The potato chips that come on the side are homemade and delicious, and we always get fried pickles which are also great. I love coming here and getting beer and hot chicken, and do it almost every week. The service is also amazing- everyone is so friendly and kind every single time. I have zero complaints and definitely will be coming back all the time! I also have recommended this food truck to everyone I know because it's definitely a gem that everyone- tourists and locals alike- should try.

Appetizer smoked wings with white Alabama sauce. Yummy! The food is from a different company. I believe the servers are the owners. Great place to get a bite and beer. I'm not a beer fan no review on that.They have two locations. I visit the location on 5012 Centennial Blvd Nashville TN 32709.

Omg this food truck is amazing. I ordered the Carolina Style Pulled Pork with Mac and cheese and collards. The bbq is kind of sweet, with a molasses style base. It's a sweet barbecue and not too much vinegar. The Mac and cheese is a shell style mac. It was creamy and really good. The collards were also on point. I usually ask for vinegar but these didn't need any extra. 

The cornbread was sweet as well and not crumbly at all. Totally a winner here. Great food truck.
R36o1SecB5htL2hV-CvPPg:
This took the whole southern fried food thing to another level! Nashville definitely has a positive with this one. They are definitely competing with the new orleans-style restaurant right down the way. Yummy in my tummy is all I can say

I don't think you will be displeased with this place and definitely can share a portion on a good day. Eat well and don't get too full and stuff your belly because I can tell you that is super easy to do that. 

However do you want it? However do you need it? I know one thing you just need to eat it! Get that tune in your head and try to get it out. Eat well

This took the whole southern fried food thing to another level! Nashville definitely has a positive with this one. They are definitely competing with the new orleans-style restaurant right down the way. Yummy in my tummy is all I can say

I don't think you will be displeased with this place and definitely can share a portion on a good day. Eat well and don't get too full and stuff your belly because I can tell you that is super easy to do that. 

However do you want it? However do you need it? I know one thing you just need to eat it! Get that tune in your head and try to get it out. Eat well

This little place in the Farmers Market is a hidden gem.  The food is consistently delicious and the staff is friendly.  We especially enjoy the Shrimp with Green Chile Rice and the catfish.  If you are lucky enough to visit on a day they are serving beignets, you are in for a real treat!  If you feel like enjoying this delightful Cajun fare with a delicious craft beer and avoiding the hustle and bustle of the food court, grab a beverage at the Picnic Tap and dine at one of their indoor or outdoor picnic tables.
c1D7T81Yn37XxTtmncp8Ug: I'd been wanting to ck this place out since they opened but wasn't sure about kid friendliness (still not.) BUT, Im already excited to come back soon for a date nite. I'm giving five stars due to the awesome service I got from Penny the bartender on a drizzly hump day. I had a cold & was grumpy but she turned my frown upside down with friendly thoughtful service to a girl dressed in sweats. I thought their hip factor might not allow such things but I was in luck! I had a KY bourbon barrel ale at a great price ($5) & a turkey burger with kale slaw. I don't even  like slaw but their version was healthy AND delicious!  The turkey burger w mozzarella, pomegranate ketchup & sprouts was one of the most flavorful I've ever had. "I'll be back"

Delicious! I ordered a burger and sweet potato tots, when the plate arrived it was almost too pretty to eat! Everything we had was excellent. A little pricey but you get what you pay for. Service was great, beer was cold. We sat on the patio as the atmosphere inside is a little loud, but the patio was very pleasant. Small parking lot but they have valet. Will def be eating here again!

We went during the Family & Friends soft opening. The lamb burger and chorizo burger were both juicy and cooked to perfection. The pickled vegetables on the lamb burger were a great addition to lighten up the burger. The runny yolk of the fried egg on the chorizo burger is the perfect addition. I highly recommend the onions rings. I would suggest getting a side of the smoked mayo as a dip for the onion rings. The Krispy Kreme doughnut milkshake was simply delightful. A great way to end the meal. 

As far as ambiance, the modern red, black and white theme is playful. The only negative I would say is that the staff does not reflect the vibe and edginess of the restaurant.
GJtRRcx44M9ptMUYxccUeg: The BEST lunch I've ever had. Can't go wrong with anything on their menu; but my favorite is their Turkey Rachel. Desserts are the best too. Will always come back.

Absolutely delicious! We ordered the chef's breakfast tacos (comes with two) and drip coffee. The tacos were so flavorful! I really liked how they used corn tortillas and the bacon jam is perfect touch of sweetness to offset the avocado and bacon. Definitely the best breakfast on our vacation! Also, parking in the side lots is free (as long as you don't go underneath a parking arm).

Phenomenal 5 stars. Great quality foodie food. I tried the tomato soup, roast beef sandwich (drool) and green goddess sandwich. I felt the green goddess could have used some salt or seasoning. Bland but healthy and crunchy. Also tried 4 different kinds of cookies. If I lived in Nashville, I'd be a regular.
M3yOPL5TRCU0G5o8Ciicbw: Favorite spot in Nashville. Stupid Coop, AKA, Kevin and Dustin with a "D" are the best. They are hands down the best bartenders in Tennessee and are sure to show y'all a good time.

Friendly bar tenders.
Nice outdoor area covered and  WITH HEATERS for those chilly nights.

Decently priced drinks, music was loud enough to enjoy but also carry on a conversation. A nice spot to come with friends.

Full disclosure: i have a long standing love affair w Rebar that totally biases my review and will lead me to not mention any of its obvious flaws.

Rebar is my favorite place in nashville to kill a couple hours in the afternoon. Great patio thats never crowded in the daytime. We went again this weekend for our traditional post-marathon bushwackers. Honestly I think everywhere in Nash makes these the same but Rebars gets my vote everytime. Just boozy enough without tasting it and just melty enough to drink without it tasting like milk. We always complement this healthy choice with their dump truck of fries. Yes thats right. To go along with their construction theme ( which by the way....what a strange nonsensical theme...) they'll deliver you a Tonka truck full of French fries covered in cheese,  bacon and olives.  Sooooo good. If I venture into the rest of the menu,  ive done both the wraps and fish tacos and have been impressed with all.  

There are games and am electronic jukebox inside but honestly nothing beats the patio worth friends and cold bushwackers.
l3S_LsPam6ejdQo37V4kVg: This place has awesome- home cooked food and also supports an incredible cause. They support helping women who have been victims of human trafficking, homeless,  and/or  in prison get back on their feet and integrated back into the community.  They make many of the products they sell in the store and also have global items. Everything goes towards their mission. Great way to support local as well as global.

I spent a wonderful afternoon in "The Thistle Stop Cafe" sipping coffee and nibbling on a delicious "Vegan Vee" gluten-free oatmeal cream pie.  The atmosphere is warm and the staff treat you like friends.  I also found some lovely items at the store a few doors down.  

I didn't know about this place before today, but I will definitely be back.  Look for the large metal thistle sculpture on Charlotte Ave.

I was visiting Nashville and stopped here for lunch; my turkey sandwich and quinoa salad were delightful!  Very fresh ingredients.  I took it to go, but the space was well lit and comfortable.  Would love to stop by again sometime.
T2tJmH2BWNCkzdTYc4W3cA:
I went here on a visit to Vanderbilt, and was pleasantly surprised by the food. I was expecting everything to taste bland and boring, but the food was actually great! 
The superfood wrap went along great with the Parmesan+mushroom grits. The selection was great for the size of the restaurant, too.
As you'll see, the line certainly shows how popular Grin's is with the local sorority girls. But, it goes quickly and is certainly worth the wait.

The one time I ate here, they had a special pasta served with mushrooms and alfredo sauce for lunch. Perhaps the tastiest thing I had ever eaten in Nashville. I am a meat eater, alas, so I must give Grins four stars for having everything vegetarian.

You may also enjoy eating at Grins to view the Vanderbilt sorority girls who eat there on a regular basis.

Venturing onto a college campus when you're 31 years old can bring back the feelings of being a slightly awkward freshman on campus but Grins makes it worth it. 

Since I chose my vegan lifestyle, I have had very few people suggest that I check out this vegetarian and vegan sanctuary. This very much surprises me because Grins offers so much and at a very central location. Albeit, you're on the Vanderbilt campus but it's supper easy to find and I didn't have any issues with parking or access.

The special Fall 4 course vegetarian/vegan dinner lured me with a nifty price tag of only $30. Being that I had never dined here before, I'm not sure how it is normally set up during the day, but when we walked in... it was a quite pleasant ambiance, not what I would expected.  
The wait staff were students who were charming after an initial hiccup of my friend and I sitting at our table for nearly 10 minutes without being addressed by our server but she was doting and great the rest of the meal.

Let's chat food, shall we?
I was very happy with each selection ranging from the house-made bread with the Fried Goat Cheese Salad (I personally refrained from the goat cheese as I am vegan and they accommodated this request throughout my meal beautifully!) to the Vegetable Bourride (tasty soup) and Handmade Gnocchi with Roasted Mushrooms. YUM. Just when I thought that I had exhausted my palate, a delightful Apple & Pear Crostata that consisted of soy chai ice cream and candied ginger capped off a truly fabulous meal!

I had an event prior to dinner or else I would have brought a Kosher bottle of wine to pair... which is a great option to uncork here.

I am very interested in trying Grins out for lunch and will update.
vwrdjcP5pAhpANbSPEzAmw: Happy with the burger and fries ordered. It was an extra bonus when I was able to order a Jarrito!!! Being a Mexi-American these are the little things that put a smile on my face. Burger was quite tasty, huge, and filling. The place is literally a shack on the corner near some newly built tall townhomes. There is outdoor, shaded seating even a little screened-in hut if bugs are out. We took ours to go. Still warm when we got home to chow down on. Will be back!

I have never understood America's obsession with the burger, but this place has made a believer out me-perhaps it is the Porter Road Butcher meat? Burger cooked medium with blue cheese-heaven! Service is amazing, and the fries are absolutely outstanding-hand cut, thick and fresh. Love the horse radish ranch with them. Seriously, I've had burgers at all the "best" places in Nashville and none of them hold a candle to this place!!

It's exceedingly nice out today, so I decided to bop the few blocks over to the Shack for lunch. I ordered the standard cheeseburger and fries for $8.95 - prices have gone up a bit - and was told that it would be about fifteen minutes as there were two orders ahead of mine. That was fine with me, so I headed outside to bask in the sun at one of their picnic tables. Exactly fifteen minutes later, lunch was delivered in an unassuming black styrofoam container.

I attacked the fries first. They were thick, hand-cut, crispy, and ridiculously delicious. Seriously, just. Man. I could've probably had just a large order of fries and have been happy with that. But then I wouldn't have had the burger.

The menu sums the cheeseburger up pretty well: a 1/3lb patty from Porter Road Butcher, melty Muenster cheese, a bun from Provence. Onion and a pickle slice came on the side. The meat was juicy, lightly seasoned, and complemented by the buttery bun. For the price I wish it had been a little bigger, but ultimately it was still delicious.

If it's sunny and warm out, you couldn't do much better than a stop here for lunch.
6xzI0yNPXI5POpjoN7po0Q: Have visited this restaurant for both lunch and dinner and both experiences were great. Seating and service are quick, efficient and honest. Great recommendations from the staff for first time visitors as well as experienced diners. The perfect portions are served.

My husband and I stumbled across this gem of a restaurant when we didn't know where to go, and wanted to try something new.  Glad we did!   

Our first visit:  To start, we picked 2, yes 2 (don't judge) appetizers.  The hummus platter is amazing...my fav:  the honey macadamia nut hummus.  Yum.  We also got the spinach dip.  Good, typical.  We were full, but opted to split a flatbread as well.  We got the White Out because I was feeling particularly cheesey (yes, lots of cheese:  feta, mozzarella and parmesean)...It was great.  We also split a bottle of wine.  We were expecting the bill to be huge, but it was really reasonable.

Our second visit:  They were so slammed that we ate at the bar since we had to catch a concert that night.  My husband got a Woodford Reserve and Diet, and I got white wine.  (I love the way they house their wines behind the bar.  It's like they're on tap, haha.)  Yum.  My parents were also there, and we all split the hummus platter...yes, it's that good.  Then my parents split a special flatbread called The Californian (or something similar).  The sauce was raspberry jam.  Yum.  My husband and I got the Spicy Shrimp and Chorizo.  Also yum.  

It's delish, trendy and fairly reasonable.  Although we've never done it, it is apparently great for large parties.  We will be going back for sure.

Man was this place packed. There was apparently a Christmas party and a wine party going on. Despite all this the service was quite on top of things. They had a great selection of beer and wine also. We asked the bartender his recommendation for a flatbread and he didn't lead us astray. It was a lobster and arugula flatbread and quite satisfying. Although for the price I would have liked a little more lobster.  The ingredients were very vibrant and fresh and the dishes come out with a decent presentation.
nKJ6RnjIVfWrXO2DUrBgCw:
Ever feel like you don't really need to look at a menu in a standard Mexican food restaurant?

It's like they all pretty much have the same menu, right?  Nachos in Bellevue is that place, but they have a just enough specials that differentiate themselves from the standard 'tacos, enchiladas, fajitas' fare that sets them apart.  They have a very large vegetarian menu portion, which is great for my veggie wife - another nice change of pace.  They do have really good cheap draft beer and drink specials (2 for 1) during a long happy hour of 3-7 on either Monday thru Wednesday or Thursday, not sure which.  Their house marguarita may not be a labor of love, but it sure gets the job done.  The layout is very nice and upscale; the restaurant took over the old 'One Hundred West' white tablecloth restaurant (it was an upscale $20-$30 per entrée restaurant that imploded in short order).

I would generally rate this restaurant a 3-star, but the one thing that has set this particular restaurant apart is their service.  Super friendly from the second you walk in, it isn't uncommon to have your hand shaken by the maître d' as you walk in.  The servers have also been super helpful, which is great if you have a toddler like we do.  The last time we were in there, the server (Chuy?) couldn't stop mussing my son's hair and engaging the 'little amigo', the little guy wouldn't stop giggling.  And they are prompt.  Like I am pretty sure there is either a time machine or Minority Report-type setup in back to prognosticate what you've ordered.  Don't think I've ever had to wait longer than 2 minutes to get a beverage or 10 minutes to get an entrée, and I'm pretty sure the chips and salsa apparate on your table when you arrive.  Again, a life-saver if you have a hungry, cranky spouse and a two year old built like a linebacker.

Keep your eye out for coupons - they frequently pop up in 'The Clipper' and other places.  They also have no problems splitting checks, either.

For my money this is one of the better tex-mex places east of the Mississippi.  It's not anywhere near the quality of good tex-mex in Texas or Arizona but it's not bad either.  They work had to please you with what they have - fish included.  

I've often asked for their pork instead of the beef or chicken that some dishes use by default.  It's usually a winner.  They are happy to add fresh jalapeno or poblano slices to your fajitas to jazz it up for you as well.  Just be certain to let them know you want the bell-peppers too or the cook might substitute instead.  

Their drinks are not whoo-hoo fantastic but they are pretty darn cheap during happy hour.  Some of the cheapest in town.  I give them a 5 star for effort.

I really, really like Nachos. Absolutely one of our go-to places on the west side.  You know how some places just feel comfortable and familiar and reliable? This is one of those places for me. Very friendly service. We can always get a table - or a place at the bar. They immediately bring chips and salsa to the table. AND they make a mean top shelf margarita (skip the house margarita). There are some days that is more important than others, if you know what I mean.

Lately, the chips have been served warm and crispy and fresh. Hope that keeps up! No salt on the chips, which is ok by me since the salsa is good. I've eaten several different dishes here. My favorites are probably the Pollo Bandido - flavorful chicken with a delicious cheese sauce, fish tacos - a huge amount of fish, and the taco salad with chicken - tons of chicken with a really great flavor served with guacamole and other yummies in a crispy shell. 

Some nights get busier than others. They sometimes have a trivia night, if you are into that kind of thing. Other nights there is live music. There are usually plenty of kids there, so definitely a family-friendly spot.

Maybe it's not the most inventive mexican place in town, but comfy and delicious every time. Did I mention that I like Nachos? I do. I really do.
uzHR9nIc9Es2dLpKDPXRZA: I feel as though I need to update my review and really call out people that haven't the faintest of a clue when it comes to real Indian food.  Woodlands is the most authentic Indian joint in town, period.  You can't find dosa's, special thali's, or a variety of different curries anywhere else in the Nashville area.  Paul, maybe you failed to notice your surroundings, but the majority of the customers at Woodlands are Indian.  They probably just like the idea of Indian food, but can't handle the spices.  lolol
Great Indian food. Caters to vegetarians and nonvegetarians like. Service was good. Food was delicious. Buffet style.

Been coming here for years... never had a bad dish! I regularly order malai kofta,  bhatura, masala dosa. I never leave hungry or disappointed.  The service is pretty darn good and the food comes out pretty fast. Even though it is a vegetarian restaurant,  I wouldn't hesitate to recommend this place to anyone
JhcAR32Mck-dXTMsz1OSFQ:
In the Ancient Greek days of yore, when Dionysus roamed the earth delivering delicious drink to dried-up dullards, he established four central tenets of the sports bar industry:

1. Large, diverse draft beer selection
2. Regular drink specials
3. Abundant flat-screen televisions 
4. Gluts of greasy goodness 

Jed's satisfies each of these criteria, with extra points for location (right next to Centennial Park) and strong service.
