The draft list is extensive enough to satisfy without being so large you get a hangover-sized headache just trying to decide which beer to order.  The Scrimshaw Pilsner is a new favorite of mine thanks to its inclusion on the tap.

Jed's also boasts another Bacchus basic: two-for-ones.  If you like to double your trouble, you'll usually find a special here.

If you're going to Jed's to watch the big game, you'll also get to watch ten others.  There are enough televisions to satisfy sports-obsessed people like me, although the night I went, they were blasting the CMA Awards while the Predators were on mute, a fact that was not lost on my Canadian girlfriend.

When it comes to grease, well, the sliders do the trick.  Savory beef, gooey cheese, sweet buns...they're ideal beverage companions.

Jed's is my new favorite watering hole.  Dionysus approves, too.  And so will you.

I've only ever been to the Friday night open mic here but I love it! It's always busy and everyone is really friendly. Get there early for a seat! The beers are pretty expensive but the sweet potato fries are delicious and a huge portion for only $4! Also the bushwacker is absolutely damn delicious. I plan on coming here some weekend just for those! 
Fun vibe and a nice outdoor area

Big Fan....

Good regular prices and even better during Happy Hour.  The staff is nice and accommodating when it comes to putting games on.  I've tried several items on the menu and they all seemed good, especially the nachos.

Nice location and great patios upstairs and downstairs.  The whole place is non-smoking, which is a huge plus for me.
5qYX6iR5qFsGHrcjYlLMeg: Great time here with some office buddies yesterday. The service was fast and the food was great. I have always loved the burgers and topping selections at this place. The only thing to make it better is to have different cheese selections.
Food was served quickly and correctly. The cashier was a delight who I could tell spent most of her time smiling and spewing genuine southern charm.

Simple food properly prepared and served.  Fresh, tasty hamburger patty on the Little Hamburger (1 patty) or Hamburger (2 patties) with all the toppings you want, and no extra charge for extra toppings except cheese and/or bacon.  Fries come in a cup, but with so many extra ones in the bag that a small order can easily be shared between 2 adults.  We order Light salt and then season to taste.  Fries come hot, fresh and tasty; take out a few at a time from the bag to keep them hot.  Drinks are from a machine with a 100 choices, and refills are free.  Water is free from the machine if you ask for a cup at the counter.  Service is fast, as they begin to cook the number of patties immediately upon you placing your order.  Bun is a little soft so sandwich wrap or 2 hands are needed to hold it together while eating.  Peanuts are available from a large box and you can eat all you want while seated and waiting for your order.  The made to order burgers are ready in about 5 minutes and you need to listen for your order number to be called out.  Prices of sandwiches are good for a fresh, tasty made to order burger, Good for drinks with the free refill, and a bargain for the huge order of fries.  Haven't tried the milkshakes yet.  Overall, our go-to place for fast food.  Free parking in back.
gZPFgzPKJYAvobR77Q97WA: I'm a burger snob! Lol they serve up a pretty good burger though. No complaints here! Staff is friendly!
It was fast and delicious! I got turkey club which is just a turkey burger but it is great! Highly recommend!

This Tennessee based chain has better than average fast food burgers. They are real burgers, big and juicy, like restaurant ones, although you can't order them medium, which is a shame. I mourn for the waffle fries though. I think they stopped serving them a couple of years ago. Bad decision on their part! I occasionally get a craving for their veggie burger, which is flame grilled and tastes great, nothing like a real burger, but good in its own right. 

I wish there were more of them around, because I usually only get to eat there when I'm in Donelson or Cool Springs
gKavBVCK5G0cPJ9moV6iOA: This was a lil off the beaten path for us, but thank you, beaten paths!! I was hooked at a look at the menu and the wording, "dive bar". Nothing like a gooood dive bar! If you go here, use your iPhone map direction app b/c Google maps will lead you on a wild goose chase. The atmosphere is laid back, which is exactly what we were looking for. We started with a dozen raw oysters. They were a lil more briny than we're used to, but they were good. Hubs had the combo platter (oysters, calamari and shrimp) and he liked the option of having the shrimp and oysters raw or fried. The hush puppies were a little disappointing since they were plain (no green onion or spice added-hummm?). I had the shrimp & grits and it was very good. Now, I'm spoiled by a jalapeño or seasoned grits, so while the grits had a great texture and taste, I was hoping for a lil seasoning. (Thank you, Bonnell's in Ft. Worth for spoiling me). The cream sauce made up for the difference though. Now granted, this is TN and not TX, so they're probably not used to adding in the kick we get in TX, so please don't take that as a criticism, we're just used to everything having "Slap Your Momma" or jalapeño in it.... We ended the meal with the bread pudding which was awesome!!! I do not like cooked raisins, but raisins included, this was pretty close to New Orlean's good. Kristin, "Sparky", the waitress is darling and full of energy! She kept our drinks strong and flowing and had a great energy! I'd recommend this place, esp. if you are traveling and like a lil off the beaten path...

Can't believe this place is open so late, what a great find! The food was decent, the wait staff casual, and the street level had great big walls that opened up to the sidewalks. The upstairs has a treehouse feel to it, and it was perfect for an extremely casual get together with friends.

Love this place! Nice and relaxed ambience, mostly patio seating. I was craving for mussels so I found this place. My bf and I each ordered an order of mussels. It was so tasty (in a butter and white wine sauce) that we ordered a 3rd order for us. They have a lot of drink specials so be sure to ask the waiter because not all the specials are listed.

I'm still salivating thinking about their mussels...
xdVigWrfe3BqCSL1SaTgqA:
This place is surprisingly good - especially for a food court. 
My wife and I shared the gyro plate and it was plenty of food for lunch for two. The gyro meat was flavorful and sliced thin over white rice (I would have preferred something other than white rice, but that's what it came with) with a salad and pita quarters. 
Everything was tasty and the folks on the line were fun and friendly, albeit a bit rushed. I would return often if I was in Opry Mills. I was also impressed with how clean the food court is at Opry Mills. There were several clean tables for us to choose from and the whole place had a nice feel to it.
Just finished having a gyro at Pyramid café. Probably one of the best gyros I have had. The staff are quick and the food is right.

Hands down, me and my wife's favorite place to eat anywhere! Since we began dating in high school and they were located at Hickory Hollow Mall this was our after church stop. 

I get a gyro with seasoned fries and my wife gets a chicken Greek with mushroom. I am emotionally unstable when I see their sign. My nose can detect this culinary masterpiece 30 miles away. Would trade my first born if they would open a restaurant in Chattanooga.
ImO3aF_axcKe5pwFf-jQeQ:
Emmy Squared was a total off-the-wall decision, I had never heard of the place until I read an article saying "best burger in Nashville." Let me tell you it did not disappoint.

Call me infatuated, but I think Le Big Matt might be the best burger I've ever consumed. The Sammy Sauce is magical... I can't even describe it!

The Detroit style pizza was also fantastic. I ate a slice of the Colony Squared and it was amazing. Baked with a honey glaze on the crust and a perfect amount of heat in the toppings.

I would recommend going plain on the fries if you're getting an entree with a lot of flavors as their loaded "Zia style" fries, though delicious, are a LOT to consume on top of a flavor and topping-packed burger or pizza.

Kaysha, our server, was attentive, considerate, and our glasses were never empty. Truly great service! The manager even came over and chatted with us for a while to ensure our time at Emmy Squared was enjoyed. His sincerity was refreshing!

I will definitely be back as this was an incredible dining experience.
This is the best pizza in Nashville. Hands down. Detroit style, good sauce, and great customer service.
The service is great. Attentive. And real. None of that fake stuff. The Colony and Vodka pizzas were insane. So good. Tiny place with a cool vibe and I'm the heart of the Gulch.
Zx-dx9e4Hp-mpDV1JbhEwg: Wild Wasabi was a gem right up the street from Music City Center. Located in Cummins Station which was cool. Parking is free as long as you get ticket from restaurant. We ordered specialty rolls and we were not disappointed. The service was a little slow, but it actually coincided with our Miso & Hot and Sour Soup, which were both very good. We also tried the tempura fried ice cream which was excellent.

Sweet and welcoming staff. Knowledgable of their menu and makes repeat guest feel right at home. Any dinner dish you order is fresh and delicious. Great spot in Nashville! Check it out and give it a shot. Not sure how they do lunch but have heard it is buffet style with a lot of variety.
This was a good spot! The sashimi fish was fresh and delicious and the rolls we're very good. If I lived here, this would be my go to, unpretentious sushi spot.
jzXJDMGB81tZK-EISK38IA: I love this place. I'm so glad to have a non-chain drive through coffee shop in east nashville. Since they've moved to their new location, I haven't been inside, but the service is always prompt and the coffee good. No complaints here!

For my first time, a client told me the  lattes were really good. Well when I went in, they suggested an espresso milkshake... nailed it. It's a perfect blend of espresso and creaminess to complete your cold coffee craving. The vanilla flavor was perfect!

This is becoming one of my favorite coffee shops in town. I love the convenience of the drive thru window. I sort of wish there was more breakfast or foods offered in general but it's nice and easy knowing it's a quick stop for coffee. Added bonus, ice cream. Though I've yet to try it I've heard great things. I love the chai here, and the staff always delivers it with a smile. Very friendly service inside and out. Inside there is a nice sitting area that is spacious and quiet. There are pastries available too. The prices are on the cheap side for coffee in east Nashville, so that's a plus. A frequent for me and a must try, you won't be disappointed.
rDg7qsQwE55xxJiU65_N7A: Great atmosphere, sleek and sophisticated.  Good top shelf Scotch selection and great unique cocktails.   Saturday lunch at the bar is amazing. Half price food!   The burger was voted best in Nashville 2010 and it's easy to taste why.  Freaking delicious.... Good friendly service thanks to Dennis behind the bar.  Highly recommend.

My day trip to Nashville meant I didn't have to spend too much time away from the fam (yay), but it also meant I didn't get to explore much of Nashville (boo). I spent the entire day in the lovely Hutton Hotel and visited this onsite restaurant not once, but twice.  Yep, that's twice..once for lunch and once for dinner.  

I'm typically wary of hotel restaurants, but have to admit this one was pretty good. During a working lunch our party of 10 enjoyed their private room, which even had a TV that we were able to use for a presentation.  I started my meal with the corn gazpacho (eh) and then followed it up with the flat bread (yum). And my colleague was sweet enough to share her fries that came with my burger.

When I stopped back in later that night for dinner I decided to get my own fries and burger. I'm usually not a burger type of girl, but this one was so good!

The lunch service was a little on the slow side, but they were on their game during dinnertime, which allowed me plenty of time to get to the airport and head back to the fam.

Came here with a group of people for a business dinner on a Sunday night. The ambiance was great- low lightening, modern decor. There are L-shaped couches for seats at some of the tables. We were seated promptly, where the table had a variety of olives and flat crispy bread. While I'm not a fan of olives, it was unique. The food was quite good- I tried their old world flat bread, which was flavorful and crispy. The stuffed dates were so unique- a combo of sweet date and savory cheese, all wrapped in bacon, was really rich. I couldn't eat more than one. My entree was the original short ribs, perfectly cooked, tender, flavorful and the sour cream and onion crisps complemented perfectly. Could have used some veggies though. Had the banana beignets for desert, which were okay- the sauce they served it with was rather bland. 4 stars because the service was spotty- didn't have our waiter there when we needed him and the servers seems hectic, even though the restaurant wasn't busy. Not bad overall, though.
ehTKJRxy6csk5iiK_-pU1w:
You know how sometimes a restaurant gets an extra star because of things other than the food? yea, this is one of those reviews. It gets an extra point for value for your money and friendliness of staff.

I don't eat sushi (what kind of person willingly puts raw fish in their mouth anyway? .... shudder .....) so I got a chicken teriyaki bento box to go for lunch. I think the to go part was ill advised on my part as the smell of it teased my stomach all the way home with promises of deliciousness and caused me to, totally unintentionally, speed. Good thing everyone else on I24 was going faster than I was. 

When I got home I unwrapped everything and was really floored by just how much food I got for my ten dollars worth, and that included a tip. Here's the rundown: An iceberg lettuce salad with a homemade gingery asian dressing. cup of miso soup with seaweed. (I will be leaving that off any future order. I guess I'm not a miso soup fan either). three pieces of vegetarian california roll. large portion of white rice. large portion of teriyaki chicken. I could have easily divided the main dish into two portions if I so desired. but i was greedy, and hungry, and ate it all. Oh but it didn't stop there. three pot stickers. a side of soy sauce. and, finally, two large orange sections to end on a sweet note. this a restaurant that knows the value of a dollar. ... or maybe they don't cause there isn't another restaurant around that gives customers more food for their buck than this place does. shhh... don't tell them. 

 the teriyaki chicken had really nice charring on it. I love me some sear on my meat. pot stickers were good. the only thing that i didn't like, and it's a pretty big drawback, is the soy sauce they use. I Did. Not. Like. the soysauce. Don't know what it was about it, but there was just this flavor that was unpalatable. I had to set it aside and find some leftover chili sauce in the fridge to dip the potstickers in. that wouldn't have been that big a deal, but the teriyaki chicken for all the rest of its deliciousness did have some of that weird soy sauce flavor. unfortunately soy sauce is in so many dishes, and I don't like theirs. If it wasn't for that coming here would be a no brainer. I still might come back because the flavor of the chicken was good despite the soy and the value for your money is awesome.

Always fresh and flavorful. Service is good, not fast but then no pressure to vacate the table either. Not a good place if you are in a hurry. The lunch prices are less than dinner. Some items are about $6 but many are $10 or more. My family likes the bento boxes, the rolls, and the nigiri sushi. The nigiri is a bit pricy, but very good. Try the fire crunchy roll or the Ginza roll. Yum!

Ginza is one of my favorite places to go to for sushi when I am in the Green Hills area. The sushi is fresh and always very tasty. My favorite rolls to order here are the california roll and the yellowtail roll. They also offer combos that are affordable. I have also had their gyoza and it is so delicious. :D

The only reason i give this place 4 stars is that the service can be a little slow sometimes. Otherwise, the staff is nice and friendly. :)
L2COUkl21Xdk4rjUx-RyDg:
Our power went out so we went for a walk. We had no idea this place was right down the street! We had to stop in. 

The hot dogs were great. You could really taste that they were quality all beef dogs. I got a poppy seed bun, which is my favorite way to eat a hot dog and I got it covered in cheese.

I will totally be going back and I'm way stoked because I saw on the other Yelp photos that they serve deep fried candy bars and Oreos. Hell yeah. I need that in my life. I've never tried one before. 

They had a nice drink selection. Beers, Sanpellegrinos in different flavors, bottled water and a soda fountain.  

The atmosphere was cool. They had nice indoor and outdoor seating.

Another favorite hot dog spot.  I wish this had been open when I live on Belcourt!

I alternate between the Diet Starts Tomorrow and Buffalo Dog being my favorites.

The housemade hot beer mustard is a MUST with the fries.

Friendly, quick service.
I'm a big fan of hotdogs, especially Chicago style. But the ones at The Dog of Nashville beat anything I have had in Chicago. They are the best dogs around.
_4zUuKFW9GmmNYfswqaXNQ: Great place , reasonably priced awesome food , friendly staff .. No reason to take any points off ... So full marks to Copper Kettle
Tender flaky catfish, succulent rosemary pork chop, mash potatoes and gravy, fried okra, corn bread, mango tea, ... Comfort food heaven. Welcome back old friend, it's good to see you.

oh man, i'm going to pop. I just had lunch at Copper Kettle and it was fantastic. That shouldn't be a surprise, though, because it is always fantastic.  I had the baked chicken, mashed potatoes, and mac + cheese. So good.

I have never ordered off of the menu, but I hear their crab cakes are good.
uV3ryZ1m4wB18CtoeuOZGA: I have always seen this place all over America and on tv. It looks so simple and generic. Theres a cop sitting by the door who looks like he ate a little too many pancakes himself, how he stays awake sitting there I don't know. But the fact there is a cop here must mean it must get rowdy. Our server was really friendly, and got our food out pretty quick. The food is nothing spectacular, but what place open all day is mostly filled with drunk customers is? It did its job. Slattered my waffle with butter and syrup, just like Anthony Bourdain did,  and I was in heaven.  I prb would not come here sober, but like I said you know what your getting when you come here and it will not let you down.
This is the best Waffle House in Nashville. The service is just what you want. The food.... it's perfect.

ate here on vacation around thanksgiving been to a lot of waffle houses in this country and this one was very good / waitress was friendly and the restaurant was clean / food was good and the service was outstanding / next time im in nashville will b stopping by
kBOZOdcDEZ0ZfUnXth2uqA: Wow! I read the yelp reviews and decided to order the coffee filet. The filet was cooked perfectly. The crust that the coffee adds is remarkable. We really enjoyed our meal here. We had the steak egg rolls for an appetizer they were really good as well. We love Stoney River for the best steak in town, but were excited to try their bunch menu. It did not disappoint. Everything was very good from the service to the food. The steak was obviously the best, steak burger was fantastic as well. We will return to get the waffles with wild cherry sauce. Salmon and the french onion soup were good, not great. Had a great night out with my wife. Steaks were grilled to perfection our wine was excellently paired. Our server Chris answered all of our questions and picked out a spectacular merlot. He really made our evening!
5arcOlmZx0_ZMUxMiPbZsQ:
HOLY CRAP!!!
Los Arcos on Nolensville Rd is the BEST Mexican Seafood that we've found in Nashville so far!!
NomNomNom

The family and I came here after investigating Plaza Mariachi. I had the Chile Verde which was wonderful. My wife had three different tacos (chicken, carnitas, and steak), and the Kids had burritos. We all enjoyed the food and loved the salsa bar. I loved the roasted Serrano chilies and all the different salsas.

The place was packed on this Friday night but the service was still good. Had to speak to our waitress in Spanish because her English was broken, but we had a great time. 

We were going to get dessert (Fried ice cream, and churros with Ice cream) until we found out that the only flavor of ice cream they had was strawberry and not vanilla...just didn't sound good, so we passed and went elsewhere. 

Overall, it was a pleasant experience. We will certainly be going back! Give it a try!!!
Love the lunch buffet, it's fresh and enough variety to satisfy.  The menu is great, lots of choices you won't find at the other 50 Mexican restaurants on Nolensville Rd.
w-Semojy2wIGygwDEnNW7A:
Went for a weekend lunch using a living social voucher. Overall, good quality. Nice hot meats that rotated between 7 meats. I enjoyed that the meats were minimally seasoned so you could appreciate the taste. They do provide steak sauce if necessary. The salad bar was great. 

I tried the white wine sangria ($10) and it was yummy. If you order soda, it comes in a can.

Overall good, but come on an empty stomach and stay for a while to get your money's worth.

Reading the other yelpers' reviews on here was very perplexing.  And like so many others, I don't see any possible justification for the low scores, so had to check it out.

Admittedly, hubby and I had never been to a Brazilian steakhouse before.  Who are these other yelpers who have experienced so much that leave us lowly native Middle Tennesseans "in the dust" culinary wise I wonder?  We've been to many places and experienced great dinners from all sorts of realms - so while this was new for us, it's not like it's our first dinner out.

We visited on Saturday night at 7 PM with reservations between Christmas and New Year's.  The restaurant had a few other guests, but not too many at all, and just felt "cool."  We were seated at a table where we could look out over Belcourt Avenue through the (permanently closed) glass-paned garage doors, and it provided basically dinner and a show.  We're Cool Springs people... and couldn't go over all the cabs dropping off diners for Sunset, Cabana, and here.

Note - very recently, like the past month, I'm told a second Brazilian steakhouse opened on Second Avenue next to Melting Pot. Don't know if that means anything, but just thought I'd mention as a possible explanation for the low crowds.

Our very kind waiter explained the steakhouse what's-going-to-happen, and brought us our drinks and beginning sides.  The salad bar was very well appointed and presented well - all the items were in geometic, interesting bowls and perfectly-molded together-but-crushed ice, it was really lovely.  Nice greek salad, excellent smoked salmon, fruits, chicken salad, olives... I don't know what more you could ask for honestly.

We finished our salads and enjoyed some absolutely fabulous rolls (small warm cheesey yet yeasty, yum!) and then once our waiter magically appeared at exactly the right moment with fresh plates, we flipped our little cards over to green.  Within like 30 seconds, the first "meat steward" appeared with filet on a sword, and we took our little tongs to accept a slice, then steward #2 was RIGHT behind him with BBQ chicken wings he popped off his skewer.  Not 30 seconds later, another meat steward with Parmesean Pork, another beef something or another, pulling right in like it was commuter traffic and there's a guy with lamb and grilled pineapple - yum!

We immediately saw the value in pace of eating, so we flipped our cards to red to enjoy for a bit.  The filet was excellent, BBQ chicken excellent, lamb excellent... was it the best ribeye ever?  Not for my taste.  There wasn't any meat in the 12 courses we tried allowing for a repeat round of "visits" that I'd say we disliked, but we did have favorites.  Our waiter checked on us periodically, and mentioned more than once if there was any specific meat we wanted to visit just let him know.  A BBQ rib selection was a favorite as well, but honestly we were so full - the only repeat we asked for was the grilled pineapple for the "palate cleanser" appeal.

Our service throughout from both our designated waiter and all the numerous meat stewards was very good - we never felt like we were waiting for anything.  The experience overall was very good, because honestly it's something different.  We can go have a very nice $100 dinner lots of places, but only here can you have multiple meat stewards come by with kabobs on swords and try a little bit of everything - and at this point in life we are all about trying something different.

We did use a Groupon for $35 discount, but our tab which included 18% grat (their norm when customers use a coupon) for the other dinner and three cocktails was $90.  A little pricey, but not ridiculously so considering the uniqueness in my opinion.  We would very well go back, probably for a special occasion and using a Groupon/Living Social deal again since that seems to be easily done.

While Bombasha is no Fogo de Chão, it's certainly a great alternative for Nashville. We went here with a group of seven on a Saturday night. We didn't have a wait since our table was ready to go. That was certainly an impressive feat for a busy restaurant on a weekend.

The salad bar is a bit on the small side, but it was just enough to get your palate off to a good start. The array of meats was smaller than one of the big chain churrascarias. However, the meats were tender, flavorful, and the wide variety kept my palate interested. I particularly recommend the Parmesan-crusted pork. Mmmm! I loved the coarse sea salt on the house special, too.

You must--MUST--try the chocolate mousse cake for dessert. My husband and I shared a piece, and as stuffed as we were after such a large meal, we both could have eaten our own slice. Our friends tried the cheesecake, and we each snagged a bite of that as well. Phenomenal! Even if you aren't impressed with the meat fest that Bombasha is, you'll certainly enjoy the dessert portion.

I won't lie. For Nashville, this is a pricy place to eat. But given the sheer volume of food you're presented with, it's a fairly good value. This isn't somewhere I'd go more than a couple of times a year, but that's usually the case for our "special occasion" restaurant spots.
o0XILIZR6cdoBjJALeOJaA:
First off, I will say that this past weekend was the first time I have been to Chaffin's in at least ten years, maybe a little longer.  

I felt the need to review them after reading what several other yelpers have written to somewhat "put things in perspective."  You don't go to the Barn for the food.  It's a cafeteria-style buffet, pretty much on par with Shoney's or when you were in college.  That's how it was 10 years ago, that's how it was Saturday night.  The salad bar was fine, some of the veggies I thoroughly enjoyed, the cobbler was quite good - you get the picture.  If you select the roast beef, be sure and add the au jus beside it in a crock pot.  If the barbecue looks a little dry, then pick the saucy barbecued chicken.  Yes, that will be messy - but what do you care?  You're at the Barn.

The live performances at the Barn have as diverse a range of themes, characters, and actors as the buffet line offers starches.  You'll see some pretty good shows there, and come on - you are supporting local actors!  These folks are not on caliber with Broadway, although every once in awhile you might be pleasantly surprised at the talent, but we live in Music City.  We need to support local musicians, local actors, and local non-franchise businesses that are their outlets.  Plus, it's quite entertaining.

For this past weekend, we were in the BackStage area.  If you read from the yelper that refers to this space as "hot, super cramped, and inferior food..." well - they don't visit many live entertainment performances.  You know how close together the chairs are in Zanie's?  There's an entertainment philosophy that audiences laugh more and react better when the person next to them, REALLY next to them, is laughing or gasping.  That's what they're going for on space, and it's the same in the larger room as well.  The a/c - well, it's Middle Tennessee folks.  May and 93 degrees.  Get used to it.  Inferior food?  The mainstage folks go through the same buffet line the backstage does.  If anything, the backstage seats 30 minutes earlier than the main stage, so that area gets a little bit of a head start and as such it's a little fresher.

We saw the Murder Mystery show with a groupon, as pretty much everyone else did, too.  Was it cheesy? Of course!  Did you think an 80's themed murder mystery show at Chaffin's Barn wouldn't be?  Was it a little long?  Maybe.  Were the actors headed straight for Broadway (or Star Search as one kept chanting relentlessly)?  Probably not.  I did recognize one of the actors from some of the other local perforamnces I've seen and he was quite good.  I won't name names so as not to offend anyone.

In the email confirmation, you are asked to dress up in '80's or outlandish attire.   We did.  Of course by doing that I got picked to be one of the "suspects" and wear a goofy hat all night.  Of course it was cheesy, that's what I was counting on.

My table didn't follow the clues very well and get very far figuring out who committed the murder that happened right at our very feet, literally.  Probably this was because we were visiting and enjoying some adult beverages along the way and taking the evening in for what it was.

The waitstaff is great, and yes - they are actors from other shows for the most part.  Unlike most budget-friendly restaurants I've visited - our glasses were never empty and plates were cleared very promptly.  Yes, they need to do that because the tables are quite small, but again - laughter is contagious and your plates will be gone before the show starts.  I had about the same amount of "personal space" at the last Chamber luncheon I attended at a conference center hotel, and that plate never got cleared.

With the groupon deal, (which I suspect is almost evergreen), we paid just under $60 for two adults.  Again, thinking back to dinner is Shoney's basically... that means we paid about $10 for dinner (which you can spend at any fast food on the planet) and $20 for the show.  That's a deal.  Price a pair of concert tickets or passes to any other live performance... you'll be paying $50 each, especially after Ticketmaster charges.  So if you do pay full price for a show at the Barn, once you realize the tickets themselves are roughly $50 and dinner is $10 at Shoney's... it makes more sense.

So go, enjoy.  Wear a boa if they say dress up.  Hug up to the person next to you and have a good time... it's what you're supposed to do!

Our family wants to give the new theatre owners a huge shout out for the improvements to the menu. This was our first time back in a couple years and even our teenagers remarked on the delicious array of fresh and tasty food. As always, the acting was phenomenal! We saw the musical, Sister Act, and even my husband enjoyed it. Looking forward to bringing the rest of the family next time.

I must tell you that I have alway enjoyed chaffin's, but it isn't for the food it is for the entertainment. These are local actors and actresses that do an excellent job at bringing some semblance of culture at a fair price to a very unappreciative community. 
I can tell you that if this venue closes it would be a great lose for Nashville and the surrounding area. 
No this is not TPAC but the shows they put on are every bit as entertaining.
lekpmrVopRSaId-ZxrNVHQ:
NOT a gourmet, hipster burger place...but that's ok.  Everything about this place screams Sisco Food Services, frozen boxes of pre-made food...but you would be wrong!

Sure I can almost guarantee they use frozen french fries, and onion rings etc.  But I don't care.  their burgers are made fresh and they are delicious. Spicy Fries are tasty and crispy.  You can;t get a med rare burger, and you won't find aioli within 1000 yards of this place, but they are some tasty burgers.  Old School, Mom n Pop, really great tasty burgers.
All the fat moes I been to are good i grew up and TN and moved to texas and have been craving these for 3 yrs

I hear tell that Fat Mo's has a chocolate shake to die for. My problem is that the burger and fries are so good, I cant' bring myself to order something else and the root beer is just the perfect compliment to the burger and seasoned fries.  My quandry is great ...

I have a pretty moderate appetite so I get the little Mo with cheese. It's supposed to be for kids but trust me when I say that the Little Mo combo is really enough for an average sized adult with a normal appetite. 

The main thing is the seasoning in the beef is out of this world. They also sell seasoned fries which are my favorite in town. My husband and i love to get Mo's on Titans weekends.
vWmh3kJuehx3tDp6rrtqgA: BEST TACOS EVER! I had the hot chicken (when in Nashville) and the fried avocado. I would go there everyday if I could. Friendly and quick service. Their drinks were a bit pricey for how small they were.

Pretty cool hipster taco place. By no means is it Mexican food if that's what you are in the mood for. The southern friend taco is literally a friend chicken strip, and some lettuce in a tortilla. The sauce is what mad either better, but not super impressed. The spicy shrimp taco was alright, but mainly a coleslaw with a little shrimp in a tortilla. I might go back to try a breakfast taco- cuz that sounds bomb! The service was great and friendly!

Best Mexican food I've ever had! I had the Monster Vegetarian Plate and even though I don't love cooked veggies, but it was amazing. The queso and guac were absolutely to die for. 3DQ2h_AAIEBNHBsES_UvWQ:

They are very respectful of vegetarians. My food was cooked first and they had sense not to include eggs in my rice. I loved this part about the restaurant.

Just finished dinner, was delicious.   Had the steak and chicken. The only reason I didn't give 5 stars was I asked for medium steak and it was well. The service was excellent an the show was good. I have seen some reviews here complaining about price but for the amount of food you get it is a very good price. I usually leave these grills hungry and I left here stuffed.

Best Japanese Steakhouse you will ever visit. Best food I have ever had. My family and I have been going here for years. ai9OgENCC63Hw7VYWRkV2Q:
Love this place. Love the atmosphere.
Love that the location is totally random. (Keeps some of the riff raff out.)
The food is great. (Try the bbq nachos for dinner. Chicken & waffles for brunch.)
Drinks are delicious. (Try Babs on a Budget.)

My one complaint..
THE FOOD PORTIONS ARE SO SMALL.
And I have a small stomach so that's saying alot.

Haven't been for a show yet.

Wonderful little restaurant/bar/music venue.

When my friends in Nashville asked where our group was going and I mentioned The Stone Fox they all remarked on how isn't that the place on the West side?  Which is true.  The Stone Fox might be something that you would expect to see over near The Pharmacy but rather is on the other side of town near...nothing.  It's in a residential area.  In fact, The Stone Fox is made from an old house.

The beauty of this is the benefit of the porches.  In addition, the inside has a very homey feel to it, like the front room that has small lighting and large antique maps covering the walls.  The food is a delight, with their late night menu serving healthy apps like the kale and onion hummus pizza (gluten free).  

I would go so far as to say that this place is a hidden gem.  Because it is still newer and in a non-commercialized part of town not very many people have heard about it or been to it yet, meaning you can slip into it on a late evening and catch a really amazing live show on their inside small stage and eat really tasty, healthy food or just shoot the shit on the porch area with friends (back porch is bigger).  The night we went there was a really great 2 piece band from Mississippi playing.

A shining star in Nashville. French philosopher Henri Bergson's principle of duration explains how once we acknowledge the greatness of something, we kill it. The Stone Fox makes me think of this, because I am a bit weary of praising the sweetness that is The Stone Fox lest I jinx it. It's one of those things you hope never changes, but you can't help but fear that something--some outside force or something internal--will cause it to lose that spark. This place has amazing shows, fantastic food and drinks, incredible decor (lots of maps; you won't be able to miss it, but make sure to take a look at the huge mural map of the United States on the back wall) and accommodating staff. It wonderfully meshes a classy restaurant with a grungy bar-house. It's a place you can take your grandparents to (Monday nights: Bingo!) and also your party-prone friends. William Tyler, an amazing local musician, owns the place along with his sister. This must be how they book so many incredible musicians. I can't stress enough how much I enjoy their lineups. It never disappoints. The only thing that I don't like about The Stone Fox is that it's so far away from my house. 


Also, when you see the staff and the people who frequent this place, you'll know it's called The Stone Fox for a reason. Wowza!
DuB7JAFDaR6RUJRLU1Avbw:
For some reason, every time I went to the old Chipotle, I left with the food being cold, and that bothered me.  I decided to try this out, just because I had a burrito craving, and always enjoy a new experience, and I like this location better than the West End Ave. store.

First, the traffic situation is much easier.  You don't have to deal with trying to either get in, or find one of the so few parking spots.  Second, since there are fewer surrounding businesses, most of the spots are going directly to Chipotle.  Third, the way seemed a little faster (I probably hit it between the rushes), and finally, the burrito was warm, which makes such a difference.

If you have the choice, I'd go to this Chipotle, It just seems better overall.

I crave burritos on a regular basis and it seems that Chipotle is the best Nashville has yet to offer up to burrito lovers. It's sad to be honest. Chipotle will never have burritos that will compare to my personal favorite of nopales and tofu rancheros from my local taquaria in San Francisco. But its what happens when you move away from the source. If there are some veg friendly places I am overlooking, please drop some knowledge on this transplant.

This is your standard Chipotle. If you like Chipotle in general, you will like this one. I work across the street and this is the best vegan option for my long days on campus. I tend to burrito here frequently (burrito is a verb here). 

If you don't like Chipotle, you won't like this one either.

I find that Chipotle is a fix, but is not quite fully satisfying. It's like when you really want to have a satisfying beer and there is only light beer. Yeah, you will drink it, but it's no IPA. It serves it's purpose well.

Chipotle is one of two fast food places it will actually eat at.... Until an article comes out and says it's actually not as healthy as I think. For now, they're quick service atmosphere, fresh ingredients, and price serve up a wonderful workday lunch. They service is always fast and to the point.

I only wish they offered more of their products without red onions... But while the rest of the world accepts them, I can't imagine that will be anytime soon... so I'll continue to eat there.

"I love Chipotle, Chipotle is my lifffeee!" - cred to the little kid on YouTube. Hope you're a millionaire from royalties by now!
a-1d7gNKRY9UwL7VLt-jmA: I live in the neighborhood so frequent Crow's on the regular... I just want to say that Kristen is the BEST server and bartender. What a great experience I had here the other night Amy the asst.manager took great care of our party and was giving suggestions on all the awesome food that we had which included Pizza Rolls,Chicken Wings, Cheeseburgers and more next time your in Green Hills stop by and see her she takes great care of all her customers
Love love love me some crows nest. When I worked over there it was a twice a week go to. I miss it!
w1rUV5W_TJ0M0MRwwrseWQ: This place has the excellent variety and selection of vegetarian and vegan Cuisines that Marvel a Southern Delight I come here very often and indulge in the world famous Nashville hot chicken biscuits they also have fresh baked goods such as apple fritters glazed donut and the lunch menu is as exceptional as well with things like corn dogs Jackfruit BBQ sliders vegan barbecue ribs the hot chicken so come on down I approve it

If you love Hot Chicken you NEED to come here! Meat eater or Vegan, it makes no difference because you will be blown away! This is easily the best Nashville Hot Chicken minus all the cruelty due to their own vegan "chicken" substitute. 

Oh and they have vegan pastries to add icing on the cake! Mmmmm, apple fritters!

My favorite breakfast spot in town. If you grew up eating Chick-fil-A but have since gone vegan, you will be stoked when you eat here. The chicken biscuits are out of this world. Everything else is delicious too! 

The owners are very friendly and always aim to provide the best customer service possible. Every experience I've had here has been spectacular! Love the vibes and the prices are more than fair for what you get.
DaQ6YSXmoXQH1qYXNSR1hA: I want to give a shout out to these guys. I ordered the Mild sauce on 15 wings... little did I know that the Mild is very little short of melting a stick of butter in the microwave and pouring it over the wings. In any event, I called and got the manager, who explained this issue and even offered to help me out on a freebie on the next visit. Just a caveat... DON'T ORDER THE MILD IF YOU WANT A BUFFALO STYLE SAUCE. Great staff but the brand as a whole is in need of letting people know what the deal with the Mild sauce is.

OK, yeah, it's just a Hooter's, but it wasn't as bad for me as it was for some people, so I thought I d share. 

We went here on a Saturday night to watch a Titans game...this close to the stadium, I thought for sure it would be really crowded and service would be horribly slow, but this wasn't the case. 

We got plenty of attention form the waitress, and she was very pleasant and relatively quick. The food was Hooter's food, nothing spectacular, obviously, but let's face it; that's not why you go to hooter's, right? Right.

We were there for over 3 hours because the game went into, and I expected to get some angry glares in our direction, but they were OK to let us t\sit there even after I quit buying beers. 

Overall, I'd say it was better than your average hooter's.
Nice large Hooters, that is the restaurant. Although they do have other large hooters within the large Hooters.  You would  think we had an earthquake the way the Hooters were shaking in the Hooters.

Great home style cooked meat and 3! Perfect atmosphere in a neat area. We will definitely be coming back and making this a usual spot for breakfast or lunch. 

Say hey to the fish at the waterfall pond on the way in!

Yummy! The chicken is oh so good cooked just right moist inside and crisp outside. I had cucumbers & onions very good, and broccoli salad yum! My husband says best cornbread he has ever eaten. Table next to us I hear the lady say now this is how fried chicken is suppose to be!

Why eat "fast food" when you when you can get good food quickly!  Monell's always serves good food to happy customers.  My teenage son wants to eat there every Saturday.  As one friend said, "This is healing food."  Michael King, owner of Monell's, hit a grand slam with the combination of good food and great atmosphere at the Germantown location.  I was glad the Bransford Avenue location was opened to allow more people to enjoy the food.
NFXW1frFS79BHHzjhePVQw:
Had lunch with a friend. Shared some empanadas and the big sampler. Everything was awesome. 

Good people. Fun vibe. Great food. I'll be back!
What an amazing Colombian restaurant having tried a couple in Nashville I can definitely say it is the best one! The flavor of the Bandeja Paisa is really good!
It's very authentic and good. The place is clean and the staff is very sweet. Tired a little of what they have. Try it
m_t0u1Z9bPmKRdEVBa-MJQ:
Not bad, BNA, not bad!  Eating when traveling is rarely fun or healthy but things are changing!  

I was CRAVING sushi while waiting to board my flight and had walked by this sushi place--it may have been seeing the sushi that made me want it so badly!

As everyone who tried it has mentioned, the Southern Charm was really, really good.  I had an attentive, but not overbearing, server and that is a huge plus in my book.  She was available when I needed her but not intrusive while I ate.  The sushi preparer was fun to watch and really made a fine roll!

Of course, you can only get to this place AFTER you go through security, so it's not a social destination, but, trust me...if you are in Concourse C and you get a craving for sushi, you are in for a treat!
Surprisingly this was excellent fresh sushi. They even have a southern charm roll with French fried onions.... So good. Quick service, friendly staff and fresh food.

As far as airport sushi spots go, this is as good as I've ever had! Our server/bartender Rusty was very friendly, attentive and helpful. The food was good and the drinks were great as well. Next time we fly through Nashville, we hope to stop by again and maybe see Rusty too!
Gksv0lbchfKxljBm5puCBQ:
WOW! We had an amazing and unexpected meal at porta via last night. The food was phenomenal but the highlight of the evening was the drinks. Mixologist & GM Jay Polk was behind the bar last night and made the experience very special. Jay was extremely friendly and honestly there is no one on the west side that does cocktails like this. We will definitely be back soon!


Oh and get the carbonara. You won't regret it.

This is one of my go-to places for lunch.  The Apollo and the Meatball bolillos are top-notch and the Insalata Mista is always fresh and the balsamic dressing is really tasty.  The pizza is OK but it's not my favorite place for it.  All in all Porta Via is a great place and it's a good value for your money.

Yum, yum, and yum! My ladies and I came here and had dinner. There is a reason why there is a wait to eat. Everything we had was DELISH. Do yourself a favor and make a reservation if you plan to visit. :)
iDdLFO2gQBh_Ovxd8obaEQ: This place ended up being pretty good. We will definitely go back. Black pepper chicken and coconut shrimp were excellent. Reasonably priced also.

Very much enjoyed my visit to New China Buff awhile back. Incredible selection and nice staff. Polished off 3 plates and finished off the experience with a bowl of ice cream. Already looking forward to returning and experiencing more of what they have to offer.

Food was very good and at a great price. Service was very attentive and friendly. I'm surprised there were not more people eating here. Wish there was more sushi, but it was a slow night so I'm not entirely surprised the selection was small. Will return soon. 
Health Score: 95.
VdB1YL718sAxae12PGgplw: Delicious food. Great cocktails. Excellent service. Interesting concept where tipping is not standard. Greeted immediately upon entry with attentive service throughout the meal. The menu is unique for this neighborhood.

Excellent quality and solid execution in every phase of the game. The thought put into each of the dishes is evident. Their novel approach to tipping (gratuity is included) and cocktails (half portions are available) is refreshing. There is always something new on the menu.

The Lamb Mapo Tofu left me thinking about it days later and the fried rice is always a rotating preparation based on seasonally available ingredients. TKO is a much needed addition to Inglewood and my wife and I are sure to become regulars.
Very unique take on Chinese. Love the cocktails, the ribs are the best I've had and the steamed bun with sausage gravy is the stuff dreams are made of. Go. Now.
9k2xBR26pc5KpS9KglAizw: Was greeted with a smile from the owner , the waitress was extremely friendly , nice fire going in the fireplace, the best way to describe the food is good ole home cooking, but the stars of the show were the peach cobbler and strawberry shortcake

Okay, here's another buffet place I can't help but love. This is usually the post church hang out, and on Sunday afternoon we're typically not alone. Everyone in here is in their Sunday best and enjoying themselves. You can't help but just chill out here, the people that work here are super nice and are always ready with coffee, which I need after being drug out of my warm bed for a warm pew. 

The place is set up like a country buffet; the food is in one room, and a large seating area is in another room. The decor is grandman-ish and there is a fireplace in the main dining room. The menu is your typical southern fare, biscuts, fried chicken, veggies, mashed potatos and pie for dessert. They do have small Purity ice cream samples in a cooler in the back of the room that holds the food. Get the birthday cake flavor one with the sprinkles and chocolate on top. It's awesome.

I'd say some would complain about the price, but I didn't have to cook it, so I really don't care.

We are at a conference and saw this as we were looking for a department store. So glad we decided to try it. It feels like you're at Grandmothers house. The food is really good and a good variety. The salad was very fresh also. 
The owner has a beautiful story of her life, also. I feel like I've had an enjoyable meal.
PYSsPOcHprXNNVBVw5XQRA: We were in town for a couple days and were looking for some traditional southern fried chicken. This place is a gem! Great service and great food. Jay's hit the spot for my whole family!
All the staff and manager are extremely friendly. The food is very good, big portions and lots of selection. Definitely better than cracker barrel

Finally visited Jays with my husband for Fathers Day. We had a late breakfast and no complaints from either of us. The biscuits were perfect and the home fries were amazing. Can't wait to come back and try out lunch.
yqq1Fvt7WtduIO3GwjCP1Q: The very best wings and Moscow mules I've had in a very long time. I'm definitely coming back here on my next visit to Nashville. The service was great as well.

Tuesday 2 for 1s on various libations.  Alternatively, I would generally opt for Mafiosos if you are in the 23-33 age group.

If you want to watch the game.....this is NOT the place.  TVs are beyond TERRIBLE.
Went here for lunch today, the bbq pork tacos were amazing! The French fries are really good too! Service was great and prices were good too! I highly recommend it!
1nUibAh6maHrHiVwo_xIlA: Good spot. Good location. You can eat or drink in our outside. Food was alright also.
Hit this place up before a Sounds game. Rachel was great at the upstairs bar. Check it out.
This place is awesome! Great food, drinks, music, staff and overall atmosphere! Two Thumbs Way Up!
3UPkzmnVGoDCCvE-29EpwQ:
You have to try this place. My words won't do your stomach justice; the only thing that will suffice is your walking through their door, looking up at their menu and ordering whatever you see first.  Then come in almost every day until you try everything on the menu.  After you try everything on the menu, then you can start the process over until you decide what's best on the menu.  After 2 months, 25 lbs weight gain I've decided to say no more trying to figure out what's best, IT'S ALL GREAT!   

Ugh, Now maybe I can focus on losing these pounds that I've gained due to "The Ridge"!   You win!
Tender and great taste. Everything was cooked really well and the sauce was tasty. Staff is very friendly as well. Worth stopping in and enjoying.
Great service, great food, had half rack of ribs - delicious!! What else can I say! You will enjoy it!
ie2xkZABFoBLX64UebMV9g:
Excellent bakery with Brazilian pastries and delicious coffee. The restaurant is well decorated with a great staff. 
Uma excelente opção para quem quer matar a saudade de casa e comprar alguns produtos brasileiros. ;)

Absolutely love this place! I had been driving by it for a couple of months and always wanted to try...Well I finally did last week and I will definitely return. This is a mom & pop restaurant/cafe so expect yummy home made meals and not a fancy steakhouse. Waitress was extremely nice and the pastries are delicious. If you go here please don't skip the pastries as they are the best part! Also, don't forget to check out the mini grocery shop they have inside.

This place is legit. Not only is the food amazing, the staff is super friendly and accommodating. Our young kids even loved it. They hooked us up with a free dessert just because. Definitely worth trying. Can't wait to go back!
Ueq5srlADo8pef4UcuqQTg:
after a fun week in nashville it was finally time to go home. morning flight? no problem, but need to eat first. asked a local tsa agent where he would go eat for breakfast.... he said noshville. BEST corn beef and hash i have ever had. hands down.
staff was awsum and was happy to serve you.
Great All American Breakfast.  Lots of ham and the eggs were perfect.  Love the little potato pancakes.  Had fast friendly service, just perfect.

They are prone to being slow, but are getting better.  Lately they ask "do you have at least 20-30 minutes" before they take your order.  Actually that's a good idea, because it's not fast food sitting in a case like most of the other places.
iTtpjd-zG7osm9CDQwMzbw: OK, let me preface my review by saying that I am a New Jersey Italian guy.  In my, personal opinion, Jersey and Manhattan have the best pizza.  I have tried all of the major chain pizza spots and I have never been that impressed.  They were alright, but not what I was used to.  Tonight we tried E. Milano's and, to my surprise, it was like being back in Jersey.  The crust was perfect.  It has nice body and yes, it does not fall apart in your hand or in your mouth.  You have to chew it.  Like I said, it has "body".  It was not over sauced, too oily or skimpy on the cheese.  It was every bit as good as a Jersey or New York pizza.  I was pleasantly surprised.  Dang!  Right here in the beautiful, free state of Tennessee.  The counter guy was nice too.   Check this place out.  Mangia!!

We were in a desperate search to find a place here in Nashville with a legit Buffalo Chicken Pizza. They just added this to their menu and it was amazing! Huge chunks of chicken, good buffalo sauce (we asked for extra), and good balance of cheese. Heading there again tonight!

What a wonderful surprise to discover this gem! Their pizza is excellent, with fresh ingredients and melty mozzarella in good size portions. We have had almost everything on the menu- skip the wings and the pasta- but go all in on pizza, sandwiches,  and Stromboli. 
They are great for a last minute lunch or dinner, and we make sure and "need" to get a pizza at least once a week.
mebHSxnIrWDt1q4VOLverg:
Good food for the price. If your looking for a quick lunch or dinner like others have said this is not the place to go. I ordered takeout and took over 40 min to get food to go. 

Overall food was good. Had the spicy jerked chicken, I would say it's more of a 4 out of 10 but I like spicy food. 

Plantains were great! I also had the collard greens which were tasty as well.

Gave A 4 out of 5 b/c I was expecting to wait so did not make me upset. And for under $10 did fill me up and I can eat a lot.

Will try again on another business trip!

Ok, let's start with locations. There is one in the Nashville Farmer's Market and the other is located in Midtown on Hayes St. For some reason I prefer the Hayes St. Location. Not sure why the food always tastes better to me there. But anyway, my faves are the spicy jerk chicken and mango chutney chicken. The sides are good as well, my go to's are pineapple sweet potatoes and cabbage. That cabbage is BOMB! 

Something that you won't hear much about but definitely should is the raspberry herbal tea. I actually bought a gallon on my last visit ($10?!) but it made me happy. Ha! Definitely check out Jamaicaway. Either location!

Had the jerk salmon and it was great ! Could have been a little more spicy but they did give me some type of spicy sauce  and that helped . Rice and peas were absolute bomb ! Sweet potatoes bomb! Over all a good 8 out of 10 !
-ZJjxOYTWYbxD3yaz1CvCQ: New to Nashville. This place is amazing! A little hole in the wall. Everyone was super nice and the food was hot! Great value too.

I will never forget that day. Came by with my
Girls after having a horrible experience at a seafood place in Clarksville TN. This place was amazing. We were all at the table eating our food not saying anything it was so amazing lol. Oh we will be back :)

I'm in LOVE. I came here yesterday and everything was cooked to perfection. I ordered the shrimp platter and the shrimp were so big and delicious that after I finished the meal I was stuffed (in a good way). I love supporting small businesses and it doesn't hurt that it's black owned either. 

It went at about 2 p.m. so there was only two other customers in there. As soon as I walked up to the counter, I was warmly greeted. It was like I walked into a room of friends. One of the employees actually recognized me from going to the same high school. After I placed my order, they told me it would be 10 minutes before it would be ready but it was only about 6 minutes. My meal was $18 and some change after tax. Not bad and definitely worth what you get.

I'm definitely a new customer and plan on returning time and time again!
Q4tTrdDJ95_PviqNh6w74A:
One of the exciting things about moving to Nashville was seriously the fact that i would have a sonic burger nearby!  I know, I know that sounds horrible.. 

But then again, when you come from Ca. the idea of some where that serves half way decent food at a decent price, and serves it in style of a classic drive in is amazing. 

I know you probably take it for granted as just another fast food chain locally. But its really more than that to me. Compare it to a MC D's or burger king, or any major chain. The service they offer comes across, the food doesn't come out smashed down in a sack that looks like they took turns sitting on it before passing it over.  This and In-N-Out burger set them self apart by keeping things simple, cheap and oh so Delicious. 

The advertising is classic, they use to tease us with the commercials even though there was no sonic with in hundreds of miles of us.... Now i can truly appreciate a cherry limeade anytime i want.... 

Don't overlook this gem people... embrace it and all its delicious'ness!

I'm sorry. I generally abhor fast food and everything it stands for really...but I can't help myself, I love being bad and getting a sonic blast in the evening and pulling in to a drive-thru to get it. As far as the food...eh.....it's fast food, has that fast food flavor pack taste. If you want/like that sort of thing Sonic will suffice. 

I don't really have much else to say about Sonic, or this particular location except that morons like to pull in to and out of the driveway going 10 miles per hour on to Gallatin traffic, which is generally much faster! Oh and that I have fond memories of going to my first Sonic in Ft. Collins Colorado and wondering why they didn't have Sonic in the greater L.A. area. But you don't need it with In-n-Out which basically blows all other fast food out of the water!
Clean, great employees and food was fabulous. Finally a fast food location east nashville/ Inglewood that is fast! Will be a regular customer of this location.
JKW1kPI7gi4LuiQcxdRfjw: There is absolutely NO DOUBT in my mind that this is by far the best sushi restaurant in Nashville. First of all, they serve Blowfish, which requires a special license since it could potentially kill you. Don't worry, that never happens in restaurants, mainly to fishermen who don't know what part to eat :) Sit at the sushi bar, because the sushi chefs are very friendly and if you're particularly sweet, they might give you some free tastes of amazing stuff! I ate till my pants were tight, and loved every second of it! Definitely going back to this spot.

I'm surprised that more people haven't reviewed Shintomi. I would agree with Caitlin that it is the best sushi place in Nashville (but only if that is not counting Peter's in Brentwood). I love the little cucumber and crab palet cleanser they bring you first thing. And the sushi is absolutely very fresh. You simply can not go wrong at Shintomi. It's not the cheapest sushi place around (is cheap sushi ever really a good thing? i say no.)  but keep in mind that this place is catered to the Green Hills / Belle Meade crowd.

This is my favorite sushi bar in Green Hills. I always order carryout and their prices are lower and rolls are better than any other place in Nashville. Edemame + 4-5 rolls and one pair of nagiri for around $40. Great for Green Hills. 

The atmosphere is not bad for dining in. 

Highly recommended.
LGY50abwQOxJPkq0NKe99g: I found the 8th avenue location,  what a wonderful setting for coffee, and open on Saturday & Sunday mornings too.  Hooray for Gluten Free Sweets to go with Coffee.

Oh, hi there delicious veggie sandwich.

Four words of advice for you, friends: Get the Bunny Food. With a salad. And poppyseed dressing. (Okay, so that's ten words. /shrug)

I'm not sure why it took me so long to try out FiddleCakes. I'll admit, before giving it a shot, I'd pass by and think "aw, cute bakery." It appears small from the outside, but when you walk up the steps there's a spacious front yard with picnic tables... and when you walk inside? Dining rooms beyond the order counter! And, hey... actual parking spaces around the back! What's not to love?

Prices are moderate for the amount you get. But the quality more than makes up for it. Well done, FC. Well done.

Delightful! A friend and I escaped to Fiddle Cakes this afternoon around lunch and I am seriously enchanted to meet Fiddle Cakes. 

Starting with coffee and teas the experience at Fiddle Cakes is only enhanced by the cakes, gluten free treats, cookies, cupcakes, soups, salads, and sandwiches. Ranging from $3-15 the food is fresh and unmatched. Details in the dining space are straight off of a Pottery Barn or "Real Simple" home decor page with mismatched furniture and shutter tables. 

Today, I had my first Chi tea latte and I think my affair with vanilla lattes could be over. Thank you Fiddle Cakes, thank you. I can't wait to see you again.
gl3OOTSCBR3hMDSQX-dvsg: This was our first time to Hooters and it was a good experience.  Our waitress Aisha was very friendly, helpful and efficient. Food was good as well.
Great place! Yummy delicious freshly made hot food unlike the one in Murfreesboro that serves cold wings. Good service
The renovations have definitely made a positive impact on the place. There are also a few servers who do their best to give all guests a good experience.
aiThB016Yk5mcauVKtSpNA: AMAZING AUTHENTIC JAMAICAN FOOD. Simply no complaints. We ordered the curry chicken, oxtails, smothered chicken, collard greens and rice & peas loved it!  We drive from southern Illinois (2.5hrs) for Jamaican good and we're not disappointed!

Pros:  The food is amazing.  Delicious and on point.  Could rival any eatery in Queens and Jamaica.

Cons:  The service is kind of crap.  No matter what time or day I walk in, or how many customers there are, it seems the staff is like Abbott and Costello meets Lucy and Ethel meets the WeebleWobbles...really unorganized, bumping into each other all the time, and confusing orders.  It's haha funny until the eighth time; I mean c'mon guys.  It's not that hard to be casual, yet efficient, at the same time.  For example: if you chose to leave your restaurant music on but perhaps turned the volume down, you may not muck up your customer's order (as well as when ringing it up), wasting your time and their's.

(During another visit, I asked a younger female associate if she could turn the music down.  She looked at me like I just ordered sushi and reluctantly adjusted the volume).

My bottom line is:  their service would match their food if they maybe focused more on the customers and less on they themselves "having fun".

My first experience with Jamaciaway was pretty great and I'm looking forward to trying it out again. I enjoyed a plate of goat curry, collard greens, and mashed potatoes, along with some delicious cornbread (alas they were out of johnnycakes when I went through the line). The goat was a little heavy for me, but then again it's goat. Sides were good- a little on the salty side, but that's par for the course with any meat and three (or two) in town. It was nice to see so many vegetarian options available, although I don't expect I'll be taking advantage of them any time soon- next time I go I'll be wolfing down a plate of jerk chicken.
Mdj5GGI5Zu1ETkNFnojDZQ: This Chinese place is actually pretty good!!! Their pork egg rolls and spring rolls are AMAZING!!! So delicious that I literally ate them all on the back home & wanted to turn around to go get more!! Talk about delicious!! The egg drop soup is also absolutely delicious. It's that perfect consistency that you expect from an authentic egg drop soup. SOOOO GOOD! I will admit that their lo mein was a bit on the flavorless side. Like not very good at all, but it's very reasonably priced and tastes amazing once you throw some Duck Sauce on there!! So give and take!!! All in all, Id say that this place is a great place to get some good, cheap Chinese food. :) they're friendly and also fast

They estimated delivery to be 40 minutes but managed to get to my place in 20.  They're usually fast but never take longer than their expected time.  The delivery guys are usually frustrating (ie/ talk fast, hang up on you, can't figure out how to reach you, impolite, sometimes lazy, etc, etc).  Aside from that, I do like this place.  It's cheap and most of the menu items are good.

Good
- BBQ spare ribs - sweet and salty
- steamed dumplings
- general tso's chicken- downright addictive!
- teriyaki chicken on skewers - chicken is tender 
- Beef Lo mein (fatter noodles)
- pork egg foo young - it's basically an omelet with a salty sauce
- pork chow mei fun (thin noodles)
- sesame chicken
- sweet and sour chicken - fried with dipping sauce
- "chinese vegetables" - long beans in black bean sauce - it's salty
- Boneless spare ribs
- Chicken rice soup - especially great when you have the cold!
- beef and broccoli - kind of bland

Mediocre (or bland)
- pork fried rice- rice was hard
- Scallops- pretty sure they're not scallops...
- chicken with cashews.
Finally!! Delivery Chinese to Green Hills that's awesome. Loved the steamed dumplings and crab wontons. Lo mein and pepper steak were good. Price was great. Delivery quick. Very pleased :)
TpfKgu0UN4BBzyL92nihTQ: Went for dinner ..we had the salmon dinner and cod soup..both were very good .The salmon was pan seared crispy but was nice and moist inside ..the Potato Cod soup was excellent and our waitress Stacy stated it was one of their specialties. Our waitress was very friendly and efficient..We left happy and full.
I was in hurrying and order the bangers, it is surprisingly yummy, over my expectations! Recommend!

They have a small menu and they're one of a handful of sit down restaurants in the Opryland hotel, most others are more mall-food-court style. I had the lamb burger and it was delicious! They pickle a lot of their own veg, so that was a nice addition. 

The setting is very pub like, lots of wood and low lighting. You seat yourself and we were served pretty quickly. And our waitress was awesome!
Ed5e1KqM3mihmexmVIjXwA: Flat out...it's a great meal. Had the blue crab dish, the mushroom pizza, and a few whiskey neat. Great service, sit at the bar and say hi to the person(s) next to you. You won't be disappointed.

I'd love to keep this gem to myself- but it seems the secret is out! Had dinner there last night with my son-- didn't have reservations but were lucky enough to score spots at the bar, instead. The bartender was great- very helpful and attentive. We decided to order small plates-- with a couple of starters to share. Everything was fabulous! Since they change the menu, I'll list exactly -- We had the heirloom tomato and ricotta salad- sourdough bread to start. Then we ordered all the plates under seafood/meat. The beef tartare; Royal Red shrimp; Blue Crab and Mussel Toast. Hard pressed to pick a favorite-- but if I absolutely had to, it would be the Mussel Toast. I would definitely return and recommend!

Wonderful. Great space in a part of town we have never been. Easy to get to as well. Started with Shrimp, Crab and the Fennel Peach salad. All 3 inventive, perfectly seasoned and excellent. Had the Golden Tomato pizza. Outstanding. Finished with  2 desserts: Neopolitan and Tiramisu. The ice cream wins hands down. Neither were overly sweet, as it should be. Service was 5 Star. Can't wait to come back and sample all the pies.
larqjPOY5TfoFcQoK_7FgA:
I have no idea how anyone could rate this place anything less than 5 stars. 
My husband and I loved everything we got.  The oysters, our meals and our drinks were great. The service was amazing. 
This is a MUST try in Nashville. 

The oyster ceviche is my new favorite food!

We attended the soft opening, and enjoyed a variety of dishes off of their abbreviated pre-opening menu.
The baked gulf oysters with crawfish and lemon butter were fantastic. The table next to us got a dozen raw and they looked great as well. I overheard the manager mention the possibility of $1 Happy Hour Oysters, so that's extremely exciting for landlocked oyster lovers like myself.
We had gumbo (very good, but not quite as sharp of a roux as I prefer, but the texture was perfect) and a Caesar Salad () for course number two.
Our main entrees were Cajun Pasta that was cooked perfectly in a rich cream sauce with a ton of crawfish and andouille sausage, and the Cajun Burger made with brisket and andouille and cooked to perfection as well.
Didn't try out the bar, but I'll be back for Happy Hour. The service and food were both fantastic, and this genre of restaurant with reasonable prices is desperately needed in Nashville.
So happy to be among the first to try it!

Really enjoyed my first stop into Saltine and REALLY appreciated the fact that they offer lunch for us late diners until 4pm---hello, that's one of several reasons why I stopped by in the first place!!
Beautiful looking spot, btw; I'm loving how much they transformed the place with its currently beachy blue nautical theme after the interior had served as a dark and stark brewpub for so many years.
Obviously they're brand new and just trying to get things in order, but it was a pleasant experience overall despite the odd hour and new servers. I practically had the joint to myself, which was oddly refreshing---service itself was still spotty and new(blown taps, semi-confused servers, etc), but everyone was so friendly and welcoming otherwise that it wasn't even a big deal.

As for the food, how cool is that raw bar they offer?! I'm an oyster freak, so I was beyond tickled to see so many oyster varieties there! Being the native Nashvillian I am, I had to try their "hot chicken style" fried oyster dozen, which was every bit as divine as expected---the oysters tasted plump and fresh and were fried to perfection, and even though the "hot" was laughably weak as expected, the spice itself still tasted quite nice. Served up with white bread and pickles, it was a pretty cute little local nod. Really interesting idea to offer just a DOZEN fried oysters in 3 different styles for those of us who aren't into a big basket of fried calories usually only offered at seafood joints.
The reason I knocked off a star though was their seafood gumbo---it was a bit runny, had no spice or zest in its roux, and the promised shrimp/clam combo was practically non-existent. So yeah, a bit disappointing compared to the beauty of their oysters, so hopefully they can kick that recipe up a few notches by the time I eventually return. 
And that said, I can't wait to return and experience their steady improvements. These guys definitely have the chops to be the upscale seafood joint Nashville needs far more of, so I suspect all those little growing pains will be gone the next time I eat some of those tasty underwater treats.

**Went back for Mothers Day because my mom loves her some fried oysters: FANTASTIC! Plump, flavorful, perfectly fried!! Mom has a favorite new seafood joint. Wish I could be just as enthusiastic about their poutine: AWFUL! Dry pork on limp fries with barely a hint of cheese...hopefully they can fix this dish AND the seafood gumbo.**
ds7_Qebtt-tSDXHYcefr8A: This is the cutest coffee spot I have EVER been! If your in music valley definitely stop by! Great customer service!

First of all, this was the sweetest camper and I had to stop. All I ordered was an iced coffee and it was fabulous. Had a lovely convo with fellow customer who also had an adorable camper. Wish I had tried something else on the menu. Worth the stop!

Really fantastic little stop in Donelson.

I finally had the chance to grab a quick cup of coffee en route to the flea market a couple weekends ago. The woman working was efficient and super pleasant, and really helpful. I only grabbed a coffee, but it was fresh and hot, super flavorful and reasonably priced.

I love supporting small business, and it's primarily because of experiences like this. No frills, just authentic, sincere service with a smile. Can't wait to go back for a real breakfast! Love having this little stop here in my area!
0qrSkugigIhLIN4_0onM5A: Several colleagues and I had dinner here this week (large group).  The food and service were just as fantastic as we've always experienced.  Steak cooked to perfection and flavorful, trout was fresh and tasty, and chicken dishes were great.  We also had crab cake appetizers which were delicious and chocolate cake for dessert that was decadent and hit the spot after dinner.  We were all also happy to see the fresh bread and butters were still served!  All our group dinners at Chappy's in the past (pre-Ramsey) were fantastic and this one was no exception.  Thanks Chappy & Starr for another successful dinner!

We visited Chappy's last week at 9pm on a Friday night, when my hubby and I were driving around hungry.  Saw the restaurant, been meaning to go, jumped in and asked if they can seat us, but we were dressed casually (casual chic of course), and the sweet lady gladly welcomed us.  
They even sat us in one of the cozy side rooms w/the curtains.  The server was great, very knowledgeable of the food and wine selection and very friendly.  We ordered steak and grouper.
My hubby's steak was the bomb, I tasted it, my grouper was okay I guess.  However the toppings for the grouper was delicious (mushroom brown sauce type of topping).
Wine selection was awesome, bread and exclusive butter selections were delicious.  Appetizer - delicious, some type of crab dish.
They are pricey, but we'll visit again, except next time, I'll get the steak or seafood dish.
GREAT music.  Awesome awesome Chocolate Godiva martini, my gosh!  I'll go back just for the martini and music.  All the feelings of New Orleans in there....

Bottomless Brunch Bliss

The staff was charming and fun. Our bread steward was very funny and we talked quite a bit with our waiter about such diverse topics as Cuban cigars and pubs in Sarajevo.  

The $27 four course brunch was great. You start with hot baked bread served with four types of home made butter. This indulgence is shortly followed with your four selections. You get your choice if Soup ( I chose artichoke oyster), salad (choice of dressing), main course ( I chose red snapper with crab meat ) and dessert ( I went with bread pudding). Everything we received was wonderful, fresh and well prepared. Oh did I mention that the price includes a "bottomless glass of champagne"? We thoroughly tested the "bottomless" claim and I can report that it's true! We made our slurred goodbyes and headed to our respective Sunday naps, full and happy.
Z1YH3dvDwdM6_isWY4jtrQ: Wow THIS. IS. THE. REAL. DEAL!  The restaurant is only 1 week old everything was excellent from the cocktails to the food to the service. Incredibly creative food -  Make sure you get the Chicken Thigh Blackberry thing.thing... YUM.YUM and Oh YUM!  We look forward to visit #2.

Went here with some friends in December and had an amazing experience. We had to wait about 15 minutes for our table to be ready. Not only did they apologize for the wait, but the chef sent out a complimentary appetizer to snack on while we waited. Once we got to our table the owner came by several times to check on us and the chef again sent out a complimentary appetizer to apologize for the wait. We were blown away by everyone we encountered there and by the amazing food. This place is definitely worth giving your business to. We need to encourage more Nashville restaurants to value customers and provide great customer service!

We went to Peninsula last night just to try it out, absolutely my favorite thing I've had in Nashville. We just got back from Sicily where we had fresh shrimp crudo daily, so we were a bit skeptical to order their red shrimp and tomato toast. We were totally blown away. It was definitely as good and fresh as anything we had on the Sicilian coast. My favorite thing was the rabbit with Garlic broth. It blew my mind, one of the best dishes I have ever had. I don't take the time to write reviews like I should but I had to take the time to write this one-it was just that good!!
xXgE_rj-tK9v_MIH3mXpaQ: Reggie and company cook up the real deal at this BBQ-to-go shack in East Nashville.  Named for Reggie's daughter, Dee's Q has some of the best fried catfish, meaty wings, tender pork shoulder and succulunt baked beans in town.  The ambiance is great, thanks in large part to the strobe light.

Heavenly falling off the bone, spice rubbed and slow cooked to perfection barbecued ribs. Service was like my going to my grandma's house for a holiday dinner: sweet and courteous, showing sincere concern that I had a good experience. What they scrimp on decor and building materials, they make up in spades with food and service.
