This place is literally a shack with a precarious bigger seating area shack next store with the BBQ cooker in the backyard out back waaay off the beaten track. It wasn't even listed on my rental's navigation system.

The sauce, which is served on the side, is perfectly among consistency,  hotness, spiciness and sweetness. 

Back to the service, I didn't experience anything similar to Paul H. The older woman behind the counter was sweet, courteous and considerate to a fault. When she asked how everything was, I knew she really meant it. 

I'm only in town for the week but I will try to pack in as many meals there as I can.

All hail the Dee's for being a hole in the wall that delivers on yumminess. I think to myself as I approach this little yellow shack, how good can this be? The answer is pretty darn good.. 

We normally order the rib and chicken. I am not in to pulled pork sandwiches so have no insight on that. The ribs are very good but unless you are looking for very spicy BBQ, I would get the normal sauce. The same goes for the chicken. They smoke it all on sight and the smell as you arrive are lovely. The sides are standard southern fare and seem to be adequate. We have tried some of the appetizers and they are good but not earth shattering. 

All in all if you are in a hurry, in need of some smokey ribs and don't have 3 hours to smoke them yourself, Dee's is a handy alternative.  They do have a tent thing you can eat in but it's pretty sparse.  I suggest getting it and heading over to Shelby park for a picnic in a more picturesque setting.
Dq8xEHck5DugMQZVLDpwxA: The best evening Ethiopian Food in Nashville.... the lamb tibs are worth making many trips to this place...And the star is Sambusa with the crispy exterior and the lentil filling hits all the right taste buds... And the coffee is sooo good..the family who runs the place is very friendly and always smiling
These sambusas though. And this strong coffee is hitting the spot. This place is the real deal.

Attention coffee roasters, you can get a pound of Ethiopian green coffee beans for $10 at this wonderful little place. Good coffee and friendly service. Wish they offered coffee ritual Ethiopian style!  Lovely.
PG1L06w6sonKGsTJEbCZIQ: Easy, quick, and the staff is very nice. Good food too!  If you are in search of a hole-in-the-wall type place in Nashville that isn't fried food, look no further.

We are from out of town, and with a gnawing hunger in our bellies, set off to have something light to eat before dinner, a snack of sorts. We stumbled across this place, and decided to try it out. We were NOT disappointed. 
I had the chicken philly platter, and the plate was piled high with delicious chicken smothered in cheese, on a bed of rice flavored with saffron, a Mediterranean salad with I gradients I picked from a small selection, pita bread, and lovely Tsatziki sauce. Yummy, to say the least. 
My companion had the gyro, which was stuffed with a lot of meat, and he was more than satisfied with it. 
His combo came with fries. 
Awesome place. A hole in the wall, but worth checking it out.

I work just down the road from this restaurant, so it happens to be somewhere I have a lot of experience with. Overall I think the food is really quite good, but let's not mince words: the location is a dive. They recently spruced up the paint and gave it a bit of a makeover, but lipstick on a pig blah blah blah. But hey, I'm not scared by divey locations, not if the owners appear to care about upkeep. There is a difference between a dive and a sh*thole, and this is a dive, which is fine. Anyway, the food is really what counts, and for my money (and not much of it either!) the food is really quite good and consistent. Trivia: these guys used to be in Harding Mall, back when it wasn't a Walmart, and moved up the road when it got knocked down. Anyway, food: I'm not sure what they do to their "ranch sauce" but it's delightful and if you get fries always go for the seasoning they offer. I generally stick to their gyro meat (a blend of lamb and beef) but the chicken is a strong choice too. The food is fresh and the staff is friendly. I would call this more "Mediterranean" than Greek (splitting hairs?) but mostly because the people who run it are Egyptian, so I think that brings a slightly different flavor profile to the food other than what a straight up Greek influence would. So, yeah, this is a down and dirty kind of place, great for a fast lunch and some arabic language TV, which is always entertaining.
aWhSMQ-osXLCh8bQRV1-og: Loved it!! Grew up eating pizza king in Indiana and was looking for something similar - found it!! Such delicious pizza with crumbly sausage, square pepperonis and square cut pizza. And the employees are super friendly and accommodating. No, it's not gourmet pizza, it's not meant to be. It's comfort food. Eat it!!!!

Wonderful surprise!

My first time visiting this restaurant today. Pizza buffet was out, and I was not disappointed. They serve a thin crust style pizza with a robust tomato sauce & stringy cheese, (y'know, the way mozzerella is supposed to look). The salad and pasta aren't top-shelf, but it is consistent with the $6.99 price tag. I happened in on a chance, and although the outside of the restaurant is decieving, it has a lot of seating  and the place looks clean. Definitely going to come back here for a pizza fix!

I dont like the pizza.  The beer buckets are cool. The games in the back are nostalgic. My server was excellent! Anthony with his signature pink hair is a doll and really knows what he is doing. He delivers and serves at the same time. Yall outta give him a raise cause he works his butt off!!
W7zbwJQOKfNEXH16ShqbIw:
Something about this chain is so endearing to me... and this one is no different. 

Got my chocolate chip pecan waffle while the boyfriend got a beef hash with sausage gravy. My waffle was kinda dry and his topping was kinda salty, but exactly what we expected, which is comforting in itself. Consistency.

Staff was great, made a joke about me being easy in the wafflehouse way.  A good chuckle, a full tummy, and ready for the next stretch of our roadtrip.

Breakfast fixed in front of you and with a quickness.  I dont mind the truckstop cafe atmosphere.   eggs, hashbrowns 100,000 ways and coffee, something almost primal about it.   if you want your pancakes with whipped cream and fruity a$$ toppings, hop on down to that other place.

My 4 star rating is mainly for the service.  The food is okay with the waffles being pretty darn good. The prices are very reasonable and meet the quality of the food. 

The servers and kitchen staff are fun to watch as it looks like organized chaos.  The service is outstanding and everyone helps each other to be sure you're not missing anything. 

If you want a fancy meal, look elsewhere.  If you're looking for something to fill the breakfast void,,, this is your place!
75zH6kUTxqkAJVIhuSdICQ: Great service and great smoothies. I heard about the spot while attending a music concert in Nashville

Excellent Ginger Shots, one hot with cayenne and one sweet with honey and grapefruit.  Best smoothie I've ever had it seems with the Bee Green smoothie.   I don't live around here, but I would frequent this place if I did.  Also reasonable prices. Keep up the great work.

On days I won't have time to leave my office for lunch, I make sure to stop into the Urban Juicer on my way to work and grab one of the lunch boxes they have prepped. They contain a 1/2 wrap (both the super chicken and the curry chicken are wonderful and not spicy), a quinoa salad with side dressing, and a 12oz juice. Delicious, healthy, and super convenient!
Vg0_7YIyaabkDHKoWV8V9g: Sooo fricken amazing! Perfectly simple southern fried chicken you can't stop eating. Mind blown! Great prices and awesome service!

We went here last night with another couple and had a great time.  

Highlights: 

- Fantastic Latkes
- Deep fried bacon burger
- Buy-one-get-one beer happy hour

The menu was well crafted and had a variety of options.  It's a small place and it turned out that the head chef was working the front last night as well as the kitchen.  He was incredibly informative and friendly and we had a blast hanging out there.  We started with Latkes for the table which were spiced perfectly. I had to try the deep friend bacon burger after his recommendation and my girlfriend had their poutine (holy cow it was good).

The meal was heavy, but we expected that with what we ordered.  There were a number of other options that seemed lighter and that would be fun to try when we go back.  I can't find their menu online, but I'm sure it will be up somewhere soon as they get more set-up. I would highly recommend trying the deep-fried burger (mix of bacon and burger).  It's a treat. 

Also, outdoor seating looked awesome.  Can't wait to come back in the Spring.

A quick review based on one touristy visit.  It was hot and I wanted to eat inside.  Yelp lists it as the closest place to Centennial Park and the Parthenon so I was just as happy to stop here.  The waitress recommended the Hot Chicks basket as being representative.  Well, okay. See pic. Tasty, boneless, and the mac and cheese was fine.  You don't come to Nashville for the food, and I was taking a bit of a chance ordering this meal, as previous attempts at fried food with the ubiquitous pickles upset my stomach.  The hottest spiciness was only about a 2 out of 5... and I'm from Minnesota.  But this combination worked, and the dipping sauces were good.  Friendly staff, small and quaint dining area.  I didn't have any of the liquor.
vi5CbxgB_ondX_OmRVoM7Q: Restaurant is open again - closed Sat & Sunday.

Went to Jim's Coney Island last night after a ball game, and I must say the place was a pleasant experience.   I would actually give this place a 3.5 based on the following.  A had a Coney with coney sauce,mustard and onions.  And its was pretty good, but would have been better if it were $2 instead of $3.  The food i give a 3, but the owner Ron and the atmosphere i give a solid 4.  First off this place only stays open to 8, but here i was strolling in at a little after 9 and he welcomed me in.  After asking if i enjoyed the dog, he asked if i'd try a new menu item called a "loose burger' which is basically like a sloppy joe on a hot dog bun, and i must say it was might tasty (plus it was on the house :) The atmosphere is nice considering it's in a run down gas station.  It feels like a bar, with flat panel tv's, cool musical decor, even a little (and i do mean little) area to play music.  Plus there is a nice area with picnic tables outside.  This place is a welcome addition to the Crieve Hall area, and it's a good spot to grab a bite.

Finally made it into the restaurant.  M-F lunch.  Had a couple of coneys, the kids had hot dogs in various states of undress (ketchup only kind of kids).  Owner couldn't have been more personable & friendly.  I ordered a combo but ended up not eating the chips so he rang it up differently to save a little money.  Not from Detroit, have never had a Detroit Coney - but the chili & dog were a great combo.  We'll be back when we're in the area.  Support your local businesses!
ceN3LjaLJsB32dY3PYgX9g: We tried a sample of the Caesar chicken and it was amazing; the Caesar had so much flavour in just the chicken alone. My boyfriend decided he wanted to  try the roast beef. The roast beef was a little lacking, it really needed something crunch to give it some texture and the flavour was all one note. Also they added way too much mayo to it. I would come back but would probably just get the Caesar chicken.
This was the best crepe I've ever had! It was light with a slight crunch and plenty of filling inside. Try the #7. Good food.

I ate my first crepe ever at Music City Crepes inside the Nashville Farmers Market. I ordered the Philly cheesesteak crepe, which was filled with steak, onions, peppers and cheese (I left off the mushrooms). Those fillings were very flavorful and the crepe around them was delicious as well.

I also had a few bites of a banana and Nutella, which was a fantastic way to end the meal.

Overall, I can't think of a better place to journey into the world of crepes. I can't wait to go back!
f-XDCUvtS22XIgIzXUtWOA: Favorite hookah lounge I've been to. I always stop here after I go to sitar next door. (Every time I come to nashville) the only flavor I've tried that I didn't like was pineapple. It burned my throat a lot for some reason. Also I wished they played something besides rap and the top 40. Something more relaxing would be nice. Overall, still my favorite place to go.

This place has by far the best hookah in Nashville.  I usually cough at least once during a hookah smoking session, but the hookah smoke at Tarboosh always slides down my throat just like oxygen.  Yeah, there are a few harsher flavors, but they're few and far between.  My favorite is the triple apple.  It literally tastes like homemade apple pie...in gaseous form!

I love the atmosphere too.  I see a lot of reviews saying that the atmosphere is trashy, but that's kind of the point, if that makes any sense.  Hookah is a Middle Eastern tradition.  When I go to a hookah lounge, I want to feel like I'm in the Middle East, minus the crazy terrorists and religious wars.  Tarboosh does that.  No, it doesn't have air conditioning or heat (as far as I know), so don't come here when it's really hot or really cold.  Smoke at home.  I always come here on pleasant nights.  They keep the windows open, and there's even outdoor seating.  There's not even any need for indoor smoke filtration because it all escapes into the outside air.  This place is basically a small shack.  It's all wooden walls and concrete floors, and there's cushions on benches lining the walls.  There's also a nice widescreen TV hanging up in the front playing Middle Eastern music videos, adding even more to the raw, Mediterranean-ish vibe.

They also serve fresh, flavored, hot tea.  This goes really nice with hookah, especially if you pair the flavors right.  For example, I love my triple apple hookah with cinnamon tea.  It helps the harsher hookahs go down smoother.

The only complaints I have about Tarboosh is the pricing and the absence of alcoholic drinks.  The price isn't so bad if you have two people, but the thing is that it's $12 for one OR two people, and if you have a party of three or more, you have to start paying per customer.  I can't remember how much it is per person, but it seems a bit unreasonable that you should have to pay extra for more people, even though you're all smoking from one single hookah.  I guess that's just their way of making extra money.  And, like I said, there is no alcohol.  I'm sure it's just because they don't think it's worth getting a license.  But I like to drink cocktails with my hookah.  It's not a huge deal though because I still love the tea.  If you want to drink, I suppose you just have to go to Aladdin's.
Small (read: basically a trailer), but the hookah is tasty and fairly cheap. Good place to hang out with friends before a night out.
U39eiGXwlUOYnnUy5o46xA:
Love, love, love.

I became addicted to the strawberry cake from Anne's at Boxwood Bistro (now gone, sigh) and they let me know the origin.

A birthday with a whole cake from Anne's Cakes is a GREAT birthday, and well worth the slightly incremental price for this versus a grocery store cake or other bakery.

Super friendly, super nice, when picking up cakes I've heard nothing but raves from other patrons doing the same and swearing by the many other flavors, so I think I can safely say there's no way to go wrong here.

Call, order, be patient and flexible, and enjoy.

After searching the world over I have found the existence of THE Perfect Coconut Cake.  I can't explain the pure decadence in this delightful cake, nor can I praise it near what it deserves.  DELICIOUS!!!

The muffins are good - particularly the pumpkin and chocolate chip variety - but it's the amazingly friendly service that blew us away.

The proprietor (Anne, I presume) is the sweetest and most welcoming Nashvillian we've met in a while - and that's saying something. 

Do keep in mind that Anne's primarily sells products wholesale to cafes and restaurants. So while you *can* buy individual muffins, you cannot buy a single slice of cake - just an entire cake or pie. 

So throw a party or invite over six of your closest friends. It's a great excuse to buy a cheesecake. :-)
U7spKEIFx51500U4Fi0tGA: This place is near work, so I pick it up on the way to the office when in a rush.  I get the chicken burrito.  It's legit.  They give you it on a to go place with salsa, hot sauce, sautéed onions, and some jalepenos on the side.  You can dine in as well with table service.  This is a Mexican restaurant that Mexicans go to.

so, then. i reviewed this place some time ago, i just had the info wrong:

http://www.yelp.com/biz/taco-lopez-2-nashville

on to my review:

all right, nashville...

if you didn't know by now, the taco stands you see on the side of the road are (for the most part) INCREDIBLE. i can understand if you're reluctant to try them, as they really aren't too terribly inviting. this particular joint would be a perfect "first timer's" place to check out street meat tacos. they've cleaned up this spot quite a bit. i remember stopping here many nights after 3am in anything but sober condition. back then, it was just a motorized stand/cart/truck like the others. they now have seating and a card machine. woooo!

anyway, for lunch, you can get a half chicken, a gang of rice, a gang of beans, homemade tortillas, and a couple of times, fresh cut fries(??) for SIX DOLLARS! this is a criminal amount of food for next to nothing. and it's sooooo good. as far as the tacos go, i'm an al pastor man, and the al pastor here is second only to san luis. the horchata here is a little heavy (i'm not really much of a fan, anyway), and they charge you for each can of soda you get.

anways, you get the deal. great food, great prices, and they speak english for the non-spanish-speaking -types. check it out asap!

eric b

aaaaand the update:

just an update...

the great nashville taco price war of 2009 has reached tacos lopez. they are NINETY NINE CENTS EVERY DAY!! i just decimated an order for the first time in a while and they were a mite better than i remember. show this and all taqueria lopez locations some love, nashville. the best tacos around for real.

eric b
This is the undiscovered gem you are looking for.  4 tacos rice and beans for less than $9.  Uhh Mazing!
JycfyMakty6nHVwy2wggIQ:
I have been shopping at Global since they were located on Church Street back in they day (where Suzy Wong's is located). Their selection of asian foods is stream lined to the essentials which is important for me. They really stand out in terms of their offerings from England.

My favorite...the huge bag of Rice Crackers for a reasonable price!

The location is certainly odd...but go for it!

As others, I started buying Asian groceries here when they were on Church Street - met Deb Paquette (at the Bound'ry then and now the XO at Etch) a few times there.  Unfortunately, Jim Reed's property folks forced Ed to leave, and the move destroyed his walk-in clientele.  The place is now closed.

RIP - another one bites the dust...

This review is in thanks to Claire B. who basically told me that if anyone in Nashville would have Vegemite it would be Global Market. She was correct and because of her (and Global Market) I can now satisfy the weirdest of my pregnant cravings. They also have Tim Tams (more flavors of such than world market offers), Mi Goreng, and various other things that otherwise would be impossible to find in this city. They receive 5 stars based on that alone.
Global Market is located in a pretty sketchy area in a windowless building that I would not have been brave enough to venture into by myself.  The owner is very friendly and helpful. But the place itself is a little messy and unorganized. Although not so much so as to take away any stars.
Thanks again Claire B.!
o37egH3jB6egfgCbHIdoCA: Delicious authentic Italian style pizza. The staff is straight from Italy and it shows in their food. Definitely a late night staple.
The pizza is to die for. Our delivery girl was an absolute delight. She arrived twenty minutes earlier than projected and was such a sweetheart. I would recommend this restaurant in a heartbeat.
This is a great place to have lunch if you work in downtown. Their eggplant Parmesan pasta is delicious
i6Sb4Gm5O-w1rtVZWwzbyA: Phenomenal experience tonight. Came in 35 minutes till closing with 7 custom orders due to a food sensitivity. The staff was super nice and understanding even late on a Friday. Juices are priced what you'd expect but the ingredients are organic and fresh. Would definitely recommend.

I enjoyed the "'mint to be" smoothie! It was by far the best smoothie I have ever had. Immediately as you walk in the door you're welcomed by the entire staff! I wish I could give more than 5 stars! This place was awesome!
Addicted to Orange You Smooth smoothie.  Great vibe and great option for healthy nutrition
DcjdC_yJZY9i-5DupjGj6w: Restaurant quality pizza in a take-out restaurant. Had this for the first time tonight and it was spectacular. Got a veggie pizza and a build-your-own with meatball and basil. We loved the pizza and everyone at the shop was really nice. We will be back for sure.

We were feeling lazy and wanted to try this place out for dinner. We ordered the Shrimp pizza and their Lemon Pepper wings with delivery to our home. The pizza was amazing!!!!! Full of shrimp and jalepenos. we added beef to our pizza for more meat and it was soooo good. 
The wings I was not impressed, hence instead of 5 stars I gave 4. They were over cooked and didnt have any Lemon Pepper on them. The delivery driver was nice. Very friendly. We will order pizza from here again, but definitely not the wings!
We ordered the pesto, margarita and pepperoni.  Pizza was awesome. Pepperoni is extremely greasy!!!
-V0NjJcmkHxemSIW5_I5uw: I have been back to Cooper's twice since my original review. Now that the bar is well-stocked I have really enjoyed it. They have an awesome range of beers including some good high gravity beers. I still haven't had a chance to have a full meal, but The dessert I did try was yummy and the food at the other tables looks great. I have heard from a friend that brunch is amazing. The servers are wonderful and attentive. I look forward to coming back more often in the future and hopefully it will soon be patio weather.

I visited this restaurant last week and was so pleased with the addition to the neighborhood. I was incredibly impressed with the beer selection and the friendliness of the staff though my first beer's carbonation seemed a bit off. 

I was incredibly pleased with the food though. The pretzels came out warm and doughy and I had to fight my boyfriend for the horseradish/cheese accompaniment. I was also impressed with my soup/salad combo with the seasonal salad and the soup special of roasted squash and garlic. 

I can't wait to go back and plan on hitting up the happy hour soon!

WARNING! Lengthy review but it is a review of 3 different visits. It will explain my 5 stars....if not prepared to read entirely, skip to the last....

First visit to Cooper's...
My man, my friend, and I went to Cooper's not too long after it opened. We had a great time and really liked the atmosphere. I kinda thought it was a little bit of bar and little bit of restaurant mixed together. We didn't eat full a full meal, instead we munched on the zucchini fritter thingies and they were delicious. Also we were really impressed with the beer selection and did not expect it to be so rounded.

Second visit to Cooper's...
Horrible. We went with a moderately sized group, including fellow yelper Kevin D., and could not have been more disappointed. Prices were high, the menu was schizophrenic, and the service was lousy and not at all apologetic. By the time we received our food we were debating who we would sacrifice and eat so that we would survive. The food was not impressive.

Third visit to Cooper's...
I did not want to give it another go, but it was late, I was hungry, and we live right down the street. SO GLAD WE DID! First, they slimmed down the menu and it is now much more cohesive. We sat on the patio and was greeted by my all-time favorite East Nashville server Alaina Jones! She is back in Nashville! Lainey recommended the Ahi Tuna appetizer saying that it is "sick," and she is right. Perfectly cooked, and the sauces on the side complimented the tuna in the best way. I ordered the Grilled Steak Salad and I don't think I have ever had a simpler yet yummier salad ever. EVER. Red Onion Marmalade is my new favorite. David wasn't even hungry but decided to order something just because all of my food tasted so good. The Pretzels delivered. Smack me in the mouth with greatness.  The food and 5 drinks brought our tab to $55. That has never happened in East Nashville. Thanks to David for taking me back. LAAAAAAVVVVE. Go now.
spPkaal5fJVlNIKz1WoH-g:
Great service at this Publix. Only issue was bad produce, brought back to store for full refund. 

Manager has special ordered items for us, and butchers do a great job with speciality cuts.

Always clean, everyone is always super nice and helpful, and if they don't carry something you really wish they would, they'll get it for you! Bonus. 
Good prices on produce, rotisserie chickens are always hot and fresh, and their other hot bar items are awesome when you really don't want to have to prepare anything (read as: get the chicken tenders, they're awesome). They often have good Buy one, Get one deals, so keep your eyes open for them.

They are just so nice here.  It makes grocery shopping such a more pleasant task.  The store is always clean and the produce is always fresh (which is more than I can say for certain other grocery stores nearby).  The deli sandwiches are so good (not unique to this Publix, but that doesn't make it any less true).

My only complaint is that I sometimes don't really "get" the layout of the store or why they put certain products where they do (mostly having to do with gluten free and/or organic items).  Luckily, the super-friendly employees are always around to help!
UxLkYuEVdlMYwIw6ioZesQ:
My experience with the Ombi bar was when they would have the "Left Can Dance" dance parties after hours here. It was really fun, the crowd was 18+ because it's a restaurant.  The music was really great, they played tons of indie dance music, eletro, and new wave. It was super fun.  This event has sinced moved to Foo Bar in East Nashville... but the Ombi Bar room was always really great. 

I really like the look of the bar, it is very modern with sleek wood panels beveled and layered across the ceiling to make the room look like a cove. They have small metal chain screen to separate the bar area from the tables at the side.  It's pretty cute, it lets you have a peek-a-boo view of the diners throughout the room.  It's a classy place.

I started going to Ombi years ago when Left Can Dance began at Ombi (not really a Buddytown thing, by the way - I saw some other reviewers make that mistake).  I went every Friday all summer and got to know the staff really well.  Terrell, the bar manager, is still a good friend of mine.  Make sure to try his Manhattan when you drop in.  

LCD got kicked out when they reformatted from bar/restaurant to focus more on the fine dining aspect.  This is also when they dropped "Le Peep" from their name.  The food there is amazing, though definitely not cheap.  The bartenders and servers are trained and tested in their wine and fine whiskey knowledge so you can trust them select a good match for you.  

The layout and design of Ombi is very sleek and trendy, but not in an obnoxious Gulch-y way.  A great time to check it out is when they do a wine or food tasting so make sure to check their calendar.  Another good time is at the (monthly?) Forward parties where they offer great drink specials like $6 name your gin/vodka martinis.

Ombi - you intrigue me.  I've heard about you here and there for months and I finally went for a visit during restaurant Week.  That said, what you served up more than met my expectations and I will certainly add you to my list of favorite dinner spots.

The atmosphere is almost cave-like...but in a good way - maybe a sleek European cave with industrial accents.  Anyway, the dining room is pretty intimate, especially for the number of tables they include.  

Our server was thoughtful, and as there was a wine tasting event going on in back, he strategically sat us so we wouldn't be bothered by the noise.  Very thoughtful.  

The food was very nicely thought out too.  Despite the fact that we were ordering froma  special menu, I felt as though it was fairly representative of Ombi's normal fare.
  
First course: I started out with an arugula salad with green apple, blue cheese and a balsamic vinaigrette.  In a word, crisp.  It was lovely.

Second course: I ordered the gnocchi with brussel sprouts, cauliflower, madeira and parmesan.  It was also tasty, but it felt more like a side dish.  Plus, the portion was tiny.  It was a good thing that I could share my partner's trout - which was excellent.  It was dusted with cornmeal and pan seared, and then served over a medley of roasted root vegetables.  Yum. 

Third course: I ordered the vanilla bean panna cotta with bruleed peaches.  Holy cow this was good.  It was light and perfectly flavored and lasted for about thirty seconds after it was served.

All in all, I really enjoyed my experience here.  I'm interested in checking on one of their wine tasting events and sqampling from their full menu.  But, Ombi did a great job capitalizing on the opportunity provided by Restaurant Week.
APJmC1vBW39h3NToBdhdig: This is very entertaining. The food is delicous too. I think there is a lot of promise for this concept with different stories and such. I will recommend this to friends.

Beyond Amazing!!
This was the most awesome experience!   Don't google too much, just live it.  The evening was something I will always remember.   The stories, food an company was just wonderful!   I would give it 10 stars if I could!!!!

If you want a unique Nashville experience available nowhere else in the country get yourself to Dinnertime Stories!  This two hour dining concept held in the old world setting of The Standard downtown Nashville takes you on a journey to the regions visited by Marco Polo along the silk route.  LePetit Chef is your animated tour guide, introducing you to each of the six courses along the route, using 3D visual technology.  Each course is served in a unique way with props and ever changing decorations, music and lighting.  The whole evening is interactive, since seating is at a long table with all guests interacting and sharing their experience.  Between courses you can choose to add an entry into small travel journals, and you have time to read what other diners have shared on their journey.  It really is hard to put into words this totally immersive dining experience, it's something you must see and taste for yourself!
a-gkPi0KFpRNGiJfJ8ARAg: Went on a Wednesday and it was not very busy which made it more romantic. Our server Scott was perfect: attentive but not overbearing with excellent suggestions. We expected good food and got amazing food. Eggplant toast was legit, a tiny bit too salty, but still very delicious. Seafood stew and pork shoulder entrees were full of bold, yet balanced flavors and serving sizes were medium to large which makes the price easier to swallow. Go there now. Worth it.

This has got to be one of the nicest restaurants with the weirdest location.  After that, it's nothing but adorableness.  

Miel is adorable - from outside to inside!  Very elegant and sophisticated with just a touch of trendy.  My only complaint is that I got stuck with the cushioned bench seat against the wall.  I felt like I was sliding off of it.  There's also outside seating with a fire pit that somehow obscures the fact Charlotte Pike is just a few yards away.  We were also told that the two adjacent house and building will be turned into a little specialty shop and a room for private parties, respectively.  

Miel also has a local farm that they get many of their ingredients from for the restaurant.  This attention to using local and fresh ingredient is evident.   The dishes are inventive and mostly well executed.  A few dishes were excellent, while the others were just good.  But, all in all, our meal was very good.  

My dinner companion's (DC) first course was the foie gras, which was swoonable, and mine was the sweetbread, which started out too sweet but got better with each bite.  DC's main course was the Crisp Duck Breast, and mine was the sirloin steak.  The Crisp Duck Breast was, in fact, not crisp and turned out to one of those "just good" dishes.  The sirloin steak was good, but not anything overly special.  I would've preferred it to be a bit more rare since it was a bit on the tough side.

But it seems that dessert is where it's at for Miel.  Although we heard great things about the profiteroles, we ended up getting the cheese plate and the panna cotta.  Both were very excellent, especially the panna cotta, which may be the best I've ever had.  (I was told the secret is the goat's milk.  Shhh.)

I've read service can be spotty and snotty.  Who knows?  Maybe we came on a good night since the service was excellent.  Our waitress was friendly, knowledgeable, and attentive without being overly solicitous.  Seema, the owner, is very active on the floor, seeming to make a point in serving at least one dish to each table.  It was a nice touch since she got to chat with each table.

The bill was something like $160 for all of the above plus two glasses of red wine and a cocktail.

Miel is definitely not my weekly hot spot to go, only because I wouldn't want to foot the bill that often.  But I would definitely go again for a special occasion or to bring out-of-towners.
Fantastic meal. Definitely worth a visit.  Not at all snooty as some reviewers have suggested.  Great local food and fantastic wines!
jVjJx4hMN-7H0BzvThoimg: For those, like myself, who are unfamiliar with Ethiopian food, this is a great lunch spot with a friendly staff, where you can say "I'll have whatever you think I should have," and not be disappointed. The nice lady at the counter recommended the combo beef and chicken tibs with spicy lentils on injera bread, and she was not wrong.

This was my first foray into Ethiopian cuisine, and I was absolutely pleasantly surprised. I ordered the veggie combo, and with the owner's help, I decided on the lentils, beets, greens, and cabbage as my four vegetable choices. These, with the injera bread, were perfectly prepared and quite filling. For good measure, I also ordered a veggie sambusa, which was delicious as well. 

With a tip from an Ethiopian friend in mind, I wished the owners "melkam Gena" on my way out (I came the day before the Ethiopian calendar's Christmas), and they both seemed genuinely excited by the gesture. Very gracious and nice folks that make delicious food for vegetarians and omnivores alike. I like forward to returning soon.

The ONLY negative I can think of is that, given its location downtown, it does require some strategy and determination to get to. Not a mark against the restaurant, but definitely something to consider.

I have been eating Ethiopian Food for over 20 years and in various cities and countries.  This is a gem.  Food is excellent, hot (space) sauce is freshly made and service is wonderful.  The owners are extremely nice and take pride in what they make.  I am so happy I can walk here for lunch and will be treating myself weekly on going.  Thank you very much Ajora.  See you next week!
6xvBd0qBwgqC5EKt2aLTAA: This is basically the best takeout Chinese in West Nashville... downside is it's pickup only.
Very good flavor, texture, service!   Family owned, and they really care about quality and their customers.

Great food! I have lived in Nashville for almost a decade and have been searching for some quality Chinese- how lucky for me that I moved next to China Panda! We love the dumplings and the lo mein is delicious too! My Chinese food hating husband even loves it!
M1w-dOmbD0OjM5T0tLtiJg:
This review is based on the awesome yoga brunch hosted by Eio and Shakti Yoga. I will say brunch and yoga are my kryptonite so these things together were just too much fun. There was an awesome DJ and the owners even participated in yoga as well. The sense of community was great! After yoga we were served juice samples and chia seeds with beets, granola and pineapple which were all delicious. Pretty much everyone opted for brunch after the killer workout we all had. I will say the line was a bit daunting at first but it moved quickly and you can use your wait time to figure out what you want. There was someone there to make sure you had a menu in line which was very helpful. I opted for the Cesar with rotisserie, my fiancé ordered the western and our friends ordered the flap jacks. We were all super impressed with our meals. The Salad was very very simple  and not heavy at all. The Western was delicious with the bacon and veggies being the star of the show. The flapjacks were really good as well from what I tasted. It were a little on the sweet side for my taste. I would definitely suggest getting like bacon or something to kind of cut the sweet and break it up or ordering them as an appetizer. In true brunch fashion we had the $5 mimosas of course and my fiancé tried the oolong cocktail which was tasty, not that boozy but definitely tasty. I will say it was a bit of task when they were busy to get another drink due to the fact that you have to go up to the counter against to order it if no one is walking by that you can grab. No one likes to get tried on during brunch...just saying. 

Long story short I had a great time here and will definitely come back. The outdoor seating and sense of community were great here. The decor was awesome the food was unique, healthy, and flavorful. Price point was also pretty reasonable for the quality of food you receive.

EiO & The Hive is a fantastic restaurant if you like healthy, delicious food! They have something for everyone...organic meats to vegan dishes. The vibe is uber-cool too as a "honey hive" for community building. I meet clients there for lunch and also go there with my family on the weekends and it's always a good experience.
The food is great. The mixed drinks are soso. My alcoholic friends didn't finish their bloody Mary's.
cXbxYg_OODeznLp0aCIqmA: The best!! Clean, fresh, creative, huge portions and full bar!!

Discovered this place today as I was driving by and decided to give it a shot. I'm glad I did. Gulfcoast shrimp burrito was fantastic. The guacamole was even better. So happy to see some decent restaurants popping up along Charlotte Ave.

Really love this place.  The staff is GREAT and the atmosphere is casual. I love the 90210 with fresh jalapenos added in. That's really all i have eaten there, but not sure why I need to eat anything else.  I understand about the price seeming a little high, but the food portions are so big that i take 1/2 home and then i have 2 meals for the price of one.  Great location, friendly people, something for everyone on the menu. can't wait for the cocktail lawn to open.
cKfonqHkDUx-G0_ksHASbw: Great and friendly staff. Every time I go the staff is incredibly helpful and just as excited as I am about my smoothie. Great place to get refreshed.

I love this place! It's so convenient for me it right down the street from my job. When I don't want a hard breakfast I come here. The staff is always nice and the ingredients are always fresh. Always look on groupon cause they usually have great deals for this place.
I'm so happy they opened one in Nashville! It's been two years since I last had Jamba Juice when I lived in Phoenix. It's WAY better than smoothie king! Can't wait to go back!
7evVLlKovx8DV4CfqJgXww: We have been to Lyra a couple of times now both for dinner and happy hour and it has been a wonderful experience. The menu changes with the season and does not disappoint. Big flavors with fresh and creative dishes. The servers and the management are friendly and knowledgeable. Will recommend to all of my friends.
Super yummy. I had the chicken and it was a lot of food. Tasted really good. Also, the hummus is amazing.

Ate at Lyra last night with dear friends.  The chef explained and described the dishes to us.  The waiter, Justin, was charming and helpful.  He was knowledgeable about celiac and what I could eat.  The food was a delight of new tastes and textures.   If you want a new experience, a charming atmosphere and knowledgeable and talented staff this is the place for you.
sTo-Xzn4MfpglsRnZggDVw: I tried this place because of its convenient location to where I work when I saw a Groupon offer. I didn't regret it! This is one of the best sandwiches I've had in Nashville. (I tried the Club OSB).  Fast and super friendly service. I will DEFINITELY be back and would HIGHLY recommend this place!! Next time I will try the Turtle Pie, too.
Very good service food was amazing. The staff was very informative and made recommendations. Little hard to find but well worth it when you do

Five stars for the friendly, helpful and efficient staff. The food is good for a very small setup without a full kitchen.  I'm sure that this place is very popular with folks working in that office complex but probably not worth a long drive unless you just want to check out somewhere new and get a good sandwich for five bucks.  Try a specialty bread, like jalapeño cheese or cranberry.  
It IS hard to find though. Go into the building with 52 on the front. Look for the very small signage. This place is stuck in a small office suite down a hall of office suites. Enjoy!
8rIrPv5dq77fOTVgDgx6jg: Loved it. yep Loved it. I food was very good. This is a great place to eat by yourself or bring a friend. I had a dinner meal here. Everything was tasty. I have a gluten allergy and they had gluten free soy. A lot of places don't. So I was happy. Don't think you could go wrong with this place. Its a must at the Gaylord Hotel.

Mmmm I really enjoyed this place. First off the ambience here is amazing, you overlook the water and the gardens and it's just stunning. 

We stopped in mid day to tour the hotel and were hungry, many of the restaurants in the hotel aren't open at lunch but Wasabi was so we decided to give it a try. Their menu is a unique blend of sushi and other Asian fare as well as more Americanized sandwiches and such. 

I had a bowl of the crab bisque and tuna nigri sushi. My friend had the soup also and the vegetable spring rolls. We loved all of our food but that soup was amazing. The only downside was that a bowl of soup was actually the size of a cup, it was very small and quite steep at $10 for what you got. But everything was delicious and service was great. Our server was very helpful in making sure I knew which items were gluten free. Great experience and is definitely come back.
Great food and amazing sushi!  Wait staff was on top of refilling drinks and asking if we needed anything.  A must if you are staying at this hotel!
2RRJt-hq-nWeJGZwf94s7g: Welcome to Nippers Corner! We could not have been happier to have this family owned restaurant within walking distance. We ordered a pizza and salad for pick up and wait time was less than 30 min. Fantastic pizza and delicious sauce  - which could not be said for anything within 15miles. I know this will be our new go to spot for Italian and happy to support their second location.
Best pizza in Nashville. Authentic New York style pizza. Also has great chicken parm, subs and salads!
Pretty average joint for pizza and pasta. We were pleased but didn't think it was anything over the top amazing. Great family atmosphere and the slices are huge though!
NgCRvX9F-y9VNJozOi9EHA: I don't often write reviews but this place is worth mentioning.  In a sea of gyro spots on  Gallatin, this is by far the best.  It's small & unassuming, but don't let that fool you:  the place is clean, the food is fresh & the staff is extraordinarily nice. Meals are made to order  so everything is fresh (expect to wait a few minutes for your meal).  But it's fresh, delicious & plentiful at ridiculously reasonable prices.  My family of four is fed really well for $23 -- I can't get that kind of value at a fast food joint.  All that aside, what I really like best is Haji, the owner.  Always smiling, he tries to make everyone feel welcome.  He'll have a conversation with you, tell you about his family & ask about yours.  That's community right there.  He is accommodating, always saying "when you're here, you're home".  Rest assured, we will return again & again!
Without a doubt best gyro place on Gallatin. The food is fresh and the guys who work there are super friendly. The baklava is pretty great too. Definitely recommend checking Haji's out.

What makes this place 4 stars is value. 7 bucks gets you a chicken and gyro plate combo full of meat salad and rice with bread and a drink. The food is good for fast food, I looked and say u just want a gyro sandwich its 3 bucks. So while the food is above average the big thing about this place is bang for your buck.
ac2mDVLL0g-y_iBtt3W3DQ:
I am so surprised at all the terrible reviews! I love Which Wich. To be fair I usually went to the one in mount Juliet before it closed and have only been to this one twice but I love the sandwiches. Sandwiches are my favorite and getting to pick from the awesome amount of toppings just makes it so much better. 
Some of these reviews talked about the long wait and yeah, it does take a while but everything is custom so of course it takes a while! Also you're at a mall food court so it's usually really crowded.

I'm giving Which Wich 4 stars not because it's the best ever but mostly because when I was trapped at a conference at the nearby Gaylord Opryland Hotel this was my little escape from the bubble (and if eating to a mall's food court feels like an escape you know it's desperate times). I could grab my little sandwich bag, check off what I wanted, and get it exact-o-mondo. Not overpriced, no long ridiculous wait. Just a satisfying experience. Sometimes that's really all you need.

This is like a combination of Subway and Potbelly, but better than both. I'd never heard of Which Wich, but their sandwiches are definitely superior. I got a "small" chicken sandwich, which really wasn't small at all, with lots of delicious toppings. I love their hot peppers and their sweet peppers. The clerk was kind enough to advise me to get the sub heated. He said it would be way better that way, and I wasn't disappointed at all. I also love their ordering process. You just choose a bag (each bag is labeled with the kind of meat you want), check off your toppings, and hand the bag to the clerk. No standing there pointing to ingredients or repeating 5 times that you want peppers and not onions. And they have a huge variety of toppings. I wish there were a Which Wich nearer to my house!
-iQdl2pQoJQUbzC42ld7Fg: I have to say I absolutely love the new look at the rosebud, formally Mambu. The wine is exquisite, service exceptional, calamari amazing, and the entrees me and my fiancé ordered were the best in town. We will be back and if your looking for a great atmosphere with great food I suggest you stop in.

Well prepared food, reasonable prices and excellent service. Rosebud Bistro is an excellent addition to Nashville's restaurant scene. 

I had the steak & frittes. The steak was tasty and the fries were perfect. The aioli was a added bonus. My wife had the lamb ragu with house made parppadelle. It was easily one of the best pasta dishes either of us have tasted in Nashville.

I highly recommend Rosebud Bistro.

Everything was fabulous! Cooked perfectly and presented beautifully!  Jan was delightful.  Favorites included the pomme frites, pork tenderloin, lamb pappardelle, and the sour cream ice cream was divine!
XBlrI-zlrZE4QAT2HtNRMQ: This is some of the best soul food once tasted outside of my house, in a restaurant in a LONG time in Nashville. I've been to a lot of them but this place is the real deal. We had the chicken and dressing  (chicken in the dressing, flavorful & just the right consistency. Juice not dry.), Mac & cheese (fye), ox tails (tender but needed some rice for that gravy), leg quarter (crisy & spicy), slaw (sweet & creamy), spaghetti  (good), hot water cornbread on point. Few pointers: turnips seasoned nicely but cold, baked cornbread too sweet & gritty for our taste. Also there has to be a better way to sell the Kool aid than in a 16 oz bottle for $1.99 or the price has to come down. Maybe $.50 for refills or smaller cups. Overall my family had a great experience & wonderful Sunday dinner without the cleanup. A little small so not ideal for BIG groups but we were comfortable with 7 ppl & the customer service is Awesome! Oh & the catfish, was fried hard & seasoned to perfection. We'll be back & regulars for sure.

OK...This is my second time eating here i ordered the chicken wings,mac&cheese and collard greens with cabbage mixed the wings was not seasoned at all even the little sauce on them wasn't good the mac&cheese was the bomb the greens were perfect but i should have went with my first intention of getting the whitening fish which i got the first time i went...OH and the drinks there are by color (koolaid) but be prepared they are so sweet you can't even drink them for real you must have ice and let it dilute first lol...the inside is super small so dont go as a group somebody's not getting a seat  but overall if i go back i will go with my first instinct fish and sides or try something else on the menu...You never know till you try.

Wow. Best find in Nashville. Everything we had was mind blowing. The best place is super cute but small so you might want to do carry out or uber eats. We both had catfish. Cooked perfectly. I had the sweet potatoes. Oh the sweet potatoes....  mmmmm. I just left and want to go back. Seriously go! Just go. You have got to try this.
HuECL2Vymf6WXvnE1_bvRA: This was my second visit to Caliber and first for lunch. LOVED the chicken salad. The croissant was heated and the chicken salad was delicious -- a generous portion of chicken salad and plenty of chips. The complimentary cucumber water was also tasty. I then left with a toffee nut latte - great taste. So glad to see the community support for this coffee shop as there were lots of people there at 2 in the afternoon. I'll definitely return!

Not bad! Very glad to see Donelson get this sort of coffee shop/cafe, as it's very overdue. I'd like to try it again now that they're open for real (we went during the soft opening) and try more things. The scones were lovely, though I would love it if they warmed them up before serving them. They were okay cold, but I bet they'd be amazing warm.

My friend got a cinnamon latte and loved it, while I got drip coffee. They didn't ask me what kind I wanted, so I guess they only have one type, presumably a medium roast, but it was fine. Service was a bit weird -  the coffees were delivered to our table, but then I had to ask for/get up to get cream and sugar for mine (nothing at the table yet). They had little plastic stirrers but no spoons for those dining in. Would much prefer spoons, please! Not sure if refills are a thing here, since it's not self-serve and no one came around to ask. I'm sure they'll work out the kinks soon.

The lunch menu looks interesting, so I'll be back to try that and see what the other coffee offerings are like. The revamped building looks really good and the chairs are surprisingly really comfy, so points to them for the atmosphere. I can see this as a nice spot to chill out with coffee and a book.

I don't like regular coffee. I like "foo foo" blended drinks. My fiancé always drags me around to all the trendy / hipster coffee joints. Most of those places exclude me from their menu by only serving hot coffee or iced coffee, yuck. Caliber has blended frappes. I've tried 4 different variations and none have disappointed. Two thumbs up for catering to not only the trendy / hipster coffee crowd, but also to us not so trendy "foo foo" blended drink people.
9WjiuPSGe7LoDNnINRvk-g: I happen to think that El Torrero is the best Mexican I've had in Nashville thus far on my quest for delicious, family-run Mexican eateries.  My fiance and I ordered the cheese dip (it's white! - that's a prerequisite for me) and split the chicken fajitas.  They were delicious and the two of us couldn't even eat them all there was so much food.  Chips were warm and delicious.  Margaritas were tasty and strong.  Staff was nice!  What more do you really want from a Mexican restaurant??

Every now and then husband and I will venture into this belle meade family infested restaurant (picture kids being babysat by their portable dvd players while the parents eat) just to consume tacos. This is one of the few places (other than Taco Bell... which shouldnt count) where you can get a bean filled taco instead of a meat one. The menu is huge and the staff is friendly.

We have been going to El Torero in Nashville and to their Atlanta locations for over 20 years.  This is by far the BEST Mexican fare in Nashville.  The staff are very friendly, and the service is outstanding - - even when it has been months between visits, they remember our drink orders and bring them by the time we are seated.  The chips and salsa are great.  Try their Nachos Fajita Mixed, Single Special ABC with Chicken, Enchiladas. or Tamales.  And for something a little different, check out their French Fries, they are amazing!  You won't be disappointed in this little gem of a family restaurant.
d_aKMAzEgqLw0c9w-_IYcw: I went there on comedy night Thursday.  The drinks were nice and strong, I've never really seen a bartender work so hard.  The people there were nice, wasn't too crowded, and the comedians were definitely funny.

LOVE this place! The food is OUTSTANDING!! I can be a picky eater and everything I have tried on their menu is fabulous. I live in Atlanta, and frequent Nashville and this place is ALWAYS on my must to do list! For food I would suggest the wings.. I have never had better.. the sauce and the way they are cooked is incredible! The chicken salad wrap and the fried mushrooms were among my favorites to! They almost always have a live band on the weekends which is a plus. The owner Steve is always there, and pays special attention to making sure everyone is having a great time! A go to place if your in town looking for a relaxed bar.. ( the drink specials don't hurt either :)

Was in the mood for a burger and wanted to try this neighborhood joint. Got here just as they were opening up at noon. Place was empty but looks clean and fun. Service was great and the bar food was really good.  There's a lot of room with a great little stage. We will be back at night to catch som live music.
wGxcInvCfQKVgaVRsK3L2A: Not lying...the definite bomb of Gyros...they rock..they have lines on the weekend...the inside is nothing to look at and I really hate those places that take pictures of their food and post it in the restaurant..it looks like shit and makes you think the food might taste the same...but this place is so good I'd drive there from the other side of town to get some!!!

I lived close to this place for almost a year and never noticed it's been there forever. Thanks to yelp I found this place 3 months ago! I love their salad with gyro or chicken. The food is delicious and inexpensive. They also give you a lot of food, I could never finished all of them. I always wanted to try their Kabob but it takes too long for them to cook it since I usually have the food to go. They suggested me to call them ahead before going there. I called them one day and the person who picked the phone seemed either too busy or didn't understand English and just hung up the phone...so until now I still haven't had a chance to try the Kabob yet :( This is why I'm taking one star off.


2013/#12

OMG!! This Location - is hands down the Best I've ever had, the best in town, the best all around!!! I had it last weekend for the first time, I'm going back today and I will go again on Friday.  I've been to the other locations of 'Chicago Style Gyros' in town and they ALL bite the dust compared to this location!! I don't have to beg for more sauce and the lemonade is fantastic!!  They are the Best in the business and they will get my business!!!  (Tip: Look past the decor and focus on the food...)
nSGdCxRgAymQs06mY0wSlw:
To be tucked away in the middle of a indoor plaza, Macke's has a lot of charm and a nice ambiance. 

Like another reviewer said, this is a popular place for the local "ladies who lunch." I was talking to one of the family members of the Macke's family of restaurants while eating at their Kate's Kitchen recently, and he was surprised when I told him Macke's was my favorite place between there, M., and Mack and Kate's, since I'm in my 30's. My husband and I go for their Sunday brunch, so the crowd may be a little more diverse than the weekday lunch crowd. Either way, the ambience is not "stiff" or snooty. 

I have always enjoyed their brunch menu. I love their bread basket. You never know what's going to be in there, but I'm always hoping some of their blueberry muffins are in there! The inside is more dimly lit and romantic, the "patio" (in the indoor plaza) is nicely sectioned off with fashionable dividers and has a nice cafe feel. I actually like sitting outside the restaurant on the "patio."

I actually hosted my bridesmaid luncheon here. They were easy to work with and did a great job with the menu I wanted, and everyone was impressed (my three bridesmaids, my mom, and my mother in law).

Very classy atmosphere for Sunday brunch with very good service.  Ordered take-out, including a burger off menu without any trouble and it was ready in less than 15 minutes.  I think the lobster mac-n-cheese will kill me (rich) but it sure was good. Conveniently located near Trader Joe's and Whole Foods.

It's funny because Macke's is kind of one of those 'secret' places you may want to keep to yourself. I'm sure the owners don't love that since Macke's is a little off the beaten path even though it's right in the middle of Green Hills.

Above where Davis-Kidd used to be in Grace's Plaza, there is now Macke's. The owners started out with a restaurant in Kingston Springs and opened this spot a few years ago. I used to sneak in to Macke's for lunch since I knew there was a good chance I wouldn't run in to anyone I knew there. 

I think the interior is pretty refined. I would feel out of place there in shorts and a t-shirt. There are tables out in the atrium/hallway of the place (across from the secret County Clerk's Office). The food is outstanding, though. 

I can definitely recommend the salads, and they used to have a great steak sandwich on the menu that was fantastic. They also have daily souffle specials for dessert. The server will probably ask you before you have finished your meal if you are interested since it takes a while to cook them. They are delicious. A bit more expensive than I usually like to spend on lunch, Macke's is a very pleasant and relaxing place to dine. They are also open for dinner which I hope to try soon.
UXNTU11-9x8SkZTfx8KIpQ: I haven't been to Waffle House in the decades since I moved north. What a great place. Sure it's basically a greasy spoon joint, but still yumcious. I got very friendly service and fast too. So many places seem to be understaffed, but not this place - lots of servers to keep customers happy. Nice stop as I travel.

2am fine dining at it's best! 

As a Californian I have never heard these waffle houses prior to visiting Nashville. I'm used to Roscoes and the disgusting establishments called Denny's. But this place was definitely an experience for the ages! 

We came here early April after a night of drinking. Some of my friends throw out the term waffle house and I had no idea what they talking about. I thought it was some kind of southern slang so I dismissed it. One of my friends almost slapped me. Have you ever had that time where a friend hasn't heard of something so wonderful exist. Haven't you heard of Goonies?!?! Wait you haven't seen The Godfather?!?! So the proverbial shouts came. "DUDE we NEED to take you there now!!! 

As soon as I stepped into this place I immediatley knew why people love Nashville so much. The environment was lively; people talking, cooks grilling, waffles flipping. I had made it to the foodie graceland. 

I ordered an omelette with hashbrowns and the group ordered about 4 different kinds of waffles. I don't remember the omelette but the only thing that my taste memory collected was the delicious peanut butter and the pecan waffles. 

The staff was very friendly and the service was really fast. I'm definitely coming back here again next time I'm in Nashville.

Ah Waffle House.... I have a lot of good memories here. My dad snuck me quarters when my mom wasn't looking so I could run and play Britney Spears on the jukebox (which would drive my mom nuts). My family still mentions how Polly (long retired now) the Waffle House waitress was the best waitress at any restaurant ever.

Look the food is greasy and bad for you. It's highly likely you're gonna feel it for a couple of days. But when you take a bite of those hashbrowns (scattered, covered and chunked for me) it doesn't matter.

Oh, one thing- I know its a Waffle House, but don't order the waffles. They are always either over done or under done. I know it doesn't make sense but trust me- order something else.
Psynglln2Cg-30XhVP_28g:
I really wish I had come here on a Friday or Saturday because it looked like a place where I could have a really good time.  Unfortunately, I arrived late on a Sunday night and the place was pretty much empty.

Dooley's looked like a casual sports bar that had plenty of room for a DJ and dancing.  It was split into several rooms, so if you wanted to find a quiet nook to talk, you probably could.  They even had some pool tables.

We only stayed for one drink, which was strong enough and a reasonable price.  Both of the gentlemen I was with could have stayed longer, not because they liked the bar so much, but because they thought the bartender was seriously hot....  Unfortunately I made them push on to the next bar on our pub crawl.  Don't know the bartenders name but she was dark haired and tall....

Next time I am in Nashville, I intend to try out Dooley's again!

I am from Detroit. This place is owned by the same people who own the Dooley's in Metro Detroit. They bought and run the place to make it Detroit themed bar. It's a risk, but it is their prerogative to do so.it 

I was very happy to watch Monday Night Football there, and happy to see that they had the Tiger game on as well (not happy with the end of the game). Sitting in the bar, I met several people who hail from the Detroit area, and we were all amped to watch the Lions game. 

There was a group of people watching the Cardinals v. Brewers game, so it's not like they shut out other people who want to watch other games. 

The owner, Mike, was very approachable, and talked to me for awhile. They were running a $1 draft special during the game -- and that is always nice. The bartenders were attentive, and nearly had a beer waiting for me once the other was empty.

I ordered some wings as the night went on, and they were about what you could expect in a bar. Someone else near me ordered some pizza, and it looked great. 

Now, trying to be impartial, it would be annoying if I walked in to watch sports and only have Michigan/Detroit sports on. But, as others have stated, there are other bars to go to. 

If you are from Detroit, or a Michigan/Detroit sports team fan, this is the spot for you. The only thing that was missing was some National Coney Island hot dogs being served.

Neat bar. The entrance betrays it's huge interior. I stayed at the bar but definitely noticed a huge middle area with tables and then a large area after that with pooltables and more booths. It was not that busy but I assume that is because it was Sunday and apparently Tennessean's don't drink on that day. (Ohhh I just called you all out, ha ha.) The bartender was only mildly friendly, probably due to the lack of patrons, but you never get a second chance to make a first impression. 

The music was pretty good and loud and had there been more people around, I would think that there would have been a small dance floor in the center area. I thought the stone fireplace was neat too. 

I hope they can pack this place with people. Reading over some of the reviews mention the place being dead. Not sure why, but hope they can rectify that problem. I hate to see places close.
b6NGQeURjEfpOxOuAxX-_Q:
You certainly don't expect the best Krystal (perhaps) in the entire franchise to be on the one of the most challenged corners in Nashville, but it indeed is.  This place honestly should be used as the corporate standard.  

Since I live only a few seconds away, I'll embark on a guilty pleasure here once every two months.  EVERY time, the staff is on the level of a Chick Fila staff in an affluent area...very kind, very efficient, very consumer-centric and extremely accurate.

The food is always photo-ready prepared.  Seriously, it sounds like I'm gushing, but I'm serious.  It's always hot, the mustard is in the perfect amount, the pickle is placed perfectly.  

The place is clean inside and out.  It's worth the drive or stop at this Krystal.  Corporate...if you're reading this...this location and management team deserve a reward!

We don't have Krystal in New York or LA so this was new to me. It's better than White Castle. The pups are delicious. Especially chili cheese pups. I take all my out of town friends here when they come to visit!

-- Ross Senack

Just drove through this location on NYE. The ladies at both windows were the most exceedingly polite, on-the-ball fast food workers I've ever encountered. I asked how long the shift was - she sweetly replied she's been working since 3am the night before - meaning the 30th. (It was 3:23am on the 1st.) They deserve raises & medals. The order was correct and warm. Both women were on the mature side, I assume covering for the kids who wanted off for the holiday. It was amazing to see people stepping up in that way and staying SO positive in what for me would be an incredibly strained moment.
4Ygd9CYAJSXXOhREELhzNQ: I woke up Saturday morning wanting the bacon egg and cheese sandwich I had here on Friday. But to my dismay they were closed. One star taken because they aren't open on the weekends but the breakfast selection is what you need in your life. The menu is simple and it just caters to those simple things you want for breakfast. My sandwich was on wheat toast and just really hit the spot. It's real food, priced right and cooked correctly. I will definitely be back for breakfast.

If you are at Cummins Station, this is probably your best bet for a quick and tasty lunch. Great selection of sandwiches on their menu and they usually have an interesting special on the board as well. I probably end up here about once a week. I get either the special or the Santa Fe chicken sandwich, which I honestly think has gotten even better than the first time or two I ordered it. 

Although I have not tried them, I've noticed that they have breakfast sandwiches, too. They also always have a case of premade sandwiches and salads for when you need to grab a meal REALLY quickly. Oh, and soup, too. They really do seem to have a little bit of everything. Fresh, yummy, easy. 

Very casual vibe, but comfortable, too. You can take your meal to go or grab a seat at one of the tables - inside, upstairs (kind of hidden, but handy extra seating), or even at a table outside.

So, I work in Cummins Station: which means this is one of 5 lunch options for me in the very near vicinity. I tend to favor M Cafe over anything else. I've been going for over a year, and have had a good selection of their food. 

Let's start with breakfast: I'm mostly a breakfast wrap kinda girl, because they're $4, made on the spot (which means it takes a few minutes -- sometimes it takes more than a few minutes, if there's more than 2 people in line for breakfast, I just go somewhere else.) Note: when they were $3, they were the best deal in the whole building. Now they're just a good option. I've also had the pancakes, which were delicious, the syrup that they sell in store is actually delicious. I love their mixed berry smoothie. 

For lunch, I've yet to have something I didn't like: their grilled cheese (comes with bacon + jalapeno-peach jam?: nice combo, sweet and salty and delicious) the chipped beef, which smells like heaven and tastes amazing, I'm a big fan of their Southwest chicken wrap, which seems to come on different flavored wraps depending on what they have. I've also had the Sunshine BLT wrap, the chicken pita, and various other specials -- all have been lovely. When their soups have peeked my interest, they've been delicious. The sandwiches range in price from $6-9. I do think this tends to be a little expensive, but they do have a rewards system in place, which I guess softens the blow. 

I tend not to eat their cold sandwiches, don't know why. Their cold case salads seem a little underwhelming/weird to me. The desserts, btw, tend to be fairly heavenly, so don't be afraid to indulge yourself if you see something that might peek your interest. The coconut cake they make occasionally is something of a dream. 

The staff has always been very nice to me -- exceedingly nice, actually. I have nothing but the highest compliments for their level of service, which I'm a stickler for. Oh, and I like the shop -- its full of interesting things, the ambiance is always pleasant, although it does get loud.
9IC7SB4GLZi-oS-w85j5AA: They have sum of the best fried chicken!! An make sure u get the hot sauce they got there to go with it

My husband and I absolutely love this place! This is our go to Chinese restaurant in all of Nashville! We order online and pick up at the restaurant. They always recognize us and greet us with welcoming smiles. Some of the sweetest people you'll meet! The portions are big and very reasonable. We highly suggest checking it out!

Well first I would like to say that the sesame chicken combo I ordered was scrumptious, only my egg roll was a little over cooked. However I was a little bit skeptical of the appearance of the store. Everyone was friendly and the lady that took our order did it promptly. My friend and I didn't wait long for food. When it came it was fresh and really hot, like burn your mouth hot. Lastly I love the way that they repurpose boxes from the near by liquor store. If you order enough stuff they put your food in a box! Reduce, reuse, recycle :)
LuHb5HCrsGKN5VvekMOmuQ: I had delivery from here tonight.. the depth of flavor is much better than other Chinese joints...
Really good for classic Chinese food. Fried rice could use a few more veggies but it's really good regardless. Super cheap too which is awesome, and portion size great! Very fast.

THEY DELIVER TO EAST NASHVILLE!!!

the crab rangoon are adorable little deep fried triangles of deliciousness.

the orange chicken actually tastes like oranges, the good kind, like orange zest; it's the best i've had in town.  the dinner came w/egg roll & either rice or lo mein.  it was FANTASTIC on the bed of lo mein, highly recommend.

the veggie mei fun noodles were good too.

the chicken fried rice was good but heavy on onions; they tasted good so it wasn't a negative if you like onions.  

both times we've ordered, they tell us 40-45 minute delivery time, and both times they've arrived 5-10 minutes earlier.

the portions are huge, flat plastic containers that are 8" x 5" x 2" & will give 2 generous servings (based on our eating portions.

the above items (orange chicken, mei fun, chicken fried rice, crab rangood) cost $28 w/o tip & was way too much for 2 people; with so many good options on the menu, we always over order; we had left overs for lunch & dinner the next day. 

happy eating, east nashville!  finally i know where to go for chinese delivery!
YZp9amy4p5qbmPpClVfBhw: Hot Chicken, Lemon Pepper Chicken,  Straight up EPIC CHICKEN! Nashville has a new contender for the Crown! Be in on the Ground Floor of this promising new taste maker! Don't. Miss. This.

The best Hot Chicken I've ever eaten. In fact I'm on a low card diet and this is the only time I thought it was worth it to indulge. I recommend the "hot" choice. Super spicy food is not usually my thing. Rarely do I order buffalo wings hotter than mild. This hot chick though! Perfect heat. You can taste the chicken while your nose runs a bit. 

They cook it fresh to order. The guy taking my order told me my leg quarters would take about 18 minutes. He was right and it was amazing.
Fresh fried chicken so delicious, shrimp was very good and the fried broccoli quite the surprise it was very very good! Could not get enough. Great food, fresh product at very affordable price.
sqjixMtnm0jRnPfRLzQczA: Maybe Cilantro has stepped up their game since all the bad reviews? Maybe they were just having a good night tonight? Or maybe the Yelp complainers just needed something new to bitch about? My boyfriend and I had a 5-star experience. We had the fajitas and the spinach tortilla burrito. Service was fast and friendly, food was great, place was clean.... Go eat at Cilantro!

Really good spot!! Wife and randomly walked in on a Friday night before hitting comedy club. Food was really good! Drinks were spot on!! Service was good but could have be better. Understand it's a Friday night and it's busy but a time we felt forgotten. We definitely wouldn't mind going back nonetheless. This particular night was $2 tacos

This place has a fun atmosphere, a friendly wait staff, and the food is always a delightful surprise.  It is a no frills place that does not disappoint and they have recently added a patio that is great during the cooler months.  It's nice to have a neighborhood non-hole-in-the-wall Mexican restaurant that is not overpriced.
sPMAnWj-uipCJwFPc1ROTQ:
In a time where most of the restaurants are full, it's crowded as it can be, and looking for a time to sit and relax with a hot drink, this is I guess the only coffee shop open (the other one close to the lobby was closed). Long line, but luckily they had the muffin I like. The cashier was kind of rude (I guess serving coffee number 1,000) and did not even ask to warm my muffin. 
Other than that...as good as a Starbucks coffee can get.

Elegant, sophisticated, modern, yet understated.  That comes to mind when I think of the gorgeous Conservatory Bar inside the newly re-opened Opryland Hotel.

Some friends wanted to go during the opening weekend and we decided why the hell not?  Once your inside the resort, most of your worries disappear, at least mine do.  The Conservatory Bar is located in the Garden Atrium next to Solario Mexican restaurant.  

We sat at the bar and were quickly greeted by the bartender.  We took a few minutes to look over the menu and we finally ordered.  Clearly they were very busy, our bartender was working the well and looked slammed.  

It took her a couple of extra minutes to make our drinks, but she acknowledged our wait and upgraded our vodka at no charge.  I love that kind of service.  She made a damn fine martini by the way.

We were offered some of their house made Limoncello.  Drinker beware, it was very TOUGH, but had a really bright citrus flavor.  It's definitely meant to be sipped on and not shot like I did.

Prices are a bit more expensive, but that's given for a hotel/bar of this caliber.  I didn't pay too much attention to  the menu, but did notice they offered wood burning oven pizzas. 

I could see myself stumbling out of this bar after a few drinks and making my way back to one of their rooms.  Sometimes it's fun having a staycation  and spending your money at home.
Very nice setting within a cavernous hotel.  Excellent service and superior drinks.  Price to match.  Four drinks for $60 with tip.
a9OiNtilLvM-jhnlcrXATQ: My husband and I lived in Crieve Hall for years and came to this Hardee's almost every weekend.  It is a wide variety of people that come - young, old, white, black, etc...  It was recently renovated and is very clean and the staff is always on target - a few a long term employees that go above and beyond...  i know everyone has a bad day here or there but they consistently rank high in our minds...!  Keep up the good work...!

best biscuits in the world. now they have a breakfast platter which is great.

best biscuits in the world. did i mention they have the best biscuits?

also tried their new coffee and its a major upgrade. their chix biscuit is thick.

ive eaten all over the world and they have the best biscuits. also NOT all hardees/carls jr are made the same. 

biscuit and gravy is stellar

JEANS: OK by far
ORDER: counter

I love Hardee's breakfast! It is the best in fast food! The people here was very polite and fast. The store was very clean and well staffed. The food was hot and fresh! It was so good! I will be back for breakfast for sure! Loved it!

There isn't really anything else I can say, other than I saw a 'Rise and Shine' slogan on the order board. I was really happy to see this. I vaguely remember this from the 80's / 90's but everyone (That is older than me) that I've talked to says that Hardee's was better back then. I personally can't tell any differences in food quality, but it is still cool to see the old throwback slogan again.
DIkXRqrDdW_86tSfdE1TWQ: We love this spot! Every taste of food we've tried has been delicious! Try the guacamole! We always get great customer service, too!

Went to lunch on mother's day here and had a great experience! The guacamole is the best I've had in Nashville! Salsa is so fresh as well. My mom was bummed they were sold out of tamales (her fave). Had the tacos (so good) and mushroom quesadilla was great as well. We will for sure be back (esp to try the tamales). Also thought it was amazing the owner gave us roses for Mother's Day. An amazing personal touch!

My family of 5 just had an excellent dining experience here. From the servers to the homemade chips & salsa, to the fabulous burritos, taco salad & quesadillas everything was top notch. The prices were reasonable as well! We will definitely be dining here again the next time we're in Nashville.
sISgBD7NCs7EmUyWfZxHTQ:
I have tried their brisket and hot sauce and love it.  I really appreciate their baked beans and potato salad too.  Add some texas toast and you have yourself a real nice bbq plate.

**walks out the door to go to Tom's**

Edit: I didn't know it when I wrote this review, but these guys have the best ribs in town.  I can't stop myself...

Me likey for East Nashville. My favorite part about Tom's is their BBQ. I am more a thicker sauce style guy when it comes to the Q. And, Tom's hits that just right.

The service is a bit slow, so be sure to have your patience on. The menu varies depending on demand and what time you get there.
