Typically, anytime around lunch you can expect to get the variety of smoked meats. They tend to run out of things as the day progresses. You add your own BBQ after. They have meat plates and sandwiches. A BBQ brisket sandwich (bun and meat) will run you about $5. They do have plate specials too. No alcohol is served here.

Tom's also has your assortment of vegetables and sides to go with any plate. No different than any other meat and three in the south. 

Tom's is the best for wanting to grab some grub to take to a party. They sell their meat by the pound to go. 

Overall, Tom's is good for East Nash. Don't forget the napkins.

Oh barbecue joints... How I love to visit the best of the worst of you.  Nashville, or major cities in TN in general, need to get ready for my barbeque road trip across the country, because Tom's is going to top the list of places I stop when I do that.

The service here is super slow.  I could probably go open my own barbeque restaurant and cook my own food in the time it took to get the food from these guys.  But then I would have had to do a lot of work, and with this choice I just get to stand around and watch whatever dumb movie is on the tv up on the wall opposite the counter.

Literally when we walked in, they told us we had to wait to order because they forgot the key to the cash drawer and could not process the order.  The guy operating the register was pushing one hundred and ten, and though he was behind the register, you could have convinced me easily that he had never even seen a cash register before in his life.  The other guy who was doing the cooking also seemed new, but who knows.  Finally a guy who seemed to have everything together came in, I can only assume that one of these men was Tom.  Which one I will never know.  

The food though was not only the largest portions in the history of the world, but it was also quite good.  I got pork, baked beans and fried okra.  The pork was awesome.  It was moist and fell apart in a wonderful manner as I poured the barbeque sauce over it and pulled it apart with my fork.  The baked beans were perfectly blended.  Just enough sweetness mixed with whatever other spices they put in the beans.  I could probably just go back and order sides.  Or just the beans.  Seriously. 

As for the fried okra, I mean, when you fry a vegetable it is always delicious so really no surprise there, but the portion that they gave was quite good, definitely more than I expected.

And then there was something totally unexpected. A corn cake.  It is basically a pancake that is made with corn meal and fried fresh when you order.  It is the most perfect addition to barbeque that I have ever experienced.  I don't know whose idea this was, or if anyone else does this, but I really liked it.  All Nashvillans should try it.  Really all people, but I am trying to be realistic here.  

Without the sides and the corn cake this would just be another good place to stop for meat, and not really even then because the service is so bad.  But all tallied up, 4 stars.
-uzQNt-3jsRK5okUuJiQLA: Shopping at Green Hills Mall brought me to this friendly Greek Cafe.  I now understand why it is rated 4.5 stars!!  Had the Greek salad with grilled onions and chicken.  Homemade dressing was lite and tasty.  Would recommend for the price!!  Great find!
The food here is consistently good and the portion size is always large. Friendly and clean. This is my go-to for casual Greek food in the area.
This is my favorite restaurant in Nashville!! The chicken platter is absolutely delicious. The staff is really friendly. There is always convenient parking. The prices are very reasonable!
NWjQ3f2b3cL7CGalFwwY_A: Very clean, easy to understand staff. Nice bathrooms. Perfect sandwich. Love that they have avocado now!

Ate here last night and the people seemed very friendly. The food was fantastic as always and the veggies are as fresh as they come I have ate here a countless amount of times and I will continue that tradition

Great customer service, they were kind and quick, & we came in very close to closing time! Our sandwiches were wonderful, the artist didn't skimp on anything like most locations! Everything seemed clean & everything behind the glass looked fresh. Very satisfied!
dw-nDDdR7ZpmcHAi_IHU1Q: friend at work who is from chicago said chicago style beef sandwich was very good!

Pizza here is a delicious thinner crust type pie. It's got something that definitely keeps us coming back. The owners/staff are excellent and friendly. Don't be fooled by how young they are - they know what they are doing. They recently started offering a variety of yummy wings, too. Several flavors. And, I know this is silly, but if you dine in, they have the old school Coke taps that I prefer - and chunklet ice to boot. Affordable, fun, and tasty. You should definitely check them out!

Finally found a TRUE pizza place in Nashville. Stopped in for a late lunch and had such a wonderful visit! The pizza was perfect! Fabulous crust, thin and crispy, but still filing and tasty. The sauce is delicious, so flavorful and full of body. 
The manager, Douglas, was so awesome! It was great to find out he was from Chicago, and we knew of some of the same spots up north. Overall we LOVED this place as did all our kids (who are VERY picky). The prices are great and the service is wonderful. If you want REAL pizza, not that crap that the "chain" pizza places crank out, DEFINITELY stop by Bellshire Pizza!
7AkxF0kYb_RGQ8iWeldSLg:
Christopher's boasts some of the best pizza in town.  What makes it even better is that each specialty pizza has a cute monicker like "Fancy Pants" or "Mo Feta Mo Betta."  The "Little White Lie" is my personal favorite; I recommend adding spinach.  The quality of ingredients and crust outweighs that of so many other pizza restaurants in the area.

I'm still not convinced the place has quite the right layout for live music, however.  I tend to go for lunch when there's usually no one playing unless I know the night act.

The servers are always superb.  A lot of them are the musicians who play at night so they're relaxed and friendly. Grab a Yazoo draft and settle in.  I know you'll be glad you did.

Great pizza and fantastic shows, you need to make it there before it closes!!! I came to Christopher's Pizza for their SpeakerHeart Wednesday night open mic show thinking I was going to hear mediocre musicians and was absolutely floored. One after another, musicians took the microphone that just blew me away (most of which I stalked online after). On top of it all, their Carnivore pizza was a dream come true: dough, cheese, and ridiculous amounts of meat. I really can't speak to how great a night of good music, good pizza, and good beer really is, but Christopher's Pizza has it all.

Delicious Pan Pizza, Good(?) Live Music, Good Price.

The Food: Wide variety of delicious pan pizzas.  I got a spicy chicken pizza which was quite good.  The girlfriend was able to order off the menu and got a pretty solid veggie pizza (though no tofu).  A wide variety all-and-all.

The atmosphere: Great when there isn't a terrible band playing.  I get the feeling that the live music is generally pretty good.  However, the one time I was in their the band was warming up for our entire meal.  Less than delicious.   Also, the room is cramped, so the band can be playing and the drummer can practically reach out and grab a piece of your pizza.  Those are the the only reasons I had to give this 4 of 5 stars.
0LVODzlrdeHlA6KJEmsOyg: I first tried this food truck at a local beer festival and so glad I did! It's my new favorite addiction. They were at a brewery in town so I decided to go get lunch and beer at the same time. Got to try out new items on the menu and was not disappointed. Love the nachos. So full of flavor and that corn salsa is delish! The Nashty was a burrito also flavorful but it was too runny with too much retried beans. Still good though. The only complaint I have is the queso and guacamole were tiny portions for the price. 4.00 each! We had too many chips left over and no dip. The guacamole was a little too sour. The queso was pretty good. Can't wait to see what they have in the store that's opening soon! I'll be able to feed my nacho addiction anytime!

I first discovered this food truck at a local beer festival. So now whenever they are near I'll try to catch them. My favorite is the Nachos. Lots of flavor. Looking forward to their restaurant opening soon.

Great burrito. Sad the packaging is so excessive! I know not everyone thinks about the packaging that their food comes in, but this was so wasteful! A huge box for my burrito :/ plz take into consideration that your customers care about saving the planet!
vEfyDHrxWN0pfuCRNlocJw:
Yes, the service is slow here. It's a VERY high-volume pizza place. I will say first-hand, however, that these guys work their butts off and do the best they can with the staff they have. I was here on a Friday night and my order was delayed by almost an hour. I stood there waiting and inspected their business. 

Bottom line: they need more people working at this location. The folks behind the counter were nice, efficient, and scrambling to get the pizzas out the door as fast as possible.

I can't think of a time in Nashville where I was more inspired by the hard work I witnessed in a restaurant. They need more resources from corporate to make everyone's lives easier here. Keep up the good (and hard) work!

They always take care of us well. We order pizzas for large groups and it gets here on time, hot, well prepared, and delicious. The delivery team is so nice. The staff at the store are helpful and thorough.

I have to say THANK YOU to the fine folks at this location.  I had accidentally ordered online to the wrong location.  Totally my bad.  When I went in to pick up my order at this location, they identified the issue, contacted the other store, and they just made it right.  They remade the order and I was out the door in 10 minutes.  They even found me a better deal on pricing.  They went our of their way and made my mistake right.  Thanks for not making me feel like the bonehead that I was!!
lSfMGqkDaMvqm-KCa6Yr2Q:
I'm from Boston and I love small cafes with good service. This cafe has it. It's a hidden jewel that the "Seattle" chain doesn't come close to. I had one of the breakfast sandwiches - egg was fresh, nice cheese, and great sourdough bread! Latte was excellent. 

I will definitely be back.
I really appreciate their customer service, and they have plenty of options for non-coffee drinker's!

Found a great little coffee spot yesterday in Lenox Village.  I read the other reviews and I would say this place has definitely worked any "new business/employee training" issues out. I ordered a 16oz. sugar free vanilla latte (same thing I order at the big coffee chains) & was very pleased with the taste. It cost $4.50 which I personally am OK paying for a good coffee to get my day started right. I checked out the lunch menu and suggested to a co-worker we should try it out. We went the other day. I ordered the free bird salad. A nice lunch portion of spring mix with a scoop of very tasty chicken salad promptly arrived at the table. It was wonderful! If you like friendly faces, great service, good coffee & food, I would strongly suggest checking this spot out!
I1Qbx7TzdT0pduEtUA8wiQ:
The American Diner experience is alive and well at Marlene's
If your looking for the traditional American diner experience wander down to Marlene's. Great food, good conversation and prices you can afford.  If you go take Marlene a T-shirt and let her know the one she is wearing is "ugly."  Local Police, Fire and Sheriff have started a tradition of bring Marlene a t-shirt to advertise their agency.
Coffee is hot and always fresh and the tall friendly burnette waiting on you is the owner Marlene.  The place is small a sits right along the road so if your not paying attention you miss this place.

Friendly place with good food & coffee at a good price. I have stopped here several times for a quick cheap breakfast and once for lunch.

The patty melt is EXCELLENT for lunch, I had mine with onion ring - crispy, not greasy and good.. For breakfast it's pretty much the standard fare pancakes, eggs, grits  and the rest of the usual accessories served up fast & hot. 

Motorcycle friendly parking...even the local motorcycle cops frequent this place.

This little hole in the wall was absolutly fantastic. A lot of food for the price. Too much food almost. The cheeseburger was very tasty. The pork tenderloin sandwich was awesome. Onion rings, potato salad, and deviled eggs were all delicious as well. Will definatly be back.
C0_lO52mC1s5Tu1caHY2jQ: Wow!!!!! These guys are so polite and offer great customer service! Food is great and delivered quickly! Ansley knows me by name now; her customer service is amazing! I am blown away by her professionalism! She needs a promotion to supervisor! She never losers her energy or friendliness even if it's super late and she is probably exhausted! Great job guys! It's why I treat myself weekly! Thank you!!!!

Always been a great experience at this location.  Pizza is always fresh, hot, and tasty.

My family and I have been ordering from this location every Christmas Eve (and for multiple birthdays) for almost 10 years, and have never had an issue at all, and everyone has always loved the taste.

The price has always been right, the order has never been wrong, and everything has always been ready for pick up on time every time.  Employees are always working fast and are polite.  It's easy to get in, pick up quickly, and get out fast.  

This is an ideal pizza take-out location if there ever has been one.
This Papa John's is really good! Every pizza I have gotten from here has been great!
FygWE7b7ZN7JKKxbqKtMZw:
The food was fantastic my friend and I had two different dishes and both wa amazing  .. the service was very good scale of 1-10 I would give it 10..
Try it you will line it!

I had a great time here yesterday. The food was tasty and made very well. They were short staffed but our server was ever so kind and patient. He took care of the guest alone while taking dine in and to go orders. He made no fuss and all were served. My guest and I left him a nice tip as he deserved it. I believe that the wait was an uncommon occurrence but the quality of service is what will bring me back.
Great food, great service. My wife and daughter  are vegetarian, and eat here often. We eat at both locations regularly and my favorites are Hung Ray and Tom Kha
FszFxgcs7S8Hl2zlzw3YNg:
I'd been eyeing this place (well, eyeing their website) for quite some time.  My bf & I haven't really explored much of the mexican food Nashville has to offer.  Other than Baja Burrito that is...

Anyway, we went on a Saturday afternoon before we took a tour of Yazoo.  I'd never been to the Edgehill Village area, so I was pretty impressed with the area.  The boy ordered a margarita made with the Jose Cuervo Black, and I had a house margarita on the rocks.  Both were VERY good.

For eats, the boy ordered the chicken enchiladas and with red sauce, and I had the black bean tamale.  Both were equally delicious.  I think though, if I go back, I would prefer to do the combination plate again, but with 2 items, then split it with someone!  The amount of food I got for one item, plus the sides was a LOT!

The only downside was the price.  While, not expensive, our lunch was around $40, and we only had 1 margarita each.  I would of liked to ordered the guacamole with chips, but it was $8.99!  Luckily guacamole came with my meal though.  I'd LOVE to go back on a weeknight and take advantage of their daily drink specials!

If you're looking for a good place to eat yuppie-fusion Mexican, and like me, you wouldn't darken the door of Rosepepper, then Rosario's may be the place for you. While it's a little sanitized and overpriced, there are some unusual takes on traditional Mexican dishes, and the ceviche menu is interesting, including a mango ceviche. I had the carne asada tacos, which were great, but they still were served with lettuce, sour cream, cheese, etc..., which I'm not a great fan of.

You'll pay a lot more for the setting and atmosphere, but the food is good. The margaritas are also decent, but the ones I had were small and rather weak. I guess I'm just used to the real thing at La Hacienda.

Had dinner here a couple weeks ago, it was still in it's soft opening phase and working out a lot of kinks.  First, it is a little nicer than most Mexican restaurants, and we felt a little out of place in our t-shirts and shorts.  The food was surprisingly good; spicing was delicate and unfolding.  We had to pay for chips, which is lame.  The server was wearing plastic gloves when he delivered our food and the food around us.  That is gross.  Our waiter was very young and uncomfortable and acted like it was his first day on his first job; clueless and kind of wide-eyed at his responsibilities.  This was ultimately charming for us.  Finally, and the reason for the 4 stars: we asked about getting a pitcher of margartias (maybe this would have been more appropriate at a typical, casual Mexican restaurant)... they weren't sure what to do, so they brought us each a margarita and a pitcher of margarita, and then just charged us each $5 for the margartias.  Free pitcher of margartias will cancel out just about any problems with a dining experience.
5EPgeoxoSLLZbOL8chDjLA:
I must say I was a little worried when I entered a BBQ joint with a full bar.

I was raised on BBQ in places that had little lighting, where side dishes were a luxury, and Wonderbread and sauce are the only distractions one needed from the main event.  I myself also love making BBQ, which I adhere to as a religion, rubbing meats and brining birds days in advance, and slow smoking tough animal protiens until they become cookie dough over the span of half a day or more.

The shrimp corn dogs with avocado sauce were good, as was the Pig Burger.  A nice, fatty, flavorful burger with Benton's and a fried green tomato on a potato roll.  I got it with 'Pate of the South' and they didn't flinch at making it medium-rare, as per my request.  I had some greens on the side, which were good, but not exceptional.  

The brisket on Texas brioche was quite good too.

I am headed back there to take my CEO and our executive team there tomorrow night - I plan on diving in more to their BBQ specifically and I will update as soon as I do.  Assuming I still have a job.

UPDATE:
I went back there with the CEO and the rest of the bosses and it was a great meal.  Shame on me for not getting BBQ my first time there.  I got the STL Spare Ribs (I'm a bad, bad Jew) and sampled the brisket.  Everyone there loved it and we had a pretty good mix as the home office is in TX, and we had people who hailed from STL as well in our party.  I am still employed, so it was a success.  Also had the onion rings, greens, sorghum beans, guacamole - all of which hit the spot.

Finished off with the John Daniels (we go way back) banana pudding - perfect comfort food, and sampled the peanut butter cup which was also fabulous while it lasted.

The service was also excellent.  I still feel guilty eating in a BBQ joint that is so clean and has so many sides, but smoked meats dissolving in your mouth is worth it.  The only thing pretentious are the prices.  Upgrade to 4 stars!

One more note, watch out for the cocktail prices - TEN DOLLARS PER!  I think someone has themselves confused with The Patterson House.
Great! Green bean casserole and the pig burger. Also fried green tomatoes bentons bacon and caramelized onions on it. Wow!

I actually went here with very low expectations after reading some of the mixed reviews on Yelp and hearing mixed opinions on this place from my friends. I also had low expectations as I am not into barbecue stuff. However, I was pleasantly surprised by food I had at Blind Pig. I went for lunch on a friday and almost all of the tables were full: quite a success for a place that opened only recently.

I had the blind pig burger which was pretty innovative which was very good but was very heavy. What I liked about my burger was that there was a fried green tomato in my burger and the bun was really good, their fries were also very good. On the downside, I asked for a well done burger but mine was probably closer to medium but that was probably our waiter's mistake. My friend had the pulled pork platter and he was pretty satisfied too. 

The desert was probably the best part though .. we shared a peanut butter chocolate cake which was amazing .. one of the very good deserts i had here in nashville.

Very good experience overall .. I would recommend this place if you like barbeque.
ndZtt-lKybHsQFv-iVL3fg:
Me and my girlfriends were here for a bachelorette party so we came on a Saturday morning for lunch.

It was just your run-of-the-mill Mexican restaurant. Nothing blew me out of the water, nor do I have any complaints!

Attentive service, not over-the-top friendly but it was fine. 

I ordered the huevos rancheros and really enjoyed them.

My bestie is pregnant so me and her shared the fried ice cream, yum! It was huge, were definitely the envy of our table. It was filled with orange ice cream instead of vanialla so I have never seen that before.

Yes, it's that kind of Mexican joint, but worth it. It's a neighborhood joint with solid amerimexican food. Legit, and tasty refried beans, salsa and guacamole. The drink specials are hard to beat anywhere on the east side. The service is solid and they're very grateful of their patrons. Ask for Juan and a strong $3 margarita.

I cannot rave about this place enough!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!  Best carnitas I've had since we had homemade ones in Chihuahua mex!!! it makes all of the other mexican restaurants in Nashville taste like cat food. Try the Milenesa too!! just good food and great service!
okNkHNqzJYy3rIUtVNw8pw: Best breakfast tacos I've ever had. The Chorizo and egg are amazing. Most days I have to force myself NOT to stop and get one or 2. They are huge and you get 2 for $3....best deal in town. I admit I was skeptical about it being in a gas station but I was wrong. They are better than any Mexican restaurant

This review is only for the breakfast tacos though I'm sure the quality also applies to lunch and dinner. 

Breakfast tacos are delicious. Fresh flour tortillas, good serving size, salsa options, everything I want in a breakfast taco. The prices are great and the food is even better. I've been missing breakfast tacos since I left Texas years ago and I finally have an easy option. 

Note that this is the same chain found in Stripes brand gas stations in Texas.

I don't want to be hyperbolic, but I can't recall having better breakfast tacos anywhere in Nashville. 

When I first moved to South Texas I thought people were crazy when they told me some of the best breakfast tacos in the Rio Grande Valley were from a gas station. As soon as I tasted one, I realized how wrong I was. 

In Texas, Laredo Taco Co. is inside Stripes convenience stores. In Nashville, it's inside an Exxon Tiger Mart. Still, they're making the flour tortillas fresh and cooking the eggs and bacon on the flattop. 

I got a bacon and egg breakfast taco and added refried beans and cheese. The bacon is diced and cooked into the scrambled eggs. The refried beans are rich and delicious (charro-style). 

These are really good breakfast tacos. Check them out.
o7ua-cp9QZ7iZXNvjb-S2w: Took a small leap of faith to dive into this gem with shabby exterior.  Slight lingering scent of incence that you would either love or hate, typical diner set up without too much ethnic flare... unsturdy table.  Then the food came, and all else is forgotten.  Crunchy, moist falafel drenched in thick yogurt sauce was a great appetizer.  The lamb gyro melt in my mouth like flavored butter, the mountainous rice/hummus/salad that came with the meal is stunning in both taste and appearance.  I left very full and happy.  $20 fed two hungry grown men with leftovers, can't get better than that.

Had another hankering for lamb. And this was on my list to try.

The lamb platter $4.99 lunch special came with fork tender pieces of lamb, rice, and a salad. It looked huge. So, for $4.99 + tax, it was a bargain.

The lamb was on the fatty side -- which is exactly the way I like it. It tasted great -- the only part I didn't like was that there was a slight, congealed fat "mouth feel", which is a turn-off for many people. And I thought Middle Eastern food is supposed to be spicy; this was not. The next time I come here, I'll ask if they use special spices (baharat -- much like an Indian-style garam masala) for their diners from their original area of the world. And if they can any to my dish.

I was intrigued by their "Middle Eastern coffee" on the menu. It was actually Turkish coffee, which I've never had and always wanted to try. It's very finely ground coffee brewed in a special way. It came without sugar or milk, which is how I normally take my coffee. But I had no clue if this was the way it's supposed to be served, so I drank it. I personally detest black coffee -- but this was pretty good. It had a mint-like quality and a hint of sweetener. And I noticed the fine coffee particles at the bottom of my cup -- which apparently is supposed to be there.

The place doesn't look very inviting, but I can happily eat in hole-in-the-walls. Nice decorations and feng-shui'd interior designs are not a requirement for me when I eat out.

Well this was an exciting find!  I wanted a falafel pita sandwich and the Middle Eastern Restaurant higher up on 21st by Grand closed down.  So, as you can see I yelped and found this gem down by Hillsborough Village. It's located above Pizza Perfect and is actually quite dark.  I also think they allow hookah smoking at night so that does leave a little smell.  Either way I can't completely comment because I got my pocket to go. The service was quick, I didn't wait long.  They call them pockets and actually look more like a burrito.  The falafel, tahini, veggies were on top of the pocket and the sides were wrapped up.  I'm used to eating it inside the pocket but it was tasty nonetheless.  It was also a solid amount of food.  The $8 lunch special included a side (I got fries but they also have lentils and something else) and a soda.  For eating around the Vandy area this is a good deal, especially because of all the food I got.
zFjFImEtJJWttHNfydc0nA:
First things first. The restaurant is on the fifth floor and there's to be another restaurant on the ground floor, opening soon. 

Stopped in for lunch - turkey avocado sandwich with a 
Cesar salad with an ice tea to drink. 

Service was good but slow (apparently had to make the tea - in the South and not having it ready!). 

Sandwich was packaged meat with thin smear of guacamole (not avocado slices) with generous and fresh lettuce, tomatoes and onion. Despite presentation deductions for sliced turkey, this was delicious (even at $14). But the Cesar salad blew me away - large and with lots of freshly sliced Parmesan cheese. In fact will have the other half for dinner tonight.  While I thought price was a bit steep considering the ingredients, the taste and overall atmosphere was great. 

Several TVs and live music at night with an outdoor seating / eating / drinking area might make this a great spot to hit after a Preds victory!

This place is fun! We stayed here and Melissa at the bar is awesome. Took us a little while to get our food but the wings were good. Wings are so easy to screw up so that was impressive. I have nothing bad to say about True!

A MUST visit while you are in Nashville! It's located at the new Cambria Hotel on 8th street. Fantastic atmosphere with a very impressive rooftop view of Nashville! One of my big takeaways was most certainly the attentive staff. They have a bartender named Kevin who knows Nashville inside and out and will be your best source of "Must Do's" during your stay here. That is if you even want to break the great conversations had at this relaxing hidden gem!
C8IwRkXKd_Vk_MigwQxAKw: Not a BBQ expert but this pulled pork plate I ordered is delicious! My mouth was watering (I was extra hungry already so factor that in lol). I forgot it was airport food!

I was starving after enduring the first leg of a cross country flight and thought i'd check my food options during a two hour layover. I was thrilled to find a place that had legit enough BBQ for airport food and enjoyed my pork sandwich. It wasn't as fresh as a freestanding location I'm sure but it was perfect to a starving Southern expat in dire need of nourishment.

This is some damn good airport food!

I grabbed a to-go plate of pulled pork with spicy bbq sauce, greens and mac 'n cheese to eat at my departure gate.  More than one person interrupted my gorging to ask where I'd gotten such delicious-smelling food and one woman went so far as to make a snatching motion at my pork.  No way, lady.  This pile's all mine!

Looking forward to my next layover in Nashville, as long as Whitt's in my terminal!
quUQsNwaNWSLedV1W7Geyw: This is some of the best chicken I've had in Nashville!  Definitely a unique flavor..  different than the other places in town.  Sweet, spicy and smoky. Even the seasoned fries have deep and different flavor that makes me want to eat more and more.  Will be going as often as I can!

Great hot chicken. More like Hattie B and less like Puckett's - very delicious. "Hot" is the perfect flavor and temperature if you like burn - unfortunately "medium" tastes the same to me as mild. Their pricing is very confusing : on the main board it said $12 , on the paper in the window it says $10, and I was charged 9.50. Not a big deal, but a bit confusing

This is really good hot chicken.  We tried this on a Thursday afternoon shortly after arriving in Nashville.  Red (the owner) was friendly.  We tried the chicken medium - not having a scale yet for how hot hot is - and we realized we could have gotten hotter.  That said the chicken had good spice and a lot of flavor.  The toast was nice and big and tasty with some of that spice.  The fries could have been a little better, but if you're looking for hot chicken, and don't just want to try it at Hattie B's, definitely give Red's a try.  Recommend that chicken 100%.
ZN8kZhe5ZJxQ57ahg_Umfg: They have lumpia, Filipino fried appetizer/snack rolls, super hard to find anywhere in the states, super delicious and great little snack ($3!). Great selection of desserts. I don't know if the menu changes up but they had Mexican-Coke (real cane sugar, from a bottle) marinated chicken and sides for a lunch type meal, didn't try it, but it smelled and looked delicious!
The passion Sophia puts into her dishes goes above and beyond my restaurants. It's like sitting down to dinner with grandma. Great food, using the best ingredients. Yum!

I've never had Filipino food , and after eating here it will not be my last time. So glad this was the place we decided to eat at the Farmers Market today. I could go back and eat another course. There were so many great flavors in the Pansit. My brother had the Kare Kare and he really enjoyed that, too. 

Highly recommend trying this place out.
f7cBlnRSSow3M6YkWUNHbw:
Lol no shame in my game, I love coming here when I'm shopping at Opry Mills. My favorite pretzel is the pizza one with some marinara or cheese sauce on the side. I realize I sound like a 5 year old but they're really good, don't judge me! 

My only complaint is that the line can get backed up pretty quickly. It seems like they only have a few pretzels already cooked so you usually end up waiting for them to cook it. I also wish there was a seating area because I feel so awkward walking around with a massive pretzel, sauce, drink and bag. Other than that, I love this place!
Although the service from the employees isn't that great (what can you expect?), the pretzels are consistently delish!

Low carb diet or not, Auntie Anne's pretzels is one of the best little places in the mall.  When I was there last time, I was lucky enough to get a free half pretzel that they were handing out because it was their birthday.  Warm and soft, with plenty of salt, I love to get a pretzel from here.  They also have other options that seem to change such as ones with cheese, or Cinnamon.
xvu0enRUvnfsG8MDZAnu2A: OK. I almost didn't try this place due to the poor reviews on yelp, but decided to give it a shot. And then decided to come out of yelp hibernation to give them a good report. My meal was excellent. I had the "hot mess" - fries, topped with chicken, queso, tomatoes, and sour cream. The chicken was delicious. I wasn't sure what to expect when it came to "ranch chicken" but it was pulled chicken that's been braised or slow cooked. The queso was good - not too thick, just the right amount of spicy. Fries were piping hot and crispy. Tomatoes were fresh. I'm 99.99999% sure the bacon was Benton's. I wonder if this is why people think "liquid smoke" when they taste it? Benton's has a really defined, intense smoky flavor. Anyway. Service was quick. Would try it again!!

First time to Nashville. Decided to try a food truck dedicated to bacon. Not very friendly at window. Prices a tad high. Not as many bacon items or load of bacon as I would have expected, but the burger was awesome. Worth my one day red meat treat.

What is bacon-y with a hint of bacon and rubbed with bacon all over? A nation of bacon. The Bacon Nation food truck graced the 12 South area earlier this month in support of dog adoption (and cat).  

I love me some bacon. I really do. I love the 50/50 burger meat from Porter Road Butcher even more than just plain bacon. And, you know what, Bacon Nation makes the Double Bypass burger with 50/50 meat, layers of bacon, cheese, lettuce, and even a piece of white bread in the center of it all. It was bacon in all its glory as I consumed it.

Downsides of the truck: it's on the slow side for getting your meal in comparison to a number of the other trucks in the city, including some other trucks that serve burgers. All and all, the Yelp rating system says that 3-stars is A-OK and 4-stars is Yay! I'm a fan! I've chosen to go with the 4-stars out of respect for bacon and my love of 50/50 meat.
Go3GtC1yLModu489NPtfMw:
I've been to Mirror a few times, and each time I have something new to praise and something new to gripe over. Maybe it's that balance that keeps it as one of my favorite spots in Nashville...

In any event, not to start off this review on a bad note, but there is a general air of pretentiousness floating around this place. I can't say I blame them entirely for that, as the food is generally fantastic and the drinks are just as fantastic (just ask Stephanie, the bartender...she'll be quick to let you know that she was voted the #2 bartender in Nashville). During one visit, I came with out of town guests rather early in the evening. There was not a soul in the restaurant, yet the hostess asked us if we had reservations. Uh...it definitely didn't appear as though we needed them. So maybe make reservations before you head out.

The menu changes often, but be warned that the website does not. So if you've never been, I'd advise against checking out the menu online at the risk of having your heart set on trying something that isn't featured anymore. That being said, the mini Grilled Brie Cheese Sandwich has been on the menu every time I've been, and it is by far the most decadent sandwich I've ever had the pleasure of biting into. I usually get a double order of this one as a standard, and play around with whatever else may be featured on that visit. 

All in all, a great place to go with a group of friends, or a date, if you're ok with picking off of each other's plates. Everyone I've brought here has enjoyed it. The decor is cool, and the staff, if not a wee bit nose-in-the-air, are generally friendly and helpful.

Six years ago, they hooked me with a simple cocktail, the American Beauty.  From there, our love affair began.   I love food, and I love that at Mirror I don't have to just settle on one big plate of food.  Instead, my tastebuds are able to go on an amazing adventure with all of their tapas.....Brie Grilled Cheese w tomato soup sauce, Blue Cheese Polenta Fries, Gnocchi, Bacon Wrapped Crab Cake, Fried Green Tomatoes with Goat Cheese, and on my last few visits, the amazingly rich and delicious Banana Bread Pudding.   As a whole, the service has always been great.  I may have had 1 or 2 not so great experiences, but nothing that has ever kept me from coming back.   I hope Mirror never leaves me, because I would be heartbroken!

I love taking guests here from out of town. They all seem to enjoy it just as much as I do. It's tapas style with small plates, bigger plates, and they also have entrees if you need them. I've always gotten a bunch of small plates to share with my friends, and that's definitely one of the fun things about going to a tapas place...getting to sample so many different items. That said, I've never had room to try one of the entrees, but they all look just as yummy as the rest of the food.

The atmosphere of the place is great. A really nice interior, there's a section with couches where you could curl up and have a drink. The bar always seems crowded (although they allow smoking at the bar inside which I don't quite understand...). There's also some tables outside to enjoy when the weather is nice. The tables inside are pretty close together, so you might have someone eavesdrop (or you eavesdrop on them). But you may find out about a great dish you should try that way! 

My favorite has to be the prosciutto wrapped onions. I could eat plate after plate of those. My vegetarian friend swears by the (tiny) grilled brie sandwich. We usually have to order two of those since she doesn't want to share.

Definitely save room for dessert. The chocolate pots du creme was one of the best desserts I've ever had. The menu does change so it's always fun to go back and see new things to try.
qpgkWql42qgW095udiCLYg:
Two all-beef patties (thin as paper), Special sauce (thousand island dressing), Lettuce (shredded wisps), Cheese (processed yellow something), Pickles (I'll give you this one), Onions (whah?) on a Sesame Seed Bun.

I do like when the beef patties are more than 1/3rd off the bun.  It is especially neat if you can get the patties off the bun on both sides.

What can you say?
This is one of nashvilles best fast foods, the food and service was splended. I'd recommend coming here it is a delight!

Sadly, I go to this McDonald's a LOT, and I love going through the drive-thru.  Every single time I go, whether it's breakfast rush or at 4 pm on a Tuesday, the drive thru staff is so cheerful and polite.  I don't know whether they actually do enjoy their jobs, but they sure act like they do.  It really is what keeps me coming back- they always grin and thank me and tell me to have a great day, no matter whether I've seen them before or not.  The very few mistakes I've received with an order were quickly corrected and apologized for.
Xh1Jv2iwxJS-mQtHtIz5HA: Excellent and authentic tacos! I was a little skeptical walking in, but I'm so glad I tried it.  There are only a few places to get truly excellent authentic tacos in Nashville, and this is one of them. Waitress was very kind also.

I am not kidding when I say I had the best fajitas of my life here! They were amazing! Foodies dream to find a place like this. Incredible food at low prices. This was my first visit, so I can only talk about the fajitas I had but other people who frequent there tell me everything is delicious. I completely believe them. I cleaned my plate. And the place is super clean. The owner runs a tight ship. He definitely has a gift for cooking up some delicious Mexican food in a warm setting. Check it out. You won't be disappointed.
So delicious and friendly! We are traveling but if we were local, we would eat here often. We will be back nest time we are in town for sure!
mN6fOLYGCVOwXHN1KGPeug:
I was lucky enough to get a gift-card for TinWings as a wedding present, and it's helped so much! Not having to cook some weeks is a huge weight off my shoulders.
And as another poster mentioned, their pricing is higher than what you'll find at a typical take-away store, but for good reason - the food is delicious and the ingredients are very high-quality. These ladies also put twists on recipes that you wouldn't expect, but without sacrificing quality. Simple and tasty! And the women who own the store do most of the cooking, shopping, and baking themselves.
If you're looking for basic take-away food, head somewhere else, because this place is definitely a step above!

I'm appreciative this place does not sacrifice quality ingredients in their food at he sake of trying to get prices down. I personally can tell the difference from processed or frozen foods vs. fresh, quality foods. My husband picked up two meals the other night at $40 and we have gotten 6 servings out of it. Not bad I think for a delish Home cooked meal and perfect for on the way home when I don't feel like cooking! The tamales were out of this world and the shrimp gumbo had plenty of fresh large shrimp.... Some of the best shrimp I've had in a while!!! We will absolutely be returning!!!

You get what you pay for and LeeAnn and Ursula are the best of the best. Any menu you dream of those talented ladies can make happen and if you are stumped - it's ok! Let them run with it and they will produce some of the most exciting tummies you've ever had.
TGiTwNYy3ZvOoHTT7ZHpgg: Food was fresh, took a little while but she explained it was because they cook the hot wings to order. Guess that's a plus. Gave it four stars only because it was nothing over the top

I've always loved KFC. And this is the closest to my house. The chicken is always well cooked and delicious, and the service is usually not bad. They are usually not the friendliest, but certainly not bad. I've never had a truly bad experience here and I think you should try this location.

4 stars only because it wasn't a buffet. 
Food was fresh and services was great. Restaurant was clean too.
I-nrEtjboBLoEvGiSAcmvw: This place is amazing! My husband and I ate dinner here last night for our anniversary. Service was top notch from start to finish. The food was delicious. Our server even brought a complimentary bottle of champagne to our table for our anniversary. I can't wait to go back!

I was skeptical about going because the menu seemed bland and expensive but I was so wrong! This is the best meal I have had in 24 years of living in Nashville. Each bite was more delightful than the last. My New York strip was perfect. The complimentary French fries with dips and Parker house roll were wonderful. The potato gratin , macaroni and cheese and Brussels sprouts were also delicious. The view was outstanding even in the ladies room!  The room is beautiful and bustling with happy diners, though a little noisy. The glassware sparkled .This is now my special occasion go to place.

I've been here a couple times (only for drinks) and there isn't much to say but super cool view, with a very fancy vibe. 

Drinks/cocktails are pretty pricy, around $17 dollars, however they have traditional cocktails and "rebellious" cocktails that have a Nashville twist on traditional creations. I would recommend going with their twist, as most of them taste better than the traditional. 

The environment inside the restaurant is very classy. Very dim lit, formal clothing, and 5 star decorations. The waiting staff resembles this same style of class with formal interactions and professional service. 

I haven't had a chance to grab real food, but did share a chocolate cake with ice cream that was delicious! This is a fantastic item to split with someone if you are craving something sweet. 

Overall, you can enjoy all the above with a pretty sweet view of the Nashville skyline. Because it is winter, the patio seems to be closed, however it appears that it may open up once the summer comes back. Would certainly recommend you check this place out.
1DAI96tu5hcP_lvKBHVixw: This place is like Cheers....they always know your name and somehow, after a few times, always remember your order. I especially enjoy the way they arrange their sauce packets so diligently. My favorite taco bell in Nashville!
Yelp reviewing a fast food chain like taco bell seems so funny to me BUT this location is amazing. Staff is so kind and the food has always been perfect and fresh ( as fresh in taco bell standards )

This is where I go for my fast food work lunches. They are quick, friendly, and accurate. I've gone to this taco bell once a week for a few months and they have NEVER screwed up my order or given me the wrong drink. The lunch crew there is fantastic!
OGtK8D9vn_Dvcp1GJqJ3jg: It's fast food. I'm not sure what sort of review you're hoping for, but this is a typical Taco Bell. It's all the hangover food your want, but this location gives it to you at a little slower pace.

A visiting patron from Pensacola, FL.   This location was close to the hotel I was staying which is a Godsend for me because I do not have transportation.  The facility was Clean and I was served hot tasty food.  What's important to me is the customer service received from both the cashier and kitchen staff. The ladies were very friendly PLUS provided EXCELLENT  customer service !

Service was served with a smile.  Thank you.

So i rolled up on Shawn at 1:13am wanting something to eat not only did he help me out he also offered me a drink that now I love the  Dragon Fruit slush  and and and he sounded like 5:30pm like he just got to work and its time to get off... i will always come back to this location all smiles  
Tried to give 5 stars it wouldn't let me
-yFbTwzGllU3kHfRmJWO0A: Little slice of heaven. All you can eat breakfast specials. Clean and remodelled after the great flood. Plenty of space to relax. Not a crowded breakfast dinner spot. Enjoy

Now I know this place is at a truck stop but it seriously has pretty good food. Did I mention you can have seconds? Or thirds? Or fourths? Plus it has a decent salad bar. I try to eat everywhere around town at least once and I have visited this place on more than one ocassion.
 
 This truck stop was completely remodeled after the flood and is very nice. I know it's in a not so nice part of town but they have security so it is never been an issue there. I would feel good about letting my wife go there at lunch alone if that tells you anything. The place is always clean and the waitresses have been very nice. Sometimes you might have to wait a little bit because they have a lot of tables but I've never had bad service there.

 One day when you and friends are looking for someplace to go to lunch suggest this place you will not be sorry you did!
ED1yWQgM6-Mz7QKhDYCXrw: Pretty good! Service was fast even though our server was exhausted. Great server, good food. I had the grilled chicken salad.

This is my go-to Waffle House location. I have been here over twenty times so I think I am inclined to give them a review. Since the only two reviews thus far have been off one bad experience instead of giving them a few tries to get a good grasp if the place is truly lousy or it was a one time fluke. This location has always came through for me. Yes, they will occasionally goof up the order, but they are always quick to make it right with no excuse. This is a Waffle House, so I am not expecting Ruth Cris, I know I'm getting greasy, bone warming, American diner food, and this place always provides excellent cooked products for the genre it is operating under. Sometimes it is busy and you have to wait if you like a booth, but I can say I have never waited beyond ten minutes. If you order on the go they are great at that as well. I have ordered to go more times than I have dined in, so it's legit to get it on the run.
This is the one you want. Late night snack . Literally took them 5 mins after I called. Walked in and it was ready to go.
eCZ4jBuyOjVhkrTMcXR0Yg: Went here on a Groupon.  Comfortable, casual, family run restaurant. The owner's son, Michael was our server, very knowledgeable and friendly.  The food was very good, not spicy, with lots of fresh herbs and made per order.  Kanafa and Arabian coffee to finish up was delicious.  We want to go back to try other menu items.  Definitely worth a try!

Easy 5 stars. Coffee was perfect, the falafel is crispy on the outside and soft inside, and the veggie plate was abundant. Everything was very fresh, and a great amount of care was given in preparation. Going back ASAP.

I really enjoyed the lamb shank and loved the falafel!!! One of the BEST I've tasted in nashville! The stuffed grape leaves weren't as tangy as I prefer but good... the pita was deliciously fresh! I wish the tzatziki sauce was a little more seasoned... the atmosphere was ok but would really add to that good cook they already have!! I'm not a fan of country music in a middle eastern restaurant.. Celebrate the whole experience! I'll definitely be back!
Jnxn6VH6L0fwSZDuBTjpTA: This is a great subway location because it is in the children's hospital food court and offers both healthy and fresh ingredients. Also be sure to hold on to your receipt because you can take a quick online survey to get a free cookie the next time you visit! At subway, I usually order the Black Forest ham footlong and when toasted on Italian herb bread, it tastes amazing. The service is super quick and friendly as well. My only complaint is that the base price for the footlong subs starts at $5.50 and increases depending on what kind of sub you get.
lbKIeDhgehoKsJvYQrLQnQ: Super friendly service and massive fry for a fry lover.. definately didn't have the typical funnel cake taste to their fries like most checkers do now. Worth the stop if you end up wandering out their like we did
God bless this Checkers. This Checkers is the pinnacle of Checkers on the Gallatin Turnpike. The employees are not only kind but they are also funny! 5 stars!

This Checkers truly saved my life, and I am not being dramatic. After a long, hot 4th of July in beautiful Nashville, me and my gal pals were quiet hungry for an evening snack. After stopping at many establishments that were all closed and/or claimed they were out of meat (what????), we finally landed upon the holy land that is Gallatin turnpike Checkers. The employees were funny and kind, and the food hit the SPOT. God bless.
wBo_8-g29R29-1QVWYu7uQ: Great class!! Highly recommended.  I took the class add black-eyed pea with Eddie Alexander. He was very thorough help led the class go fast, and really had a deep understanding of the laws in Tennessee. I really really really recommend this class from Eddy.

I really enjoyed my class with Donny Miller. He was very knowledgeable informative and excellent instructor. He made sure that everyone properly prepared and comfortable for the test. I would defiantly recommend him to anyone that needs the training.

Norman taught my class & did a wonderful job. He made the 5 hours a breeze. You can tell he loves his job. He taught the information so well that I finished the test in 15 minutes with a 98. So a special thanks to him!
lnCLpUgxG8In0M7fv7liFw: Best coffee in a wide radius of Peabody. French roast is exceptional. Baked goods are well above average, outstanding even considering the library basement set up. Friendly service and rarely a wait in the morning.

I work on the Peabody campus, and Iris Café is my go-to stop when I need a pick me up. I don't handle coffee well, so I appreciate that they serve up delicious chia, matcha, and black tea lattes. I go to Iris multiple times a week, and the staff is always friendly and polite. The inside of the café is a bit bleak, but you cannot beat the views from the patio in the spring and summer.
Pr6evSEG2vmtS8ny7K4nVA: Food was OK. Much better than most bar food. There are a TON of TVs. Will be good for games but not really somewhere I'd go on a Friday or Saturday evening again. Service was extremely slow. They seemed very understaffed. Hopefully will be better in the coming months.
Nice happy hour the voodoo wings are the best dry rub around . The atmosphere is one of a kind . Great selection
Great location, fun brunch!  Food is good, music is really fun.  Great place
KbhKbjG7fVM2vTtO3qbe0w: The experience and food here is truly amazing! The ambience is perfect for casual dining or even formal dining. Cocktails are unique and each one is delicious. If you want an out of body experience with Indian food come check out Chaatable! We ordered multiple dishes and every single one of them were finger licking good. They brought back memories of times when we lived in India and an amazing twist that made our taste buds craving for more! Don't pass by this restaurant it's a true gem

Fabulous décor. The wall of 40k bangles just sets the whole room. Popping with vibrant colors of hues from India. The umbrella lamp shades were a really cute idea and personable. The whole space felt cozy. We used online reservations and seating was a snap. A wide variety of seating was available. Great space at the main bar. The outdoor space will lend itself well as the weather permits. 
I've eaten at all 4 of Maneet's restaurants now and this is my favorite. This is NOT your typical Indian restaurant. So if you plan to walk in and order your Tandori Chicken and "usual" Indian choice, give it up just for this place. Surrender you carnivore habits and go with the wide variety of vegetarian dishes. With that, the pork belly was the best dish. The pan fried brussels sprouts were laced with salt, but tasty. Spot on chik peas chutney were the perfect starter with the samosa, very rich flavors. Even the potato skins were laced with flavor and snapped up quickly. The Firecracker beef fry was a huge portion and dripping in the well balance of heat/flavor. 
Order a side veggie plus Firecracker and you'll be stuffed. The portions of the dinner dishes are for 2-3 people. Above average cocktails, but nothing that was mind blowing. The Blue Tea Gin , Let's Part-tea was simple and elegant. 
Our Savor™ rating is 4 out of 5.

Check out our regular weekly episode of Sips, Suds, & Smokes anywhere online or on the radio.
www.sipssudsandsmokes.com

WOW! Talk about a food coma.  First off, the atmosphere is to die for.  It really does add to the dining experience.  The cocktails are top notch, we ordered a few different ones, but our favorite was 'Resting Beach Face.' It was the perfect mix of refreshing with a little kick.  

The food was so delicious.  We ordered a big variety so we could get the full experience.  We started off with the 'Most A-peeling Snack' which is plaintain chips with an indian spice blend, great starter to snack on with cocktails.  Then we had the samosa chaat and pork belly hot appetizers.  For our main dish we split 'The O.G.' which was amazing!  Just a classic indian dish with tons of flavor.  We added the brussels on the side and the flatbread (similar to naan) to soke up the delicious sauce!  It was a ton of food, and we definitely had leftovers. 

The food comes out as it is ready, so if you want a more paced meal I would suggest ordering as you finish each course so you don't end up with a table full of food.
C9KvsTqi617Yg_sNjG2UDA: We had a group of 8 people that attended the 8:30pm dinner at Tailor.  The food arrived in a very timely manner and everything was explained very well.  A few of us had some dietary restrictions and the staff and chef had very good alternative items for them. The host and chef Vivek was very engaging and friendly. It was amazing how he told his story about what the different courses meant to him and his family.  The food was authentic Indian. Very tasty!
Very unique experience you wont find anywhere else in Nashville. Food, staff and atmosphere are all top notch. Highly recommend.

Exciting chef's table experience. It's like having a master chef invite you into his kitchen and explain each aspect of a multi course dining tour de force. Simply the best food and complete dining experience you can have.
CRe_j_zZ9XynzSc2Sc0ggA: Came here for a late lunch. The staff was super friendly and didn't mind recommending food and drinks to us when we asked. The food was good. I had the pub salad and added grilled chicken and ranch. My husband had the jalapeño burger and really enjoyed it. He said his fries were great as well. We had chips and salsa for our appetizer. The chips were tasty and perfectly seasoned. I'm not sure if the salsa was homemade, but nonetheless it was good. Had a moon on draft. It was great. They have a good size selection of beer.

Same great Corner Pub food as Bellevue and Brentwood. Very sports-themed and the atmosphere is really cool, with numerous big screens and practically every kind of beer on the planet, including some great locals. Like everything downtown, prices are higher than other locales but not terribly so. Still a pretty good value for the quality and amount of food you get. Also Lauren is a really good server!
Chase was the best server I've ever had in Nashville! After lunch we went to the bar and Miranda BLEW OUR MIND. She's the best. 10/10 would recommend this spot: Probs see ya tomorrow.
bA4_MDnT5fK7NJaw_YNJaA: We stumbled in here because it was close to our air Bnb. The beef pad Thai was so good! We enjoyed the wontons. Sushi was pretty good, although some of the salmon skin was still on the rainbow roll.

I just received my first takeout order of pad see ew from Aiyara -- the price was a little more than I'd expected, but the amount of food I received more than made up for it (in fact, it was enough for about three meals). The broccoli and carrots still had a fresh texture, the overall flavor was great, and the seasoning was perfect. (FYI: I ordered a "4" on the heat scale, which was on the hotter side of mild.)
Great service, great prices, amazing food!! There was live jazz music and such a wonderful atmosphere. Best Philly roll and Pad Thai in town!
sKkIIJHAFev0a28vboj60g: Went on their second day open- food was great and service was even better.  Nathan did a great job making suggestions and entire staff was on the ball.  We had the potato nachos, Pollotate, and bbq chicken pizza.  Highly recommend the Pollotate!  Really nice outdoor seating area and great beer selection.

Had lunch here the first day of a girls trip. We sat outside in the sun and had the are all to ourselves. The draft beer (lil darlin) was very good as well as the Chicken Bacon Avocado pizza. Only reason I am giving 4 stars is because some staff were not friendly.

First time in Boombozz. Was pleasantly surprised by the amount of TVs and different sports options. Happy to see a place I could bring children and still enjoy myself. Our food came with bacon on it (though the item ordered mentioned no bacon at all). After bringing it to our server's attention, he immediately got a manager who came and apologized, went back to watch a new sandwich be made THEN came to explain to us how the bacon got on the plate in the first place. A simple mistake but they handle it with the customer's best interest as their number one priority. We will definitely be back!
KzMgip1V94YCTsU-H6vtFQ: We loved Jordan, we loved the food! Highly recommend the Tokyo chicken. Rolls were delicious. Excellent service. We're from Chicago and are good snobs and we both would highly recommend

Unbelievable food!!!  Wes was our server and was amazing!!  He recommended the right dishes for my wife and I and was a joy to be around!  Couldn't be any happier to have an amazing sushi spot in Nashville!
Phenomenal sushi AND dessert (pb&j). Cool vibes, decor and staff. Clinton helped us and was one of the most fun and knowledgeable servers I've ever encountered.
Gmj8oyfKmr0ezXaB2XVuOg: I was driving down Murfreesboro road and needed a to go lunch.  I saw a taco truck connected to a brick and mortar restaurant and decided to stop by.  I ordered inside with my taco truck go-to, a torta Cubana.   This was probably my favorite torta Cubana in town.  It had fresh avocado and all of the meats were great.  The bread was a good thickness resembling a Kaiser roll.  It was big, but it wasn't too big like some others.  I'll definitely return.

This is my go to for great tacos and tortas. I recommend the pierna torta and the lengua tacos. So good! It reminds me of home in NY where you can find so many hole in the wall taco places that really deliver great food. Antioch really has some choice places and this is one of them.

Late-night tacos are always the best. Pulling up you'll see the truck but come in to get a seat and a table to enjoy what will be brought to you. 
The tacos here are spot on but they have many other options on their menu: tortas, sope, quesadilla, and more! 

Come in any time and be surprised at how much you love a traditional street taco.
bg4byZlEDCUVbC16VHxjlA: Awesome "feeling" as soon as you walk in the door!  Tim made an incredible shot. Yay, Nashville!!

Nice place. People were friendly, staff nice. Gets kind of loud, especially a couple of patrons at the time that would just NOT stopping talking, extremely loud. The bottle DIPA was a little pricey at $10 a bottle. In Atlanta, DIPA drafts are about $7.50-8, but never that high as a bottle. Will probably give it another try in the future.

Pecker's is a gay-friendly bar located off of Hermitage Avenue; their secret is that they also offer good chicken snacks. I have had several good times here; the bartenders are very attentive. I can't let it go unspoken that the drinks here are slightly overpriced for its location and the experience it could provide. One cool aspect of this bar is that they have some pretty cool art installed around the common areas! Upon closer investigation you'll notice that the art is available for purchase, but you'll soon realize that is WAY overpriced. Aside from this travesty, Pecker's is a chill spot. Y'all should check it out if you're passing by.
2h10228FGBXalIvesfeNQA: I LOVE this food truck! They have great tacos: barbacoa, carnita, pastor(spicy pork), pollo, asada, lengua. My favorite is the barbacoa. Mulitas are also very very good - just enough cheese to hold the meat together. We've tried different combinations both times we've visited the truck and have not been disappointed. Pickled vegetables are a nice addition to the plate. Agua Fresca horchata is also very nice and refreshing. It helps to calm the flavorful hot sauce. Their health inspector rating was 99! We'll be back for more!
Best authentic Mexican street tacos in town! Their carnitas are Bomb.com, by far my favorite meat here. For only $1.25 a taco, you can't go wrong!

This place is the bomb. Arguably my favorite taco in all of Nashville. There are a couple of these trucks around town, all of which do not move, but are stationary in parking lots. We used to always go to the one right by the zoo, but since moving a few years ago, we are much closer to this location. I didn't see it on yelp, so I added it. I had to let the yelp world know about these hidden gem tacos. Each taco is only $1.25. They are absolutely delicious. Authentic style with meat, cilantro and onion folded inside of two small corn tortillas. Something about these little guys just blows some other taco places out of the water. The service has always been friendly for us and they are pretty quick with the orders. It is in a parking lot, so I would grab them to go, but whatever works. Bottom line for all you taco lovers, get here
PA6yX2D4ZyqQCE4Xa6I_fg: Again one of those neighborhood restaurants that stay consistent...even when they change the name. The proportions have gotten a bit smaller and there's been a bit of a price hike, but they have always stayed with the same good flavors. I personally love their Carnitas and Fish tacos. The chips are so good and the salsa is so yummy I could drink it by the glass.

Thank god. After both our go to Mexican places sold out (they have the same generic menu, prices went up and quality went down :( ) we tried this place on a whim. So good. The grilled chicken was smoky and tender, enchiladas were on point, and the salsa was fresh and spicy. Service was polite and friendly and quick!! Our new fav!

Probamos los tacos de carnitas y steak. Los recomiendo. Son grandes y con doble tortilla. Las porciones son grandes así que con 3 tacos por persona es más que suficiente. La salsa y totopos saben bien. Lo mejor de aquí es que todo está muy llenador . Buen servicio y amabilidad. Hablan español
vF7XZtU8qZgLPZpsTWpX5g:
There are few times in life when a meal is so expertly crafted that it deserves comment. This is one of those times. 

During a romantic dinner with my wife I was blown away by the sophistication and subtly of the Spicy Potato Taco. The attention to detail makes calling this meal "Tex-Mex" woefully inadequate. 

Our tacos were followed with a main course of Bean Burritos (I recommend getting them grilled!) and Cheese Rolls-ups. 

Service was impeccable and the decor modern. 

Five Stars.

This Taco Bell has been my constant for the past 8 months. While the drive thru may be long, the love put into their Crunch wrap supreme is worth the wait. A wonderful place, where we all find ourselves at 11:30 pm on a Saturday, never fails to follow through. I love you Taco Bell. We will never forget you.

This place is the best I love this place it's insanely good like people that don't like Taco Bell have so serious problem because it's just so good like there's nothing bad about it and what's not to like
WsYf3_6_GaOX1K1CXtAKag: I had the pleasure of checking out this spot last night and, as expected, it was wonderful. From the folks at the Nations favorite 51st Deli just down the road, this new restaurant lives up to the very high standards they have set. The same high-quality ingredients, a few familiar faces and large portions at a very fair price are exactly what I anticipated and they delivered! I tried the house "Joint Burger" and also the lamb burger and both were excellent.  There are fries, tots, wings, and milkshakes on the menu, so it hits all the right notes. Go and check it out!

Went in today on the advice of another local food site, and I wasn't disappointed!  First off, it's counter service, but they have a full service bar as well, about 10 or so beers on tap as well as liquor.  They have kombucha on tap, four different flavors.  They carry Coke products in bottles, but have Pepsi fountain drinks, as well.  The menu seems pretty well rounded for a small place:  Burgers, veggie burgers, Impossible burger, wings, Nashville chicken sandwich, and more.  I got the house burger (two patties, cheese, grilled onions, lettuce, tomato, pickles and house sauce) that was great.  Someone else at my table got the Impossible burger and pronounced it very good.  The portions of fries or tots are enough to share, for sure.  Great deal for $3 each.  They have gluten free buns, but I got the brioche bun which was awesome.  I WILL be going back!  Highly recommended.
Burger and fries were very good. Great atmosphere and service. Excited to try chicken sandwich next!
dZrcqm22sNgZu7oHJxFkkg: This is actually the BEST Taco Bell I've ever been to like I was very skeptical at first after reading the reviews for this one but WOW it hasn't let me down at all. The food is always hot off the press and freshly made and it's well made too, not hastily thrown together. I highly recommend this Taco Bell. Anchor Down!

This is a wonderful Taco Bell location. They have self service ordering kiosks making it easy to customize food, mobile ordering and cashier orders. Food quality has always been fresh and hot. They also being on campus serve Coca Cola vs Pepsi drinks and honor T-Mobile free Tuesday offer.
UEVrsWZ_1JmQ_l2jWcRYNQ: Stopped by after all the favorite taco haunts were closed on Sunday. We had a good experience. The service was good, prompt, and friendly. We had the Baja fish tacos and Texmex burrito. Portion sizes are huge. Fish tasted fresh and the presentation on both was nice. The tacos could have used maybe a green sauce, but they still had good flavor. We'd definitely come back. For the vegetarians, they had 5 vegetarian combos which seemed like the pretty standard stuff- enchiladas, quesadillas, chalupas, etc.
We really enjoyed our Mexican dishes at Cilantro!  The food quality was excellent.  Service is great!  Have been 3 times & always good.  Cilantro is now our favorite Mexican restaurant!

I love this place. Extensive and interesting margarita selection, gigantic portion sizes and tasty quality food. The patio out back is great during nice weather. My go-to neighborhood Mexican food spot. Highly recommend!
vRCFYXIQe780SjWBVc_nxw: Cole's Kitchen opened only a couple of weeks ago, but I have been there 4 times.  The restaurant has a whole new vibe from Curt's last restaurant, but you will find reasonable prices and fabulous food.  You must try the mushroom mozzarella puff.  It is listed under sides, but I prefer it as an appetizer!

Pre-lockdown experience.

There's not a better neighborhood in Nashville to be a laddie who lunches than Green Hills. Germantown and 12 South could never. A late autumn Monday found me in need of having lunch with an ex-banker turned friend and I wanted to see what the next iteration of Firefly was like. With the same owner/chef at the helm, I didn't quite understand the name change. I figured lunch would clear the air.

It did (gone is the funky fun of Firefly; no beads, no lights, no measured chaotic energy) and lunch was enjoyable -- very Green Hills. The dining room is a bit nondescript but entirely inoffensive. It's cramped but you know this already. For these reasons, I don't think I'd eat dinner here (despite the substantial and delicious-sounding offerings) but lunch (scarcer clientele) is very doable. Our waiter was perfectly pleasant. He wasn't the most learned re the menu but I wasn't bothered by it. Again, it's lunch. 

Despite dining with a banker, neither one of us were in the mood to drink (a Green Hills faux pas?) so we kept it sorta healthy while we talked shop. The hummus appetizer was satisfying. I forwent the pita for crudite', mostly because I like to say the word (not because I enjoy raw carrot & celery sticks). But I was not leaving Green Hills without saying - and eating - crudite'. I stayed on brand by enjoying a lunch of shrimp salad and chicken salad - both served on individual beds of greens. I could have gone for broke by ordering the egg salad and tuna salad as well but that was just too much fashion. The salads were even better than advertised. The chicken salad showcased freshly pulled chicken and the shrimp salad was a cool, creamy, crunchy delight. I'd order both again (I believe they were specials of the day).

If I sound like a grandma, so be it. If I'm in Green Hills, I'm going to be a laddie who lunches. It is what it is.

Cole's Kitchen is excellent! My wife and I wanted to try and new place for a date night and LOVED it! The atmosphere was quiet and intimate. We sat indoors (with tables well spread apart due to COVID), but the outdoor patio seating looked like a favorable option as well.

The crab cakes we got as an appetizer were crispy and delicious. We split the burger (with fries) and catch of the day (sea bass with mashed potato's and green beans) as our main meal. The burger had excellent flavor, and the ciabatta bun was a nice touch. The sea bass practically fell apart when cut into, but had a great crust to it.

This is a hidden gem for Nashville! We will definitely be back.
-Q32uZ1kdfhd9-KgTATx-A: Absolutely Delicious Food! We stumbled upon this quaint little food truck and can say we are glad we did! We tried the pork char sui, the pork steamed buns and the Korean fried chicken. It was sooo much food, but so worth it. (Saved some for lunch tomorrow ) Definitely coming back!

I am so glad I stumbled upon this place. It's off to the side of L and L market. Everything  is delicious and has great combinations of flavors. It's always hard to pick just one item because everything is so good!
We love Camion Chino. We had the carne asada tacos and the Korean fried chicken. Absolutely delicious and will definitely go again.
BNMnyrNZNQNxQ7Qz1Jos7w: I was so excited to try this new local brewery and man was I blown away. The pizza is really great and the beer? Well that's fantastic too. I had the golden ale. Highly recommend this place and I will be back many many times.

Ordered pizza and wings for pick up. There was some confusion for several people as to where to pick up and pay. They wouldn't let me pay over the phone, but I got over it. Looking forward to this place being all the way open - the patio was pretty busy. The food was good for the price.

Great pizzas with nice crust. Chorizo pizza has jalapeños so it does have a little kick of heat. The fresh peaches on it help balance the heat. The Meats is not too saucy- which we love and the pepperoni, sausage and bacon have a great flavor. Can't wait to try more and dine in. We had to carry out only due to virus situation. So glad to have this open in Donelson!
TVO-R_N5CGCNum4-TjrLDA: So so so good! Staff was very nice and food was excellent. Tables have plexiglas dividers which is super cool!
Very cute place.  Loved the cat decor.  The wings were great, we would definitely go back here.

Had an opportunity to dine here last week. It was a treat. You must start with the Malaysian flat bread with the curry dipping sauce. Think of the flat bread as if a piece of naan and croissant had a baby... match made in heaven.  The pan seared dumplings touched on classic cuisine. I gravitated to the twice fried wings. They were exactly what I was looking for: extra crispy (but not dry) with a sweet asian spice. I almost ordered a second round. 

One of our guest could not make it, so we now have two reasons to come back;)
6RxJLsIoRSkUfvOxYP0sKQ:
This. Place. Is. LEGIT. One of the hardest parts of moving to Nashville for grad school has been giving up on finding good, homestyle Chinese and Taiwanese food. Enter Mama Yang and Daughter. They are filling Nashville's (and my stomach's) dumpling void with food that brings me right back home to my Chinese family's kitchen. 

I have now tried everything on the menu and I can tell you with confidence that you really can't go wrong, but the standouts are definitely the beautifully handmade dumplings. All three types are BOMB - pork has a great texture and nice cabbage contrast, veggie is deliciously sweet from the carrots and corn, and chicken has yummy mushrooms that round it out perfectly. The wrappers on them are not too thick, they have a generous amount of filling, and the bottoms are deliciously browned. Plus each order comes with homemade sauce and pickled cucumbers?! What more could I possible ask for! Eating them, you really feel like each one is handmade with love. That said, my personal favorite is the veggie, and I will keep singing its praises to anyone who will listen to me. 

They're operating out of Citizen Kitchens, so you can only order takeout (no dining in), so be prepared for that. Order via their website and then just daydream about dumplings until your pickup time. I think they're also at the Richland Park Farmer's Market down the street, too, selling frozen dumplings so you can have dumplings on demand!

Authentic and tasty dumplings! Extremely friendly service, as I had a specific packaging request that they accommodated easily.

Hand-Kneaded Scallion Pancake: 3/5
This may have been because scallion pancakes are best eaten fresh, but I found mine to less crispy than I would've liked. However, it's hard to fault Mama Yang because they're a takeout-only operation right now.

Mama's Original Pork Dumplings: 4/5
The filling was perfect! Classic pork and cabbage. They also nailed the skin-to-filling ratio, as each dumpling was generously stuffed. In addition, both the sauce and cucumbers served on the side were flavorful and complemented the dumplings well. The only thing is that I prefer my dumpling bottoms to be crispier if they're pan-fried.

Crispy Banana Wontons: 5/5
A unique way to end the meal! The crispiness of the wonton skins survived the takeout process, and the banana filling and chocolate sauce made up a delicious flavor combination.

As I write this, Mama Yang & Daughter is operating out of Citizen Kitchens in West Nashville, but believe me when I tell you, I'll one day be able to say "I knew them when..." These are the dumplings I've been searching for since I moved to Nashville and was so surprised and saddened to realize our Asian food options were dismal compared to most major cities. I actually mentioned this while picking up -- that I've been searching for a place to make me miss NYC less -- and Grace, the daughter of the duo, told me that was a big part of why she and her mom teamed up after she moved from NYC and realized the gap in the food scene here. She also told me she was *really* hoping I liked it now that I'd admitted I had so much hope (no pressure!). And, did I like it? How about, more accurately, "Was I already considering going back the next day for the vegetarian ones (because Friday in Lent) and the banana wontons before I was even done eating?"

I got Mama's Original Pork dumplings and a scallion pancake. The dumpling dough was tender, the filling fluffy almost and, though light all around, packed with a punch of flavor in each bite. Plus, the scallion pancake was perfect and really did perfectly pair with the white gourd drink as the beverage section of online ordering noted to me.

I will be definitely back -- if not tomorrow, at least a few times a month for, well, ever, if they'll have me.
PoaNRg8e1QpLehz4Cocebw: Great place in 12 South that doesn't feel like it's catering to the tourism crowd. It has a hearty feel not just from the decor, but the staff and fellow customers. A great place for a quick bite to eat or to watch the Titans take yet another L. Try their BC Burger and the fried pickles. Also, let them know Jack sent you.

I LOVE this place! Great service, food, and drinks. Now that the weather is nice, they open up the windows to let all the fresh air in. Check out Trivia on Tuesday nights.

Oh, and if you're a little Karen, please stay home in your hazmat suit.

Let me just say this, Wrylin and Kaylin are all stars. I've never been to a place that makes me feel so happy and at home. I just recently moved to Nashville from NYC and Bottle Cap opened their arms to me as my new watering hole. The staff is truly incredible and so personable with great attitudes and spirits. I definitely found my home-base bar. So happy to have found this place.
Ibf7OyrUN-Nl49MiO0821A: This was one of the best burgers I have had in a long time. Super fresh, high quality, delicious! You will not be disappointed. Vey friendly staff that make you feel welcome and appreciated. So happy to have them in the neighborhood.

My girlfriend and I went early on a Saturday night and we left very happy. The jalapeño burger and fried pickle burger are next level good. Great meat, great bun, and the meat to bun ratio is perfect. Also, highly recommend the cheese curds. 

Staff is very cool and welcoming. I hope this place does well.
