Stopped in this afternoon! Owner is super friendly. Great staff. Fabulous burger. I had the fried pickle burger with lettuce bun - so so so good! My friends loved their meals as well...turkey burger and hot tots and fried cauliflower. We will be back! Great addition to the East Nashville community.
JEGtNfNe43X8vyapxKWquA: Awesome service, great deal on margaritas and chill environment. Definitely will be back!

Love this place for takeout! We always get the taco salad and enchiladas! Takeout comes with chips and salsa which is always great to have! Perfect place for authentic mexican and we will continue ordering.

Came here because it was close to our place. Tiny place with great staff and service.

I ordered a chicken Chimichanga. It is a flour fried tortilla and I ordered it with chicken. It is topped with sour cream and a side of rice and beans. Very good and fresh!
zaOhxcCakvXke6FQRcFg2w: Another amazing Crema experience! This little roastery meets coffee bar is tucked into one of East Nashville's yet to be over developed neighborhoods making it feel like a "Secret Crema":) A very small walk up counter with delicious drinks & baked goods and their same warmed to perfection customer service makes this a lovely place to grab a quick drink or hang with a friend! Kudos to Crema on another home run magic coffee experience!

If I could give it SIX stars, I would. So thrilled this location opened up on the east side. While the original Crema is always a staple, this is a great substitute for a quicker, classic Crema experience. It's unfortunate someone could leave a 1 star review thinking they would host a business meeting here when that is clearly not the intention of this space. Shame. 

Always the best cappuccino in town!
No joke BEST roasted  coffee in Nash.  I even do a monthly mail delivery for that reason.  So worth it.
GTlGJH0lceTK0ICS5SLTlg:
Great Detroit/Deep Dish style pizza with lots of fun flavors to choose from. Each bite is so flavorful, makes you crave for more. I had a piece of the Colony pie  (pepperoni) and Vodka pie (plain-vodka sauce) and it was amazing. Recommendation is to order a few flavors and share with friends. They're nicely cut into square-pieces. 

One small thing is that for some slices, although Detroit-style does have thicker crust, the slice was a little too thick compared to the other slices with a lot of breading and not so much meat and sauce. It was only a few slices like that, but other than that, it was amazing. 

They just recently opened at Vista Germantown apartments and I got to try their pizza at the apartment's event. I'm so glad it's located by me so I will definitely be back. I've heard a lot of good things about their burgers and waffle fries, so I will have to go back and try these.

The pizza here is amazing. We got the Nashville hot chicken pizza and MVP and couldn't decide which was better! The staff is also top-notch. We will definitely be adding Emmy Squared to our list of go-to's and recommendations to others!

I'm a pizza slut. I love it all. But this may have been the best pizza I have ever had!  My family did 3 different orders and each one out did the other. The service was above and beyond the second we walked in the door. With the service industry being so short handed they did a phenomenal job. I cannot wait to spread the word of this place! Def a must go.
9WkBLlgBMPPS5es6hcQsEw: The truck is very eye catching, great music playing from it, employees were super friendly, and the food was some of the best food truck food I've tasted! I tried the chicken empanada and the chicken was moist, well seasoned, the cheese was melted perfectly! Also the dough was fantastic, great texture and flavor!

Perfect empanadas! Not overly greasy and very high quality. My family's favorite was the lentil empanada. We also really enjoyed the fried plantains. 
Try all the different empanada fillings for a fun meal!

Such a fun truck! I visited the Chivanada II at the Music City Food Truck Park. The beef empanadas were recommended as a crowd favorite and they did not disappoint! I also got a side of the platanos maduro - I love fried plantains!! The food came out quick and hot.

Highly recommend!
nQfH7T3neXbTa6Xkz7IFoQ: Amazing food, great ambiance, and good service! Would definitely go back. Great place for a romantic dinner or small group dinner.

First off... the Brussel Sprouts were AMAZING. drinks were great!! Our waitress was a bit odd and didn't know how to split the checks, but not a huge deal. But the real MVP of the night was the bartender that ran all the way down the elevators, out the door, and across the street to an off-site parking lot to give me back my sunglasses that I didn't even realized I left at the bar. Thank you so much!!! Most people wouldn't have gone thru that effort but I was truly shocked and thankful he took that much time to find me & give them back. Thank you sir!!
Went here for my 21st, server ashtyn was so wonderful and accommodating! They let us easily change our reservation and were so kind and prompt with service! Definitely check it out!
4xsB0nDdU2HPQcRGiDuRoA: Great speakeasy with solid cocktails and a fun apps menu. The atmosphere pays homage to its former occupant: a car repair shop. The patio is super cool.
Fantastic drinks; excellent tacos. Montucky Cold Snacks in the cooler. Live DJ on a Sunday too! We'll be back!

What a fun spot! We went here for drinks after dinner and had a great time! The location is great- this part of town needed a cool spot like this! Plenty of seating but still an intimate vibe, and the patio was covered and heated but still felt like a patio. The seats were comfy at first- by the end of our evening, we were eyeing options that weren't the tall barstools we were sitting on; next time I might ask to sit inside. The drinks were unique and very well made. We didn't order food, but the food menu seems great for a happy hour or late night snacks. I'm excited that I discovered this place and can't wait to go back!
Zhj3JqPcEmeiY6uBpIMmIA: Delightful staff, fabulous cappuccinos and pastries! Lovely and funny selection of reading material to boot and fantastic patio! Prices are slightly high but the quality is superb !

The coffee here is always great and the baristas are always super friendly. If anything, the vibe of this place is one of a kind and truly worth the visit! I did however order the Chilled Breakfast Bowl and it was weirdly salty. It was greek yogurt topped with peach marmalade and granola but something about it was off. Overall, I'd recommend visiting!

They place is so beautiful inside. So much creativity all over the walls and almost everywhere you walk. Best coffee shop for pictures for sure. Only reason I can not give them 5 stars is because the coffee does not taste like it was sent from heaven. I made a YouTube video on the best spots in Nashville, this place was not in the list because the taste is good and only just good. 

@Whats Up Rosko
ge6Ee3aqCCSCiGTknqQ3Rg: Probably the best food I had in downtown Nashville in a long time! Tasty Chicken Shawarma Wrap! Very friendly staff
Absolutely delicious! I'll definitely be eating here again. I recommend the chicken shawarma, especially if you are new to this food!

With all of the many options at this location, I am so glad that I gave this Shawarma a try! The cashier was so friendly and patient as I made decisions on my order. When I say my order was ready quickly, I mean I was confused when they called my name so fast! The employee making my wrap even opened it up for me to see upon finishing it! Once I got to finally enjoy my beef & chicken shawarma wrap, the experience got even better! All of the classic meat, veggies, and tajikistan flavors were so well done and so balanced. The meat stood out in the great way that it was cooked. The fries I got on the side where also incredible!
jZ_tVIYc47iXuZe3AbsGjw: Just tried their Chicken sandwich. My husband had the wings. Both were excellent. The spice mixture was outstanding. Complex and unique. The chicken was moist and tender. Service stellar. Love this place!

Hot chicken?  Well, there's a new cock of the walk  And it's name is Flamies!  The hot chicken factory.  Everyone has "their" place, "their" reasons why it beats all the rest. Or maybe in the other camp and believe the whole hot chicken thing is over done, over rated, and just over it. 

Flamies does a lot of things right. Lots of heat if you want it. Low heat if that's your style. And everything in between. Crispy on the outside. Juicy inside.  It's good flavorful chicken. Takes about 15 minutes which means they are cooking to order. 

The sides potato salad, Cole slaw,  and Mac and cheese are okay, but unremarkable. Probably food service bulk. 

Place is very clean, and smells good, like fresh cooking. The music choices don't go with the concept and are at a volume level of a dance club rather than a restaurant. 

They are following all COVID guidelines that were in effect at the time. Lots of sanitizer. Social distancing.  Clean bathrooms to wash your hands and staff had masks. 

Definitely worth it, especially if this is your neighborhood. Lots of parking, great service, and friendly staff. 

Everything but the music that's why I give 4 stars
We got the tenders, Nashville hot style. The chicken was good size jumbo tender, juicy with good flavor. Probably won't do the side next time, we got mac and slaw...meh.
Z8c0t6xPfM8u1H1ZUcWlOw: If I could come back to anzie blue every day, I would! The iced chai was delicious & so was the matcha late... we even went & ordered a second! This coffee shop tucked away in Nashville has a very light and uplifting interior with a nice outdoor patio space. It's perfect for those who enjoy the indoors and those who prefer to enjoy the balmy summer air. The breakfast sandwich (on the bagel) was tasty too! Oh!! & If you want to take some cute photos, go inside and strike your favorite pose in front of the photo wall. I hope you enjoy ab as much as I do!

Excellent space! Wonderful food! Fabulous staff and entertainment! Fun, wonderfully decorated, hella clean restaurant with an incredible head chef.

What more could you ask for? Anzie Blue is the best place for brunch in Nashville!

What an amazing place - had a fabulous grilled chicken salad with raspberry dressing and the Tito's lemonade was great too.  The jazz music playing in the background was a added plus! Highly recommend!
jJ4zZY2cZj6VRY5pUL6lIg: Sooo excited to find a Vietnamese place in south Nashville! It's about time! I bought the beef/special Banh mi, cream puffs, and Thai tea w/boba. The sandwich was so fresh. I love that they  didn't skimp on the pate, the bread was soft, and it was all just all around tasty. The cream puffs were huuuge and tasted just like how my mom used to make them. The boba was soft and chewy and the Thai tea has a good flavor and wasn't too sweet.  They had a bunch of other desserts by the counter that aren't listed in their menu, so I'll have to try some of those next time.  I did notice that the pricing on the menu was high for some of the appetizers and pastries but realized it's because it's the total price for a set of 3 -4 of the items. You can buy them individually though. I'll definitely be back!

Loved this cafe!! The bahn mis are $5-6 and worth it! This isn't le grivales in Houston Texas that pumps out Bahn mi for 3 dollars but it's still worth the price you pay. The sandwich is a good size and the meat is well cooked and flavored well. On top of that, the woman working the front is very pleasant! I would highly recommend people come check out this location for a tasty sandwich. 

The boba here was good! Not the best I've ever had but still very tasty. It's tough to find good Asian cuisine or desert in Nashville so I'm going a little easier on the quality.

Very impressed by this spot! We got the rose milk tea and coconut milk tea and both were incredible. The sandwiches were incredible and so well priced. Atmosphere is very cute inside. Would really recommend this place to anyone who loves Vietnamese food!
3MuUUkRYnx56VAYyENvQDA:
Up-down is a cool experience and it's nice to have a new spot to hang out in East Nashville. 

I don't recommend the cocktails. They're pre-made and served on-tap. The one I tried had mint in it and tasted like toothpaste. 

Other than that, the arcade games are fun and it's a nice place to hangout with friends.

This place is an absolute gem in East Nashville. Fun, fun, fun!

But want to give an extra shout out to Robert who has excellent taste in the films this place chooses to show. He def knows his stuff, all perfectly appropriate for the overall ambience.

This place was so cool!!! It had amazing pizza, a huge choice of beers and ciders on tap, and an amazing atmosphere. I went on a .10 night so all games were a dime, $4 allowed for me and my girl to play for 2-3 hours. I would definitely recommend!
1my6j5BxJysPqUkkXsKUFQ: I always enjoy seeing a business I frequent expand and that is what Donut Distillery has done. They're a locally owned and have expanded to the brand new trendy 5th and Broadway district. They are located on the Assembly Food Hall near the entrance. They are still serving up their delicious boozey donuts that we love at that East Nashville location. It is well at home here in their 2nd location and I can't wait to see them grow even more. Todd and Shawna (the owners) are the best people with the warmest hearts! Enjoy your libations and liberations, while also having awesome customer service in the process. Also, word of advice - all places in the food hall are cashless, bring you credit/debit card for purchases.
Cute donuts that taste great! They were super fresh and what's cool is you can see them being made!
Fruit pebbles donuts were amazing. I think I hit up this spot in the assembly hall multiple times on my business trip.
tACGRiKglIXVjUpVsYODfg: This is 10000% the best place for food in Nashville especially for your after bar snack.  They made all of us feel like family.  The food is INCREDIBLE. hot dogs of all kinds, huge turkey legs, Mac and cheese.  You name it!!. Come set outside and eat and jam out at 3am. Didn't ever matter. We will be back!!!!! Thank you guys so much for being such a huge part of our Nashville memories!!!!!!- Nikki and Val
Quick, easy and great tasting dogs and crinkle cut fries. Also super nice staff! So good I forgot to take a photo before I ate half of it. Check em out!

Fun spot in Nashville that just opened up. The owner is super nice and is awesome. He opened up his place to me on my bachelor trip to music city. His team made some solid hookah and in the middle of the city.
p08guKw-66hwQTiskaAYiA: Delicious freshly made waffles and local ice cream served with homemade sauces. It tastes just as yummy as it smells! Owned and run by local family. Definitely recommend!

Let's be honest, waffles make the perfect base for a dessert. Especially when it's used as a base for a freakin sundae! Absolutely. 100%. Yes please!

I got The Baker pre-made creation with their super cute, trendy bubble waffle, which has key lime cheesecake ice cream, key lime pie flavored icing, graham cracker crumbs, and whipped cream. 

They're a super small local business and deserve so much attention and love from Nashville. You have to check them out

Amazing! A good selection of vegan and gluten free options. Each component was fantastic (they even have a special ice cream blend that Pied Piper makes especially for them!) but the waffle takes the CAKE!
RSioVOeEJbO0dR5oO78frg: Consistent & great bang for your buck! I've tried both their tacos and burritos, all never lacking flavor or quality. The two sauces that I've been given each time were key to adding some spice, which I found to be a great customization as someone who likes a bit of spice. Each time I have also been happy with the price of what I ordered compared to the size of the meal, especially that HEFTY burrito!

Great food and nicest people ever!! My friend lost her phone and the guy running the place routed through the trash for us multiple times. Turns out she had just dropped it outside... but it's the thought that counts right?! Definitely a must go place if you're ever in Nashville. Great vibe and fast getting the food out too!

Good chicken deluxe burrito. A bit heavy on the canola oil they cook the food in, but still tasty and good value. I'll just ask them to take it easy next time with the oil. 'Christy Cookies' at the register for a grab-snack was a nice touch.
fBc5T97oE9Dd-DvG04FHKA: This place was awesome Ended up here after shitty service at a near by place and it was worth it they have good specials awesome food and even better service.

Just Yes.  Guac - yes, though the chips broke too easily because the guacamole is thick and chunky, not pastey.  Maybe best to use a fork to put the guac on the chips.  Chicken tacos - yes.  Taco al pastor - yes.  The soft tacos themselves are fresh and pliable.  Could there have been two of them with each taco so when the juices seeped through they wouldn't fall apart?  Sure.  But they were delicious, so that counts for something.  Yes?  Yes.  Prices were fair.

Delicious!   I'm from California.. And these a
Tacos are like I'm home!the michelada yum!   The birria tacos with the consomé super yum!
DRtI59XyJHpolvr2WX8J2Q: This is a hidden gem tucked inside the Marriott. A relaxed atmosphere with outdoor seating boasting perfectly roasted shishito peppers, refreshing arugula Caesar salad and a perfectly seasoned Nashville Hot chicken sandwich. Server Cody did a phenomenal job with timely service and attention to detail!!
Tim and Carley, were great . They were very friendly and helpful, really made you feel at home. Really made for a good time
Stopped in here to meet some friends because everything else was booked up. Not a large menu but everyone was pleased with their meals. Big call out for the chicken sandwich- pretty incredible!!!
cquDeQN7gzg72tcI4hi9Rg: great espresso! it's super cute and could see myself getting some work done here. it's a little bit hidden, apple maps will take you to the back of the place you have to drive or walk around to the entrance. they have oat milk witch is awesome! i had an oat milk latte which was great and a chocolate croissant. it was a little bit slow but there was only one person working, so understandable.

Really cool new coffee shop at Rocketown. Friendly service and spacious indoor seating. While understanding their responsibility of being a specialty coffee shop, they support programs in Rwanda. Check them out.

I am so glad I went in to this place! I wasn't sure going in to it, but you can't judge a book by its cover! The second I walked in I felt like I was in a perfect coffee shop. There was space to stay and chat with friends or do work comfortably. The barista who served me was so incredibly kind, she made my day. As a barista myself, the espresso was going to be the real test. I went with an oat milk flat white. This is what is really going to have me coming back because it was delicious. The flavor of the beans they used combined with the perfect extraction and steamed milk was fantastic! I really can't wait to go back to try more, and possibly sit to do some work there!
efYk_lMnXplDILUKRaKbRw: Food is decent, but every time we visited this location the employees acted as if we were we were a nuisance to them.
Not a bad location at all given that it's hard to get into/out of on Murfreesboro Rd. The staff knows their stuff and my order was correct.
RyMeRG1wGIMnj3LA1x6jLg: This place is AMAZING. This is the best Italian food I have ever had in my life. The food is perfectly made and the workers are very friendly. It is a great environment over all. I highly recommend this restaurant. The workers are on it and are so helpful. The food comes out pretty quick which is also very nice

Excellent food and friendly service.
I grew up in Italy and I didn't think I could ever find true Italian pizza here, until I met Egidio. Also, the pasta is made from scratch, and you can tell. Try the tiramisu'!

Awesome pizza! The dough was so perfectly chewy and slightly charred. The tiramisu was also spectacular. 

We visited the first night it was open and they were definitely still working out some kinks. The service really could use some improvement but as I said, it was their first night so it's to be expected, I suppose. 

One other note - they had not received permissions to sell wine or liquor yet on opening night but it appears that they will have a full bar in the future. They did have an excellent Italian amber ale on tap that was super tasty - can't remember the name but it was really good! 

So excited to have a place like this with Neapolitan style pizza in the neighborhood. Will definitely come back and am excited to see how the service improves in time!
uL3qjBUpIqTPvVRnFC7Ung:
Sweet Moo's inside of the Opry Mills mall was my first time eating rolled ice cream. The employees were very nice and entertaining and the ice cream was a nice treat. They have many flavor combinations you can choose from and then unlimited toppings you can add into that. We split a "Like it" size (small) of one of the chocolate/fudge combinations. We added on sprinkles, coffee crumbles, caramel and strawberries. The most fun part was watching them chop up the toppings into a melted cream then the cream solidified on the large freezer slab and they patted into down into a large rectangle and rolled it. 

It was a little bit hard to eat but I liked the novelty aspect of it. I would definitely try it again!
G8pVaj3yS503ah8fxncDVA:
Best bakery and lunch spot in Nashville for an eclectic cafe meal with decent vegetarian options. Dozen baked all their bread and pastries in house, daily which makes for what I think, the best bread in Nashville. Combined with the mix of traditional and experimental dining options, this is one of my new favorite spots in Nashville. The menu offers breakfast and lunch on weekdays and brunch on weekends. It consists of salads, sandwiches, tartines, soups, and baked goods. You can even mix and match 1/2 a sandwich or tartine with a salad or soup. All the ingredients are extremely fresh and for the most part local as well. The portion sizes are not too big, but definitely filling. I had the egg in a hole with caprese salad which had  toasted homemade sourdough and an egg over medium. The bread was toothsome, buttery, crunchy, and not sweet unlike most american bread and the egg was perfectly over medium with a somewhat runny center. The salad was extremely fresh and not overdressed. You could tell it was made to order. My friend tried the benton sandwich which had such a beautiful combination of peaches, pesto, tomatoes, and bacon on a baguette. He was very pleased with it. We split a pastry that was like a croissant with strawberry filling and it was divine. So flaky, buttery, and moist but not too sweet and perfectly paired with the strawberry preserves. Dozen serves Crema coffee, which is some of the best in Nashville, and gives free refills. 

Though the spot is a little out of the way, it's certainly worth the trip. I went on a sunday for brunch and was pleasantly pleased to not find a line  or 45 minute wait or slew or bachelorettes in matching t-shirts. Just some young hip locals and family's enjoying a modern and cozy spot. Dozen is minimally, but expertly decorated to keep the vibe casual but still alluding to the artsy neighborhood it is located in. Even the bathrooms are decked out in cool graphics.

I love this place and will definitely be back.
The most amazing split baguette with avocado!  Wonderful customer service...will definitely return next time I'm in Nashville!

There are so many bakeries around town that never make the grade with us. My husband typically being the toughest critic of bakery goods in our household. And even he has proclaimed the baked goods from Dozen to be excellent. From the pecan squares to the chocolate chip cookies, he loves them all. My hands down favorite are the ginger cookies. They pack a punch from lots of candied ginger but they are pure chewy goodness. Her madelines are also great as are the blackberry bars. 

She uses local products and high quality products instead of so many processed ingredients with unnecessary additives and preservatives. You can find her at the Nashville Farmers Market and the Franklin Farmers Market
nQeu2F4xOiKoXIp-9engUw: Come support this place! Wonderful staff, wonderful drinks, GREAT food. You should come by here if you're in Nashville. You won't be disappointed.

Update

I am changing my review from a 1 star to a 4 star. This is all due to the lighting quick response from Nate, who messaged me and rescued the situation. He provided a detailed explanation of why the issue occurred, how they were going to make sure it never happened again, and offered to help find a suitable venue for my team dinner. Even though I had already found another venue, I really appreciated his response. I will be putting 5th & Taylor back on my rotation list.

-------------------------------------


This review is not about the food, because they cancelled my reservation and I was never able to sample it, but their service. Long story short, I made a reservation on Thursday for Monday night, got a confirmation text on Sunday night and 10 minutes after receiving the text got a call from the restaurant cancelling it. Apparently, someone decided to book the whole restaurant the day I happened to have a reservation and they were kind enough to call and tell me the good news that my reservation was no more. I loved the fact that she followed it up with, " I can help you make a reservation for another day". I was a little upset as this was a team dinner for co-workers coming in from out of town and I had to scramble to find another suitable restaurant. When I mentioned that it was very inconvenient to be told this late that my reservation was being cancelled, the answer was less than satisfactory. In short she didn't care and offered a hollow apology and that was that. 
I understand that they are a business and if someone is offering to buy out the whole place they won't say no. But at the end this business is focused towards customer service so if you're causing a potential customer an inconvenience, try to offer solutions and if you can't then maybe an incentive to remain a customer.

The food was absolutely amazing.  The taste, variety, and presentation were exquisite. This is a good choice if you are looking for something different than typical menus.  We had duck and bison meatloaf. Both were excellent and a different taste than we've experienced before. The atmosphere was very nice - mixture of industrial warehouse and arts. The only reason for not rating a 5 was due to the waiter forgetting to take our order after appetizers and also promising to refill our drinks 2x and forgetting. We reminded him the 3rd time and he brought it right out.  He may have just been having a bad night and all of the other waiters looked to be very engaged and attentive to their customers.  I highly recommend this restaurant for any occasion.
16tjKOvMw_nOgNViq2LwOQ:
I'm a fan! I was pleasantly surprised by this touristy spot. The chicken delivers, and the sides are great. I enjoyed the pimento mac 'n cheese but would probably trade my black eyed pea salad for cole slaw next time. 

On the negative side, the wait can be long. We arrived soon after it opened and only waited in line for a few minutes. I'm not sure I'd wait much longer. Once ordered, the food comes out quickly. Due to all the people, it makes for a pretty chaotic dining out experience, which gives me anxiety.

I used the restroom here, and it was pretty gross. Considering they had only been open an hour or so at that point, management should take more time on this. I would've mentioned it to a staffer, but they seemed totally slammed by the lunch rush.

"Shut the Cluck UP!" That's their saying =)  Woe 50 people deep in line and I queue up.  Some are locals with work badges trying to get some lunch. Others are taking selfies at every corner and some are nice retired kin folk offering me tips and trivia about the place. I met some real nice folks in line. The line moved quick and I didn't see the menu until I was at the register.  The worker never rushed me and said "thanks for making it to Hattie B's." He knew I wasn't from there.  The line grew longer and the crowds were packing in but the attendants were kind and kept asking the kitchen if my order was ready.  Good job buddy! This is just another reason why I love Nashville.  Nice people
     So I ordered 3 wings and you can choose what level of "Hot" you want. Southern which is no heat to Damn Hot!  I just went with Mild, a touch of burn.  It was just enough spice for me.  My sides were Red potatoes and some Southern Greens. It comes with slice of bread and pickles.  The bread to calm the burn I'm sure.
     There's tables and picnic benches inside with lots of window lighting.  The guy behind me ordered up the Damn Hot and two beers and he was sweating like he ran a marathon.  We just looked at each other and just started laughing.  If you come to Nashville try Hattie B's.  Don't forget to take a bunch of pics by the truck out front.

Can't compare to other Hot Chicken but this was very, very good. As I took a minute to check out the menu the cute girl behind the counter reminded me "we've got chicken". Keep it simple and it will be good. Just have to decide how hot you like it.
HvBLY3-R1KxocZapeAAvZQ: Everything you've heard is 100% true! The management and staff are all AMAZING! There is not a single thing that isn't impeccable from the music playlist to the handcrafted cocktails, food and of course the wine selection! While visiting Nashville we enjoyed dinner and brunch...the mimosa bar was AMAZE BALLS!

We had an amazing experience at Barcelona . Our server was Rylie was so knowledgeable  and helpful on the menu among guiding us threw some cool spots in Nashville. She also went above and beyond for our experience which as visitors we can appreciate. We bonded over her Love for Chicago and also good food but most of all her southern hospitality. Thank you Rylie for making our experience at Barcelona amazing! My husband and I loved all of our dishes. It's an upbeat friendly /mingling kind of place with loud music . We wanted a more up crowd and  this was perfect but for a quite dinner date this maybe to much. I would recommend it to anyone local or visiting if your wanting a local place.  Check out the crockets/bacon wrapped dates/ olives/steak with truffle oil and I'm sure many more. The empanadas where something to skip but that's because I've had many in great tapas restaurants and the flavors weren't  vibrant enough for me . They werent bad but there were so many other things ok their menu that I would skip those. Hope you guys enjoy this place.
Probably my favorite Nashville spot. Wonderful atmosphere, great music, and amazing food!
XqSLir6xs3I6ntf-xIQzrQ:
An expensive plop of food served unceremoniously over ridiculous looking plates; there's an undeniable pride, enthusiasm and joy in the labor and love that went into this food. 

The people are wonderful, pizzas magical, deserts delightful. 

Beard award or not, I hate that I love this place.

Perfect dinner last night at City House. Our server was awesome - funny, helpful, engaging and super nice. I also loved how the restaurant was bright and open a we could see the fire oven from our seats.

We started with the scrapple dish + watermelon. Having lived in Philly, this wasn't the first time we had scrapple - but it was by far THE best scrapple I have ever had. The watermelon and mint that made up the dish was perfect - I don't know how or who came up with this combo but it was incredible. 

The belly ham pizza was also great, as everyone else has said. The cheese on it was especially good! We also got the baked grits - a bit too tomato-y for my personal taste, but the baked ricotta on it has a great smoky flavor. 

We finished with the pecan pavlova with chocolate mousse and berry compote and seriously KILLED it. Awesome flavor and textures all together.

Arrived to Nashville after epic, mostly-non-stop drive down from DC. 11 hours later, dinner at the City House. It's firmly ensconced in a hipster neighborhood surrounded by new condo developments emerging from the wreckage of an industrial shipping past. Nate greeted us and showed us the menu comprised of ingenious mixed drinks, oven fired pizza and assortment of appetizers and main dishes. We settled for the much-lauded belly ham pizza with an egg cracked over it. Yes, a raw egg. It was good, though the raw egg addition may not be for everyone. Later, we sort of felt a little pushed to order more, though I think one of the pizza may satisfy two. Eventually relented and ordered the ham and another pizza (margherita). We could always take it home,.... which we did. I get that a place wants you to order more, that's business. Just feels a little pushy.  I'm an adult. I know how to ask for more if that's what I want. But aside from that, the atmosphere is trendy, friendly and pretty unique. I get the sense that maybe the servers are instructed to pitch the add-ons....just like everywhere else I suppose. But it just felt...kinda insistent. Wish they'd let the great food speak for itself. It's good. People will come. We did! 

Don't sour your patrons with the pressure sell.

Oh, despite all that, we really wanted to try the desert.... It was the icebox cookie cream cake. We demolished it. Again, man, the food speaks for itself.
TcUGGwfWNrOovDNPoYqlJw:
I was surprised there was only 1 review for this place and that it seemed to paint this place as mediocre.  I disagree.  There are 4 Mexican restaurants within 3 miles of me and this is the only one I go to.

Ok - this is not a Rick Bayless restaurant but the quality of the food is always good.  You will find a pretty standard "Americanized" Mexican menu with lots of burritos, enchiladas, beans and rice. My favorite dishes are the carnitas and chori pollo. Again, we're not talking about James Beard award winning food but it is simple food with fresh ingredients well done.  I've been eating here for years and I have been happy with every meal.

Finally, it is worth mentioning that the family that runs this restaurant is just great. They are the main reason that I keep coming back. It is just great how they constantly show appreciation to their customers.  I have no hesitation recommending this restaurant.

Excellent little place for Mexican food. We needed a place to eat at after finding out another restaurant had a long w Friendly staff, clean restaurant, delicious food -- great place if you're driving through and need a quick bite to eat or hang out with a group of people.
Myself and family dine here frequently. Food is always excellent. I love the shrimp fajitas. My daughter loves to soft chicken tacos. My son loves the taco salad. BTW service is excellent.
G8I9weN2VpCAHs2UEmwf2g: Great place to eat, pizza was awesome all New York style so nice and this with great cheese and made in a real pizza oven. Everything smells good and all the food is fresh and made right in front of you. The whole time they are running around and yelling and I am standing there thinking oh man I better have my order right or it will be "no pizza for you"!.. go you will not regret it only open during daytime hours Monday thru Friday..

Pizza is the best here.  Double slice pizzas are awesome.  Gladiator had a hidden layer of pepporoni, topped with sausage and mozarella.  Cheeseheads unite!  

Lasagna also looked really good, eggplant parm was tempting.  I splurged and got the tiramisu which was outstanding!

The NY style pizzas looked really good, the cheese was a golden brown all over, describing this makes me drool.  So if pizza was ever your favorite food http://www.youtube.com/watch?v=P3dSvjJpyhg

The food was fantastic. I can't wait to go back and try more. I had a slice of their pepperoni pizza, a slice their 'gladiator pizza', and a salad. You'll need a real appetite for the gladiator and it was delicious. Loved the salad; lots of oregano and olives. The pepperoni pizza was classic pizzeria. It's exactly why you go to an Italian New York pizzeria. It's what you don't find at the pizza chains.
 What I was most blown away by was the customer service though. Some people just have a gift for it, a certain flair. 
The two ladies working the counter today both have it.
pWpi_cCH8IxwWy7pRmtq2w:
My husband and I just ate at Nomzilla for the first time and loved it! There was a groupon available so we decided to try something new. The employees were very friendly and helpful in making our selections, and the food came out fast.

We both had the crunchy shrimp roll, he also had the spicy tuna but substituted the pineapple for avocado. Both were very tasty and fresh. I also tried a "build it yourself" option and made a rice bowl which was great.  

We also both thought the decor was cool and different then the typical sushi place.

I've been here twice, and I'm about to go for my third time tonight. A friend told me she heard this place was terrible, and I logged on to Yelp because I feel the need to defend this local eatery. Let me say a few things about Nomzilla:

1. Before you come here, you should know that this is a make-your-own-sushi kind of place. If you don't know what you like, Thet can offer up some tasty suggestions based on your preferences. In my own experience, I told him I have a taste for spiciness and am not vegetarian. He then whipped up an awesome grilled duck and asparagus roll with sriracha for me that was unique, tasty, and HUGE. So if you're not receptive to trying new things, then maybe stick to a classic sushi joint like Samurai. All I'm saying is don't hate the player cause you hate the game!

2. Yes, the paint job in Nomzilla is BRIGHT. Yes, the presentation of your food is simple and inelegant. Doesn't feel like your typical Japanese restaurant when you walk in. If you care about your visual perception during a dining experience, okay skip Nomzilla for now. It's suitable for casual eats. Personally, I don't eat with my eyes so I could care less about the decor or the presentation.

3. There are complaints about the price such as "a rainbow roll is $11". Okay, where can one find a place to get a rainbow roll for significantly cheaper than $10? If you know, then PREACH IT sista sis cause you know I haven't found it. Like I already mentioned, their rolls are huge. When I have two rolls here, I am actually stuffed... that never happens when I go out for a night of sushi. 

4. The ingredients in the sushi all tasted fresh, from the veggies to the fish to the rice. I can't emphasize enough how good that duck was, and I'm a Chinese girl from the west coast who has enjoyed more than her fair share of Beijing grilled duck. If this is what "grocery store sushi" tastes like, then someone needs to tell me where they're getting their groceries because apparently I'm not shopping at the right places.

5. Finally, I find complaints about the temperature of the ingredients to be borderline offensive -- on the contrary, I'd like to thank Nomzilla for keeping me from contracting some terrible disease by keeping their ingredients at the appropriately cold temperature before serving it to me.


Alright I'm done. Nom time.

Great sushi, a lot of big fat pieces of salmon, crab and all these other things you want in your sushi. It's not the cheapest place (I'm not meaning it's expensive, either, just not the cheapest out there) but certainly very good and you get value for your money. I like the funky design as well
fkwnW_Ht2Rw9shAC7tTVCg: After an exhaustive search of hotels downtown for a bachelorette party, I was finally able to secure two rooms for an affordable price at the Omni. Upon making my reservation via phone, which I never do but had to in order to get the Hotels.com discount, I arrived to find out that the wrong date had been used to make my reservation--we were trying to check in a day earlier than our reservation. Jacob, the staff member assisting me, was extremely professional and worked with the manager to change my reservation on the spot, which was a huge sigh of relief. They even let us check into one of the rooms early so that we could decorate before the bachelorette's arrival. I was so impressed with the hospitality and accommodations at the Omni, and if I ever find myself needing a room downtown in the future, I will look no further. Such a beautiful hotel and a great experience!

Lovely hotel. Brand new, clean, friendly staff (with the exception of some staff in Bongo Java). My husband told them it was our anniversary and they upgraded us to a lovely suite and put champagne and chocolate covered strawberries in our room. They went above and beyond to help us and other guests and it was wonderful! Just a few issues...

1) very thin walls/glass. We were on the 19th floor and we could hear trains, motorcycles, music from the lobby bar, etc. Also, we could hear people getting off the elevator and the people in the room next to us had a baby (we have kids, so we get it) but the baby cried from 3:30 am to 5:00 am and we could hear every moment of it. I felt so bad for the child and parents, but I also wished the walls were thicker so that we weren't listening to every moment of the meltdown.

2) the pool is not nearly big enough to accommodate the number of guests the hotel holds. People were laying in the deck, waiting in line for 30 minutes for a drink, servers were slammed and ignoring guests because they literally couldn't handle the crowds.

All in all it was a nice hotel and we would go back. We were  very excited to be taken care of  and treated nicely for our anniversary and that made it more than worth it!!

This hotel deserves 5 stars with no doubt. I had a great king bed room with zero problems. The other people I traveled with had minor issues such as a broken refrigerator or a shower issue but the hotel promptly fixed it. Not only did they fix it but they also provided a nice fruit basket as a courtesy for the inconvenience. 

Location wise is great. You are short walk from downtown where all of the restaurants and live music are located. There are also awesome restaurants within the hotel if you are feeling like staying in.

Lastly, their gym provides you with everything you need. I would definitely suggest this hotel if you are in the area.
3CFVBCfjdCvESS1ogBv21A:
This pizza is delicious! I tend to be a reserved critic so I'll say this pizza is "pretty hard to beat." 
Their seating is unusual (which I enjoyed): long open tables (meant for sharing) in the same "room" as the kitchen (so you can watch them making the pizzas). 
You won't find the price as cheap as some chain pizza places, but what they make here is worth what they charge. Also, the location is a wee bit inconvenient, and I could see their parking lot filling up quickly, so here's to hoping they open multiple locations soon b/c everyone should try this pizza!

I want to say thank you to not only amazing pizza that brings me back to my childhood but also to the amazing staff and pizza chefs. Pizza chef Aaron was so nice to my 7 year old son Isaac and gave him a small piece of dough to roll out and play with while he tossed pizza in the air. He sat and talked while he worked and had amazing patience for my little guy that was asking question after question. Thanks again desano and to Aaron for making our family night such a great atmosphere to be in. Awesome pizza !!!'
Ordered a large pizza for take out. We had the San Gennaro which was so delicious we finished the whole thing!! It was spicy with a touch of sweet - a ton of flavor!
Mm9zXIgMQMzgDt4GShSWrw:
Great for a fancy night out, no complaints here. However, there are so many other places in Nashville that are more enjoyable and make for a better, slightly less expensive meal
.

Went for a birthday dinner. The food was amazing - the service was not so good. Started out with the vadalia soup - creamy, perfectly seasoned, and great texture. Then had the filet with truffle mac & cheese and roasted fingerling potatoes. We ended up ordering a 2nd mac & cheese because it was simply that good. The waiter was new and messed up a few thing (no forks on table, forgot drinks, messed up my husbands dish by forgetting no pork) but overall very good food made up for not good service.

I'm an unrepentant food snob.  Strike that, I'm a food bastard.

I read reviews for chain restaurants, and just think that people that suffer that excrement are assholes.  One of the current presidents of our organization used to be an executive for Chilis; the stories would terrify you...  

I recently bumped into a fellow who is a server at Capitol Grille, and he was simply a pretentious bastard.  Really made me think less of the place, because frankly I would assume I've had better experiences from an amuse-bouche at other places than that kid had ever had in his life.

Ahem.  So anywho, the wife got a major promotion, so I decided to take her out for a nice lunch.

This place hit on all cylinders from the beginning.  Fantastic, attentive service.  An older gentleman, cursing that I didn't remember his name - just a hoot of a server.

Started with amazing bread, with fresh butter kissed by fleur de sel.  I'm not sure where it all went, but it wasn't on our table long.  The wife and I started off with their onion soup.

It is an onion soup that truly retains the delightful characteristics of sweet onions, they pour that over a small brie sandwich and there is some Benton's bacon in the mix as well (the wife eschewed that, as she is a veg-head).  Pure heaven for both of us - truly hit the spot.

I had a gin cocktail - truly hit the spot.  For the main course, I had a sandwich with their house-made pastrami - better than the home-made stuff I've had - absolutely exquisite!  The kraut and mustard were on point as well.  The wife had a salad that made her afternoon, and of course she snagged some of my frites.

So, on the strength of an odd-houred lunch alone, I'd say this place can't fail, and again, kudos to our server!
8WNQxb0CiROek9SW7PopDQ: I was very pleased I had prime rib and potatoes and spinach and it was great. They had so much to offer I'm going to definitely go back. I've had the same entree three times and they are consistently great. The service isn't spectacular but I go for the food and sometimes I carry out.

City fire! A great place for bottomless mimosas and a happy hour on the weekend! I've been here twice and had reservations each time... And each time I had to wait over 20mins to be seated. So that was a bummer because I actually like the food. The shrimp and crab nachos were amazing and the shrimp and grits were great! 

The free 2 hour parking is also a plus being that you usually have to to pay for parking in the gulch!

An often forgotten about gem! 

We've eaten here a couple of times now and found ourselves pleasantly surprised. Food is yummy and price tags are reasonable.

Drink specials and resident discounts make this place quite inviting for icon peeps. 

My only real complaint is that I feel they desperately need a lunch menu. I feel like everything is a little too "heavy" for anything but dinner. 

Everything we've tried so far is pretty tasty. It's the second best Hot Chicken in the gulch (Party Fowl two blocks away being #1 if you have two hours on your hands to wait on your food).

My usual order; Hot Chicken or Jerk Chicken!
e_MDOxwYA6b8XThonDDI_g: Came here today for the first time. Ordered a cheese pizza and a pepperoni, sausage, and mushroom pizza. It was all very good!!! I ate a pizza almost all by myself!! The owner came out and was very friendly. We will be back.

Came here for lunch while exploring East Nashville with some friends. I was beyond impressed! The food was so incredibly fresh and the owner very personable. It is perfect if you're looking for delicious, fresh food in a casual and relaxed atmosphere. Plus I loved the decor, all the local art on the walls really made it feel quaint and homey. There wasn't much traffic inside which is a shame because this was some of the best quality pizza I've had in a long time. I would highly recommend!

As a native Chicagoan I have to say the pizza is on point. It's like a cross between Chicago deep dish and a New York slice - my friend and I were pleasantly surprised! We tried pizza from a few places nearby and were not impressed.

The restaurant itself is cozy yet spacious, and it feels like you're in someone's well maintained home. The owner is very friendly and inviting. He is also quite knowledgeable of Nashville so it was a bonus chatting with him as an out-of-towner. 

I highly recommend swinging by after some shopping in the nearby area or after a couple of cocktails from the Saloon across the street.
czJM3pm_cAbz8Ohr7vGtJQ: We have eaten here about three times over the summer. We enjoy the food and of course the kids and myself enjoy the games. I have had several different meals and the $10 game card with the meal is a great deal if you are going to play, and the $24.99 dinner and movie combo is pretty good deal also, Highly recommend if your in the area...check it out...CASINO for KIDS....lol!!!!

Love this place. Awesome food and drinks. Kids love the giant arcade. Every time we are close to a Dave n Busters it's guaranteed we'll eat there. HIGHLY recommended!! For adults and kids or just adults.

My husband had his team Christmas party at Dave and Busters and it was great. The manager took really good care of us, including being very proactive to keep our reservation in a tidy folder and greet us with all the information we needed. ---- even though they were completely slammed and had a huge private party coming in later. He checked in with us a few times and gave us a phenomenal server. We had platters delivered right to our bowling lanes and everything looked and felt very clean and not yucky like regular bowling alleys. The arcade was fun too. Plan a party there, you won't be disappointed.
8jwsNjvVNJiYN7AnxHdMLA: Amazingly wonderful texture and flavor in the kolache dough. The fillings are all great. The "Breakfast Kolache" (I think that's the name) tastes like a gourmet, scratch-made version of Hot Pockets®. I hope this place stays around for a long time!
Five stars for the vibe, four for the food. The "above average cinnamon roll" was basic dough drenched in gobs of white frosting. But still, really cool atmosphere, nice staff:)

I first tasted Yeast Nashville before they opened and was impressed by their Yazoo Jalapeno bread. When I saw that they had opened their physical location, I had to stop by. The experience was by no means unpleasant, but I did notice that my sausage and cheese kolache had generic American cheese. It was also warmed up in a microwave prior to being served. That's probably why the prices were so reasonable!

My hope is that the cost-cutting measures they appear to be taking will disappear in favor of higher quality ingredients and better warming methods as they become more and more popular.
-qNVtEd8Z9MtCfC5dii6nQ: We decided to eat here because of the healthier option of an omelette and fruit, and the proximity to our hotel and we were delightfully surprised at the options the menu had to offer. On our table alone, there was excellent coffee, super fresh squeezed orange juice, Huevos Rancheros that my husband said was the closest green Chile he'd tasted since we lived in CO, the kid had the tall strawberry stack (pancakes) which was gorgeous. I had the surfer girl omelet, which was phenomenal. What really got me were the gluten free options: of a piece of toast or a gf pancake! This foodie was absolutely giddy to be at a restaurant that caters to the gluten issue. This was a real treat for me. The ambiance was adorable, prices were decent and the place relatively clean. I liked the simple, clean decor. It was charming and homey.  Our server "Cathy" was a charming, sassy and attentive. We will absolutely be back!

We were greeted at the door by a smiling hostess that made us feel welcome from the time we stepped foot into the door. Once seated, we began reviewing the menu and began with a latte from the coffee bar. Yum-sers! On to the food.  The wealth of choices made you want to order everything!  Our knowledgeable waitress kindly offered suggestions, however. I landed on the steak and egg with a side of the grits of the day and French toast. All palate-pleasing choices!  I can't wait to return to try out other menu choices!
Great food! So many options to choose from, the menu was huge! Our waitress was so pleasant and friendly. I had a mimosa and it was amazing. Orange juice was so fresh. Overall great experience.
FGGDERcsTDYt6uLHPWEKrw: Martin's BBQ catered for my wedding reception and it was absolutely amazing! Even the communication to get the menu right was done smoothly. They are quick to contact you back my email and phone. I got exactly what I wanted. The prices are great! We had a guest list of about 130 people. I highly recommend them!

First time here. Staff was nice and hospitable. Was in town visiting bro-in-law. He picked this place and I was not disappointed. Ordered brisket and pork ribs; half dry, half wet. And even got to taste the chicken wings. I enjoyed everything. The wings were fresh and crispy and had a lot of smoked flavor. The ribs had a good smoked flavor and were tender and so was the brisket. The sauces were good. One was a lil too hot for my tastes for BBQ though.  Really enjoyed the mustardy, vinegary, and slightly sweet sauces. Will definitely go back next time I visit.

This is probably one of the best places where I've had pulled pork! My best friend and I ordered the BBQ taco and half rack of ribs. The BBQ taco has a base layer of a corn pancake and is topped with their cole slaw. We had fries, broccoli slaw and Mac and cheese for sides. The broccoli slaw was memorable! The Mac and cheese was mediocre. The fries were perfectly sized and seasoned. The meat on the ribs were falling off the bone!
sn4-BbWfsdQ4UVukfRl2Nw: Came from California to visit this plantation. I've visited few in the past and I have to say the guide was pretty good in explaining about the owner and about the mansion itself. Wine tasting was also very good. My two boys enjoyed and thought they learned a lot from this experience.

Very fun tour and wine tasting. This was worth every penny! Amy gave a perfect tour w/ lots of fun facts. The wine tasting can be done for $10 if you don't want to tour the mansion again. We had low expectations of the wine, but loved all of them and left w/ 2 bottles. 
Loved being a tourist in our hometown.

What an awesome experience! This place is a must see if your in the Nashville, TN area. 

My friends and I came here after looking into fun places to experience in the Nashville area. In our case, we looked online, saw that they did tours...and wine tastings....and decided on heading here. 

The tour was exceptional! Our guide was amazing and made the experience beyond fun. A few interesting facts about this plantation include: 

1. All the winners of the Kentucky Derby are decedents of one horse that came from this farm. Pretty cool right?!
2. The civil war was in the front yard of this plantation. Literally!
3. And one rather daring daughter tested whether her diamond was real by scratching it on the window. Check it out when your there. 

Beyond that, the winery was amazing! Unfortunately we weren't able to sit outside and enjoy our wine, since it was raining pretty heavily the day we arrived, however we all bought bottles and had them shipped to our homes.  

As for the wine tasting, I want to warn those going just for the wine. While there is a wine tasting, it is a limited tasting. They only allow you to try 3 or 4 wines total. This wouldn't have been an issue for us the day we arrived, we would have just bought bottles and shared, however due to the rain we really couldn't. There is no space in their wine room to just sit and enjoy.
6vLzKfms128nygOffzdXnQ: This review is really written by my 2 year old toddler.  It's his favorite restaurant when he is really hungry, the word "buffet" comes out and we have to take him here.  He then eats about three plates of yellow fried rice, bbq chicken, and the lo mein.  It has a great international selection (not just Chinese), which includes plantains, fresh seafood,  whole fried fish, bbq meat, and just everything you can almost think of.  What I love about it is you always get seated right away, no hassle.  The servers are prompt, sometimes friendly, but always busy trying to clean or pick up your empty plate.  It's one of the largest buffet I've seen.  I like that it is on Nolensville Rd, which I call the international rainbow road.

This is by far the best Chinese buffet we have ever experienced - and we have been to quite a few.  Stopped here while traveling through Nashville.  The decor was very nice, the selection incredible (including sushi), and the quality was top-notch.  The dinner price ($10) was a very pleasant surprise to boot.
Best bang for your buck! Their sushi is surprisingly great for a buffet! The hibachi bar is great too and free plus a tip if you choose (and u should) :) We eat here often.
-Nf2K8OmZ3OaApWamQHrbw: This is my go-to when I'm not in the mood to cook and need something quick and easy. The guys who run this place are always so kind and the food is consistent every time. My husband and I have been frequenting this place for years. We get a chicken plate and it's plenty of food for the both of us. Highly recommend!
Incredible food and the price is right.  Huge serving for $7.  Falafel plate is the best I've ever had
This place is awesome. Cheap and filling. Great gyros. They really fill up the gyro with meat and put as much sauce in there as you want. Life is good.
pwrqXRSyuMIb1ysh4WQKpw: So came to Nashville with the wife , she likes country music I wanted hot chicken needless to say we both scored , 400 degrees hot chicken was great I had the 200 and it was perfectly Cooke , spiced  and served sides were awesome , get the citrus sweet tea , loved it with the chicken , perfect , perfect and perfect

200° their medium heat chicken is right on par. my nose didn't run like the menu said but equally tasty. beans where on the sugary side but it was a nice complement to the heat from the chicken. byob gotta love the combo. friendliest service that calls you out by name multiple times makes sure you don't need anything, I'll be back.

While 400 Degrees was miles better than Boltons to me, there's just not beating Hattie B's and Prince's. They both represent the two versions of Nashville hot chicken the best. I'd put 400 Degrees at a solid #3, and considering Aqui's humble beginnings that's pretty impressive. Aqui (owner) was cooking that day and was all too sweet, friendly, and welcoming in a way that only an old friend could be. That's something you will NOT get at any other hot chicken joint. 

Key here is to call ahead (if you can get through), and if not clear your afternoon because it's going to be 45m-1hr as its going to be made fresh just for you. Not getting that any where else either. 

400 degrees was a sloooooow burn. Ate a whole wing before I was writing my will. 200 degrees is the right amount of burn. Don't bother with 100 or 0. Why are you even going to get hot chicken??? Legend has it there's an 800...who are you that just got excited. You are not human. 

Go get some 400 Degrees next time you're near downtown, and bring some water to cut the sweet tea with :)
UTg2aZ9zXLK8_0K-UnCVUA: We visited on a Monday night. Moderately busy. Our server Sara was extremely friendly and helpful. Good selection of brews. Highly recommend Shrimp and Grits. Shrimp and lobster enchiladas were great. A must stop in Nashville. Good place to watch people on one of the busiest corners in the city.
Great atmosphere and service. Mac & Cheese and their IPA was awesome. If you're in Nashville try this place- you won't be disappointed.  Sat at the bar.

This.place deserves more than 3 stars! Just finished the quesadilla's and will start with the Avacado chicken signature sandwich now. Service is good and quick. And they serve Kolsch beer! They know their beers! Well done!
Not 5 stars because this place has no wifi! Please guys, enter ,the 21st century! Takes money out of my pocket to post this as I have an international cellphone......
zkevOUm1kDU7PUKte4GKpQ:
A slice of the Pacific Northwest in East Nashville, from the coffee culture, to the ambience, to the tattooed lumberjack hipster fashion show of most of the people in the room, I felt like I was back in SE Portland.  That is to say, this place is awesome.

These guys know quality coffee, featuring premier artisan roasters from around the country, and their pour-over preparation lets the flavor profile shine.  The bean prices are a little steep for the average hipster budget, but their celebrity cast of beans are flying in fresh from around the country.  BP - how 'bout starting to roast your own?

To summarize BP in a few words:

Quality coffee. Hipster baristas. Motorcycles. Local produce. Artisan. 

Yup, it's hipster city. However, I don't care. The laid back atmosphere and the attention to detail (in the coffee, food, and aesthetic of the place) is enough for me to want to come back.  I am no coffee connoisseur, but that Salty Whiskey Caramel Latte (try it iced!) is so dang good that even if this place sucked all around I would still go back for it. 

The reason your coffee takes a while to be made: quality. Their food isn't terribly complicated to make which is why it comes out faster. Its mostly local and very fresh. As long as you have an appreciation for details, you'll be satisfied here. 

However I do agree with another reviewer on here who I think mentioned the chairs/stools. They have no back, which can be a little harsh when you're sitting there for awhile either doing work on your laptop or catching up with a friend. 

Also -- if you love documenting cool artisan places, I don't think you can get one bad angle trying to take a picture of this place -- it's perfectly decorated and put together.

The coffee here is A++. I don't remember the name of the brew, but it was damn good. The menu can be a bit intimidating, but I went with the barista's recommendation and it was completely on point. This repair shop-turned-coffee place is housed in an old garage, and is rustic and modern at the same time. It's extremely spacious, but still has a cozy characteristic to it. I love that it's not cramped like a lot of the ones back in NYC. The garage doors were all open, and with the rainy Nashville morning that day, it was the perfect setting with the perfect cup (or shall I say two or three cups) of black coffee. I probably could've sat there all day.

The only reason I'm giving it 4 stars instead of 5 is because their breakfast biscuit sandwiches were so heavy. Overpriced, and just brimming with grease and too much cheese. It was quite messy and I got over it after two bites.
Fyudi29jdmSZyM_F9Txg_g: This place is /amazing/. It's affordable, the service is personable but fast (there was a long line in front of me and I was still in and out in less than 10 minutes), the food is delicious. The gyros and chicken sandwiches are served in a fluffy pocket pita and are stuffed full of meat and veggies. The fries are soft (not my favorite), but they're served warm with a delicious seasoning salt, so it's still worth it. This place is my favorite Bellevue stop for a working lunch; between the friendly service and the yummy food, it always makes a busy work day better!
Always wonderful! The food is fresh and fast and all the people who work there are super friendly . Especially Amil. He always sings a song for us and makes my kids laugh with his great humor.

Sam's Kabab is one of the only places in Bellevue that can give you way more than what you pay for!  Go there for lunch(between 11am-1pm) and you can get a gyro sandwich, fries or rice, and a drink for $5. The sandwiches are HUGE and they mix everything together inside the pita so you get a taste of it all with every bite :) The owner is always happy and remembers what you like.  

Downsides: Parking is a nightmare in the shopping center.  Not nearly enough spots, in which case you may have to park at the McDonalds or Hardee's and walk over.  There is one employee there who is seriously more interested in what's on TV than fixing your meal. I'm not kidding, he once stopped mid-way through making my gyro to watch part of a soccer game. Finally, the tables and bench seating rarely get wiped down. The wood on the booths always feel sticky and the tables always have crumbs from the previous customer. I've been here many, many times with a packed restaurant or just us and it's the same every time. That is probably my biggest complaint.
p0mxscqr7BgMR-I4pGM3Kg:
Workers here are the cutest ever! 
But other than their appearance, if you've read my past reviews you'll know I need atmosphere and this place does not disappoint! It's just the cutest thing ever!!

Very clean, open cute house feel! The gelato is beyond this word... Seriously! :) 
So all n all, a must visit!

Everything a gelato place should be. The young lady was very friendly and recommended other places to visit in Nashville. The dark chocolate gelato was the best I've ever had. It was rich and flavorful. I tried the coconut also. Parking is difficult here so my husband had to do the drop me off and circle the block plan. It is delicious and worth the aggravation on no good parking.

Great stop for a coffee or Ice cream or even a shake!

Pros 
Espressos, lattes, coffees
Hot and cold 
Fresh cookies
Honey in a jar 
Great decor 
Outdoor and indoor seating 
Good for small groups 
Samples!
Pints to go 
Portion cups 


Cons
More flavors would be great!


Really nice stop after having a meal. Great for the sweet tooth. They have healthy flavors too like green tea.
CTzHghpa1I5CDCtfHtTY4Q:
What a great bar! We were new to Bellevue and this place is the best. Great prices and always good service. Lots of TVs and a good crowd.

Beer is cheap - that's a winner. But all that aside - this unassuming bar attached to a Kroger center and a liquor store has some of the best food in the area. I got steak and biscuits and it is making my mouth water just writing the review update. Go here - don't get suckered into going to publicity.

Looking for a good place to eat with the kids, and it worked great.  Wife had spaghetti with meat sauce...huge portion and it was pretty good.  I had the catfish special and it was definitely a good deal.  Kids meals had good options as well.  Will be back
It's like a sports bar opened inside this mom & pop's meat and three. A neighborhood joint with good food.
bZcW_CPll-WaAgciSLPyrA: A great, quaint spot with excellent food. The tuna sandwich is scrumptious and I had a cinnamon sugar doughnut filled with a homemade vanilla filling. I don't even like doughnuts with filling, but tried it because the guy helping me highly recommended it. Like he suggested, it was unbelievably good! My only problem was the parking. The only parking I saw nearby was a lot that charged $24.00?! I decided to run into the bakery and order to go in order to avoid this crazy high charge. When I mentioned it to the people taking my order they recommended I go to the car and move it. He said the parking lot is highly monitored and that I could get a bid on it immediately. Sure enough when I got back to my car there was a guy walking around checking all of the cars. They were so kind as I was with my differently angled daughter (who doesn't walk) to actually meet me out front to get my order, but the poor parking options is a deterrent for sure and the only reason I can't give this place 5 stars!
Food is fresher than fresh and staff is super friendly and helpful.  I wish their were some more selections for lunch and some more kid selections would be nice too.   Otherwise, a great place!

I don't usually do yelp reviews but the food is so exceptional I had to. We woke up late so wondered around a little till we saw D'Andrews Bakery. We didn't know we walked into what would be the best food we had while we visited Nashville. We had the breakfast sandwiches. I had the bacon and my husband had the sausage. The homemade buns were delicious. I looked around to see if I could buy and take home a dozen. Even the mayo is homemade. After that I had to try his pastries. I wanted to buy one of everything but limited it to 3. Each one maybe better than the last. If you go you won't be disappointed.
hWiXoJFDZrzPY33RjG8yeA:
OK, here is my opinion of Provence. The food is good but not earth shattering. The coffee is good but not earth shattering. The desserts are pretty darn good so I really have to give them props there. However, their crowning jewel is the bread. Their cheese selection is yummy too. 
I am someone who can take a loaf of crusty bread and some cheese make a meal out of it. I think opinions about this place will all be in how or what you order. 

The other thing to bear in mind is that you can buy their bread in the local Kroegers as well as most of the health food stores. I personally LOVE the Tuscan. It's great for sandwiches, dipping in Olive Oil, and toasted. There are some loaves you can't get in stores including a fruit and nut one that my hubby loves.  Visiting this lovely restaurant and shop is worth it, there will definitely be something there you will make a staple.
Their latte menu is creative and delicious! My latte was on point. Friendly staff, cute atmosphere, and delicious bread.
Our favorite breakfast and coffee spot in town. Staff is AMAZING and food is always consistent and on point. LOVE this restaurant.
OIMcX4bwh_ue9Tz3I2l7dQ: This place has the best sausage in town. Great flavor and a crisp skin. The rest of the menu is good too. The singer on Friday nights, sets a good atmosphere. The beer is cold. I recommend stopping by this place for the food and atmosphere. Get a combo plate so you can try it all!

Best brisket I've had east of the Mississippi!  Great smoked sausage too.  Smoked to perfection and just the right amount of spice rubbed on.  I didn't even touch the sauce... and that's a very good thing.  Don't miss the cowboy beans either.  Could have done without the Longhorns gear on the walls ;-) Boomer Sooner!

I am a native Texan, so trust me when I say I know what real, authentic Texan cuisine is supposed to taste like.  And this is it.  The Judge is an Austinite who knows what Texan fare is all about.  I even talked to him.  He's super friendly, just like most Texans.  Anyway, the food is mind blowing.  Seriously...until I came to the Judge's Vinegarroon, I had no morsel of a clue that such REAL Texan food was available here in the Volunteer State.  I ordered some barbecue tacos.  Yes, you read correctly.  BARBECUE + TACOS = MOUTHGASM.  Two Texan dishes combined perfectly to make...well...the perfect Texan megadish!  Now, there were three different kinds of tacos you could get.  Brisket, barbecue chicken, and catfish.  I got one of each, and every single one was to die for.  The brisket was my favorite (not surprising, considering that BBQ brisket is just about my favorite food of all time).  The chicken was awesome too.  It had that great, smoky, Texan taste to it just like the brisket.  And the catfish was a very pleasant surprise!  It had coleslaw on it!  Yes, coleslaw!  As soon as I bit into it, I started hearing in my head, "I like catfish cookin' on a creek bank, kind you can't find in the city, y'all!"*  Surprisingly, it was even good with the barbecue sauce!

Speaking of barbecue sauce, it was great too!  My favorite was the hot sauce, but I like everything hot.  The hot sauce wasn't that unbearable either.  Even my Tennessean mom liked it.  It had just the right amount of spice to keep things interesting.  Another unique thing about these tacos was the shell.  They were soft shell tacos, but the soft tortillas were corn tortillas!  I'd never had soft corn tortillas before, but they were really good!  They had more toughness and thickness to them than flour tortillas along with more flavor.

I also noticed that there was a full service bar there.  I didn't order any alcohol because for one, I had to go to work right afterwards, and also, I was with my mom, who doesn't drink.  But I was impressed just from looking.  There was beer, liquor, and wine, which is hard to find in Nashville.  Most bars (at least that I go to) either serve just beer and liquor, just beer and wine, or just beer.  I also saw on the menu that they have live country music on Thursday nights at 8:00 PM and $1 PBR's!  You can't get much better than that (except maybe penny Lone Stars**)!

Anyway, I will definitely be visiting this restaurant in the very near future...multiple times!  I may even become a regular!  I'll definitely be taking my girlfriend here, who is also a proud Texan.  I'll have to check out the bar and the music.  Once I do, I just might update this review!

One last thing I'd like to add is that there is a FREE PARKING LOT here, which is an extremely rare occurrence in downtown Nashville.

Overall, I am crazy about this place after the first time, and if I could rate this more than five stars, I would!

*Randy Houser reference
**which by the way, makes me wonder if they do serve Lone Star beer here
_mL3Bf0XwDLKvguY8vWr3w:
I LOVE International Market!  This Thai owned family restaurant has been around 30+ years.  My family used to walk down the street to eat here as a child.  The food is delicious and still reasonably priced in an age where everything seemd to be skyrocketing.  Although their delicious papaya juice is no longer "on tap", you can buy a similar bottled product.  Spicy chicken on a stick, crab wontons, the eggplant, and bamboo shoots are some of my favorite dishes.  You can now even order specialty dishes from the kitchen in addition to ordering from the buffet classics.  For dessert, treat yourself to the white rabbit milk candy, rice candy (comes with a sticker), or a fortune cookie!

The atmosphere here is casual and colorful with art, instruments, and import items displayed on the walls, and available for purchase on shelves lining the entrance way.  Parking could be a problem as the area has become more congested with development over the years.

While I haven't ever really done any shopping here, I've stopped in for a quick lunch on countless occasions, and I must say, it's a very tasty little place.  It's right next door to Belmont (practically on campus), and it's the sort of place that caters to young people with big appetites and not a lot of cash.

I typically just breeze through the buffet when I visit the Market, and they have all the basics: Pad Thai, spicy and sweet and sour chicken, et cetera.  Nothing there is the best I've ever had, but it's all pretty solid.

The staff at International Market is very nice, and because servings are large and prices aren't, it's the type of place that the locals frequent.  I've bought little Asian candies and treats here before, but that's about it as far as the non-restaurant items.  I know quite a few people do actually shop here regularly, and it seems like a great spot to pick up the essentials for your Chinese, Thai and various Asian cuisine concoctions.

I love this place.  Its down the street from my house, so it is very convenient.  The portions they give you are huge and the price tag is small, which makes for a great place to eat when you're on a budget.. and if you're like me and can't eat all the food they serve you, you take home the leftovers and have another meal for the next day.  the veggie rolls are my favorite, especially when craving an egg roll.  They also have a decent little asian market, which is great for when you need some hard to find asian ingredients.  They finally started carrying matcha powder (which is green tea powder) that is a great little ingredient when you want to make an interesting treat (i use it to make green tea cheesecake).  They also have black sesame seeds for a great price (just think of smokey flavored sesame seeds, so good).  I also like to get a pack of the rice candy when checking out..  a nice treat to carry around in my purse.  The staff here is really friendly, so thats another reason to keep going back.  Also, I don't think its on their menu but you can order Thai Iced Tea when checking out..  one of my favorite things to drink (although it can sometimes be a little too sweet).  I don't have a negative thing to say about this place.  I think the owner of International Market also owns PM across the street... so if you're paying attention, you'll notice that employees are always running back and forth across the street to take care of both spots.  I really hope Belmont doesn't run them out... as I've heard rumored before.
cL3_SRjQv6YURfCom5YSGw:
Upscale thin crust pizza/chicken wings with a large selection of craft beers.

My first sentence leaves a a lot to cover.
Pizza: Thin crust personal pizza. Much like Blaze pizza but their toppings are more adult in nature. Put as many ingredients on the pizza as you want and go sit down to get your pizza delivered.
Chicken wings: wide profile of flavors, traditional and boneless. I would have preferred blue cheese dressing over ranch.
Craft Beers: this was new for me. You are given an electric charm that unlocks each tap on a wall of 30 beer/cider/wine taps. Pour as much as you want and taste as many as you want. The price is per ounce. I got to try beers I would never have selected and found some I really liked.

When in the West end, I recommend PizzaRev for a meal or a get together.

Build your own Craft pizza. I thought I be spontaneous and give the pepperonis pizza a break. I had Alfredo sauce on my thin crust pizza with chicken and veggies. It was different. Alfredo sauce could of been a little bit more but it was good quality. Thin crust cooked on stone evenly. The concept like everyone mentioned going down the assembly line and picking your toppings.
Forgot to mention my wings got the green chili wings. Not a fan and not spicy.  

I think the beer tapas what attracts people you get a wrist band synced and you pay by the ounce and you can try a little of different variety of beer. Or go straight to what you like. The gentlemen that help pick my beer was super helpful and funny the same time. I'm not a beer drinking person but I liked the cider beer. Defiantly a memorable dinner over pizza and beer.

My husband works near Vanderbilt and has been singing PizzaRev's praises so I new I had to try it!

We went on a Friday evening and the place was busy but not crowded which is much appreciated.  At times everything in Nashville seems overly crowded with a huge wait!.

Another thing I enjoyed about PizzaRev is everyone gets their own pizza.  I FINALLY get to have what I WANT on my pizza.  Not what my husband wants and not what my daughter wants! 

So here's the setup...  You pick a crust (I believe thin, gluten free, or thick), then you select your sauces, cheeses, and toppings.  After this they place it in a pizza oven and you pay.  They also have a huge beer, cider, and wine wall which you pay by the glass and serve yourself.  I did get a glass of beer which was good and pretty cool to poor my own.
