The only issue is the cost was $10 dollars for the glass!  

Anyway, I really enjoyed PizzaRev and will definitely be back.  If you are going to drink a lot of beers be prepared for an big bill:)
qe5fWtR9u-Pqg1pJBR5G0g:
Big fan of Jack's Bar-B-Que. I'd never been to this location, but in our visit it was what I'd come to expect from the other Jack's BBQ.

On this visit I had the Ribs, coupled with the 9-1-1 sauce I may have found my new favorite thing. 

Decent parking and good seating. I personally recommend this location over the one downtown for ease of access. 

If you are a fan of BBQ I recommend it highly!

Great spot. Family that lives in town said the neighborhood is not the best but who cares. Great store!  Clean, friendly and a great mix of people. 

Holy Hot Links!!! The food is awesome!  We stopped in with 11 of us and walked out the door under $100 and everyone was full plus a huge bag of left overs. We shared 2) family packs and a chicken pack. We had to get another pound of meat and glad we did. The turkey, chicken, pork and beef were all perfect. The sides we well above par and the sweet tea had plenty of sweet, almost chewy!

I wish we had BBQ in Chicago but this will be on the list for next time. I would almost suggest having carry out at the campground.
Love the dry rub ribs at Jacks! Tender, lean, moist ribs with excellent smoke built in. Sides are good, bar b q beans is a go to. Grab a cold beer and a half rack of ribs and you are set for a treat!
WfVEze1lZ4vTs5_UcBXGSg: Love their bakery but wish the deli selections were fresher.

Overall, the best grocery store in town. If you're a regular customer, you will get to know the staff personally, and they have always been friendly and helpful. There are no automated checkout lanes, but the store employees really make the experience worth it. The prices are competitive, and the lines are seldom very long. (Balancing one against the other, this store definitely beats Wal-Mart!)

My neighborhood store, very helpful employees and specials.

Fresh Produce and a good variety of Fresh Meats & Seafood.

Easy in and Out !
xH9Z40Z66Q60vxbB70XiMw:
Tried this place last weekend. I ordered the brisket taco, steak taco ,elote and sangria. The tacos didn't really have any flavor to me, they were both topped with cilantro, onions and Pico de gallo. They were just ok to me. The corn was very good. The sangria was close to my top 3 sangrias Ive had. Very good. 

My fiance' ordered the carne asada taco, chicken taco, rice, chips and queso and 2 $3 shots. The chicken didn't have any flavor but his carne asada taco had a spicy flavor which was good. Their queso is pretty good it had tomatoes in it. The rice was regular nothing done to it to make it different or flavorful. 

The atmosphere was really cute and the staff were very nice. It will be really nice once the weather warms up.

Overall I would try this place again and try something different. Their menu looks good. I think this restaurant is a great addition to the restaurant scene  in Nashville.

Girls night out for Mexican cuisine at the Little Donkey. I had the sparkling margarita which was perfection - 100% recommend this drink - it was not too sweet but hid the taste of alcohol in the different mix of flavors. I had a steak salad which was the perfect size (I was starving and finished the whole thing!) I used the dressing but sparingly because the ingredients were so fresh there was enough flavor without it.

Plenty of free parking, park your ride and come to enjoy some tacos! I personally can't resist their picado chicken salad but the steak tacos are mouth watering. DON'T skip the guac, it's quite possibly the best in Nashville. Also, it's way cheaper than the other trendy Taco joints in town (win win)
71auHh4HQ4U-MFdaajahhQ: Came in with a friend for the first time and the food was amazing. The waitress helped us the moment we walked in and it seemed like our food came out within 5 minutes. Not to mention I definitely enjoyed their Margarita!
Great food! Chips and salsa are endless.. as always. The Chori Pollo and fajitas are amazing! Service is usually pretty good, even on busy nights!
Just traveling through when we found this gem. Awesome service and great food!!!! Who knew we would find great Mexican found in Tennessee.
DF_cgKLV_YMDkBaNlE0XoQ: So just a heads up if you go inside and order and theirs alot of people inside request your order to go you will receive it faster today I waited 45 minutes for a small ham and Swiss sandwich it was good but others who placed their order to go after me were eatting at their table
Decaf Latte? MIND BLOWN: POOF! Seriously I cannot function without lattes and up until today I did NOT know u can get a DECAF LATTE and NOT be awake until 6 a m! Best news ever!

As a visitor to Nashville I had to find Sam & Zoe's and I'm glad I did. It's a small wooden building in an industrial/commercial area. I could see how parking might be an issue on a busy day if you don't like to walk up the street. I ordered a cafe au lait and an everything bagel with cream cheese. The coffee was flavorful and the right temperature. The bagel was fresh and warm with a generous amount of cream cheese. I almost got their yogurt parfait because it looked so good. I recommend trying this little place  - and by the way there is a nice covered porch out back.
xxx_0Ol7lbK0hVzv7x0-Yg:
We've been back to Lucky Bamboo a few times in recent months, and I have to say that we've been more and more impressed with the food each visit.  This is the best, most authentic Chinese food we've found in Nashville so far, whether you order off the menu or try the dim sum weekend brunch.  

I particularly like the shrimp with mixed vegetables, which is served in a flavorful white sauce made with garlic and sesame oil.  The shrimp is cooked well and the plate is generously portioned.  My husband is partial to the seafood pan fried noodles, which is essentially the dish I described above with assorted seafood and served over a huge pile of lightly fried noodles.  We also very much enjoy their egg drop soup - very tasty without being overly salty, and always served piping hot.

We experienced the dim sum for the first time this morning, and were pleasantly surprised.  After reading many a negative review here on Yelp, we went in expecting the worst.  Instead, we received a nice assortment of hot, fresh dumplings and beef short ribs.  We also could have had some of the noodle dishes, fried appetizers, and desserts that were floating around, though we chose not to.  Our favorite dumpling was the shrimp and pork shumai, served with a sweet and spicy dipping sauce.  I'm not one to eat pork very often, but did very much enjoy this dish and would order it again (my husband actually ordered himself 2 plates of the shumai and didn't regret it for a minute)!

I will say that while the food has improved dramatically, the service is still lacking and in need of some serious work.  We had to wait 15 minutes for our water glasses to be refilled, the carts did not come around regularly, and the dish we ordered off the menu was cold by the time it reached our table (and served without rice, though the rice was supposed to come with).  I believe this is due to the large space of the restaurant and its many tables, and also the expansive menu and many ordering options.  The servers are very nice and knowledgable, and Jack, the manager, is always extremely kind whenever we're there - but the entire staff needs to be trained to work a bit more efficiently.

Overall, come try the food at Lucky Bamboo.  It IS good food and a place Nashville needs to satisfy us picky former New Yorkers who like our Chinese food.
The service is outstanding! Everyone who works here is super friendly, and the food is great! Try the pork belly!

I really enjoyed my food i ordered. I got the sesame chicken, spring rolls and chicken on a stick! Even though the delivery took a little bit longer the food was still pretty good when it got here. I would advise they do have a $10 delivery fee, but other than that i might have given it 5 stars overall. I will order from here again, and would be down for definitely checking the dining room out as well. Wontons with chili oil were good as well, just wish they had more meat !
feWbeHR9_O0yhxJqmyACXw: An exceptionally wonderful dining experience. Our server Lola was terrific. We'll be back many times.

Came here for lunch with my Mom around noon. They were serving brunch only. They were super accommodating and were able to give me egg whites instead of a sunny egg. I had the Say My Crepe dish. They also changed the sausage to bacon for me, and left the peppers out. The dish was amazing. I was so impressed. Wonderful flavors. My mom had Unbreak My Eggs with bacon. She said the bacon had awesome flavor and was delicious. The house potatoes are excellent! Service by Jenni was superb. Could not ask for more. Also love that they offer Stevia as a sweetener! Bonus points! Really love the Art Deco decor along with the old photos of the actors on the walls. Will be back when I am here again on business!

The upstairs lounge is a great spot for a casual atmosphere with amazing craft cocktails and sweet and knowledgeable bartenders. 

I am from out of town and was looking for a place to have a few drinks with my friends. We decided to try the upstairs lounge for something a little more casual/fun and it did not disappoint. Aside from the sheer beauty of the place, the music selection was fun and offered a great variety. We sat at the bar, but there were cocktail tables and couches as well. 

At first, I was in between a few drinks and the bartender (Gary) helped guide me to something I would love. I tried the Bad N Rougie and it was absolutely delicious! It was so refreshing and beautifully made. Gary and the other bartender Roger really made our night amazing. The service was incredible and the drinks were even better. My friend had the Apples N Herb drink and she loved it. I will definitely be back to see these bartenders next time I am in town!
n5FS-R7aFxFSBeHfVCAuRQ: Great little spot. Came in for drinks and got the monstrosity of a Bloody Mary. The big fix. It was insane! It was literally a whole meal. Great decor and awesome service. Shout out to the bartender. She was great. Just didn't get her name.

So glad Burger Up was  crowded!!  Tried out Flipside and it was awesome.   I had the ABC salad, dressed and seasoned perfectly.   My friend had the Pollo ranchero.  Chicken was very tender and the toppings were fresh and delicious.   Love the decor, especially the tv!!
Enjoyed my time at flipside.  Food was really good. Love the atmosphere.  Good for families.  Stopped back in late night for a drink, fun times.
VOEMn_VFIXdURXLPPms_TA:
I've been here 3 times now, so I figure its about time I leave a review! 

Both of their appetizers...Ayib Begomen and Sambusa...are great choices.  I'm big on cheese, so I fancy the Ayib Begomen in particular! My only complaint about the appetizers is the portions are pretty darn small if you plan on sharing them with someone.  

The food is tasty. I've had the Beef Tibs, and QeyW'et..which were delicious.  The Chicken Curry & Peas was so-so...but I don't think that's a true Ethiopian dish.  I was never a big fan of the bread..Injera, but after my last visit, it seems to be growing on me!   

I also love that they have plenty of vegetarian options.  

Great place for a date night!  :)

My beau and I both agree that the food is delicious and the staff is very pleasant. We ordered 9, 10 and 12. She kept our drinks filled and offered more injera when we ate what came with our meals. I highly suggest.

Last night was my 2nd time here.  My friend and I walked in at 9:45pm and they closed at 10:00pm.  We asked if it was okay and they seated us without hesitation. 

We ordered a Curried Lamb dish to share. It was amazing. They were pleasant,  thry never rushed us, and the food was amazing. 

Being from Nashville, I am never sure of ethnic fare. I recommend this pkace highly.
YaEwp8emNzySe-d0MKVbOw:
Good sushi and price is affordable. 
The interior isn't fancy at all. 
I've eaten there a couple times and don't recall any issues. Good for lunch.

This will definitely be my go to sushi spot from now on! They've got great sushi for great prices! My friend and I went during lunch when they had a lunch special: two basic rolls with a soup or salad for $7.95. She got that and I tried the the crunchy shrimp roll and the green dragon roll. Both were delicious. You would think that for only being $3.95, the crunchy shrimp roll wouldn't even be that good, but it was pleasantly way better than I was expecting. The environment was lax and clean which is always a plus and the staff was super friendly too. The menu had a lot of variety and again, for competitive prices, so I will definitely be coming back to try what else is on the menu!

My absolutely favorite place ! No matter for sushi or Korean food. Their sushi is very fresh and very well made. Unlike other places they really put a lot of fish on their rolls instead of just big chunk of rice. You can tell the sushi chef really pay attention to the customer and makes good sushi. The Korean food is very authentic and delicious. I love their side dishes too. The only complain would be sometimes you need to wait a little. But I don't mind since they probably have the best sushi in the area.
VHKG5Wro7ozuPdKl4pajbA: I was in the area and wanted to eat something that was quick and healthy. I went on my  trusty Yelp app since I wasn't familiar with the area. The Grill is an unexpected treat past the produce section of the store. I ordered the Pesto Grilled Chicken & Swiss Sandwich,  full of flavor and tasty. I did get a side of fries with my order (I couldn't resist), served with Whole Foods Ketchup brand. The service was quick and fast, the meal hit the spot

If you are shopping at Whole Foods and the samples haven't filled you up, skip the long buffet and make your way over to the Grill (right by the seafood bar). The food is exceptional, fresh, and cooked right in front of you. I've been enjoying the food here for years and it never lets me down.

They have a special menu that changes daily, and on my latest visit they offered a crab cake BLT that I couldn't pass up. My BF was tempted by the gouda burger and we both were blown away. Whole Foods has SUCH a good burger; they are juicy and delicious. My crab cake was out of this world, not a lot of breading and it had a ton of meat. I have heard good things about their fish and chips but have not had a chance to try them yet due to the other amazing options they provide. 

Plus: the prices are so reasonable. The burger was $10 and my BLT was $12. They included 1 side and since it's not a typical restaurant, the credit card slip doesn't have an option to tip (though I'm sure you can if you have some cash). They also offer root beer on tap and iced tea. 

I LOVE this gem within Whole Foods and consistently have exceptional food here every single time.
Food is great, but as it is near the coolers the air temperature is cold.  It also takes an awfully long time.
Zg8yIhVMtp7CuBLhJk-sbw:
This place is an interesting case. For starters, I have had two different occasions now to partake of their chicken offerings, and I will have to say, I thoroughly enjoyed the eats both times.

Our family hosted a Super Bowl party a few weeks ago and we got 30 wings for the group. I was pleasantly surprised when I asked if I could divvy up the wings in different flavors of sauces, as some places are rigid and will not allow you to get different flavors on the same order. I got 30 wings, so each set of 10 had a different sauce. This was an excellent way to sample their choices, as they have a ton of sauce options!

Their "Hot" flavor is simply Frank's Red Hot sauce, which if you're like me, I love Franks, so no problem there. I also ordered 10 in "Hot BBQ" which was tasty, but I didn't get any heat flavor at all. The last 10 were garlic parmesan. These. Were. Tasty. Excellent choice for a differentiator and/or for those in your group that might not like spicy options. I will definitely get these again.

Separately, I went in for their lunch options. I ordered their lunch special that included 4 jumbo tenders, fries, and a drink. It was $7.99, but the portion was excellent, and I was nowhere near hungry for the next meal time after lunch!

The last thing I will mention is that their stand alone options are a bit pricy for simple wings/tenders/fries. This is the only reason for a 4-star rating instead of a 5-star. If they were a tad more moderately priced, I would frequent much more often and highly recommend them!
We love this place! They have a great variety of foods and it is so good. You have to wait a few minutes because everything is made to order buy it is well worth it.

Wing Express has the best Buffalo Wings and prices in Hermitage, Tennessee!  You'll pay less for the same menu items at Wing Express as you would at BWW's.  Consistent and delicious food with reasonable prices.
ZhExDd48ZtOj2DFjXPgfpw: Excellent food. Attentive Service. Very affordable. Great mission. Wandered in for brunch while visiting. Found full service coffee and tea bar, and an excellent well rounded menu.

I've been here a few times now, what keeps me coming back is the great food!  Love the sausage roll, bleu cheeseburger, & killer potato salad.  

Outside of the great grub, it's also cool that they help out the community by helping underprivileged & formerly homeless folks learn culinary skills by working in the restaurant.

Awesome, affordable place with a great social cause behind it. 

Went with my parents. Dad ordered the steak (Friday nights) which was perfectly cooked and charred to his liking and seasoned so well. The potato side was incredible! Cheesy goodness!

We got the last bowl of artichoke soup. Huge pieces of veggie, delicious.

I ordered a baked potato. Enough for two meals, and I thoroughly enjoyed (especially the aroma of) the bourbon sauce.

Mom's salad was beautiful, the Moroccan chicken.

And the lava cake plate got completely cleaned. :)

My parents come here often. Great for an affordable, casual dinner out.
v63vLjagqV609L5NweBJ6w: Okay nashville NEEDED a place like this! Being from Miami, I find it hard to find  yummy Puerto Rican / Cuban/ dominican food here but this place got it right! I got the mofongo with chicharron de cerdo (fried pork) and it's a must! My friend got the sancocho and loved it! If you love this kind of food this will make you happy. Yummy flavors done right
The food was great. I got the plate and my wife got a shrimp mofongo. Awesome service, clean environment, great food.

My husband and I had a late lunch here. He had the pulled pork and I had the pork with eggplant. So delicious, large portions- multiple rice options and salad options. We will definitely come back for more. Don't forget an order of tostones and Pina juice!
AS7hUQSS8zg8D7l3hPXj2g:
Mirko is a treasure. We went with a group of eight, and everyone agreed that we'd be coming back again. The pasta is freshly made. If you've never experienced the difference between fresh pasta and the stuff you buy in a box, you really owe it to yourself to try it. I ordered the four cheese ravioli with alfredo sauce. Amazing!

The prices are quite reasonable too. You'll choose a sauce for around $4 or 5 and then a pasta for around $5. So pretty much $10 for an entree. Not bad at all, especially considering the amazing flavor and wonderful service.

Last but least, an extra hat tip to Missy Johnson from Mirko Pasta. I emailed her before going to the restaurant regarding a food allergy. She was very thoughtful about going through the menu and personally recommending the right dishes and combinations that would be safe for me. I really appreciated it.

We had a great time all around. Add this place to your list of places to eat in Nashville. You won't regret it!

I love Italian food, but let's be honest, I love any kind of food that's done just right.  Mirko does just that.  If you're in the mood for freshly made pasta, freshly made sauce, and perfectly seasoned everything, then look no further than this little joint.  It's tucked next to boutiques, bars and restaurants, but make no mistake!  It definitely stands out.

Thanks to Living Social, we made a trip here last week for a low-key date night.  After humming and ha-ing over the wide variety of options, I went with the gnocchi/Norma sauce combo, and boy am I glad I did.  I could have licked that plate clean, but luckily for everyone else, I was taught some manners.  Boyfriend went with the lasagna, which was also VERY good!  But back to the fresh, meaty gnocchi... back to the mouthwatering eggplant, and big chunks of mozzarella, and the melty ricotta... Yum!  I will definitely be back for more of that savory, flavorful goodness!

Did I mention that the service was impeccable?  Because it was.  The staff was attentive and friendly, and my soda was refilled before it went flat/watered-down (maybe more times than I should've allowed).  That night they even offered half off of desserts, but I was so full from the tasty meal I couldn't do it.  Next time, Mirko!!

If you want a low-key Italian meal full of fresh flavor without breaking the bank, this is your spot!

Pros:
Good atmosphere
Gluten free pasta
A large selection of pastas 
Seemingly authentic

Cons:
No bottled water
Tap water only
No one likes Nashville tap water
Please add bottled water to the menu

Also...

Service was great.
Semi private seating in the back. 
Nice relaxing jazz music.
We'll be back :)
hATWUNyABGYZShvHslXV_Q: Fantastic.  Had set out for the Pharmacy or Mas Tacos but both had really long lines, so we opted for the Birdhouse. Chicken is double fried, crispy and tasty. House sauce tastes very authentic.  Good shoestring fires with a heavy dusting of spice. Greens are spicy Southern style with a little kimchi which works really well. Staff were really friendly. Overall a great experience with great food.
Instant East Nashville favorite. Tried the fried chicken tenders and Korean BBQ brisket with kimchi greens, everything was delicious. Great, quick service! We will definitely be back soon!

Wow! Oh my god, wow! The tenders tossed in their house hot sauce (Phoenix) are amazing! I'm a huge fan of Nashville Hot Chicken, but I've got to say this beats NHC by a pretty wide margin, especially the flavor. I love the breading. This is not your typical southern-Asian fusion. The fries are on point and have the right amount of spice without being too salty. If you get the hot chicken, get the slaw, you'll need it and it compliments very well together. This is going to have to be a regular when I come to Nashville.
1RerTYaZEAfmSARDhsZ-8Q: My husband and I stopped here on a Thursday afternoon to get something to eat. The food was amazing and the service was great!  This is a perfect stop for live music if you don't want the crowds of the big name bars on Broadway.  To top it off, they have happy hour M-Thurs. $3 domestic bottles and 1/2 appetizers. I highly recommend the meatballs.  Delish!
Place was pretty legit differently stick to broadway street for chill hip hop music and dancing, this place was awesome

Stop by last night after hearing how nice the place was and was not disappointed. The staff was super nice and attentive to us. Love the chandelier, tables, bar tops  as they all seem to be a good balance in the room!! The whole place seemed way to nice for Broadway but I'm not complaining. Finally a place to listen to music without stepping in who knows what. Wish the best for them, me and my girls will return this weekend!!
eWmcdBJPPQ_iSaNU8uIpFQ: Burgers were great, sriracha mayo was amazing, the little guitar they served my two year olds fish sticks on was awesome and staff was super friendly. Appetizer was cold but I still enjoyed it. Food came out quick. Valet guy was incredibly nice, should have caught his name.

I was here for CMA fest over the past week and spend quite a few hours in here a few different days. We had Jackie as our server and let me tell you she was OUTSTANDING!! The food was decent but the service was incredible. :)

Let me just start by saying our waiter Allen was AMAZING! He was very nice and was always there when we needed him. And he had only been there for 4 days! We've never been to hard rock and let me tell you it was great and we will be returning!
vY86ZkQp-bAJMk8VYZ33ZA: Hidden little. "To go" sandwich shop with a few outdoor tables.no indoor seating.  For under 10$ i had a great queso soup and a Wonderful Philly Cheese steak. Very friendly staff and great prices for downtown area of Nashville
Great place for a quick lunch with a cozy, sunny outdoor seating area. Had some excellent veggie beef soup - and as a Yankee, I don't often see fried bologna sandwiches on the specials menu!

Amazingly delicious soup, sandwhiches and cookies! The portions are perfect and the prices are great. This is a popular lunch location and there's typically a line. However the line moves quick and the wait time isn't bad at all. You definitely want to make sure you stop here for lunch, you won't be disappointed!
QwIoMcD_Nd7DV7o-rmr2ug:
Came here for a Louisville football game and was joined by many UofL fans from the area. Double Dogs on Charlotte is the Louisville Bar in Nashville and is a great place to watch a Cards game.

The food is great, they have a number of beers and a different beer special every day. Plenty of screens and they can get audio for any TV for your table specifically which is a great touch when a number of games are running concurrently. Go for college sports, especially if you're a fan of the Louisville Cardinals.

I've come here quite a bit especially for Preds games. The atmosphere is great even just for lunch. The burgers are all really good so I can't just recommend one. For appetizers I do however recommend the queso or the spicy cheese toast. I've never had a problem here with food or service even when they have been packed.

We've placed to-go orders here on the way home & so far every time the orders have been right on! I'm not a big hotdog person so I personally order a hamburger here. 

I can tell by the taste of the hamburger they use very high quality meat! Lol yeah u heard me!  
But seriously, that's the reason we grab something here because it's easy on the way home, prices are great, we loved the quality, & so far they haven't messed up a to-go order so hey that means something now a days!  Give em a break on busy rush hour times...everywhere is busy at those times no matter where you go in Nashville now! Be patient, have some beers & relax! Make your life good no matter where u go!
rMtVhAJN1GVoO23o686hcQ:
Pub feel and good service.

We went here on a Friday night and immediately got seated. Our friends ordered the pretzels to share and they came out in just a few minutes. Sooo buttery and crispy on the outside. I would definitely get these if I go again. They come with a mustard and beer cheese. 

I wasn't super hungry so I ordered the small spinach salad that came with almonds, watermelon, goat cheese, and a blueberry vinaigrette. The spinach was very flavorful and fresh. I also ordered the gouda mac and cheese for $4 but was a bit disappointed. It came in a small soup cup and was not super cheesy. I think it would be better if it had more gouda and topped with bread crumbs.

Overall, good meal at a decent price. Happy hour until 7pm too!
This a is a great spot. Ordered the shrimp poboy and the house sauce was delicious.  Real Cool atmosphere also

Any place that does 2 for 1 beers gets 5-stars. Food was great and the service was awesome. I had the blackened catfish with grits and asparagus, there was a nice heat to the rub and the portion was perfect. Cool place to sit for a while, kind of a bar theme with lots of tvs but not noisy or cramped. Really glad we came across this place while visiting Nashville and it felt like we got to experience the city getting away from the touristy part of town.
mIU6Rfo47ZFiyXUAOkz70w: I have gone on a major quest to find the best chicken salad in Nashville and maaaaan I've had a lot! You can tell by just the look of their subs of how fresh they are! They are not shy on toppings ether! I literally have only ever ordered One Sub from them and thats right, The Chicken Salad Sub. Its not typical in any way! THE BEST! The half sub is more than enough for one person, so you will defiantly get your moneys worth!

This is my favorite locally owned sub shop I think. For one thing, the bread is really good and they heat the subs which is even better. The other thing is that they are generous with their meats and cheese and the size sandwich for the price is wow, really fair. 

I normally get the pastrami but I change up the cheese to provolone with all the fixins. They have zaps potato chips. I also have tried all their soups and think the tortilla is really, really good. 

Lunch normally offers a substantial line but they are efficient and great. The inside of the place isn't all that dynamic but who cares the sandwiches are great!!
Cute little sub place, good food, nice people.  Be sure to check out the special of the day.
FjFeInRmWs-34HO1aC0EjA: Great pizza , usually quick delivery from nice ppl. Tip a bit extra and they'll remember you and hurry a bit more.

It was a choice I never thought I would have to make.  Starve or go to Roma Pizza to recover from a day spent being a costumed mascot for a charity event while my wife was out of town.  I weighed the options and decided against the slow starvation angle and decided to wander down to this Roma.

I'd loved, then grown tired of, then grown sick of Roma in Green Hills before, but as stated these were dire times and I really was exhausted after 7.5 hours of frozen-costume shenanigans.  There was never anything wrong with Roma, it just became that thing you had too much of due to it being convenient and eventually grew to dislike.

Ordered online, drove down, first step into the Nolensville Road store and I was a little confused as to why all the seats were on the tables (no dine-in that day).  The place is new relatively, but looks like it's been there for years.  All I can figure is that the store must be smoking to age so quickly.

My order was ready about a minute after I arrived and I was on my way.  Pizza was ok, not the greatest but fast and good, cheese sticks were just about the best I have ever had (and better than I'v ever had at a Roma before)

The veggie pizza was a bit chunky... I'm not sure what does that to a pizza, but it's like the cheese was textured wrong.  The taste was ok.  Maybe that's a style, don't recall it from a Roma before.

Their veggie pizza was not all that great the next day.  Not bad, but not a great second-day breakfast.

Seemed a good little chain pizza joint though

I love this pizza joint..I found it one day after work on my way home in March...so I stop here whenever I need a pizza fix and I don't want to fight the traffic around Vanderbilt and 21 south...besides the pizza is delicious!!!

I love to get the large pepperoni with Jalapeno and onions with extra cheese and sauce..I love it..I get the thin crust and they cut the round pizza into squares making it easy to drive and eat..because it smells so good I have to dive into during my drive home..I know eating hot pizza and frustrating traffic isn't safe...but at least it helps me endure the darn traffic here in Nashville..that's another story in itself!!!

They make subs, calzones and salads and pasta but I just do the pizza..I am a pizza person...I recommend this pizza to all pizza lovers..Very good at a Great price..you can get a large 2 topping pizza for only $7.99 plus taxes..Great all the way around....a NO BRAINER !!!
pqiFIcObG_qW16x9cHcznQ:
Love this spot. The space is beautiful and the coffee is great. My drink of choice is an Americano with a little vanilla. Super friendly staff. One block off of Broadway, so it's just a little tucked away, but still accessible. Definitely recommend. 

Parking lot is kind of small, but there are reserved spots you can park in. Oh, and they need pastries, but have some great snacks.
The best blueberry muffins in town. Bethany thank you for the great service and coffee.

This place is fantastic. Great service and great food and great ambiance and and and.
The Omelette of Fire is incredibly delicious!!!
5aZC1FwNiaRayaz7QhAN-Q: Amy's is a lovely experience. The food is delicious -- my husband and I have tried about 10 things so far, including appetizers, entrees, and desserts. Our favorite dish is wet beef tibs & a veggie sampler. The lamb tibs is great, too. The first time we went our toddler was with us, and Amy brought out some mild potatoes and carrots for him to try. My husband can't even go without getting the baklava. SO GOOD. The espresso was great. Oh, and Amy and her husband are the sweetest people. There's a market attached, too. I am so glad this has opened near me, and I will go back EVERY chance I get and take all the friends who are interested. WE LOVE YOU, AMY!!

We are Ethiopian fanatics! We have eaten at all the Nashville locations and many many other states from Texas, to Washington D.C, to NYC. Today was our first time trying Amy's and we were amazed. The food was absolutely delicious, fresh, with high end ingredients. We had special kitfo, veggie platter and tibs and could not have been happier. In addition to the food the service and bright clean dining room were stellar. The owner visited our table to check on us. She also learned to make desserts in Italy and we are taking some home with us, so I can't report on those but they look fabulous. Whether you are a first timer or a regular Ethiopian eater you will not be disappointed! So glad to have Amy's in Nashville.

We are Ethiopian fanatics! We have eaten at all the Nashville locations and many many other states from Texas, to Washington D.C, to NYC. Today was our first time trying Amy's and we were amazed. The food was absolutely delicious, fresh, with high end ingredients. We had special kitfo, veggie platter and tibs and could not have been happier. In addition to the food the service and bright clean dining room were stellar. The owner visited our table to check on us. She also learned to make desserts in Italy and we are taking some home with us, so I can't report on those but they look fabulous. Whether you are a first timer or a regular Ethiopian eater you will not be disappointed! So glad to have Amy's in Nashville.
MTB9CelShC-x4jj31BJYAg: I've been to three of their locations now and this one for sure feels the "homiest" of them all. Grand opening today and all drinks are free all day! Super friendly staff, clean, fresh, inviting environment. Great job, guys! My group got the Maple Bacon crepe, Sunrise crepe and breakfast tacos. For drinks: the Nutty (iced), Butterbeer coffee and pumpkin spice latte. So delicious!

I came here on the recommendation of a friend who lives in Nashville, and I'm so glad she recommended it! I wanted to try about six different crepes because they all sounded so good. I settled on the Highlander, and my brother got the Maple Bacon. They both had excellent flavor. Also got a Macaroon (mocha hazelnut) to drink; half sweet was perfect. 

I enjoyed the atmosphere a lot. It seems like it'd be a good place to write or study. And there's a red phone booth with board games inside, which are presumably there to be played by patrons. Love that!

This has become my regular place for coffee and whatever meal I happen to be craving when I'm there. They have their own parking lot, which is becoming a rarity in Nashville. The food (y'all gotta check out the pizza crepe) is always really good and I haven't had a drink I don't like, but what really stands out to me is the service. No matter when I go in or who is working, they are always kind and pleasant. Today was a particularly long day at work for me, so I took a short break to run to Red Bicycle for coffee and some breakfast. I was tired and struggling to articulate exactly what I wanted, but the barista was patient with me and made sure I had my order to go quickly so I could get back to my job. The customer service is always above and beyond, and I'm so thankful I work near this coffee shop!
punjQyM6mXEIfvWyYVBa2Q: A great choice for our late breakfast. Interesting combinations of traditional breakfast fare, but not so over the top that one forgets it's all about bacon, eggs and cheese (or some other combo). Good service and  prompt refills of coffee. Located on a busy highway, but had a relaxing atmosphere.

The food is always fantastic whenever I come here. First time I came I had the steak and scrambled eggs with a side of their delicious, hearty home fries. The beef was served with creamy chimichurri sauce which is always a plus.

Today I had their BLT with no expectation of it being as extraordinary as it was. It was dressed in a blue-cheese aioli that took this otherwise simple sandwich over the top. The sourdough bread was soft and toasty and the bacon was served very generously. 

Whether it be breakfast or brunch, Fenwicks is the place to go. Great food, lively atmosphere, and fast service. My only complaint if I had any is for them to include a lot more lunch items on their menu. I'd love to see what their kitchen could do with the usual lunch classics.
The food here hits the spot..try anything breakfast and the burgers. The coffee is among the best. Love the kind folks who work here too.
TKnU6PBXrXH4iVLCPSgHEg:
Blackstone is one of those places you love to go to, but for some reason you keep putting on the back burner on places to return to. 

I do like the place, and I love their beer. I realize this place has to go in competition with Yazoo, and probably doesn't win too often, but I love their dark beer. I forget the name, but I highly recommend it. It has itself set up sort of like an English pub but more dinner friendly, and I must admit it makes me happy that they have a fish and chips dinner. 

The fish and chips platter is really good and really large, so be ready to put away some food. I really like their chili too, it's got a kick too it, but doesn't over do the spices so you have heartburn upon contact. I've tried the pizza as well, but beware of the olives. I believe they use kalamata instead of the standard green and black you'll get at your local Pizza Hut, and adds a different kick to the pie.

I'd say the price is fair enough, and this place gets huge props for actually having parking instead of me having to pay at a parking garage.

The beers were wonderful, and the food was delicious.  We went once, and then went back the next day because it was all so good. Try the Chipotle BBQ pork tenderloin with smoked Gouda grits - to die for - and their awesome St.Charles Porter beer.  Fun atmosphere and great service.

Found this place simply by driving by and we are glad we tried it. Walking in we were a bit skeptical as the decor and atmosphere inside looks old and a bit run down and the only reason I marked it down a star. Our waiter, Andrew, was great! Very friendly and welcoming. He brought us samples of beer so we could be sure to enjoy our selections. We ended up liking the pumpkin ale the best. For dinner we tried the Octoberfest dinner special and the mushroom stroganoff. Both were very delicious, especially the sauerkraut with the special, which I normally do not even like! For a birthday dessert Andrew brought a slice of chocolate cake with candles and the people at the surrounding tables helped sing happy birthday, which was unexpected and fun. We will definitely eat here again next time we are in town!
W5bx6PacwlKhEDP_M9jUmA:
A very nice neighborhood coffee shop. I came here for a few hours for a cup of joe and to do some work. It had a really homey feel to it. The barista was very nice and helpful. She made me feel really welcome.

The internet clocked in at about 6 Mbps, adequate for an online meeting I needed to attend. Great spot, will definitely be back.

Great coffee, cozy place to hang out, live music and other community activities. Also they have board games available for anyone to play. 
Even the food is made local, good price!

There is a chair, to the left as you walk in, it's a dark, broken-in, beautiful leather chair. I sit there with my coffee on a chilly autumn day, and pretend to read the editorial pieces in the Nashville Scene. When someone is already sitting in my chair, I get really angry inside, but don't show it. Of course I'd never show it. I'll even smile at the person. But I'm not happy to have to sit in one of the hard chairs at the table. But we live in a democracy, and I don't actually own the chair, so I can't really do anything about it. And the coffee's damn good. So.
w0peMClztE-VtO8lQg1Srw:
They make your sandwich almost before you order which rocks and it tastes crazy good on a sunday morning coming down. Thanks j cash. 

But the price is too high or make the sandwich a foot long...either would work. I microwave mine at about 11am on sundays after my jimmy johns lovin lady and I have retrieved one of our vehicles from a downtown nashville parking garage.  Ie, following a typical saturday evening of embibing in the local haunts.  Doc Hollidays Saloon downtown is great...get the table just above the dance floor to the right of stairs...best place to sit and drink on 2nd.  Anyway, kick it on saturday and get you sanich' on, on Sunday!
Great! Fast, easy, no mistakes and no wasting time or money! Thanks for the great food and attitude.
Sandwiches aren't necessarily mind blowing but the service, delivery, and quickness are top notch.
XjuqPaZGBL_9FdV200Vqag: This may be my favorite place to get food downtown. How can you beat two rolls and a soup (or salad) for $7.50?! I only get the veggie roll so I can't comment on the fish but the veggies are super fresh and delicious. I never have to wait for more than 10 minutes for my order even during the busy lunch hour.  I would highly recommend this hidden gem in the arcade.

Unassuming little place in the Arcade. The owners were so friendly. They gave free sample of their rolls. Ordered pork bulgogi lunch special. It came with rice, dumpling and some lettuce to make wrap. They were really good and the price was reasonable. Oh, the also had self serve hot tea, really good tea too. One sweet thing that we saw was when the owner offered a sushi roll to lady worker who was sweeping the floor. We thought it was very nice of them.

Had a few rolls and the bibimbap for lunch the other day.  The rolls were pretty good, but not mind blowing.  The sushi selection here if very basic, but it gets the job done.  The bibimbap was a great vegetarian option with plenty big portion sizes.  A good, quick, healthy option.
Qjm-W7SpkCuTxacD10Wt2g: Used Raes for a corporate catering event and everyone loved the food!!  Incredibly fresh - great service!!!

Very good gourmet sandwich shop.  The prices are a little more than you will pay at any of the chains, but the sandwiches are fantastic and probably big enough for a couple of meals.  I finished mine, but felt like I needed a nap afterwords.

We have a rule to find a good sandwich shop on every vacation we go on.   This one fit the bill nicely.  I had a Cuban which was very good.   My wife had a monty Cristo and she said it was delish.     Prices were on par for most sandwich shops.   I would recommend
fv4sHN-EEpy5wDXyTVxb5g: I seen Juice Nashville on Yelp, and just really wanted to try it. Just to give my body a break from solid foods. I walked in to a very clean, great atmosphere, very helpful and professional stuff. I wish I would of got the clerks name because she was great. I told her i never juiced before and she pulled out some popular flavors.  I tasted a lot of different varieties and was very surprised not too sweet and not to bitter on any of them. I have been Wowed. Started off with the 1 day, and will hopefully/eventually work to 3 days a week. Definitely will be going back.

Stumbled upon Juice Nashville at the East Nashville Farmer's Market, and I'm hooked. I've been telling everyone I know about this juice. Stephanie is super nice and you can find her at the East Nashville Farmer's market on Thursday and the West Nashville Farmer's Market on Saturday morning or order online. Everything I've tasted is amazing and so good for you!

Nice small space to get your health fix for the day. Great variety of different cold pressed juices and almond milks with clever / punny names. Pricing is not bad at $6 a bottle; I've been to places that charge $9 for the same thing. They also offer samples if you appear to be a confused newbie, like myself, which is nice since $6 for a bottle is still a decent chunk of change that I would not want to end up throwing away. Overall, if you're looking for a refreshing juice (especially after all the holiday treats), this is a great Nashville go-to.
0SfpdMhddPXRJgp3d3z3uQ:
Stopped in for a quick bite and to.try the place for the first time. Staying at the Lowes Vanderbuilt Hotel nearby and didn't want to pay room service prices. Always looking for fresh healthy stuff when I travel. 

This place was exactly what I needed. Had the Asian Salad and added the tuna. Fresh greens and very tasty. Really hit the spot. Nice place, friendly service and good menu. Will be back to try some more stuff before I head out of town. 

Stop by and give it a try... I think you'll be pleased.

If you are looking for a healthy lunch, this is as good as it gets! Great options, really enjoy the paninis and the drink options such as Strawberry Mojito and Flavored lemonades.  The atmosphere is super clean and inviting.
Great food!! The service was very fast and friendly as well! Everything was delicious! Lentil bean soup was amazing!
qTicP3qlsW6zqLV7P1Uz_g: Don't let the office park location fool you. Four of us got four different dishes and everyone loved their meal. Reasonably priced and good eats!!!!
It was excellent. Sushi, broken. Oodles, gyros, fried rice, green curry , shrimp soup, Egg rolls, sesame chicken were all great. The bar sucked we waited forever for drinks.

Surprisingly good. My wife and I were a bit skeptical about having sushi at a place that also offers Thai food, but the sushi rolls that we had were really good. Fresh ingredients and great flavor. I recommend the TNT roll and the Steamed Dumplings.
Jy32cgXAP7SvEUMzbmHhUw:
Stopped in to the Picnic for lunch today with a friend. It was a pretty day so we sat outside under a huge umbrella. At my friend's insistence I tried the pimento plus. Toasted pimento cheese on a croissant with bacon. I got potato salad and frozen fruit for my sides.The croissant was wonderfully flakey. The pimento cheese was fantastic. The bacon was bacon. The combination of the three of them was perfect. It is a huge sandwich!!!! The sides are small but great. Potato salad is a standard side but the frozen fruit is what I wasn't expecting! When I see frozen fruit on a menu I think just that. Frozen fruit, maybe thawed. But this is more of a dessert. It is frozen fruit with banana added. It is then blended with condensed milk and topped with whipped cream. I could have eaten much more but thankfully it was only a tiny bit.

The interior is cute. Very frilly and done in yellow and blue. The staff is great. The restaurant is clean. There are also ready made items you can grab and go. 

Parking can seem intimidating but of the three times I've been here I've never had an issue parking.

I AM IN LOVE! This is the cutest place ever! Staff was lovely, very helpful and had a great attitude. The artichoke and lemon soup is the best I've ever had. The chicken salad sandwich and BLT were also great. Fast service, reasonable prices, great people. And they have a cool take out grab and go section, so perfect for anyone with a busy work schedule needing to get lunch quickly. Can't wait to go back :)
Great place for great food and a family environment. Have tried almost everything in the menu and its all amazing! Dont forget to get the punch drink
pUqTr1eNl1-_LATAito3nQ: I wish I'd known about this place when it first opened - I shudder to think of how many delicious meals I've missed! First, yes, spotlessly clean, with no annoying smells. All due respect to other local Asian groceries, it's one thing for the fish section to smell like fish and the ocean, and another for it to smell rank. Second, great selections - all the different types of fish sauce, for instance. AND the staff speak enough English to clearly explain what each item is used for, why not to use Vietnamese fish sauce with Thai cooking, for instance. Lots of noodle choices. The fresh vegetable and fruit section in back is fine, though I'd like to see something showing where items like the Yu Choy came from, and a bit of a crisper wouldn't hurt. The frozen shrimp are a concern - they're the typical cheap imports that have been dehydrated to save weight, shipped over, then pumped full of who-knows-what to plump them up, giving the shrimp that metallic aftertaste you find so often nowadays. But it was good to see the larger fish vacuum-packed and available at reasonable prices. I'd like to see a little more variety in the Japanese pickles available - very few other than the bottled, which are awful. If you want those, try the market next to Sonobana restaurant, across town. Still, for variety, consistency, and helpful English-speaking staff, Bangkok Market can stand up against anything in Los Angeles that I've seen.

Alright, there's a new guy in town when it comes to Southeastern Asian groceries.  There are many things different about the Bangkok Market, compared to various Asian markets around Nashville (not to mention the USA).  It's extremely clean, and I'd even go as far as to say beautifully designed.  Word on the street is there was an interior designer involved in the creation of the Bangkok Market...  I immediately noticed upon entering that something was missing from this Asian market, something prevalent in maybe any other Asian market you'll encounter.  You know, the smell..

The smell of raw chicken and fish - not to be found at Bangkok.  Why?  Well owner, Pornchai, in our conversation stressed how important it is for the place not to smell, and so he doesn't plan on selling those items.  Though there is an extremely diverse selection of frozen animal products in the produce section, including Asian whole fish and black chicken.  Yes, BLACK chicken!!  Did you know this even existed??  I was told it has a cleaner taste than the common yellow, and is most often used for medicinal purposes.

I touched on my "conversation" with the owner.  Well, it was more like a personalized tour of the entire store.  Why I was so lucky to get this opportunity I have no idea, but Pornchai walked me through the whole store and described many of the items to me and went into detail about Thai cooking I'd never considered.  I didn't know there are coconut milks with different fat percentages (and I'm not talking about what is known as "light coconut milk" - Pornchai laughed at my mention of "light' coconut milk!), the ones with lower percentages to be used for soups, and the ones with higher percentages to be used for thicker stronger curries and soups.  And some even designated for desserts... In our conversation Pornchai stressed that many of the foods offered in the store are chemical and preservative-free, which seems to be an important point at the Market.

I don't think I've ever seen coconut milk in small cans - cans which have just enough for a recipe, so you're not stuck with leftover coconut milk and no idea what to do with it.  Bangkok Market has these.  Also, I have never seen teeny cans of zillions of different curry pastes - only jars of red and green - but Bangkok Market has got ALL flavors ... and I was gifted with one of each to sample.  Red, green, yellow, massaman, and panang.  Holy HELL.  It's gonna be a curry-eatin' year for me, which apparently has more health benefits than I knew - I was told that Thai and Indian people have a very low risk of Alzheimer's disease, and the proof seems to be in the curry (as opposed to the pudding). 

The first time I even noticed Bangkok Market existed was when I was driving to Home Depot, and just before making the right turn onto Powell Dr., I did a double take when I saw the Bangkok Market sign and said, "What the...?!"  It's new and it's awesome; I just hope it lasts despite it's unassuming location.

I actually loved this store. I went back twice in an hour Haha. The lady at the front is super sweet.The items in here are authentic and the food is made like momma used to make it. Their produce is a little over priced but eh. It definitely had a Thailand feel to it .
l8J8u8IhkadGAChHOOf6GQ:
Kids eat free tonight, thank god or I would be broke, not because of the prices but all these kids LOL. I'm digging the 1/2 priced wings too.

look forward to our party this week.
I haven't had a bad experience yet, however it does seem like they've scaled back the portions.

Amazing happy hour. I don't believe there is a better Sweet and Spicy Boneless wing in the world.

If you like the sweet and spicy sauce, you need to try it on the ribs. OMG they are the best.
OoVbRMiltVxxnWByd9CBxA: We came over after work on a Friday. Empty and quiet. It shouldn't be. We had the wings, peach cobbler, and the famed Espinosa margarita. Great food, drink, and of course the service. Down home but classy. A nice surprise on Dickerson Road. A nice reprieve in what's becoming an over trendy side of town. We will be back for sure.
Hands down, best mac n cheese in Nashville! Go early, when it's fresh and cheeeeeesey!!!!
Best southern cuisine we have ever eaten, I'm from memphis and Homa blows any restaurant in Memphis out the water. The food was simply amazing!!
i3GAJ8IL6z_jXj9GLkpFpQ: Great little neighborhood place perfect for coffee in the morning and drinks in the evening.  My husband had the perk and cork sandwich and I had the Mediterranean panini.  Both were delicious.  Atmosphere is great in an awesome neighborhood.  Enjoyed sitting on the front porch drinking our coffee.

Love this addition to the neighborhood. Riverside village needs a good coffee shop and this place is great. Coffee is reasonability priced, everything baked fresh, and service is fast. Support this local business!
Amazing!! We are here visiting from out of town and wanted a little cafe style lunch. Love the quaint home setting. The owner is so wonderful. Everything is so fresh, presentation is great.
JWUkEIayb3F8la3TWe-eyw:
I went to visit Nashville this weekend with a group of friends and we decided to go visit this restaurant on Saturday night. This restaurant offers complimentary transportation so their shuttle picked us up at our hotel on time and dropped us off at the restaurant. We were seated in a quiet room in a corner table. 

I ordered the petite filet mignon which was a little small for my taste, but it was very good. A friend and I split the baked potato and a bottle of red wine, which was great. The service was pretty good and I enjoyed the food. I would definitely recommend this restaurant.

Great steaks!  I was attending a conference and ate there with clients twice that week. The ambiance of the restaurant was really interesting. 

It is an old stockyard office and I think a bank. It is pretty pricy $$$$.

They have a pick up shuttle service that is really great so you don't have to drive. 

Fish was ok. But you know it is a steak place. 

Wine list was very substantial. 

Josh our waiter was great!

Next time I'm in Nashville I will be back.

Some of these reviews make me wonder. Are they real. Are they the competition? I'm not sure because my husband and I enjoyed our visit VERY much. It started with being greeted like royalty and, well, who doesn't want that? We were seated in a lovely, clean dining room. Even the young children at another table were on their (almost) best behavior. Our service was amazing - Fady was professional without being stiff and rude, knowledgeable, funny and on point. John, the manager was personable and professional - he really knows Nashville! Our food was perfect - salads, steaks, mac and cheese and spinach and mushrooms. Not one thing was bad! Cooked exactly how we like! Mac and Cheese is not my favorite - it often taste like spit to me (yeah, gross, I know!) but the mac and cheese was excellent at the Stockyard. Do yourself a favor - go enjoy a good steak there. A glass of wine and some conversation. Nice romantic night out - we will go again!
JXNr7z-uAT8n1yS6z77IHA: Hadn't been here in years since they closed 7 or so years ago. Heard they rebuilt after the flood and heard good things so we tried them out for my wife's birthday. Everything was great!  It was busy for a Wednesday night but the service was prompt and the food was really good. We really enjoyed the coconut shrimp appetizer. For entrees we had blackened Mahi, shrimp and grits, pistachio salmon , fried oysters and fried shrimp. Everything was  cooked properly and tasted great!  I definitely recommend this place!
Love this place ! Sunday breakfast on the water was wonderful. Eggs Benedict and cocktails are a great way to start your Sunday. Must go again soon.
Atmosphere was very good.  The mahi-mahi was tasty. Bartenders were fast and friendly.
FnEWE7CdxTolKWEgVKAlsg: This place has awesome sushi and chicken teriyaki. The parking is tricky but worth it. The sushi roll I had was Baker Twins. The ingredients in both dishes were fresh. There is enough food for two people. I will be going back. No pictures from this visit. My total bill was around $25 including a tip.

Spicy pork bulgogi is amazing!! Got that and some sushi to go. We sat at the bar and waited and the bartender was very nice and very helpful with any questions we had. Fast and friendly service!!!! 5 stars! Try it!!!!!
Wonderful food,setting and staff. I am a definite repeat customer. Love the panko salmon!
ny4I5fE9rGSC5toJkQqgEQ: We really liked City Tap House a lot! With all these new restaurants in town, it's hard to find a cool spot with good food. City Tap House has both! We went for brunch. It's a beautiful space, easy to access with a parking garage right next door. The menu is very interesting - everything sounded so good we had a hard time deciding! We were very pleased with our entrees and look forward to trying them out for lunch and dinner. My only negative: service could have been quicker and a little more attentive. Definitely will be back though!

We made a quick stop here before heading towards bourbon street. The bartender was super friendly and made me a delicious mojito that I couldn't resist ordering three more of. We also tried to some small plates that so surprisingly good for some late nights bar food. The hummus was fresh with soft pitabread and the bartender let me order some veggies with it as well. We also got the crab cake hush puppies and we wanted to order two more rounds. They were not to fishy and had an awesome dipping sauce that made them so good. Well defintely be back next time in Nashville.

I went to City Tap Nashville on the opening night of their dinner service. Normally, this would be an iffy decision...you know the drill - slow/sloppy service, servers not knowing the menu, general confusion all around. This place was the opposite from the moment we walked in. 

To the left, there is a large bar with giant, and I mean giant, screens for pulling up a chair and not moving for hours while you scour the 50-some odd beers they have on draft.

We sat in the open dining room and our server (I still haven't forgiven myself for forgetting her name) immediately came over and helped navigate the lengthy beer menu. She knew a lot about the beers and where they come from and was able to help my Miller-Lite drinking self seem a little more daring (bonus: I actually really enjoyed her suggestion). The service overall was great - attentive without being overbearing. But onto the most important part...the FOOD.

The food was. so good. The menu is not your typical bar & restaurant menu. They offer elevated dining options at completely reasonable prices. We started with the Korean BBQ short rib tacos and a big pretzel (from the bar menu). I have had the tacos at the Philly location so I knew they were good. But having them again...they were exquisite and I wanted 12 more. The pretzel was great too. It was fluffy with a perfect crust and featured two dipping sauces (a light queso and a maple dijon that I wanted to pour all over my body). After apps, we had two pizzas and crispy Brussels sprouts. I'll start with the sprouts because whoever decided pomegranate seeds belong in Brussels sprouts is the hero we need, not the hero we deserve. The pizzas, which have 6 slices and are easily shareable, had crispy crusts and were not greasy. We got the Quixote (spicy, savory, perfect ratios) and the Honey & Goat Cheese (sweeter option and better for sharing because richness).

I think City Tap will do really well here because of the versatility it offers in its menu and in general compared to its surroundings. It's very accessible from Broadway and a breath of fresh air from honky-tonk row!
2kfa2IBX3oy2zQX4PAkEpg:
Oh okay. Look, let's not mince words... Or grounds. This place apparently is exactly what the name implies. $5-6 crafted coffees. Yummy-looking separately arranged pastries. Grounds with more exotic origins than most luxury cars. Bearded, tatted, tight-panted baristas. Friendly. Cool, eclectic, sorta-precious atmosphere. It's not for everyone, but it's clearly for some.

On to the coffee, I had the Daredevil espresso/ Slayer + milk. F*cking delicious. 

Hip? Yes. Fun? Likely. Good? Yes.

My wife and I went here twice over our weekend trip to Nashville. We visited the East Nashville and Germantown locations. I ordered an iced vanilla bourbon and an iced caramel whisky while my wife ordered a mocha. Coffee was good. Not mind blowing but definitely worth the trip. Both locations we visited had good atmosphere. Decor was warm and welcoming and the tunes were good. Baristas were friendly enough and quick.

Atmosphere is everything here. The building floods with natural light creating a beautiful ambiance. Having the coffee made in the center of the building pulls you into the intricate process. I ordered the seasonal cold brew which was the most interesting cold brew I've had in my life. With hints of citrus and cinnamon I was blown away. Highly recommended.
H1QVoIgfovydsFfOZfuSPg:
This review is for the beer only -  I didn't order any food.

My husband and I wanted to try some local brews, so I was thrilled to find Big River!  We went to the bar and ordered a sampler - we got to try 9 beers for only $6! We were most impressed with their seasonals and their stout.

Fantastic food, fun and value.  A must visit local in the Nashville local flavor scene.  Well dressed and better than expected service staff.  Excellent appetizers, great service and presentation.  Jambalaya is second to none and absolutely fabulous.  Yelpers you won't go wrong with this place.

Great bar, great atmosphere. Dimly lit, upscale and low key. They won my heart with their outstanding IPA. Crisp hoppy taste with a pine needle finish. I say again OUTSTANDING. I ordered the fish tacos based on a recommendation from my friend. The flavor was delicious with 3 tacos being just the right amount. Served with a side of black beans and rice.. perfect compliment to my furious drinking of the IPA. This place comes with my highest recommendation. Enjoy.
-nUV-Z7_RdHY1dLsnuo0xQ: Twice per year I fly in to Nashville to visit family in Mississippi under less than optimal circumstances.  The journey is always trying. Denisa at the Ten Bar and Grill makes my trip so much tolerable.  Food is good and she makes great drinks.  Thumbs up and thank you.

I give them 4 because the sandwiches we had were fabulous AND the drinks are free, but the service was a little spotty. Order was slow so mgr gave us free dessert which was cool. I didn't complain she just offered. Managers reception bartender was s bit slow (not her fault). They need to make two separate lines one for mixed drinks and one for wine and beer to speed things up. We had to wait in line for quite awhile for a glass of beer while the bartender made mixed drinks for 6-7 ppl. . Staff was friendly too. Will come back again. Price was good for room. Sandwiches were really good....did I already say that?

We got two orders of wings. One buffalo and one bbq. We also got an order of fries.

Let's start with the personnel. The service was as great as can be expected. Our server couldn't have been sweeter...when we saw her. This place was slammed packed and severely understaffed. Anyways, the food was great besides the fries. The wings are crispy and full of flavor. Both have their pros and cons so try them both!
h5Y6iLn5VogLWcfT5tnANg:
I knew of Olan Rogers from his youtube videos but I didn't know about this store when I stumbled upon it. This store has a very chill environment with great sodas! I unfortunately didn't get one of their floats but I wish I had. 
They have great merchandise and great customer service!
I did their 4 bottles of soda for $6 deal and the soda is very simple and delicious!!
If I had known at the time that all tips helps college funds then I definitely would have tipped, sorry Olan!! I would return if I didn't live all the way in Virginia.

AMAZING waffle ice cream sundae. Little on the low side of variety but I easily forgive then on the basis of the awesomeness of deliciousness. It was almost too sweet for me (I don't usually go for such decadence), but the waffle was a great balance to the chocolate. If I just had an espresso, I would have bumped it to a 5.
The cinnamon bun waffle mondae is out of this world. One of our favorite food items we tried during a week stay in Nashville. A must try!!!
c6fWBSm-EHVwyFszTC5Mhw: Visited this place for Brunch: Cheesesteak eggrolls are a absolute MUST!!! Paired with their white sangria that comes with a cute popsicle inside! Parking is easy peasy...Thanks for Valet! It's not complimentary but relatively cheap at 4 bucks.

WOW.  What a great brunch. Country fried chicken was fantastic. A definite redo. Crab cake was done perfect as were the delicious red velvet waffles. Thanks for the memorable brunch. Best brunch ever we'll be back soon

This place is the best that we have been to in Nashville for dinner and drinks! The wait staff and bartenders are fabulous!! The food was spot on and the presentation was very good. Tawnie took extra special care of us, and that made us feel so welcome and appreciative. We hope we will see her again on our next trip to Nashville.
VImm_9zCKblqG4NwtnKj5g: Great spot for trying stouts and porters or any draft beers really. It's hard to find a good place with a decent selection of dark beers and they are great about letting you try them all out. Never tried the food but great drinking spot for sure.

Broadway Brewhouse is a chameleon of a bar that caters to all segments of Nashville.  Starting at the top, you have your beer nerds who are always looking for the latest draft and go weekly to scope out the selection.  Next level down, you have local white collars who chill after work or after a round of golf on Sundays for 2-4-1.  Moving on down the ladder you have the college kids and 20-somethings who still like to go crazy with the bushwackers.  At any time, any day of the week, you are likely to encounter a different mix of people.  Go early, go often, and figure out which crowd you'd like to be a part of.

Maybe this place only merits three stars.  Yes, the bathroom is filthy and cramped (though creatively designed).  The whole building practically feels like you're camping, especially with the garage doors open in the bar area.  I don't tend to order food here beyond the chips and queso.  Stick with the great beer and the ever-changing ambiance, though, and you are guaranteed a good time.

And dangit, I love the place.

We are from out of town and stepped in to have a beer.  Let me tell you. This place is incredible.  The chicken was perfect and had great flavor.  Nachos were better then expected and the food took less then 10 minutes to get to us.  Then at the end of it all.  Nothing on the menu is more then $9.   Will be back.  Awesome job
SAp1Nk3XLbQ-zeJKyASICQ:
On the way back home from the only real vacation we'll take this year. My man just had a birthday and he got a gift card for Cheesecake Factory...Yea!!

We would have had to wait for about 15-20 minutes for a table so we sat at the bar to take advantage of the happy hour appetizers. We got the Chicken Pot Stickers. Lots of chicken in them, lightly crunchy on one side and soft and chewy on the other. Awesome.

I ordered the Steak Diane and Chicken Madeira. Got the steak medium, it was perfect nice and juicy. The Madeira was stated to be their most popular chicken dish, it was very good. Although I still like the chicken marsala better. 

My man got the California cheese steak sandwich with fries. He was very satisfied with the sandwich and the fries. 

Both dishes were a nice size. I couldn't finish mine and neither could my man. We both took some home.

The Cheesecake Factory is good any city you're in. This one is great. My fiancé and I have been here a few times and we just went over the weekend for late anniversary/ celebration and it was so good! We ordered the egg role sampler which the salmon and avocado ones were our favorite. Then he ordered their best garlic pasta dish and he ordered me the tuna tatiki salad which was super yummy. Both of our dishes were incredibly tasty. My greens were great and the tuna was good too dipped in the sauce they give you. Exactly what I was craving! And his pasta dish O M G. SO GOOD!! It had peas, sundried tomatoes, mushrooms and bow tie pasta but the sauce was where it was at! Then we ordered our cheesecake of course and he ordered my usual raspberry white chocolate and then I ordered the salted Carmel and my goodness it was delicious! It was a little heavy but in a great decadent way. I wish I had some right now! And our waiter was super nice and funny and spoke proper English which is just something I always look for in people even though it's weird lol we asked him how he was doing and said doing well which most people say doing good which is technically improper English and totally makes my skin crawl so he was 5 star in my book for that alone and he performed excellent customer service! Don't remember this name sadly so I can't give him a shout out but every time we go here we always have wonderful wait staff. We love you guys!

Second time visiting here. Food was good and service was even better. They actually let us know they would soon do a shift change which most places don't. Both the outgoing (Alicia) and incoming (Bob) servers were refreshingly efficient. They not only provided everything we needed but, anticipated many. Kudos to both. And oh, the cheesecake that we took home was scrumptious.
PTT3f1d8_aBK3DNTPL1pXg: The French Quarter Cafe is one of Nashvill's hidden treasures, it is a small local and locals only type of bar.The Tuesday night Jazz band is the big attraction for me,about 15 men of talent playing a set of songs mostly arranged by members of the band and sure to feature several solos throughout each and every song. The band divides thier show into 2 parts of about an hour to an hour and 1/2 each and often features a "guest performer" or two throughout the night. Whe I say locals only dont get me wrong the bar is full of friendly patrons and is off the beaten path, for a Nashville bar,not your typical touristy type place with alot of polish and fancy gimics to get you in the door. Yes the wate staff can seem a little less than friendly but that is not the case more like very busy and doing thier best to try and keep everyone pleased though not everyone might perceive it that way.I looked at the $7.00 cover charge on a tuesday night to see the Nashville Jazz Orchestra as a deal figuring if they split the door with the owners of the club thats about $0,25 each the members get from me and thats a real bargain in this day and age , you should translate that to read these musicians are not here for the money but for the love of the music and it shows in the form of a nearly perfect performance !!! As for the food it is not that bad I would recomend the blackened red fish and the french fries are very good just dont tell your doctor/cardio/personal trainer friends you ate them.....

I've been to a handful of shows at this place, but I'd never sampled the food until today.  I decided to treat myself to a late lunch/early dinner and see what type of tasty Creole treats they had to offer.  While they don't typically open until 5:00, I showed up a little after 4:00 and they were happy to seat me.

French Quarter Cafe is a medium-sized restaurant/bar, and it's ideal for small shows.  The sound during performances is better than average, and they have acoustic insulation on the walls and ceiling, which helps quite a bit.  The artists that play there are typically singer/songwriter types, but they book a variety of musicians, so you can check their website to see what type of folks play there.

The staff at French Quarter is exceptionally friendly, and they served me really quickly.  A fellow named Tom (who I think is a manager or co-owner) waited on me, and he was very nice.  When he found out I'm in a band, he asked for my info so he could think about booking us some time.  Very kind fellow.

The food, which is obviously Cajun/Creole in nature, is decent but not as good as I had hoped.  I had the gumbo, and while it was tasty, it was relatively bland and definitely not anywhere near as spicy as I expected.  They also serve lots of sandwiches and a pretty good selection of beers.  While nothing at French Quarter is particularly expensive, nothing seems quite as tasty as you'd hope for a $12 meal.  Regardless, it's a pretty colorful local spot that's worth checking out if you've exhausted most of your options in the Five Points area.
Mouxr49lzmaIoMc1Maq70g: Easy to order online.  Fast and courteous delivery.  Their pizza is always super good.  We love the square deep-dish.

I always am reluctant to review a chain because I feel like they don't really need more publicity than they already get (and I prefer to support local places first), but I have to say... my husband and I have ordered pizza from Jet's MANY times and it's always been really good. As I've said before, I'm really pretty picky when it comes to pizza - it's hard to make it so bad that it's inedible, but it's also really hard to do it justice. I would say that Jet's is one of the top three pizza joints in Nashville, because when you get down to it, their pizza is supremely tasty and satisfying.

I've had all three crust options now (thin, hand-tossed, and deep-dish) and all are really yummy and vastly superior to your other chain pizza places. It's also nice that they have seasoning options for the crusts, so even those doughy bits are delicious and delightful. I think I huge sticking point for me when it comes to pizza is the sauce - I don't like too much of it, and I don't like it when it is too thick and too much like tomato paste. Jet's sauce is light and fresh-tasting and also kind of sweet; it gets no complaints from me. We always seem to get an order of Jet's bread as well, which is really just cheesy garlic bread and is totally superfluous. I mean, do I really need a side of bread with my slice of bread? Probably not, but it's so yummy, we can never resist. Just be warned that if you ask for a side of ranch with your bread, be sure to check when you pick it up because they ALWAYS screw up and give marinara instead!

Prices are good here, and there are always tons of coupons and deals that you can get off their website. I'll just mention them when calling and have never actually had to produce one while picking up, so it's nice that they're happy to have their customers reap the deals over and over again. 

There are a few other pizza places in town that I probably prefer over Jet's, but in terms of convenience and hours of operation, it's hard to beat this place. Great for when you want to dine from the comfort of your own home (or are having a late night craving); pizza without pants is where it's at!

I feel like we put Jet's to the ultimate test...Friday night delivery at 7pm.  They passed with flying colors.  The gentleman who took my order over the phone was super nice and warned me about the $2.50 delivery fee prior to finalizing my order.  The $2.50 seems a bit high, but it was well worth it.  The pizza arrived within 35 minutes piping hot.  We tried the 8 corner deep dish pie.  The sauce was perfect and they were generous with cheese and toppings.
9Vbz0D3pHIkKUrRXSvgrAw: Chill place to grab a few drinks and indulge in bar food. Often very smoky inside, so don't come if you can't handle that. It's a fun place to play pool and mess around on arcade games without spending too much.

I went in around 6 and had a few drinks to start off. Definitely a local dive bar. I had the prime rib sandwich which was awesome.  Next thing I knew it was 1 am and I was shit faced! I'll definitely be coming back next time I'm in town!

such a cool, hole in the wall, small cozy bar! they didn't have a few specific drink options, such as Jameson or Crown but had good substitutes! everyone kept to themselves and the jukebox was fun! for sure going back! very laid back!
6Fkm5UzXYRDa41fDW31NxA: Decent Chinese take out spot.  I came in on a Saturday night during dinner time.  There was no one eating there but the phone rang there every five seconds.  I ordered some food to go; chicken broccoli and beef lo mien.  As busy as they were I got my order pretty quickly.  Food was good.  I was happy.

My family eats here every now and then, and the food is great. We usually always deliver, because the actual restaurant isn't that pleasurable. But the food is great, usually a dinner plate will fill you up, but I do wish you would get a little more for your money, that would be my only issue. Other than that, I love Chinese food and will be going again!

I'm a regular customer, I get delivery every time. They are always fast to both my work and home. The food is always good. I usually get the Crab Rangoon or Fried Dumplings, General Tso's Chicken, Fried Rice, and Won-ton/Egg Drop Soup. It's always fresh and always delicious.
-mBc7M9Q3fEIxuUxwOleZg: I went into this lovely Cafe in the morning. It is across the street from the Pancake Pantry near Vanderbilt. It was not very full so I was very easy and quickly checked out. There are so many things to look at they have food ,salads, cakes, pastries cookies. Expect to pay a little higher price but not bad for the area you're in. I had their scrambled eggs he came with red potatoes and 4 pieces of some kind of bread with nuts in it that was really good. Eggs or cooked fully and not running. And I had a lemonade that had ginger in it that was really good. And I also purchased some cookies which turned out to be excellent. The atmosphere was nice and inviting and they also have free Wi-Fi. I've only been there twice but both times have been a pleasant experience I recommend you giving it a chance if you like small little Deli bakeries.
