This bakery is completely charming, as is the other location. 

Pastries that were amazing: 
Almond braid 
The raspberry & cheese filling one 
And salted chocolate 

They serve intelligentsia coffee, an awesome coffee company out of Chicago so my heart went pitter patter. It's outstanding coffee. 

If I lived in Nashville it would be one of my haunts. Definitely make a pit stop here!

This cafe is one of my favorite places for lunch when I am researching in the TSLA just up the hill.  Located inside the NPL it offers fresh sandwiches and salads.  Weekdays they have specials and on Saturday they have a cup of soup and grilled sandwich option. Try the grilled cheese with spinach and artichoke! When you finish you can also take home some great loaves of bread!
n_8tTXnYJpA_VcM7hnmyCQ:
Great little neighborhood bar.  Nice selection of local beers and a small menu but bursting with deliciousness.  The Centennial has a little patio and a decent amount of parking.  If you live in the neighborhood I would just recommend walking. 

Try the burger and the Turkey club.  

The crowd age range is between 30 and 60, casual dress.

One of our favorite spots now. The staff couldn't be friendlier, they always ask for your name. The bar is always clean. We've always had prompt service. They're one of the few places in the whole city that serve reasonably priced drinks and a meal. The first few times I ordered a burger because I thought it would just be normal bar food--but I've been so pleasantly surprised. Their fried green beans and queso--wow. Just order it. Their centennial wings have such a unique sauce. The spicy chicken sandwich is the only grilled chicken sandwich I have ever ordered more than once. My favorite though, is catching a Preds game on Taco Tuesday. One of the best tacos I have ever had. Props to the kitchen and staff!
Your favorite neighborhood dive bar. Great beers on draft. Great service. Has a few games and a breathalyzer. And all the love for Patrick Swayze.
EvjOPotlf9pXRXzVvN4GaA:
I've heard a lot of people give Caesar's a hard time, but I've always loved it, even back when it was next to the old Lionshead Cinema. It's hard to find good Italian in this town, when we're dominated by Olive Gardens and Bucca di Peppos, but Caesars, I agree is good unpretentious Italian cooking. 

They have all the favorites, including clams with white or red sauce. Actually, they do a pick your pasta, pick your sauce thing. The carbonara is great and comes with asparagus.

I'm also going to rave about the delivery. I just found out about it. Yay! Somewhere decent that delivers late. I will be taking advantage of that very soon.

The buffet is great too. Go if you haven't eaten for two days previously and you'll probably be able to try everything. There's pizza and at least five pasta dishes, plus salad, all for $5.99. Great deal. I will be going back for that. 

Caesar's has been there a long time, and there's a reason why. It's just good Italian food made by Italians.

I've been coming here since I first moved to Nashville. I've never actually gone there for anything other than their lunch buffet. I can't remember how much it is off hand, but I'm pretty sure it's under or right around $10 a person, without a drink. They have a decent sized salad bar, followed by a pasta bar of the usual suspects and then a rotating door of varies pizzas as well as garlic bread. There pizza isn't my absolute favorite but it is good. My real weakness is their garlic bread, its greasy and buttery and delicious! I definitely recommend it to any and everybody!

Like Italian on the cheap? Well your in for a treat with Caesar's. We go to lunch there often and get the buffet. For under $10 (w/soft drink) you can eat until your sick. The atmosphere feels a bit like they tried very hard to make you feel like your in Italy however it feels more like a old pizza shop to me. Pushing that aside its a wonderful place to take some friends and enjoy some extremely unhealthy food any day of the week.
6KoyHbN2qSUsioqrjXLeEg:
This new place is aptly named. If you have a question like "Where can I find a good freshly made in house pasta?"; "Where can I get a beautifully panseared Halibut?"; "Where can I get a dessert that is yummy but will not break that last button if I have already eaten an appetizer and an entree?", then this restaurant IS the Answer.

I ordered the lamb and pork ragu on top of in house made pasta. It was not as heavy as I worried it would be. The ragu was also very flavorful. I will say that while I enjoyed my dish, I got major FOMO after trying other's plates. The steak they have on the menu was tender and juicy. And as mentioned above, the halibut was well panseared. It was accompanied by Swiss chard and bacon (never bad when put together). That would be the only thing I would change about my dining experience.

The staff was attentive and gave great cocktail and wine pairing suggestions.

Stopped in for lunch and was pleased to see they were serving brunch.  I had the burger n fries and my wife had their sandwich.  We both kept glancing at each other with astonishment at how good it was.  It is a must try.

Absolutely LOVE this new neighborhood restaurant! Owners Chris and Victoria know how to put together a memorable menu and experience. My wife and I have been 2x and love the cocktails, apps and entrees. The tartar is amazing. The bourbon and gin cocktails complex and enjoyable. The pasta and fish a delight - and what's best of all is that all their intentionality in designing this experience actually all came together in a way that feels natural, warm and welcoming. And so much fun to bump into so many familiar faces. Answer is a great addition to Sylvan Park. #grateful #culinaryhappiness
hAh77Er8UtUGbD0Q147NTg: My wife and I loved this place. We had the vegetarian sampler. It was very delicious and very filling. This was one of those rare occasions when I was wishing I wasn't getting so full, so that I could eat more, because it tasted so good! We chose this restaurant because we were in the mood for Ethiopian food, but I felt like having a beer, and the other Ethiopian places we had been too, though also tasty, did not serve alcohol. The service was a little M.I.A. at first, and there were TVs on, but those seemed like minor things compared to the food and the kind and polite demeanor of the servers.

My very first experience with Ethiopian food and now, I am totally ADDICTED! It might look like a total hole-in-the-wall place, but I swear they have some of the best food I've ever eaten! I went with a friend and per her recommendation, ordered the Veggie & Meat Combo sampler. I was a little concerned about ordering a lunch entree that was $14.99, but oh my goodness, did I get my monies worth! I practically had to have my friend roll me out the door! The sampler came on a HUGE platter (like half the size of our table!) and had a generous spoonful of each item: Salad, cabbage, beets with fresh goat cheese, ground beef, lamb, chicken, split peas, chicken and carrots, and some of THE BEST collard greens I've ever eaten! And don't even get me started on the injera (Ethiopian yeasty flatbread)! It's been nearly two months since I visited and I can STILL remember how great everything was! Can't wait to go back!
Delicious! Heads up to those of you who are gluten intolerant: the injera is made traditionally (gf) & no need to order it separate. It's all Gf! So feast & enjoy!
GJUSPJThoiOd4FsXvMvB4w: Nice location, open doors to outside seating area. Tons of TVs for sports, college football season, typical barfood menu, prices are excellent, wings are a feature on the menu. Good place for some quick food and to watch a game.

I love this restaurant.  They have great prices, wonderful food, friendly service, a wide variety of beer selections and a great location.  My husband and I try to eat here as often as possible.  Parking is the only challenge - but it is achievable.

First off, the atmosphere here is awesome. What a wonderful place to not only enjoy some food and drinks, but to watch sports. They have enough TVs to play every game broadcasted on any given day, but I digress....

I was visiting Nashville for the Labor Day weekend and it so happened to be the opening weekend of college football. I arrive at Double Dogs off of a suggestion that they would be showing a particular game that I wanted to watch. I got there an hour early and sat at the bar with my back to the street, facing inside of the restaurant and all of the TVs. I waited til about 15 minutes before my game time to ask them to put it on. Unfortunately, they couldn't find it.

What ensued next is the reason for this review as I have never seen such wonderful customer service for something as minor as the only guy in the bar who wants to watch a singular football game. Mind you, this place is PACKED! People coming in, looking for tables, and trying to get seated every two minutes. The managers along with the hostesses, spent the next 30 minutes trying to find my game. I was even on the phone with Direct TV trying to figure this out. And the whole time, they kept a smile on their face and continued to look for my game.

How did it turn out? Direct TV didn't get the game I wanted to watch. So what did they do? They streamed it through the internet for me. I was astonished to say the least.

I would go back to Double Dogs 10 times out of 10 because at the end of the day, you know you will get wonderful customer service. Oh, and the wings and pizza were great to!

Thanks Double Dogs for going above and beyond the call of duty to satisfy lil ol me!
FNP4tlM-KYf--vTk_Gf9nQ: Fun concept. Well done. I got full before I was able to try all the variety constantly moving by. Desserts too.

My daughter and I had a fantastic experience there. The food was very fresh and we couldn't stop eating. I am looking forward to taking the rest of my family there when we go visit our daughter that just started her college at VU. It was entertaining to see the food go by you and reasonable pricing. The service was nice. We were never in a sushi restaurant that the food rides by you and you get to pick and choose. Like I said very entertaining and different. We are from the west coast and wish something like this was close to us. I recommend this place highly.

This place is cool! 

My wife and I have been looking for a sushi restaurant with the conveyer belt system for awhile now, as it creates a kind of novelty dining experience. We've only lived in Nashville a few months now, so when my wife saw their ad in the Scene, we jumped at the chance to eat mobile sushi. 

The overall atmosphere is semi intimate and somewhat communal with small tables and bright colors.

The conveyer belt winds through most of the restaurant offering small plates of sushi at interval pricing. Pricing per plate also varies by lunch or dinner times. We happened to go on my wife's birthday during lunch. When we got there, there were only about 5 other people which was good, because it meant that we wouldn't have to wait long for sushi or worry about missing out by being at the end of the train. 

During lunch, prices range from $0.99 to $3.25. And if you go on your birthday you get $15 off if you spend $50 or more. It's quite a deal during lunch, because we had a hard time reaching $50 to begin with. 

My favorite thing about the train is that they offer more than just sushi on the train. They have appetizers like edamame and Asian style salads, and desserts like cheesecake and brownies. 

Sushi Train also offers food other than sushi. They have a menu with noodle dishes and other items. They also have tables away from the conveyer belt for larger parties. 

I highly recommend checking it out.
G0glQr3Ygr5CGcACepWDSw: Food was great! You may want to take someone with you that speaks Spanish. Spicy pork was a good choice as well as the chicken from the grill out front.

Killer Tacos...I love the Tacos here!!
I am so huge into the corn tortillas with the beef and cilantro with lime slices and then the salsa verde on the side...Not your typical Taco Bell Mexican Fast Food !!!
They need to teach Taco Bell a few things about heading for the border !!! GREAT food!!!
and cheap!!!
Great food on a budget!! Order anything, you can't go wrong !!!

I had driven by this placed hundreds of times in year's past before I was adventurous.  Now that I seek new authentic places I stopped by for a quick lunch to go.  The window which you order from might be intimidating, but the food is great.  I ordered a platos taco and a carne asada burrito.  The taco was delicious with the onions and spicy pork.  The burrito was large so either order a few tacos or one burrito.  I recommend trying it out.
iKeQ5dOM1M89AFA-U3Mc_Q:
Doughnuts rolling down the line...litrerally raw dounuts drop in a fryer...float along...a flipper turns them over to cook the other side...up, up, up the conveyer belt under a waterfall of glaze....boom, scooped off into a box and handed to me....hot, fluffy, goey, sugary wonderfulness...

This krispy  cream runs the line continuously to package for area stores and if you want Hot Doughnuts, they intercepted them as they come down the line...can't get ant freasher than that...Hot coffee, hot doughnuts.....yummmmm

We came here for a quick treat and goodies first later. The woman was very nice. We ordered glazed dozen and she asked if we wanted to go now or wait couple minutes for fresh batch. That was really nice of her instead of giving us the cold ones in case. I then ordered an ice cream cone and got it for free. Either for being nice or maybe she thought i was military. Either way veryyyyy pleasant. Also ice cream cones are only $1.49 and taste better then dairy queen down the road and almost $3 cheaper!
Krispy Kreme has the best donuts out of all the chains. Their original glazed donuts melt in your mouth. The store sells them fresh and hot!
nX-AW2Y9VtJBpq304lNDNg: Never disappoints...awesome as always!!! First time at this location and it still brings me back to my high school days in El Cajon, California where the original Oscars started..Must come to Madison, TN...

Delicious! I had the fish and shrimp tacos and my husband had chicken, pork, and steak tacos. We enjoyed all of them. I was a little sad the guacamole didn't have more flavor. It was a bit bland and could use some love. I will definitely be back for my taco cravings though!

Update: Still my favorite Mexican food around! I brought a breakfast burrito to work and got my co-workers hooked! Now I call in an order and pick them up when everyone gets a craving! 
My husband also knows that if I don't want to cook, I'm going to want Oscar's!! The carne asada nachos, any burrito, any combination of tacos, a quesadilla... I love it all! Make sure you get the red sauce they offer. I could drink it!
The food is made fresh to order with quality ingredients. Things aren't just a pile of flavorless mush like the other mexican restaurants in town. This is flavorful and authentic. 
Keep up the good work Oscar's Taco Shop!
cDZPH0ZkHf2SMCqhrKUI2g:
Ribs and okra were delicious.  I'm more of a dry rub fan for ribs, but their ribs are a combination that was very good.
The "hot" wasn't too hot, just a little spicey and the flavor was great.

I had heard a lot about Smoke Et Al but every time I saw the truck, it was at a food truck event and I was lured by another delicious Nashville Food Truck offering. Not anymore. On Thursday they were at Centennial and having been in Philadelphia for a week, I was appreciating the weather. So I went over and got a Shoulder Sammy and potato salad. It was delicious. 

The meat was perfectly seasoned. I would venture to say its the best all around BBQ I have had since moving to Nashville. The bun was grilled and crispy. The sandwich was juicy but not so much that I couldn't pick it up and eat it. I had to eat it over the plate so I didn't bring any BBQ goodness back to work with me on my shirt. It was the perfect BBQ sandwich. I'm usually not a fan of vinegar based sales, but the one that topped the sandwich was delicious. And it was pretty to boot. The purple slaw made the sandwich look even more appetizing and added a nice tang and crunch to the sandwich. 

  Potato salad to me is like meatloaf, everyone seems to like their mother's best. I am no exception. That's why I was pleasantly surprised when I took a bite of their potato salad and loved it. It was really creamy and flavorful. 

I will be searching out Smoke Et Al in the future for more deliciousness,

Holy freaking cow.

Simply put, Smoke et al served me the best pork sandwich I've had in Nashville - ever. Their tagline is "beyond bbq" and while they still serve authentic bbq - perfectly balanced smoky flavor, tender but not overcooked meat, good crusty bark, and a sweet tanginess, they add a gourmet touch that other Nashville BBQ hasn't attempted. The light and tangy finishing sauce was not overwhelming. The three apple and cabbage slaw added a perfect crunch and light lift to the sandwich and would have been a better choice for side than the good potato salad.

And, it was the most meat I've ever received on a BBQ sandwich.

I'm looking to buy a parking lot near my office so Smoke Et Al can just park there permanently.
6PYnDIRhQjpHxvM-CQNVXQ: Even though this place is a franchise chain.... The food is exceptional. The burgers are by far better than a McDs or Sonic, no comparison. The shakes are old style as well.  Hence the name Wayback!

This place is great! We are on our way home to Texas from Tennessee and decided to take a chance and try a new place. Food is great, prices are great and the Customer Service is WONDERFUL! So happy we took a chance!

Great home style burger with lots of options for toppings/fixings. Kiddos got the most chocolatey milkshakes ever, and the fries were good. Definitely a family-run franchise with great service. Just wish I lived closer!
busrTWT7Umr-CrTYuadNKQ: Best burger I've had in a long time.  I couldn't have made it better myself.  Super fresh tomatoes crisp lettuce and über juicy meat cooked perfectly. I'll be back
Nicest folks, delicious home-cooked food with lots of variety and yummy ice-cream based treats. A true local casual indie. You feel like you've stepped back a few decades...in a good way.

Dairy King has been here for 62 years and owned by the same family for 40 years. That is awesome.  The name Dairy King vs Dairy Queen is also pretty funny. They have a great meat + 3 lunch and the ice cream stand has every frozen classic you could ever want. There are photos on the wall from 1979 when flood waters rose 10ft and then went back down in 30 minutes.  Twice in one year! Stop by this often overlooked Nashville institution.  It is really like going back in Nashville time. The family that runs it is very warm and they treat you like an old friend even if its your first visit.
c-vU89IMyKbDxPg0YMSVxQ: I have to give Piranha's five stars for great food, excellent service, plenty of room inside and TV's everywhere.  I ordered many different dishes to put out on the table and have our party try different things.  We ordered the hot chicken waffle sandwich, the pastrami, the donut burger, fried pickles, and chicken wings.  The hot chicken waffles were super yum!  I never had these before and I loved them.  The pastrami and donut burgers were very good.  I was not happy with the fried pickle spears as the batter was doughy, not crispy like at Robert's.  This didn't distract from the excellent review though because we enjoyed our time here and will go back again. I feel awful I can't remember our server's name.  Super nice girl, long blond hair, maybe 25.  We talked about Panama City Beach.  Thank you!  You were fantastic and we enjoyed your service.

Our son told how good the donut burger here was, so we wanted to see for ourselves.  The service was pretty good and the live entertainment didn't suck either.  First we all tried the breakfast shot, which was whiskey, butterscotch snapps, a shot of orange juice and a strip of bacon.  It was pretty good, and very smooth going down.  The donut burger was kind of messy, but very, very good.  We enjoyed our meal, and would love d visit again if back in town
Awesome place! We rolled up here and didn't know where to eat and someone recommended this place. Great fresh burgers, service was awesome and definitely will be back!
JuHa7dMYCZ9xci50M7nG1g:
As a one time experience, I'd give the place 4 stars. As a restaurant in Nashville, it's a 3 Star place.

We had a blast, but it was all an illusion. As we walked in, we ooh'ed and ahh'ed over the decor in the hall. But then I ran my hand over the embossed tiles on the ceiling and was disappointed to discover they were plastic. This was to be a metaphor for the evening. The food was only a hair above mediocre. The salads were limp and not fresh, but the scallops were perfection. The sea bass was wonderful. The steaks and side dishes were cold, and bland. The desserts all tasted as if they came from a box. Perhaps the dress code was an attempt to help us all believe in the magic?

The magicians were charming and wonderfully talented and the server was amazingly attentive. I'm so glad I got this amazing experience. Do I think I could've gotten a better meal for the same price? Heck yeah- I know I could. I probably won't be back. $650 for 5 people went a long way for us. Definitely worth a splurge or great for tourists.

I would give this place six stars if I could!!!  I went here with three friends on a busy Friday night and loved every minute of it. It was so busy we had to sit at the bar but our bartenders were helpful and fun and encouraged us to walk around and see the various magicians up close. When our food was ready they came and got us!  There were several magicians working the room and I witnessed the some of the most amazing tricks that I have ever seen. Once our meal was finished we made it to the "magic show" which is a small show in a 40 seat theatre which was an excellent way to finish off our evening. House of Cards does run a little expensive but keep in my mind that it includes some of he best magic your going to see anywhere.
What a perfect evening!! The food was fantastic and the entertainment even better. Our server was a lot of fun; the "formal" attire added to the ambiance of the evening-magical.
EPZFFN08cNd_tfycvhCvUQ: I really love this place. This is my hangover recovery lunch time spot! It's never crowded or noisy, and I always feel better when I leave here after a meal. You just can't beat their lunch time prices. The sushi is always very consistent as well seeing as it's always the same chef. $10 for sushi, salad, and soup. Yes, please.

Having been here several times now and tried a variety of sushi I must say this place is pretty good!  The service is great, quick and very personable and the food is excellent!  If you are in the mood for sushi I highly recommend this little hidden spot in Bellvue.  Won't say it is the best in the world but by far it is my favorite for sushi in Nashville and I don't think you will find any nicer staff as far as service goes.

This was quite the pleasant surprise!  I'd walked by here many times and never gone in because it just didn't look very welcoming, or like it would be great quality.  And I'll be honest, when you're inside, it still doesn't.  But then you order and get your food.  And it's fantastic!  Probably the best sushi I've had in Nashville (not that I've had a lot here).  Fish was fresh, I really liked the creativity of some of the rolls (while not resorting to cream cheese and other very non-japanese ingredients).  Service was great.  Boyfriend doesn't eat sushi, but he enjoyed is hibachi chicken dinner, which came with soup and a salad.  And actually, the miso soup was probably the worst thing about the meal.  It was just so flavorless.

I'm not sure I'll dine in again.  Ambiance was really quite lacking.  But I made sure to pick up a carry out menu as we were walking out the door and I'm certain that I'll be ordering very soon!
Nn6FKnj5smJ_Ihx0uD4gjg: Great local pub!  Awesome service, knowledgeable and friendly staff.  Everyone that works here really takes pride in the food and the atmosphere.  They have yummy daily specials and great happy hour prices!  The Pizza Burger was amazing, and the wings can't be beat.  It's my favorite place, and totally worth the drive if you live outside of the Bellevue area.
Great neighborhood hang out! Even though families are welcome, it's STILL a neighborhood bar...language and cocktails fly freely.

If you want fried food and friendliness, there's really no better place.

"Alright already!" I wanted to say when the bartender raved for the third time about the hand breading on the fried pickles---but then i tried them. I would have told me at least four times.

The tater tots are obnoxiously crunchy and delicious. The buffalo chicken tenders are stupid tender. 

The beer flows and flows, but don't expect taps fancier than Lagunitas IPA. This is not the place for a fine rye Manhattan.

We'd have gone back just to hang out more with Brian, the bartender, but I was worried I'd eat another 650 tater tots.
ts3sub_rBt9AlHLFrkBOHw: Great prices! Fast service! You can even order by the slice which is awesome! I will definitely return!!

Spoiler alert: This pizza isn't actually perfect. However, it is pretty freaking tasty! When I was in law school my roommate and I started a tradition in the fall where we would order a large pepperoni to go and then split it while watching football all day on Saturday. That tradition bled over into basketball season. Then the scale brutally insisted that the tradition must die. Some of the best mozzarella I've ever had on pizza, I don't know what is different about it, but something is. Excellent crust and sauce as well, all-around they do things right!

Bar none, my absolute favorite pizza in town.   I am a thinner crust kind of gal and I like a sauce with nice balanced flavor that doesn't over take the pizza.  I like a generous amount of cheese and a crust with a little bit of a bite to it that will hold up to the sauce.  Pizza perfect meets all those criteria and then some.  

My favorite is the perfect which would be like a 'supreme" at one of the chains.  It's got the meats and the veggies and they do a great job getting a little of everything all over the pizza.  I also like the blanco.  The pizzas are always good and while they may not have exotic toppings, they stick with what they do well and they deliver great pizza every time.

I think their prices are really reasonable for what you get.  The side salads are good and of course like any self respecting pizza place, they have root beer on the fountain.  They also have beer and all is priced reasonably.  If you are from some place known for their pizza like, NY, then I think you will find Pizza Perfect is about as close as you will get to what you are used to.
T7PZ97fGBG3AUz2aS69-9w: Best.Mexican.Ever. (Or at least in town). Looking more closely at the bad reviews here points to a bunch of out of toe era headed to the airport (& who's in a good mood running late to the airport, right?). Listen: this place is PACKED with locals 24/7 for a reason: solid consistent quick food and service. Good enough for nashvillians: good enough for Obama; good enough for this guy. 'Nuff said
I was in a hurry, so I just ordered the taco  salad . My order was ready in less than 5 minutes! The cooks seasoned the ground beef well. I was impressed with the salad. The service was fantastic!

We must have had the stars align given the other reviews... Server for dinner was fantastic. Food was superior to BOS. Nachos were served with more than ample portions of toppings, enchilada sauce hit the spot (red and green). Would recommend.
-V4aH1YR67gdcakjCjU0bg:
I was in for Dinner last Friday.   The restaurant just re-launched and was pretty dead.  I was not expecting a whole lot since the place has been around for a while and there were virtually no customers.  I figured the food was going to be terrible, but the ambiance was so nice I figured I would give it a shot.  

After 10 minutes on the patio  I was enjoying the beach bar atmosphere so much that I had decided that if the food was edible I was going to come back again, even if just for drinks.  To my surprise the food was actually fantastic.  From what the server told me they just got a new chef and things were improving dramatically.  I got the brisket which was moist and tender and had a nice pink smoke ring.  My wife got the cuban sandwich with a side of sweet potato fries.  The cuban was fantastic! and the fries were the most crispy and delicious crinkle cut sweet potato fries I've ever had.  They go great dipped in the sweet bbq sauce.

After dinner we decided to hang out and have a drink in one of the many flat screen-covered lounge areas on the patio.  Great place to have an inexpensive meal and/or a few drinks with friends.  I will be going back soon!

Went for lunch today and had the 1/2 chicken plate $9
I was thoroughly impressed at not only the great flavor and texture of the chicken but the portion size was huge. I will definitely be going back to try the smoked wings. As a Nashville native if you're looking for good smoked BBQ at a good price definitely give this place a shot!

This place is fantastic. We went last night for a quick bite before an event and we could not have been happier with the food, the service, or the atmosphere. We had a ScoutMob, which is what encouraged us to try this spot as opposed to any other place along 21st.  We really weren't expecting anything special, but it's always fun to try a new place and support a local business. The first thing I noticed is their menu - only about 10 items. I love a place that does a few things really well, instead of a lot of things poorly. In this case I was not disappointed because as soon as we all took our first bites, we had one of those moments where you look around at your friends and think, "What?! Is this really happening?" 

I had the Cuban with wedge fries and it was absolutely delicious. The pork was moist and flavorful, the ham was just salty enough, the cheese was melty, and the pickles were crisp. Plus, they serve the mustard on the side, which I prefer. My husband had the pulled pork sandwich with the white BBQ sauce and chips and it was equally incredible. The BBQ sauce was good, but he barely ate it with his sandwich because the pork was cooked to perfection. Plus, he really liked the bread - and trust me he is a bread snob. Our friend had the burger with a freakin' fried green tomato on top. She was just as impressed as we were. 

We sat in the main dining room at a bistro table and it was cozy and comfortable. Our server was friendly and attentive, but not annoying. Honestly, I can't say enough positive things about it. I can't imagine how any of the other reviewers had such a different experience. 

Another great surprise is that they have a frequent customer punch card. My husband already wants to go back for lunch this week. The only reason I didn't give it 5 stars is because I feel like we need to go a few times, try other items, and sample some of their adult beverages first. 

Go grab the ScoutMob and give this place a shot. You won't be disappointed.
nTvkfKB5XKrXhC41XsVwsg:
Here is another 5 Star for the food only....for the meat Shawarma to be exact. This Kurdish Middle Eastern Market seems to be getting more attention lately for the Shawarma as they keep running out of meat earlier. 

I stop by for a small beef/meat shawarma, loaded, spicy and with garlic sauce, when I am around that area. Check out the pic as it shows the freshness of the product.

They recently added a new food stall with a new variety of foods, but I keep coming for the shawarma!

This is the best shawarma in Nashville.  It's spinning fresh daily and the bread is made in house.  I moved to Nashville from LA, arguably one of the shawarma capitals of the US, and this place rivals some of the places in LA.  It's not overly salty and the flavor is nuanced.  The homemade bread is a bonus.  I've never been to a shawarma place that makes its own bread in house.

A small serving area in the back of a grocery serves the best shawarma that I have found in Nashville.  Very nice baked goods, we tried the cheesebread and zatar, both were tasty.  Service was excellent and very friendly.
vn_C5NikAwROmsxkZVpoPA: I got to craving a calzone and saw this place out my hotel window. Ordered a calzone with pepperoni, sausage, spinach & onion and my mom ordered they're boneless wings with sweet bbq flavor. Calzone was really good - cheesey, golden crust & decent marinara sauce. Boneless wings looked more like popcorn chicken but was told chicken and flavor was really good. Got this for less than 20 bucks so I think we came out ahead.

Believe it or not this place had really awesome pizza, the sauce was delicious,  the pizza was hot,  even the canollies were good, At least I found a decent pizza place here, that's a really hard thing to do when your away from home.

So its 2AM and I skipped dinner and naturally, I'm very hungry.  I stopped at Vandy's Quiznos (closed at 1am), looked at Papa Johns (done at 1am), and Jimmy Johns (closed at 12am).  I decided that it was time to go back to my dorm and order something online.  The only restaurant that was open at 1:30am, offered delivery, and took the Vandy card was Roma Pizza.  The price was really cheap!  I paid less than $14 for a large house salad ($4) and meatball sub ($6) + tax and tip ($4).

The house salad was delicious and really big!  It had iceberg lettuce (no nutrients, but tasty), topped with mozzerella and cheddar graded cheese, diced onions and tomatoes, pieces of green and black olives, and green peppers.  With my choice of dressing of ranch.  I love this salad!

The meatball sub was also pretty good. It had a nice thin but tasty and firm bread, their tomato sauce is perfect (it reminds me of my favorite marinara sauce that was discontinued because it was $20 per small container) and the provolone cheese works well together. The meatballs are flat or maybe cut in half, which makes it a lot less messy.  My complaint is that the meatball didn't exactly taste the way I wanted it to and it tasted weird at first bite. But I really liked it anyway.

The proportions were so big, I didn't come anywhere near eating half of it!

I think I've rediscovered Roma Pizza!  This is now my preferred late night restaurant (up to 2am on weekdays and 4am on weekends).  I also have to try the ziti because I vaguely remember it being my favorite.  If I happen to find myself hungry beyond the 2am marker on a weekday, though, white castle is it!  They're open 24/7 and I think I'm addicted to their cheeseburgers. I have yet to find a healthy or possible vegetarian restaurant that is open later...

Also, the delivery guy was so nice!  He's clearly delivered to Vandy many a times.  He knew the difference between Vandy and Barnard hall! Yay I don't have to walk. (just say by the dumpster or by the circle)
MfIBwI-7nXfmyQNFYFeeWg:
Coming from SoCali, and being a big fan of pizza, I love CPK! My husband and I have been dining there when going on nice dates for years. We even have our own CPK cookbook! Needless to say, we're experts of the restaurant. 
The food at this location has always been wonderful in our experience. The customer service has been outstanding. I have been so pleasantly surprised at the willingness the waiters have to bend over backwards to meet our needs. Some of our favorite menu items are no longer available, but they will often still make them for us! Love this place and would highly recommended.

I've been here a couple of times now and this is a great place to get some pizza. I've had the Sicilian and the BBQ Chicken and liked them both. It's in a good location with plenty of parking and has a great atmosphere.

Enjoyed the pear and Gorgonzola pizza...looks a little weird to have a salad ON TOP of a pizza but it is tasty.  The avocado BLT egg roll appetizer was a unique bite that we enjoyed sharing too.  A little slow on the drink refills, but overall a very nice experience with a large group.
SIc1YcqUBMdXO3LxgCur2g: The place with everything.  Breakfast, lunch, coffee, beer, kamboucha.  Nice ambiance with a wide variety of people.  They have a good selection of drinks and baked goods.  Andrew was very helpful and talkative.  He recommended a good beer for me to try.  I had the prosciutto and basil sandwich.  It was very good.  I had the side chips which I guess could have substituted with a salad that would have paired better, my fault.  Seems like a good place to hang out any time of the day and I would recommend to others.
Excellent place for any occasion! Great coffee, tea, beer, cocktail, wine, and food options. Friendly staff and great location in a fast growing neighborhood.

I like Frothy Monkey. Good coffee, good menu of food. But the 12 South location is a no for me - just too, too crowded, no parking, loud, busy, etc. But I'm a fan of the downtown location (I can walk from my office) and the new location in downtown Chattanooga (my mom lives there), and I was excited to try out the new Nations location. It's HUGE. And bright. And airy. And modern. And otherwise unchanged from the other locations - good breakfast, good coffee, good service, good company.
TkwOJiETWAMj6s_KlqJT4Q: This is a fairly new location for Waffle House, and I been here a half dozen times. It stays pretty busy here. Most of the times I call ahead and grab my daughter a couple of waffles for breakfast. But today I sat down and had breakfast with my pops. Food was great. I had the all-star breakfast with smother, covered, and peppered hashbrowns in place of the grits. All the food was prepared well and I had no complaints. The food was out super quick, less than five minutes from us ordering. Service was great, although they seem to hustle you out of there. Meaning as soon as we looked finished they cleaned off our table, but insisted there was no rush. Yes, I can see we are welcome, we will be moving our fat rears ASAP! All in all, the staff here are nice, service is good, as well as the food. Until they prove me other wise this place is a four star.

What a pleasant experience. Good food, EXCELLENT service. The staff here was amazing! So so kind, friendly, hospitable and again the service was outstanding! I will definitely be back to this Waffle House location. Thank you to Angie (our waitress) and all the other employees for making us a delicious breakfast and warming our hearts at the same time :)

This Waffle House was so incredibly pleasant from the moment we walked in the door on! Everyone was SO friendly in there, and greeted us with truly cheerful smiles and conversation. Every person, every step of the experience was wonderful. Angie was our server, and was patient with us, attentive, and so very kind. I don't write many reviews, but this Saturday morning breakfast was one of the best experiences I have had in a long time. The staff in there truly seemed like they were all family. No fake put on corporate "family" or "team" environment, but FOR REAL family. They all seemed like they were genuinely having such a fun time together and enjoying life and their work, and they provided the best service! Amazing! Such a nice experience. Thank you sweet Waffle House people!!!
7zt3kcVAcSoMP_XffifV8w: Infuriated after a visit to BookmanBookwoman down the block, this place was a surprising and welcome departure from an establishment disrespecting its products. Quite the contrary, in fact...with the elder owner engaging us in a length conversation and dropping gems like "Hog Heaven", "Helloooo", and "Hell Yeah", the dialogue was as superb as the selection. Sadly it's easy to get great advice here and purchase these solid investments in your culinary future elsewhere, but at least get some fresh coffee on your way out. (It's also not the place if you don't have the time/patience or mood to have said conversation -- you'll hear any of the generations utter the same phrase to different people)

I bought loose tea here today(which I'm drinking right now, pretty good!). I went in simply because I was strolling Hillsboro village, like I do about once a year, and the cool espresso machine in the window caught my eye so I though I'd stop for a minute. About 45 minutes later I left with a earful of knowledge and opinions from the extremely talkative man behind the counter, and a bag of tea. He closes his eyes when he's talking to you a thousand words a minute probably because he's so afraid you're going to walk off. And that would kill him. His goal apparently is to educate you on whatever it is you want to talk to him about. Very smart. I don't think he cares if you buy anything or not but he sure did convince me to never buy from over-priced TeAvana again. And the difference between cheep coffee and not.. And how they flavor the fruit in fruit teas.. And he correct way to tune a piano... And how tv is bad for you..  I eventually had to just walk off but I'll go back! When I have more time and want to learn some more.

Loved this quaint little store. When we walked into the store I thought yikes this place  is so unorganized. We noticed a lot of name brand kitchenware that is difficult to find. The owner of the store answered all our questions and helped us select our Cooking knives. He was very, very knowledgeable
and seemed to really appreciate our purchases. They were a little eccentric but that really added to the charm. I thought the prices were also inline with other kitchen stores I had been to.
ZyIUgLSqbfDRMGumMLjmUg:
my wife and i ran into the owner at a store. we exchanged greatings. and went along with our morning. we did not know he was the owner operator of the buisness. we did our research and decided to go and try it out. i love a family owned buisness,.i will try them all
. 
i will say without a dought love the food, it's not canned, it's cooked. we will go back
.
the only reason we give them 4 stars is the service. its slow and non attentive.

"Hidden Gem" is the term that I would use for Smiley's.  It's a down-home Meat and 3 spot with a cute (I'm a guy but I'll still use that term), clean dining room.  Grandma's recipe hotcake cornbread brought out to you first thing (think comfort food version of chips 'n salsa) whets the appetite for freshly and lovingly prepared entrees.  Well maybe entree is the wrong word...grub may be more appropriate.  Owner John Smiley is quick to come out and meet and greet anyone brave enough to venture out to Inglewood's newest Meat and 3.

My friend said the food rivals Loveless Cafe.

I love the food! Go on Wed or Thurs to get the meatloaf or the baked chicken. I always get the mac n' cheese, so good!

Overall, I've gone back 3 times and I tell everyone about it! Definitely eat here!
wmIHzXwvMt0awpZ3F69h5w: First stop after we landed in Nashville International Airport, and boy was it worth it. Their Uncle Sam Burger was shear perfection. It was by far the juiciest burger I've ever eaten. A must go to place!!!
Had the Jumpback Jack burger tonight at a neighborhood festival, really fantastic! Flavor off the charts, burger was juicy and the bun was spot on. Highly recommend this food truck!
Stopped at their new location on Bransford Ave in Berry Hill. The best burgers in town!!!  Also, tried the pork rinds, they are awesome too!
EUjzFmMEabdojQq7CPHHYA:
This Las Maracas recently opened in the Kroger shopping center in Bellemeade. It's a pretty average Mexican restaurant and has a good assortment of Vegetarian options to choose from. I ordered an enchilada, a burrito, and a side of rice. The rice portion was huge and delicious. The burrito and enchilada were both just average. They were both served covered in the same sauce which was a bit too sweet for my liking. 
 The evening that Husband and I were there seemed to be exceptionally loud. There were a lot of families with young kids but even after they all left it was loud. Maybe it's just the acoustics of this hall shaped dining room. This place was clean and had a high health score but I don't think we will be back. Tequila's which is located just at the other end of Hillwood is soooo much better and close by.

In one word: Reliable.  

When I'm craving Mexican food, it's b/c I'm really hungry.  And the mark of a great Mexican place is that they bring you your food Hot and Fast.

It's a great deal.  And there's no such thing as "Gourmet Mexican."  If you find yourself at a place that says it is, be sure to order the "El Ripoff" cuz that's what you're looking to get.  

Mexican ingredients are inexpensive, and therefore Mexican food shouldn't have high prices.  

If you haven't noticed, so many great Chinese, Korean, Italian, and other ethnic restaurants have Mexican ppl cooking your food.  It just makes sense that real Mexican restaurants would be the same way (hopefully that's not offensive, it's not intended to be).  

This place is a good deal.

This was our first visit to Las Maracas, and the staff was as friendly as it gets. I ordered the vegetarian combo G (a cheese quesadilla, spinach and cheese enchilada, and bean burrito). It was delicious. The white queso is creamy and flavorful. The top shelf margarita was tasty as well - it has Grand Mariner in it. 

This isn't fancy gourmet Mexican, but it is extremely satisfying and economical. The atmosphere is decorative, clean, and very friendly. My fiance and I both agreed that it was the best Mexican we'd had in Nashville yet. We'll definitely be back!
74OprXnWEpLcTwxjt5ioWQ:
It can be a bit daunting to choose your food when presented with a panoply of trucks, pushing all manner of delicious dishes out their windows. 

Such was the scene when I visited Cheekwood during my trip to Nashville over the weekend. 

But after doing a couple laps of the trucks, carefully checking out each menu, and looking at the food that many of the other diners were walking away with, I and my husband eagerly approached the last truck in the line, Banh Mi & Roll Factory. 

And I'm so glad we did. This was the right choice all the way around.

I ended up going with the bún vermicelli noodle bowl with chicken. I had seen another couple happily slurping noodles out of their bowls, and on such a mild spring day, cold noodles with protein sounded delightful.

Happy to report: This dish lived up to my expectations. The noodles were lightly chilled and cooked perfectly -- not too mushy or stiff. And the additional ingredients -- including the chicken, cucumber, sprouts, fresh basil and carrots -- were plentiful and full of flavor. The chicken in particular was better than I was expecting, perfectly seasoned. 

On the side I got the gi cun Vietnamese spring rolls with shrimp. These were a bit different than the spring rolls I'm used to -- actually better, in fact. Maybe the basil was fresher in these than what I typically get elsewhere because these had a bit of an herbier flavor to them. The peanut sauce was also heavier in hoisin sauce, rather than peanut butter, which I also now know I prefer. 

To drink, I got a Thai iced tea. This was sweet and slightly rich. 

We didn't have to wait long for our dishes -- another food truck plus. 

If I lived in Nashville, I could see stops at the Banh Mi & Roll Factory truck becoming a thing. Oh well, I'll just have to hope I come across them whenever I return and urge others to try them out in my absence.

I tried their vermicelli dish today and it was perfectly sweet and sour with lots of crunchy vegetables. My favorite thing was the pickled daikon and carrots. I chose chicken with my dish but really wished it was grilled chicken (like at vietnamese restaurants) instead of the sweet, shredded chicken. Overall, I still enjoyed the food and look forward to trying out their roast pork banh mi, Thai tea, and coffee next time.
Great food every time! Fantastic fresh ingredients. Perfect service! Bang Mi for lunch and spring rolls for dinner today!
U7esGceQIZ_1IG6Ib0c1lQ: An employee left their boots at the register and there were several loud friends of theirs at the register, but I guess we have to be gentle to fragile millennials. The ownership must be great, because the burrito was great. I just wish it came with a few chips for the price. The soda was also flat.

I don't turn down a friends suggestion to grab a bite at Chipotle.  They are fast and food is really good.  I have had their burritos, tacos, and salads.  It is all good.  Working downtown, there are plenty of lunch choices, but always know that I won't be disappointed when I drive over to West End.

Also, their music is awesome.  Need a "Chipotle" pandora channel.  :)

First time here and glad I finally tried it! It was really good and fresh. They definitely give you a ton of food as well. Place was clean and people were very fast and friendly. I'll have to try this place more often!
Xnbhi4zBotblvFU1X1iqMA: This is very nice authentic Resturant. I tired the buffet and the regular menu. The meat was so tender. The atmosphere was really nice as well.
The food was very good. The vegetables were roasted which tasted very good. They said they might remodel soon and it does need it. I would definitely recommend it. Service was good.
Best middle eastern restaurant in Nashville. Highly recommend.
W42YA0DMCQiJg8xahHZCdA: This was my first time at Green Leaf's. I went during the week to grab a to-go lunch. Staff was friendly and everything was fresh and delicious. My only wish would be more vegetable options for salads.
The Far East salad was good.  I asked for the grilled chicken instead of the soupy chicken that was supposed to come on it.  Green Leafs can be a healthy option in the mall food court.

Friendly staff and service, I ordered my food and it came up quickly. My salad was really good, I got it with honey mustard dressing.  I ordered the turkey chipotle panini and it was no mistake I really enjoyed it! I will definitely be coming back!
Cot1dK6clvB6WkCgrrXA2w: This Arby's is great, staff is delightful, food is ready hot and quickly! Definitely recommend this Arby's as your go to bite!
I like the new turkey sandwiches here, but good old fashioned roast beef is never out of style!

Very friendly staff at this location. I usually go in at their lunch rush. They are able to handle lots of customers in a short time span which is refreshing. I am obsessed with their Gyro specials. You get two Gyros of your choice for six dollars not including tax. You can mix and match. They have Greek style, Turkey, and Roast Beef.  This location is always clean and bathrooms tidy.
4M36VEsL-XV0JQ50g12EfQ: I eat here once a week. I love their teriyaki chicken-on-a-stick. I order it every time i go. The food is great, always hot and you get a lot for your money. And I think the girl behind the counter is cute, lol. Never had a problem with hair in MY food. Best chinese food in Donelson.
These guys take pride in their food and it tastes that way.  I really like that they offer sushi too. Definitely enjoy their food.

Food was surprisingly good!  Got 3 dishes for my family of 5.  First was Szechuan chicken.  Nice spicy kick to it, very good ingredients.  Next was house special lo mein.  Very good, if maybe a little short on the meat & shrimp.  Last was beef & broccoli.  Very good quality beef, good tang to the broccoli.  Overall, I'd give it a hearty thumbs up.  As Arnold said, "I'll be back..."
lmQEIb_gWJ5uaMGd_DpF-w: Great service and relaxing atmosphere. The selection of cocktails and wines are interesting but I enjoyed my mangria.

Great addition to east nash! It's such a unique concept with a wine shop and a fun winery all in house. The cocktails and wine were great. We enjoyed appetizers and pizza that wee delicious and not overpriced. Everyone loved the Italian dip! Highly recommend and a much needed change for east nashville places!

Wow what a cute place!

When i first walked in i sat at the bar. Neither bar tender acknowledged me for at least 10 minutes or so. So u finally asked for a menu. 

I then asked about getting food brought out to the patio and they the bartender made a statement that was rather rude.. along the lines of noooooo because they need to keep up with "people". I'll leave it at that. So finally i get the chance to order and i did. The frosè. It was so good and cute!

My dinner guest arrived and we ordered the Bianca it was sooo good and then a dip trio. Which was so nasty. 

Rebecca made everything better! People like her make me want to come back to places even when the 1st impression rubs you the wrong way. Overall i would recommend this place to my friends and i plan on going back!
MVpEYpq5tBZpbMhWuoNdPg:
Two words: Cheese Dip.

The white cheese used in their dishes is what makes it. I've been a Las Palmas fan since my parents first took me when I was about thirteen years old (so almost twenty years - whoa!!) and I have yet to find another Mexican food restaurant anywhere that quite hits the spot.  Now I live in Seattle and really miss out when I'm having a Mexican craving.  It's the best, hands down!

I'll start by saying that I'm not really a huge fan of Mexican food... in fact, I went on a mission trip to Mexico for a week and a half and disliked the food so much that I kind of starved myself.  finicky? yes!  Anyway, my parents came to visit me from out of town and this was literally the only sit down place that was open this late in West Nashville.  I was kind of reluctant to go, but my parents kind of dragged me in (they are a bit more adventurous than I am).  

At first look, the place seemed a bit sketchy (not gonna lie... I was scared), but I've come to realize that this place is really a hidden gem.  The staff there are really friendly and they try to encourage you to speak Spanish (which can be amusing when my dad tries to prove his skills).  The prices are really good and the atmosphere is that of a local pub (do we have that in Nashville??) ... its just a comfortable place I would go to take my girlfriends for a few drinks and to hang out and yet at the same time it would be comfortable enough for a family with young children to come.  

And the food was so tasty.  The salsa is great on their homemade chips!  And I ate everything on my plate, which was HUGE.  The steak fajitas with onions, peppers, and guacamole was so good. mmmm. I'm craving it right now :P

Some side notes, apparently the salsa can be made super spicy, so for spice enthusiasts, like my brother, this is definitely something to look forward to.  Also, while some people have complained about the slow service, I'd have to say that when I went, they were very attentive...

This place is located off of Charlotte and can look a little questionable but it's a little hidden gem if you are looking for some good mexican food this side of town. My husband has been here 3 times already and I finally went with him. We went on a Sunday for lunch. 

Food was great. I had a enchillada and my husband had the chili verde. The service is not the fastest but it's not slow either. The place was half full and the waiters were very nice. 

I know we will be going here again since it's close by. 

Check it out if you are in the area and want some Mexican food.
f3ES0mojElMhSZCrwHiM8w: Solid 4star for a casual gyro spot.  Clean reataurant, nice workers. Happy to build it any way you want it.  A lot of gyro places come and go around town.  This one's been here for a while and I hope it stays.

This place has really great cucumber salad! I mean it's not to die for but it really good and if you like a hint of spice you will want more! The environment is friendly and I would come back with more family and friends. Great place for lunch and dinner.
I love this place. Good every time. It's really a neighborhood gem. We go all the time and it's fast, great and affordable.
V1o_BeHcTehuFD7ue-8H2A:
The Well is a fantastic example of a coffeehouse that you can make your home. The staff is friendly and will remember you, the atmosphere is nice (Music is usually a bit softer and not in your face like many places), tons of natural light, they have an excellent affordable drip coffee (Huge win for me), and a great mission behind the business!

Minor suggestions:

- The coffee drip isn't served hot enough compared to other coffee shops. It's totally drinkable when first served, but gets lukewarm really quick. I often have to get a refill just to get warmer coffee. Maybe the thermoses aren't good enough or they wait too long before making another batch?

- Food has improved a ton in the last year, but still needs some work to be on par with nashville's other coffee shops. (Granola in particular is delicious, but has way too many rock-hard cranberries. Would love more bagel flavors & cream cheese flavors. Would love an equivalent to Bongo Java's "Bongo Basic")

- I think it would personally be nice if the upper room was a more dedicated quiet space to work. In the morningtime, it usually acts as this anyway, but things can get noisy fast if a group of people move to the couch seating area in this room.

While visiting my parents here in Nashville, my dad insisted that we go to The Well. I work at Starbucks back in California, so I know coffee pretty well, and this place has it down. My dad was looking for some new beans to take home, so we browsed the wall for a couple minutes deciding which beans to get. We decided on the Brazil. 
In regards to drinks, my dad got espresso, and I tried their iced chai. My dad loves their espresso, and says it gets sweeter as it sits. I was too scared to take a sip (I tried espresso at my Starbucks one time and it was awful!). My chai was great. Not too sweet and not too spicy. It was a little more mellow than some chai's I've had (way different than Starbucks, if that's what you're used to), but I definitely enjoyed it. 
While the barista was making our drinks, my dad was asking what kind of beans he was using for the espresso that day. He explained which ones he was using and described the flavors, and the nuances in the different beans they use depending on the day. He was very knowledgeable, and definitely knew how to describe flavors well. If possible, I will be returning here while I am still in the area!
Great hip place for locals to go and work or hang out. The service is great and you have to try the iced coffee brew "cold brew" if you love coffee.
tX7fAS6C9vArD88yaPeqnw: This meat and three is a neighborhood staple!  The food is always hot and fresh. The waiters are very friendly and attentive. I had a chicken sandwich and it tasted as if it was grilled in my own back yard. Go here!

I really like this restaurant. It has that wonderful mom and pop, I value every customer feel. In particular, Kim is great and an absolute pleasure. The food is always prepared well and tasty. They food reminds me of eating in someone's house not just another restaurant and I love it. They don't always have milk, I take it in my coffee, but that's not a selling point for me. Good customer service is any business's best selling point in my book and they've got it in spades. With all the trendy places in this part of the city that make you feel like just another "customer," it's nice to find a place that still cares about each person. I ordered pancakes, grilled cheese with bacon, grits, scrambled eggs, and hash browns. The hash browns, which are a very rather small portion, weren't that great, but everything else was delicious. I will definitely keep coming back.

My parents, daughter, and I went in around 11 am on a Saturday morning. We experienced great food and friendly servers. Unfortunately, some of the patrons felt the need to curse and discuss private things loudly like it was midnight and they were at a bar. My 6 Year old daughter was right beside me and I thought that it was extremely inappropriate for adults to carry on in that manner. I'll have to check the environment before dining with my family again.
piPnsKY7GE-B5KVQs8ubsA: Café Fontanella is a great place for some good Italian food or pub grub, Bring your sweetie and listen and dance to the Thursday night fishing club

Best Italian food in Nashville!

Terrific menu, prices,and staff. In a cozy country spot, and it's so great, me and my significant other went for a walk after dinner. Really glad we randomly chose this restaurant.

Also, live music. And not just the sleazy, margaritaville kind. Really good, interactive artists that care about their music and their audience. 

Can't recommend this place enough.

Stopped in for a drink and munchies before we toured the Mandrell mansion. They had some interesting beer options and some examples from the Pritchard distillery which was just down the road. Friendly bartender and patrons made the wait very pleasant. We'd return for a full meal, the food was that good.

The bartender said that they had outstanding Italian food. We would have returned but the fact that we were staying in Hendersonville made it a long trek for a return meal.
RqBsawBnbfChhSp57C3Bqw:
I was reluctant to eat here based on the reviews, but I was hungry and they had the Beyond Burger so here we go. There were 2 of us and the restaurant was not busy. We stood at the door for several minutes with several different servers looking at us with no acknowledgement. Eventually our server, Justin, came and seated us. 

Justin was very attentive, courteous and efficient throughout our dining experience. While slightly over-priced, we ordered the Beyond Burger and Justin wanted to ensure we knew it was a veggie burger as other customers had ordered it before without realizing it. The burger and fries were delivered quick enough and they were delicious. 

Justin checked on us several times during out meal and all was well. 

In the past, I have not had a great Fridays experience, but this one has made me reconsider, especially if they continue to carry the Beyond Burger.

We tried the Jack Daniels hamburger well done,  provolone cheese, caramelized onion, tomato, pickle, lettuce, with jack Daniels dipping sauce. Great choice it was juicy and so flavorful. Not to mention the staff was very friendly and service went flawless.

Staff was very polite and the food was awesome as always. This was one of the best Friday's I've been to in a long while! Our waiter Thomas...... Was awesome and made us feel right at home! Thomas you need to move to Texas and take over our Friday's!
E3dd7pkFFCpc4xpipenUyw: We ordered the Mediterranean sandwich and turkey sandwich. The Mediterranean was absolutely delish. If I had one critique it would be a little less dressing and oil. The turkey was just your basic turkey sandwich. They have gluten free bread options which is nice. Big portions too. We brought our pups and they were welcomed on the patio. Great little hidden spot.

Easily one of the best sandwich experiences I've had. Southern Engine piles on the high-quality meat and combines the sandwich ingredients to perfection. The side pickle spear is crisp and traditionally tasty. Price and value intersect accordingly. Check out the right side of the menu for the specialty sandwiches. I had the SEC and it was amazing. I'd order it next time, if I wasn't excited to try 4 others!!!

Do yourself a favor and grab an exquisite sandwich creation at Southern Engine.
Literally stumbled onto this place while at Antique Archeology. One of THE BEST roast beef-smoked Gouda sandwiches I have ever had! Warning-order the 1/2 sandwich or be prepared to be crazy full!!!
GrsFMEYd3d0UWYo7WpxTPw:
I have a deep, deep love of little Caesars, judge me all you like! I blame being poor in college and this place being cheap and convenient, and their seductive garlic butter sauce. With this particular location being literally two minutes from my house, I just can't say no when someone mentions it; I'm not in college anymore, but sadly, I'm still poor!

The staff is very friendly, and they even gave us free crazy bread to compensate for our longish (maybe 15 minute) wait while we were here. I will definitely return!

This restaurant is usually understaffed for employees, and weekends there so slammed. The first time I went I had to wait 15 to 20 minutes for my hot and ready pizza to be made, which supposedly should be made all day so they are ready to go when customers show.

I felt bad for the girl who was working that day because she said her coworker hadn't showed. The next time I went back I called ahead, and my hot and ready pizza was ready when I arrived. However there were 3 to 5 other people waiting on pizzas as well.

The pizza is actually always really delicious! And very inexpensive
pizza was good, the cashier said I was pretty , and it was cheap. 10/10 would go again om
Qg17JxnBMiRDEM5uE5yzOA: Great, quick, easy sandwiches. Just wish the staff were a little more friendly. Standard greeting at register is, "What did you have?"

Food is well priced and is always great. 4 stars due to the extreme lack of customer service. Workers won't greet you, they will just stare and wait for you to order. No real sense of urgency. Nevertheless, the shakes and cookies are amazing. Will return.

I had this place for the first time in Houston and was so excited to see it hit the 'Ville... I had the Italian and broccoli cheddar soup and my guy had the pizza sub... It was just as tasty as I had remembered and those hot peppers are an awesome addition to any sandwich.... I think I will def be a regular here... Quality meats veggies and bread and toasted....I'll take it along with good customer service. The guys that were working were so personable like they were your buddies from around the way and their suggestions were winners with my guy and he's hard to please.
r9L9r7YxzpC1U32GYQboWA: They have a great buffet for lunch (11am to 4 pm). All of the food has been fresh & hot & a good variety of food. Our new favorite place!

Visiting Nashville on a business trip, we had lunch here on a quiet, rainy late October afternoon. The service was good, the food great -- and A LOT of it. The Tabouli is like a food organism! Yellow rice is fantastic,  and my side salad filled a whole plate. We WILL be back.

The people who own/run this restaurant are really very nice and the staff is very attentive.

I have had both lunch and dinner. I always opt for the buffet at lunch time, because it is both convenient and has everything I want on it. The meat dishes are delicious and the homemade hummus is very tasty. My favorite item is the lentil soup! I promise you that it is the best you have had (if not, please let me know where you can find better).

You need to give this place a try!
YEIZTgcM95btX9n_ibeUuA: Watching live music that was great and our waitress Judy was amazing. Definitely will be back here and hopefully Judy is our waitress again!

While staying at the Marriott we stopped here for a late night snack/meal and was pleasantly surprised how nice everything was.
First the all glass atmospher is very cool with a Manhattan/posh style. The serve is also excellent and very friendly.
Got the spicy chicken sandwich which was a little on the small side but decent. We then split the brownie a la mode which was very yummy and filling. 
Bar wise we had a couple glasses of wine. Bar menu isnt huge but everything they have seems to be nice and all speciality drinks made well. 
Overall a nice spot to stop in for an end of the night drink or a quick bite to eat.
Surprising delicious. Expected ordinary hotel food. The bourbon glazed wings were delicious!
Eg1kM6XhpMuUmg25A89HuQ:
Phillip's is a neighborhood Deli with much much more than the usual deli offerings. 

I had never been to Phillip's and I walked in craving a good turkey club sandwich, but I left stuffed/ satisfied from one of Phillip's famous laded baked potatoes and a small soup.  I don't know why I ordered the huge potato amalgamation of cheese, bacon, butter, and sour cream, but it just felt right!   And it was!  Very tasty and filling, plus the price is right at under $4.00.

I like this place a lot, it has a very downtown city feel, which you don't always get in Nashville, as many delis and restaurants are located on multi lane roads.

Phillips offers an outstanding breakfast or lunch option for those located in the Downtown Nashville Area.  Located within the arcade on 5th ave. between Church and Union.  They're breakfast specials included loaded Hot bagel sandwiches with egg + cheese + and your choice of meat plus sauteed peppers and onions for around $3 bucks! As well don't miss Phillips famous Deli Sandwiches, loaded with meat and cheese on a variety of breads or toast.  Corporate Catering is also an option for those businesses in the downtown area.

Phillip's is just a great deli.  This is one of my favorite places to eat in the Arcade.  Phillip's always has a line out the door during lunch but I never mind waiting, and it only takes a few minutes.  They have a great selection of deli sandwiches and salads.  I always get the salad.  The house salad is huge and has a little bit of ham and turkey sliced on top. You can get your choice of dressing, I love their house dressing. It is a simple oil and vinegar dressing but they add very tasty herbs like parsley, basil, and oregano. And the best part, the house salad is only $4.50.  incredibly cheap.
he5tYNPN2QWQT4nhQHVYvQ: Amazing food, great service! Always get my food made to order! Highly recommend "The Mexico" I get it almost every week! Wings and burgers are amazing as well!!!

I've eaten at Rocky's Wings a few times now and finally have to rave about their food. Their wings and fried fish are some of the best I've ever tasted. They've got a wide variety of choices on their menu and I am stuffed and satisfied every time I dine on their food. Don't miss this hidden gem!

When I moved to Nashville from Memphis, it was difficult adjusting to the lack of Wing establishments in the city. Rocky's is the closest thing to perfection in the entire city when it comes to taste and flavor.
IBe1ubALseL7MoHo2tT7VQ: Shalimar is hands down, not just the best Indian Food in Nashville... But the BEST food in Nashville.This place has the best service and food. You can bring your own wine with no corking fee! Recently it was snowing in Nashville, NOTHING was open, and they welcomed us with open arms (at 3pm!) - you won't be disappointed!

Legit.  They cater.  (I'm giving them a 4 instead of a 3 because the food was probably a 3.75 to 3.8 and it was a probably a 200+ person affair).
My limited experience is that Sitar which is located off West End near Vanderbilt is better.
The best Chai in town with wonderful vegetarian options. The vegetable korma is a dream and the service is great. Very nice atmosphere as well.
JuTD00dKkVRUPeVqdjjrbg: Consistent good food. Friendly prompt service. The salad bar tops any I know. Reasonable prices. I can always get what I expect.

We had a large party of 9;  7 of them being girls aged 9-12.   We were greeted with no hesitation and after being seated our server took care of us immediately.    7 young girls I'm sure for many servers the party would have been overwhelming.  But he was fantastic with them.   The service was surprisingly fast and some of the best of any kind received in a long time...from anywhere.  One in our party has severe peanut allergies and the server and manager were fantastic in assuring us that her food was peanut free.  The experience was made easy because of the wonderful server and manager.  The food was chain food but a good variety for so many taste buds.  They were all happy!...making my night easier.

The Little Black Dress Club Nashville Chapter held a happy hour here on Wednesday night from 4:30-6pm. We had seven women in our party. The service was excellent. Our server, Shawn, could not have been any better or more attentive. The $5 absolute cocktails and the $5 appetizers where perfect for our ladies and everything was delicious. LBDC would highly recommend a week night happy hour at the Green Hills Ruby Tuesday.

~Shay Ashcraft
Co Director, Nashville Chapter
d13997Q9A5KUjnvoLU7iaw: I love tacos and these tacos are the bomb diggitty!!! The al pastor tacos are my favorite!

It does not get better than this in Nashville. I live in East Nashville and often make my way down Nolensville Pike to come to this taco truck. Ah, I can taste the carnitas right now. Absolutely delicious, perfect meat. Friendly service, and it's in the K&S parking lot so that means cooking adventure time. Can't go wrong.

Amazing! I got two tongue tacos, two pastor tacos, one barbacoa mulita and a grande horchata. Holy shit, everything was perfect. The meats were seasoned-well, and tender. The horchata was flavorful. And my total was $9. I need to learn Espanõl.
E2Jj7w2MGo-H5aYJWtCBcg:
Tried this place for the first time yesterday and it will not be the last. Driving down Wedgewood, I noticed this truck in the parking lot of a beer and cigarettes store. Half a dozen Mexican dudes were lined up at the window. The smell of the cooking food reached my truck window, and I immediately pulled into the lot. In addition to the Mexicans, there were a couple of hipster-looking dudes also milling about. I placed my order quickly enough, but I guess they have only one guy cooking, because I had to wait until all the rest of the orders were handed out. Which is fair, but still...if you see a crowd when you go, expect a wait for actual food to appear.

It's worth the wait.

I tried the al pastor tacos. Two tacos with perfectly spiced pork, topped with diced onion and cilantro. A small portion of escabeche and a small cup of two hot sauces (red & green) accompanied the tacos. The pickled carrots, onion and jalapeños were delicious. The salsa verde was pretty typical. The rojo was quite hot, if a bit thin. They sell Mexican sodas, including the Mexican Coca-Cola made with cane sugar instead of corn syrup.

The projects are right across 12th and there were at least two shady-looking characters milling about the intersection and parking lot. One was chanting something incoherent to no one in particular. The one on crutches would hobble up to car windows and yell at the occupants of the vehicles. This had no noticeable effect on the patrons or staff and did not diminish my enjoyment of the food, but...if you're the nervous type, forewarned is forearmed. One woman threatened to run us all over with her car because she was in a hurry to get to the beer and cigarette store.

Al Pastor and Asada tacos were delicious. The salsas are spicy and flavorful. Carnitas was missing that crispness but still pretty good. I loved their Asada and pollo burritos as well. Can someone update where this truck went?!?

AHA!  Finally found it, I think!  Someone posted a link to Metro's website for inspection scores, and I figured that _they_ would know where it is.

... aaaand, they say 1120 Murfreesboro Pike.  Not at all in my neck of the woods anymore; shucks.  (The score was 98 by the way.)

For the review:

I have eaten there many many times and tried every single thing except for the big asada plate and the tripas.  I used to go once or twice a week.  Yes, everything is magnificent as others are saying.  You just can't go wrong.  Tortas, burritos, quesadillas, tacos, sopes, mulitas, just the best, and cheap even for a taco truck.

Really love it, and I _will_ still make some special trips, but alas not every week....
26i3Tkz3VIS0CNIZ3JUMYw:
Will definitely come back for more and I live 5min away from the store,,, My baby loved the pizza
The family love to pizza

I'm not really sure why this store has such a low rating. Every time I've been in here the employees were always nice and the store is kept clean. I even got a high five from the guy making my pizza the other day! I do see that there's a high employee turnover but what do you expect? It's a minimum wage pizza joint. It's a good place for teens trying to make some extra cash while in school then hopefully they'll leave there and go on to bigger and better things! My only complaint is I wish they'd let you know ahead of time when they're out of an ingredient. It's not just this location though! Almost every one let's me know after I've already ordered. No biggie because the price is right and it's the only place that I know of that will let me substitute a meat and not charge me extra.
Friendly staff most of the time (kind of high turnover).  Great tasting pizza compared to chains.  Always have coupons or specials going on.
WSwGEbq6V10QOS-_JlfONg: I'm from LA so I didn't have high hopes of finding a good taco out here. These were hands down the closest to the best tacos al pastor I've ever had. I'm eating here again before I fly home. Amazing.

Tom J. doesn't know what he is talking about.

As a first generation Mexican American whose parents are from what is arguably the most Mexican state, Jalisco, your review is in complete error. Hard shell, ground beef? Are you sure you didn't walk into the Taco Bell down the street? I was raised in Mexico and Southern California, San Diego and Los Angeles areas. I have lots of family and friends in livestock and agriculture. My grandfather was one of the best providers of blue agave mezcal to the surrounding tequileras including Siete Leguas.

I would ignore Tom J's review.

We checked out this place based on reviews from neighbors on Nextdoor and absolutely loved it!  This new restaurant has all you could ask for: bright and fun decor, clean environment, sweet and attentive staff, and delicious food!  They have a lot of your typical Tex Mex staples, but the food is very fresh and you can actually see and taste the real food items and ingredients that are used in their meals, unlike your typical Mexican food joint.  My husband ordered the Pollo Loco and it was a freshly grilled chicken breast served on a bed of rice, with a block of cheese, avocado, and a side of their home made tortillas.  I ordered several a la carte items and they were equally delicious.  Their pico de gallo was divine and served in a large stone canister - for $2.  I could rave on and on, but you really must try it for yourself! Highly recommend!
EA822ZoRvW0reS5mlNv1-g: Great sandwiches and fast service.  The bread is outstanding and all selections are fresh and tasty!

The staff here is what makes this a great Jersey Mikes. I highly recommend trying their breakfast sandwiches. The amount of care and precision they put into those sandwiches makes my morning.  Lunch there is a huge rush, so go early (1130am) or later 1:15pm.
I love the staff here! I think they are the reason I keep coming back. The subs are good especially if it is toasted. Dining in around lunch time can be a beast so be prepared to stand in line.
lBUKuGMhn5E6SZDyJ0BebA: Been in Nashville for a few weeks on business and this is the best food I've had since I got here. The pan roasted salmon was on point

Everything thing you heard is true. This hotel is beautiful. The moment you walk in your greeted by some friendly faces. The beautiful decor and the lighting fixtures so pretty. 

Tavern dining is straight ahead once you walk in. Hostess sat us down and our friendly waitress was so accommodating. Our group came later in the evening for dining it wasn't pack. 
Our food didn't take long the kitchen had our food out in a reasonable time. 
Food was impeccable tasty delicious. I didn't have anything but nice things to say about this place.
