Restrooms are for both genders. Garage wooden style doors. They don't have door handles just the Occupied signs. To open, you have to push on the door hard. I was a bit confused on how I was suppose to get in. I'm pretty sure the staff gets a kick out of it, how confused everyone looks trying to open the doors.

The food was really good! Service was a tad slow. There were three of us so we ordered the poke to start (very good) then had the strip steak, kale salad with salmon and salmon entree. Portions were good and the salmon was cooked perfectly. This was a great spot to start our girls night out!
Agdp78S4SJ2g1huOx1N1Qg: What a fun spot in the lobby of the museum! My husband and I loved the energy and the live music! Our waiter was a dream! He was very attentive but also gave us a little history of the area! My husband got the hamburger. It was a normal, tasty burger. I ordered the mini biscuit and fried chicken sandwiches. This is the FIVE star. The chicken was great and the biscuits were divine!

This eatery was inside of the Country music hall of fame and museum.
Had pulled pork sandwich. 4 different kinds of sauce came with the sandy.
Sauce in Tennessee is really something. Extremely friendly service.
Live music also. In Nashville you hear live music everywhere all the time. They were all good.

I really thought the food was surprisingly good here for the restaurant being smack in the middle of the main foyer of the Hall of Fame.  There was a guitar player entertaining nearby. 

I had a Southern Fried Chicken Salad that was really good while I rested my poor feeties while waiting for the rest of my family.  If you are looking for a bite to eat, this is a great place versus the To Go take out on the other side of the walkway.
ZjMr_ImqmMKbc1BfmLOtTQ: Just the kind of place I love eating Mexican at. Good margaritas, good prices, and if you listen really hard you can hear the cook singing mariachi songs in the kitchen.

Great Mexican food place with some awesome prices.  The waiters are really nice and attentive.  You have to try the cheese dip, it's freakin amazing, and you can get 99 cent beers during happy hour.  What isn't to like?
We were looking for something close and fell into this place. Service was good and margaritas were ok but I've had much better. The salsa, guacamole and tamales were delicious. I would go back.
ESRpD9zHlr8UyVgMQlSA0Q: Nero's Grill is a delightful dining experience. The atmosphere is comfortable and cozy. The food was very good. I had a sirloin steak and it was delicious. The corn fritters that they bring to the table are delicious. I am gluten free and was delighted that I could eat these safely. Our waitress, DeeDee, was very nice, helpful with the menu regarding my gluten intolerance, and made our dining experience so pleasant. I had a specialty cocktail and thought it was delicious. This restaurant is conveniently located in the heart of Green Hills.

My boyfriend and I went on a whim because we were in the neighborhood.  We decided to sit in the bar area which turned out to be great!  We sat at a table but the next time we go, we will sit at the piano bar.  The piano bar would be a great place for a light dinner and drinks or maybe a birthday celebration.  The food was good and so was the service!  We tried the salmon and pulled barbeque.  We weren't crazy about the corn cakes though.  While we were waiting on our food, we listened to Mark Sorrell on the piano.  He really interacted with the customers and contributed to the fun but quiet atmosphere.  We were also thrilled there were TVs in the bar for us to catch the Bama/Clemson game.  We will definitely go back!
According to the owners, this business is closing on January 5, 2014.
A3nANRlcfW1rqIgJQ9QJ8A: We ate dinner here last week and ordered a few things off the menu to try out. EVERYTHING was delicious! We had the cheese dip with taco meat in it and the chips are house made and fabulous! The bbq taco and enchiladas were excellent! Margaritas were great! We will be back often!

Love the lunch specials.  $7.99 and free parking in Nashville is like finding a unicorn these days.  In and out quickly. And they fry fresh flour tortillas to make their own chips, which is a great touch.  Looking forward to brunch and having many drinks here!

Great lunch today! Got an order of hard shell tacos (nothing fancy) and they were so good. Shells came right out of the oven, toppings were super fresh and meat had some tasty seasonings.  

Also chips and salsa to start were top notch. 

Excellent service as well at the bar. 

Perfect drop in for a quick lunch if you work in the Metro Center area. 

Will be back for sure.
Vv0p1WerkuIkcfhxREHU2w: They finally got gluten free bread and for the first time in forever I got to enjoy an actual sub! The bread was fresh, they change their gloves, cutting board, and everything. It's a little extra work dealing with an allergy customer like myself but the gentlemen working and the manager helped me humbly and with big smiles. This location has gone above and beyond my expectations of a Sub Shop. Five stars isn't a high enough rating. You can count on me coming back. I am ecstatic that a gluten free option has been added to your menu! Thank you for such a positive experience!

Now I'm not normally one to Yelp a fast food chain (because technically I would call this fast food) but having been all over Nashville and tried many of their Jersey Mike's shops, this one is my favorite.  They have just recently renovated to make it more open inside and I find I frequent it a lot since there's a Target right across the street.  I like this one the best for so many reasons.  One, the service here in the best of any of the other franchises I've been in.  The employees are nice, conversational, and clean.  Let's just say I've been in some JM shops where I'm pretty sure there was a guy with the flu making my sandwich (or he was, before I left in disgust).  But here, these guys are great.  And they make the growing line move quickly.  I am a sucker for a #7 as either errand running energy or hangover cue, and these guys somehow manage to make one that tastes better than another of the other shops.  So I'm Yelping a fast food joint, proudly, and saying thanks for satisfying my sandwich obsession guys!

I do realize that Jersey Mike's is a chain, but DANG, they make probably the best deli sandwich I have ever tasted.  Whenever I come to Nashville to visit my parents, the first thing I always do (usually on the way back to our house from the airport) is to go to Jersey Mike's for a #9 Club Supreme.  Most of the sandwiches are pretty standard deli variety, but they are of exceptional quality and freshness - the breads are always freshly baked, the meats are cut in front of you as the sandwich is ordered, and the lettuce, tomatoes, and peppers are always of good quality.  The prices are reasonable and are very justified given the huge sizes of the sandwiches.

The location of White Bridge Rd. is especially clean, and the people that work there are quite nice.  The White Bridge Rd. location also has a pretty extensive seating area with 4 plasma TV's that are usually turned to sports.

I highly recommend Jersey Mike's for a great, great sandwich!
W_B1ffzD0pHmQYEwGG_8Qw: Wonderful friendly people and a great selection of foods from the Middle East and beyond. Halal meat and chicken and a phenomenal bakery that makes incredible breads!  Don't be afraid if you aren't from "the Middle East". Go and experience some new tastes and smells!  The folks are nice and helpful!  Oh and they have an awesome olive bar too. Yum!!

Very friendly owner. Many options for grocery, and, best of all, fresh baked bread.

I had thier lahamajoun and it was delicious.

This place is great for middle eastern grocery shopping!
Very friendly stuff, all kind of international food. Reasonable prices. Excellent meat selection. Highly recommended.
D6YZOcTv0z9jc3Z4fwh1zw:
Great BBQ downtown.  Quick, easy and good.  Friendly staff.

Try the mac and cheese, it might be the best I've ever had.

It's a new day for the Arcade!

So impressed with the brand new 417 BBQ. Excellent service, well decorated and spotless, actually takes American Express (hard to find in the Arcade). Homemade chips and brisket did not let me down, great sweet BBQ sauce, and the sweet tea was just the right level of sweet. Keep it up!

Great Value.  Great flavors.  I had the brisket sandwich and the coleslaw.  The brisket was sliced just right and was super tender and flavorful.  The coleslaw wasn't too sweet and went well with the sandwich.  It wasn't a huge portion and was just right for lunch.
BwXW4cfFoJQOMYazk_30BA: Good, quick Chinese takeout (though I ate in).  Lots of food for the money.  Chicken with vegetables & egg roll was very tasty.  Place seemed outdated and restroom was like a gas station's, but that's ok.  The family that runs the place was super friendly.  They were laughing, singing, and having a good old time. I'll be back!
As for fast chinese joints, this is the best in Donelson. Large portions, decent prices, and the quality of the food is always consistent.
I love this place! It's very good food and they make it fresh the minute you order. Not mention the prices are crazy good! Trust me and eat here!
E8nFvonCaW63W9d3zUYhgQ: Went here on a Sat. night in November.  Staff was nice and the atmosphere was great.  Lots of flavors to choose from.
Great place with some good music. Nice place to smoke some hookah and have a drink before going out. Deff will come back next time I come to Nashvegas
mOhQ3gjsYsaOZcoT10iQtg: This place is closed but the same owners opened at the Centennial Cafe on Centennial Blvd where Jefferson st  ends. Great food, prices and service.
great inexpensive lunch! the burgers and fries are great and my coworker raves over the omelets.  the catch, they except cash only!

I head over at least once a week for lunch. Couldn't ask for much more. Don't go expecting a fancy restaurant- this is a diner! Great food, great prices, great service. Extremely fast and easy, as well as delicious. The cornbread is one of my favorites in Nashville. Cash only, so don't go with only a card! 

Meat + 3: Huge portions, and only $5.99.
VSwd3muRlEtT6Cbhea6Qwg: Sushi burrito made my way. I had never heard of a sushi burrito before. We were headed to the farmers market and a coworker told me about it. I love sushi so it was game-on for me. I got to pick out all of the ingredients-all of my favorites. It was wonderful! The sushi chef was full of personality. It was quick and easy and I am definitely going back.

I had high hopes for Green Asia when I first heard about its opening.  Healthy Asian food that wasn't going to be deep fried and then drenched in soy sauce?  Count me in!  Unfortunately, life did not seem to play out this way.  For the most part, Green Asia serves the basic Chinese food that I have come to expect from any food court or take out venue.  It was a disappointment, but once I wrapped my mind around it and accepted Green Asia for what it is, I find that I'm more of a fan.

We eat here at least twice a month on our farmer's market visit, and they have always had my Asian staples on the menu, which I appreciate.  They serve up the only General Tso's chicken that I actually enjoy eating.  It isn't very spicy at all, which may not sit well with traditionalists, but my sensitive mouth enjoys it.  I also like that they serve brown rice alongside fried and lo mein.  As far as their vegetables go, their edamame is probably frozen, but so is the stuff I make at home.  I also recommend the steamed broccoli with cabbage over the mixed vegetables that are underseasoned and coated with soy sauce.  

All in all, Green Asia's not bad.  They give me way too much food, the price is good, and they always have the three things that I want on a plate.  Bottom line - it works for me.

Grabbed 4 pieces of nigiri, 2 salmon and 2 yellow fin tuna. Very tasty! Better than I expected quite frankly. Staff was friendly. I didn't try their hot food but I can recommend their sushi for a quick snack while shopping at the farmers market.
x6f6chhOX-ZtCUTM8BHvKg:
Great atmosphere (fun, colorful walls, contemporary art, lots and lots of light).

Tasty burritos, with ingredients that actually tasted the way they were supposed to. veggies were full of flavor, and tasted really fresh. Same with the chip salsa. They had 4 or 5 salsas for self-serve, and the two I had were great. Their choices for ingredients in the burritos were great. Way more variety than most places.

Their drinks were great, including options such as iced tea, horchata and mango juice.

The guys behind the counter were super friendly and joking.

Also, they give a discount if you show them your student ID.

Finally, someone gets it right!

The market is flooded with Burrito joints and I am not complaining one bit! However, they usually always mess something up for the value. Mr. Burrito Fresh gives you portions, chips, salsa, queso in various sizes and fresh ingredients all for a price you don't double take. If you have $7 and a hearty appetite then head over and get a ton of delicious food and be prepared to go back often!

i have been looking for the best fish taco in town.

This is it.

Flour (or corn) tortillas lovingly grilled on the grill, topped with the tastiest fried fish I have ever had on a taco.    And here is where it goes from good to perfect.  The cabbage and other toppings are subtle, not overpowering like at some places, or vacant like others.  Perfect preportions, perfect balance, great taste, thoughtfully prepared.

I got it with a delicious pinapple juice blend (might have been fruit tea but I could not taste much tea, either way it was delicoius). 

Also their complimentary salsa bar included a very spicey and wonderful avacado salsa.  Top notch.  Good thing it was so spicey, otherwise I would have eaten the whole bowl.

very friendly staff, colorful and comfortable atmousphere, great location.
bZHFrdCMyxWMtWcEPlom2A: This was my 1st night in Nashville and this was a great start. They had excellent food and entertainment.  It is a must see if you love country music and down home cooking.

So it was my last night in Nashville and I wanted to wrap things up with a dinner/show experience.  I had missed the Grand Ole Opry show the previous night, and wasn't looking forward to paying for tickets to The General Jackson, which I've done in the past.  The girl at the hotel recommended the Nashville Nightlife.  At just under $40, it was considerably cheaper than the alternatives so I decided to go.

Make no mistake, this has tourist trap written all over it, from the kitschy gift shop to the over the top cheesy emcee.  The food buffet is just about what you would expect from a strip mall buffet - heavy on the grease, pretty light on the flavor (though I did go back for seconds on the fried chicken and meatballs- when you've made the conscious decision to cast aside better judgement, it makes the experience a whole lot more enjoyable.) Drinks and dessert are included in the price, but beer/wine is extra.

The decor is very dated and has the feel of a mom and pop local venue just trying to scrape by.   I initially saw that as a negative, but as the lights dimmed and the show began, I realized that it added to the hometown charm of the Nashville Night Life.

The show itself was exactly what one would expect at a tourist venue,  good and bad.  Each of the performers played to the crowd with a forced sense of enthusiasm that made me question their sincerity.  They all seemed to be former band members or backup singers of renowned country stars, and each of them were hawking their CDs in the back.  In this age of auto tune and the plethora of competition singing shows, it's easy to be critical of such performers.

But as the night wore on, I came to realize that they were pretty amazing musicians and singers.  I found myself clapping and singing along to some of the numbers.  

If you look at if from a sheer economic viewpoint, the buffet was probably worth about $14.95.  Was the show worth $25? In a town where there is free music on every corner? It's certainly debatable, but what is not debatable is the fact that I had a FANTASTIC time at the Nashville Night Life.  Sure, it's a tourist attraction, but if you know that going in, you will have a good time.

Would I go again? Maybe / maybe not

Would I recommend it to others? Absolutely.

We just wanted to relax and watch a show and were not disappointed!!  The owner came over a few times to talk to us, the waiter was really nice. Not a fan of buffets but it was pretty good as well. I'm a big fan of older country and this place served it up hot!!  Then out comes shotgun red and Mark Hall. A great show for the whole family.

irz4vD1aWi_D2T9qtcA8DA: I'm so glad to have found amazing ceviche in Nashville and just down the street from my place! The Plaza Mariachi market is easy to find on Nolensville Pike and the ceviche spot is indoors of this place. The entire building is decorated like it's outdoors; lots of colors and flowers everywhere and live entertainment. The ceviche place is a walk up type spot and the menu is right there, either a la carte or you can create meals for about $13.99 from what I saw. I went with the ceviche del Camaron (shrimp) and got 2 of those a la carte for $3.99 each. Totally worth it since they pile them up high and it's a ton of great food. To drink they either have Mexican Cokes or bottled water. I was very happy with the quick service, the prices, the food was really really good and reminded me of Sundays back in California. It was 95 outside and nothing seemed appealing, until El Ceviche Loco popped up on Yelp. I love some good fresh cool ceviche. I got there and with so many choices, I could not decide. A girl who'd helped me with the popsicle dilemma a few weeks ago came around to save me again. She chose the Ensalada de Jaiba (crab salad) Tostada, which I wasn't even considering! SO good!!! A great find and worth a try. Sorry for lack of foodie pics- I dug in immediately!

We happened to stumble upon Game Point on our walk from Dino's to Pied Piper Creamery. After filling up on ice cream, we popped our heads in.

We had really just planned to take a look and then get back to our plans for the evening, but we were enamored with the place and decided we could squeeze in one game. The deciding factor was that I found a cooler of Las Paletas popsicles -- we'd been dying to try them on our trip -- and we found a newer version of one of our favorite games.

There was a young man and woman behind the counter who were helpful and kind during our visit. I was able to have a quick conversation with the guy to learn the history of Game Point. I also watched as he helped a new group narrow down options of games to play based on their interests. It was clear that the employees loved their work -- when traffic slowed in the shop, they picked up a game themselves and played at a nearby table. 

We enjoyed our popsicles and played a quick round of Fluxx. Our brief visit was great -- the space is clean and the vibe very chill. The bathrooms have some seriously creative art on the walls and it's worth a visit to catch a glimpse.

If we lived nearby, Game Point would absolutely be a go-to hangout. There are a ton of drink and snack options, and I love the cupholders on the tables to keep condensation from making the games soggy. This is a perfect spot and it's a great fit for East Nashville.

My wife and I went for a date night outing and we loved it.  They have a huge assortment of board games to choose from.  My wife and I decided to assemble a puzzle I brought from home.  It was the perfect place to do this.  The snacks were perfect.  I had been looking for a place like this and found it.  You will love it too.  The other folks there didn't bother us either.  Everyone played their games and wasn't too loud or difficult.

Really awesome, fun place in East Nashville. Rick is the game master and he would love to teach you to play that new game you've heard about, or settle that Catan rules debate your family has been having. 

I think there are two features of this gem that make it five star worthy.

1. Game Master - an asset like a game master is crucial because he helps people discover games that they would enjoy based on what they like and then teach them how to play those games so that the learning curve isn't as difficult. 

2. Selection - I'd estimate between 125 and 200 games are available for your playing pleasure. It's like going to the book store and perusing and seeing all the fancy covers of books. That's what this feels like when choosing a game. Lots of old favorites, as well as hot new games. 

A bonus is that they also have a shelf with games for sale. So if you like a game you played, you might find it for sale. 

I would love to come here again to try out more new games.

Chick-fil-a is known for great customer service, and while I did wait a bit for my food, I felt very welcomed and cared for as a customer. Having a fast-moving kitchen will come with time. Overall good experience.

I don't typically go to fast food restaurants, however, this Chick-fil-a is on my way to work every morning, and I stopped twice for a quick bite of breakfast, and I have been blown away from the employees here. I am very pleased and is the absolute number one reason that I consider returning on my way to work again.

You guys cookout is the most underrated fast food chain. 4 times here till date and every time my satisfaction is elevated! This is no less than a Southern indoctrination for me. 

Can't speak for sitting in but drive thru is the way to go here. The service is quick enough that every order of ours has always been right. The staff are young and always have been polite. 

To the newbies and transplants who have not tried cookout, this is a fast food you have got to try. The food is fresh, solidly tasty and perhaps the best bang for the buck. 

The challenging part is navigating the menu, which is overwhelming but I doubt any other fast food chain has a dedicated reddit thread discussing the perfect menu and milkshake order. 

And, that reddit thread was discovery of gold for me. If you know what to order that will satisfy your hunger, Cookout will be your guilty pleasure. We seem to reach one step closer to curating our perfect order, but we are not there yet. 

Currently, our favorite combo is Big Double + Double side cajun fries + peanut butter and snickers milkshake + the cookout special sauce. It marginally overtook the last fav Cajun Chicken Sandwich + double hushpuppies + butterfinger and reeses cup milkshake + honey mustard sauce. 

While everyone should map their own treasure hunt, here is what makes Cookout the best fast food chain I have had: 

1- Quality: The milkshake is fresh and seems inorganically organic- it doesn't have a bunch of chemicals that will compromise flavor and quality. It is very much of the milk. The ground beef is the best flavored juicy dynamite waiting to explode your tastebuds. The cajun chicken beats a controversial competitor off the park. Cajun fries are the best. Tried too many they make it the best. They are all good. Period. 

2- Variety: The menu and combos you can get will boggle your mind. You can make any combination of milkshake out of the 40 flavors. You have a chicken wrap and Quesadillas for sides? Yea happy discovery. 

3- Price: the Big tray and Jr Tray are the best value tray ever, but the latter does not offer a milkshake substitute. Two big trays cost us $14 (roughly $7 each). Oh and we have not finished our meal, we were stuffed and saved dessert for tomorrow. 

If you are a decision- challenged human being like I am always decide on the menu before you go. Otherwise, you will be lost. 

I'd give it five stars but: the side of chili, although tasty seemed to have a mistaken quantity of salt. sometimes the fries quantity can be a little inconsistent (just marginally though). And, the previous times I did not know there was a cookout special sauce until now. 

Special shout out: That cookout special sauce is the best condiment to the hush puppies and fries. 

I have no idea how they are in business with the price and quality but they have a major formula for success! Cookout should be a rite of passage when in Nashville.

Stopped by the Sum Yum Yum truck at the 2013 Nashville Street Food Awards in Centennial Park. I wasn't originally going to stop there, but standing in line at the Waffle Boss truck and smelling the Sum Yum Yum truck MADE me go over!

The bread was soft with a nice chew to it...the chicken had a tangy lemongrass flavor and all of it was topped off with pickled cabbage, shredded carrots, cilantro and a wasabi infused mayo that packed some heat (in a GOOD way!).  All of this crazy flavor and freshness from a food truck for just $7!

I will definitely seek out this truck again!

Just tried Sum Yum Yum for the first time on Food Truck Friday at the Gateway Village in Murfreesboro.  Sum Yum Yum was yum yum indeed!  I tried the Lemongrass Chicken Banh Mi with an egg roll as a side.  The first bite set my mouth on fire, but I acclimated to the flavor after that.  I really enjoyed the freshness of the sliced cucumber and carrot mixed with the spicy chicken.  One hoagie roll was very filling and the egg roll was crispy and delicious.  The service was very friendly and made sure we were accommodated.  The chef told us that he would gladly adjust the spiciness if we wanted.  I will remember that next time since I'm a little weakling when it comes to heat.

xsNTQmCVM2OcVCiXNqs6hw: Well worth taking the cash. We have become too dependent on plastic anyway. Some of the best food in town. Either suck it up and take some cash, or miss out on the best food ever!

The cheesey Fish w/ the crown on his head kinda screams "trashy joint," once you step inside it is the complete opposite.  The place is clean, neat, and updated.  I had the fish sandwich combo w/ a extra side of clams and it was under $8.  It's much better than going to Cpt D's and paying $10 for less food.  King Fish has an authentic feel and you're paying for food that some one has prepared, unlike Cpt D's and Long John Silver's who serve manufactured frozen food that you can buy yourself from Walmart (Gordon's Fisherman).  Filling and inexpensive.

The best thing about King Fish is the shrimp, whether you have it fried or grilled. Not usually a fan of fried shrimp, I really enjoyed theirs. You can tell it is fresh and it was lightly spiced and perfectly battered. The grilled shrimp were also nice, moist and lightly spiced. The various flavors of tabasco sauce on the table provide a nice accompaniment. I went with friends, so I got to try a nice selection of their menu, including the gumbo, which was a little watery but tasted great. 
This is a great place to go when you're craving good fried food, but they also have a meat and three-like vegetable selection. 
Their prices are reasonable, but not cheap, and the restaurant appears spotlessly clean and well decorated inside, almost reminiscent of a chain. I had heard King Fish touted as a hot fish place, as it is often listed or reviewed with them, but it is not, and this might disappoint the misinformed.
6uJUJe5Le7UYr7R1mbiTkA: Great place. Every time I go (which isn't much) the people are super friendly. They never mess up my order and I never have to wait. No complaints here!

We rolled in around 6:30am, and rolled out with a couple piping hot breakfast sandwiches that were some of the best fast food sandwiches a person could ask for. The service was super friendly. Yes, the card reader had trouble. Yes, we had to wait about 10+ minutes for our food. Yes, the bathrooms were locked. But guess what - bring cash, be patient, and ask nicely for them to unlock the bathrooms and they will kindly do so. Seriously... We are giving these folks five stars because the breakfast sandwiches were so nice it was as if we'd made them ourselves at home. I went here for lunch yesterday and didn't know what to expect. The place is so small, I passed it twice with my car before finding it. It's just left of Captain D's--I love how the official Luna website snarks about the view of the fast food joint--and they share a parking lot. 

The restaurant was even smaller inside than it looked from out (this isn't a bad thing...I'm just saying, it doesn't accommodate a whole lot of diners). You order at the counter, and the server fixes up your plate from a buffet. It's a very traditional meat-and-three type of establishment. I had the beef lasagna with creamed corn on the side, while my dining companion order the chicken and sausage. We stuck around chatting long enough that the chef brought over complimentary apple pie at one point! It was yum.

Luna is definitely a hole in the wall and nothing fancy, but the service was great, the people were very kind and the food was pretty darn tasty. I'd come back here, no question. From the number of people having lunch there midweek, I'd say Luna is doing quite well for being one of the new kids in town.

I stopped in for lunch today after some co-workers had been last week.
I had the Italian  meatloaf, mashed potatoes, creamed corn and a taste of the green beans.
I'm a Yankee, so meatloaf always came with brown gravy.  This was Italian meatloaf   and came with a red (tomato) gravy that was just great!.  I'd have been just as happy with this stuff laying on top a plate of macaroni.  The mashed potatoes were just that...potatoes mashed with a little salt and pepper....tasted like potatoes.  If you are used to butter and milk in your potatoes, these would seem watery, as I have seem posted, but I think these play better to the vegan crowd, and who needs the extra fat, right?  Ya want creamed potatoes???   Get that fix somewhere else.
The sheltered creamed corn was amazing,  fresh tasting    sweet   creamy like a thinner version of corn pudding.  GREAT comfort food.
The ghetto green beans were awesome, but maybe too peppery for some people.  There was lots of bacon, lots of black pepper, and some red pepper.  I would have loved more, but my cholesterol level held me back.
I'll definately  go back.  I'm anxious to see other things on the menu.  I'll be checkin daly!

* Great Atmosphere. The restaurant was clean, smelled nice, and was well decorated.

* Friendly Staff. The staff was friendly and eager to help with the menu. 

CONS: 

* Location. It's in an area that isn't well maintained.

Discovered Geadello's frozen pizza at a Turnip Truck sampling and met the owner, Joe, who is a fabulous guy and true pizza enthusiast (with Italian and Brooklyn roots -- figures!). Anyways, the frozen pizzas are spot-on for the frozen category. Not soggy or skimpy, just the right amount of sauce and cheese, and a crust that actually tastes like bread (not grease). I've had the whole wheat and plain frozen varieties and both are really good. No funky ingredients or chemicals, either.

I've also ordered Geadello's for my office in Cummins Station and my coworkers all loved it. Geadello's order-in options expand way beyond pepperoni and cheese, and Joe was able to customize my order for dairy & gluten sensitivities. I knew the pizza would be delicious (and it was) but what really impressed me with the Geadello's delivery order was the salad! Really nice mix of kale, spring greens, pumpkin seeds, dried cranberries, shaved brussel sprouts, cherry tomatoes, and other delicious veggies. Definitely way better than your average "pizza place" salad.

Overall, I highly recommend Geadello's. Both their frozen and fresh pizzas are the best I've had in Nashville!

I ordered a large take-and-bake sausage and pepperoni pizza. It was delicious! Cooked at 400 degrees for 12 minutes on the oven rack. Crispy crust. I would have loved some sauce to dip it in! 
I also bought three take and bake pizzas for later. 
The highlight was tasting the sourdough bread while waiting for my pizza to be created. I also bought a loaf for breakfast tomorrow. 

Location: There is a large sign on the street. The store is located at the back of the main street building.  

I love that this is a locally owned startup! Great job!

Wow! I have tasted many pizza's in my life, but this pizza is far more the BEST pizza I've ever tasted! I happened to be in the Whole Foods Market (Franklin, TN) tasted this pizza, and talked to the Owner Joe who is a nice man who has awesome ingredients for pizza and pizza crust. This pizza is phenomenal, and the crust is like I've never tasted before. It's crispy, buttery, and flavorable! His business is located in Nashville, TN, and his pizza's can also be purchased in Franklin, TN at the Whole Foods Market.
Qge0fYnr28bVu5hIp-rjwQ: Great food, great service, no waiting in lines..... love it!  Worth the money. Even my teenage son loved the meal... and he is super picky!

Who knew such a classy place existed within the same arena mostly known for sporting events?  Yet right in the heart of the Sommet Center in downtown Nashville lies the Suite Level Club, a cultured affair of taste.

Are you going to watch your favorite pop artist perform?  Cheer on the Preds?  Catch the latest show?  Well then, make plans to come early and reserve a spot at the glorious buffet that is the Suite Level Club.

Granted, it seems almost a bit crass to call this a buffet; in fact, the restaurant itself refers to it as the "chef's table".  

Whatever you call it, know that there are tons of hand crafted meal options to be enjoyed until you are content.

Open 1-2 hours prior to most events, the Levy Restaurant offers a multitude of hand-carved meats, main dishes, side offerings and unbelievable desserts.  Just make sure to call ahead to place your reservation.

The atmosphere is classy-but-casual, where you will find diners dressed from formal even wear down to shorts and sport shirts.  And don't worry - the staff excels at giving you great service as well as getting you to your seat on time.

I have eaten here many times before hockey games and have never had a bad experience.

This is a buffet  on a much better level than your average buffet.  

They usually have 3-4 choices of meats including a seafood and fowl choice.  Most nights they offer a carved meat.

They usually have 2-3 Salad choices and 3-4 starch choices that sometimes include veg.

They often have a cheese table set up.  The choices are not extravagant but often interesting.

The dessert table has turned into a "shot" sized sampler table.  They used to have larger servings for desserts but I am sure folks got theirs and then had one or two bites.  This is wasteful so the smart option is to serve bite size servings.  I am a fan as I don't really need to eat a full size serving and this way I can try a couple of different desserts.

The staff is wonderful and always attentive without pestering you while you are trying to eat or talk.

It is highly recommended to have reservations however on a slow night you might be able to get in.

If you have a chance be sure to visit and eat here!
8CCLze2jLi_0hWHkv0IkDg: Wonderful oasis! Relaxed college vibe. Clean and comfy. Food is fresh and tasty, and the people who work there are so friendly and kind. I'll be back!

I popped into Joe's today for an Amazing Veggie sandwich. It came out in a timely manner, and the girl who helped me was quite friendly. The sandwich was good, but what made me really happy was the amount of food I got for the money. I was able to get two tasty meals from it. Only criticism? Would have preferred a side of fruit rather than vegetables to go with my vegetable sandwich.

The really amazing part... best gluten & dairy free cookie EVER. Seriously, that was delicious.

If you're looking for a greasy spoon, this ain't your place... If you err on the healthy side and like to know that the food is grown locally and prepared with care, get to Joe's ASAP!  Tasty food served in a great, clean atmosphere!
b4GPlPrcZLZY-TDvr8u3Ug: Since the new reconstruction, this McDonald's has greatly improved. I just tried it today since it has had the makeover and the tea was good, not sour. Fresh, crisp fries and plain sandwiches. Perfect, still needs to improve a little on customer service but besides that it isn't half bad now.

So, I stopped by to get my coffee. Since I have been traveling so much,McDonald's coffee has become my favorite. I ordered my usual "decaf coffee" like most places they didn't have it ready so they gave me a number thingy to put on the table. After a few minutes a lady brought my coffee, she asked if I needed anything else, she also said she was going to check back on me. Maybe  twenty minutes later, she actually came back and checked on me. I don't know is someone from corporate was hanging out, but the staff seemed very active and interactive with customers. I truely enjoyed the short experience. The place was also very clean.
xjxcn-YKM-_aM4yw4Qu42A: Really good Mexican food. Don't let the location fool you. We had the enchiladas and the California Steak burrito, and the burrito was a bit betters. All in all, we would definitely go back.
Note: They ARE open on Sunday's! I used to eat Oscar's at least once a week when I lived in Knoxville, so I was thrilled when we got one in the neighborhood. So so good! Burritos are best.
Best guac around!! Fast and convenient Mexican food without long lines or wait times. The fajita taco salad is my favorite! Oh and the fresh made chips are to die for!
hKq0ulTKBNSAdTlnNbjR3A: As far as Burger King experiences go, this one was pretty good. The staff is very friendly and even offered a kids toy to one of the kids with us that didn't order a kids meal.  It's this kind of service that makes you want to come back to a Burger King!

Is it lame to review a Burger King? It must not be because I keep going back. They have this very basic burger called a "stacker" that I just can't get enough of. 

You can get the Stacker as a single, double, or triple. I think they are running a special now for $1, $2, and $3 respectively. These burgers aren't huge. I like the double and my husband prefers the triple. 

Here's the idea: bun, meat, cheese, bacon, and special sauce (aka tangy thousand island-y sauce). There may be some ketchup on there too. That's it. Well, plus that canned charbroil taste they spray on there. 

At any rate, it's terrible for you, addicting, and amazing. Sometimes in the summer I sneak an ICEE in there too. Shhh.
l3xN428CnbxmlFhzP95tuQ: I had the strawberry basil mule and it was fantastic. Warning - did not realize they have an extremely limited menu Sunday and Monday nights. Had the pulled pork flatbread and sliders and they were both really good, but only having like 7 items to choose from was difficult. Also had the chipotle bacon and was underwhelmed. Service was fine.

Came from out of town and visited this place for the shareable plates, great cocktails, awesome menu item names (named after Johnny Cash, Grateful Dead etc. songs).

Absolutely loved my experience here. We ordered the 'Aint mo mac' cauliflower, fried green tomatoes, and the Nashville chicken and waffles. All food was delicious topped with tasty sauces. 

Waffles were quite soggy but the great taste more than made up for the sogginess. The cauliflower sauce could have been further reduced for a more potent flavor and to thicken it up (it was a little watery). Overall, would order all three menu items again - no buyers regret here!

Our cocktails were delicious too, and weren't too sweet! (I find most cocktails to be way too sweet). 

Interior was well decorated and dimly lit for a nice ambience. Service was excellent.
Great food with excellent service, looking forward toward going back. We arrived at 5p, opening time for the day, no wait. I'm sure that will change quickly.
0CNti1ZrS6d1Ar2nu9J7Og: Excellent place to eat. Food is fresh and amazing. If you want great food and service without the crowd or fighting traffic downtown then I highly recommend here. I had the atomic burger which had just the right amount of heat to it and the grilled cheese. Hands down a great place to eat.
Great Food....very clean restaurant and Paul was awesome in greeting us and giving us some suggestions on our first visit

I saw all the great reviews and came in with big expectations. It's a burger, folks. What makes it different here is that the burgers are stuffed with the cheese of your choice. I got the smallest burger, which couldn't be stuffed. My friend ordered a larger burger and was able to eat it but there's no way I could've gotten at that without a knife. The sweet potato fries were very good. Super friendly/attentive staff and the place is very clean!
Ppfih0Ek1DzO3DgIJ1riEg: Ordered delivery chicken shawarma sandwich and hummus. Loved the sandwich. Hummus was just okay to me. It seemed freshly made however I just didn't love the taste. Still plenty good enough to eat, just not the most flavorful hummus I've eaten. I will be back though for the shawarma and sandwiches!

I have tried shawarma at so many places in different states and different countries and I can tell you pita and grill's shawarma was one of the best shawarma's I ever had. Very authentic taste. Plus you can customize your sandwich. I like to add almost everything minus onions. Me and my sister got the one chicken shawarma and one beef and we shared it. For me I liked the chicken better but that is just my own taste. I want to go back and trey their falafel too.
Vi5s_YhaNFOR-P6vmsinHA: The spinach dip is so so good. Several of us had the Greek salad with mid rare salmon and I'm still thinking about it. Fun, festive, great food and thoughtful service. Love this place.
French Dip sandwich was excellent. Food was hot, juicy roast beef, and  firm bun. Wow.   Onion rings , beer batter were good Husband got a chicken salad - large portion. Alcohol drinks were enjoyable

Stopped in to try this new place for lunch with a friend and we were both very impressed! Overall truly a 5 star visit! The host opened the door for us and was very friendly and I love how open and fun the atmosphere is. Now THIS is the spot to watch a game with so many TVs everywhere. They said to get there early too.

The service was wonderful- great timing and attentive. Love the menu! We shared wings with spicy/sweet sauce. Delish. Cooked perfectly and were crispy on outside and juicy on inside. I got a Reuben and it was amazing with so much corned beef I could only eat 1/2. My friend had the oven roasted chicken with smashed potatoes and broccoli and it was also huge portion and delicious. 

I can't wait to try happy hour! The bar looked fun and it's a great place to meet up with friends after work. Highly recommended
VBW-XIF6KyHpwkUcUylvyw:
Stateside Kitchen is brand spankin' new as of Feb. 2019. It is located in the posh Dream hotel, in Printers Alley. We were lucky enough to be there for their very first dinner service. We felt like movie stars from the moment we entered this brasserie style "dress to Impress" space. Glam. Chic. Black. White. Brass. Yet as casual as you need it to be. If you are vacationing you can certainly come as you are as well. 
We started with the pressed sushi and it is as gorgeous as it is tasty. The sashimi platter was fresh and beautiful. For our entrees we enjoyed the melt in your mouth pork chops, filet and salmon. My guest said "I never order salmon. I only like it the way I cook it." So I offered her a bite. Her eyes lit up and she said " I'm ordering that next time!"  The setting was lively but not too loud. The later it gets, the busier it gets. You are a few short blocks from the heart of Broadway. An easy walk. 
If you haven't been to Printers Alley in a while, or ever for that matter, it's time to visit!

Incredibly chic restaurant located inside the new Dream Hotel in Nashville! We had the deconstructed mimosas which were such a fun take on an original brunch cocktail. Atmosphere was lovely, food was delicious and our server Dustin was fantastic. Highly recommend for anyone looking for a good brunch spot!

This is a great looking space. The vibe is fresh and lively without being terribly noisy. Our server was on time with all orders and everyone in our party of 10 loved the food. I had the Whole Roasted Branzino with sliced lemon, petite greens, brabant creole potatoes. The fish was crisp but the flesh was moist. The greens were fresh and chilled and the potatoes are amazing.
2kEIUUxpjGrvlqCGj8RH8A: We enjoyed our lunch here today! My chicken Apple club flatbread was delicious, and I loved my pomegranate smoothie! My husband enjoyed his chicken caprese sandwich and blueberry smoothie, and our son inhaled his chocolate chimp smoothie and cheese quesadilla! Everything tasted fresh and healthy. I'll be honest, the bacon wasn't the best but it's a healthy restaurant so that's not a shock. The two ladies working were friendly, fast, and helpful as this was our first visit to this location. We will be back!

The best place to go in Bellevue for smoothies. Great prices and great quality. They have a lot of options and they are all great, imI haven't had one that I didn't love. I garubtee you will find something you like.
I love this place! My favorites are the Jetty Punch smoothie and chicken and three cheese quesadilla. Service is always fast and friendly!
XauY4RGt_kGgu5otwYZf0Q:
Despite the other reviews, I decided to try this place.  It was worth the risk. 

I ordered the lasagna, tiramisu, and fried mushrooms. Lasagna was really good as was everything else.

I will order from here again!

We have been ordering from Napoli Pizza and Cafe for a few months now and are very satisfied.  
The pizza is always good and the order takers and delivery drivers are very friendly and helpful.  
Also it is a wonderful thing when you can order late on a Saturday night after being out and not wanting to go back out.  
Also their meatballs are incredible.
HxIRT1cPylU6xedXiBTgSw:
This is the perfect addition to the growing Green Hills restaurant scene. Their dough is made fresh daily!!! Read that again. The nearby pizzerias use frozen dough from a corporate HQ. I choose to support locally ... especially when it is better. And it is. 
They can make any of their pizzas with a cauliflower crust. Lots of vegetarian and gluten free options. They will also make half and half pies with the toppings of your choice, which is great when you don't want two whole pizzas for two people. 
After hours and on the weekends you can park in the bank right next door. Super convenient. There is also valet and a self parking garage on site. Your choice. 
The front patio faces a busy street but it's fun and vibrant to sit out there. The huge patio doors are wide open to the restaurant. Love that. The back patio allows dogs. 
Download the app for a free pizza. The crust is just the right amount of thickness so as to not overpower the fresh ingredients. I have not tried anything on their menu other than pizza, but it looks like they have a nice offering of salads and sandwiches and the like. 
For families, there are some cute chalkboards on the walls to occupy the kiddos. Also a decent bar menu to occupy the adults. ;) 
We have been in many times, and will continue to do so. See ya soon, Brixx!
Great pizza and cheap food and drink specials, and open later than anywhere else in green hills

Let's be honest here: Brixx has full potential for being extremely successful and busy every hour of the day, and I can't wait for that day to happen (hopefully soon ). 

I can safely say this is some of the best pizza I've had and one of my new favorite pizza places to eat at. You can tell how homemade and fresh this pizza is. Super cheesy, juicy tomatoes, and not a single spot of soggy, Lake Michigan-sized pools of oil and grease. 

The prices are ridiculous, too! (In a very good way). Where else can you find an 11-inch pizza for only $10-$13?? Some places in Nashville have the same sized pizza for almost twice that price, and nowhere as tasty as Brixx.
rs2OZH_vIKgW2Zk59OnZ2A: The outside of this coffee shop is stunning, and the inside is perfectly modern and cozy. The cold brew is wonderful and their all plant based menu is delicious. On top of it all, the staff is extremely kind and welcoming. Definitely recommend this place.

I got an iced mocha and toast with jam and my husband got an espresso and avocado toast.  Everything was great, and I really appreciated that they didn't pile on a ton of jam on my toast that I would have just scraped off (like almost every other place we've been to)!  The bread was so good! My coffee snob husband gave the espresso a B.  The one thing that would make this place even better, would be to add some tables to their benches outside!

Yes yes and yes.  Do you like going to church?  No, ok great then what about a coffee church?  This place is a legit reconverted church that is now a third wave coffee shop and (I swear it's not that weird) they have great vegan food and coffee options + nice staff.
dyuxIFvYX1VL5CBVyMejKQ: Ah Lenny's... pretty good Italian sub and probably the closest taste to a real Philly hoagie here, it has the depth of flavor Jersey Mike's misses.  the pepper relish is the key. my only complaint is that it is too salty. Give  them a try.

Usually, I am pretty plain when it comes to subs. I always get turkey and provolone, with all the stuff (lettuce, tomato, onion, etc...) Lenny's turkey sub is pretty good. I personally prefer Jersey Mike's turkey sub, mainly because I love the oil and red wine vinegar. 

But, if you like an Italian sub--Lenny's can't be beat! It's definitely authentic, and the hot peppers are SO good! 

Lenny's is definitely cleaner than any sub joint I've been to. Makes subway look like filth. And it honestly makes Jersey Mike's look a little sub-par, in regard to cleanliness and freshness.
ep8X3Fx2oXW9pE5bkdACGQ: I closed a real estate loan tonight here and had dinner with my kids.  The tenders are awesome!  The place was VERY clean and the atmosphere was great.  They need to advertise more because it's relatively unknown.

Otter's is a chicken franchise out of Louisiana - they serve big, moist chicken tenders, and some other stuff. 

This franchise location is about 5 times bigger than the cramped one over on Demonbreun, but East Nashville denizens would probably eat their silly fedoras before being caught dead in a chain restaurant, so the place is usually pretty empty, which is a shame.

The best items on the menu are easily the fried tenders (easily as good or better than that one place over by the Belcourt) and the Otter's Sauce (basically ranch with a heck of a lot of Cajun seasoning in it). Also give the fried pickles a shot, they're delicious.

The weekly specials are quite good - $2 drafts on Sunday (I think) and  half-price wings on Tuesday, for example. The wings are usually $1 apiece but 50-cent wings are totally worth it. They're large and good-quality, but are dunked instead of grilled so they're more saucy than normal wings (the wife prefers this, I prefer a more standard wing).

They also have NFL Sunday Ticket with a bunch of TVs - I frequent the joint on Sundays to watch the Saints play :)
Q4cn2oFL-AKiYr14devS8Q: I ordered a salad here on DoorDash and it came exactly as ordered! The amalfi coast was super fresh and very filling even though there wasn't any meat. I would highly recommend this place to anyone looking for a fast fresh healthy meal!

Great spot for a healthy & quick lunch!!

The workers are super friendly right off the bat and will assist you while choosing what to order. There's a bunch of selections, all healthy, which is a bonus. 

Our food was ready within 5 minutes of ordering. The entire space was super clean and the food was super good!

The only suggestion I have is to ask for the sauce on the side. I didn't do this and felt like my bowl was drowning in the miso sauce. But, if you like a lot of sauce, then don't ask for it on the side!

Will definitely be returning for further lunch stops!

Great place to grab a quick lunch.This place is embarrassing close to my office in the gulch, yet, I admit to having it delivered on numerous occasions during lunch. Grabbagreen has around 30 locations nationwide. They offer fresh ingredients, locally-sourced when possible. One of my favorite items on their menu is the Grabba Bowl, which is similar to a Chipotle burrito bowl without the Mexican Flair. You can choose to get your bowl with a grain (brown rice, or quinoa depending on the bowl), or lettuce. The best bowl they have is the Patagonia Bowl, It features delicious chimichurri sauce, steak, pepers, corn, black beans, red pepper, and red onion. So good!
2XlnquSnBb1ayfsw3Lk71g: Kitchen Cabinet isn't a destination, but it is a pleasant way to wind up a visit to Andrew Jackson's Hermitage.  They have a fairly limited menu, but they do have a selection of sandwiches that are all nice and fresh.  I've been twice and have enjoyed the chicken salad as well as the ham salad.  My guests have also enjoyed the quiche and the chicken sandwich.

For a gift shop cafe, it was pretty good. I ordered pimento cheese sandwich, and it was very delicious. Everyone at my table enjoyed their food, as well. They have a nice variety of salads, sandwiches, and a few got dishes. The dining room is flanked by large windows on both sides, looking at nice greenery.

This place is pretty good, we had the chicken salad sandwich it is homemade. I highly recommend that everyone try it if it's available we got the last one it is very tasty it has big pieces of chicken cranberries fresh cherries and celery on wheat bread. You won't be disappointed!
_RTSWhnAlMtvoZOybDGpuA:
Pretty tasty. BNA Terminal B doesn't really have many food options that are fast-serve so it was either this, Wendy's, or Freshens.

I had the 3 piece tender combo and it was gigantic. Comes with a small drink and your choice of side, I got french fries and upgraded to a bottled water for 50c extra. I haven't had Popeyes for a while and everything was tasty. It's reasonably priced especially for airport food too, it was about $8.50 with tax.

I received my meal before I even walked away from the counter to find a seat, too.
Mmmmmmmm. Spicy chickens strips, there is nothing like them.  Love the spicy gravy bits on the mash potatoes.

Stopped by for a bite during my layover and Popeyes caught my eye. Popeyes is one of my more preferred fast food spots. And no matter which location I've gone to, the quality is essentially the same.
I ordered the popcorn shrimp & chicken tenders combo which came with popcorn shrimp, chicken tenders, a biscuit, a side (I got seasoned fries), and a drink.
It was my first time having the chicken tenders but they were juicy, soft on the inside, crispy on the outside, and most importantly - fresh!
The popcorn shrimp was okay this time around, as well as the biscuit. You can't go wrong with the seasoned fries here too. I get them every time!
I think this is one of the better food spots to stop by while waiting for a flight and the prices aren't far off from any non-airport location.
rvTwUXskBrjf932Rx5zG4g:
Just moved and needed some cheap and easy takeout.  Mission accomplished.  I for one like my takeout Chinese food from a place that specializes in takeout only so I can not leave a review with respect to the dining experience.  Food wise, it was delicious.  We got the sweet and sour chicken and combo lo mein.  For a whopping $16 (sarcasm) you can feed 4 or get 2 meals out of it for 2 people.

The woman didn't speak English,  no big deal. No veggies in the sweet and sour chicken, no big deal.  This is what you get for the money.

The one thing I couldn't help laughing about after reading the reviews on here...there literally was 1 customer, a middle aged man, alone in a booth awkwardly sitting facing the door eating his heart out the 10 minutes I waited for our food.  It was a Friday night and the adjacent Mexican restaurant had copious amounts of patrons pouring in and out.

Bottom line, great food for the price if you want takeout.  I would never personally sit down and eat there though.
zlqDnmvydtig44hhdih2nw:
Dear taco lovers: 
You. Have. To. Try. Taqueria Don Carbon. As a taco connoisseur myself, I can safely say these are some of the best tacos I've ever had. This place has the real deal Mexican food truck tacos where the price and quality of the food is unbeatable. 

I got 2 asada and 2 barbacoa tacos for only $5! And they were relatively good-sized and packed with the goodies. This ain't your typical late night fast food 1am craving-type of taco. This is a 24/7 all day craving taco. 

They also have a ton of other options, like tortas, quesadillas, sopes, burritos, and just about everything in between. This may be my new favorite taco spot.  Totally worth fighting the White Bridge / Charlotte traffic

This place is awesome. Great tacos reminding me of off the beaten path joints in Texas. Very authentic and very friendly staff. The owner is going to need a bigger place once the word really gets out.

These are the best tacos that I have ever had. I have lived in LA and these tacos are the best that tacos will ever be. There is no getting better than the tacos that this establishment serves. I have tried EVERY TYPE OF TACO and even the pollo is amazing. Good stuff, get you some absolutely satisfying tacos. My favorites were the carnitas and the al pastor.
UQRN2WdTyy0vU6ebc683kg: Just saw the show tonight. Highly recommended. You will start the evening with a smile and leave with a smile. Stevens is still the consummate entertainer and you won't spend a better evening.

I have been to the CabaRay twice, once when it first opened, and again this past weekend with my parents, who live in Ohio. If Ray Stevens isn't enough to get you to come out (and believe me, he is plenty reason enough) the food and staff will seal the deal. I'd like to tell you about this past weekend, as it was hands down the best experience I've had anywhere. 
We bought tickets for Friday night (so we thought, my husband made a mistake on the date, and we had tickets for Saturday instead.) When the staff discovered the mistake, they bent over backwards to accommodate us. Our VIP table wasn't available, but they got us in one right next to our chosen table, and it was still incredible seats. They initially didn't think they had enough food, so they refunded us the meal price, then came up with incredible meals for our whole table. I cannot say enough wonderful things about the staff, and the building.
The building is beautiful, from the gift shop to the chandeliers and the place settings. Ray made all decisions himself from what I understand, and he has impeccable taste. The food is tasty, and the CabaRay wines are delicious. The dishes, the tables, the centerpieces, and the setup of the dining room are elegant, and accommodating to anyone, regardless of disabilities (my parents are elderly, and did not have an issue with getting in, and being seated comfortably.)
Now, for the show. There is simply not a better showman on Earth than Ray Stevens. I am generally a fan of metal music, but since I grew up with Ray and his music, it will always have a place in my heart. After more than 60 years of doing what he does, he still sounds exactly the same. He does all his hits, impeccably, and the show is exciting. He tells jokes, plays instruments, sings, and just shows the world why he has had such an incredible career. You will not be disappointed. He is a super nice guy, and takes time to meet everyone, sign autographs, take pictures, and hang out with the audience after the show in the piano bar.
If you're visiting Nashville, please don't pass up the opportunity to see and experience this in person. It is worth every cent and then some. If you live in Nashville, go...and go again...you will not be disappointed for a second.

So I went to taping of the TV Show Ray Stevens CabaRay Nashville in his new dinner theater. The place place is wonderful and well thought out. Music is top shelf. Roast Pork was amazing. Ray Himself great guy. You'll enjoy seeing his gold records in the lobby.
QgOt5iW0dWhVws_tuATwqg: Some really good BBQ food over here. Doesn't matter what it is but it's always really good. Really fresh. Great fast and friendly service over here. I'll keep coming back and back and back.

I currently live in va, however when i go back to tn to visit I always make a stop at whitts. They have great food and wonderful service. Even the 1 time (out oof many) that there was an error with my order they immediatly fixed it and gave me a little something extra. They are always so friendly, food always hot and fresh and time to prepare is quick. I think whitts i think yumm!

Today after meeting with Steve Dunlap, owner of Whitt's BBQ in Madison, we put up another campaign sign, then my hubby and I grabbed some lunch with our former Councilperson (and my campaign manager) Jim Forkum.  The turkey BBQ sandwich was my pick, my husband chose BBQ pork on cornbread, and Jim wisely grabbed a family pack to share with his wife after a full day of teaching here in MNPS.  It's a takeaway place; the drive-through line was hopping!  #WhittsBBQ #EatLocalDistrict9
sehT3ooK7yJUkiUoDQA_wQ: One of my favorite spots we stopped at on our trip to Nashville this last week. We kind of missed the window on lunch but decided to get a snack before going to the country music hall of fame. It was about 2:15 when we arrived and wasn't too crowded. The staff was fantastic, our server in particular (it was thurs 5/23 around 2pm if that helps!) was awesome. The beer selections were great. My husband ended up trying one of the burgers and really liked it. However I had the pretzel with beer cheese and it was so amazing. Super fresh soft (huge!) pretzel, and the beer cheese was so beyond fantastic!  I wish we had more time to get back there for a second meal while we were in town!  Also, I'm a huge hockey fan.. I've always loved Pete Weber's call (and love listening to him when he's on SXM NHL radio).  I LOVES the glass tops on the tables from the playoffs, and all the pete and Terry memorabilia was super cool to see :). I'd highly recommend stopping in here before/after a visit to the country music hall of fame!

We found this spot right outside of the Hall of Fame and the Bridgestone arena. Service was quick and our waitress was friendly and gave good local craft recommendations. Seating is tight and I wouldn't try it anytime after an event let out but if you're downtown during the day, it's a neat little place to grab a beer and a quick bite. The menu isn't extensive.

Food was great and our server was attentive. We liked the popcorn idea.
Place was inviting but very cold.
pRlmfVuj-I-haJ_uAkyuTA: Loved!  The atmosphere was nice and the food was great.  The salmon club was definitely a winner.  The homemade soup was fabulous.  I will definitely be back!

Drove in to Nashville for the weekend. Needed a quick bite to eat before heading out for the evening. Limited menu but understandable. Ordered Wings and Fries and a very nice Paloma cocktail. It was great! Fresh hot and fast!

Trae and Drey are the best combo in Nashville. Service + Personality = A+ 

Makes me want to stay here, eat here, and party here! 

A Nash Bach I'll never forget!!
w0sjGrU8zHgLgxbVyE5SBA: I always wanted to try jack n the box and i was not disappointed. i enjoyed my breakfast and coffee.
My wife and son had never had the opportunity to eat here . I love jack in the box. And now they do too
8Xt7UXzgLTvmzUSVpDaOpw: GO FOR THE BRUNCH!!! This place is a bar BUT they also do the restaurant side very well.  It has a great atmosphere and really good food!!! On top of that, they do 2 for 1 mimosas!  I got the breakfast burrito and it did not disappoint!!! It was delicious!  The staff is super friendly and helpful.  Overall, I was so impressed!  I will definitely be back again to try their lunch/ dinner menu!  I have been back late night and it's a great place!! They always have good music and well priced drinks! They have a late night menu too.  I tried the loaded tater tots and they are on point!! A perfect late night snack!

Staff is very friendly, place has really good vibes. Good choice for a night out with friends! Would come again 10/10. We visited all the way from Indiana and was left with a really good impression. Definitely dropping by again next time I'm in Nashville.

Fabulous service and atmosphere. Came in on a Tuesday when we were in town and they happened to have trivia that night. It was so fun and all the bartenders were playing. Everyone was so friendly and food was delicious, so good to have some authentic crab dishes in Tennessee
X7iA5ibtUvWIANTCakbUdA: It's Krystal, don't expect a 5 star review. But the lead employee, Ethan, at the drive through window has been there the last few times I've been a customer. CTaS3dtVDJ5Dk3ACI7blYg: Garlic hummus, mashed avocado, balsamic reduction, goat cheese crumbles and diced tomato make this the best "avocado toast" in Nashville. I know, every restaurant in Nashville serves AT but this version is the perfect combination of flavors. Great vibe in this place- music, art, coffee, beer, and great food. And the only place in Nashville that servers a Fluffernutter!
Super Avocado toast was amazing! Cute space, great murals and excellent parking. Would recommend for a quiet space to work outside or a nice meetup with your friends.

Went here for the first time today and had a great experience. I got the breakfast burrito for $5.75 and a chai latte, for about $6. Literally the best tasting chai latte I've ever had!
