I will be back!
iAID8DtHvyE5W4OyOvCczQ:
I was so excited to hear that one of my favorite Nashville taco joints was opening a second location. I'll preface my review by saying that as of this posting the location has only been open for about a week. So many of my frustrations will likely be worked out over the coming weeks.

But I'll start with the most important part of the visit -- the food. We started with the dip trio, which was delicious. All three were tasty. The guacamole was fresh and chunky, the queso was cheesy (and actually tasted like real cheese) and the salsa had an earthy yet spicy flavor that I really liked.

I ordered two tacos: the cheeseburger and my beloved hot chicken taco.

The cheeseburger taco was a little busier flavor-wise than I anticipated. I think it was the garnish on top (not sure what it was). But the beef, black bean spread and all the other toppings were delicious. I could have omitted the corn tortilla in the middle. It got lost in all the action.

The hot chicken taco was as amazing as it was the last time I had it at the downtown location. The chicken is near perfectly fried and seasoned. It has just enough kick. The mayo and crema make for a zingy spicy taco concoction that I could eat for days.

The restaurant operations, on the hand, need some work. The service was slow, but the servers were for the most part apologetic. For as many waiters and food runners buzzing around, it took much longer than expected to be greeted, and our food took even longer. Thankfully, the food was worth the wait.

I typically don't visit a new restaurant so soon after opening, but I will make exceptions when Pancho & Lefty's hot chicken tacos are involved. I'm sure the restaurant will find their groove soon.

Stopped here for a late lunch. Parking was a challenge to locate for a outta towner.  The margarita was delicious. We split an order of big-ass nachos -- huge amount and really good. We'll go back. Staff great.

We were looking for something quick and easy and we ended up here just to grab something to go.  We sat at the bar to order and wait for our food but ended up ordering drinks and dining in.  It was a Saturday night and pretty late, but the service was good, and food and drinks were better.  We had an order of Baja fish tacos, and an order of El taco Verde (veggie). side of beans and rice.  Who knew meatless Saturday would be so good!  We liked the flavors, especially the Verde.  But the fish wasn't bad at all.  We look forward to coming back and trying some more options!  Oh and the Tequila "Old fashioned"  was a nice spin on a favorite.  Would like it more if they had a Happy Hour!
lTjKirWEXIAusvongL_mgw:
Never disappointed!  Shout out to Justin and his staff for the warm welcome. 
We've eaten at the locations in Florida, NC, SC, GA, and now Tenn.  A must try is the five and dime with their signature dark bark roast coffee. Yum!!

Amazing air conditioning as we walked in! They squeeze fresh orange juice at the front, so you can smell it's sweet scent as your order, very tempting! 

We ordered and instead of a number, the order is kept track by your answer to the question of the day! Super cute idea. Ours was our favorite Disney Character (Kovu, from the lion king!) 

After we ordered we sat outside because it was perfect temperature. They usually call out your order, but since we were sitting outside the server kept his eye out for ours, and brought it out to us. All the staff was suuuper friendly! Seriously above and beyond service. 

My partner got the five and dime biscuit. "Fantastic." He's a man of few words. 

We shared the Three Layer Cake. A beautiful heap of soft hash browns, amaaaaaazing Gouda gravy, and a fried egg on top. I like my eggs super runny, and all the eggs we got were not very runny; which my partner likes. 

I got the Sunshine Garden Bowl. Lots of tomato! Some feta cheese and two eggs. It was a perfect balance of spice, and a great healthy choice. I was torn between the bowl and the chocolate chip waffles, but I figured I should eat healthy for baby, haha! 

I got the iced maple vanilla bean latte, and it was fantastic!! It wasn't too sweet, just right. 

They had a cute little bucket that said "Dirty Dishes" where we put our dishes away after eating. The tables are all cleaned and sanitized after eating, yay! 

Hubby loved it, we'll totally be coming back :)

Oh my god. This place is crazy good. We actually went back a second time during our trip. The bam yo yo is soooooooooo freaking sinful and good. If I could pack this place up and take it how. I would. They are amazing and I hope they continue on for a long time. Amazing food and wonderful staff.
xz-da0duuT4R24bW554L8A: Soo good! They are brand new, and the service is amazing. They work to cook authentically, and the dishes are rich and flavorful. Like others say, start with the chicken skins! Delicious. I got the Cambodian curry with chicken and the flavor is robust and so good, I was so full and couldn't stop - but I was able to have leftovers and take some home. Hubby got the Cambodian Fried rice with steak and chicken and the flavor again was right on the mark, with half to take home. Prices are affordable and generous with leftovers you can take home. Definitely will be back.

I have to say this restaurant is gonna be my new spot. Food taste fantastic and it was delicious. I would recommend this lovely restaurant to all my family and friends. They also have great customer services as well!

Visited again, today for Fish cake. This is very first time I see fish cake with real flaky white fish. Amazing fish cake!!!

This is fourth entre I have tried and so far all were very classic - if your taste buds crave for South Asian delicacies.
JSnNc1WIH37vfYFgVBOu7g: We stopped in at the Honest Coffee Roasters in L & L Marketplace this past weekend, and it had some great options for coffees and teas. Service was quick, and our coffees were good. We both got iced Cubans. They have some seating, but you can also find some other seating around and outside the market. Worth stopping in if you're in the area.

So. Freaking. Good!! I'm a coffee snob and this place was phenomenal. The barista recommended one of their popular lattes, the First Lady, and I loved it! I didn't care much for the rest of the marketplace but this quaint coffee shop made the drive worth it.

Food & Beverage - A little expensive, but the lattes are definitely worth it. We also recommend diving straight into their full food menu to get your hands on an acai bowl or a veggie pizza. They have a great selection of options no matter how hungry you come.

Seating & Amenities - Honest Coffee is inside L&L market, and if you get there before noon, you'll have plenty of seating options. The wooden tables and chairs are not entirely comfortable, and you're definitely not guaranteed to sit near an outlet. We've also had some Wi-Fi issues on the occasional visit.

Environment - The coffee shop is contained within its own space, but you definitely get some traffic (and hear the noise) from the L&L market. Inside the shop the music is low, borderline unnoticeable with gentle whispers of conversations nearby. 

Parking - The market has a dedicated parking lot, so you'll never have issues here.

Something to Try - An Acai Bowl, healthy & delicious!
xCwY3wTayU4iUOLkLCr3tg:
Terrific spot with good food, super cocktails, and this morning @lonnieleomusic crooning a great selection of perfect brunch tunes. Kenny served us at Bakersfield in downtown Nashville last night and recommended Big Machine - where he also works. 
Really, great brunch place and perfect last stop on our Nashville vacay. Will make a point to come here next time in town.

Such a cool place!

We came out here from downtown to hear Kelsey Lamb sing. Such a great venue with the singing set up outside under the umbrellas. 

This was worth the Lyft drive from downtown even without Kelsey being there  We had the wrong location. She was singing at the downtown location. Ha. Once we found that out we headed back downtown. Both these locations were awesome. 

This place has a variety of seating both inside and out. They have corn hole and darts for those not wanting to sit still. 

Oh, and their fried cheese curds were to die for. Oh man, those were amazing. 

The service was good too.
Awesome entertainment highly recommend stopping by and getting their frozen vodka lemonade best drink on the broadway strip!
5qXt2UdNCC_mW9R1840LzA: My fellow Californian transplants! This place has legit California Burritos! Their sauces are really good too. I'd write a longer review but let's just keep this place in business.
I eat carnitas at every Mexican joint I go to. I'm on a mission to find the best in the world, and while these ain't it, they are very, very, very good.

Incredible food! I got the carnitas burrito and everything was just right. The meat is so flavorful and the ingredients are fresh as ever. Make sure you give this place a try! My buddy got the carne asada tacos and said they were off the hook as well!
fO5tsZ2MJhRQhSmj9dKYnQ:
This is a must-visit-at-least-once kind of place! Practically next to the Opry Resort and in a mall parking lot, you'll definitely want to go early and go hungry.  The restaurant is beautifully and elegantly decorated with a sizeable gift shop situated at the front entrance.  I went at a good time and only had to wait about 10 minutes to be seated.  The giftshop is packed with all things Paula, and there's a cardboard cutout of Ms. Deen for photos.  The restaurant manager was kind to offer to snap my cousin and me next to Paula's likeness and her dazzling smile.

OK, now for the food...  The meals here are served "family style."  I was slightly hesitant after first reading that, thinking that we'd be grouped with strangers at a big table.  Thankfully, that was not the case to be had!  Instead, for a flat price, you get a choice of 2 or 3 meats + 4 veggies, a dessert, and a beverage.  It's about $20-25 bucks from what I recall.  The BONUS is that it is all you can eat!  Yes, folks ALL you can eat! (Didn't I say to go hungry?!)  When the food is served, you get, let's say, a platter of meat and serving bowls to help yourself.  It's like being at home for Thanksgiving dinner and someone says, "please pass the mac and cheese."

My cousin and I were moved by the cloth napkins and the elegant glasses of delicious sweet tea topped with a mint leaf.  Our waitress was very kind and quick to bring anything we needed. Paula knows how to do hospitality!  Oh yes, but back to the food!  We had fried chicken (salty), but well-cooked; roasted chicken (it looked rubbery to me, so I didn't try it, but my cousin said it was good); fried catfish (a-ma-zing); green beans (Chile, green beans ALL DAY LONG. If you like green beans, you MUST get the green beans!!);  mashed sweet potatoes (fluffy, but a BIG let down as any hint of sugar was nowhere to be found-- and they had an half an inch of melted butter standing (not swimming, honey, STANDING) on top-- yikes! You had to tilt the bowl to excavate the potatoes. I know Paula sings the praises of butter, but Sweet Moses!); and macaroni and cheese (it must not have been great as I only took a small taste and moved on).  We also had a basket of bread, which I will describe as a Red Lobster Cheddar Bay Biscuit attempt? knock-off? second cousin twice removed?  Picture a block-shaped, 3-inch thick, cakey, dense biscuit with cheese trapped inside, topped with garlic butter. If you took a pinch, closed your eyes and sucked it down...  You just may be able to tolerate it until dinner is served.

I cannot conclude without mentioning the dessert. Sad to say that this was a letdown. I tried what they call "peach cobbler."  Sigh...  I'm a Georgia Peach and so is Paula.  Paula, now you know how we do cobbler in Georgia.  Have you gone Nashville on us? Is this how they do cobbler?  What was that thick gelatinous flour-mush with 3 peach slices hiding for their lives on the bottom of the bowl?? I returned it and got a replacement dessert. I can't even recall what I got as a replacement as I was so horrified by the "cobbler!"  

All-in-all, I was very glad to have visited the restaurant and I would definitely go there again to experience great hospitality, ambience, perfect sweet tea, catfish, and those AMAZING green beans!!

Stood in line with three other groups at 11 am opening. Place fills up fast. After being seated at a nice booth with a window overlooking the Cumberland River, waitress was prompt with drinks and biscuits. Then our order did not take long either. We got the 2 meat/4 sides choice.  There is a larger one for more than two or four, but there are no plate dinners or entrees separately from the two Family style orders.  We had roasted chicken and the fried porch chops that come in gravy. Sides were yams, broccoli casserole, black eyed peas and green beans.  Dishes were all delicious. Cheese biscuits were good but not better than Red Lobster's in my opinion.  Ooey gooey cake was wonderful!

The food was great and the staff was friendly. The ribs were the bomb.com. 

They were in a bit of a bind on the day after the 4th of July. They did not have enough staff and the wait time to sit down was roughly 2 hours. 

The staff, in the midst of this, was friendly and courteous. 

I would go back.
fKP9ALt3WZ6WipB98eGIJg: This McDonald's is wunderbar. Service is great, everyone is very kind. Our order came out promptly and with a smile :)

I visited this store at breakfast. I had a very pleasant experience. I was both shocked and pleased. The service was great and the place was packed, as usual. However, they moved the drive thru efficiently. The food was hot, the coffee was good and I was very happy, as I left. It was a great way to start my day.

This is not the Mc Donald's closest to my house but I wish it was! Stopped in today and the drive through people actually talked to me like a friend instead of a bother. They delivered up great hot food really fast. Can't say enough about how good this experience was!
C8pZeqR_g_lDa0vom1ECdQ: Awesome authentic food with powerful but not overpowering flavors and a super friendly staff

Too many people in a closed place. You can walk I. To the end of the restaurant and place an order then pick it up from another window . They will text you when it is ready. 
The inside was cozy with multiple styles of furnitures and tables as well as a chimney that some kids were enjoying.... I walked across quickly so I may be mistaken. Customers seems nice and cool, may be not intoxicated yet since it was early in the evening. But definitely a war cozy feeling inside, with acceptable music volume.
I ordered Ramen bowl 1 for 16$ + tax+ tip. Not cheap but the quality was good. The fish quality and cooking was perfect, with some crunchiness.
I did not take a picture since I was to hungry eating it at my hotel room watching American Ninja finale (irrelevancy). Still took a picture in the recycling mini bin.
Not sure about parking, since I just crossed the street from my hotel.

Ordered the Spicey Ramen for pickup. FYI get your pickup in back.  I couldn't see the big red sign from the front. As for the ramen the favor was delicious and a spicy as I was thinking it would be prior to read a review.  It WAS spicy in my opinion. My mouth and lips were tingling before I even finished.  The separate (cold) non liquid food came in a  separate bowl to mix together at home. So even though the liquid came hot, when mixed together it was damn cold. Since I'm in a hotel with out a microwave I sat there and ate cold ass $20 ramen. Thankfully,  it was at least  tasty cold ass soup.
_CgZegTdPPSh36pm5BNGmQ: Thai Ni Yom is a new restaurant in L&L Market in Nashville's West End.  I love Thai food, so I was excited to welcome a new local spot.  On my first visit, I ordered the drunken noodles (medium) and the vegetable spring rolls.  The noodles were tender and the overall dish was delicious. I love spicy food, but be forewarned that the medium heat level here is very hot.  If you need your sinuses cleared, this will definitely do the trick!  The spring rolls were crisp and had a good sauce for dipping.  You can call beforehand or order at the restaurant (online ordering is not available).  Thai Ni Yom is takeout only for the time being.  There is a small seating area inside, but it is closed during covid-19.

Having grown up in Asia and growing up with Asian food, I think this is as authentic as it gets in Nashville. Great place, amazing (non Americanized) food. Nikki who is one of the owners is extremely pleasant and passionate about the food provided to customers. I think they are short staffed since they are a small business and just getting started so the wait maybe longer than one might expect but  totally worth it! Must try!
My girlfriend and I have had Pad Thai in so many restaurants, from Kc to florida and back. This pad Thai was the ABSOLUTE BEST Pad Thai I've ever had. 10/5
_kGt3lW_MTGaRVdytRSk_A: Better than Ok because it's my guilty pleasure.  I love their chicken, mashed potato and gravy.  This particular establishment whilst friendly and fast never feels really very clean but who am I to complain, I just want my chicken and its cheap.

I've been looking for a KFC with beyond chicken and stopped in here. Line was long but service was friendly. The chicken nuggets were warm and moist and had this amazing flavor. Tastes and looks like chicken. I am sold. Same size and regular nuggets. I took the chicken nuggets to go and didn't eat them for 20 mins and by then they were not crunchy but they were oh so good. Only issue is these are addicting.  First the first time I didn't even care about sides I just wanted more nuggets. They got this right.

They have the beyond chicken.. I have  been waiting patiently for this to be available somewhere near me and I found it.  We went pretty late. There was no wait at the drive thru. The staff was knowledgeable  and pleasant. Now for the beyond chicken. They were nugget  sized portions. The seasoning was great and had a great crunch. The price may be a little steep if you aren't accustomed  to paying vegan prices but I definitely  feel it was worth every penny.
jOQmc0QemMn_DN0JL3xBuw:
Authentic and tasty no frills Indian street food. Word is this place is connected in ownership to Taj Indian....a fantastic spot just down the street. 

Inside is clean and very simple. Staff was friendly and the food was really solid. I will be back!

We try the posh Indian places in Nashville looking for street food, and always circling back to this location. It is a hole in the wall, but the food can make you develop cravings. They got me when I found out an order of chola bhatura comes with two bhatura? Who would had "thunk" it?

This is different from the usual Indian restaurant so we weren't  familiar with most of the menu items. But the gentleman was very patient and answered any questions we had. And the food was genuinely some of the best we've had in years!  Will definitely go back again soon. 

*Bonus- it's adjacent to a giant Indian grocery store,  so you can pick up some naan, frozen samosas, and other tasty snacks to enjoy at home while you're there!
WFPHfjWYoqDCMo5P5gniPQ: Awesome place. Everyone is always so friendly. I have never gotten a bad meal. Can't wait to watch a Sounds game from here.

We visited this spot this past week to watch the Nashville SC game. Let me start by saying the views and general vibe of this place are fantastic- bar overlooks downtown, side of the building overlooks the sounds stadium, TVs everywhere- the environment is unbeatable. 

This place, for me, is like an elevated sports bar. Great beer selection, 2-4-1 happy hour, and plenty of sports to watch, but surprisingly delicious food- the wings were to die for as were the loaded tots! Everything was also very reasonably priced, which is a plus. 

Staff was overall, extremely friendly and accommodating. I docked them a star because of Covid protocol- everyone has a mask on, but the host sat us In a weird spot and we felt a little too close to others- and when we asked to move she wasn't really sure how to respond.

Other than that strange interaction, this place is a home run (pun intended!)

Great customer service. Staff is welcoming & nice. Great beer selection. Just an overall solid sports bar. Huge space. Indoor/outdoor seating with the bar running in both sides. Overlooking the baseball field is a cool touch. Haven't had any food yet but the menu looks great. Highly recommend for groups and/or to watch any sporting event.
GFACw5ZUjQWA7Uw1xRz-QA: Watched the Titans vs. Ravens game there on Saturday, it was a packed house and we even got there early. We had heard the service hadn't been great since the relocation to Germantown. Well....it was phenomenal the entire time we were there! Sunshine was our server and she was fantastic! We always had full drinks, she was constantly checking on us which was amazing considering how busy they were. The food was great and the happy hour deals were too!! We will definitely be back!

We love this place! It's got plenty of seating, great service, and an overall good vibe. Their happy hour specials are some of the best in Nashville, and their food is great. They also have an awesome outdoor rooftop area with a fire pit which is great for summer nights.

First time I've been to Jonathan's in Germantown. It it's so cute!! I sat at the bar while waiting on my friends to show up. The food was good. I had the pizza rolls and spinach dip. My spinach dip was tasty but kind of room temperature. They had amazing specials on appetizers and drinks. The atmosphere was really nice too. I'll definitely go back.
3W-x6oPytjamSbMnizEB5w: While service is slow and they had a hard time getting my order in without me having to repeat it 6 times. I ordered the following for me and a friend: Hawaiian chicken sandwich, blackjack chicken sandwich, garlic parmesan waffle fries and the summer griller bbq Slaw dog. The total was 19.62 which isn't bad for the food I got. The fries were heavenly, best waffle fries I have ever had. My Hawaiian chicken sandwich was very delicious, with the right amount of sweet and salty. My friend's blackjack chicken sandwich was way too salty but still had a good flavour. The BBQ Slaw griller while good was lacking something. Maybe stronger pickles or jalapenos would improve the flavour some. The only reason stars aren't at a 5 is the fact they weren't organized. If they work on that and the overly salty blackjack sandwich the whole experience would be better.

Outstanding customer service!!!  Food has been great every time!  We usually have delivery but today we decided to dine in.  Every person working that we had contact with was super friendly, made sure everything was good, we had everything we needed.  The restaurant was super clean, you could tell they stay on top of it. While we were dining there was a girl mopping the entrance and dining areas, she made sure to forearm us to be careful on our way out and her and the girl at the counter told us to have a good evening.

We're happy we chose to dine in this evening as the service at this location matches the food quality/taste! We'll definitely continue to be customers here but because of our dine-in experience we'll make a point to 
Come in!

Thank you!!!

I'm very happy with my experience at this location. I have dietary restrictions and they were able to answer all my questions and make suggestions! I really appreciate the patience considering how easy it is to get frustrated or impatient but they were great and understanding!!! I'll definitely be back! Thanks
iqvP4fAl6i6yQqiMcibPoQ: The ambiance, service, drinks, and food was all excellent! We came here because we wanted a variety of food options and a patio and this place has it! I ordered a margarita flight and we ordered the deviled eggs and ceviche for appetizers - all really good. Then my husband and I shared the picnic platter with the salmon and Cajun shrimp which was really really good! The sauces that come along with it are delicious. I would definitely recommend this place for a fun, chill night with good food.

Great little hang. Friendly staff, delicious tacos, live music, plenty of tv's screens, and a dog friendly patio. This place has a warm neighborhood vibe to it. They completely remodeled this space and it looks great. Happy to have such a great local spot in Germantown. Yay Sonny's!
I enjoyed this place. Just stopped in for drinks and apps but everything was tasty, the bartenders were nice, they had hockey on tv, and the place had a relaxed and fun vibe to it.
rVOtaL7v_pwZmLJbKL45jA: Rarely is a place like this worth the price. Eddie V's is worth the price. The parmesan Sole, Korean calamari, and scallops were delicious. The drinks were very well made as well. Only negative is the lack of good beer options.

I've been traveling and entertaining business associates for 19yrs. I loved in Nashville for 9 and went out often with friends. In all my travels I have never had the quality of service I received at this Eddie V's. The staff from our waiter Anthony to the manager Ross made me feel like a queen on my birthday. We had some last minute cancellations and they accommodated us anyway. I felt like a celebrity!  The food was amazing and I felt like the value was outstanding!  Definitely a home run!  I will be back and anytime I want to impress a business client, this will be my first choice!  Thank you for an amazing experience!!
The drinks and appetizers were delicious, great atmosphere and our server, Chris made the experience five stars! We will be back because of her , thank you!
059fmQtYFQv3-hINwfpGsw: Second time here. Had previously been a regular at The Jam. The food is great and the service has been awesome as well.  Try the breakfast biscuit. Slap yo mamma good. I'll make this a regular stop!
The food and coffee are amazing! The people are incredible too! Be sure to order the breakfast burrito and matcha lemonade. The farmers market on Saturday is a plus!! Oh it's so cute too!

Great coffee and light food options! I like that they use local ingredients and have healthy options, especially vegetarian and vegan stuff. There's lots of plants and natural sunlight, plus it's close to home so I love to get work done here. All of the employees are so kind and remember faces! Pretty much everything I'm looking for in a local coffee shop.
eucw9-atN4B0lvFhsZGc6w: I was pleasantly surprised by this spot. Went there for dinner. The Pistola cocktail was absolutely delicious. The smokiness of the mezcal came through despite a strong spiciness and the carrot taste. I had to have two. The crispy blue cheese olives were really nice. The carrot appetizer was recommended by the server and I would've never ordered it but he was correct. Outstanding. The oyster pan roast starter wasn't as memorable. I had the bucatini with chicken and it was creamy and rich. Ricotta, lemon and walnuts added for the perfect zest and crunch. To finish the meal we shared the chocolate hazelnut dessert and I could've had about 2 of them by myself. The spot is in a strip mall and I was surprised at how great the food was. I'll be back for the daytime menu soon.

Great little spot for breakfast. Got there when it opened, and it was quiet, but picked up in the next hour. The baked eggs were delicious. Only reason for 4 vs 5 stars was because the service was pretty slow; took about thirty minutes to get our food, despite being the first people to order. Otherwise, highly recommend!

I'm so excited about this restaurant!! It's in the same shopping center as Children's playroom which is a huge win! Not only can you drop your kids off for date night, but you can also have a lovely dinner and not even leave the parking lot! 

I recently got to go to this beautiful restaurant with my beautiful friend and we had such a lovely time! They have a great happy hour, so many dishes to choose from and the cocktail and wine menu are excellent! 

We split some oysters and they came with mignonette and a Bloody Mary sauce, which was great since I don't do vinegar in any shape or form :)

I tried a glass of the sparkling Brut Rosé on their happy hour menu and it was delicious! 

For my dinner I chose the skate fish and it was lightly fried and so tender and flaky. The side salad had vinegar, but I had plenty of food with the skate! 

Everything was cooked perfectly and the service was top notch. They had customers spread apart and require masks when not eating and had hand sanitizer and extra masks. 

Check this place out!!
4ANpPj8vE5Ve38hARepwOQ: I have watched several videos on YouTube about this dish. So I ventured out today and got lunch to bring home...I can just say AMAZING!!!! Best mexican food I have had in a thousand years...Return customer for sure!!!

MacHenry's is an absolute treasure. It's quality, it's quick, it's inexpensive, it's (depending on what you order) healthy, and it's delicious (like DELICIOUS delicious).

Although I've never been to the physical location, I now get it delivered once or twice a week. I used to get Arnold's delivered, but I'm Team MacHenry's these days.

Did I mention it's delicious? Ya'll.

MacHenry's is the new kid on the block just southeast of downtown in a former Jack in the Box across from Metro Police Headquarters. Certainly Nashville is a city replete with meat-and-three joints, some forgettable, some good, and some great.

MacHenry is going to become one of the more competitive such establishments in the area with high quality, down-home cookin' from scratch. I've tried several meat options there including a roast pork loin with marvelous seasoning, juicy and tender in a light gravy. The catfish is a huge filet with beautiful crispy batter, although I hoped the actual fish had been a bit flakier.

The two side dishes I've sampled were amazing. The fried Brussels sprouts with seasoning are perhaps the best in the city of Nashville, and I don't even like Brussels sprouts! They just left everyone at the table stunned! The mac and cheese is incredible, a far cry from the fake cheese sauce poured over shells that many restaurants attempt to get away with. This is real, melted, slightly crispy cheddar cheese over quality pasta were talking about!

Everything here is homemade and the freshness shows. And the honey butter rolls are simply impeccable. Customer service here is fantastic as the owners make you feel very welcome and taken care of. I highly recommend a stop at MacHenry's Meat & Three!
NOgKww4Wr5BMgOgEb9BIfQ: One person on the front and two cooking in the back, one of which was new. Very short staffed, but Team was doing the best they could. 2ntwsTW112z4csqvmM6IEA:
Visiting Nashville from California on a Sunday and visiting some distilleries (not having ate much).  We asked a couple of people about where to eat and drink. Somewhere they go, outside of Broadway.  They suggested this place and I said no since I'm always disappointed with Mexican food outside of California.  The gave me a ramen place and choppers for drinking.  When we arrived to choppers my BF didn't want to go across the street for the ramen, so I finally gave in and ordered from the truck.....
OMG!!!-  so glad I did!! These were the best birria tacos I can ever remember having!!  They were so good I had to go back and order some more.  And like a crazy groupie hounded the chef.   :)   He probably thought I was crazy. I can't believe I actually hit the lottery and found a good Mexican place outside of California and Texas.  I'll gladly eat my words at this place! Nom nom nom   Goes great with the drinks at choppers.

Maiz de la Vida is a Nashville business I am super excited about. Got 2 minutes? I'm happy to talk your ear off about this amazing business in Nashville called Maiz de la Vida (I'm only half kidding)! 

Why is Maiz de la Vida so cool? Their tortillas are made in house - from masa they nixtamalize themselves on their very own molino (mill), which they named "El chulo"! Maybe you know a lot about masa you're like: "Nixtamalizing their own corn for masa? That's cool." But maybe you're like a lot of people (me included until very recently) and you're like: "I don't understand anything you just said." So here's a little info so everyone can understand exactly how cool it is that Maiz de la Vida is nixtamalizing in Nashville. The process of nixtamalization was first developed in Mesoamerica  centuries ago. Essentially, it involves boiling dry corn/maize and soaking in a solution that includes slaked lime and then rinsing it before putting the nixtamalized corn through a mill that turns that nixtamalized corn into masa dough (if this was ground, sifted, and dried, it becomes the masa flour you've probably bought at the store to make tortillas) which can then be made into a number of finished products. Most restaurants serve food using tortillas they buy (there are a lot of reasons for this and there's nothing wrong with it), which makes fresh house/home-made tortillas a TREAT. A delicious, tasty, amazing treat. You can have this treat at Maiz de la Vida.

My first interaction with Maiz de la Vida was back in August of 2020, when I bought some masa from them which was delivered to my house. Just from that interaction, I was a fan. But Maiz de la Vida obviously had big plans for themselves and for Nashville - because this weekend I got to try some of their food from their AMAZING FOOD TRUCK.

As of January 2021, the food truck can be found at Richland Park Farmers Market from 10-12 on Saturdays and Chopper (the tiki bar in East Nashville) from 12-7 (I think; check their Instagram for more up-to-date information) on Sundays. I've seen on Instagram that they can have longer lines on Saturdays (so I've been told to go early if you don't want to wait in a long line and they do sell out), so we checked them out on a Sunday at Chopper. On Sundays at Chopper, you can take their food to go or they'll bring it to you inside Chopper. I hadn't been inside a restaurant or bar for several months due to the COVID numbers in Nashville, but Chopper only had 2-3 tables of people in their very large space, so we decided to eat it there and order some cocktails from Chopper as well. 

So - now that I've talked (but really written) your ear off about nixtamalization and how a food truck works - here's what you came for: THE FOOD. It's good, really good. Like all I've done since I ate there is tell people about it GOOD. On our first visit (we'll be back), we tried their mole negro enchiladas (with green chile chicken), their elote on the cob, and their beef quesabirria with consome. 

The enchiladas - I LOVE MOLE NEGRO. It's delicious, so complex (sweet, savory,  subtly hot from chiles), and always surprising (because there's no ONE recipe for mole). The chicken was also good! But the real surprise on this plate of excellent food? The escabeche (pickled veggies - jalapeno, carrot, radish)!!! I'd buy a jar. And the tortillas - OBVIOUSLY. A great dish. I'm gonna eat it again.

The elote was also great - classic, tasty, with just the right kick from the "3 pepper mayo" and piquin powder. Definitely a yummy side dish that hit me with a strong punch of nostalgia. 

BUT THE QUESABIRRIA. To be honest, it's why we came. I saw it on Instagram, and I had to have it. New to quesabirria or birria tacos? Birria is a slow-cooked meat stew from the state of Jalisco - traditionally goat, I think. Quesabirria is a sort of mash up of a taco and a quesadilla with consome (delicious soup)? A tortilla (a fresh and delicious AF tortilla in this case) gets dipped in the consume and then filled with cheese and the beef or the mushrooms - which we haven't tried yet but heard is great - and then griddled until crispy. You can also get a cup of consome to dip the tacos in again. Maiz de la Vida's consome is delicious - we got it, dipped our tacos, and drank the rest. Maiz de la Vida serves it with raw onion, cilantro, and a lime wedge. The consume is delicious, the tortillas are amazing, the cheese was melted and gooey, and the braised beef was delicious. This might be the best bite I've food I've had in I don't even know how long. Need a pick me up? Maybe Maiz de la Vida's cheesy, meaty, saucy, tasty quesabirria is exactly what you need.

Okay, I have now written the longest review of all time. BUT THIS IS GREAT FOOD AND YOU SHOULD TRY IT. You can support a great local business and eat amazing food at the same time. Check it out (check their Instagram before you go to double-check hours and locations of the truck)!
Best tacos I've had in Nashville!!! I've been looking for an authentic taco spot to compete with the tacos I had in California and this is it!!
51V09dHr3OBE_Bvbc_5ioQ: My parents and I found new places to hang out with and great outdoor place and awesome food also delicious and great band as well definitely come back again also great service as well

We had the opportunity to have lunch at this establishment. Our waitress, Michelle, was amazing. xN-ioR8KzrEcha9G9pV7lA: Jacob at the bar offered great conversations and recommendations. Stopped in for a sip before going elsewhere and it was the perfect start to a late weekend afternoon. I can't say enough good things about this place! My cousins and I went to have dinner and we all ordered pizzas. They were the best pizzas we have had in a very long time! Super chill vibes and delicious drink and pizza. Best option for a chill night out in Nashville. Safe and professional staff. A must stop for your Nashville stop.
_yNmoxOl_ZyS-VUCluii8Q:
Great food. Funky Attitude... But mostly great food. 

We walked in and looked at the menu on the wall. Everything looked great. My wife told me she wanted phō. I told her that I think it's pronounced faw.  We decided to split that and try the lettuce wraps since the pho bowls were huge. 

We ordered, and my wife politely asked the guy taking our order if it was pho or faw. He went full teenager on us, and let out an exasperated sigh followed by what sounded like Fuh under his breath? yab1Kp34YwJPJFHLrcFMtw: Been there several times and although they struggled with service they are much improved!

My kids love Chick Fil A so we are regulars at the Bellevue location.  These guys are always friendly and smiling even though the line is always long. This chick fil a is a bit slower at bringing out food than most others I have been to, but they are much busier than any other in the area most days. They always have lots of staff, so I fully believe any issues with speed have nothing to do with their employees and everything to do with how busy it is here! Great pizza for after hitting the bars on Broadway! Made to order in 6 minutes and was delicious. Reasonable prices and a clean place.
Super good pizza! The crust is amazing and the staff is so friendly. Love that they're late open too!
io1zE4Tj2c0a6YKw2zXKkg: Service was prompt and on-time. Staff was professional and helpful. Chicken was hot, as were the sides.  I certainly will frequent this business in the future. Thank you KFC Harding Place.
5DJ2sDvM_XRndlB24DnxYw: I just had my third dinner here, and it continues to astonish.   The menu is always different, but it has always had a number of interesting vegetarian options.  I've now had three of the best meals I've had in Nashville at Adele's.    The service is spot on and excellent -- Ben and Liza are both great -- and the manager, Cindy (sp?), was warm and kind.  This is really a fantastic place.  I can't imagine anyone not loving it.

Oh how I love this place. We sat at the bar, and our bartender Haley was a dream. So friendly, and had the cool factor down. My girlfriend and I shared a few plates. Meatballs, yes. Spaghetti, yes. Pork belly, yes. And the chocolate mousse to finish was devine. I always order an old fashioned wherever I go, and this place passes the test with flying colors.

Completely forgot to take pictures, that's how good the food was.

My man and I popped in here for dinner during my last night in Nashville, and what a send-off it was! We loved the wide open warehouse-style space with intense moody artwork and a dim flickering candle at each table.

To share for appetizers, we decided on the asparagus salad and the strawberry ricotta bruschetta. The asparagus salad was really good - I love warm salads - with a poached egg mixed in and in a petite portion. I think one of my favorite things about Adele's is how shareable everything is. The strawberry ricotta toast was ok - to be honest it came off more bland than I expected it to be (lightly tart, a couple pickled shallots, nothing too complex) and we ended up seeing the same strawberries in our panna cotta dessert.

For dinner, we had the gnocchi and the tagliatelle. I was torn between the tagliatelle and the bucatini (tomato + cheese), but ultimately decided on the tagliatelle because I wanted to go lighter on the dairy. It did not disappoint. It is described as a pesto dish on the menu, but the pesto was perfectly understated in a tangy brown sauce. The pasta itself was al dente, which I appreciate so much, and came in an appetizingly portioned tangled pile of carby goodness.

The gnocchi was one of the best I've had. U DA BEST I EVER HAD. Usually gnocchi is soggy and starchy, but in this case it was wonderfully burnt on the edges and even chewy. Topped with raw arugula and tossed in a tasty sweet pea puree, it was incredibly yummy and fun to eat.

For dessert, we decided on the panna cotta as a palate cleanser. Loved the light milky pudding contrasted against the crunchiness of poppy seeds, and then the sharp tartness of strawberries.

Would love to come back when I visit Nashville again!
6jr6xW05PcKutwr5lbVVAg: The steak here is amazing, expensive but the amount you get and the quality is spot on. The asparagus dish we had was not great, but the mashed potatoes and brussel sprouts were delicious. The desserts are also very delicious, the carrot cake is unbelievable! Overall I would highly recommend eating here. The service was also great.

So I pretty much put Mortons, The Palm, Ruth's Chris and Flemings in the same category.  They all serve the exact same perfectly cooked steak and sides.  They all have sophisticated atmospheres.  They all have more trendy bars and classy well decorated well lit dining rooms.  They all have great wine lists (maybe Ruth's Chris has the best).

How is Fleming's different?  They are a little more reasonably priced than the others.  Sunday nights they have a $33 4 course prime rib special.  

I went there the other night and sat at the bar.  The house salad was VERY fresh.  It had a vinaigrette, fruit and nuts.  My filet was perfectly cooked and the sides (served family style) were mushrooms and baked potato.  The bar was happening and I got to witness Michael Phelps win his 8th.  I'm going back next time I feel like a good steak on a Sunday.

Fleming's is always consistent in high quality food. I travel for work and eat at the bar. Brian, the bar tender there,is superb. He was attentive, but not creepy and over the top. There was a small mix up with my food, prob because I was being a bit picky...but he noticed without me having to say a word and had it fixed right away. That is why you go to Fleming's! I had the filet mug on small plate and Brussels sprouts. The steak was cooked to the right temperature, and I really liked the flavors. Also you can't beat their happy hour wine list, and I like the 6 and 9 ounce pour options.
IE-5GIHlAINr0zO4lrJ_Lg: This place is a well kept secret. It is off the unbeaten path. Parking can be tricky but it totally worth it. Bun to hot dog ratio was perfect. It's a cheap eat. Apologize no pics the hot dog didn't long. I will be a repeat customer.

Hot diggity dog, this place ROCKS!  The Rebel YELP equates to pure and ridiculous love. That Tennessee chow chow is so flavorful, and all the toppings were fresh, sassy, and complimented the hot dog so well.  The bun? Tender, huggable, yumbo.

I love this place. The buns are amazing quality and the dogs are great! I love the Chili Cheese Dog. They were out of Chili in my last visit but I was recommended the Pimento Cheese Dog and was not disappointed. I work in the area so this will always be a lunch staple for me.

We started our Nashville weekend at Roberts. It was supposed to be one of many stops on our first day! Problem is we never left. WKPu6-QSUp4VHVGPehbGSQ: Stopped in for lunch and will definitely be back. Great friendly service and very clean. We ordered the sushi lunch special and it was more food than we could finish. The fish was fresh and everything was very good!

I was running errands and was craving sushi so I dropped in to LJ Asian Cuisine for lunch.  I don't know why but I didn't have high expectations.  Maybe because it's located in a run down strip mall.  Anyway.....I ordered the gyoza and the Alaska Roll.  The gyoza was probably the best I've had - totally didn't expect that! Always love the sushi and they have the best pad see-ew in town. I love coming here. I enjoy a lot of things on the menu so I end up ordering like 4 things for myself Everytime I go. It's not the cheapest place in town but I will splurge on it when I'm in the mood for it!
ZNxFIwgPnmLu6ouQPPpR_Q: Love the overall look and feel the place. A good jump off point of the night if you are with a group of friends. Amazing! Try the crab cakes and the cheesecake sopapilla. You won't be disappointed.

Couldn't have asked for a better brunch experience. We went on a rainy Saturday so we sat inside but I did notice outdoor seating. 4Cal2AE6cr7m6nL20Rz2kQ:
Hard to say what hasn't been said about the Ryman. It's literally one of the best places in the country to hear live music as it was built as a church before the days of amplified acoustics.

A few tips: the first two rows of the balcony are some of the best seats in the house. The first two front rows on the floor are folding chairs. When seeing a show the merchandise area is on the second floor; get there early if you want one of the limited edition hatch print show prints of your show as they sell out quick.

Spend the extra $4 and go on the Back stage tour. If you're lucky enough, Buddy will be your tour guide. I learned more about the Opry and Nashville in general in the hour or so of this tour then I could have from any book or movie. Buddy has met all of the greats including Johnny Cash, June Carter Cash and even Elvis and has amazing stories to go with these encounters. Including the fact that the lights in the Cash dressing room were surrounded by metal holders because Johnny Cash would drink a bit too much and then come in and smash out all of the lights. Buddy even explained to us that the Ryman Auditorium has the second best acoustics in the world (The Mormon Tabernacle is number one). He was extremely informative and quite hilarious.

After the backstage tour, I decided to walk my way through the self-guided tour. This was also very interesting and definitely worth the hour or so it takes. Ryman Auditorium is a beautiful building filled with a ton of history. I wish that the Opry was still held year round at Ryman and I would love to come back to see a show in the future.

Birth of the Bluegrass, 1945! The history in this place is incredible. I was only in downtown Nashville for 12 hours and experiencing this venue was one of the funnest nights of my life. I've learned my lesson and will purchase tickets early next time because there is no general admission, which might be obvious for some events but not always the case when a rock band is playing.
MPCknINXdfg1aWnBK7B7gQ: Amazing! We had a bit of a wait for the food and then our food was sent to the wrong table, but I will say they definitely made up for it in service and good food quality. The food was excellent and very fresh. I really enjoyed the Z Bar Chicken sandwich; it has this amazing kale salad on it, umm umm good. My husband really loved his burger which had a blackberry jam that reminded me of BBQ sauce. Great food and service! You must try this place!
Delicious bison burger, exceptional service, and a reasonable price. Will return!

I'm definitely not "hot&cold" about this local place, so fun! With the best of everything in the treats world under one roof, how could you not love this place? From homemade ice cream (unique & classic) to the las paletas (love the minis!) and the pure chocolate drinking shots, yum! We got a four scoop sampler (c'mon, we shared between 3 people!) & the elderberry ice cream was hands down our favorite, yum! We can't wait to go back!

I made my husband take me here b/c I wanted a paleta.  And I will get one some day (hopefully avocado...yummm...).  But this time I got distracted by Jeni's ice cream.  

I asked for a taste of the wildberry lavender, I don't know why.  I don't like lavender in food--I like to smell potpourri, not taste it.  But I was feeling adventurous.  And thank heaven I was.  This is quite possibly the best ice cream I've ever had.  This really tasted more like orange (which, while it may not have been what they intended, was just fine with me since I love orange).  And it was at least as creamy as any gelato I've tasted.  A bit pricey--$4 for a small--but yeah, this is $4 ice cream.  They sell pints for $10.  I didn't get one tonight.  But yes, I will at some point be ponying up $10 for a pint of ice cream.  It's just that good.
2JKbWGJeE3LdwHJZSeAGEg:
I've frequented Portland on 12south but have only been to the east location once. I have to say I like this one better, aesthetically I mean. The other location is cold and dark, however that won't stop me from getting their iced tea, which I've successfully weened myself off of. However I know there will be one day where I'll need an ice tea...yes need...and Portland will be the place I go to get my fix. Either East or South. 

I have to also say that not only is this place more open and well lit, the nice person who got my iced tea made the atmosphere extra welcoming. I'm not saying the folks in the South location are snooty or something, they just seem more 'serious business' than the East location. 

Yay tea!

Love this place very close to home and they don't rush you through a line. My barista was friendly and didn't seem hungover sleepy or any other bad names these workers have been getting! My favorite chill spot on a lazy sunny morning.

I am by no means a coffee connoisseur and this review focuses on the ambience on the coffee shop, not so much the coffee itself. I ordered an iced white mocha in the 16oz (you get a choice between 16oz or 24 oz) and it ran me about 4 bucks. Price is pretty comparable to Starbucks, but I thought this cup of white mocha tasted a lot better than getting the same drink at Starbucks. But again, I really don't have a well defined palate for coffee aside from the fact that it keeps me awake to study! 

What gives this place 4 stars for me was the fact that it's quiet. A lot of coffee shops I've been to recently (The Well, Barista, Bongo Java, for comparison's sake) have been extremely loud, extremely crowded, and full of either overexcited college students or rambunctious hipsters. Being a grad student, my only goal in life is to find the perfect place to study. And I think my search is over, thanks to Portland Brew East! This is a two level coffee shop, and I absolutely love that. Both of the floors were pretty quiet - you don't really hear much aside from soft background music provided by the coffee shop and the whirring of the cappuccino machine. The upstairs level is spacious, filled with natural sunlight, and covered with tables for you to work on. The downstairs level is a little bit more cramped due to most of the space being taken up by the kitchen, but there were definitely still a good number of tables downstairs. What I love about this place is that there's a significant amount of space between each table - you're not sitting in people's breathing space here like you are in a lot of other small shops! 

I came in around 1pm on a Sunday and there was no line. My coffee was made to order and I found the best little nook to study in upstairs. I love everything about this shop! My only complaint is that there's no private parking lot - you're subjected to street parking, which I absolutely hate. If there's a reason for me not coming back here, it's probably the parking situation.
4kViSyQIJxn7kg-uGrYboA: When I drive in, I have to park in the former car-wash next door, as the main lot will be full. Then I'lll wait in line for ten minutes. Then I'll wait ten-fifteen more minutes for my food. But then the food comes, and they sure do make a mean sandwich. Definitely the best sandwich you will find in Donelson. But skip the brownies - surprisingly those were no good.

This is for all those travelers out there who want to eat healthy and are stuck at the airport hotels. Go to this restaurant . First bite thank god, fabulous. Sooo flavorful. I had Andy's orbit with spicy hummus and a glass of wine, I might add. Did I ever luck out here. Walk the distance If you are stuck without a car, it is worth it!!!. I forgot one thing, for gods sake fix the outdoor tables!!!!

Phat Bites is absolutely DELIGHTFUL. It's a great break from the standard sub shop you find on every corner. 

Their unique vibe carries throughout the menu the beverage selection, the staff, and the decor. 

Now let's talk food: I am never ever ever disappointed with my selection. My favorite is the black bean burger: a giant patty of black beans, sweet potatoes, cashews, carrots (I think...or maybe those top it...) all on ciabata...Wow. I just mean wow. They have an impressive selection of hummus and their pita is to die for. 

The chicken salad salad is SO amazing. (i'd recommend splitting half out for later before you add the dressing). 

Headed there again for lunch today!
GvLJDYT797K2utTSH-ZpKw:
Ribeye steak sandwich was very good and me wife enjoyed the chili & cornbread. The chili had a bit of a bite

Service was very good

As close as Cheer's as you can get. Friendly, family-oriented and great food.  

Happy hour is filled with locals talking sports, local news, etc. -just a great gathering atmosphere.  

Dinner hours - you will have couples out for a laid back dinner to families with kids. 

Occassionaly have to wait briefly to use the restroom but the overall character, food and service makes up for it.

The servers were a delight and it's plain old-school Nashville good. It is a lodge-style pub-bar, so some of the comments that are shocked by that, are pretty amusing. But unlike many pub-style restaurants it's very child friendly. My cheeseburger with crispy bacon & bourbon sauce was as good as any of the top burgers in town. Very approachable, refreshingly nontrendy, with a lodge/pub charm. We love it!
8brk96MD077VJTGOUI67Qg:
Absolute must! First time here. Frequent the area for business, wanted something different. 
I was offered something off menu...a smoked burger. We talked for a second and we decided to put some pulled pork on there as well with cheddar cheese. Got to the hotel and slapped some slaw on it with the BBQ sauce (hot) and went for it. Best burger/BBQ sandwich I've ever had. Tons of smokey flavor and cooked perfectly. The slaw added that crunch and the sauce didn't overpower the taste.
The owner was really fun to talk to and passionate about smoking meats. I trust a man that gets bored and decides to start smoking burger patties. I will be returning!!
Great Brisket!  Easy! healthy! Good!  Great to have a place like this in Bellevue.   Order on your way and take home or enjoy a simple outside table.  Not fancy, just good.

We were so happy to get some good BBQ in Bellevue.  We had the family pack brisket with two sides.  The brisket was fantastic! We got the hot bq sauce which wasn't too spicy but tasty. I think their corn bread is fantastic.  It's more like a fritter almost, but really good.  The regular slaw was pretty average, but the broccoli slaw is better and we really enjoyed it. They have some outdoor picnic tables, so you might prefer getting this food to go.  The guy that waited on us was very friendly. We will be back for sure!
AF4AyG3BS3f76hTvp22MrA: Since we moved here in June, we eat here or get takeout at least once a week. SO good. The service is always amazing, and everything is fairly priced. Everything we've tried has been amazing. Some of our favorites: Spaghetti carbonara (I have dreams about that stuff), chicken piccata, chicken Parmesan (sub or entree), Italian wedding soup. Everything is always great. The garlic bread with hot marinara is such a great and simple starter. Love this place!
Delicious Italian classics. Fresh garlic bread and marinara brought to the table as a complementary appetizer, fast service, tasty entrees, ridiculously good tiramisu.

I have visited this place twice and so far I love it!
I was looking for a locally owned Italian restaurant after getting tired of chains. 
Their carbonara is delectable! Very rich and tasty!
I don't know if I can find such a good carbonara anywhere else. 
Lasagna was also great. A lot of food for one!
They server warm bread with marinara sauce which makes it hard not to get stuffed before getting my entree. 
In Thursday's they have a half off wine bottle specials and I enjoyed my pino Grigio!
I'll definitely go back!
_1wGbJlj9gRIcbGikrhUmA:
First time eating Vietnamese food. This place is super hard to find off Charlotte Road. Its like you see this massive strip mall sign then a huge hill. You would not be able to find this location off the street unless you knew what you were looking for. 

So I asked the server from the list of house specialties what would he recommend and I prefer chicken. He pointed to one, that I couldn't say, and said "medium heat please" We started off with some egg rolls, and they were pretty good. My friend asked for the hottest beef dish, another point to the menu- sure we'll try it.  (this is a terrible review when I don't know the name of the noodley, soup, i ate- my apologies) 

Well they were both good. Our server was a little bit slow, and we had to flag him over (I hate doing that) to get our check because he was busy playing with his granddaughter? We look forward to going back and trying some different dishes. 

2 drinks, the egg rolls and two entrees were $25.00. Not bad.
My favorite authentic and spicy Vietnamese place in Nashville!! Have been coming here for years! A favorite of my family!

What better way to stave off a drizzly Fall evening that a steaming hot bow of beef pho. I arrived at Miss Saigon and was quickly seated. A nice pot of ginseng tea was ordered, a large array of herbs teas had been recently added to their menu. Other additions include a full craft beer bar area and tiki lounge themed patio area out front. The tea arrived and I was quick to order a first plate of shrimp spring rolls. The vegetables and shrimp tucked into the rice paper roll were fresh however the wrap itself held a chemical aftertaste. This was soon to be forgiven however as my rare beef pho (#27) arrived to the table, accompanied by the standard plate of fresh sprouts, basil and jalepenos. Pho is only as good as the broth and this broth was full flavored rich and satisfying. Come for the great tea menu and stay for the pho- no better way to welcome Fall!
C-4LVKSCE3aw9mztfdjxeQ:
Good country cookin' to -go! The woman working the phone was cordial and efficient. Our dinner was hot and ready when we arrived. There was one small issue with the meatloaf meal which the manager was more than happy to adjust, and the rest of the fare was delicious. Only my Granny's green beans could have tasted any better!
 On nights like this when personally preparing dinner feels out of the question,  I will not hesitate to ring cracker barrel.
Delicious food! Cracker barrel has the best pancakes in Tennessee! Service was great, they were friendly and fast. Good location. Plenty of parking available. I wish they had outdoor seating.

Thank you Cracker Barrel for accommodating our party of 20! We were in town for a family reunion of sorts and CB is just one of those places you have to visit while in the South. The food was out very quickly and the servers tending to us were super nice, despite our many requests. Thanks again CB!
jL251AHFcygyUEfAAF3h2Q:
Great selection of bagels, especially in the South! I opted for the jalapeño cheddar biscuit which was fluffy yet dense; more like an English muffin than a traditional biscuit. Very good!

Not sure why there was only turkey sausage but it also was a nice compliment for the breakfast sandwich. 

There was also a large coffee and coffee drink selection, with many different flavor options as well. 

The service was quick and friendly. A chill neighborhood spot to soak up the effects from the night before.
I like it! Good bagels great coffee and nice hospitality.
If they had Vegemite to spread on bagels, I would give this place five stars. Good food, good service, good coffee, a nice environment, plenty of parking and reasonably priced. Top value!
6KDyCZF7Gc2MgXoqtwQtKw:
3.5 stars: We went in on a weeknight for dinner and tried a multitude of different foods. We had a large pizza, some wings, cheese sticks, jalapeno poppers, and cheese bread.

I will say that every one of the guys in here are some of the most friendly I've seen. They really want you to enjoy your experience and food, and genuinely seem to appreciate your business.

I wanted to like everything more than I actually did though. The pizza toppings, meats, cheese, and sauce were actually very good, the dough was not. Everything was cooked very well, so no complaints there, but the dough tasted as if it were previously frozen. It was a very dense dough with an odd flavor, not light or flavorful at all.

The cheese sticks and jalapeno poppers were just ok. The cheese bread was as you'd expect, standard pizza spot fare. The wings however, while they looked delicious were very odd. The sauce on them (we opted for buffalo) was great, the wing meat however was strange. It was not what any wing place around here typically serves. It was chewy and not overly appetizing from a self-proclaimed wing connoisseur.

We did not try any of their sandwiches or other options which I hear are very good. They did have a full bar of toppings and fresh items behind the counter that looked tasty. Their prices were acceptable and again, the service was spectacular.

Stop in for some of their food if you're in the area and you'll see just how nice these guys are.

Very, very impressed. I tried the Music City chicken sandwich. Chicken was fresh and juicy. Veggies tasted fresh, and even the fries were great! Onion rings are filling, and large. Delicious! My roomie tried the Cheesesteak. Very pleased. Tasty meet, and the cooked onions are a great addition. Will definitely go back. The staff was friendly, and it didn't take too long.
WOW! Finally a delicious food, and good prices too. I definitely recommend.
2Z8YwDy6fUUTFFSSNXbLBg: THE BEST VEGAN FOOD IV EVER HAD. Beautiful presentation, knowledgeable staff, and incredibly creative healthy food options. A must have.

This is one of my favorite Nashville spots. I even got my meat loving husband to give it a go and he loved it. ALL the desserts are so amazing I don't know how they do it. The Cesar and the spring rolls are my absolute favorite

I love the space and the food was great too! I sat at the bar and ordered my zucchini noodle pasta with the house kimchi with my cold brew on the side. The zucchini noodle was decently chewy/crunchy with lots of flavor and the kimchi was equally flavorful.
vHgtIxUNujLbloMyOMLyEA:
Just recently I went for the first time. The weather was perfect so we sat out back... I'd have to say, I felt like I was in an enchanted forest! It's beautiful. 

My friend and I thought we would be adventurous and try new wines so we did the "half glass" (for half the price!). Excellent wine selection and the server gave great recommendations. It was getting close to closing time so we made a mad dash to order one last glass of wine, this time a full glass. The server brought it out and we looked at each other - "This is a half glass" the server "No, it's a full glass, but out bartender is terribly inconsistent." 

Downside: Well... needless to say, I had quiet the (unintentional) hangover the next day :(

Bright side: I got more than my moneys worth! :)
I took my girlfriend to our first date at rumours east. We had the hostess seat us outside on the back patio.  Its absolutely beautiful. Service was A++.
This place was amazing! Looks like a little house and I ordered the best peach cobbler I've ever had in my life. Not too peachy, not too gingery or cinammony, just the perfect balance!!!
JwWM19w-kSc4Tx4a46sNHw: OK, ok, I will admit that I ordered chicken tenders at a BBQ joint. But I'm not big on pork or bones, so I wasn't left with many options. They were huge, crispy, moist, and seasoned perfectly. I used the spicy BBQ sauce which was also super tasty. Other people in my party had ribs, sausage or hot link, macaroni & cheese, cole slaw, and baked beans. They loved it! I noticed they have a patio on the water, but the door was locked (probably since it was cold out). The people that worked there were very friendly and my son and his friend took a pic with one of the Neely's. The walls have tons of writing on them. Make sure you bring a fat, black marker with you so you can sign the walls too. Doesn't show up too well with a pen, lol. I also agree with the person who said use your navigation, because you won't see it easily without it.

my gramps is OBSESSED with the neelys...so when my parents came to visit, we had to go scope this place out so that we could report back to the old man.  that report is as follows:

-sampler platter:  i didn't try the pork ribs, but they looked tasty.  i did have the turkey, which was tasty, the pork, which was the best thing on the plate, moist and flavorful, and the beef, which was DRY.  i made my dad eat the rest of it.

-beef ribs.  super meaty, drenched in sauce, delicious.  there were a few tough spots, but they were outweighed by the tasty goodness.  i got so into eating them that the staff tried to take a picture of my sauce covered face to put up on the wall.

-sides:  the potato salad was DELICIOUS...i LOVE yellow potato salad.  the mac and cheese was super ooey gooey and yummy yummy in my tummy.  the fries were good and crisp.  the bread was a nice addition to soak up the sauce in my stomach.  the one thing that was blah, and i mean BLAH, was the slaw.  lettuce slaw?  really?  it was wet and non-crunchy and...yeah.  gross.  just gross.

watch out for the sweet tea.  i could feel my teeth rotting out of my skull, the sugar content was so ridiculously high.  you have been warned.

It's hidden in a plaza near a hotel I usually stay at when I'm in Nashville .If it wasn't for yelp I wouldn't have known it was there.I could've walked there from my hotel.

The Ribs were the best ribs I've had. I got the dry rub and added some sauce as well.

This is now my first stop when I arrive in Nashville 

Service is great great BBQ joint
EnGnfn_6KMTY2enUD3hqdw: AMAZING! MY first impression was the decor and the staff. The decor is a hot metropolitan look with an Asian flair. I really liked the outdoor lounge seating and patio. The menu is great mix of Asian fusion. I have been several times and love everything I have tried and highly recommend the Asian Wonton Nachos and Chicken Penang. They have a full bar including wines and sake - with some great creative cocktails. Suzy Wong's is definitely somewhere to try over and over!

I've been here plenty of times and I must say this place is always satisfying. Their drinks are amazing. The hibiscus margarita is a must have. Don't get the steak appetizer, it's a rip off. You get a tiny amount of meat for like $9. Tasty but over priced. Other than that everything is always good. They're service is great. Unless you're uncomfortable in an LGBT friendly environment I wouldn't recommend coming here. So if you are not closed minded or a homophobic,come here to get some of Nashville's best Asian fusion.

We had an absolute blast at Suzy Wongs! The waiters are AMAZING and they were super attentive ( Thanks Ryan!!) The brunch food was filling and delicious. The drag show was equally fun! My only critique is the venue isn't really set up for a full on show. It's two separate rooms so some people aren't getting the show when others are. But the Queen made sure to share her time equally. Also, the shows are spaced out 30 minutes between each other. So she lip syncs a few times then you wait, so I would have liked a longer full on show. But overall we had a great experience and I'd come back!
Eflrt6oscSeO2H7Ge15clA:
I would disagree with users on this sire regarding Cha Chah. I found the wait staff to be highly engaging, quick to serve, but maintaining the right distance as not to be annoying. The food was quick to arrive and we did not wait to be seated at 6:40 PM. Also, Cha Chah takes reservations so just call a couple of days in advance if you really want to sit right done. 

Onto the food, I supplied many of the pictures. Lets start with the dips - we were a party of two out to try anything. For dips we got the red pepper tapenade, the black bean & mango, and the salmon with capers and dill dip. The dips came with a bread basket included. All of the dips were enjoyable but I fancied the salmon dip the most - it had the consistency and look of a cream cheese, while my friend feasted on the black bean with mango the most. 

Drinks - I forgot. You must try the Black Tea Mint Julep. 

After dips, we got tapas. We greatly enjoyed the sea salt steamed artichoke - don't forget to eat the leaves when you finish the heartsand make sure you use the lemon butter. We also got the cilantro octopus. My friend was not a fan because, "all I can think about is tentacles, tentacles, tentacles, and it creeps me out."  I like fishy tasting sea critters so I ate all the octopus.

Next, the main course: lamb loin with some cucumber and cilantro yumminess and my friend enjoyed a scallops, shrimp, and clam dish of some sort. The lamb was cooked and seasoned perfectly and the cucumbers with tabouleh and cilantro gave great additional flavor. 

Dessert:  poached pear cobbler. It tasted like apple cobbler and I personally thought it had a little much brown sugar, but what a guy who doesn't normally eat sweets is sound critiqueing dessert is beyond even that guy - me. 

I highly recommend cha chah.

We went to Cha-Chah for a double Valentine's Day date, and it was just what we were looking for. Overall, we really enjoyed the tapas; although, some were more of a value than others. 

The charcuterie and cheese plates were just okay and not really worth the $4 per cheese/meat. We fell in love with the queso dip. The sangria was tasty, but it wasn't Spanish sangria. It was more of a Latin fusion-type drink - still good, but not what I was expecting. All in all, we'd recommend to friends, particularly during happy hour.

Disclaimer: only been here for brunch.  And its most likely my favorite brunch place in all of Nashville.  Allow me to explain:

I love Cha Chah's mostly-glass building, its always so warm and bright inside.  The servers were friendly, which is a huge plus if you have me out of bed before noon on a Sunday.  I was meeting a friend I hadn't gotten to catch up with in a long time, and this place was perfect for that.  Bring a significant other, a few friends, maybe even visiting family, and have a lovely brunch here.

The brunch menu changes with ingredients that are in season, which I love.  What I've had when I went there isn't even on the menu anymore, and that's fine with me.  I love changing menus and the fun excitement it brings to try something new.  The first time I went I have a flat bread that had sun dried tomatos, a poached egg, goat cheese, and a few other things on it...I have the picture on my phone of it to show people when I say why they should check this place out.  I had a friend that had a grilled salmon salad while we where there, and I kept sneaking bits of it.  The bloody mary was spicy, the coffee perfect and rich, the decor simple.  If you are looking for a delicious, quiet, quaint-yet-modern style brunch, this needs to be a place to try.

I just looked at the latest menu online...there's a good chance I'm headed there this week.  I haven't tried dinner there, but as Nashville lacks really good brunch places, I can tell you this is a rare and lovely find that you need to make part of your fall explorations.
NCJkOqbMpeONS2LXwlUy-Q: Such good ramen!! The chicken is Smokey and the pork is delicious!! Definitely try some pot stickers on the side. Best we've ever had!!

This isn't Chef's Table, it's out of a truck; fun, inventive, unpretentious, and a little ridiculous. I had the "snoop"; lightly spiced pork broth with strong smoke flavored pork, bouncy ramen noodles and a perfectly oozing, lightly brined/marinated soft boiled egg. 

Two food trucks going head to head at Nashville's Yoga Festival. Ramen versus salad bowls and wraps. A bunch of yogis waited in the cold for their quinoa salad bowls, but for me, ramen wins every time and Smokin' Bowls hits the mark.

Don't let the exterior of the truck sway you from trying this delicious smorgasbord of Asian fusion yumminess.  Tried the "Snoop" and it was the perfect amount of spice (got a 3). Pork was delicious and the noodles were perfect. I love Two Ten Jack for Ramen, but you cannot get it "to go", so this is mos def the next best thing! Also sampled the Potstickers (must get!) and the French Toast (very good!!). Highly recommend - will satisfy your Ramen/Asian cravings.
nTSXLSVKq1PhrcGFzjgiaw: Amazing german food and fantastic selection.  No better German food in Nashville, hands down
Great restaurant, awesome food. I don't know what people ordered to say there is bad food here but they are crazy/stupid.

Better than its reputation.  The old historic gerst haus literally had grease dripping from the walls.  The new one has a pretty good atmosphere and a huge, highly affordable menu with both german and american faves.  

It is also a great place to stop in get some fries or kraut and enjoy one of the excellent german beers that they have on tap.  I recently had a black lager that was probably one of the top ten beers I have ever had in my life.
uXS5oyhAM_MfxdBUpHVZkA:
Adorable little shop in Germantown: the owners are very friendly and knowledgable.  Selection of pasta is great (the have tons of them, both homemade and imported) and so is their sampling of cheeses, cured meats, olive oils and vinegars, as well as other Italian ingredients. 

But the best part for me so far? Oh my heavens, they make some amazing mozzarella!! Fresh and subtly salty, it is glorious! Go in the morning on Saturdays to make sure you get some: they only make it on the weekends.

I went here for the first time last week to try something! I felt like I was in Little Italy the smell was out of this world! This is definitely an amazing little place to have here in Nashville!  I purchased sausage and asiago cheese ravioli and it was great! I can't wait to go back and try more! Everything looked so good even his take and bake dishes! This definitely beats the chain Italian restaurants any day! The owner even helped me with questions about his pastas very helpful! I can't wait to go back!

Awesome place with reasonable prices, comparable to most any large grocery. The staff are extremely helpful and knowledgeable about every product. The owner even gave me a free loaf of bread (which was delicious)! The product is superb with imported goods as well as high quality, Tennessee products, like Benton's bacon and Sweetwater Valley Farms Cheese... not to mention the yummy pastas and sauces made on site. Definitely making this a regular stop.
_g9FzFkfI37dXKaQMiafgQ: Stayed at the springhill suites and walked over to eat dinner. Portions are big enough for kids to share and prices are fair. The only downside was that it seemed they mixed fresh and older stale chips in the nachos.

This place was under duress when airport closed. Way under staffed because all flight crews and passengers but staff was great. Chef came out to talk to guests comped us two pieces of the best lemon berry cake.

I am not normally a hotel foodie, but I was staying across the road without transportation so I decided to give this restaurant a try. I am so glad that I did. The staff was very friendly and the food selection was great. I ordered the Basil Chicken with sautéed vegetables. I would order that again as it was juicy and flavorful. Worth a visit if you are in the area.
AFk6UXFbH-idH31KhFQVCg: Love to hit this cafe when I find myself in the 8th South or Berry Hill area. They may serve the best coffee on the west side of the Cumberland. The staff is friendly, the customers are laid-back, and the exposed brick gives the space an artsy post-industrial look. If you are looking for something more adventurous than the corporate coffee experience, try the amazing Vietnamese iced coffee.
Changed name to 8th & Roast.  Had the pour down setup from the '30s. Very excellent!

A fun and hip coffee hangout.  They have free wifi, it can be a challenge to find a plug in spot for one's laptop, as many other lap toppers have often beaten you to the punch.  I am no serious coffee snob, but I can tell some good beans from folgers or the like, and these folks pour a darn fine cup o' joe.  My significant other is a former Starbucks manager/trainer, and she also gives their coffee her seal of approval.   When I am in the area, this is my default java joint.  They serve scones and like, which they will heat up for you. Go to Roast Inc, you'll be glad you did.
rDuHKSzTFJT_iyetAsTboQ: Love the vibe of this place. Wandered in here on a random Saturday night looking to see what East Nashville was all about and was not disappointed. Loved the live music, not too loud but jazzy enough to create a chill atmosphere. Wait staff was friendly and eager to help. The drinks were plentiful as well, ranging from draft cocktails to wine to beer, with a heavy focus on local/craft beers which I particularly enjoyed. Most importantly, the food options were delicious. I got the Grilled Cheese with Bacon and my friend got Buckwheat Pancakes (any place that offers breakfast for dinner is worth checking out). We got the Coconut Cake at the end and it was wicked good. Will definitely be back soon.
Great beer.  Wine on tap.  Live music every day.  Great staff.

We stopped in on a Saturday morning for some breakfast/lunch (not brunch). We found a spot to park across the street. Parking can be a little funny since they've got a small lot, but that's how much of East Nashville is. The building is new. The space is clean and open. They've got a nice sized stage towards the back of the restaurant and a large bar along the side. 

Our server was very friendly and attentive. She answered all our questions and helped me decide between the burger and the catfish sandwich. I picked the catfish sandwich and the potatoes. We also ordered the masa hush puppies, you get 5 of them for 7 bucks. I didn't have any of the coffee as I'm not a coffee drinker. 

Our food came out shortly after we finished the masa hush puppies. For the price, I would probably skip the hush puppies, they were good, but not great.
