The catfish sandwich came with two bottom buns, odd I guess. The sandwich came with either potatoes or a salad. Being that I love carbs, I picked the potatoes, which are roasted potatoes. I enjoyed the potatoes, I'm glad they've got the skin on them. The catfish sandwich was interesting. It comes with cole slaw and hot sauce on it. The hot sauce wasn't too hot if you're not a fan of hot sauce. I liked the catfish sandwich, but if I go back again I think I will try the burger.

UPDATE:

I went back, had the burger & it was AMAZING. I have to say that it's probably one of the top 5 burgers in Nashville. The burger comes with two patties of beef, but it doesn't feel so heavy. A lot of places will serve you a burger that's got such dense meat. It comes with jalapeño mayo, which isn't spicy, but adds a great flavor to the burger. The bun is pillowy soft & has a nice crust to it. I ordered it with a salad because I'd had a nice breakfast a couple hours before. Next time I'll get the burger & potatoes & call it a day.
a5dAEJescHGr_srX8Ckg-Q: I'm literally here everyday. The Zulema breakfast sandwich is great and have one the best salads. Huge portions for the salads and they take the time to fill their wraps. If you're in the area, take up on their taco Tuesday and I guarantee you will need a nap from all the food you will consume. Support this business.
Food was amazing, people were helpful and friendly. Parking situation is a bit confusing. But overall a great place to try if you can find it lol

Super friendly family ran business, very welcoming and pleasing environment, loved it, left the airport to my first meeting to find this place, definitely worth the stop. You can tell they love what they do, and they are very good at it. Will be coming back when I stop into NASHVILLE Again! They habanero hot sauce is delicious!
aBJWHzk6jfAj4h92clH21g: I do not know the names of the two men who were the sole helpers behind the counters this morning , Sunday the 21st, ( with the exception of the bagel cooker in the back) but they did an EXCEPTIONAL job! I assume a few others did not come to work today, but they handled the long line better than when there are 5 workers behind the counter. They need 5 gold stars each! I have seen this Breugger's flip and flop but when the bearded manager appeared ( I assume he is the manager, he is always there) every bagel sandwich has been GOLD! He always remembers my order and needs to be commended. Both kept calm and cool as line formed. Good job guys!
We usually eat here when we are in Nashville.  It's the go to stop for breakfast.  The bagels are good and the service is friendly.  We like their coffee too.

Went there today and got the pesto chicken sandwich and the creamy tomato basil soup. Both were DIVINE! The staff were also delightful and helpful. I had to wait 10 minutes for my ciabatta bread to unthaw and I didn't even mind because they were so great about it!
wYCSGIHJPhLxahYPM3xvGQ: Good choices, service is usually really good.  I hate that they took the fresh veggies off the hummus appetizer.  Mixed drinks are hit-or-miss but beer and wine are always good.  Overall, a good choice for south Brentwood.

This is really a hidden jewel in south Nashville.  From the outside it is very unassuming, but the food is extremely good.  The staff always aims to please!!!  You won't be sorry for choosing to go here!
My husband and I love the patio, it's close to the house and the service is always good. Great Happy Hour and LOVE the pizza.
o6C0AFmyRNaBl2nFTfvQvA: Noted that I received good service from all the servers here at lunch time.  Pizza was hot and fresh.  I will be coming back again

Great service, great location, friendly and most important the food is always perfect. The dining is always clean and no to many people dining in even this location has a all u can eat salad  pretty happy canoe her

Good meal, great service.  The 5-star rating is because of Angie, who was extremely helpful and friendly while I ordered a lot of food over the phone with no menu.  I had quite a few questions about options and prices, and she was more than happy to help in any way possible.  I haven't left a yelp review before, but I wanted to recognize her for her awesome service and beyond-professional demeanor.  Will be ordering from here again because I know it will be a hassle-free, pleasant experience.  Thanks, Angie!
uA65fNR33daws3d2SoEvIA: Stopped in before seeing a play at TPAC! Step in off the street and its deceiving! Beautiful ornate ceilings, marble flooring- absolutely stunning inside! Had a cocktail at the bar which was good! The place was busting at the seams was packed!! I think a lot of folks had the same idea about stopping in for a drink! Tip- get there early on Saturday nights!! A hidden jewel, though! Worth it to go in and sit a spell

Amazing gorgeous hotel. Everyone seems to know your name right away and they address you by name....it's pretty cool.  It's like a stay in a fantasy world where everything is perfect. 
I left my hotel keys at a nice restaurant tonite, they called the hotel told them and when I showed up .....not able to find my room key, they had already made me a fresh one,
Definitely a place to stay!!!!

This hotel is superior. Very clean and large room and bathroom with tons of amenities, like TV in bathroom, evening delivery of cookies and water to room, luxury towels and bedding, excellent in room coffee, friendly staff, etc. I highly recommend you stay here if visiting Nashville!
bjVkLzC8-xREEIjSouI2cQ:
Come on Sunday for the blues jam! 

Food and drink are all excellent.The staff is friendly and knows their customers. And seriously, the guys that host the blues jam are some of the nicest and most welcoming folks around.

Whether you're a musician looking to build some skills, or just a music lover who wants to hear some amazing talent, you should make Twin Kegs II a regular haunt.

A dive bar made for Movies. Great karaoke on Fridays. Such a fun time we closed the place down. No kitchen just grease trap snacks but that's awesome too. 

It is a rough area of town and it did get rough inside once with a slight issue between two guys. I say just keep singing and let the fools fight.

Great burgers, cheap beer.  Awesome little outpost in an area without much else.  My only complaints are early closing and a lack of bourbon selection.  Dog friendly, spacious patio, friendly crowds.  10/10 dive bar
XrKGHH3ftQug9JlsPjssTQ: Best fish sandwich in Nashville.  Perfection.. Get the spaghetti and green beans. Great service and very friendly people.  This my spot for my got fish cravings.

Excellent chicken. I got the medium and it was more then hot. It was very hot. I can't imagine the two levels above that. They fucked up a friends order and didn't seem to care much. You can go next door and get beer and bring in. The chicken was cooked perfect. I also got the white beans and collards and they were excellent. Great food!

My favorite meal while in Nashville was right here at Bolton's. You must try hot chicken - you just have to. Juicy, tender and delicious chicken meat fried to crispy perfection and coated with spicy habanero powder. The wonderful man behind the counter warned us about the spice options and he was right - even for serious spice lovers the MILD option was the way to go. The medium was too much heat, to be honest, and I adore hot food. 

Served with two sides, pickles, and two slices of white bread, this was a memorable meal. The baked beans were unbelievable and the turnip greens were excellent as well. We enjoyed everything about this restaurant - the servers and owner were friendly and humorous and they cared a lot about their customers.
ii8dw3vTRecmP_iOiaP0Hg: What else is there to say that hasn't already been said about this great restaurant? I love Maggiano's and every single time I have gone the service has been impeccable, the food has been delicious and the portions have been very satisfying. Great place, inexpensive and delicious food!

My husband took me here on our anniversary. I do not like Italian food, but this place is AWESOME to say the least!!! The waiters are super friendly and professional and the food is great!!! The atmosphere is relaxing and very romantic! I highly recommend this restaurant to anyone!!

We loved our server, Addison. He was awesome and demonstrated true customer service from the heart. 5 star service at its best. He was able to articulate the menu with recommendations. We asked to Speak with the manager, Michael and he was very receptive on our compliments about Addison. We will definitely return and request Addison.
9I7VNu6_VclomWuINtIFaw:
Delicious food, great prices!

I ordered a steak taco and a pork, cheese, and bean pupusa and boy was it good.  I tried to finish the papusa, but it was just too much food to finish after I had already eaten the taco and a bunch of chips with salsa.  

I'll definitely be coming back again to try something else I haven't had before.

This is a cool little hole-in-the-wall restaurant that doubles as a market. A little hesitant at first sight, we tried this place out. The food was surprisingly great! The burritos are fabulous. They only downside is that it is super warm inside on hot summer days.
Amazing Mexican food! Best in Nashville and very authentic! The pazole was perfect and the pupusas were delicious!
8CSdj8LF593qwMcduzHglQ: I was in Nashville and wanted to try something different than prior visits. A friend recommended Sunset Grill, I read some Yelp reviews and chose the Coca-Cola short ribs. Let me just say that I wasn't disappointed and will be back in the future. We also ordered chicken quesadillas and my friend raved about the salmon.

I LOVE this place! Sunset Grill is my favorite restaurant in the Hillsboro Village area. The food is outstanding - my favorite dish there is the house made gnocchi. YUM! They have an extensive wine list, very knowledgeable, professional and efficient wait staff and I also really like the decor. My boyfriend and I often go to Sunset Grill before heading over to the Belcourt to catch a movie. This is also a great place to bring a someone for an anniversary, a special occasion, or out of town guests or inlaws.

Love the late night menu.
BEST creme brulee. YUM.


This place was slightly expensive to come for dinner. That said, the smoked duck dish was really good. The ambiance is nice. Spacious enough for large groups. Highly recommend it!
D1fjLsZb7eDnE81XtYXMhg: This is how you do salmon lox. Thank you, thank you! Bagel was fresh, not toasted and delicious. The salmon was not too salty and had great flavor with the capers and everything bagel. The dill cream cheese was an awesome touch. Wish we had a place like this up in Columbus. Well done!

The best bagels ever. Seriously. I grew up in New York, vowing that is never eat a bagel made anywhere outside of a 90 minute radius of NYC. How naive I was. 

Perfect crumb, perfect crust, perfect toppings, perfect cream cheese, amazing crew. 

Get yourself to Bagel Face now. Seriously. Best. Bagels. Ever. Not just best in Nashville, the best ever.
The spinach, feta, and egg bagel on cayenne cheddar was to die for!!! I need to come back to Nashville again just to have another one.
OOnuozzEfen6PQn7xK3ujA:
Great Cafe Cubano and friendly conversation about Cuban politics and the island's future.

Felt like I was back in Miami.

A fabulous joint! The atmosphere isn't as bad as some of the reviews implied. It is actually rather comfortable and eclectic. Got the usual lecture about ordering in spanish (no problemo) and no cell phone use once your food is served. The owner is a cantankerous sort of curmudgeon, but that just adds to the atmosphere. We figured we were only going to have one shot at this as we were just travelling through so ordered a Cubano, a tacos de lechon (pork tacos), and a couple of empanadas de pollo (chicken empanadas). Everything was great with really good seasoning and great flavors. The empanadas were really tasty with a fine filling and delicious pastry, but perhaps a little dry. 

For a small place the food was really outstanding with authentic flavors. If our travels ever bring us to Nashville again we will be Back to Cuba!

It has been several years since we were last here, but I am happy to report that the food remains great. On a Thursday night they were reasonably busy, but there were enough seats so that as diners came in they were able to find seats.

Once again we enjoyed the empanadas (both beef and chicken). They are crispy and well stuffed with nicely seasoned meat. They are served with a really nice, slightly spicy chimichuri sauce. I preferred the beef, but I think that is mostly because the higher fat content in the beef gave it a more unctuous mouth feel. Both are delicious, however.

We also shared a cubano sandwich. These are really great. A perfect blending of meat flavors and textures. Yum! The cubano is a LARGE sandwich. Even though we were both hungry we still brought home almost half of the cubano. Blame it on the delicious empanadas!

We also had a side of tostones, fried plantains. This was the only part of our meal that we didn't find outstanding. They seemed a bit dry. When we had these in Ecuador they were usually served with a slightly spicy crema sauce. However I don't think this is the Cuban practice.

Fun funky atmosphere with art and photos from pre-Castro Cuba. Usually there are Cuban tunes on the sound system.
vLFHb1YeRuWMJOSdZklJUg: Fantastic ambience, customer service and food. I have been wanting to try this place for a while. It's bright, open and the food is light, fresh and filling. I will be back and I definitely recommend!
Always awesome.. Tony and crew work magic at both locations.. keep it up!!! Nashville needs you!

Another great visit...the taco special of the day was Peruvian Steak...wow!  And if you've never had turnip greens or think that you wouldn't like them, do yourself a favor and give these a try.

Bottom line...love this place.
XI99CG-lx3OH7oVeRTejvg: Amazing food with complex flavors. Great service. Very reasonable price for the quality of food. The pistachio ravioli was incredible. I should have taken a photo but I was in too much of a hurry to get started on it. A+

First visit review - we reserved our spot using the Nowait app, and initially expected to wait about an hour. Thankfully, we were seated in 15 min. First gold star of the evening! Our server, Kiley, stopped by our table, filled our mason jars, and offered us bread. She answered my initial questions about the pizza options, and left us to continue deliberating. We finally opted to get the Bucatini Rigate - what an excellent choice that was! It came with a spicy lamb ragu, and was so rich and savory, I have enough leftovers for a nice Sunday brunch. Make sure to add the crispy risotto balls, and the roasted brussels sprouts, to your meal. Come to this restaurant hungry - you will probably still leave with leftovers. 

Last, but never least (for me anyway) - dessert. They have 3 options - the only one you need to know about is the Nutella Crunch Cake. Kiley made a new best friend in me, after she brought us out surprise samples of some of their gelato options. I had not been expecting that, and was really impressed by her attention to detail and overall friendliness through the evening. 2nd and 3rd gold stars of the evening!


In summary, Pastaria = delicious, fresh, wonderful dining experiences.
Amazing. Wine carafe, Risotto balls, bolognese, Nutella cake. O.m.g. Yas yas yassssssss. Thank you to the wizards in the kitchen! Service was spot on too!!!
tLVnL8CqQYiMU6E_aayYog: Super tasty vegetarian cuisine, all fresh, mostly raw, made with love. Was a nice reprieve for a northern visitor over loaded on southern fried comfort food. Unlike those place, my girl and I left here feeling amazing and energized!

I LOVE GRAZE! I've ordered take out from here many times (Roasted veggie burrito plz) which is always great. I finally had the opportunity to come in and have a meal at the bar on a Sunday evening and I loved everything about my experience. Danielle was my bartender/server and she provided the great conversation and delicious drinks! I ordered the brussells/mushroom sandwich and it was fantastic! Great flavor and great portion size. I hope to make this my Sunday ritual. Thanks Danielle & all the other friendly folks Graze!
It's all the phenomenal! Fantastic, mouth watering, good for your soul and mama earth! Citrus Cesar, Mac and chorizo, hot tempeh and loaded potatoes. Yes!! Dimitri provided excellent care. Xo
WRhmIqpoCbztI7IZwNsPyA: We were in a hurry and glad to get Alex as our server. He was prompt and friendly without being annoying. The bacon cheeseburger was very tasteless, with hot, yummy fries. Chicken quesadilla was good, with a nice smokey taste.
Great experience, very clean, very nice and accommodating staff...  the food was great and came very quickly.

I ordered seafood, my man ordered steak. Both were very good but the salad bar was pretty picked over. Waitress very nice and facility was clean but a little dated. Overall, a good place to eat. Mashed potatoes were great!
Sd8NrlgB6SY6GEtIjPupfA: Love the "dive" appearance.  Great East Nashville location.  Plenty of parking. Outside eating area.  My daughter ordered the Gyro fries and seemed to be in a trance, saying they were "so good" over and over.  I ordered a Gyro. It was flavorful and plentiful.  Great service, owner on site.

Always great. Service, food, and quality. They are always about customer service. Glad they have Yelp sticker in there window so I can give them a review. Tonight spicy chicken sandwich and grape leaves. Try this place.

I love these guys! Best gyros in east Nashville, super fresh and great flavor. The portions are big and they always remember me and thank me for coming back. If you have a large order, it is smart to call ahead.
OfjIf_YzD8dC7YlsV8a71Q: Very tasty burger and the fries were exceptionally good!  I ordered the Cowboy Uno Combo.  Friendly service and extremely clean inside.  If you're really hungry I suggest getting 2 burgers, but 1 is perfect for a light appetite.

I've been eyeing the BurgerIM near me in Mt Juliet and was a little skeptical of a place serving burgers similar to White Castle, BUT since this was is near where I work I decided to try it. First impression blew away my expectations!

Ordering 4/5: The menu is pretty simple to follow and easy to understand and order
The Atmosphere 4/5: More urban, metal decor with some pastel colored lights on the side of the bar, but it seems very family friendly. Also good to go grab a beer after work and watch a sports game.
The FOOD 4/5: 
I had a Wagyu Western Burger with the onion ring and BBQ sauce which was very tasty, and then my 2nd burger was the Spanish beef with the Classic toppings and it was possibly better than any burger I've had at Red Robbin, or Burger Republic. My only downside was my side of Sweet Potato Fries as they were limp and soggy feeling with some salt on them.

Overall, I'd definitely go back to BurgerIM again, but I might stick with regular fries or onion rings.

They are the best, and their sliders are perfect and it's better than cheese cake factory sliders. Also, the workers are very nice and kind. However, I wish if they can open another branch near to downtown because this branch is far a way from the downtown it's near to the opery mills mall.
B2sGXSn26OpQmRfJx4QD8g: just another meat and 3 On Murfreesboro road?  I think not!    Family run business that not only serves lunch with a quickness but breakfast too!   I definitely intend on stopping by for breakfast but don't try on the weekend as they are not open.  I had the turkey and dressing with pinto beans and greens.The dressing and turkey were on target but i think the beans needed maybe a piece of hamhock or jowl for flavoring.  The greens were excellent but I always add a little pepper sauce and butter to mine.  The meat and 3 menu rotates daily but there is a also a great selection of sandwiches and burgers.  Fresh homemade desserts are served so if you get there late and something was popular, guess what? you missed out!  Walk in, seat yourself and a server will be with you shortly/  If you are solo, don't hesitate to sit at the counter but a word of wisdom, if you sit by the cash register you will be inundated with with the constant traffic of people paying for their meal and ordering to go.  The place is smallish and definitely gets crowded for lunch.  As I always say, it's a MEAT and 3, I didn't check for veg options!
Happy to have found this little spot not far outside of town. Best place to go after a night out. OG diner style food. A+
One of the fastest and freshest Burgers I've had in a long time. The ladies who own it also work there everyday. Great food and service but beware thats it's small and very busy during primetime.
CybLEjtw_fhkiITdCN5GZg: I revisited Bread and Co. to give my beloved chicken salad another try. This time, I opted for the salad version and did not get the low fat one. I had the curried version and it was spectacular! I loved it and everything else in the salad was great: crisp lettuce, colorful peppers. The honey mustard was excellent. I only docked one star because it was so expensive. About $12-13 bucks. However, it was worth it. It's one of the better chicken salads I have had in a long time.
I went here for breakfast once and actually liked their avocado toast. It looked like they have a decent breakfast/brunch menu. Service was a little slow.

I have tried a lot of their takeout food because my work would order lunch from here all the time~ I would recommend clarifying the type of bread you want because even if it says multigrain, they might give you sourdough or white (happened 3 times to me).

Breakfast:
- Omelets comes with 2 slices of hearty multigrain bread slathered with butter and mixed greens on the side. The bread is really grainy and chewy with a toasted outer texture. The ham and gruyere omelet is simple and savory, but filling. The morning market veg omelet has very flavorful roasted veggies inside with goat cheese sprinkled on top. The healthy turkey was not too good- fluid leaked out of my omelet and the turkey was dry. 
- the avocado toast is overpriced for the amount of avocado spread they give you. The avocado spread is also too salty.
- The power wrap is a egg white omelet wrapped with a sundried tomato tortilla and a side of cut fruit (melons, grapes, and strawberries). The turkey, mushrooms, and spinach in the omelet taste great. 
- The last breakfast food I have tried is the waffle. Waffles at breakfast places like IHOP taste better. The waffles here are thick, dense, and felt a bit greasy. 

Sandwiches (come with a pickle and chips):
- Iroquois was my favorite. Almond tarragon chicken salad with lettuce and tomato on cranberry pecan bread. They give A LOT of chicken salad, as shown below. The sweet cranberries and crunchy pecans in the bread complimented the other ingredients well.
- Turkey, bacon, and vermont white cheddar had pulled turkey meat (not the packages kind in the grocery store but actual muscle fibers). This was A LOT of meat as well! 

Salads (says they come with a cranberry pecan crisp online but never got one; only occasionally came with a breadstick):
- Chicken avocado chop was my favorite. The tortilla chips were not always crunchy, but the seasoning on the chicken breast and smooth avocado made up for it. My body felt great after eating this healthy mix of black beans, corn, tomatoes, romaine, and arugula. The salad dressing is citrusy but creamy with a hint of sweetness.
- Strawberry fields was also a yummy salad. The sesame vinaigrette was light and balanced out the heavy blue cheese and pecans. They give around 3/4 cup of strawberries and around 1/2 cup of chicken salad. The amount of spring mix present in the take out container is almost the size of a bag of spring mix you can buy from the grocery!
- Chicken pesto quinoa bowl would feel healthy if it wasn't saturated in salt.
ZYS967VjyavLFLG9Qrc9gQ: The atmosphere here is very laid back...the menu is simple and straightforward...after ordering you can get some condiments on your right along with your choice of drink...I ordered the combo shrimp po'boy and crawfish etouffee...my food came out quick and the staff was friendly....the shrimp was very flavorful and the etouffee was very rich and creamy...the portion size was perfect...I  enjoyed...please bus your own table offer eating!!

Ate here for the first time today. I ordered the schrimp po-boy and crawfish etouffee and my husband had a po-boy and chicken and sausage gumbo. The po-boys and gumbo were great but the etouffee was Delicious!!

I just had Voodoo for the first time (ordered dinner through postmates) and I have 100% found my new favorite restaurant in Bellevue, and I can't believe I've been missing out on this place for the 2 years that I've lived here. I had the half & half and went with the roast beef & gravy poboy & shrimp gumbo. Both were FANTASTIC! So flavorful and huge portions. I'm originally from East Texas & have visited New Orleans several times. I have had plenty of good, authentic Cajun food and this place is just as good as the Cajun food in TX & LA. I will definitely be back!
GiSQ8fXpAs3M9Y27BgEd4Q: The nicest McDonald's I've been at. The staff here is extremely nice and I love the radio station decor. They don't have too many of these unique style McDonald's anymore after tearing down the Solid Gold McDonalds in Wisconsin so it's refreshing to see some continuing the tradition of a bygone era. The food is the same as it is across the country but the staff and atmosphere are the best reasons to come there.

This McDonalds was pretty convenient, fast, and well maintained. The décor was fancy for a McDonalds, and the service was great. Loved the little Nashville display stations they had up in the restaurant. Made the venue that much more for the morning after hangover food. Two thumbs up, as far as McDonalds go!

Fast service at the drive thru, 
good breakfast, three meals under $10 (without drinks).
gR6ICS7qYX0DX675kJBsBQ: This place always rocks.   Consistent and worth it every time!  Service is always friendly.
Delicious food, great service and good value. Enjoyed  the slow and low plate for a true taste of Nashville.
Pretty decent bbq, but the sauces were... unique. Not a bad place all in all. The staff were great, very friendly folks.
zqEmarWiEUQ5jl9R4t8CnQ: Them popsicles are poppin. Your friends are fake asf if they haven't dragged you here yet.
We've been twice so far and we love the atmosphere and good food/drinks. We plan on making this a go to spot!

This is a great addition to South Nashville. Don't let the exterior fool you, the interior of this plaza will surprise you and make you feel like you are in Mexico! Everything is bright and clean, with a few delicious dinner options. My favorites are El Ceviche Lock and Tres Gauchos. Order the shrimp ceviche from El Ceviche and the grilled shrimp and plantains from Tres Gauchos. Polish it off with a mangonado from Paletas Tocumbo!
tygJjsQDeVrm2Y4sHB79sQ: Great place to go for a big 32oz dos Equis draft beer before a show at cannery, mercy lounge, or the high watt. Or after getting a tattoo at black 13 inside Cummings station here. The fish tacos are actually pretty good. 3 tacos and a big beer usually fills me up. Nice staff.

It's located at the end of a red brick building that is full of cool looking restaurants (and some offices). It's your typical Mexican food-nothing really creative or foodie about it. However the lunch special can't be beat. The portions are big and the prices on the lunch menu - even for the combos - are in the $5-$7 range. The atmosphere is relaxed and accompanied by your typical Mexican instrumental playlist. I'd definitely frequent this place for lunch if I lived here.

Great food, great people, great value. We find ourselves coming back again and again.  We were actually present during one of the previous negative reviews, and I guess we (and the buildings security cameras - bonus: the place is safe!) saw different things. Nice to have a place like this in Cummins!
wuLxBeWahgkQPv1E8GLYwg:
I think this could be called "upscale-casual" ? My dinnermate and I had a really difficult time choosing which flat breads to order because they all sounded really good. ALL OF THEM. We ended up getting the chicken feta (with jalepeno sauce involved somewhere) and the Kahuna (chicken, pineapple and canadian bacon!) with added marinara sauce. We got a few draft beers and had a great attentive waiter....so I'm definitely coming back to try some more FBs (salads and stromboli also sounded delicious). After tax, tip, and 2 beers a piece our bill was $48. Not terrible! 

I think if you like pizza-related foods and/or beers and/or watching football on TV and/or not watching football on TV but smelling pizzalike foods cooking in a hot oven, you may enjoy Music City Flats. 

Also points go to them for having tons of booths.

My wife and I love this place.  We come here every time we are in town.  I try a different flat every time and have never been disappointed.  My wife loves the dessert flats.  Staff is friendly & they always have great wine specials!

Yummo! I love the various different flatbreads that they have.

I have so many favorites - I can't just choose one but here are my top three:

- Kahuna: BBQ sauce, roasted chicken, shredded mozzarella, Canadian bacon, freshly diced pineapple chunks and a ranch drizzle.

- Buffalo Chicken: Roasted chicken with crumbled blue cheese and mozzarella cheeses, caramelized sweet onions and diced crunchy celery drizzled with a mild wing sauce

- Turkey, Apple, & Brie: Sliced turkey, crunchy sliced apples, creamy brie and shredded mozzarella cheeses drizzled with natural honey blended with cinnamon & sugar

Of course, there are other great foods there too, for instance, the baked macaroni and cheese with lobster. (Don't knock it until you try it!)

Lastly, Monday nights: 1/2 off bottles of wine! Who doesn't want to participate in that?! The best part - it's from 5PM - Close! Talk about an extended happy hour!
zzfj1-iPfw0cwnOjY0yUgA: I recently stopped here for lunch.I had debs chicken and 2 sides.The staff were friendly and service was super fast.That's a good thing when you only have a limited time for lunch.The chicken was very good.It was moist and the sauce is really good.The portion sizes were quite filling.I had to get a takeout bag.I will definitely be returning.

Ate here last night with a friend. Found the place fairly empty which isn't surprising based on the location I guess. The place is kind of hidden on Stewart's Ferry.

We found the food very good. I had the country fried steak sandwich and my friend had the club sandwich. Both sandwiches tasted fresh and were filling. The fries served with them were also good (not homemade, but good).  I also had an okra and tomato mix side that was good.

While we did not see any health violations such as ones listed in other reviews, my only downside would be the waitress who was working there while we were there. Granted they were were not busy, but she didn't seem to want to be there at all.  We got the impression she was family and a high school senior and kinda forced to work there. She was friendly-ish, but when we walked in she was sitting at a table with her boyfriend(?) and just told us to sit anywhere.

One of the employees did have their two daughters there who were coloring. As we were the only ones there for a while they became inquisitive and spent part of the time at our table asking questions and going over pictures on our phones.  They were darling and ok to us since my friend and I are both parents and my friend works with kids, but this might have bothered some customers.  We personally found it kind of relaxing though. It almost added to the family feel of the place (including the moody teenage waitress).

We will definitely be back.  Best meat and three in Nashville if ya ask me.
I love this place. Good food. Nice staff, clean kitchen. Not a 5 star restraunt  obviously but this place is a great little diner. It feels homey and the peanut butter pie is amazing.
Du_bDvBfxowdDPSGItKdzQ: The 6oz barrel steak is great. Cooked medium. I find their vegetable skewer yummy or steamed broccoli. The side salad is always crisp & fresh. Service is quick & friendly. Great place to come on those protein days.

Went for happy hour. Two meals 15.99. Got salmon and chicken, baked potato, sweet potato and two salads. Two glasses of wine 6.50. But Richard our waiter was the best part. This guy loved his job and treated us great!

I'm a really picky health-oriented eater. This restaurant rocks! Their veggie kabobs are perfectly grilled/seasoned. All of their sides are huge portions of nothing but the freshest veggies & sweet potatoes. It's perfect for a steak lover or a vegetarian. The service is always impeccable & friendly. From 3-5 during the week, a couple can have 2 meals for 14.99 & the choices are fantastic. Seafood is delicious & perfectly prepared as well. We usually always have enough leftover for a 2nd meal the next day. The bar is separate from most of the restaurant & seems to do a great biz, especially during their happy hour!
DCyOSmePdGjAuSt7jldXYg:
I would give this place above average. We stopped in for a quick meal/snack. We got the pulled pork platter with mac and cheese and French fries. I was pleasantly satisfied with the pulled pork. It was sauced up well and although it wasn't Kansas City BBQ or Memphis bbq it was still pretty good and much better than any fast food restaurant in the area. The Mac and cheese was really good and creamy cheesy and the French fries were different and pretty good too. 

I would go back, as I said there is nothing else in the area quick sit down and eat that is appealing.
Loved it!  My food was delicious and the atmosphere is so cute.  I will definitely be back and I can't wait to try more things!

I met a friend for lunch and he wanted to go here. Apparently this is owned by the folks who also own Mack & Kate's. 

Good thoughts about:
1. Quality of the brisket. It was fork tender, nice outside bark, almost like a deluxe pot roast. Sauce was good and kind of different, might have had some red wine in it?
2. Mac & cheese was very tasty although I like my wife's Mac & cheese texture which is a little firmer and has some crispyness to it. Still, I would order it again.
3. I talked to the manager and he was knowledgeable about all the famous bbq places in central Texas. That impressed me because he took the product and craft seriously. 

Could use some work on:
1. Crazy amount of onions in the green beans. I don't care for onions so inedible to me. 
2. Iced tea s/b Community Iced tea or Luzianne or Lipton. Also, lemon slices were by request. Newks and McAllister's show respect for the customers by offering large, high quality lemon slices. Why can't all restaurants figure this out? If you charge $2 for a 10 cent product (iced tea) then adding good lemons should not be a financial burden. 
  
I would go back to Smokies and I will have the brisket again!
Hel_hVlM2_QArXG0q4VNcA: Came in and was seated instantly, chips. Were delivered and drink order taken before I had set my phone down.  Great service, thanks

This is our go-to place for comfort Mexican food.  The food is always good and the service is good.  I have never experienced a long wait here regardless of the amount of customers.  The food always arrives fast and fresh.  The prices are some of the best around and you will definitely leave well fed.  I love this place!

My wife and I frequent this restaurant whenever we crave Mexican.  We've never had to wait for a table.  Everyone is quite friendly.  We have also never had a bad meal.  Their salsa is great, especially with their tortilla chips, which are thicker than the usual flimsy ones.

My go-to meal is the shrimp chimichanga with an extra serving of delicious Mexican rice.  My wife usually get one of their nacho selections.  The portion sizes are generous, without being overly-indulgent and the service is quick.

A bit of advice: Don't try to drink the Monster Margarita by yourself.

We are definitely glad to have this hidden gem in our repertoire.
g5W4j6-NcwVeOwQyMYUi6Q: Wow the bloody marry was awesome. The outdoor look to it was cool looking. Awesome local bar. You like Bourbon and Whisky this is the place to go. I recommend this place to anyone from out of town who doesn't want the wild Broadway Street bar scene.

Pleasantly surprised. Live near the place and sadly never see it busy. We decided to give the dinner menu a try and have to say that all the food we ordered (burger, hot chicken sammies and smoked salmon dip) were pretty delish. Glad we finally tried dinner, because we'd love to see this place get more business it deserves. Would like to go back to try their brunch!

Such a great new spot! The decor is as beautiful as the artisanal cocktails they serve. Try the lobster roll, you won't be sorry. Plus they have old school games and fun and friendly staff. Get a feel for the real local side of Nashville without the headache of having to wade through a sea of basic bitches on their bachelorette party outing. This place is a gem.
lV6w971tAAOx8Q_Xfa_bPw: A dozen visits and always more than satisfied. Saltimboca, Caprese, Spaghetti and meatballs ... All great. Wednesday is half price wine night!  We've had several servers, all have been very good.

Mom  &.I  go to Bria for special! Just a plan evening, at Bria' s! With good wine/good with always great servic! 
Definitely  recommend !
The food and service were fantastic. This is a great local spot and I would highly recommend it to anyone
Sy4VvCqRsPB0IN8u-0wVtQ: I feel like this place doesn't close. It's quick, good and affordable. Wide variety menu and excellent staff. Also I believe they deliver.

So good!! Restaurant quality. We ordered delivery on a rainy Saturday and got the Chicken Sub, Lasagna (which comes with breadsticks and a great lil side salad with lots of fresh veggies), a Cannoli, and some Sweet Tea and Coke. Everything tasted great. The lasagna has lots of meat, very flavorful, and the perfect amount of cheese for my taste (totally opposite of Roma's, which is almost all cheese and sauce). The BEST delivery I've had yet in Nashville for Italian entrees. I can't wait to try their pizza!

Passing through town and ordered a pizza for a late dinner in our airport hotel. This was one of the best pizzas we've had in a long time! We travel all over the Midwest and will definitely make our way back through Nashville just to grab another pizza from here!
exRWvXsuEZe_-jguq9aniQ: Great Mexican place. Salsa was the best I've had in a while. I wasn't expecting much when we started hunting down last minute Mexican. However, it was a pleasant experience and I'll frequent now. Only negative- waitress said no daiquiris? Sorry- not huge on tequila.
Super good food and service in this unassuming place. Not your joint if you're after a good margarita--ours were watery, nothing fresh but the lime wedge...everything else was top notch.

A new mexican restaurant in East Nashville... nice. In my opinion a much better option than Las Maracas up the road and , to me, the best Chile Relleno in town. This is not the soggy battered type of Chile Relleno, but the naked, stuffed chile...and done perfectly...not hard as a rock...like La Hacienda (the worst Relleno in town). I got the Combination 13 (burrito, Enchilada and Chile Relleno) all of the food was very good. Good Salsa, a nice spicy hot salsa as well as good queso. My wife and I will be back.
pP2Rm4rJyloEQbddwXyV-Q: Love it, best Cajon I have had sense moving out of Louisiana, try the boudin balls, they also have their own Smokehouse for sausages and pork chops
Great place for lunch but be sure you have the time all of the food is made when you order so it takes a bit of time bur worth it!

Excellent down home cajun food. Not quite NOLA but close enough for Tennessee. Shrimp po boys are great and the catfish dinner is enough food for two days. Service is sometimes a little slow but friendly.
LNOE8rK5L6NgcM3A06JJew: Solid beer variety and quality, they allow dogs outdoors, parking is limited in their small lot but there's usually street parking available. I've liked almost all the beer I've had there but nothing stood out to me. They have free popcorn and do movie screenings, they also screened the Preds games during playoffs. They allow you to bring outside food, I suggest getting a pizza from 5 points just a few minutes away from them. Overall a good hang!
Mosaic IPA was great. Open and airy inside with a great patio out back to enjoy with cornhole, etc. Worth a stop to check out

Really liked this brewery! They have a great selection of beers that change quite a bit. I was there 2 times in the past 3 weeks and they had multiple different beers, which I liked. 

+ ping pong tables 
+ games 
+ outdoor seating in the back 
+ good location if you are hanging around in East Nash 
+ not too crowded (yet) 
+ hours, I think they are open every day 

- they were out of pretzels when we were there both times, waaah. Love brewery snacks.
fnBifLT87rhowfaKmbXdXA: Really great!  They have so many different versions of baklava, and so far, they've all been great.  Turkish coffee is really good here, too.  Thought about 5 stars, but after some discussions with the wife, we decided on 4.  Really good.

After months of searching for a middle eastern bakery in Nashville, I recently discovered Basmah. I consider this the treasure on Nolensville Pk. They have my favorite dessert, Kanafeh, both cheese and pistachio. Absolutely delicious. The owner is very friendly and allowed me to sample the items before I purchased anything. If you are in need of a middle eastern sweet treat, I highly recommend a stop by Basmah.

I am a fan of Basmah Sweets and Pastry shop. For weeks when it first opened I would drive by and wonder....ummm what is in there? would I like it? then I would scare myself about trying something different...what type of sweets or baked goods would this be?/
So finally one day, I bucked up my fear and went in...and THANK GOODNESS I did!!!
I love the sweets here..

The worker /owner was very friendly, answered all my questions and allowed me to taste the sweets before I purchased any. I would definitely have to write the unique names down because I forget so easily. I enjoy the Baklak the most...figs, apples, nuts, sugar...and pastry dough...very very very good!!! I have been a few times now and I always end up with the same thing...even though I taste tons of stuff that are really good...I guess I am a creature of habit.
But I will go back again and next time share some photos of the great sweet treats!!!
vC9Zi1IcvVXrI8GEj4w2vA: Best breakfast sandwiches in Nashville! I wish they were opened for breakfast on the weekends. Maybe it's for the best that this place is not on my way to work!
Breakfast review: friendly staff, delicious biscuits with in-housed cured/smoked breakfast meats. Nutella pop tarts, need I say more?

Always a great experience here. They offer way more than just meat here. I usually come for meat, coffee, and eggs. They have fresh baguettes here as well as buns and rolls. The meat selection is excellent from what I have tried. The sliced deli roast beef and turkey is a must try as well as the chorizo. My bf is absolutely obsessed with their meatballs that they have already packaged and in sauce. They don't have them all the time but definitely check for them next time you're there. We also buy our dog the raw dog food here occasionally which he loves and is a nice treat for your pup! Another favorite here is he Switter's black coffee which is rarely in stock because everyone loves it too I assume. PRB is a great butcher shop that offers seasonings, jams, cheeses, rubs and even soup stocks. Every time I come here it is a pleasant experience and I find something new I didn't know they offered!
zXXzimAYL2m4Jkul0iHUPw:
I was ready to be disappointed due to the negative reviews but was pleasantly surprised.   Friendly owners, good oyster po-boy and tasty beignets.  Everything was served in a timely fashion and we had no complaints.  A good experience overall!

Chris
Great food, drinks, and atmosphere. The top floor view is amazing!

Best rooftop in Nashville. 

Cool wine bar also.
CsavgbTzQl1sfiGduHbkcQ:
This is a small store off of Nolensville Pike which hosts a shwarma sandwich shop in-store. I struggled a bit with the organization, but I love that there is a fresh pita shop hidden inside the market. While I was hesitant to grab loaf of pita from the stacks and racks within the room, I was impressed by the quality and freshness of the loaves. I will definitely be stopping by in the future to purchase some.

In terms of the other fare, there is a small selection of canned, bottled, and refrigerated goods in a tight space. I ended up only purchasing a mint yogurt drink and a round of dried figs. However, there are plenty of other snacks and refreshment options available, including guava drinks, other yogurt/cheese varieties, and baked goods. I suggest coming with a recipe in mind as there are several ingredients here you can buy in bulk, particularly specialty oils. 

I'm definitely going to order shwarma from the front next time, but I'm looking forward to fresh pita bread as well.

I have been to the Sulav International Market twice now.  They are among just a few places where you can get authentic shawarma.  It's definitely a top 3 sandwich in the area.  Sadly no one else is ever at the restaurant at lunch when I come, so don't be afraid to try it out.  The restaurant is one of the better offerings of the diverse Nolensville Rd area, and adds to my theory that any restaurant on Nolensville Rd with a restaurant built into a market is delicious.

Okay first of all, I'm not a big fan of shawarma especially the kind that's wrapped in very thick bread. I also find the filling of meat very bland. 

However, this place is out of this world! They sell the best shawarmas(labenese style)! I tried both beef and chicken shawarma and I have to tell you that the spices come really well with the meat. I  love the hint of parsley in every bite. I also tried their chicken kabobs and its so tender and juicy. I felt like the chicken just melted in my mouth. The chick pea soup was okay though. It was kind of bland for me. Honestly though, if you really want good shawarma then you must give this place a try! Not to mention the prices are very affordable for the amount of food you get.
VMTqE1OZC1G-lYuSrt15JA:
I always forget about this place, just because it's off the beaten path--a little tricky to find, even for someone familiar with the area!  But I love it, especially when the weather is nice.  

It's a floating restaurant and bar right on the Cumberland River, and you can come by boat (lots of folks do that) or drive.  Indoor and outdoor seating, so something for everyone!  Food is decent--again, I'm a seafood snob, being from the FL Gulf Coast, so I'm not going to think anything is as good up here.  But, I've had everything from Grouper to shrimp to crab legs, and have never been very disappointed.  

It can be a fun place for kids, but you will really need to watch them--no running around!  After all, you are on a floating dock, and the railings are just ropes.  So be warned!  

Minus one star because of the mosquitoes--oh go ahead and spray yourself down with bug repellent beforehand--they do have citronella torches but they only go so far when you are on the water!

Going to Blue Moon Lagoon is like going on a vacation to the beach for dinner. That's what it feels like anyway. You can choose to eat inside or out and they have a back patio for private events. Its located waterfront in the Rock Harbor Marina so you are guaranteed a beautiful view.
Unfortunately as a vegetarian you may be limited to the salad section of the menu. If you are a pesco vegetarian (meaning that you eat fish / seafood) there are plenty of choices. You can view the menu on their website before going if you like.
Everything that I have had here so far has been good. They have the best pecan pie that I have ever had. Husband ordered it and i accidentally ate half. oops!

they are open wednesday through friday ( 2pm until 1am)
saturday & sunday (11am until 2am)

Man, I really like to eat at Blue Moon, it reminds me of seafood places in Florida, but with a redneck Nashville twist.  Located in West Nashville, this is a good place to come and get away from it all, there are no other restaurants like it in Nashville.  It is right on the banks of the Cumberland River and has docks so you can pull a boat up.  They have things like burgers and all sorts of seafood.  The crab cakes are a really good starter and have a little bit of spicy mustard in them, which makes them different.  Honestly, I have not had any seafood entrees there because I usually get a burger that has BBQ pork on top of it!  Weird I know, but is damn good.  The outdoor seating is awesome!
f9yBZy6IRQwX1yyy_9X6xA: Visited today during lunch time, Shontel was our server. The service was amazing. She was wonderful with her friendly hospitable nature. She was quick to get our drink order take a dj out to us, catered to all of my wife's needs with Splenda and special order on my omelette. All around a great server and a great representation of IHOP's Service

I love Ihop. This one had just opened and was extremely busy, but the waitress was nice and did the best she could. Food was great, like at all Ihops. 
Would like to back now that the new restaurant rush is over.
The cleanest IHOP I have ever been too!...Waiter (Lenora) was very friendly and the Post Roast Beef Sandwich was off the hook!!
-P7CxFXqAyZ7_ep4r4W-Zg: Last night I went to Cracker Barrel on Harding Place. I love the feeling you get when you walk in to a Cracker Barrel. It seems to take you back a little in time. I grew up in the country so I really appreciate good country cooking. I had the Roast Beef, Dumplings, Carrots and Corn. It was delicious. The service was great and I felt like I got a lot for the money I spent. But the highlight of the evening was the new carrot cake they have. You could tell the cake was freshly made. The cake and the icing melted in my mouth and the flavors were intense. They also have a lot of new healthy meals. I think its a little too early for Christmas decorations but I recommend Cracker Barrel to anyone who loves country cooking.

My three favs at the moment:
1) Chicken tenderloin sandwich
2) Meatloaf (Cinnamon apples, Baked Potato, Hashbrown Cassarole--I like my starches...what can I say?!?!??!)
3) Pancakes with cherries and whipped cream on top.

Yum!

I totally missed this place when I lived in DC.

On a side note....why isn't it common practice for the Cracker Barrel wait staff to bring out your rolls and cornbread BEFORE the meal.  There isn't a sales reason not to...and everything in customer service says its a good idea.  I think it might even cause some to purchase it extra...assuming their meal didn't come with it.  I kinda feel odd telling my waitress every single time to please bring them out early.  This seems 100% intuitive to me....  

Review: 4.4 out of 5

Very Good Service and the Breakfast was Excellent. Went here on a recommendation from a coworker. Ordered the Uncle Hershals, with biscuits, gravy, and country ham. So good and so filling. Quality food and as mentioned the server was excellent.
gN3VmmeNlcGTpuJfowWV9A: Probably the friendliness Taco Bell I've ever been into, and this is a reoccurring thing. Very impressed!

The manager Robin is amazing. She went above and beyond what was asked, and she had an amazing attitude. Usually at 11pm the drive-thru workers at fast food restaurants are extremely rude and unhelpful. Robin exhibited the complete opposite of this. I don't know her, I'm not a friend of hers, just someone who will be giving this Taco Bell more of my business because of Robin the awesome Taco Bell drive-thru employee. It's the little things like this that make a night out with friends so enjoyable. Thanks Robin!
These folks KILLED. IT. We had a huge order and they were so positive and happy to help up. Thank you!
R6fcBIFBhfZx33z7qD0lqQ: My favorite Deli in Nashville. Really the only authentic Deli in Nashville.  Sure its in a strip mall in Belle Meade, but get over it. The owner is a great guy and he employs very nice young kids from the neighborhood. But the food is the main reason to come here. Any of the signature sandwiches are great, but even the plain Corned Beef (Goldie's size on Challah) hits the spot. Also highly recommended are the HUGE potato latkes...

Blonde Brownie Sundae: 10 out of 10
Sandwiches: 9 out of 10
Cheesecake: 9 out of 10
Building: Occupied by Local Taco

Why? Why? Why? Goldie, why did you have to close your doors? You were SO much better than Noshville!!!

If anyone ever had the warm blonde brownie with ice cream at Goldie's, you know that it was the best dessert in the state of Tennessee! And the sandwiches were incredible. It took me back to my days in New York City every time I walked in. There must be a way to save the business!!! WHY!!!!!!!!

I like this place better now that they have moved to Murphy Road.  I can stop buy for a quick nosh and then go up the hill to get in 18 holes at McCabe.

Growing up in Chicagoland in an area heavily populated by Jewish families, I have always loved Kosher and Kosher-style food and even attended Passover Seders of Jewish friends.  Goldie's is authentic Jewish food even if it isn't strictly Kosher; it is as good as any Kosher-style deli on the north side of Chicago.
8MdZJIIm3My0jdbzYiweeQ:
First day at The Tower was great. I stopped in for breakfast at this unique local and fresh ingredient concept. I had the tower burrito which was phenomenal. It was fresh and quite tasty to the last bite. The service most
Importantly was top notch. Everyone was attentive and and courteous. I'll definitely be a returning consumer.!

Love their coffee and chicken salad.  Staff is always friendly a courteous.  There is room to hang around and eat but not a ton of room.  If you like juice they can also do a fresh juice.  I recommend calling in your order.

So we came back again of course. I live only minutes away and I've been dreamin' about that BLT for days. This time the owner Abraham was there, and as soon as I asked for oil or vinegar, he knew me from the review!

He is a super awesome guy and very friendly, he obviously has a lot of pride and love put into the business. He took heed of my last review and added oil and vinegar to the sauces for people with mayo allergies. 

Not only that, he offered to give me a free sandwich next time we come in. This is, and will be, my new favorite sandwich shop in east nashville. The atmosphere, shop, staff and food are all superb. The bar has been set pretty high by these guys.
ETQKdyfkVjdGj62aS4dknA:
Philly Cheese Steak in Nashville? 
Yeah sorta.

Very clean place, run by a friendly Asian couple. The husband assembled our cheese steaks with the discipline of a sushi chef. 
They were flavorful without being gristly or greasy, on a pretzel roll, pretty damn good. No cheese sauce, they weren't having it.  
Still, it didn't matter. The fries were perfectly prepared in very clean oil. 
These two get really simple things right. I'd recommend anything on the menu based off of what I experienced. 

This place is a good fix for a chomp right next to Fat Mo's in the the strip mall. Easy to miss if you aren't looking for it.

My review is based on the Onion Rings. The best Onion Rings I've ever had and I never expected this place would be a place for Onion Rings. The burger was Delicious too!(Two patties)! Why spend $7 for fast food burger when you can get fresh cooked buger and fries from here. He actually slice fresh onions and batters them on the spot. The Cheese Stake is good but nothing to write home about. It seems that everything is pretty good here. This place is a solid go to for a meal and cheap.

I used to work at a Philly Cheesesteak place, and for years I had missed the taste of a truly great Philly until I ate here.  I love fat mo's, but seeing as how they are right beside each other, I will never buy a Philly from mo's again.
wKjyDzCK9i8FpgckMozwDw:
I love this service! My job is pretty labor-intensive, so when I get home, I don't want to think too hard about what I'm eating or doing. You just have to take the package of your choice out of the freezer and let it thaw, then just cook it. It only takes a few minutes, and you don't have to chop anything or think about dinner too hard. There's just me and one other person eating the food, and we always have plenty of leftovers for another two or three meals, depending on how hearty the food it. 

The downside really is that you sometimes get food that you don't like so well. Also, the sodium content is very high. Still, for what you're getting, it's wonderful, and it's good for us to eat something we wouldn't normally get.

Perfect for our busy family.  We are not Vegan or Vegetarian, so I add lean meats to some of the dishes.  I was skeptical, but I'm so glad I tried it because now I rely on these meals to help get me through the week.  The food is fabulous.  Second week in and haven't had a thing we don't like.  My toddler eats most of the meals without a problem.  The Tamale Casserole- Heavenly.  I would eat it every day!

Convenient, affordable, healthy, and vegan! I'm insanely busy right now, with no time to cook the variety of dinners that I am used to preparing for  ourselves, and my husband is still learning how to cook (bless). My veggie chef arrives on Sunday afternoon at our doorstep. Each meal is compactly packaged into a freezer bag (total of 5), with a label on each that provides cooking instructions, nutritional information, and ingredients. I defrost the one we want to use the night before in the fridge, and come home from a busy day to a wonderful dinner that even my novice chef husband can prepare by himself!

The entrees are creative and provide plenty of variety, both from a nutritional and menu perspective. They don't over salt the food - which I greatly appreciate - most of the flavors, seasonings, and sauces I have tried can hold their own without any added salt.

If I could change one thing I would ask that they provide a few extra meals to choose from to truly customize the meal selection for the week. As of now, they only offer 5 meals to choose from at a time, so if I don't want one, you must double up in another. 

Extremely affordable and plenty of left overs to take to lunch the next day. This service has made my life so much easier! I plan to continue to order even after my schedule slows down again - much cheaper that ordering take-out late week night!
Hb09xY41jKoQoCvt4JK0Xg: Great atmosphere and space to have a rye and a Genesee...or several. Simple, but perfect cheeseburgers and fries. Weird art, twinkle lights, and good music. Bartender was friendly and fast. Also, their cheap prices make me happy.

Cool little "dive" bar and restaurant.  I visited twice while in Nashville for a late night burger and beer.  Great vibe, the clientele is as eclectic as the musical selections playing in the background.  I don't know the cooks name, but if he's got a beard, long hair, and is wearing a stocking cap, park yourself by the bar and watch him work, he's fast as lightning whipping out orders of delicious burgers, very entertaining!!!! Great place to begin or end a night out with friends! Try their cheeseburger with an egg on it, delicious!!!!

Here's the thing. I didn't eat anything here, although I'd like to have a bite next time around. 

Reasons I will be back again to try the food:

- super cheap beer
- cards against humanity
- friendly and cute bartenders
- jukebox with shania twain 
- cardboard cutouts of steve austin and snoop dogg

If that doesn't make you wanna go here then I don't know what will.
Wwjh5cKADjp9jHF0W-Pa7g: Honey Gold is my go to but Honey Hot is my bf's favorite. If you can call in your order, do so because they cook to order and can take a few minutes. They're a little high (I'm a college student) but well worth the price. The blue koolaid is to die for, the rolls are a great combination of sweet and salty, the fries are seasoned and the wings are delicious every time. NBW is honestly a gem of Jefferson St.
Honey gold combo w biscuit fries and red koolaid -- my fave for years .. wish they were open on Sunday's .. hungry thoughts !
They are with out a doubt the best wings in All of Middle Tennessee!!! Honey Golds and Honey Hots!!!!!!
R5bdlFT5iNS9sm0Ow6iIpA: We're staying at a hotel nearby and decided to grab dinner here. The food was really good and our waiter, I wish I'd gotten his name, was wonderful. Made us feel really at home and not like out-of-towners.

Generally when my husband & I are in a new location, we like to eat at places we've not been to before. Our Saturday agenda was gonna be very busy so we decided to pick Shoney's since it was close by. As soon as we walked in it appeared that it would be a little bit of a wait. The hostess asked if we were willing to sit in the bar area. Bar area... at Shoney's??? Um, sure! Surprisingly they serve alcohol here. I drank 3 Mimosas, yum! The buffet was fully loaded and was delicious! I'm not normally a huge fan of Shoney's, but the same food was on a completely different level compared to ours in Knoxville. It was so good that I even insisted on us going back the next day, haha
The sirloin steak in butter and garlic was to die for specially after a long long day and Stefanie was fantastic
5675HMUmVh1SoRLkpnMKFw: This place is so authentic. My wife and I do consider this a special treat for us that we will make a special trip either to Nashville or to there Brentwood location. They are by far the best Mediterranean food I have had here in Tennessee.

Stopped here for a spontaneous dinner .. SULTAN's GRILL upstairs from Kay Bob's BBQ (take the stairs up the backside) ... Very interested in this restaurant because I lived in Turkey for two years and love unique ethnic foods ... Have been to most of the gyro and Mediterranean places around midtown and this was next on my list.... In a few words:: authentic, spicy, delicious, a few dishes had some real "zing".... Started out with a chai tea with fried kibbah and a slice of spinach pie ... Tea had peppermint leaves and was steaming hot!!! The spinach pie was different than what I expected... My mom makes "spinach wraps" Greek style and really shreds the spinach and uses thin flaky phyllo dough... This was more like a "hot pocket"with a thicker dough/crust but just as delicious... The fried kibbah was great!! They're like little fried meatballs .. interesting lemon zing !! ... Chai tea was not what I was expecting but still delicious... Served in a coffee cup and with a big sugar pour dispenser (was hoping for little sugar cubes).... For dinner I had the chicken shawarma plate while my sweetheart had the felafel plate -- was nervous (because it was a new item for him) but he loved it !! He says "it was fresh, hot, and full of a lot of different flavors" My dinner came with a side salad and what appeared to be basmati rice (yum- light and fluffy)- chicken had a tangy spice going on -- nothing like the Greek but that's what made it perfect-- not all Mediterranean "menus" are the same, I explained to my honey--- they may have the same ingredients and same overall goal, but the ways and means of preparing and cooking and presenting are very different ... 
Service was fast and friendly; no "spectacular" service personalities or effort to engage in southern hospitality--- pretty straightforward with order and service --- that's pretty much it--- 

Notes about tables::: 
----Each table is covered by a little "cabana" -- can create a cozy mood -- however this also creates a dark table-- (and really needs to be done differently) because the little table candle just doesn't do it... so if you need to read the menu, bring a pocket flashlight. The candle needs to be bigger, or change it and offer a small table lantern.
----A few of the table arrangements are low to the floor and don't have a chair but rather have a large fat cushion... (This must be a traditional setting?)

Belly dancing happens on Saturdays, with live music.

Great experience; We will definitely be back. Here's a few photos.

I was skeptical as it's sort of hidden and was empty when I went for dinner, but the food is very good, large portions, and the service is great. Definitely try this spot if you like Mediterranean food.
F8Y_XCZ3QT4BW5-_EwErzg:
I know this is a "chain" Mexican restaurant for Nashville, but I tend to favor this location over the others for two main reasons:

1) It's pretty new (a few years)
2) It's closest to me (since I'm all that matters!  ha!)

A little smaller than the location on Hayes street, but just as tasty.  It's your typical Mexican restaurant, with a large, head-spinning menu complete with order-by-number.  I find that most of the meals are pretty large, so I stay away from the combos and usually just order the chicken burrito with white cheese smothering it--YUM--and a side of Mexican rice.  There are plenty of vegetarian options too.  My comrade that usually goes with me gets the chimichanga and claims its the best.  

Cheese dip is good, but not the best--however, we get it anyway.  Again, your basic white cheese Mexican dip.  For a good CHEAP meal, check out Las Palmas.  I suggest the Franklin Road location if you are trying to choose between them.  New(er) and clean, and the service is better than decent and quick.

When you go to Las Palmas you have a pretty good idea of how the food will be. It is good, inexpensive casual dining and consistently of quality.  Where this particular location shines is the service they provide, it is excellent every time!

Based on service, freshness of food, taste and affordability, I'd give it 5 stars; however, it's not where you would take an important date, unless she just got off work and wanted a margarita with a meal. This is more of a family style restaurant with affordable food entrees.
The cheese dip is a must, and the Chicken and Shrimp Fajitas can feed two @ dinner (shrimp is deveined). =)
c5zCZbqxx09GjNo5O8UcVg: I came here for brunch a long while back and loved it. If you're looking for a very nice brunch spot in the Nolensville area, this is it. You don't have to drive all the way downtown and it isn't crowded either. I don't remember the name of what I got but I think it was a Kobe burger and there was an egg inside it. It was delicious!

The service here was great. I went for the brunch and definitely indulged on the Scarlet Ohara's a yummy 2 dollar cocktail. It was made with Champagne, Peach Schnapps and Cranberry juice! Yum!!  I ate a biscuit that was covered in 
Hollandaise, Sausage, and enough Eggs for 3. My meal also came with home fries. I thought the portion sizes were great for the price. I will be returning. Our waitress Tess was very sweet and attentive!

My girlfriend and I came here for dinner one night out of the blue, having just moved to the area.  We were impressed by the food for sure! We were both craving hamburgers. She ordered the Kobe Burger, and I the truffle burger.  While the service was par (at best), the food was spot on.  She loved her burger, and the fries.  I thought the burger was fine, but the truffle topic is a bit played out.  Anyway, while the service was less than attentive, it was adequate.  the food was good, and the prices were fair.  we have been raving to our friends that this has been the best burger we have sunk our teeth into, even though it isn't a burger joint at all.  We have only been here once, but guaranteed we will be back here again. If you live in the area, and are craving a good burger, this is the spot! beats burger repubilc, though we haven't tried Burger shack yet.  like I said, we are introverts :-)
CkYoNqqNAfRxatyylgN15A: Really good food and great bartenders. Prefect place to catch a professional game! Also a really good location.

Clean, quick and casual! Great little spot for a hotel restaurant. The omni in Nashville is and outstanding property. For a town with out a ritz or four seasons this is as good as it gets!  The barline staff are fun and quick. I sat at the bar away from the music but thought the music was good and would come back and actually listen to the acts! Not my usual m.o.  I ordered the pulled pork and the chili. Yes I know a bit overboard. But I was quite pleased all the way around. Tasty pork and nice sauce. I skipped the usual fries and top bun so I could focus on the pork and chili!  The chili is actually made with steak tips. Very very meaty and tasty. Nice presentation and the cornbread with a slather of butter- just over the top !

Yep!  I eat it all.  Actually thinking about coming by tomorrow for a burger??
Great place to come for lunch! Amazing food, great atmosphere and super friendly staff. Amazing location right in the Omni and fantastic prices too!!
TOLkyYvbMJMzMYp0fXkdaA: One of my favorite thai places in town.   A little on the higher end of pricing but its the only place I've been to that has given me my Pad Thai NATIVE spicy.   Spring rolls and steamed dumplings are always a good way to start off the meal.   I also highly recommend the Tom Yum soup.  One of my favorite places for the soup in Nashville.

Located in the same shopping center as Barre 3 and J. Alexander's, I can easily imagine Golden Thai being overlooked. I stopped by for dinner on a random weekday night and found it rather empty at 630pm. We ordered the Hung Ray Curry (mainly because it wasn't listed as just a red or green curry and we had never heard of it before) and also a basil, beef noodle dish. Both dishes were good, but not particularly memorable. We enjoyed the fact that the they truly delivered on the spice level we asked for. No joke. I do think I will need to come back to try other dishes people have raved about such as the green curry and the chicken larb wraps. Regardless, I am happy to have access to a Thai place without a 30 minute plus wait!

If you can, come during lunch. Its at least a $3 savings for the same amount of food!

This is my favorite place to get Thai food in Nashville, though admittedly I am not a spicy-food person (I cannot enjoy my meal if my face is melting off) so if you want a review on spice level, keep looking, but if you want a good place for great Thai flavors and service, then please continue.

I love this little restaurant in the corner of a shopping plaza on White Bridge.  Its so cozy inside, and very quiet, a plus during the cold months here.  Their wonton soup is simple and perfect.  I love their fried rice with tofu (crisped up which makes a nice addition to the dish) or with chicken.  Their curries are fantastic too, the panang being the best, and the wild ginger sauce with chicken is a new favorite (seriously, this ginger sauce is great, not too ginger-y and so many colorful and yummy veggies!).  The appetizer platter is great to split, and they make really great dumplings.  If you're feeling bold, try their spice rack, they have some great homemade concoctions in there.

The service here is just great.  People are polite and helpful to those of us with no clue how and what to order.  It's also a favored place for take-out.  They are fast and have yet to mess up an order.  I adore this place and how full and peaceful I feel when I leave.
3awWfOPccIIZi5G9zedZew: Must have! This well ran, delicious authentic food truck is DELICIOUS! Everything is always very fresh and it's always very quick service. Worth every penny and worth the drive no matter where the truck is parked for the day! Would recommend it to anyone and a definite event MUST HAVE if they are attending and present at an event you're at!

Definitely trying this again when I get the chance.  I got the California burrito.  It wasn't my favorite thing in the world, but that was my taste preference.  I had many people around me (at a firework event) say they loved it.  Next time, I will try the tacos or the other burrito.  It all looked amazing and the staff was friendly and clean.

Absolutely authentic! Not tex-mex not regional not certain place somewhere style. Just pure unadulterated tacos mexicanos. No lettuce or sour cream and cheese here folks.
Marinated steak chopped up to order for Carne Asada, Braised Pork shredded when you ask for Carnitas tacos. Al Pastor like it is meant to be. Corn tortillas Warmed on a griddle and flavored by the meat that is placed on them. Cilantro and diced onion are the only toppings here just as in Mexico. Every plate comes with lime wedges and they have red and green fresh homemade salsa. Legit mexican sodas and horchata are available.
These are the friendliest guys you could meet anywhere. No frills because it's all about the food and it shows.
fy7kLejLjyVsbVycL6gqfQ: Amazingly enough this great little dive bar with a good band had the best fried green tomatoes I've ever had... I cannot come back to Nashville to come to this place!

SO fun! this bar was the first bar we went to while in Nashville. The live music was awesome and the drinks didn't cost an arm and a leg. The giant Jenga they have in the back was really fun along with the photo booth. We had such a good time here. And their QUESO was AMAZING.

This was my favorite and last stop on Broadway. I would have loved to stay longer if my friends weren't ready to go home for the night. The band played a medley of 80's-00's hip hop for most of the time I was there. It was very nostalgic for me. Reminded me of middle school and high school dances. There was a much younger crowd here. Didn't hear any country music. It was a Saturday night. Didn't catch the name of the band. Only crowded on the dance floor. Plenty of places to sit in the back.
vwxHvJSDGYM75tmjKcMXtg: Staff always pleasant and helpful, food is good, they do stay busy especially between 2-4p so be patient

As far as Sonics go, this place is one of the best. There is a Hispanic gentleman that runs the drive thru and he is always very helpful. Not in the greatest of areas, but I find myself frequenting this Sonic quite a bit.
jaJhPD1mn5aqWUr61-nxyw: Went here for drinks with friends one night. I did not order any food, so I can't speak directly to its quality. I will say that several of the tables around us did, and everything looked/smelled delicious. As for drinks, they had a pretty good selection of house mixed drinks and a well curated beer list. The 2nd floor patio area was really nice and had a nice view overlooking Gallatin. Great spot for a drink during a night out on the town!

The food was well above the average bar food, and, with every TV showing college football, we were in heaven. We had the pretzel and beer cheese which was pretty good, but the tenderloin sliders were to die for. I don't drink beer, but it's a good looking beer menu. Service was pretty good, but it is evident they were a bit overwhelmed with the crowd. We will definitely be back!

First timers. Waitress was awesome. Recommended the right things and she liked Guinness...so that was a bonus in my book  Place was nicely done. Beer Cheese and Pretzel was very good. Pricing was a little steep. But still very nice experience.
5d6Re2uKakZvY-QPJgTvUQ: Bakery awesome...Cant wait to try lunch...

We are so excited to have shugga hi bakery open in Cleveland park!  Our food was delicious- christian got the breakfast burrito, I got "the Catherine"(eggs and bacon, and my mother-in-law got "the bomb" (a hot biscuit stuffed with eggs spinach etc) everything was fresh hot and delicious. We also had some rum and lemon cake which was equally good.  The staff was exceptionally friendly. I'm excited for them to become a staple breakfast nook in our community. They also have live music on Friday and Saturday nights! :)

Okay, so let me just say that this place is so good! This was my first time here and I actually enjoyed it. I was a bit skeptical because I hadn't heard many people talking about this place, but it did not disappoint! I went with a large group (10 people) at 11am on a Saturday and we had no trouble with seating. There was only one waitress working when we got there, so it took a while to get drinks and our food ordered. I had a Peach Sangria (wasn't on the menu) and it was SO good. Plus it was only $5, so you can't beat that. The food was great, too! There weren't any issues with what everyone ordered, which usually happens with large orders. I got the Dickerson Sunrise (added cheese to my grits) & was definitely satisfied. 

Overall, this was a great place. I definitely recommend it. The food was hot and delicious and the drinks were amazing. I will for sure be back!
wLV_IZfy6nrNbplZ5sHJKQ: Mellow Mushroom gets 4 stars solely on the basis of its House Pesto, which is a delicious pesto-based pizza with cheese and spinach and tomatoes and mushrooms.  The crust is not very crispy but it is tasty.

Mighty Meaty pizza is awesome. For the amount of meat on this pizza, it's a great bargain.

Buffalo wings are pretty good too. Not as much variety as McDougals but still can compete against them.

Service overall is average. You stand around waiting for a host(ess). Servers do not refill your glasses timely but it's sufficient.

The only reason I don't give them a 5 is because the bartender wasn't that great. 

They have an awesome beer selection and the drunken fun guys (app) was awesome! Sooo good. 

Standard bar fare/pizza and not sure on any other food as we only had drinks and an app. Neat atmosphere though as you can see into the kitchen and watch them toss pizza dough so kids would probably enjoy.
apzsxzeSo_MAuW8hkcAh1A: Most awesome place to hang out. Erica at the bar is so sweet and nice. Definitely check it out!

My boy and I decided to grab some dinner before we headed to the club here. 

1st of all i kind of like the setup, it fits the name Cave. Upon entrance you immediately see the bar with a few bar tables and chairs. To your left there is the smallest one person bathroom I've ever seen! but hey it serves its purpose. Around the corner from the bathroom is where the live band performs and more tables & chairs with a tv mounted on the wall. I really wish we could've stayed to hear the live band but plans were already made :(. 

I ordered a bacon cheese burger meal with fries and i must say it was really good!! fries were kinda cold and hard but the burger made up for it. My homie had wings and apparently they were delicious because he wasted no time putting them away! Wait staff was a bit slow getting us our order but I'm wondering if they are faster when the place gets fuller..???

Looking forward to checking out the live music next time!

Literally a nice hole-in-the-wall. Someone knocked a big hole in the basement wall, which is the entry into the low ceiling room where the DJ spins the discs and the brothers dance. Cool, in temperature and in vibe! I mean its in a cool stone-lined basement. The dance area is to the left of where the deejay or performers play. A nice dark-oak bar is just inside the front door before the hole-in-the-wall passage into the rest. The cover charge is often around $10 on weekends, usually less weekdays.
