The Cave is a black bar but white folks are welcome as in most black bars in Nashville, and vice-versa. I wish the rest of the country could be as tolerable as Nashville.
gjkw7CkbvSnovjpwkfsArA:
I was over charged for my delivery and my drink was wrong. When I called about it they just wanted to pick up the drink and give me a free drink later. I won't be ordering from them again. Not happy!

They fixed it. Pleasantly surprised :)

Another reviewer notified me that I might have been reviewing the wrong Mixed Grill location (not affiliated, that I know of) and I think he's right. I feel terrible and I can't delete my old review. So here's 5 stars, and I will visit this location soon! I'm sure it's lovely!

Yummy
Very clean, very nice staff. 
Substituted the tabouli for the Greek salad on the falafel plate. 
The tabouli is my new favorite! 
It was much chunkier and lighter dressed than I've had before. 
Loved the additions of veggies on top of the hummus. 
A thick hummus with plenty of olive  oil 
drizzled on top. 
Will be back
UJPWeNYm4VrkvcAAbqAv_A:
This place is great! Look at the low-star review's for Seafood Sensation and tell me the author's don't appear to be unintelligent. I've been eating all sorts of spectacular food in Nashville lately, but this place made me want to take the time to write about my experience. 

My lady and I ordered a shrimp and an Alaskan king crab platter and were super satisfied with the buttery goodness this place puts out! Fatty shrimp and beautiful crab with corn, broccoli and potatoes, all perfectly cooked. The seasoning is something from a fairy tale. The owner, Darren, even hooked us up with a coke on the house!

If you like delicious seafood, check this place out.

I just moved here and have been looking for a great seafood place.  This place is really good.  I ordered the Dungeness Crab and added shrimp and Andouille sausage.  It came with potatoes, corn and shrimp.  Loved all of it.  The seafood was cooked just right and everything had great flavor.  Friendly staff.   Will make it a regular stop.

This place is walk-up but not fast food.  Cooked to order and you can tell there's love in it because it was unbelievably tasty.  We shared the alaskan crab platter with shrimp and andouille and everything was perfectly cooked and incredibly flavorful.  My husband just sat there with his eyes closed after we ate and said, "I'm remembering the crab..." :) 

Can't believe it took us so long to hear about this place, but we will be back!
ThdW_5-pE2wdPnPOS8RGtw:
Great food that made fresh daily. All of this combined with an affordable price tag. Def get an order of the Basil Hummus

Friendly service and great management.

Very positive experience at Belle Meade location!

Very fresh foods. Great menu. chicken or steak kabobs over rice. Awesome Greek Salad with their own Greek dressing loaded with onions, tomatos, green and red peppers, purple onions and huge black olives!  they're fresh made limeade is particularly tangy and sweet

I really liked this place! I dined here after going to hot yoga while I was visiting Nashville. Would love it if this chain traveled to Chicago.

As a more health-conscious eater, I loved that the menu included calorie content and nutrition information.

I had the chicken & orzo soup which was just okay. I was expecting it to be more of a lemon chicken & orzo soup; it was instead like a chicken noodle-type of soup, but made with orzo versus noodle. You could definitely skip it; nothing special here.

I had the cauliflower rice bowl with chicken; so yum! Tasted healthy and fresh. Loved the dill and the tzatziki sauce; really makes it feel cool and refreshing.

Because I have such a terrible sweet tooth and must end each meal with dessert, I had Yaya's chocolate cake. So. Good. Moist and sweet. Heavenly. Yum, Yaya, Yum.

I would definitely return... really enjoyed!
I4eymkW2zyz6_bdic09ASQ: Sloco is a top notch gem of a sandwich shop.  A place where quality, real flavor and the art of well-prepared meat are still important.  It's casual and comfortable where you can be you.  Sloco is a lovely, friendly place to go to enjoy a locally sourced, thoughtfully planned casual dinner, and a glass of beer or wine, and the best melt-in-your-mouth chocolate chip cookie you've ever tasted.
I was surprised they had so many interesting vegan options and I went with the homemade seitan sandwich with carrot slaw. Very tasty.

I was very excited to see a place like this at the Farmer's Market. I absolutely LOVE all their vegetarian and vegan sandwich options, their cute storefront, and their "eat local" philosophy. I had the vegan quinoa meatball sub. While this was not the best sandwich I've ever had, it was delicious and I loved the uniqueness and creativity of it - I had never heard of "meatballs" made of quinoa. If I lived in the area, I would definitely be trying every single one of their fantastic-sounding veg options!
4u_FN69s_HeGCXYupnIscw: This place is amazing! The food is very fresh and cooked to perfection. We ordered loaded nachos with chicken. We were pleasantly surprised to see queso cheese in the bottom of the pan, chips stacked and later with well seasoned tender chicken, avocado cream, cheese and diced tomatoes. My husband and I finished maybe half the order, 4 people could easily share these nachos. I had the chop salad, so delicious! The orange pepper dressing really compliments the salad. I highly recommend eating here.

I was extremely impressed with Solario.  

The food was great and the service was wonderful.  Our server was very helpful and attentive as well as personable and friendly.  He made one very minor mistake, but fixed it immediately and did more than necessary to make up for the mistake. 

I ordered the fajitas and they were delicious.

Probably the best meal I had while in Nashville.

Surprisingly good.  I'd read bad things about this place but I came for lunch and was pleased.  The oxacan empanadas were small but tasty with a nice pork filling and a tasty green sauce drizzled on them.  The green chile braised pork burrito was excellent with lots of flavor.  It's not cheap but I have to give it four stars.  Good service as well.  

Chris
T4v7qgTIhDFoDYxTMus5Yw:
Excellent place to stop by and have a quick bite to eat. The ambience is inviting, bright, and cozy. With plenty of greens plants to make it a wellness place. 

Everything is vegan. We had the quinoa chili and broccoli cheddar soup (not too cheesy). Both are delicious. Fantastic values. Comfort based. Flavorful. Perfect for a quick lunch.

Love the aesthetic and vibe of this place. I got the sobro bowl and loved it. For such big smoothie bowls I was pleasantly surprised by the price! The people were very friendly and I got my food quickly. That bowl filled me up for hours so you definitely get your money's worth.

Was in the downtown  nashville area looking for a smoothie that didn't have added sugar. Found this place. Got the cocao butter, loved it.Nice and cold, not watery or icy like some "fruit" smoothies. Left, came back 30 min later to get the pro bowl, loved it. Note thst the bowls are essentially a smoothie with fresh fruit, granola,  etc on top. Consistencu you eat with a spoon, not a straw.
O5w-GuNCARANWyDzUO3ReA:
Best Ethiopian food. Point. Blank. Period!!!
If you are looking for awesomeness, then you have found it. This place gives me the joy to eat.

Definitely preferred this food to what they serve at gojo. Some might find the lack of menu unpleasant, but I enjoyed letting go and trusting what I was served- and it was excellent. Just enough meat and veggies to fill us up with some leftovers to spare. And the injera was sour and soft- perfect. Thank you Z. We will be back regularly!!

So so good. Don't expect to see your favorites on the menu, because there is no menu. We sat down and waited, thinking a waiter would stop by but after some time watching the activity in the kitchen, all of a sudden our table was full of delicious food. 

The dinner was finished with freshly roasted coffee, freshly roasted as in she brought a cast iron pan with smoking coffee beans to the table for us to smell before grinding it and brewing. 

Don't expect fancy service, or much choice, but you won't be disappointed this style of service. Some of the best Ethiopian food i have had.
_V3YkIkqAn4V6U-cAykVtw: Was too busy enjoying my lunch to review last visit. Going back today for lunch. The best thing about this place is the freshness of everything. On my last two visits I've had separate dishes and freshness was consistent. Learned not to order the ice tea-as they charge $1.95 per glass (ouch). Water is my choice next time...Ha!

I ordered red curry with tofu, spice level 3, for lunch today and have to say I was quite impressed with how good the curry was. A little thicker and full of flavor, just like I like it! It wasn't quite as spicy as I had hoped for, but it still had some heat to it. I will definitely eat here for lunch more often.

Crab ragoons and plum sauce is amazing! I would say the starters I tried were all very good. Bed teriyaki was good with mushrooms and gyoza. Miso soup made me feel a little sick. Pad Thai I think is hit or miss here. My in law got it and he said it tasted terrible. And I had noticed in reviews. Some are good and some are bad. Lunch specials are a good price tho and my food was ok. Four stars mainly Bc the regions and plum sauce. #crack
PpS_33wwVA959bD6nHPlMA: Things are off to great start at Burger Up's newest location. We went on a Friday at 7pm and only waited 20 minutes for a two-top. The space looks like the original Burger Up but with more seating. Our server was friendly, knowledgeable, and attentive. The food came out quickly and was delicious. The bison burger and truffle fries are a dangerously good combo! Several staff members from the original location lend consistency to the brand, but many of the employees are East Nashvillians, which gives Burger Up East a neighborhood feel. I plan on coming back soon!

On the route to another burger place in East Nashville, we passed the newest Burger Up location that opened in July [formerly the Turnip Truck's spot]. The giant outdoor patio with strung lights immediately caught our eye and looked like the perfect location to enjoy the evening temperature dip. The service, food, and ambiance lived up to our expectations and the highlights: 1) we didn't have to wait 1 hr - 1.5hrs for a table and 2) we could enjoy a full service bar! 
TIP: we saw multiple large groups of all ages [inside and outside]

Gorgeous interior, friendly staff and delicious food ! They were extremely busy but they made us feel welcome and important. We had a great interaction with a male manager, he was helpful and very hospitable. Will definitely be back !
wo10FSEvfor857XR_Q_LsA: I stopped at my local Kroger yesterday for some good ole comfort food and saw Dan's Gourmet Mac and Cheese!  I picked up the Krazy Kale and the Dan's Mac Attack.  OH. MY. GOSH! It was sooo yummy - the mac and cheese was super cheesy and saucy and the flavors really popped - it was better than I remembered it.  I still can't believe this is a frozen dish.  I wanted to buy the other varieties there but they were out :-(  Guess I'll have to stop by that section next time to pick up more!

I was shopping at Costco in Ohio awhile back and they were giving out samples and omg. I tried every single one and bought 9 boxes. I was so sad to hear that they wouldn't be in Costco in my town just yet. Wish I had a deep freezer at the time I would have bought many more! So delicious!

A food truck that makes mac & cheese. Does it get much better than that? They drive around blessing the streets of Nashville with magnificent cheesiness. This beauty in this institution is the simplicity of their traditional mac&cheese. Four cheeses, butter and noodles. This isn't just Mac&Cheese, its Mac&Cheese that truly warms your stomach. Granted when I was eating it, it was pretty cold outside. But nonetheless, it warmed my stomach. Great food, adorable truck, definitely recommend it!
TitQg4QZ5OEWd6rsRmdYXA:
Living in NJ just minutes NYC my entire life I know what good pizza is.  And in Nashville there is literally next to nothing.  I've tried at least a dozen or more places and they have all been HORRIBLE.  I get better pizza from the frozen food section at Kroger or Dominos.  But then I tried Obies.  Working late in Midtown/Downtown options are scarce.  I tried their plain slice and it was really good.  Thin and fold-able,  very nice crust, cheese and sauce were on point.  If the plain slice is good I'm down.  The pepperoni is also good,  though they put the pepperoni's UNDER the cheese!  BLASPHEMY! lol but it's still a great slice.  Last night I had the slice that pushed me to make this review.  A meatball slice with some hot sauce on it!  BOOM,  it was great!!  There dough is on point,  I NEVER eat pizza crust typically that isn't covered in sauce and cheese but I eat theirs every time.  My only preference would be that the outer crust be a tad smaller but it's really nit picking.  I have been to Obies at least a dozen times.  The only other pizza place I have been to multiple times other than Obies is Brooklyn Brothers in Franklin, but it is such a ride that I often don't do it.

The owner is a great guy as well and very friendly.  They always have good pizzas and they sell BY THE SLICE!  Nashville doesn't even know what this means lol, I know 5 Points sells slices but their pizza is not as good and way over priced.  For a large pie with 3 toppings and 4 beers our bill before tip was $84.  That is just STUPID!  The pizza was good but not that good.

Parking sucks but they are working on that and sometimes there are annoying kids packing the place.  It's a small shop so I would recommend getting it to go.  They have other menu items but I have not tried them yet. I will soon and I will update my review.

Try Obies,  you won't be disappointed.

I absolutely love this restaurant!! I am a Vanderbilt student and I use it to order Pizza and Calzones and chicken fingers and everything in between all the time.  The deep dish hawiian pizza is TO DIE FOR! And their delivery service is absolutely amazing!  I accidentally ordered my delivery to come to a place all the way across campus and I had to call the delivery driver back after he had already arrived to the wrong place to ask him to come to where I was.  Not only was he happy to do it but he would not accept any additional tip for the service.  Absolutely amazing food (literally melts in your mouth) and great service.  Would recommend to anyone!!

We were craving pizza late night and this was he closest place to us. We walked over not knowing that they closed in 10mins but the person who was working was still very nice and was more than happy to create a fresh pizza for us! We ordered the 4 cheese specialty pizza and it was gone in about 5 minutes!! The crust was so good and they were very gracious with toppings! Thanks Obie's!!
rrrOJ_vYXuQaouyLZROThQ: Our service tonight was spectacular! The management was fabulous and our waitress Lacy was an absolute delight. They made our dining experience much more enjoyable.

We like to eat here at least once a week. We always get the same thing - Sunshine Chicken Salad. Comes with fresh fruit that has a sweet, orange dipping sauce. They are always pretty full but there is hardly ever a very long wait. We also love the cheese fries appetizer. Service is always great and the food comes out quickly too.

We used to eat at this restaurant in Cool Springs before it became a Famous Daves, but we stop in Bowling Green to eat at that one if we are going by.
WOW!  The best freakin bacon cheese burger on the planet!  Good fries too.
v3MpkPhTn13KoPCLsxCeuQ:
The food was good, the place seemed clean and the people were nice enough.
I didn't like having to stand in line to pay with everyone else when I had called the order in. I also think I was overcharged. The menu board said $6.95, but she rang up $7.90. They were busy and I was in a hurry so I didn't hassle it. It's a nice change from the fast food around here, so I will go there again.

This is one of my go-to places in Nashville. I used to work around the corner and came here multiple times a week. Falafel is awesome, the tabbouleh is tastier than everywhere and that white sauce is ridiculous! I almost want to tell people not to come here because I don't want it overrun by tourists!
Unpretentious but very good and freshly prepared Mediterranean type cuisine. No place for a hot date but good for casual lunch.
22fzAJbot9r6iA394ZEKxA: The ambiance is intimate, drinks are outstanding & unique, and the food- wow. (Side note: Their burger is the best burger I have EVER eaten). The service was on point - attentive, knowledgeable, classy, & made good recommendations. Excellent place for a fun gathering with friends or a romantic date night. And the champagne garden is perfect. It's set up well enough to be able to enjoy it for most seasons through out the year I can't wait to go back. Well done, Geist.

My friend and I were celebrating my new job and were treated with a Cesar salad, compliments of the Chef. We appreciated the surprise and loved the flavorful salad! We ordered the burrata appetizer. The inside didn't ooze out as expected, but the flavor was delicious as it sat on top of deliciously seasoned spaghetti squash. My friend and I split the tile fish and the steak. The fish had a nice, clean taste and the farro and sweet potato underneath was a perfect pairing of flavors. Absolutely delicious!! The steak was also extremely delicious prepared to a perfect medium temperature. 
Geist offers Moët by the glass so of course we had to try it! We both loved it and were happy to splurge. We both enjoyed our other cocktails, Pink 75 and Toronto, their version of an old fashioned.
We ordered the key lime pie for dessert. It was so delicious, and the creativity of presentation is an A+!! (Picture included) 
All of the servers were extremely knowledgeable about the food and drinks offered. We thoroughly enjoyed our night and can't wait to return!
DOPE.  Glad to see another newcomer doing things right.  The squid was cooked perfectly.  Let me repeat, could not have been cooked better.  Go check them out and order it all!
ymLl9bWlfHpL-ooWx8ko5w: If im ever in nashville again I will definitely come back. Nice atmosphere,delicious food and coffee.  The spanish scrambled eggs were really good.

Popped in for lunch with two colleagues, one who is on a low carb eating regime and the other is lactose intolerant. Perch has menu options for both and terrific service to go with it.

It's not speedy if you get there after the rush has started because they only have a limited number of crepe making tools, but it's worth it. I was in a lunch mood, so I went with the crepe stuffed with turkey, brie, arugula and dried cranberries. Very tasty, but a good bit of Cholula was needed to bring it up from bland. It was a good sized portion and plenty filling, even for a dude like me.

Coffee was nice and rich. Thoroughly enjoyed.

The omelets looked awesome. And the bacon? Whooooo, boy. Nice looking stuff there.
The Perch in Nashville has perfected the crêpe! They also have free parking, which Is nice given the location. I loved their lunch crepes as I'm not a fan of sweet crepes. #downtown #lunchdate
R6PorOrxyf9sc12mLx97Kw: I've been watching my eating for a while and have had trouble finding a satisfying and not "sad salad" that you so often get at restaurants. Chop Shop salads are huge, satisfying, and perfectly chopped. They remind me of some of the bigger salad chains you'd find in the Northeast. Only reason for four stars is the ambiance of the spot - but it really is made to take and go. Happy to find Chop Shop to combat my feelings against sad salads!

Stopped in for a quick Saturday lunch. Pleasantly pleased. It's in a strip mall near Centennial Park. My wife had the Chop Shop Salad and I had The Head Chef. Both were pretty good with nice portion size.

The atmosphere is a little dull. No music. Just a TV with the football game.

All in all, a good lunch option.

We went here today and had a wonderful lunch. The salad was fresh and the creative choices were many. The staff was polite, clean, helpful and were flashy and skillful with their huge knives! My only complaint was about the bacon...super yuk on the bacon. We took our salads out to the park and enjoyed a great meal. The salads are gigantic! You could easily share with a friend and save some money. The shop itself is nothing to write home about...but then who cares when the park is across the street.
MHHd9GbHDVWEuBSbr0-R8w:
This review requires a two-part preface: first of all, I know it's a semi-chain with locations throughout the eastern U.S., but it isn't ubiquitous enough that it escapes needing a review.  Second, I worked at a Jersey Mike's out in Hendersonville for over a year while in high school.  That being said, the following is a perfectly objective and necessary review.

Of the roughly eight or ten J. Mike's locations in the greater Nashville area, this one is quite possibly my favorite.  I know exactly what it takes to keep a Jersey Mike's clean, and this staff in particular has it under control.  The slicer is always sparkling and pristine, and all of the surfaces in the place are spotless.  They make their subs with as much meat as is allowed by corporate, and the staff has always been quick and friendly (though not exactly talkative) when preparing my food.

The question of Jersey Mikes versus other sub sandwich chains is hardly even a question.  If you've ever had a Jersey Mike's sub, you know just how superior and gargantuan they are in comparison to Subway and the like.  This specific location, however, is one of the best.  

The location is great--it's just up the street from The End, The Exit/In, Gold Rush and several other great spots.  There is a little place outside the restaurant where you can dine (though it isn't exactly a patio), and though it isn't as cheap as a true fast food joint, it's certainly affordable.

These people are fast, friendly & make a delightful sammy. The roast beef is especially tasty or try the cheesesteak if you're in the mood for something hot. If you order a giant, you'll easily have enough for 2 meals.

The best sub shop I've ever been to. The bread is so soft and fresh. The meats are sliced fresh. I recommend the Philly cheesesteak with pepper jack cheese and banana peppers! YUMMMMMMMM!!!!! Awesome, quick service at all Jersey Mike locations.
aJo2RCCULkpQwrEL2V6eAg:
Been coming here since the late 90's it's the best part of visiting the in-laws. 

Pizza is really good thin crust, Fletcher's all the way!

Wings: Don't bother

Beer: Ice Cold 

Service: hit and miss
Best local pizza you will find. It's best to order a large w/ everything. They have a lunch buffet during the week that is pretty good, and an excellent value.

Just stopped by Fletcher's as my friends told me this was a good place for pizza, pasta & sandwiches etc.. I had the lasagna and added a side of meatballs. It was a generous portion and was delicious!  It was a perfect symmetry of noodles to sauce to meat and cheese. It was served with warm buttery Garlic Bread and a side salad. 

Friendly customer service and a clean, casual dinning area. Parking is easy. They had some desserts, but alas, I was too full to try any. 

I can see why the locals eat here. ;) next time I'll try the pizza...
d1ITZ6toMoZ0J1Yt1WMbTg:
Corner Pub's Green Hills location is the original, and I think still the best, though there is a definite difference in the crowd.   This location brings in more "regulars".  It's a lot smaller too, but has a pretty kickin' deck.  

As far as food goes, it's pretty standard bar fare.  I do like the grilled buffalo skewers though--I feel like it's at least a tad healthier than chicken wings!  Also, the hot turkey and cheese sandwich is one of my favorites.  

There isn't any live music at this location, but it's a fun neighborhood place to watch sports, or sit on the patio and have a beer.  Parking is pretty horrendous though--watch for signs at nearby businesses where you might be towed!

This is the best neighborhood/ sports bar in Nashville. Friendly wait staff and great quality bar food. Best chicken fingers, pulled pork, and chili anywhere!! You can count on CP for cold beer and Shannon makes a great Margarita!
The poo poo BBQ platter on Smoking Monday is great!  I make 4 meals out of it. Several very sweet waitresses and bartenders.  Plenty of tv's to watch and a nice covered patio.
RriEN-KB0IceP7lHFiiW8A: Had a fantastic experience here while eating for lunch today. We were sitting next to an elderly couple. The waitress brought them their check and the couple stood up to leave. Noticing the woman was having difficulty with her coat, the waitress helped her put it on, handed her her cane, and then took her arm and helped her to the register, then her car. It's so nice to see people helping others. We've always had great service at this restaurant and today was no exception.
Good quality for an all you can eat so long as you don't come in right before closing. Friendly staff and tasty food. I will definitely return.

This is a great little Asian Buffet.  I have had the privilege to dine at some of the best Asian style restaurants in the US; San Francisco,  New York and Chicago.  This one isn't anything near those but for this area in Tennessee it's very good.  I will definitely come back again.
0du-PwMeyRIOrW3MKVeHiQ: Would have gave it 5 stars but didn't really get to try the food. Staff was great and couldn't have ask for any better treatment. Place been open 2 months and the bartender said that live acts should start next month. Over a nice bar/restaurant for adults to socialize. Will repeat next time I'm through Nashville.

Besides the hotel itself... A girlfriend and I were looking for a cheap getaway just weeks in advance and the bar in the hotel surely got us to book at the motel 6. The saloon is awesome hands down and meeting John and the staff was a pleasure. John surely knows how to host a good time! Being in the industry myself it's far and few between you see someone bouncing around and as dedicated in running a place as him. I'd totally like to make an appearance every time I'm in Nashville!

The wait staff are great, the food is great. It's a laid back place, easy environment. When we pulled into Nashville the hotel staff recommended Jack's barbecue (which was also great), but it's definitely not the only show in town. I would definitely recommend stopping here especially if you're staying off of Trinity Road area. John is the bartender and very welcoming, attentive and entertaining. Live music is , could improve.
OY2_L2zFgBfOTCQFP7FXrg: Ok just had the build your own burger with cheese plus bacon and it was great!!! I licked fries for my side topped it off with a Root beer. The place is small I found a seat outside and watched everything popping off during Summer FEst. Over all nice burgers just!

Delicious and a good value for the money.  All burgers are double burgers.  You get your choice of four toppings.  I had the turkey burger on wheat with cheddar, pickles, bacon, guacamole and grilled onions.  I also asked for a side of BBQ sauce.  The burger was seasoned well and cooked perfectly.  If you eat turkey burgers regularly, you know that some can be dry and lifeless.  This one was fantastic.  Four of my colleagues ordered as well, everyone enjoyed their selection.

I don't think you can go wrong with this place...it is nestled in the Delta Portica of the Gaylord Resort...there are different restaurants in various places at the resort...firstly the scenery was so nice as there is an indoor river where you can get a boat ride and watch people sail on as you eat...

You basically build your own burger and they have the traditional beef burgers but also offer turkey burgers and veggie burgers...you complete a card of the kind of burger you want and your toppings and sides...I got the turkey burger with fries...your burger basically comes naked (unless you order toppings) and you get the condiments from the condiments stand...my burger was juicy and filling...the fries are regular thick fries...all together it is a basic burger joint that will fill you up if you don't feel like venturing out to Nashville for a meal you can eat right at the resort...
7uDSvZ7cM8uEedrcAwdnRQ: I stop here on my way to work at least twice weekly for breakfast and it's fantastic! Great hospitality along with the food. The omelets are delicious and prepared just right as well as the rest of my order. The chef definitely knows how to cook and his professional care into each plate served is shown. I shall continue to coming! Great job, Mallards Family Restaurant staff you make it good. Thanks.

Stopped a Mallard's Family Restaurant for lunch after visiting Country Stars graves.  I ordered a cheeseburger and sides and it was a fine cheeseburger. Fresh, tasty, perfectly cooked, great service and the place is a place from where you find food places in a small town.  My Dad would loved this place. I'll be back.

I grew up in a small town in West Tennessee. I was probably 15 before I ate at a non fast food that wasn't a meat and three southern diner. For that reason, most of my adult life I've avoided them, however seeking some remembrance of my youth I've been dabbling a bit. Unlike some of the downtown more famous southern restaurants, this place is AUTHENTIC. It's decorated in a mish mash of blue collar decor and country music singer photos. If I didn't know better I'd think I was back home. Staff is more than friendly as one would expect, as is the menu. I had the catfish plate with pasta salad and turnip greens. The catfish came out hot and fresh, cooked correctly, but the portions weren't gigantic. The greens were quite above mediocre. I fully recommend them. Pasta salad was interesting, not outstanding but they didn't make the mistake of making it "mushy family reunion style". If you're in my hood, which is growing with East Nashville spillover and like a good, no bs, authentic Meat and 3, head to Mallards.
WtH2z1TlxLgh5mZrGQnkuw: So, we pull up to M.L. Rose and my husband says, "oh great, another snooty, boring, look at me I drink craft beer, kind of place!"  I started to giggle as he's so negative all the time as he tells me young people have ruined beer!  He says this as he doesn't want to spend $7 on a snifter of IPA, but I digress.  We walk in and he's pleasantly surprised not only by the decor and clientele, but the beer selection and menu.  Great bartenders, older crowd, lots of beer to choose from and great menu (small but most things you'd want on there).  He tried a few new beers he hadn't had before, like Elvis Juice and Wicked Weed Pernicious, while I stuck to my vodka drinks.  It's so nice going into a craft beer restaurant where they have a full bar.  Plenty of TVs to watch and even an outdoor patio/bar that's dog friendly.   I bet Titans football is going to be crazy there with the garage doors open!  The only food we had was a quesadilla as we were going to a late dinner and didn't want to pig out, but it was deliciously simple and very filling.  I saw the burgers coming out of the kitchen and extra would be a good word for how they looked!  I hadn't been to any of the ML Rose's before, but I certainly will try others after this experience!

Stumbled upon this place while shopping at Marathon Village. Craving burgers and we were not disappointed.  AmaZing burgers, perfect waffle fries and a great selection of  beers. From the hostess at the door to Paige at the bar we were treated like welcome guest.  We will be back.
Wow!!!! Atmosphere is bananas and the waitress was very attentive. I mean she knew everything we need.  Liz is her name and she is awesome. You won't be disappointed.
ulKUOaPahi2rUhGQYyYY4g: First time Bojangles customer. Friend brought me for the Cajun buscuit. We got there ten minutes til close and received wonderful customer service and the food was hot and very good. Definitely impressed!
i just love there cajun chicken sandwich / first time we ever ate at bojangles /there not around where i live / however when ever we down that way we will be eating there / wish you were in kc

This Bojangles is surprisingly excellent!  I'm not a big fan of fast food, but the food that I've gotten here has always been hot and delicious.  There's always a couple minute wait at the drive thru (which I consider a good thing!) but the food comes out steaming and fresh. 

Everyone that works there is friendly and goes out of their way to make sure that you have a great experience.  Whether you're eating inside, or going through the drive thru, I would definitely recommend it for fast, delicious food!
PFcLisniXcud3ck711Smyw:
So I saw this truck and was like, what? Of course the owner is named Jon and he's from Slidell, LA. But I didn't head to Nashville to get jambalaya. 

My friend got the chicken and waffles (half mild & half spicy). I snitched a bit of waffle and it was really tasty. She knew from experience that all spicy was too much for her and the half and half was just perfect.

The items are made to order so it can take a bit of time. But definitely a truck with an inventive menu.

Awesome!!!!!!!  By far the best food truck in at Main Event Parking Food Truck Friday nights.  The Best Red beans & rice Eva and I'm super fussy. Why? Cos I make my own red beans and rice and know what it takes to make these great. Jonbalaya nailed it! Perfectly cooked red beans not too squishy and the rice was cooked to perfection too - not clumpy, soggy or gluggy. Then the OMG the stew flavour so awesome dripping all through the rice. The spices were subtle and not overpowering Then to top it all off Andouille sausage slices. WOW!  I'll be back for more of this. My hubby (fussier than me) had the shrimp and grits and we swapped tastes. His shrimp and grits was so fresh and yummy.  Flavour burst-arama!  But even though it tasted amazing I'm still glad I had the red beans and rice. Jon told me he was originally from Lousiana - so that makes sense why this food is so authentic. Yay Jon and family. For your wonderfully authentic super fresh and masterful food. Hope to see your food truck again cos I'll be heading straight for it.

Great Jambalaya!

Jonbalaya food truck was at Elmington Park off West End on the Saturday before Fat Tuesday or otherwise known as Mardi Gras.  I ordered the jambalaya in the spirit of the week.  It was very good.  The rice was cooked to perfection and the meat was tender.  It is hard to believe a truck can cook such wonderful cajun food.  This is on my list of food trucks that I must seek out in the future.
0loG1bKgcg8zdC39934GCg: The staff is not the most friendly but I love the dumplings and crab Rangoon so much that I deal with it. Seriously the best crab Rangoon around.

This place deserves more stars. I was visiting Nashville for work and was way too tired to go out for dinner. I got delivery from here and it was really very good. It was comparable to delivery I get in San Francisco, lots of veggies and very fresh.

They deliver to my house and work very often. The food has never been disapointing. The steamed dumplings are excellent. I have had the pork Lo mein, most of the shrimp dishes, soups and the crab Rangoon. The only thing bad I have to say about them is one time they put msg on my Chinese donuts instead of sugar! It was horrid, but they brought ms more within ten minutes.
DFD3cHf4OeORHuEUlSaIAg:
Pacifica is probably my favorite downtown lunch spot.  It's always really busy, especially between 11:30 and 1:00pm, but the line moves quickly.  

I'm a big fan of the Thai Basil, and the pot stickers are a divine appetizer.  I wouldn't suggest the sushi though--they only have four rolls to choose from, and they are not that good.  If you have a craving for sushi, go elsewhere!  Oh and the Pad Thai and the chicken fried rice are a tasty choice too!  

You can really go wrong here--choices to please everyone that likes Asian food, and it's quick and inexpensive.  I do really miss it!

I am a pad thai freak and Pacifica helps me get my freak on...during my lunch break!  It's so nice to have a tasty sit down Asian place in the downtown area.  Like most places in the downtown district, the service is nice and fast! It is pretty nice inside, but nothing too special.

Pacifica is an Asian restaurant with many types of Asian cuisine ranging from Thai to Chinese.  It is a bit more expensive than some other places downtown, but the ingredients seem fresh, so it's worth it!

The pad thai was exceptional!
Food was tasty!! And the staff was so nice! I would definitely return here, and if I lived closer, I would eat here much more often.
AHjwEsmD0zeK6ie-QtjXAA: Deliciously prepped and grilled Argentinian cuisine with an equally beautiful plated presentation. The variety of sauces served with the meals are perfectly matched to enhance the flavors of the accompanies meats. Sides are all delicious. I've had the sirloin, chicken kebab, potatoes, grilled avocado, and grilled vegetables. Haven't picked one item that didn't deserve 5 stars. Go try!

This restaurant is inside of Plaza Mariachi.  You just walk up the stand, and place your order.  They get your phone number, and will give you a text when your food is ready.  They cook your food right on the flame (flame grilled) where you can see it.  It is Argentine food.  I tried their ribeye and I'm just one of those people that eat Well done meat.. it was pretty good Argentine steak.  For being well done, it was so tender.  The Mac n queso was pretty ok.

This place is amazing.  We have had steak, chicken and shrimp and all were delicious.  The ribeye for $22.99 is enough for two persons and is the best buy for steak in Nashville.  The fried potatoes are also wonderful.
C6QYjAlwZ8KFx0rV7Qp53g: Great concept, loved my drink. Try the banana old fashioned, I can't remember the name. Only critiques would be the lighting is too bright inside the bar, and music just a smidge too loud. Will definitely be back!

Minerva Avenue is a super chills spot.  It is unique among Nashville's speakeasy scene.  You got all the exclusivity without the pretentious feels.  The drink are legit, generous pours and made with all fresh ingredients.  Top shelf and classy all the way around.  You need to drink here.  #HomeIsWhereTheBarIs

Had dinner last night, the sandwich I had was excellent the service was exceptional, JoJo my server was wonderful and on the ball.  I met the owner Robert even had a drink with him.  The place was warm and inviting with a fun speakeasy type ambiance.  You even have to knock on a big wooden door to get in.
0riuM7MffnNxwRGqeNvdOg: Good service and great burgers. Their food is greasy, but it's a yummy treat every now and then. They have a cool electronic mix a flavor soda machine that's pretty fun, and there's never a long wait even on weekends.

I used to go to Five Guys all the time when I lived in D.C. and then kind of forgot about them. This is why I was super excited when I saw that a Five Guys had opened up near my apartment! I love going here from time to time. It is a typical experience. 

I have always appreciated the customer service at Five Guys. Coming from California, they remind me of the excellent service at In-N-Out. 

These guys are always willing to have your order made according to specifications and that's why I appreciate their time and effort. 

TIP: Never order anything larger than a small fries because you get SO many! My boyfriend and I share a small and still can't go through it all (even though we LOVE their fries).

I come here somewhat regularly. The workers are very nice and polite and the food is superb. I always get the little bacon cheeseburger and the Cajun fries. They will give you Cajun seasoning on the side too if you'd like to try it without totally committing. They give you a TON of fries...a scoop or so more than fits in the cup so that's why I generally get the little size. I highly recommend Five Guys! The burgers and fries are amazing and by far the best of the "fast food" burgers. I wouldn't necessarily classify them as fast food but for what it is, it's the best. Hands down.
Yh1c1N5B6due79hEZ4Jy3A: Super cool laid back vibe, awesome staff making great cocktails and even better dj on friday night spinning old and new grooves. Oddly enough also a tremendous soccer bar for early morning games, well worth a visit for 10 minutes or 10 hours......
Love this place! The perfect spot to go for a conversation with great friends. Warm lighting, great drinks and no TVs!

Went here last weekend and had a hard time finding it, but once I noticed others walk in the front door and saw what looked like a bar just inside, I followed suit, and was pleasantly surprised. This looks like Nashville's take on a LES cocktail lounge. It breathes Nashville with an influence of other cities, but seems like it belongs here. It feels less forced than Pinewood Social or the Patterson House, but I've always felt more comfortable in smaller bars. I really enjoyed my time here. The drinks were lovey as well.
HoDYyczaBBJ3VmIb3qwn3g: Literally every time I go here, with every person I have encountered, they have always been so friendly and happy to assist.  It really leaves an impression.  Thank you for the great service! :)

It takes a lot from a fast food joint to persuade me to review it, and this Taco Bell has successfully done just that. 

Remodeled with a brand new interior, this place found my wife and I during a moment of hunger-induced weakness after a 10 mile run. It all cancels out, right?

The inside was spotless, the staff uncharacteristically friendly, and the food simply.... Good. 

Maybe it was the endorphins, but I really enjoyed my meal here.

I have quite literally been to hundreds of Taco Bells across the country and this location is standout to me. I have always received the kindest service here...evidence that it is definitely under great management!
9ny4OGRDyQCa5woBmxj6iA:
PHOgettaboudit!!!
This is THE place for Pho! 
I've eaten up the hill, down the hill and around the corner and these people do it better! 
I always get the Shrimp Pho and spring rolls with no pork! 
The pho is perfect by itself but I always add about 2 quarter sizes of sriracha and the same amount of hoisin sauce and a little spoon of that awesome but lethal chilli sauce! Mix it up well with their basil and jalapeños which are always bursting with freshness! 
Enjoy a Made from scratch, made with love soup! 
 I'm not sure why anyone would say they didn't like this place unless they simply do not appreciate  Authentic Vietnamese. I'm there at least one to two times a week and it's always consistently great! 
Man, I almost forgot to mention their Bahmi was Bangin'!
Get the Thai tea as a dessert :)

This place was a calm quiet oasis for a rough Saturday morning. We split a bahn mi, and honestly, it wasn't that great. But that didn't matter, it was not my mission objective when I walked in the door. That was Pho. The king of all soups, a magical delicious healing elixir, and on this they did not fail.

I got the supper bowl, which is a combination of everything. Meatballs, raw beef, tripe, etc. It was great. The beef might have been the best in a pho I've ever had. It was shaved and tender and cooked slowly as it swam in goodness. 

Since they delivered on the Pho, solid 4 stars. The place is very clean and our server couldn't have been nicer. AND they left the top of the straw wrapper on, I like that. 

Go here and walk out feeling better about the world.

I've eaten at every Vietnamese restaurant that serves Pho in 10 square miles of downtown Nashville, and this is the best. However, it is slightly more expensive than other Vietnamese restaurants, but they take debit and credit cards, which is a convenience. Great Pho. Great Spring Rolls. Get the supper bowl for the full experience.
l0DgOgLJDoUFHUYjw1uhTw:
Out of all the chain-based fast food joints around Nashville, Hardee's is one of the better ones and this Hardee's in particular is always top-notch. The staff here have always been friendly, speedy and helpful and the restaurant itself has been spotless for every visit (often a rarity in the fast-food world).

I'm usually headed here on Sunday mornings for a bacon, egg and cheese biscuit (no other place on earth compares), but their lunch and dinner fare are also worth the trip. On the most recent visit, my partner had their turkey burger, and I have to say it was remarkably good. I've been going with the Memphis BBQ burger (burger topped with pulled pork, bacon, onion strings and cheese), which never disappoints. I don't know if their burgers are grilled fresh or frozen, but the resulting taste is pretty dang good. They offer regular fries, curly fries and beer-battered onion rings, so there's a good selection of starchy sides to choose from for all tastes.

Can't give a fast-food chain a 5-star, just can't...
But would like to.  We had breakfast here, twice in two days.  Fried bologna, egg, velveeta on biscuits...DAMMMMM that's good!  Friendly people, clean restaurant, good coffee.  I can't speak for lunch/dinner stuff, but this is IT for fast cheap tasty breakfast.

I've never rated a fast food restaurant before but agree with the other viewers that this one is one of the best chain fast food joints around.  The food came out hot, quick, and with a smile.  If only McDonald's could hit on all three.  Thumbs up on the new self order kiosk that gives you 10% off for ordering with the touch screen.
G8qUUwXPhmQABBIbpbV47A: Excellent food. Very nice and clean. Atmosphere is great and so is the service. Would def recommend trying it! Prepare to pay $17 for a grilled cheese but it's an excellent grilled cheese so...

I took my mother here for brunch on a Sunday afternoon. The wait was only about 10 minutes. And it's lovely inside. I had an iced chai latte that was absolutely delicious. I went specifically to try the lobster roll. I had heard good things about it. And while tasty, it's not as good as the lobster roll on the menu for lunch at Bricktop's. My mother had a smoked truffle grilled cheese sandwich with fries. It was tasty. But perhaps not worth the price. I will say that the fries are delicious. And delivered and plentiful portions. We finished with a great looking banana split. It was fun to play with it and put it all together, but there was nothing earth shattering about the taste of the ice cream. The ambience is beautiful, and I would certainly eat here again. But it didn't wow me.

If you want a relaxing atmosphere after a long day, come to eat dinner or just have a drink at RH cafe. They are next door to Panera bread in the Green Hills Mall. Service is impeccable, the atmosphere is superb and who knows you just might find a piece of furniture for your new house or a lighting that you fell in love with. This place will pack lots of pleasant memories in you.
JXucCTkmYXql9C8HGPbG4Q: I must say, this place got off to a rocky start.... when Koi first opened, service was slow & food was mediocre but not anymore! Now, my boyfriend and I are regulars, dining in at least once every two weeks.  The food is awesome and the service is equally as great. As a bit of a sushi snob, I am happy this place exists as it's one of the only spots in Nashville and definitely the only spot on the Eastside with fresh uni (the sushi chef said it gets shipped in every Thursday). The prices are reasonable, the selection is vast and the sake is good. Give it a try!!!

Updating my review because we wanted sushi and decided to give this place another shot. I had red curry tofu for lunch from the 8th Ave location which was awesome, but this review is solely for the Main Street location. We got 6 different types of sushi to go tonight and every single one was really good. We tried the dragon, spicy dragon, Fuji, red devil, inari, and spicy crawfish rolls. We sat at the bar and drank draft beer - a super extensive draft beer selection - while we waited and the bartender was nice enough to let us try a sample of beers we were unsure of ordering a pint of. Friendly bartender, great beer selection, good sushi. We will be back for sure!

I was visiting for the week from St. Pete Fl. i admit it was a bit wait in line, i think people are just anxious to try it. I believe it's because their food is awesome. Yummy rolls and awesome bar. It's worth the wait. My favorite is eating sushi called the sunflower and some of my moms yummy soy sauce noodles =D.
YpUiKahyrFpc8MuRrT7Y6Q: I have been going to this take-out place for years, and it never fails to disappoint.  I have only received excellent service from the staff. Even though I no longer live in Nashville, I still crave their amazing Sesame Chicken!

Evergreen Express is a great little place for Chinese take-out, tucked into one of the strip malls at Nipper's Corner.  The fare is simple but tasty, always seems fresh, and quite inexpensive.  

I've ordered from them a half-dozen times and can recommend the Assorted Vegetables (delicious flavors), Orange Chicken (a spicy, tangy sauce with strips of blackened orange peel), and Sweet and Sour Chicken (just breaded chicken in a sweet and sour sauce; no vegetables).  Both the Egg Rolls and Spring Rolls are very good here.

Good food/great price & very friendly owners/staff.
They serve lunch/dinner size portions & through the week you can order either size day or night.  My roommate & I will order the dinner size on weekends & it's plenty of food to split between the 2 of us.  My fav dish is the teriyaki chicken w/steamed rice & hot/sour soup.  The spring rolls are good too.
L4POqDySd7lSnLPd8mmpiw: This place never disappoints. I might be lucky but every time I go the workers are very nice! I always make sure to check my bag before leaving but if it is wrong they are always very nice and apologetic and usually if it's wrong they fix it and let you keep the food they accidentally gave you! So hey, that's a win win. It's hard to come by good customer service in fast food, especially when it comes to them messing up orders, but this place rocks at it. They take fault for messing it up and fix it in no time! Very happy I live close to it because it's the best in the business :)

Admittedly I've grown up with White Castles and have to pick up some sliders when able.  These may not be everyone's favorite but if they are this place was great.  Service was friendly and funny while we waited for our order.  The burgers were hot and fresh and delish.
The only problem with White Castle is there is not enough of them. This location provides good service in a clean environment.
fkQQ8obAMODd38wbl1vcQA: The concept of this place is pretty interesting: a restaurant and a boutique combined into one.  Intriguing.  So me and a girlfriend decided to check it out, which seems fitting as upon entering, my first observation, aside from the lovely black and white space that reminds me so much of something on Sex and the City, was "wow, there are no dudes in here except the ones behind the bar." Ladies, this place was born for your girls nights out.  Admittedly I am  not a cocktail girl, preferring classic drinks as opposed to girlie ones, but I must say both  my girlie drinks, the Gidget and the Pillow Talk, were fabulous.  Our meal was delicious too.  I highly recommend the mahi mahi tacos.  We then proceeded to finish off our great meal with a tour of the little boutique and, despite our best intentions, walked away with a beautiful necklace each.  The service was excellent, the meal and drinks delicious, and the vibe very fun and trendy.  Like I said, ladies leave your men at home.  This is a great place to gather with girlfriends.

Swanky and cool!  Very LAish.  The Kocktails are carefully crafted with a large selection of unique appetizers which are off-the-charts fun and delicious.  The interior design, decor, and artwork creates a solid cosmopolitan vibe with a great selection of sexy and classy Kouture.  I was very impressed and will back for a third visit this week!  Nashville/The Gulch so needed a place like this.

This place is exactly what Nashville needed!   What great Food, Awesome Cocktails, and a Hip Atmosphere!    It just opened up in the Gulch and I'm sure it's only a matter of time before it is 'The Spot!'
JgSOSivikoK_jn5l8KJ9Sw:
I dragged my husband here because I know that someday, I am going to want to come here and regret not ever coming here when we were living in the neighborhood.

I wasn't sure if this place was going to be busy enough to require reservations; I made them, anyway. It was a Saturday night and by the time we were seated, the tables immediately surrounding us were getting filled.

I remember a few years back when Outback's Bloomin' Onion was in the news for having around 2,000 calories. I also remember how I ate a good half of one shared with friends a decade or so ago. This time, I passed on it, living on my fond memories of having had the chance to try it when I used to be able to burn the calories faster.

I had the 1/3 baby back ribs and grilled chicken. Husband had the Norwegian salmon. 

I liked the ribs, but didn't think the meat was falling-off-of-the-bones tender. The BBQ sauce was hardly there; while I would have liked to have my ribs drenching with the stuff, I was glad that there was enough for me to enjoy without regretting it later.

The fries were okay. I didn't finish them, which meant that there were enough limp fries in there for me to pass over.

Husband thought his salmon was a bit on the salty side. He usually gives me the salmon skin, but this didn't have any. I deliberately ordered the 1/3 ribs (when I would normally order the 1/2 or even full rack of ribs) just so that i can give him the grilled chicken in case he didn't get enough fish. I don't know what he thought of the chicken. He did say it was seasoned in some fashion. It didn't have skin (I love chicken skin), so I was glad I didn't have any.

Craving cheesecake, so I had that with the raspberry sauce. I hardly ever meet a dessert I don't finish and I finished this one.

Husband knows advanced calculus, but for some reason, undertipped the friendly server by accident. But my husband is European, so tipping is usually my domain. I have to go in there one of these days and throw a few extra bucks their way on my next visit to make up for this oversight. As it is, I deliberately walk on the other side of the street in the off chance that the waiter will remember and come out and berate me.

I love Outback Steakhouse. I worked here when I was in college as a hostess and then a server. So the fact that I still love it 15 years later is impressive! 

I've always been in love with their ranch dressing. They make everything fresh in the morning, that is why they aren't open for lunch (except Sunday's). The steak is pretty good, too, and the seasoning they use is super tasty. 

Most of all, I appreciate that Outback Steakhouse has a gluten free menu! It's one of the first places I ate out when I was first diagnosed with Celiac disease. I can have a lot of things here, including the Wallaby Darned adult drink :) and the Thunder from Down Under dessert! It's a warm, flourless brownie topped with vanilla ice cream, hot chocolate sauce and chocolate shavings. So good! 

The parking at this location is a bit challenging, but that's the only downside.

The service was Great, the Porterhouse was just OK ! 

Parking was very tough, I had to circle the block 3 times and found no street parking available before I found their underground parking garage that was also full and the restaurant was not that busy. 

What will they do if a crowd shows up?
KBenyZTF_BQCI2n6Khgutw: Great fish and chips, supreme pizza was perfect and chicken tenders were also very good.  Will return again

Just had dinner before a movie. David was our server. He was quick and efficient. He made sure to offer suggestions and engage with us without being over bearing. He waited until we had truly gotten into our meal to check back on us which was awesome because most server rush back before you have a chance to start your meal. The food was awesome (and timely) and the drinks were good. My boyfriend's drink never got below half way without a refill. Kodos!!! This is by far one of the best sports bars we have been to and that says a lot. We are big sports fans so we go to a lot of sports bars.
I work at the mall and come here a lot for lunch! Anna is so great! The guacamole is one of my faves
VGEDy6y-ebsS5MaAYty1Sw: They serve the best egg crispy chicken noodles in Tennessee but one thing about that!!   Its not on the menu folks; u have to know what you are looking for and once u do you will be amazed.   Decor is ok but its from the previous owners that operated at this location but so far they have been serving great vietnamese dishes besides egg crispy noodles.

Food is great; atmosphere not so great.  I was the only diner in the place for lunch though. The staff is super friendly.  It is located in a strip mall off Richard Rd in Antioch. I also had the crispy noodle dish which was awesome. I will definitely go back.

I thought the food was good, although not quite 5 stars in my book.  Being from southeast Asia, I'm probably more critical of Asian food than my Nashville friends.  Anyhow, the eggrolls were tiny, although better than average.  And, the sauce was a little thicker and less vinegarly than what I prefer.  I like to wrap lettuce around my eggrolls, but I only got one piece of lettuce.  My meatball pho was a bit too salty.  I tried some of my friend's chicken with crisy egg noodles and it was definitely delicious, so I may have to order that next time.  Overall, a very good restaurant and I certainly will go back.
gDpUnOqTZClHHLp2UXqdig: Visiting from out of town, ordered for delivery to hotel - really - really  fast delivery!  GREAT pizzas and calzone!  Good pizza crust flavor!
Love, love, love the grilled chichen pizza. They're always quick with my orders. And really nice  !!!

Ordered through Inndelivery.com when i was staying at the red roof inn. It was delicious. Toppings were fresh, sauce is amazing and the crust is perfect both crispy and chewy. Try it and you won't regret it.
C6QHnVF4STwlRbxiq3lgjQ: Just ate here. We had great food And the service was awesome!! Everyone was so nice!! It's been a long time since I've seen food workers enjoying their job. Wish all food places had such sweet workers. The food was perfect! I love that I can make it with the stuff I like to eat. Will definitely be back!

This Qdoba location is highly visible from West End and is situated in facing both West End and 21st.  It is kind of a weird angle for a building to be built, but hey, I guess it helps to catch your eye!

I consider myself to be a Mexican food aficionado, hence why I have reviewed so many Mexican places.  I would consider Qdoba to be more of a fast food style Mexican place, than an actual restaurant.  

I have only had the burritos here and they are pretty tasty!  The salsa they use is super zesty, I think it compliments the burrito well.

Always fast always friendly. Today I tried some of the new knockout tacos they offer. Got the bohemian veg loaded with cheese, black beans, corn, verde salsa, fresh cilantro, guac and topped with cojita cheese. Delicious!
ZzKuqTj_fjlStijadL5noQ:
A SAD DAY !

Word on the Street is; Fiddle Cakes Both Locations Have Closed.

A sad day for those with a Sweet Tooth and Food Allergies, Please tell me I am misinformed or when & where you open your new location.

I am thankful to hear that you still have a thriving catering business. 

We Love Fiddle Cakes !

I had a two-week training in downtown Nashville so I was here a couple of times during my stay. Kudos to them for vegan options! 

For lunch I had the curry tofu sandwich (tofu, veganaise, carrots, red onions, craisens, celery, curry, and lettuce). All I have to say is: NOM NOM NOM!! It was delicious. I also had a coffee freeze made with soy milk, and while it was good, I could tell that the soy milk they were using had a lot of added sugar in it. I felt like I was drinking a milkshake!

I also went here for "breakfast" one morning. I put breakfast in quotations because they did not have any vegan muffins available when I went, so I ended up getting a soy latte and a vegan cookie. Don't judge. The cookie was a chocolate pecan combination and IT WAS AMAZING! It was one of the best cookies I have ever had, let alone a vegan cookie. Yummy.

It's probably a good thing that I don't live near here because I could easily gain 5 pounds eating their food. It's delicious!

Holy ****.

If today was any indication of the food this new location is going to be serving up, I've just become a FiddleCakes fan.  In honor of their grand opening, they offered up trays and trays of free samplings from their menu!!  I'm talkin' tomato and goat cheese pizza, vegan pizza, gluten-free pizza, bacon and cheddar scones, gluten-free cranberry bar-things, cookies and brownies.  Now, it had been sitting out for a bit so it was all a bit cold and not hot out of the oven.  However, it was all great.  I'm getting the feeling FiddleCakes specializes in special restriction baking, with so many gluten-free and vegan options, which certainly don't go unappreciated.  

I'd really like to sample all the things I DIDN'T taste today, like the coffees, the frozen coffee drinks, the cupcakes.. Lord.  If I run to Fiddlecakes, eat, and walk back, I'll have burnt enough calories in order to really have a feast at Fiddlecakes and not pay for it on the scale.

And I've got to say.. thank you FiddleCakes for giving out all the free stuff today.  It's very inviting when a business gives out tastes of what they offer for no charge, and now I'm more inclined to go back and buy something!
Oo9jD4_U2lXJR7J98ixwUw:
We had a wonderful multi course meal at Deacons last evening.  After a terrific appetizer course we shared an aged porterhouse and also a huge rib eye and along with a couple of terrific sides we still brought food home.  
The wait staff was over the top attentive and made sure we lacked for nothing. The location is good, close to the best hotels and the broadway strip is just far enough away

First time at this hip new restaurant, and already looking forward to a return visit! Highly, highly recommend the lobster chowder. Also totally LOVED the house roasted chicken with collard greens - who knew something so seemingly simple could be so incredibly complex and over-the-top delicious? Huge portions, good prices and hip ambiance. A definite 5 stars.
Great service by Scott and Rihanna (spelled wrong probably). Food was amazing and great atmosphere.
tR7wVyDTtjV2LJSlxrMglQ: An extra star because the fried doughnuts have become a fave for potlucks and seriously good, just like we make back home drenched in sugar not a glaze in sight, inject them with your favourite liquor for a special treat...or just jelly

I love going to this place because they have a drive through... If you go up to the drive through and the get owner, he says "SUPPER READY!' Then you must ask if the egg rolls are good or anything is good for that matter and he says 'THE BEST IN TOWN" I don't know why but it cracks me up every time. 

Everything is under $6 on the menu including dinner portions and the the egg rolls are indeed fine as well as the egg drop soup. i mean come on this is cheap Chinese, it is what it is. They are quick and consistent. The sesame chicken is good. My husband get's the General Tso..

Food is ok for the price and a decent meal. Kind of a run down place that looks like it hasn't changed since the 80's. 4 stars for not putting any condiments on the tables inside. I don't see how this place has operated for so many years without putting at least a damn bottle of soy sauce and napkins on each table inside the restaurant.
pP5-CEsXKxjzm00HIpwjTw:
Tin is right. This place rocks. It doesn't look like much, but the tacos are awesome.

Each taco is 99 cents and is very small (on ~3-4" corn tortillas), so you'll want to order a few. My usual favorites are also my favorites here - pastor and carne asada. The barbacoa was okay, but not as good as the others.

You also get some pickled carrots and fresh radishes on the side which is such a nice compliment to the food. Definitely will be back - and will hunt down the other locations!

If your definition of amazing Mexican food is a pile of beef, beans and cheese, like so many Mexican restaurants around Nashville, this is not the place for you.  However if you have an appreciation for authentic Mexican flavors, this is the place to be.  I ate here for the first time today and came away impressed, especially for the price.  As others have described the meats have great flavors, and simple toppings of onion, cilantro, carrots, radishes and lime provide authenticity and great flavor.

I'm giving a four star rating because nothing truly wowed me.  But I definitely plan to go back and try more items and different meats, so maybe I will find that wow item!

I LOVE THIS PLACE!
Amazing authentic delicious Mexican food! 
The employees are always happy to serve you and they always provide fast service.
I could eat here everyday! The menu is simple and easy to order. I love their green and red sauce. 
Don't be skeptical because its a food truck, These people know how to cook and their health scores are always high and posted on the truck!
And yea every order comes with pickled carrots, limes and fresh radishes.
You can also call to place your order ahead so you don't have to  wait there long! 
615-290-7840
1A7BfewtAi27Tln39Tlgfw:
Excellent hot dogs, a new addition. Got a Chicago and a picnic - sweet onion relish was awesome.
Many tennessee breweries represented. Friendly staff, fun environment.
Excellent service. Had a beer I didn't like and the owner switched it out for me. Great beer and selection. Love it. Recommend for the best local beers in Nashville!!!

Most Sundays post-church, you will find me eating Swagruha at this lovely joint! I am so glad the Farmer's Market has such a cool place to have a beer, enjoy a flight, or fill a growler. The beer is fresh and the selection is always diverse and tasty. The beer-tenders are very friendly and helpful with those who aren't always the most decisive, like myself. I always look forward to my Sunday routine at The Picnic Tap!
t-poH7FNN9uKAheezIxpFw:
I can't vouch for lunch, but I couldn't pass up the chicken biscuit sandwich when I saw it on the breakfast menu. 

Despite the cheese being a Kraft singles and the lady having to strain my eggs because they were so watery... (gross)...

The biscuit was everything you can ask for- buttery, flaky, gently crispy on the outside. The chicken tender was tender and juicy. The eggs were not spongy, and by the time I opened it up, the cheese was melted perfectly.

What a blissful last meal in Nashville.

My friends and I visited Nashville this past weekend and unfortunately never made it to Swett's actual storefront in the city. We were in search of lunch before our flight home today and discovered that Swett's had an outpost in the airport, so we finally got our barbeque. I got a pulled pork sandwich with their spicy BBQ sauce and sides of mac-and-cheese and turnip greens. The pork was very good and I loved the BBQ sauce. The mac-and-cheese was great, too. The turnip greens were also good, but needed salt. The service was fast and friendly. All-in-all, this is excellent food for an airport and quite good in general.
Always wonderful. amazing food, down home recipes, great prices! They feed you like your momma would!
5kjTHnn86cELzmuaXkfY3g: The young kid warming up pizza was very nice and helpful.

Nice size and great pasta! Perfect location north of Broadway, near Printers Alley, as well as proximity to BB Kings/Wildhorse/Brazilian Steakhouse. The service is rock solid. Some tables aren't too clean though.

The Sbarro on Commerce is a place that I've visited a few times when I've been in Nashville for CMA Fest (the big annual country music festival).  It's a quick, easy, and inexpensive way to get good pizza while you're out and about on 2nd Avenue taking in the fun activities that downtown Nashville always has to offer.  The staff has always been friendly and the pizza is always good.  The other reviews I've noticed on this website are way too harsh.  This is not a 5-star restaurant - Sbarro has good pizza and this location couldn't be any better for those who frequent downtown Nashville and need a quick meal.
lW7LfqJI9r4FpKvxb7Ec9g: Saving grace after a downtown area that beat me over the head with country music. It was a decent size, with standard beer prices, a stage, dance floor, and lot's of top 40! Try it out
Enjoy going to hear bands but really enjoy the dancing when the dj comes in on Saturday nights.

This is where On The Rocks used to be, but they have completely re-done it and I love it...you have two sections...one is laid back and chill with food and comfortable seating and then on the other side is where you dance and go a little crazier. It is a great fit for Demonbreun and those that are looking for something else to do other than the Tin Roof and D. Mguiness. They are securing live band venues with popular musicians and have intergrated technology very well...

For example, you can walk into the club and you will be able to write messages seen on multiple tv's. If you were having trouble breaking the ice to a cutie in the bar, you could message over all the tv's in a text format "Girl in green dress...meet me at the bar so I can buy you a drink"....very clever....

AND THE PRICES ARE GREAT!!! $5.50 FOR A JACK AND COKE--I did not think it be possible...(I am a vodka drinker personally, but I always look at the prices of Jack and Coke because if they are reasonable, everything else is...I am weird...what can I say...)
ZrsB3DVzC7nNI2a4PVJrmA:
Just bought a house in Woodbine/Glencliff area of Nashville.  Definitely a fixer-upper but I'm down for the challenge.

The first call went to Hiller Plumbing.  Who hasn't heard their annoying ass jingle on the radio?  I called them first to get started... I figured... huge company, tons of trucks in Nashville, not bad online reviews... Turns out their service is way over priced and sales people (NOT plumbing specialists... SALESPEOPLE) are as bad as their dumb ass jingle.

The second call went to a handyman who got his arse fired in a heart beat as he caused damage to my sink. 

Third times a charm... I called a previous landlord to see who he used for plumbing for his properties.  He said Jack Ward and mentioned he's been using them for 45 years.  45 YEARS.  Little did I know that Jack Ward has been in business since 1947.  That's dope as hell... servicing Nashville for 69 years... WOW.

The guys (Ryan, Jeff and Bentley - SHOUTOUT) came in and correctly diagnosed the problem, explained it to me in layman's terms, were polite as hell, and fixed the issues in 7 hours.  My home is 1950's ranch style, so we had lots of vintage parts that needed to be repaired or upgraded and the did it without flinching.

Thanks for getting the job done, saving me any more plumbing nightmares.  You've got a customer who will spread the word and call you back for more work I need done in the future.  Thanks guys!  Cheers.

I called Jack Ward & Sons because there was water pouring from underneath our kitchen sink in our new-build home our second evening in the house and the builder-supplied warranty plumber was not answering his phone.  One of their guys (I wish I could remember his name!) called me back within minutes.  He made it very clear he was happy to come out for an emergency call; however, he gave me a few things to look for to see if it was something we could handle on our own and avoid the expense.  With his help, I figured out the source and determined I was likely going to be able to fix the issue myself, at least until the builder could address.  He was even kind enough to call me back later that evening to ensure everything was good and we didn't need him to come out.  I so appreciated his help as he really saved us a chunk of money!  We had used the company once before for a minor clog in our rental and I'll be sure to call them for any future plumbing needs.  They did good and reasonably priced work.  Seems to be a great and honest company from all of our experiences.

I've had Jack Ward & Sons replaced an outdoor spigot, diagnosed why the pipes in my house were shaking, and service the water heater. The plumbers are always very polite and don't seem to mind that I follow them around and ask a lot of questions. 

Every time I've call an appointment has been set up for that day or the next. Their prices seem very reasonable, and they get work done quickly. When the pipes inside my house were shaking they quickly determined it was a Metro Water Issue and only charged me for the service call fee. 

Thanks for your help Jack Ward & Sons!
ESkjE8HTcghidl4sKJr-CA: New to the Nashville area a visit to Resturant Depot is like going to the Toy store when you were 6!  The staff is helpful and courtious.  A huge selection of all types of vegatables, meats, suppplies, and just about anything you can think of.  I have a feeling they will soon know me by my first name!

Yee haw I've scored a card.  Duck $12, Argentinian Reds $11.99 and a 5lb hunk of boneless lamb for $24.  Many treasure here, you can buy 1 piece of something or a catering pack.  Dress warmly as the warehouse part that is the cooler is icy cold.
Always willing to go the extra effort to make your shopping experience smooth and efficient, thanks for paying attention to the finer details, great team here
3zfXupWFqA809Cez3oQbSw:
This place is amazing. The delivery is so fast. Within 20 minutes of ordering I had my food. Reasonable delivery charge as well. The food is insanely good. When my order came it was steaming hot as well. If you call try the baked ziti. So good. Can't wait to come back soon!

Adding to my previous review- their pizza is amazing! I always order online with Eat24 and get my food within 20 minutes! The delivery driver is always friendly as well. They have definitely made a loyal customer out of me.
Fantastic pizza! Awesome service; fast delivery; fabulous chocolate cake AND cheesecake too!

I ordered delivery from here and it was fast (although it was around 4 pm when I ordered). The garlic bread was delectable and the pizza was just okay but that's probably because I ordered something I normally don't order. Highly recommend!!
4ijsuCOtiM7k2AUi6dxivw: I just experienced a winning combination at Tasti D-Lite.  I don't know if the mix was just right today, my ice cream server was more skilled, if I wanted a dessert so badly that anything would have done the trick, or an unknown reason... but I had a Tart 'n Tasti topped with raspberries, and it was absolutely delicious.  This is the best Tasti D-Lite experience I've had yet, and it even beat the pants off my last Krave experience which I've touted as being above and beyond the quality of Tasti D-Lite.  Go Tasti!

This was so yummy! I had never tried it before, and I am a Sweet Cece's fiend, so I was a little worried I would be disappointed.  Au contrare!  The girl at the front was extremely nice and helpful and she definitely gave me my money's worth!  I got the Nutella flavor, which they make fresh while you're waiting.  It was delicious!  I topped it off with some Oreo pieces for the crunch, and I was in froyo heaven!  I will definitely be going back to try more flavors- they have over 100!!

When you're on a diet, there's nothing better than looking at those low calories, EVEN on a large!  I'm a huge fan of the plain ol' vanilla, but the peanut butter chocolate isn't shabby either.  

The location is super convenient.  The interior is fun and urban retro.  My only drawbacks for not being a 5 star rating are (1) they run out of flavors way too soon before closing time and (2) the employees could use some southern hospitality training.  At the core, however, this place gets big kudos from me.
feIQTnvT8FBXPhRTVWcJRw: First was a little scared to stop because of the look of the building. Food was amazing!! Made to order burgers and fries! Mouth watering Mushrooms. Will be back.
I ordered the small burger with the deluxe toppings. It was a giant burger and very tasty! We got the seasoned fries and they are my new favorite thing to eat.

This Fat Mo's is probably just as good as any of the other Fat Mo's around Nashville, but since it seems to be the only one without a review, I thought I'd put it on the map.

Pros: Satisfies that intense craving for a good, solid heart attack. If you want to die a slightly slower but just as joyful death, go for the deluxe (not the super deluxe). There is something magical that they do to the beef. Maybe it's just black pepper, maybe it's pixie dust. But it's delicious. (Think of it as a pimped out Burger King.)

Cons: Doesn't satisfy that craving late at night (because this location closes too early). Also, warning: be prepared to hate yourself if you dare to finish an entire burger.
KbCQENsTjAo5gpLVu3pLXg:
I have been eager to give this place a shot since it opened up a few buildings down from my office on 5th but kept forgetting it was there. Glad I finally gave it a shot. I don't think For a quick lunch the beef rice bowl with medium hot sauce and a dollop of teriyaki is hard to beat! 

One caveat: do not purchase the "Japanese Sprite." I was lured in by the interesting bottle. Unfortunately the interesting bottle is filled with a revolting liquid that tastes like bubblegum and sadness.

I used to eat here a lot when I worked near it.  It is a nice change of pace to the downtown restaurants for lunch. The food is good, not the best Korean ever but it certainly tastes good.  The couple that runs this place is really nice and always made me feel very welcome.

A friend and I stopped into this "rice bar" whatever that is for a Friday lunch. We did not have the sushi, noodles or a rice bowl but they looked pretty good. The place is fairly bare-bones, fast food booth type seating, minimal...everything. 

I noticed a sign with various photographs of something cheesy called Doria. I wasn't familiar with it and a quick google said: "Westerners looking for something familiar to eat in Japan may enjoy this take on Italian food. Buttered noodles, vegetables or meat covered in a sauce, usually cheese."  I ordered seafood doria on white rice and my friend ordered chicken on fried rice.  

 NO, SERIOUSLY it was so amazing.  Fried rice with chicken, covered, no, drenched in white cheese. It reminded me of the rice nachos at Las Palmas. It was insane. Unhealthy, but delicious.  We will be back and we will bring friends. If you are looking for a carb and cheese attack, this place is not to be missed.
mp24FaM8jpJxaowkiID-iw: Late night post-club cravings .... Krystal will satisfy! Try the Krystal Chiks - juicy, succulent chicken with mayo and pickles on a bun. Heaven!

One of the better Krystal's around. They serve a good economical tasty breakfast. The staff is very friendly. Many mornings the same customers are there to get food or coffee and to talk before work or church on the weekends. The Krystal burgers and chili are great too.

Oh, Krystal - you are my dirty little secret food.
