I don't know if I randomly get a Harold and Kumar moment, but suddenly I am just craving these little things (yes, I know they went to White Castle). The regular ones are ok, but the Philly Cheesesteak ones are the bomb. Interesting people watching at this location, but the manager is always cool.
Miy9w9lKGL_AzqIMkSvB0g: Stopped here on my trip to Nashville because I've heard amazing reviews. I got the cheesy bean and rice burrito with a cup of salsa and it was aaaaamazing! Super quality ingredients and really fast service.

Five points is a cool area but the only thing I had been missing is a taco truck. So when five points tacos arrived, I was extra excited. But when I received my tacos, I was disappointed. The chicken was so light in color, it didn't appear to be seasoned or flavorful. Oh how wrong I was. The chicken taco was delicious! I was confused. How did they season this chicken without any color? I don't know and I don't care. This is my go to spot when I've had too much to drink at one of this hipster bars down the street. Only thing missing is chips and salsa but that's asking too much of a taco truck so I digress.
Best place in Five Points for authentic Mexican! We had nachos, tacos, and the mulitas! We loved everything. And the people who run it are so nice. Highly recommended!!
3zuoA52txAW6o1AJPf6w3w: Small shop nicely decorated. At 3 pm all the kids on display - some even doing their homework. Definitely a family business ( I have a lot of respect for that). Grilled cheese with bacon delicious and priced right too!!

There was one particular day I decided to walk down to broadway for lunch and I saw this cute little restaurant tucked away out of hind sight. I've walked downtown past this place on numerous occasions but never noticed it.

I dropped in and tried the crab cake on texas toast and boy was that alot of food! The sandwhich was huge and I must say I couldn't eat it all. I will say that I was pleasantly suprise the crab cake sandwich consisted of mostly crab and not packed with alot of breading which is actually a good thing. I got my monies worth I'll tell you that!

Great for take-out and the wait!
I am so sad to see this place closed! It was delicious food and I was looking forward to going back next time I was in Nashville. Please open one in Chicago!!
YK3Kt4It6ACDYC0yrDQdCA: Had an excellent latte here today. The decor is beautiful and spot on. Friendly staff. AWESOME looking pastries but I resisted :) I'll be back!
Nobody knows coffee like these guys and trust me I know coffee.They are gonna be yuge. Had a delicious covfefe. They are gonna make billions.

A friend sent me a message asking if I had tried Retrograde yet. After all, it had been open for 3 whole days... But I had just returned from the road and hadn't heard of it, so I did a little digging. They had me at "serving Onyx Coffee Lab coffees." Onyx, based out of Fayetteville, AR, is one of my favorite US roasters. 

On my first visit to a coffee shop, I often order the drip coffee just to see what's happening there. I've heard some people say they judge a Thai restaurant by the quality of their Pad Thai. I don't know about all of that, but Retrograde's drip was excellent. Not to mention, it was free... Pretty sure that's a 'week one perk only' type of thing, but was a pleasant surprise nonetheless. 

The space itself feels great - I stayed for about an hour and did some work. The hardwood floors are beautifully refinished, and the design felt warm and good blend of modern and throwback. 

Parking out front was completely full when I arrived. Tip: there's a lot in the back (almost feels like alley access off Douglas & Joseph) and then a stairwell up to the left hand side that takes you right into Retrograde. 

I'll certainly be back.
nJ_u4qdGuwdilKHvtUok5Q:
Hot good, made my way and the manager was the friendliest I have ever met at a Burger King, especially late night 
Thanks!

I visited this location for lunch today and to my delight it has gone through a update on the building. Very nice, but what was even more impressive was the food! Hot and fresh French fries and my special order burger was perfectly made as I requested! I am very picky about my hamburger fixings and frequently encounter rude behavior and 'rolling eyes' from the person taking my order when dining out. Not the case here. They are really professional in demeanor and appear to be a well managed group. I got my special order just as fast as any other order!!! Fantastic?! Maybe or maybe not.... I couldn't believe how fast it was and thought they probably didn't get my order right. To my surprise my order was exactly as I ordered! Kudo's to this BK on doing it right, keeping it clean, keeping it friendly and exceeding  my expectations! This is MY BK hands down going forward!!!

I use to not like this location at all, but I gave it another chance! I am so,so glad that I did!!!! The food is always fresh! My schedule always changes so I have visited several times at all different hours and it is always great! Cleanliness and friendliness are important to me and they knock it out of the park every time I visit! They must of made mgt. changes here because it is without a doubt changed to the good!!!! Thank you BK! I only wish they would update the 80's building here and freshen it up! White Castle across the street did and it puts this BK image to shame! But WC can't even touch the service & food quality at this BK! Take good care of the manager here BK and hire more like this one because they know how to run a business !!! Sincerely a BK Fan!!!
fQkwFGtRQEkXvkpzB9lcDw: I joined Yelp just so I could write this review. As of today, 10/07/13, this joint has been open a little over a month, and I'm tellin you, they're going far! Rodney, the owner/head chef, LOVES his food. His heart is in it, and you can taste it. No matter what I've ordered, be it the Gumbo, Red Beans & Rice, or even just a side of spinach, this dude puts his soul into it. I expect them to do well and expand easily. Can't recommend this place enough. Go into Cajun Cove and tell 'em Jonathan sent you. :)

Talk about Cajun food! I LOVED their food!

I had blacked chicken on angel hair pasta. It was with spicy cream sauce. It really was outstanding. The portion is big and it was just the right spice.

The chicken was seasoned so perfectly and it went well with the pasta. I would highly recommend it!

It seems like people do a lot of take out but it's a cool joint. It only has 3 tables. It's very very casual.

I love this place! I'm definitely coming back!

My first visit was back around early June when it was crawfish season. My little sis had told me about this place as she heard many reviews from her friends. We called up the restaurant and asked how much was the crawfish boil and they said it was $7/lb. A good price if you ask me!

We decided to eat here for early dinner one day and we were greeted nicely by the owner. We sat down and well like one reviewer said the seats were uncomfortable, yep they were haha but I wasn't complaining! We chose an appetizer to try, which was the wings but the owner said he ran out of wings due to graduation parties. That was a bummer for us but he suggested to try the clam strips. We placed our order in for 2 lbs of crawfish also as it was all we cared about.

The clam strips came out piping hot with a side of fries. We both tried the clams and they were soooooo tender!! The basket was plentiful to share and by the last strip I wanted more. Our crawfish came in and they were piping hot as well. They were also soooooo tender and had just the right amount seasoning! We ate the crawfish in one sitting and we regretted to order more! That's how good it was!!

I would definitely come back to try more of their food as I'm a sucker for anything Cajun besides crawfish lol!
0FIfHHrNbcc_hXbCjhU8HQ: A little pricey but given how healthy and quality the food there, I think it's worth it. I really like their chicken avocado wrap and order that almost all the time but they do have a variety of lunch options.
I appreciate a great place with plenty of healthy options and treats so satisfy every taste right here on the wonderful Vanderbilt Hospital campus. I'll be back again & again!

My colleagues and I come here frequently after clinicals or whenever on campus. It's a great place to sit down, but it does get busy. The food is great, although the service can get slow during peak hours. I highly recommend the Chipotle Bean Burger and the Chipotle Turkey & Avocado burger. Simply amazing and not what you would expect from a retail chain like this
mOt8sceynF20Q6LIxNaSEg:
We stopped by PDK while out on Saturday evening.  It appeared it was a soft opening day. It was extremely well organized and the staff was extremely warm and friendly. 
The kids loved the chicken and waffles.  I had the large side salad (which was huge, not large) and it was great.  I also had the salmon with flash-fried Brussel sprouts.  It was all wonderful, along with the great staff who checked on us regularly. 
We will be back often, for sure.  This is another great fit for Bellevue.

Great food and service, a little pricey but worth it. All of the food I have ordered has been above average. My favorites are the pork tenderloin sandwich and shrimp poboy. Burgers are good too, not a fan of the fries. Always quick and fully staffed.
First time here and it was seriously soooo amazing. The biscuits are to die for! Service was great! Love this place!!
LnN7LbbqFK1OvFjbkhUjaQ:
Never have had an issue with my order service and food has always been great!
Been eating here weekly for 2 years now.
Very impressed with how courteous and quick the staff was at this location. Food was great as always!

As a first timer I was skeptical of the rave this place gets, so I tried it. From the minute I stepped in the door, I was greeted with a happy face and somebody who seemed like they genuinely enjoyed what they do. The food was great and the service was above anywhere else, at this price, if not more. I'd also like to thank Orlando. Being a first timer, He really made me feel welcomed every step of the way!
DoQ46rGW4qMWnkN39FvLEg: OK, i've only been here after a long night out at the bars so even though i may not always be of "sound mind and body"  I do know that this place has always been pretty good.  I can only attest to there breakfest-ey foods, more recently there Country Fried Steak Breakfast.  This thing was ginourmous! It covered my entire plate!  The pancakes are always fluffy and delicious and of course very filling. My buddy (slightly inebriated) decided on the "all you can eat" pancakes for $4.99(what a deal)  His hopes where to break the 22 pancake record. But he failed miserable at a paltry 9 pancakes.  O well can't blame a brother for trying.
This place used to be good but I think one of the problems it is under staffed. I give 4 stars because they did try to make our bad experience up to us.

I like it here. It's the average IHOP. The food is always nice & I never really wait long. No complaints. My boyfriend is Hispanic & he doesn't speak English. The waitress, Jennifer, is very nice & speaks Spanish! I loved that because I didn't have to order for him. Besides that, she is very friendly & attentive. I never wait on a refill or anything. I will continue to ask for her to care for me in the future.
RMKpKqA9LtN8qqFRJc89ZQ:
This restaurant epitomizes the stereotype of "You only get the best Chinese food in sketchy strip malls." China Spring doesn't look like much, until you walk inside; then it still doesn't look like much, but with a large fish tank. 

Appearances can be deceiving though; for all that this restaurant looks as though it probably slaughters its own chickens in the back, the food is DELICIOUS. The steamed wontons are some of the best I have ever had, and I have never had a bad experience with their sweet and sour chicken. Prices are fantastic; if I can get two meals worth of food for $7.50, why ever cook for myself again? The food is so cheap that it almost concerns me: What are they actually putting in this stuff, other than essence of pure delicious?

I don't come here much but I don't have any complaints. When I call ahead of time, my order is always ready by the time I get there. When I've gone in and ordered, I think I usually wait about 10 minutes. The food is pretty good. It's the first place I started to visit for Chinese food so my review may be a little bias. If I go somewhere else and the Chinese food doesn't taste the same, I'm always a little disappointed. I stick to sesame chicken most of the time so I don't know much about the other dishes.

A pleasant surprise on Clarksville Pike! The food was surprisingly good. Colorful, flavorful, and or excellent quality. Best Chinese food I've found in Nashville, and I've been looking hard to find it. Their noodle dishes even looked delicious! Plus, they had the best executed General Tso's chicken I've found south of Virginia, if that is your sort of thing. Crispy, sweet, but also plenty of heat. 

The service was also excellent. Rare example of very small outfit where they actually had table service. The server/waitress was very friendly. Although the speed on non-lunch special orders was a bit slow at lunchtime, the lunch special orders were very quick to arrive. Ambiance is surprisingly good, but they should ditch the fish tank. They should also ditch the cheesy wall menu, it is low-class and at doesn't show the lunch specials. Two members of my party accidentally ordered full meals, rather than lunch specials, because it wasn't clearly displayed that lunch specials are available. They could also stand to turn down the radio.

Overall, 5/5 on the food, 5/5 on front end service, 4.5/5 on kitchen service, and 4/5 on ambience. Will become a favored eatery.
wV5zySt7-ww971CKyONrFQ: So my mom and I have a weird tradition of getting bbq takeout from somewhere for the day we put up her christmas tree. Idk how this tradition started, but it did, and we've been going somewhere different each year for about eight years. This place was one of the best places we've tried over the years. No sauce needed on the pulled pork (it's just not usually my jam) but mom said the sweet heat sauce was great. Slaw was a tad too spicy to add to her sandwich (she's a wimp, imo), but I thought it was absolutely perfect. The mac and cheese is creamy and not weird and grainy at all (which is often the flaw in the mac and cheese plan), the white beans were on point and the greens were delicious. Both times I've been here everyone on the staff that I've spoken with has been kind and super concerned about the quality of the food. The place is legit and we're lucky to have them.

I stumbled upon the East Nashville BBQ Company while taking back streets out of downtown Nashville to avoid traffic on the interstate and am glad I did. I'm not really familiar with this part of Nashville, so this place may be easy to find, but it seemed a bit out of the way to me. I was really hungry and it was well after the normal lunchtime, so I was expecting a warmed up leftover sandwich, but ended up with a solid BBQ plate. 
I ordered a pulled pork plate with beans and slaw with a drink. The meal was good, a lot better than I expected given the time of day, but it was the folks working there that earned another star on my review. The ladies were very welcoming and took time to talk to all the customers while we were eating or waiting for our food. I could tell that they took pride in their work and the business which is very refreshing in this day of chain stores and disinterested employees. 
A decent lunch for under $10 and pleasant company - what's not to like?

My wife chose the pulled pork sand which which was served with a bag of chips and a soft drink. The pork was excellent with the sweet BBQ sauce

I chose the chicken legs and the meat fell off the bone. The mac & cheese was very good

Price for both was $14

Great value
13xACwAI6L3KcdH7LvIJ6Q:
I'm almost afraid to mention it, but if you are downtown for an event (TPAC, War Memorial, Municipal, etc.), this is the place to go if you want a nice meal/drink but not a crowd. 

On numerous occasions, I have bypassed the long beer line at War Memorial to hop across the street and have a delicious cocktail in between sets or acts. Not even kidding, one of the last times I did this the same folks were still waiting in line for their overpriced keg beer. 

What really sets this place apart, is the food. I never expect much from hotel dining, certainly not in the bar; but this is the exception. The BF and I popped in here after the roller derby Saturday night for one last drink and much needed sustenance. We ended up splitting a cup of the crab and corn bisque (pretty darn good! More corn than crab, but high flavor). My boyfriend had a Reuben w/ fries and I had a cheeseburger w/ fries. I never order burgers, but after a long night of roller girls, I was famished. I didn't try it, but my boyfriend loved his Reuben. It was piled high on marble rye and the cheese was literally oozing out the sides. My burger was the same, cheese wise. It was cooked to a perfect medium well and smothered with delicious cheddar, crisp lettuce and onions and a fresh tomato (which is a feat this time of year). The fries were equally tasty. 

After all of that, the tab was very much on the cheap side. About $30 for two large meals, a cup of soup a beer and a soda. Not bad at all!

Gourmet bar food, in a hotel lobby??  Yes!  I stayed at this hotel, and day after day observed others eating delicious, beautiful food.   Finally, I had my chance to eat, and was delighted with my steak salad, perfectly prepared and tasty.  I also got to "borrow" a couple cheese beignets from a friend...they might be worth a return trip to Nashville.

The food comes from upstairs at Speakers Bistro, so I can only assume that it's good, as well. 

Also, the staff here is very friendly and attentive.
This was a really nice bar in our hotel lobby.  Clearly overpriced as any hotel bar is but the bartenders were very friendly and it was convenient!
vc3cJrMklS-NaMAvcEmY8A: With 2 kids, we had a layover of 2 hrs and definitely didn't want the usual fast food. Didn't look at the reviews until afterwards but yes I can see that if in a major rush, u may get impatient. We had a wide range of food... Rib Platter, sliced beef, mac & cheese, french fries, Texas toast, green beans & cole slaw. Keep in mind, this is an airport but the food is on point if u have time. They have a to-go counter that helps but sit down & enjoy if u can... I know we did. Only downsides, the restaurant area is a bit cramped ( but which one isn't) luggage bags & all don't help, & ask about the spice level as a few things had a bit more kick then the kids expected (but they still enjoyed). Would definitely do again if flying thru Nashville with time on our hands.

Stopped here for a long layover and wanted someting different than fast food.  I had the Beef Brisket - Ordered it sliced but they brought me chopped.  Either way it was delicious.  I opted for the cloe slaw and beans as sides.  The slaw could be bigger but the beans were really good.
I would recommend this place if you are in the airport looking for something different.

We were stuck at the airport and had to eat dreaded airport food! Thank God we chose Neelys!!! The bbq was amazing!!!! The fries weren't anything special but dip them in the wonderful bbq sauce and it will change your life!! The service was slow but I would endure the service again just for that wonderful taste again! My sister and I throughly enjoyed it!!!!
Yu7gMbmsmfTIh1wGX7Eetw: New chef. Great new offerings. Love the drinks too. The bartenders are great. Ares is always top notch and a great host too. Michelle is super sweet too. They really get to know they're customers. Nice addition to the neighborhood.

Delicious!!!!! LivingSocial brought me in and I can't wait to come back!  The appetizers, dinner, drinks and dessert were all perfection! I truly enjoyed everything that I had and there are no complaints!  The staff was very friendly and helpful with the menu selections, the atmosphere was cute and comfortable and it was exactly what I wanted! We had the egg appetizer that was beyond words, the pork chop, which was the special- so amazing, the pork tenderloin, the Mac and cheese (you must get!!!) and then the homemade ice cream after=yum! The martinis were trouble because they were so delicious I drank them too fast  

I Definitely will be back and loved it all! Would definitely recommend!!!

I love this place!  The Beef Tataki was amazing and the cheese plate was fantastic! I also had the cucumber martini and it was wonderful, very refreshing. This is one of my top 5 favorite places to dine in Nashville.  Don't miss out!
JVTH7rpVjt0-VsCOAcRrDw:
It was so good our first morning in Nashville - that we decided to go back the next morning!
All 5 of us lived it!
They're very very friendly too!
Right around the corner from Omni Hotel.
Tasty, reasonably priced hot breakfast. Lots of outlets so was great for business travelers or just to get a charge before my flight. Staff was friendly and cleaned off my table when I was done

Staying in Nashville for the week and this awesome cafe is right around the corner from our hotel. We've eaten here two mornings so far and are in food heaven! Everything is delicious. The service is incredible and everyone is so friendly and welcoming.
I8KMfyZoEqzLQtChv2X5fA:
Until we're settled in, it'll be fast food for the time being.

I had intended to go to Hog Heaven, but since they don't open on Sundays, I went next door to McD's, instead.

I liked the place. It was very clean, the cashiers were friendly, and there were plenty of seating.

Standard McDonald's fare. Which is what I like about chains -- you know what you're getting.

While strolling around town I needed something yummy to snack on. Service was fast and the employee that took my order was very friendly. 

Unfortunately, the interior has been recently updated but for some reason some of the chairs have already been ripped up and are covered in green duct tape. There is a TV in there which is nice. Bathroom was clean.

Difficult getting in driveway off of West End and finding parking. They really should work on the entrance to the parking lot. and the floors and tables are dirty but great location on West End across from Vandy. Plus, free wifi and long hours. I think they're open 24/7...
pU3tfNy3BtoVLxFElwVlxQ: Pie In The Sky Pizza was just getting good again when it closed and we wondered what was coming in. This BBQ place is a terrific replacement. I love the fact that you can order by the pound rather than having to order more ribs or brisket than you care to eat. The ribs are my favorite in Nashville.

I've had a bbq a million times, but I've never found a place where the homemade sauces are as much a draw as the meats. This may be the best experience I've ever had with 'cue. Owner brings the sauces over to tell you all about them, which is really, really cool. Shows the pride here. The people running this place seem very genuine, and warm!! Great service. 

I'll be coming back for sure!! And bringing a few bottles home. That blackberry bbq sent me to heaven.. basically drowned my brisket in it. And the spicy green beans kicked up with green chile peppers? Hell yeah! 

#bbq #charcoalcowboys #barbecue #sauces #brisket #nashville #yum #america  @ Charcoal Cowboys BBQ
Really great BBQ! New favorite ribs in Nashville and five different sauces to choose from! Sides we're delicious too, Mac and cheese tasted just like my grandma's!
zuP6BplIz4y6QGuq7Ftm1g: Interacting with the wait staff and managers at this restaurant was an absolute joy! I came in on  Thursday for the Nashville Rock n Roll 1/2. I had dinner there with my friends Thursday and Friday night and a full breakfast this morning.  From the chef to the manager wait staff and kitchen staff all I can say is thank you...kudos...merci. And last but not least the entertainment was phenomenal.  I definitely recommend this venue.
Best menu item for lunch is the bacon, cheese and tomato sandwich with tators. Some local employees are given a 10% discount which is greatly appreciated!

If you're staying at the Double Tree,  by all means this is a nice hotel bar in which to take avatar of happy hour and maybe get a nibble. They have TVs at the bar and outside on the patio complete with fire lamps on chilly evenings. You might also catch a little live music scheduled daily. If you already have a room,  definitely worth your stop.
IjF6swxGAObtg1kR5swLBg: Just a great vibe, laid back and amazing coffee.  I also enjoy the daily freshly made sandwiches.  They even have fruit as a side.
Fine cup of joe  extremely friendly and the coffee is the real deal!!!!!  If your near opryland and need a real cup of coffee this is the place!

This definitely gets four stars for the coffee, hospitality and ambience, but the food is severely limited. As a group of four (at about 8am) there were only a few bagels and muffins. There are no breakfast sandwiches or additional offerings, unless you prefer a lunch option. I'll be back for the coffee but I'll plan to eat first!
nygeujEUaSDkD53e12shRg: I keep coming back because I love the pizza, but the staff isn't always friendly. The lady who seems to be the owner is kind of cold, but I think she may just have a dry personality.

Sir pizza is a straightforward pizza joint and that's what we love about it. The place can't be bigger than 1000 sq  - Order at the counter. Get a cold beer and a thin crust pizza and hang out in the handful of booths. The best way to describe their pizza is nostalgic - thin crust, sauce, toppings and cheese. We dig this place because we have a realistic opinion of what we are getting into. It's not a craft, high end pizza joint and they don't try to be.

Sir Pizza is my favorite. Tuesdays are 2 for 1, so I try to make it down there during the week to cash in on the special. Everyone is friendly and the service is always great. I just wish I didn't live so far away!
FUxjp9FJOJVef8iaKfhAiA: I love this place.. We hit it up about once a week-- the owners are very friendly and are always accommodating! I love the new putt putt game and golden tee is always a must! Stop in and give them a try:)

Best bartenders ever! My wife and I have lived just a few blocks away for years and just a few months ago went there for the first time. Now we would consider ourselves "semi-regulars." Marilyn greeted us right away and introduced herself. She prides herself on knowing everyone's names. She is the top reason we keep going back.

The service is outstanding. It's never too crowded and Marilyn is verry attentive so you never have to wait for service.The beer is cheap and the food is decent. It is a cash only bar, but there is an ATM next door. You can easily have a few beers and a pizza for under $20 so you don't need much cash when you go.

They have a few pool tables, darts, a jukebox, big screen TV, and several smaller TVs around the bar. There is a stage, but we've never seen a band playing. They started doing free burger Wednesday nights so if you're looking for something super cheap to do during the week, then this is your place.

Outside they have some nice open air seating and a corn hole set although we've never seen anyone playing. This would make a great place to bring a group of friends wanting to hang out on the weekend, because you've got plenty of seating without having to battle too much of a crowd. 

We've also found the clientele very friendly. Most will introduce themselves and accept you as part of the group even if they've never met you before. Marilyn makes sure everyone knows eachother as she frequently introduces new clientele to the regulars so everyone feels at home there. For anyone new to the area and looking for a good local joint, we definitely recommend you check it out.

New ownership took over bar on June 1st. Place is already being renovated and cleaned up. Darts, Golden Tee, Silver strike Bowling, Pool and every sports channel imaginable. Great food and tons of beer choices. Highly recommend!!
lvi4u_XVzvL06EAj0hUmfw:
Had a good experience. Setup is very similar to Pie Five or Subway. You go down a line, picking the options you want for your pizza.

Unique pizza options, nice staff, and good atmosphere. If you like Pie Five, you'll like Pizzafire.

We just moved to the neighborhood, and this is THE BEST PIZZA we've found in Nashville!! The owners are so sweet and friendly and the service was super fast and amazing. Price point was perfect. Pizza is phenomenal. We will be back all the time!! BIG FAN!!
I think they've worked out the kinks. My pizza was done in just a couple of minutes, and it was great! Also very cheap for how fresh it was. Highly recommend.
NaYxok8rRnMNKXykKqkzQQ:
Great sushi bar! Best since Sam closed up shop.
Don't let the buffet fool you. Quality sushi prepared fresh. Many varieties to choose from.

I ended up at King Buffet simply because it was the first place that I saw when driving around famished on Murfreesboro Pike. Happily it ended up being exactly what I needed. Though I often object to Nashville Chinese food options, this place was actually really good, and more importantly really cheap. For $4.50 I got a complete lunch that included not only General's chicken and rice, but also soup and Won Tons; what a deal right? Mostly patronized by the masses that work on Murfreesboro, the lunch buffet seems to be its biggest attraction. For only $6 a person, their buffet is a seriously good deal. I'm not saying King Buffet's Chinese food is going to blow your mind, but for the price, you are definitely getting the better end of the deal.

The staff was very friendly. The waitress was super sweet and goo-ed and ga-ed to put a smile on a fussy babie's face from a family sitting across from me. She also smiled a lot and continually expressed her thanks as I finished up and paid for my meal. The food had a surprisingly wide selection including sushi. The food was not bad at all; not amazing, but hey, it's a buffet. I'll go again.
Nli_jk1atYhVCcJhv0l3Zg: If you've never been to the Nashville airport then you don't know that it is long in the tooth! It looks like it's getting a makeover because it sorely does need one! My guy and I were on our way home from a two week driving tour of the Southeast. We started in Nashville and we ended in Nashville and we just loved it! However even though our hotel gave breakfast, we had to leave earlier than the hotel was starting breakfast to make our plane. We headed off to the airport and didn't expect to find the best things for breakfast, But we didn't expect to find what we found either! There is nothing but Starbucks and a couple other fried up restaurants with all disgusting food. Finally we came across Quiznos in terminal C. Thank God we did! They actually had egg and cheese sandwiches, bacon egg and cheese sandwiches, ham egg and cheese sandwiches, and different kinds of breads. Nothing fried! Hooray! I had just the plain egg with tomatoes on wheat bread. My guy had ham egg and cheese on wheat bread. Very very good, healthy, and just what we were looking for! Can't wait for the next trip to Nashville when they hopefully have added more healthy restaurants and better choices!
Very kind managers. Decently kind employees. they made me a v tasty meatball sandwich with jalapeños.

While on a layover in Nashville waiting at the airport we stopped in at Quiznos for a bite to eat. We were so tired of traveling and definitely ready for a meal. I saw this poor employee working alone with a long line of customers, yet despite her stressful situation with so many orders to fill and only herself to meet the demands she greeted us with the most cheerful smile and a warm welcome. Nyshol filled our order promptly with a friendly smile. The sandwich my husband ordered was perfectly prepared as was my sandwich. We ordered the Classic Italian and the  Chipotle Beef sandwich. Both were toasted, warm and perfectly seasoned and ready for pickup in no time. The thing that we were most impressed about was the cheerfulness of a lone employee working extremely hard, quickly serving up great food.
byoDydOvUHzbSv4VtD9Cpw:
I work off of Jefferson and this is one of my two "go to" locations for a tasty but quick lunch in the area. (If your curious about my other favorite stop, I usually head the opposite direction on Rosa Parks to B&C BBQ at the Farmer's Market). The food is fresh and they move you through the line quickly...and that's important since there is ALWAYS a line wherever you go in MetroCenter at lunch. My favorite meal is the Cancro Special which isn't listed on the menu but is simply a roast beef & provolone sandwich with pepperoni on top. It is good cold, but if you have time, I recommend you order it hot, as a wrap, with onions, banana peppers, and chipotle mayo. If this seems far from your taste in sandwiches, the turkey is always a safe choice. 

One of the other nice things about this particular location is that they have a fruit tea that changes daily. In the hotter months, it is nice to be able to swing by and get a good fruit flavored tea that you can top off before you head out the door. 

The only thing that actually keeps me from giving this location five stars is the lack of their famous (in my mind) white chocolate and macadamia nut cookies. Every other Jersey Mikes I have been to have these, and I can only attribute their absence to the fact that these are franchise locations and therefore do have some choice in what they serve/carry (not every location has fruit tea either). However, if you do find yourself in another Jersey Mikes that carries these wonderful tasting, sugar shock inducing creations, I highly suggest you try one.

Go here rather than the subway next door.

This is a chain that is "coming soon" to Chicago, so on a recent trip to Nashville I decided to check it out.

I will openly admit that I am a snob when it comes to sub sandwiches.  I worked for a few years in a sub place, so there are a few indicators make a sub shop legit rather than just a sandwich place.

This place is legit.

When you walk in, you order in front of a windowed meat cooler.  They slice the meat and cheese right there, too.  It is not possible to get a fresher cut of deli meat and is a vast improvement over the meat packets that subway uses.

The bread was good and the assortment of fixin's were more than sufficient for the subs.

Reminds me of Hungry Hobo, and that is a complement.

One of my co-workers is a regular at this joint. When I say regular, I mean, he stops in for lunch at least 3 times a week. The sandwiches are very fresh but they are about  $2 bucks more than subway. Definitely worth every penny tho. I had regular size supreme club, ate half during lunch and then the rest about 430. Still good at 430, didn't get all soggy.
rxs7GHCeRh9M5iqJ3VKgNA:
I was quite impressed with Pepper Palace. Plenty of peppers from cool and mild to hot and fiery, all neatly arranged throughout the shop.

Plenty of pickled peppers and potted peppers and powerful peppers for your pleasure and they're not all that pricy if you want to pay with pennies or paper or plastic. Perhaps pick your peppers and possibly prace the place with your purchase. Please.

That's it. My keyboard hurts. Stop by Pepper Palace for jams and salsas and dipping sauces.

Hot sauce, wing sauce, spicy mustard, bbq, just basically anything sauce- sign me up. I'm the girl who dunks all of her food in liquid. My poor husband had no idea my sauce addiction was so bad when he married me. Apparently failure to mention my embarrassing eating habits is grounds for divorce in some states. Lucky for me, Tennessee is not one of them.

They have over 10 Pepper Palace locations nationwide. Their headquarters is somewhere in Eastern Tennessee. This particular one is located inside of Opry Mills mall. Personally, I think the farmers market or somewhere in the Gulch would have been a better fit for this specialty store. Either way- I found myself in the tourist trap that is Opry Mills, and was so excited that they had a hot sauce shop. 
At first glance, the store is almost overwhelming. They have so many samples and options! I had no idea where to start. Can I try them all? Can my tongue (or stomach) handle that? Let's find out. I sampled probably 15 different sauces, mustards, and salsas before decided what I wanted to leave the store with. (Meanwhile my husband is standing by the exit door asking me if I REALLY need more sauce..... the answer is yes BTW) 

I ended up purchasing a hot sauce, a spicy mustard, and some salsa. It came out to just over $30. 

At the end of the day, they have amazing sauce. If you love hot sauce as much as I do, you are doing yourself a disservice by not stopping by Pepper Palace.

I love this place.  I have been to many condiment stores both in the U.S., Canada and abroad - and this place was well-stocked with friendly customer service.

They have a HUGE selection of both dry rubs and marinades.  Sooo glad I discovered this place.

I don't know what Troy S., below is griping about - there were samples if you're into trying samples - and a huge variety of fantastic spices, even Trinidad hot pepper spice mixture which is very difficult to find.  I will definitely go back - and I can order online from home if I run out before I head back to TN.

Keep the variety going, fellas.  I love your store.
UcThXwCOpIgYL60qDIMT-g: Happened to find it by accident. Small, quiet, and good value for a healthy portion. Close to TPAC, which was a plus. I will definitely return. The owner/cook was very friendly.

This place is great! The lunch special is amazing! Gyro, drink and a side (salad, fries, or rice) for under $7 is unheard of in downtown Nashville, and it even tastes really good! I always have enough to fill me up and feel that the value for the money is great! This is my favorite place in downtown!
This place is the best. Really cool, kind folks that run it and the food is great and affordable. Refreshing escape from chain restaurant scene in downtown Nashville.
XRDcLPEmTyZihws-_sC3_A:
thank goodness your fruit tea is so delish, b&c!

i almost had to stop coming here when you stopped selling my favorite chocolate macaroon cookies.

amazing bread and fruit tea means i forgive you.  free wi-fi means i stop here at least every other day when i'm home.

also... this location has an awesome waffle bar at breakfast time!! mmm...

Bread and Company is the best of the best when it comes to the bakery/cafe type of establishments in this area. It's a local establishment with three locations in Nashville (I'm quite sad that the Cool Springs location closed as that was the closest one to my house). However, I still randomly eat at all three Nashville locations when i can.

There are so many things to rave about. Here's a rundown.

1. They have THREE types of chicken salad. Talk about decisions! They are all good, but my favorite is the almond tarragon chicken salad.

2.On the make your own sandwich (or 1/2 sandwich) you can choose all the fresh veggies you want, and you can choose TWO cheeses with no extra cost! Hello muenster AND provolone!

3. Unique spreads for your sandwich. My favorites are the artichoke spread and sun dried tomato aioli. The always will put some artichoke spread on the side for me to dip my chips in. Healthy, I know...

4. Amazing salads. I've never been able to finish one because they're so big. My favorites are the Belle Meade and Strawberry Fields. They also have amazing homemade salad dressings. You can buy them by the bottle there.

5. The best fruit tea around. I get my money's worth when I dine in. However, you'll need to take out a small loan to buy it by the gallon!

I never leave here hungry. 1/2 sandwich fill me up. I do know my husband likes their soups, chicken tenders, and sweets from the bakery.  They do have good tomato basil soup. I've had some of their cold sides and they are all good. But I normally stick to sandwiches and salads.
KruOBMcKFVs2BFfBd6z0lg: This place is such a welcome addition to the East Nashville dining scene.  I've been there twice now and both times I've had the tom ka gai (or coconut milk soup as they call it), which is truly outstanding.  Everything else I've tried has been good - not amazing, but still good.  Ambiance leaves a little to be desired.  Still, East Nashville is psyched to have it's own decent thai restaurant.

Friendly staff.  Great curries and fresh rolls. Decent bottled beer selection. The coconut soup is amazing. I've always been partial to Thai Taste and Siam off Nolensville, but I no longer see a need to travel that far for delicious Thai food.

Friendly staff.  Great curries and fresh rolls. Decent bottled beer selection. The coconut soup is amazing. I've always been partial to Thai Taste and Siam off Nolensville, but I no longer see a need to travel that far for delicious Thai food.  Hopefully my review will be posted this time, as I think some balance is needed for what I think is the best Thai place in town.
mVsfGBtzgXhlfdpRv2FVvw: This is my new favorite place to eat in Nashville.  I've been there several times for a late dinner and the food and service were superb.  The first time my sister and I visited, we ordered the Mixed Grill and let the chef choose 3 meat options for each of us so that we could better sample the menu.  One thing was better than the next.  My personal favorites are the lamb, chicken shwarma and the gyro.  Their salad, hummus and baklava are the best in town as well.  All in all, a fantastic meal.  I prefer to order from the menu rather than the lunch buffet.

I had the lunch buffet for the first time yesterday. Service was very friendly (maybe different owners/operators have taken over since the bad reviews) and food was good, especially for a $7.99 buffet. They also give you a loyalty card and your 7th lunch buffet is free.

Lunch buffet is 11 am - 3 pm mon-sat
restaurant is open for dine in and carry out 11 am - 10 pm
hookak (shisha) 8:30 pm - 2 am presumably mon-sat nights

I have been at least 10 times, and I'm surprised at the bad reviews.  I've mostly been to the lunch buffet, but I have had dinner there twice and plan on going again tonight.  The staff has always been very friendly, and everything I have ever eaten there has been good.  I'm honestly very confused by people's poor reviews.
2N0ICJapuD-cDth0s_O9OQ:
This place is incredible. I have never felt better about my health since eating their food and incredible juices and smoothies. So different than anything else. Healthy, convenient, delicious. Perfect for families, professionals, or people trying to make better choices when eating. 

Nashville is a better place because of this!

Went in today at the recommendation of my massage therapist for smoothies. I was so impressed! The bowls and smoothies are absolutely delicious and all vegan, organic, and gluten free, with lots of dairy free options as well! Truly a little random gem in green hills! Finally a good and healthy organic option in the green hills area. They also sell pre made foods for your healthy/fit lifestyle. I highly recommend the strawberry banana bowl and the pitaya bowl! :)
Awesome concept and friendly staff.  I had the strawberry protein bowl for lunch.  Tasty & filling!
2y9qnRm4AkzP9O-DPNbCLw: Thank God, we still have a Krystal somewhere in west Nashville! I've been a loyal customer since the mid-1970s and seen them through several upgrades and menu changes. The staff is great and the place is always clean. Hard to believe it has survived being right on the edge of the ritzy Belle Meade community these 40 years. It goes to show that good taste isn't only for the upper crust. Krystal is for all of us to enjoy!

I've been coming to this location since I was about 5 years old.  Krystal is my favorite fast food place, and although they aren't all the same, nor always the same quality, this location has always been the best one!

I eat here (as a treat from my normal healthier eating habits) about once a month.  The buns are always moist (as sometimes Kyrstals have a dry crispy bun that will ruin my meal!), and the food always tastes perfect.  Service is always fast and polite.  This location always gets a facelift every few years and stays looking new.  Always a good meal!

The little meat squares are just delightful! the bread is always soft and fluffy, the pickle is always crispy! They never let me down. The staff knows my order by heart. 10 krystals with a half order of fries and a diet RC cola. It's been harder to get to my favorite Krystal burger because I have been expanding my taste bud pallete. Love this place. BAD FOR VAGITARIANS.
f0qy_jMfakYtRQLADjmC3w: Nice location even though parking can be a bit of a challenge if eating in. Typical McDonald's fare. Quick and hot. Nice indoor atmosphere.
Dondrell. Or D as he called himself, was the best service I've had from this place. He was attentive and made sure I got everything I wanted. I hope he will move up in this business.

I used the drive-thru today and had a great experience. I visit this McDonald's frequently and always have a good experience. 
I regularly have a special order of a quarter pounder plain w/o cheese and they get it right. 
There was only one time they were out of Diet Coke but overall I always have a great experience here, I highly recommend it!!!
jfQtGGJeXM9Lf9aZlPHxpQ: Yum we think this place is delicious.  They have a limited menu but what we have tried has been great.  Tacos are a must!  Oh ya and the margaritas are pretty damn good also.  We give it a thumbs up for sure.

Not for the faint at heart, Agave has over 100 different types of tequilas, which is probably the largest selection in Nashville.  Girls love this place because they have an endless selection of different kinds of house margaritas.  I am pretty sure some girls I went with ordered grapefruit margaritas, you would have to ask them because after leaving, I did not remember much!  Agave has a great covered patio that we sat on, which was great because the weather was perfect.  However, as the night dragged on it got a little cramped and crowded with people piling in to the place It has a pretty good location down in the gulch and is easily accessed from either Downtown or West End.  Also, parking is not a big deal because they have a sizeable lot.

I enjoyed Agave more than I thought I would. I will say this place has maybe 8 pages of drinks and only 2 pages of food; I guess that's why it calls itself a "Tequila Lounge." If you are a big time tequila drinker, this place may impress you. I'm not much of a tequila connoiseur so I'm afraid I can't judge them on that, but with that many pages of drinks and tequilas, I'd say you are in good shape! If you're looking for a deep selection of Mexican food, you may want to try someplace else. The salsa they bring you here is delicious. I think my friend and I went through two bowls of it. I had some delicious tacos -- which I think were on the appetizer section. My friend found something vegetarian which she enjoyed. I thought the food was a notch above your standard Mexican and still not that expensive.

It was a nice evening so the windows and all were open. It's a fun place to people watch even if there aren't that many people strolling up and down the street. All in all, I thought it was a fun place to spend an evening.
dZFlKW6M5YIhPHrLhsdCTA:
Went to this restaurant today. The restaurant had clean tables and clean floors. We were waited on promptly. The the attendant was very courteous. 
I ordered two chicken burritos and a medium tea. The tea was okay but for me the tea was too sweet. I had to add water to thin the sugary tea. It ended up tasting like brown sweet water.  
The chicken burritos were good. They were warm and tasty. I received my order in about five minutes.
I would recommend this restaurant. The food was good. The tea too sweet!
Very disappointed to find out this place did not have a Chicken Tender Special for $2.99.  The beans were good though.

I came by this Taco Bell on 6/13/19 and I had a great drive through experience. The customer service was the best I've received in a very long time. They were extremely friendly and had my food out pretty fast and it was piping hot! If I'm ever in a rush and in the area I'm glad I have a place in mind to go to. S/O to Trena E for great service too!
e_rHcZom67eE8vepInkxXg: I walked up at the sight of the menu, and smell of the fresh ingredients wafting through the window. I ordered the special because I could get a taste for all of their flavors at once and I am not sorry that I did. You must try the pesto! The alfredo was the perfect consistency and flavor balance, and the tomato sauce is good, but better with the juices from the meatball dripping in it. Do not leave without tasting one of the cannolis! The ladies fill them fresh and they are delicate and creamy. Just right!
Chicken Parm is magic. Faster than most food trucks too. Loved the little arugula side salad and cannoli made with love!
Delicious!!  everything is homemade , meatballs were awesome , reasonable prices and great customer service.
KzvujtYJIKJOLqQSWKee4Q:
Consistently decent Pizza close to home. I think the workers do try to do their very best truly. It is fast food pizza but it seriously feeds my boys and that make my life easier. 
In the past they would mess everything up and give a free pizza next time. We did that Dance for about 6 months and gave up and went across the street to Dominos. Fast forward to the flood and we would come here to get pizza and they were very kind. 
I love the drive through...epitome of laziness I realize. 
Now if you read all this, please note, their wait time as the population of Nashville is growing has increased to 2 hours! So order on line and go pick it up.the rewards are good too so sign up. Or see how long it is and if too long after ordering, call them and change to pick up. The wait time has to improve once they get more drivers and all of nashville stops ordering from this particular location. 
They must be doing something good for such a long wait.

I love papa J's pizza usually but this place is hit or miss.  My first time was a 2 star pie but last nights pizza was up to 4 star quality. Keep working on it! They have good online deals but the online ordering didn't go through. I had to call. Ask for a military discount. Love the sauce but my hips don't! Thus why I'm the duchess at Planet Fitness next door!  lol They did a great job recently with a large overnight order to my sons childcare nearby for his birthday. The kids loved it! I asked for 11am & they delivered by 10:45am. The pizza cookie was a bonus. 3 1/2 stars now.

Consistently decent Pizza close to home. I think the workers do t Roy to do their very best truly. It is fast food pizza but it seriously feeds my boys and that make my life easier. 
In the past they would mess everything up and give a free pizza next time. We did that Angela for about 6 months and gave up and went across the street to Dominos. Fast forward to the flood and we would come here to get pizza and they were very kind. 
I love the drive through...epitome of laziness I realize. 
Now if you read all this, please note, their wait time as the population of Nashville is growing has increased to 2 hours! So order on line and go pick it up.the rewards are good too so sign up. 
They must be doing something good for such a long wait.
JFHIc0buwpn4-QHbW5sMHg:
so was a little scared after reading the reviews but glad i went.

overall the product is on point and fanastic. tastes just as good as i remembered from my years in tx which i think is their home base. i still say these are the best wings in the market. fries were fresh and hot too.

the staff is really young and not that polished but overall id go back. 

in tx they always said order will be ready in 14 minutes and its about the same here. i timed it.

JEANS: OK by far
ORDER at counter

Came in late on a Sunday night and it was one of the few places open. Very clean, great customer service and food was made to order. Wings and fries came out like they had just been pulled from the fryer and veggies were fresh and cold. Great neighborhood spot.
My take out was a little cold by the time I got it home but the flavors made up for it. Delicious, and the sauces seem homemade. I'll go back!
Ri0IdkA2tQkHG3CgeOnGaQ: Tabouli's late night menu never ceases to amuse me. Why you ask? Because as a far as I can see only 4-5 things on it are actually Greek. Nevertheless, I must say that the Greek/Italian/American fare late night options were pretty tasty. Their beer selection is above average, and they get extra points because the last time that I ended up there they were having beer specials to get rid of beer that they didn't want. On debate night they also had two specialty drinks that weren't great, but were a really clever way to get people into the night's festivities. I think the best thing about Tabouli's is their hookah. Our server was nice enough to make us a special blend of strawberry and mint after gauging our inability to choose a flavor, and it ended up being delicious (especially when compared to Tarboosh or Aladdin's). I also have to mention, that their service was more than fantastic. Layla, who had the bad fortune of having to serve myself and the rest of my debaucherous posse, was great the entire night!

If you haven't been to Tabouli's since it's been completely renovated in 2009, please (please!) give this place another shot. This is hands down the best hookah bar in the Nashville area if you're 21 & up as most of the other hookah bars cater to college freshman & end up looking like creepy opium dens for teens. I'm guessing it's the quality and cost of the food ($10-$15 for dinner), but the platters (gyro, veggie, hummus, etc...) are huge and are more than enough for 2 big eaters to share. The pita is grilled - be prepared to order more for just an extra buck b/c it's going to go fast. :) I highly suggest the falafel or the veggie platter, but I don't recommend the greek salad (Athens on 8th Avenue has the best in Nashville).

The hookah flavors are fairly traditional, but get jasmine-mint, rose-mint, or apple-mint, and you'll never again want for a mango bubblegum cola craptastic incense flavored hookah again. 

Tabouli's has a great waitstaff that keeps the coals coming. Stevie is my fave, ask for her & by the way she loves hot chocolate, which you if you grab her an extra cup at Bongo Java next door (b/c Tabouli's doesn't serve it), she'll love you for life.
oe9PLAVQxIILIO7eIHOK2g:
China Star has been at this location for many years. They have struggled at times with their health codes rating however the food has always been consistent and good for buffet food. Their codes problems have been more related to equipment condition issues.  My group of friends always say the best time to visit a restaurant is after a bad code rating because they are trying to fix the problems before re-inspection. 
My favorite has to be the green beans and the general chicken.
Sure do miss the soft serve ice cream machine replaced by 5 gallon cardboard cartons of institutional type scoop ice cream. Not a big fan of children serving themselves at a buffet since you never know where those hands have been and the last time they were washed. Having said that, unaccompanied children are a reality you have to accept at a buffet. (But in my perfect world parents would serve their child's plate.)
So if you are looking for a Pei Wei or PF Chang experience, this is not your place. If you are looking for inexpensive good tasting Chinese buffet, belly up to the bar Billy!

I was recommended this place by a friend back in September and decided to try the place's delivery a week after. It was excellent. I've gotten it at least once a week from there every week since. I have only had one even remotely bad experience with the food out of 35+ meals from there. I will say I have only tried the buffet once and that was.. saddening, because I know their delivery is way better.
Where did the owners of this place go?  If they opened another restaurant I want to know because this was my favorite Chinese place and haven't found another good one since it closed.
7_erbDC2n4I6Zg_e25wcIg: It's nice that the YMCA lets the public access this location. It's my favorite juice in town! They use a cold-press to make their juices, and they only use organic ingredients. They have acai bowls that they make too, which are really good for breakfast and lunch. It's a must-try!

Good! Expensive, but good!

You get a pretty nice serving of açaí. I've lived in LA where açaí reigns and I'd say this is about as close to LA quality açaí that you will get. I was kind of confused though because it's more expensive than any bowl I've ever gotten in LA... y'all might wanna check on that.

I got their standard bowl and it was delicious and filling. A nice refreshing treat. I wasn't too excited about the wait though. Took longer than I anticipated (though it wasn't horribly long).

The Franklin Factory is a cool space. You can't sit inside of Franklin Juice but they've got seating in the Factory's main area.

Vegan and vegetarian friendly. Will come back if I'm in the area!

Welcome to Bellevue! We all needed this! The Kona bowl is like dessert for breakfast and I don't have to feel bad about eating it! Healthy and delicious doesn't always work out and you guys nailed it!
clFbbzTwOm4k6loWZl0DGw:
Had a Groupon for 2 entrees and 2 well drinks for $15. What a deal! Even at regular price, they are pretty reasonable. 

Well Drinks $6
Appetizers $4-8
Entrees $8-10

Wings on Wed/Thurs are $0.25 - oh heck yeah. Pretty tasty but not hot at all.

There's live music but they weren't good at all. We sat outside in their cover patio area which was nice.

In the mix for great bar food in Midtown. The reason being that there is both food for the ladies and gents alike. The lunch menu was different than in the website. The beer selection is solid and cheaper than brewhouse. Staff is friendly and attentive. A beer distributor came in while we were eating and the server thought to make sure we had another round available while he changed all the kegs. Go grab some lunch.
Rc4PVsKyv5VuSKcqRvQrEA: Great cheap and tasty pizza. I can only speak on this location but the owner is SUPER helpful. I was teaching a class at church and needed two or three pizzas every Wed. I went in and talked to the owner. He hooked me up with a deal. Every week, I called 15 mins before I needed my pizza and when I arrived, they were there waiting for me just like magic. At the end of class, he wrote me up a reciept for the six weeks. It was great and I will be going to get my church pizzas from him from now on. No more Papa Johns and their too sweet sauce.
YUM! My husband and I got a meatball pizza to go the other night, and we devoured the entire thing! We used a sharing spree and got a large for $6.00. What a deal!
I swear their pizza is the best. I love this place and try my hardest to come eat here. I have been eating here since I was in 9th grade since it was walking distance from school.
m2eHKE6wcmYhZysdyvbzsg: We go out of our way to buy quiches and pastries at this food truck.  So, so good.  We are so glad he comes to Orinda each week!
Best quiches, ever.  Lots of great pastries.  His pumpkin pies and pumpkin cheesecake pies are worth the wait until the fall. His crusts are fantastic.
AMAZING!!!! Pumpkin pie and quiches. He's at the Moraga Farmers Market on Sundays. The cinnamon rolls at this place are like cinnamon cake in a roll! To die for!!!!
mq3JkTyu7DEbmoeKjsXQqw: Staffing was poor, pizza is amazing. It's definitely worth the wait from time to time though as it's a very unique and obviously hand made pizza house. Also it was a pretty fun atmosphere and good selection of local beers.

I chose this location over the other two since I'd read on CitySearch that the Granny White location was better for sit down eating.

I ordered the Fantasy Pizza, and my co-worker ordered a pepperoni and mushroom calzone.  Niether of us left hungry..

The fantasy pizza was great!  I ordered it well done, and the crust was just right crispy.  There wasn't too much pesto, so the pie wasn't greasy, and the blend of ingredients was just right - garlic, spinach, pesto, sun dried tomatoes, all on a thin crispy crust.

I was glad we went to Pizza Perfect, the rest of the guys we were in town with went to eat at the Hard Rock, and had a bad experience (I could have told them that..).

I'll be back to this location the next time I'm in Nashville for sure..

I had no choice but to dine at this spot a few dozen times while I attended Lipscomb.  It's right across Granny White from DLU, and it's a quick, cheap spot to pop in for a leisurely lunch.

As far as cuisine goes, this Pizza Perfect offers mostly the same selection as the one on 21st.  I honestly prefer the taste of the calzones up at 21st, but the pizzas taste nearly identical (from what I recall, anyhow).  Either way, they were tasty enough.

This Pizza Perfect is a bit smaller and more appropriate for a quick sit-down lunch with friends or family.  In fact, it's just the sort of place where you'd find Lipscomb students dining with their parents after church on Sundays.  Just adorable.

Also (a little secret)...now, I'm not sure if this is still the case, but while I was at Lipscomb, there was a narrow stairwell beside Pizza Perfect that ran up to the roof, and you could sneak up said stairwell and check out the sunset from the top of the building.  They very well may have sealed this little stairway off.  If not, they might not want me telling people about it.  Regardless, it's a selling point as far as I'm concerned.
G3Zd640VOpMgxXizb6aO6A: Classic Sonic like all the others. Brand new location with nice outdoor furniture! I don't LOVE their food,  but the drinks and ice cream are the best! I always feel bad because I never have cash to tip - they seriously need to add that option when paying with a credit card!
dfABpANQiZQStEJ8XS5_EA: Finally!! A decently priced Mexican restaurant in the Belmont/12 South area. This place is the best adaptation for this location and I hope it's here to stay. The prices are very reasonable (most items under $10) and it's not that fancy smancy, overpriced, we don't offer free salsa, and have annoying waiters that want to explain their tortilla soup in great detail b.s. that you find in the music row area. This place is legit, quality Mexican food, and it's honestly better than most local joints. It's actually more comparative to Chuy's or actual Tex Mex than anything else. The portion sizes are huge! The beans and rice aren't watered down and the enchiladas rock! The patio should be awesome in spring and summer too. Hopefully you will support reasonably priced restaurants like this in the Belmont area! Give it a shot.

As a native Texas, I was raised to LOVE both interior Mexican food and Tex-Mex. Since recently moving to Nashville, I feared my days of eating chili relleno, tamales, tacos, moles, queso, guacamole and salsa would fade away. Growing up in a state bordering Mexico, I've been taught to trust Mexican fare less the more north I go. Against all the odds, La Fiesta, right here in Nashville, passed the first test. 

I walked to La Fiesta for lunch and ordered the chicken tacos and and a la carte pork tamale (I wanted the shredded pork tacos but they only serve those during dinner hours). The tamale was HUGE and pretty darn good. I will say, they top the tamales with red sauce, cheese and ground beef. I'm a firm believer that tamales shouldn't be smothered in anything (I question what their hiding if I can't see the tamale). Nonetheless, this tamale was YUMMY. Next time, I'll ask them to leave off the extra tamale toppings and just spoon some of their fresh salsa on it instead.

The tacos: I was pleasantly surprised. The tacos were filled with shredded chicken that had been cooked with tomato, bell pepper and onion. There was a nice flavor that, topped with their fresh made salsa, was a nice treat.

I'll definitely go back to give La Fiesta another try. They do have a liquor license and serve a variety of liquors in addition to wine and beer. On tap, they have Dos Equis Lager, Dos Equis Amber, Bud Light and Yazoo Dos Perros. Happy hour is everyday from 3-7pm. The regular drink prices are pretty good ($2-3 for a pint and $4 for a 32 ounce domestic beer) so I'm curious about their happy hour rates. 

Give them a try, it worth it.

Upon observing that many people use "I'm from California" as a sort of badge to convey their palate's expertise in finding the authentic flavors of Mexican cuisine or calling out the way in which an establishment has epically failed, I attempt to shy away from using that exact badge of know-it-all-ness although it creeps out now and then. My Californianess is going to creep out today though, because I've been totally skeptical about the Mexican food here and after many tries we found the place that we keep going back to. Not perfect by any means, but  I LOVE La Fiesta. While the tortillas aren't homemade in the back and I've not seen a sign of horchata anywhere- these folks aren't full of cheese-smothering bullshit.They're just folks from Mexico making standard Mexican food, but with better flavors and more hospitality than most other spots in the area.

They're one of the very few places I've been able to enjoy a meal with my small dog on the patio. 

Everyone who works there, as a rule, is incredibly friendly. Some are even ridiculously warm and sweet. A waiter was even kind enough to fully explain his family's salsa recipe and write it down for me. They chat with us, and are nice to our dog, and one of the ladies who waitresses makes me want to hug her every time we go in.

The food is good.  I almost always get the camarones al mojo de ajo (shrimp in butter and garlic) and you cannot go wrong with that dish. Throw some of the shrimp, a slice of avocado, a squirt of lime, and maybe some hot sauce on a corn tortilla and take another step toward early heart disease. 

I'm not generally a rice person. La Fiesta's rice is the exception. So moist and flavorful. 

My boyfriend got the chicken mole last time we went (it was on the specials list) and he thought it was great. 

Point being, out of all the Mexican places we've tried in the Nashville area this is the cleanest, has the best food, could be on any side street in the Mission in SF, is inexpensive, has decent margaritas, pleasant atmosphere, and most importantly genuinely sweet, lovely people working there.
qCOr-SXLYq5zT9H7jwBB_w:
After hinting that I couldn't wait to try HoneyFire's Hoosier corn pudding (yep, I'm an Indiana girl), my husband surprised me by picking up a brisket dinner with Mac and cheese and the corn pudding.  The corn pudding was just perfect- not too sweet, mildly seasoned, and not runny/custardy!  The Mac and cheese was delicious- cheesy with a hint of green chiles.  The brisket slices were really great, also.  It was a huge portion!  They had the perfect amount of fat and smoke and we ate every bite, unlike some places where we have to remove a significant amount of fat.  The 2 sauces that came in the to go ramekins were both on point!  The 'heat' added great flavor without dominating the meat, and the 'sweet' was balanced just right!  Both of them together made the perfect sauce for me.  The cost was $21 (plus tip, because my husband said the person that took care of him was really friendly), and I am totally willing to pay for that kind of quality in Bellevue!  
I cannot wait to go back and try their ribs!

We stumbled across this place and found a gem!! The staff is super friendly and the BBQ is legit!! Had the whole shebang plate and there everything we had as advertised. The brisket had great bark and super flavorful but the best surprise was the banana peanut butter cookie pudding. Not to mention a full bar and a bourbon selection like no other. I wish I lived in Nashville so this could be my regular hang out.

I love my BBQ, practically paid for my college serving bbq pork, chicken and ribs at a MTSU so I like to joke I have a degree in Aerospace Operations and BBQ :).  That being said I was excited to go check out this new bbq spot in Bellevue and they did not disappoint! I think it could be the best restaurant at Bellevue one! We ordered the wings nachos and brisket and each had big flavor and delicious! I am looking forward to trying the smoked sausage next time. We will be back soon! Glad to see a local spot in Bellevue killin it!
yT4l7HVC-8tepyFv27qD6w: This little hole in the wall serves up some super tasty food! The people are kind, food is delicious, & the price is cheap!

Absolutely delicious! It is hard to find Latin American food in Nashville that is both authentic and tasty. This place hits the spot. I had the tacos dorados and the pupusas which I miss having grown up in LA. Definitely going back soon.

Great food at a good price. Baleadas were excellent just like the plátano fritos. The people were friendly and I definitely recommend to those who like Honduran food - lord knows there aren't many options in Nashville.


Also, ignore the 2 star review below.... the clown even says he thought the food was good but he just got a slightly dirty table (that he seated himself at). Boohoo. Reviews like that can be detrimental to small businesses, but he just wasn't coddled enough. Go gentrify another establishment.
px1n_QrCT6eX7n-ZV4z2Sw: Excellent wings! The louisiana rub is amazinggggg! Ask for extra crispy. The staff has always been friendly and efficient.

This was my first time at a Wing Stop and it won't be my last! Great food, great service, great atmosphere. I'm a sales rep and noticed they had wifi and outlets everywhere. It will be a good spot to stop for a working lunch or pick up food for customer lunches (they also have boneless wings) or lunch and learns. Great addition for this area!

First time to try today and nothing short of amazing wings. Because of the recent popularity of hot chicken, some wing stores take pride in mislabeling their heat levels, for instance medium is almost inedible, but here they were labeled about right. Huge mound of tasty fries for a couple of dollars. Lots of room inside for eat-ins and they also feature a simple app for takeout orders. My service was quick and served up by a cheerful, talkative young lady. If i had a negative it is that its located in a small parking lot not very easily accessible and near a filthy, gang infested WalMart so I probably won't visit here after dark.
cosvZvfx1ElZsQV5LW3X0A:
Fetch is the newly revamped Hot & Cold - it's also pretty similar in terms of menu and decor. The idea was to have a more convenient spot for people to come pick up their coffee and/or pastries, since most people who go to Fido's end up ordering food. They offer Las Paletas, coffee, pastries, and even kombucha. As someone who frequents Fido's for their local latte (local honey + cinnamon, so good!), it was nice not having to wait in the usual long Fido's line just for coffee. 

They also currently have a few spring/summer signature drinks, which includes lots of different coffee and tea options. From taking a peep at the menu, I also think they're planning on introducing beer on tap soon as well!

Great coffee! I went into the sit down version and was annoyed with the long line, so was happy they had a smaller shop for coffee. There was one person working so it took a little longer than I wanted it to (why I did 4 stars.) But the barista was really great and also an amazingggg latte artist. I agree that the space isn't as "comfy" as the other, but as someone who lives in nyc, this is a nice spot to grab a coffee and people watch / meet with someone / do some waiting.

FETCH! is Fido's to-go counter part, located right next door. I stopped in to grab a coffee while in the area. All I wanted was an iced coffee, so it was a nice change of pace to avoid the normal lines in their spot next door. They had some pre-packed items to go in a refrigerator in the back, although I didn't check them out.  The coffee was served up quickly. The space wasn't inviting and cozy like Fido, and I wouldn't grab my coffee and sit there to drink it. Definitely a place to get in and out of.
_ZnlXUVbNgLSa5f3pIjdSA: The bacon burguer was good, chili was very good. The slide for the kids was great. Good service
Excellent burger and the fries are bomb as hell. Went for lunch and they knocked out my order promptly. Service was very friendly as well. Its a lunch time must!

We hit up Hugh Baby's on Friday evening and could not write enough good things about our experience.

Ordering was quick and easy, and staff was friendly to help with out (somewhat) picky child. The burgers came out quick and were the perfect size. I could defined put 2 or 3 of them down on a good day, but my burger & fries were just the right amount.  My wife had the cheeseburger with everything on it, and she's still talking about how good it was four days later. I went with the mushroom & swiss burger, and again was the perfect ratio of toppings to meat. Our son enjoyed his plain cheeseburger & chocolate shake. 

The seating area is really a cool setting, especially on this cool Friday evening as they had the garage door open to the outdoor seating area. Made of a nice environment and just an overall enjoyable experience.

Can't wait to make it back for trip #2.
lqjnEMpunyshKpsKa9a66A: This is the best food I've had from a food truck. The meat portions on the sandwich were generous and were in line with most bbq places. The line was long because so many folks wanted it but they kept it moving. Nothing short of awesome.  May need to start bringing a second truck with all that demand. Great that the opry lets you bring it in so get a front row balcony seat to set the container on the ledge cuz it's almost too much to eat in one seating.

Well, those previous bad reviews were a year  ago. They mUstve heard the mesSage cuz we loved it. Some of the best bbq I've had and I've had a lot. Good bbq is tricky, I know. Maybe they're up and down but our experience was delish including the Cole slaw and sauces.
hVtsleCxdP7rPx0YbduBQA: I agree with previous reviews that the service isn't great (just a little slow). I only sat for a chai latte & muffin - the latte was very good and the muffin was standard. A little pricey for a cafe, but hotels tend to have higher food prices.

Beautiful place with a lovely atmosphere. We stopped to have a relaxing morning coffee on the outdoor patio. The barista was able to make a flat white, and it was properly done! Very cool place and would come back again.

Absolutely loved it here!! Our server was so wonderful to interact with, which usually either makes or breaks the experience.
Coffee is perfection here!! I chose pour over. Food was wonderful and flavors were exquisite. Beautiful decor. Modern and elegant feel. Didn't want to leave. :) Coming back soon!!
FJ3OEwJqi9AA_ZiCNnf_3g:
I have ordered three times from this location as well  but for inside pickup. Yes parking is a pain and I would never waste time in a drive through. However, they have always gotten my order correct even down to the random sauces I asked for. Food was always ready within a couple minutes after clicking "make my order."  I don't know how you can be overcharged when making an order through the app. You choose your own items and pay for them yourself. 

My only suggestion would be to expand parking to areas around the restaurant so that cars can park when the drive through line is blocking everything.
This Chick Fil A is as good as all the ones I've ever been too. Food is great as usual! The only issue is the parking situation; the lot is not meant for the crazy crowds of Chick Fil A!

So very happy to have a brand new Chick-fil-a near my neighborhood in Nashville! This CFA is an awesome addition to this side of town! Per expectations, this location did not disappoint in being an above & beyond fast food restaurant. Very clean interior and exceptionally kind staff. 

The drive through line can be a bit long at times which makes it hard to enter the parking lot if you're just trying to dine in or pick up a to-go order, but it moves fairly quickly & you can get in and out in good time. 

This location also offers the curbside pick-up via the app, which is an excellent alternative to waiting in the drive thru or going in and getting it to-go! It also offers the other app options like carry out orders, drive thru pre-order, and dine in order where they bring it to you at your table!
ueSknQNYtV0jQmGEoDTerQ: We lucked out and hit the BBQ weekend menu. The coffee chicken and pulled pork were amazing. Beer selections were a little sparse but thought out and more importantly local (except for the bud lights) and the staff were friendly and welcoming! If you are a fan of bearded Iris and a fan of good food this is a no brainer!!!!

This local restaurant will amaze you. The weekends are really something to enjoy why?, the ribs of course. Their tender and smoked to perfection. Make sure you try the sides as well. The ribs go good with their potato salad and southern bake beans. Ohh, they also have a white chicken chili with a kick. Will warm you up, not to spicy, but just enough to open up stuffy sinuses. Plus it's lite for when you wanting a soup but nothing heavy. 
Almost forgot, if your a coffee drinker they have a local coffee that is sure to please. Either way you won't be disappointed. Local and healthy.

We were just in town for a visit, but Will and Rak made us feel like locals! They provided excellent recommendations for food and entertainment, and their own selections were delicious. It was very convenient to have a market that stocked staples as well as homemade food so close to home. The white chicken chili is not to be missed! The owners clearly want to be a part of their community, and the locals who come in make the visit a great experience.
Pnh2dLVC_yeudmLSdWGzhQ:
I heard multiple recommendations that Prince's was better than Hattie's B's. From the two experiences I had there, although the initial wait to order for Hattie's B's is longer, the wait to receive your food at Prince's is honestly a bit unreasonable (after visiting twice). 

I definitely recommend the wings over the chicken fingers, which are a bit dry. Unfortunately, they ran out of wings on the second night. The spices are amazing. The fries are nothing special and the pickles are a bit soggy. The surrounding atmosphere is really what makes it. It's next to a brewery and distillery, and there are a ton of outdoor games (ping pong, bocce, cornole, basketball, etc.) Great place for food and drinks if the weather is nice!

Came here on a tip from my Uber driver about how to get Hot Chicken without a mega line. The food was pretty tasty. I loved the hot sauce, and the breading was really flavorful and crunchy. I wish I'd gone with the wings, as the tenders were a bit dry. I also found it really salty, but I get the sense that's how it's supposed to be. "Hot" was definitely hot, so take their advice when they describe the spice meter. All the staff were super friendly and quick with service. Nice spot for your Hot Chicken hankering!
