Way hotter than expected - in an amazing way. Not a generic hot sauce, baked into fried chicken. Helpings are very filling. Everything is cooked for order so can be a little wait.
c6hWRqafQMueVq9WWBp0VA:
That's right folks you heard it here first. The words affordable and downtown used synonymously in the same sentence. Breakfast wise I had one of the most delicious omelettes with all the fancy garnishments. I paid eight dollars and a the portion was more than enough. 

The reasons for being downtown vary for a lot of people. Maybe it's a trip for business or a fun weekend abound. Or like me you frequent the area daily for work. 

The sum of all these parts is simple you won't find a better "bang" for your buck downtown. For a meal in the single digits (what you might pay for a latte) there isn't much of a risk.

First of......let me just tell YOU about this little gem I found this morning!! Best tacos, quesadillas, and fruit cups in the whole state of TENNESSEE!!! This has to be a MUST if you can make it here. Food is fresh and amazing!! I got the carne asada Taco, pollo asado taco, taco rojo, quesadilla, and fruit cup...now I know what your thinking..."DMAN, she eat all that?!?" The answer is NO! But I would have! 
I had two friends with me! Like I said, this is a MUST! Luna Llena Taqueria has taken over my heart. Luhhhhh this place!

Found this place while exploring and looking for a bathroom. The smell when walking in the door is fantastic. The food is very fairly priced and plentiful portions for the price. The food is fantastic. My group ordered a quesadilla, a burrito bowl, some tacos, and the potato casserole. All of them were fantastic and got demolished. I forgot to take pictures before eating as it was that appealing.
YuatbQ1sfTFnmfgjzVDv-g: Went back a second time. The patio is awesome and the food was delish. The seasonal vegetable salad was to die for. I'm not a salad person but this....SOOO GOOD! Pizza was good too.

It's great to have a new restaurant in Green Hills (previously a food desert for such a busy area!).

This place is excellent and they really care about the quality of their food and your satisfaction with it! If something isn't perfect, they are so quick to fix it happily.

I've had several of the salads and a few of the pastas and there are still tons of things on the menu that I can't wait to try.

I've tried this place two times. The restaurant is nicely laid out and attractive. The food was delicious but...

1. The service still hasn't gotten ironed out a few weeks in. My salads are an example. On the first visit, I was brought a second salad because nobody knew I had received the first. Not the worst sin, but on my second visit, I was asked if I'd ever gotten my salad (I had.) while I was eating my entree. It seems something is amiss with salads. And the waiting time for my food (both times during off hours) was too long for simple items, especially salad. 

2. The price point is a bit on the high side. I had a half Caesar salad, bolognese, and soda, and my bill came to over $30 with tax. That seems steep for what I got (complexity of recipe, portion size, expense of ingredients). They will do fine in Green Hills, but look at the menu prices before going. 

The food quality got 4-5 stars but couldn't salvage the scoservice and price. 

Update: I appreciated the personal response from the restaurant's management and have faith they want to work with feedback. The personalized and timely response raises my review from 3 to 4 stars.
FPbh-t9fTEc5x2SCresK6A: Coffee is awesome! Roasted in house. Chorizo egg burrito is excelente! Highly recommend especially for those coffee connoisseurs.

This is probably one of the best secrets going with coffee in Nashville.  Not only is the coffee amazing, but it's located in Plaza Mariachi.  The owners take coffee very seriously and bring an authenticity to it as well as try their hand at a few creations.  Iced coffee is on point but the secret is Cafe Con Leche.

We totally loved this coffee shop! The coffee is exceptionally good. We bought 3 bags of their Brazilian Roast and Mundo Blend to share with friends and family! We loved that you could also walk around and enjoy the shops in the plaza and listen to the music. Josh and Misty were so awesome and served their fresh roasted coffee with excellence. We are already planning to go back and take friends.
Yu_tXvUoyOj5SpeDUOZwnQ: Wow. So f$$king good! Wow. Hands down the best vegan fare I've had. This place is worth a visit. I dare say, better than meat has ever dreamed of being. Order out to speed things up, and make sure you have plenty of napkins. We dived into our sandwiches and cheesy tots with glee. Two thumbs up.

Think you've had the best vegan food? Well if you haven't been here then I would be willing to bet ya. 

Seriously, this place is amazing. From their frozen and refrigerated deli items to pop up dinner events to weekend breakfast/lunch dishes--the BE Hive never fails to impress. 

I honestly would recommend anything they have but my must-have favorites would be their taco meat, queso (seriously be-hive what's a girl gotta to do get this stuff on tap??), and chiken. 

If you are stopping by for food on a weekend, I highly recommend the Chickn Philly, CrunchWrap Supreme, and Breakfast Tots. 

Can't wait to see how they expand and continue to grow. Because I am all in favor for more BE Hive in my life.

This is one of my favorite places in Nashville! I count down the days to every weekend because of their deli! My favorite thing I've tried has been the Reuben loaded potato bowl...so good! Their cookies are amazing as well. I practically stalk their Instagram to see what they're doing every week, and I love that they change it up with the specials but also keep a regular deli menu. Everyone who works here is so nice and accommodating! Overall there isn't a single bad thing to say about this place. You won't be disappointed.
NsmuVeQ0znxG2nq9T5uGug:
Love the place! Good food. Had a brisket w/fries and I killed it in 3mins. 

I rate it a 4 star because I wish they had TVs inside. It could be just 2 at least. My friends and I wish we were watching the game while we were eating.

The good: I love Edley's barbecue, but I think Central may be my new favorite! I had been to the one in Memphis and loved it so I knew I had to try the one in Nashville when it opened. From their pulled pork to their ribs and nachos, you really can't go wrong! It's all sooo good. I also love that you can pick your sauces and they have a station for sauces so you can try 'em all! I was a bit worried about parking, but there is a huge garage with ample FREE parking. I know, this is unheard of in downtown Nashville nowadays!

The bad: If it weren't for the service I would give Central BBQ 5 stars. Typically when a new restaurant opens up you expect the service to be spotty, but it was worse than expected. When we arrived the line was out the door and was veryyyy slow. That was because they only had one person working the register at dinner time on a Saturday night. They also have a rule that you can't get a table until you've ordered your food, which means more people were standing in line and out in the cold (since the line was out the door). Second, the table next to me ask for a plate to split their food. Instead of offering to grab them one, the waitress pointed to a table where they could "go get" one.

Awesome awesome awesome. Walk in, order, they'll bring it out to you. Quickly. I was in there on a Thursday for lunch. Place is packed, but the turnover was quick. Sat down, ate good food. And left. Their sauces are good, they give you too much food (as a good bbq place does). Pork was good and brisket was really good. Someone I was with had the wings and I tried one, also really good. I was really pleased here. Sides were good too.
hVHN_73Cuk89G13mKU4CFw: I've eaten my fair share of chicken from fast food chains to Amish restaurant to fine restaurants serving chicken. So far Hella Hot Chicken and Bolton's Spicy Chicken & Fish reign as my favorite. Hella Hot Chicken (HHC) is a hole in the wall. Its exterior facade mirrors its interior environment, dilapidated, grimy, and uninviting. The toilet room stinks and there are little bugs strutting around like it was naturally at home. But if you can ignore the ambience and leave your preconceived expectations at the door then you'll rewarded with very good chicken. The chicken is crunchy, lightly breaded and adequately oiled. The spice is potent but isn't overwhelming. I also recommend their homemade juice bottle. I bought the mango juice and it was good. Remember, when you visit, be patient. Unlike Church's chicken which bask under a heat lamp for who know how long, freshly fried chickens require time so if you don't want to wait, I'd suggest you do call ahead and do carry-out. Good food requires an exercise in patience and inconvenience. If you want fast and convenience then by all means visit fast food chain or the supermarket. I'm giving this place a 4 out of 5 stars, a star deducted for low hygiene. I hope the owner realize cleanliness is good for business, clean up their act and rise above the pecking order to s

I've eaten my fair share of chicken from fast food chains to Amish restaurant to fine restaurants serving chicken. So far Hella Hot Chicken and Bolton's Spicy Chicken & Fish reign as my favorite. Hella Hot Chicken (HHC) is a hole in the wall. Its exterior facade mirrors its interior environment; dilapidated, grimy, and uninviting. The toilet room stinks and there are little bugs strutting around like it was naturally at home. But if you can ignore the ambience and check your preconceived expectations at the door then you'll be rewarded with very good chicken. The chicken is crunchy, lightly breaded and adequately oiled. The spice is potent but isn't overwhelming. I also recommend their homemade juice bottle (hint: mango flavor). Remember, when you visit, be patient. Unlike Church's chicken or Popeyes which bask under a heat lamp for who know how long, freshly fried chickens require time so if you don't want to wait, I'd suggest you call ahead and do carry-out. Good food requires an exercise in patience and inconvenience. If you want fast and convenience then by all means visit fast food chain or the supermarket. I'm giving this place a 4 out of 5 stars, a star deducted for low sanitation hygiene. I hope the owner realize cleanliness is good for business, clean up their act and rise above the pecking order to success.

Probably some of the best grease (I mean chicken) in Antioch. They never answered the phone so I could call ahead of time so I had to wait about 30 minutes to get my order just to get home and realize it was wrong. (Fault of my own) BUT!! The chicken still has a really good flavor. And is actually seasoned right. The customer service was also really nice even with the long wait. I saw the potential in it being good if my order was prepared right sooooo I will be going back. Just be sure to check your order before leaving.
8yy8PtUdZv_3XxmmrLNhvw: Friendly staff, great food. I order a veggie quesadilla and it was great, they also offered me a sample of their new smoothie like thing which was great in taste.
I had the craftsman breakfast sandwich and it was really delicious. It took a while to make (about 15 mins) but it was worth the wait if you have time.
The breakfast burrito is epic.  These guys git a good thing going here.  Coffee was solid as well
ez3QHFQDCbdhzBtU58dbKg: Amazing tasting food. Food is the best authentic Kurdish tasting food. The food presentation is beautiful! Friendly service. The restaurant is a must try experience.

Went with a party of 7. We ordered a variety of dishes from the menu including shawarma, kabobs for the kids, falafel, and the mixed grill. Then finished off with Turkish coffee and baklava. Wow!The flavors were spot on! Food served fresh with friendly and attentive staff. We will definitely be going back...ASAP!
Incredible service and food to match the great vibes! Exciting flavors that made me want to come back for more. I highly recommend this restaurant for anyone looking for delicious new flavors!
QFGzX6DW9Fsdfo6uSGVhjg:
Went to this place for my birthday. I think this place is a good place to chill and vibe. Music is good, service is good, and the prices are reasonable. Drinks are less than $8 and the hookahs range from $12-$20! Will def be back! 

The only reason I gave them a 4 is because they don't do a body search before entering. There is no telling what these folks bringing in!

I like coming here because it is in the neighborhood and is rarely over packed. I have been going here since easily 2014, so I guess I am a regular. 

Weekdays are my favorite days to go for a quick, affordable drink and good music or even watch sports. I don't smoke hookah but I enjoy the environment. 

The have good service and if you happen to be hungry the shrimp basket is so good!!! 

Tip: It's typically free on weekdays, booths are rarely over $150 (split within your group) and there is usually a couple free sections that are first come first serve. Every hookah location in Nashville that plays hip hop all night will charge for a booth.

What a great find in a strip mall in Antioch! Great hookahs, lots of tables and they have beer. Score!

Staff is all super friendly (don't forget to tip!) and the vibe is really relaxed. They have chairs outside if you want to sit there (though it does seem to be a set group who sit out there). They have a section up front where the older guys gather to play card/domino games. And they have an Xbox room in the back where you can hear dudes cheering each other on playing racing games. 

I haven't eaten here, so I can't comment on the food. I will say the bathrooms aren't the cleanest I've ever been to, but they aren't too bad either. 

The hookah is cheap, the vibe is chill and did I mention they have beer? Easy fun place to have a smoke with friends.
nXlIBnOyb-dgi8MSHIxY9g: New place in Nashville! It was fun! We stoped, because our 15 year old wanted to throw an ax. Please note, you have to be 18 to throw. Food was good, though. Highly recommend the Pizza Fries - super yummy!! Burgers were really good, too.

Came into town for conference and signed up for ace throwing for 1 and was paired up with an awesome coach David. He talks like a real sports coach, talking about body mechanism and how to correct incorrect posture- I love it! We played 3 games and I had an amazing time! Would highly recommend this place! Sign up and reserve your spots online via the website!

DAVID is a GEM! 
I was terrified to start throwing and he was the best. He incorporated a bunch of fun games and really got into it with us. If you're coming here, ask for David and you won't be disappointed. Also, the Nashville food and event  recommendations he gave us made our trip incredible. THANKS DAVID, you were the best!!
WxZ2Ua5hb7g3hZqWjw5k6w: This little shop just opened up not too long ago and is a great local spot to grab a 'za and some wings to go. You can order right through the app and pick it up in no time. The pizza was great and had the right amount of toppings. It looked like all the toppings are fresh, not from a jar or can which is nice. The wings are great!! Perfect size and perfect pepper flavor. Really good place. I'll be sure to order from them again! Worth a try for sure instead of going to Papa Johns or Pizza Hut.
Awesome food with quality ingredients and generous portions.  Service is wonderful and the restaurant is immaculate.
I ordered a cheese pizza and it was phenomenal. One of the better pies that I have tasted here in Nashville, especially for the price. Their garlic sauce is also unique and very good. 5 stars.
u86rWYwiqTDk-c17qorF6w: Love Wingstop on Elliston Place! Great food, great service, and big screen TVs. Watching Wimbledon and enjoying lunch. I must comment on how clean the bathrooms were. I hate to put this comment in with food, but I've been to many upscale restaurants not this clean. Thank you!

We love Wingstop on Charlotte Pike and stopped in the newest location on Elliston Place. What a friendly group of folks! We ate inside and watched College football on their 5 TVs. Great Service. Great vibe. Great location. We will be back!

BEST. WINGSTOP. EVER. The food is always hot and my order is always spot on. They're quick, clean and EXTREMELY friendly. Chris is a phenomenal employee and he's one of the main reasons I keep coming back!
Ff7CVmNORVLNsV3L-lrkvw: This place is great! The restaurant is very clean and nice decor. The food is really amazing. We had the hummus and the Suzma (yogurt based cheese) as apps. They were very fresh and yummy. I had osh (traditional Uzbeck meal) which has lamb over rice and veggies. It was very good. Someone else also ordered the gyro plate and chicken kabobs, and everyone thoroughly enjoyed their meals. The prices are fair for the amount of food served. If you're looking to try something different this is a great place to try! The staff is very friendly as well.

Excellent Customer service and obsoletely delicious food. Took my family here and my picky  child couldn't stop eating and talking about the food we tried various dishes such as the Trout, Salmon, Lentil Soup, Osh - which is their specialty. 

This will be our family site for many more meals. 
I highly recommend the Osh Restaurant. You won't  regret it.

We were so busy eating that non of us took a photo to share!
Really pleasantly surprised with this little place serving authentic middle eastern.  Will definitely be back.
qLWIKYCCsu_b4NNqgmE3JQ:
This Cava is located right in the heart of the 5th + Broadway area and was a great place to stop for a quick, healthy meal in the middle of a busy day in downtown Nashville. Everyone working there was so friendly and helpful. There was plenty of seating inside and some open tables outside as well. The bowls were fresh and delicious! I love all the options at Cava and have never been disappointed; this experience was no different. Some of my personal favorite toppings include the honey harissa chicken, the crazy feta sauce, the falafel, the crispy pita strips, and the yogurt dill dressing, but I try to mix it up every time I go since there are so many options. 

I would definitely recommend this place to anyone in downtown Nashville looking for a fresh, easy Mediterranean meal!
Great Greek salad for a lighter lunch. I wouldn't say the service was stellar (girl at the register kind of threw my food at me)- but overall solid 4 stars on the food!

First time trying this place & id definitely go back! Not a fan of the taziki but their version of Caesar dressing & the garlic dressing (not together) was super good! Everything tasted really fresh. I got the grilled chicken in mine. They also have organic cane sugar (I think that's what it was) sodas. I got the black cherry it was DELICIOUS!! Also don't forget to get the pita chips- seriously good for scooping it up! Yum! I don't really love Greek food usually either. Bonus: there is a bathroom in the restaurant which means not searching around the mall for one!
7pI1m7PY0ddlm3vJUVX33w: Gyro Star's food is really delicious! The food is fresh, hot & well seasoned. Hands down best Gyro spot in Hermitage/Nashville! I ordered the Falafel platter, It has a generous serving size, A lot of fresh rice, Salad, Hummus spread & warm Pita Bread on the side which was a lot of food for the best price! You will not be disappointed! Also, the place was very clean & the people were friendly & very respectful. Love this place! Will be my Go-To spot for Falafel Meals.

This place is fantastic. The service is not super fast because they cook it to order. The two guys working today were the absolute nicest guys. They took our order for six people without writing anything down. The food is fresh great quality great taste. This is such a pleasant find in this area
This is a great place to order and pick up. It was our first time trying it out and we will be back. It is close to our house and food was good!
_vetnsfyQcbeYfxoiTIOPA:
I was working hard to get into Zeppelin on Saturday night. They had availability, but OpenTable was giving me hell due to technical issues. I attempted to call the establishment, but to no avail. After giving up for 6 hours, I was able to make a reservation on Google for 6:45. The time the reservation was made was at 6:20pm. I scurried to get myself gussied up for this occasion. Being in the midst of a pandemic, it isn't often I get to treat myself or give myself to get cute, so this acted as an occasion to do both.

Upon arrival, I was a bit confused on where to go. Zeppelin is atop the TownePlace Suites, which is Marriott owned. Zeppelin is independent of Marriott and does not have an affiliation minus the space it shares with the hotel. Entry to go into the restaurant is on the side of the building and does not entry into the hotel itself. You take an elevator up to the top.

Inside Zeppelin, you're adored with beautiful modern décor with pink lighting. I love the lighting in here and even decided to be basic and wore pink for cute Instagram photos. The interior really is sleek and stylish. The music met the mood upon entrance, and Dolly was singing over the bathroom and outdoor speakers. The restaurant music was more vibey towards low-fi beats meets house music without the headache.

I love having rich dining experiences that will carry me for a while. This was one of those times where I went for a solo dinner to celebrate a special occasion and it met that 'rich dining' expectation. So, what do I mean by rich? I mean you feel like a million bucks dining here. You get the topnotch service, the delectable tasting food, and the view that makes it worth the while.

The food and service carried this experience. Jeffrey, was my server, but he is actually the service director of the establishment. He was acting place of someone who wasn't able to work that evening. He obviously is a team player and has a great team working with him. I enjoyed conversing with him and he is obvious and in very deliberate in ensuring the customer has a solid dining experience. That is another thing that needs to be understood when dining at Zeppelin; it isn't a just dining, it is an experience. 

I tried some exceptional items including: crispy brussels, charred carrots, beef kalbi ribs, and key lime pie for dinner, then for drinks their old fazzerac and a glass of red wine. These items are sharable and could mix with anyone's palette. I pretty much gave Jeffrey free reign of what to send to me since I wanted to get a scope of their offerings. I was more than content. 

The brussels were my favorite out of the two apps. Honestly, I love the carrots, too, because the spreads that accompanied them on the plate were flavorful and creative. The brussels were sweet, savory, charred, and smoky all at once. With every bite, you experienced a new taste and it takes you off guard a little bit because you're not sure what you're supposed to focus on. That was the fun part of trying the brussels. 

The ribs were decent. I felt they were flavorful and accompanied the apps extremely well. I took leftovers home and they tasted just as great the second time as they did as the first.

Drinks and the dessert were perfect adds to the meal. I really liked the key lime pie, I am the person who prefers fruit desserts over chocolate any day. It was light, refreshing, and a lovely ending to the experience. 

I look forward to returning and hope you get there soon, too!
Amazing service and amazing people. Good drinks menu isnt all that not a lot of options but definitely an overall amazing place

Had a wonderful time at the bar inside Zeppelin. Great attentive service from multiple bartenders. Everyone was friendly and helpful. The vibe is very cool with music that's not too loud. Nice rooftop bar outside too with multiple fire pits. Ran into some friends from high school here too. I think this is going to be our new bar to hang out as locals.
XzzGAAk_E3FJ9bdri71oCA: Very solid cheese steak. The Amoroso make it. Classic philly style with the wiz too. The meat is not the greatest but it's top 2 cheese steaks in Nashville. Only thing they are missing is the crab fries and white cheese dipping sauce! Would highly recommend and will be back.

Food it's good looks like they're going through growing pains not ready for a crowd I waited over an hour for one sandwich system went down but they could have gone back the pencil and paper for the orders I will be going back.

So I don't write many reviews, but this place deserves mention.  There are downsides to it;  there is no inside dining, but they have some tables next to a nice grassy area;  you have to wait in line outside, but they have put up tents to keep you in the shade or out of the rain; and the biggest downside to me is that they are only open Wed-Saturday 12-5 PM.  I finally was able to try the cheesesteak today, and all the downsides melted away!  The sandwich is large, plenty for two for lunch.  The roll, which I'm told is from a Philly bakery, is wonderful, soft, chewy, and very tasty.  The beef is more than plentiful, tender, mixed well with the grilled onion, and very good.  Lastly, the cheese is literally the melted nectar of the gods.  All together, it's the best cheesesteak I've ever had, in or out of Philly.  I want to shake the owner's hand!  Highly recommend you overcome any of the above obstacles, drive to Bellevue, and grab some heaven.
7OC_kL9FajIxa1oaEcEONA:
The best food in town and excellent service 100/100
-Octopus
-Wagyu Skirt
French Onion soup
-Chocolatte Panna Cotta

Delicious food! Pimento hush puppies were hit!! It's small in quantity (only 3) but boy were they delicious.

Jumbo shrimp ain't so jumbo haha but nevertheless really good! 

Def my new favorite happy hour!

Loved this place. Cool beachy feel. Nice service and great food. Medium rare Kobe beef burger and pork belly tacos. Yumm! Stayed right a cross the street at the Tru Hotel. Came back our last night in Nashville for dessert and glass of wine.
LgItzDqxncbd5x-TQFvUPg: Went to bad axe with a bachelor party and had an awesome time!  Sam was great!  She was helpful friendly and helped make sure we had an awesome experience!

Love this place and the excellent adventure!  Booked online for a small group of 4 and felt instantly welcomed! Colt was our trainer and knew just how to show us what to do and what NOT to do!  I always throw better after a tequila shot and a beer. The place is new, clean and best of all, FUN! Super experience on a weeknight to boot!
Great time! My family and I spent a few hours here and we had a great time. The staff was polite and attentive. We will for sure come back when we are in Nashville again!
wv3pQnSxA3sR6QqBova-xA:
Welcome to Nashville Sixty Vines!
Love the Wine Tap and the food was awesome!!!
Santa Maria Tri Tip, Crispy Zucchini, Bacon Wrap Dates and the Meatballs
pork & beef, herb roasted san marzano tomato sauce, pecorino roman.

My friend and I came for a week night dinner and it was the best experience!  Our server Sam was super knowledgeable and kind.  She helped me pick out 3 wines to get 2.5oz pours of to create my own flight.  I did one white, one rosé, and one red.  All were excellent!  We shared the burrata to start was was absolutely delicious.  I had the margherita pizza which was excellent and my friend had a pasta dish!  We both had enough for leftovers to take home.  Overall the service was great, the wine was fabulous, and the food was excellent.  It's a really beautiful space too.  I can't wait to be back!
Can not say enough about how great and friendly the staff (especially Kala, our waitress) was! The food was so fresh. Such a cool atmosphere!! Will be back!
8wRT3Bt0LUtXeh1-dqTs0Q:
We ordered food (cha shu rice) and drink combo, karaage rice, oolong milk tea, and Xia Lon pao.  

With not many good Asian food and boba tea in town, our expectations were not high even though we know this is part of Taiwanese franchise. 

Drink- Even though I prefer drink to be little thicker, but it was pretty good and boba was soft. 

Food- Cha shu was diced small and but cooked well and very juicy. With other siding on the plate it was nice. Karaage was over the bowl of rice. Chicken pieces were big enough to actually taste the chicken, and it was very juicy. 
Xia Lon pao took little longer than other food. But they told us at the time of order, so it came out within reasonable time. Only the down side of this was few Xia Lon pao were stuck together, and when we separated out, all the juice drained.....

From the land of great boba tea in SoCal, this place is a home away from home. The milk teas are great and the food is an A+ set up. The pricing is quite reasonable. Boba shouldn't cost as much as a meal. Will come again!

The food is deliciously made and attractive.  My friends and I have been a frequent customer since it has been here in Nashville.  The ramen is amaAaaaazing, and consistently great.  So is the rest of the menu.  I can say,  I was disappointed that they wanted charge me for water last time i was here!!! Lol.  If you are a bubble tea and ramen fan in Nashville,  you should try it.
MJgHI9yuGej00bemw0R_uw:
Agave's is amazing. The price is fair for the delicious food you get. We have been four times in the past 3 weeks. It's that good! The people who work there are nice, and they're very fast with takeout orders. 

Try and see for yourself!! You won't be disappointed!

Hard to find breakfast spots that aren't crowded, but this newcomer to Belle Meade is a great addition, and it's BIG with lots of seating, so no wait. Friendly, fast service. Menu for both the gringo and lover of authentic Mexican food. We had chilaquilas with carne asada, which were perfectly prepared. Mexican coffee, unsweet but with cinnamon, was made extra special with the small pitcher of steamed milk served with it. Biscuits served as a free app were not worthy of the calories though, and I'd like to see some other breakfast burrito options. Retried beans also were done well.

Fajitas for take out were great, loved it was chopped up and mixed with veggies. Can't believe it took so long for us to eat here we will now! Guac could have been better (pretty sure it was from a package vs freshly made).
cughmwlt4Y19rUgADPNd6g: Really amazing shredded beef burrito and chicken burrito. Not a fan of how creamy the guacamole is, I prefer it a bit chunky.

Kept hearing the hype about this place from co workers and classmates and wow, they weren't kidding.. ordered the burria tacos with consume and their guacamole and chips. It was so good and also was made very quickly.... The line started to form as I was eating but I noticed they went really quick, but also heard you can order online? I will for sure be back here again. So good !

The tacos are very filling and tasty. You're really full after two. I got the taco special which is 3 birria beef tacos with consommé. The consommé is so good! I ordered a drink of the Jamaica. My total was $13.40. That's not bad. I just haven't paid so much for tacos. That would be my only gripe. The staff were nice and friendly. I'm glad this location opened up in Donelson which is closer to me.
hEv9nJEcdswcxmX503HSJw: a place with toro and uni and its fresh omg. no need to say a lot this place has become my go to place.
I love Bite a Bit! I'm so glad it came to East Nashville. I've had the wings and the sushi. All of it is amazing!

This place is amazing! The food is really fresh and the service is great! The crab wonton was flavorful and though it's fried it wasn't a grease bomb. The torpedo roll ( I think that's the name) was delicious and the fish was very fresh. My husband for a noodle dish and asked for a 2 hot and it was HOT so just a heads up If you can't handle the heat. So delicious
jD4X5BpjoXgweqeCs64Ppw: This place was really good. I loved the whipped goat cheese and the bone in pork chop. The potato skins were also the best I've ever had.

Great staff! The only thing I might do to try and make it Fun is to find something else to put on big screens instead of the News! Food looked amazing will come back to eat I just am not sure what vibe they are going for.
We went to this place thinking it wasn't a sports bar type restaurant and well it was... the food we had was good, fried potato skins with pimiento is always right!!
eW46KumQ5fQ6dyYwAHRXuw: My first time trying E G & MC was this evening. The only thing better than the cocktails, the tapas, and the atmosphere was the service. This was pre-dinner, so I have two cocktails and tried the shrimp and crawfish dish. The food was powerfully seasoned but not overwhelming and the shrimp was cooked perfectly--no overdone rubbery seafood here. I was hesitant to try the Southern Hospitality cocktail, because I don't like overly sweet drinks but it was perfectly balanced and the vanilla wasn't too overpowering. My favorite was the dressed up + nowhere to go, and good God was it delicious. I'll be calling to ask where they get their jam (or if it's made in house). The host was so inviting and warm and our waitress was always around when we needed but not bothersome. I was smiling the whole time. Will be adding this to my go-to cocktail bar list.
Great atmosphere & the food was bomb! The Mac n cheese w sweet potato is A MUST GET. Sooo good. All of the staff was VERY friendly & did a fantastic job. Will be returning!

This place is definitely cool vibes and ambience, we sat outside and tried 4 different drinks between the two of us and it was good. Then we got the tapas sampler and it was plenty to share between 2-3 ppl. I would come back to try the entree for sure. Good music and a cool place for sure!
a76apdjXLdeHUuYRZH54rQ:
The food and atmosphere at The Nashville Grange are absolutely amazing. 
I have had lunch & dinner there and thoroughly enjoyed every delicious bite! The service was impeccable and I highly recommend this new Nashville restaurant!

We almost didn't eat here, the menu looked a bit to untraditional for us. But it ended up being the best meal we had in Nashville. We both got the pork chops and they were absolutely amazing. Prosciutto stuffed cabbage??? Oh my!  And the charred carrots were better than I ever would've expected. 
Weston was our server and absolutely amazing. He had great descriptions of everything on the menu and awesome suggestions. He was doubling as bartender and his drinks were fantastic as well. I've never had a server more knowledgeable about what is on the menu. 
Jax was the manager and she was awesome. Chatting with tables, bussing tables, delivering food. She really helped do it all. 
The presentation of the food was obviously something they take great pride in. The prices were very fair for what you get. 
The amazing service and teamwork obviously comes from the top down. Just a great experience.

The atmosphere here is really nice, though you do sometimes get loud groups here since the hotel the restaurant is located in is just up the street from Lower Broadway. There are floor to ceiling windows looking out on Broadway, providing a lot of light. Of course that also means you're going to periodically see party wagons going by, which could be distracting (or fun depending on your perspective).

The menu is full of southern style food. I had the biscuits and gravy with a side of bacon. The biscuits were very good, and the gravy was pretty good but not super tasty (and not even a hint of spicy). The bacon was good, but not amazing. I definitely think calling it "Life Changin' Bacon" is a bit ambitious. I was thinking this was going to be bacon that they put brown sugar or spices on. It was just plain bacon. Good, but not life changing.

The service was a little slow overall, but they were apologetic and said they were short-staffed. I wasn't in a hurry, and the service was good and friendly (just slow), so it didn't bother me. But a restaurant in the Grand Hyatt should never be in a position that they're short-staffed, especially when they have reduced capacity for social distancing.
VfjeIA7g9UdQvRokWeBo6g: Awesome pizza. Had the margherita, capricciosa and another one that I cannot recall. All pizze were very good, a bit of a soft crust for my taste, but this is what is the Napoli style pizza. Fresh ingredients, very tasty and delivered in a timely fashion. Excellent overall. FYI, I am from Italy and this pizza joint is legit and could definitely hold its own even in Italy. Will definitely order again!
Very good late night pizza stop  1/2 block off Broadway and the bars. The pizza dough was light, had a good chew. And the sauce was just right. Highly recommend to everyone.

Solid Neapolitan pizza. Buffala mozzarella, calabrian chili's, and other high quality ingredients are evident in the pizza. It's difficult to get Neapolitan right, Desano has done a good job at replicating flavors you will find in Italy.
UlrEGYuRd5pxW0n4iCR9CQ: I love that they have lots of different dipping sauces! I wasn't a fan of the ranch but the Buffalo was good! I'll definitely eat here again and try new dipping sauces. The fries had the same seasoning as the chicken.
The place to go in Nashville.  The food and service is top notch.  Family from CA is coming in town in a few weeks,  and Chicken Guy is number One on our places to visit.

Always skeptical of any chicken place but this was blown away by how good this was. 

I went on a Friday around six and it wasn't super packed but the lines moved can't anyway. 

The chicken is cooked amazing. Not greasy, they cook it fresh daily without it eve being frozen. Not too much coating either. 

Over 20+ sauces to choose from, I had the Special Sauce and Donkey and both were good 

The fries have great seasoning on them. 

Milkshakes were decent. 

It's a bit pricey but where it's at, you know it will be.
8uBU5Jnjz1IwnR2wCSO0Ag:
I had this place saved for a few days until fate stepped in and made me unknowingly walk by it after a Yelp event at Third and Home. 

The shop is ADORABLE inside. I was in love with the aesthetic, almost retro-y diner-y feeling of the shop. 

I went with their Oishii specialty drink, which was an "almond matcha shakerato". Not sure what that exactly means, but that's beside the point: it was DELICIOUS. Smooth and creamy matcha drink with a splash of sweetness.

Had I not eaten at the Third and Home even beforehand, I absolutely would've tried their omelette. Instead, I got a carrot cake cream pie as a late dessert. Very sweet and delicious as well. Super cake-y and a perfect choice for a quick snack. 

Gotta come back soon!
Such good coffee and the guys working there were soooo handsome good vibes too. We went three days in a row!!

lMoonshot Coffee Bar is in the first level of the new-ish TownePlace Suites hotel - but you can access the coffeeshop from outside without going into the hotel. I wish it had been around when I worked on James Robertson Pkway a few years ago! 

The coffeeshop is charming - very cute inside. There's some seating, but it's not large. We got our coffees to go. My husband got a classic Americano, but I tried their "Snow Day Drink Special" - Cardamom Rose Latte! Definitely a sweet treat over the snowy weekend. They also have a small pastry fridge with some pastries and sandwiches and drinks. I grabbed a lemon poppy seed treat for later, which was sweet and tasty. According to their menu, they also have some breakfast and lunch options. The service was great.

There isn't a lot in the immediate area around the coffeeshop other than the detention center and bail bondsmen's offices, but it's a quick walk from James Robertson Pkwy/Musicians Hall of Fame/Capitol/Nashville Public Square Park. I'm not sure if they have any dedicated parking (if they do, I didn't see it), but we were to street park easily and quickly nearby (but that it was a Sunday during the bad snow of February 2021). 

I'm not sure if they offer any online ordering (Toast, Doordash, etc.), but we were in and out quickly after ordering our coffees, and everyone inside was masked (except for people eating at a table that's a distance away from the ordering/coffee counter), so it felt like you could safely get a coffee during COVID. 

All in all, a really charming coffee shop with some great coffee, good service, and a lot of style. Stop by if you're in the area or walking around this part of downtown/Germantown.
TjGBwgF3KZphbUqhxx5gHQ: So, I ordered the chorizo, al pastor, and the hot chicken tacos. Let me tell you I have lived all over and part of that includes the southwest where they know what true tacos are.  These tacos did not disappoint.  The chorizo? 15/10 amazing, so much flavor I wish I had ordered a second one. The al pastor? 12/10 amazing, delicious, wow. The hot chicken? 20/10. But let me preface that I had to take a break half way through it was so hot, like I couldn't taste subtle flavors for a good 10 minutes after. Was it worth it? 100%. Would I do it again? Absolutely. My mistake? Not ordering sopaipilla. I saw it. I wanted it. I didn't order it. Don't make my mistake. Never had sopaipilla? Get it. You will thank me. Anyways, go here. It was a 2 mins drive from my Airbnb and will be a go to every time I come in town.
We went to Pie Town Tacos on a Thursday and it was empty and I do not understand why! The food is fantastic, the staff are friendly, and the patio is great.

10/10 the BEST tacos in Nashville! I'm a vegetarian, so I always get the tofu chorizo and they are so delicious. It's such a breath of fresh air to have a vegetarian taco that doesn't feel like an after thought (i.e. every topping tossed together). The tacos are simple (topped with just onions and cilantro) but honestly I prefer that as it it more authentic. Everything from the seasonings, to the corn shells to the salsas are truly *chefs kiss*. If you're looking for the perfect taco, look no further!
_3hT6D26_1uGR_W1RcmUaA:
Honestly, the fact that you have to scoop your own toppings into your cup was a bit of a nuisance at first. 

But later on after experiencing many different topping combinations. I have had a change of heart. It is the most  genius thing ever. There are no other boba shops that will let you do that. By all means. I am no boba connoisseur. But i know good milk tea when i taste it. 

Let us not forget they have a freaking trash can that is touch activated! All the staff there are always friendly and courteous. The owner is very passionate about boba tea as evidenced from them getting all their boba tea supplies and ingredients straight from Taiwan. 

With many boba options popping up in Nashville. I am happy that i have a boba place i can rely on! 

P.S. they have drip style Vietnamese coffee!
Great bubble tea!! We have gotten it delivered several times and have enjoyed it thoroughly!

I stumbled upon this place when leaving the TJs/Target/Panera shopping plaza, looking to quench my thirst after engaging in a full day of capitalist shopping with my mask on, and it now has quickly become my new favorite place to get boba in nashville! 

Many options, tasty boba, you can even customize it if you dont want that much ice / sugar in your drink! 

Staff is great and friendly! got my drink very quickly. I would highly recommend!
K7l98hoZ3c6Xkb45HKjp4A: Came here on a Saturday for lunch and loved it! The Assembly Food hall was pretty busy (not surprising for a weekend) but the line for Velvet Taco was short and sweet. I had the elote and chips appetizer and rotisserie chicken tacos and both we amazing! Very flavorful and filling. Definitely need to come back and try the other taco options and the red velvet cake.

Velvet taco is in the newly opened part of the Assembly Food Hall at Fifth and Broad!  We went for lunch.  There was a short line to order.  They take your phone number and text you when your order is ready which is great because you can sit anywhere in the food hall!  For lunch I did 1 taco and elote and my friend did 2 tacos.  If you are super hungry definitely do 3 tacos!  The menu is really creative and fun.  I loved my chicken taco and the elote and my friend loved the cauliflower taco!
Fantastic tacos, elote & chips with a marg! Definitely recommend. Tucked away in a corner at 5th & Broadway food court. Go find it!
HQt5nxRVlJKTw9stwwRY5Q: 5 stars just for the bourbon glazed beignets. It was so powdery & soft, the galze was around the whole beignet. I definitly would eat 2 of these. The Hot chicken & chedder was good we got it medium per suggestion but I felt like it wasn't too spicy would definitly try the Nashville spicy! The chicken was a bit too crispy I wish there was a but more chicken meat and less crisp. Both of the sanwhiches chicken was a little small for the bread but overall it was a quality meal! Also please only go it you have a long layover, worth the wait, but also consider how much time you have.

Hey! This place is really good! Had an amazing chicken sandwich. Pro tip: "mild" is the equivalent of a fast food spicy chicken sandwich. So, mild still has some kick to it.

Good for Nashville to have some local flavor in the airport that is quality and reasonably priced. There are a lot of airports around the country that could really learn from this.

I mean... this was good... and was also literally the only sit down option that had a full menu and drinks in Terminal D today.  The airport is under construction I think.  I had a two hour layover and I was REALLY looking forward to trying some Nashville Hot Chicken. 

I ordered the tenders with slaw and ordered it Nashville Hot.  Mind you... this is not the hottest sauce they have. I kid you not...I actually lost my voice for a few minutes.  Nose running.   Eyes watering.  I had finished a spicy Bloody Mary before my food came and had ordered another one... and then could barely drink it.  This Florida girl does not have the taste buds for this AT ALL.  I was sweating, then I got chills, then I couldn't talk,  I had no idea what was happening to me LOL!

Don't get me wrong, the food is good.  The slaw was actually REALLY good and the chicken was cooked perfectly.  The bartenders were great and FAST and ATTENTIVE which I super appreciate for an airport.  If I come through Nashville again,  though,  I'll try the sissy chicken I think
QfzhKUUUEOvHK7OuHJ5Cqg: We were across the lounge at an axe throwing place (also fun) and someone there mentioned the Cation Lounge and we were all in! I love that they have some local beers too while you hang out with cats. It's a unique experience and the cats are awesome!

What an awesome place! I visited the Catio for an hour of "kitty time" a few days ago and was in absolute heaven. There are so many tiny kittens and sweet older cats to play with! I was surprised by how friendly they all were and how unabashed they were around total strangers. Such a fun activity!
Really cute cat cafe we came across after going axe throwing in the same shopping center. Staff was friendly and they have lots of drinks to choose from.
zJInzEAjDXcKBw3Uj0UhSA:
I stumbled upon this place while looking for the Parthenon! Omg I got pulled right in just from the menu 
I was going to have catfish then I asked the young man what he likes and I went with it ! Chicken on a bun with this amazing sauce and a macaroni and cheese side to die for ! Thank you  it was amazing
I can't believe Red's doesn't have more reviews!! The hot chicken is the best I had in Nashville! Incredible quality and incredible prices! Highly recommend

My wife and I like to try new places to eat when we visit a city. While in Nashville we received a tip to try Red!'s 615 Kitchen. I like hot and spicy meals  so I ordered the hot chicken tenders. I am so glad I did not try the extra hot as the hot really were a little much for me. However, the chicken had a great taste and I really enjoyed the fries.
   If you want to sit outside, you look like that you are in an actual chicken coop. It was obvious that a lot of locals stop and get their meals to go each day. I am sure it is a hit with the Vanderbilt  college students. I give it 4 stars for its uniqueness, plentiful serving size and great taste at a decent price.
  A little history- Red's 615 Kitchen opened May 23 in West End at 115 27th Ave. N., Nashville, according to the company's website. The business, which also operates as a food truck, serves hot chicken, a catfish sandwich, various sides and more. Hog Heaven previously occupied the space but closed in November after more than 30 years in business. 615-400-1454.
akx1WMGLdSLX_QspMBn1mg:
If you're like me and love Mediterranean food, get some reservations at Lola
 ASAP! I Had a wonderful experience with a good friend at the newly-opened Lola this evening! The space, attention to detail, and the food was absolutely amazing! Highly recommend trying their food for any occasion - the options are plentiful and I'm definitely coming back! 

The Branzino was cooked to perfection and the ribs melted in your mouth! 

Definitely the most rich chocolate torte I've ever had, but it was DELICIOUS

My family and I had one of the best meals in a long history of good eating. We were treated with great warmth and care with a complete lack of pretentiousness. And the superstar is the food. We felt like we were special guests of the chefs kitchen. Every morsel was uniquely seasoned and scrumptious. As a big eater I was hesitant about small plates but we shared multiple dishes and I walked away absolutely content. The slow roast beef rib is well worth the price --three of us shared this epicurean delight. Vichyssoise soup fed our souls followed by uniquely prepared artichoke hearts and OMG braised pawns. And dessert--geez. Best flan ever and a chocolate cake the  has to tasted to be understood (savor it to the last moment). Service was excellent --friendly, professional and helpful. Uniquely European restaurants that is well worth it. Oh, terrific Spanish wines are a treat.

There is a lot to like about Lola. The space is very appealing - bright, open and it has a wonderful soaring cathedral ceiling. The service was everything you would expect. The food is well-plated and generally innovative and tasty. Selection of old-world wines (especially from Spain and Portugal) is different and welcome; however, while the Tempranillo offered was serviceable, I wish there were more selections of such wines by the glass as I would have loved to try a hard-to-find Portuguese Douro. 

On this first introductory visit for a late dinner, we ordered the following:

Mediterranean Salad - fresh and zesty with mustard greens and very good
Charcuterie Board - nice selection of tastes and good
Smoked Trout Dip - radish slices didn't really work for us but good
Pan Tumacca - should have been served hotter, but garlicky and very good
Octopus - should have been served hotter, but excellent
Cheesecake - lighter than most and citrusy and bright - excellent

Maybe it is too much to hope for (especially so early in its existence), but Lola unfortunately did not deliver on the promise of transporting us to a cafe in Spain, at least on this night. I've had the pleasure of visiting neighborhood tapas restaurants and bars in Madrid (as well as Barcelona, Seville, and Toledo) and, while Lola has its appeal and I am hopeful for its future, it does not yet offer a fairly comparable range of tastes or vibe in this writer's opinion.
tvmoUTS2O3EP2swVoEipMg:
Amazing vibe, amazing decor, amazing food, amazing drinks. 

This place is PERFECT for a late Friday afternoon visit. We stopped in and had many a cocktail and some appetizers (poke and crab dip) and they did not disappoint. 

It was a chilly day, but it was nice and warm and sunny on the rooftop. Loved it. I had so much fun here. Can't wait to return!
Great food, great views, great drinks! We stayed at the Virgin Hotel for a "staycation" and really enjoyed this rooftop. Had dinner here last night and came back from unlimited mimosas for brunch.

Honestly can't complain with the quality of the hotel bar food here. Was it the best cheeseburger in the world? Not necessarily but definitely comparable to burger places in the city. Also had the grilled cheese and tomato soup and was satisfied. Everything was spaced appropriately due to COVID. The waiter gave great service and the scenery is great! I went at night so a brunch visit is definitely on the list. Summer time with the pool is certainly going to be a vibe.
5lTI5Ahun9KFDSwBhTOwtA:
Unless I'm getting lucky every time I come here, I don't see what the negative reviews are all about. You have to wait for food at any fast food place and at least you know that means they're making it fresh. My local popeyes and I'm happy that it's here.

-- Ross Senack
This place has awesome food. As for the service, these kids are great. They are funny, nice, and kind. I love this place. Great service! Fun atmosphere.
iqUjgdqNjfZIz0Zs4Y2_cQ: My goodness that was delicious. Prices are pretty typical to a high end steakhouse, but every single thing we ordered was top notch. Will be returning.

A nice hotel restaurant. We were completely satisfied with bringing this back to the room. A nice range of options on a modest size menu. 

Chase is a terrific server. Very personable. Classy and attentive. He's the standout.

We stayed at the Hilton hotel for 4 nights and on a Wednesday night, we decided to come eat here since it's located inside the hotel. The actual restaurant seating area was closed but you could sit at the bar or at any nice couch/lounge section! 

Our server (I think her name is Whitley?) , was super sweet and was super informative on the best items on the menu!

We ordered a Caesar salad for me and shrimp & grits for the hubs. Appetizers, we got meatballs and wings.  We also got a chocolate lava cake with cookies and cream ice cream as a dessert.  10/10 across the board! The wings are definitely a must - it's super light / airy crunch with great flavor. Their sauces are definitely homemade. The shrimp and grits... wow! Super buttery and creamy with amazing shrimp and gravy sauce. 

Must ask if they have dessert because the lava cake was SO decadent and warm. 

Will definitely be staying at the Hilton hotel and coming to this restaurant every time we come to Nashville! If you are diamond status for Hilton, you should definitely stop by because they do offer specials for diamond status members - free things! :)
gdFcsJ5swnsyuFTzBRofkA: My family loves this place! Very healthy yet tasty smoothies, juices and acai bowls. Very friendly staff with excellent customer service!
Love this place. Great organic products and friendly staff. Would definitely recommend!
In town for business and stumbled across this place.  Great selection of fresh juices and inventive blends.  Worth the stop.
iEqF0NwXMMdOC25Xz0orbg: One of the best margaritas in town! I am picky and normally don't get anything fruity, but their spicy strawberry marg with fresh muddled strawberries and jalapeños is perfect and delicious! I have gone for lunch two weeks in a row and seem to devour my tacos before getting a picture. I have tried crispy fish, pollo verde chicken and pork belly...all generous portions and delicious!
Feel like this place needed some reviews. Stopped here for a snack and a drink. Awesome stuff. Really friendly staff. Tacos were awesome. Will be back for the whole deal

This place is the bomb! The food and margs are delicious! Service is extra friendly, and you can't be the location with indoor and outdoor seating options. Bottomless mimosas for Sunday brunch are a great deal!
gVxh7PO5mDP5hAXG-XbtYA: I was quite pleased with the service, very attentive but not overly. I had the taco bowl, so good and plentiful. My husband had the fajitas well prepared! Dessert churros in chocolate and caramel oh my! Margaritas were tasty enough liquor but not excessive. This is a new business, off to a fantastic start, Good Luck!
I'm a Nashville native. Like Alex Green, Jere Baxter, Ewing Lane, Hunters Lane native. This place serves the best tequila mixed drinks you'll ever have.
Brandon is the bomb waiter! He was courteous and attentive. Knows a lot about jalapeños & recommended the perfect dish for our mid afternoon snack!
KPofPh5SmzKumg-PpbAEYw: We finally came here and were not disappointed. Friendly staff, good selection of bottled Mexican drinks, great menu! I can't wait to come back and try more food. Cash only & they are inside the ice cream shop.
Enmoladas, moletes and the other dish forgot what it was but i definitely recommended really good

Wow!! So fresh and delicious. Literally the best mole I've ever had  I ended up dipping my son's fries in it just to get last drop. I was unfamiliar with Oaxacan cuisine and looked it up on Wikipedia. Apparently the area is known for its cuisine and Frida does not disappoint. After leaving my wife and I were looking at the menu for what to try next time. I had a tluyada and it was so different from standard Mexican fare. Still very latinamerican tasting but unique. Spice level on the dishes we had we're pretty mild which was great because it allowed the lighter flavors to shine! Can't wait to go back! Also as other reviews have said it's in the back of an ice cream shop and you have to try the ice cream too. Delicioso!!
7gvhTYbmnyAiGuG-TRlf8A:
If you live in the Lenox Village/Mill Creek/Old Hickory Blvd area & you like sushi, YOU HAVE GOT TO CHECK THIS PLACE OUT!

I am so glad that I don't have to travel to Downtown or to some other big city in order to enjoy good sushi. First of all, this place is hidden by the leasing office for Lenox Village, so it's not in a high traffic area. It's a good little spot for a casual date or if you just want to get your sushi grub on in private.

I was kind of discouraged when I walked in to sit at the bar & it was cluttered, but my dismay soon turned into jubilation as I opted to sit at the sushi bar, which ended up to be much better. First hand watching of the preparation. X-tra cool points to the chefs for their customer service & for  wearing gloves! 

The ambiance was gorgeous. It had low lighting with soft techno and jazz playing in the background. They were even playing Jill Scott (WHAT?!)! Very hip atmosphere. Had flat screens in there as well. Great service from the hostess who also doubled as the waitress. The feel of this restaurant kind of reminded me of New York or Miami. It had that sort of feel. 

I ordered a Philadelphia roll and a Volcano roll. DELICIOUS! I mean it was REALLY good and flavorful to the point where you don't even want to put soy sauce or wasabe on it to alter the flavor. 

I am very particular about the temperature of my sushi. This was perfect. Not freezing cold & not room temperature. Just right. The hot sake was hot too! :-) This was quality sushi. Can't wait to go back and try the tempura and other hot menu items. This is my new secret spot. lol. Thumbs up. Enjoy!

Finally got a chance to try Sushime tonight. I'll always welcome more good sushi options in town. 

We had the following: 

Shrimp Tempura Roll - yes, it's tame... but it's also one of the best I've ever tasted 
Spicy Scallop Roll - slightly above average 
Ocean Roll - this was odd... the picture looks nothing like the description and the actual roll was like the picture, not the description. mixture of tuna, yellowtail and some others... very clean and fresh with lots of fish... recommended 
Eel Diablo Roll - I love unagi, and this roll delivered.. the tempura asparagus in the center really complimented everything 
White Snake Roll - "Is This Love?" No, sadly it was average at best... kinda disappointed, and I think David Coverdale would be too 

The presentation was great. Rivals some of the most expensive and trendy sushi spots I've been to around the country. Prices were average for Nashville, but the portions were great. Not too much rice, which is good. 

Also had a full Japanese menu. I'll have to try it sometime. Glad this place is just down the street. 

The bad (if I have to be picky): 
The ginger wasn't that good. 
No low sodium soy option. 
The chairs are not comfortable... this place needs more booths.

I love the sushi here. I was recomended a roll off of their specialty list,The Las Vegas. It did not disappoint. I have also had a Bento Box and it was FULL of food. So much food I took some hoe to my Fiance and he had a FULL meal too. The area is great and so is the atmosphere. I would recommend this place if you dont feel like going Downtown for some decent sushi.
WWjP7p0nlUQl9BzCZzgjCQ: Cool atmosphere, great wine selection. The Mississippi rabbit was outstanding, and oyster ceviche was very good.

It was a busy Saturday night and we didn't have a reservation, so we sat at the bar, which was actually to our benefit because we were able to be served by the bartender Heather, she was amazing!!  We ate pretty much everything, tuna tar tar, Brussel sprouts, barbecue shrimp, shrimp toast and the crawfish etouffee.  Everything was absolutely delicious, I would highly recommend, the barbecue shrimp was our favorite, I could eat them every day!

Wonderful ambiance. My friend and I shared the fried oysters and the fried shrimp toast. Yummy. Not overly fried either which was nice because I hate when you get a fried food item and there's too much grease. I then had the ink squid pasta with scallops. Another good choice thanks to our server Paul. I'm a sucker for pasta with cream sauce so it hit the spot. I don't live in Nashville but if I visit my friend again I'd definitely consider this as a nice upscale option. :-)
GczZuKVQtsWLUgchZW9L-A: Very nice rooftop bar with great views. Fri and Sat nights get crowded and music is good. Can be a little difficult to get an expensive drink here.
Super cute place with an awesome rooftop! The burger was hands down one of the best burgers I've ever had! Service could be worked on. Definitely a great brunch spot!
Outstanding bar at top of Thompson Hotel. Early Sunday afternoon place to be. Great drinks, beautiful folks, a DJ playing vinyl. Nothing better. Crowded but fun.
f63mRXYxfiuHzY5KajPVjA: I was a little leery given the reviews but our food was good. Service was good even on a busy Friday night. We waited at the bar, ordered drinks and hadn't paid and they had a table ready. They kept our chips filled and drinks topped up. I had the cxn mole wwith avocado on the side ... Delish with the side veggies and rice. Husband loved the beef fajitas. Nice night!

One of the best restaurants in the Gulch. I have been here twice and both times where above average. Last night we had a couple great margaritas and ordered the Guacamole that they make at the table. My wife ordered chicken tacos and I had the Sea Bass and both tasted great. Our server Samantha did a great job and I overly appreciate her making sure my wife was involved. She enjoys her job and it shows. We will have made this place our go to for date nights.

Cantina Laredo offers...Freshly made.  Prepared at your table.  Guacamole. Yum.  And it's not just avacado mixed with lime juice and salt.  It's the Guac King mixed with onions, tomatoes, and other tasty goodness.  

Besides immediately ordering the guacamole, I suggest your next order to be the Pomegranate Margarita then finish it off with the Enchiladas Veracruz. The Enchiladas Veracruz are chicken, spinach and monterrey jack stuffed and topped with tomatillo sauce, veggies and queso.  So good.  I know I have your mouth watering, right?!

It's kind of pricey.  If you order what I like (above), you won't get out of there without spending at least $30 per person.  BUT it's worth it!  Go on a Thursday ladies where they offer 1/2 off wines and 'ritas!
7fH8SmEBinkv2AI7B8dOWA:
I went to the Palm with a group of 10 and they were able to accommodate us last minute (it was a Wednesday slow night downtown). Our server was extremely attentive, and didn't forget a thing. 

We started with fancy champagne and wine, and the service for those items was impeccable. We actually chose the restaurant based on the availability of the type of champagne we were looking for, and they did not disappoint! I also tried these "special" cocktails made with muddled raspberry and thyme and holy oh my goodness...I can't stop thinking about them! I must go back to have another! 

Our appetizers (shrimp cocktail and crab cakes) were incredible, and the Caesar salad I had before the meal was delightful. My dinner was a filet, perfectly cooked, and enjoyed bite by bite. We ordered sides of goat cheese mashed potatoes, cheesy scalloped potatoes (holy wow), asparagus, and Brussels sprouts. Everything was absolutely WONDERFUL. 

We ordered dessert, and that's my only "meh" feeling about the night. It just wasn't great. I had the cheesecake and also tasted the carrot cake and chocolate cake. All were just very average. No problem, though. I'll remember those cocktails, the champagne, and steak way longer than I'd remember a dessert anyway. 

Thanks to the Palm for quickly accomodating our group. We had a WONDERFUL time.

I attended a large group dinner at the Palm this week and thoroughly enjoyed myself!  Same consistent decor and environment as other Palms I have been to, excellent, quick service (especially for such a large group) and delicious food!  I think I licked the lobster bisque bowl clean...it was so good!  I shared 1/2 a filet and 1/2 of the shrimp linguine with one of my coworkers - what a nice combo!  We also shared the flourless chocolate cake and the NY style cheesecake - both were sinfully delicious!  I highly recommend this place for a business dinner...you won't be disappointed!

Absolutely outstanding. From the atmosphere to the service to the food and cocktails. Sat myself at the bar because I wanted something a little less than a sit down dinner there. Got a Manhattan with Angels Envy and it was like I made it at home....nice and boozie.  Ordered up a bison burger and the smaller sized wedge. Wedge was  outstanding candied walnuts and blue cheese were really well balanced and nice and crisp. Chatted with the bartender about the shrimp cocktail that the people next to me ordered and about the "pop" they give the cocktail sauce. As it turns out my burger was given to the wrong person and they needed to remake it. Immediately the bartender offered me a shrimp cocktail while I waited. What great attention and courtesy. That too was fantastic. Burger came later and it was tasty too. Obviously ended up getting a lot more dinner than I anticipated but I would very highly recommend this spot when looking for great food ,drink and service in Nashville.
0t1jvk8ZV3f3xqA0RmDVYw:
My friend and I went here for dinner after drinking a lot the night before. We wanted healthy options and that we got. We both got a salad and mine had salmon in it. We also ordered more beer.

This spot is right on broadway but down an alley and attached to their arena. It's great because everything else was packed but we were able to get a seat to eat here.

I would go back as the food and service were great.

Stopped in before a show at The Ryman. I got the Nashville Hot Rotisserie Chicken and chips. Wife got the burger and chips. We also got an order of pimiento cheese and crackers. Pimiento cheese was the star of the show. Chicken was good but could use a sauce or something. My wife said her burger was very good. Also had a Cuban Latte which was excellent. Quick service. Good food. Good place to grab s bite without waiting forever like so many places around Broadway.

My wife and I were just looking for coffee and we found this spot at Yelp. Friendly staff and nice decor. About the coffee, we both ordered americano and they were better than Starbucks, for comparisons purposes.
ElqoiaCSUA8oyyvixox9lQ: Wonderful place for breakfast.  Egg sandwich with poached eggs was perfects. The eggs were amazing and the bun incredible!
Most probably the best cortado in Nashville - a super smooth, delicate espresso and great attention to detail. The courtyard is heavenly on a mild day.
Great local coffee joint. The spot is cool and the coffee is great. Met a friend here and she had breakfast and I had a special latte. So good.
gVU0U7gNBMQxKTzqmRtdLg: Great food, great service, highly recommended. Definitely the only place I would suggest on the main strip. Order the soup and Sammy!!

This restaurant had great sandwiches and their homemade chips was so good! I got the salmon BLT with the chips and was surprised how much food it was...I couldn't eat the whole sandwich. The service was friendly and our waitress took the  time to share with us some places we should visit while visiting Nashville. Next time I will try the grilled cheese... it looked delicious!

Had a very early lunch with my family today at Merchants (including 4 very young kids). The place was very clean, service was good and very attentive. I was recommended this place from a friend who visited Nashville last month. They got it right. They are known for their duck fat tots and they were pretty good (had a bit of a soapy aftertaste). Kids has soups and home made Mac n cheese (kids likes the tomato bisque and chili). My wife and I both had the chicken fried chicken which had potatoes and spinach. This platter was terrific and white pepper gravy made it. We also like the fried green tomatoes. I included a couple pics. The only complaints are that they rushed our food out while we barely got into our apps. Also the Mac n cheese was less of a real mornay and more like gloppy cheese with pasta.
Cfl4gpf9oneiuZUExMtA4w: Service was great ... but the food!?!?  WOW!!! Ordered a bunch of different dishes bit the two that took me over the top were the Navratan Korma - just amazing! And for dessert... the Phirni.. that was the perfect dessert after such a great meal!  I could have had four of those!! Trust me .. order your own.. don't share.

I'm glad this place opened up in Donelson. It's pretty decent Indian food. Good curry, naan and rice. If you've never had dal you should try it here. Would like to see a couple more curry and lamb dishes, but what there is tastes good.

Great food and great service.  I am not sure why yelp is only showing some of the negative reviews for this place but filtering a ton of 4-5 star reviews.  I ordered the chicken vindaloo and it was definitely the best of that dish that I have had in Nashville.
GST3wg-wej15vHeCvaXE6w: This is a great bar! Go up to the top level and check out the views. The drinks are also good and it's a low key environment (at least on a Sunday afternoon).

I really liked this place.  It's a big space that has a few levels.  The first level has a live band and long picnic tables for seats.  The bar area is pretty long and getting a drink wasn't too bad on a Thursday night.

We ended up on the roofdeck and saw the amazing views of the bridge, Nissan stadium and Broadway.  It does get a little bit breezy up there so be aware.  

Great spot for live music, good options for food/beer, and it's not grimy compared to other bars.

Outstanding lunch.  For first timers, ACME is located in a cool gutted warehouse right along the river.  It has a "foodie" feel with gift shop area containing jams, cornbread mix and other southern surprises.  The food is southern eclectic.  We had a party of 10 this trip.....very casual...very fun place.  While everything was great, what received the most rave reviews was the Mexican corn on the cob (comes with some dishes) and the southern pork shoulder tacos.  Man alive....if you dropped some on your forehead, you're tongue would slap your brain silly.  Exaggerating?  Not really.  Give it a try..
NwFiM3dHbT4ryjwf3SLM8g: Ordered Music City Dog and Big Daddy Dog. By far the best hot dogs I have had in Nashville...period!  Keep doing what you are doing. Don't lose the quality!

Daddy makes a dang good dog. I had never seen a gourmet dog come from a cart before, but those days are over. If Daddy's cart catches your eye, and you've got a hankering for a dog, stop on over, and they'll fix you up something tasty. These guys are great!
The best drunken decision I have ever made.....ever. The Seattle dog will change your life
CTZEnTsW2JYN2NH1BCAaZw:
My husband and I are from California! We're total foodies and we are so happy to have gotten the opportunity to eat here. There was no wait and friendly service. The food was delicious and I would most definitely recommend the meatloaf. If we're ever passing through Nashville, we'll make this a must-have :) 

Also, 5 stars for the atmosphere!
Excellent food and great service!! My steak was cooked to perfection and the Brie appetizer was amazing! They serve the best bread and olive oil before the meal too!

Had a great dinner with my parents here last night. The food was really good and it was a nice atmosphere. Mom had an amazing prime rib, i had seared ahi tuna with spinach ravioli and my dad had shrimp and grits. The grits were friend into a patty so it was a different type than we have had before. The waitress we had seemed to be new as she was not very attentive to our meal or drinks but she was pleasant.
aDgughL1vDootnXe5kUWGQ:
This is the second day this brix and mortar location is open. 

I couldn't wait. My temperament is also very forgiving today. Dying for true ramen in Nashville and my gratitude may overwhelm, so take this review with a grain of salt. My boyz and I are kicking it up this Sunday. Supping on ramen and topping off the day with Star Wars. It doesn't get any better than this. 

Starting off with a glass of cava and a Japanese white ale. 

They are already out of duck buns and out of gyoza. We were warned and so I'm not grumpy. Well-being still high and intact. 

Our server today is a pretty young woman who used to serve us at Zushimaki in Franklin. And she says the ramen is great. She's Thai so I'm going to hold her to a much higher standard with that endorsement. 

The okonomiyaki were pretty darned good. We ordered two. It could have done with a little less tamari/sweet soy sauce, but otherwise, very tasty. Served in cast iron skillets--another nice nod to humble Southern cooking. 

The hot chicken buns are excellent. Perfectly fried, crispy, respectable heat inside steamed folded Chinese bread (bao) -- exceptionally pillowy, with a slathering of mayo and piquant pickles, respectful nods to Nashville's famous hot chicken, a la Japanese bar food style. We ordered another one. It was as good as the first. 

We all ordered the Tennessee Tonkatsu ramen, two without egg. Another nod to Tennessee--the pork is pulled pork rather than braised, seared char siu. I get the nod of respect but didn't want pulled pork in my ramen. 

We ordered these add ins that cane in ramekins-these let you doctor your broth, like pho. 

-miso butter--this transformed the broth and gave it depth--a little salty, buttery miso
-Sichuan bomb (floral, sichuan peppercorn paste)--be careful and taste this. It is floral, a hit of black pepper
-spice bomb (sriracha hot and a little sweet)
-karashi takana (salted pickled mustard greens) -this should be a pickled counterpoint but was saltier than it should have been
-chili oil

The broth was nice and hot but could have been hotter, steamier. Black garlic oil put a ring of char in the bowl. Wood ear mushrooms come in the bowl--dark, blackish brown, a toothsome counterpoint to the noodles. 

The broth is almost neutral tasting and begs your adornment. Mouth feel is a bit thinner than I like but the miso butter gives the broth a smooth, fatty deliciousness and is just salty enough. 

The ramen noodles are thin spaghetti noodles. I like my ramen noodles a little thicker and a bit more toothsome. These tasted just fine and were cooked nicely. 

Missing for me were: raw chopped garlic and sesame oil. Garlic is on the dinner menu but not available at today's lunch--too bad. Garlic transforms a bowl of ramen. Also missing was sesame oil. My broth would have exploded with garlic and sesame oil. 

The cocktails look excellent but I was told Otaku doesn't serve these until evening.

All in all, this is a damned fine bowl of ramen for Nashville. 

We will be back.

Anticipation at its finest. This place kicks ass. I had Paitan Ramen, plus spice bomb sauce, and it was amazing. The cocktails are also on point. I had every intention of taking my leftovers home... But there were none to be had. Go here. Now.
I'm not a pho type of person because I don't eat it often. But I did n i loved it. It was phenomenal. I forgot what I had but it's the most common one...like Nashville something. Lol.
OWOOc0YjU_kioLeEgo5VCA:
Loveless Cafe in Nashville has been cooking up their famous biscuits and southern food since 1951.  They now serve over 450,000 guests per year.

The Loveless Cafe serves down-home country food.  Their comfort food will remind you of grandma's house.  In 2005, USA Today named Loveless Cafe as America's "Top Down-Home Dining Spot." Loveless' famous "Biscuit Lady" Carol Fay became famous for her biscuits.  She has been on many television shows including the Ellen DeGeneres Show, The Today Show, the Martha Stewart Show and Late Night with Conan O'Brien.  The video below is Carol Fay the "Biscuit Lady" with Martha Stewart.  The Loveless Cafe serves their biscuits with amazing homemade preserves.  I was so excited to see they made and served my favorite preserves, blackberry.  It was perfect and delicious on their homemade light biscuits.  I do not get a commission for sending you to their site.  I truly just enjoyed The Loveless Cafe.

Loveless was a cute quaint little joint. It looked like your typical family style sit down restaurant. We heard that we had to have the biscuits and the ham. I ordered biscuits and gravy, bacon, and the chicken biscuit. The biscuits and gravy were phenomenal! They were just like mom's homemade stuff. The bacon tasted like it was fresh from the farm. The chicken biscuit was a complete let down. It was super tiny. Honestly, if you want a chicken biscuit, Chik Fil A is a much better choice. The peach, strawberry, and blackberry preserves were AMAZING! The waiter was super helpful and friendly, along with the rest of the staff. The BBQ guy, Eddie, was friendly and gave us the scoop on Nashville. The thing that was the biggest disaster is that we were initially sat on the patio seating area, after 30 seconds of siting there, we started to smell what we could only guess was vomit, so we asked to be seated inside. We were at this place on a Thursday morning and the wait was 10 minutes. Not bad considering the rest of the reviews on here mentioned much longer waits on the weekends.
Incredible southern cooking. The chicken gizzards and livers were the best I've ever had and the moonshine Marry was an amazing drink. Enjoy!!!
tU692E8N0xBQ7Ogc78gN2g: Best place to hang out and have coffee east of the river.  Great coffe, good bagel sandwiches, and excellent hummus if you are just in the mood for a snack.
I usually run into Bongo Java in 5Points to get their Peruvian Coffee Beans to grind and brew at home. I have never experienced the food here but I do recommend that you at least try the beans.
The seasonal sleepy hollow coffee drink is amazing! Breakfast sandwich on an everything bagel is to die for.
PwbeSKSCBnqXJlAxEaUrnA:
This oasis of slightly-more-upscale Tex-mex has been a favorite of mine ever since my old buddy Simon Newman introduced me to it back in ought-six.  Also, NEWMAN!

I love the menu here.
