They have a nice mix of offerings from all over the Latin-American world, not your typical offering in most of Nashville, which features low-scale Tex-Mex restaurants that all essentially have the same menu, prices, and maybe staff.  Rosepepper on the other hand has cool things like home-made tortillas, battered and fried sweet pepper rings, cool salads and soups, great fajitas, and burritos.  And dammit, they have great marguaritas, even the house ones, not the high-fructosey citric acid watered-down nonsense you get at every NachosCasaDeHaciendaLasPalmas place in the rest of town.  Great selection of tequila and draft beer, too.  And they have Mezcal. for when it is time to make really bad decisions, and fast.

Service here has always been good, and they have a really nice patio for outdoor eating, can seem a bit grungy at times, though.  But really, can you trust a clean Mexican restaurant?

But a word of caution, for the love of all that is holy, do not, I repeat, do NOT go here for Cinco de Mayo if you value your sanity or sanitation.  I have been schnookered into going here once for that auspicious occasion and it was a nightmare.  They are not only packed to the gills with people spilling out everywhere, they also have WAY WAY too few bathrooms to accommodate hundreds of people with bladders full of tequila.  The lines to the bathrooms were so bad for the women especially that they had taken over the men's room.
Excellent margauritas and superb spicy dishes.  Definitely a cut above your typical Mexican restaurant.  Plus, a fun bar and lively crowd.

Typical Mexican fare done just a little bit better than normal.  Excellent margaritas and fishbowls of beer.  Portions are huge, me and my wife often will split a single entree and are still satisfied.  Also if you save room then you get to have the chocolate chimicunga which is awesome.
KquItUiyLRxiasJ3P_xGMQ:
While the sandwiches at the Taproom are good and the beer selection is exceptional, the live music is what it's known for within the industry.  To the untrained eye, the musicians might just look like weird older guitar teacher types, but they are mostly session players and seasoned songwriters.

I like to pop in to the Taproom around midday from time to time, because the atmosphere is nice and they're typically slow through the afternoon.  They're hours are kind of hazy, but according to their website, they open at some point between 11:00 and noon.  They have a great patio that overlooks the burgeoning little 12 South district, and the servers tend to be pretty helpful and kind.

While I hear the quesadillas are pretty good, I usually go with a burger or a sandwich, which are always solid and filling.  Their dinner menu is a bit different, and it includes entrees like ribeye and chicken breast.  Plus, if you stop in on Wednesday nights, you're likely to see legendary session bassist Dave Pomeroy, and he's a total trip.

The beer selection makes 12th South on of my favorite places to talk to complete strangers at. The busy atmosphere on the porch lightens the oppressive, looming mutual decision to continue speaking or go like this never happened. The other people having fun, endears the possibility that the exciting/boring/embarrassing stories being shared are meaningful/weird/wtfstartrek. 

The beer selection is top notch in the city. Just don't get drunk on a first date. Unless the date sucks, then by all means.

Also the Mac n Cheese is incredible.

Always a great place to come and enjoy a large selection of local beers. Taps change frequently so there are always new choices. Some of my favorite eats are the wings, ceviche and tacos al carbon. They have a nice outdoor patio which is pet friendly. Don't be in a hurry as the service can be hit and miss.

www.nashvillerelocationexperts.com
4klUc-NroGEM1vGzp2-0Qg: This place was fantastic! I got the jambalaya and it was just the right amount of spicy! My father got a dozen oysters and devoured them. Overall great place and delicious food!

Great place to hang, good craft beer and awesome seafood in East Nashville. This place is good for having a beer with friends and/or eating lunch or dinner.

This place has the craziest hours - they are pretty random and not open for as long as most places so check the hours before driving out there

First, parking. There is not a lot of parking directly in front of the building and its next to a small strip of other places that are all full as well. Also, its right next to a traffic light and in a busy area so I just wanted to warn you on that. It was starting to get packed and we came in the middle of the day at like 3. Maybe there is a back parking lot that I dont know about? 

When you walk in, there's a bar to the left and that place got pretty jumping. To the right is a decent sized area with booths for eating and it's not super well lit so it's definitely a hang out and have a beer atmosphere. Someone said it was small but this place is not small. They have a HUGE outdoor patio area that is so nice and you can bring your dog.

The food was awesome. I had the blackened grouper sandwich and it was so good. It came with fries and they were good but nothing special. The poutine was just some fries on top of gravy - is that how they do that? I thought the gravy would be draped on it so that was a downer. It looks like you can buy seafood too as they had a deli type of case.

Service was good, I think the manager waited on us, and they kept that place moving. You can see them cooking and you can see them putting the food out. I tried a few craft beers and they were pretty awesome. Decor was southern hipster feeling with wooden boards nailed up on the walls and painted by hand. A lot of beards going on in this place.

Beware the lady in the parking lot hitting people up for money with her cute dog. It's East Nashville so you got that mix of the hipsters revitalizing the area mixed in with the people who are broke and live there.

Rudie's is just what Riverside Village needed.  You don't have to be big and fancy and charge insanely high prices for excellent food and service.  The crew here are great -- some of the friendliest staff I've seen at a restaurant.  I've never been displeased with any of the items I've ordered, and they usually have a good selection of beers.  It's not uncommon to find a brewery hosting an event at their location, so keep up with their news on FB and Twitter to stay in the know.  Oysters and seafood in East Nashville -- FINALLY!
7pwFfnB6FH4lflXWrQ2Tqw:
Not the best BBQ I've ever had but it's up there.  The combo is the way to go - you get to try 3 meats with 2 sides.  I ordered the ribs, brisket, and pork shoulder.  All of them were delicious.  The meat on the ribs fell off the bone!  I got the apples and mac n cheese as sides - and I was a little disappointed.  The mac n cheese wasn't as cheesy as I'd hoped...
They have 3 sauces to choose from - Texas, Tennessee and I can't remember the other.  The tennessee is spicier but all three were great!  Waiting in line is never fun but it was well worth it!

Great bbq joint settled inbetween all of the honkey tonks. 

Southern bbq with some great sauces. Lots of slow cooked tender meats and the apple sides are to die for. 

Always love Nashville, and this is a reason why.

I visited this place last weekend on my first trip to Nashville. I was dying for some good southern food and a local recommended Jack's BBQ as one of those landmark bbq's in Nashville. He suggested ordering the ribs and the brisket. The brisket was just OK. The ribs and the cornbread were the best food I tasted there. The sides were nothing special. If you are going to this place it is simply for the meat.
  The staff was extremely friendly.  When I went the place was PACKED, but we found seating and there was additional seating upstairs. They have like 12 sauces so it was cool to taste all of them (and they were much needed to counteract the dryness of the brisket).  Overall I would recommend this place but it is not worth the 30 minute wait that I experienced. There are definetly other good bbq joints in Nashville that could do the same job.
Ak4AuvhY28m4MVn6EjUTvw:
I was questioning my selection of restaurants when we walked in and the place was empty but we were pleasantly surprised. The ambience isn't the greatest but it was quiet and we could actually hear the conversations we were having. We started off with the fried green tomatoes and they were amazing!! My colleague had the wedge salad and I wish I would have gotten one too. I had the short rib with navy beans and Brussels sprouts and it was delicious!! I liked the navy beans with the richness of the short rib - good combination of flavors. 

Service, drinks, and food were all great! Standout place in Nashville.

First off, they have 2 entrances which are kind of confusing to new patrons. One enters in to middle of the restaurant in front of the bar and one is by the host station.  It would be nice if they labelled which was which. I saw tons of people coming in through the bar entrance and being told to go to the host station at the other side of the room.  Other than that. Service and food are excellent. They offer vegetarian and gluten free items. The veggie tower was delicious and filling. And it was gluten free. I'm more of a salt tooth than a sweet tooth, so I don't usually order dessert, but I went for the bread pudding and wow!  It was served warm and Alá mode, with caramel drizzled on top and a fresh strawberry. Definitely coming back for more.

First we did not have a reservation for a Wednesday night, as we were  just getting into town for Harley Davidson retail show.  Walked in and a waiter named Alvy came by and said do you mind sitting at the bar.  Of course we did and thankful we did!  Best seat in the place!!  Met the Executuve Chef Jeff Moore, and spoke with us the whole night!  We both had the Filet and topped with a scallops special for the night, and Brussel sprouts!!!  Oh my goodness what an marvelous experience!  Friendly staff especially our waiter Alvy!!!  Oh we had an appetizer a waffle with spicey chicken breast and syrup!  Wow! Desert was apple fritter bread pudding with homemade ice cream!!  The Cabernet was good as well, Smith and Hook!!  Any how, we must go try lunch before we go back to Atlanta!  Thanks again to the Chef and staff for a wonderful dining experience!!  May they all be blessed!!!
w__UZfNB79VSXmFUWhAVyA: Drove through Nashville today and stoped by this place. Very clean, calm vibe. Sushi is pretty good, average. The only negative thing here to say about Nama is the slow service. Even though we were there during a non busy time, the waiters took a very long time to start our order, bring out things like sauces and even took a while to bring out water.
The chefs choice sushi rolls were excellent! Happy hour is an incredible deal. You must try the salted caramel cheesecake it is dangerously delicious!
Great service. Great location and ambience for a fun night out.  Creative sushi and hot dishes.
6XzU96Ip5FzZ6Idxiy_TtA:
In town visiting for the week and I had SUCH a hard time making a decision on which BBQ place to try. I finally (literally after a day and a half) settled on B&C BBQ and I am SO happy I did. 

Ordered::Brisket plate with baked beans and garlic and cheese grits, 2 corn muffins (I asked for an extra), and an extra side of a 1/2lb of pulled pork. 

Brisket:: best I've ever had, no comparison, and BBQ is my thing. Pulled pork and brisket is what I order every time I get BBQ, so saying it's the best, means it's the best. It was perfectly prepared, and so incredibly tender. I want this in CA immediately. 

Baked beans:: also the best I've ever had from a restaurant. Also something I order whenever I eat BBQ (I won't go to the BBQ place if they don't serve baked beans, that's sacrilege), and these were amazing. Perfect temperature, very nicely seasoned, and thick. 

Garlic and cheese grits:: holy crap, amazing, to die for, GET THEM! They have a laundry list of grits you can try, so whatever your poison is, go and pick it ((note that not all grit flavor combinations are offered everyday BUT garlic and cheese IS)).

Corn muffins:: they were really good, but would have been better with butter (that's my fault). Either way, worth eating, maybe not worth asking for more than what they offer. 

Pulled pork:: unfortunately this was the least favorite of the meal (though still good). However, even though it was pork, it still tasted an awful lot like chicken. Which is disappointing because I just LOVE the fatty,greasy flavor of pork. Everything else made up for this and then some, so this is NOT to be used as a deferment, merely a suggestion that if you aren't a chicken fan and you prefer the fatty goodness of meat like I do, steer toward she brisket ( and maybe the ribs cause those looked amazing too! ) and not the pulled pork. 

B&C was NOT set up how I expected HOWEVER, I was pleasantly surprised with what I got, so to all you locals choosing other places over this one, knock it off and appreciate this gem!
great BBQ and great sides.  Cheese and garlic grits are fabulous--and I am not even a fan of grits!
Loved the ribs! Squash casserole and baked beans were also excellent. Great place for a good, fast, and filling meal.
JPoLb8jZYc5e7WjI2coVkQ: Great food! I am not a Vietnamese food specialist but this stuff was great! I had the dumpling soup and the spice was perfect with added vegetables this was a light, healthy meal that was reasonably priced and tasted great. Staff was good enough and the portions were more than enough. Make sure you stop by here for a bite to eat!
Brought in our family of 5 for dinner. Friendly and attentive staff, great draft beer selection, awesome egg rolls and pho. All 3 of my picky boys loved it!

The Pho here is off the chain. I had Pho Ga which is chicken noodle soup !! It's the best I have had since leaving Houston. Not just good or great AWESOME!!  The restaurant is clean and service attentive but the food speaks for itself.  Must try this!!!!
Zc710cEmB6LtUcMALGNZHw:
Never sit at the bar and watch them prepare food when you are hungry. You'll want to try everything that they are meticulously preparing for their customers!
The fresh ingredients makes their salads and sandwiches pop with so much flavor.  Aviator salad has a unique favor with nuts and chimichurri that I would eat it all the time if I lived in Nashville!

I rarely make it out to East Nashville, but I am pretty confident that's about to change. I've been dragged over the bridge by friends several times in the last few weeks and I'm quickly realizing that East Nash's food kicks Brentwood's food in the rear. 

Toward the top of the list of reason I will be a more frequent visitor: Silly Goose. 

I hear it used to be smaller, but I cannot imagine that this is true, because the place is still tiny. There was about a 25 minute wait at 7pm on a Tuesday, so good luck to you if you try it on a weekend. That's all the negativity I have. Oh - and there's no booze on the menu. meh. 

I had the Citrus Mint Ginger Tea and the Roscoe with a side of couscous. The Roscoe is basically a gourmet BLT with a few extra [read: delicious] ingredients - YUM. The presentation was amazing - they even managed to make a sandwich look pretty. The staff was attentive and friendly and carry iPads for you to swipe your credit card/sign/tip with, which pleased my inner nerd. 

I also like that the food is organic and not one million dollars.

This has become one of my favorite, healthy eats in Nashville! Everything on the menu is amazing and fresh. Their dinner menu is flawless. It's a bit pricey ($20 range entrees) but well worth it. The vibe is casual, but trendy.
g33s03KL3BTPgTPg_6r_Kw: By far one of the best Thai restaurants in Nashville (there seem to be quite a few here).  I can't give it 5 stars, though, because it's not *quite* as good as my favorite Smiling Elephant!  :)  At any rate, Thai Phooket is located in a strange spot near the stadium and based on the exterior appearance, you would never guess that such delicious food lies inside!  The interior is really nice, clean, spacious.  Our server was delightful and friendly, too.  Most servers are mediocre in their pleasantry, but this man was so kind and funny, and was very attentive.  He definitely got a good tip!  I ordered a mango veggie stir-fry and 3 Tsingtao beers, and the total was only $18 - not bad, eh?  The stir-fry was out of this world and unlike any stir-fry I have tasted before because it had such interesting flavors with the addition of the mango.  Mmmm!  I would love to go back and try more dishes!

I've passed this place a couple of times and never really noticed it because it's not much to look at. Don't let the outward appearance fool you. This is a great little hole in the wall restaurant. I will say that the hostess was fairly rude and walked away from our table midway through us asking her a question. However, our server was really pleasant. 

For an appetizer, I got the veggie tom kha soup. It was good but a tad too sweet for me. I ordered the panang curry with tofu (no green beans, add cashews) level 2 spicy and it was really good. It originally came out with chicken instead of tofu and while it took several minutes to prepare a new plate, I could tell it came out fresh and that they didn't just remove the chicken and add tofu. My husband got the drunken noodles with tofu and I loved them. 

I didn't think either dish was spicy, which I do prefer over something being too spicy, but I would have liked more heat. Based on another reviewer, I assumed a level 2 would be a lot spicier than my dish was. It may have something to do with my food being made wrong at first so I'll give it another shot next time. 

If you are downtown or in East Nashville and want tasty, affordable Thai food from a sketchy trailer, try Thai Phooket!

Authentic quality dishes that are reasonably priced. Very tasty traditional Thai food. Don't let the exterior scare you away, as the food and great waitstaff are worth the visit.

Feedback: I recommend the business better manage the climate inside. I've been here in the summer only to sweat my butt off and I've been here in the winter with friends only to literally wear a ski coat and earmuffs the whole time because it was so flippin cold inside. In today's world, that's unacceptable; take care of your customers! Customers remember food quality and cheap establishments...
g-YOzV9YIPmo6vZBHQuuUg: We tried this place for lunch as it is right next to our hotel, the hyatt place. I am glad we stopped because it hit the spot. To start we tried the chips and salsa and the guac. The guacamole is on point. Best I have ever had. It's full of flavor, chunky and we ate it all. The salsa is a light green and also very flavorful. For lunch I had a Mahi Mahi taco, a chicken taco and a braised pork with habanero salsa. All three were delicious, but my top choice was the fish. Tacos are $4 a piece and with the chips and guac, 3 tacos and a skinny margarita my tab was around $25. Worth every penny!!
This place has a great selection of tacos. I recommend the fish taco and the pastor taco. Also the chips and guac is a solid choice for an appetizer.
Yummmmm! Their margaritas are amazing and the queso... I have no words. Servers and bartenders are kind and personable.
lAm4Tqt4fEpZO7YAQN0DSw:
This was a quaint restaurant with great food and service.  The place was very welcoming and cozy.  It seemed like it was an old craftsman home turned into restaurant.

We ordered the umami tater tots which exploded with flavor in your mouth.  We also had the monkey bread which was savory with hints of Rosemary.  You do the bread in the candle tallow butter which was a kinda neat.  First time I ever dipped bread into a savory candle!  We also had the beet salad which was well balanced withe the sweetness of the beets and the tartness of the vinaigrette.

Overall a great experience!
One of the best brunch spots in East. Definitely worth the price and quality service.

Went here for brunch with 3 friends after walking Shelby bottoms. Everyone really liked  what they ordered (1 breakfast burrito, 1 avocado toast, and 2 breakfast sandwiches). Very ample amounts, fresh and tasty.  Loved the cute cozy old house atmosphere and the service was very good. The Bloody Mary was stellar (walker's spicy mix I think). Definitely will return soon. The dinner menu looked great too--will try that soon.
izb2BG_ujl2Xl1CBm34Ufw:
Went for the grand opening. Good personal size pizzas (10") with variety of choices (including vegan). Good location, with parking garage behind it. 

For the grand opening, they have $3.14 entrees and they gave away t-shirts and Yeti's.

Your Pie Pizza has many different options. You do not have to get just a "pizza." They have options of a panini, and salads! The best part about it is, you do not have to order it like it shows on the menu. You can make any changes on your order that you would like, add or remove items. My favorite for Pizza is The Nat, it is pure yummy!

Amazing experience. Pizza, however you can possibly imagine it. 19,040 possible combinations of dough, cheese, sauce, meats, and veggies. All for the fixed price of $8.49. How could you go wrong? Sky's the limit. Loved it.
TJ3rnd66ErA9mbrXf396ig: Great music, great drinks, GREAT crowd!! LOVE the weekend Bathroom Attendant. She is the SWEETEST lady & she keeps that bathroom in line!

We live in Ohio and a new luigi's was opened at Polaris, same great food as in Nashville. These places are also attached to Wanna B's karaoke and Tequila Cowboys. Spent the evening for dinner, then went to watch a good country band then finished up the evening singing some karaoke. Good night with our friends.

While walking around trying ti find a less packed bar ee popped into this one. We found a seat at the bar and were able to get drinks fast and order food. Both the drinks and food were great (we ordered the TC salad and quesadilla), music was awesome, and the bartenders were fantastic.
Y0WIZpnXFFT5VH6WBVCxlg: Selection of baked goods and suites is fantastic. Went there for danish, to be curtly advised that they only make them on Friday and Saturday. Went there on Friday to get danish and they were sold out by 9 AM. My first piece of advice is if you want to danish go at 7 AM. But, if you're like me on vacation, who wants to get up that early and I guess they never thought of making more than one batch. The woman Behind the counter is a bit curt, could do without her but my kudos to the baker.

Lovely little place! Delicious breakfast sandwich, simple but yummy. Would love a spicy version as well, or perhaps one with a touch of bacon? Service was genuine, quick and so kind. Really felt valued as a customer.

When we moved to Nashville the thing we mourned the most was the loss of our wonderful local bakeries.  Then a year later we discovered Sweet 16th. Muffins that taste like muffins!  Real scones!  

The sweet young couple that invest so much of themselves in this place are the best.  They are friendly & talented.
EXfIURX2NtTp_G1Lje3VaQ: Chances are, you are not cool enough to hang out here.  God knows I'm not, but that doesn't keep me from occasionally infitrating this hispter hotspot.  Food is excellent tex-mex: try the spicy burger and the fried avacado.

Groovy little dive bar/restaurant.  It is usually dark, crowded and smoky, and I mean that in a good way.  Drinks are affordable, especially at happy hour, and they serve food until the wee hours of the morning.  The food can be kind of hit or miss, although when it is a hit it is usually fantastic, and when it is a miss it is still pretty damn good.  The tortas are a personal fave, but there really are no bad menu items.  The nachos are huge so either bring you appetite or bring a friend.

Alleycat is just downright fun. If you go on Saturday nights, they have a 80's dance party, which is really a great opportunity to cut loose in a very low key setting. I hate getting up and dancing, but here I really had a good time. I guess I see the point about the hipster comments, but I have never felt not cool enough. It really isn't like that at all. 

In the summer, bands play on the patio, which is small, but it works.

The bartenders are really down to earth and nice, even when they are really busy and they have good beers. Plus, they mix a good strong drink. I've eaten here and it's good, but I usually end up there late night after the Red Door East, so I think of it more as a drinking hangout. The place just has a really good vibe.
KYQ_ULE4PXNlPMBtXOYrOw:
Atmosphere:
I've been to the one in NOLA - this is fairly similar atmosphere wise.

 It's located down the street from the new Sounds Stadium (Germantown area). 

Parking can be hit or miss but there is a pay lot across the street if this lot is full and there is no street parking. We arrived for lunch around 1pm on a Saturday and it was not busy. I can see where seating on the inside can be an issue during busy times but there was plenty of outdoor seating.

I will note I felt the menu on the board (written in chalk) was not spaced out very much and I found myself bouncing back and forth between menu items but that was minor.

Food:
Since I've been to the one in NOLA I knew this had to be just as good. I definitely did not hold back when it came to ordering from the menu.

We ordered the Muffaletta, Pig Mac, Brisket Sliders, and Mac N Cheese.

Muffaletta was probably the best bang for your buck out of the items we ordered. I'm not a big Muffaletta guy but I thought this was good. I've only ever had the muffaletta's from New Orleans and I was not a very big fan of those. You get a very big portion for only $12.

The Pig Mac was the best dish out of everything I ordered. Two Pork patties, lettuce, cheese, onions, and special sauce on a sesame seed bun - way to stick it to the red headed clown. Every flavor on this sandwich was fantastic. I doubt anyone watching their caloric intake would consider it but it's definitely a go-to for me!

Brisket Sliders - I really enjoyed the meat on this. It was very tender and the sauce was delicious. I'm just not a real big fan of small plates. Definitely the least bang for your buck on this one.

Mac N Cheese - I've read / heard several people rave about this. Maybe I've only ever had good mac n cheese in my life because it just didn't do anything for me. Don't get me wrong it wasn't bad but it didn't knock my socks off either. I still think this was a better value than the sliders.

Service:
You walk up, order, and they bring it to your table. Food came out fairly quick.

Overall:
I'm glad I finally made my way here. New locations, especially so far from the original, tend to make me hesitant but I was not disappointed.

Awesome place! Tried the shrimp pizza boy with mac n cheese and oh boy was that delicious. Also took a bite of the Brussel sprouts and wow I now love Brussel sprouts. I would definitely recommend it to anyone. Wished they had one in Texas.

This place was delcious! I had been wanting to check this place out after a friend raved about it, and I went and had lunch the other day. You order at a counter and they bring everything to you, including your drinks. They have servers walking around to refill the beverages, and clear away any plates. 

I tried the turkey sandwich, which had arugula and sprouts, and a side of their mac and cheese. YUMMY! I consider myself a mac and cheese aficionado, and theirs was on point! 

I can't wait to go back and take more people to try this great place.
XiDhSghC-9Hz19hBlLpbyg: Love the crepes! They are so tasty and make a delicious breakfast. I also think the coffee is great.
Super cute place in Germantown! Delicious crepes and amazing coffee! Really nice staff :)

Great service and fantastic drinks. We tried the berry iced tea and the honey cinnamon latte which were both delicious. The one area they fall short is the food. It's reasonably priced, comes out fast, but it's nothing to write home about. We took the staff up on their recommendations but The Greatest was dry and the bacon tasted a bit cheap. I'd still come here again, but I'd most likely stick with the drinks and crepes.
zPiBTTqr9PyUwjhDsdfH2w:
Atmosphere: 8/10 - It's casual, but nice.
Noise: Moderate
Food: 8/10 - Great taste; small portions!
Drinks: I just had water, but good selection.
Originality: 7/10
Service: 10/10
Cleanliness: 9/10 - Flies outside, of course.
Price-Quality Ratio: You pay more than you get.  It's good, but a bit over-priced.
Overall: 9/10

I'm giving Burger Republic a 4 although I wasn't super impressed with my particular lunch choice. My sister wanted to grab a bite to eat and we both needed a little hair of the dog so we headed that way. I ordered the Ahi Tuna Burger with no bun just lettuce wrap (I like that they offered this to me when I said no bun as this is how I eat all of my sandwiches). They Ahi was very dry and my sister who eats here often said it always is. I should have just ordered a regular burger honestly b/c it does fit within my food lifestyle so that was my bad. 

Service could not have been better. From the second we walked in until we walked out our server was attentive but not overbearing. He brought over fresh drinks every time he saw we were low or almost out (we let him know we needed a little hair of the dog and keep em coming). 

The atmosphere is simple, not crowded, and very laid back. Parking is super simple with lots of spaces. 

Overall, I'll be back but I'll get an actual burger next time!

Great place, Great food and really good shakes.
their house fries are amazing and the burgers were juciy and perfect. have to try once with an egg on top. AMAZING!!!!!

First place i ate at since moving to Nashville a week ago. everything was good and my wife and i will be going back this weekend again.
pG3BViWKznBrEPlbV9wxPA:
I go here all the time just because I live right upstairs. I went first for lunch after moving in to my apartment mostly because I was too exhausted to go anywhere else. It was AMAZING. I had their Mac and cheese which basically changed my life. Not kidding. Not to mention the portion was big enough so I had it for dinner as well after I packed the rest up. 

I came back later with some friends for drinks and the bartender was so great. Kept our drinks filled to a reasonable degree and was very talkative with us. I'm not sure if he wanted tips or what but he was great. 

I come here a lot with my friends and it's super easy for me because I can just take the elevator down and walk.

6.30.12
Good food, drinks and atmosphere! 
Upscale, classy and trendy.
Great Bar and the kitchen is open late for quality bar food even late!
9d5saeqjLfkInGItJb1IIA: The best meal in Nashville yet.  Not fancy for sure.  But great fish.

This is THE definition of a hole in the wall; off the main street and attached to the back of a laundromat!   Do yourself a favor and skip the sandwich part of the catfish, just get the catfish platter with two delicious sides.  The sandwich is white bread which is not appealing, but the fish more than makes up for!  The fish itself was moist, flavorful, and fresh -- and the seasoning and fried outside was perfect with a little kick to boot.  

The portions of fish are large too which was wonderfully unexpected.  And the service was especially good for a take out joint.  I'll have to go check out 400 and then make an update to compare.  Bonus: they have "wangs from the hood" if you're into that -- I'm sure they're delicious.

Any place that bills itself as the spot to get the "crunkest" fish sandwiches in town has to have something going for it.  East Side Fish, as hood as it may seem due to its name, aesthetics and location, is totally the bomb.

East Side Fish has one table inside and a picnic table out front, but I typically opt to take my fish sandwich to go.  They offer trout, tilapia, whiting fish and catfish, but the catfish is definitely the way to go.  While they are technically fish sandwiches (because they provide you with two pieces of white bread), it's best to just eat the massive fried pieces of fish on their own.  It gets a little sloppy otherwise.

East Side Fish has some delicious side items including greens, fries, slaw, beans and more, and you can get a sandwich with up to three sides for about $8.  They sell sodas and juices, but they aren't included in the combos.  It runs a tiny bit pricey for a little fish joint, but it's definitely worth it.  Any cheaper and I probably wouldn't trust it anyhow.

Nearly as cool as the food is the decor of the place.  They have an amazing mural of several guys fishing and countless fish wearing crowns.  The place looks as ballin' as it is, and that's really saying something.
w9grvGu-5bHNucOeReHzfA: The staff made this place special.  The atmosphere made it perfect.  It was a chilly Fall night, and we were seated next to a pretty fireplace.  The cocktail specials were creative and tasty.  I had the ny strip and it was perfect.  Depending on your seats you can watch the kitchen in full swing.  Great experience for some one how loves to cook almost as much as they love to eat.

We took some family out for brunch here - can't wait to come back for dinner! The food was incredible and service was great. 

We tried some of the oysters and a charcuterie build your own (speck, andouille and some cheeses), the andouille is fantastic as advertised. Just as wonderful as fresh off the asado in Uruguay! I only eat meat on special occasions so it was well worth breaking my diet for. The plate and oysters were the perfect amount and our party ordered the lobster flatbread (good, but not particularly noteworthy) and shrimp toast (same), then followed up with dessert. 

One dessert is enough to share with four here! We got the donuts and the chocolate peanut butter tart. Donuts were amazing, fresh and hot, perfect way to end the meal.

Next time, we'll come for dinner and try the pork chop or one of the other highly recommended entrees. Looking forward to it!

The reason for no 5th star: Unfortunately it is packed to the gills and that really ruins some of the ambiance. We made reservations in advance and felt like sardines packed in between other parties. I would rather have to make reservations in advance than sit in such a crowded noisy space, but it is what it is and the food will keep us coming back.

I came here the first weekend I moved to Nashville with my parents and it was delicious. My dad is very picky when it comes to Ribeye steak and he said it was the best one he has ever tried! I was shocked. I tried it as well and it was amazing. I got the kale Cesaer salad and it was really good, you make it yourself and stir it around. My mom got the shrimp and grits and loved it as well!
5iooYc7woWdiRU1uq9jpNg:
This is a great place!  There's a lot of places that serve Pho, and each one of them have their unique taste...it's hard to choose which one is the best, but this is a place I go to since it's near by my work!

Carrot & Beef stew was a little heavy to my taste.  The flavors in the Pho was pretty awesome.

Definitely the best pho I've had in town. The prices can't be beat, service is fast, food is fresh and flavorful, and I love that they offer their bowls in S, M, & L sizes since I have a nasty habit of cleaning my plate/lacking portion control sometimes. I still maintain Kien Giang has the best non-pho Vietnamese food, so I'll head there for my bun, iced coffee, banh mi, pancakes, etc. However, the meat quality, broth, noodles, and overall pho-tasticness of Vinh-Long's magical soup will keep me coming back for sure. FYI they don't take credit cards and if you're hankering for some sinus-clearing goodness on a Monday night you're SOL...I discovered that yesterday, humph!

I have been here dozens of times and while I'm aware that they have lots of other items on the menu, I've not tried them because the beef stew is that good. In fact, you probably shouldn't eat here because one little taste is all it takes to become hopelessly addicted. I now cannot go more than a few weeks without a fix. Had some today and will return soon. 

For the uninitiated: bring cash and order the bread to go with the stew.
K3t9JDcutfnkivCOL-fMrA: Great breakfast and friendly service. Dana our waitress, was terrific, efficient and really knew her menu. Loved the city grits! Restaurant is clean, relaxing and pretty.

I'm very excited because this is right down the street from me! Looks like it will always be crowded so be ready to wait on weekends. Everything on the menu sounds super good- it's a bit pricey for a breakfast place that I wouldn't consider fancy but very worth it (very clean and more of a diner style than upscale) . Delicious beignets and crab cake Benedict. Any of the pancakes very good and filling too

This place was great! The wait was long, but worth it. Yelp has a waitlist feature I would strongly recommend. I had the lobster and Brie omelette which was INCREDIBLE. Also, the portions are gigantic. You definitely get your money's worth. Everyone at my table loved their meals - this place is definitely a crowd pleaser. I would highly recommend if you're wanting to check out a new brunch place, but be prepared to wait for both the table and your food (it is constantly busy!).
8pGeXrLvLR5lq6zigbyBDw:
This is the best BBQ I have had outside of Texas, where I lived for a few years. It's that simple.

The brisket is tender and flavorful. A lot of the brisket in other places are dry.

The pork ribs have strong smokey flavor and are tender without being soggy.

If you are a visitor, go to this location instead of the one on the main drag. There's rarely a long wait here and the wait on the main drag can be an hour long - or longer.

Great food! My boyfriend had the brisket and I had the smoked chicken. Both were great! I liked the sweet BBQ sauce best. We checked in on Yelp and got a free dry rub. The only reason it got 4 instead of 5 stars was due to it being a little pricey, and there was no music playing.

I grew up in West Tennessee where pit bar-b-q is an art form. This ranks high. For those expecting Texas style or Carolina style bar-b-q, you are in the wrong state. Pork was moist and smoky; sides were tasty. Good food, good value and hospitable staff.
KvCmKEV_pL0qMmw3Q8Yagg: Great tasting BBQ.  I'm picky and they are amazing. A little on expensive side tho. They pack everything nicely when you're going through drive thru.

Great lunch there today! This was my first visit to this location. Pulled bbq sandwich, cole slaw, potato salad & one of their chocolate chip/toffee cookies.... all were great. Even better was the friendly young lady at the counter..it was refreshing to have a great customer service experience in.this part of town (those can be hard to come by, it seems, in.this area). 
My only suggestion to management is to add a gratuity jar to the counter area...I am sure.many people would like to show their appreciation to the front counter staff & the peeps in the kitchen cranking out awesome food!! :-)
Great tasting food! Good atmosphere and very friendly staff. Wide choice of selections. Best fruit tea in town!! Never had a bad experience.
St_OHgYAn5czLy0naZwseA: Best indian food in nashville! Always have a nice experience at sitar. The naan is always fresh and the food is flavored perfect every time. The staff is friendly and the food is served quick. Always stop by sitar when I take my trips to nashville :)

Eaten the lunch buffet a few times and always glad I came. They change it up depending on the day so you can go multiple times and chances are end up eating different dishes each time. For a lunch buffet it is fantastic.
I really enjoyed their lunch today. Very nice and lots of variety.  I am definitely coming here again.
pWT3WAUvww4iqF-v2htBXA:
Allowed me to leave  a review in Spanish for 357.000 Latinos in Tennessee.
primero quiero hacer notar el order y la limpieza, la comida estuvo muy bien presentada y los tragos realmente buenos, el estacionamiento ademas de gratis es espacioso, el happy hour, asi como el menu fueron mas que convenientes por precio y calidad, pero quiero hacer un especial mencion al excelente trato de los meseros. sin dudas un lugar para recomendar y volver, definitivamente esta en nuestro top 5 list en Nashville. totalmente recomendable! un truco: hagan reservaciones por adelantado el sitio esta de moda y todo el mundo quiere estar ahi, especialmente los fines de semana.


Ariel Luna
Owner of www.argentinagrille.com
& www.vivamarketinggroup.com

The interior of this space is almost unrecognizable to anyone who frequented the former Cooper's on Porter. Gone is the wall behind the bar, which now opens to show inside the kitchen. The weird booths in the middle that segmented the restaurant have been replaced by two brick columns, which gives it a much more open feel. We sat in the back, where an open garage door overlooks the patio, allowing in the fresh air while still keeping us out the rain.

Their menu design needs a little work, but they have an impressive wine list, though more bottles than by the glass, and several beers in bottles, though only two drafts. We didn't get an appetizer, though they had a nice selection plus a special not on the menu. Instead, we split a Caesar salad. The dressing was really light despite coating the lettuce entirely, but could have used a little extra oomph. The croutons were made from the same bread we were served with olive oil -- nice and garlicky.

Speaking of garlic, let's get to the entrees. I ordered the half portion of the potato-suffed ravioli with crawfish tails, butter and lemon. Very flavorful, with a nice garlic-laden broth of some sort as a sauce. The crawfish tails were huge, very shrimplike for what I'm used to (I'm from around here, so growing up I ate my share of "crawdaddies" that didn't resemble these at all), and the potato filling in the ravioli was basically creamy mashed potatoes -- smoother than the consistency of the usual ricotta. The ravioli was slightly undercooked on the edges, but I still inhaled it. And a great deal for only $9 ($17 for a full portion).

He tried the pizza with lamb sausage, fennel, tomatoes and arugula. Not too crusty, but not cracker-thin (we both prefer a crust on the thinner side), and generous with the toppings, though a little skimpy on the sauce, but that may be their style. No complaints -- and at $11, a decent deal for gourmet pizza.

We arrived around 6:30 on a Friday night and were sat immediately, though probably just in the nick of time, since there was a huge line out the door when we left. As far as service goes (since I've seen complaints), our drinks took a while to arrive, but our server was very apologetic. And at least two other people (owners?) stopped by during the course of our meal to ask how everything was -- I imagine they just want to make a good first impression, which they certainly did. When our food didn't arrive as soon as she had promised, our server offered to buy us a second round of drinks, even though we weren't complaining. And then our food arrived immediately after that. So ... free drinks for not-really-that-slow service? I'll definitely be back.

Although to second another reviewer... where was the hostess stand? And more importantly, this place needs a website, or at the very least a Facebook page. I know it's owned by the same folks who run Eastland Cafe, so I figure they are just trying to do a soft opening for a few weeks before they start heavy promotions, but if it weren't for a food blogger posting about them, I wouldn't have known they were open.

I went to Pomodoro East last night. It has been a go to for date nights for my husband and I, and we have been here 3 other times. Last night the food was amazing but the service was slow. I will give them 4 stars because I have never had this problem before. Last night we ordered at 7:30 after waiting 10 minutes for 3 beers. We asked for bread at 7:50 and it came 5 minutes later. Our salads and appetizer (calamari) were out around 8:00. Our food came out at 8:30. The food was just as good as I remember and worth the wait but the wait still seemed ridiculous.
I got the rigatoni. The sauce was amazing and there was a ton of sausage in the bowl. I usually get the bucatini with cream sauce and poached egg (HIGHLY RECOMMENDED)! I have also gotten the shrimp pizza which I like, but it is very garlicky. The calamari last night was a little chewy. The beet salad was so good! It was a pleasant surprise that it came with a ball of fried goat cheese! 
Overall definitely going to come back, but hopefully they fix this wait issue.
65wbUrdLo5IdslsnAmWmJA: A very welcoming and relaxing environment. And the food was perfectly prepared. I would happily return anytime.

Had terrific food here last night with very mediocre service. Decided to come here for  dinner Friday night on our company as they told us dinner was on them for a Christmas gift. Took a while to be acknowledged that we even existed and when the waiter did come literally all he said was any questions? Ready to order? I'm sorry, at an expensive place I expect oh perhaps a greeting, maybe some suggestions, you know... service. So we order apps - shrimp cocktail for me which was amazing and so very fresh. We still have our menus when our apps are done but eventually he comes back and gets our dinner order. Oh and tells us his name after we order, wow thanks for the introduction! Had to grab him walking by later to ask for bread which we saw all the other tables had. I had a boring and mediocre salad for dinner and a crab cake (I don't eat meat). Crab cake was delicious. My friend had the 8oz filet with brussel sprouts and mashed potatoes. She said it was very good. Dessert was carrot cake and it was also incredibly tasty. All and all the food was excellent but again, the service left a lot to be desired when I saw the final total on our bill. Would I come back? Maybe, but I hope I get a different waiter next time.....

Amazing dining experience. All the wait staff was very friendly and attentive, as well as very knowledgeable. Our food was excellent...every bit of it! Although they're not on the menu, crab cakes were available. The best crab cakes I've ever had- hands down. 
We got the dry age rib-eyes. Melt in your mouth delicious. Mashed potatoes and Brussels sprouts were also very good! Highly recommended!
Icu-z8o386uHuj0bB345Cg:
I will start out by saying this is not an inexpensive place to eat.  However, the food and service more than match the price you pay.  I had probably the best steak of my dining-out life at this establishment with truly fantastic service.  Plus, it was my first chance to really explore the Opryland Hotel, which to quote my companion, was like being at Disneyland.  

Parking in pricey at the hotel so I suggest walking over from the Opry Mills Mall.  The hotel itself can be very confusing (there are three gardens, which I didn't know until I was lost in them) so if you make reservations here be sure to give yourself enough time to find your way around, especially on the weekends when wandering around becomes impeded by lots of children and families.  The restaurant has patio seating, which isn't actually outside, just overlooking the garden, which is really nice.  Its romantic and relaxing and good for any season.

The service was top-notch, our drinks constantly filled and our food delicious.  My steak was perfect, and I recommend getting sides to split between two to four people (that had to be the largest asparagus I've ever seen).  Even their baked potato was gigantic.  They also had the best creme brulee I've ever had in my life.  And their after-dinner cocktails are truly fun.

Do not come here and expect to not spend money.  Steaks can run upwards of $40-$50.  The sides alone are $10 (but again, they are HUGE).  This is a place to go for a special occasion.  I really enjoyed being able to sit "outside" by a garden in the dead of winter.  The hotel is a beautiful setting for a great meal.  

I will absolutely be coming back to this great little steakhouse gem.  If I could remember our servers name I would tell you to request him, he was perfect.

This restaurant is in the Gaylord Opryland Hotel and we almost didn't eat here because the other restaurants at the hotel we tried were just mediocre. We're pleased we took the chance. From the the moment we stepped up to the checkin desk the experience was 1st class. Our server Amir was friendly and attentive. His explanation of the menu and his suggestions were spot on. Our steaks were flavorful and cooked perfectly. We also had Caesar Salad, mashed potatoes and Mac and cheese. All were excellent. And the lobster bisque was some of the best I've ever had. This was the best meal we've had in Nashville.

After arriving late in the night we were able to get a table rightWe were a bit under dressed but the atmosphere is beautiful. It was a little hard to hear because of the water flowing nearby. What really made this restaurant was Wayne our superb waiter. He made dining at this restaurant an experience. Filet was delicious, lobster bisque is a must. Would highly recommend.
YV83252nBml7BmQLdAniYg: My dining party ended up at this place as our second choice, rest assured there was nothing second rated about this place. I had the roasted chicken with pepper jam plate, chicken liver pate with bacon fat and smoked peach preserves, and cornbread! I couldn't believe my taste buds. The smokey flavoring permeated the food and the atmosphere. I really liked that the ingredients were from local farms, so make a reservation. The food experience will be a worthwhile venture.

I had been looking forward to dining here for quite a while.  So maybe my anticipation was so overwhelming that I just wasn't wowed the way I felt I should have been.  Don't get me wrong, everything was tasty but it wasn't over the top.  

I went with a couple of girlfriends and we shared the mussels, bone marrow and a side of fries as appetizers.  I will say that for me the bone marrow appetizer and the side of fries were the absolute highlight of my meal.  My friends were not as impressed with the bone marrow.  It was a first for one of them so it could have been a textural thing, but for me it was sublime.  Oh, and the fries!  They were hot, fresh and crispy.  Even when they reached room temperature, they were still satisfyingly crisp.  They did not become a cold, limp mess.  I liked the dipping sauce that accompanied them - a roasted red pepper aioli....  But I also loved dipping them into the broth that bathed the mussels.

For my main course I had the bone-in pork loin.  It was quite tasty but just did not push me over the top or motivate me to lick my plate.  I brought half of it home (which is NOT normal for me) and my husband polished it off as a late night snack.  Dessert too was good but not extraordinary.  We shared the donuts and they were a nice finish to our evening out.

Highlights included the cocktails - my Dragonfly was well made and the mint and cucumber were a refreshing addition.  The chocolate martini was another tasty way to end the evening.  Although the star of the evening to me was the bone marrow.  The downsides were cramped seating and it was quite loud in there at times.  Service was good but I completely see what others mean about the hipster comments.  I sometimes felt a bit old while sitting there but that was totally my doing, not theirs.

Overall, I would have liked to give it a 3.5 but I will round up because I think my downsides were more of personal preference.  Although one of my dining companions and I agreed that it was not nearly as good as Etch......  Will I return?  Not sure.  I may go back to try a flatbread and have another Dragonfly (and more bone marrow, please!) but I don't think I will spend all the extra money on an entrée.  Maybe my palate is just not James Beard nominated material.....

I am beginning to feel like Nashville food offers some options just as incredible as San Francisco. Lockeland Table is on the cutting edge of this movement! I could barely wait last week as Friday approached, because friends were visiting from the Bay Area and we had reservations for Lockeland Table. They both commented that the interior reminded them of something straight out of San Francisco. There were so many incredible cocktails and wines to choose from, I didn't know where to begin. I had a cocktail of vodka, pomegranate, and I'm not sure what all. It was sooo delicious! We ordered the corn bread to start. Normally, I don't really like cornbread. But this was no ordinary cornbread, it was moist, not overly sweet or salty, and had more flavor than I anticipated. 

My boyfriend and I ordered the shrimp and pork dumplings. They were just like delicious dim sum dumplings, with the sweet chili sauce to dip in. Incredible! Our friends split a salad, which looked fantastic. 

For my entree, I had the trout. It came with roasted sweet potatoes and some other veggies. The fish was perfectly cooked, and the veggies were packed with so much flavor some of it almost tasted meaty it was so powerful. I was very impressed. My boyfriend had the bone-in pork chop, which was uber tender and flavorful as well. It came with mac n cheese made with a smoky cheese, so savory! It also had a side of greens that had just the right amount of vinegar to balance out the flavor. 

We left feeling like stuffed royalty. This would be an incredible place to take a date you want to impress. It's not stuffy or very fancy, but more of a laid back, friendly vibe. I'm liking Nashville more and more!
65_MIvrauhGs_jssN4-uUQ: Good place but glad we went for a date night and not a family night. Not sure I would have found something my kids would eat. Sushi was ok but main entree was very good. I would return for another try and probably just skip the sushi next time.
Delicious and economical dinner. The pho and bun were amazing. Definitely coming back to try more from the menu
Great Pho and Ramen!  Also the bahn mi was the best I ever had. We will be back soon.
XSUHgA7ZUF-ae1za7ElrYg: The hubby decided to go here for dinner and I'm happy he did. They let us sample everything and were very nice. The food was soooo good. Super flavorful plus they aren't stingy on the portions either. I got a half and half with jambalaya and drunken chicken (FYE), mac & cheese, and peach cobbler for $8!! You can't beat that. I would definitely return to this place. So happy to have them in Antioch.

Amazing food and very welcoming.  First time there and they offered and gave me samples of what felt like everything.  Ordered lunch and brought home dinner.  I do hope they decide to come open in the Houston, Texas area.

We stopped by for dinner after a friend recommended it. We were greeted by smiling eager employees who let us sample pretty much everything on the menu. We made our selections which were delicious, affordable and large portioned. They also included free cobbler. We will be back.
VpK-Q7tzCyAZUjIv19-TFg:
Excellent service! 

Once again, my fellow Yelpers did not disappoint. Offerings were fresh and accompanied by travel themes which is a favorite subject of mine.

Staff offered suggestions that matched with Yelp reviews. Menu had many great choices. Decided to share two popular plates - Avocado toast from the all day breakfast menu and the SFO sandwich from the lunch offerings. The SFO, which represented one of my favorite cities,  included kale, avocado spread, turkey, mustard and aioli mayo. ingredients were fresh and all tasted yummy! 

As Yelpers mentioned, Khalil and Nate were very attentive, but didn't hover. Extremely helpful with details on our many inquiries. Lol. 

There were many coffee selections in addition to the food offerings. Opted for the latte with two shots of expresso and added honey and cinnamon. Was interested in the dynamic of coffee and honey and pleasantly surprised by Kahlil's suggestion of cinnamon too ;-)

Great end to an overnight trip to Nash Vegas! Porter location had cute boutiques and other services, and walkability helpful to digest plethora of food. Will definitely return!
One of the best brunch spots in East Nashville. Waffles Benedict is a must. Coffee is fantastic. Light hearted vibe with a great staff. BE YO SELF at the Terminal Cafe.

This cafe was great! It is small, and somewhat cramped when ordering, but the staff is helpful and welcoming. The menu is pretty big, and the food was phenomenal. I got the breakfast sandwich, the biscuit wasn't too big or dry, and the promise of brown sugar bacon came to fruition. The eggs and cheddar were awesome too. The chai was beautiful and spicey, very tasty. The coffee smelled amazing. Everything came out hot.  Overall a cool atmosphere, and a hidden gem in east Nashville. A bit pricey and small but totally worth it. Definitely will be back.
SYL6bNTsT9P7iNt3A1Cwqw:
FANTASTIC food! Great beer selection. Their cocktails are always delicious. 

Only downsides are the service really is kind of terrible... you guys need to step up your game. All three times I've visited, I waited 30 minutes after being seated to even have an order placed. And parking is a pain.

I was already a fan of Party Fowl so I was excited when I learned one would be opening right by my house. I got Sunday brunch with my parents on their first weekend open. 
Parking was a big concern of mine with the area, but they have done an excellent job with making space for everyone. We got in and were immediately seated. I was impressed with how they had redone the space(much better than how it used to look). 
We placed our order and promptly had our appetizer(biscuits and a apple butter) brought to the table. They forgot the plates for sharing, but they weren't needed as the apple butter was good enough to make you not want to put it down. We waited a while for our food, but I didn't think much of it as A. Everything is always fresh B. It was very crowded as it was opening weekend and they were probably loaded in the kitchen. Nevertheless, our waitress brought us out a basket of piggy chips(which were amazing) for our wait. This was a great gesture on their part. 
Our food arrived shortly after and it was excellent. I had the hot chicken and stuffed French toast which was phenomenal. I got my chicken Nashville Hot which has a serious kick, but still retains real flavor opposed to some hot food just tasting like "hot". As we were eating, the owner, Austin, approached our table and introduced himself as well as apologized for the wait. Once again, a great gesture especially as I was not upset about the wait given the circumstances. 

All in all, my experience was wonderful. Yes, it took a while, but I am happy to wait on opening weekend. As we were leaving, a couple waiting for a table asked us how it was and we assured them it was well worth the wait. I am excited to see the success Austin and his staff will have with this business. I am glad to have a place like this in Donelson.

Had the catfish Po-Boy sandwich and it was delicious. A little pricey but I do not mind paying for quality food. Service was exceptional and manager stop by our table to check if everything was ok, even if he is a Detroit Red Wings fan. Will definitely have to be there in the bar when the Pred's play the Red Wings. Hopefully the Donelson area will support this venture. I know I will definitely be back and judging from the crowd I think others will too.
vtzOsFAfonLZm95gaOZfdg:
Just tried Brother Z's for the first time -- I will most definitely be back!! Super crispy, flavorful, and the owner is so friendly and welcoming! I got the lemon pepper, garlic pepper, and Cajun dry rubs. The garlic pepper and Cajun were my favorite. 

A must try if you're a wing person! Highly recommend.
Fresh wings, good sized, especially loved the Cajun and Mystery Sauce. Reasonably priced, worth driving over into unknown territory. Very surprising considering the extra modest structure.
Excellent! Way better than Buffalo Wild Wings. Owner told us we'd be happy, and he was so right. You can't go wrong having their wings -just delicious.
pbjHwv2yT4pueZOtz7UeRQ:
Great calzones and they deliver!!!
Hummus and pita plate was also quite good.
Prices are reasonable and delivery was prompt.
I've never been to their location but I can definitely recommend their food.

A little awkward when you walk in. Kind of hard to find out what to do. We were the only people there on a Sunday afternoon. Great food, I would say get it to go. The atmosphere is not the most pleasant. The chicken plate was good and the staff gave us some hummus to try. Both very appetizing.

This is my number one go-to quick and easy place for pizza carry out on the east side. Prices are reasonable, pizza is always hot and fresh, and they have great service. I know they have a new delivery service that they have been using, but I have not used it. I highly recommend this place. Give them a shot!
avivi8rQFEaR9iH_CZ1iBA: Exceptional in every way. Every dish was incredible and the customer service was amazing. Happy for this new edition to my neighborhood!

This place was one of the best meals I have ever had. We made our reservations 2 months ahead of time, it was the last stop on our Honeymoon trip. The service was amazing, they gave us complimentary wine to help celebrate and a few other dishes on the house because the kitchen was running behind. Everything we are was to die for!! I wish I could move to Nashville just to eat here every night, this place was heaven!! Highly recommend to anyone going to Nashville!!
Great spot! We started with oysters, which were excellent, then had the tomato salad, pork gnocchi and red snapper. Everything was delicious and the service was excellent.
oJvOiVyzqWqOfvaRH_TflA: This has to be the best Listening Room I have ever visited.  I have been twice and now I I am hooked.  The small plates and Flatbread are outstanding as well as the acoustics. My next move is to become a Vino club member.The short ride up from the Huntsville area is well worth it

Our bartender was awesome, so friendly and personable, as well as the host and concierge. We didn't make it in to a show, but I've heard great things. I just wasn't interested in the performer unfortunately. 

They have totally customizable flights from their wines by the glass menu, which is really nice. I do wish they'd have a few suggested flights on the menu as well though to keep the tastes complimentary.

We ordered the brussel sprouts, which were pretty good. We got sold on the wild mushroom flatbread. He said it was award winning. It was fine, lots of rapini and pretty salty. Another guest gave us the last couple pieces of his margherita flatbread and I liked that one more. I wish we would've tried the duck pizza instead, to be honest.

There were a lot of enticing menu options. I'll definitely be back.

Brunch this morning. Good food huevos rancheros and build your biscuit. The decor is awsome and concert venue looks great. It has a warm fire place right in the middle of the room. Will return for dinner and drinks.
7rJjMU7YVDtLwOvHbQ1tbg: Ruby's is consistent with good food and fast service. Their timing between salad dinner was perfect. They also have WiFi here which worked great. Nice for those of us that travel alone. Great choice.
Love the salad bar and the prime burgers.

Quite the opposite of all the negative reviews. Had dinner with my parents here on a Saturday night around 7:30. The restaurant was packed. There was about a 20 minute wait, it wasn't bad the hostess had a great attitude. Once seated our server Marcie was very energetic and very attentive, not once did we have to wait or ask for anything she was very on top of things. I noticed she had several other tables along with ours yet her service never lacked to be so busy. The salad bar was fully stocked, everything was fresh. Our entrees (steaks) were cooked just as we requested. The food was great as was the service. We will definitely dine here again and soon!
gqq_UziZzsW25cNu6pXvzg: We  used doorstep delivery twice and both times the food was fantastic. What we ordered: Elvis Green Chile Fried Chicken and the Baja Tacos (shrimp and fish) Both meals were excellent. The dressing used for the Baja Tacos was perfect for the cabbage.. The tortilla-(soft) incredibly tasty. The Elvis Green Chile Chicken was so tender and the Lay's potato chip coating was delicious. The green chili sauce - was very very good. Lie to your waitress and say you didn't get enough Green Chile Sauce. We eventually ran out & because it was delivered, we fought over licking the container to get the last of it. So, if you eat in....tell them you want MOooooar Green Chile Sauce.  The Chips and Salsa were also incredible. The chips were light and not greasy, but did tend to break when you heap 3 oz. of the best tasting salsa in the world on it. So, can't fault Chuy's for being a grubbing hungry salsa eater. Again, used Doorstep Delivery and the food arrived hot, and just as good as if we were in the restaurant. (Atleast, I think...haven't Dined-In, so ... if it was any better....you are in for a treat.)
Best servers ever! Our server last night was great AND had fun joking with us at a Star Trek themed bday dinner.
My husband and I love this place. We love the creamy jalapeño sauce and the Tres leches cake. Those are the two main reasons we go there. The food is good but the service is not so good.
BEyfonv_0czOFgpJmLP6iw: Stopped here with the wife for a bite and beer. Had the Poboy special with Braised Beef Rib and man was it delicious! (forgot to snap a pic before I devoured it) Can't complain with Shiner on tap either!

I had the roast pork sandwich and my husband had the special which was a braised beef rib Poboy. We also ordered the beef fat fried potatoes. Everything was outstanding! Also love that they have a bar with beer on tap. I know I will be craving that sandwich again soon! Locally sourced meats available for purchase and a locally owned shop. Definitely will be back!

Quality, quality, quality. Normally, me and my partner are all about the food deals, but we decided to splurge on some good meats and you can taste, feel, smell, and see the difference. 
We stopped in and shared a roast beef sandwich, but that name makes it seem mundane. Let me tell you, this sandwich was magical. Greens, carrots, garlic mayo, flavor punch. We also shared the potatoes with duck fat and omggggg--i don't even like potatoes, but I'd change that policy for these. 
They keep their menu simple, but I honestly can't wait to try the other things. 
We also walked out with a purchase of a beautiful sirloin and bacon, and I can't tell you how happy I was with the product. We will definitely be back.
CJCnOLSFgOuWjK5jeWqlJw:
I've honestly only been here for Sunday brunch.  And I take away one star just because of the ridiculous wait times, even though I often knowingly subject myself to the torture--I've waited up to two hours to be seated, but that's not the norm.  Plan on an average of 45 minutes though.  

The positive to the wait times though is that there is a small bar right up front where you can order mimosas, bloody Mary's, or just a good Diet Coke while you wait.  The menu is packed with everything you can imagine.  My favorites have to be any of the eggs Benedict choices or the panhandlers (all start with hash brown style potatoes and then are topped with various yummy things).  Really hungry?  Go for the Eighteen Wheeler--French toast, 2 or 3 eggs, sausage, bacon, and potatoes.  And yes, I've had it!  

Breakfast dishes not your thing?  There are plenty of sandwiches, salads, soups, burgers, and chicken to choose from as well.  It's one of my favorite places to brunch in Nashville, but again--get ready to wait!

Get the Biscuit and Gravy-style eggs benedict. I will be recreating in my own kitchen for the next few weekends.... And that's only because I live in St. Louis. 

Fresh squeezed orange juice (with free refills) is also hard to beat.
Incredible food menu, especially for breakfast. True the service isn't the fastest (keeping it from a 5-star rating), but the food and atmosphere makes this place a great place for breakfast.
vSvbdFKfr6u5-zykn49CWA: Delicious and wonderful! Server was great and atmosphere was chill. I would definitely recommend this place to friends.

We love this place. Great sushi, and the best pork gyoza I've had this side of Japan. Amazingly it's also very affordable. Family owned business where the owners take a very personal interest in your satisfaction with the meal Definitely the best sushi place you're going to find in East Nashville.
Sooo amazing! The sushi was to die for! They give you generous portions of fish and I felt like the quality of the fish was worth the price. This place should do really well in East Nash!
7T7GA57J9hlGNlPNlHHhqQ: GET THE FALAFEL SANDWICH. The best I've had in the States!! Fresh bread is also a great option here, as well as almost all of the Arabic staples and ingredients that you can't get anywhere else.

Thank God i have found an Egyptian grocery store. I have just moved from Los Angeles and now that I am in Tennessee I realize that I should have been more thankful for the overcrowded, loud smelly grocery stores. 

So this grocery store is small, crowded, poorly organized and stinky - its like you are in Egypt. If you can get over those things this is the place to get middle eastern foods such as: pita bread (real pita bread, not american style), feta cheeses, middle eastern spices,beans, teas, pasties. And because this is a christian market they will have pork products such as mortadella (Middle eastern deli meat), 

They also prepare sandwiches at their deli that smelled wonderful, but i have personally have not tried. 

I would have given 5 stars but based on my American eyes they need to improve the smell, organization and overall appearance

I am still looking for a good Latin/Spanish market but for my multicultural family but 1/2 the search is complete.

Hookahs and accessories yay!
Okay so we had an Egyptian connect who told us to check this spot out since we were in the market to purchase our own hookah. Now mind you homeboy (the owner) seemed like he didn't know a word of English and communication was near impossible for me but we made-do. Got an authentic Egyptian-made hookah, a big box of the coal and 6 full boxes of assorted flavors (and a bag of tea biscuits lol) for $100. It was a fantastic trip to Little Egypt, the owner was super friendly and so were the customers who all stood around watching us being all excited about our purchase! 
Actually one bystanding customer was so awesome she insisted on us opening the box to make sure all was well inside and sure enough the small little clay thingie that holds the tobacco was broken so the owner gave us a new one. Saved us time and gas from the return trip we would have had to make!
mFd-j_KM90IOamGmoN5AQA:
I'm going to lead with one of the best side dishes I've eaten in quite awhile.. Chile glazed cracked potatoes. Should have left off the ed on cracked and left it at crack potatoes...addictive. 
The concept is small plates if you so desire or if you're like me you throw elbows if you get too close so I just ordered an array for myself. Great drink menu for the drinkers.. they make in house syrups and definitely focus on quality. Atmosphere is spot on.. really cool vibe. Well done! 
P.S weird addition.. but the bathrooms were some of the best I've seen. Really cool concept.

Tonight was my second time going to Nada and it didn't disappoint!  Their chips are amazing, light and flaky with just the right amount of salt- and the salsa is all also wonderful.  In my opinion, while the tacos are good, they are not the best part of the menu.  The side dishes is where they really shine, especially the corn!  I have also enjoyed their wide variety of cocktails.  The last time we were there, we treated ourselves to the churros for dessert and they literally melt in your mouth.  Tonight our experience was especially enjoyable as we had a bomb ass waitress named Lacey who kept us laughing and well served for the duration of our meal.  I wanted to ask her to pull up a chair and join us, she was that fun.  She made us feel special and well taken care of, but it was clear that she was that way with all of her tables.  All in all a fantastic experience and I definitely plan to go back soon!

Our visit to Nada last night started out with a wait... a looooooong wait. Went at 6:30 with a party of 4 and were told there was a two hour wait. We waited just shy of two and a half!!!! Luckily we were there so long we got seats outside and drank ourselves into oblivion while waiting. Finally sat and were pretty hangry. The manager came over and apologized, gave us free queso and some free Rose. You sir are a gem! That's how you cheer up four local ladies. I got the salsa trio and the chips were salty and delicious. Loved the salsas, all very fresh and yummy. Tacos are small, but tasted great. My favorite was the shrimp, then the fish and lastly the chicken. At the end they gave us some churros too. All and all we loved the food and drinks... but next time I'm making a reservation for sure. Thanks to the manager and server for really taking care of us!
fJPpi1toRgY6yocnc5ujWg: Lulu catered our office lunch today and we could of not been happier! We had the Rotisserie chicken, sauteed kale and roasted potatoes, it was a huge hit. Miranda was extremely helpful and accommodating. We will definitely be using Lulu during our busy tax season this year!

Nashville is on a tear lately and this is why! I absolutely love this place. I had the Good Thing bowl and it was healthy and amazing.

We also had the Veggie Burger and it was very good also. The design and build out is super cute and comfortable. White brick and nice lightly stained wood accents. Cool, unique spot. Parking is getting difficult in Germantown, but that just Germantown.

The free vegan ice cream (made with coconut milk) is a nice touch at the end. Also, Rasberry Matcha muffin was dope! Highly recommend!

Great atmosphere. Very friendly staff. And good healthy food! I had the good thing bowl with the chicken.  Lots of flavor! And the free ice cream machine adds a bonus. Honey cinnamon flavored! Was really good. Will be back!
YrVz82RplLG3t9u5IRGTgA: I expected great and got god dam amazing! Flavors I have never put together before with interesting and unique beverages and high end liquer. Order everything and expect only the best. I would be back if I could afford it or got a third job.

Highly Recommended! The Urban Cowboy Public House more than met expectations.  We arrived with a group of 6 on a Saturday evening and were lucky enough to get seated right away. Something unique about UCPH is that you order your food and drinks at the bar and each come out as they are done.  So if you order multiple plates of food(which I recommend) as the portions are smaller but still very good, they will come out in order of which ones are ready first.  

The hand crafted cocktails are tastefully done, and the food comes out much quicker than expected.  You won't have to wait long for either which is nice.  Whether it's date night or a night or with friends, I would highly recommend UCPH and will be coming back.
Visiting Nashville and this bar was by our AirBnB and we came here twice. The bar is super cute and the Rose Paloma was so good. I wish this bar was in Seattle.
Ug4NowBgKzrB6LFCQSOVjA: I lived just down the road from Dolce Vita, and I always ordered from this place whenever I wanted to take a pizza home. I can't recall the exact price of their carry-out special, but it's a good deal and I recommend calling first and asking them what it is. Overall great pizza for a great price!
First time ordering from here and it was excellent!!!!  Decided to try something new and was very well rewarded.  Great product, guys.  Delicious!!
Refreshing to see a pizzeria of 2-sizes of Gluten free crust-delicious to boot! These guys nail it every time! Luv the 5 included ingredients model!
_bFrglhAvNh5SXiPQarA1A:
Good mall food!  Bourbon chicken is sweet. Blackened is salty and spicy, but I would not call it hot at all. Cajun Chicken is pretty spicy, and leaves a bit of a burn (in a good way, if you like spicy food). 

I get mine on white rice, which you won't see at the counter. You have to ask for it. They've got a steamer full of it right behind the counter. 

Overall, it's very good, and a nice change from typical fast food.

Cajun...there was no cajun today, staffed by a nice Chinese lady, we landed at 10.45am and had orange chicken, stir fried cabbage and spicy chicken.  It was awesome, way better than panda express and for $5.99 each I am still stuffed 7 hours later.  Etoufee was on the menu but no where to be seen.
I liked their food. Nothing Cajun about it. But they made my shrimp hot as I wanted and I loved it. I am not a big fan  of mall food but I loved it and it is cheap.
fzBcbK539fhZ6cZVp90vEQ:
Kobe is my go to place for affordable sushi. They have an excellent "2 rolls for $10.95" deal which is hard to resist. I recommend the Yum Yum, Spicy Salmon, Volcano, and Rainbow. 

I've tried the Thai offerings here and they were okay. I have tried the bento boxes yet; that is next on my list. 

The place can get pretty crowded at lunch so it's best to arrive early. They are also closed on Sundays.

My husband is from California and I'm from Florida, we know our sushi and we worship it. This is by far the best sushi in Nashville.

The building it's in is a little sketchy looking from the outside, but they have been upgrading it over the last few months and it's starting to look pretty nice inside! 

The food (I'm only reviewing the sushi) however requires no change. It is incredible. Every time we come in we have the same server and the same old guy is back behind the bar rolling sushi. They can be a little slow on the service, but you won't be disappointed. It's always worth the wait!

The prices are lower and the rolls are larger. If you want the best Nashville has to offer sushi wise then this is the place to go. 

We haven't tried anything else on the menu except for the squid salad and I could eat that all day. Don't miss out.

My coworker and I ordered take out here today for lunch. We were both very impressed with our sushi. The quantity was perfect, quality was great, and for a good price. We will definitely be ordering from here again!
MVKoCY1JwngXmyYxc7oxFA: Everything about our visit was wonderful! The food was great, enjoyed a few good beers, and our server was lovely, I'm not sure what her name was but on our check it said "baby slice". We will definitely be back the next time we visit Nashville!
This was the BEST pizza I've had.  Had the mushroom one and the maple beer and spinach salad, everything from start to finish was great!  Service was excellent. Neftali was the best!
This was the BEST pizza I've had.  Had the mushroom one and the maple beer and spinach salad, everything from start to finish was great!  Service was excellent. Neftali was the best!
yGlPsqK8ck33EgvUINIfvQ: A group of 6 of us walked in on a Friday night for dinner, got sat right away and our server was Naomi. She was one of the best servers I've probably ever had! The food was pretty basic, but still tasty.
