Love this place! First time in Nashville, and this was the first bar we stopped in. Has a great vibe, had the hot chicken as an app which was so good  and had the cider beer! Got to listen to some amazing music by Josh Melton & Mark Burke - they were hilarious and really talented which only added to the great time we were having! 3 floors, awesome decor, live music and roof top bar... what more could you ask for!

Amazing atmosphere!  Multi levels- we are on the rooftop bar and had sushi and drinks. Delicious and excellent service. If you are a Luke fan or country music fan this is the place to be!  Just opened last week and it's a hit!
xLYj6MYwEIs5HFcBLB7cfA: Kudos to Bar Louis for having such incredible servers! Of note is "Tim". He served my date and I the first time we came in together. About a week or two later we visited again, but decided to sit at the bar. Tim recognized us and came over just to say hello!! He wasn't even waiting on us but still took the time to greet us and see how we were doing. Bar Louis is lucky to have Tim as part of its staff!  We will definitely be back!  Thank you Tim!

Bar Louie is a great place to stop for a drink. I'm a fan of The Dirty martini and they have a decent beer selection. As for food, I've had the Totchos and the Sliders and both are good. They aren't great, but good enough.  Overall, I recommend and I'll be back.
Decent. Atmosphere is great, especially on the patio where you can hear each other talk. Food mediocre, drink options vast.
y_cUvG1Fn2yHZL84NYaS9g: Celebrated 4th of July here - great view of Music City's fireworks, tasty drinks, and listening to the symphony made it a great night!

The view of this location is top notch and is very impressive. I have been here a few times and the service is always slow and not that friendly. I think they are just going through some growing pains. I have no doubt this place will be successful in the long run. I would recommend checking out.

We really like this little bar. But it's only a location for good weather. The view is great, and the prices are really reasonable considering the prime location. The food menu, while offering high-quality pub fare, is rather limited. Still, I really like this to establishment When seeking a patio.
xIikDI4ny1YSl1XCIix_IA: Clean, friendly staff, outstanding service, and delicious food! Wouldn't choose any other Mexican restaurant!

Even with the bad reviews I decided to come in and visit the new restaurant. Despite what everybody has been saying I had a great time the food was great the salsa was amazing the servers were new but that is what is to be expected with a new restaurant I will be back
Love this place, The staff is always smiling and very attentive. The food is well worth the cheap price and I will continue to come every time I'm in the area.
wU2yYCoGq1iK2ILZ0dPMzw:
My boyfriend and I came here from out of town and it was by far the best jerk chicken I've ever had. The chef Troy is from Jamaica and introduced himself to us giving us a run down of the restaurant. Veggie Village just opened a few weeks ago and it is a place you can't miss out on. 
The interior and decor was beautiful with a "home" feeling and a Caribbean style influence. The owners Gale and Troy very welcoming and the recipes are amazing!

Try the jerk!

First off, this place deserves 15 stars! Troy and Gale (the owners) are amazing hosts. The food gets stars,the service gets five stars and atmosphere/location gets five stars.
My girlfriend and I each ordered the jerk chicken with two sides of peas and rice as well as a side greens. The chicken was without a doubt the best chicken I have ever eaten period. It was butting with spice and so juicy and tender it could be eaten with just a fork. I could eat this meal for the rest of my days (I wish I could) sadly we are only visiting Nashville but we will always recommend this place to anyone visiting and we will definitely eat here again next time we do... thank you so much for the best meal of our trip Gale and Troy!!!!!

Fantastic find! The couple who own it are warm and hospitable. He will tailor-make the food to your liking. She will give you a little history of the 100 year old home that is beautifully decorated and newly opened 9 months ago. They cater as well, so they are quite busy! The jerk chicken and cod were delicious. The vegetables had a great Caribbean flavor.  Their prices are very reasonable.
-RCy49doG-2XARGzr9Do5Q: Lamb Chops were delicious; vegetables cooked superbly... ingredients in dessert awesome .. loved my dinner; will return

been here a couple of times now and is always good-went out last night wit the bf and i ate but he didnt rlly want nuthin-now from havin worked in restaurants sounds like two broke ppl we're not but thats wat it looked like lol-server still gave us great service  steak was peeerfect as always-not over seasoned juicey and tender-perfectly medrare-quickly becoming my favorite chain --bearded white dood that was our server last night and short thick blk girl that was our server last time we were here before last night-yall rock keep up the good work

This location is rather scary. As you drive in, you may be thinking, "Is there really a LongHorn steakhouse here?" It is the one nearest the airport, if you're wondering. The neighborhood has just obviously gone downhill.
However, it wasn't so scary once we stepped inside. This restaurant has probably been here quite a long time, and the decor could stand some updating. Everything is very dark. The restroom is old and dingy but clean, and that's what counts.
We were meeting friends for dinner and were seated fairly quickly, which was really nice. Our waitress was prompt, professional and engaging. She did a great job through the entire process...Kept our drinks refilled, brought out more bread numerous times, and of course delivered our salads and steaks and removed empty plates...All with a smile.
The food was excellent! Yes, we were hungry, but my ribeye was meltingly tender and seasoned just right. The crisp salad and loaded baked potato were also divine.
So if you find yourself cautiously driving through this seedy looking part of town, have no fear. You are almost there! Stop at the LongHorn and enjoy a great meal.
PYnWk5rs30MQjwuBO5B1IQ: One of the best BBQ joints in town, and you can smell it for blocks and blocks. The pulled chicken sandwich with Alabama-style white sauce is awesome. Take it to go and sit in the park for a great, low-key lunch.

It's great and nice to see a booming Nashville economy.... But, the tourist BBQ spots are ridiculous, the wait times, etc..  a little further is Hog heaven, terrific ribs, the chicken sandwich amazing, perfect Mac n cheese..  check it out, save the hassle of downtown.  Plus the staff was super friendly.

Great barbeque. Friendly and fast service. The pulled pork plate was great. The "hot" sauce is not very spicy. 
There is some seating on site, but it's limited. Of course you can always walk across the street to Centennial Park.
dy-VWai8AV5Cix2zmpJntw: Sorry to find out they are closing. Good food, atmosphere, and staff. Just too much space and overhead. Hope they can reopen again in a more suitable space

Good place. The food is pretty authentic for a chain. (Like, I had something with orzo in it or whatever and my Dad had a sausage dish that he said wasnt greasy.) They handled a pretty big part I had with me well. Wine list wasnt horrible either.  Its not overly stuffy like some Italian joints can be, so thats cool too.

What a tremendous surprise and treat.  We had no intentions of dining out, were not dressed and arrived on a whim early on a Saturday.  The place was empty.  Beautiful decor and atmosphere.  The hostess was very pleasant and did not make us feel uncomfortable in that we were underdressed for the establishment.  The wait staff appeared to be anxiously awaiting a busy night.  We then embarked on culinary delight.   The butter for the bread was incredible, like spreadable cheese.  We had veal meatballs and sweetbreads for appetizer, first time for me.  Loved it.  Dinner, veal chop milanese and feta and spinach ravioli.  Everything was very fresh, not overdone.  This really is a phenomenal addition to the new Nashville foodie scene.  We loved everything and every minute.  Price reasonable, plan to return again and again.  I know every new place has growing pains thus probably the reason for the bad reviews in the past.  We eat out ALL the time, have been to nearly every restaurant in Nashville and, believe me, this place is a winner.
hhxrgOoLJImsLtPR1d3uJg: I just recently had K.O wings and they were good! I can't say they were the best I've ever had but they were good. The biscuits were amazing though!!!

My wife was working late so that left me and  my son to fend for ourselves for dinner, and I didn't feel like cooking. Well my 11 year old son would eat chicken every day if we let him, so we decided on wings. We normally do the BW3 thing or Pizza Hut or some other place like that, but I decided we needed to try some local, less advertised, mom and pop type place. GREAT CALL! 

First off, while the place may not be all light up, don't let the look  of the building fool you. Its what comes out of that kitchen that counts. It took about 20 minutes for our order to be done, (and there were a few folks who came in after us and got their food first, but I am assuming they either called their order in or were regulars who order the same thing every time), but the wait was worth it. 

The wings, unlike most buffalo wings you eat, actually had meat on them. I like to eat hot foods, so my son got the mild wings and I got the hot wings. Hot?? Someone ought to check the recipe because I swear acid is one of the ingredients. They were really hot but I couldn't stop myself. Unlike most places that have hot sauce just for the sake of being hot, these were hot but with flavor. Amazing wings. 

This brings us to the sides. I got cole slaw, which was great, and the cajun fries were the best I have ever had. Even my wife liked the Slaw and she hates everything. That brings me to the Baked Beans...

As I have said, my wife is the most finicky eater in the world. I let her try a small spoon of the baked beans, thinking she would turn her nose up and say a half hearted "they are alright". Wrong. After I gave her that spoonful and asked her what she thought, her eyes lit up like a kid that opens a present on Christmas Day and gets that Pony they always wanted. She then not only cleaned the bowl, but God as my witness, STARTED LICKING THE BOWL! By the time she got done, that bowl looked like it had just come out of the sleeve. I, in the 10 years we have been together, have never seen her react to a food like she did to those beans, EVER.

While the biscuits were good, compared to the rest of the meal they were not on par with the chicken and the sides, but thats like saying that you have two players on your team, Michael Jordan, and "only" Kobe Bryant. 

In case you haven't guessed it, yes, we will be back, again and again.

Those who say they are the best in Nashville won't get an argument from me.
This is my go to spot for late night food in Nashville.It stays open late and the wings and fries are great .
5DDPX5QQS43uACR1MiCVPA:
This place is set in a mall - close to the grand ole Opry house. It has a great bar and good, hearty food. 

On the night we went, a woman was singing, playing music and having a grand time on stage. She was good - kept guzzling her beer and was a terrific entertainer. That's what Nashville is known for - their entertainment even in the bierhouse. (No she wasn't singing country)
Loved everything Beers, pretzel& beer cheese, sausages, schnitzel but the MUSTARD is outstanding!!
Really good onion soup! Very nice staff and people in general. Beer was tasty and cold. Can't wait to watch the music. I'll be back.
60P_f-X24tukCIlnWiOkbg:
Decided to look this place up after seeing pictures on Instagram. If you go to this restaurant, you should know that the sign of the establishment is "Go Go Sushi," but there is a small sign on their door that is "Funk Seoul Brothers." 

Upon entering, you get a Japanese feel - music, tv with Japanese videos on the TVs on the walls, bright tables, and decor. 

I ordered the Crunchy Sushi Burrito ($12), and it was nice. It was the only non-raw sushi burrito option. The burrito was a good portion, but I would have liked some more Sriracha aioli sauce. My mom ordered the Ponzu Kale poke bowl; she ordered a large ($12) and enjoyed it very much. 

The service was alright. The water jug was clearly empty and no one refilled it until I asked an employee to. Other than that, I would return.

Good food if you get the right thing (Kimchi crack rice, spicy tuna burrito). Their service is pretty poor/slow unfortunately, and their ordering line is pretty inefficient. It seems like a bunch of young hipsters that are way too laid back to be the face of a business. They are frequently out of the things you want order most. I think this place has a lot of potential as a very unique food spot and hope to see it get better over time.

So glad I don't have to chase down the food truck every time I'm craving a giant sushi burrito anymore! I've had the spicy tuna burrito and the crunchy burrito with shrimp/crab. I prefer the spicy tuna but both were great! I love this place and the burrito is filling. Next time I'm gonna try one of the poke bowls and see what they are about. Just a tip, the sign above the restaurant on the outside doesn't say "Funk Seoul Brother" it was another sushi name but on the window there's a few signs indicating that this is the former food trucks permanent location.
MGXIPVN409ilHG-UMPtc8g: Came with a party of 17, had exceptional service considering we had enough people for eight tables. We had a reservation, so we were sat immediately. The waitstaff was polite and friendly. We ordered family style, 2/3 dishes were delicious, but that comes down to personal preference. The place is clean and professional and the servers are attentive.
we had an excellent lunch, service was exceptional.  I had the lentil soup, yum.  then we shared the marguerite flatbread, also the meatballs.  all authentic and fresh ingredients
Came in tonight for a early dinner service was great and food was very good and fresh don't usually visit malls because of crowds but will definitely start making this one of my go to spots!!!
K29f9OJYkbxgK5P5ZqUzqA: The hotel is beautiful and modern but still kept an historic feel.  The staff was extremely friendly and very helpful.  The gym was top notch including two Peloton bikes which is a rare sight!  Our room was on the 11th floor but you could still hear the street noise. We loved the room was all controlled by buttons.  Our only complaint was at 7am the contractors were using hammers and nail guns right above our room.  I called the front desk and they said they would handle it. Twenty minutes later I called again and asked for the manager.  Michelle personally went upstairs and told them to stop.  We were given a complementary breakfast which took a bit of the sting out of our 7 am wake up call during our vacation.  That was the only con of our 3 day visit so I call that a win!

The only ding on our stay here is the street noise. We were in a corner room on the 2nd floor and the street and construction noise were incredible at times. The whole city seems under construction though and the convenient location to everything downtown comes with increased street activity. Just know this can be a thing here. 
The hotel is otherwise wonderful. The restoration of this building is lovely and the staff is super friendly and attentive. Everything downtown is walkable except in the worst of weather. Our room was beautiful, a mix of historic flair and modern controls and amenities. I would not hesitate to recommend the Noelle to anyone.

LOVED brunch at Make Ready because Key is THE BEST SERVER EVER!!! We came in with a Group of 12 and she charmed us all immediately! We fell in love with Nashville because of Key! Give this girl a raise!
4uc7huVjfw0fjEgwd3Lpcg:
The interior of the restaurant is very nice! Great addition to one of the growing areas of Nashville! I thought the food was just above average. The night I went they seemed to be trying to catch up from their afternoon traffic - so service was kind of slow. My waitress ,Janiste, was really nice but kept us waiting too long for drink refills and to bring my receipt and card back after I paid. 

4 stars overall (which is fair) but there definitely is still some room for improvement in quality and attention to level of customer service.

Excellent new addition to the Bellevue area. Lots of great sushi and Thai dishes. We tried the Red Curry and Drunken Noodles. Really good. Service was attentive and knowledgeable.   We will be back!  Lunch portion and prices about $5 less than at dinner. Anxious to try more menu items!

In Bellevue you're stuck with chain restaurant sushi. Good news fellow West Nashville creepers. 
Welcome to The Eastern Peak. Stopped in today for lunch and was blown away. Do yourself a favor and drop the whole "land locked" bs. This, IN MY OPINION, is the best sushi in Nashville. 

Solid music choices (no country :) ) great sleek ambiance, NOT FILLED WITH TVS... that seems to be a problem these days.

I'm not sure if it was the owner or manager, but a very nice gentleman stopped by our spot at the bar and asked if everything was fine. That's how you know a restaurant cares. They openly want you to tell them what's going on. He even asked "if you think of anything we can do better, let us know." 

We will be back weekly. Because well... I'm willing to pay for things worth being bought.
fU1RsqC7vSp6_Maz9yNCuQ: One of the best hot dogs I've ever had. The buns are a bit too much bread, but the better for holding all your toppings! I just tear off the extra on the corners and get down. Seriously, so good.

I'm not going to say much here, except... Damn. 

Clean place, friendly staff at counter and grill, TVs to watch the sports teams sport at each other..

Dogs was about $4-$5 and the first hot dogs I've ever had WORTH that price. Simply a-maz-ing.

I will certainly be back.

SO. GOOD.

Great variety in toppings, reasonably priced.  Their coleslaw is a bit unique, and amazing. Service is always great, not to mention quick.  Get the onion rings, you will not regret it(unless you're allergic to onions. then you should probably skip 'em).



Sad that they cut the happy hour special down to M-F only, as I'm usually only in the area on Sundays, but no big.
XLYgT6XG8iuNdQ5h703_bA:
Love it. Amazing deserts, sandwiches and juice bar. The breakfast burrito is killer and the biscuits are the best!!!!
Highly recommend to vegans and non vegans. (My hubs isn't vegan and he loves it) 
Check it out! You'll be back :)

A hidden vegan treasure in meat-obsessed Nashville. 100% vegan. 100%% awesome. Don't let the name fool you. They have a lot more than just desserts and it's all wonderful. 

While I love this place I've been waiting for over half an hour for my food now. It's not that good.

I love this place! I came across it a few weeks ago when I was at The Wild Cow. Since it was my day off i decided to go to Khan's to see what else they have. Their Vanilla Lavender Cupcake and Peanut butter gluten free brownie was Heaven in my mouth. I even had my skeptical relatives try it and they loved it as well!
k76iJ85psmDFnZ4Q6GjW7Q: Tried this based on a community message board reccomendation. Tucked away in a shopping center it looks unassuming at first. Once inside it is well decorated and the service is good. The food is some of the better Asian food in the area. My family did the shrimp fried rice, pad see ew and beef bulgogi sizzling plate. They were all solid with okay taste. The menu is broad enough where I can try new things on multiple visits. I do recommend!

I love Dao. I travel from Green Hills to Bellevue just so I could get their food. This place gives you great portions for your money and it's probably one of the best places for Chinese around the Nashville area

We are new the area and decided to try this place out.  It was great!  Large selection and service was great!  Presentation was beautiful which is hard to find in other nashville Chinese restaurants.  Will be back soon.
0VzZyzrWQZ9KAW1vejEVkg:
I was directed to this restaurant by a local chef I met while traveling for work.  He could not have been more right about the quality and service of this place.  I can honestly say that this is the best meal I've had while traveling around the country in 2013.  We received great service from our waitress Christine as well as the entire staff.  

Highlights from the meal were:

-goat cheese brulee
-trout in a pesto sauce
-fried green tomatoes
-green pepper mac n cheese

All of these were excellent, and ensured that the next time I visit Nashville I'll make a return visit to Eastland Café!

We did not have reservations, and there was an infant in our party, when we tried to get a table for four (adults) the night before Valentine's Day. We were thrilled to be told to come back in 45 minutes, and decided to have our dessert first, across the street at Jeni's. True, we did still have to wait another half-hour or so when we returned. But it was so worth it, and the staff could not have been nicer, apologizing multiple times for the wait. Then, when we were seated, they said they were bringing "a couple of our small plates for being so patient." When the freebies came, they happened to be two that I was planning to order: chicken croquette and the goat cheese brulee, and they were both amazing, especially the brulee. Everything else we ordered was delicious: the cheese plate (with fresh figs and pear compote), the shrimp and grits (a first for the other Yankees in our party - she raved), and my favorite, the pork belly, as I (the other Yankee) have never had it - with sorghum. Yum. Great atmosphere, nice people, fabulous food. I was even tempted to get another dessert. ... Next visit!

Eastland Cafe has been around in East Nashville for a while now.  The ownership and the chefs have changed but two things that have been constant throughout it's history is terrific food and David (or is it Daniel?) who has waited tables there for what seems like forever.  Those two things alone have kept us coming back.  Great flavors and ingredients combined by talented chefs and a wait staff that is experienced, knowledgeable and willing to guarantee you like what you ordered.  Tonight was no exception.  We took some folks from out of town and they thoroughly enjoyed the meal.   A few things we ordered:
Goat Cheese appetizer with red peppers, sun dried tomato with honey and balsamic.
Spinach Salad, Arugula Salad
Pan Seared Trout
I had the Pork Belly which I have never had.  It was tasty.  Basically, it's uncured bacon with a little bit of meat surrounded by fat that is sauteed  in a pan.   A little rich but I was promised that if I didn't care for it they would  bring me another choice.  It was served on braised greens with their signature green chile macaroni and cheese (which is awesome, by the way!)
Overall the meal was fantastic, the wine was perfect and the service was impeccable.  I will return again and recommend this restaurant to anyone looking for a fine dining experience.  We have about three or four high quality places on the East Side now and Eastland rates high against anything else the rest of Nashville has to offer.  Try it, you won't be disappointed.
JZIZwIM5yrHMFByQPwU2IA:
I can't believe I've never written a review here of all the times I've been here. 

Hands down, my favorite Mexican / Latin American restaurant in Nashville. Hands down. Real tacos. Real mariscos. The real name is 'Tacos Y Mariscos El Amigo' 

I always home here for lunch. I get the Tacos carne Asada. They have all of them though. 

Asada
Pastor
Barbacoa
Lengua
Chicharron
Carnitas
Tripa
Chorizo
Buche

They have tostadas, ceviche!

I'm addicted to their tamales.  The chicken dinner is also out of this world good and the prices can't be beat!  I have a hard time eating mexican elsewhere now but there are two big things missing which is why I'm not exclusively an El Amigo diner when it comes to Mexican food: chips & salsa and drinks.  No margaritas or dos equis.  I typically carry out from here but it's so so delicious.
Stopped to get a bite after dropping kids off for camp.   What a great find,  excellent tacos!
gxwut4Ruxc63pY644MldWw: Good meat and three. I was the only person here for lunch. I don't know why. The food is really good. The fried okra seems homemade and reminds me of my mother's okra. It may not be the best meat and three in town, but it's really good and convenient to my work. Give it a try!

Updating my review of Lucy's Country Cafe from Nov. 2012. My opinion has not changed one bit. I still am a regular and still think it is the best meat & 3 in Nashville. Recipes are hand me downs from Lucy's grandmother and everything is delicious, especially the fried chicken. Veggies are always fresh & tasty. Will continue to be a regular & recommend it to all my friends.

My wife Brandi and I had a GREAT lunch today at Lucy's Country Cafe for the first time. Owners Bobby Pinson and his wife Lucy showed true southern hospitality by checking on the tables to get input from their customers. They also obviously have a passion for good country cookin'. I would officially put the quality right up there with the areas most popular meat&3's or country buffets. You can taste the love in the food. The staff was first class and you felt comfortable from the minute you walked in. Brandi had a meat&2 for $7.99. Chicken Casserole, Turnip Greens and Mac & Cheese. I had the buffet because I wanted to try all 3 of their entrees. Fried fish, Shepherd's pie & Chicken casserole. For my veggies I had turnip greens and corn. Listen people, this food tastes just like your mom or grandma would make when you come in for a visit. It's a feast of country home cooking. One of the biggest issues with "home cooking" places is that they often over-season their veggies. That's not the case here. Everything was very tasty and was not over-seasoned. The food was hot and fresh. My favorite of the entrees was the chicken casserole. 100% YUM. The others were terrific as well. My wife LOVED her plate. We also had corn bread (one of the best i've ever had) and rolls (like mommas) which were brought out in a basket and handed out by the server. She was precious. They serve their drinks in a red solo cup which oddly enough made me want to find a sharpie to put my name on it. There is also a cold bar with salad, slaw, pasta salad, potato salad, fruit etc. Lucy's is also a great bang for the buck. Meat&1 is $6.99. Meat&2 is $7.99. Meat&3 is $8.99. All you can eat is $12.99. You can also just get veggies and the cold bar for $7.99. What's for desert? The BANANA PUDDING caught me off guard. It is not the "normal" banana pudding with wafers and chunks of bananas. It's just pudding....and I was skeptical at first, but now I want a gallon of it. I also had Bobby's Dump Cake. A buttery cake topping over cherries, pineapples and other cobbler goodness. WOW. Rich and YUM. Do yourself a favor and go to Lucy's Country Cafe. Open 11-3 Mon-Friday. Bobby Pinson is a smash hit songwriter (toby keith, sugarland, rascal flatts) and a recording artist in Nashville which is why, i'm sure, he was inspired to do a "Sing for your Supper" on Wednesdays from 7-11 with Live Music by friends of Bobby and a dinner special for the guests (no buffet). It will be a weekly visit for me. 5 stars because of the overall experience, atmosphere, quality of food, service and value.
Pp6i9J7ipSXrpVij3VaTFw: I was in the mood for some comfort food so a friend recommended City Cafe for lunch. I was indecisive on what to eat so the server treated me to 3 samples: red beans and rice, gumbo, & the chicken tortilla soup. As a New Orleans native I am hesitant to eat red beans and gumbo outside of Louisiana but to my surprise it was delicious & well seasoned. The chicken tortilla was my favorite. I ordered the barbecue chicken, fried okra, & macaroni & cheese. It was delicious also! The restaurant was clean, comfortable environment to dine in, & a friendly staff. I will be returning in the near future and recommend this restaurant to others who love home cooking with a cajun/creole twist.

This is truly a no brainer....GO Here!!!!!  I have been here on 2 occasions for lunch.  The service was exemplary. The food was fresh and delicious.  They offer a meat and one, two or three or a vegetable plate. It will cost you about $10-$15 but is well worth it. Get the cherry cobbler as well. It is to die for

Our 1st visit last month was great... gumbo was the soup of the day. Took a bowl to go for my Louisiana raised hubby.  He gave it a thumbs up!  2nd visit is as just as good.  Tortilla soup and veggies filled me up. The corn cakes are a treat!  So good!  And.... the smoker was kicked up to cook the brisket for the 3rd Friday of the month.  I am coming back for Brisket tomorrow!!!!! Chef George is a jewell!!!
2IbyhYMs-JpZTxPNQ2ICQw:
Bad news: our washer is broken. Good news: we discovered Madeline. 

What a sweet little place. While we waited for our laundry my husband I feasted on veggie burgers, chicken pot pie soup, and house made peanut butter and chocolate macaroons. Seriously, doing laundry has never been this fun.

In addition, our server, Elyse, was a sweetheart and made sure my coffee was full the entire time. 

The staff is really friendly and down to earth. Is it silly that I can't wait to do laundry again?
This place is RAD! Do your laundry and grab a beer... Have the chicken pot pie if you want to be full for minimal cash. Super nice staff and good atmosphere!

I LOVE this place! My husband and I live on Cleveland Street, and we were really excited when we found out about Madeline's - it's in walking distance, and you don't have the INSANE lines that you would find at the Pharmacy. We've visited a few times since it's opened, and we haven't been disappointed. I've had the black bean burger and roasted quinoa, and I had no idea quinoa could taste so good (seriously, I was expecting something that tasted like birdseed, but it was smokey and delicious). I'd also recommend the Little Harpeth Chicken Scratch on draft. My husband usually gets the Madeline burger and roasted red potatoes, and loves them, too.

And the desserts. Oh my lord, their macaroons are amazing.  And the cheesecake. And the torte. And the coffee is really nice, too.
4csoz78GQbCFvb8iAXxMaQ:
I'll give this place a 5, simply because it's a TGI Fridays. Anywhere else would've been a 4. Why? Because I went in with pretty low expectations. A large chain in the most crowded area of Nashville is normally a recipe for disaster. I must say I was pleasantly surprised.

I went in with a friend from out of town and we were promptly seated by our extremely friendly and kind waitress. She was great. She pointed out specialties on the menu, and was very knowledgeable about all the dishes offered. My friend was trying to decide between two menu options, and she gave her opinion on which one she would choose and gave him reasons why. And they weren't reasons like "that one tastes better" or "I don't like that" either. She said that the first of the options was very traditional, essentially came from a box, and you could find virtually the same thing in a number of different places. The second choice was made in Fridays every days and was fresh. It may have been fluff, but it came off as honesty, which was a nice change from most sales pitches you get at chains. 

As for the food, it was rather delightful. Started with Tomato basil soup- in my opinion, better than Jason's Deli (although I'm sure it's not near as... real). It was tasty. Then I got the Parmesan Encrusted Chicken. The presentation, the flavor, and the portion were all perfect. It was a shockingly good entree. It was served with a tomato-mozzarella salad that was eh.... Take it or leave it. Finished it off with peanut butter pie for dessert. It was good, but about what you'd expect from Fridays.

Tip, tax and all, I paid $17. Pretty snazzy for appetizer, entree, and dessert. 

Overall- 

Pros- Friendly and helpful staff, surprisingly good food, affordable price, clean restaurant. 

Cons- Rather chaotic and loud at times.

I won't go out of my way to go back, but I wouldn't hesitate if shopping in Opry Mills and someone suggested it.

Great visit to a TGI Fridays in Opry Mills.  The food was cooked with a great temperature, and had full flavor.  The bar drinks were mixed great and tasted amazing. The atmosphere was great on a Friday night.  Basketball on the TV and good music going throughout the restaurant.
Went here yesterday Thursday Was greeted @the door. The service was ok..they bought my food before I could finish my appetizer. .but it was good food
4psm-jktkSmMmrl7R2vwjA: I ordered the four corner cheese pizza just to start off small & it was good. It's better than the known pizza places like Dominoes, Papa John, etc. I love the tasty sauce. I used a coupon, so the price was okay.

I would argue that Jet's pizza is the best in Nashville. And that says a lot coming from a Chicago boy being spoiled his whole life by Chicago style pizza. 
The staff at this and any location is always very friendly and helpful. And the delivery service is always so quick! They've never messed up an order and I admit I can get complicated sometimes (hate to say it). Highly recommended! Please do yourself a huge favor and order Jets on your next movie night in!

Good pie.  I was actually looking for a more local place but appears they are hard to come by.  Online ordering and delivery was super convenient;  love websites that make it easy to do business. 

Nice varieties to choose from and flavor of the buffalo chicken pie was great, no high sodium like other places (listen to me Domino's) and extra ranch dressing on the side was a nice touch.  Delivered hot enough to burn the roof of my mouth.  When will I learn??

More importantly the kids loved it, too!
jHrp-GQ2iSKUgHd_dvnWqg: Love this place. The massuman, red curry and spring rolls draw us to their truck once a week and the owners are awesome! Love them!

I love this food truck! Deg Thai has the best red curry I have ever had. For the longest time, that and the spring rolls was the only thing I would order. Why stray from perfection? Finally, a few months ago, I branched out and tried something new, and had the beef massaman. It was so delicious. I loved the cucumber relish that came with it. After that, I tried their Pad Thai. I had only tried this dish once before, from another place, and was not impressed. Deg Thai made a Pad Thai convert of me! 
The owners are super friendly, and remember me, even when it has been a few months since my last visit. 
Follow them on Twitter to find them.

I'm not giving DegThai 5 stars just because the guy behind the counter gave me a wink. Very unexpected. 

I finally was able to track down this food truck. They supposed to be in Brentwood last week but was canceled due to maintenance issues. 

My Thai rule is whenever I come to a new Thai restaurant for the first time, I will always get the Thai tea first. I tried Thai Tea at different Thai restaurants before and only Ginger made the cut. So I went ahead and ordered one here. Damn, It was so YUMMY and REFRESHING!! I would buy 2 more if my bladder could handle that much fluid (No restroom around!). I also ordered the Sweet Toasted Chili with chicken. It was good but they could use less salt on that. 

Overall, this was a very inexpensive lunch and the Thai tea was so amazing! I will come back for more and more!

Track down this truck if you haven't tried it yet!

2013/#44
-ceJoMSIAYcfGILikcqLsw: Ate here for the first time last week with my boyfriend.  Was pleasantly surprised at how good it was.  The restaurant looks very unassuming from the outside and certainly isn't in a 'hip' part of town, but once we got into the restaurant, it had a quiet, somewhat elegant atmosphere.  Both of our dinner pasta entrees were very good.  I was surprised (again) at how good the shrimp was.  Ever since moving from a shoreline state, I haven't been able to get decent seafood anywhere.  But this shrimp was actually really good!  The salads  were ok, but I did appreciate the homemade dressing.  Dessert was yummy, too. Plus, we liked the low corking fee for BYOB.  We will probably eat here again.

If you like to spend more time writing scathing reviews than you do eating delicious food, Mama Mia's isn't for you. It's slow-simmered, southern Italian goodness made with love and attention to detail and that detail is making your tastebuds as satisfied as Harvey Weinstein in an open bathrobe. Our waitress/server/food angel was a sweetheart who did everything (for everyone) to make our meal reminiscent of one you'd have in a favorite aunt's home on a Sunday. And it was a work lunch - on a Wednesday. 

They have an extra-attentive parking lot attendant-slash-lettuce-delivery-man, obscure Pavarotti playing on a loop, Italian-ish decor and unremarkable wholesale furniture but that's not why we drove there. No sir, we pulled up to devour authentic North Boston-style Italian fare, only better than anything you'd find in the Boston area. In fact, I'd put their baked ziti up against anything eaten by anyone who would dare compare food to what the Olive Garden serves. 

If you're easily offended, you may not like Mama Mia's because you probably don't like anything. That long-running fight you've had with your brother about lawn tools, that's actually based on parental psychosis, gets in the way of your enjoyment of the little things. Or the big things, like fine dining. So let it go and give society a break. 

If you simply want a good meal and like to take your time and actually enjoy the company of other people (while eating delicious food) then Mama Mia's IS your cup of tea. They're kind, they're local, they've been right there for 20 years and they don't care about your brother or your lawn tools. 

GOOD SOUTHERN ITALIAN FOOD DONE RIGHT.

Bring your own Bottle of wine if you wish! I'm Italian and I will say that this is the best Italian in Nashville! Everything on the menu tastes delicious. Sometimes there is a bit of a wait but it's totally worth it!!
ZeEk0ePOZZXgo7EMDZ0kAg: I follow them almost religiously across Nashville, head straight to them in a lot full of other food trucks, and think of them when I'm craving a lobster roll. It's pricey if you're not prepared, but dammit it's worth it and filling, they do not skip on the jumbo pieces of lobster. The rolls are wonderful, the Connecticut is my favorite, and the menu changes enough that you can't help but want to try something new every time.

This is the most delicious, and probably most expensive sandwich I've ever eaten but I will definitely have it again. Though, it was lunch time and I could have probably eaten 3 of them, the lobster portion was generous. I ordered the Maine Roll which was served chilled on a folded piece of grilled Texas toast with a line of mayo in the middle, and the person I was with ordered the Connecticut Roll which was similar but served warm with drawn butter drizzle and lemon. I ordered the chips as well but they were out. We went half and half on the sandwiches and both were amazing. They were $15.25 each and over $19 with tax and a tip, so it definitely won't be something I munch on regularly, but it was awesome. Will be back.

Out of the corner of my eye, I saw the food truck and line and wondered what it was.  Turned around and parked and was so glad I did!

Enthusiasm was high among the customers in line and waiting for their orders.  Most were like me, on their way home.

The Maine Lobster Roll was excellent!  Having eaten these rolls all over Maine a few years ago, I can definitely say these were on par for the course!  Enough fresh lobster to fall out of the sandwich, a nice toasted piece of bread, and some butter.  Simple and simply out of this world.  

Washing it down with a handcrafted Blueberry Soda was the perfect way to end this most Maine of meals.
PU0k0ylp9ZIjTAadluXXdg:
I eat here once or twice a week because the prices are great, the food is tasty, the service is always friendly, and the restaurant is clean. Sometimes it doesn't smell great in there but that's because of how frequently they bleach the kitchen floors. Worth it to me knowing that I'm eating from a clean kitchen, something a lot of cheap local restaurants struggle with. 

To get: beef pad see yew, pad Thai, crispy basil chicken, or drunken noodles. To go is a great call too, always ready by the time I get there. The included salad has awesome peanut sauce on it, always a better bet than the small spring roll you can choose between. 

Be ready for: paper plates, crowds of vandy kids around dinner time, that bleach and pad Thai smell (it's bearable!), and a great meal for $10!

True Satay.  I'm happy.  Takeout is easy, abundant and fresh.   My fave is the shrimp w/ red curry and noodles.  But the chicken with peanut sauce is very tasty as well.....oh, but the hubby loves the pork satay with sesame ginger.  Okay, that's almost the whole menu.  Seriously, you can't go wrong!
Stopped in for a quick dinner. Had chicken and shrimp satay skewers. Excellent. Will be back.
AX90mZMbYR9oPNNmOj-WQw: We ordered the artichoke sandwich (we added chicken) and the grilled vegetarian sandwich with those fresh cut fries. The sandwiches were fantastic... The bread was a little crispy on the outside and chewy on the inside, the seasoning and combination of flavors was excellent, and we loved the ability to customize our sandwich and decide among a number of topping options. The fries, cut right there and cooked in peanut oil, were awesome... Crispy, the right amount of salt, and with the skin on. We ended the meal with one of their great cookies... Super thick, crispy on the bottom and doughy inside. The staff were all incredibly nice, attentive and went the extra mile. They accidentally made one of our sandwiches one size smaller than we ordered, so they made us a whole other sandwich for free in addition to the first one. Highly recommend this spot if you are craving a great sandwich and fires. By the way, we heard they have free cookies on Tuesdays. :)
Try the Italian grilled!!! Fantastic and great service too. Everyone was very helpful. I'll definitely be back

Every time I walk into this location, I am always greeted with a smile and prompt service. I LOVE the Italian (grilled) with hand-cut fries and fresh lemonade. The sandwiches are AMAZING, but the service is even better. Great management and great food.
FZuzdnT1k5Dx_VcVXpK5fw:
Fresh and healthy food, reasonable price!

I ordered quinoa sandwich. It was very tasty, less salty, and lots of vegetables. It was pretty fulfilling! I hope more of this kind of healthy restaurants will open in Nashville!

Weakness is that each parking spot in the garage is made very tight and might be a bit challenging for some folks who drives large vehicles. Also, the restaurant is very crowded and loud, so you may have to yell at to each other to have a normal conversation with your folks.

True foods is the best spot for healthy food that doesn't compromise taste! They have a super vast menu with several vegetarian and gluten free options. They also have amazing cocktails made with fresh ingredients.

Some of my favorite dishes are the charred cauliflower, Brussels sprouts, steak tacos, and the seasonal salad!
Food is wonderful with a lot of integrity. Great place to hold a lunch meeting. However, no public wi-fi.
tlrrzblo1FMCl3c9Zju81g: A fairly good eastern European charcouterie counter, and therefore the best deli counter in Nashville, bar none. East Nashville's hipster butchers (who I appreciate too) will have to wax their mustaches for another 200 years before they can produce something that can stand to Pick's Hungarian Winter Salami. Also has a small number of fresh and frozen Russian and Eastern European foods, as well as shelf groceries. Service is delightfully Russian.

i feel compelled to give this place four stars, as everyone else already has :p

but for real, this place is cool. it really is a little piece of russia here in nashville. i have a russian friend that always had the BEST chocolates at his place, and he hipped me to this place when i asked him where they came from. now, all the super yummy ones that i remember him having are unfortunately not carried anymore. but, they do carry kinder brand candies which i remember from living in germany when i was younger (army brat represent!!). the deli meats and cheeses always look so good and i know one day i will fold. i've noticed the milkshake assembly by the register, too, so i'll have to check that out one day.

all in all, it's worth coming by here and checking the place out. a nice little unique business in nashville that i hope sticks around for a long, long time.

eric b

I've been going here for quite a while and finally feel compelled to extoll it's virtues after me and Pablo just scarfed down a packet of lamb dumplings and a whole honey cake, Quite delicious.  You can find a good selection of meats and cheeses here  including that gourmet delicacy HeadCheese.  All good value.  The dumpling selection is terrific and be sure to pick up a jar of beets with horseradish to daub the dumplings with and a jar of sour cherries to drop in your vodka when you get home.  All great comfort food and pleasant service but slow because there is always 3 or 4 peeps in line.  Russian beer and videos available.
N_jrvwCbi6IO0IiI0o81pQ: This place is legit . Bar tenders know what they are talking about . I very my enjoy every visit I have here . Awesome staff, great wine .. And the popcorn is incredible.

We came in looking for interesting new cocktails and didn't find any. They have a good list of classic cocktails, though. Our negroni and sazerac were very good. We got the lavender almonds and the plate of tartines-- goat cheese with roasted grapes, eggplant stuff, and carrot harissa. The carrot harissa might have been the best. I liked the relaxed atmosphere and staff, and the feel that this was an easy place to pop in and out. A nice way to start a night.
The full cheese/meat place with red wine was so delicious. Good size for two people, but not enough to be the full meal. Lighting is dim and food was amazing
DuqAq7XvpakIWlHAk70aXg:
Fun dive bar and the place is HUGE. Great place to play pool and do some awesome karaoke! 

Drinks are cheap, bartenders are awesome and some pretty good food which was not expected. This place is kinda like Cheers lol. I traveled all over Nashville to find a place like this
Yeah it's a dive bar, don't come if you don't like talkin' to cool beer drinkin' locals...friendly service...we're from Arizona and this is our kinda place, felt at home

I don't know what is wrong with these reviews but I came in here tonight expecting nothing special. But my lord, Cliff and James were fucking phenomenal. Came here just to catch the the basketball game (WARRIORS!) and ended up having four pitchers and a pizza! Turns out the pizza is pretty fantastic, my favorite in Tennessee. BUT THE SERVICE IS FREAKING AMAZING!! 


Apparently they have breakfast. So we'll be back tomorrow for breakfast.
vjZyFjcc8A9HAUuJczOgqA: For the area (industrial) its a welcome place to find. Had the chicken taco salad.. no complaints.  Definitely must stop in!

For the ONLY place in the immediate vicinity/business park of work (not including my company's onsite cafeteria), I am glad this is a yummy, friendly, fast place to go.  I've only been here a handful of times, but I've always been satisfied.  So far, my favorite has been the taco salad, and I love getting it with shrimp!  They close at 4, so don't wait for a late lunch or dinner pick up on the way home.  

Plenty of indoor seating, and several tables outside, which is great if you need an office break!

I'd also like to point out that the staff at the counter is ALWAYS nice, smiley, helpful, and patient...even when my coworker and I ask ditzy questions for 5 minutes.  Ooooops...thanks Taco Express!

I love the food here at Taco Express. I order it through delivery at my job and it is always so delicious. Last time I ordered from them, they sent me a cookie for free. Such kind people and such great food!! Thanks!
f8WKIeT7HMAOedo54Nrd7Q: Yum. Great addition to the Sylvan Park food offerings. Appreciate the daily drink specials. Parking is a premium. Suggest walking if you can. Grateful for street parking a couple blocks back. Fun place to hang out after work. No fans on the front patio. Should be an awesome spot come fall weather.

This is our favorite BBQ restaurant in Nashville. Also this location is the best, with a great front and side patio and a full bar. 

I recommend so many different menu items, the wings, the brisket sandwich (only served during lunch hours), the ribs (only served on the weekends), and the BBQ Nachos. The BBQ Nachos are by far my favorite and probably my favorite BBQ nachos of all time. The chips are more like thicker sliced potato chips which makes it easier to get more meat, cheese, and toppings on each bite. They also serve thick moist cornbread with some of their meals and it is so good it's almost like a slice of cake. 

They have a great beer selection that focuses on local breweries. The bartenders and staff are very friendly. This is also a dog friendly patio, so bring your pooch along. The staff will give your pup some water too! 

We love this restaurant and eat here all the time. It is the best BBQ in Nashville.

Great BBQ, service, and atmosphere! Had the pork platter and for $10 it's a healthy serving of pork with a giant, crumbly piece of corn bread and two sides of your choice. I like that banana pudding is an option as a side since it allows you to be savory with a little bit of sweet without having to order dessert. 

Just wish they would have brisket available all day, or until running out. I understand it goes quick and is a lengthy cook process, but instead of stating "lunch only" for all brisket options, why not cook more? If it runs out during dinner then it runs out, but at least they are able to accommodate the dinner brisket lovers.
cSA7NXxqObZqkU0LeNg5zw: Probably some of the best BBQ in Nashville. No frills but done well with smoke and love. Bread of the bbq sandwich was as good as the meat itself. Bake beans with brisket burnt ends was a good side too.

Delicious. I've only been once but I'll be back asap. Tasty, smoky brisket in a sandwich made with some high quality fresh bread and a heirloom tomato cucumber salad as a side, for the same price where elsewhere your only choices for a side would be fries, cole slaw, mac & cheese, etc. But you don't get barbecue for the sides: the quality is there on the brisket too.
Street food at its best. Authentic and cheep. Very simple side of the road kind of place if your simply looking for BBQ you won't go wrong.
fBRoUhkpEXLI_QQUJ0du8Q: Wow!  Completely surprised by this tiny place in Nashville!  Amazingly flavored foods from fried to steamed to stewed to stir fried.   We tried a little bit of everything and nothing disappointed.  Momos, pakuras, mutton, breads, dal, chicken... you should try it all!  Everything was freshly made and brought to the table when ready.  Ask the server what to order and be ready to try new flavors and delicious tastes. Can't wait to go back!

You MUST try this restaurant!  The food is excellent, and everyone who works there is friendly and welcoming.  I went with a friend, and we ordered the vegetable momo (dumplings), vegetable thali (a large selection of vegetable dishes served with rice, crispy pappadam bread, AND dessert), and chhola batura (fried bread that managed to be both soft and crispy, served with a chickpea curry).  It was a ridiculous amount of food, but we couldn't stop eating.  We also had the masala tea, which was so good that I purchased some in the market to make at home.  I have no doubt that I will be returning to sample more dishes from the extensive menu.  It is a wonderful and worthy addition to the international restaurants that grace Nolensville Road!

If there's an option to give 7 stars I would do 7. I had limited experience with Nepali food but I'm so glad I started exploring with this one. Vegi monos is the best, which are very similar to dumplings but it's their stuffing that set them apart.Their masala tea is also a must try. Right amount of spice. Went with colleagues ( one of them Nepali) and we love this place!
h_UbaqduIhtIQvYV7p2oVQ: Tucked away in the most unexpected part of our neighborhood, this place is a hidden gem! Delicious crust, generous toppings, feta-cheese breadsticks were yummy, too.   It's a must-try kind of a place :-)

This has become our *go to* pizza place. The people are always incredibly nice, and the pizza is awesome. 

It's best to do carry out or delivery, since the inside is small with only a couple token booths. 

They also do more than just pizza, but I've never been tempted to stray from the known delicious.

My wife, son and I are glad we made the effort to come here! We're from Memphis & were planning to visit Nashville's Parthenon on a Sunday, & used GoogleMaps to find restaurants open for lunch on a Sunday. Castrillo's was on my radar quickly, and their website showed a great menu at great prices, very close by. But the GoogleMaps "street view" at the address showed what looks like an empty lot, and around the corner a "K-Market" convenience store. We almost gave up, until a photo here at Yelp by "Jacob G." made me realize Castrillo's of Sylvan Park WAS the building K-Market used to be in! So we drove to Nashville yesterday and went straight in to Castrillo's Pizza before hitting the Parthenon..

Menu (and pricing) was as awesome as their website led us to believe, and the gentleman in there was as nice and friendly as you could hope for. Sure, it had a "hole in the wall" kind of atmosphere, but that only added to the charm of the place, honestly.  Main concern was that there's only two booths to sit in, and no other obvious seating, but I gather most of their business is take-out.  We ordered a pepperoni personal pizza for my son, a meat lover's personal pizza for my wife, and a spaghetti with meatballs (add mushrooms, please) for me, and some bottles of soda. I ran to the restroom (very nice and clean, by the way!) and as I came out I could see our man in the kitchen working hard on our food.

Not long after he brought our son's pizza, then soon after my wife's, then my spaghetti.  My wife and son had super-high marks for the pizza. LOVED it! I took a bite and was impressed; worth going back for just that! My spaghetti was good, too; I'm more of a meat sauce (bolognese) loving dude, but if you prefer marinara sauces that are veggie-filled and sweet then you'll enjoy it heartily!  The bread sticks I got with my pasta were great, too. My wife and son wanted to try the delicious-sounding Sweet Cinnamon Sticks, but we decided sticky fingers weren't a great idea when visiting the Parthenon, so we decided to try them next time. And there WILL be a next time...when we visit Nashville next, we'll go out of the way to this place. Well done.  As we finished & paid, the man there (bearded fellow...I never caught his name, but he was terrific) made friendly chatter with us and it was clear how familiar he was with Memphis as well as Nashville. Great time, great food.  Must eat there again.
XbWcdLmM6qwA3d2Y7qME-w: Fantastic curries and great service - moreover, they have South Indian dishes like chettinad and sukha - wonderful! Portions are a bit on the small side, but that's minor. Check them out online, because the last time I looked, they had coupons for every meal.

Um, why didn't I try this place sooner? If you are looking for a wide variety of veggie AND meat dishes, this is definitely the best Indian stop in town. After one visit, I know I'll be a regular. 

So much flavor! Love it! We tried a few of the usuals: mulligatawny soup, a daal, chicken tikka masala, chana masala, and garlic naan. Everyone has their own version of mulligatawny and this one is definitely unique. It's creamier and almost nuttier tasting that others I have tried. Everything else was amazingly good. We were really impressed and told the main server - who seems like he is also the owner - "The food is great! I think this is the best Indian food in Nashville." Without even flinching or cracking a smile, he says, dead serious, "It is." Well, he sure is confident. He did chat with us a little and told us how incredibly picky he is and that he doesn't let anything come out of the kitchen unless he thinks it's amazing. It definitely shows.

They aren't wimpy with the spice either. If you order it medium, it's actually medium which was a pleasant surprise. (Usually in Nashville I have to ask restaurants if there is a higher rating system beyond the scale they just described so I can get some true kick)

Looking around the place, all the other dishes looked pretty great, too. Someone at a nearby table ordered chili naan which I will order next time in. Sounds good!

I did also notice that they could use another primary server. The service was highly variable. A table of four next to us with some relatively well-dressed 40 somethings got AMAZING service. The server/owner spent about 50% of his time at this table making jokes and chatting away with them.  He told them, "Don't even look at the menu. I will bring you some things and if you don't like it you don't pay for it." They got a kick out of that and seemed to really like everything. In stark contrast there was a casually dressed 20-something couple sitting nearby that probably waited 10-15 minutes for water and maybe even longer before they got a chance to order food. We had good service when the place was relatively empty but by the time we were done the place was packed and we waited as long to get our check processed as it took us to eat our whole meal.

Food was unquestionably amazing though. Definitely will be back - and often!

I found this place by accident and was pleasantly surprised.  Food was really fresh and the atmosphere didn't feel like a 3rd world country like some Indian places.  No hookers, drug pushers or general vagrants...good place.   I had the Mixed grill which consisted of shrimp, chicken, and lamb i think.  Or at least it was some meat like.  Very DELISH.  Highly recommend if you are in the area looking for food.  Not to far off is Deja Vu strip club too.  Didn't get a chance to go, and probably b est since the pungent smell of Indian curry wouldn't make the strippers trying to give me a lap dance too h appy.  But if you don't mind pissing off or making a stripper uncomfortable with your smell, this place is very close.  You can have a good meal and a lap dance all in the same hour.
UJ_WRq3rxi7s61-ZJJ1aGQ: Thai Star recently moved to this new location closer to 100 Oaks, and this a big improvement over their previous location. I have not been here enough times to judge them on all of their food, but their Kee Mao(native) was spot on. The thai iced tea was a tad off, didn't taste quite right(maybe too watery?) The service was excellent, although the food took a while. It's worth checking out if you are in the 100 Oaks area.

MMMMM.  This place is really good and they focus on Thai Dishes as opposed to some restaurants that dabble in a variety of Asian cuisines.  They serve their dishes at requested degrees of heat ranging from mild to hot.  I ordered the Six O'Clock Train Spicy Fried Rice.  The description read, "For people who like spicy Fried Rice" and the Chick Patay served HOT!  My mouth was on fire but I loved it.  I heard many of the regulars ordering a dish called Holy Basil.  One of my friends ordered the Holy Basil with Chicken so I had to try it...it was amazing too.  It was a perfect blend of sweet and spicy.  This place is great.

Service was great (although, our server was just a teeny little thing and had to have an older employee deliver alcohol to our table, which seemed to take a long time). And overall, the food was great as well.

We sat on the patio, which isn't exactly a serene environment with the railroad right above you and the busy road beneath you. However, it's great that they have outdoor seating available and I'd still sit on the patio next time.

We started off with the chicken eggrolls which were honestly some of the best I've ever had (and I've had many). For entrees, I ordered the Pad Thai and my boyfriend ordered the Holy Basil with chicken. He thought his was excellent and we both thought the Pad Thai was good, but didn't rival Smiling Elephant or Koi. 

We will definitely return to this place and take advantage of their delivery service.

Quick tips: 
they have a decent selection of domestic and imported beers (bottled)
they deliver within a 5-mile radius
aWrIkkuO04M-f2cHfjwnHA: Good location, food, and drink.  Nice red chairs and roomy eating area.  The flat screen with the slideshow must have taken down for repair.  I usually like to watch it.
Good location, food, and drink.  Nice red chairs and roomy eating area.  The flat screen with the slideshow must have taken down for repair.  I usually like to watch it.

I love this location the staff are always Professional and for the most part they are on point they do forget or the Bell doesn't always work for drive thru but the food is always good and for breakfest it's a nice place to start your day
rHavP-Q1bzhu85EumguKrQ: My husband and I stopped here for lunch and we had a really great experience. We tried lots of things from the buffet, and not only was there a lot of variety and things you don't normally see - onion pakora, mango pudding, etc - everything was really well done. Also, my husband is a vegetarian, and it was very veggie friendly, which is always nice. The service was very nice and attentive. Overall, it was a really excellent meal - we'd come back here in a heartbeat!

I've been to Cusine of India for lunch and dinner, and would recommend it for either meal.  The buffet is good for carnivores and vegetarians alike, and unlike other Indian buffets I've been to, the staff didn't seem to have any trouble keeping it well-stocked with naan.  However, I'm always a little dubious about buffets, and that didn't change at Cuisine of India.  All the food you can eat for just $7.99?  It just seems too good to be true, and Cuisine of India is not different.  The items in the buffet were varied and tasted fine, but weren't the best Indian food I've ever tasted.  Which isn't really a surprise.

If you're looking for quality instead of quantity, you're probably better off ordering from the menu.  I thought the naan and the vegetable dish I ordered, served with piping hot long grain rice, were flavorful and well-prepared.  

I like the simple, unassuming decor of the restaurant: criso white and navy blue tablecloths, a wall of windows, and a few decorations.

Excellent food at dinner.  The sag paneer was especially good, as were the chicken tikka masala and the lamb rogan josh.  Good variety of ultra-spicy (which I like) and mild (which my wife likes) dishes.  Very friendly to families with toddlers.  My colleague was in heaven because they serve papadom here.

Service was friendly but very slow.  The lunch buffet is less delicious than dinner, though still good.  The chai is fantastic.  Parking is easy.

Overall, a good experience.
cVryxrPw2keGcg2LwUBoPQ: Loved the spicy burger. fries were fresh, burger was juicy. love the non corporate feel to this place. Wish it were near my work 30 minutes south. But def a great place to get a fresh cooked burger.  Put your own toppings bar was fresh and well stocked. Great Price as well. def will be back soon.

From a first impression it's a 4/5. The wise option (having onion and something else i can't remember cooked into the burger) is super radicle. I had the number 1, wise, with fries and a drink it came out to a bit under 8 dollars.  I'm typically not a fan of the thicker cut fries but these weren't bad at all just added a bit of extra salt because I'm crazy.  One thing that was different than most burger joints though was that they had a condiment bar which I oddly preferred because I could stack as much stuff as I wanted on it including jalapeños which I can't live without. Plus all the condiments we fresh nothing over ripe or whatever. Overall it's a 4/5 would give a 5/5 if they had thinner fries. Will probably come back to try more of their extensive menu.

I was studying at the nearby Starbucks when my friend and I decided to try Wiseburger. I checked the menu online and figured I would try the salmon burger. I was only skeptical about it because this was my first time ever eating there, and I didn't want to start with a salmon burger so I went with the 1/4 spicy burger. I called my order in and then went to pick it up. As far as scenery goes, it's not much to look at, but it has a simple feel that could accommodate intimate coworkers looking for a nice place for a burger. It's straightforward and no unnecessary frills. The food was pretty good. It reminded me of a home cooked burger, which is a plus because sometimes you want home cooked food but don't have the time. The toppings were fresh and I wouldn't doubt that they picked them up from the nearest grocery store. I enjoyed it and definitely will be going back for the salmon burger and other various menu options.
poruVZH9V5p3u170Pi3iYg: Great place to take a client or just have dinner yourself. Stopped in last week. Everything was great. I had the Wedge Salad and a steak. GREAT
Exspensive without a doubt, but delectable. Awesome food. Great Wine. For special occasions, you have to try at least once in your life.

I've always liked this Morton's, although I can't seem to get here enough.  Quiet, dark and a cozy feel, they serve up some very good steaks and fish from this kitchen. Not you cheapest meal in Nashville, but a very good choice to walk to from hotel.  I always feel transposed like I'm in a great steakhouse in New York when I'm here.  You leave the street and feel welcome ...and can relax thru a nice dinner because no one will rush you.  A solid choice downtown
xoBFIHHEPVkbJNVtvublTw:
How possibly could food this good come from a truck I ask you?! We stopped by the Franklin Farmers Market on Saturday morning. It's been a while since I've been to the farmers market in Franklin, I was surprised with how many booths and people were there. Anyway, we made our way to Crepe A Diem. They were easy to spot because of the red truck AND the long line. If a food truck has a line, you know it's good. 

We skimmed the menu and decided on getting a couple things and sharing them. We decided on the Louisiana crepe, which consists of Andouille sausage, provolone cheese, caramelized onions, and red pepper - Rouille sauce ($8.50). We ordered the garden sausage sandwich, local pork, spices and fresh basil, scrambled farm egg, sharp cheddar cheese on a croissant ($8.50). We ordered both sides, hash brown, potato au-gratin with cheddar and chives ($3.99) and cheese grits, 'Beaver Dam Creek' local stone-ground grits with parmesan cheese and roasted tomato, garnished with sweet local basil ($4.99). And if that wasn't enough, we got the cinnamon and sugar crepe, Hatcher Dairy butter, cinnamon sugar, maple syrup ($4.50). 

You can tell they really work fast in that truck, it didn't take but ten minutes maybe for all the food to come out. I've always thought that most food trucks usually charge more than one would pay in a restaurant. This might be because of the convenience of it, but I think Crepe A Diem's prices are spot on. You get QUALITY food and it's not a little portion either. This garden sausage sandwich was a monster. I really loved the pork sausage inside, it was seasoned well and paired well with the cheese. Same goes for the Louisiana crepe. The sausage in that was so flavorful. I'm not a huge fan of red peppers or onions but loved this. The sides were great as well. This hash brown is probably one of the best I've ever had. I like this thing better than most places french fries. And the grits, can you say 'cheese'!? I've never really liked grits but this these didn't taste like your average grits. Lastly the cinnamon sugar crepe was a nice way to cleanse the palate after a hearty breakfast from Crepe A Diem. We will be back.
Amazing steak and egg croissant this morning at the Franklin Farmer's Market!  We will definitely be back!

Magnifique! This is a wonderful little food truck. I ordered the apricot and brie crepe with almonds and honey. Wow, delicious. And only for $8, I got a pretty good deal. The crepe was huge and stuffed with all the good fixins. I usually go to The Perch for a dose of crepes, but I have to admit, this place might be better! 

Keep up the good work!
FMQD9NrrTvQ3MRSANhPOKg: Be far the best Waffle House in Hip Donelson!  The staff is always friendly and the food is always cooked to perfection. They really go above and beyond to make each customers experience great. If you're looking for a friendly place to have a great meal then look no further. We love the Waffle House!

So you might ask why review a chain restaurant? As a Realtor I often get asked, where are the good eateries in Nashville. 

As a traveler, that is usually the first thing I think about before I see the attractions and the "Historical sites" ;) 

Well today I had a hankering for Waffle House and I went to this one.

My Review:
Y'all come to the best Waffle House in town! They're super friendly and cook your food correctly! My favorite one in this area. They have amazing culture at this one, they make you feel welcome and they have a positive energy. ;)

The staff here are super nice, the food was absolutely great (only bad part - eggs had A LOT of butter on them, but you can easily wipe that off), but there was no toilet paper in the bathroom. If we are ever in the area again, will def. come back!
TKzt7jlAgLRJe4vzQje1bA: I love this area. So I use any excuse I can to go there. Today, it was to get some work done which left me in need of a good coffee shop. There is parking in the front and back, but they were full upon my arrival. I've never had a problem finding parking on one of the side streets though. I was digging the inside vibe. Lots of natural light and plenty of seating. I thought the staff was really friendly and the prices were normal. Drinks are good and they had a nice tea selection. I got the bacon, cheese, and egg bagel and it was delicious. I don't know why but their bacon was something special. It had a stage on one side so they must have performances every now and then. Overall, it was nothing fancy but it's a good place to get some work done and enjoy a nice beverage. And yes, like many of the reviews say, you do leave with the scent of Portland Brew. Buy hey, there are worse things to smell like.

Great coffee shop out on 12 south. Some of the best prices I have found in Nashville. I love to come and study here since it has such a great vibe. They also have live music sometimes which is amazing as well. Highly suggest visiting here if you live in Nashville or are coming through town.

My search is over!  love the minimalist space and the TONS of seating.  Iced tea is delicious.  I am happy I came in here.  They waste no space on the unnecessary, I feel like you can rely on their being seating almost all of the time.  Plus I really enjoyed the halloween playlist.  Looking forward to working here all of the time.  It also appears that they are fine with outside food, which got me thinking about Edsley's across the street, therefore ending the problem of relying on bland coffee house food a la The Frothy Monkey.
x-SXtoUmGhABNBrsD__q7g: Love this place. You walk in and you are greeted with warm smiles.  The selection is simple and awesome - jerked chicken, curried goat, etc. I got the jerked chicken with rice and peas, plantains and a salad. The chicken was cooked just right but if you want it hot (relative term i know) you will need to ask for sauce. I will definitely be back.

My wife is from New York and has been wanting some Jamaican food. We heard about Island Vibes so we stopped by for lunch and ordered the Oxtail meal and the Jerk chicken lunch special and it was delicious! %100 authentic Jamaican Flava...  We definitely will be going back!!   Someone wrote about a gravel parking lot not true it is asphalt just saying!
When I ate here, there was a lady that worked that Sunday, and she was very nice. It was my first time to try Jamaican food, and I had the oxtail. It was good.
ajeJQlYpzALo8WTe9gdxCQ: This place had some  good food me and a friend went for breakfast and we were seated promptly as we walked in which that's a first our server she was very attentive to our needs I ordered the country ham,scrambled eggs,grits and toast which was delicious my friend had ordered pancakes,Turkey sausage,grits and eggs which she was not disappointed at all it was my first time eating there for breakfast I have been for lunch and dinner at other locations and I have not been disappointed they have good food I shall return.

Amazing food! We've been to Cracker Barrel a lot but this was so delicious! Sasha was a wonderful server. She was very nice and took care of us. 
Thanks for a great experience!

I have never gotten great service at cracker barrel. Until today! Marsha is fantastic. She helped us with our order and was so polite the whole time! She brought out our biscuits before our meal because she knew we would have to wait. This lady is fantastic. I'll be back to ask for her Everytime.
MwFLVJ_f_ITjvZI6SRPAdw: I've been coming here twice a week for 4 years. It's my favorite Indian place and it's $8. A nice Indian family run it and can't say enough good things about it.

Samosas (veg & chicken), butter chicken, chicken curry, onion dosa, rice, naan, and all accompanying sauces, etc were so delicious. Very spicy, just how I like it. Service was decent, if a bit slow, but they had to cook fresh dosa for us. We will definitely come back again.

I always think about buffets when it comes to Indian Food for lunch.  This place is surprisingly convenient and delicious.  This family cooks in the spicy Andhra fashion and be prepared for a kick.  My favorite dishes are the goat curry and the peas and cheese.  At about $7 for a stacked plate, its one of the best deals in Nashville.  The friendliest people work there BTW.
q9lrGqhePkegQsgwGjpX-g: So for having family who are New Orleans natives and growing up with authentic Creole cuisine, this place not only met, but exceeded my food expectations. They had Shrimp Po boys and beignets for the special that night which we took full advantage of. The shrimp were cooked perfectly- the sauce...the seasoning... down to the the fresh baked bread - everything was delicious.  The beignets were just like Cafe Du Monde's. We got the pleasure of talking to 1 of the owners and once he found out about my New Orleans connection, he brought us a bowl of gumbo to try and my husband was literally licking the bowl. The lounge itself was really cool - old school style jazz club meets modern speakeasy. And the music was great with a variety of singers. Definitely will make it a habit to come here every time we are in Nashville. Thank you, Mike and Marcy!
Awesome bar!! Monday is salsa night, the band plays amazing.... The staff gave a, great service!! I will definitely be back when i visit again. Cover was $10 but it was worth every penny!

What a special place. I heard about this place by WOM. I walked in off the street with my girlfriend. We stopped in Nashville on our way to Chicago. Anyways, we were greeted with smiling faces and eager to please service. The food was delicious and drinks strong. Mike took great care of us!! Out of our short two day stay, Rudy's left an impression and memorable time. Hands down! Gotta check out this place out. Oh and the artists, you're so close to them and they mingle with crowd. Awesome conversations. Thanks Mike!
cxQ3CKel3EEZLYEyu9v3og:
This place is great for large groups!! There's is live music by the bar are out from and the restraint is all the way in the back. It's nice being able to hear the music while also having conversation that doesn't require screaming. The back is open to a nice view of the rive, stadium, and bridge.

Got a variety of things to share and for the most part we enjoyed everything.

GET IT:
-catfish was delicious! So tender and crispy
-fried green tomatoes were perfectly fried and the pimento cheese added a nice kick 
-sweet potato has was the best bite we had there. Very crispy and lots of flavor
-Mac and cheese was VERY cheesy 
-stuffed peppers are very unique! They cheese adds a distinct flavor without overwhelming the dish
-chicken tenders were pretty big and tasty

SKIP IT
-deviled eggs: very dry and bacon not crispy
-shrimp corn dogs: I appreciate the creativity but the taste just wasn't there. The batter was very thick and overwhelmed the shrimp

I brought a group of 22, 19 of them being teenagers, and we have a great experience. Miranda contacted me about my reservation after having some challenges trying to make it for such a large group. We were immediately greeted, and they verified our seating. Jacob our waiter was attentive, personable, and patient; his service was excellent. The food was delicious. Next we were able to take the kids upstairs for the live music. The restaurant and bar area were very clean. I would definitely visit again.

Choices gets a solid four stars.  Good food, kid friendly, service always seems a little slow even though the place is never that busy.  A good choice to try if you have a large group, as they are able to accommodate large tables better than most other places in Nashville.  The food is good enough to keep coming back, it's just that it always seems to take a long time to get through a meal because our service has always been slow.
5L5svXbsXTXxLJcjly9o_Q:
It's oh so difficult to find good lamb in this city. I had a lamb kabob at Hot Kabob's today and it was perfectly medium rare. The lamb wasn't fatty at all. The chicken and steak kebabs are also really really good.

I thought that it was just a little take-out place, so I was surprised when we walked in and it was an actual sit-down restaurant, although small. They do a good business in take-out though. While we were there, there were at least three people picking up food. 

I also didn't know that the owner of Hot Kabobs also used to own Porta Via in Bellevue Mall, which closed down (surprise, right?). I ate there a couple of times back when I worked at Bellevue and always loved it, so I'm not surprised that Hot Kabobs is so good. 

The cucumber and tomato salad (with mint and spices) that comes with your meal is fabulous. Our bread and salad actually came out first, but I recommend saving both to eat with your meal, because they complement the meat and rice very well.

The portions are pretty huge, so I think next time I go, I might just order off the side items menu. It actually seems more like an a la carte rather than side items because you can order the kebabs on their own and choose your own sides to go with them.
