They don't have any alcohol, and I don't think they allow BYOB either, which is kind of a strike against the place, but it is good enough to overlook that fact.

How funny...I had no idea that the owners were involved with Porta Via...that makes a lot of sense. I used to LOVE their Apollo sandwich! They used to be next to Bellevue Guitars on Old Old Hickory before that...wow.

I have been going to Hot Kabobs religiously ever since it opened. Now that I no longer live in Nashville, Hot Kabobs is the first place to go on my list when I visit. The khoresht fessenjan is, quite possibly, the most amazing dish I have ever had anywhere. I must tell this story.......

I went to visit my brother in San Fransisco and decided that I'd try khoresht fessenjan somewhere else. I figured that San Fran might have what it took and there was this cool new website called yelp to help me. I located what was supposed to be a good place with the dish. Needless to say, it wasn't even close to the dish that Hot Kabobs serves. I was thrilled to return to Nashville and tell the owner this story!

Sorry, had to get that out. The owner is awesome, the beautiful waitstaff is generally fun with a little prodding and the food....yes. I know they serve other stuff at Hot Kabobs....but I don't care. The khoresht fessenjan is different everyday - I like it when it's more tangy. I've spoken to a few Iranians and they've told me not to even bother trying to make it....which is ok because that forces me to return to Hot Kabobs whenever I can!

I had no idea what to expect from Hot Kabobs, but I had driven by it a million times so I figured I should check it out. I ate there last week, and it was really tasty stuff.

You might miss this tiny house that's now a restaurant so keep your eye out! I was the only person in the place when I went in, but the girl at the counter was happy to walk me through the menu and give me suggestions on what to have. I ended up with a sampler platter which had both chicken and beef kabobs. The meal came with some tomato and cucumber salad and great jasmine rice. As I was waiting for my carry-out order, several other dinners came in and took a seat. It definitely seemed like they were all repeat customers who really enjoyed this food.

It's a pretty inexpensive meal that I know you'll enjoy. They are also open for lunch with daily specials so I know I'll be back to check those out.
dAtU1ilhVlt_uid1vTTbAQ: Absolutely the best breakfast around! Service was quick and Bruce, our waiter, was so friendly! We made a special trip to come eat here because of several recommendations and it was well worth it!

The jams seemed expensive but once you taste any and all jams price doesn't matter. Be sure to try the pumpkin butter. Country ham is a little expensive but again worth it. Chicken was also good and not greasy

Sunday morning, arrived about 830 and seated immediately. Lucas (A-1 server and menu tour guide) provided hot biscuits, jams and recommendations. I ordered steak biscuits and my wife ordered BBQ pork, scrambled eggs and grits. The steak biscuits were great, steak cooked as ordered. My wife's food excellent, BBQ pork the best BBQ on the trip...portion size suitable for Vince Wilfork or other huge dude...BBQ served on excellent corn cakes...she also raved about the grits. We left around 930 and the waiting area and porch were packed.
taQSr43QAjuhjfrwthzt9w:
Excellent food! I ordered the chicken and waffles, which was a great choice.  The chicken was seasoned to perfection and was also nice and crunchy.  The waffle had great flavor as well. 

My brother ordered the catfish so I also had the opportunity to try that, it was delicious! I may order this the next time I'm out for a visit.  

I would definitely recommend trying this place, just make sure you order ahead to ensure that you don't have to wait too long.  It seems like it can get pretty busy in the evenings.
Great chicken. Only took me 12 minutes to get in, order, and receive my food. Mild sauce is amazing.

Love this place. Near my office. Food is consistently good. I usually get the three hot tenders but have tried their catfish as well. Only complaint is that it takes so long bc they're always busy! But that's a good thing for them lol I try to call ahead so I can pick up and head out quickly.
Mm5rQS6FJ4eREcfVq0KYww: This was my first time to this Moe's.  It was a Tuesday, right before the noon rush.  I ordered a veggie bowl (rice, beans, peppers, corn relish, guac, lettuce, jalepeos, etc.) and a side of chips.  The line moves fast and your food is ready by the time you get to the register.  So, other than saying that I tend to like these type of restaurants (mexican style food and you pick what you want to create your entre), I really like the food.  It was quick, decent price, tasted good and the place was clean.

Love Moe's!  So glad we have them in Nashville now.  The one thing that I can honestly say about this place after several visits, they have THE nicest fast food staff ANYWHERE.  It goes well beyond the "Welcome to MOOOOOOOOOes shout" when you walk through the door also.  For one strange fast food moment, I always actually feel like the guy or gal in the plastic gloves actually cares about my day and experience.

The food is really good, but full disclosure, I only get the rice bowl.  It's relatively healthy and makes a great quick dinner when you just don't want to cook.

So, why no 5 stars for this location?  For heaven's sake, Mr. Owner/Manager/Decision Maker, you close at frickin' 8 pm!?  This ain't Bucksnort, Tennessee!  It's Nashville on the busiest, hip-hoppin' spot in town.  Really?  8 pm?  Where are my orthopedic shoes?  I, alongside, over 15 (yes 15) strangers stood outside the door at 8:21 scratching our heads.  After I got in my car, another three to five potential Moe-lovers pulled on a locked door.  

The food is just too good to hold back on us after 8.  Otherwise, I love you.  That's all.

A catered lunch on 4/17/15. My first time eating Moe's and it was pretty good. The Fajita Bar was delivered and set-up 15 mins. before our scheduled "Lunch and Learn" began. A wide variety that included chicken and beef, Mexican rice, black beans, tortillas, guacamole, tomatoes, salsa, shredded cheese, lettuce, queso cheese sauce, chips, and cookies with sweet and unsweet tea. We were able to go back for seconds & still had food leftover to offer to other departments. I will definitely recommend Moe's for our next clinic luncheon.
ZIfvFrdyT1L9mS-NpGhuMA:
This is my favorite Starbucks in the Nashville area. I stop by most work days after work & then on the weekend in between breakfast and lunch. I've never had to wait a long period of time. 

They have plenty of outlets to charge devices, which is a great plus - this includes outside, which is super helpful. 

Their baristas are pretty great - and Ashley, the manager, is awesome. They are always pleasant and helpful - and willing to accommodate. The last time I placed a mobile order,they had run out of one of the things I ordered. I was greeted immediately & received a genuine apology & they gave me two of my second choice to make up for it. 

My only complaint about this location is sometimes they train baristas at really busy time - which I understand they have to get used to, but it definitely slows everyone down.
Very good fast friendly service convenient I have been going here for years and the service has been better every time especially since the renovation.

These guys are great. We come here all the time for work. The staff are friendly :-)

This is a great location to meet up with potential clients (if you're a business owner). It's also a nice place to just hang out. There's plenty of food places nearby, and the neighborhood is super nice!
beMGOiI-19QWcB0l5WnhEg: From the pickiest eater to any gourmet fan there is really something here for everyone. We took my grandmother here for her birthday and it was simply perfect! The service was so friendly. They even offered to cover the vent by our table and brought our to go box to us after we forgot it. The food was so fresh and so tasty. It was such a nice elegant lunch. They even had plenty vegetarian choices to choose from.  And the price was very affordable for such a nice place. Everyone was impressed. I am sure that we will be back.

I have only been to Martha's one time. One of my coworkers LOVES this place so I decided to take my parents when they came into town. The location is fantastic, we walked around the plantation after lunch so that was great. The food was excellent. I had the chicken croquette and it was amazing. My dad had the craw-fish cakes and he too loved them. The food is amazing. The only thing I was not crazy about was the hostesses. It was a bunch of teenagers just hanging out and since our table was right by the door we could hear there entire conversations. Next time I would just ask for better seating. Amazing food, a must go.

I've been coming here for the last eight years, enjoying Martha's contemporary twist on classic southern cuisine. I was sad to hear that this spot had closed their doors in May. Of course, you can attempt to recreate many of the dishes with the help of her cookbook, New Southern Basics: http://www.amazon.com/New-Southern-Basics-Traditional-Today/dp/1581822413/

The news is that Martha Stamps plans to launch a new restaurant focused on local and sustainable foods--possibly in an old house with a garden.
ji_mV3tIdATJhU4i1t0nJQ: Went with the classic and have to say one of the best chicken salad sandwiches I've ever had. Soothing restaurant. Nice people. Definitely will come back. And they have a drive through!
My first time to this franchise. My wife of course recommended. As a guy I wasn't too sure, but it was actually really good and want to go back!!

Yum! We had the chicken salad with grapes & nuts ( can't remember the name they called it) & the pimento cheese. It's VERY VERY GOOD! 
We'll be back, again and again! It was delicious.
ZDY-jvetOMOE5BMM8e_BcQ: Tori was our rude waitress! She had me laughing so hard I couldn't breathe. Great food good time.

Very funny place. You should not be upset and be ready to have a lot of fun, that means getting ready to be embarrassed in front of everyone. All the people here have great sense of humor, so definitely bring that with you. Very good drinks and food to add up to the fun.

Great stop for a beer, just off Broadway. They specialize in sarcastic service, so be prepared.

It's loud, it's rowdy and it's a good time.
sF3mLyRkVV6aQKPCnzy0rA:
Went for an early Sunday morning breakfast.

I will be back.

The service and food was great.

I could not ask for a better breakfast and the coffee was excellent.

We've been in at least once a month for Saturday breakfast and we have had all the menu items, and a lot of the specials, and the common theme is local and fresh.
The staff is always amazing, and they put up with a lot from my 7 year old flirter. 

Pro Tip: let the wait staff talk you into or out of anything; they are NEVER wrong.

Location is well off the beaten path in an industrial park near the freight yard.  Make of that what you will.  At least there's ample parking and little street traffic which alone is unique and rare in Nashville - and a nice change from the strangely cramped and over-crowded feel of most of the hip parts of town.

If you're looking for a total gut-grenade breakfast, with cheese in nearly everything including the hash browns and the grits, this will serve your needs nicely.  The breakfast menu is funky-south:  I had a crawfish gouda omelet with hash brown casserole on the side and it was stout and tasty  The sausage I sampled from other's plates is a spicy, somewhat fat almost cajun style link - and it's delicious.  Will do this again.
gutmUqtDfbNsImXOX3Zo4Q: Nice place. Very simple and open. Service is great. Ramen is very good. Prices are a tad high but if you like ramen then no big deal. The Hot Chicken Bun is awesome...very tasty! I ordered the Assari Shoyu with Soy Egg. It had a lot of flavor. I liked it a lot.

I've been twice and it's been great both times. Portions are perfect size. And it's not hella crowded when I go. I hope this place stays forever. It's great to have this place as an option for dinner.
Amazing ramen. I travel all over and search out great ramen wherever the road takes me and this is top 3 in the country.  Eat it! Thank me later!
0IcEAPMDFDVJ8d2UvYOkEw:
Great gyros. Meat is nicely seasoned, portions are very generous and price can't be beat. 
The wife likes the gyro salad, with meat on salad (you tell them what you want on the salad), and I like the gyro platter. 
The saffron rice is moist and delicious. 
This is our favorite gyro place.

This place has great gyros, great combo plates, great Philly cheesesteaks, and burgers. All the food here is good stuff. You can eat in a manner that is good for your body or not...it's all good. The ambiance is lame but the food is great and the place is clean.

To simply put it the food was plentiful and tasty. The employees that worked there were courteous and welcoming. I had their chicken plate with a side of salad and rice. It tasted fresh and clean which is a must when visiting any establishment. I plan on making more visits in the future!
zEx7aJ4BFOGizSv39DgOaA:
My group had a wide variety of sushi, it was all good, fresh and delicious. The food came quick, service was good but not annoying.

The decor was more of a chinese buffet tacky... hey, just my opinion!

The food was excellent, just not big on atmosphere. I will go back here over and over and over though!

Ooooooh yeah! Been a while since I had been here,  hell I could've been married or whatever at the time but to say that this place has gone down in quality sushi would be an understatement! May I suggest their "specialty" rolls, outta this world! Accompanied by a nice bottle of Nigori Saki and POW! Outta this world delights to the senses!

I've been going to Fujiyama for 6 months now and have always received excellent food and service. I've worked for 20 years in the restaurant business and this place impresses me. Typically we start with the lower priced rolls (Alaska, Boston, Spicy Tuna) and the move to the more expensive kick'n rolls (Manhattan, Dynamite, Rainbow). Last night a friend ordered the Beef Teriyaki and I was blown away by the presentation, it looked like it came off Food Network! Always a great choice, I love this place...
0Ib_MPoFNwXnfKtNOjOx9A:
This is a great spot to sit in if you are stranded at the airport or have a layover.  There is live music to get you in the Nashville mindset, even if you are not able to venture into the town.  

They have a full bar, good specials and pretty good quick food options.  This place is a little bit less busy than Tootsies, but then again, I think it is around C-8 gate, so it is off the beaten path.

Service is pretty good and if you say you are in a hurry, they move quick for you!

Adorned with Gibson guitars and memorabilia, a good quick lunch in the airport. Beer is cold and the food is what you expect.  My turkey burger was cooked perfectly. Served with fresh avocado, lettuce, tom, onion, pickles and your choice of side. I got fries. Pair that with a nice tall Shocktop and you'll be all ready for your flight!

If you are flying Southwest and boarding in the higher gates, pass by O'Charley's, the food court, and all the other places along the way.  This place is always welcoming by the bar staff.  The food is good and you can always enjoy a liquid refreshment while you wait.  They also have flight monitors on the wall to track you flight and manage your time.
DV2kXpcVdGYWlFfzz9APjg: Pupusería Metapaneca is a recently opened Salvadoran restaurant at Agora Plaza. I loved their loroco pupusas. Very clean space. Staff only speak Spanish.

Whenever you are the only "gringo" in a Latin restaurant, you need to take note. I enjoyed my visit . This is an El Salvadorean restaurant.

I got two pupusas, chicaronne and queso. They make each dish to order. I loved them. 

The menu said "pork chop", but properly listed it should say, "Pork, chopped." They were delicious. 

They also have a home made El Salvadorean slaw and it was great.

Go there

Super tasty!! Food was really good, ambiance is very simple, just what you'd expect from a small place in a strip mall. The horchata was not good, so that was a bit disappointing, but the pupusas and carne asada were fabulous. We got the pupusa mixta with chicharron beans and cheese, and one with loroco and cheese. I'll be back for sure. Entrees were $10-$15, pupusas $2.50 each, portion sizes were big but not crazy.
b7h2tE1o_SasFrHSJg4VsA: Great experience overall. Had a 20 min wait but hostess let us grab a cup of coffee while we waited. Once seated, our waitress was knowledgeable about the menu and had great recommendations. Several of our party ordered the Hot Chick Benedict and was not disappointed! I would definitely recommend this hidden gem of a brunch spot.
I love this place. Great people, delicious menu, and there are new cocktails coming! Can't wait to see what the future holds for this place.
31oc95cX9zQPHVvRMvSFCQ: Customer service was excellent. An item was left out of my order and they immediately made the correction when I came in the next day.

For this genre of restaurant- a fast food type of Asian takeout- this place rocks! Yeah, it's not authentic but it IS pretty good quality old fashioned Americanized Chinese food. Service is great every time and they will happily replace any meat item with tofu. Prices are cheap and you get a lot of food!! I'm talking 2-3 days if you eat normal sized portions every day.

Their lo mein (soft noodles) are so good. And if you get the larger size it is seriously a shit ton of noodles. Blows Panda out of the water.

I know it looks a little sketch on the inside but give it a try!!
Excellant for a strip mall restaurant. They had no problem with our special orders and everything was delicious.
IlbSutHdQ00EJsa5Zxdw8Q: This is a small truck but the food is delicious! We got the Colorado burrito and al pastor and carne asada tacos--so flavorful! Nice owners as well.
My boyfriend + I crave the chili relleno burrito on the regular! This is one of our favorite food trucks in the neighborhood.
Tasty burritos made in the typical style of the Mexican state of Chihuahua. The lomo de puerco burrito with red sauce is amazing!
pokS0UOGVKuILWtS1HNLBA: People always bash Applebee's, but it's really not that bad. There are lots of tvs to watch the game, the waitstaff is always friendly and accommodating to me (esp this location- great service!) and they have a diverse menu with cheap prices for a fair amount of food. I'm always a fan of their appetizers (esp during happy hour) and their fries. We ordered ribs from them last time I was there, and they were cooked well with plenty of sauce. Not fine dining, but a great place to hang out and catch the game.

I've given this a 5 as purely on attitude of the waiting staff which ensured we had a great time. Our waitress was a fairly young girl who got our drinks order got the grandkids children's cups chatted to me and my wife and the kids all with a really happy demeanor. Food was all great as was the service. There was also an older man who bought us our deserts and teased the kids about not saving him any ice cream. Attitudes go a long way and made a good experience a great experience.

This was exactly what I expected from an Applebee's, service and food wise. No wait sitting down, and only a slight wait for our food. 

Quality was above average, and my lime fiesta chicken was particularly good. Definitely going back.
e7NPtfoNcTbL-ceQBsO0PQ: Charming, lovely coffee shop. The delightful baristas are talented. The casual atmosphere is comfortable for studying or conversation. After 15 years in Nashville, this is my favorite cup of coffee.
The cold brew was good, not super strong. Pretty limited food menu, but the breakfast sandwiches are yummy. The place has a very distinct vibe

I want to preface this review with the fact that I don't know much about coffee. That being said, the coffee drink that I did get was delicious (iced vanilla latte), my sister's cold brew was very good (she said so and she knows a lot more about coffee than me.) This place is new and up and coming, perfect for this part of town. My wife works down the street and I feel like we will be back here more often than not. Plus...the cinnamon rolls look awesome!! (Refer to my picture)
_owOZDOPwZbBSCxcYOAJTw:
Got in at 1130 for lunch.Young lady took our order with a smile.Ordered the Tenders,came out fresh.
Lady came out to check on us.Then the Manager,walked the dining room  area ,checking on you.Excellent customer service,excellent food.

God yes. A Popeyes. Nearby. And a good one too. I work in Metrocenter and the options are pretty limited when it comes to lunch. But. Venture just a little further down RP Blvd and there you go. Hot, juicy, yummy fried chicken. That's not KFC. Waiting just for you.  Line moved pretty quick during the lunch rush hour and the chicken was fresh from the fryer.  Perfection.

Why aren't there more Popeyes in this region? KFC isn't pulling its weight, Mrs. Winner's never has anyone flowing through the drive thru, Bojangle's isn't worth the wrapper it's sold in, so it's a mystery to me.

As a native to New Orleans, I can tell you that Popeye's is the closest I get to cajun food in Nashville. And you know what? It's not that sad of a story. 

Every Tuesday there's always some deal where the chicken is 25¢ a thigh or 3 wings for 99¢. The sides are just as addictive. There are people who solely go to Popeye's for the red beans and rice.

The neighborhood that the store resides in borders the inner city at the Northern end of the Farmer's Market. The area has gotten gone through a few attempts at urban renewal in the past 5 years. So don't worry about where you are, worry about how long it will take you to eat delicious Popeye's.
AFfCl78WtJcFpH-UEFG6fw:
Love the sushi here! I definitely prefer some rolls over others, but all are good size - plenty of filler and topping (not mostly rice like some places). My favorites: 101st roll, and love the mango roll! Rolls with raw - new orleans, Phoenix, sexy lady, spicy girl, mardi gras.  

This place is worth a try.  You won't have a wait, and service is always friendly.

It was amazing.  The choices were staggering, one of the best sushi restaurants in town.  I was a Peter's and Sushime fan, and this tops both.  Service was great, the decor so elegant.  I will be back many times

This is a solid place for sushi and other dishes. What's really cool is that they will customize any sushi roll for you. Literally, you can make one up and they'll do it for you. A friend of mine likes his whole sushi roll deep fried (don't ask me why!) and they'll do it, no questions asked. Not sure if this happens at any other places but this is the only place I've ever seen them do something like that. I enjoy Kohana but my only complaint is that the atmosphere is very quiet and stuffy.
r4grxU_cFpq_QE3OCXGAVQ: This is the cleanest & most friendliest McDonald's I've ever been in. Everyone seems genuinely happy to be working here. I don't think one person didn't eagerly welcome me without a smile & really mean it. I don't think I want to leave here. & the food was hot and fresh. *** Free pie with any large value meal!!!!    *Free*

This is a newer McDonalds in an up an coming little area of Hwy 100...I have stopped in here several times for a quick bite or something sweet while in the area and the service has always been quick and friendly, food is freshly cooked, and the place is clean.  I must say this store stands out above several of the others in the local area not just because it is new (several of the others are too) but the staff!

The MCDonald's on Hwy 70 is closer but I will continue to drive to this location!  Fresh...Hot...Friendly!!!  Bonus...at night everything is cook to order!  Some ppl may not like to wait but I love it and am happy to wait a few minutes for fresh hot food!  Bah-dah-bop-bop-bah I'm Lovin' It!!!
7ADfnOJnK7ysbAo-kBEmuQ:
Awesome service and very fast! Went to a Krystal close to my place and they refuse to honor my coupon. Their excuse was "oh we were told not to honor any coupons yet." Funny thing was I was there yesterday and they honored my coupons. Bunch of lazy workers. 

Not this place though, I pull up my coupons and they honored right away. Service was so fast too! Before I can text my wife I am bringing home Krystal, they were already done. Wish more places was like this!

We got 4 Sliders, 4 Corn Dogs, 4 Chicken Sliders, 2 drinks, 2 orders of Tater Tots and some kind of donut for under $30.

Lots of fast food options in this area, but this is the only locally owned fast food establishment I've seen - so happy to be able to get huge meals, quickly, affordable and supporting local.

I am a native of Donelson and Nashville, but have lived on the East coast for many years now.  My wife and I were in town for a quick visit and wanted to get some Krystals in the middle of the day since we do not have them where we live.  We were very fortunate that the staff on site were quick, courteous and efficient.  Our food was fresh and we were in and out of the drive thru in under 5 minutes.  This particular location was a staple of my entire existence growing up.  This store is probably at least 45 years old and has not changed very much.  The problems most people complain about probably pertain to the same problems in other fast food venues.  There are not enough quality people out there willing to work hard for basic wages.  This store used to be open 24/7, but has since decided to close later in the evening.  I can say I even worked at this location when I was 21 for about 3 months before the low pay and long hours scared me off!  I believe the long waits and the inconsistent service all boil down to paying a livable wage and having caring, supportive management at the helm.  This just was not the case when I worked for them and I think it still might be an issue.  However, if you go during normal business hours you are still likely to get fast service and fresh food here, as well as other Krystals.  The later it gets into the evening, the greater the likelihood you will experience subpar service.  Pay people more, encourage them with great management practices and you will create an atmosphere that produces quality all the way around for its customers.
y6ctGreF-mxRmNm9K0XpCA: Guatemalan food is finally getting some representation here in Nashville and I couldn't be more excited. I've been to this locations several times with a party of 10+ and the service has always been great. The food does take a while to come out but that's never been a huge issue. On Sundays they have marimba players so that is always a plus!

The steak was delicious.  I ordered the churrasco plate.  My friend ordered a burrito suprema, but only because the server told us that the chimichanga is not fried.  I don't know if she knew the menu that well.  When we came in there were not many people.  She told us to sit anywhere we wanted, then she started sweeping.  It took a moment before she addressed us.  I could tell that she did not wash her hands.  She was also running back and forth in and out of the restaurant.  She was very sweet, but could use a little but of server training.  Also, the menu could use a spell-check, and a bit of sprucing up.  They called the salsa "tomato sauce".  I was also not a huge fan of the mayonnaise served as a side item.  Those things are the only reason that it did not get 5 stars.
Great place. Awesome tasting authentic food!  Love the fish tacos.  Just flew in looking for something different and I am pleasantly surprised.
zIq0Rec9-EYoKznTYswZuw:
Dropped by a couple of nights ago for some after-dinner drinks.  The atmosphere is superb.  Sexy, sultry, slick.  the bar was buzzing with mature professionals.  Long narrow patio outside.  Told the bartender to make me a good martini - it was a little pulpy but served its purpose.

I'll be back for dinner....

Micro District is a pricy and hip Italian inspired restaurant.  The décor here is really nice, modern and funky at the same time.  It is a multi level restaurant (I prefer to dine near the bar).  They have a good menu filled with tasty and unique items inspired by Tennessee and Italy.  Some of which are a nice departure from traditional Italian restaurants, like the corn risotto.

The night I went, I had the saffron orzo with tomato.  The orzo was so smooth and perfectly cooked, not too garlicky.  We started with a really cool appetizer that was called the Moroccan flatbread pizza.  It had hummus, lamb, and cucumber!  Weird, but good!
 
The service was phenomenal, truly a well run restaurant.  A bit pricey, with most entrees priced around twenty something dollars.  If you are ready to splurge, this is the place!

My buddy bear and I had a lovely after work time here yesterday.  A couple of appetizers filled us up good and we called it dinner, including a generous meat and cheese plate that we didn't even finish.  At $14, the meat and cheese plate was a lot more than what you get for the same price at other fancy places... probably 4 or 5 cheeses, plenty of salami and procuitto, apples, walnuts, some other stuff.  mmm....

The baked formaggio, as described by Beth W., was indeed scrumptious and memorable.  Here's a great example of a great dish that really is mostly about the chef finding awesome ingredients.  The tomatoes were so flavorful and dense, these puppies reminded me of my Italian grandmother's massive garden that she's kept for 50 years.

The people watching was more than adequate.  Atmosphere was cool, great lay out up front in the big round room, the big wooden table in the middle surrounded by little dining tables.  Also, a guy from the kitchen wandered around at the beginning of the night with a pan of lemons roasting in garlic and olive oil and herbs to make the place smell delicious.  Smart!

I also have to give it up for the location; miro is in the bottom of Nashville's most luxurious condo building, and their are great views of the bustling midtown streets through the big windows.

Our waiter was pretty clueless but trying his best.  He had no idea about the wine, but was happy to send over the sommolier or a sample.  Even after consulting with the sommolier he tried and sounded like a fool, but he was cute.

Its a little pricey, of course, but not obscenely so.  I wish they had a happy hour special on wine or apps, because this place would be a great semi-regular place to unwind afterwork.
5cS3PUWheu6cjEgO2mtKNg: This pizza is the truth, way better than any of the chains near by. And the menu selection is crazy. Try the thin crust

They are open til 5 and have pretty rad pizza. My favorite part of this place is that they are open until 5, which is great if you want pizza late. Also, the pizza they have is pretty solid, especially for a place that is open til 5

If you want delivery call them, tried to order on here and it didn't go through.

Anyway, ordered the Philly sandwich and the sampler app. Sandwich was a good normal philly, bread was awesome and had a good amount of filling. The app was a little disappointing at first until I actually ate some. The chicken was excellent, onion rings and cheese sticks were good too. Came with warm honey mustard and marinara. Very friendly on the phone, even told me to call grub hub to make sure they weren't sitting on my order. Only reason for the 4 stars and not 5 was the price seemed a little high for the sampler. Glad I gave them a shot, will eat here again.
zLD-CFRdLV9Jr-GWL7gwPw: Great value place in East Nashville.  The menu is sparse, but it is full of all-stars.  The med platter is full of real deal Mediterranean classics.  The beetteki and specials are uniformly of high quality and the prices are spot on.  It is a little bit of a hidden gem, but give it a shot and I doubt you will be disappointed.

My husband and I just returned from eating at Zavos, a Greek restaurant right around the corner from our home. It doesn't have a sign outside, so if you're lost it is directly across from the Family Wash at the 4-way stop. Bottom line, it is incredible. My husband had the beefteaki and I got the special of the day (Thursday) which was a noodle dish covered in vegetables and sprinkled with Parmesan cheese. They were both incredible (being pregnant, I ate all of mine and started in on his, so I can attest to both). We also had the Mediterranean plate as an appetizer. So yummy.  The service was warm and friendly and the atmosphere of the restaurant was very relaxed but chic. The prices were exceptionally good. All in all, I hope that whomever is reading this will give this restaurant a try because you will love it.

This place is a great little diamond in the rough in east nasvhille. It is right across the street from the family wash and they dont even have a sign up. But they are open most nights until at least 10 except for sunday i think. So basically the food is just incredible. The menu is small, but their regular items are simple and delicious. It is mostly traditional greek food, but it is not your normal gyro place. They have some small sandwiches, but their best dishes are the daily specials. I recently had this beef rice mixture baked in cabbage leaves with a sauce and feta cheese on the side, it was incredible. They also have moussaka some nights and even a lasagna. The portions are not huge, but you easily get what you pay for. The ingredients are fresh and their baba ghanoush and hummus are great. A great establishment to eat for cheap that is also not lacking on flavor or freshness.
gylN_Qc6zXpgAwDQJXphIw: I visit here at least 3-4 times a month, their food is so yummy and fresh, the meat is tender and juicy, and everything is at such a great price that there's little room for complaints here.

Pupusas are fantastic and generously portioned. The tamarind drink (32 oz glass), same. The woman (owner, I'm thinking) helping me? Give her 6 stars. Prices are excellent, place was spotless inside. How is it that you can have a basically perfect dining experience at a small, out of the way restaurant like this, and the exact opposite at a much more publicized, line-out-of-the-door place like Martin's or Pharmacy? No need to answer. Just saying.

Siiiiiii siempre voy a este lugar con mis amigos para darles una buena experiencia!  This place has wonderful baleadas (a typical food from Honduras where my mom is from) and they also have good pupusas and horchata!  The staff treat us great every time. Each time I come they know how to make me happy and are always doing little things to add to the experience--whether it's for me or the guests I bring. I saw they will be selling paletas there soon too.
KCVLunGXXLTVnP2sP2bhuQ: Our first visit and we were skeptical upon entering since we were the only people on a Saturday night.  Our fears were quickly dispelled upon tasting the fresh salsa and chips.  I had papusas and my husband had shrimp Diablo which had a spicy chipotle type sauce on it.  Both dishes were very tasty.  Also tried the fried plantains which added a sweet touch to the meal.  A very casual place with surprising tasty authentic fare.
Excellent authentic Mexican. Not much on decor but the food is great and the price is right. I'm gluten free and they made me chimichanga with corn. Amazing.
Simply amazing food. Great service. Probably the most authentic South American food I've had in Nashville
mOs27lvpjjBZV1H3VBMMZA: Very pleasantly surprised, from the yeast rolls with rosemary butter to the fresh grouper with salsa and hush puppies, just amazing food and service. Thank you Micah for your recommendations! My husband had the chicken piccata very good

Was very nice place to grab a bit. Somewhat pricey but to not have to leave the hotel was a plus. We sat at the bar and our bartender Barbara was a pleasure to talk with. We will eat here again before we leave.
Great late night dining! Very convenient if you're staying at the hotel! I recommend the pizza and club sandwich! Delicious
KclsUl42Ktpdm749Uo1BMA:
We've visited Nonna's Pizzeria three times, usually for early weeknight Date Nights and once for a late glass of wine & dessert.
The first two times were close to perfect - no wait, friendly & knowledgable service, and delicious, fresh food. The third time was less charmed, with an overcharge on our drinks and yellowing mixed greens, hence the four stars here. 
For appetizers, try the spiced nuts, which have an exotic robust flavor that will have you reaching for more. Also, the olive tapenade is a highlight, a generous portion of served with Cosi-like flat bread and carrot sticks, like all of the dips. Skip the dipping oil, a rather bland oil which deserves a bread with more crumb. The star of the show here of course is the pizza - a crispy thin crust and thoughtfully arranged, generous toppings. Yum!
Try a piadini  - an Italian take on the quesadilla with flatbread - my grilled zucchini was hearty and filling.

Love the atmosphere. It's small but refined. 

Pizza is very good. A bit pricey but the quality an taste is well worth it. 

A must go for people that want to support local good business.

This restaurant is a hidden gem in Nashville! It seriously couldn't be more delicious. I absolutely love it here and think that the pizza is some of the most delicious pizza I've ever had! I highly recommend this place to anyone. It's a great hidden spot for locals that I don't think a lot of people know about. 

The staff, food and drinks are amazing. Once you go here, you'll be itching to go back for more!!!
_t01hjhgbCX6i04x9G6J5Q: I love blue coast! The staff is always friendly and food is great! I love the fish tacos! The Nashville west location is the best!

I am never disappointed when I visit the Church Street Location.  I work downtown and it is convenient and worth the price.  My favorite server is Fatima.  She is always friendly and she recommends different products.

Found this place on a short lunch break downtown.

The reviews do not do it justice.

I enjoyed Blue Coast Burrito quite a bit. They had plenty of options for how I wanted to build my burrito. The line was out the door when we arrived (5 of us) and we got through in a very timely manner.
A9R0ztoNK04vgLvDfZ_fZg: This place just recently changed ownership and is now dynasty buffet, so I gave it another try.  I love it! It is now my favorite buffet. All the food was good, my absolute favorites being the creamy macaroni salad, the super tasty beef empanadas, the honey chicken, the crab rangoon which had a wonderful sweet cream cheese flavor, the tiny bite sized circular dessert thingies, and the cheesecake look alike cake (didnt have a label) that i topped with a bit of chocolate pudding. All this made for a delightfully sinful eat fest that I cant do often! If only I could double my metabolism! Lol. My least favorite items were the wonton soup and sushi, but they were still ok. The food and service here was much better than my last buffet disaster down the street.  I definitely recommend and dont bring the calorie counter:)
Based on the price, they offer the most to me! The quality is really good, service is very good too! :)

I loved this place, and today was my 2nd visit. They have one of the largest Buffet's I have ever seen which made the choices unlimited.

 I also love knowing this is a Small Family Owned Business. The people that work there are Friendly and the restaurant was exceptionally clean. 

NOW, let me tell you about the Won Ton Soup. It is the best I have ever had. I just want the soup part - not the Won Ton, and this broth is not the usual watered down chicken broth that you get at most Chinese places.

 I look forward to going back and trying all their other soups as well as some more of the food choices. I think it will take 20+ visits to try everything and I'm up to the task.
OtLo6a-20k01NDubRme17w:
I had gotten a groupon to come here and I hadn't been in years.  I had come here when I first moved here with my family, and as that was a while ago, my memory was not so good.  So this groupon gave me an excuse to go again.  And I will be going back.

First of all, sitting in this restaurant is like sitting in a crazy relative's house.  There's strange, kooky little things everywhere.  There was an X-ray on a lamp near my table.  This might weird some people out.  I rather enjoyed it.  You could almost make a game of trying to identify the things scattered about.  As its an old house, its a very nice intimate little space too.  

Our service was great, with a very fun waiter who had great recommendations for the menu.  We decided, in order to get the best view of hte place, to try a bunch of their small plates and split them.  This was a very good call.  Their hummus trio is spectacular.  I loved the calamari too, as its covered in cornmeal before frying, which makes it lighter and less greasy in my opinion.  The cured salmon pizza was great too, a lot of stuff on it that blended together well.  The shrimp dumplings were my favorite, very flavorful with a little spice to them.  Also, being completely happy in our gluttony, we saw a white cheddar andouille mac and cheese as a side on one of the entrees, and asked if we could have a portion of that to try.  It was, in a word, decadent.  Like having dessert only savory.  I could have eaten a bowl of that happily.

I will be going back again, to try more of hte food on the menu.  Its delicious and reasonably priced, and even the goofy surroundings add to the dining experience.  They have a great patio too, for when its warm out, so keep that in mind.

Mambu is set off of West End Avenue in a small house that can be spotted easily because of the valet stand out front and huge front patio section. As we walked up the ramp into the restaurant, we were both pleasantly surprised at how quaint and quirky it was decorated and our excitement for this new experience began to grow. The décor is one of my favorites in Nashville, right up their with Firefly in Green Hills, and definitely sets the tone for the meal to come.

The Happy Hour specials change but our waiter told us it was $6 Wines and $6 well drinks. I have also seen them run $5 frink specials for Mojitos, Margaritas, and more so maybe call ahead to find out what they are offering that night. I sat this one out on the alcohol front and Cole had a glass of the house Cabernet. Our waiter had our mouths watering while thoroughly explaining the many featured specials and the menu was full of appetizing choices. The menu also features small plates which invokes a Tapas style feel and is perfect for sharing and trying more than one option.

 We started off with the Corn Fritters ($7) that were the perfect combination of spicy and sweet, with an unexpected surprise of black beans and green chiles inside. The fritters came on top of a sweet chili sauce that was great for dipping but not enough if you are sharing and both love the sauce as much as we did. 

 We were in the mood for a steak but with our $50 budget in mind, opted to share the Flat Iron Steak ($21) in a red wine demi glace with sautéed Zucchini on top of hickory-bacon Gruyere macaroni and cheese. The waiter kindly separated the steak into two meals without us having to ask and even with splitting, I wasn't able to eat all of it. The steak was cooked to perfection and the zucchini was grilled with just the right texture to compliment the steak. The star of the show however was no doubt the bacon macaroni and cheese The macaroni and cheese is as good as any I've had but the bacon hints, while not overpowering, are definitely present and catapult this dish to legendary status. It is worth a trip to Mambu just to try a bowl of this heavenly sent concoction.

Read More - http://uncharterednashville.blogspot.com/

Took out of town guest and were delighted with the unique selection of both the food and beverage--the pear ginger martinis were a hit, we all four ordered a different main course so we could share...outstanding!  Decor generates a laugh including the mens room!  The only improvement in our evening would have been to dined inside...outside the fans were overbearing plus the tables/chairs could use some comfort and visual improvemnts. We will be back and plan better so we can dine inside!
X0Y8zp2UlvCiFAVZBn0a7A: A simple concept that they over complicate.  Really.  My friend wanted a gyro ans the employee working there said she needed to put a "wrap" sticker on her bag.  Um, why?  Isn't a gyro already rolled up and wrapped in a pita?  Strange.... Anyway, this place is on par with all the other sandwich places...the concept is more novel because you write your order on a bag and drop it off as your order.  They call your name and poof, you get your grub and griz down.  Open on Sunday.

Dangit, I really don't want to give Which Wich this high of a rating because it is a chain and there are so many good locally owned sandwich shops in the downtown area.  I have to give it to them however, they make a mean sandwich.  

Which Wich's whole deal is that you can basically customize your sandwich from a huge selection of choices.  WW stands out with the additions: Peston, olive tapenade, hummus, red peppers, etc. etc.  It is very easy to take a pretty basic sandwich and make it something pretty cool if you play your cards right.  They also have a pretty good selection of veggie options so you veggie friends can join you without any difficulty.  

One thing I can't recommend is the salads.  They basically use the same shredded lettuce from the sandwiches and pile sandwich toppings on.  Not something that will make you happy you ordered a salad.  

Overall WW is a pretty solid place to pick up a sandwich.  It is a solid step about most other chains although it is also a solid step below some of the quality local joints around.  Good for a quick hit lunch, but nothing that will change your life.

This is quickly becoming my go-to lunch spot downtown. 
It's easy to order, just pick a bag and mark what you want on it, and the staff is exceptional in efficiency and attitude. 
The buffalo chicken is one of my favorite "Wich's" and a close second is their Rueben. 
The price is fair, too. I use my State ID and get 10% off my order. 
Next thing I want to try is the milkshake. I have yet to have one but I keep hearing they're awesome.
mJk551wWLMMUx0CYoZ7uyQ: Love their food and cheaper / more portion size than fast food. The wait is longer but worth it. I like the they have variety and huge portions of fries, not that I need them lol.

If you are looking for a GREAT burger, Fat Mo's is your place.  I don't know if it is the little bit of extra pepper in the meat or some secret spices they use but regardless in town which one I visit I get a great burger.  They also have a good gyros and fried mushrooms.  Get the fat mo combo which consists of a cheeseburger, fries(regular or spicy) and a drink.  While all the other joints burgers are getting smaller, you can still get a mansized burger at Fat Mo's.
I love the ultimate moe!!! Fries are always fresh, burgers are always fresh cooked. I highly recommend this place.
dP4KBHqspn2jFhOq_KbtKA:
May be it is the sentimental value of when I lived in Bellevue at the old Ridgelake apartments in the nineties and ate at US Border Cantina at least weekly that makes the food taste a little better or maybe it is better than average. In any event, I rate most Mexican places in middle TN on whether I think they are using the "silver label" or the "gold label" Sysco/PFG/US Foods Mexican menu. I think USB probably uses the gold label.

The menu isn't inventive; it's probably 90% the same as every other localish Mexican place within 50 miles.

The carpet is dirty but the lights are dim so you won't notice too much.

But my favorite chimichanga in Nashville is still US Border Cantina. It's crunchy, cheesy, and meaty. The rice is decent, as are the refried beans which seem to have actual lard in them (yummo). 

The salsa above average, and the chips are served warm.

Our service was fast, which was good because we were in a hurry.

When compared to other local Mexi places like Las Palmas and Nachos, it deserves 4 stars. Probably 3 when compared to Sopapilla's and the other "stepped up" places.

Like stated in other reviews this is the place to go for veggie tamales. Husband hates mushrooms so he orders the veggie enchilada's. I eat them anyway because I love tamales more than I dislike mushrooms.  Lots of veggie options and pretty good salsa.
This place is decent. A typical small Mexican restaurant. I usually huevos rancheros or fajitas.
8mEueCqI1LMmuxfGVTsJXQ: We have followed these guys around the city from farmer's markets to their store front to events. They are really great people with fantastic business ideas. Their new store is no exception and fits perfectly into East Nashville. Freshly prepared (And usually locally sourced) food that can be heated/cooked with simple instruction saves so much time, tastes so much better, and is so much healthier than drive thrus or frozen meals. You can actually do most of your standard grocery shopping right there- lots of meats and produce as well as a good supply of health food. The prices are VERY reasonable- especially when compared to stores with similar products, such as Whole Foods and other health stores in the neighborhood. You can even do some home decorating while you're there as they have a fun supply of artwork from local artists. Go check it out.

My favorite place to get a sandwich or salad for lunch. Also 95 craft beers to choose from (soon to be 200 I'm told). For those of you looking for another turnip truck, this isn't it. If you're looking for an amazing steak or a great pick 6, this is it. For vegetarians, they will gladly make you a veggie sandwich (the bread is amazing) if you just ask for one. I love Boone and Sons.

They have lobster rolls. Proper New England style, eggy yeasty split top bun and nothing getting in the way of the heaps of lobster. Work the visit. The meat counter cannot be beat price wise in East Nashville plus tons of hard to find beer and soda. The layout is weird. Having a rack of vintage clothing being the first thing you see is weird. Some empty space in the fridges but they sell local hamburger you usually can only get at the farmers market. Their staff seem super nice, guy was filling in the day we went and was nice and cool. 
Look for their specialty salts and seasonings and enjoy.
And get a lobster roll.
tV10M4tasdLyNAOOxnoNZw: This is an excellent, inexpensive taco place. The smoked chicken is my favorite. They smoke it on a huge smoker at their storefront and it's so flavorful! The half or whole chicken is a great deal. We eat here regularly and it's always excellent!

Comida muy sabrosa! The asada tacos are super yummy! As well as there smoked chicken it has a lot of flavor! There service is always nice too. The waitress is always checking up but not in an annoying way. Muy amable Toda la gente. My boyfriend and I love to come in here for the delicious food!

I'm from Memphis and I have been coming to this place for 10 years.. it's a must stop if you enjoy burritos. Always fresh and meat is GRILLED to perfection.  Very small inside so best thing is to pull up and someone will come out to take your order
choA-QS1y1AvU2BtG035Bg: Simply wonderful. I can choose anywhere in town, and continually choose y'all. Blessed be your days, Domino's of Donelson Pike.

There was a minor mix-up with my order but the store manager did more than make up for it. While I didn't escalate this issue, I am sure the look on my face combined with the fact I am recovering from a sinus infection showed my disapproval. Not only did he cook my pizza when I arrived, he had it delivered free of charge and threw in some cinnamon sticks just so I would leave happy. My location was even off the map in where they deliver. That is going the extra mile. Mistakes happen but more places should take note of this. I give the customer service 5 stars! I will be returning to this location instead of ordering from anywhere else.

Not lying, this was the best Domino's I've ever had! They want more typing, so they're hijacking my review until there's more. Really, I ate the pizza for lunch and had a crappy steak for dinner. I wish I had more pizza.
ZIMdEfiP954EaLOX0S84pw: This place is great! We drove by it hundreds of times and finally gave it a try. The food is wingz are delicious (we tried buffalo and teriyaki - a bit salty but good) and they actually do a nice fresh green salad. The staff is so friendly and cheerful. I'm kicking myself for not going sooner. If you're a girl maybe don't go by yourself after dark because the area can be a little iffy. We're just so happy we finally found a place to get honest good wings in East Nasty.

Food- 5 stars

Location- 2 stars

Ambiance- 2 stars

Staff-5 stars 


I have to admit, our group was questioning the place when we got there, but, had to try it as this place has over 4 star rating on Tripadvisor after 200 some reviews.  The wings and tenders are awesome.  I tried the spicy garlic and teryaki which were very good, but, our favorite were the cajun dry rub.  Probably the best dry rubbed wings I've ever had.  

The staff and especially manager were very appreciative of us "out of towners" showing up to their establishment.  Also, we called ahead and she told us that we could bring our own liquor into the establishment, which was very nice and saved us some money.  Worth a try if you care about the food more than the setting.

Seriously, so delicious AND well-priced! I've now tried the Gold and Hot, Chipotle, Buffalo, Honey Bourbon, and Hot Garlic Parmesan and they are ALL yummy! Honey Bourbon was a bit too sweet and salty for me, but I tend not to like sweet sauces anyway. None of these are super hot if you're a real hot food person, but they still have a kick (they have a sauce called Extreme Heat that I haven't tried, but if I'm really craving hot, I go to a "hot chicken" place) and are just plain tasty. Weds.=50 cent wings, Thurs.=79 cent tenders, and they also have "tender bitez" which are like little boneless wings 10 for $3.99 regular price. Call ahead and they'll have your order ready for takeout!
VHBuV0yVmZ9NK2fU_6Pogg: Whiting fish, macaroni and cheese, & cabbage is what soul food is all about and it was delicious. They had a steady flow of patrons during lunchtime but the line moved quickly. They dropped the fish when I place my order so my food was hot and fresh. The dining area has approximately 10-15 tables but I took my order to go. Plentiful portions that included one meat and two veggies with hot water corn bread for $8.00 (tax, tip, and beverage not included).

Sands was a good meat and three/greasy spoon. Ample seating inside, good location close to downtown and really friendly service. I had the turkey, stuffing and gravy, and my friend had the fried pork chops - both were great.

Will definitely be back to try the fried chicken!!

In Nashville for the recent eclipse, relaxing before we head to our flight. 

Get whatever you want. The three of us shared our plates and everything tasted fantastic. 

Great music and classic decor. Wonderful local spot.
PsDw8g3zKT3LbcH4OEuoyg: Great food tons of fun ... they dance which was cool ..... sorta weird this johnny rockets looks different but its the same comfort food i know from home.

This place has great service. Gets really really busy but what else do you expect from a mall restaurant? Even with there being so many customers, waitress still got food out relatively quickly. I did have to wait on the milkshake, it takes them a second to blend them up.

Hello yelp! Community

Today I went to Johnny Rockets with a friend and was surprised to see there was no wait time. Normally when I'm shopping around Opry Mills I see this very long line at the door wrapping around the restaurant.

We were seated within moments after being greeted by a smiling waitress that was very nice. The staff here were very attentive and always made sure we were doing okay, a plus for me whenever I eat out.

After we ordered our food it took about ten minutes for it to get to our table, hot and fresh. The reason I gave this place four stars is because it was a little overpriced. A 20oz drink should not cost $2.29 and kids drinks (even iced tea) should be free, my friend's little girl got one and it was $1.30. Now, I'll just say that I do understand food cost and so on, but really I'm a big believer in kid's drinks being free and kid's meals being cheap. Now, the kid didn't get anything of course because Johnny Rocket's have good-sized fry portions for at least two people to share, let alone a mom and her baby.

Overall, it was a great visit and my only qualm was the price of everything. It's typically a fun place to go and I see myself going again, but I think that I'll seek out a Steak n'Shake next time.

yelp! on, yelp!ers
fgAhcqxGZHcC7RtXxYwFeQ:
Picture this: A small restaurant called "Sub House" with a hastily prepared "BEST DONUTS" sign in the window that's next to a liquor store. If there's a more appropriate description of authenticity, I'd love to see it. 

What you get inside is exactly what the windows advertise. Well, the word "best" is a frightening one, but it's not necessarily wrong here. The donuts are great.

While most donut shops close after breakfast, this place has found a loophole in the system. Come lunch time, they start selling sandwiches (which, according to their large sign, is apparently their main focus). The sandwiches are a bit expensive for the size, but they're pretty good. 

Convenience is what really shines here, though. The donuts and sandwiches are good, but the location is even better. Located right by the highway, this is on the way to pretty much anywhere I'm going, which makes it an easy quick stop.

I want to jump on in and say that for the most part I hate it when a business self proclaims they are the best at anything. So usually when I see something as bragotious as a sign on a Sub Shop that reads "Best Donuts" I am the first person to run and try them just so I can tell everyone how wrong the sign is. The problem is every now and then you run in a place where the sign is actually true.


THIS PLACE, MY FRIENDS, CAN KEEP IT'S SIGN.


In fact I was thinking about making my own sign saying that this place has the best donuts in town with a picture of me smiling as I shove another one in my mouth! It's weird this place has donuts with the sugary taste of Krispy Kreame and the consistency of Shipley's. Bottom line these are the best Donuts in Nashville. You should have known that though. Heck, it said so right on the sign!

The sign speaks for itself, "BEST DONUTS" it says, and it doesn't lie. The folks that run it are friendly and fun and the food is great.  Best blueberry cake donuts I've ever had.  Just because it looks like a dive doesn't mean the food's not good!

Don't forget about the lunch sandwiches either, they are delicious!

In my opinion, the donuts are better than any other place in Nashville.
z4tr0BH74LYsuQ98SyLcpQ:
What a superb spot to enjoy lunch!
Not to knock brunch, happy hour, or dinner but I can only speak on my first and fantastic first hand experience with Germantown Cafe East that I had on Monday.

Having never stepped foot in the previous project (same owners) Allium, I was bright-eyed and bushy-tailed over the casually elegant dining room. There were just a few others seated during our noon nosh session which surprised me due to all of the popularity of the Germantown Cafe moniker and with this being recently opened? Hopefully others in the community will take notice of what a solid option this place is for a meal.

Let's dish about the tasty Black Bean-Quinoa Burger that made this vegan one happy camper... with corn-avocado relish on a wheat bun, and fries (we substituted for asparagus)  priced at $8.95, you can afford a healthy and delicious meal any day of the week! Couple of things that I must point out, the bread was fluffy and light and didn't bog down the meal at all. This is hard to achieve and much appreciated from me. Many who order "veggie" burgers know that consistency of the burger can sometimes be wonky or not the best but this patty was simply divine.

Our server was friendly and efficient and we thoroughly enjoyed the ambiance and views of downtown. Parking was super easy as there is free underground garage parking available. I hope that the Fifth and Main development can woo more retailers and commercial residents to the ground floor to help entice consumers to give this area a shot and to help support this delightful eatery. 

I look forward doing my part by coming back for dinner and drinks!
Great restaurant although I didn't like the pork belly tater tots.  Flavorless and greasy. French onion soup is divine.

Brunch is amazing right now because they offer some amazing croissant french toast.  buttered bread cooked in butter topped with maple syrup.  Too much of a good thing = greatness.  Getting a flask of mimosas is definitely the way to go.

Theres a bread pudding benedict that looked delicious, in fact I wanted to try at least 4-5 dishes, did I order two, maybe.  I love french food, but you can order american.  Grapefruit salad, super healthy.  

Oh yeah, TIP:  ask to add some cranberry juice to the mimosa.  It's just fun to drink pink champagne.  Oh wow, I sound like a lady.  Speaking of... 

a friend was wearing a Yellow dress.  A wandering wasp thought she was a huge dandelion and flew into her sunglasses.  There was a wasp sting, but I'd like to report the service was attentive and manager took time to say sorry for that experience, and that no wasps were harmed in the making of this review.
Qd4IwlrHSPCVLorGs5RERg: One of the best taco bells I have been to. I frequent here often because I live around the corner. The food is always quick and hot. But what makes this location is the employees. They are by far the best fast food restaurant staff I have seen. Where I went to college they had one of the largest gross making taco bells in the country, and for a good reason. A lot had to do with their staff. This staff is just as good if not better than that staff. I enjoy being greeted with a warm welcoming feeling when I am the consumer at any establishment. These employees at this location sure do have a bring future!

Got lunch here and they were extremely fast! I didn't wait in line long for anything! They guy took my order fast. Pulled up to the window and the woman handed me my food! I am very pleased! I highly recommend this Taco Bell!
This is down the road from where I live so if I get a craving for Taco Bell this is where I go.  The staff has always been friendly and prompt both at drive thru and inside.  Solid 4 stars for me!
VlU01R6Q9NvjM4DEMqys9Q:
Bistro 215 has an excellent atmosphere!  It can get really crowded on the weekends, so be prepared to wait.

The burgers are so good.  They are perfectly juicy, not that big, but cooked just right.  I really like the French fries too.  I also recommend the crab cake sandwich.  With tarter sauce, it can not be beaten.

I have never had bad service or a bad meal here.  The wait staff is excellent.      It is a good place to eat dinner and then go see a movie at Green Hills

I love Bistro for lunch. For starters their bread with dipping oil is the best I have found in Nashville. They seasoning they add to the oil really makes it. I really like their Chicken Club  (on a Croissant). Their burger is pretty good too as is the Spring Herb Pasta. I wasn't much a fan of the Spinach Pistou because I didn't like the combination of Pesto and Marinara. Also, remember that if you go for lunch on your birthday (and have your ID) your entree is free! I've used that for the last year or two!
Great place for brunch.  We frequent this restaurant for Saturday or Sunday brunches.  Great service!  Good food.
3WC1k7yP7WSBTl-HBP26zA: My first time at this location the service was good in the sandwich was good, I don't know what else to say about it other than it did lack some of the typical Jimmy John's energy.

I love this Jimmie Johns. I've ordered delivery from this place I know 4 times this month! The food is always great. One time it did take them about an hour to deliver (one sandwich) and one time the order was wrong (no hot cherry peppers on my Vito.. ), but I still keep coming back because the food is so damn good!! I
Nothing Special but not bad either. Turnaround time is slower than the west end location Not "freaky fast" but reasonable. Sandwich was good as always.
JJ1PdKT3BrLNmAJGAzLTWw:
Where do I start....Ed's is an experience, and product, that people of all races, gender, shapes and sizes who love soulfood and southern cuisine should try. I've had both the white fish sandwich and the catfish. Both are quite wonderful, but I prefer the lighter, cleaner flavor of the white fish. Pour on the hot sauce and enjoy one of Nashville's best kept secrets.

The service is great too, with an incredibly friendly and talkative staff. There's not a lot of seating inside, so you may have to take out. Also, be prepared to wait a few min if you go during lunch or dinner.

A North Nashville tradition since 1972, serving fresh hot fish sandwiches, catfish and whiting, fries, spaghetti and coleslaw. What makes something so simple worth having?  Two large pieces that hang of the bread so much, they might break when you pick it up,  dressed with hot sauce, onion, dill pickle and cheese or without. And pizza? Well, let's just say what's in a name. They haven't sold pizza for years but the name stuck. Anyone that does not know this is not from here! So first timers, don't embarrass yourselves by asking! 
     Be prepared, cash is the currency of the day but rest assured you won't be hungry an hour later.

I love this place. The fried fish is so good. I love going to Ed's me and my son will just love getting our fried fish sandwiches from here. They have the seasoning just right and they fry the fish to perfection. This is definitely my go to for Fried Fish. I love this place. The service is great.  I highly recommend.
9PYS91OpSMhUGWxOLSckHA:
A lot better today a lot of staff. Was greeted at the door
Happy Hour open to close 2.25 to 5.00 ..great specials as well. Owner stopped by and chatted with all the customers todays.overall a great eating out experience.
The owner is so friendly! He comes out talks with you and just makes you feel like your family! Food is yummy! Ribs fall off the bone! Steaks are tender and juicy! Chicken has tons of flavor!

This restaurant recently opened up near my office and I had a wonderful lunch there this afternoon. The servers were extremely attentive and the food was excellent. I had the chicken flat bread and my friend had the club sandwich. Both of them were very big portions and tasted wonderful. 

The owner stopped by to welcome us and chat about the area for a few minutes. I will definitely be back there often as there are many other dishes on the menu that sound tempting. It's exciting to see a restaurant like this in the area!
HI90gPApLuPdEhoyNuEhow: crispy cheddar cheese is the fastest to get on your table as appetizer and it is great. wings need to get better. beef fajita is good. oreo mountain cake is awesome

I go to Chili's for their very tasty, yet also affordable soup and salad combo. I love the selection of soups and the salads are almost always fresh and flavorful. I have to be honest. One of my favorite things about this menu choice is the endless bowl of chips and salsa! Chili's Chips are some of the best in town. Not only do you get all you can eat at your table, but the wait staff will often bag up a warm and toasty helping for you to take home and have for dinner...that is, if you can wait that long. I gave Chili's 4 stars, only because I have gotten a couple pieces of wilted lettuce in my salad or a soup that's been over salted in the past, but for the most time this is not the norm. The service is excellent and the atmosphere is fun and lighthearted!

Founded by internationally renowned chef Jacqués Chili, this Michelin star-winning modern Chilean establishment was rated as the best restaurant in the USA by Zagat in 2013. Chili's is by far the greatest meal I've had in recent memory, and undoubtedly high on my list of the finest dining experiences I've ever had. I had the Chicken Bacon Ranch Quesadillas with 2 Frozen Strawberry Margaritas and and my palate has never been graced by such an exquisite marriage of flavour.
6nILr9A6ZQJgQIt__j-vSA: My wife and I are here for breakfast this morning during our stay at The Westin. It is listed as a separate restaurant from the hotel, but it's actually just a part of the hotel Lobby area. That being said, the breakfast was delicious and beautiful. Service was excellent and not badly priced. We always stay at SPG properties, and we will be back again. If we weren't staying on the property we wouldn't have eaten here, but in any event it was a great breakfast.

Stopped in for breakfast this morning before a conference and was pleasantly surprised with the speed of service and the quality of the food. I had the cinnamon French toast with pecans and the serving was generous, with a side of fresh fruit to top the toast. They serve Starbucks coffee and certainly aren't stingy with refills. The service here was far better than at my hotel, so I'll definitely be back.

I'm super impressed with this restaurant. The service was outstanding. My waiter, Paxton made the most amazing ice tea and was super attentive. The food is incredible. I ordered the Buttermilk Fried Chicken and the taste was amazing. I added a side of carrots and this meal just exceeded all of my expectations. I though I was being lazy eating in the hotel restaurant and it turns out that this place is a super gem.
GRGAUuZW0s4qxdGlN9eD5g: Sat outside (shady), had Crispin cider, the humus trio, and the vegetable pesto flatbread. All were pretty good! Great love music from "Born Sisters"!!

5 Stars for the service! Amanda was very accommodating to our group of 10 people last Wednesday evening. I think we ordered 12 appetizers and lots of beverages... She handled it all flawlessly with a great attitude, humor and attention detail! 

The food hit the spot for a needed quick fix, but the service is what will keep us coming back! Thanks Amanda!

Visited here while in town on business. I was working next door at the Music City Center so I came here twice while on lunch breaks.  The close proximity to my work made this an easy choice, although there are plenty of other options up on Broadway Street.  However, time was a factor and this place hit the spot in a pinch, which is all I needed.

The service was pretty good and I have no complaints about anything in particular.  The menu is simple and offers standard bar food.  Nothing over the top, but I didn't have anything that was bad either.  My first visit I had a burger and it was made well.  On my second visit I had a salad which was pretty good, although I think it's hard to mess up a salad.

The staff was incredibly friendly and they were very appreciative of our business which was nice considering it kind of comes off as a chain establishment.  They quickly served any need our party had.  I can see the place getting crazy busy during a Predators game, but that was not the case for us.

Altogether we had a decent experience and while it's not my first choice I would come back for a quick bite to eat if there were no other options.
VJrpldDtMSBFwWnYKoSH7w: Best pulled pork in town- very moist and full of flavor.  Staff is always nice, and it's mainly a to-go place- good for picnics, family get togethers, etc.  Smoked chicken is great, greens are so good, and bbq baked beans are tasty as well.

I've only been in town a month, and i've only gotten food here once, so I reserve the right to change my opinion. That said- a whole, bar-b-que, succulent chicken for 8 dollars and fifty cents? Could life get any better? That's cheaper than a convenience store pizza! Crazy! We got a rack of short ribs (16 dollars, a solid half a pig, enough for four, or three if you are very hungry) and it was so good we didn't even need the sauce, so just used that on our own pork the next day.

It's mostly a take out place- which works out for me, since it's on the way home for my dude, and I can call him up and ask him to pick it up- which I plan to do regularly. They do have some outdoor seating- I didn't see any indoors, but i wasn't looking, either.
Wonderful food, great service, and the best vinegar BBQ in Nashville!  Prices are reasonable and portions are huge.
zr3z36d9rRX68wKcHstOmA: It's a Sonic, if you have been to one you have been to them all. But I can say the staff here is amazing, they are super uber friendly and it makes you feel warm and fuzzy, if that's possible at a fast food place. Sonic has always been on of my favorite fast foods and always fills me up in a pinch.

Never thought I'd leave a review on a fast food chain, but every time I come to this particular Sonic location all the servers are so friendly and hospitable!  They've always been very polite and chipper (which is incredibly refreshing and unexpected when grabbing a fast food meal).

Whoever does the training at this location is doing it right! Thank you, kind service staff!

Who doesn't like Sonic? Well I know I do, especially for night time treats. The unique selection of icy treats always surprises me, like they have a peanut butter bacon milk shake! That's impressive for a fast food place. Go here and enjoy.
-p70N-1fbYVz3NSffvd7UQ: My go to lunch and dinner place for Shawarma, pitas, and handmade fresh Gyros.  Takes a little time, and is it worth it or what!  Best Middle Eastern restaurant around IMHO. Great french fries, even made it to The South In My Mouth Blog.  Great kind and welcoming fellows who appreciate everyone. Service and kindness are consistent.

Very nice, fresh pita bread in the store. But the restaurant is very good, also. If you've ever had a 1-minute gyro at some other place, you will appreciate how much better these are. They take a good 10 minutes to make, and they are quite good. When they ask if you want regular or hot dressing, be aware that the hot is extremely mild, so no worries either way.
Flat bread/pita bread was very good! Fresh and soft! I looked around, but didn't try anything else! Their prices are very good!!!
quk1d6aRxJf4amQnf49F9Q: Got lucky and hit them at half price drink and 99 cent corndog time. Food was good, drink was cold. Had I not ordered the 8 piece mozzarella sticks I would have had lunch for around 4$ but closer to 8$ with the sticks.

This place is as good as the commercials make it seem! I wish I was a fat man because I wanted to order at least one of everything off of the menu.  The food was so good and so filling.  I wish there were more locations in and around Los Angeles.  I definitely have Sonic cravings now that I've actually tried it.
I like the new ownership, better service! My only request, please deep clean the bathroom, it always smells like poop!
I_07_9imdwynBJsf_2HqZA: Fish sandwiches are outstanding!! The whiting fillets are huge & almost boneless!! The building however, needs work... only drive thru available

I came across this drive-up only fourth stop on my Nashville BBQ tour after a recommendation from the guy working at Hog Heaven. Let me begin by saying, "Get the fish!" Do yourself a favor and skip the BBQ. The rib were good but nothing special. They lacked the flavor of better ribs, and the sauces consumed the flavor -- preventing the meat to shine. 

Okay, with that being said, the fish is fantastic. I got the catfish with onion, mustard, pickles, and hot sauce. It is served as a sandwich on white bread. Wow! The fish is cooked to perfection. The batter is peppery and complements the fish perfectly. This drive-up dive does fried fish  better than anyone else. (That last statement is pure speculation; I haven't tried much fried fish in TN.) Even though the ribs were mediocre, the fish salvaged the review and was so good I gave the four stars to the fish.
A trip to Nashville is not complete without a fish sandwich from TJ's. It's meaty and the seasoning on the batter is so flavorful cornmeal batter.
Ld7K9CU1bgovY0kM_VpUXw:
After a couple of failed attempts at going here, my husband and I took advantage of our off day and made our way to Eat Well.  

I had the red beans and rice with hot chicken sausage.  He had the patty melt with gouda and caramelized onions.  We both had mint iced tea.  

The service was very friendly and prompt.  The food came out quickly.  The red beans and rice were so delicious and savory.  I'm usually not a fan of chicken sausage, but this stuff was amazing, though it wasn't quite as spicy as I was expecting.  I got the medium, was full about half way through.  

My husband declared the patty melt to be the best he'd ever had.  I tried a bite and have to agree.  Gouda on ALL the sandwiches!  Forever.  

The mint tea made the summer heat nearly bearable, and they brought out a pitcher for our table.  

Our total was $18 after everything, and we left feeling incredibly satisfied.  

Hooray for Eat Well!

Amazing Patty-Melt! Don't think I've ever had a better one. 
Looking forward to trying more of their items. 
The service was friendly and helpful too.
Great food! The Juices are out of this world.  Happy to have this place in our neighborhood!
Zm0gXfbwqa8vfBgsMx7otg: Everything was yummy, I had the pulled chicken and my hubby had the brisket sandwich.  The service was awesome,  true southern hospitality.

This location just got new owners and it shows.  Wonderful staff, great service, and even better food!!  Baked beans were hands down the best I've ever had and the barbeque chicken sandwich tasted fresh and delicious!  Very clean and nice inside plus the convenient drive thru :)  I'll definitely be going back here again

First time eating here and the Friday special (pulled pork) was recommended.  It was unbelievably great.  I also got the cole slaw and barbeque baked beans.  I'll be going back to try other things.  Highly recommend this place of you're thinking about trying it.
McE_Yj6-dfRmh3RR3hfVsg: Awesome little find tucked away in a shopping center in Bellevue.  So glad a friend knew about it and took us there after our Girls Spa day.  I ordered the quiche and lemon artichoke soup and was very happy, but pretty sure you couldnt go wrong with anything on the menu.  The pimento cheese and cracker type starter will be your first indicator that it is going to be a good meal!  I haven't had any of the desserts, but if they taste half as good as they look, i am sure they are amazing.  Quaint, "tea room" type of feel to it that gave it some character to match the delicious and inventive food.  Highly recommend

This is Bellevue's "Delicious Little Secret" .They kinda go by 2 different names right now, Cottage Café (which was left from the prior owner) and "Crumb de la Crumb" (which is the name of the catering company).  I can't say enough about this little gem of a restaurant. 1st:  the relaxing music that plays at a volume where you can still hear the jazz vocals, but not loud enough to drown out the person you're talking to across the table.
