Actually, the person at the next table, who is singing along is louder than the piped in tracks...but who can blame them for singing, humming, whistling etc along?? It's comfortable, and contagious and fun.  2nd, the service: Fantastic. The "Husband Part" of the owners usually waits on me.  Handsome, charming, attentive, knows the menu, etc. always keeping an eye on the tables looking for that subtle raise of an eyebrow that indicates that they need some attention. 3rd:  THE FOOD.  THE FOOD. THE FOOD.  Oh my gosh The FOOD!!!  Just like the owners before them, AND the owners before THEM, they have kept the incredible chicken salad sandwich recipe.  GOOD MOVE!!! But their own creations are UNBELIVEABLE.  The She Crab Soup, The Lemon Artichoke Soup, the dressed avocado salad, the mac & cheese, ALL SO GOOD!!!  You CAN'T leave without ordering a dessert. They have such a different and unique array of sweets. Some of them infused with amaretto, or rum, of coffee liqueur, etc.  Try the vanilla Lemonade. FANTASTIC!! This is a MUST for anyone that has guests coming in town and wants to go someplace new and different. The used to only be open M-F during working hours for most people. They have just recently added Saturday which has been my saving grace. They are not open for dinner (yet), only brunch/lunch so please check their hours before leaving the house. I LOVE this place and can't say enough good things about it.  10 stars!!!

Delicious, delicious, delicious. Stopped here on the way to Atlanta once with my boyfriend and this time when I planned to meet some friends in Nashville, I made this our first meeting place. 

It used to be inside an antique store but now the store is totally gone and it's just one large restaurant. That kinda bummed me out because I loved the charm of that, it felt very Southern. 
Regardless, the food was still on point. The she-crab soup is the best I've ever had and I don't even like creamy soups.
I also got the berried bird sandwich because when my friend got it last time I couldn't stop drooling over it. It was just as scrumptious as I expected.

I also love the fruit tea as well as the vanilla bean lemonade. I can't pick a favorite between them--the addition of spices to the fruit tea is brilliant, as well as the gorgeous flecks of vanilla bean in the lemonade.

I do think it's rather expensive for a lunch meal and for the portion size but I'd still be back. I have yet to try their baked goods but have heard great things!
R8Jue3SLDadcgM7JrEHFTQ:
My roommate (a New Yorker) and I used to live within walking distance of Dragon Garden and now we drive from Madison because their egg rolls are so good.

Best egg rolls (they're made in-house and we get 6-10 every time), best shrimp Mai Fun, best sesame and General Tso's in Nashville. Our friends have ordered other dishes and said the same of those. 

The owners are great and welcoming people, so make the effort to pick up your order if you have the time and tip them well. They've certainly earned it!

Best Chinese delivery I've managed to find in Nashville. All the classic gringo-Chinese favorites you'd expect. Truly expansive (by that I mean broad and big, not expensive with a Southern accent. It's actually quite cheap!)

try the happy family

I have been eating at Dragon Garden since i moved to Nashville in 2002 and they had a location on west end.  I dont diversify a ton, so i've only really had the Sweet and Sour, sesame, and general tso's chicken.  Each of those dishes is packed with flavor and delicious.  The TSO is the best flavor i've ever had, which includes dining in NYC.  I currently live in LA and can't get anything close to the flavor to curb my appetite.  The lunch special is more than filling, and has a nice price of 3.99.

Since i'm a long time customer, the staff is always extremely nice to me.  The owner's children often work the phones, which can be a little frustrating if you call in.  I've always either eaten there or gotten takeout, but i have heard from friends that delivery can take up to an hour and a half on some occasions.  So i recommend the food highly, but pass on the delivery.
ax4J4cpEXs5T8eEsI8nLHg: Quick, fast and straight to the point. There is no one taking your order so the food is limited. They do have all of the normal things. Chicken sandwiches, fries, chicken nuggets and salads. No shakes or any of the specialty items. You check out sort of lunch room style. Didnt matter to me either way. The food was still delicious and hot. It located inside the "Batman" building at the 4th street entrance.

In my building so I go here a lot. I eat here almost three times a week..I love their 12 piece chicken nuggets with Buffalo Spicy sauce...ummm good !!! I usually don't do fast food but I love their chicken..

Be still my heart! I had NEVER had Chick Fil-A before Tuesday. OMG! It was amazing! We have a couple here in the Chicago area, but had to go out of town to "experience" it. The chicken sandwich was to DIE FOR, and sauce is a hybrid of honey mustard, bbq, and heaven all rolled together. The lemonade.. is like sunshine in a cup. OMG! Definitely worth it, and i dont even care if it wasn't the biggest Chick Fil-A restaurant, it was amazing!
Qg40trf_nz0FnXInzGusEw:
Back in my middle school days my mom would take my sister and i here for a special treat. I had been waiting to have a newer experience though to review it. Well Husband and I ended up here one Saturday afternoon and to tell the truth this place hasn't changed all that much.They still offer all of my old favorites along with some new things as well. We ended up having lunch here. They have a small sandwich menu as well as some savory pastries (listed by matt c.). All sandwiches have the option of coming on a croissant or your choice of bread. The vegetarian options include a veggie sandwich (which i ordered) and egg salad (which husband ordered). Both were good however the egg salad was a bit too herbed for me. As for drinks I highly recommend the aloe vera soda's in the case. Yes, they may sound scary but they in no way taste like aloe (flavors like blueberry, apple, & peach) and they have fun jelly bits in the bottom. almost like a mini less scary bubble tea. (husband hates bubble tea but likes this stuff). And I didn't see it advertised anywhere but I saw a lot of people with a fruit tea looking drink. 
As for the pastries go I recommend the blueberry danish and the chocolate roll cake. If you want something to go I highly recommend the apricot glazed alpha phan. That is if they still have any by the time you get there. 
The only downside (and subtraction of 1 star) is that there is no line. If you stand there and wait patiently everyone who walks in the door after you will be helped first. You have to be somewhat aggressive (which is not my nature at all).
And I do know for sure that this is not a Chinese bakery. I was always told that they were Japanese bakers trained in France. Wherever they are actually from doesn't matter. It is Japanese style. (Although Matt C's stars around his "is" are quite convincing.)

One of the most unique bakeries in Nashville.  I can say from experience that this is an authentic Japanese style bakery.  They have excellent bread, but if you want to try something different they have a wide assortment of baked goods that you will not find anywhere else in Nashville.  I would highly recommend the curry pan and the melon pan for a start.  Both will give you an interesting taste of what another country's baked goods.  Their strawberry cake is also the best I have ever tasted.  Worth a trip out of the way.

Alpha Bakery has some of the most unique breads and pastries in the Nashville area.  If you're in Bellevue, you really should give them a try.  The building is not fancy and this is a small business who doesn't spend a lot of money on marketing and advertising, but the food is great and made fresh every day.  Many people that I know who go there found out about it by word of mouth.  
Many of the offerings and pastries are Asian inspired.  They are delicious and really are like what I and other family members have had in Japan.  The curry pan is a great savory treat if you like curry.  As another reviewer mentioned, the sweet Asian pastries are not overly sweet.  A lot of people in the Asian community come here to get things they can't get anywhere else in Nashville.  
If you want something decadent, the snowballs (puff pastry stuffed with whipped cream and sliced bananas) can't be beat.  Both the almond and chocolate crossaints are flaky and fantastic.  
The breads are absolutely worth trying as well, from the Asian alpha fan to the dinner rolls to the best challah in town.  It absolutely melts in your mouth when it's fresh and beats Publix's challah bread hands down.  
They do serve brunch now, and those offerings are unique as well.  If you just want a sandwich, I highly recommend the chicken salad.  It's very peppery and isn't overwhelmed with mayonnaise.  
Last, but certainly not least, are the cakes.  The strawberry cake here is my absolute favorite cake that I ask for every year on my birthday.  The cake itself is super light and not overly sweet with a light creamy icing and a layer of fresh strawberries in the middle.  When I've served this at family gatherings or parties, it's gone in a flash.  They also have an amazing Asian inspired chocolate that is Japanese in style and not overly sweet.  They often have slices of the cakes available, but you do have to order whole cakes ahead of time.
tdXlu_cPzrgDuuZbc6He9A: I love this Arnold's. great food and nice people. You also don't have to deal with the line you deal with st the 8th ave location!

As a longtime fan of the original, this location lives up to expectations. Hidden behind Green Hills Mall, this is a well kept lunch secret. Always have great meat & 3 options and worth saving room for dessert if you can.

No frills, If you are looking for a classy dining experience this is not for you. It's basically a cafeteria with meat and sides. However it is delicious! I had the brisket and my partner had the pork chop. All our sides were extremely tasty. All the food was salty, so if you don't like that you may be out of luck (I say it's a once in a while food). Also I wish the hours were better, or at least open on weekends
wR7hImstt_9jh45kFY5PXg: This is definitely the best Subway on the west side by far! Great sandwiches.i particularly like the sandwiches.

This is by far our favorite Subway. Have been going there since they opened. The staff is always friendly. Food always fresh. Even when the line is out the door they are able to get your order done quickly. Order is always correct. We eat here about 3 times a week.

I've had some pretty awful experiences with Subway, but here lately it seems like they're stepping their game up. They're certainly a better choice in comparison to most other fast food. This location in particular, along with the green hills location are probably the best ones in Nashville.
5eayf0Y0TY1ufCWXAk_a3Q: As a Nashville local, Baja Burrito is one of my favorite lunch spots; so, getting it at the airport is an extra treat when I fly.  While the airport version doesn't give you the full "original" experience, it definitely is worth the stop.  The major differences you'll see is the lack of my favorite "spinach tortilla" option and a few minor menu items.  Otherwise, it's tasty and fresh.  Not sure about the other reviewer's experiences, but obviously they have done some major improvement since the unfortunate experience.  If you're here for a layover, definitely check this place out!

No, it's not "real" Mexican food, but I love it anyway.  This is one of the best burrito places in town.  Fast, fresh and cheap, the way this type of food is supposed to be. What I like about the food here is that the cold ingredients are not ice cold, so as to upset the warm ingredients.  I hate to take a bit into a great big chicken burrito, and have the chicken be cold because it was sitting next to the cold salsa.  Just a pet peeve. There are more where that came from.
Love love their Chicken burrito. Love how I get exactly what I want in it. Great taste. Very quick service and kind people preparing your meal. I love coming here! Nothing bad to say.
GLH_fQI9epIC-8t2oOSpoQ: YUMM! I was so pleased when my husband and I visited Daddy's Dogs on 1/21. The vendor Chris was very kind and whipped us up a couple of delicious dogs. I had the New York, he had the Big Daddy. I highly recommend this stand. They maintain a clean workspace, quick service and offer amazing toppings that will satisfy anyone. We'll be back!

Flew all the way to Nashville from Washington to have good fun and great food. I can say for sure Daddy's did not disappoint. By far one of the best dogs I've had. 

The employees are super friendly, and very quick to deliver the best quality dogs possible. I even got to chat with the owner/operator for a bit!

All I can say is GREAT food, a true must if you're in the Nashville area.
New favorite hotdog joint!!!! So cool all the hotdogs are named after different cities. Music City dog was delicious! Large proportions that fill you up and getting food from a cart is so much fun.
2OcYrGeyIhR_ODtl1xVf-Q:
I beg to differ with some of these bad reviews.  There is a large group of us that go after church on special occasions.
The service is always very good and the breakfast bar has plenty of options for a typical breakfast.  If you are looking for prime rib and eggs benedict it's not your place.  If your looking for a good affordable breakfast it makes the cut. And please keep your snobby location views to yourself., it serves no purpose.

Every one in our group ordered the breakfast buffet. Over all it was an great dining experience. The food was good. And the waiter was excellent. He stopped by often and refilled our drinks without having to be asked. One of our group ordered pancakes from the buffet and the waiter brought whipped cream for him. They had a wide variety of food that was well made. You could ask for buffet pancakes and eggs made how you like them. The only reason it will not give it five stars is that we found some food crust on a fork. The fork was cleaned so it was sterile but it was still a bit of a turnoff

First off I saw one of the comments referred to this particular Shoney's as being in the ghetto. I live in Woodbine in this said neighborhood, who knew I lived in a ghetto? Vanderbilt is in this neighborhood as well...NOT THE GHETTO. 

Saying all that, back to the restaurant. My friend/sister and I go there at least 2 times a week. They make the absolute BEST turkey club in all of Nashville. I kid you not. We are retired so we have gone to many of the restaurants new and old. Now days it's a theme to fancy up things like club sandwiches...Shoney's still makes an original "no need to fix it, it ain't broken" club sandwich. I like the buffet. When the Chicken first comes out, you will not find better. The buffet is never "nasty". They are maintaining and cleaning any spills. I've never seen rancid or over ripe veggies or lettuces. The hot food represents Old fashioned cookery. Some of the veggies are overcooked, but that is how they taste good with the seasoning. Their greens are just the way I like them. Cornbread muffins to soak up the "pot licker"...mmmmm. Shoney's makes the best sweet tea! You can taste the brew. I've never gotten a bad glass of tea. The cost of the buffet is so reasonable in my opinion. You can't expect "fusion", "gourmet"...it's "country" fare. 

As far as the service goes: we go often and get great service. My tea glass never stays empty for long. We bring our family sometimes and everyone leaves happy. Our favorites are Tessa and Kayla and Lorenzo. But like I said we never get poor service. The Managers are always on hand. They do everything. From cooking, to fixing the deep fryer to mopping floors. The bathrooms always have paper and soap in the dispensers. They are as clean as most places we visit. The problem isn't the servers, it's the customers who have poor eating habits. I have seen whole groups come in and leave a horrid mess for the servers to clean up. My friend is blind and they will help her to the restroom...they see we have many napkins. Our glasses never stay empty for long. I didn't give the restaurant 5 stars because that seems for a fancy gourmet place. I love this Shoney's and now I am craving so I gotta go.
EsBfxRx8SbeY6Oamz2BkxQ: Have been here several times over the years. Always get great service, whether delivery or carryout, and the people who work there are always friendly. This location has been there about 25 years, which tells you something about how they handle business. I'd absolutely go here before other pizza places in the area.

Sometimes you need late night food...like when you missed dinner and have been out most of the evening exploring Nashville and enjoying every second of it.  Sometimes what you want is something decidedly unhealthy.  Enter Domino's, specifically this small Domino's in Hillsboro Village.  I walked in, ordered a small pizza, waiting 10 minutes, and BOOM food.  The staff were very friendly despite the late hour and intrusion upon their delivery-heavy workload.  It was fast and cheap and a pleasant experience.  I would happily come back here for more late night bites.

All of my life, I have lived with parents who forbade me from buying large buffalo wings.  However, recently, I have become free of these restrictions.  And so I called Domino's and bought the juiciest and tastiest buffalo wings could find.  It was a stunning beauty of the BBQ flavored variety. I worked on each bone like it was my last.   It was delicious!
LpxysM0CVOceYqjUsY6Y4A: Kalamatas is our favorite place to grab meals! Wally and his staff are wonderful, and always do their best to give good, fast service. My favorite meal is the Chicken Gyro plate, and my husband's is the Fruity Chicken plate. You cannot beat the awesome Tuna salad also - NO MAYO! Lots of good stuff - capers, onions, fresh tuna...it is yummy! We highly recommend this is you are in the Belmont/Hillsboro area and need a delicious and quick meal.

I absolutely would recommend Kalamata's to anyone!  Their service is wonderful and the food is even better.  I might be biased because I go there multiple times a week, but there is always a great special or other Greek/Mediterranean style dishes.  You can be as picky as you want and ask for ingredients to be taken off of your dish without a problem.  The small family size restaurant makes you feel right at home and is a great way to change up the normal routine of sandwiches for lunch!  If you like salmon then you better head over there for salmon Thursday.  Definitely one of my favorite local restaurants to go to throughout the week.

I LOVE KALAMATA'S. There stuffed salmon on rice is absolutely to die for. I have not had an item on their menu that i don't like, and I try to get something different every time. Plus, they have excellent customer service!
MJlS0hmRsDCRIgIzAF2sTQ: We have had 2 great meals.... best shrimp tacos & the best ever Eggs Benedict thanks to chef Johan & our great & personal waiter Matt. Thank you for making our stay at the Union Station Hotel & Nashville a memorable experience!!!
Amazing drink menu! We tried 2 and they were delish! The hotel lobby where Carters is located is like a princess castle or something from Europe. GORGEOUS! With tons of eye candy everywhere!

Me ,my mom and my friend came and stayed at the hotel we decided we were going to eat and service was great cocktails were amazing and even the service from the server is great and even the management offered us a 10% off our meal and I think that was very nice of him because we waited a little bit for our meals .
zwTeKW4iQgjBklEi-kaKpQ:
For Nashville, I feel that I am getting much much closer to finding a za like Chicago. I preordered a pizza and salad for pick-up using my Groupon.

I ordered the Chicago Deep dish 14" veggie pizza.

Although more like a thick crust than a deep dish; the pie is gooooddd. They do make it in the same style as a deep dish Chicago za. The tomatoes on top were very delicious. The vegetables were also very good. I like the crust and it had the appeal of a nice hand turned crust. I would say it maybe needed to cook for just another minute or so to take out the little bit of chewy left in the crust. They do use a little corn meal.

The salad was saved by the vegetables, cheese and dressing (which to my liking was the right touch). The salad was huge for the price. The only green part of the salad was the green peppers. The salad is made with iceberg lettuce. They gave me four little cups of dressing! Can you say lunch tomorrow!

Although on Nolensville Pike, its worth the drive of 15 minutes from the city for this pizza. Heck, in Chicago, you would drive an hour round trip to go almost anywhere. And, the price is fair for the quality and quantity. 

The restaurant is more of groups of two-four. More a pick-up and take home pizza place. The ladies behind the counter were friendly, and I suspect made my pizza! Ah, a woman's touch. :)

It is kinda hard to find, but worth the investigation. Their menu looks outdated but is current. And, Fontanini Italian beef from Chicago is honestly the best out there. I will go back soon just to try the Italian Beef sandwich. After all, it is their namesake.

I've been jonesing for Portillos - and this was awfully close to my memory of a delicious Italian Beef -- right here in NashVegas!  Super thin slices of season beef and a very tasty roll with the right combination of softness and chewiness.  Hmm!  I like the "jus" on the side - so I control how soggy it gets, but to each his or her own!

PS - it is a little hard to get to in traffic; it's worth it to go a little out of yoru way to make all right-hand turns.

This place is completely authentic and great. They serve a lot of things besides Italian sausage, beef, and Chicago pizza, but I don't know why anyone would ever order any of those things. This is a place you come to when you want real, delicious Italian beef. The concept, stated in other reviews, that Chicago style eating is "almost foreign" is bizarre. There's enough meat, fat, and grease in this place to satisfy the most hardened Southerner.
BYLlM7-Lu1mqHmlmBnPtJQ:
Came into town from LA.  I knew the hockey game was on and I was staying with the wife at the Hyatt up the street.  I asked if they would turn the Kings game on and they did with a smile. The service was very friendly and helpful.

For the food, I ordered the pulled pork slider appetizers and my wife ordered the pulled pork nachos.  Both food items were wonderful.  They were not like the typical greasy various food.

The atmosphere was not bad,  we came in on a slow night so no live music which we were ok with since we were there to watch the kings game.  Very clean place though, I would highly recommend this place.

Sobro sports bar grill food was excellent staff very helpful.
American traditional food restaurant while watching your favorite team at play
The staff is excellent and the food was great. So much so, we went back a second time for light bites. The sliders were delicious and the quesadilla were yummy. Salads were good too.
DOb8-Z1525QH8PRIdHOfww: At 2:30 AM my window attendant corrected my order. I can't tell you how much I appreciated that because I don't eat meat or dairy. Late night? This Taco Bell won't let you down!!!!

I always receive great service and food from this location.
I have loved the Mexican pizza for years, light beans & double meat. 
They have a great value meal menu.
F2Um35uLYEx-HnHEyYKxAw:
I've been here two times now as a guest. First time was actually my Birthday and was with a group celebrating upcoming Birthdays as well. Second time I was there for a showcase.
Now as a guest I learned you have to be a member of this place. As you see not very many reviews, why? Because this place is meant to be private and secluded. Whether you're business, a country star, or just someone wanting privacy from the public eye, I think this is a great idea. I can understand there are those that just want to get away and relax in a place feeling like it's your own.
Only way to get in is by thumbprint that unlocks the door. Or just know someone!

As a member the staff is required to know who you are by name and it has a LA style atmosphere. DJ and dance floor included with nice tucked couches and booths giving you the freedom without judgement.

They do have a menu to balance out your drinking. You can rent out the place or just pick a spot with your friend's membership.
Great venue and cool atmosphere to have an event around 50 plus and good sushi for Nashville ...

We had my husband's 40th Birthday party at Citizen and it was perfect. Easily accommodated our 70 guests and the staff was amazing. Planning the party was a breeze with the event coordinator. All of our guests had the best time. Can't wait for our next event at Citizen!
35xfN-Xa9Sg0hmeZ0aEjMQ: The food was delious as always. There was alot of noise behind the counter tonight and their was guys hanging outside restaurant. Not good
WkeCW0WCOKjZj4G2TuEdVA: Just ordered via app...got the  $ 7.99 2 topping carryout. ..the pizza was excellent. ..definitely worth the price. ..will be back!
Ordered online...they were backed up on New years day. .had to wait extra time but the pizza was excellent. .they need to work out some kinks in the service but the food is worth the price. ..
The girl in the front was very nice and enthusiastic. The spread of the sauce and toppings were great and tasted excellent.
HR8YcViyiuHAv89KwpVZbg: Honestly, they showed up really quickly. My pizza took less than an hour from placing the order to pick up. It came exactly as it should have.

I ordered a pizza online tonight from this location. The pizza was delivered fresh, hot, and delicious. The delivery driver was very gracious and kind. You can't go wrong ordering the thin crust Veggie pizza (easy on the cheese) from Pizza Hut...it's my favorite delivery pizza!
QSlFP_AwiOsg7sbUhqKQmg: Got delivery when traveling through town. Delicious, plentiful, reasonably priced and timely. Next time I'm in town, I will definitely eat there again!

The wife and I stopped here before grocery shopping.  We recently moved back into the area and have been trying new places and this is one of the best by far.  The portions were huge and the food was delicious.  I can't wait to go back and try the rest of the menu.  My mouth is watering as I write this just thinking of the food. We will be back.

This place must be fairly new because I couldn't even find them on Google Maps. But I came here a few weeks ago. I love Gyros and not so much of the typical mall gyros so I know the difference. I ordered the mixed plate and it was really one of the best if not the best I've have in a long time. Lamb and chicken were cooked perfectly and then a few toppings to make it even better and for a good price honestly. Definitely recommend you try this place out!
qBHwXD1OiUnI9q7HaBOPHQ: This place is a hidden treasure of many taco trucks/stands that are in the neighborhood! I have been going to a different place nearby and decided I won't be going back due to lack of service and correctness of orders. The man running the truck is nice, friendly, quick, and attentive. Salsa is spicy and they include all kinds of veggies and peppers on the side ! There's a tip jar at the window I suggest you use it to show your appreciation :)

Lord almighty!!! Just take all of my money. I love this taco truck, I just cant explain how delicious their tacos are. Plus they are never stingy with the salsa verde!! And I always have the most genuine interactions with the employees. Dont forget to bring your cash. They do NOT take card.
It's one of the best Taquería in Nashville that I know of I really like it. The food here is authentic and their horchata is the best.
2qu3N66OMmn95ejmFAMtPA: This restaurant is easy to miss but a great place to find yourself for lunch.  Just make sure you have plenty of time for a leisurely meal as the service is very slow, making you feel like you are on island time.  But despite that, it is worth it. The food is fantastic and well priced.  For $8-10 you get a full plate of mouth watering,rich, filling Guatemalan food.  I highly recommend the papusas which look sort of like a pancake but taste a lot like a Mexican tamale.  The roast chicken with mole sauce is excellent as well. Get a bottle of Fanta or Mexican Coke to wash it all down.  They do have vegetarian options as well.  Don't let slow service dissuade you from trying out another East Nashville hole-in-the-wall gem.  Give this place a try! I'm glad we did.
Service isn't spectacular and the language barrier is comedic gold- but the food is pretty great! The bean and cheese pupusa is divine.
We had delicious pupusas and tacos with fresh chips with beans to start. Very authentic. Service was wonderful! Great prices, too. We'll be back!
4Xkpyuk4QQnAK9WBDHdHzg: I can't help it, I love this place. Don't be scared off by its appearance! It's pretty comparable to any other buffet style Chinese restaurant, but it's only $4.98 a person. 4DJLDRWdfl0OsEcbS2_nHA: Great neighborhood dive bar. Karaoke starts at 10pm on Friday and Saturday. They always have ALL the games on their 6 big tv's! Happy hour is everyday from open until 7pm. dRYfyAnwZGfDKs83fOXotg: I have been coming to this location nearly 15 years now. They keep the place nice and clean, and you will usually see the same faces behind the counter. They are polite, courteous and quick. I am watching three of them work at 100% of their ability to get through a very full lunch line. Never stop moving, never being curt with the customer. A+ and will continue to come back.

Eat fresh! I definitely love getting a semi-healthy meal and the fact that it's so close to work, but the parking is HORRIBLE. They have like 4 spots, and there have been a few occasions where I have had to park all the way around the building and walk down by Wendy's just to get there. I know they can't really help that, which is why I'm still giving 4 stars, but still.. it sucks. The people are usually friendly, and of course the sandwiches are speedily made, but I definitely steer clear during peak hours unless I'm desperate. Lines get long, and workers get cranky, it's just not a good combination.  But still, it's the only Subway in Green Hills so I'll take it!

I work right across the street from this place, which is convenient because that's where you would need to park anyway.  One day I'm going to start calling a wrecker to tow everyone that is parking in our lot to eat at Subway.  That might cause an implosion in Green Hills!

The folks that work there are always very cool to their regulars.  We get all kinds of free stuff from them, usually cookies.  The cookies are excellent.  They are extremely friendly and they will honor odd requests for sandwiches.  

You may know how Subway works, but here are some tips:

1.  Don't touch the glass.  You can just tell the people that work there what you want without pointing to it and leaving a fingerprint.  I don't know if they get upset by this, but I'm there so much that it has become a pet peeve.  

2.  If you want all of the vegetables, you can say "run it through the garden."  I heard someone say that one day and they knew immediately what to do.  Yes, you sound like a moron, but I'm just throwing that out there as an option.

3.  Don't mix Ranch dressing with any other sauce on a sandwich.  That's just gross.  

4.  It's a really fun game to look at the line in front of you and try to guess what each person will order.  Hint:  race, gender, and weight are the most helpful stereotypes.  Giant guy ordered the foot-long steak covered in ranch....  Shocker!

5.  There are people that literally eat there everyday for lunch.  I can't handle that, but you'll notice it if you go there enough.

I've eaten at this Subway at least once a week for the last 10 years.  I'm thinking that is probably 700 or so visits!
u7heKYrYeSWF0HIMm9E2Jg: Friendly people. Convenient drive thru. They're open super late! It's cheap and it satisfies cravings :)) try triple steak stack or their soft shell taco. Love it.

Why are you here?  For fast pseudo-Mexican food.   This place delivers fast hot food.  

Did you know that Taco Bell has some of the lowest calorie fast food?  A soft chicken taco is 160 calories.  A crunchy taco is 170 calories.   Take that 480 calorie McD's salad.   BUT if you have sodium issues, you may want to check out their online menu to see if you can put together a lower sodium alternative - most items are high in salt.     

Strangely enough, the interior is very clean.  Much cleaner than the typical fast food place.  I think the reason for this is that 90% of their business is drive thru but for what ever reason - kudos to Taco Bell for a clean interior.  

Also passes the dog test - I usually have dogs riding with me and dogs are very excited when the Taco Bell bag comes through the drive thru window.   Five paws up rating.

There fast. Big parking-lot.Fast Drive through. Needs to be updated inside I was born in 83 and Around the early to mid 90s is when this furniture first started being used. AC is on point. Drink machine in lobby sucks.
lqrY1tHhPkZsiOPfpoydvw: Love Burger King! Just make sure you don't do the drive thru at this location unless you want to waste all your gas. You'll have plenty of time to walk to the Kroger across the street and get some. My only complaint is that they don't serve breakfast all day. I usually like to eat my breakfast in the morning so it would be nice to be able to order at night, right before I go to bed so that way it would be ready by the time I get up in the morning.

Cracker Barrel, eat your heart out.  This is what comfort food is meant to be.

Housed in an unassuming storefront in a non-descript strip mall, Dinner Bell is just what your momma ordered.  The daily specials vary, from chicken fried steak to roast beef to chicken and dumplings. i03Bu6rZ1nqJ_5WTFBUGjA: The bad reviews shocked me.  Service was quick and food was great.  Will definitely come back again!

Seeing all these negative reviews made me wanna go try the place out itself and to be honest I wasn't let down by how amazing there pancakes were . Super cute restaurant tucked in the back of this relatively large shopping center.  We found it on Yelp when looking for breakfast options near our friends' apartment in Nashville.  

We were greeted nicely and asked if it was two for lunch, seeing as it was 10:50am. We asked if breakfast was still available and they said they could squeeze us in.  Score! It was our first day in Nashville and all we had on our minds was cheesy grits and biscuits and gravy.  Breakfast selections are varying combos of eggs, meats, potatoes, grits and biscuits. First off I love this place BUT I went there today and sat at a table waiting for the rest of my party mo one even said hi just kept walking by me I looked down and stuff all over the table 10 mins latter my guest arrived we walked up to the register very nice person there ordered the food and went ahead and put a tip on the card bad mistake they gave us our drinks we sat down food came very quickly food was very good I ate all of it that's rare for me looked at my drink realized it was empty and so was everyone elses we waived someone over she took our dishes we asked for two to go cups of tea the other two did not want one our supposed waitress went to another table asking them if they were ok got them all refils and brought another table bread we had to remind them about the tea she got another waitress to get it and did not tell her one of the people in our group asked for lite on the ice when the drinks finally arrived I asked witch one had lite ice and she rudly told me I was never told that and then just left I was so disappointed in the customer service today bit like I said the food was outstanding.

The laid back atmosphere is a much needed escape from the conference I was attending. It's clean and decorated with a chic urban look. We had the deep dish Margherita and it was amazing!!! Between two of us the 9 inch pie was enough for both of us, and it's a true deep dish so get ready for the mind blowing experience of getting to indulge in this masterpiece! Sorry we didn't photograph our food, we devoured it as soon as it was placed within an arms reach. You'll just have to take my word that it's gorgeous and tasty.

Solid 4 stars. Friendly staff, good drink specials (beer + whiskey shot for $5?!), deep dish pizza, free comedy show on Thursdays, outdoor patio with giant Jenga and Cornhole. 

The food came to our table shockingly fast but somehow cold. Not quite like the original in Chicago, but I'm not faulting them for that. We will be back.
Check this place out! The pizza is delicious and they have great drink specials. Service was wonderful and the atmosphere is inviting, comfortable and fun.
8l7Jb_FZnOTvDR2lX7TvCw: I visited Fonda Cublete during lunch.  The restaurant is renovated, and the owner is very friendly and helpful.  He recommended the pork spine soup.  We were given an English menu.  The soup came with a ton of shrimp, tortillas, slices, and onions.  It was similar to menudo, but more American pallet friendly.  They had a decent amount of customers at the time we went, but it wasn't too busy.  There are a few other menu items I would like to try next time.

Safe to say the best authentic Mexican restaurant I've been to in Nashville! I highly recommend this restaurant to anyone! The staff is extremely friendly and helpful to those unfamiliar with dishes. I will definitely be coming back! Try the flautas, any of the soups especially the one with the pork neck and shrimp, and also for kids they have really delicious quesadillas! I recommend having the Fresca con cream and any aguas frescas!

Impressive Fresh Mexican Cuisine !
Chicken Fajitas with corn tortillas
Massive amount of food for one.
All fresh ingredients and no fast food here.
Table sauces were mild and spicy hot,
both with good flavor.
No chips and salsa - sad
No unsweet tea and got nailed $1 for water - Whats up with that??
Restaurant was clean, people were friendly !! Staff was attentive.
Menus in Spanish and English too.
They are known for the BEEF Stew as well as Fajitas.
Not Cheap, alittle pricey for lunch.
But Fresh ingredients and good food !
I would have been happy with half the food for 
about $7.50 fyi.
No Lunch specials noted...
dfjMedVxJomf4QaWbcdnqg: What a surprise to find this restaurant in Nashville.  Delicious Venezuelan food. We had the arepa special and it did not disappoint. The best tasting arepas that we have had outside of Venezuela. We also had the guanabana drink it was great. We loved eating here.
Excellent choice for you are having the most delicious and healthiest breakfast in the World

A Venezuelan friend of mine brought me here for lunch today. The food is amazing. Fair prices, super clean and new renovations inside. If you've never had Venezuelan food I highly recommend. Got the steak & chicken with rice and beans. The empanadas and fried plantains are a must as well.
qMrWq1CimcZlE02mIBH9SQ: This place is so awesome ! The food was so good - my favorite plate was the Pork Gyoza ! My service was outstanding, the second you walk in I was met by Matthew - He's so welcoming and made our experience that much better with a gleaming personality and so stylish, definitely coming back.

My husband and I never went to Little Octopus, but we were really excited when we heard Bar Otaku was opening in the Gulch. We arrived at 6PM on a Saturday night and were able to be seated immediately at the bar overlooking the kitchen. We loved that because we love watching the kitchen magic. The space is intimate with fun decorations and cute wooden booths. I ordered a gin based cocktail from the menu- it was simple and refreshing. We then ordered several menu items (all small plates) and we loved them all! We each got a tuna handroll and cane with spicy mayo we could use as a dip. I thought that was really creative. We next ordered the pork gyoza, which were yummy pan fried dumplings. We also ordered the salmon onigiri and the friend chicken thighs. The chicken was so good! Perfectly fried and breaded and so juicy. Wait a second to eat these though because I definitely burned the roof of my mouth. We then ordered the green beans which were served cold in a sesame sauce-very tasty and easy to eat with chopsticks. And then, of course we finished off the night with a range of grilled skewers: shiitake, pork belly, sausage, and chicken meatball. These were so good! We were so satisfied with everything we ordered and the prices were so low. Each item ranged from 3-10 dollars, with the exception of some specialty items that were very expensive. Most of the menu items we ordered were $5. In Nashville, this has become unheard of. We will be returning again soon!

Came in because someone during the NFL draft threw a football advertising this location at us and we happened to walk by. They had a great promotion going on for buy one type of beer get one free and the waitress, Rebecca, was incredibly helpful and upbeat which fit the atmosphere perfect. A definite recommend for this location for drinks and the food is incredibly fair priced as well.
LfbkgbvGdZ7Lm7w6v3oTnQ: I love this European style coffee house. The menu is very expansive and everything sounded so good. We ordered two Turkish coffees. The presentation and coffee was very authentic. I wish I had a coffee house like this near me back home. I definitely recommend stopping by this place for a drink.

Great cafe, incredible breakfast options with the biggest list of coffees and other beverages I've ever seen! Nice sit-down place with good options. We enjoyed items ranging from eggs, Bacon and potatoes (American Breakfast), Belgian Waffles with mixed toppings, and delicious Crepes with fruit compote and whipped cream. Seems like it would fit right in in Europe.

After work I stopped by this new local spot located in the Sobro apartment building.  The decor was warm, intimate, and inviting. The staff was super friendly.  This would be a perfect date spot.  I had the Tiramisu and Carrot cake. Both were spectacular. The manager explained that all their pastries and desserts are made in Atlanta and brought down weekly.  I had a fantastic bourbon cocktail with sparkling wine and meant. Possibly called the Humingbird. This place is an excellent addition to the Sobro scene.
6pmFo4zyeap__Tb1Rj-4ow:
Pleasantly surprised!!!
I'm not really a teriyaki (Yoshinoya..etc) type of person, and I would never actually order teriyaki in Japanese restaurant, but my dad was craving it so we got teriyaki at the Opry Mills Mall.  I was kind of surprised at how much I liked it.

Granted, it was still a bit too salty for my own taste (I generally like things milder), my brother and my dad loved it!!! We ordered Chicken Teriyaki with the chow mein-type noodles, and also a Beef teriyaki with fried rice.  You would expect it to taste exactly the same except with different meats and different carbs... but the beef DOMINATED the chicken by far.  The beef sauce is a little bit sweeter and has a little bit more depth than the chicken sauce did. And it went perfectly with the rice because it soaked up all the sauce, which gave it more flavor.  On top of that, the beef comes with broccoli too!  The noodles ended up just tasting greasy.  The chicken itself was very good.  You can taste that they cook the meat first, then add in sauce, because you can taste the flavor of the sauce, without it being overpowering, and you can still get the crisp of the light char on the chicken. 

Each of the bowls came with cabbage and carrots to balance out the salty---good choice. 

All in all, great find in a mall food court!

While not high end Japanese it is still enjoyable. I have been here a few times since they opened when the mall was reopened. The first few times were great then they got crappy. Well a month ago I decided to give them another shot and it was worth it. We returned today and had the same experience, good noodles, yum yum sauce and I actually enjoy their orange chicken. It is worth it if you are at the mall and can't afford or don't want to go to the chain dine in restaurants, i.e TGIF, Chili's, etc. The food isn't overly greasy and has good seasoning. I usually share a plate with my fiance since I usually don't eat much at these Asian fast food places and it is still enough for both of us. Great value overall.

Maki translates from Japanese as 'true'. Well...Maki's food was nice, but it wasn't really true. It's a fusion cafe. It's sort of Ja-chi-thai-kor-namese and I am not criticizing the food so much as its description. 

The service is typical for mega-mall eateries. It's efficient, but that's it. It is clean, and there is a big bain-marie with fried rice, noodles and Chinese style vegetables. The main part of the meal (in my case beef teriyaki) was cooked on a hot plate while I waited. It was very nice...as were the veggies and the rice. It's just that it paid little heed to fair-dinkum Jappo tucker.

This mall is filled with the usual chain food choices and Maki stood out as being a little different. In short, I enjoyed my meal in the Asian style and recommend Maki for people who like Asian food and are pretty hungry...because the portion was quite large.

Finally, it seems most of the reviews of Maki, which don't give 4 stars, are focused on service. The service, for the style of place was okay, and if you can get past that, I promise you will enjoy the food.

I will go back again.
NXaw6ox4vQkLfQ13sq_oGg: Beautiful, quiet, welcoming place to come have coffee or a cocktail (or both!) and do work or meet friends. I'm glad I stumbled on this place while visiting from out of town!
Yummy yogurt and granola for breakfast.  Good coffee too.  All with a friendly staff.

Fantastic little local spot. Good for coffee/working during the day and great beer / cocktail selection during the evening. Also has a fantastic outdoor area with pingpong, etc. for breaks during your work day.
VvItX6hHEPyOUIGyvrTQCw: Had a great lunch in this new and trendy restaurant!  Just passing through from Atlanta and so glad we stopped!  The wedge salad and the Reuben sandwich were excellent. And David took great care of us. We'll be back!

What a great place! We came here with a large group for a Bachlorette Party and after we sat down, the owner of the restaurant came by with a large dispenser of one of their signature drinks. He told us he wanted to help celebrate and it was on the house! On top of the wonderful service, the food was absolutely AMAZING! Half of us ordered the pesto chicken sandwich and french fries and ate ALL OF IT. It's a great place for large groups and small ones as well. If I ever come back to Nashville, we will have to make a stop here.

This place is AWESOME! It has something for everyone and right in the heart of downtown Nashville. My husband and I first had a drink at the R.E.C Room which is super cool. I had a frose which was amazing. Then we ventured down to the Ribbon Room and it was DELICIOUS! Their bread is made in house is spectacular. The entire meal was fabulous and I highly encourage everyone to go!
BRewtSoPssadaodaIS_Sdw: Giving this place 4 stars for providing a good get away from the country food while staying in Nashville for 5 days. Kimchi fried rice yum, bulgogi delish, scallion pancake not quite a scallion pancake but good.

Food was amazing and moderately price (our group ordered one of almost everything to try it all and We were impressed by every bite.) Staff is super nice and helpful - not being super familiar with Korean food, we obviously had questions and they had answers. Vibe is fun and cool. Will definitely be back! So glad it's in our hood.
The food itself was good.  The serving of kimchi was tiny and you have to order it separately.
NLXDrbuKGW4TZZ4OH_m5hw: Extremely welcoming and kind staff with an awesome atmosphere. The tortillas are out of this world and hand made. Everything we had was delicious- especially the veggie tacos.

Loved this place upon first arrival. Extremely kind and welcoming, greeting us as we walked from our car! Answered all of our questions (we had several about ordering vegetarian options), and were fast with their service. The veggie tacos were great, but the tortillas stole the show! The rice and black beans were both good, and both are vegetarian (the chorro beans are NOT vegetarian).
Our son had the mac and cheese kids meal (comes with fries, which we chose to eat ourselves, and not give to a toddler). As all kids do, he loved the mac and cheese (homemade goodness, not from a box).
Loved the atmosphere and the staff! Looking forward to returning!

Very nice setting, picnic tables under the lights with table service!  The servers very nice and the fajitas burrito was excellent.  Connie liked shrimp-corn-pineapple taco and the chicken taco.  They brought our dog water!
ap5bxhjMs3f4alh7Zu51nQ: This place was awesome! Excellent service and fantastic food. The ambiance was great. Will be back.
Love Superica! Originally from Texas and I am picky about my Tex Mex. This place is great! Love the puffy tacos and traditional tacos. Definitely try this place!

This wAs a fun and yummy restaurant! The food was fabulous and we had a terrific server who knew everything about the menu.  The food came quickly and was served nice and hot!

A parking garage was right there and made the whole experience easier.  In warmer weather there is a really cool patio that would be fun to hang out in too!

Go! Have fun! Not super expensive either!
AKAYI-HTy78i4Tr_n8UEYw: Great food, fast and friendly service and inviting space.  You would never know that it's the first day open.  I will return soon and often!

Went Friday if opening week for lunch. Line out the door and about an hour long wait for food - which MGMT prepared me for so it was cool. 

Told the cashier to order me her 3 favorite tacos. Tortillas were AMAZING. Fillings were unique but not pretentious. The one with poblanos was a stand out. Rad vibe. Can't wait to get back and try the margaritas and breakfast burrito. And fingers crossed for a dope Mexican lager selection .

Also ... to the dude who reviewed them 2 stars based on waiting an hour for food ok their second day open: go to Taco Bell ya schmuck.

Stopped in here on my way out of town. A huge fan of the breakfast tacos. I got the #1 and # 2. The tortillas are some of the best I have ever had. Service was quick and friendly. I already wish I would have gotten a few more tacos to take with me for the drive back home. Yum!
vQzYGIeT53iQnUJi0H4amw: Jerry's is AMAZING. Excellent, always fresh, made in house ingredients. It's a gem in the middle of east Nashville. Gyros, chicken wraps, and they'll even top your fries w gyro meat and cheese for only an extra $1! Jerry is always incredibly fast and friendly as well. Highly recommend.
Eclectic beer selection & random snacks. I LOVE it!! A goto when I need a six pack and some toys to go.
CTYMtDTGxMRFpgSfcuAFHA: Everything is delicious. I am not vegan or vegetarian, but all their food & drinks are absolutely delicious. I'm so excited to have Sunflower Bakehouse as a new addition to the Donelson community. It was much needed.

So cute, and I love that the manager jumped on the register to take my order, while the girl in front of me had every question possible but it was just the two of us in line. Customer service!!! A gem in Donelson!

Already loved Sunflower Cafe but when so when this place opened I knew that is was going to be the bomb.com. This place is all vegan but even for non vegans that I have brought here they have enjoyed the food as well. There is a difference between the Sunflower Cafe and the bakery. The cafe is more a "meat" and three but the bakery has separate menu items. My favorite is the pizza rolls and the sausage sandwich. The desserts are my favorite as well some of the best cupcakes you will ever have with the perfect amount of cake and icing. I will definitely recommend everyone try this place. 1ByZRHC-Vg2Z9-QAxxbwcA: Get the damn Banh Mi sandwich and thank me later. They ran out of the baguettes at the end of the night and I promise you, the wrap version is 100!!!! Best late night spot in Nashville.
Killer burgers in a not so obvious location. Do NOT skip out on this joint, especially if you need late night fuel
Their cheeseburger is quite possibly made of crack. It's so good. We had something else that I don't remember, with avocado in a flour tortilla, it was just ok. But come for this cheeseburger.
MeQSPeGqXGa0HvweoRJzSw: Prices are quite steep for fresh seafood at this place but I guess it's a premium you pay to ship the seafood in landlocked state. We ordered clams and shrimp with Cajun and all in mix. The flavors were bold and not oily but on the sweet end. Service was great by Libby. If you plan on dining here get on the yelp waitlist ahead of time.

This is a new restaurant. Grand opening was June 2nd ( I think). This restaurant will be around for years to come. First, let me say that the garlic aroma in the Restaurant is an automatic winner for me. The hostess was pleasant and the server Carlos was amazing. Love this place.  Great oysters, shell fish and spices!!!  It's one of my fav little places but it's getting popular fast, so get in while you can fit in!!

If you hurry you can possibly catch them before they get their liquor license and BYOB!!!  We brought in a full cooler.
HadZS0pCsKtBt1DsnUPn6g: This is my favorite food truck and catering group in town. Adam and Crystal put together creative, delicious, southern-inspired food that has enough variety to keep you wanting more and more. A must- have in Nashville! Find their truck!
great contemporary southern food. comfort food but not greasy - standouts were the fried okra and dessert pies.

We arrived in Nashville, shipped and finally made it up to the Broadway District for some live music,  plenty of people on the street and a full city block of food trucks. One you don't want to pass up is Southern Spoon. We tried every one of their small plates (Appitizers) which consisted of fried pickled okra, sweet potato wontons, southern egg roll and a black eyed pea salad, We also had their North Carolina BBQ sandwich and their banana pudding beneigts, all of which were so flavorful, freshly made right in front of you and delicious you will be full and happy! Great, amazingly wonderful food truck meal!!! You are one lucky town Nashville!
WXxlJiG1QIae0Dasbf-fzA:
Regular McDonald's food. It is located within 2 minutes of Nashville International airport.

+1 for cleanliness, spacious seating area. Wi-fi very reliable. Funny incident - by the restroom, there was a guy sleeping on his laptop. Apparently he was working and decided it was nap time.

Drove to this place because it was the only drive thru open at 4am in the morning. The employees were surprisingly nice & were on top of their game! I highly recommend this place! I ordered a Big Mac meal & it was delicious! I rarely go to a McDonald's but I'm glad it didn't turn out awful! Lol
They really come in clutch with their coffees. They get my order right every time. I LOVE IT
3yFcFud_1b6l8w1CNQfcew: Slim Collins cocktail!  Cauliflower nuggets and bacon wrapped dates were a great snack.  Clean, up to-date atmosphere and great live music.  We went 2x in our first day visiting Nashville because of the laid back atmosphere and bc our 14yo could "get in" to listen to music.

Wonderful experience!!!  We had Jimmy as our waiter, he was very nice and attentive!!  We met the manager Jake upon our arrival and we were very impressed with him as well!!  Portions of food were good size and the food was good quality!!  Will return again when we visit a Nashville again.  A nice change from the bar food in alot of other places.

This place has everything! Cool vibe, amazing/eloquent drinks, and bomb food. I got the Mass Appeal, and although I was skeptical about the "egg whites," it takes this drink to another level. The Reuben was flavorful, and perfectly toasted. And the waiters/waitresses were so attentive. This place is definitely worth a stop on your way down Broadway.
VksUdmHPB7H0JzLLQ63KrA: This is such a fun pop up! The food menu is Hawaiian inspired and delicious, and the vibe is really cool, lively and laid back. I'm glad Babo puts on this event! I loved the wings and the loco moco. They also serve up a killer frozen drink and Kona lager, along w a full bar. Def a good Monday hang. Duddde! You have to try this, it's awesome  Haha for real though. I went in with my husband and 2 friends. We basically ordered the entire menu besides the spam sandwich and ramen... not because we didn't want them, but our stomachs can only handle so much. The rice with pork loin is LEGENDARY! The best pork loin I've had. We loved the fried chicken dish with macaroni salad. The chicken was crispy, juicy, and the sauce had the right amount of spice. Food was SO YUMMY! I wish I could eat is every week and try everything on the menu. Wings were so good I took some home for my boyfriend to try.
HNHPqR1mi2QEwJNNU8IL_g: Store sent the wrong pizza but called me to make it right after I notified them of the issue.

We ordered a small pizza and an order of mozzarella sticks.  The food was delivered quicker than promised and we got what we ordered.

We were told about this place from the front desk.

Great food and quick delivery for sure. Thank you for taking care of me. Eddie, seriously you are the man!! My pizza was hot, poppers were still crispy. You delivered it  with a smile and a great attitude. New lifelong patron!!
yOc3BbR4pua-7El5ce8lHg:
The team did a great job! We were served quickly, and the team members were all very pleasant. The female team member who took our payment chatted with us while our credit card was processing, and the male team member at the window did a great job of verifying our order as he handed it to us. 
This location can be hit or miss with the crew, but today they were awesome!

This location got hit really hard for Fourth of July. The manager did a great job of keeping it together and I really felt for the employees who were struggling because their teammates skipped out on work. Sending lots of love and good vibes to everyone who works at this location you all are great! Sorry for the rough night but you all kick ass!

Just stopped by for sodas. 2 large seltzers and 1 Diet Coke = $2.18. Can't beat that deal. Drive thru was about 5-10 minutes total. No complaints. New insulated cups were cool, like a hybrid styrofoam (not certain how environmentally friendly they are, but my drink stayed cold for over an hour).
kzciod0GIetB9GPdV9nDvw: Chicken pesto ceasar and roast beef and horseradish are my 2 favorite sandwiches here, and I've tried almost their whole menu! This quiznos has one of the most friendliest staffs I've ever met. They are very patient, professional and helpful. Quiznos has better meats than subway and their cookies are also better 

This location is the only one in Nashville and they do a good job with mobile orders

I visited the West End store for the second time in a few months. The customer service was excellent. Everyone there working was helpful and courteous. The food was hot and looked so delicious, I couldn't hardly wait to get home. I have to thank all and especially Adley.

UBCOE-7SXWrg2kPLSuF4YQ: Favorite pizza is the Shroom Pie. Their ranch sauce and hot honey sauce are both incredible. Ranch is one of the best in nashville. The kale Caesar salad wasn't my favorite but wasn't terrible.

Insanely good Nashville Hot Chicken! Probably the best hot chicken I've had in town. Great food and the specials mon through Thursdays are great on Monday is chicken and ribs and a side for ten dollars can't beat it nowhere
Av_XS0ESX4Jpwvg6D2CfVQ:
I love this place, we've been eating here weekly since they've opened and it just keeps getting better. Already my go to spot for a quick bite or after work drink.

The food menu has an Americana diner style mixed with comfort food providing delicious entrees such as a classic patty melt, pot roast, frito-chili-pie and much more!

Cowpoke Burger....amaze balls!!! Best burger I have ever had. Also, a pretty awesome environment and nice staff.

FkwC74Eicxji7cQak3u5RA: Best old fashioned in Nashville& it's smoked so c'mon, that's even better. Top notch bartenders who enjoy the theatrics of a well-made, well-presented cocktail. Been in a few times to this ridiculously cool speakeasy, and let me tell you it's always a blast! Great cocktails, amazing cigar selection and probably the best pizza in Nashville area! Good selection of Bourbons and other alcohol beverages. Staff thoroughly knowledgeable. Great Bourbon selection. Food was good. Setting is quality for a speak easy. Now one would think you reap what you sow when you go into a cigar smoking establishment and to a degree that's true. But having gone into enough of theses smoking establishments this one ranks with the worst in town if not the worst. I arrived home and before my wife was within 10 feet of me she knew I had been to a cigar bar. There are several in Nashville with better smoke and fume mitigation systems than this bar. Will I go back, sure I loved the place, I really want to go, a lot, but only if they decide to do something with the smoke evacuation system.

We were greeted with warm friendly faces and felt right at home as soon as we walked in. Our waitress was terrific and provided wonderful service from start to finish. We had plenty of beverages at all times which you'll need to cool your taste buds from time to time. 

The menu was a bit smaller at this location than the other Gordon's location we saw online, but there are still plenty of entrees to choose from. The chicken is spicy hot as advertised, and if you choose the regular hot chicken the spice heat is tolerable and the chicken has great flavor. I would advise getting a smaller test portion if you decide to try higher degrees of heat on this chicken.

I chose fries as the side, but next time I may choose a more mellow side to counteract the spice on the chicken because the fries also had a bit of spicy seasoning on them. I liked that the entire meal was served piping hot temperature-wise, and we had to sit for a minute to let it cool down. 

I noticed they had a nice craft beer selection which would go great with the hot chicken, and they also had a well-stocked bar for those who just want to relax and have a few drinks. There are several flat screen TV's on for the sports fans, and it looked like there was a good isolated area for large groups and parties. 

I like this restaurant and will try it again sometime soon.

82HewnAO5_FLCDPET6m5KQ: This place is great! The coffee is great and the people are friendly. The prices are also cheaper than other coffee shops.

The best latte I've had in months! I haven't written a review in ages, but Elevate Coffee inspired me to come back and share for several reasons. The bottom line is this: my husband and I had the best lattes we've had all year--and I'm not one to exaggerate. But when I find something good, in addition to good people, I like to share about it. I also love supporting independent businesses that are creating something awesome, and I really want this business to succeed and grow. I have no doubt given the quality of coffee and service that I received that they will do well. 

Here's what you need to know about Elevate Coffee: 

1. The owners chose a great location to serve a community severely lacking in quality coffee and independent coffee shops. (Yes, there are a few Starbucks around, but they're neither convenient for me nor do I think they're very good. Starbucks lost my love and loyalty when they moved away from hand-pulling their shots. It's just never been the same. I always prefer independent coffee shops.) 

2. The owners moved here from the Pacific Northwest--so they know coffee. (I'm also a recent transplant from Seattle and the West Coast, so this was especially awesome from my perspective.) Elevate opened just a month ago, in the middle of a pandemic. Impressive!

3. Elevate is drive-thru only, consisting of a tiny, adorable yellow-and-white striped coffee stand or food trailer--perfect for these Covid times. There are two driveway entrances to their location, and two drive-up windows on either side of the stand. With their yellow-and-white motif, they are like a little ray of sunshine. They've done a lot with very little.

4. Most of their specialty drinks are named for mountain peaks. So far, I've only had their latte--lavender, to be specific. Note: This item is not on their menu, but when I saw that they were serving lavender lemonade, I asked if I could have a lavender latte, and they were happy to oblige. The espresso was so smooth, it did not have the acidity, the bite, the burnt taste, nor the chemical aftertaste that can sometimes happen with either lesser-quality beans or poorly pulled shots. It was my version of perfect, smooth, and full without being overbearing. And although I chose a lavender syrup, it was not overly or sickeningly sweet, which can also kill a good latte. Admittedly, I ordered the lavender because I didn't yet know their level of quality, and sometimes a syrup can help cover a multitude of coffee sins. Next time, I might skip the calories and just try their basic latte, as it has its own level of natural sweetness. (Then again, I love lavender everything and I might have just started a personal addiction.) I'm also curious what kinds of beans they use. I'll have to ask next time. Whatever they are, they appear to be wonderfully sourced and roasted. 

5. Elevate has a loyalty card, and also a system that will text discounts and specials when you sign up. 

6. They also serve non-coffee items along with a few simple breakfast options for something quick and on-the-go, none of which I have tried. 

7. It seems like they've put a good deal of thought into their business, which I really appreciate. I love their name, their logo, their clever names for menu items, their thoughtful location, keeping overhead small and going bigger on product quality and service. Well done.

If you're into good, quality coffee and supporting the indie guys like I am, I recommend trying them out. 

You can see their menu and find out more here: https://www.elevatecoffee.coffee.

Elevate is AWESOME! Service is always fast and friendly, and the coffee is delish! They also offer a rewards program that texts you with coupons often, plus you get a free birthday drink. I recommend the candy pecan latte - it's SO good!
BhwwkrSLzEIkCVH4Tkmh0A: Waiting for table at Barcelona Wine Bar and stopped to get a couple beers. Giant beer menu and great looking burgers. Definitely a fun spot with good music playing

This is quickly becoming a neighborhood spot to frequent for us. We have been a few times since this restaurant opened during COVID, and every time is better than the last.

All employees are wearing masks, tables are spaced appropriately (with plastic dividers between the booths), sanitizer (with googley eyes!) on every table.

The people who work here are just plain kind and fun. We always find ourselves staying longer than anticipated because of the fun local energy of this spot. They have a phenomenal beer selection and the staff always helps me find a great new pick. 

And last but CERTAINLY not least, the food is on point and reasonably priced. Highly recommend the Greg Brady - nothing better than Mac & cheese on your burger! Add cheese fries, finish with fried Oreos - and you might be a little more dense but dang will you be happy after this meal! 

Overall, incredible local spot - Great quality, friendly faces, and doing what they can to keep everyone safe!

I love Jack Brown's and had no idea one had opened in Edgehill.

I love this burger, but the sandwich as a whole leans on the slightly sweeter side thanks to the pepper jelly and the thick slice of cream cheese -- just something to be mindful of it you're not a fan of sweet and savory. It's also spicy but not unbearable. The Waygu beef patty was cooked perfectly, and the soft-yet-sturdy buttered and toasted bun did a great job of holding everything together.

The burger as a whole is on the smaller side compared to other similarly priced "specialty" burgers I've had recently, but it was still filling.

The crinkle-cut sweet potato fries were crispy and tasty, especially when dunked in the JB sauce.

I look forward to returning to explore more of the menu.

Review for concert experience (vs restaurant)

Wifey and I were so impressed with Brooklyn Bowl on their opening night concert - Old Crow Medicine show - with many special guests. 

The vibe inside is amazing - the lighting, stained glass windows, not a bad area in the house to see your favorite venue. They do offer stools to sit on for ADA on main floor if you ask - they will put a seat on the edges for the person requiring (not the entire party). 

The sound was clear and the acoustics are very clean.   Views are great from all angles.   They have a full bar - see photo for prices.  

Special shout out to the employees - Manager Nick was super helpful when assisting us prior to concert starting (we were first in line).  We also had multiple conversations with wait staff who introduced themselves to us and seemed real happy to be there. 

This is an easy 5star - see my tip on parking which is tight in area.

Fun place, great music venue, cool outdoor terrace where you can watch Sounds game ( until rude customers pull up stools and sit down along rail and block everyone view ). Food is Meh... mostly fried or heavy bread pizzas. Eat before going.
7cr-FYscJgV6rDnV7Wantw: Pure decadence! Go for the chocolate, stay for the chocolate! Oh, and they have very good coffee. But the main attraction is chocolate in many forms. They make amazingly delicious hot chocolate, with different options for both the chocolate and the milk. Rich and creamy! The desserts are also incredible. I had a croissant filled with Nutella, and I was in heaven. If you have a sweet craving and are near The Gulch, or if you are simply a chocoholic, you have to check this place out.
i got the seven layer cake from this cute little dessert shop this afternoon and it was delicious! the staff were very friendly and attentive!!

Definitely a must if you are in Nashville. Located in The Gulch area. So much chocolate. My daughter had the waffles and loved them. I actually didn't get chocolate. I had the avocado pretzel roll and it was delicious. We had breakfast but would be great place for dessert or a treat. Coffee good too.
qepgYnobwEyWr4oAfDDhyw: Great new addition to the Nashville farmers market pavilion. Everything they serve is vegan and packed with flavor. We tried a variety of their tacos along with the corn side and enjoyed each bite. Creative preparations and the tacos are street taco size so you can easily try several. You order at the window and then pickup in a few minutes while they prepare everything fresh right in front of your eyes. There's dining tables in the pavilion or you can take them outside and find a picnic table nearby. Really happy they've got a permanent spot now so we can enjoy their tacos anytime!

I will never understand how vegan businesses are able to make soy-based food taste like real meat. SVT has outdone themselves today. You could tell me these tacos were normal al pastor and asada tacos and i would believe you. Soy and seitan have never tasted so good. Easily some of the best tacos I've had. 

My mom and I shared 4 tacos, an horchata, a side of corn in a cup, and a concha/pan dulce for only $24!! Fantastic deal for fantastic food that will fill you up

Very tasty! I'm not vegan but appreciated all the dairy-free options, so enjoyed the horchata and a pastry and a few tacos. I would've loved there to be more toppings on the taco, like sauces or vegetables or just more to give different textures and more complex flavors. But it was an enjoyable experience.
WIqLvIZBcr_sBZQ9mE8irw: I've had two crepes from this food truck and they're really good. I honestly had never heard of savory crepes but I war Pagani surprised. I've had the steak and cheese crepe which was good but in my opinion may need a little more sauce.... but I think that's purely my preference because I O.D on sauce on most things! The tomato pesto crepe was really really good! Funny is that I haven't had a desert crepe because I have the worst(or best haha) sweet tooth, so I guess the Nutella crepe is next on my list. Definitely definitely recommend this. I also like supporting people who seem like nice people, and I got great vibes from these guys. Give them a try!

This was our second experience with the food truck and it was amazing!  We had the steak & cheese as well as the strawberry/banana/Nutella crepes.  This time the wait was a little longer as they were at Arrington Vineyards, but their estimate was on-point and that allows you to not stand around for your order.  I believe they allowed us to pre-order through the food truck site when it was in our neighborhood.  

I'm planning on stalking this truck!

I guaranteed you won't be disappointed. The crepes are fantastic and the customer service was excellent! They were up front about the wait time before I ordered, which I appreciate & it wasn't long at all. I can't wait to indulge  again this Thursday!
u_o-LMqy7Phu7WcHi1ydsw: I had a layover At the Nashville airport  and was looking for a place to eat because I had a 2 hour layover. I got there during breakfast. The place was crowded when  the waitress finally came to my table breakfast was over‍. All I wanted was a chicken and biscuit but because it was switch over time I got a chicken sandwich (400 degrees) this chicken sandwich was great!! It was so hot I could not feel my mouth. I only ate half. When I ate the other half later it was even hotter. What i liked about the sandwich was that the spice can from spices and not necessarily from a sauce. I would definitely visit again.
We split the Buffalo chicken wrap and the loaded tots; delicious! A bit of a wait, but the airport was busy today!  Check this place out, simple menu, but enough for an airport bar.

Rated lower than it should be on Yelp! It must be rated lower by the Popeyes, KFC, and other chain places lovers who have limited taste buds.

Flight Crew recommended, and it was a very good place to sit and enjoy a drink and chicken wings tenders. 

Abeda is a great server and cares for customers.
t2dM6yz400KMu7n263HCew: First of all I am seeing all these negative "reviews" because the owner is expressing his First Amendment right on his sign....however these same "people" would scream if their comments were removed from Yelp....that being said we have always gotten great service and a friendly smile while in the store. We stopped many times while making motorcycle rides. Good parking and plenty of room for MC to park....people trashing this store probably have NEVER been there and are looking for any reason to take down a Conservative business....
This place is amazing! Cheap gas, all the right drinks and snacks, and awesome folks running the place! Definitely recommended!!

I went there the other day and got me the best fried bologna sandwich I've had in a long time! The people are all nice and kind! It was so good I went back the next day and got me a mushroom Swiss burger and ff . It was really good also. So far everything I've ate has been very good!! 
  The inside and out side of the store is great also you can learn about history. Take some pictures and have great memories of a little store out in the country. The inside is so cool if you got a gun buff in the house! My new favorite place to go and eat and just look around!! 
  They have donuts that are made fresh everyday! They have t shirts and hoodie for sale! 
  They have these life like cardboard people that you can stand beside and take a picture with!!
 It's a very cool and unique place to go too!! So good take a friend or take your grandparents ! I really enjoyed this little place.
8VA4_dn8wS7s7mh2upXbdg: We've been big fans of the Germantown location of Steam Boys, so when we were at 5th and Broadway, and noticed that Steam Boys was present in the Assembly Food Hall, we knew we had to try it! We got the xiao long bao (soup dumplings) and boba tea. The food was fresh, delicious and super well-made. Soup dumplings don't always travel well, but these were packaged in special foil containers and then placed into a larger outer container, and served with sliced ginger and vinegar in separate cups, so the experience was ideal -- they were just as fresh as the other locations. I've had a lot of soup dumplings in my day, and these were the best I've had outside of Shanghai (even better than New York, and actually better than some places in Shanghai!). The boba tea was also excellent -- they were able to adjust the sweetness to my liking and my wife's liking as well. A cool bonus about being in the food hall is that we were able to supplement our meal with a couple other special things from other vendors in the mall, although next time, I think I might just get it all from Steam Boys as it was so good!

So happy I ordered from this little Gem on Fifth & Broadway Assembly Hall location. It was my 1st time trying , Staff was super friendly & answered all my questions. We ordered dumplings and a large soup to share to go. I laughed with the staff about my non spicy option but ayyyyeee, my guts are wimpy, what can I say! Lol! 
The portions were a very good size. Order was ready quickly. 
I will definitely be back. Love the great customer service, the vibes & great food!

Place is pretty new and it's located right on front of the entrance to the food court on the right side.

Food - Great tasting food with that authentic taste to it and we'll prepared for all items.

Prices - A lil high but kinda going rate for a specialty place like this.

Service - People that work here are nice and helpful. 

Place is really clean and kept up but then again it's a new place that just opened.

Overall great place worth checking out and would be willing to check it out again.
jJSgDuY9sLzUfZ91_YSCog: Our experience was excellent.  Their spice level is on par with authentic Thai food.  We had fried oysters fir appetizers.  The oysters are 5 star themselves.  They are breaded perfectly and taste equally good. We had beef salad, drunken noodles and pad Kei mao for dishes.   All meet or exceed our taste experience.  The service was spot on as well.  I didn't go without drink.   If you like Thai, this is it.  Great experience!

I love this place!   It's my go to in Nashville.  The service is fun and efficient.  The owner is always there and talking with the guests (love you Nina).  The food is the best.  You can choose a soup, a rice dish, or a noodle dish and you will love it.  It's fresh and spicy.  Thank you Thai Esane  for being our #1 choice in Nashville.  See you soon.

I recently moved to the Nashville area and this place came highly recommended. I have always enjoyed Thai, but as far as authenticity is concerned; in many cases it is hit or miss. Not here! Incredible food, great ambiance, very friendly staff. This place has it all.
