I started out with an order of the calamari. I was told that it's fried to perfection and not rubbery like some places serve. It definitely lived up to its expectations. Following I ordered egg rolls. Fresh cabbage, and a very flaky crust which reminded me of a well made baklava. Both the appetizers came with a sweet and spicy Thai dipping sauce. For my main entree, I ordered the Sausage Esane, and sticky rice. To be honest, I was expecting steamed rice. Nope, sticky sweet rice that you could either eat by hand, or easily pick up with chop-sticks. Very good flavor, incredible consistency too. I realize that some may say, "how can you mess up rice?" Well, generally speaking, it's usually too soft and mushy, or it winds up coming to you crunchy. The rice here was perfect. The entree was well seasoned. Lemon grass and dill. Two of my absolute favorites. The portion was hearty and was garnished with iceberg lettuce and cucumber slices. Also served with a very spicy sauce for dipping. 

My server Chris was very informative. Told me about some of the local favs around the area, as well as recommended a couple places for some more skin art (tattoos). He was very attentive and polite and made my first experience at this establishment a very enjoyable one. I will definitely return and I highly recommend this restaurant to anyone that enjoys the same foods. Check it out if you are in the area.
SORfr76--4UporsAlVrtbw: The food and service is excellent ! Love the crab cakes and blueberry cobbler. Called ahead to order and it was fresh and ready on time. Will eat here again when I'm in town.

So good! I ordered the crab cake sandwich, which came with the side of golden reef chantillí mashed potatoes. They were both really good!! And I got the black sand which was a chocolate avocado mousse with cool whip and crushed Oreos. Divine!! I would definitely go back. Check their pop-up Hours on their website! Thanks for a great meal!

Phenomenal. Excellent customer service (the Duffie twins rock!) and the food was soooo good. Fried fish sliders better than any vegan "seafood" I've had, and that was my favorite cuisine before going vegan. The Chantilly potatoes and desserts (peach cobbler and butterscotch cookie) were out of this world too! Wish I could go back more than once every 2 weeks, but I will definitely be there every time!!
4TsGea1wyy71w-3Uh8G5mQ:
Dinner here tonight 11-19-2021, giving this 5 stars based on the food as my burger was pretty outstanding and came with fries for $15. I did the Sweet Water Valley Farms Cheeseburger cooked med rare, and it was cooked perfect. Is located inside a hotel that was close to ours, we didn't know the location until we pulled in. Return, maybe if in area again.

Pros-
Burger was great and cooked perfect.
Service was okay.
Place was clean.

Cons-
Food came out of kitchen very slow, and this place was not busy at all.
Parking really sucked as this place being inside a hotel, and also the fact it was very cold in the 40s here tonight in Nashville made the longer walk inside somewhat more miserable for us So Cal spoiled warm people.

Had a great lunch with my husband and in-law's. We enjoyed Manhattans, a very tasty guacamole appetizer, and ate cheeseburgers for our entrées. Having lunch at the hotel was super convenient, and the staff was very friendly and accommodating. We're looking forward to ordering the dessert on the menu for our room service later.

My wife and I stopped here for dinner here after a long day of driving. What a relaxing and pleasant night we had! 

We were seated immediately and our server came right over with menus and asked if we wanted a drink. We both had the Makers Mark Old Fashion. It was excellent. I had the cheeseburger and my wife the chicken quesadillas. Both came out promptly and hot. My burger was cooked just right was juicy and flavorful. The quesadillas were sized very generously (I ate the last one) and delicious. 

Not rushed, it was a soothing ending to a tiring day. Go there! You'll like it.
tYZ_yj9rJEDH-BbgKIctXw: Busy on Saturday mornings for good reasons. Food was great, staff was helpful, and the breakfast cocktails are delicious and generous. Would recommended the Elote Hash and the Vodka Kale Tonic.

My roommates and I came here about two weeks ago. First we got our name on the list through the Yelp app, which is always a power move. We waited about 5 minutes to be seated and parking was plenty. I was very disappointed to hear that they did not actually have their watermelon juice, even though it was still on their menu. I was pumped to finally try a juice so that was definitely a bummer. I ended up getting their biscuits with sausage gravy and it was just fine-- not very flavorful at all. This was not my first time at first watch and I know they have better items on the menu, so I would come back. I do think their prices are a bit high for what they serve. I hope to be able to try a seasonal juice next time.

So, since my last review of a First Watch was scathing due to atrocious customer service, I figure it's only right to give a shout out to this First Watch.  We weren't planning on ever going to another First Watch but nothing else was open so early (9:30am) and we were on a schedule.  The level of customer service here was the exact opposite of what we experienced last time.  Friendly greeting, no chaos, quick and attentive service, and wonderful food.  We both had the turkey sausage gravy and biscuit.  The food was served hot.  We didn't feel rushed or stressed.  The Hermitage store could learn a lot from the staff at this location.
PRK5CTAOyidhiICu5fZwdg: Great sushi, fish is really fresh, friendly staff. Ample amount fish in the sushi, which is unusual these days

Decided to try this place for lunch and so far it's amazing!
This is in the old Ihop location and showed up on nashville.eater at some point. It is not in anyway related to the other place by the same name on the other side of town. 
Had the Chengdu Spicy Beef (right? Who even has that on a menu?) and Chicken Satay. Satay was delicious but the Chengdu beef was amazing! Perfect portion size for a lunch as well, and no skimping on the protein. Also the spring roll was great, and no little packet of manufactured sauce, it appears to be made in house and is delicious. 
I can't believe this place hasn't been reviewed yet, but I'll continue to add pics as I eat my way through the menu!
Love this place! Such a great atmosphere with amazing food. The menu is extensive but in the best way, you can't go wrong with anything you choose. Highly recommend!
rYJpJq1LIt-mIVpQ1WSjjA: Such a cute place to stop for a treat with amazingly friendly and helpful staff! Great atmosphere with beautiful paintings. We loved the chocolate covered strawberries and the soursop, cheese, and cheesecake ice creams! Will be back!

OMG this ice cream shop is currently my favorite! The owner is super friendly and will make you try all the ice cream! What got me was the "popsicle" it was strawberry with chili lime mix and chamoy. Man the sweet and salty spicy mix blew my taste buds away! I also had the mangonalada which comes with fresh mangoes, chamoy, mango ice cream and chili lime mix it was DELICIOUS! The ice cream is as good too the gansito has chunks of gansito in it and it make every bit the best! The oreo mint and ferro was good as well not too minty. 

Gansito is a snack cake with strawberry and chocolate similar to little debbie.
Chamoy is a condiment from Mexico that is made out of fermented fruits.
Hands down one of my favorite ice cream shops in all of Nashville, I've actually been to quite a few similar spots around the states and I still enjoy The Bee Ice Cream Shop best!
4HE6POeYQtB_k6ziyRoqlg: Literally never had a bad meal here! I love the fried green tomato BLT and the chess pie. Great for breakfast, too!

We've been driving by this place's packed parking lot for the past few weeks and finally decided to see what the fuss is about. We went for breakfast. It's great southern food. Everything was delicious, inside is cute and service was good. Will definitely be going back.

The Nashville Jam Café is a true breakfast gem. Everything about this place is charming. The cafe is situated in a house converted into a restaurant in a mostly residential area. We received fresh coffee as we were being seated, and one of the staff members (possibly the owner) described the specials. Our food was absolutely delicious. We ordered a biscuit basket complete with jam samples, the hummingbird pancake--a short stack of pancakes with bananas, a cheesecake batter, and whipped cream, and an omelette with grits. The biscuits are incredible--seriously don't pass these up! They were fluffy and buttery. The grits are made with gouda cheese and are quite possibly the best grits I've ever had. The pancake was equally delicious--definitely a huge portion so be prepared to share or have some leftovers. The owner and staff members were all very warm, and we had the pleasure of chatting a bit with the owner as well which provided such a homey, welcoming feel. I highly recommend the Nashville Jam Café for a hearty breakfast and experience.
GXFMD0Z4jEVZBCsbPf4CTQ:
The hype is real: Hattie B's hot chicken has the wonderfully addictive quality of good spicy food where it hurts a little bit, but you just want to keep eating it. (Heat level: Hot; probably would've enjoyed the Damn Hot though) It's very affordably priced, and the service was really good, with the cashier offering me a sample of one of the many local beers on tap.

I wasn't that impressed with the sides--greens, beans, mac & cheese--and there are just so many places to get great versions of these dishes in the world. Or make them yourself, or get invited to someone's house. But, as I mentioned, very affordable. Next time, I'd try the black eyed peas (they were sold out during my visit, though many would say they sold out when Fergie joined)

Also, I'd probably get the tenders. I know those aren't real fried chicken, but you can easily eat the whole thing, and absent boneless skinless thighs, which make the best fried chicken, they're a worthy fallback. 

Wait time was about 45 minutes on a Monday night. But if you're in Nashville it's just something you have to do.

We waited more than an hour. But it was worth it! The hot chicken was probably the best fried chicken I've ever had.  The side dishes are not impressive but good enough. I had the breast wing combo with baked beans and coleslaw. I suggest ordering online and pick up instead of waiting on line.  But eat the chicken quickly so that it doesn't get soggy.

Eating at Hattie B's was one of the best decisions I made since moving to Nashville. I have now had both the jumbo tenders and the dark meat platters and both were so juicy and crispy with the perfect amount of spice. I ordered the "hot" both times just to be safe! Spicy but not uncomfortable.

The sides were amazing as well. Pimento Mac 'n Cheese, potato salad, and cajun french fries- I have zero complaints. 

On top of the phenomenal food, the service was great! Friendly people taking orders always makes you want to come back for more!

There is a pay lot for parking right around the back but there is limited seating so be prepared to wait in line.
qdUx7wxwnT80MkaqGCTfSQ: Such a great place. The only knock is how busy this place can get but that's only bad for me. The food was great and service was better. If you are near Nashville this is a must stop and I hope for you the wait isn't to long.

Since leaving Philadelphia for the Bootheel of Missouri, I haven't eaten at a good deli until now.  Noshville satisfied my craving for a good deli with a nice menu including sweet & sour cabbage soup, whitefish, lox, knish, blintzes, etc.  The potato and onion knish was amazing.  Came back and took some whitefish salad home to Missouri.  Great place for breakfast!  Hubby enjoyed his corned beef on rye sandwich and the cole slaw so the sandwiches are good too.  If you're looking for a breakfast joint in Nashville you can't go wrong with with Noshville!

This was a great choice for breakfast this morning. I will caution that the wait is pretty bad on a weekend morning, at least with our larger group, but it ended up being worth the wait.

Their coffee was fresh and of better than average quality. The food selection was good, a combination of deli and breakfast greasy spoon, and the food came fast and hot. I can see why they had such a wait!

I had a matzoh ball soup and a tongue sandwich (who knew they made those anymore?!) and both were truly grand. Everyone else at the table enjoyed their food, as well, and that can be tough with multiple generations.

I am looking forward to making this a regular haunt.
OZi0fOn8-ISUwhEAWC_8Gg:
Amazing experience! This is a wonderful addition to not only East Nashville but the city in general. 

The menu has just the right selection so everyone is satisfied. Ramen, skewers, appetizers, and salads. There are even a few sushi rolls if you're not feeling like noodles. My friend and I sat at the bar while waiting for the rest of our group to arrive and had a couple of cocktails: ginger/pineapple tonic and Dutch on the Run. The tonic was as good as you'd expect, but Dutch was spectacular. The bartenders were very knowledgable and also gave us and other people at the bar samples! I'm okay with that.

Most of us ordered the ramen bowls, which will definitely fill up anyone. My friend ordered the crispy Brussels sprouts, and let me just say: our drinks were great, so was dinner, even the dessert we sampled, but the BRUSSELS SPROUTS, GET OUT OF TOWN. They were that good.

I will definitely be coming back here. The space is so cozy and the Japanese tavern style is unique for Nashville. It's been a while where I've been so wowed over a new restaurant.

Disclaimer: they have a policy that does not allow you to take leftover ramen home.
Great atmosphere with even better food and cocktails. It was a local recommendation and well worth it! Try the Japanese Fried Chicken and the Takoyaki and you won't be disappointed!

I love a good ramen and we found this one through YELP.  I tried the tori paitan shoyu which is a chicken dish. It was delish.  Everything was balanced throughout meal. The only drawback is that is was quite crowded and it was an hour wait.
uMyf6tfLnTSpi2LdB-R84g:
Went here for brunch with my boyfriend, I got the Caramel French Toast Casserole and he got the Cure. Both were great, although the Cure was a ridiculously messy sandwich. My French toast casserole was sweet but not too sweet, and the potatoes that came on the side were decent.

The service was solid, and we had no wait. I wouldn't recommend eating on the patio though with all the construction going on outside.

Bloody Mary Bar on Sunday's and Bottomless Mimosas! We stumbled upon this place and I'm glad we did! Everything on the menu looked delicious and they have a variety of food, a little bit for everyone. Great service too!

Ok, I quickly have become a fan of this place for happy hour. The service has been good except for my 3rd visit. The shepherds pie is amazing, the only one that I have seen in town with lamb actually in it. Their scotch eggs leave a little be desired but the beer selection and TV's make up for it. Coolest thing about this place is their pour your own beer wall which features locals brews! Highly reccomend to bring some friends here to hang out. Next goal is to swing by during a soccer game.
CBo-YlPMKs3vdYJQRtuznQ: This is a family style restaurant. Some people in my group weren't too fond of passing food around and others thought it was fabulous to get to taste and try many different things. The food was good nothing amazing, new nor outstanding. Just typical southern home cooking. I don't have anything bad to say about it. I thought it was nice they seat you sometimes for e.g. If you are a small party hey sit you with other guest you don't know. We mingled and got to me some nice ladies and again others weren't too fond of it all depends on your preference.

Sometimes the experience overwhelms the food at a restaurant. Such is the case at Monell's.

Don't get me wrong, the food is pretty darn good. But you go here to experience food in the south. The setting is fantastic. The atmosphere is excellent. The family-style dining is unique. The house is amazing. The service is good. 

And the food is pure south. It's not epic, but it's good. And in abundance. 

If you want to "experience" southern food, eat here.
Great family style environment with amazing food. If you're in Nashville, this is a must!
2OXCAbz3x_MrQSYj1zarIA:
Loved breakfast here! The menu is vast and has so many options- and the cocktail menu had some great new drinks I've never seen before. 

I had the whole wheat waffle with the jalepeno syrup and berries. It was a perfect balance of spicy and sweet for breakfast. Also had a spicy Bloody Mary that was a great addition. 

I really enjoyed the decor here- we checked out the bowling alley which was a fun idea for a restaurant! I would love to come back here in the summer and check out the outdoor space!

One of my favorite spots for any time of day! Great place to work with bottomless coffee, awesome location for grabbing a bite before an event downtown, and cocktails can't be beat. Definitely recommend the fried broccoli and asking the bartenders for their drink recommendations - they always make something to be my new favorite! Reservations are needed for any group on a weekend, but during the week they do a good job of getting walk ins seated.
Beautiful place and great atmosphere.  An escape from the loudness of Broadway.  The drinks were made by highly talented bar tenders.  John Taffer would be proud, lol. I enjoyed my drinks at the bar.
_RvXPNxWcRw833a8B3vSOQ: They were very busy but quick. Mac n cheese were great and the pulled pork nice and moist. Great variety of sauces.
The brisket was amazing, perfectly barked and still juicy. The "cold slaw" and green beans were delicious and fresh. I didn't have the Mac-n-cheese, but it looked tasty.

This is a fantastic BBQ place! Visiting Nashville for the first time and came here after a tour of the Grand Ole Opry. This is a BBQ place where you walk up and get the order Got the pulled chicken sandwich extra large combo with green beans and fries. When I got my order they had 6 different sauces at the table and at the counter they had Georgia mustard, hot chicken sauce, and Alabama white sauce. My favorite sauce was the Memphis smoked sauce. The chicken was great, the fries were well seasoned, and the green beans were good. Only thing I wish I did was get the pulled chicken and not the sandwich because the bread masked the sauce. Other than that def a place to visit. It is a chain and I hope they open one up in New England real soon! The best thing about this place is the honor all branches of the military. At noon everyday they will sing the National Anthem. What a great way to honor America's heroes'!
quk6TFDQyuQ4g0KuIb9qUA: Pretty good vodka pizza. Good flavor and good thin crust. I like that they give you a container of fresh basil to carry out! Only thing I would change Is more sauce. So ask for extra sauce as there is very little.

my favorite pizza joint in nashville!  it's affordable. i get a gigantic slice of pepperoni pizza with extra banana peppers on the side every time i go there.  and it costs less than $4.00!  the crust is thin and crunchy, the toppings are generous, and the sauce is just right.
The pizza was outstanding! Always a chance to not get what you expect when someone says NY thin crust. Not the case here and garlic knots a great choice as well.
xBPg6gzunjpEUT6FiRrpog: Wish I would have found Big Al when they first opened. I work in the area and happen to see the location on Google Maps. Ordered the baked BBQ raspberry white chicken with mashed & corn. The BBQ sauce was sweet & spicy. Very tasty. I was surprised by the weight of my to go box. Then I saw the large portions and was very satisfied. $12 with drink was a fair price for the amount of great food.

Big Al and his crew, Mike, A. J. and his beautiful, Vanderbilt-student, niece serve up great food and a fantastic experience from their modest restaurant in a Germantown neighborhood. We came for breakfast and left with enough fantastic food to cover lunch and dinner. In addition, we had big smiles on our faces because Al is bigger than life as he works relationships with his customers who all become his friends. There are as many hugs as meals served in this wonderful place. It is unlike any other we've been. We felt like we were part of a special family, a special place.

Make sure you come hungry because Al doesn't skimp on the portions of his food or his love. 

Don't miss Big Al's for the food or the experience. We loved sitting at the counter for the best interaction with Al. 

P. S. Single ladies, Al is available and you couldn't  find a sweeter man.

Just had one of the most wonderful experiences in recent memory at Big Al's. We were greeted warmly by Al himself and he chatted with us periodically throughout our breakfast. 

If we lived in Nashville, my boyfriend and I agreed we'd be there all the time, even just to enjoy a cup of coffee and the company. 

I haven't even mentioned the food yet, which was amazing. Best biscuits and gravy I've ever had--different than most, with a kick from extra black pepper. My boyfriend's hash browns, prepared fresh by Al, were fried in bacon grease (?). Enough said.

In short, this place was a perfect way to cap off a great visit to Nashville. We will most definitely be back!
-ejuzGbu3ZHUndhLTQafJg: Nashville has so much to offer but for Mexican this is the place to go! The food was beyond delicious, I had the street corn and the tacos cosineros, (i think that what they were called the chicken one and the carnitas) so tasty! The drinks were also very good. As of the food and the drinks weren't enough to impress me the service was impeccable. I look forward to visiting again when I return to Nashville.
Delicious brunch, great ambience, and speedy service! We had the breakfast burrito and huevos rancheros, totally recommend. Can't wait to return.

Service was prompt and waitress made several great suggestions. The Mexican street corn is delicious! The Korean Barbacoa tacos are quite possibly the most delicious taco I have ever eaten. Will definitely be returning!
vZMCdePJn_tX_gFyMW9YUw:
Awesome, very unique drink and beer menu. Lots of
Space and super cool vibe. Can seat large parties and huge bar. Had the chicken app and it was incredible. Wonderful addition to Nashville's food scene! A must try!!!

Kuchnia & Keller was an absolute delight. The interior has a modern, open feel without being pretentious. They offer bar seating, standard tables, as well as large community-style tables. 

We started with the smoked white fish appetizer, as well as a few cocktails. The drinks were well mixed, with an eye for detail.  Our entrees were equally tasty. We also sampled the layered Devil's food cake with pretzel and corn flake crunch which was delicious. Service was incredible throughout.

I will definitely be back to further sample the unique menu!

My husband and I ate here for the first time tonight and really enjoyed everything the restaurant had to offer! We tried two appetizers, shared an entrée then ended with a delicious dessert. We sat at the kitchen bar and loved watching the chefs prepare all the yummy food! Plus we got to decide what we will have next time. This place has it all, great management, staff, ambience and delicious food, simple but full of flavor!
Bcb36UXi2iy2s0xY1kkFzw: The best service I've ever had!!! Not only was the food phenomenal, the bartenders made the experience one to come back for. Jenna was a sweetheart, Kait was a delight and Jason and Josh were absolutely hilarious. Whether it was busy or not I never had to wait for a thing. You won't find better bartenders anywhere. The frisky frog definitely lucked out with them

Giving this place five stars based on my experience the past couple of Thursday nights.   First of all, this place is much cleaner, newer, and fresher
than any of the other bars on the street.  The stage and lighting are phenomenal, and the band, Smashing Pilots is OUTSTANDING!  Geared up in grunge, these guys rocked the roof off the place.  I couldn't believe my eyes or ears... all 90s rock!  Unlike anything else in Nashville, definitely a MUST SEE.

Went on a Friday evening for happy hour and really enjoyed the atmosphere: nice, clean, and safe. They played a variety of music which was cool. I got the buffalo wings, well seasoned but not crispy. My friend had the catfish and fried shrimp. I tasted a shrimp and it was really good, well seasoned, and jumbo size. I'll be back because you can't beat a bar that offers $3wells with good food.
Jzb2IQSwPfULWzdl9Bq5fg: The place to go if you're wanting to try some quality, Southern comfort food. The incredibly friendly staff helps add to the experience.

Great, family-style country cooking. Biscuits on par with that other place way out in the country... but all the sides are much better. Go for brunch and pig out on the fried chicken and cobbler. Love this place!!

Came here on the a Wednesday on the way to Bonnaroo in 2014 and this place was phenomenal.  They seat you Thanksgiving style around long tables, and just start bring dishes out.  The other family seated at the same table was really nice and it was a nice change of pace to sit with people we didn't know, it was very genuine and added a special touch to the atmosphere.  The food was southern cooking at its finest, complete with some of the best fried chicken I've ever had.  So, next time you are in Nashville, do yourself a favor and go to Monell's.
F23zFNnWhIjh3EPx2vr-pw: O. M. Geeeeee this place is amazing. Have you ever had guacamole with honey and Spanish almonds?! Yeah me neither until we ate here. I recommend the fish tacos, refríes black beans and the roasted chicken... all delicious

I tended to like this place more than the other reviews. The staff was very nice and I got a spicy pork taco which was great with rice and a side salad. We also got nachos which were some of the best I've had in Nashville. Maybe a bit pricey, but I'd come back.

I am naturally drawn to any establishment where I can walk from my home and get an amazing margarita... or two. The weather tonight created the perfect atmosphere for queso, patio sunshine and a great time with friends. My rating for the margaritas? 5 stars. I would pay a dollar more for a grande mariner margarita any day of the week. The queso was surprisingly unique when paired with spiced mushrooms and potatoes for dipping. The fish taco was great and my chicken taco was not outstanding. Cons I can foresee are limited indoor seating/no AC. This new spot will flourish during the summer, but might run into limitations during the winter months. I will always supposed new places to eat in West Nashville and I am looking forward to supporting this restaurant for my taco needs in the future!
Sf_hAk2T3_3-sgpF0gl2lw: Delicious sandwiches and salads.  What sets them apart is homemade dressings, condiments, side salads and even pickles.  You need to go!

Great food! Great customer service from friendly staff! Always quick service, too! 
I order corned beef and Swiss which comes with their hot chow. So delicious! I have had many sandwiches and salads and all are awesome!

We had the roasted chicken/provolone and the roast beef/gouda. Also tried all 3 truffles  (smores was the best of the 3) and a peanut butter bacon brownie which was ok but not something I would really crave. We thought the sandwiches were very good but a little pricey for what you get. $8 per sandwich. That's it. Side and drink are $2 more. No kids menu but they were happy to accomodate. Still $8 though...with no side. I would probably go back just because I'd like to try another sandwich but definitely won't be for a family weeknight meal! Also, all menu items can be made gluten free, per the menu.
01Vuw9VgqJVIwThiGg3sRA: Was not dissatisfied one bit! Great atmosphere nice patio and the Italian and pork bauimi were fabulous! When we were waiting in line we saw and smelled the grilled vegetables and had to add the veggie sandwich in. It was just ok, they could lose the mayo and maybe add a balsamic vinegar which may have gave it the pop it needed. I'll still give them a 5 overall!
We had the Italian sandwich. It was very good. We also had the bacon potato salad; that was excellent, and we had two different types of beers that were very good. You should give this place a try.

Upgrade!!! 

The new location is so spacious! This was a good move for Mitchell's Deli. Sure I miss the quaint community courtyard but they had to move; they had grown out of the previous location. To help me forget about the courtyard they added some thirst-quenching high-grav beers. 

We recently went for the SLEW fried chicken pop-up. So good. So, so good. Also greasy, I can't not mention that. However, that isn't Mitchell's fault, they were just loaning the space. 

When you pull up the first thing you notice, or at least the first thing I noticed was how they transformed the old pillow, furniture store. It's a completely different building. The built out the front to add plenty of seating. As a potential nod to the old location they installed an island with bar seats. Because of how large the new location is there was plenty of room between the bar seats and the counter. People are able to form a line without feeling too crowded. The market feeling is less noticeable in the new location and it doesn't feel as quaint. Do with that as you will. 

I feel like the biggest change for me is that Mitchell's Deli was once a breakfast or lunch stop. Now it will be a dinner stop as well. With the beer and larger dining area I will be more inclined to stop in with some friends so a low-key night full of delicious sandwiches. If you haven't been up to see the new digs I highly suggest that you add it to your list.
wL_5DIYA06jXzqV3FMhfBg:
There are very few places where you can get  good quality food in a nice relaxing ambiance and your wallet still feels quite heavy! Coco's is that kinda place. The food is simple Italian food, the difference is the quality of ingredients and simple cooking approach. 

Unlike some other italian restaurants, the pasta, the sauce, the meat all seem fresh and quite light. Cheese is not the overpowering taste ( true for Finezza as well) here. 

If you want Italian food , this is the place to go in Nashville.

Half priced bottles of wine on Wednesday! Food always tastes great, servers are very attentive. When you walk in you feel like you're in Italy. Careful when walking around the floor is very uneven. Adds so much character to the place.

Coco's is one of the few spots in Nashville that I frequent on a regularly basis. The ambiance is awesome and the newly renovated outdoor eating area is fantastic. It has great prices and I love specials. I normally try to stop in for Pie and Pint night Tuesdays or half price bottle of wine Wednesdays. I've had 4-5 pizzas and 6-7 other entrees/sandwiches and everything has been wonderful. They also have Groupon and LivingSocial deals fairly often. The only thing that takes down a star is the service. Don't try and go when you are rushed for dinner!
6FX09pOrazvGT9s6_nQ7CQ: Amazing. Great choices for all picky eaters/foodies. Fresh food, GREAT coffee. Hope to go back soon!

Amazing coffee, I've never enjoyed a coffee with banana flavor but this place changed my mind. Slower service but this place is very popular with locals, the place was packed on a Monday morning wth people working on their computers.
Great rustic downtown location, very busy and slightly disorganized, really nice food, but some arrived cold. Still, I'd try it again, you can see what they are trying to do and I like it.
pkoIykVt8DkhGg29fwXFwQ: They are just amazing! Customer service is exceptional and food is really oozing with deliciousness!
Wings are amazing. I got the hot and the honey hot. Not only the amazing food, pop has the best customer service.

I usually do not go out of my way to write reviews, but this place deserves it. Food is delicious, fresh, and I've always been impressed the service.Not to mention everything is seasoned with skill.

I'd suggest the honey gold jumbo wings. 25 wing combo comes with wings fries and two drinks for like 30 bucks.

Only downside is they really don't deliver. Which I don't get because delivery drivers are so cheap to carry. Anyway, this place is so good I drive myself - imagine that...
mU-Hac9udz0s7pAIQ1L5BA:
Best lambchops in Nashville. Fantastic Turkish dining just feet away from Target. We got the lamb chops and the lamb stew, and both were fantastic. The chops are flavorful and tender at any heat (I got well done and a friend got medium)! Meals comes with six chops, rice, yogurt, and salad. The lamb stew was also great--hearty, subtle spices, tender meat and green beans. Great customer service! We had a fantastic meal here and everyone should try out this Nashville gem.


Update: We had the kunefa for dessert. Save room for it! We ate it too quickly to get a picture.
We loved everything we had at this place! Service was great, and the food was excellent! We definitely plan to go back soon!

I visited on a Sunday for lunch and I was very pleased.  The restaurant is located in a retail center that has seen it's day, but is certainly safe. Frankly, while eating here I felt as if this restaurant would really do well on West End / Vandy area - it's hidden.

The staff was helpful and "seemed" native to the cuisine. The decor tries to do it's best to overcome the exterior surroundings - but no matter - the food is really tasty.  

I was greeted promptly and warmly to this early lunch with a basket of warm home made bread and olive oil mixed with spices.  The Sunday crowd had yet to arrive.

I ordered the hummus as an appetizer and out came a very creamy fresh nutty rendition drizzled lightly with oil and dusted lightly with paprika.  Accompanying was hot grilled pita bread. Nice start....

Waiter recommend mixed grill plate and it was very good. You can tell it wasn't cooked on a griddle or the like. The lamb kabob was perfectly medium and te chicken was cooked well - not special. There was one mini lamb chop that was good and the ground lamb patty (forgot name) was very tasty.  The dish was served with a side of white rice cooked perfectly aldente.  

Also, included an uninspired veggie kabob.   It would have been nice to have a very small salad of greens on the plate.

I will give this restaurant another try.... this place didn't blow me away by any means but maybe it was because I went on a Sunday for lunch.  There were only about 4 tables full.
z3jfgtlDBww3jTPLosPeew: This is, hands down, my favorite restaurant in Nashville. The staff is friendly and helpful (they'll even give quick tutorials on how to pronounce the names of some of the dishes if you ask politely), and they have on multiple occasions suggested something new for me to try. I have never had a bad experience!!
Good food - I had the bulgogi which was great, but the beef bulgogi is better than the pork bulgogi

There is a reason why this Korean restaurant in Nashville has been voted the Best Korean Restaurant in Nashville for 6 years in a row!
My suggestion:
Get the Jjampong.  It is a spicy seafood noodle soup that will knock your socks off, and also clear those sinuses when you have a cold.  It will definitely get you warm and fuzzy on a cold day and the Banshan (side items) that come with this will definitely fill you up! 

On a different occassion, I have tried the Dolsap bibim bap (Mixed rice with fried egg on top served in a stone bowl).  This will arrive at your table sizzling, and you'll receive a red bottle filled with Kochijang (hot red pepper taste) to add to your liking.  BE CAREFUL!  This bowl is hot, and the food will stay very warm as you mix it all up...I had trouble taking big bites of this dish because the iron bowl was keeping it very nice and warm!

If you haven't (or have), experienced most of the Korean restaurants in Nashville, definitely try this one out.  I have yet to try any of the other food on the menu, but I will update once I have tried the different dishes.  

It's quite a drive from where I live, but I will definitely take the time to go out there just to experience the tasty food.
cBHzDxKCsosdDI4UR3w-eg: The Family Was had a great run on Greenwood Ave. It really was East Nashville's version of the Cheers bar where everybody knows everybody. It makes me sad to drive past this shuttered location, but I look forward to the Wash reopening in their new location later this year. It will never be the same, but perhaps it will still be great.

I take visitors to Nashville here...  
The shephard's pie + Guinness = ultimate josh comfort food.  
I usually come here promptly @ 6:00 when they open with my 2 1/2 year old.  They are great with kids too!

I have to preface my review with the fact that I only have been to The Family Wash to see the Gypsy Hombres perform.  They are amazing!

Now for the review of the establishment itself - yes, the Veggie Shepherd's Pie is as good as everyone says it is!  I have had it with the roasted garlic too which is excellent.  One time I had it, whoever made it was a bit heavy-handed with the rosemary, but other than that it has been delicious every time.

About the other food - okey dokey the Gorgonzola Chips (sans bacon) are so yummy!  Perfect snack with a glass of wine!  The hummus w/ tapenade & baked brie are also delicious.  The Roasted Beet Salad is good, as is the Roasted Half Chicken, the Baked Macaroni & Cheese (you'll be in a food coma afterwards!), and of course the Veggie Shepherd's Pie!  I don't eat red meat but hear the regular pie is diggity as well.

The wine list is pretty good - very affordable options.  My husband is a beer drinker & he likes the selections.  I also like the kitschy decor, and the funky atmosphere.  I'd highly suggest making a resservation, to get a good seat for a show.  They tend to fill up by 7ish.  Also - they do not have a full bar - beer & wine only. 

So, the Gypsy Hombres play Friday November 26th at 8....have you made your reservation yet?
wGPFrEM29qcgwSZVRP4XqQ:
Deserving of the five stars everyone is getting.  They have a tremendous selection of craft beers (canned or bottle) and four draft selections.  We are calf killer brewery fans so we're pleased they had this. 

Sandwiches are great and of good portion.  Really loved the pastrami and Swiss (#8)

We will be back!
Owners are fantastic! The number 6 is delicious! Must try! Definitely worth anyone wanting a great beer and sandwich!

I don't want share the secret but  this place rocks! Drove by a couple times and then I checked my Yelp to see good reviews. I found the spot after a couple of passes as I was a little tired from working overnight, but I would not be denied nor disappointed for my effort. I was able to navigate the entrance to stumble my way to the counter and ordered the number 3 which was excellent. I being the northeast sandwich snob was delighted by the quality of my sandwich which consisted of turkey, roast beef, salami, cheese lettuce tomato. All  items on the sandwich were fresh and great care was given it's in preparation. I will spread the word and encourage the rest of you to hit this spot as you will not be disappointed!
OJEiB_a2I_gxC-ZeayI94A: Wasn't sure going in whether or not i would like this place,but it was very good. My girlfriend pushed me out of my comfort zone on breakfast. I usually eat normal breakfast ie eggs, bacon or sausage,biscuits etc.. but i decided on the Crabcakes Cavallo after eyeing the menu back and front a few times. Best breakfast decision i've made in a while. The ONLY place I would rate above this is The Ruby Slipper in New Orleans, but just barely and it may have been because i was really hungry in NOLA, and it was a new place to me, however if it was on a scale rating the Ruby Slipper would be a 10 and Another Broken Egg Cafe would be a 9.99. We will definitely be back!!
Super yummy! Servers were so sweet and great, definitely recommend for a little breakfast to get your day started!

Eaten there many times the Benedict specials are amazing. Today I got to-go just a burger and fries but the to go experience was amazing. The manager took my order and didn't mess one thing up even on the over medium egg I got on top of my cheese burger. She even brought it out to my car so I wouldn't have to come in. Great experience and delicious treat when I got home. Thanks ABE!
ivlp7pYlFX9Qy4T8uBhzpQ:
We had bought a deal (SharingSpree) to West End Cafe and finally went this past weekend. Arrived around 10:50 am on Sunday: plenty of (free) street parking, some customers, no line. 

The first thing that struck me is the decor with the beautiful interior. There is a full bar, several HDTVs, and modern everything. There isn't a sign prompting to order at the counter, but with the menu posted above, it can be inferred. I did notice at least a couple parties hanging back by the entrance, unsure whether to sit down for service or approach the counter.

Prices are reasonable for the portion sizes. I did think $4 for a cup of soup was a bit high, but everything else was manageable.

My fiancee and I ordered the chipotle chicken sandwich (mayo on the side), garden omelette (with egg whites) and a cup of the Tomato Basil soup. From the time we placed the order to the time the food was ready was approximately 10 minutes, which seems reasonable considering the custom orders. She liked the sandwich, I liked the omelette and we both liked the Tomato Basil soup. We both liked the house-made chips, and we ended up leaving about a third of them behind.

I would give the place 5 stars, but I can't bring myself to do so considering I've only been once and it was on a Sunday morning. Some complain of the wait on weekdays, so I am left giving it an above-average grade.

Great service and oh so friendly. Seriously. I am a coffeaholic and Megan asked me if I wanted a takeaway coffee. The food was very very good and the menu had great choices.  We went at 1:00 and my wife had lunch (strawberry fields salad) and I had the turkey bacon omelette - getting breakfast was a need. Lol.  Can't recommend this place highly enough for the all around pleasant experience!

Hubby and I headed out for Memorial Day coffee and our neighborhood shop was closed. We spur of the moment decided to check this place out and were pleasantly surprised. The placed was slammed and they were not prepared for it.They said to expect a 25 min wait on our food which we did. I enjoyed the smoked salmon and egg white omelet and hubby gave a thumbs up for his turkey AND bacon omelet.  The Bongo Java coffee was delicious. We'll go back..........maybe for Happy Hour from 4-6 pm, lunch or breakfast.
c88cG9DstIqOKcurNtx9QA: I've never been disappointed with The Post. Their Pecan "Sausage" on GF biscuit is absolutely amazing. Best GF biscuit I've ever had!

I really like this coffee spot on the East side! I joined a friend and got a specialty iced coffee with honey and it was very good! I typically stick to lattes but decided on something a bit sweeter. The space is very nice and has plenty of tables, food, and smoothies! My first impression was positive enough that I want to come back again and check out their food and other offerings!

We went here every single day we were in Nashville.

The coffee is insanely delicious.

The food is a vegan's dream.  It rivals or tops the stuff we've had at dedicated vegan restaurants.  (Plenty of options for omnivores, too.)

Bonus points for having an Americana band serenade us for Sunday brunch.  

Very convenient layout and wifi situation, whether you're planning to work, study, or people-watch.  

I'm also pretty sure I saw a scene from Nashville shot here (yeah, yeah... I'm allowed one guilty TV pleasure, ok?).

Neat things in the neighborhood nearby if you're up for a wander.

Basically:  why aren't you headed there right now?
P2FsVo9tcKzjjiG1Ly-thA:
I live close by to the Farmer's Market and often find myself there for a quick lunch. I've been to El Burrito twice, and both times I've been pleasantly surprised with my food. The first time I went, I had the enchilada plate, which included 2 enchiladas (maybe 3?) and 2 sides. My plate definitely weighed close to three pounds. It was a ridiculous amount of food for a small price, and it was pretty tasty too! Today I decided to opt for less food and went with two tacos and a side of rice. This was definitely more of a reasonable portion.

The only thing that takes away a star for me is that their ordering system is a little confusing. Whereas most burrito places you can see the toppings to add, they have theirs in a back cooler, which is fine but I'm not yet familiar with all of the options. They also have a nice little salsa/jalapeno area to add to your meal, but again, for a first (and second!) timer, it's a little confusing.

Service both times has been outstandingly friendly.

One of our favorite places to eat at the Nashville Farmers Market. Everything is great! We've had pretty much everything on the menu and loved all of it. Burritos, tacos and nachos supreme are our favorites. Food is always fresh and the staff are all very very nice! Always feel welcomed. Nothing but good things to say about this place. Try the nachos supreme!

My usual go-to at The Farmers Market, and I'm not even a huge Mexican food fan...its just really tasty, ultra fresh fare delivered with an authentic flair! Their tamales are probably even better than their big ol'burritos, quite frankly---they just are perfectly cooked and flavored...and I love all the different toppings and side veggies they offer as well. Plus, you gotta wash it all down with their awesome cantaloupe/melon juice...it should be medicinal, it's so refreshing and restorative: I feel like renaming it "hangover cure"!
Not to mention their friendly and welcoming staff who are always willing to assist you when you're baffled by all that saucy Latin selection....try these guys sometime and let them show you why they're still there after all these years!!
1BjbKfKQNuVucKFN7taNZQ: A go-to place for one evening when we are in Nashville.  Food is good, entertainment is good, staff is good, atmosphere is OK as it's just a hotel dining room but that's easy to ignore.  It's the entertainment that your there for and some are great and some are not so and that's what makes it fun.  Always on my list of places to go when asked by friends going to Nashville.

You don't go to the Commodore Grill for the food. You go for the music. The menu is okay and the servers are friendly, but there's never enough of them. But it's your standard bar food, and not a very extensive list, so just be prepared for that. But one of the top places to hear songwriters in Nashville.

I was pretty surprised by this place. It's the bar/grill for the Holiday Inn where we are staying and they have a ton of live music at night starting at like 7pm to about 10/11. The musicians are pretty impressive because, well, NASHVILLE!! Everyone here is super nice and friendly. The drinks are okay... as expected at a hotel like this. The food is pretty good. I like their buffet for breakfast.
mW-Whzi8dASVhkajlhd1dw: LOVED their sauces. Wish they were a big enough place to bottle and sell them, would have been worth checking my luggage to being them home! Fun atmosphere too.

We stopped here for a late lunch after reading some of the reviews on Yelp. 
I had the Drifter sandwich and my wife had the pork BBQ sandwich. Both were excellent! They also had 2 for 1 beer specials. 
Will definitely stop here again.

I can't really speak about the food, except the tater tots which...seriously who doesn't love tater tots.  

Drifters is a dive bar in the best sense of the term.  No frills, Cold drinks, laid back place.  Great beer list, everything from High Life to a good local craft selection.

My wife and I came because we heard the music floating through the air one night.  Sat on the back patio and listened to Luella Woods and Tim Caroll swap off singing and playing with some killer local talent playing with them.  

This has become our Summer place to come and grab a drink and listen to some music.
JODu4xqBU80zZ9mKKWrbYg:
It's fun to go early and dine at the bar. Excellent service. Very cute, little place that feels inviting and cozy.  Filled with very good looking people. The chicken liver pate is fabulous. We had tri tip and the chicken. Both beautifully presented and not too salty!  The menu is concise and the interesting wine list isn't too big or intimidating. Lots of friendly, smart help from the sommelier.  Entrees in the $25 range. I really love the aprons that the servers wear. 

Whoa! Those hotel rooms in the back!  Is this Nashville?

This is a hidden gem in the gulch. Very small, so RSVP is recommended. A huge whiskey selection and food is excellent. The fresh fish was Barracuda.  A bit pricey, however the dishes do not disappoint.

Absolutely delicious. 

My man and I came here for dinner last week to celebrate his birthday. I told him to pick any spot and he chose 404 Kitchen. Walking in, I didn't know what to expect because the place looks like a swanky, hipster cozy bar more than anything. We were greeted by the chillest and funniest waitress. We mean this in the most loving way possible, but homegirl must have eaten an edible before her shift or SOMETHING because she was LIT.

To share for appetizers, we ordered the burrata and the diver scallop crudo. The burrata was so decadent - a large round chunk of smooth cheese, grainiest and softest in the center, over a bed of pickled thin-sliced squash that was addictively tart and sweet. The crudo was good too - scallop was fresh and the bits of orange peel mixed in tasted quite compatible, but the seasonings came together a little too salty for my taste. Both dishes came in very fun-sized, shareable portions.

For our entrees, we had the pork bolognese and then the tri-tip. 404, I am impressed and gotta tell you, both dishes blew us away. The pork bolognese was rich and scrumptious, everything you would expect from your favorite hearty tomato-based pasta dish infused with a smoky bacon flavor. 

The tri-tip was absolutely incredible and I love the way it came together. The beef was soft, juicy, over a bed of buttery moist rice with sweet peas mixed in. Drizzled on the the plate was this amazing chimichurri-style pureed garlicky green sauce that I scraped clean with every last bite of my beef.

We really enjoyed the food and appreciated the chance to celebrate here!
FJVm1bbnUp4EsE1h99QqNQ: Great services and delicious food! I ordered a gyro with chicken and lamb and fries. Everything was fresh and seasoned well. The saddest part about my meal was seeing that my food disappeared. Lol I'd definitely come here again.
I really enjoyed it! Awesome to be able to get a good gyro right by the house. Score.
Great gyro meat, salad and hummus! Friendly service and large portions. Best gyro I've had!
H3Tmgv94pbGvBIKZ4Rs9Cw: Tin Angel was one of my must stops in Nashville. Decided to return after several years. Still love the horseradish sauce o n the fried green tomatoes. The tomatoes were crispy, almost overcooked. The trout ala panchayat was delicious. Cooked through but still moist. Salad with tarragon vinaigrette. Rice pilaf to complete the entree. Tried a cote du Rhone white that complemented the fish well, though a lighter red would work too. Great service at the bar!

First time visitor, passing through to Texas

I'll begin with our service. Less than impressed. She gave the impression/vibe she would rather be somewhere else with an odd apathetic smile. Awkward. 

However, where the server seriously lacked in southern hospitality, the food made up for her inadequacies in ACES. I ordered the pork chop with winter vegetables. Hands down THE BEST thick cut chop I've ever had the pleasure to devour. It was so juicy and tender, I cut it with my fork. Unbelievable goodness. 

Dessert was perfection. Crème Brulee that literally melted in your mouth. 

Glad we stopped in. I'll bookmark for a future return visit.

Brunch on Sunday, more like lunch since it was closer to 2pm.  Started with the sweet potato biscuits which were good, but a little doughy in the middle. It is actually a biscuit with sweet potato spread in the middle in case you are wondering.  Split a fish with shrimp salsa, grits and poached eggs special - great flavors and we ate every bite.  Also got a salad with fruit and blue cheese, but at first it came out with no cheese or dressing... not sure how they forgot that.  Rounded our meal with 2 spicy bloody marys - decent.  My folks got the bean and ham soup and steak omelet dish which they both said were excellent.  Nice atmosphere, decorations and layout.  I don't know that you need to go out of your way for this place, but if you are in the neighborhood it is worth a try.
ow7hDGkfVJH65nniXH1a0g: An actual treehouse once resided in the trees of this little lot where this bar stands. I miss it as the endless mutations of Five Points continues. Spending an evening at The Treehouse, run by a chef from Seattle with Spanish culinary influences, was still pretty fun. Their cocktails are what cocktails should be; their wine list is excellent and fairly priced; and the small plates are fun to share. The food I tried-- heirloom tomatoes with pickled red onion, goat liver pate on crostini, homefries-- wasn't as impressive as other restaurants nearby, but still good. Go for the drinks and ambiance, and maybe splurge on an entree and see if it is better than the small plate offerings.

I don't understand why this restaurant does not have at least four stars. We had dinner there. We were seating at the bar - super nice. Overall, the atmosphere is lovely and intimate. The place is beautiful without being too 'fancy'. Very nice staff. Excellent food. We had a salad and then lamb for my friend and tartare for me. Both were tasty, well prepared, fresh. We really liked that place. The reason why I did not put 5 stars is that it is quite expensive. But worth it!

Every single dish was exquisite. And, they were easily able to accommodate some of our vegan friends, who are raving about how yummy everything is.  We are from the San Francisco Bay Area, so we think we are a bit spoiled but this restaurant was ever but as good as the tastiest restaurants in San Francisco with a wonderful southern flair.
WrgdQF8kzvONbZctSPlF4A:
Excellent Japanese restaurant in the heart of downtown Nashville.  Sushi is pretty good, but the traditional Japanese menu is what really sets it apart.  Huge selection of grilled, fried, and raw dishes that you just cannot find at many Japanese restaurants.  

If you are going for volume and value I would highly recommend the combination platters.  You get a grilled dish, tempura, bento box sides, rice and 5 pieces of sushi for less than twenty dollars.  It is a great way to sample a lot of different dishes and completely stuff yourself for not a lot of money.  

Also, do yourself a favor and try one of the imported sakes.  Order them chilled not hot and they come in a great wooden box with a side of salt.  Sprinkle the salt on the corner of the box and enjoy.  This is the way they do it in Japan and it is way different from the hot crappy sake that you get in most places.  

Above all try something new, close your eyes point at the menu and see what you get.  You rarely will be disappointed.

The quality of the food has been a little uneven over my past couple of trips.  I am not sure if they have had some turnover in the kitchen or sushi bar, but it always seems like something is a little off.  On one trip the sushi was not very good, on another the bento seemed a bit sloppy.  I would still recommend it, but Ichiban is no longer a sure thing.

I've spent the last 3 weeks trying to convince my wife to get sushi with me. I don't like it that much, but I've been craving it like pregnant women crave ice cream and pickles. Finally, at the end of our long weekend road trip, she caved. Unfortunately she waited until we were in Nashville vs a coastal city, but I wasn't complaining. 

I opened yelp and as it was freezing cold and we were walking from the country music hall of fame, I picked the highest rated place that was less than 1/2 mile away. 

They offered a decent lunch special which allowed you to get 2 or 3 rolls, but the selection was limited. It came with soup and salad. 

My wife got the veggie roll and spicy tuna. I went with the salmon and yellowtail. All the rolls were made fresh and quickly as you would hope for at lunch. 

The downside of this place was the service. It wasn't bad, but it was borderline abrasive. I know they were probably trying to help the work lunch crowd that was in a hurry on a Tuesday, but we had been seated less than 5 minutes and the waitress asked 3x if we had decided. 

Overall left happy with my warm soup (did I mention it was cold?) and tasty sushi. Hopefully those cravings don't come back around for a while.
_3U2YwnbcdtDc1UVvjUrkw: We came here because is it was the closes coffee shop near us. I have to say I'm embarrassed. The manager was extremely and helpful. The food was amazing. The coffee was delicious. I would definitely return.

I had a half order of the bacon tater and half of the berry berry bo-berry waffles and both were absolutely phenomenal. The bacon tater was cheesey, crispy and deliciously topped with salsa. The waffles were so yummy, light & fluffy I couldn't get enough of them! I couldn't choose between the two I'm so glad I got both! The service was prompt & friendly, the whole atmosphere was pleasant. 10/10 would recommend & absolutely will be back!

Try the White Chocolate Tiramisu. We liked that the coffee was put into mugs rather than a to go cup first.  Inviting us to sit and stay awhile rather than just in and out.  We will be going back for more coffee and to try a breakfast sandwich.
4TDMSTCQuj8F0WjxJWOkGA:
McDougal's on White Bridge Rd. has become my favorite place for chicken. I appreciate that their food is freshly prepared and never frozen nor filled with preservatives. The chicken is juicy and has just the right flavor; both grilled and fried chicken tenders are excellent. 

Hope, the restaurant manager, is amazing! She is always very friendly and helpful and clearly does a wonderful job. Every time I visit, Hope and her staff members are hard at work, and they welcome me enthusiastically. 

The one change McDougal's desperately needs is improvements to its parking lot. Currently, the uneven mixture of gravel, concrete, and potholes threatens to damage cars and to cause patrons to trip and fall. Paving is urgently needed, and improved lighting in the lot is called for as well. These improvements are important for safety and to bring the facility in line with the delicious food and outstanding staff. 

Overall, the combination of great food and a top-notch crew keeps me coming back frequently to McDougal's.

Excellent food, do yourself a favor and get the Honey Bee sauce.  The fries were a little salty but I like that so it's not a problem for me.  The place is kind of run down and could use a good scrubbing but didn't feel overly dirty. My table was a bit sticky.  But all in all I definitely will be back.

Can't believe I'm here again .....  yum!!!
I also like the HoneyBee sauce and was told that some times the taste difference (...I had HoneyBee sauce one day then another day I could swear They gave me different sauce !!!) is due to the timing and settling of the mix i.e. They make it fresh but did you get the first pour or the last ??

I don't care
I'm still having it ;-)
8ikj9GaC2oEf_leisl-Wrg:
This is a no fuss country breakfast restaurant.  This is one of the few places in Nashville that has vegetarian Gravy! And also a drive-thru!!!! Yes, a breakfast drive-thru!!

All in all its pretty standard biscuits gravy, eggs, eggs sandwich, pancakes, and potatoes.  They also offer turkey sausage and bacon in case you aren't down with the swine.They also have a lunch menu, and claim home made desserts.  You can have a huge breakfast for under 10$ in the heart of East Nashville, is a good damn deal.

Unassuming and simple, just the way I like it. The breakfast was perfect: Coffee, scrambled eggs, sausage, grits, and of course, a hot biscuit with gravy and jelly on the side. If you're in Nashville, skip the ridiculous lines at more trendy places and come here instead. I certainly plan on coming back.

Love it...tourists stay away locals only..I wish. Unfortunately the popularity has caught on lots of hipsters in the parking lot, miss the old crowd. Actually miss the old Nashville minus the hipsters.
s4QmperO7u5flVDhlTcC7Q: This is this best place for really decent and quick Mexican food. Really good for the price if you are on a budget.

I was SO excited (and still am) that Oscars has come to Nolensville road- North of Old Hickory.  In my opinion, their fish tacos are the best in town.  At 2.19 a pop, I can get two and be in fish taco heaven for under $5.  I don't go here for the service or the ambiance- those two areas are lacking, but the cheap, fresh food and close to home location brings me back at least once a week!

On my first day at my new job, my boss got us lunch from here. It was a great welcome. I have only had the chicken nachos and the chicken taco salad, but they were pretty good. I have a hard time ordering anything but the taco salad because it is so delicious. Its in no way healthy, but it yummy. They take a fresh deep fried flour tortilla bowl and pile it with rice, lettuce, cheese, sour cream, pico,  quac and grilled chicken. I always add refried beans, because they are yummy. They also have green and red salsa to choose from. I go with the red, its spicier. 

The prices are good and service is quick. It is quickly becoming one of the places I wish wasn't so close to work. Another place that is murder for my diet.
sZQBvfZFywHWu-LcRRrRpw: So good!! The gumbo is great and the crawfish are so yummy. I look forward to them coming to East Nashville every year. Try them out, you won't be disappointed.
This is the best place in Middle TN for Cajun Boiled Crawfish, shrimp etc.  Not only is it the best tasting, but the best price as well.

Handsdown! The best crawfish in the city. I try to make it a point to stop by and bless the fellas. They also have good boudin bites, the alligator bites are good as well. No need to go to New Orleans, they brought it to you. Very affordable and nice setup outside to enjoy your seafood feast. Go check em out!
xEEUmM8gtHG4ZOFYDBmHJw:
My husband and I are new to Nashville, so we had never been to Aura. We decided to give them a try, since we had a gift certificate from restaurant.com. First off - we did read many of the past reviews on Yelp, so we knew what foods and drinks we wanted to try. 

I'll start with the good: The things that we liked, we REALLY liked. First off, our waiter was excellent. The place was fairly empty, since we came in on a Tuesday night. Regardless - he did a wonderful job taking care of us. 

The edamame hummus was amazing. We also ordered the crawfish potato skins,  which my husband really enjoyed. (I'm sure they were great, I am just personally not a big crawfish fan,) 

Our main dishes were both "good," but the thing that really blew us away, was the side order of macaroni & cheese. PHENOMENAL! We ordered it plain, without any of the meat choices. I highly recommend the mac & cheese!

I wouldn't say that anything was "bad," so I'll say the next part was "okay": We each ordered a cocktail, before we had our main dish. The were both good drinks, but neither one of us were blown away - and, they were definitely more expensive than most restaurants that offer a similar quality cocktail. 

All in all, we had a very nice evening at Aura. I agree with some of the past reviews that there is something missing that keeps me from giving them a better review, but I can't put my finger on it. The bar was packed when we were there, but the restaurant was fairly empty. Maybe they need to somehow incorporate the atmosphere or the bar area, more into the dining room...I'm not sure. 

To sum it up, we had a very nice experience, and we plan to go back again!

Went there on a date and everything was really good. We had the crawfish potato skins and the goat cheese fondue as appetizers. Both were impressive. I had the filet mignon and the girl I was with had the grouper with scallops. The Filet gets 5 stars but the grouper gets 4 because it tasted a tiny bit fishy. The scallops were perfect. For desert we had their special of the night which was home made pumpkin cheesecake. It was out of this world. The chef here does a really good job and the place wasn't too packed. I will go back for sure.

Great restaurant...my friend and I visiting from out of town came across this place on grouping so we went for it despite the description of Latin Asian and southern flavors. It was a slow Thursday night when we walked in great ambiance low lit and "swanky" drinks pretty good I got the jacks penicillin thought it would be more of a smooth whiskey drink but was more of a spritzer... my friends drink relax thyme martini was amazing!!! We stuck w appetizers and salads which were more than enough (and we are big eaters) the alligator ribs were very very good not heavy in the sauce on them and fell off the bone...beer shrimp and grits w jicama slaw was also amazing a little heavy but super flavor punch.  The rainbow Kahle salad was sweet and refreshing and the Caesar. Which I usually never order was delish when topped with toasted capers and roasted peppers...I would absolutely recommend this place
iMqoAux5nfccxIZMHj3dIw: Delicious food! Turkish coffee was delectable! Lamb shank cooked to perfection. Friendly staff, clean environment. Thoroughly enjoyed the experience and the food, and will definitely be back in the future!
Yum yum yum!!!! I was told by my daughter I HAD to come here and she wasn't lying!! Amazing, authentic, and fresh! If you decide to skip...you are doing yourself a disservice

A terrific little place tucked away in a strip mall on Thompson Lane.  The service is slow but courteous.  And the kubideh and chargrilled Cornish game hen are fantastic.  Save room for the rosewater ice cream.
hyqQrok-ZwXUASBVLGWBuQ: A quirky joint with much better food, service, and ambiance than the place across the street. If your choice is between these two, go with this one!
One of the best sushi place in Nashville! Love their Takoyaki and Shrimp Tempura Roll!
I go here all the time! Great sushi, great place, good prices. Good happy hour specials. I've had multiple rolls and I've never been disappointed.
RAVXMJYeSaXeURryxMrwcQ: This Panda Express is pretty clean and the employees are quite friendly. I got my order through quickly and the portion sizes were decent. I ordered half fried rice and half chow mien with Beijing beef and orange chicken. The orange chicken was crispy and had a good balance of sauce. However, the Beijing beef had way too much sauce and it felt like I was drinking a bottle of sauce. Overall, it's in a pretty nice location and I would recommend trying out this Panda Express if you are in the charlotte pike area.
This is a legit review. Best food I've ever had in my life. I am not hungover the least bit. Order the crab Rangoon is the bomb. Orange chicken with 1/2 lo neon and 1/2 fried rice .

Stopped by for a quick lunch and was pleased with how polite the service was.  The food was terrific as expected.

Take this place for what it is, though: popularized Chinese fast food.  It's nothing too fancy, just good fast Chinese food.  The place is clean, and the staff is nice as can be.  Give it a try!
fjCSG6HyOm7aCsWN2Rz47g: One of my favorite places in Nashville. They have a huge selection of great food and drinks, lots of Tv's, and reasonably priced.

Awesome place for a big group to mingle. Went after graduation with big group of friends and families. Waitress was super nice and accommodating, nice play areas with ping pong tables and such. Good food and awesome drinks. Definitely recommend!

This is a really fun place! Not only do they have fun activities...ping pong tables, shuffle board tables, foosball tables, etc....   But has really good food. The wings are some of the best in town... Smoked, not fried.
pFoS41r45N2VL0sHIGdJ-g: Tracy at the bar is awesome!!!  She makes the best drinks. I love this place. Great addition to this area. Steak is outstanding. Cheese plate is my favorite. Love it!!!  Tracy rocks!!

I had the most pleasant lunch here on the enclosed patio.  My salmon was cooked perfectly and their extensive wine by the glass menu was perfect via an ipad. I enjoyed my meal here and the service very much. I will be back.
Great afternoon meal with a refreshing wine. Kelly was our awesome server and so engaging with his stories!
Yhla6fekWUwcf3Nkwkg1mg:
Made opentable reservation. Always a positive for me! Bloody Mary was spicy.. Love! Large group and we were well taken care of. The kitchen is small so food came out in waves. My shrimp & grits was beyond great. The Brie appetizer was mouthwatering! 

Enchanting place... Cool ambience. 

The only downside was dessert. Whip cream , mascarpone, blueberries and crispy shortbread. Just wasn't good but not bad. The pastry chef (I think) came out and asked if we liked it & when we were nicely honest... Was a bit defensive with an explanation that was to long. Great place! Will go back.

Went to Sunday brunch with a small group of girls. This place was adorable! It reminded me of one of my favorite Charleston restaurants. 

The brunch food was delicious and reasonably priced. I had a fried green tomato BLT that was amazing! One of the girls had quiche that was a smaller serving than expected. But overall the service was great, it was a friendly, and I liked the ambiance. 

While we didn't order any alcoholic drinks after our long weekend in Nashville, the bar area in this house-turned-restaurant was gorgeous! Would definitely recommend for brunch!

Excellent experience at Magnolia South. Our waiter, Brian, was super attentive and informative. He even took the time to tell us about the background and give us a tour of the historic house that the restaurant is located in. Not only is the service excellent, but the food is phenomenal. The grits from the shrimp and grits entree are exceptional and the we highly recommend you finish with the vanilla bean crime brûlée. We loved our experience here and will definitely return next time we are in Nashville.
4Tio7gIFngYOps2mJba2kw: In town for a conference, we followed the Yelp reviews to Shipleys. The store was very clean, and the woman behind the counter had a friendly smile.  The glazed donut was a symphony in my mouth, light, airy with just enough glaze! Mmmmm! Thanks Yelp!
Used to go every Wednesday morning before the new owners took over. The new folks are nice but things are different.
The donuts are so fresh and delicious.  I was particularly impressed with their superb customer service!!  The girl was so helpful, patient and friendly.  I will be back frequently.
WRSUURl5C9bQd-uoutOVVg:
I didn't eat, so I can't review the food, but this rating is for my overall experience. 

I love Yazoo and Jackalope brews, but I have always felt like their taprooms were just ok. Fat Bottom heard my silent prayer, and answered in spades. 

Large, well designed bar inside. To get there, you walk through the actual brewery and smell the yeast and hops in raw form. Once in the bar, it's spacious and the dark wood gives off a great vibe. 

There is a beer garden next to the taproom that is awesome. You have outdoor seating, benches, and some well maintained grass in the concrete jungle where Fat Bottom lives. 

Staff was super friendly (also a complaint with the other taprooms in town, it's really hit or miss with service). Not so here though. 3 separate people helped out and they had smiles from ear to ear. 

Overall, GO NOW!

It takes a lot to get 4 stars from me, and whilst not exactly my kinda place to hang out, this business is first class.  We four arrived at 7.30pm.  it was packed.  Long benches and some tables.  We managed to perch at the bar.  Decor was woody all over, nicely done.  Bar Lady was attentive and efficient, smile never left her face.  Kitchen was pumping out the food, all nicely plated, lots of burgers.  We ate bangers and champ mash, two porter rd sausages with mashed tater and gravy $10, friends had chilli cheese wedges,  $5, a generous portion, my chums had a pint each of something $4.50 and enjoyed it.  There is an outside gravel and seated area which would benefit nicely from some firepits and perhaps moveable seating.  Liquor license is applied for so hopefully I can get a drink there soon.  You could really tell peeps were enjoying working there.  Entrance is round the back, brewery is at front so it looks empty from main street.  I would go back earlier and on a week day.

I really can't say enough good things about this place. Very good beer and truly excellent food. Good vibe, attentive service, great music on the speaker system.

I came on a saturday around 6pm. Plenty of seating available and server came by pretty quickly. The outdoor space looked nice but we decided to sit inside. My friend and I tried four house beers and they were all solid. No crazy flavors and only around 8 beers to choose from but they were well done so that's fine.

The menu was also small but solid. As a vegan, I always check out menus online before eating somewhere. This place had a falafel sandwich that looked like it could be made vegan--fine. However, when we showed up, the special was a vegan bahn mi with seitan! Jack pot! And it was AWESOME. As good as any seitan sandwich from a 100% vegan restaurant. They should seriously think about adding it to the regular menu. The fries were very good too. Thick and handcut. My friends got the fat bottom burger and he said it tied for the best burger he's ever had.
smIt3VKkrwzl54RJ3PaRsw:
Best Chinese food I've had in a while. I'm here on business and highly recommend. 

The young lady running the register was polite which goes a long way. 

I'll be back!
Hands down the best Chinese food around. I always call in my order and when I get there it's always ready and hot. Staff is very friendly and smiling.  You can't go wrong with the place.

It is the best American Chinese food that I've had. The ingredients were fresh and the food wasn't too oily. We ordered crab rangoon, roasted pork chow mein, and sweet and sour pork.  I am definitely going back again. If you decided to eat in, the ambiance is relaxing and there is plenty of space. Very clean and nice.
YDfQlJKpocV51jCL5Eavrw: Springwater is a diamond in the rough. I am glad I decided to visit this unsuspecting dive bar. The music was great, the crowd was cool and the beer was cheap.

This place is awesome. We drove into town for a show that was a whopping $5 cover. Cheap beers, good vibe, fun atmosphere; no complaints! Cash-only. No liquor, if it matters to ya. I love how old school it was with the vending machines everywhere. Jukebox had killer tunes. The sound system for the stage setup was perfect. Loved the back room area where the bands load-in and it had the bench seats; so great. Played pool all night/morning. We had a blast. I will def be back! \m/

This place rocked it was one of the ONLY bars opened on Thanksgiving and welcomed this tourist from the west.  Everyone was totally cool and shared their SF stories with me.  Also one of the cheapest bars I have been to.  Thanks guys for taking me in.  Hope to visit again
6UUPUr8bRMSg0u443o98kQ:
Having discovered Viener Fest about two weeks after it opened, it's become my "day off" place to get lunch. The food is good and the staff is friendly.

Their red cabbage seems to be pickled with balsamic vinegar. It's got a very nice sweet (but also tart) taste to it, and their spatzel is good as well.. being a bread nut, I order my sausages served "European," which basically means they stuff it into a baguette. Their pretzel appetizer is nice too, and their home-made yellow dipping mustard is wonderful. I usually ask for another side of it when my meal comes for dipping.

I recommend!

First off, the atmosphere was just fantastic.  I really liked the outdoor seating and quaint bar area. My wife and especially loved the 30's era German music playing during our lunch.  Between the influence of the beer and the music, it briefly seemed like we were no longer sitting in Nashville but in some Bavarian pub. I had the holstein schnitzel which was great except a little heavy on the butter.  The cucumber salad and red cabbage were both very tasty.  My only complaint about the food was the frites which were obviously frozen and were served to us lukewarm.  Definitely plan to return and try some of the sausages next time!

Absolutely wonderful bratwurst tucked into a baguette! Excellent red cabbage and spaetzle.  This makes a great new lunch choice as well a for dinner.
Sides are just $1, so go before they figure that one out!

Also, their food is not the heavy fare I am accustomed to from German restaurants.
Empty at lunch so far, busy at night.
tmldiz-m9CNXZfiZglDGsA: My husband and I ate here while visiting Nashville and we were very pleased with our experience here. I would give the restaurant 5 stars for ambiance alone - cute little place, 2 stories and had a very romantic atmosphere. We called ahead to inquire about dress code, which we were advised casual and jeans were ok, however once we got there we noticed many of the men were in sports coats so next time we will probably dress more business casual. Our food was also excellent - the only reason for 4 stars is because my husband ordered a steak, which was one of the specials, and when we got our bill we were surprised at a $50 steak price. None of the other meals on the menu were more than around $30 or so, and we just felt that with such a mark up for a "special", especially for a rib-eye steak (not even a filet), we should have been informed of the price just as an fyi. It was vwry good but not $50 good. So we were disappointed with that, but otherwise wonderful service, excellent food and ambiance. Would definately recommend, just check the prices of those specials.

The pasta all' Amatriciana is the best i've had, including at il mulino in NYC. The black eyed pea soup was awesome too. The prices are pretty high. $20 for pasta about $10 for soup. My friends had pasta too and they all loved it. You kind of have to valet park. Parking on the street is pretty cramped at night. Service is really good too. We sat up at the bar, and it's nice.
Such a nice Dining experience. The restaurant is well appointed with a soothing decor. Our waiter was fast efficient and attentive. The lasagna was really good. Extra for having Coke Products!
FX89yIMKDvg6yhlQTLtmWw: First place we ate in Nashville was pretty good.  Great fried pickles appetizer.  Able to watch the Panthers game no problem on Sunday, despite the bountiful Packers fans.
