No doubt this is a Packer bar, as the Titans game was on one TV and this place is 10 miles from their stadium.  The chicken wings were pretty good and they had great deals for buckets on NFL Sundays. Great sports bar

Had an excellent time here. Didn't eat, but the food looked and smelled amazing. The bartender and waitresses here are super nice and very helpful. Drinks were good and nothing was overpriced. Will be going back next time we are in Nashville. This is a good time place with superfriendly people.

Just sat down & was greeted in a very timely manner by a super friendly & genuine waiter named Shane. He had a great smile, kind eyes & a friendly demeanor. My boyfriend & I placed our order with Shane for a CUSTOM bad-ass Bloody Mary & an order of their famous hot chicken & waffles with a side of hot water corn bread. The Bloody Mary is here & fab with our hot chicken being freshly prepared in the kitchen. I give this hole in the wall 5 stars! Thanks Shane god the great service!
4fUrcm8sFSprCbHqa8pdog: I've heard various reports on JK's over the years. Finally I had to try it for myself. I enjoyed dinner here with my Dad in May of 2014. I've been to a lot of steakhouses in various cities. And I understand that different people value different things. Personally, at a place like this, I want good service, good atmosphere, and high quality food. All three were achieved at JK's. It's a throwback, Plantation-house style dining experience. It's a little cramped, but that' part of the charm. It's reasonably priced for a higher-end restaurant. Our steaks were cooked perfectly. The sides were well prepared. Our waiter was excellent with very good "feel" for when he needed to come by. I also like this restaurant because it has a very local feel. It's not right on West End or 21st. You have to be actively seeking it out to find it.  A great dining experience and I'm sure I'll be back.

Amazing as usual. If you like tomatoes don't shy away from the tomato salad. Don't let the onions and blue cheese scare you the combination is delicious. The blue cheese is mild and a great compliment to the tomatoes, vadalia onions and vinaigrette. Well you aren't reading to hear about the salads you come here for the steaks! I enjoy a good cowboy (bone in) ribeye. And they do this well. Now I am from Steak country so don't adulterate my steak with toppings just make it medium rare and bring it out. The steaks are tender, perfectly aged and expertly cooked. I have never gotten a bad steak. The ribeye is a 20 ounce so prepare to take some home. I recommend the twice baked potato and broccoli but once again you are going for the steak. Enjoy!

The food & service here is A+

I had fried oysters, and a bleu cheese fillet  :-)
Great !    I come from a seafood area, and these were amoung the best....
Our waiter was a very fine older gentleman (48 years of service there)
Outstanding !

We were a large group, who had a few cocktails and maybe a little
Too loud and crazy, but none the less we were treated like gold.
I have not been that impressed in quite awhile.
PNby7mawC0ecfg-uEp0OVQ: Best burger I've had in the area. The home made pickles were a real hidden gem. Cool dude running the place. Got the farm burger and it was great, gotta were hot and fresh and the price was really good. It's hard to get 5 stars , check the stats.

Awesome little spot with amazing food that supports our military and first responders. Definitely a must stop spot. 

Our party had the 'Mericano Slider, White Cheddar Mac and Cheese, Brisket Slider, and Bacon Cheddar Tots, all of which were delicious! 

Nice place outside to eat with tables, shade, fan and a tv! 

Super nice guys running it, with some awesome flag socks to boot! Merica

1. These guys support military and first responders (discounts for them). 
2. The food is amazing. The buffalo tots are out of this world good. 
3. Great prices!
4. Fast friendly service! It was raining when we went and they had this enclosed tent for us to sit in. They kept it warm with heaters and had a tv playing too!

Gotta love small family owned business like this one! Great people, great food, great prices, can't beat it!
k8JazPdt5ZojaKC1TzYJnA: Aww man this place is really good. Ordered a classic gyro sandwich with fries. It was made fresh and was out in a jiffy. I even ordered extra tzatziki sauce and it was quite tasty. The people working that day were extremely friendly. Inside was very clean. Dirty tables while people are standing around is a huge pet peeve of mine but that was definitely not the case here. I would definitely recommend this place for fantastic Greek food.

This place is great! Tried it for the first time and was very impressed. First of all, the staff are extremely friendly and accommodating, which is almost always a good sign. I had the shawerma (a marinated chicken dish), which was very tender and flavorful. Also tried the hummus, which came with fried pita bread that was unlike anything I'd ever had. Because it was Sunday, we also received free baklava with the purchase of two meals, and it too was outstanding. Overall, a very good experience, and will be coming back very soon!

Lots of bang for your buck here - portions are delightfully massive and prices very reasonable. The food is delicious every time. Might I recommend the gyro platter with lamb, pita, rice, and salad? Or a gyro sandwich, can't go wrong there either. Not a ton of seating, but quite a clean and quiet place. Love this addition to the area.
30OhTA38fp8xuqW4O2D6Eg:
A hidden gem in Nashville - stellar tap list, plethora of locally sourced food, and a bunch of house made items including cheese, tempeh and pickles. Not to be missed. Our server (T'anna) was extremely knowledgeable abt beer and food and offered us tons of helpful suggestions. 

We were in town from Denver and were pleasantly surprised to find this oasis in a sea of BBQ and fast food joints

It's the Music City, so naturally I was looking for something local with good food, good local beer, and live music. I am staying by the airport and didn't want to go downtown after work. I found everything I was looking for and more in this place! While it is very unassuming- in a regular strip mall behind a bank - it has so much character! The beer menu was full of local Tennessee and surrounding area options. The food was made with local seasonal ingredients. And there was live music! To top it all off, it had a 2 for 1 beer special going that day. I will definitely go again the next time I'm in town.

Absolutely loved this hometown taproom! Flew in late Friday night and found thus great spot close to our hotel. It is definitely a local favorite, they had a live band and a down home feel. The minute you walked in the door you felt like part of the town. 

Great food, service and beer! They had roughly 20 beers on tap mostly a local collection. My boyfriend and I both had great beers and even got one on the house since I got the last little bit from the keg. I had the grilled cheese with pulled pork. It was fantastic and made with fresh ingredients from locals. My boyfriend had the pulled pork sandwich which had great house-made coleslaw. Would highly recommend either sandwich!

Definitely will be back to visit during future trips to Nashville!!!
HSY2c6NbngjDHkuwZi7C2Q: The chimichanga was absolutely delicious, and they smothered it in queso sauce which made it even better. My margarita was reasonably priced and I was still able to taste the tequila (which doesn't always happen with affordable margaritas). My fiance loved his fajita burrito and their queso is great. The staff was so friendly and attentive, we had Mario and he couldn't have been nicer. We will definitely be going back.

Food was great, drink was even better. I think they possibly served me the best strawberry margarita ever. 
Can't wait to go back!!

It's nice to know there's good Mexican food in West Nashville.  The staff is nice and accommodating.  The food is always prepared quickly and well.  Be sure to get a tasty margarita to complement your meal.  The salsa is pretty delicious.  I would, however, avoid going when it's karaoke night (not sure what day of the week that is).

In essence, I would describe this establishment as Las Palmas with slightly better tasting food.
KBpQUlER5geOpk9mrWz_Ww: Awesome food at a great price. The whole family loved it. Food was homemade, fresh and hot. Kids meals were plenty of food and only $2.99. Jambalaya what's hot, spicy and delicious. Cajun salmon Caesar, perfectly done! Hope we can go back again when we visit Nashville.

Raz'z is a great spot.  Stopped in here while staying out near the airport and had a great meal.  Also spoke to Raz off and on for a good 2 hrs.  Awesome guy really cares about his craft and his clientele.   I had the wings, side of gumbo and the miss Linda topped with grouper.  All three were very good.   If you're staying out by the airport and are looking for a non chain restaurant give this place a go.

I visited Razz for the first time last week.  I have to say, I was pleasantly surprised!  We were seated without any wait at all, our food was great, the service was super-attentive, and with the varied menu, we look forward look to visting again to try other selections!  Highly recommended!
vP62uQOxqHrUbBaJ7sWY9A: I've been looking for a decent Chinese restaurant since moving to Nashville from Chicago almost 4 years ago.  Well, I've finally found one!  My family and I originally tried E Hur Wei the Wednesday night before Thanksgiving and were all pleasantly surprised.  Not only by the portions but also the quality of the food, which was great!  On my way home from work last night, I decided to give E Hur Wei another chance and again, was not disappointed.  My only recommendation would be to amp up the spice.  I ordered the General Tso's Chicken extra spicy and it was still not even spicy.  If that's the only issue though, I can live with it.  I've only ordered take out, so I can't speak to the in restaurant service.  I will say, the service I received when picking up my order was good though.

Love this place! Quality restaurant where price vs. value they are truly second to none. Great food where quality is never subpar. They never let you down. I went in to order a to go order without calling and before I had finished half of a very well poured drink my food was ready. Great service, great food, and a great time. I would bring my parents here or the best most beautiful date!!! Highly recommend trying out EHURWEI!!!
Food is GREAT, best Chinese food in town but they need better service.
K0mA7srJ-1aFZhSUzMWM_g: Favorite lunch spot. Friendly staff, fresh ingredients, and fast. Love the summer roll!
Ramen is quite good. Simple eat-in place with decent food. Surprised it hasn't taken of with the Vandy students yet.

This place has fresh ingredients and really good sushi. I've been there a couple of times and had a good experience. It replaced a Korean place which unfortunately didn't last long, even though they had really good food. Hopefully this one stays. It has become one of my favorite lunch places in the area.
ZAnLKdfQhX0pj3cwo3JSDg:
3 of my girlfriends and I went to Little Octopus last night for dinner and it was wonderful. Our favorites were the avocado app, the bok choy, the fingerling potatoes and the tofu. My girlfriends also enjoyed the sardines and green papaya. We also bought a bottle of Italian red wine (I forget which bottle) and loved it. The cocktails were also delicious (we went all out.)

I recommend this place for sure. :)

Amazing dishes!! I got the Valencia Rice Bowl and it was one of the best dishes I've ever had! My only complaint are that the portions are very small, and it's pricey for that little of food. I would definitely recommend getting an appetizer or two. Service was a bit slow. We were one of only a few groups in the restaurant and consistently had to flag down the waiter.

Fresh, local, seasonal ingredients with the menu changing fairly frequently. Nice ambiance and friendly staff. Unique fusion style. Try the watermelon, which is comprised of ahi panca, lime leaf, shiitake, and mango butter!!! The unique taste is definitely worth a try and don't forget to eat the lime leaf with the watermelon!
jAabqRopleTaCnX-XDG0CA: I tried out Elliston Place for breakfast, although I know burgers and milkshakes are their specialty.  I got an egg and cheese biscuit and it was good.  Their breakfast food is simple and comforting, all you really need which is what I like for breakfast.  The staff was wonderful, we had Linda as our server and she was warm and attentive.  The coffee was much better than the standard coffee you get at a diner which is big for me!  My friend got one of their milkshakes and said it was the best he has had in Nashville.  Will definitely try it out again.

Cute retro restaurant. You feel like your back in time, enjoying a classic diner experience. The food is great, portions are big. Great staff, everyone is friendly. The only issue is parking, they don't have a lot.

This place holds memories for me, and all of them are good. I can't remember how long this place has been around, but I remember coming here with family almost twenty years ago. Now that I'm back in the city, my mom and I decided to see if the food was as good as we remembered.

Right away you'll notice the old school decor. The tables and miniature jukeboxes at each one were exactly as I remembered. We were allowed to seat ourselves, and we chose one of the booths. It wasn't busy when we arrived and only one other group came in when we were leaving (arrived around 6:15pm). Immediately I decided on a strawberry milkshake, grilled cheese cheeseburger and sweet potato fries. I was hoping it would be good and I wasn't disappointed. 

The milkshake was really two milkshakes (they give you the container it was mixed in and there was enough leftover to refill my own glass which was great since it was $5), the sweet potato fries were crisp but soft and sweet on the inside, and the burger was phenomenal. The toast used was buttered and grilled and the bacon I added on was thick and worth the additional price. I was stuffed and couldn't even finish my fries. My mom ordered the herb roasted chicken, green beans, and mashed potatoes. The chicken was so good she wouldn't even share. She wasn't a fan of the corn bread though. We didn't order any pie, but next time we'll save room for some. 

I will say, the restroom wasn't the best (no obvious mess, just old), and the booth seats were a bit saggy in the middle. Overall though, a very enjoyable meal and experience, and I will definitely be going back for more shakes and to try the pies.
ijiuTK4UjT0wGBGP6fQq5Q: Disappointed to see that so many disagree with my rating. In my opinion, consistent in good service and good food. I am not much for a chain restaurant, but this is my go-to. I've never once been disappointed. Drinks are pretty good and decently priced. My steak has always been perfectly cooked to order. I'll keep coming back!

Crab cake was cold by the time it hit our table and steak was unevenly cooked (med-rare). Wife's steak was spot on; however, and she's pickier ('medium +') than me. The manager apologized and sent cheesecakes home with us. I like Outback and Bloomin' Brands, and these folks are trying hard to get in a routine (and in all fairness, it was Mother's Day madness). Love the new floor-plan!

Very clean and the server (Norman) was great. He had a nice personality and went above and beyond. The steak did not come out right at first, but Norman made sure  it was fixed correctly. We only had to wait around 7 or 8 minutes to be seated. Very nice experience for the first time at this particular restaurant, will definitely be back.
d32ypk0TUSxzYxKnMMuFNg:
I finally had Le Sel for the first time today, and couldn't wait to race over to write a review--I LOVED it!

For starters, for anyone who visited the location prior to its transformation, you'll immediately see a total 180 (from the previous occupant, Music City Tippler). The aesthetic is light and airy and sophisticated. My friend I dined with immediately said "I feel like I'm in New York!" It definitely emits a vibe unlike any other in Nashville.

For lunch, I went with the three course meal (a bargain at $24) - the best french onion soup (rich and great flavor, perfect croutons and a great proportion of cheese : croutons : soup), a BLT (I'll get to that in a minute), and apple tartine.

The BLT was unreal. The thickest bacon I've ever seen, sandwiched between two slices of buttery, flaky croissant--accompanied by juicy marinated tomatoes and a house mayo/garlic situation that was fantastic. SUCH a great sandwich.

Dessert was the apple tartine, partnered with a vanilla-esque ice cream--and it was good, but not ultimately my favorite sweet ever by any stretch. 

My friend had oysters and the Bibb salad and loved it all.

Service was fantastic, environment was amazing--can't wait to go back for drinks and dinner! Love this place. Definitely recommend.

The food at Le Sel was very good. Especially the appetizers were nice - pork terrine, duck liver pate, and mozzarella. Our main dishes were good but I'd say not great. I had the flat iron steak with a huge mountain of fries, which were very good as fries go, but I think I would have preferred more vegetables on offer. The creme brulee I had for dessert was excellent.

Where I deduct a star is the atmosphere. We sat in almost complete darkness, making reading the menu rather difficult, and the noise level was so high that it was hard to have a conversation. 

Overall, it was worth it for the food, but also pricey, so I don't think we'll be rushing there again anytime soon. There are so many other options for great food in Nashville, and perhaps in a more quiet setting.

The long and short of it is that you will not be able to go wrong if you go to Le Sel.  My wife and I went with another couple a few weeks ago and had a superlative dinner.  Our waitress had a comprehensive understanding of the menu that would have been impressive on a normal day but was even more so since the menu had just completely changed three days prior.  The food is excellent - even better than you would expect of an establishment of this high caliber.  The bone marrow appetizer was a particular high point.  Not only was the dish itself excellent, but the owners Eric and Max have pioneered an experience that everyone needs to enjoy - the Bone Marrow Luge.  It is quite simply pouring a shot or two of your favorite beverage down the hollowed out half bone straight down your gullet.  Not only does it make you more refined, it is a lot of fun and drinking a good whiskey out of a bone is darn good.  To summarize, don't hesitate to go if you have an opportunity.  You will not regret it.
iVELyU76Ej483EI0eVOJNA:
We walked up and down Honkey Tonk land in search of the least touristy place to eat and grab a beer for lunch. We were about to settle for less when I pulled up Pub5 on Yelp. It was perfect weather for the rooftop so we headed upstairs. We were seated right against the wall for a perfect people watching view down below. Fortunately, we didn't need to people watch because there was a couple of crazies sitting near us and we eavesdropped their whole convo... live entertainment!

They had an impressive beer selection but, the waitress was a bit unsure on explanations of a few of them or recommending anything of a particular style. Everything on the menu looked pretty enticing. The pimento cheese was delicious and and excellent choice of starter for the four of us. I was leaning towards the fried chicken and then a couple other folks at the table ordered it so I went back to the Trout. I did get to try the chicken and can easily say you won't go wrong either way. I loved my trout and it was a perfectly light lunch. The most surprising part of the dish was the side... or should I say sides. 

It came with a flash fried Kale Salad... wasn't too sure what to expect out of that. I say "sides" because I had two mountains of kale on either side of my fish. The kale was lightly crispy and still a bit chewy. It had house made chips and dried fruit mixed in with cheese sprinkled on top. Everyone at the table had to taste it and all agreed it was way better than they anticipated from the sound of it.

The service was a bit slow considering how busy it was and we kept having different people come to the table and even finished with a different waitress than who started. It must have been a shift change or something but, it definitely slowed things down and created some confusion with the wait staff. Not a big deal. I would be back for that kale salad alone!

Great food. Fantastic. Had the warm goat cheese small plate, and it was amazing.

Our particular server was a bit lackluster, though.

The mussels appetizer was probably in the top 3 for best mussels I've even had (and living in New England I have great mussels often). Warning though if you don't LOVE garlic you won't like them. I was at a business dinner and it was torture to resist sopping up every last drop of the broth with the yummy bread. Absolutely loved my perfectly cooked medium rare filet sandwich (oddly soft delicious bread) with an unusual corn side. Others at the table raved loved the salmon and scallops. Could not beat the rooftop dining. I absolutely loved this place and will go back the next time I'm in Nashville for sure. 

Would have given 5 stars but food was sloooooowww and we were the only table occupied on the roof deck when we got there. Our apps finally came out with no plates to eat them with. Asked the waiter. He "went to get them," vanished came back and ignored is. Another guy swooped in and seemed really in top of things (manager maybe?) and our dinners came out soon so seems like there was maybe it was just one initial glitch with out order/waiter.
rApvxmeA7JSlj81MmC0CdQ: Excellent pulled pork. Mashed potatoes, mac and cheese, and okra are all delicious as well. Wasn't impressed with the pork and chicken tacos--they were pretty much just thrown together with very little meat compared to tortilla size. Could have been an off day, but everything else is well worth the money. Great, quick service!
I had no idea they were in this area. Went here for the first time today to place 2 to go orders. Great service and good food. Will definitely go back again!!!

I didn't know about Dickey's until our office catered my retirement lunch, but it was great Barbeque. I'm anxious to go to the restaurant to try some of the other items they have. Great pecan pie also!
J938AdRE5sjveY7gdE9eMg: I normally do not like Waffle House but this one was pretty decent. The kids voted for it, Food was good and price was reasonable as well.

This is a great waffle house. The food was great and the portions were big. I had their manager's special (texas bacon, egg, cheese biscuit and triple hashbrowns) for $4. It filled me up. I think the woman taking my order was being trained at the time and my order took longer than usual to get correct. I prefer someone taking time to get my order right than to mess it up so I saw this as a plus. 

Great place.

Came here very late for breakfast. I bought the bacon, egg and cheese sandwich with hash browns. The food was surprisingly good, we were pleasantly surprised. Good spot for a quick and cheap breakfast.
-6nqH2tdNHQdqb8LvILlBw: I was craving sushi and happened to pass by Sushiyobi. I went in not expecting great sushi, but was pleasantly surprised that their rolls were very generously sized and the fish was decently fresh. I stopped in with a friend around 8pm and the service was smooth. We sat at the bar and got our food within 10 minutes of ordering. I split two rolls (Alaskan and over the Rainbow) with the friend for $20 (including tax/tip). There was a substantial amount of sashimi in the rolls - something that I always look out for at typical sushi joints. Overall, I have no complaints about my experience here. I would definitely come back to try other rolls and entrees, but probably only often enough to satisfy my sushi cravings.

We are not in California.  Its NashVegas so check your attitude about your fabulous experience at (no name dropping, but....) Nobu.  This is just really, really good sushi that is re-donc-you-less-ly (sound it out people) inexpensive.  Located close to Vandy and Music Row, we hit this place up for a bachelorette party.  With 8 demanding semi-intoxicated women, the service was fast and the food was excellent.  I ordered a grilled beef salad which was located on the menu right beside seaweed salad which I happened to know was a wee little tea cup saucer sized dish.  Well, turns out not so much for the grilled beef which was a full size jumbo salad  The key to ordering this is to ensure you don't get it as an appetizer when you are surrounded by hungry females.  The two bites I got in were amazing though - great, thin cut steak with a teriyaki marinade that was subtle. 
For dinner, I got the crispy shrimp with cream cheese roll -sooo yummy.  
Ambiance isn't amazing, but it beats the pants of Ken's Sushi, my other go-to place so I can't take points off for that.  Although I do have to say it's likely not going to be an amazing date place but rather an excellent low key lunch.  Amazingly, every single person at the table loved their food, and that's a first.  And, not wanting to give this place undo credit, and feeling that extra 'Yelp Elite pressure', I opted to go back the following week during the cold light of day without a buzz and was equally impressed.  For super fun, be sure to hit their website for cute home touches where they offer you "Direction" and "Reservation" reflecting either a total loathing of plural or a cute homey authenticity that defines this place.

I have not set foot in this restaurant. I do however, often call to order in. Sushi rolls have always been fabulous. 
One evening they were out of the Korean beef I wanted, so I ordered chicken teriyaki. I was expected the same ol dish only worse, as it was late in the evening. 
I could not have been more shocked. This dish tasted like I had just made it myself! Freshly cooked everything. Even the salad was very fresh. 
It's small, and that Demonbreun strip is transient. I hope it endures and maintains that quality.
gzQ0kbAHrAJhELi7rrAfaQ: My first visit was ok.  I will try them again.  My sandwich was dry but I did not know about the sauces.  They were on menu but didn't see them and nothing was suggested or offered during my order.  They do serve large portions for the wraps.  The drinks are infused waters but you can pay extra for energy drinks in a can.  I like the concept and they have only been open since March.   Recommend trying for yourself.

I love the new Nashville location of Clean Eatz. I tried the salmon bowl with cauliflower and brussels spouts - delicious!!! Ample portions. Friendly service. Plenty of parking.  A great addition to Green Hills.

I was in town for just a few days and was looking for a place to eat well (clean and moderate calorie) - this was PERFECT! For those who watch calorie counts but enjoy food, this is ideal. You can actually just pick what sounds best to you instead of being left to order another grilled chicken salad! I had the goat cheese panini (GREAT) and a PB&J smoothie (ALSO GREAT). Even the water was good because they'd added honeydew to it. Before i left, i picked up an energy bar from the cooler for the next morning, and I loved it too. I'm actually planning to go back today before I leave town, even though it's the wrong direction and will add 20mins to my already long drive. I'll definitely look other locations when I travel! (And the young lady working the counter was very helpful and kind when talking about how they prepare their items - great service!)
vycwVzUYY2-KoXvMtUAiEw: This is one of my favorite Whitt's locations. Clean, friendly and great pork barbecue. If you don't like vinegar based barbecue - this is not the place for you. Wednesday's are discount pork sandwich days.

FOOD WAS ABSOLUTELY AMAZING!! Ahh the loaded turkey baked potato really hit the spot. The amount of meat that came with the potato was just right. Also, the BBQ sauce was unique and amazing. Definitely worth it.

Had pork sandwich but added vinegar bbq sauce along w thick big sauce in addition to mayo and slaw comes on it. W pickles too. Sooooo good. Service was good too. Very fresh. They did a good job. Will be back.
j0ex3PZ8SlwWdJYRF0BLhQ: Great lunch special. 6.95 for entree, white or fried rice, and house soup! The staff is always friendly and my food is always piping hot. I recommend this place to anyone Downtown looking for a deal and great food. It's located in The Arcade so its obly open during lunch hours.

again, had to try it so it wasn't a fluke that it was as good as it was the first time. (did that make any sense?!) No surprise here! BAM! Had the chicken tamarind this time to stir it up a bit.  So good! 
 This is for locals on the lunch grind DOWNTOWN YO!  
11am-3pm seems to be the hrs. of the arcade, WHY? WHY?

Kapow!  That's right I am dropping the 5 stars on this one.  Most of that is because I am so thrilled to finally have Thai food available as a downtown lunch option.  That is it fast, good and cheap as well definitely puts it over the top.  

I have now been three times and been ecstatic with my choice every time.  For the record I have tried the Red Tofu Curry (awesome), the Pad Pik chicken (scorchinngly good) and the Spicy Bamboo Chicken (really nice).  I can easily see this place becoming a weekly lunch stop for a guy who only eats lunch out a max of 2 times per week (that would be me).  

The Basics:  Green Curry is in the arcade right by the alley.  They are only open for lunch (11:00-3:00).  They have full menu, but the steam table is almost always loaded with some seriously good stuff.  You can either 1-3 choices along with soup and rice starting at around $7.  They will let you have samples if you are not sure which delicious selection you would like to try.  They almost always have a vegetarian option on the hot bar and a ton of veggie options on the menu.  

Pro Tip:  If you are ordering off the menu it is probably a good idea to call ahead as they make everything fresh to order.  Also, by the pickup area there small to-go containers of chili sauce, siracha, and red pepper flakes to heat things up a bit.
vgE6H37Bz6ahQw90szHRjw: The pork Bao are where it's at.  Everything else is good, don't get me wrong, but the pork... they're just out of this world.  I normally consider myself very frugal, and wouldn't bother to repeat with a truck that prices the way Bao Down does... it is on the higher end of the price spectrum (especially deliveries), but it's just that good.  Do yourself a favor, order the pork bao buns; you can thank me later.

The BEST Baos in Nashville, hands down! All handmade, locally sourced meat, and very affordable. I love the traditional pork, curry chicken, cabbage... really, you can't go wrong with any of the flavors, but my absolute favorite savory bun is the mushroom and my favorite sweet bun is the sweet potato with marshmallows. It tastes just like my grandmother's sweet potato casserole that we eat every Thanksgiving and Christmas! Like them on Facebook and follow them on Twitter to find out their schedule for each week. You will make Bao Down a weekly stop for your bao fix! #eatbaonow
I've been eating bao zi my whole life. Bao zi from China, NYC, Boston, my parents kitchen and they don't even compare to the pork ones from here.
6QrNcrTk9k0jXd5ip5CfHw: Crawl away satisfied. The bar staff and kitchen staff will satiate your thirst and hunger in style. Not shy with the pour or the spice, you tongue will dance and your liver cry, but you will leave happy. The wait staff is friendly and fun. 2-4-1 Sunday with a wide range of quality beer is a uplifting substitute for church.
Great atmosphere. Wide variety of food and decent beer selection.  Had the jambalaya and it was amazing!

Large beer selection!

There is nothing better than a great burger and beer at the local bar.  This location is the closest one to our house so we've been here a couple of times.  The staff is always very friendly and it doesn't take too long to get our food.  Clete was our server on our last visit and did a great job making sure we had everything we needed.

I like to try different beer when I'm in the mood but I always go back to Guinness.  I ordered the Island Swiss Burger with my Guinness on this particular visit.  This perfectly cooked burger was topped with a spicy panama sauce and swiss cheese and tasted great.  The sandwiches and burgers come with slaw but I substituted fries for an additional $1.99.  I have to eat fries with my burger.
axBwm6x-Ro9c9Uc7WE_cgw: The salmon was recommended to my by the waitress and it was wonderful! I was impressed with the wide selection of options on the menu.

I've been here twice now, and it's a new fav! Took my wife tonight and she loved it too! Great menu and the service couldn't have been better. Not to mention it's a stone's throw from my house! Looking forward to our next visit...
Once again,  enjoyed a great lunch at Blue Cactus.  The fish was cooked perfect, fresh veggies with a nice light sauce.  My go to fish taco. Also they serve a wonderful freshly prepared gumbo.
fLEaeQgheH92zOC8NwY2Gw: Really delicious breakfast! My boyfriend and I stayed in The Renaissance on a business trip.  We had to be at work early and could not find any other place in the area open before 7. (Not sure if this is common in Nashville or not) After seeing the reviews I was skeptical, but we gave this a try and were very pleased! I have to eat gluten free and I had the garden breakfast bowl with bacon added.  It was sooo delicious.  My boyfriend got the farmer bowl and was also very happy.  The service was decent and very fast.  The price was a little steep (about  $15 per person for buffet or a menu item), but also not surprising for a downtown hotel.

My family and I went to Commerce Street for Christmas dinner because not much else was open. 
The decor of the restaurant isn't the best, kind of drab and run down. Which is odd since they just renovated the hotel. 
The service wasn't the best, but it was busy, and the server was nice.
The food was very good. Prime rib was tasty and the mashed potatoes were some of the best I've had. The entire family enjoyed their meals. It was really good. 
The dessert and cone of bread were also delicious. The cheesecake was also some of the best I've had. 
All of it was moderately priced. $29 for three courses on the special Christmas menu.
Also, they validate for free valet parking.

It's 6 in the morning and not only was our food very promptly delivered, it came in a tote bag, wings, chips, and burger were all clearly very freshly made. I've worked and lived downtown 13 years and this is my absolute "go to."
bPv2SwR1h818VsgICL_6Ow: I visit every time Im looking for a good bagel(at least twice a week) and have NEVER had a bad meal there. I've always been treated quite nice and the staff, when asked, have always provided excellent advice on what to do with "home baking". I definitely recommend a visit.

Well I live in Kansas, the land of no real boiled bagels. I celebrate Brueggers Bagels, awesome chewieness. I recommend the egg, sausage, provolone on pumpernickel not toasted or the honey walnut on cinnamon sugar.

I must say, the bagels at Bruegger's are something I look forward to in the mornings when I am up early enough to grab breakfast before work.  They do a great job of making a bagel that is chewy, but not too doughy.  Of course no bagel is complete without crème cheese and Bruegger's has around ten different types to choose from.  I recommend the bacon scallion because it both delicious and satisfying when your starving and in a hurry.  You must plan accordingly if you desire a bagel before work because it is located right next to Vanderbilt, thus it is usually over run by college kids in the mornings, so there can be a bit of a wait.
WfEc--AG2O1f78q3RuERlw: Got the sausage and egg biscuit and tater tots delivered (by Uber eats) for breakfast.  Absolutely amazing.  This place is super affordable and you get more than what you pay for!

I've been here 3 times and this place is amazing. The biscuits are delicious, soft and buttery and very fluffy. My personal favorite is the kickback with an egg. That sweet pepper jelly just seals the deal. I'm also had the beignets, I'd give them a 3/5 they were well flavored but the coffee chocolate sauce leaves something to be desired. Would definitely recommend this place over any other biscuit place in Nashville!!

This new restaurant is Better than Biscuit Love!  
Our team had their breakfast here today.  Just outstanding food.  My favorite was the tot bowl, but we also split the beignets with coffee/chocolate dipping sauce.  And we had avocado toast that was fantastic too.  There are lots of Nashville breakfast dishes with biscuits and chicken, bacon, pork etc. too.  Salads, brunch stuff.

I'd been to the Brentwood location which is soooo popular -- and I expect once people realize this spot is open, it will be quickly overrun with customers too.  I'm thinking this will become my favorite go-to brunch spot.

Only thing was I had to figure out to park in the rear and come in the back door.  That was the perfect spot given all the 8th st traffic and construction.

Perfect spot!
IEZQJcu09cKSwwn42VH4Aw: One of the few places within walking distance of where we were at, we stopped in and were more than pleasantly surprised. The food was delicious, space was clean, and the gentleman who made our food was very nice. The lamb and chicken were delicious but the baklava was ah-mazing.

They give you so much food! Such a great price. The folks are nice. In my opinion, the quality of the food is great! I've had gyros, shawarma and the hummus is solid! Hidden little gem on Charlotte Ave.
Very ample portions. Great spicy tzatziki sauce. But the best thing about this place was the owner.  Support this place!  They deserve it!
vd-xT7UugJHiqOFuWu39OA:
We were visiting Nashville and were purely looking for a "deal" on food. We found Antica through groupon or slickdeals and decided to give it a shot.
Location:
Located just outside of Nashville in a good area of town (doesnt look ghetto, but doesnt look fancy). It's in a brand new apartment building.
Food:
I visited over a month ago so I don't vividly remember what I ordered, but I do remember the pork tapas being great. The portions aren't huge, but they're packed with the most thoughtful well-placed ingredients (I had more than 2 tapas orders). After dinner we tried desert which was not too sweet, but tasted great. 
Drinks:
They carried only 4  draft beers. Two of which were from an unknown brewery. Both beers were good. My girlfriend had a few mixed drinks which she ranked as very good because of their uniqueness (one had egg whites in it). 
Service:
We were lucky to have one of the owners taking care of us. He talked to us most of the night and explained the restaurant, where it was coming from, where he hopes it to be. He also introduced us to most of the staff. Seems like a nice guy who has the potential to deliver a great restaurant. 

Overall I'm rating this restaurant 4/5 because I feel that the prices were a little too steep for the quality of food,drinks, and service I received. I will absolutely be returning to this restaurant first next time I'm in Nashville.

A very creative menu that is not "upscale southern" like just about everywhere else in Nashville.  The food and drinks are excellent, and you can't beat half priced wine on Sunday night.  A new Nashville favorite!

over all i give them 5 stars for doing something different in our city.
I'm gonna start by telling you that i ordered everything on the menu, all 15 courses.

Im going to highlight on the Best ones. 
First of all, there are lots of people doing deviled eggs these days.. and these guys take it to the top and did tiny little delicate deviled quail eggs with truffle oil.
Its Amazing, very different. I noticed a bad review of Tomato Gelee and I admit it was amazing and different, and it shouldn't have gotten a bad review. 
It is definitely unlike any other dish I've had in other restaurants.
The Pepita Butter was amazing.
The Faux Bone marrow is extremely unique dish. 
One of my favorite was the Pork belly rillons were just perfect! Amazing! 
my 2nd favorite was the Potato Gnocchi with scallops totally amazing Dish! 

The only negative thing i could say to the restaurant are things that they have to work out because they are so new. For example they don't have a fully developed wine list yet, the wait staff is not educated in wines, for example they couldn't tell me where their bottle of wines came from? from which country? and I had to get the Manager to ensure that I have a french bottle of wine. 

The seating and tables aren't fancy they are just normal of course this place has to start making some money before they could do these kind of things. but as far as judging them in their product, on their food you can really tell that thats where their time and attention has been going. their food truly is different and amazing and deserves a five star review,
OkyiiyLbPCM0HVGp3jps2Q: This is my favorite Mexican restaurant in Nashville. The guacamole is the best I have ever had--very fresh and flavorful. The shrimp is outstanding and very tender. The salsa they bring to the table with chips is particularly good as well. Unlike eating at other Mexican establishments, I never feel heavy and gross after I leave, I think because the food is so fresh and the ingredients are high quality. You get a great value for your money and you get truly authentic food. I highly recommend this place.

Best Mexican I've had outside of my favorite Mexican restaurant in North Carolina. We enjoyed the cheese dip, guacamole, and chicken and steak fajitas. Only disappointment is they don't have margaritas!

This unassuming, and let's just say it, sort of questionable looking restaurant/market is way more than meets the eye. We decided to give it a try after hearing great things about these delicious, fresh, and best of all, cheap tacos they were rumored to serve. 

The interior is nothing fancy, but who cares about the decor at Las Blah-Blah if the food is subpar? I'll take humble, authentic, and tasty, please. The dining area is to the right when you enter and is quite small, probably 8-10 tables total. But what the place lacks in interior design, it makes up for with good service and delicious and inexpensive food. I ordered three tacos (chicken, carne asada (marinated and grilled steak), and barbacoa (usually barbecued beef or pork) with pricing ranging from $1.25-$1.75. Each taco was simply dressed with onions and cilantro, with lime slices for squeezing on if you like a little extra citrusy zip, which I love and even add to my Coke every time I have Mexican. They also bring three salsas to the table for dressing your tacos and the dipping of the chips.  I think one was a tomatillo, the other a slightly spicy red, and another one that was smoky and had more of a kick, maybe salsa verde, but not sure. We also added an order of guacamole, which was obviously made to order and as fresh as it comes. It was deliciously simple, with a few chunks of tomato, onion, lime juice, and cilantro. It was all so simple, yet all so good! 

This isn't the place to impress a date as far as ambience, but if you are looking for some of the best and least expensive authentic tacos and sides, then look no further than the shady looking little orange & yellow block building at 3225 Gallatin Pike.
G4sLH98DHyejB5RcDljaFQ:
Love Starbucks. 

Hate the location of this store. While I love that it is in walking distance from my work, the location is awful. Parking is horrible and there honestly isn't enough space. 
It is a smaller shop, but food and drinks hit the spot. 
I do feel it takes a little longer to get items ordered, but this location is always booming. 
I don't think I've ever seen it slow down, it's almost always packed inside. There is no drive thru at this location

I enjoy frequenting this Starbucks...when I feel that I've given myself enough time before work to go. It gets super busy in the mornings, even at 6:30. The baristas are always kind, and friendly customers! Sure parking is sparse, but this is Green Hills! Lots of space in this Starbucks if you're needing me time or meeting a colleague.
They're always quick with orders, friendly, and make drinks correctly. The Starbucks itself is always clean and well-kept. They also keep free water with cups out at all times
iEKARo8xaNxrRAHA-5tczQ:
Clean and on point!

Many times when you go to a Mexican restaurant it can seem dirty with old furnishings and a dilapidated exterior/interior. Not this place! It's a medium sized place, but it reeks of quality and professionalism. 

Friendly staff and awesome Happy Hour (3PM TO CLOSE) with $3 margaritas and $1.35 draft beer. 

I ordered the California Burritos and holy cow that's a monster. Awesome flavor and great value for the price. Also, the enchiladas are pretty legit. Great patio!

Only downside is it's located as a part of an outdoor shopping mall. You can hear cars driving by constantly one that patio.  This is the only thing keeping it from 5 stars in my opinion.
Awesome fajitas and margaritas, waiting staff was awesome. They'll even sing to you if its your birthday!

Good Mexican! I like their patio. I do not recommend the spinach and cheese enchiladas. Canned spinach is gross and I thought it would be fresh cooked spinach. However, speedy Gonzalez lunch is yum! I will definitely go back here to enjoy some margaritas outside when it's warm.
tRy555bB3co3ur3VCHy3zw: The Falls has an amazing view both during the day and at night. The dual waterfall is beautiful. The venue itself is two levels with plenty of seating. Nice spot for a drink and snack.

I came to the grand Opry on a business trip last week. I had to spend ALOT of time with my boss's boss to rehearse a presentation with about 5 other people. Anyways our rehearsal ended around 10:00 so my boss decided to take the group out for drinks. Very nice location and very open and cool. The service is very good and quick. I had a strawberry margarita on the rocks and it was very good.

Busy place set amongst greenery & water falls. The staff really hustles. We decided to have dessert and coffee. The warm apple crisp with ice cream was wonderful! The coffee was weak and not hot - but it's a bar, not a coffee shop. I was really tempted to order the BBQ chips. Maybe next time chips, wings, and a beer.
RMt9p9efzYfV_cHwXLB-CA: This dinner was perfection! Everything we ordered (which was a lot!) was truly amazing. BEST EATS IN NASHVILLE!!!!

Picked this place out due to their location and being open on a Sunday night for a dinner meeting for work. We were pleasantly surprised! Interesting environment, cool art pieces in the lobby and good food! It's my new favorite go to spot for a nice dinner if I am not into BBQ!

I highly recommend Grey and Dudley.

Best anniversary date spot in Nashville! Amazing food, great service, quirky blue penguins, cotton candy, and attached art gallery makes for a very special evening. Oh and complimentary dessert for your anniversary (just let them know)!
qLLSKapDKrcKgkHyUtLwSw: Love the new look! It's not as loud inside anymore, so we can do more socializing. That's always nice.

My first time going was back in March. Had a great experience then and every time I go. I was able to make happy hour on day. Was the best!!! This has become our new hangout spot. We keep going back!!!
Service was excellent! Some of their cocktails could use a little tweaking, but all in all the Wknd is a great spot to go and have a good time.
Nj4UDyEqIhqrGiGCT3Lv3g: For what this is, an open air beer bar with delicious food, it deserves 5 stars however I have to give it 4 stars for allowing smoking; Because if they didn't allow smoking my husband and I could bring our toddler to enjoy some of the yummy dinner specials. The staff is very nice, hard to find sometimes in this town. Also, they have good unique dinner & soup specials; tonite's was jalapeño crab soup & rib tips.

I'm going to prefix this review by stating that Brewhouse Midtown is my favorite bar in Nashville. Having said that, this satellite location doesn't live up the standard I have become accustom to at Midtown. 

On one hand it certainly feels like the Midtown location inside - an abundance of LCD TVs, rickety looking building, garage door walls, and the ever famous plethora of Bushwackers at just about every table which themselves are worth an automatic 3 stars for any establishment. On the other hand a very key component is missing... the draft selection doesn't seem to be even half of what the Midtown or South locations carry. 

Is it probably the best bar in the West Nashville area? Yeah buddy.

Even though the draft selection isn't huge they still have better than average options including most all the Yazoos which like Bushwakers carry an instant star bonus.

I really liked this place! I came for Wednesday trivia a few weeks ago with just one other person. We got a table right away and had The Friendliest Waitress (TFW). TFW clued us in that there was a special to get a free pint glass with each Newcastle Werewolf beer order on any Wednesday in October! I realize I'm writing this review a bit too late for other readers to take advantage of this deal, but there could be others in the future (your present!) so look for posters! 

I got a huge quesadilla and was only able to eat about 1/3 of it. Pretty tasty. TFW was very attentive and gave us some beer advice. I maybe loved TFW so much because we share a birthday, as I learned when she carded me. (So we're kind of best friends now, and if that biases my review ,so be it.) 

Trivia was fun, there were about 15 teams and mine did NOT win. 

Also be warned - smoking is allowed in this bar!
LWpVee415sblWYWWRL8ROw: Oh my goodness, this is the best "meat & 3" you'll ever find!  The food is fresh, the helpings are quite adequate, and the desserts - OMG - are out of this world!  The cinnamon rolls should be a crime, but all desserts are phenomenal.  Don't miss this place!

After hearing Brian C. hem and haw, I knew I had to check this place out. Went during lunch all by myself after showing some property on the East side. Got a seat right away and the food was out in a jiffy. I loved the tableware  - the long bread dish (with a roll, biscuit, and cornbread), the shabby/chic plates, the mason jar glasses, and the historic Nashville pics on the wall match the atmosphere perfectly. I had a veggie plate and highly recommend the sweet corn puddin' (served in a pastry shell) and the fried green tomatoes (best I've ever had). 

Prices were slightly higher than I expected based on location, but still very affordable. I was a tad underwhelmed by the $5 banana pudding so I'll try a different desert next time - and there will definitely be a next time. This would be a fabulous place to take family or out-of-towners.
Great service. Sweet tea is mason jars and actual dumplings.
zFyLY26u79y2ZxderJ-HmQ:
So I can tell these guys are trying to make this place fly...

I went there last Saturday night with a girlfriend.  We walked in and the check-in girl was ultra-helpful.  We didn't have reservations, but she winked at me and said she would see what she could do.  She made me feel like a celebrity!

We ended up getting 2 seats at the bar.  Austin was our bartender, and, again, he made me feel like a celebrity.  He made a bangin white sangria and was very attentive.  We ordered 2 appetizers - the spicy shrimp & clam ceviche, which was skimpy on size and the potatoes on the side had NO taste.  I won't order it again.  The chips and dip were great, though.  

The atmosphere was phenomenal.  I love the open patio - this will be a great place to be this summer.  

So, 5 stars service, 4 stars atmosphere, 3 stars food.

Keep working, guys!

One of Nashville's best! We went for dinner on Sat at 7:30pm. At first they tried to sit us in the bar area at a high top table w/ small chairs. It was uncomfortable for a dinner experience so we asked to move. We received a very cold shoulder from the hostess but who cares, our real table experience was much better!! We watched the same thing happen three more times as the staff tried to sit people at those horrible bar tables. Anyway, the food was amazing. Some of the most flavorful food I've had since moving to Nashville! I had "the margarita" but my friend tried the margarita "del Diablo." Both were great! They even made a fruit drink for my pregnant friend so she wouldn't feel left out. We started with a large guacamole which was standard but good. My entree was the divine Veggie & Mushroom Enchilada....so much food. Both of my friends chose the taco flights. These taco flights are more like fajitas because all the ingredients come out on your plate and you have to put the taco together! We didn't realize that's how these dishes would come out but the food was great so no complaints! They tried the chicken, shrimp, fish, veggie tacos. No dessert for us because we were stuffed at that point! One other thing to mention is that the people watching is terrific!!! Can't wait to go back.

Ok,  I know Lime is trendy, but it's such a guilty pleasure--the people watching can keep you entertained for hours. The drinks are unbelievable,  and the bartenders/staff  are super-nice and very generous which makes it worth the cost.  I've never been here an not had a good time. I also love the chicken nachos--delish!  If you have a problem waiting for a table, sit at the bar! The service at the bar is great!
uxZfAoXogd5wuQtGF_vTFQ: Love this place, makes me wish I was in NashVegas right now.   I have very fond memories of  relaxing lunches here while enjoying the company of a nice young lady as we skipped class at Vandy.   Tends to get a bit crowded but that is always a good sign, just show up earlier or later.

Always a good go-to spot when you just can't decide what you want to eat, but know it isn't sushi or hibachi.  They have pretty much anything here to satisfy...my personal favorites are the California Chop Chop salad, the tortilla club sandwich and the grilled portabello sandwich, and for an entree, the chicken portabello (the chive smashed potatoes are sooo tasty!).  

You can eat dinner here and have a glass of wine or two for about $25, which is nice.  It's not fancy or pretentious either...just good food and good service.  

I also love the take-out service.  They'll bring right to your car.

I was thinking that since I was in Green Hills, that this place was going to set me back way more than I wanted it to for a weekend lunch.  But I was very pleased to find reasonable prices for enjoyable fare.  They even offered wine selections by flyte, which I think is great even though I didn't sample any of them.  

The service was relaxed and friendly, and the atmosphere was enjoyable as well.
rpyP2hhODCxhAsPmkjOX2g:
Family Pizza Large was the best I've tasted so far. 

Spinach Artichoke Dip exceeds expectations

I love sneaking away for lunch here.  Their calzones are excellent and very filling.  Big bonus....they have a bar!

They are so much better than your fast food Italian places.  The atmosphere is also very nice inside and out.

Nice menu options   Cool atmosphere great service. Spinach dip is highly recommended 

Pizza crust is perfect

Salads very well presented and healthy looking
2kaLFPa1uhGHqSRZMy2RfA: This place has pretty good authentic Mexican food.  They give you plenty of chips and salsa for free to start and the prices are very reasonable.  I had the soft tacos and they were almost as good as tacos I had in Guatemala, served very simple with meat, cilantro and onions.   And it was great that they had non Mexican meals for kids (chicken nuggets and cheeseburger).

Amazing food and great service! Went on a Friday night and was promptly greeted at the host station by the gentleman who was my server. Server was attentive and the food arrived quickly. I had both the cheese dip and the chimichangas espesciales. Both were very good. Margaritas are also great. Would recommend to anyone.

I was invited here for lunch and the food was actually tasty our server was nice and very attentive she sat us down and we ordered our food and it came out quickly I had the chicken and steak fajita and my friend had the chicken and shrimp we both were satisfied with our service.
gEn52Xe8B-sOjPeG8a5QtA: I love this place! Of course, it doesn't hurt that tacos are my favorite food. And yeah, I know, you're a suburban white chick that lives in green hills. But quit being such a cracker and venture out some. Get the carnitas. On anything. You'll thank me. And as much as I love hot sauce, theirs isn't great. The medium has much better flavor.

I really hate going to green hills (seriously the traffic is bonkers) but I have had to change my stance on this issue just to get my Chipotle fix.  I have become a huge proponent of buying humanely and sustainably raised meats as well as local and organic produce.  Chipotle is one of the few if only restaurant in town in this price point that makes such offerings and it's sooooooo GOOD. 

Understand, this is yuppified mexican food but if I'm going that route I need  holy trinity of ingreds to make it work.  Lime, cilantro, and fresh pico.  I'm never never disappointed in my visits and leave full of fresh yummy goodness which is really about all I ask of this world.   I personally go naked on my burritos (not my clothes you pervs) which means without the tortilla to cut the carbs.   I'm salivating a little just describing this.
Utterly delicious. Awe-strikingly awesome. Opposite of abysmal. And hey, the prices are totally reasonable. Go here now.
VLjiat2MmVpfl9UwIwAo1Q:
What a great experience at Night Train. This is a great location with friendly staff and excellent service.

We had the Meatball appetizer first. It was made from scratch in house and covered in made from scratch in house marinara sauce. Wow! Great flavors all around. I could have been just fine with a bowl of the sauce. It was that good.

For dinner, we ordered a large pepperoni pizza. The crust, sauce, cheese and peps were fantastic. The combination of each of these elements was perfect. It is truly my new favorite pizza joint.

We will be making the trip from Nolensville on a regular basis for sure. Can't wait for our next pie.

In my quest to fine a great wood fired pizza, Night Train pizza is at the top of the list. Friendly environment,cold beer, great pizza, 
Highly recommend you checking this place house 
Deep in the gulch of Nashville night train pizza is a must for wood fired pizza,

Holy smokes!!  If you want some legit pizza pies comes to this place and order a night train! It'll knock your socks off! Coming from a fella that has crossed the country to eat pizza is Chicago, LA, New York, Louisville, St. Louis, Vegas, Atlanta and every major city this place is the bees knees! A small but great joint that has me wanting to order another just for the taste of it! Keep making it like you you did today and you'll be alright!
UBriJCMES8rsmY0SiYjtSw: So cute. Coffee was some of the best and we sampled plenty. I have on good authority that the Rice Krispie treats are worth it too. With so many similarly styled drip premium coffee shops to chose from, this one stands out. Maybe it's partly the location, but if you go here, you should also go to goo goo across the street!
The coffee was smooth, rich and hot. We had the daily special soup which was spicy and rich. Service was fast and friendly!

Great atmosphere in the Johnny Cash & Patsy Cline museums! The baristas were very nice and smiled when I walked up. My Mocha Coffee was delicious, and very pretty! Nice to come in from the heat and sit and listen to Johnny Cash in the background!
6TNz9PRdx14NgL24f880dQ: Our favorite place for Mexican food. Not "gourmet," but I'm not sure Mexican food as American's know it is meant to be gourmet. We always leave satisfied and like to make our own dish out of the cheesy rice with chicken and have them add fajita veggies. Very reasonable too.
Excellent service and excellent food every single time! Will continue to come here no matter how far away I live from the restaurant!!! Five star people!

This is my go-to Mexican restaurant for lunch in the spring and fall because they have a GREAT covered outdoor seating area. The food is really good.  I can honestly say I've never had bad service here either.  The waiters and waitresses are always very attentive and helpful.
jcTP0pGBDsWXh-GvIXnkxw:
The food is quite good. The best caldo de res (my husband swears) and the best flan (I swear) in town. They have horchata. I have to mention that because many Mexican restaurants don't have it (weird, I know). 
The only bad thing in here is service. And they don't have hot tea. 
They also have mexican ranchera music playing and karaoke on week end nights. I hate that, so I avoid coming at that time. 
By the way, caldo de res is on week ends only.

Also, it's a place to go if you want to watch a soccer game in a crowd. They have a projector tv for that.

I'm not big on Mexican food so this may or may not be a big help..;(

The Good:  From the outside appearance you can't tell how big or small this establishment is, its a pretty good size equipped with a karaoke stage, and two bars with other TVs scattered throughout the joint. Plenty of room to seat any size party you have. Now as for the food I must say i enjoyed it! I had fajitas(chicken) which came on a very hot plate like it should with rice & beans. Sweet tea was made just like most black folks like it..sweet as hell!!  and that is right up my alley!;) Waiter was prompt, and gave descriptions of what each item on the menu we asked about.  

The Bad: Not much to say about bad, its one of those diamond in the rough places that you don't want everyone to know about because its so good!

Again, I am a huge fan of Nolensville Road restaurants. This is my favorite Mexican restaurant, hands down. Although it is a little out of the way for me, it is always worth the drive. Sometimes (I've seen it sporadically during lunch and dinner) there is even live entertainment! The prices are great and the food, incredible. I always order the Chicken Suiza enchiladas or the Tacos Authenticos with chorizo. It never sets me back more than ten bucks.
PCGb7kqME8aVG4jq2dOIqQ: Jacob Willey is an extremely talented bartender and host.  He can not only make you your favorite cocktail but he will introduce you to amazing new drinks - one of the best in the business we thoroughly enjoyed our stay!  Thanks again Jacob!  Five star service.

Excellent food! Enjoyed the steak dinner last night and then my husband had the bass and I had the risotto tonight. Absolutely delicious! The sauces were deep & complex and the service was impeccable!

Wonderful food, drinks, and service in this small, hidden, restaurant in the Holston House Hotel.

We had breakfast, lunch, and dinner here during our stay.

Standout items:

Smoked trout dip
Cavatelli with braised pork ragu
Pork belly biscuit sliders
Brisket hash with poached eggs
um2W1Gi4-zR7e17t5pZ_Pg: We ate here twice on our trip to Nashville! The rice bowl, tacos, and guacamole were amazing. The chips that came with the guac were my favorite! Super crispy and salty. Plus, the free love music upstairs made the overall experience amazing. It can be a fast or slow dining experience. If you like Mexican and are near Broadway street, stop here!

Wandered in here today looking for a quick and affordable lunch with my students. Needless to say, even the non-Mexican eaters were happy! The menu had something for everyone, was decently priced, and tasted amazing! I had the street tacos and they were to die for! Will definitely be back at some point during our trip again!
This place has the best eats on the street, you gotta check them out. Fair price too. You won't be disappointed.
TJu-3eK6xu91EBeWNmehcQ: I'm so glad that I stumbled into this market after eating at Ginger Thai. They've such a great selection of different dry & canned items, along with fresh produce, frozen items & fresh meat. I like this market better than K&S World market because it doesn't smell like spoiled fish & it looks very clean. The clerk was friendly & answered all my questions. A great local world market.

If you want to do you grocery shopping and get great kabob before you leave this is the place to do it. Everything here is fresh and organic, they source their meats from local Amish farmers and you don't have to pay Whole Food prices for good food.

The Jujeh kabob and Kubideh is on point! If you're a fan of any kind of middle eastern food this place is worth a stop! Pick up a baklava while you're at it!

3.5 would be my rating... not quite a fan but better than OK.  Wandered in here by accident.  Selection was ok. Prices were ok.  They had a Guinness energy drink, which I had to have.

I can't really get excited over this place, but it was cool... I'll be back to shop some more... They had a lot of good looking flat breads and cheeses and meat things, but I was not looking for any of those.
gMbF452jOMyEfLjLaWLx4w:
Baby back ribs have a rub which when slow smoked to perfection have a slight shiny glazed crust. They don't need any sauce to be appreciated but their distinctive orange tinted barbeque sauce of either the hot or mild variety is pretty amazing. Amazing as In "they should bottle this stuff and sell nationally" delicious. 

Pulled pork nothing super special, but just good, especially with the house made sauce. Cole slaw was chopped and refreshing. Baked beans tasted like the Heinz variety, sweet ketchupy.

Mary's apparently has been in business since 1962. Real basic place decorated like a bare bones diner with a counter into the kitchen. Clientele was very local: let's just say that us Texas toast Silicon Valley types stuck out like a soar thumb. But everyone was very kind and accommodating in helping us make the right choices. The guys hanging out in the parking lot even moved their feet to the side when we parked out front.

Dinner for 4 ended up a bit more expensive than expected. $20 for a slab of ribs (large enough for 2-1/2 people), $10 for a plate of pork shoulder and 2 sides, some extra sides and 3 sodas was over $40.

Bathrooms looked brand new with hand sensor paper towel dispensers and all. Totally incongruous with the rest of the place.
Amazing local food. So nice to find something not tourist saturated.  $60 fed 5 hungry men and we will be back. Highly recommended

I struggled with the fifth star but the hot sauce is so good Mary's merits 5 stars. I had the ribs and they were meaty and succulent. I thought they were braised. If they were smoked then they were finished wet. Whatever it was it worked for me. The dining room is bright and clean, the service was fine, and the camaraderie was unexpected and enjoyable. Next time I'll try the pulled shoulder. Any excuse for more sauce.
MutOppTPd_HgpzfZVyoVpg:
The best place to eat when I say no onions
They make my food specially for me thank you
And you 10min from me
I'm allergic to onions

Good place, good crew, awesome people. Me and a friend eat here regularly for breakfast before work at 4 am.

Definitely worth putting in a rotation of places to get food at. Bit greasy but people used to such foods should find no trouble. If grease bothers you, I find soda helps it go down better.

Cheese fries and loaded fries, though a bit small portioned, are gameday worthy. The chicken rings and onion peels are good sides, and even better stacking on the sliders.

4 stars......once a year I try my best to find a White Castle.  They bring back some wonderful childhood memories.   You have to love the soggy, onion and little hamburger patties.   A taste that is one of a kind.
j9fzpALNCgvDwCXmA5Vcjw: It is just a solid coffee shop with great food done simply and well executed. Might be their best offering of the group, or a close 2nd to the large Fido.

I love all of the other Bongo locations around Nashville but this was my first visit to BOX. The menu is refreshingly different from the other locations. My boyfriend and I both ordered The Waverly sandwich with bacon and came to the same conclusion- this is now the best breakfast sandwich in Nashville! (Former first place was Moto biscuit from Barista Parlor.) The sweet potato biscuit is an excellent slightly sweet contrast to the savory flavors from the egg, cheese, and bacon, and the hot sauce adds depth without being overwhelming. Will definitely be back.

Ughhh SO GOOD. I got the Waverly Biscuit (but left off the cheese and avocado). HOLY MOLY. That sweet potato biscuit really made me feel some type of way... like how do y'all do that?? IT WAS SO GOOD. And I think it was decently priced ($7 - I'll do it for this bomb biscuit). This place has a nice atmosphere and a good menu. A great space for children too. Lots of toys to occupy them while you enjoy your meal. Good for every meal, but their breakfast is definitely award winning. Love starting my mornings here!
6KjQQDmLqazKCq-l2Y_Lnw:
Love this place!  Wonderful staff. They really personalize your visits here and that isn't too common with big franchises. 

They know my order as soon as I walk in!

My new favorite sandwich place!  Because of this Jersey Mike's, I no longer go to Subway.  

When you come in you're greeted by the friendly staff immediately.  All questions are answered patiently and recommendations are given if needed.  Once your order is taken, they get right to work, quickly making up your sandwich by getting bread, slicing the meat and cheese right in front of you and getting your "fixin's".

Their menu has hot and cold sandwiches and although the selection of veggies is not large, you'll find that you don't miss all the extras when you take a bite into the scrumptiousliciousness.  I always get mine done "Mike's way" and then at the end of the line you can choose drink, chips, and/or cookies.  You know the normal combo type stuff.

I haven't visited other locations yet but this one in Bellevue is well staffed, clean, and inviting.  I haven't come out disappointed and I am a picky sandwich eater!

I walked in on a whim with a $2 discount coupon, and my life has been irrevocably altered. Why God do you torture me by putting the universe's two most delicious sub shops right across the street from each other in Bellevue? Penn Station, near Kroger, has been my go-to place for subs since I moved here, but Jersey Mikes is also a delicious option. I'm so torn! I wish I'd never seen that coupon.

Penn costs a little more than Jersey, but Penn gives you a sandwich the size of your head and a bucket of fries big enough to feed a small nation. I kid you not; I fed my family of four for an entire weekend off one Penn sub and an order of fries. On the other hand, Penn has the feel of a greasy burger place that makes subs whereas Jersey is the purest and most beautiful sandwich experience I've ever known.

Jersey proudly displays their meats and cheeses in a cold case. They only dane to open and slice their wares before your eyes when an order is placed. This joint is serious about freshness, quality, and taste. Their 'regular' sub at just under $7 is an ample meal. They have just the basic toppings, but what more do you really need? It's just the right mix of veggies, hots, and acrids to make your mouth water for more.

Its a little vexing how much these people love New Jersey with murals and maps plastered wall-to-wall. I've been there. Jersey comes in a close second to Tijuana for the title of "North America's Armpit". Nonetheless, these people make a damn good sandwich. So, I'm willing to overlook the fact that their decor brings back memories from my youth of fighting off Jersey Shore lepers while driving an Atlantic City jitney.

Jersey Mikes was a pleasant surprise. I will definitely be back for more!
rhS9NZKPpgL-Xrn6ihtfsA: Absolute BEST customer service every time I go in there. My husband and I were visiting from California last year and found this gem. A year later and we booked a hotel close to this location so we could have breakfast here every morning. Diane on 3rd shift is so awesome. She remembers you and always has great conversation to keep your visit personable. Food is ALWAYS amazing and cooked right in front of you.

Pretty good cheap food for the late night crowd.  You do not need to go to the movies for entertainment.  Just go to the waffle house at 1am and watch the drunks roll in.  I recommend parking at the far end of the lot to keep your car safe.  I recommend getting breakfast.

You have to go knowing what to expect. Although I had never been there, I knew it would be greasy fast food.  With that in mind, our 11 yo and 14 yo cleared their plates and said they would order the same food again. The pecan waffle was delicious and we didn't need syrup.  I got some "real food" with sautéed mushrooms, onions with the has browns.  Coffee was worth a second cup. Maria was an excellent server. She was friendly, gracious and humble. She knew her limits and did her best with a smile despite the crowd. That's when you see exceptional service- when the system is tested.  It was busy with CMA Festival 2018 but the wait was short. We were greeted and seated. I waited outside and one couple walked away. I wondered if others walked away seeing the crowd and had the greeter been outside, she may have gotten them to come in and wait because the wait was short. Our food did come fast like their promise.
6g2_uR1GadLK8rJMplW-Qg: I just stopped in to grab dinner and a few groceries and decided to try something different tonight. Suzy's Hot Ham and Cheese is to die for. It's definitely my new favorite. Simple and delicious! And the house made chips and French onion dip are the perfect compliment! If you haven't yet - go eat here! (And pick up some good beer and groceries while you're there!)

This is a new spot in the Wedgewood/Houston area and I enjoyed it. As the name states it is a deli with a market attached to it with health conscious items sold, so it's perfect for those that live in the area and don't feel like traveling to Whole Foods in Green Hills or Turnip Truck in East Nashville or the Gulch... 

I ordered the Tofu Banh Mi and loaded fries minus the bacon...I was so looking forward to the vegan chili cheese fries but was disappointed when told it was no longer on the menu. The staff was very very friendly and made suggestions for the menu and was attentive after my food arrived.

My only little vices (and this is just my personal preference)...the loaded fries could have been a little more creamier; they were dry even with the crema and the sandwich was a little soggy I guess from the marinade...I only say this is because I get a tofu Banh Mi from another place and it's not soggy at all...

But all together my experience was good and I will probably go again and just get another item...

At the time there is adequate parking along the street
Amazing sandwiches with a nice deli to grab a few groceries. Loving this new weho area
3J0X62Tlmzyo04SOgN2mLg: Honest burrito. Not bad for airport food. Fresh and fast. Comes with chips. And they have chocolate milk.

I have been craving a Burrito for a long time, on a connection in Nashville a went searching for food in the food court. To satisfy my craving I went to blue coast. 

I ordered the maximum r&b which is vegan, after all the stuff they put on the burrito is was bland, and flavor less. There was nothing I could add to it. The customer services was good, speedy. I may try it again.

The nachos are delicious. I usually grab them on my way out of town if I have a night flight. There is a really good chip to topping ratio and all the toppings are good quality. The queso is really good which makes the whole nacho entree work well. This is my favorite place to eat at the airport.
4Ye_rSE-PiyWBZljASqwNQ:
I've only been here in large groups--birthdays, girls' nights, etc.  And service is wonderful!  The pizza is pretty good too, although the last time I had it, it was a little too "bready" for my liking.  But, I still ate the entire thing so I guess it wasn't all that bad!  

They offer a huge variety of fresh toppings--my usual mushroom and black olive is always a delight--as well as a wheat crust instead of their regular.  Also, you can get soy cheeses if you prefer!  I have a lot of vegetarian friends who adore Da Vinci's, but there are a lot of tasty meats on the menu too.  

Pizza and Yazoo beer in a cozy setting?  Can't go wrong!  I take away one star only because parking can often be a bear.
Yum.  This converted house has cozy, romantic lighting and well executed gormet pizzas.  Good, affordable wine list.

Da Vinci's catered to vegans way before it was trendy to do so. I remember my vegan friends driving down to Da Vinci's from Brentwood to pick up some soy cheese pizzas. It's good to know that someone in Nashville will do that. 

I go more for the real cheese though. The crust of their pizza is really interesting. It's a cornmeal crust, so it's kind of like eating pizza on cornbread, which is weird, but good. 

They have great toppings. I love the shrimp and scallop pizza. The old house that Da Vinci's is in is a lovely setting, although it's across the street from a couple of strip clubs, but Mambu is next door too.
NjozOOgSQqGFn8Wnbn8HkQ:
A review for a fast food chain restaurant? Why YES! Because this location shocks me every single time we've gone. Murfreesboro Pike is not lacking in drive through options (more than I've ever seen in a two mile radius) but this Arby's really stands out. 

Why is this different?

* They ALWAYS get my order right. 
* Their drive thu is super friendly - even letting us order a few minutes before closing. * And happily too!
* The inside is clean clean clean
* Every thing I get looks like a replica from the glossy photo shopped marketing images. Piled high roast beef, perfectly lined up with the bun! Salads that are bursting with bright leafy greens! They never skimp and they take care to make sure that your food looks as good as it tastes. 

So if you're in the area and have all of the other chain burger places to chose from - this particular Arby's is one I have always had a great experience with!
I'm really impressed by Arby's as a franchise.  They have really stepped up on quality and customer service. Great clean location here if you are craving Arby's!!

My daughter & I dine here pretty regularly.  Normally they are quick and the food is always hot.  There are a few employees that aren't as good as others but overall I would say it's a pretty good store.
q_FUxVwG3PqmvBTnZtM7vA:
This place was great!! Very friendly crew the calzone was delicious 
Tried pep sausage and spinach 
Very good size and tastes amazing
Highly recommended 
Will try pizza next time
Always friendly. Love the pizza, even if it's not Vero Napolitano. Good stuff, Maynard!!
Try the shrimp pizza.  Seriously.  It's small, but it's flavored just right and each shrimp is like a little nugget of gold.
Yf279o0gVtB9a4qYq0JV9g: Yes, I gave this place 5 stars.  Well deserved.  I have to be honest and say I went in not expected much.
