The place is deceivingly large so they can easily accommodate larger parties. We had a party of 5 and with our reservation were sat immediately, drink order taken immediately and were great with their wonderful never ending supply of chips and salsa. You can easily eat healthy on their menu.  They have a great flavorful Kale salad which I added salmon to and ended up bringing some home.  However, I highly suggest trying the apps as we got Ropa Vieja and I fully intend on going back and making this my meal.  My husband got the taco dinner with 3 different tacos and it was amazing.  Everyone at our table was completely satisfied.  The manager stopped by to check on things which was refreshing especially since it seems the service industry in Nashville is lacking in certain restaurants.  Our server was on top of things and we didn't have to wait for anything.  Even if you just go in for drinks and apps its worth the trip!

I give Alegria 4 stars for the restaurant it used to be. The food remains delicious, but they have continued to raise their prices into the stratosphere, while simultaneously lowering the amount of food served. Customers are now required to buy their rice and beans as separate items from their entree, while their entree prices are now a minimum of 13-15$. 


So, if money isn't an object for you, this place is a must. But it isn't the type of restaurant it was when people last wrote Yelp reviews on this place.

OMG... Most amazing, unique Mexican food.  Ordered the Ropa Vieja... Plantains, flank steak... So good.  Husband ordered a sandwich with plantain chips as the "bun".   And... One of the only margaritas I have found in nashville that isn't full of sweet filler stuff.  Just great tequila and lime juice.  And, I nice atmosphere.  Sitting at the bar watching AFC playoffs while enjoying our gourmet Mexican food.  Even the basics ... The chips and salsa are extraordinary:)
8j6dorsexMjzSCNmBazH0A:
this place? amazing. simply amazing.

there is a great little lady that takes care of you, i think her name is arlene perhaps? she's so warm and inviting, it seems she really does like making sure you have a great experience. every time i go, there is always a bunch of mailpersons eating there as well, which i guess speaks volumes. they all drive by a TON of places to eat, but always go to sicily. the lunch specials are insanely cheap, and the pizza specials are no different.

it's an out of the way location, yes, but the pizza and baked ziti are easily worth the trek!

eric b

Watch the chef make and cook your food.  Every single thing is fresh made to order even the Alfredo sauce.  Love the new location.  Food is still amazingly mouth watering.  Order to go or eat in. Either way a great experience.  Ordered 2 calzones to go and watched them make a pick up order and a sit down order.

We went there on their Anderson Rd. location.  Just so glad they are BACK!!!!  Pretty much best pizza in town that we've had.  Give them a shot...You will not be dissapointed...except no delivery..;-(....but worth the trip.
8p_D0W23kkn-VC3txhdI7Q: The açaí bowl and dragon fruit bowl were absolutely delicious! Everything is so fresh and healthy. I love the unique things in the cooler too like bee pollen and to-go snacks. Great for breakfast or a healthy meal/snack!
Service is great. Bowls are delicious but why are they basically juice? Blending more ice into them might help with the consistency.

Perfect place to grab a quick smoothie! My husband and I were waiting in line for brunch at a place around the corner, and were getting hungry. We remembered we had passed this juice bar, so while he waited in line, I walked down to grab us a smoothie. It was about 8am on a Monday, there was no in line, the girl working was super nice and pleasant, and it didn't take long for me to get my smoothie. We shared the green smoothie, it tasted really good! It was sweet but not too sweet, and it wasn't super think and heavy like a lot of other smoothies I've had. I noticed they had a grab-and-go section too, which would be convenient if you're really on the run.
ExO09lWfi4FKtqtEb_DrBA: Finally! A good place to relax and drink GOOD wine at the airport. My husband and I got flights of wine and the charcuterie board. Our vacation started early thanks to this little gem. They were slammed when we got a table but the servers were fast, friendly and very knowledgeable when trying to decide on which wines to try. If you love the finer things in life this place is for you! Cheers!!!

Excellent restaurant on the C concourse. Had a lovely Chardonnay, the braised pork taco and some delicious tomato soup. Service was excellent. Only complaint, tables were a little small. Concourse life I guess.
Overpriced of course but good service. Wish they had more than 1 Chardonnay to choose from.
8NqrX9MRsjql8lGiuxlmeg:
I will first say I didn't try the pizza.  I had a salad and bruschetta.  Both were beyond amazing.  I can honestly say that the bruschetta was the best I've ever had.  

This is a hole in the wall place, a friend who is a local metro police officer told us about this place.  The decor is kind of dated and we had to switch out a couple chairs because they were wobbly, but I am giving a free pass based on how awesome the food way.  My kids and a friend all had pizza and they loved it.

This is a pretty good little traditional style Italian shop just off Elliston that serves tasty pizzas, calzones and subs.  I know in the past I've endorsed the nearby Obie's, but Michaelangelo's has equally tasty and authentic pizzas and subs.

Michaelangelo's is relatively inexpensive, and it makes for a pretty good spot to stop in if you're shopping or hanging around in the Elliston area.  It's around the...well, corner from Corner Bar and Flashback, so people often overlook it.  It's the place with the large mural of part of Michelangelo's "The Creation of Adam," and it's kind of hard to miss once you round that corner.

The place has a typically deserted dining area and a nice little split-level upstairs that's cool for a lazy afternoon.  The staff is pretty fast but not especially welcoming, but you can have a pretty hearty meal for around $7.  Try their meatball sub--very tasty.

Got a Philly cheestesteak sub. Its delicious... a little salty and oniony, but I like it!  Also, I was really happy with the delivery guy.  Even though, it took him some time to get to me, he knew where my residence hall was and was very friendly.  He smiled and said "Hi, how are you?"  Amazing! ... most of my delivery guys are kind of jerks...
IZnZZsJ5W-VBY-O084gzCQ: Good service, good food. I think this is your best choice for Mexican food in southeast Nashville. The service is great and the food comes out quickly.

This place is under new management. Joaquin is absolutely amazing. We moved to the neighborhood last month and have been here the last 3 weekends! Excellent food, friendly service, comfortable atmosphere! 5 star experience. We will surely be regulars at this mexican restaurant.
vzPISIq7pe9RTLvkUYVoLw: Great local bar. Awesome beers (and some wines!) with great outdoor seating. Recommend you check it out, neighbors ! We need more awesome places that aren't overrun by the tourist horde.
This place is great and something the neighborhood has needed for some time.  Not only that but they won the best burger in Nashville.  The seating out front provides some great people watching too!

We came during burger week and luckily there was no wait when we arrived and were seated immediately. My boyfriend and I each got the Smashbuger and wow were they juicy and delicious. Well deserved first place for receiving Nashville Burger Week 2018. The only downside during our visit was that there were beers and appetizers that we wanted to order but were told they ran out (at 4pm...). We ended up ordering the salt and vinegar chips but there were remnants of the cajun and rosemary in there too. Guess they used the same bowl to mix the chips. The beers were fine. 

We'll be back for those burgers once they settle in and hopefully they won't run out of the beers and appetizers that we want.
7gqRNDQs9ezTngtXCZ03Fg:
I go there once a week for a massage. Staff is extremely friendly and experienced. Love browsing the little shop area as well. If you're into crystals, meditation and natural healing, you will love it as well.

Food is okay. Juices as well. Can't complain, but that's really not why I go there. Great place to relax and pamper yourself the healthy way.

So I recently finished reading a book on the human alimentary canal (Gulp by Mary Roach-highly recommend it-yelp 5 stars fosho).... now those that have visited Symmetry before know exactly why this is relevant. Let me paint you a picture.

It was a hot Nashville day. My roommate and I had just spent the morning doing fun things like paperwork, getting lost, and bike riding. We were hungry and it was lunch time. Remembering this sweet groupon I had on hand, I suggested we stop by Symmetry.  Sweet patio, I thought. Oh check out these sweet specials... oh vegan chipotle pizza, fresh squeezed juices, colon hydrotherapy. Oh wait. What. Yeah. That's EXACTLY the combo I wanted.

We both ended up ordering the "probiotic plate" which consisted of sauerkraut and other pickled things such as full on asparagus rods. So I definitely made sure to capture the expression of my Southern bred/bread, duck hunting roommate when he got his platter. In short, if you hangry, this is not your place. But hey, it was a great experience. I wandered the store and checked out some healing crystals, incense, essential oils, nutritional yeast... basically your typical Bonnaroo tent. Oh and we got our 10% alkaline water on. Not quite sure what it does...but Im definitely feeling that my blood pH is so in tune right now. But yeah, it was just your typical Thursday lunch. Just eatin' my sauerkraut on a patio while reading about the benefits of colon hydrotherapy. WHICH might I add, is completely unnecessary when you go ahead and order their probiotic plate. Ya dig?

And now we will be eating hot dogs and hamburgers for the next week.

Came here just for a quick snack/juice. Got a super yummy chia pudding with granola and a green juice. My mom got one of their 'healthy' oatmeal cookies (which was so good!). They didn't have tons of menu options, but what they had was delicious. I wish I had more time to explore the store, though, seems like a really cool place.
VpqlVgFmWWqjiVjwWDYy5w:
In a nutshell: Stellar service, great atmosphere, and thoughtful food. I can't wait to go back.

I had never heard of Allium until my wife decided we were headed there for dinner one night after work. I was really in the mood for authentic Spanish tapas, but have never found any in Nashville. So what I usually end up doing is finding a place with interesting apps/small plates and making a meal of it. Allium was a perfect choice. We ordered the goat cheese and onion tart, which was rich, but had a great flavor and was a great introduction for what was to come. The escargot in puff pastry was very decadent and would have been passable at just about any fine French restaurant. We also shared the shrimp cakes which, both of us agree, was the standout dish. Perfectly briny and crunchy, with a little lemon zing. (I have thought about this dish a few times since we at there last week. I hope it's on the Sunday brunch menu.) Last, but not least, we shared the warm trout salad. The dish was just as described, but I felt it lacked a little of the imagination of the other dishes we ordered. 

The dining room and bar are both beautiful and the view is as-advertised -- perfect. Our server was around just enough to ensure we had what we needed, but he left us alone enough to let us talk uninterrupted. As we left, we spoke for a few minutes with one of the owners who gave us the background story on the building and the restaurant. A very nice guy who is obviously involved in the business and cares about customer feedback. 

We are already looking forward to heading back for Sunday brunch soon and we intend to sample more of the dinner menu as well.

This place is killer for lunch - the wife works downtown for the gubbermint, so from time to time we will meet here for an afternoon work date.

A great, open space with hardwoods, booths against the wall, and tall windows to give excellent natural lighting during the day, and soft pendant lighting at night.

For lunch, they are essentially a standard French bistro.  I love their Croque Madame - really hits the spot with a side of frites - perfect amount of Mornay sauce as well.  They also have some very good, very inventive salads and the same can be said about their soups.  I've only stolen bites, but their quiches are quite good as well.  Never super-crowded and some great dining just across the interstate from downtown.

I have also eaten dinner there as well, they have an excellent bar and appetizer list.  Their dinner options are pretty creative and they do an especially good job with fowl.  They have have a nice selection for the vegetarians out there, too.

Their specials are always quite excellent and they always seem to have some for appetizer, salad and dinner courses - I haven't had a bad one yet!

I have been here twice and enjoyed it quite a bit both times. Cool decor and great food. Also, a nice view of the lit up downtown at night.  I really like the roasted chicken with mashed potatoes and haricots verts. Good salad to go along with it as well. A lot of people had problems with the parking, but I didn't. You park in the garage, go to the elevator, go up to the Allium floor and the front door was right there. Don't know if that is something they had changed since they opened.
TrLVI1qVuoiQRgMObsFIrQ: Went to this place for the first time yesterday and will be going back again many times in the future. I had a terrific roast beef sandwich and corn chowder. Great staff. Lisa, the owner clearly is passionate about serving great food. Can't wait to try some other offerings. For such a reasonable price, you'd be hard pressed to find a lunch spot with fresher, tastier ingredients

Best vege sandwich around. Loaded with cucumber, avocado, shredded carrots, sprouts and who knows what else. Bursting with flavor. Good bread. It's called the Grassmere. Next time I'll try to have some self-control and take a picture before I've eaten it all.

This is a hidden gem that's a great place for lunch! The past two times I had the chicken salad plate (or as I like to say, chicken salad salad). It's a generous helping and they give you a side of bread that is to die for. I love their honey mustard dressing as well.

I would also highly recommend you try one of their homemade cookies. Fast and friendly service and the prices are very reasonable. Can't wait to try this spot for breakfast while I am off for the summer!
DpbHQG4QKnWJQ81kLsEosQ: What a perfect place to eat for lunch. Monday and Tuesday they have the best food for 10 bucks. 2 trip buffet. Can't go wrong with a combo. You will be so full and the BBQ is outstanding. One of the best BBQ places in town.

I only ate there once but was greatly surprised the staff, the food was very very good. The one thing I like was the building it look like a run down house needing a lots of work and the inside look the same but the charm and food really made you enjoy eatting there. The atmophere is the best I have seen in a long time and I will go back as soon as I can.

GREAT place to grab lunch. I'm a big fan of the bar b q as well....especially with the "sweet heat" sauce. The sides, cornbread and cobbler are must haves too. The owner is a huge Auburn fan so it's fun to give him a hard time.
lfUk-gziE6jWViibLuVcjw:
Nice new place, open late (1am daily, 3 am on the weekends) near the Murfreesboro Road exit off I-24, about 4 miles southeast of downtown. 

The tacos are really quite tasty. And, the kind folks at Las Adelitas throw in a bag of freshly made tortilla chips and three different salsas - a classic mild, red tomato salsa, a moderately spicy pureed salsa verde, and a spicy orange salsa - for free! The salsas are all great, and the chips were still warm.

Tacos are priced at $1.50 a piece. I tried a few each of pollo, barbacoa, and pastor. I think the pollo and pastor are tied for my favorite, but the barbacoa was incredibly tender and good as well. They are served with chopped cilantro and onion, some form of grilled onions and raw cucumber slices. Like most of the taco trucks littered up and down Murfreesboro Road and Nolensville Pike, the tacos are on the small side, so if you're hungry, I'd recommend ordering at least five.

I haven't tried yet, but the menu of items that aren't tacos is extensive. I don't believe they have a liquor license yet, but there's a rumor that they'll serve beer before long.

Wow!! I took a risk and ordered from here off Uber Eats for the first time ever since there was free delivery. I was so impressed. I am always searching for authentic Mexican food in Nashville, and this completely satisfied that for me in addition to surpassing my expectations. I highly recommend ordering the el pastor tacos!! If I were to order again I would get 3 el pastor and a side of rice and beans. The corn tortillas tasted home made and were super thick/fluffy. The meat was perfect and very tasty- especially the el pastor. Overall it was so good. They even threw in free chips! We will be going back for sure.

I'm from California, I travel a lot for work. I am usually very disappointed with Mexican restaurants outside of California  However, I was very impressed with this place. I had the carne asada tacos and asada quesadilla both were very tasty! The service was also very good. I'm only giving 4 stars because they didn't have beer.  I was told beer it is coming later this week. The food was definitely best Mexican food I've had outside of CA!
1mWTFbb1AyybubkyHl1lzg:
Yes, yes, and yes! Looking for a real good authentic mexican food? Then you must try this food truck!

You can't go wrong with anything you order from here. I've pretty much have had all of their tacos here but I think the best thing they sell here is their bbq burrito. It ain't American BBQ, it is Mexican BBQ which is really a very tender slow cooked meat. Honestly when I first had it, I was hooked and it was over with. It was so good, I honestly could have eaten another one!

I had family members from the Philippines and I made sure I brought them here to try it. It is THAT good!

Had a really good experience here! We ordered the Barbacoa Burrito and the El Pastor Burrito ($5ea). They were really tasty, but probably too much food for lunch. Need to try the Tacos for lunch, but this was our first foray into eating at one of these Mexican food stands and we were well pleased. 

I only rated it a 4 star because we have nothing to compare it against. When we have tried a few more places, I may change my rating.
The beef tongue tacos are excellent here, highly recommend them. I love this little place.
0eRWVcA5OYfuzlSj0eMy1A: We love the barbque from this place! It is in a gas station ( I think it is Shell, not positive) next door to Loveless Cafe. The barbque at Loveless Cafe is tourist priced (high). This barbque tastes great and is not dried out. The owner is always friendly and courteous. Although you can eat inside, we typically pick up a pound of barbque at their drive thru window.

I've not eaten here since the flood of '10 ( always forget it's there) but last night the husband went out and got a pound of BBQ and some slaw and an order of fried pickles for our son.  The pork was really good, moist and very flavorful.  The slaw as nice too.  Only beef was the fried pickles.... A little on the greasy side but that won't deter me from trying to frequent there more often.
3-G16lOq1sbZf-SDKGxVwA:
So sure, this a fast-food sandwich chain and nothing ultra remarkable but I have to hand it to this little shop here in the SoBro neighborhood in downtown Nashville. When you order a sandwich online for delivery, which I tend to do at least once a week, it is made exactly as you order no matter how custom you make it and its delivered in rocket  speed no matter the weather conditions  not ignoring the fact they have to ride their bike  uphill to my office.  The online ordering couldnt be easier and I love the settings you can save for even faster ordering. 
Now again, its just a sandwich - but they are delicious and three thousand times better than anything Ive ever had from Subway or Quiznos. 
Tip: add the hot cherry pepper  to any and all sandwiches.
My favorite sub shop! I always order the same thing - beach club, no mayo, add hot peppers. And it's always good! One block away from work, fast and great service! Love it!

This is a 5 star rating in comparison to other jimmy johns. You know what you're getting here. It's no 5th and Taylor, but this location delivers 100% on your expectations for fast, good subs. 

More often than not, I'll get my food before the credit card machine has finished processing my order. It's actually impressive how much of a well-oiled machine they've got running. No complaints!
cUrJKuZYmGnsKYsOpJoHQA: The food was yummy and the price was fair but the atmosphere felt unclean and confused. I'll go back because the food was wonderful and they don't appear to cook with MSG. Egg roll was great.

There isn't much in the Nashville's Arcade anymore but Oriental Lunch has still been standing since I was a kid. It's a family owned business with some bomb Chinese food. For the price it's worth it. Get it to go or stay and eat. The owners are really nice. One of the few mom and pop gems left in Nashville.
This place is wonderful! I've tried several of the dishes and was not disappointed. Service is quick and friendly and I'll be coming back the next chance I get!
7WNgNsURfs2Ag2q3sdUypw: I'm staying in town for work for a few days and didn't feel like leaving the hotel to go find food. After reading the reviews I was very skeptical about ordering from this dominos but decided to roll the dice. My food arrived on time, hot and was exactly what I was expecting from a Domino's pizza.

So we wanted to order pizza on a Saturday night  and our guests suggested Domino's ... wait - really?   Being gracious hosts we agreed and you know what - it was really great!

We ordered 3 pizza's - 2 gluten free and all were real tasty ... thin crust, just the right amount of toppings - delivered in less than 30 minutes (on a Saturday night no less)  and the price was definitely a good deal. 

I was concerned with the lower ratings on Yelp but I must say I was really happy with everything and will be ordering from this Dominos again.  

This was an easy 5 star experience!!

Even tho they have bad reviews, this was the only pizza place that would deliver to our Motel other than the room service menu that we had previously ordered from in our room that took over 1:20 min even tho the motel was slow and we were told 35 min. So when the big kids got hungry again I looked elsewhere to order for them. 
Dominos is what I found to deliver. The girl that took my order was the sweetest most patient(there was tons noise in background and I don't know dominos menus at all) person I have ever given my order too over phone. I wish I could remember her name to give her gratitude on YELP!, but great job keep up your perkiness. It puts smiles on people's faces . 
Our food was good the boys devoured it and it didn't take nearly as long as the room service pizza. They were super busy also it sounded like in the background. Thumbs up  to you.
7XgqdiQQGouDTXgiHIEjRw: As good as it gets for a Cici's. Mediocre pizza, not so good noodles but yummy brownies. This location had a very clean and new bathroom. Pizza selection as limited due to it being busy. However, I was impressed overall.

It's a Pizza Buffet and they are generally busy.  They shout "Welcome to CiCi's as you come in and also say "Good Bye" as you leave.

It's an older location, so it looks old.  However, I have never seen it dirty even after a Lunch Rush.  The Pizza's are fresh and warm and if you like they will make you your own Pizza with your favorite toppings.  It doesn't take long for them to bring it to your table.

I frequent this location often when I can sneak away from work.  If you want Gourmet Pizza this is not your place.  If you want Buffet Pizza - this is one of the best.

Only been once, due to distance from home, and it was like 7 years ago, but gotta chip in here.

Far from the house as I said, but I was hungry, so stopped in to try it.  Price stupid-cheap for all-you-can-eat.

It wasn't the highest quality pizza, but astounding variety -- and, for five bucks, ridiculous.

... but what really impressed was that the manager came up to my table and asked if there was anything I wanted that I didn't see on the buffet.

I jokingly said something like "I love anchovies, but nobody ever puts anchovies on a buffet since so many people hate them, so yeah never mind, but thanks very much anyway for asking!!"

He Had The Kitchen Make One With Anchovies!!!!
Just for me!!!!

Well, a halfie; a sausage-mushroom with anchovies on half.  I ate the entire sausage-mushroom-anchovy half (childhood favorite).

If they were a bit closer, I'd be back *much* more often.

Big, fat, solid five-stars for what it is.
w-3bSeOcplOJ1hZUghC22A:
By giving Printer's Alley 4 stars, I mean, "This is an interesting part of town that I enjoyed checking out during the day," NOT, "I like to spend my nights and my money here ogling girls at gentlemen's clubs."  

I really do think Printer's Alley is an interesting slice of downtown Nashville - this narrow brick alley, its walls sprinkled with neon and painted declarations of its "historical" character, is a pretty unique sight in downtown Nashville, especially considering its proximity to the tourism industry polish of the recently conquered river front.  Also, I hear that Sam's Sushi Bar on Printer's Alley has some darn good sushi.
This place was fun!  We were here celebrating a friends 40th bday!  Cool place!  Some great spots here :))

I can't comprehend the terrible reviews by people who didn't even give the Alley a chance... or those that review the Alley based strictly on the Brass Stables. There's so much more to it.

Give me Printer's Alley over Broadway or 2nd Avenue ANY day. 

- Bourbon Street Blues and Boogie Bar has amazing history and the music you can hear there is amazing. 
-Fiddle & Steel Guitar Bar is the Cheers Bar of Nashville, where you'll find regulars who are like family to each other, and who are always ready to welcome visitors. 
-Events on 3rd is a popular wedding venue today, and it holds an amazing history that anyone in the Alley will be happy to share.
-Kelli's and Lonnie's brings you karaoke and are popular with the college crowds.  
-Fleet Street has delicious foot and is tucked away, almost transporting you to another place and time. 
-And, yes, there is the strip club... I look at it that as the throwback to the Alley's history and nothing more. 
-Late night eats are available at the Dog House and the Alley Dog (I recommend the Dog House, myself).  
-Oh and did I mention drink prices are wayyyyy more reasonable than what you'll find on Broadway? Because they are.

More than anything, though, you'll find people who look out for each other. Strike up a conversation with one of the bar's employees, and you'll find hard working, good people who are (usually) happy to help you with anything you need.
d0_w_AALoxcv_fh39eVYSQ: I've only been in Nashville for about a week and I've already visited Sparkle twice since it's near where we are living. I like the cupcakes. They are pretty large, especially for the price. I tried the strawberry cheesecake cupcake on my first visit and really liked it. I'm not usually a big fan of cream cheese frosting but this frosting was really good. The strawberry cake was nice and moist, but I do wish it had a bit more of a strawberry flavor. Last night I had the birthday cake cupcake, which is your basic vanilla cupcake with vanilla frosting. Again, the cake was nice and moist (and I showed up after 6:30 pm so I was expecting a dry cupcake). The frosting was good but a little sweeter than I would prefer. Overall I'm impressed and will definitely return.

Best.  Cupcakes.  Ever!

Look, I'm not what you would describe as 'small'.  I'm the type of guy that has never been told "Josh, you are looking skinny!  You should eat something!"  So, needless to say, when it comes to cupcakes - I've had more than my fair share.

So, let me first go into how I got to try Sparkle!  

I am a member of 'Groupon' - which, if you aren't aware, they offer a coupon a day for a low, low price for something typically much higher priced.  So was the case here.  It was $15 bucks for $30 worth of cupcakes.  So, I love cupcakes and though - what the hell?!  Why not?

Then, after my purchase - it dawned on me:  How in the hell am I going to eat 30 dollars worth of cupcakes?  So, I thought - Hey, I'll treat everyone at work with the cupackes.

So, I visited the website - saw the flavors they had and called and placed my order.  Or, so I thought.  This is where the wheels came off kinda.

Turns out they don't offer every flavor every day.  There are a few they have most times (Red Velvet, Classic, etc) - and there are ones they make from time to time.  But, they said for me to go ahead and place my order and if they ended up not making them - they would call me back.   Turns out there were a few casualties on my list... However, I never got the call because apparently they wrote my number down wrong.  So, when I came to the store to pick up my order - it wasn't ready...and it was already a 15 minute drive from Maryland Farms over here to pick them up on my lunch break!  

They actually freshly frosted the cupcakes while I was there and had to pick new flavors on the fly.  And that seemed to take forever.  

However, as much as the experience left a lot to be desired - there are a few reasons why I give them a pass.

1)  They made it right.  They got me most of the flavors I wanted - and gave me a few freebies to make up for the trouble.

2)  They apologized more than once for the inconvenience.  They seemed truly sorry.  They showed me my order form - where they show they tried to call - and up in the corner is written my name - with the wrong phone number.  It was only off by 1 digit - so, I'll give them the benefit of the doubt.

3)  I met the woman who was doing all the baking.  They had a really small kitchen and, since I was there last, they have moved to a larger space which hopefully will allow them to have more kitchen space.  They are now parallel with Nolensville rd near Menchies Yogurt & Blue Coast Burrito (they have a big, pink awning - you can miss it).  I just picked up a Key Lime cupcake the other day that was divine!   

I have heard some people say they were dry - however, I can't even imagine.  The cupcakes I had were the most moist, flavorful cupcakes I had ever tasted.  I tried a little bit of the 'Green Apple Martini', I had a lemonade one for myself.  Also tasted a bit of the Cotton Candy one and a couple others.  NONE were dry at all.  

I do want to leave you with this bit of advice:  Sparkle seems to be very creative with their flavors.  So, if you are going to go get an average cupcake - expect average flavors.  There is only so much you can do with yellow cake and chocolate frosting.  But you should try some of their more fun flavors like I mentioned.  Honestly - I know a lot of people swear by Gigi's - and they are good.  But I was blown away by Sparkle.  And remember - these are GOURMET cupcakes.  GOURMET, by definition, is something that is out of the ordinary.  So, try something like the Key Lime or Mint Chocolate or Strawberry Cheesecake or Salted Caramel.  Stay away from Red Velvet and Ol School.  They are good, sure.  But explore the flavors you can't make in your own oven at home.

Tried this place today - and was pleasantly surprised that they were running a Memorial Day special where the cc's were $2 rather than $2.95.  I brought 4 different flavors to a family gathering and we all sampled.  The red velvet looked good, but was very dry -- mabye it was a bit older.  The carrot cake, strawberry, and strawberry cream cheese (the last one being a strawberry cake with cream cheese frosting rather than strawberry) were very good and moist.  They didn't have any chocolate ones when I was there - but I'm assuming they'd sold out...cuz, c'mon, what's a cupcake without some chocolate?  I'll definitely go again.
COUrys3dxus7M56NH06zZg: Love the atmosphere new shops surrounded by this location. Soooo cool all decorated like shops I'd seen in Las Vegas

Located in a cool place! Bunch of stores all around. The bubble tea is ok. I'm from San Diego so my expectations for bubble tea is high. But at least they cook their tapioca balls all the way. And you get to add your own toppings.

Great bubble tea! One of the best flavored tea (but no milk) teas I've tried in middle TN, which doesn't have many boba places with this option usually. I don't like milky or creamy milk teas. It was kind of expensive, but considering they let you put the toppings (boba/tapioca pearls, jelly, etc) in yourself, you can definitely make that price tag more worth it. It's inside Plaza Mariachi, which is a fun place with a good court. There were aerial silk gymnastics for a short time at the food court. A band playing. A very fun atmosphere to enjoy your bubble tea!
yUNfOLDjpn6rzKop9wqLpg:
Upon walking in to the restaurant today I realized that although this chain has primacy of place in my mindshare--that I actually hadn't eaten at a Schlotzsky's in years. They tend to make me think "2003" for some reason. I love their food, I just don't get to where they "are" very often. The menu doesn't look that different. I don't know if I never noticed their salads before or if that is a new item--but they looked great. The young lady at the register taking our order was very friendly and cheerful. She didn't know their menu inside and out--but her great attitude more than made up for it. I ordered the roasted turkey with avocado sandwich and my wife got the veggie sandwich. Service was quick and efficient. The food tasted great and was to be expected in a chain of this caliber, price, and location. My "only" qualm was that the place was quite dirty. I know that a restaurant has certain "sunk" costs when they start up and you have to get several years in to be able to profit off of the expense you sink into the business in the first year, BUT the furnishings looked worse for wear (shabby), ceiling tiles had stains from leaks, and the place looked generally dirty. 

I would go back again as it wasn't terrible and not to be unexpected in this imperfect world, the service was good, and the food tasted fine and was priced well.
eBjBM0WpDnGmJ5STqsN8dA: Absolutely loved the cajun pasta with grilled chicken and the parmesan garlic chicken wings! They also have a great selection of wines. The prices are low, and it has a great neighborhood vibe. Denny is the best. Definitely give it a try!

Went on one of their first opening nights. A nice place to grab drinks with friends. Love the "lounge area" with couches. Good atmosphere and decorated really nice. The bartender and waitress were extremely nice. Didn't get to try a meal, but enjoyed my margarita and shrimp cocktail.

There are not too many places in South Nashville that have a great liquor selection without compromising happy hour value for beers. The 404 Bar and Grill is an awesome place if you're on Nolensville Pike and you're looking for something that is not a dive bar. Dive bars are great, don't get me wrong; but sometimes I want a decent cocktail without fighting downtown traffic or driving to East Nashville.

If you're lucky enough, you'll get the bartender here who knows how to craft the perfect artisanal cocktail on the level of Patterson House. He's awesome and loves his craft. Chat him up, and you'll see. (The alternative is a slightly surly bartender who doesn't love traversing back-and-forth to the outdoor patio.)

Onto the outdoor patio - it is an underutilized gem. The string lights are inviting with a soft yellow that isn't abrasively hipsterish like the faddish Edison bulbs in the East Nashville joint of your choice. In the cooler months of fall, there's an outdoor stage for featuring music while you sip your whiskey or microbrewed beer of your selection.

Onto the food - this is slightly elevated bar food that is a step above the greasy fare of other nearby places.

The 404 is a hidden gem on Elysian Fields. Whether you want to recline on the front couches or chill out on the patio, this is the place to bring your group of friends for a semi-quiet, laid back gathering to enjoy conversation.
EupZ1kzhiZj59hQ5k77teQ: The gyro meat is amazing, the rice is delicious and the serving size is very generous!  Also the owner is so kind.  Great location close to Sylvan Park across from Pep Boys.  Love to get carry out and eat this yummy food while watching movies at home.  Try it... you won't be disappointed!

Wow! What amazing food for a great price. My son and I went to eat lunch. People working were friendly. Food was some of the best Greek I have had. My son and I both got the gyro platter. Very filling. Tabolli was amazing. Will go again

We ordered takeout tonight and it was delicious! We got both the Falafel Plate + the Gyro Plate along with a side of spicy fries. I have a dairy allergy (and am also vegan) and the gentleman on the phone was more than happy to tailor my part of the order to meet my dietary restrictions. He gave me a Greek salad instead of the rice and still included a generous Tabouleh salad as well. My husband loved his Gyro Plate and the Falafels were well seasoned and extremely flavorful. Our order was ready for pickup in ten minutes and the store was very clean. You get a ton of food for the price! Excited to have such great Greek food so close to The Nations.

I came into town after a long flight from california and figured past 12 30 at night I was going to be SOL on eating anything but chips out of the hotel vending machine...I sheepishly asked the hotel desk person if anything was open (cuz where I come from you can fughetabouddit...lol) and she hands me a menu from this place and tells me the food is excellent.

ALWAYS SO GOOD

the service is so great. everyone ive encountered has been super friendly! plus they deliver basically until morning on weekends, which makes for amazing drunk munchies. their prices are great (6$ for a personal pizza!)

2t2MfJP6WH6LyhyVQ3MR9A: I was traveling through the Nashville area and decided to stop here because I haven't had jack in the box in a long time. rOjPwI0lLZMoQEW3slnmXQ: Great patio with live music most days. If you want more of a quiet scene and not the crazy honky tonk on Broadway, this is your place.
Awesome music venue. Outdoor patio is very nice. Very nice sound and night club feel. They have good tap selection and serve food. We did not try the food.

Aweosme place!  Music every night!  And the music starts around 6pm which is EXACTLY what Nashville needs.  Good music that starts at a reasonable hour and and the music still plays into the night! I'd like to give this place 3 1/2 stars if I could. The food was actually good. I had the Pork Sliders. The bbq sauce was good. The service is a little slow though. Waitresses are a little forgetful. I had to ask 3 times to get a refill on my water. 

I did notice that some other patrons did get better service though. 

So the food would get 4 stars..... Service would get 3 stars. ... However if they don't bring me my check soon, I'm going to lower my stars.

We just had the BEST meal/layover experience with Amy at the Tennessee Tavern at the Nashville International Airport. Prompt service, a friendly smile, and a truly warm welcome to Nashville. We appreciated it all - especially while visiting during our travels home from vacation.

We'll be sure to swing by again next time we are at BNA!!!

Also -- the Mac and Cheese bites with BBQ Sauce are something I dream about... Can't wait to snag some again in the future!

For this to be a restaurant in an airport I am thoroughly surprised with how great my food is!

Cons:  Very small tables and they were a tad close for my taste

This is our standard Sunday night, we'll I'm not cooking, let's order out, place. 

Roma's is definitely known for it's pizza at all of it's locations in Nashville, and the owner seems to always be seen singing and tossing a pizza crust on the local news. I typically take the sandwich and salad route when ordering from Roma's. 

I always order the raspberry pecan chicken salad which is really tasty, especially with the raspberry vinaigrette that comes with it. At first glance you're thinking, seven bucks for a pizza place salad? The difference is, these salads are HUGE. I'm talking three servings in one Styrofoam container that I somehow shove down my gullet in a single meal. Their Philly cheese steaks are good too, and I'd say definitely try the pizza, it's their specialty.

If you're ordering from this location, have your phone on hand, they sometimes get lost or call to tell you they're about to come to your door, which is kind of nice I like to think.

The pizza is completely and utterly, AMAZING! Each time you have their pizza, it gets better and better. I have been to a lot of places, and their food is still one of my top favorite places to eat! The customer service is even extraordinary and they definitely believe in fast delivery! If you have never tried Roma Pizza, I think you need to get on it!

This place is THE BEST for customer service and Pizza! Always efficient and pleasant on the phone when ordering, never rushed. Never a long wait time for delivery and they always beat the quoted time. We get the Greek Pizza and Greek Salad which are both amazing!

Ok, so let me start by saying that I totally forgot that Applebee's was still around.  This place used to be a great place to stop in and grab a burger and a beer or 10.  When I lived in So. Cal, I used to frequent their bar alot.  I loved their Club House Grille sandwich and then they went and changed the recipe.  You know how that goes.  

In any event, I stopped in with my mom and noticed they had new menus, which is always a good sign.  This particular location was not the neatest inside but we were greeted pretty quickly and were seated just as fast.  Our server, B, was friendly and prompt.  I ordered their new mushroom Swiss burger that was unfreaking believable.  I mean, I was totally surprised.  The burger was cooked perfectly and the mushrooms were fresh and every single morsel melted in my mouth.

If it weren't for the fact that this place is way on the other side of town, I would totally go alot more.  In any event, kudos Applebee's kudos!
Always nice people working at this establishment and not once have I had a problem with my food or service. I like this location the best cause its never too crowded and always clean inside.
Always friendly service, good food, fair prices. Never have to wait. The neighborhood is good.

Burrito Ultimo. Best burrito ever! Baja salsa is the best.

Yes, I work in a medical center and yes, I try to eat healthy, but sometimes you just want a guilty pleasure that maybe isn't that guilty.  Luckily for me, there's a Baja Fresh in the Vanderbilt cafeteria, and though it doesn't have the same range of options as a larger location would, this smaller kiosk version of BF works just find for a quick fix when working late.  They have cheese dip and guac for snacks and offer their usual burritos and tacos, complete with whatever fixings you might like.  The people behind the counter have always been nice to me.  I would advise not coming here at peak hours however, the cafeteria can get really packed and seating can diminish quickly.  It's cheap to eat here, as usual, which is a plus the day before payday or if you didn't anticipate being here late.  

I have never had any issues here with their food or service.  It's a quick and easy place to grab something at Vanderbilt without the lines of Au Bon Pain or having to get the cafeteria grill options.  And we all need some cheese dip in our lives now and then.

Boy howdy, that's depressing, but them honky tonks on Lower Broadway are full of struggling musicians performing their hearts out to get discovered. And let's face it, that's why Jamie W. and I were here. What we didn't bargain for was the huge ongoing party scene that happens here, presumably 24/7. Raising your eyebrow yet?

A vegan giving a Wendy's 4 stars? 
Well, I'm not exactly ordering chicken sandwich or a Frosty but I can appreciate ordering a small side salad and plain baked potato (with chives) for when I'm on the go!
For just $3.05 (including tax), I can attempt to eat in my ride as I dart to and from meetings... fun, right? I also appreciate that they offer the "little things" like croutons, low fat pomegranate dressing and for those who eat dairy: lowfat sour cream. 

You can actually eat a healthy and affordable meal if one must take the fast-food route. I don't try to make it happen to often but when I'm in a time bind, this is a great way to stay in check.

Props to their staff too. No kidding! They always get my order right and are efficient.

I walk to this location from work. They are always really quick even if it is in the middle of the lunch crowd rush. My order is always accurate and my food is always hot and made fresh! Staff is always awesome!

This establishment provides the example of what every restaurant should be doing - looking out for its patrons! We arrived at a late hour, yet the cashiers were kind and enthusiastic. After accidently upgrading our sizes, the cashiers went a step further and warned us about unpleasant guests in the vicinity and to be careful! Never have I experienced such kindness from a food establishment. Will be coming here often because of the great service we received!

Pretty good place. Great prices. Nice selection on lunch buffet.

food was really good. waiters were really nice. i suggest going around 1pm when its less crowded. also try the shrimp scampi & steak combo

After visiting the Belle Meade Plantation we were hungry, soo we ducked into this strip mall and found Cheeseburger Charley's.  My hubs and I are from Orlando, and have not heard of this chain before, but I'd compare it to a smaller version of Fuddrucker's.

If you want a good quality burger, done the way you want it, and don't want to wait a long time this is the place to go!
