ORDER: at counter
JEANS: ok by far
OECkyGXUoEBbIUsjKj5mWA:
The skinny is that it's a moderately priced place with not super high quality ingredients but cooked really well. What you want from a good dive or diner 

The story is...I walked in at 12:30 and the place was completely empty. The guy (owner?) was really nice and in ordered the portobello steakburger which had Swiss, portabello, and pesto mayo. The bun was nothing special quality wise as was the burger. But everything was cooked perfectly;the fries were hot and crispy and well seasoned (salt and pepper). I go the Oreo milkshake which was again nothing special, had some fake whipped cream on top. Overall I'd go back again, it did pretty well I think!

East Nashville needs more good cheap eats because I can't afford grass fed artisanal whatever every time I want to go out.

I had a Portobello  burger  I give it 5 *****
Very fast & friendy service
Ordered chocolate shake also 5 *****
Not crazy about onion rings, not home made.
But definitely will go again

Working at Center 615 on main, if I am not into Hot Chicken then any time I'm up for eating out for lunch it's a bit of a slog. I just wanted a simple burger and fries.

I imagined this is what it'd be like to order from Bob's burgers. A father and son running a restaurant. He obviously cares about his food, so it's cooked perfectly but as you can tell from the storefront he's pulling himself up by the bootstraps so it's not super high quality buns and uber fancy ingredients. 

This will be my new go to place for cheap burgers. If you want to support a local small business instead of spending money at Burger King, then go here.
EmzeuEQpf8bhMGuHjO_Kmw: Have been coming to this Wendy's for over 20 years off and on. That would be sad if I went daily. The Chicken nuggets ARE still 1 dollar. Seriouly after 20 years 1 dollar?!?! The drive thru has been also a close friend. It is always busy and it moves. A good thing. I have been inside about as many times as I have fingers on my hand so really, I can't give a good review on the inside. The drive thru people are very nice and I don't think I can remember a single time I was unsatisfied. Parking is easy as it is in the a strip mall and the prices are still crazy cheap and the drive thru is consistent and easy. I give 4 stars more than I should but this place STILL has 4 nuggets for a dollar and baked potatoes' and salad. What more could you ever want in a fast food place.
3eh3fHpfOBj6agJxvjtYjQ: I don't know what experience others have had, but I have always had a good experience at this location.  I eat here once a week on average, and pass other Subway locations to get here.  The best cookies of any Subway around.
f9eY2JcIilgUjPRfHKFV7Q:
BIG cut above your usual hotel restaurant.

I visit Nashville every few weeks. EAT is a treat!  My other favorite places in Nashville are Watermark, Patterson House, and Cantina Laredo (to place this in perspective).
Frankly, EAT's steaks are better than Ruth's Chris downstairs. The ribeye glazed with coffee and served with a tarragon-artichoke stuffed potato is superb, cooked exactly to order, and NOT over-salted.  There is a fried fish entree -- light, crispy and perfectly accompanied by a tzatziki (Greek cuke-yogurt salad). Yum. A few months ago, they had a promotion where the customer picked the price on a new entree:  Wiener schnitzel & spatzle with red cabbage (all to die for!).
I AM WORRIED -- EAT is nearly empty too many nights. Please don't let a hidden gem slip away!

Came here (hotel) for a business meeting and was early, so I decided to eat at the restaurant.  I got the cornmeal breaded shrimp to start and the "Eat Salad" as my lunch.  Both were really good.  The shrimp came with two sauces and I preferred the avocado one.  The salad was goat cheese, strawberries, boston bibb, cornbread croutons and ranch dressing.  The waiter was super friendly and attentive and the place was very warm without being "formal".  Not what you would expect for your hotel "offering", but a great surprise!

Hotel restaurants are not what one might call memorable.  Frankly they are designed for one thing and one thing only, refreshing weary and indifferent travelers.  Eat bucks that trend.  Tucked away in the lobby of the Loews Vanderbilt Hotel, Eat makes a subtle albeit important statement.  With it's head Chef Garrett bringing creativity and ingenuity to his craft, Eat sets out to make remarkable the otherwise unremarkable.  On a recent visit I was pleasantly surprised by a few menu items that I have not seen other places.  

Before I launch into my review of these items, let me say that service was impeccable.  Hotel restaurants have a reputation for lackluster service, but that is not the case here.  My glass was never empty, and I wanted for nothing throughout the meal.  Attention to and anticipation of needs was the name of this game.

For appetizers we had Deviled Eggs and Lobster Fritters.  Deviled eggs are a brave menu item, having a tendency to get rubbery and stale.  These were delightful.  Garnished with small pieces of house cured bacon and a small vial of house made hot sauce, these were simply terrific.  The lobster fritters had amazing flavor though they were slightly overcooked.
For Dinner I had the pickle cured fried chicken.  Before you dismiss me as crazy hear me out.  This chicken was sublime.  The pickle flavor was mild but cut nicely through the fry.  This chicken I can honestly say is some of the best fried chicken I have had.
Dessert was great too.  The house made corn ice cream with toffee was incredible as was the salted caramel ice cream sandwich.

Concluding here is easy.  Give it a try, they might even pay for your parking!  (no seriously, they will pay for your parking if you eat at the restaurant)
9UtZvfn_O1A7OWsaZfrzWg: I order from this Dominos frequently. It's always good and fast, and I get a kick out of the order tracker

This location has been amazing. We ordered a pizza tonight and had to wait a few minutes to eat it because it was so hot and fresh. We have tried most of the local pizza places around us and none even come close to this dominos pizza. 

The management team clearly has it figured out when it comes to hiring the right people. The service is always exceptional. 

There is never a question of where we are ordering pizza from with such a great option so close.

[EDIT 6/15/16] The new location is awesome!  Clean, new, professional.  You can watch them make the pizzas, sit down at a table or bench and watch the flat screen TV.
This is literally the nicest Dominos location I have ever seen, and I'm lucky it's the closest one to my work.  Go check it out!

Excellent pizzas!  We always get the special pizza deals here, and they're consistently good.  This is our go-to pizza joint on Nolensville Rd for my work.  DeJon does a great job making the pizzas.  Keep up the good work, we'll keep ordering!
klmmVypIS-xJsB8h9H2K6Q: We got the Stromboli and it was amazing!! He made it from scratch right in front of us and it was very authentic and delicious. The marinara was also fresh and sweet. The cannoli was everything you want in a cannoli, but I do wish he out a little chocolate sauce on top. The guy working there is also super friendly! Over all, food quality was great. This unsuspecting hole in the wall is going to now be my go-to Italian spot, a bonus is that they also have Mediterranean  food!

I was pleased to learn that there was a Roma out East when I moved into my new home.  Roma is a great local pizza chain with a fairly extensive menu.  The over-the-phone service at this location always seems to be really friendly, which is more than you expect from a delivery joint.

I usually order a small calzone with olives and a house salad.  The calzone is huge and served with a big dish of their tasty marinara.  I like it even better cold the next day, and there are always leftovers.  The house salad in on iceberg, but they are generous with the toppings.  All salads are served with two pieces of bread as well so they can be a meal on their own if you're ordering dinner with someone who's not in the mood for something heavy.
Meat tortellini, bread, and salad were all fresh and delicious. Will definitely go back
i_U2fvutq61jaQ9wlkAcaA: This place is where you need to be!  No better place to spend your night.  Classic Chicago feel, top notch service, and great food.  You want to throw some darts, shuffleboard, live music.  You need to stop by!
This place has the the best siracha honey wings! They are open late and have quick service.

Although it is a smoking bar (I don't smoke) it was not bothersome at all. The staff is super friendly and just like the other reviews, the locals that frequent the bar are welcoming and nice as well. BUT my review is based on the one reason why I came here, to try the Chicago style "Italian Beef sandwich ". I have to say it was very good! I will most definitely be coming back for the laidback atmosphere and awesome Italian beef sandwich.
8TulABhI0j9-r-ce0YfWSg: Yanni's arguably has one of the best restaurant patios in Nashville.  I have only been here once for dinner and it was excellent.  I had the lemon artichoke tilapia, which was super savory and the thick sauce that coated it was just right.  Best of all, sitting under the stars and eating is just unbelievable.  The service is really good too.  Best of all, the calamari is deep fried in a batter and accented with Greek spices, unbelievable!

Yanni's is a small gourmet Greek restaurant.  I never realized how great Greek food could be until I came here - the Greek salad, spanikopita, baklava, and gyros I've had at other Greek places pale in comparison.  The filo dough on the spanikopita was crispy and fluffy, and the spinach was carefully prepared.  The feta, peppers, and olives in the Greek salad were bursting with flavor, the pita bread was soft and piping hot, and the tzaziki dip tasted very fresh.  But...the tomatoes on the salad weren't ripe enough, and they were out of house wine.  The red wine we ended up ordering instead for $4.50 a glass was good, but, in my opinion, not distinctive enough to justify the price.  

The decor is simple and charming, with framed pictures of Greek scenes on the walls and a patio surrounded by a wrought-iron fence hung with bunches of grape-shaped lights.  Out server was attentive and friendly.  

Yanni's has the capacity to serve as a casual lunch spot or a fancy dinner date.   I'd definitely go back for a special occasion, but it's too expensive for me to visit often.

Let's just say that I have a hard time taking Tart L.'s reviews to heart (maybe thats just me?) but I am glad that I listened and stopped by Yanni's today. 

We went for a lateish Sunday lunch. It wasn't crowded and service was great. Yanni himself came out of the kitchen numerous times to make sure everything was okay and to "toot his own horn" by claiming that it was "the best Greek food in the city". And he's not lying.

When we decided to stop in I was imagining a lunch of falafel and hummus, neither of which can be found on Yanni's menu. Let's just say that they were not missed.  We started with an appetizer of (vegetarian) rice stuffed grape leaves ( or dolmadakia ) and some of the best, freshest pita I have ever tasted.  I followed that by the Spanakopita and mini Greek salad. The spinach was perfectly cooked, it wasn't too cheesy, and the pastry was divine. The salad went with it very nicely. I practically licked my plate clean. 

There are actually a lot of vegetarian items on the menu and even a decent vegan selection. Especially if you like eggplant. You can check out the menu on their website. 

On top of the food the place has a great little patio that is perfect on a nice warm autumnal day or for a romantic evening. 

Don't miss out on this great locally owned place! ( and rumor has it that Yanni also owns the Athens family restaurant so if you like that place but are looking for a little something "nicer" be sure and give this place a try!)
Ic7MAg8-DYOQTEJLVZkqPg:
Suraj is a takeout/delivery restaurant and market specializing in Middle Eastern food.  I've never ordered food from the, but I did pick up a menu, and it is HUGE!  They have about 15 different appetizers (including fried green tomatoes, coconut shrimp, and spring rolls), five types of soup, over ten kinds of salad (sesame-seared tuna, caesar, grilled shrimp, buffalo chicken, etc), ten different burgers, seafood, pasta, chicken, wraps, gyros, steak, pizza, Chinese, Indian, and 15 desserts, including tiramasu, carrot cake, and cheese cake.  They also offer lunch boxes ($7.49) and large caterer-style portions for events.  Prices are reasonable, but the best deals are probably the entrees, around $7.50 each.  They include falafel, chicken shish kebab or beef, all served with hummus, pita, rice, and Mediterranean salad.  You can also eat in, but the setting is kind of bleak so I wouldn't recommend it.

At the front of Suraj, you'll find a specialty market with foreign movies and music, along with Middle Eastern, Asian, and Indian groceries: canned vegetables, special flours, Mediterranean spices, couscous, Indian mixes, tea, dried beans, dairy products, and more.  The grocery part of the store feels kind of like a warehouse, so if you go, expect a no-frills experience.  I had to go to the back of the store where the restaurant is to pay for my tea I, and the woman who rang me up seemed very nice.

Although I have vowed to never come to this restaurant again, I still have to give them 4 stars.  Their gyro is the best gyro I have ever had in my entire life!  And everything else that I've tasted was really good.  The price isn't too bad, its definitely not cheap though.  Main issue is that their delivery service is very unpredictable, it usually ranges from 40 to 90 minutes.  If my ex boyfriend called, it would usually take about 20-35 minutes.  That's right! there's a difference!

I noticed this pattern and asked my then boyfriend to discuss it with the guys that work there... apparently since they all shared Middle Eastern backgrounds, these guys really liked my ex boyfriend.  They thought it was so cool that he was Israeli and they respected him because he was a male!  I can't believe it, these punks take longer to feed me because I'm a woman!  Well, scarew a you!

Even though this place is more geared towards Indian cuisine, I will admit, they make one of the best bacon cheeseburgers I have had. At work, we always order once or twice a week. Never had I had to wait more than about 45 minutes for my order, and that's usually on a Saturday! They are always very polite and timely. I have never been to the actual place itself, just ordered to-go food for almost 2 years now. Their best kept secret is their burgers. They have fries like Tazza so they're really good too! And they always have a good coupon to offer.
ROwmXzK4hx1RNhZlKr8SuA: The customer service here is very decent and the food is also good, I don't care what Matt says; it's cheaper than the local Pizza Hut and, unlike Papa Murphy's, the Pizza is already heated and ready for you to take out the door, so you don't got to cook it when you get home and you don't have to wait until your pizza gets heated up. You can call it pie, pizza, or even a pile of poo with crust, but for its value, LC could have easily upped the price to $10 or even $15 for their HNRs. I rather buy 2 of the pizzas here at the same price as I can get 1 just up the street at the Pizza Hut (3 if you include taxes). The ONLY thing I can't stand is their lines during dinner hours, but that's to be expected. One final note, ALL pizza is processed dough with processed meats cheeses and other condiments; LC isn't the only pizzeria that does this, I don't care what they say. Pizza is called the "diet breaker" for a reason, guys, remember that.
klzsfhwQeBEIwM2_CpZMDg:
We visited here about 4pm on a Saturday, pretty "off" dinner hours so I was a bit nervous as to whether or not the service or food would be any good. 

I was relieved when we were greeted very warmly by our waiter, who appeared to possibly be one of the owners? He was friendly, and helpful. Granted we were the only people in the restaurant at that time, but he was very accomodating. 

He brought out our drinks promptly, along with place settings and allowed us time to review the long menu. I was disappointed that the soft drinks are in bottles and $1.75 a bottle - no refills. I think that is tacky for any restaurant which offers seated dining. The drink issue aside, the food was good and service was good = 4 stars. 

I ordered the Pad See Eww, the veggies are obviously from a bulk food supplier and frozen, that was evident but the temp and taste of the dish over shadowed the frozen veggie factor. The chicken was moist and flavorful, the dish was seasoned perfectly, the noodles were cooked properly and I thoroughly enjoyed it. The other people in my party ordered the red curry, soup and another noodle dish I cant recall at this moment, but each of us was happy with our selections. 

I will say for this setting, having frozen veggies and no refills on drinks, their prices are high. Maybe try a lunch special to check them out, rather than pay the dinner prices. 

In fairness I rate them 4 stars because I was impressed with the level of service and the food was good. I dont want to discourage anyone from trying this place in my hood! 

The parking is a drag, park across the street in the underground garage, not in Rite Aids parking - there is a slim chance someone will notice and tow you. But parking cant be helped for this building with its location right on the main street.

I would go here again. I ordered the pad thai lunch special with mixed vegetables, medium hotness. It was a very filling portion and just spicy enough. It was the best pad thai I've ever had...very flavorful. The service was very friendly and fast.

I have tried every Thai place in the Nashville area and this one is my favorite, which is a lot for me to say because I'm a Thai food snob. They have the best Pad Thai, Drunken Noodle and fresh rolls. The staff is friendly. They are almost always open too, even on many holidays.
agt2rNYtWR8noXumB5GVWA: This is one of 3 options for pizza delivery at my home. I use the Android app for ordering, and I have always gotten my pizza within 20-30 minutes. They are so wonderful about special requests, mine being very light sauce due to food allergies. The pizza is steaming hot and always wonderful. They have great coupons on their Android app, so I never have to deal out a lot of money. After you accumulate 25 points on their rewards program, they give you a free large pizza! Free is always good especially when it is fresh and comes from great people! They will always be my first pick for pizza!!!

I had a thin crust pizza with green olives and tomatoes and onions.  It was done quicker than I expected and it had ample toppings. The thin crust was really good. It was flaky like a pastry crust  but still strong enough to hold a lot of toppings. It needed the seasoning packet because alone it is somewhat unseasoned. I would have liked large slices of tomato but places that do that usually charge $10 more for their pizza. The manager served me he was clean and quick and professional.  I would have easily paid 18 cents more for the employees to get health insurance.

Kind of crazy to see all the bad reviews. I've been ordering from this location my last couple times getting pizza/ wings and have had 0 issues. Foods great, comes on time, great customer service. Ahmed just delivered my pizza and was great, I had to call in and get his name so I could leave this review (the manager was also great). I don't leave reviews often..

Personally, I wouldn't trust the reviews, this place has been great and I'm shocked to see so much negativity. Time to eat my food, thanks Papa John's!
LeGy65I3IfDkhE5_5nUyjQ: Went there this morning for a Cajun filet biscuit combo.  The restaurant was clean and the food was hot and fresh .  I would definitely return.
I love this Bojangles! My food is always hot and fresh. Customer is great too! Today I went and got a chicken sandwich, 8pc chicken only and a large Mac n cheese. Everything was good!

I had some issues with the morning crew at this location.  I brought up my concerns to management... and they listened.  

Now it's a brand new experience and it's phenomenal.  It's service and quality you don't see in fast food anymore.

Great job Bojangles'!
hFilH9rDQKjGlNm3Go0ifg: I usually don't write reviews on fast food however this Arby's deserves it. From the nice interior to the friendly hospitality service I give this place a five out of five. Not to mention those corned beef sliders make the world a better place!

Whew. . Read some pretty nasty reviews of this place. . Have been there twice in the last few weeks. . Drive thru. . All I can say is I've been greeted by one of the clearest, friendliest, fastest and most professional drive thru workers at any drive thru anywhere. .Im not sure but I think his name was Lee Smith. . Probably the manager. . Food was hot, fresh, fast and cheap. . Had coupons. . I'll definitely make thus a regular stop if the service and quality remain like this! PS. .this is an unsolicited and honest review. . I'm normally the guy that rips on bad service!

I've been twice both times I've been waited on by the nicest person ever the 1st time I walked in I was greeted waited on and after my meal asked if I needed anything else the 2nd time was last night at the drive in same employee same sweet professional as before and the food was great both times
bvA6-yas49ezDM5xHc9FbA: This place was wonderful. Our server Trevor was very attentive and prompt. We never had to ask for refills. He was always stopping by to check on us. The manager even came around to see how everything was. The food came out piping hot and fresh. Would not have asked for a better dinner, server or atmosphere.
Food is what you would expect. Staff is friendly and pretty fast. Not the best Chilis I've been to but not bad either. Good place to grab some quick food before a movie.

Great bar/restaurant. We come here a lot, usually sit at the bar, and the service is always prompt, friendly, and polite! The managers are always walking around, so if there ever IS an issue, we are able to communicate and get the problem fixed. The only thing I've ever experienced here that wasn't up to standards was that I ordered the Mozzerella Flatbread without meat, and it was brought out with just the bread and cheese, no garlic aioli drizzle, no cilantro, and no pesto. I was a bit confused by this. It really wasn't that big of an issue, so I didn't send it back, just found it odd. That's the only problem I've ever had there! The bar staff is great, and always floating around the bar, talking to the bar guests and always running to the back to check if food is up, always doing their own dishes, and making drinks for the well. I'm always impressed! Thanks!
Bg-pr0kSSSueJ2_3Fapu3A:
I got delivery from this location over a dozen times in the last 6 months. Probably about 7 or 8 of those times, my pizza was late and/or cold or my order was missing items. It's super frustrating, but I give them 4 stars because the delivery drivers and management are always nice and helpful.

After each problem I had with an order, I would call the store to inform them and they would offer some sort of compensation or put a note on my account to throw in some free cheese sticks or a soda next time I ordered. 

Will your order alway be correct, on time and hot from this location? NO. Will they do something about it and do they genuinely care? YES. 

Hey maybe the freebies were just a ploy to keep me coming back, but they worked!

I always have ordered before 8 and never experienced problems like the other reviews. I ordered today at 5 and the pizzas were made 100% correct, the delivery time was accurate, and the driver called when he arrived, a plus since my dogs didn't bark at the doorbell. Give them a chance for dinner time, not late night delivery.
Ordered from the store itself. The pizza was good. The cashier was friendly. Not much else to say.
o8U2DbjKpXrvZdl4i-l4YQ:
VERY efficient service here this morning. My food was ready to go before she finished the credit card transaction. I think there was just one real go-getter running the show, but he was on fire!

Food is typical BK. I only stopped here because there weren't many breakfast places open, I was in a rush, and I knew what to expect.

Another busy morning at BNA and this BK continues to crush it! Prices are high because of the terminal location, but the food is on point with BK's on the outside. They always keep things moving at breakfast time and if there is a slight delay, it is to get fresh hash browns... so, obviously, it is worth the wait!

Fast, reliable service. Pricing is consistent with every other Burger King I've been to. Hardworking employees behind the counter with an easy to use order kiosk. 

It's simple. If you want gourmet, go to a sit down place and spend $20 more. It's a freaking airport Burger King. It's not going to look like the picture but it will fill you up for less than $15.

Edit: I bought an Impossible Whopper. 4 stars because it did make me feel sick like another reviewer here. I eat meat from time to time but usually prefer not to, and this upset my stomach a little. That said, it's the least expensive veggie option that I could find on this side of the airport.
ZUZuLPmQdH49_ARGpIrSHQ: The pizza is fine. Online ordering is simple. The people are nice. But they told me when I called that they have only one driver on a Monday and the weather is bad so it took 2 hours to get the pizza.  I hope this is a one time problem because of the weather and their half price deal. I'd like to order from them again since I like their pizza.
Really good - hot, and delicious.  Delivered ahead of schedule.  Yay Green Hills!

I have always loved Dominos Pizza so I'm biased towards liking the food. The reason I especially like this particular Dominos is because the last time I was there, I clumsily dropped my pizza cheese side first in the parking lot. The pizza was ruined and I brought it back in just to throw it away and they insisted on making me a new one. It was considerate of them to do that and I am still appreciative for it.
QExq5Wz8kE7gEMot6WQLQg:
I've been making my way down Nolensville Pike sampling all of the taco trucks- I've found my numero uno. The tacos are only 99 cents and they give you more meat than the other taco trucks. The meat is very flavorful, and they have a wide variety of menu items, helpfully displayed by picture on the side of the truck. 

My current fave are the mulitas, but the tacos are great too. They serve the tacos with pickled carrots and grilled onions and chilis.

They are very friendly and bilingual. This taco truck is in the parking lot of Tusculum Cleaners, so if you dribble a little taco grease, just drop your cleaning in the 24 hour bin! 

They are cash only

Amazing! My husband and I stopped there after church yesterday and didn't have high expectations but we were starving and gave it a try. I've been disappointed by most of the Nashville Mexican food compared to the authentic stuff in California, but this is FAR from disappointing. The tacos were delicious and so was the burrito. We liked it so much we went back for dinner the same day. Very reasonably priced and the man working the window was really nice. Definitely give it a try!

Delicious delicious tacos! I love going to Nolensville Pike to get the best Mexican food in the city. There are a lot of trucks and while I have my favorite, I have seen this one a few times (parked across from my usual fav) and have wanted to give it a try. Mainly because of their sign that said .99 tacos. Now that's hard to beat! I finally stopped for lunch on a Sunday afternoon. It's in the parking lot of a gas station but there is still a small parking lot. I walked up to order. I was a little disappointed to see that they had changed the price to $1.25/taco. Not that I think that is expensive, it's just I only had a $5 bill (cash only) and I wanted to leave a tip so I could only get 3 tacos instead of 4. Maybe they raise the prices on the weekend? I'll have to keep an eye out for the .99 sign. I went up to the window to order and got 3 barbacoa tacos. They took a few minutes to make the tacos and thanked me for stopping by. I took the order home to eat. The tacos are more authentic style so don't expect all of the toppings piled on. The tacos are also a little smaller but I would say 3 was the perfect amount. The tacos come with meat, grilled onions, cilantro, a hot sauce, and some grilled carrots on the side. I will say this taco truck puts the most meat on the taco. There was a lot of barbacoa on there. The meat was cubed and really tender. The flavor was wow...damn good. The focus of the tacos is on the meat and it should be. There is enough flavor there where you don't need to pile on sour cream and lettuce and tomatoes and guac, etc. I will definitely be coming back for more!
eLGZutbjLkpCh4vG83qlyw: Amazing, authentic Mexican food.  Great service and it was jumping at 3:30 on a Saturday
Excellent food. The chicken fajitas were some of the best I have ever had, and the refried beans were delicious!

If you think this great restaurant was good before with excellent food and good service, come back out, because they have new management and the wait staff is so kind courteous, efficient and their food is some of the best I've had in Nashville! To begin with, their menu is different from all of the other Mexican restaurants in and around Asheville, where are most of these restaurants the menu was exactly the same, and the food is the same. These folks have some excellent seafood dishes as well as different dishes that you don't usually find in most Mexican restaurants. Their salsa is delicious and has cumin in it, which I really like, and they also serve different flavored waters, like Horchata, which is a favorite of mine. And they have a freshly made chunky style guacamole that is second to none for a very reasonable price. I also like for tacos, and you can get them with a variety of meats. And the new chef, owners, and wait staff were very friendly and came by often to make sure we had everything we wanted! This is a five-star restaurant as far as the food, and service, cleanliness, efficiency, and is a nice little restaurant that you should get out and try very soon! It is right off of I 24 at Harding Place and convenient to most of Nashville
WPWtZDV0zRdcmDAtMOWNbw:
It takes a special place to make me sit down and write a review. 

Since moving from Chicago to Nashville 2 years ago, I've been on the hunt for the best pizza I can find in this city. Although this pizza isn't the best I've had in my life, it's the best I've found in this city. Generous toppings, always quick and doesn't leave me feeling gross. 

Although the pizza is great, what really makes this place special is the family that runs it. They are the only employees (dad, mom and son) and are dedicated to running their business. I know when I hand them money (cash only) to pay for my pizza that the money goes straight to them and doesn't get lost in a convoluted business structure. 

The main guy always has a smile on his face, you'd never know he had a bad day in his life. 

Will continue to get pies here, and tell all my friends to do the same!

Just went here for the first time and it's the best pizza that I've had in Nashville. It's a family run business; the husband and wife were extremely friendly and sweet; and you can't beat the price. But most importantly, the pizza was delicious!

It's easy for this place to get hidden behind Bolton's (which is also great) but you should really make sure to check it out! 

Plus they deliver - according to a couple I met in there, they've delivered as far away as 6 miles. Not bad.

For what it's worth, I'm from New York and this pizza ranks up there with the best of them.

I love this place/pizza/people!  I pretty much order a pizza here once a week, if not more, and anticipate the next time I can order it again!! The owners/family who run this place are honest/hardworking/all around good people! This is a staple in my life, comfort food if you will, and the cause of some of my jeans fitting a bit snug. Try it! You'll be hooked. Their thin crust is probably my favorite! The price is definitely right! They deliver with a smile...very fast.
m1iEV705I0UZjwUrv-uPKA: Excellent. Food was reasonably priced and very tasty.  Customer service was outstanding. Their credit card machine was down, so they resolved it by comping our entire meal! That's great service and ensures my repeat business, along with my referrals.  The wife suggested adding a tomato soup to the menu for those of us who like dipping our grilled cheese sandwiches. Can't recommend it enough.
Ejeicx8j1gvBDyI1w9lZlg: Great coffee, service, and atmosphere - I love having this new coffee shop right down the street!

I'm a huge fan of all things Bongo Java -- I think Fido is the best coffee shop I've ever been to -- so I was excited that they were going to open a coffee shop around the corner from where I live.  I went today for the first time, and it is wonderful.  It is a clean, good looking space, and, of course, the selection of coffee and tea is fantastic.   The menu is broad enough to make anyone happy, although I do hope they will eventually have daily specials, something that makes the food at Fido heads above a typical coffee shop.  (Perhaps they already have them and I was just there at an in between time when they weren't offered.)

The staff could not have been friendlier. Thanks for opening this great place in an area that sorely needed a great coffee shop.   There's plenty of parking, and lot of outlets to plug in your computer.
Great coffee and as the mission of the Cafe states, a great place to "Gather, Converse, Reflect". Beautiful interior and friendly staff. Highly recommend.
xNNyypwd61CSEqmDwJvYFA:
This is my favorite Little caesers on this side of town. The people who run the counter are always very kind and professional.
It's a $5 pizza, an it's quick! I come here often and have never had an issue. I think their pizza tastes great. Yea there are better specialty pizzas, but if you just want a simple pepperoni you can't beat $5!!
Thanks for the free lunch combo on April 2, 2018.  The line moved quickly and the manager was very professional and made customers want to return for pizza after the promotion!

In the past, Little Caesars has been kind of a joke in the pizza community. Cheap, low quality, and kind of laughable compared to other pizza chains. It was the go to meal while I was a broke college student, and recently someone told me they upped their game, so I had to check it out...

Just going on the pizza alone and not this specific store, it is a HUGE upgrade compared to how it was years ago. If I'm going with a chain pizza and not local, I'm definitely going here before I go to Pizza Hut, Domino's, or that other chain from Louisville that we won't mention. Their Italian Cheese Bread is also a great side for anything, and you can fill yourself up for less than $10, or possibly a few people for less than $15.

This location is easily accessible from the road, easy to spot, and has plenty of parking in the strip mall that it's in. The staff has always been super friendly to me when I've been in there, as opposed to some of the complaints of people on here saying they're rude. Who knows, maybe customers go in in a bad mood already and take it out on the staff, and don't like the response they get in return? Either way, I've never felt uncomfortable or greeted with anything less than a friendly attitude.

Now, to my favorite part: Caesars has totally upped the online ordering game. Download the app, make your order, and pay with a card (or Apple Pay). Once you order, they'll give you a 3 digit pin code. When you walk in the store, to the right of the counter they have a warmer with a key code on it. Input your 3-digit code, the appropriate compartment opens, and BAM: Your order is right there ready to go (minus the drinks that are in the fridge, if you ordered them). No wait, and if it's your thing, no human interaction whatsoever.

Fast. Cheap. Easy. Good. If you're in a rush, don't feel like waiting, and don't want to spend a ton of money, this is an absolute go to pizza chain.
TSIQyKAgz3pwIBQisP1aBg: Had the pimiento cheese and the classic burger. Both things were beyond! Will definitely return.

Best burgers in Nashville!!! And have I mentioned they have an amazing bourbon selection?? "The Bernie" is my favorite burger... topped with blue cheese crumbles, Benton's bacon, blueberry preserve, and beer-battered jalepenos.... SO. MUCH. YUMMY. FLAVOR. I will be back.... often.

So far we have not got sick. Stay tuned for an update. 
Good burgers
Good fries
Easy to get to
uE0iQih1BfnQUTUxI3iFMw: The service was professional & the employees were very friendly. I got my food quick and it was all good. The restaurant was clean and its nice that most of the employees are bilingual.

WHO DOESN'T LOVE ICE CREAM ???
I don't get to eat it very often because of my stomach issues, but when I do, I want it to be amazing...so today, my daughter texted me while at work..."Please go and get a Apple Pie Blizzard for me"...I said..ummm Apple Pie Blizzard ..that sounds AMAZING!!

So, after work, and after my errands..I ran through the drive thru...One Med. Apple Pie Blizzard please !!!! and OMG. it looked delicious...the guy opened the window took my $5 and handed me the blizzard upside down...I guess to prove how thick it was ...
Even though it was for my daughter, I had to take a taste and I am so glad I did..it was so so soo good!!! 
I guess next time I will have to order my own!! They also make a pumpkin pie one, maybe I will try that one next..

Just to let you know the Apple Pie Blizzard is nothing but pure nirvana ..it is smooth whipped vanilla ice cream with chunks of pie filling, apple bits, bits of crust and even that cinnamon flavoring from the crunch on top of the Apple Pie..it is sooooo darn good!!! Try one and let me know what you think!!!
I need to find me one of those BOGO free coupons...
Oh by the way, there is a DQ program you can join online and get a free blizzard for your Birthday...

Just visited this location and was very impressed with the service and speed. Employee was very informative and patient with me, their service was extremely fast, and he even offered my dog a pop cup which I didn't know existed until today. Glad to see that not all fast food chains have degraded in customer service.
hJ4H1fnlqZwepZAo9gHF6Q: Were lucky to be seated quickly as shortly after the place really filled up. Initially the server was quite attentive getting coffee and water for us quickly. We were with another couple - our food ended up up coming out in a timely manner . Portions are large - my husband and I decided almost too large and next time we will split.  I ordered the farm breakfast with goat cheese - poached eggs added. No complaints and it was quite tasty and enjoyable. Our friends' order had some difficulties- it didn't come out for quite some time - not correct and it took a bit of time to get it straightened out. They just seemed a bit short staffed? Loved the coffee and fresh fruit although the honey dew melon was rock hard, all the rest was fresh and yummy.

This is a busy place but Kiera was amazingly calm and made sure we enjoyed our experience! We had a great breakfast and did not feel rushed even though the place was packed. Again, Kiera and the entire staff was superb!

First Watch in Green Hills is an excellent location for small morning meetings or a large brunch. We recently enjoyed a great meal. They accommodated eleven of us, ages 4 to 84. The menu is expansive and everyone found something they liked. Food was good and prompt and fairly priced. I will go back.
-1EGqUQFBmGEp76CE-Zk4Q: I've come here twice for the cheap tacos and was blown away the first time. I had the shrimp and fish tacos which had plenty of meat and were topped with lots of fresh cabbage, tomatoes and tasty sauce.  The fish tacos were $1.59 each, which has to be the cheapest, tastiest option for lunch in the area.
Good salsa. Enjoyed carnitas burrito . Ate outside with family and dog. Dog enjoyed my child's cheese quesadilla.  Minor tears .portion was big enough for her to share !

Tried this place again and it was a total 180. The ground beef is seasoned well and not watery and greasy. Enjoyed the hard shell tacos and the taco salad was on pint will set be going back. Only small thing is the prices have went up on some items
GUQZLcHVk5wCiZdqD9pusQ:
Had a 4 part order. Very complicated, including vegetarian substitutes. They got it 100% perfect and it was very quick. Also yummy and hot 
I wanted to leave this review so people know it is worth going to this location!

Good food. Always hot. Just double check your order before you leave if your taking it to go. I have had things from my order missing a number of times. They'll take care of it if it's wrong, just check before you go.

I had such a great experience! Miguel was THE BEST! I read the bad review on here before but was like how hard can you actually mess up a taco. I went and Miguel was so nice and patient with me ordering, was fast giving me my food, and the smile on his face left a smile on mine! So happy I ignored these reviews!
nlNAluD3re_KnCcQHuZc2A: Their soups are delicious but be careful with how spicy you get them. I asked for a medium  and it was very spicy!!! Great customer service and location. i love the place, it's super cute and convenient.

This is no doubt the best steamed bao I've had in Nashville. As a Chinese international student, I must say it is REAL AUTHENTIC Chinese. Highly recommend! The noodles are also delicious (especially the broth) except the meat might be a little too hard to bite. The service is great and we got our food pretty quickly. The price is not cheap but fair comparing to other Chinese places.

I was so impressed with this place I decided to post my second ever review. I had the hot dumpling bowl which was delicious enough to order a second round of. The pork steamed bao was great as well. Service was on point and had my food in 5-10min. Excited to have a Chinese restaurant in Germantown - I will be back soon!
IviYRPeL17h7Zr1n5DAw5w: This place just recently opened, and it's really good! The food was flavorful, and the portion sizes were decent. We definitely left feeling full and happy. It's located inside Renaissance Hotel on Commerce St. The ambience and decor of the restaurant is super cute; our waitress was incredibly helpful and kind! There were no nonalcoholic "mocktails" on the menu, so she went to the bartender and he crafted a drink from scratch that was delicious! We were very pleased with our experience here and would recommend it to anyone visiting the Nashville area.

Yes, it's in a hotel, but it is wonderful. We had cocktails at the bar one night before going to the Ryman and a breakfast feast the following morning. The staff is exceptional. The decor is beautiful, trendy, comfortable and relaxing.

I stopped in here for some dinner after being in the city for a conference. The food came out pretty quickly and the service was very friendly. I got the crab stuffed trout which didn't really come with sides, and for the price I expected some. However it was really delicious and the sauce it was in was great.
1lvDkCIAqGlDUxWmXQgKvA: What a beautiful light filled space, Stay Golden makes me happy just upon entering. They are located in the WeWork building on Woodland.  There's paid parking behind the building with the first hour free.  They have plants tucked everywhere and several options for seating.  Besides the bar there's tables and hightop booths so you can find a nook to cozy up in.  We loved the ombré painted walls, a unique touch. We started with their house brewed coffee, served in beautiful pottery mugs perfect for hugging. My daughter brought me here since she's been raving about their chilaquiles, and she wasn't wrong- they're so good!  A bed of housemade corn chips slathered with black refried beans, three kinds of salsas, cheese, and topped with two eggs.  It was a delicious mess. I also liked that there wasn't any attitude, which can be a "thing" sometimes.  Everyone was so friendly and welcoming, I'll definitely be back.
We stopped here before taking a class on the back side of the building at the candle bar. Very cute and a great place to stop for a coffee and/or cocktail.

It's fantastic I love everything about this place! The staff are awesome, it's got the best breakfast in East and the vibe is  I eat here twice a week. Only thing I wish they would change is getting their servers to bring the drop coffee as soon as it's order rather than waiting 10 mins for the barista who is working hard already to pour it.
Pmt-hrEVzRYLMgHSk7yqWQ: Corporate purpose. "To glorify God by being a faithful steward of all that is entrusted to us and to have a positive influence on all who come into contact with Chick-fil-A."

I love this Chick-fil-a, however they do not accept cash and they do not have a drive-thru. It is very convenient because I typically order on the app and opt for the curbside delivery (they have well over 20 parking spots). This location handles a high volume in catering and delivery orders so they do not have an inside eating option. They have a 10 pack of chick-n minis which I haven't found at any other location which is nice :)
The staff is always super nice and quick.

This Chick-fil-A is a unique and one if a kind location. I've visited multiple times and utilized the mobile ordering pick up. The team members are super friendly and are always very busy.

This location does not accept cash, so be sure to have your card or mobile app handy.
825S0nTj5TRtKOWPPAMlRQ: Wow, I was not expecting this! Stopped in for a late lunch and my husband and I split 2 appetizers - the Green Tomato BLT and the Hot Chicken Biscuit. Both dishes were outstanding! The BLT had 2 different sauces, so flavorful! The Biscuit was the right about of spicy, with a hint of cool from the coleslaw. And the presentation was gorgeous. Then we split a Ahi Tuna bowl, also really really good. Definitely recommend.
Great latte, cute spot. Good selection of both healthy and not-so-healthy breakfast items. I liked the coffee better than Bongo

The food was excellent. We had Nachos and a burger. That doesn't sound very exciting but both were over the top good. The Cumberland Buck cocktail was exceptional. To finish off our meal we had the best gelato ever! I'm not giving the restaurant 5 stars because A. It felt like a hotel restaurant and B. The menu only had a few things that really interested me.
AWscUqDFoXF03qIApherGw:
I've never been a fan of the ramen I've had at the Gulch location, but tonight opted to try the to go pop up in East Nash since it's close to my place. Service is quick! The lemon chicken paitan was good- a creamy but light broth, still salty but not as much as their other broths. Leaves that nice, umami, sticky, lick your lips feeling after sipping it down. Good serving of noodles and broth was nice and hot. The bite of the tomato compliments the smoothness of the chicken broth well. I did add some fresh sliced jalapeños from my own kitchen for an extra kick. 

Also tried the eggplant onigiri- meh. Would not get again.

Delicious ramen boxed with exceptional care and service. I like the way they boxed my to-go ramen. Ramen is not the kind of dish that you want to order to-go, just like pho. But Otaku To-Go someow did it will. Just don't travel to long before you enjoy your ramen. Thumbs up!

Hi guys! I ordered this food from postmates today. I got the beyond buns and the Tantanmen vegetarian ramen! DELICIOUS! The beyond buns were absolutely perfect in every way and the ramen could use more eggs (overpriced at $2 each!!). I paid $27 for the meal with postmates fees. Not worth it, but really good!

The ramen itself is $15 and definitely not a big enough portion.

The beyond sliders were $9 and a great appetizer!
5stXlc2HM9A-Sk5wfngNUg: This KFC is hit or miss. This time it was a Hit. I had the Georgia gold tenders, wedges, and slaw. Everything was Hot and Fresh. I was actually surprised! Also, the lady at the drive thru was very pleasant and friendly. I don't know if they have new management and crew members, but it was definitely a step up from my last visit.
J_U_Cx2DiKH0t5Ue6KpcnA: The coffee was great. Service was friendly. The setting to sit and have a cup on Saturday morning was very nice.
Finally, a coffee shop that focuses on a fast cup of coffee at a reasonable price. All the baked goods including the bagels are made in-house. Coming back soon.

I just had an iced coffee and a chocolate chip cookie here, both of which were excellent and reasonably priced. The ambience is delightful. The space is small, but not crowded, with plenty of room to sit and enjoy your coffee. But the staff is what made this place five stars for me. The man and woman working today brightened an otherwise dreary Nashville morning for me. I will be back.
JaXnQnaslqy4me2jaKhKJA: We met a few friends on a Friday night. I would recommend making reservations. We were seated soon after we got there because we had reservations. The service was excellent. The pizza was amazing! My husband and I split the Colony squared without the meat and it was the perfect blend of spicy and sweet. Our friends got the vodka sauce pizza and they swore by theirs as well. We also tried the waffle fries. They were good, but the pizza is pretty filling so next time no fries for us :). All in all we have found an excellent new pizza joint for date nights and friends/family outings.

Went here when it was fairly dead right before dinner rush and it was just my aunt and I.  When we arrived we were seated at a 2 top directly next to the only other table sat in the whole room. I'm talking 2 feet away. I asked if there was any way we could sit at another table and the hostess just brushed me off--"oh, well you know we have reservations coming in so...".  Not a great first impression. The food was ok. My aunt and I both got the chopped cheeseburger. The fries were the best part of the meal. The pizza looked great, but we did not try any. I would consider going back to try the pizza.

Did not expect the pizza to be very good but it was, in fact, exceptionally great. Perhaps the very best, maybe the best, pizza in Nashville. The server, Travon, was very good. I will definitely make this one of my usual places.
nF8Sb_G2PtT9ZHtgGYl69Q: Love the new decor ! I've been coming here for years ! The people are mostly locals that have lived here for years! So everyone is friendly and excited to help ! Very clean on the inside the only thing for years the bathroom has always been small and has a Oder I suppose plumbing !! But over all it's good
4zYssmZqA-P75QaFWYtigA:

We have been going to the original location for years. It is great now having a location in our neighborhood. The food and service are great. Definitely legitimate Italian.

I've been frequenting Coco's off Charlotte Pike for a few years now. The food is fabulous! This is my first time at this location; although this location doesn't have the Bocce Ball or Italian market, the ambiance is very nice here. I came here with my family, which were visiting from out of state. They absolutely loved the food, even said they were making their plans on when to return for another meal.
0KqR1zKomUJsk-_-Dx92SQ: The food was excellent. Melissa was our hostess and server. The food was fresh and hot. The salsa was amazing.

My good friend/colleague sent me here to pick up lunch for the both of us. I have a hard time with eating chicken and I do not eat pork or beef. I've been coming to this beautiful establishment non-stop since. The owner and staff are amazingly nice and professional. It's a hidden gem that everyone should know about. I've taking my sister here yesterday and I'm bringing my brother and my daughter here for lunch today. My favorite thing about the menu is that they have vegetarian options. Everything is so fresh to order and delicious. It's hard for me to have left overs...lol. My current favorite on the menu is the vegetarian chimichanga w/o lettuce.
Wow! Great place. Friendly people and wonderful food/service. I had the octopus ceviche- it's great!!! The salsa is fresh and tasty too! The price is reasonable. My new favorite in Donelson.
g7wB3PdcNIC-Pxo5hbTCow: Great atmosphere and friendly service! Prices are higher but the pizza is larger than expected. Crust was light and crispy and the ingredients tasted fresh and delicious. I love pizza and I definitely recommend this place!!

A pizza lover I am, but because of my self-imposed caloric restraints at the moment I have yet to try their pizza. However, this is my 2nd Cobb Salad from Rock N Dough Pizza, meaning the first one was delicious. I do not eat salad very much because I rarely find one that appeal to my tastes. Its a huge salad full of delicious ingredients. Fresh, crispy lettuce, flavorful ranch dressing. However they offer additional  dressings and many other ingredients i.e. shrimp.  Salad also contains some type of seasonings that added to the great flavor.
Great apps ! Great pizza ! All the food was fresh as expected. Waitress was very friendly and fun . Very modern and clean .
DrBSzjUbpW9IwJzpp9x1XA: Love this place! Great Meditarranean food. Had the sampler plate. Chicken was great too.

Amazing place.  Amir is so awesome... when we came to get good to a to plate with my daughter he insisted we dine in and we said she had been fussy and he said she is fine I will turn baby shark on. How awesome. The food is so delicious from the chicken plate to the philly sandwich!totally recommend!
Service and food are excellent! This is authentic Greek food right across from Vanderbilt!
-YljbQMbbTC9jwn8kOLitA: fries are on point probably 3 out of 5 times--impressive. 2nd best mcdonalds in nashville, after the fancy one in green hills

Thursday, September 19, 2019. At McDonald's around 6am. Outstanding service at the speaker and the first drive thru window. The lady that took my order was patient while I decided which paper coupon to use and she helped me make the right decision! She provided service with a smile and made me feel that my business was appreciated! She person the delivered my order also gave great service. Also, the food was hot and the quality was good!
Great job on Thursday, March 8, 2018.  Great job at Drive thru especially with the loud work next door. Hot and well prepared food.
3KUACt6r8syRa1t0d-5Alg: This place is delicious. I wish I still lived in woodbine just to be able to visit it more than twice a year. The tamales and lingue are delicious.
They have the best authentic tacos in Nashville. Everything here is amazing and the food is always fresh!

We love this place! Breakfast, lunch and dinner are served. They are open 6 AM to 9 PM. They are so friendly and the food is soooo good. I highly recommend the Woodbine Tacos. Can't wait to come back!
RVILjgZ2k9fqsP3uxJZMYw: Food and service were great. Presentation of food was lacking in some aspects but overall left me happy after eating. The southern hospitality was more than obvious and I will definitely have to come back for one of their famous texas toast melts!

Absolutely it amazing time , And amazing service I truthfully felt like I was in a five-star restaurant. Five stars since the moment I walked in. Place is kind of perpetually  sticky but it adds to the experience. I am so utterly grateful I was able to bring my beloved family to the waffle house as late Anthony Bourdain said Waffle House is a beacon of hope

THE cleanest and friendliest Waffle House I've ever been to. we know food is good (duh!). Having a clean place to sit with friendly services/cooks = 5 stars!!!!!! 
So so so glad I went there. Made my day!
i1CA8-en15FdmHjJ6K7h8w: Fish sandwich was really good. So was the chicken sandwich. Frozen drinks were alright. Sides were delicious.

Best dern fried fish you can find in Nashville. Fresh oysters every time I've been here. Great peel and eat shrimp straight from the gulf. Definitely try the frozen drinks. They're strong and cheap for the value. Love this place. Friendly staff hook you up with recommendations.
Great food and service. Sat at the bar and the bartenders were very attentive.  Best fried fish I've had in Nashville and the burger is legit.
MVAjzYfdM2zj3GfOD-r2CA: Tried this place on a whim.  The menu is simple, but what they do is really good. The sandwiches are really solid and they're generous with the meat.  I strongly recommend.

I wanted a pastrami sandwich so badly. I was convinced to try Mac's Corned Beef AND BOY OH BOY to my surprise.......I have never had such a MASSIVE & FLAVORFUL sandwich in my life!!!! It is simply good!!! I had the TY MAC.....and he said, "You will be back!!" Well I he was right on track!!! I surely will.
Listen, it's not Detroit or NYC, but it's the closest I've had outside of either place.
wtaAnIFXLA1b3ldCR57vOQ: The staff was really nice and it only took 3 minutes for my order right after 5pm. I had chicken (pollo) tacos and they were delicious. I will definitely be back to try other items.

I'm always down to find a new (to me) taco truck. I'm especially interested if said truck isn't in one of my usual haunts. I may be weird that way, but I like seeing what different parts of the metro area have to offer. Even if you're just the least bit familiar with Donelson, you'll have no problem finding this truck. It's on the east side of the Lebanon/McGavock intersection in the plaza with the large liquor store. Got your bearings? Good.

I arrived at 745, eager to not be one of those people who show up just before close. Well, best laid plans and all because the truck just changed its close time from 9 to 8. So... yeah. The owner couldn't have been nicer, though. I told her that I completely understood if she was already packed up for the night. She said that if I could pay cash and were alright with tacos, we were in business. Done and done. At $1.50 a pop, I was in the market for a goodly amount of tacos. I got all that I wanted, save for the shrimp. Given the time of my order, they were off menu. Instead I ordered two chickens, one asada, one pasta, one carnitas, and one barbacoa. They came with onions, cilantro, lime, and green & red sauce. Cool. After an appropriate amount of time, I had my tacos and a wish from the owner that I enjoy them. It may sound like a small thing but it's that type of caring that will keep me coming back and back.

I arrived home to dig in to my tacos. As soon as I opened the container, i was happy. They smelled fantastic. Each taco was visibly distinguishable from the other and of a good size. I sauced and limed my tacos and dug in. They were awesome. The meat was seasoned well, tender and juicy. My only dry taco was the carnitas but that's a foreseeable problem, given the meat. I was satisfied with six but could have eaten more. The name lives up to the product. After eating, I was very happy, indeed. It seems that Donelson gets more 'hip' and excitingly culinary by the week. Happy Taco certainly adds to its cred. Next time you are in the area, make sure you stop by and support. Your belly will thank you.
Yes lawd!  It's so nice to have a high quality taco truck so close by - definitely comparable to the trucks I frequently visited while living in Austin - gonna be a repeat customer, for sure.....
iVTpG9i1XCxffBIxTW9qnA: This is a great local bar.  Large selection of drinks and great food!  Love to come watch a game or just enjoy time with friends!!

Best restaurant in the neighborhood! Relaxed vibe with music, string lights, and sports TVs. Service was quick and so nice, southern hospitality that makes it feel like home. Great beer selection and variety of food options -- something for everybody. We ordered a Diskin Cider and Kombucha beer, which was the first time I've ever seen that and so fun and different to try. For dinner we got the salmon, patty melt, and hot chicken sandwich. Delicious! Recommend any of those 3. Want to come back and try other menu items as well.
Great neighborhood spot! Good beer, cocktails and food. We sat outside and our service was great.
VJ-iPgrf8lgvkV2xsNot1g: This was our first visit to Saffron and we loved it. The dining room is cozy and not overly crowded. The buffet had lots of great choices, all fresh and delicious. The naan was buttery and soft. We tried the spicy lemon pickle and several other new treats. We will definitely be back!!

We love the friendly service, dim lighting with no TVs, and of course the excellent food! The spices and flavors are exceptional! We walked right in on a Friday night with the kiddos and got seated and served immediately. Try it out!
This is our go-to date night restaurant! The service is always impeccable and the food delish. We haven't had a bad dish, but our favorite "appetizer" is Gobi Manchurian Dry, extra crispy. Try it!
HRZLjyyPlrGFRIWfcu9v6Q: Hunters Station is a food court, minus the mall, that contains only "smallish"local restaurants.  Hugh Baby's, Aurora Tacos, Vui's Vietnamese Food, The Grilled Cheeserie and a bar that serves from-scratch food and tons of craft beer. There is something for everyone including vegetarians.  What more could one want?  I've been 6 times and I've had almost everything here and it ranges from very good to outstanding.  As Dave C pointed out, it's family friendly which is both good and bad depending on what you're looking for.

What a great place!  Lots of trendy options. It is a mall food court with places that you WANT to eat and no mall.  Clean, fun. More of a casual feel than an intimate place to have dinner. But it is what it advertises.

Hunters station is exactly what Nashville needed. A place where people can come together and eat and commune together. I've seen people of all ages and ethnicities here eating together which is one step closer to cultural restoration for the city of Nashville. 

There are several great options for food here and I have thoroughly enjoyed every time I've come here. 

I would love to see more live music and pop-up shops in a place like this to encourage even more gathering of wonderful people around the city.
dIr0SZ22yXOGw6e-zH6L8Q: Don't be fooled by the casual approach of BokBox.  The food quality, execution and value rival any brick and mortar in Nashville.  It's refreshing to eat takeout that not only satisfies your hunger, but nourishes your body.  These fellas are the Kings of coal roasted chicken!

Hands down the best lunch option in East Nashville! You will not find a better crafted piece of chicken and carefully curated sides anywhere in the Nashville metro. As someone who moved here from San Francisco, I can tell you this concept would be scattered throughout that city and would thrive (Bokbox see's you Souvla). I anticipate the same thing will happen here. Run dont walk to this semi permanent pop up run out of the pop ramen window in East Nashville so you can say you had it before it blew up. The smashed cucumbers and sweet potato sides are incredible, as is their signature bok sauce. Seating is limited to a communal outdoor table so dont look for white tablecloths, servers, heaters or A/C here. Order at the window (or pre order online if youre smart. They do not take cash fyi, but who uses that stuff anymore anyway?) sit back and wait for for that beautiful box to arrive and take it back to wherever you came from to enjoy that crispy-skinned, coal-fired bliss. But dont take my word for it, go try it for yourself...

This place has mastered how to fry and roast chicken. Hands down, it is the best roasted chicken I have ever had. It's so juicy. They have a chicken sandwich called the Bokwich that is better than Chik-Fil-A, Popeyes or any other fast food spot. The chicken is crispy, not greasy. Their fries are shoestring and delicious. Each fry looks like the best fry in the bag. I would rate this place 4.5, but don't want them to lose their star rating. Their other sides need help. The kale salad is decent.
T3IuYvaRaNwxo7yvH0DKbg:
Thank goodness for this little marketplace!!!

We stayed nearby at the Westin Hotel and there is pretty much ZERO other options nearby for quick, easy, and CHEAP meals on the go!   Imagine our surprise when we stumbled in here and found that they have a breakfast menu (served until 10am) which includes omelets for under 5 bucks, etc.

They also have a great lunch menu, and we ordered off it a couple times.  Super cheap and everything cooked fresh in front of you.  The fries were AWESOME and wonderfully crispy.  The grilled chicken sandwich had some sort of a seasoned marinade on it that was perfectly yummy without being overpowering.  The toppings were crisp and fresh.  

Zero complaints.  It's a much-appreciated little gem.  (and SUPER clean!  and amazingly friendly employees)
This place has good prices and great food. Nice little market to swing into. I had the catfish sandwich, dang!! So good
Great burger at a reasonable price.  Hit the spot and service was fast and courteous.
lxJo-WX7ZfNfNzzw0L2sQQ:
Fable Lounge is a fabulous addition to the west side of the city. They're offering the craft cocktail speakeasy vibes that are easy to find in other parts of Nashville, but have yet to be found in West! Located in an unsuspecting part of West End, Fable offers a cozy and romantic atmosphere that you can feel from the outside that only gets better as you go in! 
The service was great, from the host who greeted me and recommended a part of the two-story bar & lounge based on what I was looking for, to the friendly bartenders who made us feel at home throughout our experience. 
The only things that made my experience a four instead of five star were that:
1. The menu was very limited in its descriptions & the staff was still in training, so I couldn't get a clear description from either. 
2. Our snacks and drinks were very pricey. My friend and I each had a couple drinks and a charcuterie board. We expected about $40- $50 a piece, and it was $75 a piece. You can go to a 5-star restaurant here and have a luxurious steak dinner with a nice bottle of wine (plus an app & salad) for that, so comparatively speaking the value isn't quite there. 
I'd definitely give this place another shot, or recommend it as a great date night spot... but their prices and what you get for them make it unlikely/impossible for it to be a regular spot for a local.
Absolutely amazing establishment. Great drinks. Wonderful food. Amazing staff. Even through covid, they provide amazing service and a beautiful ambiance. Ben and John are absolutely wonderful.

Last night was my second visit to Fable. I enjoyed the ambiance and service on the first experience and thought it would be a great place to celebrate with some of my medical students who were graduating. It was a great decision. Our server was attentive but not intrusive and as our party grew larger than we initially anticipated the staff was VERY accommodating in allowing us to continue our celebration. I highly recommend Fable, I almost want to keep it a secret but they deserve the business! The food looked great and tasted better. The drinks were well made. All in all a great experience!
RViM0VNKLYaqqxh9Rd1d6w: I was definitely impressed. I've had ramen from many other places but was not satisfied, unlike this place. I loved that it was a customizable ramen menu. The broth had a lot of flavor, and the spices were true to its levels. The whole meal did not lack in anything. They didn't have a lot of things on the menu that I wanted, but that made me even more hopeful for their main dishes. A restaurant that is out of ingredients is a restaurant to look forward to. I absolutely recommend this place if you are looking for a good bowl of ramen.

Very trendy restaurant with much character inside. Love the decor and style. Amazing ramen dishes with incredible creativity & a twist 
" Taste of Tennessee" with the BBQ Brisket Bowl -WHAT!!! Can't wait.

Drowning gyoza- oh YEAH!! Love the Shinigami ramen ... I've heard some comments about needs salt- disagree. Ramen should not be salty .. it should be tasty! Taste the broth! YUM!

Super happy with the service as people were friendly. Outdoor eating area is always the best. Can't wait to go back and try some more RAMEN!
Sorry No pictures !

I was really excited to try this spot because I have been craving ramen! It's also pretty close to me which is a win! I was also looking forward to trying a creamy chicken ramen which is often hard to find.

I loved that we were able to build our own ramen and get the goodies in it we wanted. That was definitely a plus. I also prefer tofu in my ramen and that's not always an option...here it was.

However, I would say that this was a little pricey for what it was. The broth was creamy, but a little underwhelming in flavor. It could use a little bit more of something. Also, they charge for hot sauces and the prices are bit high for that. I feel like if the base price for the ramen was a little lower the add ins would be a little easier to take.
A3VrcPqMLUfcmTbkDXTSaQ:
Maybe the best breakfast biscuit sandwiches I've ever eaten. I was a little worried about whether they'd travel well back home after picking up at the restaurant, but I didn't need to be: as pictured, each sandwich was carefully wrapped and in a little box to hold it steady and keep it from getting soggy (the *worst*!). 

The bacon, egg, and cheese biscuit was delicious and it was great to have a generous biscuit to sop up some of the egg yolk that dribbled out. 

The fried chicken biscuit was also really tasty, with moist and well-seasoned chicken. The hot honey sauce was a good addition to make it more interesting (you can get the sauce on the side if that's not your thing).

We ordering a whole tasting menu because why not. We got the Joyburger, 'impossible' burger, coffee and chocolate shakes, dipstick and joystick chicken, curly and waffle fries. All of it was fantastic. 

The coffee milkshake is the best flavored milkshake I have ever had. It leaves you with this sweet coffee aftertaste and I don't even like coffee that much and I loved this shake. 

The dipstick and joystick chicken was amazing. The flavors on the season were so unique. I could have eaten at least five of those each. 

The staff was wonderful. Brought out our order as soon as we arrived and set it on the car so no direct contact was needed.
My friend took a bite of the Joyland burger and said, "...I think this is the best burger I've ever had." Sean Brock is amazing and I was so happy to visit his latest!
0oFrJWbTdu6S5d2loFKmRw: Didn't know much about this place until a friend talked highly about it. Looked at the menu and they had carne asada fries and I was convinced. The fries were great and a nice size portion. Added a little queso to the fries and I had a great meal. I will be back soon.

One of the few restaurants that serve birria tacos.  Let me tell you, they did not disappoint.  I haven't tried any of their other menu items, it looked pretty basic.  They sell the birria tacos for 3.49 with cheese and without cheese is 3.45.  Makes no sense lol.  Then you add the consume for 1.99.  I was full after just two of the birria tacos.  If you are in the area, definitely come for the birria "red tacos".
The nachos are amazing! We also tried the al pastor taco and it was one of the best. Highly recommend when in Nashville!
j5q7tlanSzvrkiRL8Ms_mA:
The Philly Special in one of over 20+ eateries in the new DT Nashville Food hall. 

I got 'the Works' which was a Philly cheesesteak with green/red bell peppers, mushrooms, onions, and jalapeños. It was hit and delish. Barbara got the standard Philly cheesesteak with 'wiz' sauce. 

Very good lunch!

Texas Philly sandwich
With ribeye, caramelized onions, habanero salsa, jalapenos, queso. We came into Tennessee from Kansas City Missouri for the Chiefs/Titans game. The best Philly sandwich. Seasoned really well. I didn't need to add additional salt nor pepper. Absolutely loved it.  Very spicy.

The best Philly I ever had!!!! I'm sure the Jersey or Philly boys would disagree. Super fast service, under priced for the taste of this delicious sandwich! The bun is heated and soft and pairs perfectly with the steaks and hot melted cheese. Highly recommend trying this place
Dz1ARTZ6Xqo3v53hDvLTxQ: The food was good and so was the drinks. The waitress was very friendly. Come and check it out.
Yum yum yum. I have seen the poor reviews and I am a bit surprised. We've ordered quite often and everytime our food has been a good temp. fresh and well seasoned.

Having just moved from a very Mexican neighborhood in Philadelphia, I am pleasantly surprised to find real authentic Mexican food here in Nashville! We were greeted by our server immediately upon being seated with warm tortilla chips and salsa. I got the fajitas, it was a generous portion (have been eating leftovers all week) and beans/rice/guac/sour cream were great accompaniments included in the price. Pitcher of margs gave all 3 of us about 3 margs each, they tasted pretty fresh too. Would definitely come back!
UaN30BpYBoQMZYjLzeOgjQ: I always hoped that Nashville would get a sweetgreen someday, but Greenery is even better!! Best salad and fruit tea in Nashville by far

Love the Greenery Co! Such a beautiful space and the food is so fresh and good! A healthy spin on a fast lunch. Couldn't be more perfect for Hillsboro Village! Friendly staff and efficient service. 10/10 would recommend

honestly ??? shockingly very filling like tell me why i was full halfway through... was pleasantly surprised and finished it all anyway bc i forglt to ask for a lid and didnt wanna get back in line for one but yeaa.. also service was fast and friendly :)) only complaint is that the fruit tea weirdly tastes like anything but fruit or tea
2hQuKePRM5zmEFbhEFeCpQ: We've been anticipating the condado opening for weeks since we live a block away. Drinks, tacos and service did not disappoint! Trevor was super helpful and took great care of us. Will definitely be back!

We recently had Condado Tacos catered at work! The spread was phenomenal and everything Was delicious. The vegan chorizo was a huge it too! People didn't even believe it was vegan. The sides and sauces were fresh and tasty! The food was so good that I also had it ubered a few days later and tried some of the specialty tacos and they were so good. Then.... We also made a visit to this location and again, we weren't disappointed! The pineapple margarita topped it off! Love this food!
This was my first visit to Condado Tacos and I loved it! The service is top tier and the food is amazing. I highly recommend their steak tacos and the horchata. I'll definitely be coming back!
y5OovQyNsJ77O0AR2XPIww: So, we keep coming back,  this time for a special occasion. My wife and I love this place.  You should give it a try! Fast friendly service,  great atmosphere,  and excellent food.   Always a great experience.

This is going to be a popular place as it just had its soft opening. 

The food is fantastic! We ordered Ramen, Chicken Katsu, Gyoza, Hamachi Kama, a few pieces of Nigiri Sushi (Tuna and Scallop) and a couple of recommended rolls like the Zombie and the Spider Rolls. To help wash it all down was incredibly cold Kirin on draft and by contrast some hot tea. We topped it off with Mochi Ice Cream. 

All were excellent especially the Hamachi Kama and the Scallop sushi which is actual scallop and not a bunch of tiny scallops mixed with mayo in seaweed. 

Service was great! We even had the owner, Jim, come by to make sure everything was to our liking and spent time to chat with us. We will be making this one of our regular places for sure before it becomes way too crowded! 

Begrudgingly highly recommend this restaurant out of fear of creating a frenzy of regulars!

This is our new favorite Japanese/sushi restaurant in Nashville! Really fresh, quality food, beautiful presentations, and fair prices. The restaurant was huge and the staff were all super nice. Out of everything I think my favorite was the teriyaki salmon - it was fantastic along with everything else.
Er_6k2pvoYmYMXqzLsh_oQ: We had a blast!! Everything was delicious and they staff was so friendly. What a fun experience. Will definitely return. It would be a great date night!

Went to Culture + Co. last night with a few girlfriends, and was blown away. I don't think many places are truly deserving of five stars, meaning to me without flaw, but this place is. The experience was stellar from start to finish. We tried some of the best quality cheeses I've ever had. The plated pairings with the cheeses were extremely creative, beautiful, and perfectly complementary. The wines were phenomenal, and the list was extensive. I was a little worried about how the service would be, reading some prior reviews about it just being two people running the whole operation, but they have apparently since hired servers, and they were completely on it and never left us waiting or wanting for anything. I am already dying to go back!

This place is amazing and absolutely delicious!  A unique hidden gem I found for my wife's birthday.  The kiddos even loved picking things off the conveyor and trying them!  Will always come here when I'm Nashville.
QusAWlw0Tw_pficTWFb3yA: Definitely good food and service. On arrival I had to wait for fries to be cooked fresh. Black owned business in a urban area. Prices Reasonable!

I'm originally from atlanta but I'm Nashville for school. I haven't found a good wing place yet since I been here until I came across this beauty! 1st I would like to talk about the customer service! Amazing! I didn't know how their lemon pepper tasted so the woman working allowed me to try all the flavors. When I tell you it was good, it was good. My food took about 10 minutes to make. I took that first bite and I felt like I was in HEAVEN!! Everything was delicious!! Will definitely be back!!

I thoroughly enjoyed the hot lemon pepper shrimp tossed in the sauce, the deep fried oreos, and the deep fried twinkie! These are all must haves at Strikeout Wingz on Ewing Drive. The staff are super friendly and helpful. Strikeout Wingz is located where the original Prince's Hot Chicken place used to be. I have had their Strikeout Wingz and and it's flavorful and hot! It is not for the weak. I would only get that flavor if you are off of work the next day. Haha. Their fries are ok. I think I will ask for mine to be fried just a little crispier as I didn't like them soft from the last times that I've gotten them with my meal. I would definitely come here again.
iBVsaEB8WvL35XUwCajuHA: Wow, that was delicious!  My boys loved the cheeseburgers, my wife loved the brisket, and I loved the pork bbq sandwich.  We will be back!

Wonderful people and great food! The brisket nachos and pulled pork are usually my go to and all the sides I've had are incredible. The baked beans are out of this world and the saucy, over-the-top fried chicken sandwich is quite an experience! 

Please give them a try and support this biz!
