We ordered a fried chicken sandwich, the smoked hickory wings in the Totos sauce, Mac and Cheese, charro beans, and hush puppies. Everything was delicious! We picked up the food for takeout and all the staff were wearing masks. Definitely recommended!
43exQ04EDl3VoWEyjYJw4A: I tend to like local hole-in-the-wall places like this, YMMV.  The restaurant is in the back of a Mexican grocery store, and most of the other customers were speaking Spanish.  I got the Enchiladas Supremas and was very satisfied with the portion size and preparation for the price I paid. Can't vouch for the authenticity of the food, but the clientele would seem to argue for it.  Would definitely consider coming again if I'm ever in town.

Having been born and raised in SoCal, I'm pretty picky when it comes to Latin food. Hand over my heart, this is the most authentic, best tasting Guatemalan food I've ever had. The Salpicon is to die for, a FRESH radish, onion, mint and tender beef salad with lime juice is a meal all by itself. This place might be a little off the beaten path, but well worth the trip.

Having visited several South American countries, I was excited when my friends recommended a taste from Central America. I ordered the Enchiladas (chicken) Verde and it was delicious. They kicked it up a little with some spice, but not too spicy. They had a lot of good things on the menu. Next time I'm in town, I'd be happy to try some more.
0h2g0Xv5ZNSQedhR9R2xLQ: Great tea!!! I can't wait to try more flavors :) I got boba tea and their boba was the perfect texture! Both workers were very friendly too.
Great place for tea if you're looking for lots of options!! Love this place.

Reasonably priced teas and a wide variety of selections. Staff was friendly and checkout was quick. With COVID restrictions, tea smelling was unavailable, yet it did not inhibit the selection process as all teas are very clearly and thoroughly labeled.
GuoxUKwFBAZNiQ5vN5CNJQ: We had a wonderful experience at Sadie's tonight, and we can't wait to go back! The space is beautiful, and our service was great. I had the chicken kabob, and my husband had the braised short ribs. Both meals were so delicious! We also really enjoyed the Sidecar cocktail. The only thing we would change would be for the tables to be a little more spaced out. The couple at the table next to us even made a comment when we were seated because of how close the tables were. Otherwise, everything was perfect!

We are so grateful to have stumbled upon Sadie's! The food, the ambiance, and service all made our experience unforgettable. If we could give more than 5 stars we would! Kristen was excited to share the salmon special and brought the menu to life by sharing her favorites. The Spanish Villa cocktail with purple gin was one of the best cocktails we've ever had. It's clear that the staff is thoughtful about every aspect of the meal. Highly recommend for locals and visitors! We'll be back soon!

I think we went in the first week it was open. I usually give a lot of Grace to new restaurants since they have to work out a few bugs. This was not an issue at Sadie's. This team is experienced restaurantuers.  The service was excellent from Holly. She was great and friendly and made excellent recommendations. The restaurant ambience is fantastic. Beautiful chandeliers and very classy bar set up with a great bourbon and whiskey selection. Not too disappoint the food was excellent. We ordered the salmon kabobs. They came with two different sauces and a spicy hummus that was delicious. Food, ambience and service contributed to a great evening. This restaurant also attracts a cool and trendy crowd. If they're doing this good in the startup phase they're going to be an excellent choice going forward and will be a hotspot ongoing. The restaurant filled up pretty quick so I'd recommend reservations.
G0weNfAr8eGunromy3srpA: The spot is nice and clean. Our waiter was fine and so was the food and the taste is pretty authentic. The salsa and chips were good.

Just looking for a quick place to eat that wasn't drive through. I'm super into "not run of the mill" Mexican food. 
This place is Awesome !! 
Chimichanga for my daughter 
 Vegetarian Enchiladas for me ! 

Can't wait to try the other 6 things I wanted on the menu ! 
Nice good heat to the food , it I'm not a Sissy, I like it hot !!

The wife and I were searching for a spot for lunch over in East Nashville. We reviewed our options and went to Bite a Bit, but found that they were not open until 430pm. We searched Yelp to find Reyes Mexican Restaurant off Dickerson Pike. The one review for the first person to post one was pretty good. We headed that was as the restaurant also seemed to be new to the Eastside. We made a pack that if we unimpressed with the outside of the restaurant we would head to the always great El Fuego off of Gallatin Pike. Once we arrived at 130 pm were please to see that the restaurant was very pleasing to the eye and the lunch crowd had just started to let let out. We saw a large contingent of a child's birthday party leaving and a lot of people with smiles on their faces. We parked in the ample parking area and walked in. We were immediately welcomed in and seated with menus. The co-owner Julio was our server and was great. Julio advised that the meat in the fajitas were locally sourced as his partners family owned a processing plant in Knoxville that delivered the meat weekly. I asked where he purchased his oysters and he advised that he received them on Mondays and Thursday from company that shipped from New York. As I am a Rhode Islander I seldom have chance to get oysters so that made me order 6 on the half shell (picture attached) the oysters cold and fresh. The order came with avocado whipped dipping sauce and crackers. The ceviche app was unbeatable and a huge serving to boot. We couldn't get enough so I ordered the fajitas and the wife got the taco salad. Obviously we had to get a box as we ordered way more then we would should have. The food was great and the home made hot sauce was perfect. What a great place and for only being in business for 4 months they seem to be killing it! I advised Julio to be ready for the invasion as this place is legitimate authentic Mexican food at its best!!!
9w-LZEkP1hf363G-OYvKDg:
Tacos, Tacos, Tacos!!  Love this place.  Stand in line to order **they take cash or check only but there is an atm machine in the corner** and they call your name when your order is ready.  I tried the Pulled Pork taco, Fried avocado taco, and grilled corn.  Yummm Yummm
I'm from Texas but I will be back next time I'm in Nashville :)

This place is DELISH! They have a semi-limited menu as some of their items are only sold on certain days. The only reason why I have given this 4 stars instead of 5 is due to the limited hours they are open for service and their ever changing menu. The fried avocado tacos are the best. They also have daily agua frescas which are also amazing. Definitely worth the visit! There's usually a line out the door, so plan on waiting.

Best tacos ever. I love everything I've tried here! The sauce they put on the tacos is pretty spicy, but so good. Love the avocado tacos and elote. The cash only can be a hindrance but they have an ATM on site. Expect long lines.
UV3QgpYgHO0GGTYPPr4v3g:
Simple but GREAT! As for the tacos, don't expect anything gourmet or fancy. Incredibly good tacos with simple ingredients.

I highly recommend the fried chicken and carnita taco. Definitely get two tacos and mix and match!

The wait in line can be long if it's a busy time, but food comes out extremely quick.

Tacos. They are not traditional Mexican tacos, but they are GOOD. You know the thing you feel when you put food in your mouth and your eyes roll back into your head...you slightly slip into bliss...and lose yourself. Once your waiter/waitress revives you back with ammonia salts you soon realize you have just been hit with tastiness. Everything that they make is just excellent. They cook and execute everything to perfection. I have to tell you I really hate fancy places that try to cook elaborate meals but do not execute each part of the dish therefore it fails...miserably. This place does simple food well, by well I mean awesome. I always get the chicken and fish tacos. The chicken tacos: fried chicken tenders, lettuce, tomato, and a special ranch. The fish tacos: fried cod, jalapeños, lettuce, and a special tartar sauce. The chips they serve are my favorite style: thin and super crispy right along with their tasty cheese dip one cannot go wrong here.

We've been here time & time again. The Cubano is a favorite, but you really can't go wrong. The tacos are all winners. The margaritas are delicious. And the staff - all clever, initiative & kind.

Oh, and the free oranges are a delicious treat.
4szMVHmGXodrVGw4Zhev9g: Not on the menu (but should be), ask to have the house margarita made spicy. The addition of fresh jalapeno peppers gives the right amount of kick without being overpowering. This and the overall lively atmosphere and quality of service stood out.

Where to start!! Everything here was AMAZING!!! From the wait staff and service we received to the food and then the drinks! They even have Cokes from Mexico!!!! Winning!!!!! We will definitely be back! They had great recommendations and honest opinions for their food. Being that I'm Mexican and a HUGE critic of Spanish food says alot. In my husband's words this place was BOMB! haha
Their jalapeño hot sauce is amongst the best I've ever tasted. Made everything tasty. The colliflower taco was original and surprisingly tasty as well.
dGUyXgFW1WSYMgRypKaYew: Was there for brunch and the Food was delicious. There was a slight mix up with one of our orders but they fixed it pretty quickly. I'd definitely recommend the biscuits and gravy. Finding a non meat gravy was a real score.

We were driving through the area and needed a late lunch so stopped in here.  It was midweek and later inthe day so we were after the lunch crowd so it was pretty quiet.   The service may have been a little slow but not bad and the food was excellent.  Great atmosphere and enjoyable meal.
Food was really good but the service was a little slow.  I enjoyed the build your own Bloody Mary.
pm8H4MRrEsp2K_3ln2pvqQ: Our weekly or evening meetings  J'Alexander is always a superb selection. This place is fabulous. We usually get Naomi as our server. Her personality and passion is always the same. She knows us as the "funeral guys" our joke. She generally remembers our food order and takes good care of us.  So,  my review is go,  eat,  enjoy the atmosphere and if Naomi is working she will treat you like royalty.

A perennial business lunch favorite, you'll also sometimes spot celebs dining here, undisturbed.

The veggie burger here is outstanding, though there's been longstanding debate about whether it's vegan. Certainly you need to order it without the mayo (and possibly without cheese, but I can't remember at the moment if it normally comes with cheese). They normally coat the patty in a teriyaki glaze that's very thick and tasty, but which can be a bit overwhelming, flavor-wise. It's great for meat-eaters venturing out to a veggie burger, but if you're already veg*n, you may want to get the glaze on the side and add it to taste. If you're into French fries, get the shoestring fries with it: there's a huge stack of 'em and they're addictive fun.

Another outstanding veggie-friendly menu item is also high raw: the Thai Kai salad. You'll have to order it without chicken, and if you want it higher raw, ask for it without the wonton noodles. The dressing, with its peanut-butter-base, isn't quite raw, but the remaining thinly-chopped and stacked-high veggies are, and it's a delicious textural experience.
jZd462wsUBOIB60UEjUOsw: Really great atmosphere to sit and chill while drinking a good though weak cup of coffee. No decaf selections on their menu though :(

The coffee shop is adorable! Love the atmosphere   and decor....

I ordered a Whiskey Caramel Latte and it was delicious...this coffee was one of the best ones I've tasted in a very long time. 

There are many friendly staff members to service you. The prices are a bit higher than expected although worth it for the great food and drinks.

Highly recommend this place for the quality food and great atmosphere. The parking is decent in back, the baked goods are a nice treat to go with fantastic coffee. 

The only thing that would make this 5 stars is slightly better service. You have to kind of beg for guidance if you don't already "know the drill" and understand. I wasn't sure when/where to pay and where to order and what to stand their waiting for vs when to go sit and wait and they don't tell you unless you awkwardly ask :)
PlaECqs3oPBRo47p85zkzw: Best French fries and Mac and cheese ever!! Also real chicken. Nashville hot is like SUPPA hot!!!! Everything was on point

Amazing. One of my highlights in this 4 day trip to Nashville. Great food and great service. We tried to go here Friday early afternoon and we were told 2 hour wait- so we went somewhere else and then I for a text that our table was ready about 20 minutes later.  But, the picture of their Bloody Mary's brought us back today and I am so glad!  What a great meal to ring in the New Year, especially after our epic night (WSMFP- NYE2017). I had the Cobb salad. Super yummy. And we loved the white BBQ sauce on the chips. *** my husband got the hot chicken and said it was too hot. He was in fire. Next time he would get mild. 
We will definitely be back. It is worth the wait.

If you're looking at reviews and see low reviews about  service that's one thing.. but to say it's not good fried chicken is absurd. I thought this place was better then Hattie B's. The medium (which is essentially hot) is just the right amount of spice if you like spicy and tasteful fried chicken. I'd come back here every time I'm in Nashville. It's also a stop in the Nashville Pedal Tavern which is also a great experience if you're visiting Nashville for the first time.
7u3LzhyxgPMo__24R4dmAQ:
My smokehouse burger and sweet potato fries was perfect, and service was speedy once I ordered.  Unfortunately, after sitting down, getting a menu and a glass of water, the waiter seemed not to notice me.  The lace was not crowded - maybe 10 people/2 waiters. After a good while of quietly trying to get his attention, and him servicing several people that came in after me, I waved both arms frantically. He saw that. :-). When I told him I had trouble getting his attention, he said something to the effect of "Oh!"  I sat quietly for a moment looking at him until he blurted out "I'm sorry."  I accepted. (It shouldn't be that difficult.)  Past that he knew I was irritated and was extremely attentive.  The free dessert for checking in - chocolate  brownie with peanut butter bacon ice cream a la mode - was unfortunately for me big enough to share. I ate alone and well... Finished it. 

Ok. Kudos to mr waiter who sincerely apologized at check time due to a miscommunication between he and the other waiter.  

I depart sated and content.

We come here a lot for dinner . Only been to brunch once and it was packed! For dinner the egg rolls are a must try! We love the original Donna pasta. The service is always fast and everyone so friendly!
Had the Mardi Gras pasta, steak sandwich and medium wings.  All were delicious and wait staff was prompt and friendly.  Will definitely visit again!!
3JpJ3b8r5jMdAb1yPmchrQ:
OK -- full disclosure on two important things: 

1) This chicken is a rich, rich choice. If you're not used to food that's super rich or spicy, you might have an adverse reaction to it. Like I did. Don't get me wrong, I enjoyed EVERY BITE. But, uh, I had some eh, stomach issues afterward, so be forewarned.
2) CALL AHEAD if you can. We waited 1.5 HOURS for our small order. But that only speaks to how incredible this place is. Everyone wants some, and they're willing to wait. 

Honestly, it's an incredible local joint -- worth the wait, worth the stomach issues, worth the burning sensation as it slides down the gullet. Limited menu, for the BETTER. What they do, they do better than I've ever had before. 

MOIST chicken, crunchy skin, excellent flavor. + Pickles. Loved it. Great sides available too. 

Get in here, and get ready. It's amazing.

They came highly recommended from several locals.  We got the Hot Chicken and let me tell you, it was fire.  If you haven't been here before, start with the medium or mild.  After the burning left my lips and tongue, I can honestly say it was very good.  The area seemed kinda sketchy, but the security guard on duty was clearly in charge.

Prince's is simply the best, most authentic Nashville hot chicken experience one can have. No frills, just great food made with love and tradition. I went with a step down from their spiciest level and I was not disappointed. It was pain and pleasure at once.
DOoJrrsqGzZN2FqzvllKnQ:
Second time me and husband have been here. Very nice atmosphere,  we got to sit outside on the nice patio. Our service was nice and fast, we were seated and served right away and got our food quick, even with it being busy. 

Prices are reasonable. The sloppy Jose taco was delicious and so was the California club. Yummy

It's a really nice place to get a form of Mexican but healthy. The food comes out fast, and the staff is friendly. Occasionally they have 2-for-1 and you can get it in one glass. 
This is always a go to when I'm in the neighborhood. 

I really really like the gyro taco, and the sloppy jose with a side of the street corn. By far the best. 
The guacamole is amazing, and I'm actually not a fan of guac. There's something about how they make it that is delicious!!!

Consistently good food, friendly wait staff and a great variety/selection of options including vegetarian and gluten free. Fun atmosphere and good drink specials - for example Wednesday night is two for one margarita night! There's often a wait, so know that in advance and plan accordingly.
UlGS2NxZSiph33E3nfovRw: This is one of those places where you just embrace the unhealthy delicious options. The fried green tomatoes are yummy, the fried pickles are perfect but there are some fried jalapenos. The hot chicken is southern fried & enough to split if you're getting apps. The portions are the worth the money & my only complaint is that the beer cheese soup had the consistency of dip - but it was oh so rich and tasty.

Visiting Nashville for a weekend we went to Whiskey Kitchen - no reservations, waited about 45 minutes on a Thursday night which was not bad at all.  Had the fried green tomatoes at the bar - awesome.  Had a couple of good cocktails (except for the maple manhatten - way too sweet! - then had a regular manhatten and it was good).  Had the wings (awesome - get em hot) and the fried chicken sliders (so good).  The biscuits were perfect and the sauces were amazing.  Waitress was very helpful and had the girl I was with try a few wines to make sure she liked it before she brought out the full glass.  Would def go back and recommend.

Good food, good drinks. The only reason i didn't give them 5 stars is the wait. No reservations, can be very difficult to get in. Went very early the first time, haven't been able to get in since. And we tried on 3 other visits. If you have the time to spare, i would consider it worth the wait. If you are hungry and in a hurry, not so much...
S2n_06z4lGLZfdJ53XAXQQ: Did happy hour and got all four of the food items...and the smokey old fashioned. The drink was decent but the food was amazing!! Loved the roasted cauliflower which came with 3 amazing dipping sauces...my favorite by far was the truffle pesto! So good!  The scallops were perfectly sautéed with a caramelized crust and sweet and tender on the inside. Can't wait to go back for dinner but the HH was great in itself.  It's not cheap but worth it!
really great lunch place crowd wasn't too bad - able to get in and out easily. service was exceptional and the food that i ate was good. not amazing but good. will definitely be back in the future.

Easily the BEST restaurant in all of Nashville! I would choose it as such if I was absolutely forced to choose!

Their food is totally fantastic and creative!!!  If you like interesting flavor combinations, this is where you've got to go!!!

We almost always get the Butter Tasting for our appetizer just because it is SO good! Big fan!

I've tried multiple dishes from Etch (a few times on my own, and tasting the dishes of the people I've dined with). They are all fantastic!!!

The dessert: TO DIE FOR!!!  

Etch is a TREAT. I can't go here all the time due to the price, but when we want something that will make us feel special, this is it!
0v17Ed8jk2OgUEOHRdcTtQ: we had a really good brunch here.......I think it's better for dinner. It's new and has a few kinks to iron out for Brunch but I imagine they will have this sorted out in no time. Excellent service, our food was delicious, the manager TONI was fantastic, gave us great recommendations of things to do in Nashville. Highly recommend
Incredible service, incredible food, great experience, and consistent every time I visit.  Happy Hour is fantastic, and the servers always make us feel welcome and relaxed.  Keep up the great work!

Omg fish cake fish cake fish cake.  It doesn't sound good but it's a dessert with no fish and IT IS AMAZING.not usually a fan of white chocolate or pistachios but they are mostly a garnish and delicious.  The ice cream, the cake, the apricots. Ugh.

I had the lobster roll for lunch. It was good but very vinegary.  Also had some cold oysters, which I was skeptical of (I've only ever had them hot) they were also phenomenal. Will be back and take all my out of towners!
iObrU6cim0l3exsPzaIYvg:
In college, this was one of a selection of restaurants where we could use our meal plan money. When I graduated, I didn't care to return to most of those place now that I had to use "real" money, with the exception of Cabana. Cabana remains one of my favorite restaurants. 

The ambiance is perfect for a date or for a fun group dinner. I can't count how many birthdays I've celebrated here, and they were all so much fun! It's modern/hip with a southern charm.  There are "cabanas" that your group can sit in so it's like having your private little eating bungalow. You can even control the music.

The food is outstanding and well priced. I really think it's as good as almost any more expensive restaurant in it's genre. I don't know if Cabana markets itself as a tapas joint, but all of the small plates are wonderful so it's great to order a bunch to share. I'm not usually one to order a salad but the Organic Field Greens salad is so so so delicious! I almost always order at least a half sized portion of it, and sometimes will sub goat cheese for the bleu cheese. I also like the penne, Benton's bacon sliders and grit bites (they come with the best cheese sauce thing ever!). 

Cabana is an awesome place to go drinking. You'll find a list of affordable wines and yummy cocktails. Also, ladies night is on Wednesday and if you're female you can get $5 appetizers! WOOO!

I visited Cabana on a rainy Monday night with some colleagues, so we were not able to take advantage of what appears to be a really cool patio.  However, the atmosphere inside was also very nice - even though it was not at all busy.  The wine selection was decent despite the fact that most of the bottles we ordered were of recent vintage.

The food was where Cabana shined.  They have some great seafood selections.  As an entree, the Atlantic Grouper sandwich was plenty filling; there is some regret as I did not get to try the Lobster Brie Mac & Cheese - which locals rave about.  The appetizers were probably the best part of the meal.  I highly recommend the Lump Crab Hushpuppies, and the Cornmeal Fried Alligator with jalapeno aoili was fantastic.

I travel to Nashville frequently for work, and I would definitely visit Cabana again the next time I am in town.
I loved the privacy! We came here with a group and got our food quickly. Food was excellent.
P1Oxdcr6lZtzg2onGYXAww:
Anthony at the bar is a real pro. He will treat you like a friend from the moment you walk in. 

I've had the Deviled Eggs which are becoming a Gastropub staple, every time I've been here and Silo gets it right. The chef makes daily and seasonal changes that keep it interesting. I don't have to love everything on the menu. Challenge me. Test my boundaries. 

Twelve bucks is a bit steep for a bourbon cocktail. In spite of that I admit I keep ordering the "Cannonball" - a Bulliet Bourbon flavor bomb. 

The flatiron steak was perfectly prepared and hit the spot. 

Great bourbons and craft beers plus a limited but smart wine list make this a wonderful neighborhood restaurant.

What a great local find with a modern, insightful take on Southern cooking. Fresh ingredients from local farms make this a real find. While there is a lot of fish, beef , and pork on the menu, the vegetarian at the table said she would eat her vegetarian plate "20 days in a row."

This place is fantastic! Best place we've been to in Nashville. All four of our entrees were excellent. The flank steak was very tasty and cooked perfectly. The chicken confit was unique in a very orgasmic way. The pork chop was delicious as well as the scallops. We will go back next time we are in town. The lighting is romantic but yet you can read the menu. Service was top notch
vnSfFFE5iWT7oUzG3MUx6w: This evening was the highlight of our Nashville trip. It wasn't just a great dinner, it was the total experience. The service was impeccable, from our initial greeting by Kevin, to Paul our waiter and finally to Jack who gave us the most delightful grand tour of this historic building. I felt as if we were in a very special place surrounded by such a wonderful staff. The food was perfectly prepared, presented well & everything from soup, salad, steak as well as dessert pleased the palate. This place is not to be missed.

This restaurant was a great surprise- a friend randomly suggested we try it one day after running errands.  We called and they had a spot for dinner, I had just happened to be dress shopping at Pangea, so I threw one of those on and we headed to the Standard.
I'm a sucker for history, so I simply adored the building.  You go in through the main entrance, then head down the side of the house to reach the ballroom in the back, very similar to many Charleston restaurants.  The ballroom is perfect- you feel like you've reached some secret club in Venice.  I saw some of the other Yelpers describe it as dimly lit or strange- that's the point!  You feel like you're tucked in a corner of the city that not many people know about.  They have a small stage set up in the back of the room and that night they had a girl singing with guitar accompaniment- she sounded like Adele if she'd been raised in the Mississippi Delta.  So, awesome.
Billy was our maitre'd for the night and he was very friendly.  When he asked for our drink order, I asked for any kind of house specialty and he came back with this neat combination of whiskey, coke, a slice of orange rind and other mixers- wish I could remember everything.  I hate Jack and Coke, but this tasted great.  The crab bisque is very very good, I love the baked brie, but my favorite is the duck. 
I came back later with a group of 6 and we received the same attention and excellent food.  This was a group of 5 guys and me, and they loved the secret jazz club atmosphere. 
The house is certainly a grand example of Southern architecture and the fireplace in the ballroom was the backdrop for Andrew Jackson's wedding.  Billy gave us a tour of the front of the house both times, I'd love to see the club upstairs sometime.  If my husband and I ever do an anniversary party, this will be the spot, it looks great for big parties.

I was pretty sure I had left a review for the Standard already.

Dined here several months ago with a party of 4. We enjoyed attentive service. I had the ribeye and the mac-and-cheese, and the side absolutely stole the show. It was my favorite mac-and-cheese in Nashville. The ribeye was good. But the mac-and-cheese was outstanding.
SNuTYRfrIRpBwswsXHj1lw: Very good pizza. Toppings all over the pie. Double bacon pepperoni is fantastic. My only knock on them is that they sell Pepsi and not Coke

I am a HUGE Donatos fan (being from Ohio).  I'm glad there's one only an hour away now.  Before the closest was 4 hours.  Great service, Betty Boop was a sweet lady who made sure our visit was GREAT!!!!  (The other employees made our visit nice as well)

I'm from Indiana, and my husband is from Ohio...so we are well versed in the deliciousness that is Donatos. If you like thin crispy pizza, give this place a try! We also love their asiago garlic bread. Yum!
foFpx3znhLJvnplex8aqww: I just moved to Nashville.   I was in Chicago for a few years and Denver for a decade before that.   I've traveled to India as well so I've been around some places with good indian food..   I've been searching for a good indian restaurant in Nashville and I finally found one.  Not only is this Buffet good, it's the best I've ever had.   For starters the food has great flavor.  They actually add spice instead of making it Bland so everyone can eat it.   The buffet is large and has a lot of different items.   They mix the buffet up so it's different every day.   The Service is excellent and they are very attentive.  I've been to all the other Indian places in Nashville and this Reigns Supreme!

So nice we found an authentic Indian restaurant outside of downtown Nashville. No traffic,places to park and good food.  You can never go wrong with Chicken Masala,the spice was just right . We also tried their Lamb Korma with a little more spice  A Rum punch ,King Fisher beer and  water to get it all down. The environment was just right nice music steady traffic but not too crowded. After a long work day was so nice to enjoy good food without having to make a reservation  or wait 20 min or more just to be seated. We also notice they have a really good vegetarian menu .... can't wait to return an try a little bit more of Taj Indian Restaurant.
Taj is the best indian restaurant on the south side!!!  Very friendly staff!  seems to be very clean although I usually get it to go.  Just wished they delivered.
YvBGiNf-lPJg68RyKBsq2g: I feel like all I should have to say is "White Truffle Mac and Cheese!" It was delicious! It's a fun, funky atmosphere, and the service was great. I had the scallops and my friend had the beef bourguinon. And we split the coconut cake for dessert. Everything was fresh and tasty, and I'd recommend it to anyone!

Great lunch experience. Casual place with great food and wonderful staff.  Wife asked for Mac and cheese (was not on the lunch menu) and the staff/chef gladly prepared for her..,recommend the fried green tomato BLT.  We will be back next time we are in Nashville.
Super delicious, lots of flavor  and fun atmosphere  the burger was great and so was the shrimp with polenta and arugula  the sweet potato was to die for
XvFKxQj9lfLIUxRHrAodQA: Love Atmalogy and the vibe of the place. It's a creative set up with lots of colors! Food is delicious as well as the coffee, but a bit on the pricey side. I love this place and the only thing is that it can take a long time for food and drinks to be prepared - but they do everything some scratch so the result is definitely delish. If you're on a lunch break/in a rush, just plan for extra time. Otherwise, you won't be disappointed!
Awesome place to get away from it all and get some work done....oh and the coffee is pretty good too

I just moved to town last week. I am a huge fan of this establishment. The coffee is fantastic but the service is even better. Parker, and employee, has been awesome. I have seen him the last two times and he has remembered my order and is super fun and super nice. I highly recommend this place. Awesome coffee and awesome staff
01JQ8CecavDWtxVTsVXKqg: We had a great meal here while passing through Nashville. My husband loved the steak. I had the crab cake appetizer and the cauliflower side combined as my entree - both were delicious, well prepared and served fresh and fast too! Everyone was super nice and the restaurant itself is beautiful. Oh I don't want to forget to mention the biscuits! They melt in your mouth, such a nice treat. I don't know why it wasn't more busy on a Friday night but I think people should definitely give it a shot. I'm glad we did. They have a great wine list too!

First dining experience at Kitchen Notes went very well

Me & the rest of the world love their freshly baked biscuits & assorted jams. Its worth a visit just for them.  Chicken pot pie.....as good as I've eaten anywhere. Of course its prepared with their biscuits. Fried Green tomatoes...Excellent.

Positives: Food prepared as requested. Dining room clean, airy & edgy. [which I like]
Bathrooms....spotless. Waiter [cannot recall his name] well informed, attentive & polite.
Menu seems 'well rounded' Prices pretty average except for their drinks.
 
The Negative: Wine selection, decent but overpriced me thinks!! waaaaayyy overpriced for mediocre wine.   That's about it so far......until next time.

My favorite place for brunch. The atmosphere is so bright and inviting. The buffet is just beautiful and I could stay for hours just sampling everything. The pancakes just melt in your mouth and I won't even get started on the biscuits because they are so yummy. The service has always be great with no complaints. Everyone should come here at least once because you will love it.
NIL5qMPl9q0b5Dw0OtJRyw: Loved the pizza! Alcohol selection is a little scarce, but Shelby was great at guiding us to what was good and what we would like! Definitely will be back!

Excellent food and top flight service! My son has nut allergies and they were beyond accommodating in making sure he was comfortable with how things were prepared. They also took the time to thoroughly explain the composition of any food he was interested in trying. 

The special fish  of the day was superb. Chef has a way with fish. I would easily say that no matter what the fish of the day is you should try it.

Very cool atmosphere, easy parking and a nice crowd. Definitely a keeper! The bartender even took the time to make a cool ginger lemon mocktail for my son.
I love Nickys and the Galzin's.  Incredibly delicious and unique dishes from a wonder husband and wife combo.  I love the ambiance and the craft cocktails are to die for.
wNwNaaoMsaObsCwCENhs_A:
Best Sushi in town.
Once you've finished with your meal, head on over to Ken's Karaoke Box for the best Karaoke in town...

Ken's is just the place if you consistently want good sushi at a reasonable price. There are only a handful of good sushi restaurants here and for the $$, Ken's is definitely at the top of the list. Don't go here if you're looking for a nice, romantic, dinner, but if you want great sushi in a casual atmosphere, Ken's is the place.

I like Ken's for good inexpensive sushi. I've eaten there for years and it's a great deal. There fish is always fresh and tasty. It's not very trendy or upscale, but if you're looking for value this is a good bet.
wP2ok4O0GsR9td7Iiv1zKA: Went by for lunch and got their Churrasco sandwich. It was great, service was good and they had some tea that was intense. Wish it were a little closer to town but it was nice to have another unique meal on Nolensville.

I order from here regularly and Never have I had a poor meal. The cheese empanadas are a must try.  Every sandwich is absolutely fantastic, my favorites being the choriburger and the churasco.  I mainly get take out due to the anal space, so if you're going to eat In, be ready for a tight fit.  But don't let that scare you off, im telling you this is the best restaurant in south nashville.  Hands down

I told myself I was going to remember the name of the sandwich I ordered, because I wanted to tell everyone about it. It was fabulous! It had slices of local beef and avocado and some special sauce and an egg on top that was cooked perfectly. They were so generous with the meat that I took half of it home and made a sandwich w it the next day. The quinoa salad was amazing as was the latte. Super friendly staff. I wish it weren't so far from my neighborhood!
f9qNygjcQvh2vPmZSfEvqg: Awesome vegan food.  Wish we had something like this back home. Tried the shroom bowl and everything sampler. Both were really delicious.

Great place. My wife is vegan and I am learning. Wonderful atmosphere, everyone was nice. The food was great , All American "Burger" bowls.  Great selection of sides and extensive menu items. 

Will definitely go back !!
There is ample parking in back and plenty of seating inside this converted home. If you miss the taste of pulled pork, try the traditional barbecue sandwiches be prepared to eat it with a fork.
pXMm5R9QEfGc_3w3RvZRxw: I had one of the best sandwiches I have ever eaten at sloco today. The brisket sandwich was served on a soft roll with pickled veggies, kale and a creamy garlic spread. It was so delicious I didn't want it to end!!

This place is the best. I really can't wrap my head around the lower ratings I'm seeing. If you want quality food made with incredible ingredients, this is where you want to be. I don't mind the prices because I know I'm getting real, quality food. Everything is flavorful and delicious. Everyone I have ever encountered has been nothing but friendly and accommodating. I'm always excited to eat here!

Oh man, oh man, oh man.

I headed to Sloco for the first time two days ago, and I my mouth is still watering wanting more of that divine sandwich.

I got the shaved seitan, and my boyfriend got the pastrami.  I wish I could convey to you just exactly how delicious that seitan sandwich was--the soft, warm, doughy bread, the crunch of the pan-fried chickpeas, the sweet squash combined with the savory, meaty seitan.... I have to stop, my cravings are getting out of control.  The boy enjoyed his pastrami as well, but one bite of my sandwich and he was sold: "Oh yeah. This is better."

VEGAN SANDWICH FOR THE WIN!

Aside from our food just being damn awesome, there are lots of other things to love about Sloco: super nice people, bike delivery service, minimal waste (drinking water out of glass jars, ya'll), a promise to only serve seasonal foods, and the knowledge that whatever you order there will be delicious AND good for your body.  Nashville is lucky to have such an amazing new sandwich shop!

And anywhere that knows how to make a vegan sandwich that is as delicious or more delicious than its meat-laden counterparts is doin it right in my book.
ZhA88ILcgEP9DNwZDx7f0A:
I love this place!!! You have to come here and meet the bartender Crystal! Best ever :)

They have good live music, lots of debauchery, and strong drinks.

You can request songs from the bands that play live, just make sure you tip!

Had a horrible experience coming into this establishment with Brooke. Took 15 min to even get acknowledged on a Sunday night. Moved into an open spot at the bar and Stephanie was amazing! Cared about our experience and checked in on multiple occasions. Made us feel welcome even when she was very busy! Thanks Stephanie!
The place is awesome.  There is always a great banding playing and there is a wide selection of beers.  Overall you have to stop at Honky Tonk Central
2rR0dcyydaleMzJGG6if8g:
The hotel is luxurious and deserving of 5 stars, but what really sets it apart is its amazing hospitality.

The customer service was wonderful before we even checked in. The hotel texted me the day before to see if I had any questions, which I did, and they responded to those questions. As soon as we pulled up to the hotel, we were greeted by valet and our bags were carried to our room. The attendant set our bags down and gave us a quick tour of the room.

AND THE ROOM WAS FANTASTIC. It was very "Nashville", with the wooden floors and accents, Marshall speaker (connects to your phone), Third Man Records vinyl for purchase. Windows were floor to ceiling, so there was great natural lighting. We barely used the lamps.

There were slippers, robes, and even an umbrella for use in the closet. Bathroom was spacious and well designed. Toiletries were available. They had a stocked minibar with wine, sodas, beer, snacks, glasses, and ice container. Beds were super comfortable.

Attendants brought us ice and even some items we had left in the car within 10 minutes.

Even at checkout they were helpful. We wanted to eat nearby after checkout, and they were able to store our bags in a closet at the lobby and keep the car at valet while we searched for breakfast. They texted a thank you for staying text after we left.

Also, the day after I filled out a review for them through email, and the front desk director sent a personalized thank you email back. Little things like this go a really long way.

The ONLY remotely negative experience was how crowded the rooftop bar was.

But anyway, 10/10 would stay here again.

Love love love this hotel! 
Customer service - perfection (especially Amber at the desk and Palmer in valet). 
The rooftop is super fun. 
The restaurant is great. 
If you are going to Nashville the Thompson is a MUST STAY!

Very nice boutique hotel. Rooftop bar is not to be missed. Broadway is walking distance (25 minute walk), but surrounding restaurants and bars are top notch and modern. Rooms are nice, no storage space for clothing, so expect to live out of your suitcases. Friendly staff and a very relaxed feel, overall.
hRoAl3MoV3_D9W9Vi7x3ZA: Real solid. Not a big fan of the coffee, but the oyster sandwich was literally out of control (I said no bacon and they forgot which was a bummer). Please try the sticky buns or you will be so sad. Also, the German chocolate donuts. THANK YOU AND GOOD NIGHT.

The most incredible food experience I have had lately in Nashville. I enjoyed the creative use of all ingredients on the October menu. 
Andrew Little - we have missed  you in Hanover, PA but am thrilled to find you again here!
Come as soon as you can.

The brunch I had at Josephine gave me feelings of euphoria, bliss, transcendence. The fries and omelet I ordered were almost sensual. Sometimes I forget how amazing food can be, and why it is ranked among the top three pleasures in life (you can fill in the blanks of the other two). Thank you, Josephine. Sunday brunch is the church of Millennials.
3Dn_fT7NxzKhjy-yNrfIzw: Food was great! The crowd is a little bit older but we still enjoyed the live music and food.

Great food and great music. Everything came out fast and where weren't sitting there very long. We sat outside which was very nice. The server was great but I kind of felt bad because she was the only one out there working so she had to keep running to the bar to fill drinks then run into the kitchen to grab food. Even though she was the only one outside working everything was vary fast and she did an amazing job.

We came in late (10:30M) after traveling all day.  Brought our 5 year old son with us.  Had a great dinner and awesome live  music.   We had the catfish dinner, kitchen sink salad, and kids' chicken.  Everything was fantastic.  Super fast and extremely friendly service.  Great place in every way!
t-RHwZzlTAZeTcgrrbRtqg: Good place for lunch. The grilled cheese was perfect crispiness, but not over done. Chips with sage and goat cheese were very good. Awesome milkshakes. Tomato soups are really good and a great compliment to the sandwich.

I was headed to Green Hills to find something to eat for lunch and saw the Grilled Cheeserie. I quickly turned my car around and decided to check it out. I'd be heating about them for awhile and had seen them around at Centennial Park and the 12South Farmers Market but the lines were always ridiculously long. I figured I would've sat in Green Hills traffic anyway trying to get to Whole Foods or the Food Company so I decided to check out what all the hoopla was about. I created my own sandwich of turkey, spinach, cheese, and tomatoes on sourdough (if I recall correctly) and chips. It was hot outside and i waited for awhile but there were other interesting trucks there to check out which helped the time pass. One thing i was impressed with was they accepted credit cards as i didnt have enough cash on me. When I finally got my sandwich, I was so excited I went straight over to the condiment area to out mustard on my sandwich and when I opened it up it had bacon on it. Since I don't eat pork I had to return it. I was a bit disappointed they would get my order wrong after having to stand and wait so long but the guy took it back and started to make a new one. At the time I had to assume this because he really didn't say much so I went on hunger hope. A couple of minutes later they called my name and as the guy handed it to me apologized for the error. He also offered me a chocolate pudding which was awesome. Overall the sandwich was great! Is it overpriced? Yes by $2/$3, chips should be included for $8 but it was still a nice change from the usual wholefoods, subways, which wich lunch option.

Fricken' LOVE this food truck. How could you go wrong with variations of grilled cheese?! Answer: you can't. I used to order from The Grilled Cheeserie whenever it would come by my workplace. I love the fact that you can customize your grilled cheese and make it to your liking - the tomato soup is also really good as well. When it comes to the specialty melts, the Pimento Mac&Cheese is my favorite. If you see this food truck around town, go to it and order. Immediately. You won't regret it.
ShBW2Fx5RvPqYxwRDQAfZA: Exactly what I am looking for.  Decor is outdated, bathrooms walls awash with sharpie opportunity but the focus is on the pizza!  They got it right!  Manny is a doll!

I came to Manny's for the first time yesterday with a co-worker who highly recommended it! The food was so good and the service so pleasing that I came back again today! Best pizza I've ever tasted! Made to perfection! Seems to be a family business, and Manny himself was aiding in the preparation of the pizza! Feels like one of the family pizza places you see in movies. Not the biggest place, but the taste of the food makes up for it! Definitely a "must try" for all!
The best pizza this native New Yorker has had since leaving home in 1994, period.  Manny is the best!  You won't be disappointed.
7M8uWd8mH1k10GWDiOEB-A: This place is a good burger, I went to Republic burger based on reviews and it was just OK so I had to find out if Nashville just didn't have good burgers but this place is a great price and a tasty dang burger- it's a diner/ lunch type of atmosphere not a bar type so if that's what you want I wouldn't go here but the food was great....
Clean and neat restaurant, their green tea milkshake is good, but the burgers are even better!

We went here while on vacation, and everything was awesome! The sweet potato fries were crispy and delicious. We had the Seoul burger and a chicken sandwich. Everything was made fresh and the restaurant was clean.
m3f3ypSf8mJY1Or4vbvm9g:
Everything piece created by Oshi is stunning in its simplicity. Perri, the focused genius behind the brand, is an artist. There is no question as to why Oshi is the go-to florist for several businesses in Nashville. 

While creating all this beauty, Oshi also manages to contribute to the community, helping to create a downtown community garden. 
Amazing!

Also -- if you're visiting the SoBro location -- parking may seem expensive BUT the Pinnacle building is only $2 for 30 minutes. Plenty of time to run in and pick up a centerpiece or bouquet!

We used Oshi for our wedding and words cannot describe how wonderful Perri of Oshi Flowers is. The man is not a florist, he is an artist. I almost cried when I saw my bouquet because it was more beautiful than I ever could have imagined. After meeting with him I knew that all I had to do was give him some inspiration and let him run with it. I told him I wanted ivory, green, and lilac for the colors. My only specific requests were white and green lady slipper orchids in my bouquet and my husband's boutonniere, and lilacs in silver plated vases for the centerpieces. What he gave us was AMAZING, he made a special head table arrangement for us with tulips submerged in glass vases, gorgeous silver vases for the centerpieces with lilacs, roses, and other flowers I don't even know the names of. He did corsages for the mother, brought giant silver candelabras to stand at the entrance to the reception, gorgeous ceremony flowers, a flowergirl basket with a bouquet in it along with rose petals. Amazing. Just amazing.
1RQwJwViav_pzhT-zaDp6Q: Easily one of the best Asian restaurants on Nashville's west side.  I've only eaten from the Thai menu but it compares favorably to the better Thai restaurants in the area.  Spices are playful and flavorful.  Presentation is great.  Service is great.  I wish I'd discovered the place sooner after the restaurant previously in the location closed.

Lemongrass Sushi and Thai is a gem in the Bellevue community. The restaurant's ambiance is more relaxing than a Japanese garden and the wait staff is sweeter than plum sauce. And boy, do they know how to make a curry dish (personal recommendation - Panang) that will send your senses into euphoric hyperdrive. These are true professionals and competent artists in their craft; I'm glad they chose this cozy west Tennessee suburb as their home! You will be in remiss to overlook the Lemongrass experience on your next trip down Highway 70.
Delicious food.  Generous portions.  Reasonable prices.  Upscale ambiance.  Staff is attentive.  (We've not tried the sushi.)
R8cwgei6jr5f9b-hQvvroQ: The pizza was great. Very casual. Reminds me of a place in San Diego.   Great.  Just what we were looking for

the dough wasn't perfect but me and my buddy made some damn fine pizzas here
also they have a crap load of awesome sauces
Great pizza made to order with all my favorite toppings and a spicy pizza sauce that left a little zing in my mouth. Had 4 different kinds of cheese on mine with a chocolate chip cookie
3h5gvXC5KEILEuSen5TMkA: This is my second review to add to this little gem. My office ordered pizzas and I finally got the Philly Cheesesteak Sub. The Philly Sub is AWESOME! The prices are always great so this place really is a must try. Thanks!

Love their char broiled half pound burgers!

Gyros are awesome !
 
Platters come with good portion of meat for $8.99

They are using a gas fired stove oven like east coast not a Conveir oven that's what I like :)

And they always have good special s like carry out  large 2 topping pizza special I think it was $7.99 or $8.99

Those lemon pepper wings tho! Perfect late night, and the pizzas really hit the spot, ideal for a post night out throw down. But seriously tho that lemon pepper rub hot dam (but not spicy in a real way tho)
ddCKwEeLTVxP9e36ptRakA:
Great service on a Sunday brunch! We had a large party, but the staff was super friendly. The menu had a great variety; mostly breakfast items. 
When we got our food, it was delicious but not over-the-top. You can't go wrong with breakfast anyway! The restaurant and bathrooms are very clean. I hope they add more outdoor seating soon as the weather warms up! Great breakfast food and customer service without it being overwhelming....I'll definitely be back!

Great southern comfort food breakfast.  I came from Chicago and had the Bubba. It was so good. Service was amazing. Friendly and quick. Came back the next day and ordered the same thing!  Recommend. :-)

We stumbled upon this place while visiting Nashville and had a great breakfast. Good quality and excellent service! We were delighted after seeing the line at the pancake place just down the road.  We would definitely go back.
Fe54PN-9GABpQM30B5o08A: The shrimp and grits lived up to its reputation. The service was good. A really nice restaurant experience.

Fab-u-lous! A pre-Valentine's dinner celebration (with a livingsocial coupon in tow) the hubs-to-be and I headed to Midtown after a slew of recommendations from our yelp friends! 

The dining space is intimate with candlelight and black linens and the waitstaff was fantastic to talk to! I'd go back to just hang out with them again. 

A prix fixe menu ($45/person) was offered for Valentine's Day weekend but many of the items were also offered on their standard menu. Starting with a mixed salad with aged goat cheese and strawberries and a smooth glass of pinot gris, I was already satisfied with the meal. OHH! Let me tell you about the mini biscuits with sweet butter! As soon as our server brought those suckers out, my low carb diet was out the window!!! The main course selections were grilled grouper and prime rib with beautiful vegetable sides, both tasty!

One cool thing about Midtown besides the food and the wine selection is the fact they offer a complimentary shuttle service to TPAC and the Symphony!
Not as intimate as reviewed but food is pretty fucking good. Parking sucks unless you valet. Bars all around to take your date to get trashed at afterwards.
LqNeLawIfB1w0zPXKK8nHg: We did the pre fix menu for New Year's Eve, and it was absolutely amazing. You could absolutely taste how much thought and effort went into each dish, and everything was super fresh and cooked to perfection. The staff was super friendly and accommodating, and I was only half-kidding when I joked to my fiance that I'd consider moving back to Nashville just so we could eat at this place regularly.

This place is so cute! I was there for Valentine's Day. It was a four course meal and everything I picked was so tasty! My drinks were also very good, but I can't remember the names :/( it was the special for that night). I will definitely be coming back to this place!!! The only thing I didn't like was that it's a little too dark and our waiter was kind of rude in the beginning, but thankfully changed his attitude. 1st course, lamb sausage....yum! 2nd- winter salad....yum yum!!! 3rd-  sumac roasted lamb chops....yum! 4th- chocolate brûlée(which they added it to the menu at the last min.).... Yum yum!!!

The Meester Burger is amazing and my favorite burger in Nashville. (and grass fed)
The Pineapple Ketchup and Chipoltle Ketchup are nice twists for the fries.  Many of the drinks have to be explained to me, but the Carlsberg Elephant beer didn't! (i love that beer & it's just not available in enough places)
The Edamame Ponzu Dip with Wonton Strips is a great munchie dish with any cocktail.
It's a dark & moody place with good music and his/hers decor...like a raw wood ceiling and chandeliers.
I'm having my "business casual" wedding reception there and can't wait for my out-of-town guests to love the place as much as I do.

***Went back for dinner and had the Meatloaf made with beef, pork & lamb. It was served over mashed potatoes, with a little spice.  I must say, I don't care for meatloaf...except here. (Mom would be proud)
I had a bite of his pan-seared catfish with buttermilk biscuit & english peas in sauce. I'll order my own plate of catfish next time.
If it's available, try the Artillery Rum Punch.  (It'll be served at our wedding reception.)

Ben was our server and was a joy.
3oJFQGjjb5S1t024iK-JMA: Check-in and get a free order of edamame. I went here for lunch and the prices are very reasonable for the amount of food they give you. I got the sautéed edamame, chicken teriyaki bento box, and a mango iced tea. All were very good! I am definitely coming back!

Our favorite place in Nashville!! Aside from the great atmosphere and fabulous patio area, the sushi is awesome! 
The sushi is comparable to virago but the prices are wayyyy more reasonable! Five thumbs up! Love this place!!!

I've been here twice with different friends. Located in an old house it has about 10 tables inside, a cute fireplace,  a long bar plus a covered patio, it has a nice, cozy atmosphere. I found I really enjoyed it both times I've been. The mango iced tea is sooo addicting! I had sushi both times and really liked it. The fish tasted fresh and I liked the unique roll choices, especially the ones with spicy elements like serrano pepper! The service wasn't bad, wasn't great either. She was polite however, for being the only table in the restaurant at 4:30 on a monday I don't think my glass of mango tea (did I say how amazing it is!!) should have gone empty.  
My friend had the udon bowl and really enjoyed it too.
     I don't understand how the reviews are so mixed. Perhaps they are inconsistent when they are busy? Both times I've been have been during slow times of day. I will definitly be back!
XXSRQj02oCBr0w2xMOftLw:
I've been to more than 50 Jason's across the country and always swing through this one when in Nashville. Located virtually across the street from Vandy there's always a friendly crowd around, the staff is upbeat, energetic and friendly and the food remains consistent (just be careful coming during the lunch rush, it'll be packed). 

Meanwhile, crowds amass for a reason. There's few better places where you can order a half sandwich and soup or a salad bar for fewer than $10 and get quality. Jason's does just that, delivering fresh meats and quality offerings to your table without making them too pricy. 

The salad bar remains well stocked with quality greens, has ample veggies and coupled with a bowl of soup is a meal that keeps anyone full. While there are endless options for lunch in the area, we come here when we want to keep it simple and not spend too much money. 

Note: Keep in mind, there is free underground parking in the back.
I got the pasta and ceasar salad. Both were very good and the service was great.  They are a little expensive but u get quite a bit of good!

Best Salad Bar in Nashville!
I work at Baptist Hospital often and if I am lucky enough to be there during lunch I always head over to Jason's Deli. It is very fast if you grab the salad bar...they even have a express lane just for the salad bar. 
The sandwiches are massive and very good, I have also order a couple of there carry out platters of sandwiches for football games....no matter how many I buy they are always all gone by half time....need to stop inviting so many friends.
Fr4HGxz8_s8BxY_fZdadzQ: I can't find one negative thing to say about this place. I'm surprised there isn't a line out the door for their fresh ingredients and hand crafted sandwiches. Go out of your way to come here, it's totally worth it!!!

So tasty! I always get whatever special they are doing that day and have always been impressed with the creativity combined with comfort food of the dishes. I have eaten many meals from here but never been inside so I can't comment there other than saying that my Nashville buds enjoy the place. Sláinte!

For a sandwich shop, this place floats the boat, err, rather, rocks the ship, well it, keeps its head above water.

Ship! What I mean is this place is just that good. With a mini grocery store inside, you can find a variety of unique and specialty items (fudge mint cookies?)! 

The bread here is off the chain. No it won't bite you, but it will be bitten by you. Ok, let's get serious. Mitchell's has A quality food. I personally eat their BBQ tofu and grilled veggie sandwiches often. We even get them for work lunch events, and they have the best deleivery package around for the price. 

If you dare leave out the pasta salad or dessert (brownies!), you should recognize that you are also missing on the full experience. 

The staff here have a good system going. They are also great to suggest combinations and variations of menu items. There is also a make-your-own salad bar. 

Overall, grab your oars and bring a tooth pick.
d4ZPdoYxDnT6f70AZPGrcw: Fantastic!! Such a delicious breakfast in such a cute hipster house! The service was awesome. Was a bit of a wait but they have coffee outside for you to serve yourself

We found this little place while passing through Nashville. We were seated fairly quickly on a busy weekday morning. The space is bright, warm, and quirky. The seating inside is a bit tight, there is both family style seating and more private tables, in addition to outdoor tables.

Their coffee is great and comes in really large coffee cups. We ordered omelettes which come with potatoes, toast, and a side of fruit. Everything tasted really fresh, was nicely seasoned and not too salty.  We served by a few guys who were all friendly and prompt, but not overly chatty which was appreciated in the early morning. 

Great place a bit off the touristy path for a solid breakfast.

Scoutmob brought me here, and their delicious food will keep me coming back. Went here for brunch and ordered the Tuscan and the French Toast with a mocha. The Tuscan was a huge surprise- for such a simple menu item (two eggs over easy, on bread with tomato and parm), it was done with such classiness and great flavor that I couldn't get enough. The french toast was also great and their coffee was delicious. I love the atmosphere- with the trivial pursuit cards on the table and fun decor, it's the perfect hang-out spot with friends for good times and a great meal. Street parking can be tough, but it's worth it.
4DLIC5fF_QXg61Hwv2BPeQ: Such a great addition to downtown. Popped in for to go blintzes last week (and made my entire office extremely jealous, that vanilla sauce though!) and just grabbed the chopped salad, and a delish apricot cookie I can't remember the name of, for lunch today. Everything has been so delicious so far, and I can't wait to try the whole menu. Also a big thanks to the gentleman working the deli take out side today for letting me sample the maple/sea salt bagel chips, they're my new favorite thing.

Classic NYC style Jewish deli in the Fairlane Hotel on Union Street. The smoked salmon is an excellent choice and the extensive breakfast menu has great offerings to choose from with reasonable prices.

If downtown is going to become a pit of bachelorettes and party barges, at least there will be good bagels. 

Pros: Went there around 12:30 on a Saturday and it wasn't close to crowded. The poutine was great. The Bloody Mary's are reasonably priced. There are latkes. 

Cons: The reuben wasn't great; I prefer griddled bread, but points for fresh pumpernicm. I think I'd stick to the bagels and eggs and anything with smoked meat.

Verdict: If you ever need to escape Broadway, go here and get a bagel. It's worth the post-meal meat sweats. Also, the owner and staff all seem super nice.
7j-TfyfmfJk5QKfxRtTbqw: This is it. In my opinion, and yes I have had them all, this is the best hot chicken in Nashville. This is the best location too.  Driving a little out of town will save you the hassle of the wait if you are trying to go prime time. Their  strongest quality isn't customer service, but it doesn't matter when your there for the chicken.
You already know the chicken is amazing but the restaurant is fab too! A great family friendly space with lots of TVs. You should totally go.

I went in for lunch today and was really impressed! The lady who took my order and ran my food wasn't all that nice but the food made up for it. There is a bit of a wait for the food but everything came out extremely fresh! I got medium and it was pretty hot! I liked it a lot more than Hattie Bs. Will be back!
0e6iVLSXdgWn_6qKcjkxXQ: We LOVE Cizar's! The pomegranate chicken platter is choice, paired with the cucumber sauce, delish! Their service is always impeccable. Delivery is quick. I am never disappointed with my weekly Cizar's fix!

I plan to work my way down the menu.
Delicious, reasonable, fresh food.
Nice owners and managers.

Like everyone else.. 
I appreciate the fact that they keep it reasonable and delicious.

I tried the grilled chicken sandwich with all the trimmings and tzatziki sauce.
Next I plan to try the gyro meat with tzatziki sauce and then the philly cheesesteak..
or the other way around.. maybe the philly cheesesteak and then the gyro..
so many choices...  and I know every one of them will be good.

I had seen the ads on the East Nashville FB group. I had heard good things from friends. We were told they deliver. I could no longer put off trying out Cizars! 

The delivery was what sealed it. We ordered a falafel gyro and a gyro/chicken combo plate. The plate was awesome! I liked the falafel gyro, it was fine, but I now know that the gyro is where it's at! We will definitely order again and I will definitely get a gyro. I'll also be adding a side of hummus. They really know how to make a great hummus! If you haven't tried Cizars, you absolutely need to go. Heck, just order delivery and enjoy from your couch!!
pvE9rPVhOG8fODMrM65KFA:
Mmmmmmmm! I came here last night for the first time and got the cheese plate w/ local honey as an app and the steak tartare as my plate. It was all so fresh and good. This was my first time having tartare and I'm so glad I got it here!  My cocktail was awesome as well, and they have a great happy hour menu. $3 for beers and $6 for cocktails. 

Another thing I loved was the service. They were so on top of refilling water and everyone was very friendly. I loved my experience here!

Every time I go to Rumours, the experience is fantastic!  Their pimento cheese is the BEST I have ever had...so it's always a given. Their mussel bowls are also a treat. If you get them, make sure to add shrimp.  And their scallops...so fresh and a really great size. The amount of this entree doesn't disappoint. If you can, get their early so you can snag a seat on their beautiful patio :)

Enjoyable dinner - somewhat limited food menu, but good options and very tasty. Beet salad was fabulous. Service was good.  Location is in an up and coming area of Nashville. Back room perfect for a group that wants to chat, the noise level was moderate and music selections enjoyable.
Tnjw1ykBy1INMWRJcZWhJw:
Union common is a great place for a foodie! It's setup for everyone to share just about every plate and try many different things to widen the palate. 

The wine list is quite lengthy but man is there some good stuff on there! The wine glasses are also very cool..I'm thinking about grabbing a few for my mom! We had the Flaherty Cabernet Sauvignon. Let it breath for a second and you will not be disappointed! 

 My family and I tried the Sweetbreads and the bone marrow as an appetizer. The sweetbreads have excellent flavor. The after texture isn't for everyone, but the flavors are on point. The bone marrow was very surprising! It's served with little toasted breadpoints which complement the marrow magnificently. 

For the entrees we had the broccolini, smashed fingerling potatoes, oysters, and the 32oz ribeye.  The broccolini had golden raisins and walnuts which were perfect complements with both taste and texture. The fingerling potatoes were fried to perfection! Great crisp texture. The oysters were the best oysters I've ever had. They fly them in fresh everyday or every other day depending on the situation. The combination of sauce, lemon, horseradish, and the cracker just made it that much better! The 32 oz ribeye was excellent! They actually made it half mid rare half medium which is very hard to do! It has a great crisp texture on the outside with a juicy tender inside. 

Last but not least is the banana foster. My dad is an ice cream fanatic and even as picky as he is he mentioned that it was one of the best fosters he's every had!

We visited on Saturday evening and had a great experience. Service was on point and I think the menu is a nice detour from the many traditional steak houses we have in Nashville. Since the menu was expertly explained to our party by our server (that everything is intended to be shared, including steaks) we navigated the menu and our ordering pretty well. Everything we tasted was delicious and we'll definitely be back.

Just a great meal.  Small plates for sharing and for those like me who can't decide.  Crispy Duck, Oysters, Pecan Salad with Blue Cheese Gelato, and Lamb Chops were all fantastic.   Cheesecake Beignets were delicious.  Outstanding service and good bourbon list!  Definitely will be visiting again.
D2igy5Aexgdk8d-DV2K1vw: Aladdin's was a really nice hookah lounge to visit! The service was excellent and they have many options of hookah flavors and cocktail choices! They also have great happy hour deals on hookahs and beverages! Very pretty atmosphere as well! Overall I really enjoyed my experience here and would definitely recommend to others!
I feel like I'm on a magic carpet ride every time I'm here. Maggie, Hamza, and Aimee always make my experience incredible.
One of my favorite hookah spots. Great service, really cool environment, and the hookah was set up and maintained pretty well. I'll definitely come back.
o8H4fliNg1FYqw6noUzZzA: Great bar to have an interesting evening in.  The Karaoke is no joke, there are some seriously good singers.  The only complaint is that it is packed, packed, packed on most weekend nights.

Great place to hear some great karaoke, meet your friends for a few drinks, and/or meet some new ones!  Now, they are non-smoking downstairs (which is where all the action happens), and serving up a pretty amazing brunch on the weekends!

What I wouldn't give for their chips and salsa right now with their housemade ice cold margarita. Since eating there I've been craving them-I could make a meal off their tasty chips and multiple delicious dips. Seriously-their dips/sauces were the best I've had in a long time. You hear about people loving Qdoba's chips, salsa, and cheese- they've got nothing on The Lipstick Lounge. I've tried there crazy queso, rojo salsa, guacamole, and avocado ranch; all but the avocado ranch I loved. The quesadilla was simple yet tasty and filling. The cheese on it was exactly what you'd want in it-nice and gooey so it's not dry. I also had their Moo Carne burrito which fed me for lunch and dinner. I've never gone there for just drinks to hang out but as it got later in the evening (on trivia night) the place began to fill up like your local neighborhood bar should. People were inside as well as hanging out on the patio which is where I'll likely sit when I head back there for brunch sometime.
SWuTULUIN_pQZUhaJYLloQ: The food is delicious. The drinks are superb! There are games to play, the atmosphere is perfect! They have a great patio and very cool, clean inside atmosphere too with TVs and a projection. Check it out.
Very chill bar to play games or watch a game. There is an NES set up in the back and board games everywhere. The projector was perfect to watch the game!

Hurry Back has an amazing game culture with weekly Nerdy Talk trivia nights. The beer on tap is plentiful and diverse--not something to miss. However, the food leaves one a bit wanting. I settled on the BBQ Pork Nachos and was given a huge portion of chips smothered in white queso and chunks of pork. While the overarching idea of nachos was there, the dish lacked in substance. A few beans poked hesitantly out between the stack of chips and meat alongside a few strands of onions, but not even the handful of canned jalapeños could save the lackluster dish. The whole entree was soggy within a few minutes due to the weight of the bbq.

Amidst a laid back crowd, the location feels calm and collected. An over-21 establishment, the bar is usually bustling but easygoing, and you will definitely be served within just a few minutes by the amazing bartenders. Service is efficient, no-nonsense, and Spartan. I have no complaints or gushing remarks really, just a good, solid standard. 

Of course, you cannot talk about Hurry Back unless you talk about the games galore. Our table indulged in a retro "Where in the World is Carmen Sandiego?" board game that reminded us all of childhood memories. The restaurant also had the Rock'em Sock'em Robots classic, Battleship, Connect-4, and other nostalgia-soaked games. Pinball lays hidden in the corner and a Nintendo-64 can be found tucked away by the bar. 

This pub is low-key and a perfect hangout for groups who just want a beer with fantastic company.
EaVe0Pn7v2wt4b_jGYr5lw: Great food! Clean restaurant and friendly staff. The sushi rolls are big -- my boyfriend normally orders two basic rolls and he couldn't finish both which is a first! The pho is delicious, I've had both chicken and beef. Great prices, will definitely come here regularly.
Been here a couple of times and the food/service was enjoyable each time. I've tried the sushi and pho and both were very good. Will return.

3.5 not bad stars. Love seeing popups of Vietnamese restaurants opening up around town. Especially so close to work. So when hubby asked to have lunch, knowing he loves Viet food, I knew exactly where I wanted to try. Its the last shop in the Kroger strip in Berry Hill. It was a beautiful Friday afternoon so they had the doors open to make it feel like an outdoor experience. The restaurant has two spaces, front space that has 3 tables and a bar area and a side space with more seating. Its pretty casual, there's no host, pretty much self-seating. The lunch menu was pretty limited, but they had a good assortment of sushi. For our appetizer, we had spring rolls, the rolls were hearty and the peanut sauce was delicious! For my entree, I had the vermin chili noodles and my husband had the Hu Tieu. My bowl was good, each element was flavorful, the shrimp, pork, and noodles was cooked right, the chili fish sauce was sweet and salty; however, I wish it had a bit more meat and a little less noodles. My husband enjoyed his noodle soup, broth was a little on the sweet side, but had was flavorful. Overall, it wasn't a bad experience, the service could've been a little more attentive with refills, but we'd come back and try the pho and sushi next time.
8Wi6ExrCwlUtnhXk7at95A: Loved this place. Ribs, sausage, pork, etc are amazing. That bread was some of the best I've ever had

First time ever here. Megan was a great server. We came late afternoon around 3. The food was really good although i don't care for the cinnamon butter. But its to each there own. I had the santa fe chicken (3stars) my husband had the filet. It was cooked perfectly and the side items and salads were good also. My son had the mini corn dogs and fries can't beat the 3.49 $ price for that. Overall we were at about 50$ which is normL for a steakhouse and we enjoyed it.. Would definitely come back

Everything my family ate here on our first night in Nashville was terrific. From the ribs, burgers and fries. Atmosphere was good and the drinks were great. 

The highlight had to be our server. This guy could not have been more on top of his game and not to mention that he went out of his way to be as personable as possible. When you go there ask for Chris and tell him that the family from Chicago sent you.
wXnwZJaARSfNjSMhWUZk_A: All you can eat lunch buffet including a drink for $8!!! Awesome! Great food and a great Owner. You have to check it out.

Stopped on our way out of the Nashville Airport and what a find!  Great food and great price. The owner is quite fun. We did the lunch buffet for $10 with drink included. The food was fresh and delicious. I will definitely return!

First time here and we will definitely be back!  Sam the owner was wonderful (and funny!) and graciously left the buffet open even though we came in just a few minutes before the 2:00 pm lunch buffet close. 

The food was delicious!  Started with the lentil soup - so flavorful and and just enough spice for me. I could take this stuff home by the gallon and eat it every day!  The salad was fresh, chicken and gyro meat perfectly cooked, nice roasted potatoes, and all the fixings, including feta cheese. The tzatziki was outstanding - some of the best I've ever had. We also tried the baklava, which was good.
nygT0OIMWCXSK6RBWcdQbQ: Best jukebox in town; great prices on beer; super friendly bartenders...smoky?  Sure, but it is totally worth it.

One of the best beer deals in town if get a pitcher. Shiner Bock and Miller light are just under $10 a pitcher. 
Five dartboards, a foosball table, and a great jukebox +3 large screen HD televisions.
Smokey, cheap neighborhood bar. Shiner is a great deal! Lots of memorabilia on the walls. About 5 dart boards towards the back.
m9pMqbqGll6tq_6MoyrgGA:
Just tried this place for the first time today and was very impressed. The guy who was working was very nice and offered to let me try several samples of their different dishes, which were all very flavorful in their own way.

I ended up liking the voodoo chicken best, so I ordered that over rice. It was delicious and a little bit spicy (in a good way). The portions were good for the $6.99 lunch price too. Also, I eat gluten-free, so it was nice to see several items labeled as GF right on the menu.

Also, just FYI, it's located in an urban food court of sorts along with Quiznos and Nuvo Burrito at the corner of 4th and Peabody. There is a small parking lot behind the building and some street parking nearby. 

I would definitely recommend this place for a good, fast Cajun/creole meal near downtown and I look forward to trying more of their menu items next time I'm in the neighborhood.

Okay - so, I'm not a big fast food fan, but if I'm going to do fast food, this is where I'm gonna go.

J Gumbo is a little hard to find, in an indoor food court-type building a stroll away from Broadway, but it's worth the hunt. I liken it to the southern version of Chipotle, with plenty of southern favorites like jambalaya, crawfish etoufee and voodoo chicken - ready to serve, but still fresh.

I had the Creole Ratatouille and Voodoo Chicken, which both came served in one huge bowl. The flavors were great and the voodoo chicken was supremely spicy - just the way I like it. I had a cornbread muffin, which I probably wouldn't get again, but I just love combining that sweetness with the spiciness of Cajun food.

If you're in a pinch for time or you're starving and can't wait for restaurant service, this is a great place to quell your hunger.
We needed a place to eat in downtown during CMA Fest and we stumbled upon this place normally only open for lunch. Not fancy but the food was a taste of home for my wife and it was fast
DwxMfYlv9L7HQEwCaiw2Xw:
Golden Coast is by far my favorite Chinese restaurant in Nashville. The food is great all the time, but you absolutely must try the Sunday buffet. On Sunday they switch to their "authentic" menu and the proportion of Chinese clientele greatly increases which is always a good sign. They have those squishy bean and rice deserts and soy milk on the buffet along with bubble tea and veggie egg rolls - all of which are disappointingly absent on all the other buffets I try (or even on the main menu at some places).  

The owners are amazing. When I lived in that part of town I went all the time, and to this day the owner still remembers and teases me about my obsession with bubble tea. He has even sneaked the occasional to go cup of it to me (though I promised I wouldn't tell so, shhhh). They are also willing to add to the buffet if for some reason the veggie options aren't there as usual.

I am definitely enamored with this place. I don't make it to that part of town as much these days though, so if you go pick up some tofu and vegetables for me.

You come here for the buffet. I had the lunch buffet on a Friday. A very decent array for under $8!
