Now this place is known for the weekend buffet when Chinese people crowd the place for its authentic food (for a lot more than $8). But I was pleasantly surprised that the weekday lunch was so good and cheap for this neighborhood. Yes, I've tried the weekend buffet, and it is worth it. But for under $8 I got a lot better selection than I would have got at Jack in the Box for that price. I'm not a pig, but I could have pigged out if I wanted, for a great price. All the bad reviews I'm sure do not apply to the buffet.

We went here with a friend on a Saturday afternoon. It was the best buffet that we have had. 
The bubble tea was great, the rice dumplings wonderful. Clams, squid, etc.
7wc3OsRq0FxCtnTlpI1j_Q: Went here yesterday (Friday night) for the first time. We were seated quickly but then it took almost 20 minutes for someone to take our drink order. There seemed to only be one person working the entire dining room but at about 6:30 a few other people started helping him. Although service was slow to start, once we placed our order things moved quickly. The papusas were excellent (and huge!) - definitely the star of the show. The salsa provided with the chips was also very fresh and tasty. Fried taco, also excellent. The tamal and enchilada were not very good. Lacked flavor and the texture was a bit mushy. Overall we'll give this place a chance and will return!

An amazing amount of work has been done to consolidate the menu and decorate the place since our last visit! I had the whole tilapia and it was just like it was served  by the seaside! The sauces were perfect and the rice was flavorful. We'll be back!

The two papacito's I got were delicious! Fresh salsa, authentic black beans with tasty rice- on point! Pretty good Mexican beer selection, but the margaritas are the premix kind from the big plastic bottle- You're in east Nashville, gotta make those from scratch! Overall, great food; comfortable and not a stuffy, cold atmosphere; friendly wait staff; but the drinks need some work. This place is a few steps away from being amazing! I definitely recommend going and checking it out.
w5P6cL-KgrLGiqkf_XgfYg:
Mikey is the best front of house manager. Our table was a few minutes late because the table before us was sitting too long, but who can blame them. With this atmosphere, drinks and food, I'd stay here forever. Mikey touched the table and made sure everything was delicious. Our server Nikki was amazing and fantabulous. (Quote from another girl in the party) she was the perfect server that showed us how Nashville does hospitality. 
Everything in the kitchen was uhmazing and delicious. The tots were so good. I would throat punch someone for those tots.

First trip to The Mockingbird was definitely a hit! We loved everything we ordered, and recommend the tatchos. SO good! The mockingbird burger and the pastrami were also amazing. Not enough good things to say about the service! Loved it all!
This place was exquisite! We had the Cuba en fuego and pretty much the entire menu. The food was delicious and at atmosphere couldn't be better. But the real cherry on top was the TOILET.
EzJI5x25U5GgbYBWhu5ZQQ:
I drop by tempo after dropping my boy to school . to my surprise this place is shining with a really nice open space very minimalist but a musical incline from cymbal lamps to piano water fitter. 
the coffee its outstanding really well executed.
only been open 10 day and I already been 5 my self and 4 my wife . defenetly be coming by more often . the owners are super nice and love talking music with them defenetly need more neighbors like this around here.  a+++++++++++

Homemade tortillas and delicious breakfast burritos? Attractive space? Family owned business? Piano-water spigot? This place checks all the boxes. (You'll have to visit to see the piano water spigot.) Our food was delicious, the staff was friendly and helpful, and the owners even brought our food out and chatted for a minute. I'm already planning a return trip. Oh! And don't forget to get a chocolate dipped elephant ear! So good.

I stopped in over soft opening weekend and was wonderfully surprised! I'm a huge fan of the sleek decor and chic-yet fun minimalist menu, but more than that, I love (and was taken back) by how warm and welcoming the entire staff was! Got to speak to some of the owners and hear a little of their story, and how it ties into the Tex-Mex theme of the shop. 

I got the two tacos and a drip and was pleased with both (I can vouch for the potato and egg tacos as an awesome vegetarian option!) Everyone behind the counter was super knowledgeable about their coffee as well. Two thumbs up for the food, but another two for the service!
Tdp95AviUqzvUd3H2C5xvw: Was there for a bachelorette party. They really made us feel special. They let us reserve a table for 16 ladies in the back room with an amazing view of the river. They held a VIP booth area for us right next to the band. The manager really helped to make this all happen as easy as possible. The only thing that I would warn you is not to get the spicy chicken unless you like your food really really hot. Over all the experience was amazing and I would defiantly come back again.
The lunch was excellent, drinks, chicken tenders and salad.! The music was two guitar players that were fantastic

5 star venue !  This is one of the nicest and most fun bars I have ever visited.  The hospitality was off the charts fantastic as well.  If you are looking for a private event space in Nashville , look at this venue!! It's fantastic.  Best rooftop in the city, overlooking the stadium and the river.  Mae will treat you right !!
jyFZWwdc9YxMCt3n-uq0Kw: Got lost and found this place by accident. Staff was super courteous and friendly. Got the Gyro Combo and the portion is huge, definatly need a fork for this one. Fries were a little under cooked, but the Gyro was absolutely amazing. Toppings are served "Subway" style and all veggies and toppings looked fresh and tasted great. Baklava portions are very large as well and will not disappoint. The chicken is also made on a spit like the lamb and the owner gave us a sample that really tempted me to order more food.
The Gyros are great!  The staff are AWESOME!  Would have given 5 stars but on my falafel combo the pita was chewy. They nuke it instead of grilling to warm it. Overall, nice and affordable lunch.

Went for lunch today. Solid Gyro plate. Tender, flavorful meat with well cooked pilaf and a good greek salad for 6.50. 

I have had the falafel and grape leave wraps and they are delicious as well.

The only set back is there is no ventilation and there is a heavy gyro meat smell. Despite that, it's worth a go.  

I will definitely go back.
y9S7znlFxXzN0aUqvPJESA:
Where have you been all my life, Hot Pot? This place absolutely exceeded my expectations. Upon walking in, you will see the modern Asian décor is incredible. The restaurant is beautiful to look at and has a really pleasant ambiance. Upon walking in, our host was incredibly helpful, taking the time to explain all our options and going as far as to walk us around the restaurant so we could visualize our dining spaces. Something to understand about this restaurant: in addition to hot pot, they serve a full menu of authentic Chinese dishes as well as many of the more Americanized favorites. If anyone if your party would like to do hot pot, you need to indicate "hot pot" to you host, as hot pots cannot be accommodated in the normal dining section. 

Our party of 8 opted to do large hotpots so we were seated at a 10 person table with two burners. We were delighted to find that this set up actually allowed for us to have four different containers of broth (two large split pots) so each couple was able to have their own broth. When ordering hot pot, you can order various proteins and veggies a la carte or you can choose one of the combination platters. All the combination platters come with your broth (broth is $3 on its own for the a la carte menu), protein and a selection of vegetables and other side items (the exception being the seafood platter, which just comes with the protein). In the back, there is a bar of sauces for you to add to your food, including things like hoisin, traditional hot pot sauce, fish sauce, etc.

My boyfriend and I split the seafood combination platter as well as vegetable combination platter. It was more than enough food, and we were incredibly pleased by the prices (About $30 for our two combination platters and an appetizer).  Our waitress was attentive, and thoroughly explained all our options. I am really looking forward to dining in this place again!

Unique experience and possibly the only hot pot restaurant in the Nashville area. I wasn't familiar with this type of cuisine, but we just ordered a few things from the menu and dove in. We began our meal with a sampling of some veggies, a bone broth, and an herb broth. We also ordered some chicken that comes in very finely sliced pieces that only take about 10 seconds or less to cook. There was lots of experimenting with the sauces, but we ended up actually liking the hot pot sauce best. I recommend going with a group of 4-6 to have the best experience, and being able to take it all in. There isn't much service to be had here, since it's mostly just keeping your drinks topped off, but the staff all seemed polite enough and never rushed us. I can't really say how this compares to other hot pot places or how authentic this is, but I had a lot of fun learning what I liked as I went along.

Sauce bar is good. They have a decent variety of meats and combo plates. I tried the tube bone broth and the wintermelon both and they were not that flavorful, so I definitely took advantage of the sauce bar.
QMd8dvffw-gU3HLP3kZ52A:
There is a lot of nostalgia wrapped up in my assessment of Goten...I have fond memories of the dark halls and small hibachi rooms at the previous 21st ave location.  I continue to mourn the loss of sister restaurant, Goten 2 in Cummings Station.  They had exceptional sushi.

But this is a review for Goten in its current state.  I'm not a fan of the big open room of hibachi grills.  I have yet to have a meal in that room.  Sushi is inconsistent.  I've had some great yellow tail nigiri and some not-so-great.  I usually stick to the non-raw rolls.  They are always more than happy to customize a roll for me.  Where this place is rocking is the regular dining room (non-hibachi) menu.  Most entrees are $10 or less and include soup (miso or clear--a basic broth with a few noodles), salad (mustard dressing is a must!  the raspberry and onion dressings are also tasty), entree and green tea.  My favorite is the creamy wine sauce chicken.  I always have enough left over for another full meal.

Ah crap, now I want some of that mustard dressing.

I found this place near my hotel and had to give it a shot. I am stunned how awesome the good was. They had some very different drinks and different rolls. I just let the chef/owner surprise me and I couldn't be happier.

Awesome Drink: sake martini (gin, sake, and a pickled cucumber)

Special roll: devil's roll - spicy tuna, spicy crab, mayo, avocado, and a thinly sliced jalapeño on top. Then deep fried. Incredible!

Yum! Food was delish.  The waitress seemed to have a little trouble understanding us, but maybe that added to the "authenticity" of the experience and was very attentive.  All my food was fresh and tasty, and very reasonably priced.  Went with a group of about 5 of us on a Sunday night and had a great time.
-ZmzAg4-jR9edfePRSZfbA: I grew up near Chinatown in Philadelphia, loving Chinese food. I am so grateful to have No. 1 Chinese nearby with free delivery, usually within 30 minutes!!!  The egg rolls arrive steaming hot, the homemade mustard will clear your sinuses, the meats are flavorful and the vegetables are still crispy. I do miss hot tea, but their great food more than makes up for that!!
Had them deliver to us tonight. What a luxury!!  Courteous service over the phone. Food arrived quickly. Was hot and delicious.   Good value.

This is probably one of the best little Chinese Restaurants around. The food is great and they deliver quickly. I know of several people that drive over from outside the delivery area just to pick it up.  
I have never been disappointed.
am3QhsWBeUfKwW4LgBIF6w: The food was amazing! Very reasonably priced for a hearty breakfast. The staff was very pleasant and service was prompt. I ordered the breakfast bowl and it hit the spot!!! I would most definitely come back!

This is my first visit to a Noshville since the closure of the original (That was a sad day!). This Noshville has a little less character, is a little bit less refined as a physical venue, but it is still a good place to go to strap on the old nosebag.

The interior is a little tired, but it is still, unmistakably, a New York style Jewish Deli. The staff members are friendly and helpful, the seating is comfortable, there is an abundance of parking on the property, and the food is exactly what one would expect from this style of eatery.

We went for breakfast. Good coffee, a Breakfast bowl, potato pancakes, corn beef hash...it was all comfort type tucker, was well prepared, plentiful and inexpensive.

It isn't as good as the original Noshville, but it runs a very close second and gets my vote as a great place for breakfast and lunch within striking distance of Green Hills Mall.

We were in the area and needed a place to grab breakfast.  Yelp had mixed reviews but we thought we would give it a try.  There is a private parking lot which is a plus in this busy area of Nashville.  It was a weekend, so the parking lot across the street was also available for the overflow.

There was a wait for larger parties, but since it was just the two of us, we were seated right away.  We had a very friendly server (and I'm sorry I did not get his name) who was on top of things.  He was quick to take our orders, refill beverages and checked in regularly but not so much that it was annoying.

I ordered my usual egg white omelet.  The server asked if I wanted hash browns with my omelet.  Yes!  The BF got his usual corned beef hash with eggs.  When the food came, I will say that I would not describe the potatoes as hash browns.  They were little potato pancakes.  Cute.  A little on the cold side but my omelet was delicious and piping hot.  The hash and eggs were good but a slightly small portion for the price.  My side of bread was a whole bagel toasted with cream cheese and jelly.  

I would like to return to Noshville Delicatessen for lunch or dinner.  The menu looks good--lots of homemade soups to try out and the sandwiches look amazing.
_aZAD5C0maYABO9KgddpPQ:
Hello there yelp!ers.

So I recently went to this location for the first time as it is on the route I take to get to work via MTA bus. I was deciding on the surrounding restaurants and I felt burned out from them. I noticed this nifty looking sub shop and decided "Why not? Been a while since I had SubWay."

But this place is so much better then SubWay by far. It's cleaner, the food is fresher, it's hot and they've got a good selection of product. One of the best things about this place is the Coca-Cola Freestyle drink machine, while it's a little bulky and not as streamlined as I'd like, it was still awesome. Over 120+ drink selections and a wide variety of flavorings you can add makes this machine worth checking out.

Now, as to the reasoning behind the 4 stars? Well, one of the associates there seemed like he could care less about the guest aside from just taking their order and moving on. He had no personal touch, it was almost as if he were robotic and programmed to just take orders. It's associates like that that really annoy me. Obviously if you want people to return they'd do it not only for the food but for the workers, too. It makes the experience that much better. Now the manager there (noticeable by the collared shirts they wear) was very nice, talkative just right, and attentive to my needs. The second time I went there (because it was such a great atmosphere) he remembered who I was, which always means a lot to me as a consumer.

But most certainly someplace to check out if you ever want great, hot food, several drink choices, and a cozy, hip atmosphere. Think twice about going to SubWay over Firehouse or you may regret the hesitation!

Happy yelp!ing!
The smokehouse brisket is fantastic.  This place is welcome relief from the blandness of subway and it's ilk.

Sandwiches are made to order, still seems a little pricey for a submarine sandwich.

The Subs are pretty good and their Hot Sauce Selection is Great.
EtWAtnK13mftCl3TgsWyJA:
Snaps for being open at 10.45 ahead of time.  My first time in here but not my last.  Some nice locally made cookies and other food products (no not moon pies and goo goos).  some nice tin box cookies with Music City and other Nashville logos on the tin.  Some nice plates and fake tomatoes $3.99 (remember that for this time next year for my centerpiece).  All in all a great place for foodie gifts and non jewelry.
Friendly storekeeper.

Love this place! Wonderful friendly, helpful,staff. Great creative gifts at great prices. I love how many local products they have! Great food items, great artsy items. When you are looking for that special something for someone special try this place!

Shopping for local gifts for my mother in law I popped in to this super cute shop. They were very helpful & sweet. Besides great selection of housemade bath products there were lots of adorable gift items.

I got a trio of deliciously scented soaps & was on my way.

(BTW the MIL was thrilled)
F0x8mJivBQUCam13avn4kg:
Thiis my favorite restaurant in Nashville.
Get seated upstairs in the bar area.  There is a great tree coming up through the indoor/outdoor deck on which you are seated.  I have great memories sitting here with friends on a warm summer night.

The food is quite good with a large selection of tapas, including really good lobster, a nice softshell crab and succulent scallops.

They also have a nice selection of cigars so you can site outside on the bar paio and enjoy with friends.  It is a little sceney with the music producer crowd, but the ambience, colors, patio and fireplace are worth putting up with the chodes

Used Bound'ry for an engagement party a few weeks ago, and there could not have been a better atmosphere. We held our event in the downstairs bar, and it was just the warm, intimate setting we were hoping for.

Natalie in events and catering was a pleasure to work with throughout the entire  planning process. She was detailed and efficient, and it showed in the success of the event. We had one server for the evening and she was fantastic: attentive, pleasant, and enthusiastic. 

Can't recommend Bound'ry more highly!
This place is great! No matter the size of the party I am with we have always had great service.  The food is fantastic, and the staff is extremely nice!
tRVknko7n-J6meqL9PPiCQ:
This place was great. The price did seem a little steep but then I realized that the meats were all locally sourced and they made the (freaking awesome) chips in house out of fresh corn tortillas. I don't mind paying a little extra for that. 
The bartender (Amy?) was friendly and efficient and the margarita was exactly how I asked for it (easy on the sweet, heavy on the lime). 
I will definitely be back!

Food was delicious and bartender was awesome! Although they were out of guacamole, the salsa sampler was a hit.  Beef Burrito was filling and good size for price. Great little stop in between American Country Music Award museum exhibits.
If you are looking for the best Margarita in town check out Amy! I am from Texas and particular about my Rita's but Amy knows how to shake it on the rocks !
fmF58w_cI8Ef-WAIGM3-QA: Great food and customer service... I love the selections of wine they have and here calamari is the absolute best here I also love there lasagne and also the pizza here is great

My husband and I ate here twice in the past month. The first time I ordered the spaghetti and meatballs, which was out of this world. The second time I ordered the chicken parm, which I will never get again. The atmosphere here is relaxing and pairing that with a good glass of wine or beer makes for an enjoyable time.

We  returned recently after a long hiatus and were very pleasantly surprised. They've made some additions to the menu (trout  was excellent), and everything else was fresh. It appears they've got their act together. We'll start coming back and look forward to our next visit.
hMLcTXMk6xif0qfBi8-8sw: Loved this place, only been once, but it was almost a Holy experience. Coming in from Knoxville, treating my sister to a birthday concert in the form of seeing Chris Whitley live.  I'm a huge Chris Whitley fan and saw him years back kill it at a show in St. Louis. On the way into town, my sister's car broke down, but we still made it to the show. What could have been an awful experience turned into a great one because everybody we encountered took good care of us and displayed true hospitality, patrons and staff alike. This is a small, intimate venue for serious music lovers. I didn't know this at the time, but Chris Whitley was dying from lung cancer and would pass away only a few months later. This place has, or at least had soul the last time I was there. Gotta hit it up again soon!

This was one of our favorite places on a recent girls trip to Nashville. Great venue for live music. We went on a Sunday night when they air the show live for radio. A local recommended it to us when we said we wanted to hear good bands playing their own music. Sean McConnel was main act the night we were there and did not disappoint.
First time to visit this place. Very impressive atmosphere with great service, music, and food! Definitely would recommend this venue to anyone who visits or lives here!
sg3z6IuLkK0CfEwSGAlzYw:
Ordered pizza at 1018pm on a Friday night to our hotel, t was delivered and in my bed ready to eat by 1045!!!  so first off that made me so happy because after drinking all day waiting forever on Pizza isn't fun but this pizza was so quick! 
And it was cheap! We got pizza wings and breadsticks for $35 with a $8 tip Included! 

I only ate the pizza and it was delish!  Very pleased with our evening snack!

I love this place!  Their hand tossed pizza with marinara sauce is SOOOOO good- if you like a sweeter sauce.  They have a great meat sauce if you don't want all the sweetness. 

Their manicotti is also amazing- and they always pay attention to my "extra,extra sauce" request.

Went here for pizza late on a Friday night and we're impressed.  We were hesitant to go at first, but overall the food was good.  The service was quick and the food was cooked to order.  Originally from Philly, and the pizza wasn't as good as what you can find in Philly, but definitely on-par with pizza you can get in DC.
9bQARf2F3qvK1Ek22rqK9A:
Ok, there are a few elements to South to be reviewed. 

First is the "club" side, which you will get after 9 on Fri and Sat. 
I am not one to typically like dancing and the trash that usually follows that music, but I was really pleasantly surprised with South at night. The crowd is upscale, there is a small cover and dress code, and the music was fab. A lot of 90's early 00's hip hop mixed in with some of the catchier recent hits. Kept the ladies dancing all night. 

I have also been here for food. The food itself was well above average. I had the fish and chips, made with catfish. I am not one to normally enjoy catfish, but I was told I had to try it and it was delish. Problem occurred about a bit later when I felt like someone had stabbed my stomach and I projectile vomited everywhere. 

I don't want to pass final judgement on the food until I go back, because I have good friends who eat here frequently and have never had an issue. It may have just been a bum piece of fish. If the food sits as well as it tastes next time, I will drop 5 stars on South, as everything else is spot on. 

Side note, I am a sucker for cool beer lists, and this place has a small, but awesome selection. If you serve Rogue Dead Guy, we are cool.

Great food and great service! Nice and warm on a cold winters night. The Nashville hot chicken sandwich is fresh, tasty, and the perfect amount of spice Nashville Style! Don't miss the Mac N Cheese! We found one of our new favorites!
The band Backlit brought us in, but the awesome food and drinks will keep us coming back.

Had lunch there today, have eaten there a couple of times before. The lunch menu is pretty impressive for a place that looks like its main function is to be a bar with a huge open floor at night. I had a turkey sandwich with pesto and garlic aioli and arugula. The sandwich was perfect, and the fries were crispy and big. A colleague had the fried green tomato BLT, which looked really good, with sweet potato fries, which also looked good. I wish I could have tried their bacon gouda mac and cheese and/or sliders. 

The service was also good. She checked in on us a good amount - not too little, not too much, although at one point I did have an empty drink for a few minutes, but it seemed like she was covering a lot of tables, so I can forgive that. 

The only downside is the construction going on right across the street is really loud, even sitting inside (with the garage doors up). So, while sitting outside during nice weather might be tempting, the sound from the construction would almost make it impossible to carry on a conversation. 

They also have plenty of TV's around if you want to watch sports. Most were tuned to ESPN or NFL Network while we were there. There is also a stage in the corner where I suppose artists could perform, or a DJ could set up.
08kVZ7eYC9NGn6njjsRkQA:
Very new, and all the kinks aren't out yet, (like the wifi doesn't work), but the staff, food and ambiance are top shelf.  

Dim sum choices, spring rolls, cucumber salad, pork dumplings, excellent. Could be whole meal out of the dim sum choices. Peking Duck did not match my memories from long ago Chinatown, San Francisco.  But taste was delicious and the rice that came with it was great; probably would not order again.  The clay pot seafood dish would definitely be a reorder.  We also had a tofu, eggplant vegetable dish, which was very tasty. 

Lychee Tart for dessert was good.  Staff were responsive, from the manager to the people bussing tables.

Ambiance is A+.  There's room for improvement but I'm giving them A for effort.
Great Ambience. Delicious food !! Tried lamb dumplings and vegetable chow mein. Both are delicious . Loved fried spring roll

We went here based on our love for Chauhan Ale Masala (same owners) and we actually went on opening night. The restaurant is stunning. Great ambiance and decor. The food and drink were, as expected, phenomenal. We highly recommend this place for a special occasion or when you just need a fun date night!
Rbv6poWtyY3svGPn01WT9w:
All-You-Can-Eat lunch buffet.  They have a wide variety of pizza plus pasta and salad bar. Monday-Friday. 

Hint: if you are taking a late lunch get there before 1:30. They close to change over for the dinner menu. 

Good place to go if you're hungry.
Good pizza....great cheese, good sausage and mushrooms. Sauce was fine. Family loved it.

This is a great DIY restaurant. You sit yourself and you wait. Then you decid when you want to ask the waitress when she'll come to your table. Then after you order drinks and food, you proceed to go and fill up your own glasses of water because the waitress is busy. They make baked potatos turn into french fries by the powers osmosis.
UqnfYCflwICnojfvPOaBDA:
I can't believe more people haven't filed in to review this little gem. 

One of the saddest things about moving away from my hometown is not getting to see my momma as much. The next worst is never getting to eat here.

This is the closest to authentic Mom's cooking that I have ever found. If I had to choose only one restaurant to ever eat from again, ever, it would be this one.
Those ladies sure can cook!
And you must absolutely leave room for pie.

The best place you've never heard of.

It's the type of place that, unless a friend recommended it, you'd just drive right by. This was my first taste of actual "southern home cookin" and what a great introduction!!! I ordered the fried catfish with fried okra (I had to try it!), mac n cheese, and sauteed green beans. SOOOOOO GOOD!!! And it comes with 2 hot, homemade cornbread biscuits. All for $7.50!!! It's a VERY mom n pops type of place and everything is made fresh.  I'm drooling just thinking about it. 
GO THERE

Don't get me wrong, Sylvan Park is great. All things considered, a great meat-and-three. Great price, good food, good sides. If I need my fried chicken fix and can't venture too far from Vandy, this is my place. Good fried chicken. Way above average. Sides are great. Go for the greens and douse them with spicy vinegar. Meatloaf looked really good. Mac and cheese was a force of nature. The yeast rolls are warm pillows of fragrant happiness.

The staff is friendly, the decor is straight out of the 1950s (wood walls, old fixtures). The clientele is a really cool local Nashville crowd, good mix of young and old. Over all, the experience is great. 

So why only four stars? I know it's a Nashville institution, and it really is good. But the food is just good, not great. But honestly, the place is so cool that you still need to give it a try.

Oh, and try the chess pie. It is a gooey, way-too-sweet force to be reckoned with. Absolute heaven.
3PNq9an-C8sommSKSOoBRQ:
Lucky Nashville! We finally have an amazing raw food option that is as delicious as it is healthy! Nothing is too salty OR bland and you can tell every bit of it is prepared with heart and an extreme level of understanding of the product! I am personally a big fan of the dry packaged instant soup Chef Pam just introduced. I have also loved Greek & Italian weeks, her raw Lasagna is artfully done, yet hearty & spot on, her dressings are flavorful without being overwhelming. Every dish I have had has been an overwhelming YAY and I have never been disappointed. I would confidentally buy and try anything Pam makes and am sure I'd be just as happy as I have been with previous purchases! 

Tip: the raw chocolate mousse IS one of the best chocolate desserts you will have ever had the pleasure of eating. Also she makes fresh Peach Parfaits in the summertime that will blow you away. I am 100% won over and so grateful for such a spectacular option for raw healthy eating in the south!

Healthy food, great flavors, deliver to your door or grab from local cooler at any number of places about town.  (Whole Foods, Thistle Stop Cafe....)

Pam goes to great lengths to present delicious food that is wholesome and better for you than almost anything that's so easy to snag for a meal.

Try the Buddha Bowl, the 'noodles' and the pecan pie.  Refreshing and filling at the same time.  

These dishes will keep you comfortably full, unlike processed foods that make me go from stuffed to starving in just a couple hours.

I am a bit sad that they are no longer selling from the kitchen space on Charlotte Rd, but now I have even more reason to drop by Whole Foods.

I am not a vegan, nor do I have food allergies. I'll pretty much eat anything that's set in front of me. But I absolutely adore trying new things and love Spark of Life!

I stopped in on Saturday (they have pretty restrictive hours during the week) and was greeted by the lovely employee behind the counter. Not only did she go over all the fabulous food they have, I was also able to try samples of 3 different dishes! I very much enjoyed speaking with the employee and owner, and ended up buying the chocolate mouse, raw pad thai, and vegan lasagna. They were also offering 20% off when I stopped in, win win! 

If you have an open mind and/or are looking for raw/vegan/or gluten free delicious foods, you should definitely try this lovely place. You will be happy you did!
_uN0OudeJ3Zl_tf6nxg5ww: I've dined in and taken out, always delicious. My most recent order was for apps--I got the pan-fried dumplings and chicken satay. Both were cooked perfectly with great flavors, and the dipping sauces were a great compliment to each, not the main attraction. Was in the mood for traditional Pad Thai, and they did not disappoint. I asked for extra peanuts, they gave me plenty. The dish was seasoned just as requested, and the shrimp was just done, not overcooked and rubbery. My mouth is watering as I write this review! I'll be returning soon!

I stumbled into Siam Cuisine to meet my grandparentals around noon. I'd woken up late (20 minutes prior), was about to eat Thai food for breakfast, and was not feeling enthusiastic about it.

Boy did they show me.

I dove right in ordered the red curry seafood and glass noodles... little did I know, it was a four course meal. We started with a simple brothy soup, then moved on to a gingery iceberg salad. Eh. Typical.

When my curry arrived, it was wrapped in tin foil, which they glamorously cut open at our table using a pair of kitchen scissors. Inside was bubbling sauce, shrimp, squid, mussels and glass noodles. They also brought a huge plate of steamed rice, which I wanted them to take back, but the g-parents said to leave it. Why, God. After I demolished the entire thing, there was curry sauce left (and it was the best curry I've ever had), so I ended up dumping all of the rice in it and finishing that, too. I f*ckin' hate it when they bring rice and noodles... I CAN'T BE TRUSTED WITH THAT SORT OF RESPONSIBILITY. 

Regardless of my lack of self control, everything was absolutely delicious. Then, when I was on the verge of not-so-spontaneously combusting, they brought out dessert. Five adorable little egg rolls filled with steaming plantain, drizzled with honey and sesame seeds. They were too cute to resist. I mean, who's not a sucker for fried banana baby egg rolls? My indulgence cost me $17 bucks and a small amount of rice-induced guilt, but I'll be back for sure. That red curry whispers to me in my dreams.
Good, spicy Thai in an unassuming Nashville strip mall.  A little pricey, but not ridiculous. Pleasant service.
9TvJuG22ZyCXJVYzDaDNxw:
There was a pita pit within walking distance to my dorm while I was in college and they were open until 4:00 am, so my review may be biased :).  Lot's of selection, made fresh for you, always delicious!  I usually get the chicken Cesar or the gyro, both are yum yum yum!!  I've been to this downtown one late into the night on a couple occasions and it always hits the spot.

 I love you pita pit, I wish you were closer to my house, and I miss you.
After a grease filled week we got ourselves a sandwich loaded with vegetables and with chicken.  It's about as processed as subway so expect nothing more but at least it's not deep fried.

Pita Pit is one of the few "quick sandwich for lunch" options that we have near my office, and it's generally pretty tasty.  It feels like a mix of Subway, Chipotle, and the delis I remember so fondly in New York.  The food seems fresher than most places and the staff is always great, cheerful, and helpful.  Seating is very limited so I often just order it to go.  They also have online ordering which makes life easier if you're in a hurry, as is often the case.  The prices are reasonable and there's enough food in a pita wrap to fill you up but not weigh you down (unless you load it down with cheese, sauce, and cream, of course).  Be sure to sign up for a loyalty card while you're there.

Easter Egg:  You can get into this place from the alleyway, as well.  Can you guess which door it is?
ttkw8_b7CVOf6hM5bzTLNw:
Last stop on our trip down Broadway. Sunday night and the party was still going strong; people going down on the dance floor and the bar streaming with patrons. 

Despite the crowd, was easily able to order drinks from the bartender. Had to get a Jack and coke ($9) while in town - poured strong but not overwhelming. Took a seat at one of the tables and enjoy the NBA playoffs being played on the TV's behind the bar. Great crowd, good atmosphere, and a live band playing a variety of hits.

A good a place as any on this street for going out. drinks are obviously more expensive than in other parts of town, but for the area it's a solid stop.
Officially one of my favorite bars in Nashville. Came on a Friday night for my bachelorette party and the band was AMAZING! They played early 80s and 90s music which I love! Definitely a fun time.

This place is awesome. For a downtown bar/restaurant it's great. The food is excellent. Decent prices on drinks compared to other places down town. Staff great too. It does get crowded and they could use more tables on the second floor. I assume the empty space is for dance floor but in the afternoon they could drop a couple tables in a server food. One of my favorite places to go on the strip. Clean also. They also have a outside sitting area. But again gets crowded so if you see a spot grab it!
ZMIUDDqBsclQG4kv9424DQ:
Tasty coffee and even tastier treats.

Whether you're in the mood for brunch, coffee or a quick snack Bond has you covered.

One of the staff suggested I allow them to heat up the banana chocolate chip muffin I had selected one afternoon... Rest assured of you also opt for this little trick you will have a new addiction on your hands. Yum!!!

They also have a nice little brunch menu featuring sandwiches and other options that will leave you feeling totally satisfied. 

My only complaint is that they aren't open on Sundays which is a problem considering my new habit. Let me assure you warm and gooey banana chocolate muffin withdrawal can be an ugly thing. 

My favorite menu items: Iced Mocha, Banana Chocolate Muffins, Chicken Salad sandwich.

So good! The truly scrumptious panini really is truly scrumptious!!!!

Good choice. Coffee is good too!

One of the best reasons I love this place is that they carry hemp milk!  Before they opened, they took the time to respond to me via FB regarding some allergies I have and they listened and considered the option. Lo and behold, they updated me when they opened that they would carry a brand of hemp milk that is also carrageenan free! I love having different coffee options, and we all know Nashville has some great ones, so I can't thank them enough. I'm really looking forward to stopping by for their salad options. Hoping they get gluten free items as well.
Z37QYF_mVq7MDiCojFbEQQ:
Simple and fast. Always people which makes me say safe. 
We have brought our family here twice and love it. 

Wish these guys much success and open for dinner please!

Great place to get some hot stir fry. Which is good on a cold day. You get to place all of your meat, veggies, seasoning and sauces in a bowl and then they stir fry it with brown rice, white rice, or lo mein noodles. Don't forget to add an egg for a $1. Food is great and you basically pick the portion of food. The only con to eating here is that if they are busy you could wait awhile to get your food. Great value and always seems to be pretty clean. Recommend if you are looking for a warm meal and are hungry.

Stopped by for lunch while working in Nashville. It was our first time to a place like this, so I am not sure if it could be done better, but man did this hit the spot.

As other reviewers say, you pay for a sized bowl, which you then fill from the buffet bar of raw ingredients: veggies galore, meats, spices, sauces, and oils. They have tips for you (for example, if you want to make a dish spicy, add half a ladle of chili sauce), and for the truly overwhelmed, they have a small menu to order from. However, to order from the menu would do Khan's a disservice.

We both got the large bowls, then realized as we ate that we could have had a Leaning Tower of Stir Fry on our bowls (we only filled them to the top; did not overfill like the others we saw there). I made sure to remind Husband to add enough sauce to cover the starches, which would be added behind the counter. The cashier had a thick accent, but was very efficient. I got the white rice, and Husband got the noodles. They even have giant bottles of hand sanitiizer by the register and door (which is totally necessary because even though the meat is frozen, there is still bacteria!). 

It wasn't too long, even with how slammed they were, to get our order. The food was delicious. Each entree is served with a cup of thick, delicious stir fry sauce, which is especially helpful if you don't put enough sauce on your meal prior to them grilling it. It was a really neat experience, and our end results were delicious.

GREAT option for vegetarians, and for people like me who like a little meat but more veggies.
HxU-aiv_9aqwiqYdXeEj1g: Found this little gem in the Gulch. One of the best lasagnas ever. Warm, friendly service, and very well priced. Chef came out and spoke to us after the meal. We'll be back!!

I went here for lunch, was in by 11:10, out by 11:50, no problems there. The waiter was inexperienced, but nice and took care of everything we needed. Food was great! I had the Lasagna, others had pasta and panini, everyone enjoyed it. 

The art around the TV was nice and looked professional.

Need to fix the broken window.
Great hide-a-way spot a couple blocks off the Gulch. Upscale interior, very intimate. Tried the lasagna,  tangy sauce, quality cheeses. Delicious!   Highly recommend!
RRbZ63soGKoZ-EieimoXrw: Great neighborhood pub...with a Cheers feel.  Food is above average standard bar fare.  Fresh, local ingredients.  Pimento Cheese Burger Wrap is great.  Thursday lunch special of Lemon Dill Chicken Pasta keeps me going back every Thursday I can get over there.  Great desserts too.

I allot four stars in consideration of the style of eatery. It is a suburban tavern and the food, for a suburban tavern is good. The service is friendly, the venue is nice, the portions generous. There is plenty of parking, they don't serve non-alcoholic beer, but the coffee is good. Good value for money with a varied menu. It's a good choice for a quick, inexpensive, tasty lunch.

If you want the feel of a great Nashville neighborhood restaurant/pub/sports bar, then McCabe Pub is the place. If you want a great choice of fantastic food, ditto. The "chalkboard specials" are always a good place to start. If you want to make new friends, the waitresses and bartenders are ready. They will remember you, and what you like.

A Nashville tradition since 1982, it is still family-owned by friendly people. You will find an assortment of soccer players, softball players, etc. coming in after their game, old Nashville society, the West Nashville community (being strategically situated in grand old Sylvan Park), politicos and songwriters, and just anyone looking to quench their thirst and/or satisfy their appetite and have a good time. And all of this is still very family-friendly and affordable. I guess my only complaint is that it's closed on Sundays....
e6BZpuvEnwlzceKn5leu8g:
A must do when visiting or staying at the hotel. 

Amazing pizza and calzones! Freshly made and served. Price is well worth the quality, quantity and deliciousness.

One of the best and probably most economical places to eat at in the Gaylord Opry Resort.

Good place to have a pizza slice and a fresh salad.  The pizza is good but not great. Crust is not crispy even though it's a thin crust and we got it right after they took it out from the oven (nice and hot). The pizza sauce tasted very good and yes, the pizza is some what grease because of the cheese etc.  The salad was very fresh. Service is great!!

This pizzeria was a wonderful experience, although the pizza was very well made and all in all delectable, the pizza was just a little too greasy, but I recommend it to anyone who enjoys fresh cooked pizza.
m_kvoXITBvYDofmcKpWEmw: Absolutely love this place. Browns is the best burger in Nash. Maybe it's the simplicity of just stopping in and watching a game on a Tuesday enjoying a burger and Budweiser talking Cardinals baseball with Ron or Gordy. Place doesn't look like much from outside but you're not sleeping here so who cares. Sunday night bands are rocking and this place is always great.

Hidden in a trailer next to the army surplus store this is another Nashville gem.  If you're looking for anything other than a cheeseburger, grilled cheese and cold beer you've got better options. However the friendly service, long-standing tradition and unique environment make it a worthy stop. 

Full disclosure-- it's cheap and completely lacking any frills. However I recommend it  for an experience that's hard to replicate.

Oh, Brown's Diner! How I long for thee!  I'm in Houston, Texas now, but the cheeseburger alone is enough to make me homesick! I love the staff and the location. I just want to say, that for such a small lil place you guys ROCK! The greatest burger, the greatest place, memories are made here.
B9MAfqhVw1AxNzwu2ELPtQ:
I live in Nashville and eat here quite regularly.The Samosas are the best and can beat any samosa's i have had in this part of the country. The quality is consistently good. The service is good on the whole and the Owners are very helpful when you visit and ask about different Somalian foods on the menu that they serve.  

They also have a Barista and there coffee and the spiced tea is amazing as well. You might also want to try some homemade style crispy chicken Burgers. All the prices are very reasonable compared to the quality and quantity of food provided. My favorite Beef samosa should give this place a try.

Killer chai and friendly counter service. Inexpensive fried eats to munch on. Somali style. Under $5 for a chai and a couple of bites. So good. Got some more to go before I left. Ask for the green mango pepper sauce.

The founder of Starbucks, Mark Zuckerberg, once proclaimed "Coffee is the blood that runs through this industrial nation of the United States. It should be available and affordable to all!" He later clarified that he was referring to Mapco's new line of coffee. ("One dollar for any size!!!") 

Whether you enjoy Starbucks, Mapco, or McDonalds coffee, the Horn Coffee shop will bring a smile to your face. The owners and employees are extremely welcoming. I was able to buy a fresh cup of coffee and large (I mean large...wait my caps lock button is broken. Meant to type "large" in all caps.) Anyway, the large slice of homemade caKE WAS PHENOMENAL. IT WENT PERFECTLY WITH MY COFFEE. YES! MY CAPS LOCK IS WORKING AGAIN. BUT I CAN'T DISABLE IT. NOW IT JUST LOOKS LIKE THIS IS A BLOG POST FROM KANYE WEST CIRCA 2009. MIGHT AS WELL START WRITING ABOUT AN ITALIAN LAMP OR SOME FEATHER PILLOWS. I'LL JUST WRAP UP THE REVIEW. THE HORN COFFEE IS A GREAT PLACE FOR AN INEXPENSIVE CUP OF COFFEE SERVED WITH A SMILE. #KANYE2020
4jkpycTldteoQnKK7VPSxA: I had only been to Athens for breakfast, but decided to go for lunch the other day and I'm really glad I did! I ordered the Greek salad with Gyro added and it was the best lamb I have had! Plus our server was very nice and we got our food quick. I can't wait to go back!

Great Bellevue secret. Great food, breakfast or burgers! Greek food is fantastic and if they are serving it, the clam chowder is ridiculously good. Let's keep this gem in Bellevue. Wait staff, while small, is the friendliest ever.
My favorite restaurant in Bellevue! It's not a chain, you can get an amazing breakfast all day and the Greek food is amazing! Highly recommend it!
jyW4dP7IxpqS-_BcP1-67w: The Best Hot Chicken and service in Nashville. Recently took my son and his friend who had never had hot chicken and they loved it. They got mild and 200 and i got 400. Found out they have up to 800 degrees called Reaper which i will try next time Chicken should be ordered 40-50 minutes ahead of time and it is always juicy and delicious. Have been going there for 10 years and never disappointed. Tried most of the other ones in Nashville and this one is definitely the best!

Talk about some exquisitely prepared hot chicken that is genuinely HOT! Aqui is friendly as she can be and she consistently delivers beautiful, moist birds with excellent sides as well. I treated myself to 500 degree tenders with coleslaw and potato salad and as usual, was so not disappointed: I was literally crying tears of joy!! This is the stuff that those sad folks who claim Hattie B's as their favorite "hot" chicken((bwhahahahhh!)) would barely be able to touch. Can't wait to finally work up the nerve to order up some 900 Degree treats---oh yes, it *will* happen---as soon as I stock up on toilet paper, milk and raw, butt-blasting ambition.

This is by far the greatest hot chicken I've ever had! 

We ate at Bolton's earlier in the week and went to 2 Hattie B's but the lines were super long and I wasn't waiting 45 minutes to get in and another 45 minutes for fried chicken. 

I looked on yelp for good hot chicken places and 400 Degrees had some very favorable reviews so I figured we'd give them a shot. Best decision I made while in Nashville!

We had chicken fingers with a couple of sides. There were 3 pieces so we did 100, 200 and 400. I stuck with the 100 but my son ate the 200 & 400. The spicy rub was on point and the tenders were very moist. Perfect combo. 

My son even tried the 800 (which wasn't on menu) and enjoyed that but I'd recommend extreme caution with this much heat!

All in all, a super authentic local food truck that was worth the drive and wait (45 minutes) for the chicken fingers. The staff was amazing and I'm sure they'll have many years of continued success once their storefront opens up in the future.
2VczMMo9tWBNPBEHMnoP5w:
love this place, got to be my most favorite bar to hot up when visiting Nashville! The beer is cheap and they have plenty of options as well!

Come play some pool!
Not your typical downtown spot but very laid back, great beer and drink selection and great service. You can almost always have intimate convo here, even w/ a large group.

Buffalo is fun times. Plenty of pool and TV to watch sports or all that fun stuff. 

Cool, laid back hang.
cXmYgauFIBwqo3UMwkagDA: This place is Pho king great!!!  Very clean.  Health score is 93.  Nice staff and great food.  Try the Pho Ann special.  You won't be disappointed.
Absolute amazing. Spring rolls were fresh and peanut sauce was amazing. The pho was really flavorful.

I saw the Pho and Barbecue banner and had to go get me dem. This place is no nonsense good people and good food. I had the Pho with pulled pork barbecue and it was amazing. They have a barbecue line up ready for plates oh plenty. They have a grill where they cook burgers and fries after you order. Today special burger be Spicy Asian with or without bacon. They also had Redneck Taco, which is a huge pancake slathered with barbecue and beans. Many different sauces to try here too. Get this! Now.
8xAa50YbwlE53F652Ru_Nw: For Americanized Chinese takeout food, this is some of the best I've had. The sesame chicken, general tso's, wontons, and crab rangoons are all excellent. This is definitely my go to Chinese takeout place.
Hole in the wall Chinese take out joint, but better than the average food you might expect from one. Very polite as well, did not get a rude vibe and was pleasantly greeted.
This place is way above average when it comes to quick Chinese food. Always fresh and delicious. We literally drive past 3 other Chinese restaurants closer to our house to go to this one!!
JuaQ51ohf8Mp6s12d19jag: I went last night to watch the UFC fight. We were on the second floor. They had a bunch of TVs all around with the warm up fights and the playoff football games on. I got the Girl Next Door and it was great! We also got wings and they were ok. The wings didn't have a lot of meat on it but the sauces were good. The atmosphere of the place is definitely a sports bar and it was a good one for the fight. The service was great. I would go back to see another game/fight.
Very friendly bartenders and quick and attentive and reasonably priced for downtown Nashville
Black forest is sandwich is a killer.   Add in the cutest barkeep ever TJ and you have a homerun
QhKFaD6EAKjsDXF8tPv_oQ: Outstanding burgers and fries, honestly you can split a burger and the sack of fries between 2 people

I FINALLY tried FG. I was a bit of a skeptic but no longer. I ordered a cheeseburger and split the cajun/seasoned fries with my friend. The burger was delicious. Juicy, beefy, and fresh tasting. Definitely one of the best tasting burgs I've had in this town. The fries were also tasty as well. Like others have mentioned, they do give a ton, so definitely split a regular or large order with your peeps. Will def be back!!!

Brightly colored interior serves overpriced burgers? Totally worth it, would go back, the unlimited topping caught this condiment junkies eye! Love that......Who needs that many fries!!! The kids burger plenty for an adult and the free peanuts on the table charming. Top five burger places in Nashville easily!
HUKmAvqq7O5OQmpdykE0cw: I love this location. Its clean, the employees and nice and my subs are always good!

The new staff and management of this store not only redid the interior, they overhauled the craftsmanship of the sandwiches.

The food here is very fresh and they hustle. My favorite is #17 (Jersey Mike's Famous Philly with cheese. It's always grilled to perfection (under the new owners anyways). My other go-to is the #8 with turkey, ham and....Bacon! Oh yeah.

I'm glad this place is fresh....and fast.

This is one of our go-to dinner and lunch places!  The food is delicious, from our standard Club Sub to their wraps.  And the staff is top-notch - they learned our names and orders and make it a fun experience to get a sandwich there!  Excellent customer service, which really just pushes the experience over the top.

I would definitely recommend signing up for their points system.  It doesn't take too long to get a reward and free sandwiches are always a bonus!
e2XQkZqKw8hfqmUuDjimIg: Good hotel, good restaurant, nice rooms. A little pricy, but accommodating. Only changes, upgrade in-room coffee to Kuerig. Do love the real coffee cups and real creamer. In room 509 the shower curtain doesn't close all the way so the floor gets wet. Maintenance needs to either balance the curtain rod appropriate so the curtain stays positioned or maybe put a dab of rubber silicone to hold the curtain in place. Ty!
At first, it seemed like a no-frills room. Very clean. No fridge or microwave. BUT... They do have sleep number beds. I've never seen those in a hotel before.

As hotels go, and this is an airport hotel, this one is vgood. CLEAN, quiet and friendly staff. Even the TV worked.What more do you need. I am not a fan of sleep number beds and I think this would be the only negative. Did not try the food and went out early in the morning. They had good hot coffee and grab and go for us V early people. A place well run.
uzL7P5u-VGtf7bfq6WUlPA: Nick is an excellent server if you get him. I thought the food wasn't that great, but the others in my group quite enjoyed it.

Great food better than Cracker Barrel but similar.  Waitstaff is kinda slow and not very welcoming but the food is really good.  Smaller portions then Cracker Barrel but the quality more than makes up for the quantity. If you are not too worried about people being friendly this is a great place to eat. Their burgers are not greasy at all they are delicious very fresh their fish is excellent their breakfast is excellent and made to order I have had all of these items here and they are very good. However again you will not feel as though you are really wanted here that is the only downfall to this place. The hostess acts as if you are bothering her when she has to go seat you. But the food quality more than makes up for the lack of friendliness
This place had an actual gluten free menu. It was good food and the server knew what to do. It is a great place to go for any meal.
vpQWbT7ZKcHMVKxEVR8Cuw:
Bushwackers!
So, I was introduced to these at a BBQ place in Auburn, AL after a football game one Saturday. Alcoholic milkshake, basically. And on a hot Nashville day, who doesn't want that. Every time we saw a place that sold them, we stopped & got a couple to cool down. 

Also, I got mistaken for someone that worked there. A lady came in, on my way back from the rest room, and she stopped to ask if I mind if their party (at least 15 people) could push some tables together. I said sure & walked-off, didn't bother me any. Hope it didn't create any trouble:p

Great dive bar feel with live music.  Going to The Stillery next door for dinner but the music pulled us in here first. Bartender was wonderful and the drinks were good.  If I lived in Nashville I would hang out here.
Stop here every time in Nashville. Whoever is playing here is better than the last. Little dive bar away from the chaos of Broadway
10KnzbTaz-Yq8wADXGuOlw: Great bar!  Kinda speak-easy like!  Down an alley off the main street!  Great atmosphere and excellent service!  So happy we accidentally found this place!  Didn't try the food, but the bar service was beyond reproach!  Will definitely be back when we come to Nashville!

Alley Taps is nestled on the lesser (by A large margin as there is nothing else there) traveled side of Printers Alley.  You wouldn't even know its there if there wasn't a sign standing outside the place.  Inside it's actually about 3x larger and a lot nicer than expected.  I did it eat there, but it looks like it's more of a drinking spot than eating spot.  I'm definitely not demeaning their food as I didn't see it.  There are several seating areas, tv's, and lots of bar seating.  At 3pm they have 2-4's.  They actually pour an excellent Guinness.  They have have live bands everyday after 5pm.  This place hasn't blown up yet, but it definitely could with their offering coupled with the new hotels that are popping up.  At its present state this is a great place to come without the tourist clutter downtown.  If they get the tourist good for them.

Great atmosphere, beers, and a great lineup of musicians. Good for interacting with the entertainment, intimate, and you can still talk to the people you're there with. 
They'd do even better with more food selections.
DDceWWXBQhj0GciqoER9mw: Awesome bagels. I have eaten Bagels in New York City and this is as good as being there.

This food truck was sitting outside of the office I was working in.  I had to cover for someone and was not looking forward to it, but the bare naked bagel made it all okay!  I got my self a nice cup of coffee and a plain bagel with a generous amount of cream cheese.  It took two minutes and was $5 to make my morning.  The bagel was plain so it was simple, but it was perfectly toasted and they put a ton of cream cheese on it, so it was very yummy.  The coffee was great too, just regular with some half and half and unbleached sugar.

After hours in the blazing hot sun at Ink N Iron I decided it was time for some dinner, and wanted the heartiest item I could get for the best price, and hopefully have that item contain some good veggies and protein. After walking up and down the food truck row, I came back to Bare Naked Bagel and got the Green Goddess bagel. At this time of the day they only had plain and sesame left so I opted for the plain. Loaded onto the bagel were cucumbers, roasted red peppers, sun-dried tomatoes, pickled red onions, hummus, spinach and green goddess dressing. The bagels were a good consistency for sandwich making, they were not too thick or tough to bite through. The filling was delicious and refreshing in the heat. 

They only side they had left was the "dirty chips" which are a commercial brand, fried in peanut oil.
Kv04mUsfP557B0ECYZkKBQ: Excellent customer service the chicken is fried fresh and the lemonade is made fresh daily.

Was looking to grab some hot chicken for dinner tonight and, alas, so many of our delicious local spots are closed on Sunday. Got lucky and stumbled across Helen's. I had heard about how delicious the food is here from a coworker and I'm glad I tried it! The mild chicken here puts the medium Hattie B's to SHAME! This chicken is also fried with a crispier breaking than most other hot chicken that I've tried. The staff was very friendly and accommodating, and the food was worth the wait. Although this was my first time here, I'm sure to be back soon. It might be the best hot chicken I've had!

I got the "Hella hot" wings, their hottest ones, and they were pretty hot but not inedible. Service was friendly but super slow, even during a slow part of the day. I think it had something to do with my order (wings), because several who ordered after me got their food before mine came out.
cp7sxmJlkObOPgO8PbgXKw: Best. Pupusa. In Nashville. Of course there made fresh and there so big crunchy and delicious. You won't be disappointed
I had a very delicious lunch here today! A pupusa and a beef taco that were so fresh. I ate every bite. Muy bien. Will return!

Okay, this place looks very suspect from the outside and the neighborhood. Once you enter, still looks like huhhhh. There will be a bit of a language barrier. They brought us food we didn't order due to said barrier. Had my first torta here and it was amazing. I would put this on a must try list. Very friendly environment
WxXyx8LNI08M7sXyjvomDg: This is one of the better taco trucks. They're located in front of Margie's Scrub A Dub @ 608 Gallatin Pike North in Madison. I had tacos de carne asada and @ 1.50/ea they are the same price as El Amigo Restaurant on Nolensville Rd, and nearly as good. They are a great value because in each taco you get about double the meat you'd get in most street tacos. If you're in Madison, this is probably the only decent Mexican food you'll find.

I was afraid to eat from a food truck, and I'm also more into TexMex style than Mexican style food. My coworkers convinced me. I tried the chicken tacos and it was ok. The next time, I tried the spicy pork tacos.........OMG! I am hooked. They are seasoned perfectly.  The use corn tortillas instead of flour. The tacos are tiny, so I get 4 to have a full meal. They give a super hot red salsa and a hot green creamy salsa on the side, which really gives it that kick. I'm hooked!
Tacos are really good, and the beef ones are only $1.50 a piece..  The lady who takes your order is pretty hot too so that's a plus.
neuEVClsC0wp6Hm9IXPu6Q: This is by far the best sushi place in and around Nashville. The atmosphere is always amazing and never too crowded. The meal comes quickly and I've yet to have a bad piece of sushi yet in almost 4 years of going back! I love the staff and their service. It always seems like they are thankful for your business and appreciate you as a customer. The Nashville roll is by far the best in town!

Good sushi and reasonable prices.  They offer a multitude (so many it is hard to make a choice) of interesting rolls and the service is pleasant and efficient.  It is a bit on the small side so you may have a wait on weekend nights.  It is not as hip as the place across the street, Sushi Me, but it is a better value.  Decor is your typical traditional Japanese restaurant theme with a few buddhas, some silk screens, etc.

What a great surprise to wander into Sushi O Sushi to discover Eichii, our very favorite Sushi Chef in all of Nashville!  If you love Ingiri, sit a spell at the Sushi bar and give Eichii a try!  He won't disappoint!  For the diner that prefers a table or a booth, Sushi O Sushi's menu offers a great choice of Japanese and Korean favorites and inventive choices of sushi rolls.  My husband loved the fresh grilled salmon and my plate of spicy noodles and chicken were delicious.  Cold Sapporo, nice wine choices, all from a bottle!  Located on Nolensville Road, at the corner of Brentwood and Nashville, we will get "jiggy" with Sushi O Sushi because everyone needs a taste of Japan a lot closer to home!
sVATAHU4PPfnvtDPokCh9A:
Don't mind the reviews on delivery and go inside. These 5 stars are for the wings and sauce. 

After having Hattie Bs earlier in the day and not being completely impressed, but happy, we came to Wings late night after drinking and the wings were not only a life saver but holy delicious!! 

Soft, big, and soaked in honey BBQ. These were incredible.  Give it a shot with honey BBQ or honey mustard. You will not be disappointed.
So glad this place found NASHVILLE. Great for late night wings after drinking with friends and getting hyphy. All night long!

Went there for dinner for the family.. We got the garlic pamesan tenders , cajun bbq tenders,  hot garlic tenders, buffallo wings , ribs , onion rings and fries... Everything was really good. The garlic parm tenders were delicious.. The hot garlic tenders were nothing more than buffallo chicken with some garlic.. The ribs were ok, my wife loved them.. All in all will go back again.
BVLGDMTcCU-9-Rwp3M4l6Q:
Authentic and delicious!
The facility is old and run down, the service is slow but the people are so nice and the food.. So good! Go for the cheese and loroco papusa. You wont regret it.

This pupuseria is a godsend. I moved to Nashville from LA a few months ago, something I wouldn't have been able to do had I not found a solid pupusa spot prior to moving here. My family is from El Salvador and pupusas are our lifeblood, we make them from scratch at home and have a solid restaurant we've been going to for over a decade. The quality at this location is on par with my family's favorite pupusa spot. So far I've had their pupusas (duh), desayuno tipico with chorizo (standard salvi breakfast with veggies and chorizo scrambled into eggs), tamales de maiz and some of their fruit juices. The service is good, prices are reasonable and the food tastes authentic and is well made. You seat yourself and pay at the cashier on the way out. They have 2 small TVs in the corners (good for world cup watching) and sell desserts to go. If you've never had Salvadoran food I'd reccomend giving this spot a try. The menu is translated into English, don't be intimidated if you don't speak Spanish!
Awesome authentic Salvadoran food. Pupusas are a must. Friendly staff. Will definitely be back!
4samvXp8jtwYn_qU_10g2A:
I'm really surprised to be the first reviewer for this Food Truck.
My little town of Smyrna has a "Food Truck Festival" every month or so sponsored by Cousin's Maine Lobster truck (review to come for them as well) which is really just a few trucks in one of our strip mall parking lots,.. but hey.. it's how we roll here in little ole Smyrna.
I am SO GLAD this truck was at this months little meet-up, as I have found yet another amazing food truck I will begin stalking.

First things first- What we ordered- 

Brisket Sandwich- grilled ciabatta bread with caramelized onions, grilled mushrooms, green peppers, and Havarti or provolone cheese, topped with spicy Russian sauce or homemade BBQ sauce- we opted for the Spicy Russian, and it was just that! Spicy. But really good flavor. I don't eat peppers, so I picked off some of the brisket, and it was good. Fat rendered well, Good smoke, just an all around well done piece of meat in my opinion. 
$9.00

Shrimp Tacos- grilled, flour or corn tortilla with avocado, roasted corn, red cabbage, fresh cilantro, and a lemon wedge. Drizzled with Japanese "yum-yum" sauce.
OH MY GOD! Out of 3 food trucks, and trying the "best sellers" of each one, this was by far my favorite bite of the night. And for the price, I wish I had got like 4 of those bad boys. 
$4.00

They also serve avocado fries that I completely forgot about ordering, and was pretty bummed about it, because the line had just gotten way too long to get back in it. 
And I noticed as we were leaving, they sale BRISKET BISCUITS for your dogs! $4.00
With homemade, fresh ingredients of pumpkin, honey, cinnamon, and wheat flour 
With a sign that read: Veterinary tested and approved!  
All proceeds go to the Tennessee Death Row Dogs Organization.

So had I known that as well, I would have gotten my pooch something.
Next time!
Oh, there will be a next time! Those shrimp tacos are calling my name

Found these guys at a St Patrick's Day party. 
I had the Brisket Taco and Cajun Fries. The Brisket was delicious. 
Meet the owner. He was very nice and friendly. The entire staff was wonderful.

The service exceeded my expectations! The girl at the window was so friendly. I got the brisket sandwich (photos above) which was out of this world and my friend got the loaded fries. Both were huge portions for a great price and the brisket was so tender and juicy, I will definitely be back!
D28IsqGE9EbwQFf9A8Ve1A: Saw this food truck for the first time at the China Lights event at the Fairground. My husband is Korean so was excited to try it. He had the bi bim bop with spicy chicken. Egg poached well and flavor was pretty on point. Includes kimchi that he called "typical kimchi", as in good but nothing out of the ordinary. Bowl cost $12 which was a tad steep, but it was definitely authentic and he would go there again. Love Busan, wonder if the owner is from there? Recommend you try a bowl!

Busan Bop came to a conference we attended and I was so glad to get some Korean food in TN! I went for the japchae (they call it "Jop Chae") for $10 without meat (or $12 if you add meat) - sweet potato "glass" noodles stir-fried with soy sauce and sesame oil, with mushrooms, green onions, carrots. The noodles were really delicious but don't expect a lot of sauce as it is more cooked into the noodles. My friend went back for extra soy sauce but they had none available. There was only a little amount of pieces of vegetables but it was a small and filling portion. I would come here again but probably order the bimbimbap because it looked a lot more impressive and had more vegetables on top. They processed a long line quickly and with a short wait, which was awesome. Wish they had some gochuchang or other hot sauce available to put on top but I'd come back again!

Real, authentic Korean food! 
Hands down best Korean food on wheels in Nashville, and I have tried them all.  Owners are very friendly people and are actually from Busan, South Korea!  Wish they had a fixed location so I could go every day.
t9op7albX3mGzbtb0ydYgQ:
This place is an Oasis in a sea of haircuts. 

Cheap beers! Cash only! Smoking inside! Jukebox! Character! Chilltastic and friendly bar staff! Taco truck or they will order you food to the bar... cheese sticks please sticks! 

Nashville is known for places chock full of pretension and devoid of real value. This place is a fantastic gem. No "design" or "reclaimed wood barn" or "side eyes" here. Just a spot to drink and talk.

Nashville has a good selection of dive bars. I don't usually frequent them, but tonight, I needed a beer after work, so I hit up my neighborhood dive bar, the Tin Dog Tavern. 

It's got all the usual goodness I have come to expect: a slim and cheap selection of beers, a mixture of Edison bulbs and neon, friendly faces, and a good ol' jukebox. My favorite thing is the assortment of funky chairs in the back corner. It definitely gives the place character. 

Also, had my first PBR, and I am totally digging it. Definitely plan on coming back here when I can.
I visited this place while visiting from Miami and fell in love with the place and the staff. Look forward to frequenting it when I move to Nashville!
XJZfCBVlhiapUDlBQs2DKw:
We were celebrating a birthday in the private room.  We called ahead a few hours in advance to let them know it was a special event.  Paige (the manager on duty) called me back promptly.  She was heading into the staff meeting and assured me everything would be perfect. 

When we arrived for our reservation, the chef beautifully prepared and laid out a spread of charcuterie, cheeses, charred summer squash and roasted carrots.  Everything was made and laid out meticulously.

We salads we started with the grilled romain and baby lettuce.  For dinner we had the trumpet mushrooms (a hearty option for any vegetarian), bratwurst, schnitzel, chicken and trout.  It was the perfect mix for everyone. 

The service was exceptional and the food was remarkably.  With such short notice, the entire staff made sure everything was special.

You must try the bacon and pickles, the "Bridget" cocktail and the cinnamon ice cream sundae. A.m.a.z.i.n.g. And service is fantastic - we went for breakfast the other day while downtown - the fresh squeezed juice was amazing and if you need a caffeine boost, Barista Parlor coffee will do just the trick :).

This is overdue but loved the dinner I had here! I had the octopus and grits which was one of the best meals I've had. Also have tried the scallops and grits, burger, avocado toast and kale caesar and have loved them all.  Go! You won't be disappointed.
o6b-VwNvh8KSR902jha3gg: So fun for families! Servers are great! Food tasty and fresh, tho pricey.... But worth it for the atmosphere!

Nestled inside Opry Mills Mall, Rainforest Café surely stands out because of its fun atmosphere.

They usually have a wait, especially on a Saturday night, but they take your number and will text/call you when your table is ready.  We put our name in and surprisingly our wait wasn't as bad as it was first told.

Once seated, our waiter came immediately and we told him we were headed to a show at the Opry so we needed to get in and out.  He was extremely accommodating in this and told us more people need to give heads up about that so orders can be filled faster.  We got our drink orders in and decided on sharing their Awesome Appetizer adventure for our meal.  Food came out fast and it was delicious!  The chimis were my favorite.  Just the right size to share and to make it a meal.

Kudos for making our night!

As a child, I visited several Rainforest Cafes and loved them, so it was only natural that I had to take my boyfriend who had never been to one. We decided to visit Rainforest on a Saturday night. When we arrived, we were told it would be about 1 hr and 30 min wait (also said the average wait all day had been 2 hours). Since the restaurant is in the mall, we decided to wait. They get your phone number so they can text you when it's your time, which is very helpful rather than having to carry around a buzzer. They also have a gift shop at the entrance that you can look around while you wait. Our wait ended up being 1 hour and 45 minutes. The restaurant was very crowded and loud. Our waiter, Jackson, was the best! He was very attentive and funny. You can tell he really enjoys his job. The food was good, not the absolute best, but I would eat it again. My boyfriend and I both ordered their specialty drinks. The Blue Nile is one of the best drinks I've ever had (I ended up getting two and left the restaurant a tad bit buzzed.) Every 30 minutes, there's a "rainstorm" and all of the animals come alive, making noises and moving around. Definitely a fun place for folks who have kids. Fun experience.
8ZjJpnLyklvdj8ng0zjRSA: This was our second time to visit in a month. Once for my b-day and once for my wife's. Both times, we had excellent service (as you always get) and the food selection is incredible. I would always recommend waiting to make your decision on what you want to order until you hear the specials for evening. There is usually fresh fish with a very nice presentation and flavor profile. We will definitely return and enjoy another meal here. I know if you choose Zola, you won't be disappointed.

what an eclectic decor!!! this place definitely stands out in terms of look and feel of a cozy place. 

the overall experience was awesome! not only were the plates delicious, but the companion that joined me was fabulous! we ordered several appetizers, main dish, wine, and dessert; so it was like that of tapas, we just decided that way so that we can have more of the goodies. our server was great, she gave us good guidelines to our dishes and our spanish wine (yum!). =]  i don't recall the name, but it was one of the longest name from the spanish wine section; i think i made a fool of myself trying to say the wine ( little did i know that my guest is fluent in spanish!!!). i was really impressed with the crab & mango salad. it was very unique, but good. i'd give it 4 thumbs up (including my companion's) hehehe. =P the other dishes were great; the shrimp and tuna just melted in my mouth; great flavor, texture, and presentation! the homemade sorbetto is amazing!!! i don't even remember the three flavors b/c i was so into my dessert. 

Overall, the experience has been one of the better ones that i've had here in nashville. This place isn't a place where you would go w/ a group of friends every friday night; it's a close encounter setting where people can get intimate and really get to know one another. no wonder it has been voted as "the most romantic restaurant" in nashville.

The appetizers were exquisite (the shrimp with manchego cheese wrapped in prosciutto, the rare seared tuna with avocado...). Coupled with a spanish wine, the meal got off to a wonderful start. The paella, however, did not match up. It lacked any clams, which I have always had in paella, and overall didn't have enough flavor to make it work. It wasn't bad by any standard, but just wasn't great either. The trio of homemade gelatos finished the evening off perfectly... As for the overall experience, out waitress was attentive and knowledgeable. The decor was eclectic and charming (mural of desert, international menus covering bathroom walls, a tent room). I enjoyed this meal and would certainly return.
435u0G05-7NYJ_nhC14O_A: Ate here for lunch last week. We were sat immediately and had great service. I got the beef tacos and refried beans. Food came out hot. Tacos were alittle small but seasoned well. I will be returning!

My friend set up a happy hour get together here but... there's no actual happy hour specials for this restaurant. Bummer. They have 3 main Margarita flavors ( Lime, Mango and Strawberry) but you could get a Mango/Strawberry if your heart desires. I ordered the mango one but thought it was a bit too sweet so my boyfriend and I mixed the lime and mango together. Much better. We ordered the Fajita Texana for two. This came with shrimp, beef and chicken. The meats were very well seasoned and we were very pleased with it.

We sat on the patio and it was beautiful... very open and you have a view of town town especially of the Omni. Depending on where you're sitting, you can also see the Batman building. 

I love that they have a parking lot which means free parking and no hassle!

Being that I'm on lunch break, I can't speak for the margaritas... But for basic Mexican food they definitely stand out. Rice and beans are flavorful. Chili rellano is cooked perfectly (not a sloppy mess like elsewhere). Great little spot with an elevated view of the city.
9g9wQVMGXZRJaQccBU9c6A: I've been meaning to write a review on this place. My favorite post workout meal is the Juice Bar. I always run here after I workout downtown. Now, I see people in the reviews complaining about the price... okay let's sit down and learn. First of all, they are using ALL NATURAL INGREDIENTS, like legit berries and stuff. You know when you go to the Krogers that berries are not 50 cents... it's a good $4.00 for some berries, and that's not even the organic kind. They've got FRESH INGREDIENTS, so of course they can't charge you $3 for your smoothie. They put a lot of good stuff in there, and genuine, healthy, good products cost money.
