So that's why it "cost so much"; but honestly, with the amount of stuff they're putting in the smoothies, it's like a discount. Plus, what did you expect when you GO TO A RESTAURANT to get food; of course you can make EVERYTHING IN THE WORLD for a cheaper price at your home... So don't complain about the price because it is beyond reasonable. Also, they've gotta pay the workers. Lol, y'all are silly. Anyway, love this place. They've got the nutritional info laid out for you IN FULL (macros and all). And they've got areas for you to sit inside and outside. Y'all keep doing what you're doing.
The only thing better than the amazing juices and smoothies are the people that make them. Love this place!

This is a great place to hang out. It was my first time getting an acai bowl and it was amazing and refreshing.  I could eat one of those for breakfast everyday! Afterwards, they have a ping pong table outside that you can play on or set up a corn hole game.
dKDyKDdSJR4xHMrTvrSz1Q:
Surprising! 

We waited too long and couldn't find any local restaurants without a 40 min wait. Stumbled upon Nashville Underground and couldn't have been more pleased. The fried green tomatoes and the honey roasted brussels sprouts with pecans were addictive. 

I've had Nashville's hot chicken many times before and wanted to do something less likely to give me heartburn.  The Broadway BBQ chicken was suggested.  I paired it with the Truffle Mac and Cheese and again, I was pleasantly surprised. The juicy bbq flavoring left me wishing I had ordered my  very own versus sharing it! 

Will definitely try more on the menu next time I come to town!

I was glad that we wondered into this restaurant. I had the Strawberry salad, perfectly fresh and a beautiful blend of ingredients. Also tried a Music City Mimosa, which I loved, but thought was a bit pricy at $12. My husband had a burger, and he enjoyed it as well. The entertainment was great, both artists we saw had wonderful voices, and I loved that you could listen but they weren't so loud that you couldn't still have a conversation.

Pulled pork and Nashville hot chicken were both very good. Normally things like slaw are just a throw-in, but the pickles and slaw here were very good. Service is good especially given they just recently opened.
3VdScOpQ4N1N-EqjA9NwZg: Came in because I was starving and needed something quick.  I got the 6" rotisserie chicken.  The bread was fresh and the vegetables were great!  They even have Swiss cheese.  I would say they are trying hard here to sell a quality sandwich.  I know that's what I got!
Don't judge this book by its cover. Clean friendly and fast in corner lot that looks run down.
HcXEt2bop-wGqb8gVIkxag: These guys are awesome! This is the 2nd time I've used them for delivered catering & both times have been very impressed - Great food, great service! Definitely will be using them again soon!

First order with Apple Spice was a huge hit!  We often have working lunches and so nice to have a new option.  The quality and flavor were amazing and the customer service, top notch.  If you haven't tried them yet do it soon!!!

Today my office ordered lunch from Apple Spice and we absolutely love it! It's so nice to have a healthier option for lunch in this area AND THEY DELIVER!!! I think we're going to making ordering from them a regular Friday event for the office. Thank you Apple Spice and the food is awesome.
q39S4YaJo-OLEOX9-BJ9Lw: I had never met her before. But whoever was working on Saturday December 16th at 8 pm, made my sandwich with LOVE. Big props to this young girl.

Excellent sandwich joint! Some of the best sandwiches in Green Hills. I recommend the Roast Beef, the Club Supreme, and the Big Kahuna Melt (Philly with peppers). The guys there are fast and efficient at filling large orders. You won't be disappointed. 

If you go regularly, be sure to sign up for their rewards card so you don't miss out on your free sandwich.
This is an easy location to get in and out of. Always consistent and clean. Friendly staff. They definitely know how to make a sandwich!
5q__7PxcjaqxrzZTC_yrug: Great food. Had a kale salad for lunch.  It is delicious.  Adding it to my regular delights.

Wonderful find on Yelp! Quaint place, extremely attentive staff and the food (Breakfast) was excellent. I loved the Breakfast Burrito, and my wife had the Avocado Toast (add goat cheese). Will definitely return.
One of the best kept secrets in West Nashville. Amazing hamburger and friendly attentive staff.
zHXYNXlSO3vMrWlISzmwlQ: 4 stars for being there for me after midnight on my way back to the hotel with a board advertising 99 cent chocolate dipped cones ... I honestly can't remember the last time I had one ... and boy did it not disappoint considering I had the "ate dinner too early" munchies. Minus 1 star for the isolated cashier behind what I hope was bullet proof glass and the metal extend-a-drawer like they have at some bank drive-thrus ... made me question the safety of my temporary digs.

Probably the busiest restaurant drive through I have ever seen. Put it this way, they had workers outside with an iPad type device taking people's orders to keep it moving.

Inside was kind of small, but clean, people were nice, etc. No kid play area and this is in a little mini complex of a couple other fast food places.

They got my order right each time, and food was fine, I would definately recommend for breakfast.
rKbYkV30hgQNqESRxZ2ezw: Amazing sushi, must try the hot ginger tea! Very impressed with quality of food and service. Will go back again and again!

Love this place! Always friendly service and very timely. I usually order sushi with soup and salad, but the curry and noodle dishes have never disappointed either. Great Tom Kha and specialty rolls. Reasonably priced as well!

The curry was fantastic! Service was good and the establishment was clean. Prices are fair for the portion sizes which were pretty large. Most other guests were ordering sushi so I would imagine it is very good as well.
X7YiYajX0CGJZddnUs_80w: First time here... Being in town starting a new job and I occasionally use this app for places to try food. The restaurant isn't in the best side of town but don't let that stop you. My co-worker ordered the Philly spud and I ordered the chicken and waffles. He said it's great. And I got the chicken and waffles and omg   . Y'all should try it.
Food is exceptional! Service is even better! Can not thank them enough for their kindness and patience tonight! I will definitely be back!!
Great food! Got the seafood potato and fried fish to split with my sister. Both very good! And the potato is so large I had enough to eat later :)
xrfpHhrSZAAd5UG-KGMe3w: Love this spot. We stumbled upon this place a few weeks ago headed to another hookah place and had been coming ever since. We enjoy the food and the atmosphere. They use coconut natural coals which I like and have some good flavors. They are currently byob and I actually enjoy this even though this may be seem to be an  inconvenience to some. The waitress valarie is very nice. Overall great price hookah and food for a great night out with friends or for a date. A lot less crowded than some of the other hookah lounges, check it out.

I really don't want to write this review. I want to keep the place to myself! A new owner bought this place maybe last month, and is in the process of getting a liquor license. Until then they have bottled beer, and maybe wine but I don't recall seeing any wine. It's pretty lavishly decorated with a large dining room and separate hookah lounge that is spacious and comfy, cushioned benches that are low to the floor. I can't remember what brand of shisha they were using but it was damn good! I could have stayed there for 4 more hours if it hadn't gotten a touch crowded with younger cooler people than my upper 30's self. And my filet shish kabob came out hot and delicious. Same menu as the one on Nolensville. The food prices are very reasonable, the hookahs were $13.
Best lamp shake, so far.  Meat is so tender and well seasoned. Cake is good too. Clean and comfortable atmosphere.  Nice servers. Recommend lamp dish in lunch menu.
sij__XMC6VWjHSqkzvawrA: This has been my favorite gyro and falafel stand in Nashville. Their prices are amazing for the amount of quantity of food that they end up serving. The guys working there are also super friendly, and always seem to throw in extra. Their falafel plate and gyro plate are fantastic and I love going there whenever I'm at the farmer's market. I hope to try all of their other foods soon!

I always go for the gyro meal!  The meat is always delicious and the tomatoes are fresh!  It comes with fries, but they will substitute a side salad if you ask.  I also like to ask for extra sauce on the inside of the gyro instead of just on top.  The portions are generous for the price too!  

Oh and don't be deterred by the line!  It moves pretty quickly!

This is my favorite place so far in the Farmers Market food court.  It's the only Farmers Market place I've been to so far that has reasonable prices for lunch, and the gyros and the salad bar are great.  The meat seemed very fresh and the rice and toppings were delicious.
_vZSIIqBsw_IUmxqNVOALA: We've had a chance to go twice.. once for breakfast and again for lunch..Nashville is indeed growing with many places, but..this is one of the best - not to be missed!  The coffee is fabulous,  and the other drinks and menu items are very creative! "Stay Golden"! Open from 7-7 - watch the food prep or the wonderful craft of the coffee roasting from your table.   And next door is a fabulous gift/accessory candle bar called Paddywax!  Make your own while you sip something divine from Stay Golden.  All of the staff are top notch!  They know their stuff!!

This is hands down my favorite new Nashville spot! The space is beautifully designed and the drinks are inventive and delicious. The food is next level and everything I've tried so far has been incredible. Love that we now have amazing food and coffee in Berry Hill! I will definitely be a regular!

The iPad ordering system is SO convenient. I love not having to wait for my check or get interrupted at inopportune times by wait staff.

The food and coffee are exceptional, but don't forget the cocktails. Good Lord. Instantly a top Nashville spot for me in that regard.
A_ZeOVoEKM7P8BdoxrWyXw:
Excellent Chili's.  We went the week it opened.  Service was a little slower than usual, which was to be expected as they work out the kinks.

The food, however, was excellent-- served very hot and probably the best food I've had at any Chili's.  We'll be back!
Our server was fantastic.  The food took a little bit longer than normal, but they are still new. We'll be back!
Had a great meal and good service for 10 bucks. Wait staff was hitting on all all cylinders. A quick good pleasant experience with family. Loved the blue berry pineapple margarita.
IjjUbIP7plAbNVV8o337xQ:
This Piccadilly restaurant is on par with those in Baton Rouge, LA - where the company is headquartered as well as my hometown.  They serve all of Piccadilly's "standard dishes" and prepare them well.  

My go-to "Dilly Plate" - 

- Chopped Beef Steak w/ Onions and Gravy
- Green Beans
- Mashed Potatoes, no gravy
- Soft Roll
- Chocolate Pudding

Having grown up around these recipes, I know how they should taste.  Piccadilly Nashville never fails me!


Ms. Tammy rocks!

I've never had a bad meal here, service is excellent, I've been coming here for years and everyone knows me.  I love their fried chicken, Salisbury steak and onions, their choice of bread, mash potatoes and gravy, turnip greens and the carrot sue-flay.  The desserts are good also if you even have room after the large meal.  Try the chocolate pecan pie, yum!!

#awesomeness food great service and I can't wait to get out their every time the staff are always welcome and professional the food is always fresh and it 
Reminds me of the restaurant called belle Meade 
Cafe that is unfortunately no longer around
97PA4BTfAm6OwuDObQ4n5Q: Get the fries w/ a side of mustard! Yummy!! I had the Southern which is most popular. It's good-not AMAZING, but totally solid and I'd absolutely recommend anyone to go. Super friendly staff and nice atmosphere. On the small side, so no big groups, but can easily sit a group of four. Can't wait to watch this place grow!

Totally dug it, and one of those "wish I had thought of that" situations.

Visited with two co-workers on a lunch outing for dessert, and very different and fun.  I had the German, think German chocolate cake-like on a waffle, but also got to try a bit of the poppyseed (very tasty) and an ooey-gooey one another friend ordered that I forget the name of.  They were all good, but admittedly mine was my favorite.

Tiny place, only two tables and a few barstools with some patio seating.  Free water, didn't notice that they sold any real beverages.

They are also on a pop up Scoutmob right now, so download that app if you are visiting Monday-Friday.

I'm always excited when a new place comes in close enough for a quick Metro Center lunch, and this place won me as a regular on the first visit. 

My suggestion? GO NOW before the I-only-eat-from-food-trucks-and-drink-PBR hipsters and the I-just-bought-this-cowboy-hat-for-my-Nashvile-trip-yeehaw tourists figure out this place exists. Once that happens, the line will stretch around the block. Why? Because it's delicious, unique, and cheap. 

For starters, the place is tiny and sits where the beloved Zackie's hot dog shop once was (RIP, my friend). The people were over the top friendly and I enjoyed getting to chat with them. 

When I arrived, I looked over the menu and asked the folks what they thought I should get. They told me the "Southern" was definitely their most popular, and was a good intro to their menu. Maybe they were just really persuasive people, but it sounded delicious so I followed their suggestion. I just got a cup of water, and tax and all it came to under $7. So far so good. 

It took a while for it to come out, but they were cooking it all on the spot, and I am not one to complain about freshly prepared food. 

When it came off the griddle, the lady asked if I wanted any kind of sauce, such as honey mustard or ketchup. "HONEY MUSTARD ON A WAFFLE?!" Perhaps I'm old fashioned, but that sounded disgusting. I politely declined and silently gagged. If only I knew how grave of a mistake I was making. 

The sandwich was a beast - a large waffle wrapped around a savory piece of friend chicken and all the fixins. Before biting in, I expected it to have some sort of sweet flavor from the waffle, but once more, I was wrong. It tasted like a delicious chicken sandwich. But... what was missing? There was something it just didn't have.... Oh yes. HONEY MUSTARD. I mentally facepalmed myself for not trusting the cook and polished off the sandwich. 

I will be back to try almost everything on the menu. The American (burger), the Break-Fast (Chicken or ham with egg and cheese), and their variety of sweet waffle options all looked rather tasty. With the right selection of sauces under my belt, I anticipate pushing this guy to 5 stars in the near future.
HqXFcscNFrkK32VMVK2T0A: Just ordered the chicken tenders and the BLT for delivery from this place on Yelp... They called saying they didn't have the BLT anymore but they could switch it out for any other sandwich, so we went with the club. They even offered to add in a pizza free of charge, but I told them it was fine and not to worry about it. Food got delivered pretty quickly, and it was very hot and fresh. Sandwich was delicious and they even threw in a chocolate mousse for me! Yum.

I've been meaning to finish this review for a while. If you want delivery Italian, this is it. You won't be upset. The calzone is legit and the marinara is wonderful. $10 minimum delivery? Please! Just do it. You don't be sorry. This is the second time I order from them and it won't be my last. I was super worried, I just moved to this area and have had some pretty bad delivery Asian but this is the best Italian! Just do it. 

Update. Just ordered again. Every time I can't believe it. Never leaving my house again. 

Update #2. These people probably think I never cook.

The food was exemptional! My husband is extremely picky when it comes to pizza crust and he was very impressed. The pizza was cooked to perfection and not the least bit greasy. I had the spaghetti and meatballs with a side salad. The pasta and sauce were delicious and the meatballs were great, but, as silly as this may sound, I was most impressed with their side salad. It wasn't just lettuce and dressing. It had olives, tomatoes, cheese, and bacon on it! Say what you want about my need to share praise for a simple salad, but anyone who has ever eaten the after thought that is the normal takeout side salad will appreciate that I was thoroughly impressed.
_OB65sIprNs4eUm1SHjhxw: Came here for our morning joe as we were staying around the corner at the Doubletree.  Really comfy, clean cafe and a nice place to hang out.  I ordered a cappuccino and DH got a latte.  Both were served quickly and were really good.  Service was really friendly as well.  We'll definitely be back if we're in Nashville!
Thanks to Yelp for helping me find this place, quick walk from my hotel.  Daily brew was Yirgacheffe (Ethiopian), yum!  Sadly, not open Sundays, so we only came in once.

We were tourists getting ready to go on the Walking Nashville tour, which meets right outside this coffee shop.  We were early and had already been looking for a coffee cafe, so this was the perfect spot.  I had the house coffee, which was good, and the rest of the party had more froo-froo drinks - all of us enjoyed the coffees and the atmosphere, much better than Starbucks in my opinion.  We didn't order any of the food choices, but it looks like they have a nice assortment of paninis, wraps, bagels and that sort of thing.
jUVk21Kkf7awzvnj8oci3Q: Great little local East Nashville joint. We are lucky to have them in the hood. Awesome margaritas!!
The best pupusas in town! The manager is great and very friendly. Gorgeous interior and apparently good margaritas! You should definitely make this place a priority.

Everything I have had here has been excellent. Their tacos al pastor are some of the best. Their empanadas are light and flavorful. The pupusas are little circles of yum, my favorite is the loroco.  Now that they have their liquor license they will be my go to on the East side!
axBvAzrXqRx2kKadwg87sQ: Went for brunch. Had the creole omelette with their Bloody Mary. Omelette was fantastic with the bursts of flavor. Bloody Mary was sub-par but it's a brunch place so I didn't expect too much. Would definitely go back though. Service was quick and friendly.
Staff is friendly & happy!!  Fresh squeezed OJ is wonderful & prices are very reasonable.

My husband and I tried this place on a whim this morning.  I'm so excited to have an amazing breakfast spot close to our house now!  I got the Surfer Girl which is a vegetarian omelette with sprouts and pico on top, it was wonderful and fresh.  My husband got the breakfast burrito which he too enjoyed!  It came with skillet type potatoes (these kind of cold, which was sad but they were still good haha) and a muffin.  I've never had a better blueberry muffin (it tasted like blueberry cake).  Their coffee was also fantastic.  Our server (reddish hair guy, I feel bad I don't remember his name) was very attentive, personable and professional!   Can't wait to return to the Wild Egg!
BkdbdbYZxo6NSl3EXu0RJw:
Overall package is an excellent quality show so much better than the Jackson Showboat. Each performer are really top notch high good quality performers and entertainers in their right.  Most are well seasoned singers in the music industry. These are real people living the Nashville dream of being performers. The only reason I am not giving a full 5 stars is because the buffet food is just very basic and okay. But you are not coming here for the food, but the performances are all outstanding!

The owner, Kenny Janson started this establishment and was a drummer for Tammy Wynette. Paula was also a backup singer for Tammy Wynette. Zach Janson is new and is starting his career out while attending college majoring in music. I love Tim Watson, the funny comedian and crazy fiddle singer player out there!  Also lots of love out to Brenda Best. She's cool and friendly and a down to earth person. I bought her and Zach's CD and Tim Watson's DVD. I don't normally buy stuff after a show unless it's really good, so this is a sign that this is a great music show not to be missed. After the show the artists come out in the lobby and if you want you can just chat with them casually and ask them anything you want. They are very personable, the southern hospitality, at it's best. :-)  Country music rules! Yah! :-)

I saw this show back in March 2016, and I felt it deserved a honest and real review in Yelp. Don't judge a book by it's cover because this place is located in somewhat of a strip mall location.
Really enjoyed myself. The food and service is excellent. There is video entertainment during dinner. We saw Tammy Wynette but I'm sure they have different videos

This place started off a bit slow, but then things took off! The food was good, not great but good & there is a huge selection. The performers were outstanding. Of notable mention was the fiddle player, Tiger, who played Orange Blossom Special & The Devil Went Down To Georgia better than I have ever heard it. Kenny Janson on his drum solo was incredible! His son Zach was great as was Brenda Best. The comedian made my sides hurt I laughed so hard. The show was incredibly great! The only downer & it is small is the sales pitch for DVDs & Curios was a bit much. Wish this place was close to home, don't miss it if you are in Nashville!
XKNnyaJY5b1kfM9ha8kvKQ:
I have 2 words for you: Petit Four. The icing is divine and melts in your mouth. The cake is fluffy and yummy perfection.

The petit fours would definitely be on my dessert list for Last Meal on Earth.

My only problem is they don't offer savings if you buy a lot of them.  But a few dollars for a square of heaven is not too much to ask.

My work ordered 2 cakes there for my GM's birthday a few weeks ago... a german chocolate & italian cream (i think that's the name?) and they were devoured in minutes! Fast forward to today, we ordered another italian cream for a co-worker's birthday and i'm struggling not to have a second piece.
The frosting is perfectly sweet and the cake has a fine crumb that kind of melts in your mouth. The filling in the italian cream is kind of like a vanilla pudding - delicious and light.
I dare you (and myself) to stop at just one piece.

This was my first stop on a tour of bakeries along US 70 precipitated by the closure of Alpha Bakery earlier this year. I only recently learned of Becker's, and I'm glad I had the opportunity to check them out. This is mostly a sweets bakery. They did have dinner rolls, but the fare is primarily cakes, cookies, and similar items. I found the staff friendly and helpful. The prices are fair, but appeared to be unmarked, which I frown upon. I know businesses do that so you don't make purchasing decisions based on price, but frankly I spend less in a cautionary response if I can't see prices. I bought some dinner rolls, chocolate cookies, and petit fours. The dinner rolls are good. I don't really love the chocolate cookies. The petit fours are amazing. The cake inside is moist and not loaded with gritty frosting like industrial petit fours. Similarly, the icing is fresh and delicate, not plasticky like industrially produced petit fours sometimes are. I'd love to try their cake. 

Will totally do business with them again.

Note: There is enough space to back up a midsize sedan in the parking lot, but not much more. Bring the smaller car for this trip...your pickup or minivan may take some wiggling, as the spaces are only about one car length from the main road.
g_HU4ecX22Yzy2LGDiPIVQ:
I have traveled far and wide and have come to appreciate a good solid croissant. Crispy on the outside, soft and buttery inside, delicious all around. I recently moved from Boston where I found a few French-style bakeries that catered to my croissant needs. I was very disappointed when I moved to Nashville a year ago and explored extensively without a good croissant in mind. Fortunately I discovered this place a month ago and finally, found a croissant to rival the best across the country and even comparable to French ones! The place has a nice and desirable neighborhood feel. Gina (the chef), Shara (one of the baristas), Mac (coffee guru) always greet you with a smile. One time they messed my order and Nadia, the girl always on the back, quickly corrected it and refused to take any sort of compensation for going out of the way.
In addition they are very dog friendly. Gina makes little doggie croissants for your furry friends. Cappuccinos are decent to very good depending on who makes them, drip coffee is solid, and croissants are amazing. What else so you want in a local bakery? 
Highly recommend the butter, chocolate, and especially roasted red pepper croissants.

AMAZING pastries!!! You can tell that it's made fresh daily and infused with lots of LOVE. Warm atmosphere with friendly staff. I could see myself spending the day here studying or working. It feels good to support a local business.

Now onto the food:
My favorite is the butter croissant - soft, flakey, buttery, delicate. The almond croissant is my next favorite - the almond filling is homemade and bursting with flavor. The ham and cheese croissant has a kick which is a pleasant surprise and went well with the butteriness if the croissant. The quiche is not egg-y at all and surprisingly light yet filling and you get a HUGE portion. I could snack on the monkey bread all day while I worked here. The coffee is so smooth that I could drink it black. They also have salads and sandwiches here that I'll have to try next time but looking at other people's orders the portions are large and looked fresh. 

I also can't believe the prices - most expensive thing is $6 (for a roast beef sandwich)!!! It's a good thing that I don't live close by otherwise I would be broke and fat!!!

These folks may be on to something.  If you can make croissants the way Flour Sugar Eggs does then you have got something to offer.  I have not sampled enough of their product to give them the full unabashed gushing, but I have a feeling that it won't be long.
cAUPs_fqV7BUArF_9F9iQg:
I went to Rooster's while in Nashville on vacation for my birthday.  After having been in Memphis for 3 days, & being not overly impressed with the barbecue, we found Rooster's to have the best that we had in Tennessee.  

We did a late lunch, which happened to fall in around the Happy Hour specials.  We had the chicken tender appetizer- which was great because you could tell that they were not frozen pre-made chicken strips, the batter coating had great seasoning, & the fries were seasoned nicely.

My boyfriend & I ventured to share one of the combo meals, and had the ribs & the brisket.  The ribs were great- falling off the bone, sauce on the side- so you can really taste the rub & the brisket was delicious- it was tender & a nice smoky flavor.  The hot barbecue sauce was definitely our favorite.  We enjoyed the green beans & loved the fried okra side.  

To celebrate my birthday, we ordered a beer each, but since it was happy hour, we each got 3 for the price of 1.  The service was great, very friendly, our waters were refilled quickly, as were our beers replaced.  The owner came over to talk to us & we enjoyed the conversation.  

The only thing that was upsetting about my visit, was that I was too full to try any of the desserts. 

We will definitely venture back when we go back to Nashville, as well as recommend to others!
Great food, cold beer, stiff drinks, Great service! Laid back atmosphere. Loved the comfy couch and fire place! Ill be coming back!

The brisket here I found to be a little dry but the ribs were good. I found the place clean and the staff was friendly enough. I do really like the way the place looks with the wood decor and the actual fire place is a nice touch. I don't know if it's a great place to go out for dinner but it's great for a quick lunch with coworkers. It's a little pricey for lunch but it's worth it every now and then.
vY1sCMYRVes0ZbqAYD7j4w:
Surprisingly, this place has great milkshakes!! Tastes like homemade ice cream :)
Not to mention their sandwiches!

Which wich is great for those days when you don't know what you want to eat for lunch! It's quick like fast food, but healthier! It can be a bit confusing for first timers, but after following these few simple steps, you'll become a which wich pro in no time. 

1. Pick a bag that has your favorite meat/type of sandwich you want 
2. Customize it by checking what ingredients you want in your sandwich! I love that which wich gives you endless options from a bunch of spreads to house chips that you can add in your sandwich. Check to your heart's desires! 
3. Pay for your food by handing the bag to the cashier 
4. Wait for your sandwich to be mad. This is the hardest step. 
5. Hear your name either get accurately pronounced or butchered.  
6. Pick up your sandwich and enjoy your creation!

I like this place. The customer service is great. Only issue here is that there aren't enough people making the sandwiches which makes the line a bit long during lunch time. I won't mark them a star off for that because I like how they do service. The bathrooms are clean from my experience, as well as the rest of the restaurant. They many the sandwiches neatly so there's not a mess when I open my package custom made sandwich.
Uht7oZdAGXfSF5yjO9bRew: sad to see this source of southern comfort has shut it's doors. amazing cornbread. hands down, the best fried catfish in town. and it was the only 'meat & three' in town i know of that did not season their collard greens with ham.

Decided to check this place out after reading the reviews, and I'm glad I did. I decided on the fried chicken with mac and cheese and apples. Everything was delicious, including the sweet tea! 
The mac and cheese was so creamy and full of flavor. Definitely the best I've had in awhile. I am looking forward to my next trip there.

Oh no!

Sad to say, but one of the friendliest meat-and-three restaurants in town is closing.

Stopped in today for lunch - fried chicken, greens, baked apples and that marvelous cornbread - and was stunned to see a sign at the register saying that tomorrow (January 31, 2013) would be the restaurant's last day.

I think it's cool that Nashville is getting lots of fancy new high-end restaurants and all, but I hope Yelpers will give more support to places like At the Table that capture the Southern Soul of Nashville.

The owner is a real nice guy. I wish him luck in his next venture and hope we'll see him open another restaurant around town someday...
aabNkVnLbxNoLMpTUlZpUw: I tried out Kranky's during their soft opening week . So many things to choose from on the menu ! The menu is displayed on a screen in front of the register . Our food was brought out pretty quickly . I went with the catfish sandwich and my friend other the catfish strips and fried corn . So much flavor ! Everything was delicious ! There's kranky mild sauce and hot on every table .... try it on everything ! The soft drinks and tea are self serve ( get the fruit tea !) They have a bar area as well ...next time I'll have to try out the sangria ! I had a sweet tooth so I could resist ordering the peach cobbler . It was the best I've ever had ! There's a lot of fresh and healthy options as well . Can't wait to try the black bean & quinoa burger !!
Great Vibes. I just flew in from New York City and was in search of a place with good southern food and cool vibes. The Catfish was good,the prices are goo

I have been on the hunt for a good fish sandwich...it's here. Listen, dawg...it's here! 
Everything was bomb. Super chill atmosphere, very clean and inviting. Prices were great. 
But back to the catfish sandwich. The fries were even great! Generous size filets, yes plural- you get 2. 1 could easily make a sandwich but wth go crazy lol fried to perfection! Crispy and golden. The fish was flaky and firm. Seriously just go, eat the damn sandwich and then call and thank me. 
The tea is bomb too.
You're welcome.

There was also a quesadilla, buffalo chicken bowl and krankin salad in the mix...all that shit was  too lol
ClhLoRx8LCG2C4ByJSMA6w: Faboulous meal. Artfully decorated. Chief will personalize any dish. Waitress are very kind. Also, great environment.

Nashville needed this alternative to the Italian restaurants.  Intimate, quiet, and homey feel begins the experience, and then the food comes. The food is definitely the star here. Not a big menu, in fact not a lot of choices, but what you find will be excellent in all ways. Not huge portions but very tasty stuff come out of this kitchen.  Staff was great, friendly and present enough without being a distraction.  Nice wine list, and overall it's probably a little more than the average place in price, but a worthy experience awaits you there.

We had dinner here while visiting Nashville and this was by far one of the best meals I've had. We did the chef menu 5 course sampler. 
It started with a perfectly cooked seasoned meatball in the most amazing marinara sauce. Second course was a perfectly paired salad with fresh cheeses and olive oil that tasted like butter. 
Our pasta course was gnocchi and the risotto that is made in a giant Parmesan wheel table side by the chef. It was absolutely amazing! Our main course was the filet, cooked to perfection. Dessert was tiramisu and it was spectacular. If you want an awesome meal, treat yourself. Complimentary valet and awesome ambiance (old restored house). We will be dreaming of this meal for years to come.
OHhnQl0JCH6ApyivewNOcA:
I LOVE this place  . . . for Bellevue.

The crunchy , spicy tuna roll is one of my favorite things in the world! For $10.00 you get a spicy tuna roll covered with tempora and white sauce ( not exactly healthy but, naughty good).

The hibachi is dare I say it , onpar if not better than Kobe's. The prices are about the same but , the vegetables , and rice are much better. 

The house salad is sooooo good. Different from other sushi/hibachi places but , not so much so that it's all rednecked out with ranch dressing.

Plus , if you're a parent and want adult food on family night out the place is very kid friendly.

Real food in Bellevue?  Surely you jest?  Alas, it's true.  You no longer need to head to West End or Green Hills for some non-Applebees fare.  Located amidst a plethora of strip mall nail salons, Supercut like salons and dry cleaners Kyoto is an oasis in our midst.  
The food is great and, as an added perk, prepared in a way that will entertain the kids.  We have yet to take on the sushi, but the temppanyaki (hibachi grill) is our favorite.  I have even gotten vaguely adept at catching shrimp in my mouth in the last six months (I won't be adding it to my resume, but you know....).  
Another perk is the convenient local right next to Cold Stone Creamery so you can you birthday cake ice cream with brownie mix in that the Japanese grill dudes have yet to master.
This is my favorite sushi place in town. Their specialty rolls are amazing and the service is quick and friendly. We did hibachi for the first time for my birthday and it was equally as good.
N5tS8v0Yz4mmbFHMsxpr3g:
I love JJ's, and order frequently from my office in Berry Hill. Normally it is amazing, but today I ordered and it was NOT amazing (don't worry, there is a happy ending). 
My sandwich took almost 30 minutes to be delivered, and it was just a mess. Messy, soggy, no extra of what I ordered. Just not good. I called JJ's to let them know and to get a refund (or another sandwich, I just wanted to eat!). I don't know who I talked with, but the person who answered was nice and apologetic, offered to make and deliver another sandwich asap, and I agreed. 

It was delivered freaky fast! and was perfect. He even included a free cookie, which was delicious. The food here is always good, but wanted to positively review because of the awesome customer service.
This jimmy is very clean and almost as quick as the commercials claim. I eat here once a week at least bc I work nearby.
Excited Jimmy John's is in our hood. Ordered delivery and it was only 15 minutes til I was eating.  Got the order correct with the customizations and everything tasted fresh.
HPbl1HItJKF7et4UxKXj_Q: I walked through the arcade alley today and finally found a quick Indian lunch restaurant.  Dosa Hut's primary location is in South Nashville, and this is their express one.  They offer fast cafeteria style lunch offerings where they scoop it as your order.  I ordered the meat $10 lunch which had chicken curry, rice, chickpeas, cauliflower, a little desert, and naan bread.  I'm no expert on Indian food, but it was pretty decent.  This is a great addition to the diversity of The Arcade.

I walked through the arcade alley today and finally found a quick Indian lunch restaurant.  Dosa Hut's primary location is in South Nashville, and this is their express one.  They offer fast cafeteria style lunch offerings where they scoop it as your order.  I ordered the two mead $8 lunch which had two types of chicken curry, rice, and naan bread.  I'm no expert on Indian food, but it was decent.  A lot of rice.  I would have liked a third texture.  I think you can add a vegetable for a $1 which I should have.  It was probably a 3 star place, but I'll give them 4 as it's the only Indian option in the area and I'm no expert on Indian cuisine other than I know it generally pleases my pallet.

The restaurant is easy to miss with only a little poster above the door as a sign.  The interior is quite plain & small, best suited for take out or small informal groups.  The food is spectacular. Maybe I'm biased on Indian food, but the quality really is superior.  The chicken was tender enough to be broken with the flimsy plastic fork & the curry was not to spicy yet the lingering heat lasted for hours.  The naan was buttered, warm & fresh. The fried cauliflower is so much fun to eat! Not only is it delicious, it's bright red and easy to forgive myself for indulging since it's healthy, right? They also gave me a little dessert sample similar to a donut hole in corn syrup. 
This 1 meal provided me with 3 days worth of lunch! Great deal!
1aR_kqb9d8jmRWCwXqoJJQ: Nashville Hot chicken just west of the airport. In a little strip mall above the road. The hot dry run builds heat as you eat. Slow burn fire. We shared a wing platter. Excellent! Large temp hot and spice hot wings. The fries are sprinkled with a mild dry rub and are very good. Nice spot for takeout or eat in.
Super nice service! Chicken was tasty and tender. I'm glad that I chose to have my first fried chicken experience here at this restaurant.
Everything is cooked fresh and was excellent. The hot was really kicked up. I am looking forward to returning.
uUoIUH5PKo22qxRXxZ_Ttg: Good little hole in the wall. Margaritas leave a little to be desired, which is surprising. But other than that, the service is awesome and food it great. Used to only eat at the Whites Creek Pike Mexican place, but got sick of their incredibly rude wait staff and clearly 6 month old frozen tamales. This place is a step above.
Wow the food is delicious!  Don't judge this book by  its cover.  This is a great little family restaurant. Full bar. Eager servers. The Fajitas are awesome.
This is a great mexican place i hope more folks visit and keep it open. Super friendly, clean, some authentic stuff esp. For the location
BCxOwQBXK7bXC1cMsEHv9A:
Waffle house is franchised so they are not all equal.  This one is a good one - fast, tasty, cheap, and the staff is helpful and friendly.   Always busy but Ive never waited more that a few minutes for a seat.    You can triple your hash browns for like 50 cents and top them with half a dozen items like mushrooms, onions, jalapeno peppers, tomatoes, ham, chili (or get it on the side), etc, then take the leftover hash browns home to top with your own egg the next morning for a lazy 2nd breakfast.
Give them time to wipe the table/seats before you sit down.  Seems clean but i once sent a coffee cup back that had something on it.  Coffee (even the decaf) tastes good - bottomless.   
I hear they do a valentines day candlelight dinner here which sounds fun/funny.

After 5+ years of reading yelp reviews on the west coast, east coast, and the south, one thing becomes apparent to me: people review food with way too much emotion. I notice that most 1 star reviews receive 1 star because of ONE poor experience with ONE silly server or employee. Does an entire business deserve 1 out of 5 stars due to ONE idiot who'd probably be employed there only temporarily?

That's why it's nearly impossible to find actual 1 star reviews on Yelp based on the food alone. If the food or quality-to-cost ratio is poor, the establishment would not survive more than a year or two. The VAST majority of 1 star reviews on Yelp consist entirely of emotionally-charged opinions reflecting ONE awful experience with ONE employee who will probably be long gone from the establishment within 2 months.

To me, Waffle House deserves 3 or 4 out of 5 stars because it is what it is: easy to eat, simple breakfast food available 24/7. If you can't live with that, try finding a better affordable place to eat at 4am in Nashville. ;-) 

But maybe I'm wrong. Maybe it's totally justifiable to give 1 star reviews based on one awful experience that has nothing to do with the food. But I leave that debate to the higher beings at Yelp to sort out. :-D
Best Waffle House in all of Nashville!  Don't waste any time with the other two, just come to this one for great service and great Waffle House food.
XgmqH4xTI7MNyANg_AFjOw: This place is kind of a hole in the wall but the food is great! I had fried pork chops with white beans and greens. Both pork chops were moist and fried to perfection. My sides were flavorful. The portions were generous, especially considering the meal was under $10. There's not much room if you want to sit and eat and the location is kind of interesting, but I will be returning to this place next time I return to Tennessee. By far one of the best meals of my vacation. I would give this place 5 stars except for location and the fact that food isn't made to order, but served cafeteria style from warming trays. But, the meal that I had that day was definitely a 5 out of 5! Almost forgot... On my way out, I purchased a piece of lemon cake to eat later. That cake didn't last 5 minutes into my drive. Delicious!
Best food in Tennessee! Pork chops are amazing!!!! Take out only! Junky building, excellent food!

One of the best meat & 3 veggies restaurant in Nashville. Owner/Staff personal & cordial. Food delicious! 
I have never had a food here I didn't enjoy.
Try it out!
pCUMtXfRIGeDtp3S5jjshQ:
WARNING: The following review is extremely biased. Parental guidance is advised.

I'm pretty sure the name White Castle needs no introduction, so I'll get right down to it: I have never had a taste of White Castle. Not really. Yeah they do sell the same delicacy in supermarket frozen food section, but when it's made fresh right in front of you on that grill skillet it's just not the same at all. The meat still retain a lot of its juice (or grease however you want to call it.) The diced unions truly as described in the movie "little flavor crystal that just burst in your mouth." Be warned, if you are the kind that does not like your bun mushy, you might be a little disappointed, because those little slider roll/bun just soak up all the meat juice, and doubly so if you ordered the double cheeseburger combo.

So glad I walked in as I did, and not dilly dolly outside trying to take the pictures or checking in, because they locked the door behind me as soon as I walked in. Apparently dine-in stopped at 2:30 AM. There are certainly some colorful population coming through that front door in late hours of the day. Good food and great entertainment, what else can I possibly ask for?

This is a White Castle. Greasy floors welcome you as you drunkenly stumble into the White Castle. The old man Greg behind the register knows you're drunk. Greg knows you're going to stare at that delicious menu for awhile and be unsure about what to order. Greg will recommend a meal deal #7. 10 sliders, 2 fries, 2 drinks. You'll eat the outta those burgers. Greg is the man.
Don't know how they make their little burgers soooo yummy, but they are.   I can eat a dozen easily.
K5AjxXyHyd0SCiX4V0kugA:
This review is not a review of the bookstore part of Davis Kidd but of the cafe part on the bottom floor.

The Bronte Bistro is a little bit pricey considering the portion sizes but it does have delicious food. Me and a friend had lunch there a few weeks ago and the atmosphere is great.

It feels sort of like an international airport in France or the Netherlands. Which feels cozy and warm. Not to mention your surrounded by tons of books to make you feel smart.

Well the food is delicous. They have a lot of interesting choices and a lot of comfort foods.

The service was a little bit to be desired. The waitress only came by once or twice and that was when she took our orders and brought our food.

I would go back again no matter how much of a sniveling hipster it makes me look like.

I love coming to Davis-Kidd; it's a Nashville staple and a great bookstore. While some are so tiny you feel like you're being eaten by bookshelves, Davis-Kidd is a warm environment. It's open and packed full of books at the same time. It's attached to the Mall at Green Hill but if you don't fancy going into the mall, this isn't a problem. They have an outdoor entrance so you never have to step foot inside anything but the bookstore. 

It's a two level bookstore with a small café I've yet to try on the bottom floor. The last time we visited they had a quartet playing a mixture of Christmas and regular music, a rendition of 'Dust in the Wind' that could actually tear you up. It was just a beautiful thing, and I love that Davis Kidd cares enough about the environment they create to have musicians in with such talent.

Book wise, the selection is huge, and the prices are no better and no worse than Borders or Barnes and Noble.   

I love it.

DK is a Nashville neighborhood portal. With live local music in the downstairs and an entire section perfect for nontacky souvenir gifts, that makes DK a great place to hang. Selection of everything book and gift oriented competes easily with the national Big Boxers.
C9k02Ejz4RXwRRaKNKAYCA: SOOOO GOOD! I got Lord of the Dough and lined it with Nutella. The girls were super nice too!

Only reason I'm giving 4/5 stars is because for the price, your portion is a little on the smaller side. This was a mini size for $5.25, and a regular cone is $6. Friends of mine got the regular and it wasn't too much bigger than this one. However the flavor The is delicious and the toppings are awesome. This is "chocolate abduction." I will probably return but I will just make sure not to go when I'm super hungry

Top-quality soft serve. A "mini" version as big as a beer can (and priced about the same as the Sierra Nevada at Radio Cafe tonight). Honestly, though, the toppings do a disservice to the underlying ice cream. Not that they aren't good, as good as a packaged marshmallow from the supermarket or name-brand chocolate syrup is going to be. They're ok. But they're not blended in to the ice cream, and they are not liquified (the marshmallows, anyway) on top of it. So what you get is ice cream. And marshmallows. And some crumbled graham crackers (and not a lot of any of these). It's called The Campfire. But if you're going for the S'mores thing, a S'more is a beautiful, unified mess. Frisson's is not. But still excellent ice cream. And that's the important thing in the end.
BPWr8gxJgoWxSuX-_M98gg: I went in today for some take out home cooking. I had roast, mashed potatoes and green beans, it was fabulous. They give gracious portions and everyone was super nice. I'll definitely be a repeat customer, very excited to have home cooking in our area.
Totally excellent food/service... new restaurant.... live music Thursday night and DJ Friday and Saturday.......... very reasonably priced and awesome salad bar!!!!  Open for lunch also!!!!!!
Totally excellent food/service... new restaurant.... live music Thursday night and DJ Friday and Saturday.......... very reasonably priced and awesome salad bar!!!!  Open for lunch also!!!!!!
NZzXaHHv-iua-6v-j0KopQ: This was my first time ordering Jet's -- I admittedly don't have extremely high standards when it comes to pizza, because...pizza is great. But I was very satisfied with the food. I tried some of a Jet's Boat (basically a calzone) and a slice of the 4-corner grilled chicken pizza. Both were delicious. And the staff was friendly and efficient. If you're a pizza connoisseur, you may not be overly impressed. But me? I can't wait to go back to try some other offerings on their surprisingly large and diverse menu.

Love Jets I moved to Nashville three years ago and struggled finding a GREAT PIZZA PLACE!!! My favorite is the Buffalo chicken ranch pizza! Sometimes service can be crappy, but I usually order delivery so it tolerable.

I have to say being from Chicago and living in Nashville it's hard to top that. However, I must say that I've DEF tried several pizza spots more than 10 and Jets being a chain is top of the list. I always get the square and its consistently good. I'm never disappointed I never have a complaint. Of all the pizza places in town Jets will deliver and I like the online feature and they remember my orders. Thanks Jets!
LOs7G4gEbtYlwkVrdnMQ4w: Food was good. The wait was longer than usual because they were super busy. The waitress was friendly. Only problem is there was a heavy set white guy cooking and kept wiping his eyes and face. He also started screaming at a waitress for asking him to do his job and fix the food. He was conducted himself very poorly.

Stephanie was absolutely awesome. Great service, great to talk to, no nonsense and even funny. Just a real person genuinely there to help you. I ordered an arnold palmer and she kept it filled to the brim. She even prepared one to-go when she was getting me a to-go box for my food. I didn't even ask. Food was the same quality I look foward to, but the prices were cheaper than some in Kansas and Illinois!

Came here with my mom on a Sunday morning around 10 for breakfast. It was super busy, but we were able to be seated within a few minutes. We got a booth in the corner, which was nice. 
Our server was Lea, and she was awesome! She was very friendly and she was joking with us, which I really appreciated. My mom mentioned that it was my first time at a Waffle House, so she recommended one of the breakfast plates that would allow me to try everything. I ended up getting a blueberry waffle and me and my mom shared an order of hash browns. Lea stopped over to ask if we wanted more coffee a couple times, and checked in just the right amount. 
Our food came out pretty quickly, and it was pretty good. My only complaint is that the blueberries in the blueberry waffle aren't real, and because there are so many in the middle of the waffle, I didn't eat about a quarter of it. They're the kind of blueberries you get in frozen Eggo waffles. Like blueberry-flavored styrofoam. I sort of wish I had gotten a different flavor. 
The hash browns were good (I did add some extra salt, though I like my hash browns salty). They had good crunch on the outside and they weren't limp on the inside! 
It took a few minutes for us to pay because everyone was busy, but it wasn't an absurd amount of time and they were busy, I don't hold that against them. 
Overall, really great place! If I lived out here, I would definitely come back often!
nSRJgIiuRIT2wctA56xxBA: I am a sucker for barbecue, and Whitt's has very good barbecue.  I got the pulled pork platter.  The barbecue was definitely the highlight of the meal.  The beans were OK, but not memorable.  The slaw was interesting, but it was swimming in way too much liquid that distracted from the flavor.

This place is amazing! The rib plate with slaw, and baked beans was awesome!  Stop by on Wednesdays for their pork sandwiches! 

Also had my first cup of sweet tea! Sooooo good! We are missing out on Arizona. Lastly, the young lady, a true southern belle, helped me the ordering with her stunning bright blue eyes, I could get lost forever....oh wait back to whitts...I recommend highly for an affordable-delicious southern bbq meal....Dr. Larry, I hope our paths cross again! Oh, you too you beautiful Southern Belle!

Fast food style order and sit in to eat. Food was very tasty and service was super friendly!! We are from California and had our hearts set on some good BBQ while in Nashville on vacation. This was our first stop and we were VERY pleased!! The pork sandwich, cornbread, and baked beans were all delicious!
vPLvCKDaCxHEIXScP5xeOg: Great people, food and atmosphere. The staff is helpful and easy going. The food is incredible. We had the chicken waffles, pulled pork sandwich and Mac n cheese and fries sides. And the cafe has a laid back vibe to it and good music.

Didn't know what to expect with it being next to the airport. The chicken is chicken breast chunks which are crispy, juicy and overall good. Fries were delicious. Waffles were OK. Overall it was clean and if your are into railroads there is train memorabilia everywhere.

Had some of the best brisket I've had in a long time. I had a flight out of Nashville and wanted to find some good food that was close and this was the perfect find. Food came out fast and was so good.
dCmR7G3teIJ-yhjdV6Hi6A:
If you compare this humble BBQ shack to most BBQ establishments throughout Nashville, it would probably get about 4 solid stars. But keeping the review relative to the fact that it's a tiny shack with a pretty inefficient drive through (lol), it easily deserves 5 stars.

The prices are ridiculously low, but all the meats are cooked on an open flame in an outdoor charcoal grill. Needless to say, the food is great. I suggest calling in ahead and placing your order. Otherwise you'll have to use its odd drive through system. Most drive thrus at restaurants work well because fast food is relatively FAST. Given that most of the items here take about 15-20 minutes to cook upon receiving your order, the drive thru here ends up being a game of shuffling and rearranging the cars.

I did not mind waiting though. The scent of burning charcoal while waiting for my food brought back memories of weekly bonfires at the beach in California.

The fried okra and french fries could have been crunchier, but both were well seasoned. The grilled chicken was flavorful and juicy ($0.99 per leg quarter!). You'll need to eat two portions to be full though.

I will be back, especially since it's so close to the hospital.

$1.00 leg quarters. The BBQ and food is a authentic as it gets. From fried fish sandwiches, wings, ribs, pork chop sandwiches, gizzards,smokes sausage, polish sausage, brisket, spaghetti etc. It was alll soooo good. Soul food!

Absolutely the best fried pies I have ever had.  The apple is incredible.
The chicken's pretty good too. I think this place could be huge in a high traffic area like 12th South or 5 Points.  A taco-truck setup would also be so fun.
6BIeFcgs6aT_uyN5gfVbeA: We experienced a nice atmosphere and great service. The food was good too. I had the Harvest Jubilee Salad w/grilled chicken which was very good. We returned later (after another walk) for the Peanut Butter Dark Chocolate Bread Pudding which is VERY delicious (this coming from a new orleans girl). Coffee was very good too. We plan on returning for dinner on a friday night.

I enjoyed my visit to Cheekwood last May very much. I especially liked Pineapple Room for good Southern food. The shrimp and grits were great. I had never had them before. The service and ambiance were very nice. I was there by myself and was not made to feel out of place.

Reservations recommended!

We couldn't get near this place on our last Cheekwood visit.  When we decided to visit today I called on our way and lucked out!  We got a reservation for our party of four for 11am when they opened.  

The restaurant overlooks the West gardens.  The beautiful view along with the tables dressed in white tablecloths give the feel of picnic on the patio.  The hostess and our waitress were very friendly!  We had great service!

There were many great options on the menu but I simply had to try the chicken salad sandwich.  I love good chicken salad.  This one did not disappoint.  It was delicious.  I ordered it with sweet potato fries as my side.  It was the most lovely lunch and a perfect start to our explorations.
OTHWY-3vXgBzzZVivyfpqw: Great yummy pizza! I went here with my 7 year old friend who like most kids her age are very particular and she keeps asking to go back because she really loved her slice of cheese pizza. I enjoyed my slice as well. Would definitely go back. It's in the Opry Mills mall in Nashville. A very large mall but lots of fun.

I'm surprised this has such low reviews. It's food court food, people, don't expect fancy dining. Honestly, we were pleased with the overall value of the food. I got a bowl of ziti and my partner got the pizza meal with three garlic knots and upgraded to a larger beverage for thirty cents so that we could split the garlic knots and the beverage. So to feed both of us was only $13. The employees were all helpful and friendly, and they made sure to stick our selections in the warmer under the counter for a couple minutes so it would taste fresher. And we were given extra sauce for dipping. The cashier was smiling and pleasant. Being a Monday we were easily able to find a clean table to eat at.

Friendly workers, the cashier had great customer service. I gave him my USAA card to pay and he asked if I was military. He could've let it slide, i was unaware they do military discounts. Respectful and professional guy. Food was good. Promote the cashier! He deserves it.
UqFuKIYcXrXUoWenCughyQ:
This is my favorite Cheeseburger Charley's location. The people behind the counter always seem to be especially friendly.  It's a great place to go if you're grabbing lunch alone because there's a long bar lined with stools instead of just the booths like some other locations.

The thing I like best about this change is the topping bar.  Not only does it mean having more options than most places, but it means I get to be anal.  If I want to sift through the onions for a bit because I only like the small intact rings I can.  Somehow I just don't think Burger King would be down with that request.  

Cheesburger Charley's is also a great vegetarian lunch spot because they offer two different kinds of veggie patties.  There's even a salmon burger for us pescas, yum!

This is an easy way to get a meat salad.  Just order a hamburger or other burger of your choosing and then go to the toppings bar and have your fill of lettuce, tomato, peppers, onions, olives, and pickles.  

I get a lemon wedge and squeeze it over the concoction and voila!  You have a meat salad that taste good and fills you up for lunch.

Cheeseburger Charley's is one of the reasons I was born, and if it weren't artery clogging I would eat there all the time. I usually go for their  lbs cheeseburger (I could eat the  1/2 lbs but I just shouldn't) fries and one of their ridiculously delicious shakes. I have also tried their Beefalo and Salmon burgers and both were really really tasty. With all the burger fixings, my burger is always made exactly how I like it because besides the beef and bun you do in fact mostly make it yourself. The employees are always super friendly and it's a good place to sit on a counter and watch a game between classes or on a weekend.
zTu1c-A-7l3wQ5iKltSbsg:
Yum and so close to my house!!!  I am loving this one better than the Antioch one....people seem way nicer and the food is fresher.  Maybe because it's new, I don't know, but I love it!  I also do really love the Antioch one, but maybe I'm partial because this one is so close to me and on my way home from work.
I always get the fingers with Zax sauce, crinkle fries and we also get a Zensational Salad which is delicious.

I have never had Zaxby's before and I knew this location just opened across from where I regularly bowl so I decided to try it. Being in the restaurant business myself and knowing the challenges of training and staffing I could tell this location is dealing with this. My experience though was nothing short of perfect though. Gentlemen working front counter was kind and had his hands full with several guests while the manager was in the back cooking. He handled every guest with respect and delivered great guest service even helping me make a menu choice. 

What I loved most. They didn't sacrifice food quality for speed. The food was amazing!!!

Basically a fast food restaurant I saw a employee Spray a fryer a few weeks ago wasn't impressed but the food was good and the staff are nice the only person peave I have besides using the spray Is their hours of business wish they would follow the hours listed on their door
CqHbFM-8FuJjkrSxXyqyjw: Delicious chicken, especially the grilled chicken.  More like a flavorful rotisserie chicken.  Very juicy and nice and hot when it came to the table.  Had to wait a few minutes for the food, but when it comes to the table nice and hot, I don't mind.  This is my new favorite chicken place!

Ate here tonight after leaving the airport. I got the 9 piece chicken bites/boneless wings with mashed potatoes and also a side of plantains. I got the Campero sauce and also some ranch. I really liked everything on my plate as well as the sauces. They have horchata that my bf really liked but I didn't try it. I'm rarely in this part of Nashville but I would eat here again.

Awesomeness place great for the family 
The food is good and plentiful they deliver the 
Food to your table as well with a smile they have 
Ice cream and its fully air conditioning they also
Clean your table afterwards
1f4iuPT4B2BHm_GvjYjQCQ: Talk about feeling like a family dine in restaurant! Love this place! I remember coming here as a child with my father. Ever since my father passed away I come here and sit in the same spot my father and I use to sit in. It's nice to remember good old times. The staff is very friendly and great customer service!

Best Captain D's in Nashville 
Fresh fish 
Clean atmosphere 
Advanced coke machine option 
Fast service 
What more could you want !!

I never thought I would be reviewing a Captain D's, but the time has come where I am forced to comment on my experiences here. This Captain D's is amazing. Do I eat it every day? No. It doesn't usually sit right. But when you are craving the D, you get it. I'm always skeptical about Captain D's because it is what it is: fast food seafood. It even sounds scary. The location of this one also had me worried because it isn't in the best area of town (I live out here, too...). I have been to this location twice now and both experiences have been gold. They are always on top of their game. They're quick. They're efficient. They're NICE. They don't act like they would rather be anywhere else than work. The employees at this location seem to be genuinely content, so kudos to management for that. It really does go a long way. The first time I ate here, they took longer than usual in the drive-thru because of what I ordered (salmon...trust me, it's good). They gave us a free slice of pecan pie to make up for the wait. We weren't upset AT ALL about waiting, which made it even better that they took the initiative to not only give us free pie, but also genuinely apologize AND make sure that we had everything we needed. Some restaurants don't even go that far. The most recent trip was a dine-in trip. I was feeling fancy. They served our food on "real" plastic plates. Not disposable. And real silverware. Seriously?! Awesome! We got a great meal for under $20 and we didn't feel like we were eating at a fast food place. 

I don't always eat Captain D's...but when I do, I go to this one. Give it a chance.
5tA64kGSrJIhRc6Ek5NeLg:
We met some friends in Nashville and headed here for dinner on 3/6/15.  The only snafu was that the girl at the front counter gave us a pager and didn't record it so others that came in after us got seated before us.  There were a lot of open tables so when we inquired, she sat us right away.  

Our server was awesome- his name was Aleck according to our bill.  

We were there during happy hour which means you order a beer, margarita, wine and get one free- what???  AWESOME!!!!  This just became my favorite Chilis dining experience to date!  

Anyway, I ordered the Margarita Chicken Bowl which has become my favorite dish there and a side of corn tortillas w/ extra lime wedges and salsa.  It was good but the corn tortillas were dry and brittle.  

My boyfriend said his Sweet and Smokey Burger was rare which he wasn't a fan of but besides that he said it was good.  

I can't speak for the others in our party as far as how they liked there food b/c we didn't discuss it.  They ordered the Chicken Caesar Salad and the Southwest Pair Two that came w/ blk beans.  

All in all, a nice dining experience and I would return if in Nashville at the mall.

I have been here a few times, but on Tuesday July 29th, My daughter Noel and I went here before our movie.  It was a little after 11, they had just opened and it wasn't that busy yet. We were greeted and seated immediately which is always a big plus in my book. 

Our server arrived immediately and introduced herself. She took our drink order, I ordered soda and Noel ordered strawberry lemonade. After we looked over the menu, Noel went with a 6 oz medium rare steak with mashed potatoes and broccoli...and I went with the southwestern pick two..with chicken tostado and green chili enchilada with rice and black beans with corn salsa. It was really really good.

AMAZING! The hostess was so friendly, the server was excellent and the manager was such a team player he brought out our food. The manager took time to come back out and let us praise his staff while he was helping out. Thank you to Jackie, our server,  for having the best customer service we have had in a long time. If you go, be sure to ask for her!
a1fxUcvelysCLt64nKph7w: Smokey yet satisfying. Awesome staff and tourist free! Best patio in the city for a cheap beer and sunset.

If you ever want to try a laid back dive bar, this is the place. It is easy to find since it is just south of Downtown. It is near Hermitage Cafe and Crema. After my first visit, this place was like cheers. They know my name and my drink of choice without me even asking. The drinks are cheap yet strong. Their ritas are something to write home about. There isn't a Pre-mixed cocktail here.... Oh no, totally fresh. 

They have a very limited food menu, but the pizza is very good. Especially on a long drinking day. The best part is the off the menu goodness. On Sunday's, they have free home cooked food. It can range from chili, chicken dinners, etc. They also have 2-4-1 beer on Sunday's too.  They also have themed nights for food. Wednesday is steak night and Thursday is pork chop night. 

Overall it is a great low key chilled spot where people are not constantly asking "what do you do?", but it also isn't the spot where you meet your future spouse either.

Love it.  We just moved here from Orlando and are trying to replace our previous watering hole, and found this little dive.  Great vibe. Bartender says she's been there for 12 years and that's a good sign. Pizza was on point too
2fsv9-fFFRjt7yY_kilrEg: My wife and I both got the gyro mediterranean salad.  It was great.  Enjoyed the service and even got to catch some soccer on the TV.  Will definitely be back.

Cheap, Authentic and homely.

There certainly isn't much of an atmosphere and you notice it in the price.  However, I feel at home with Al-Jazeera blaring over Mediterranean men arguing over how to cook my chicken and wrap my grape leaves.

Check out their home-made Tzatziki sauce.....Tis the Bomb.
This is my favorite gyro place in town! Their fries are AMAZING as well. I would make an excuse to go to that part of town just to be able to stop by for lunch. The owners/workers are really nice.
Tz4I_iLZz4ex7injzr0Xhw:
Closed!   :(

I miss their food.  Was one of the last "real" Chinese places that hadn't succumbed to the buffet or strip mall fast food Chinese place.  They had the best Shrimp with Lobster Sauce in town!  All good things must come to an end, sadly.
Very very good -- although this is the ultimate "don't judge a book by its cover" situation. Kind of a scary strip mall, and kind of tacky (way beyond "campy here) decor.  But the food is awesome.

We've been coming to this location about 20 years.  Before that they were in Brentwood.  So they have been around a long time and that says something to me and should to you.

When compared to other Chinese restaurants, there is no comparison.  This is about the only 'white tablecloth' Chinese restaurant I know.  The food here has flavor and it doesn't all taste alike.  We've eaten so many things off the menu that I think we can safely recommend everything. 

These days we most often get take out but if you chose to dine in, we've always found it a quiet respite from all the noisy restaurants of modern times.  It's dark and sort of intimate.  Service is always good the place is always clean with no off odors.  Portion sizes are nice.  We usually have our own 'buffet' when we get home and can usually make at least two meals off each entree.
QRzaTTTE7QrqjW2GOQIrtA: Visiting from California where vegan options are plentiful and this place can hold it's own with the best of them! Not only is the food incredible but the hospitality even better! Highly recommend!!

Cute small building with ample outdoor seating. The food was great and the staff was even better! Quick service and a steady stream of patrons. We ordered the not bacon cheese burgers and my 4y/o ate the vegan Mac and cheese. The burgers come with a side of home fries that were amazing!

This place is simply wonderful. So charming, makes you feel right at home. The staff is so kind that you feel like you're coming home to visit momma. The food tastes fantastic and is lacking in NOTHING. I'd give this place a million stars if I could; it truly won my heart (and my stomach)
RWOtKuaguRqoZJ5JxD6EFQ: Always a great experience at Calhoun's, whether we are eating in or taking out, they are always consistent.  The vibe inside is very chill.  I love to grab a dirty martini that comes with an extra sidecar chilled on ice!  My 2 year old absolutely loved the side of fries that were served in a classic car, too cool!

Hands down one of the best ribs place I've eaten thus far in Nashville.  The meat just slides right off the bone...deeelicious.
The portions were more than enough especially with all the sides.  The place is slightly pricier than most rib places I ventured out to but it was well worth it.  
 I would highly recommend this place to anybody.
Great local spot for plain old American food. Try instead of applebee's or o'charlies. Get the spinach Maria side...SO good. Awesome patio with room to move.
hoqITwtnCoz8x_2CcMylJg:
...I used to go out of my way, over to the airport McDonald's to get breakfast from the drive thru. 
For a year or two. 
This location used to be that bad. 
It had gotten so bad it was almost comical. 
I say almost comical since I did eventually give up. 
The laughs just weren't worth it anymore. 
It was guaranteed to experience a problem. 
I would just cross my fingers and hope for mercy. 
Honestly! 
Previous problems had been incomplete and/or wrong orders. 
Long waits 
But worst of all to me was it seemed they used to try and save time by not toasting the English muffin for the Egg McMuffin. 
Yuck! :p 

A few months ago I was feeling super lazy and felt like taking a gamble. 
And I'm glad I did! :) 
I returned to my McDonalds location and 
I'm happy to report I haven't had a single problem in the time since I have returned :) 
Things have really changed. 
And changed for the better. 
Woo hoo! 

I also have to say Mark on the drive thru does a super job. 
And does it happily :) 

Thanks guys!

I haven't had too many problems with my orders from here, and for McDonald's that is pretty good! They do seem strict though maybe it's a corporate store? All that matters is that I get my order correct and hot and this place satisfies!

Always a good idea to stop for a peach slushie and a dip come  This McDonald's is fancy with a fireplace and conveniently placed near the grocery store.  Line goes quick and employees are nice. But I really liked my peach slushie on a hot day. Good looking out McD's.
i_KbABpfUzfVj0E7MVcjTQ: I had NEVER eaten at a food truck for various reasons. I was in Nashville for the CMA fan fest 2014.  We saw the line of trucks when it was lunch time. After looking at all the trucks I chose Dawg Daze. Their Greek Dawg had me at tzatziki sauce. ABSOLUTELY DELICIOUS. The feta cheese is wonderfully creamy.  Absolutely changed the way I feel about food trucks. My friend had a dawg with mango salsa--I think it was a Hawaiian Dawg. Said it was wonderful
Some of the best Dawgs ever. Hot and juicy with great flavor.

I've tried this truck a few times and everything I've had has been great! The Greek Dawg and the Reuben Dawg are my favorites. The hot dogs are grilled, the buns are soft, the ingredients are fresh. The fried pickles are great too!
DZ9lv-dRHHclsS5pEWSiTQ:
I'm assuming that this is the correct place for the Official YSB Event: Southern Skip Day Happy Hour. If so, this was a very well organized event. This is the one event I really wanted to be in for food wise so I RSVP'd early and was very happy when I received my confirmation. 

I went early with the gang but the doors opened pretty much on time at 3 p.m. So we headed in to get a good table close to the refreshments. Drink wise, we had some kind of iced tea and bourbon drink which is definitely good but not strong. Food wise, I was expecting some steaks bites and perhaps oysters but we got fried green tomatoes, deviled eggs, and mini biscuit steak sandwiches. Honestly, I've never seen any of these kinds of food before. Definitely never ever had a fried green tomato, never had a deviled egg that was so vinegary, and never had a steak sandwich with so little meat and a big bun.  No complaints because it was all free and all served with a smile. More than anything, I was happy to mingle with fellow Yelp elites from across the country.

As promised, the event ended promptly at 4 pm and we were ushered out of the room back to the main bar because another group was going to use the room. Really too bad that Yelp did not secure in larger room for this event. It would have been great to have a larger private room to mingle quietly rather than just stand around at the bar.  This event is where I gave out the last of the chocolate covered macadamia nuts that I brought from Hawaii to share.  Really sorry that I missed most but there were a lot of Yelpers from all over the country and only me from Hawaii. :'(
IjZjDGRVXFbT22Om7zJlwg: I really like jets pizza. It's a fast food pizza joint like any other but it's pizza is a little different than your typify Pizza Hut or dominos variety.

Awesome BBQ pizza, I'd probably say the best in Nashville I've had so far. Vegetarian pizza leaves something to be desired though- the veggies aren't dabbed of moisture first and it makes the crust semi-soggy, I think is what it is. The cheese sticks are pretty good but my simple palate prefers crazy bread from Little Caesars (blasphemy, I know, I know). Good for a quick weeknight dinner.

Being from the area, I'm a fan of "Detroit Style Pizza". This is probably why, whenever given the choice, Jet's is where I want to order from.

Be warned, if you don't like LOTS of Crust on your Pizza, you'll likely be disappointed by most of the offerings. 

Prices at Jet's are higher than other Pizza Delivery places, so that is also a potential downside. 

Additionally, I would say delivery from Jets, on average, is slower than other delivery places.

I still would order here before anywhere else for Pizza delivery.
H3G4upTI99ZKxtjLkDXz7Q: The best subway I have ever been too. And it is very convenient for me. Because I can shop at kroger and then stop in for lunch or dinner. Always fresh ingredients.
Always friendly, consistent quality made sandwiches! They know what "extra" means!! So glad to see the same people that used to work at the White Bridge rd location!
HVVbTMh58sIq1_uyX2lKsA: I've always had friendly service whenever I pick up an order from this location, and my food has always been ready in a prompt manner. The quality never wavers -- it's definitely my favorite pizza on the east side!

We've gotten carry out from here twice now and both times the pizza was hot and delicious. The phone service was great-our calls were answered right away and the employee was very courteous. I would definitely recommend this place if you're looking for tasty pizzas!

Updating my previous review!

I was contacted by a Jet's employee and sent credit to give this store another shot. So glad that I did! This experience was a COMPLETE turnaround from my last one. We placed our order ahead of time and it was already waiting for us when we got there. We ordered the same thing as last time, the food was hot and delicious. Honestly, no complaints about this second experience!
PEfiaqXn91eWC_IGahXdJw: Food was fresh and the customer service was outstanding.  Within the last couple of years they remodeled the dining room, but unfortunately the bathrooms are still horribly out of date.  Customer service (as I said above) was outstanding.  I forgot my coupon and they went out of their way to still honor it.  On top of that while eating they asked me how my food was.  Its the little things that make fast food worth while and this is a prime example of how it supposed to be done.

okay, had a craving for some 'greasy fish'. Going to a Captain D's is predictable and seeing the recent commercial on TV, I wanted him to 'Take Me'...
Wow! Was I so taken back by this location. The decor is so modern including the update booths. Very nice. Staff all professional, friendly and nicely matched and clean uniforms. 
The food was hot and served on plates, yes, plates. They had an option for 'real' silverware but we chose the plastic...wrapped. 
While dining in, I couldn't help but do the 'Take Me' sounds (like in commercial) but did more of the 'Sally and Harry' sounds, like Sally did. We had a good laugh while enjoying the food & music playing.
No reason not to take the whole family. 
Noticed a lot of locals coming and eating in too. 
Best to sum up as 'Bravo' to the management and especially the 'workers', who do a good job.
WSmLr421oStUdizBmBYPpw: Black bean burger very well seasoned. Sweet potato fries on point!
