The only reason I didn't give five stars is the appearance of the interior, which is a bit off-putting.  If management will replace the floor tiles, replace or cover the tables, get new chairs, and generally renew and decorate, it will look like the home of the great food it serves.  I have been a fan of the original Bobbie's on Charlotte for years.  I am vegetarian, and am happy that they will make any of their burger varieties with a black bean burger.  The veggie chili is great, as are the beer batter onion rings, sweet potato fries, and regular fried.  Their soft serve dipped ice cream and milkshakes are wonderful.  I am so glad there is a downtown location!

Good food, fast service, and a reasonable price. Can't ask for much more than that on your lunch break. Shakes are excellent as well. Tried the breakfast there a time or two as well, and even though it can take a bit longer, you get a good a portion for what you pay. Everyone is always friendly and it's become my default when I need to grab something for lunch.
QbPVaZLvIoFS-ODF8aVTkQ:
They were at the Nashville Folk Festival the other night and even with a limited menu of chicken and dumplins and pinto beans,fried cornbread this truck was putting out some awesome food!!! 
Better than my grandma made,sorry gramma,good thing she does not have a comp.
.I tried the chicken and dumplins and they were DELICIOUS!!! Then we had some of the homemade desserts,ooey gooey cake for me(chess pie on steroids!) and my husband had the peach cobbler(pa pa's favorite) I can see why,
We are going to follow them on twitter cause I can't wait to try their full menu which consists of atleast 4 diffrent meat entrees and 10 sides!!!!They also offer some delicious desserts that are homemade delights.
 They change the menu daily for a great variety of country"s finest fixins!!!
 You have got to try this food truck.Nashville deffinately has a winner here!

Food was good.  
Cooked with little salt but easy fix with a shake or two.
Fine people running this truck.
Well worth the $6.50 (cash) $7.00 (credit) for a food truck meal.
PFiKCDirTH6IKgz70nLbWw:
Panda Express is fast food chinese - but it hasn't disappointed.  My husband and I are from Los Angeles where there were a lot of these, so  we were so excited when Nashville got one!  It's similar to Pei Wei - but is clearly more "fast food."  

I love the Orange Chicken.  My husband always gets the Kung Pao Chicken.  The Golden Treasure Shrimp is great, too.  You can't really go wrong.  I also like the Veggie Egg Rolls.  

I recommend the two entree plate:  Pick Fried Rice, White Rice, Chow Mein, or Veggies.  I usually get half White Rice and half Chow Mein.  Then pick two items.... Yum!  They give you a fortune cookie too! :)

TIP:  You get more food if you order to-go....even if you eat there. :)

For fast Chinese, it's the best.
Their Beijing beef is my favorite dish but their shrimp dishes are great as well!

If you're in a hurry and need Chinese food to go, this is your best bet!

The online ordering process was easy and clear, and I knew exactly where to retrieve my order. The food tasted great; fresh and flavorful (but not enough peanuts in the kung pao chicken) and I got a big meal for the money I paid. The counter server greeted me cheerfully and she was very helpful with instructing me where to find napkins and forks.
-C_5RtXY4iIoztzIYVqMSQ: Food was good. I got my meal as take out and they were busy. They weren't very careful putting it in the box and closing my box.... ended up making a mess before I got back to my hotel to eat it. There were a bunch of kids in there for the TN Youth in Government conference and they were pretty rude to the kids (not deserved) .... but polite to me. Still good deal for some good food.

One of my favorite places to eat in Nashville!  I stopped by this restaurant when looking for a good meal downtown.  I ordered an appetizer sampler and a gyro platter.  Everything was fresh, flavorful and delicious.  You can really tell the owners take pride in the restaurant.  After I finished my meal, I decided to try the baklava.  The owner gave it to me for free which was so nice!  The prices are very reasonable too.  Give it a try, you will be glad you did!

I don't eat out without first consulting yelp but have never felt compelled to submit a review until now.  This place only has two reviews and deserves a lot more.  My wife and I are visiting from out of town and came upon the restaurant looking for the Capitol and we extremely happy we did.  We got a gyro sandwich, stuffed grape leaves, and a slice of baklava.  The sandwiches were absolutely amazing.  Soft, perfectly prepared lamb generously stuffed into a pita with lettuce, feta, and the rest.   Absolutely delicious and totally satisfying for only  $3.99.  I love a good deal and this was definitely that.  I liked but didn't love the grape leaves and baklava.  Both were good but nothing stood out.  Still, nothing can take away for such an incredible gyro sandwich at such a great price.  If you're looking for great Greek food at a equally great price, this is the place to go.
ldHR6I7Qui4zHsBQQ6FYIA: Excellent selection of affordable sandwiches! The service is great. The crew is friendly and always ready and alert to take your orders. Libby, owner of the Vandy Deli, goes out of her way to make sure you have a great experience.

cute, small deli that many employees go to for lunch! i've tried the chicken salad sandwich and the turkey chili. the sandwich was slightly dry, so definitely add mayo or mustard to it. the chili was spicy, but delicious with the homemade corn bread. very pleased with my experience every time I go there. highly recommend for a local spot to grab a quick to-go bite!
WDjVJQr9UEHjZhYXcjjedg: Good food! Good price. Very open as of 4/23/17 though it is reported as closed. The employees are nice and friendly. Clean. Decent dining area which is rare for Greek in Nashville, it seems.

I love Greek food and I was hesitant to go to this place at first. Man, I wish I had gone earlier. The food is great! Every time I go it is very consistent. The prices are also great. They are super friendly and grateful for your business. My favorite dish is the Gyro plate, and the portion size is large. Some of my co workers get the Philly cheesesteak every time they go and the swear it is awesome! I'm a big fan of this place, keep up the good work!

Zeus has reopened 1135 bell road.   Right behind olive garden.   The food is as amazing as ever.   I always get the mixed "zeus sandwich".  It's amazing.  I'm so glad they re-opened in the new location.   Come check it out.  Tell them "the coach and mango tea sent you"
RelZM_5DBaBdixZDFfyyTQ:
Great coffee. Some of the best I've had. The specialty drink i had was a blend of espresso and chai. Blew my mind. 

On top of that the owner was very friendly and the locals hanging out there were nice. 

My wife and i will be back.

This place is amazing! Opening next week they will have a full range of coffee, food, breakfast, lunch and dinner. They plan to be open 24/7 and to serve the community anyway they can... Prices are going to be recommended, but if you can't afford them, you can pay less. This is going to be hippest coffee house in Nashville... while serving the community at the same time!

I am only giving 4 stars not because I'm not impressed with this place, or delighted that it's in our little village, but because I'm excited to see what it becomes! It opened just a little while ago, and I've had a chance to go a few times and to take some friends and family. 

I was aware for the past couple of years that Old Hickory boasted its own coffee roastery and that several local coffee shops served its coffee, but I had never actually tasted how fantastic it was until recently. 

The coffee blows the local competition out of the water. I can't say it plainer than that. if you have some loyalty to one of the other local coffee brands, consider having an open mind and tasting Jacob's Well's coffees. It has everything coffee should have: flavor, aroma, a caffeine kick, and most importantly, no bitter aftertaste. It's just yummy, yummy, yummy. 

This business seems to be doing a lot of great things. I know they have had art openings, a pizza night, and some other community-centered events. Old Hickory Village desperately needs this kind of gathering place, as sometimes even an active Facebook neighborhood page can't complete with a real life meeting place 

The inside is comfortable and eclectic, and I think is still going through improvements and changes. I haven't been this week, but I know they have started providing food and have also installed a much needed menu board. I am anxious to see this place succeed and believe it has a lot of community support. The other things they are doing are fabulous, but as a total coffee nut, I can definitely say you can't beat the coffee.
zM4fAbixJUX4Hw3eQ34E6g:
Great flavors and great service.
I got the beef shawarma combo.
My only complaint is that the portion sizes are small, but the flavor made up for it and left me feeling satisfied.
Their falafels are AMAZING. Its homemade, and you can definitely taste it with how fresh it is.
The pita bread is really good too, its probably homemade too.
The rice that came with my food was dry, but the beef was very delicious (though small portioned).

Great place for the price and flavors they have. Each bite was a pleasure.

Super nice owner and great food. The wraps are in a thin almost crepe like bread, not pita but more like a thin tortilla and wrapped like a burrito then pressed to perfection. Falafel is excellent, falafel wrap with a generous amount of tahini. Chicken swarma is small pieces of chicken with a great taste. Great food!
The best place to have really Mediterranean food , chicken shawarma it's really great I go there almost every day try everything on there menu , I highly recommended this food truck
6Jg7Sx6RT8FCt8XMeETlsQ:
It's not as consistent as say Chipotle, but I can't be too snooty about it b/c it's Fast Food.  If I wrote a review about how McDonald's french fries were not as crisp as they normally were in the past, you would think I was a stuck up jerk.

Go online and print yourself off some coupons.  They've always got a $5 off coupon floating around.  Take some one with you and buy yourselves 2 baja burritos for about $10 (they always come with chips), and you'll be happy with the big meal you got for the money you paid.  

It's unique enough from Chipotle, so you won't be comparing two to each other... Chipotle is better than Qdoba, but different from Baja Fresh.

Baja Fresh: It's a chain restaurant, so in that case it's better than most

My company orders from here at least once a month. They don't deliver but they are pretty good about their pick up orders. They also have two parking spots right up front for to-go. 

The line for pick usually doesn't have anyone in it, and they're pretty good at accommodating and giving realistic time estimates for pick up. One time the forgot one part of our order and when I called to complain the manager got in the car and brought us the missing item. Along with a coupon for a free burrito or two. 

Not to mention, they have nutrition facts online.
Other chain resturants could take a lesson from these guys.  Always great food, always perfect.
kI173572bzkLY8MozgF_jQ: Walked here with some coworkers on a whim. This place was good... it's a standard southern meat and 3 cafeteria. Everything I ate was delicious. Go on Wednesday and try the squash casserole. It will make you slap your Grandma.

all these guys are so nice. Place smelled delicious. We only had the pie...pecan pie is DELICIOUS! We were the only ones here since it was early in the morning. The nice man stopped cooking and cut us two pieces of pie even though I don't think they were open yet. The pecan pie was to die for and was still warm. Would have loved to stay all day and try more food.

Good down home cooking served cafeteria style. Just like going to Grandmas for lunch. I had salsbury steak with mashed potatoes, creamy mac and cheese and collard greens. Delicious. Hubby had fried chicken moist and juicy on the inside and crispy on the outside. Dining area not much to look at but its clean. Good price great food
gZRm5kecOIqpWDv6d4nNag: This was my second time trying zaxbys. Robert J, from the minute we came in, welcomed us with a warm, friendly, and genuine smile. He made conversation with us and asked us where we were from. The sweetest guy ever. After our food was ready, he came by the table to talk to us and he really made our night. What a nice guy. We need more people like him in the world. This is one of those people you won't forget meeting.  Thank you Robert. You are amazing.

It's a typical Zaxby's!  Good chicken, good shakes!

My only complaint is the floors and tables never seen completely clean at this location.  Other than that, it's a fine place.

They certainly don't deserve a 1 star rating because they got an order wrong, once.

Customer service behind the counter was very friendly. Asked about sauces after the order was placed. Clean and large inside seating. Zax Club was made very neat and tater chips were fresh n crispy. Good location!
VxXmaSvT8lv5PdDDduB01Q: Can't beat the prices here. The burger & fries combo is soooo good! Every bite is bliss and nostalgia- tastes like a catered back yard burger!! I get no lettuce and no tomato but I keep everything else as it comes. Never disappointed. Owner is always working and kind, I love supporting and eating at Morning Glory!

Within the context of what Morning Glory is, I think it's a fine place.  But make sure you understand what it is:  It is a convenience store/deli inside Cummins Station, intended for people who work in Cummins Stations.  If you travel to Cummins Station specifically to dine at Morning Glory, you're gonna have a bad time.

Having said that, as somebody who works in Cummins Station, I like Morning Glory quite a bit.  The staff are nice, they have a decent variety of lunch food, and their prices are surprisingly low, considering how much they must be paying to rent that space.

It appears to be run by a Korean family and, as others have said, the English is not strong.  But it's serviceable.   They have a credit card minimum, but it's manageable.

The quality of the food itself is fine, again taking the context into consideration.  This is a nice alternative to other places in the building:  Cinco de Mayo (fine, but you can't eat Mexican every day), M Restaurant (so expensive I have never been there), M Market (my, they take themselves seriously -- and quite expensive), the Suchi place (which has a nice buffet actually, but is also kind of pricey).

Both Cinco de Mayo and Morning Glory win the price award in Cummins Station.

As far as seating, they have a counter inside that is servicable but not terribly comfortable.   They have tables and chairs out in the hall, but in the winter that entrance is a bit drafty.  But they serve everything in to-go boxes, so you can certainly take it back to the office.
Great place if you're in Cummings Station to grab something to go - got a great cheesesteak sandwich for $5 - - - pretty fast too.
WbOy2oYIfKm7YaF8L7Wlig: I've been to this location probably 50 times in the past year and a half. I've never had a bad experience. The food and service are great. They are the only Wendy's I have ever been to that has great baked potatoes; most Wendy's overcook them, but at this location they are always perfect!
An employee talked to my son about the Poke bugs video game. Very warm staff, very hot food! Enjoyed my soda.
Liz, the night shift manager?, is one of the warmest people you will ever meet. She is the reason for the five stars.
1GXjWFdmK-Sb9FfliaOzCA:
So today waited on by Carmenita she was very professional 
Explained everything and offered different sides she really cared about the product GREAT JOB

I understand that there are a lot of one-star review for this KFC location, but that is not my experience. I visited this location during lazy Saturday. The room was clean and a few customers were standing in front of me. They completed their transaction quite promptly and then it was my turn. The lady who took my order was courteous and I received my order in less than five minutes. I couldn't be happier.
7rMSld3Tb2v-Cj8PjzgS0g: I had their shrimp PoBoy and gumbo yesterday at Streetfood Thursday downtown and O.M.G....it was amazing!! The PoBoy was huge, shrimp seasoned perfectly, gumbo was the best I've had.  Definitely check them out!!
Roast Beef PoBoy will change your life! The thing is huge and the flavor is unreal. Catch them during crawfish season too! Perfect spice.

These guys do it right. If you ever have an opportunity to get some crawfish from them, it's the best I've ever had. I think they drive down to New Orleans every week to stock up on fresh seafood. Also, best po boy I've had, and that includes from LA. We're also obsessed with the gumbo. It's got a dark rue, and is the perfect spiciness.
fE0Du-p-2mHJKFp6aVch7g:
I love Wendy's, and this location is awesome! I go through the drive through each time, so I can't comment on the interior of the restaurant. The drive through is quick and efficient while the employees are friendly. 

There is one guy who works there who is usually at the drive through and is always SO nice. He knows my order and always takes care of me. The one time he was not there, my order was wrong. I am certain I will keep coming back to this Wendy's as long as I live in the area.

These guys care, bottom line. I visit this location in the drive-thru regularly, 4 times a week, tops, and I've never had my order messed up, and I've always had prompt service.

Being a cook, I'm all too aware of the carelessness that permeates my coworkers psyche at the hour before close. It gets easier and easier to write the orders incorrectly as the night goes on, but you always have an opportunity to make the experience worthwhile for the customer, and their outstanding service tonight led me to write this review.

The lady in the drive-thru in front of me had a huge, insanely complicated order by any sane person's standards, to the point that I was supremely annoyed at waiting behind her. I ordered a spicy asaigo chicken combo, but was told I'd have to wait another 5 minutes if I wanted it spicy. I opted out, told them I'd just have it fried without spice. The lady in front of me had such a complicated order though, that they had the time to make the chicken spicy. I was surprised when the drive-thru attendant left two other spicy chicken sandwiches in my bag after double-checking my receipt, and I'm never opposed to free spicy chicken sandwiches. Anyway, these guys are great, their bad Yelp reviews probably only reflect their bad days and not their good days. Today was a good day, indeed.
Fantastic experience! Very very friendly staff who went out of their way for our family on vacation. They even brought us out some complimentary chocolate chip cookies!
tyUYnSKKQrXd2zEBhNCxCg: I'm a huge Lenny's fan! Although it isn't the cheapest fast food route it is definitely much better than Subway in which I have been to both this week....Subways is so expensive for what you get...I got a 6inch sub at subway and 4 small pressed turkey slices that didn't fill me up...now on the other hand at Lenny's they slice your sandwich meat right there on the spot and pile it high so you actually feel you are getting what you paid for and walk away full! I will gladly go to Lenny's any day over the other places! Love it and everything is always so fresh! I love their wheat bread and their provolone cheese is awesome too! Way to go Lenny's!!

This shop should be named Fresh Food Sub Shop! 

I have been to Subway many times but this was my first trip to Lenny. I got a Veggie Sub which was really good especially the bread so was fresh! 

If someone ask me whether you will go to Subway or Lenny's Sub Shop, I would say "Eat Healthly Be Healthly"  aka. Lenny will me answer! 

Give it a try folks!

Lenny's rocks! The Philly Cheese-steak and all the classic yankee subs are fantastic as it's gonna get here in the holler. This is a fine Damn Yankee sub shop...the best kept secret due to our glamorous midway of check cashing centers and crappy fast food drive-thrus in Donelson, TN...home to lots of people on fixed incomes and partial plates in their mouths. It's off Lebanon road, real close to Briley Parkway...hidden back behind the Booger King...next to a pretty good Chinamonese place and a pretty fair place called The Ole' Dinner Bell.
_czjtkS_XllCJi_1vBc_sw: I have been here only once I used the drive thru the employees was nice and it was slow so I was in and out quickly mcdonalds gets a bad rap for their food but it's all in what you order.

I just left this McDonald's and was so happy I decided to write my first review. I've had not so good experience with this particular location in the past but I was so happy this time I decided to share my experience I just went through the drive through and got a Big Mac as well as some nuggets. Everything was made fresh and they also give me 4 packs of sauces instead of 2 without me asking. I did have to wait a while but since the update they are really stepping up there game! And if i didn't notice a change I wouldn't even be giving them the credit they deserve. Great job guys!!!
nYzlAY8g1jaMgyFAzq0S9g:
Well well, Nashville has a new market and it is divine! As soon as you walk in, you are welcomed with hospitality, surrounded by great local/regional products and produce and hit with the alluring smell of food! At first, I was reluctant to order the House Made Mozzarella Panini because it was $8 alone, but it was SO worth it! I will definitely be ordering many more in the future! They don't have much seating, but there are a few stools along one of their windows that was great for a quick bite. All to say, I will be back!

*The market has select products and produce, which is great, but it's not the cheapest place to go stock up. If you just need a few things, maybe a quick gift, this place is a great solution!

PRIVATE EVENT REVIEW:
Southernaire was the bomb. They shut down the market and turn it into a restaurant which ended up being awesome. 
Fixed menu was lovely, the wine choices were solid, and the service was stellar. 
Highly recommended if you're planning a group dinner / event in Nashville.
This is a cool (albeit a bit pricey) specialty food/gift store, connected to the restaurant, The Southern. Excellent selection of hot sauces, bbq sauces, rubs, and candles.
ztI4UzV6YRNj2mWWr9xtzQ: Thanksgiving Day, 2013 - Lucky Bamboo is back! The owners who revamped Chinatown with more authentic Dishes have given this location their treatment.  The new menu includes Sichuan, Cantonese and dim sum dishes and hot pot (Chinese fondue).  The new menu is really promising, and more new dishes are promised.  I had wonderful dan dan noodles and dumplings today, and I look forward to munching through the rest of the menu.

Now a fusion Vietnamese restaurant. Worth a shot in the increasingly competitive Vietnamese restaurant row on Charlotte Ave. Puts a twist on traditional Vietnamese cuisine. Definitely a modest. upgrade from Kien Giang that I have been a loyal customer for many years. The menu is refreshing especially the part where they pair a different soup for an entree. The place is clean and more inviting than the other casual dining restaurants in the area. I ordered the Braised Ginger Chicken that was paired with a Lemongrass Soup. It is spicy hot so you might want to request "less spicy" if you prefer. It was still very good. The servers are friends of the new owners and are very personable. Ask if Richie is there and he'll make you feel at home.

Newly reopened as a true dim sum and Sichuan hot pot  restaurant. I had the braised pork belly and dumplings with Sichuan oil. Both were very good, but I came for dim sum. Unfortunately, the dim sum chef has not arrived from mainland china yet, they said it would be three weeks. I'll be back for sure and often!
jgOr0_lGEgiDSMvGxVoJOw: Awesome bar! I went with two friends and we loved it! Adam was our bartender and he mixed me a fabulous Vesper. He mixed an Old Fashioned for one friend and he poured white wine for our third friend. Our drinks were all half priced because it was happy hour. Wow! We ate dinner at the bar and we were all very happy. I am a vegetarian and Adam helped me find two delicious dishes. I wish they had more vegetarian dishes. Otherwise, Gertie's is a great place  for drinks and/ or dinner. We'll be going back very soon!

Read a lot of good reviews on Yelp re: 404 Kitchen and Gertie's Bar which is located below the restaurant.  Visited on a Sat afternoon while exploring the Gulch area.  Unfortunately, the kitchen was not open so I can't comment on the food options.  My buddy and I grabbed one round of cocktails.  We both chose a bourbon from their impressive wall of spirits (see photo).  So many options are available that it can be difficult to choose.  Prices weren't cheap, but seemed reasonable (around $12 for the bourbons that we had). The space is beautifully done, and we grabbed a spot at the spacious bar.  Service was prompt and the bartender was pleasant.  Would love to revisit to try some food and maybe a couple cocktails.  Good experience!

Just happened in here for a drink to end our night, and so glad we did. Great little spot, bartenders were super cool and made an out of this world old fashioned. Great vibe. Want to try the restaurant upstairs sometime, but Gertie's alone is worth a visit for sure
R9qdziBWlg89-wHm47SMWg:
Love this place! Food was very tasty! Staff very personable. I had the Broken Spoke burger with fries and a root beer ...Didn't drink cause I was driving!

The burger was cooked to a perfect medium well but the bun was a little over toasted but the burger and fries were hot and served fairly quickly 

Fun decor and atmosphere... you could take your family... no smoking is allowed
iyKh1--cClUMqHaM2xsuTg: Had pizza delivered today for lunch and being one of my favorite food groups. found Basilico Pizza to be one of the best in a long time. Many times with delivered pizza you find it to be too greasy and cold...not today...this was excellent and I look forward to having it again.
HzRfQiH4SVEGAHp47p6ksA: The service was slow. However my fish was GREAT! Had some wings too. Very good. If your in a rush, this is not the place to go. I suggest call ahead.
Delicious! We had wings catfish chicken tenders fries and some white fish all delicious. Get everything in the hot sauce ts good......kinda expensive for lunch but still good

Had the chicken tenders and wings and they were great.  Wings were good size and prepared to order.  Chicken tenders were moist and plenty of food given.  Since I did not order any fish, the owner brought me a little sample...a must try for next visit.

I was pleasantly surprised at quality and quantity....good deal for under $10
xD-CCfLVb6eaojwOxZiZfQ: I got the spicy chicken sandwich meal with a strawberry lemonade. Their customer service could use some work, but the fries were so hot and fresh that they burnt my mouth. Lemonade was great and the spicy chicken sandwich was good, too. The previous reviewers must have caught them at a bad time. Every time I go to this location, the food is always fresh, hot, and my order is correct.
y6jIrZYjVaxDbGXKpSlacA:
With so many great coffee and bakery establishments in Nashville it's tough to compete but Slow Hand has nailed it! First off really pleasant and welcoming staff. Next the baked goods, coffee's and food are really creative and delicious. Finally, the space itself oozes "come hang out" and feels both spacious & cozy at the same time... well done SHCB! 
(note: I actually thought the name was "Scratch Kitchen" for a while because of the writing on the facade... gotta love mystery & confusion. Makes the longing deeper.)

My new favorite local coffee shop! They have the best chai lattes, awesome espresso drinks, yummy cream cheese cookies, incredible sourdough bread, & a great biscuit sandwich - especially the one with fried chicken! If they had a drive through, I'd give them all my money every day.

Stopped on the way to work ( Monday)
, it was casual inside and the food was delicious.
Whatever the "secret sauce " is for the French fries was a treat.

And the bacon ... well ,c'mon ... it's bacon , but it was perfect.

Highly recommend this place
8BYOZUd4fBpmLJfk2t3vjQ: We used Ezoza's Kitchen for a catered lunch at my office. The delivery person arrived early and didn't forget anything! That's quite a feat with delivery. The Baked Ziti was the best I've EVER eaten. And I've eaten a lot! The Fettuccine was equally delicious. We will most definitely order from them again.
MMe-OJeHIgRTulqKPJ-QVg:
If your looking for a simple lunch this is the spot 
They offer combos for 6.00 the staff are always nice they offer free wifi as well they resently started serving tea and lemonade together a different drink and if you purchase a 25.00 card you receive a 6 inch for free
qpLXjhAYATp_j_SBRUtjIw: This place is so cute! Everything I try is delicious and the owners are so friendly! You have to try this place!!!

I wandered in late Saturday morning and ordered a classic croissant and three cheese brioche--it was so good I almost didn't want to share! The croissant was flaky with incredible layering--that's such skill! The brioche had a smoky flavor, which I didn't anticipate; I'm not sure if it's due to something like bacon fat or just the cheese selection, but I would double check if it's vegetarian. 

I would love to see the addition of something like baguettes! There isn't a bakery in East Nashville that specializes in bread since the Frothy Monkey bakery left, and something I would love to see filled!!
Hands down the best bakeshop in Nashville! Everything I try is absolutely delicious and t he owner's Andrea and Brad always take great care of us. This place is definitely a must visit.
0LNbzt1dnlrqCAhNjK4SSA: Local and fast.  They will fix pizzas if your choice for buffet.  Four because there are never enough servers and during busy lunch hour the place can be a mess.  Staff always friendly and attentive.

I was having issues with my online order & my free coupon code for cheese sticks so I decided to just come into the restaurant and talk to them. I spoke with the manager, Stephanie, and she was a huge help! She honored my free online order code and discounted my deal box also since the app hasn't been working correctly. I also completed changed my mind and wasn't going to eat Pizza Hut anymore but Stephanie made everything right.
Great staff that's always friendly and working hard even when they get overwhelmed during the lunch hour rush
9CcYG7sneFNwy09sp7GKuA: I'm always excited when a new doughnut shop opens up in town, especially if they do something out of the norm. These mini doughnuts are a fun alternative to the usual doughnut holes, actual miniatures of traditional round yeast doughnuts. They're light without too chewy of a texture, and 3 of them is a perfect small treat. The larger packs would be great for small parties. So far I've tried the bacon one, the whiskey glaze, the fruity pebbles, the s'mores, and the rocky road. I probably wouldn't get the last two again, just too much and too sweet, but the others were stellar. Very friendly service and almost no wait at all. I hope they stick around!

Popped into this donut shop for their unofficial soft opening. The whiskey glazed donut, frosted fruity pebbles, death by chocolate, strawberry lemonade, and Reese's cup were all fantastic. Definitely come check it out!
Family friendly and tasty, freshly-made little donuts.  I have found a great place to get customized mini donuts. My family loves it!
gGK9oTurV0N5UxPGUs3_sw:
I personally love this restaurant and its decor. It's a refreshing take on quality dining in a more casual space. If you're expecting a fine dining experience, this isn't for you. But if you're looking for quality new American style food with a unique spice flair and a more casual/approachable environment, they have some great options. I had great food and service here (which is something to respect in Nashville's vastly growing restaurant market). 

Attended brunch via a resy reservation and here's what I like:
1) The pork sausage with biscuit and egg: If you have ever been to Denver Biscuit Company and are missing their Franklin biscuits here in Nashville, this is the dish for you. The gravy is reminiscent of theirs and the biscuit is so good with the fried egg/chives! 
2) The dessert. Get the Meyer lemon cake. I know you want the beignets, but get the cake. It's superior. The compote isn't too overpowering and sweet, the acid of the lemon cake evens it out. It is a great balance between the two. 
3) The cocktails are good and priced about the same as other places in that area. Personal favorite was a specially one called 'Because I said So' for Mother's Day. I hope they keep a variation of this one the menu as I liked it better than Prosecco ones at the table (but it was more alcohol forward than the mimosa variations, too). 

What I think could be better:
1) The Gypsy-Porchetta with kale and apples. The mustard on this dish was amazing on this one, I wanted more to balance out the smoky/fat. Maybe more acid on this one? And I think I would want this on the dinner menu instead of brunch, it was a little ambitious for 1030a.

the food was amazing. the mashed potatoes were a hit (ur probably thinking mashed potatoesssss?!?! right?!) but yes. the flavor just explodes in your mouth. i'd get my own next time bcus sharing it is out of the question lol. the lamb ribs were amazing once you paired it with the sides that it was plated with. very flavorful. Didn't give it a 5 star bcus the drinks were barely mediocre, if that. ordered 3 diff drinks and they all just tasted like juice. they have indoor and outdoor dining, which is always fun.

We love this place!  
Ate here 3 times in the 4 days that we were in town.  The service was friendly and good.  The food was awesome.  Whole trout is amazing.  Sunday brunch was great too.
L3IMug1NZOITEwNoDz8JAA: This is a gem in Nashville! It is refreshing to find a chicken place that doesn't serve hot chicken. I had the tenders with mac n cheese and both were delicious. The tenders were crispy on the outside and juicy on the inside with just the right amount of batter. The mac n cheese was the best I've had in awhile. I got both the tangy barbecue and classic buffalo sauces and both were amazing. Good beer selection and prices to be a smaller restaurant. Perfect portion sizes. I will definitely be back!

Chicken was really fresh and juicy and their sauces are amazing. I wish the mild wasn't so spicy, but I don't have a high heat tolerance. Parking is plentiful and everyone is very friendly. I will definitely return!
This place is fantastic! Food is great, service is outstanding and beer is ice cold! Will definitely be back.
hvN_12-k_q_wbfOfjM-HSw: When I made my order at 730pm at night ANDREW personally called & made sure that I was lactose intolerant & made the best veggie pizza I ever seen very happy this definitely deserves a raise if he's already not A manager!

Regardless of the other critiques, I've nothing but good luck here. Are the employees "lifers" looking to move up the corporate ladder? No. Were they courteous and helpful?  Yes. Was the pizza good? Yes. No complaints.
This location is great. The staff knows me by name, they're super polite, and they've even given me free cheese bread the one time they made a mistake. Plus, you can't beat the $7.99 carryout deal.
mAYXISOsRRmEkj6mLeu0Og: Incredible restaurant.   Great setting in Green Hills  while surrounded by restaurants this one sets itself apart..  Service was spot on, wine suggestions perfect, appetizers and main courses were delicious.  I'm not a local but best food I've had in Nashville.  Duck was perfect and steak w marrow was the best.  Loved it!

Incredible dining experience in the heart of Green Hills.  We started out with some incredible apps, including the tuna carpaccio,murguez, and the lamb tartare.  We then feasted on the branzino, steak/marrow, and the morrocan chicken.  From beginning to end, we were amazed by the level of flavors in each dish. We couldn't have been more impressed with the meal as a whole.  I will definitely be coming back for seconds.

I'd almost forgotten what it is to have true gold star service at a Nashville restaurant, let alone for lunch. Thank you, Santo for answering all of our questions, letting us take our time so we didn't feel rushed, so we could thoroughly enjoy a noteworthy meal. We started with some champagne, then the white bean hummus and the Mediterranean chicken. Trifecta. The decor is almost austere, but not too cold. More like chic. They have only been open a few months but I think they have already found a groove. They will soon be setting up umbrellas for the patio. Will be a perfect spot as the temperatures back off. They do have a slight dress code, so don't come here from the lake. "Smart Casual." 
The only draw back is that it is not straight forward on how to get to the front door. You have to walk past True Food, to the sidewalk facing Hillsboro. Then go up the steps.
ykrD-GI7pQpU_rHlHnEeag: Came on a Sunday mid afternoon and got to witness a true Gospel Jam Jubilee! No religious songs just truly uplifting music. Beer selection was great and tasty. They have a local taco stand and food truck parked outside. This is the atmosphere so many other places are trying so hard to replicate. It's off the beaten Nashville path but well worth it. Truly a diamond in midst of all the Nashville construction.

So much fun! We did the moonshine tasting and our bartender was so fun and sweet! Then we went outside and played cornhole, the fact that they had games was easy to stay and hang out on the grass. The only con would have been that It felt like such a backyard bbq vibe that I wanted to take my shoes off and the grass was way too hot from the sun. Otherwise it was a perfect experience, and we will be back when we come back to Nashville!

Sampled all the moonshine they would let us for $5.  Most was really good, especially the pickles!  Took home 6 jars, 3 of which were the pickles (Gifts for friends and neighbors).  The DESSERT shine was amazing, my wife loves the Some Beach (coconut) and the Butter Pecan! YES, that's right; dessert moonshine!  Don't knock it till you've tried it...
E6aDgHR-_7xn02VE3gilbA: They were absolutely amazing here. Our flight got delayed for 6 hours and we were so late for our reservation, the restaurant was closing. Additionally, it was my sisters birthday and we really just wanted to have a good time. They were absolutely amazing and served us with smiles and genuine spirits. Kevin was the most amazing server. To top it all off, the food was amazing and we got complimentary dessert. I would most definitely come back here. Thank you STK Nashville.

LOVED STK. We had a girl's night and my friend was in town, so I wanted a cool, fun vibe. The DJ played great music, and Beau was THE perfect server for our girl's night. He was so fun and had great recommendations. We ordered the Mac and cheese (omg) and tuna tartare, corn casserole, Caesar salad and wedge, and my meat eater friends ordered a steak. The tuna was AMAZING. And the mac and cheese is always a favorite. 
Finished it off with some chocolate cake, and Prosecco- perfect night out. Loved the atmosphere.

Happy hour here is amazing!!! I knew of this brand from New York and Chicago, and was excited Nashville was getting one! Stopped by for a Friday happy hour with girlfriends and was impressed with the food, presentation, specials and service. Drinks were delicious too and half off!  Atmosphere is relaxed and sophisticated.  Our server Kevin was great at making recommendations and explaining the history of the brand. Definitely recommend for a lively yet classy happy hour
GQXeK-CdSb9D4QOfS9tYQg: Thanks to The Catio, we have a new family member. He is absolutely perfect! The woman who showed my husband around was incredibly kind and the entry fee was very reasonable (Every cat cafe has an entry fee, btw). The layout is great too. The cats are in a seperate room from the bakery and my husband was particularly pleased about the cleanliness. We are so glad to have The Catio! Thank you for helping us find our little Shrek buddy

The Catio is a great addition, not only to our neighborhood, but the rescue community. I've been several times and the pastries are great, the coffee is strong and mostly- I love being able to support the kitties. It's refreshing that the staff is very friendly and polite and I LOVE the overall vibe. The prices and policies are more than fair- I've been to other cat cafes (in and out of Nashville) where they charge a lot more for entry and you can't just show up.  I look forward to the various future special events- especially knowing that the profits go directly to the cats. It's great to have something different in the neighborhood and I will definitely be there regularly!

The Catio is perfection. I tried the chocolate chip cookie, brownie and sugar cookie - all delicious. The decor is SO good, hip and fits right in with the East Nashville vibe. The place is spotless. I went Into the cat room and did not want to leave. It's so nice to see adoptable cats in a super clean, nurturing environment with levels for them to jump on, cozy beds, toys and hiding spots. It feels really good there - it's a happy place! East Nashville is so lucky to have The Catio - great care has gone into both the cat room and the bakery and it shows. Love it!
P4CSWhe3WKwTNguupAk9oA:
Great staff, tasty rum punch, and vegan chili nachos that turn heads.


Very glad to see nvr nvr join the neighborhood!

There are two options for patio seating as well.
BEST BAR IN NASHVILLE !!!! Love all the drinks, especially the Old Fashioned ! And the meatloaf sandwich is epic!

I was finally able to get over to experience this new cocktail bar. What a great vibe! Loved the intimacy of the bar area and the outdoor space is fantastic as well. (This actually my second trip here; came the first time with our puppy and hung out in the courtyard). The cocktails were absolute perfection. They also sell Original Coors in a can, which I love!  The food was awesome: get the nachos. Lastly, the service and bar staff were so friendly and fast. Even when there were 3-people deep at the bar, I only waited about 5 minutes for my perfectly mixed tequila drink. Can't wait to go back!
T0TlzWWItbspWbfcPqF5fw: Had a great experience at Mojo's today! Quick service. Delicious ice cream. Brook, the owner, was such a fun character and helped us make excellent choices. Go visit Mojo's if you haven't already!

The ice cream and cookies are well worth the wait. 
The crew was kind and even gave our daughter a free scoop of ice cream. 
We got the salted caramel with M&M cookies and it was all great.
Mojo was parked outside MorseFest this weekend. We tried the birthday cake and the double chocolate sandwiches. Both were wonderful and the service was speedy and friendly.
ACYt-FtXTGBEsA0P36s4Cw: A beautiful place with a beautiful mantra - and excellent lattes! The staff couldn't be more friendly or welcoming and it is a nice environment to work in.  I will definitely come back here. The coffee is donation based, which is pretty cool - I gave $5, but you do you. I'll bring cash next time but they do take a card.

What a lovely little gem in the neighborhood!

I had the custom-made "raspberry latte" and it was delicious.

There are displays which feature local artisans, so that made it even more interesting.

Not only that, this is a "donation based" coffee shop...whaat?

Highly recommended.

Enjoyed my first visit to Crest Cafe today.  It's clean, comfortable, and relaxing.  There are lots of places to power-up your laptop while you work, create, and enjoy good coffee.  The vibe here is wholesome and caring, not pretentious or overly busy.  This is a setting where I can focus on what I'm doing!

They recently opened and have a few more improvements planned.  I'll be back!
OqutmtWhyRttbOfOCryU6Q: Love the consistency and quality for take home or delivery!  Really the best pizza for the price out there. Deep dish with crazy crust is a must!

Look... It's round and has tomato sauce, cheese and meat on it. It's hot and it smells great. As pizza goes... a frozen one out of your freezer might be better. BUT!!! And it's a major but... You walk in with $5 (plus tax, sorry...) and you walk out with a pizza. Besides, you can't find a frozen pizza this big for $5!!! Seriously! A hot, fresh pizza should not be this easy to get!!!
Best ultimate supreme pizza I've had in quite awhile from anywhere. Loaded with toppings for just $10.
7wIsHe_7rn0e1z8KIn087Q: We stopped in because we wanted something small to eat before heading out for some drinks so we just got one taco each. I had the chicken and rice taco and it was just ok and my husband had the Nashville hot chicken taco and said the same about his. I was expecting to love this place and it was just ok. Kind of lacked flavor on both of them.

We are here on a Saturday night for live music and got treated to Keith Burns of Trick Pony fame. He performed with Will Dakota. Great performance on the patio under nice space heaters. 
We ordered four different tacos and chips flight. The flight was fun to try four "dips" with the nice chips. The chips were the perfect crispiness. The Nashville hot chicken taco didn't disappoint. Teriyaki chicken was good but a bit too salty. My husband enjoyed the fish taco. His favorite was the Korean smoked pork. 
For dessert we devoured the apple empanada. Excellent! I could make a meal of this dessert. 
My biggest complaint is that the shells are very disappointing. If they made their own shells the tacos would be taken up a notch!

Food here was really good. The avocado on my Baja fish taco was more brown than green but otherwise everything was great. Would definitely be interested in coming back and trying their margarita flight.
nT0acgBkVSEelbNTXRSFbg:
Hey there yelpers!

So, I suppose I've been going to this Sonic for about 10 or so years and I've never actually reviewed it, so the star rating is based on an average score of the majority of the visits to it I can recall.

First things first, the location is great and convenient. It's really easy to just pop over here and grab a quick bite to eat and head home as I live only a few minutes away. I think the food is not too bad, I have had better. I say this because I always get the #7 which is an extra long chili cheese coney with tots and a Dr. Pepper but sometimes the hot dog is not quite soft enough and seems like it wasn't cooked long enough or boiled long enough and I like my boiled hot dogs to be nice and soft, not firm and somewhat harder to chew. The tots are incredible and the Dr. Pepper is good here, too, and some places (as some of you might agree) doesn't seem to have good enough soda compared to other places. I'm a Dr. Pepper snob lol.

The service can sometimes be a bit slow but because I've been going here so long and the type of person I am, I still try not to count that against them. I'm a typically pretty patient guy and they've not taken so long that I lose my cool. I will not stop going here for the foreseeable future. = )

Happy yelping!

I live right over here by this sonic so I frequent it often. They do have bad and good days. I would say I've had more good experiences than bad and I've been coming here for nine years or so. If it's a bad day though, run away. It's not going to be right, hot, or worth your time.
lVdubEodgoSWdVXqpxU1Hw: This Pizza Hut is by fat one of the best I've been too when it comes to the pizza freshness and variety on their buffet. The service was amazing as well!
6yqH46TMr4GfE9lHR_iDFw: I am SO happy to update my review!  Two return visits by our office have been successful. Food has been correct and just as delicious and FAST!  They have definitely picked up their pace.  The poboys are huge, by the way.  There's always plenty to eat.  Amber, the manager, has been personable and welcoming.  Looks like we'll be regulars!
vrX8vraAe1D34U6NCFAMDQ: The pizza was really good! For a fast food chain, I was thoroughly surprised at how clean the facility was and how nice the workers were. Not to mention, they have great deals on their website!
Love Hungry Howie's Pizza. You've got to order either the thin crust or the deep dish (like Jett's). Then flavorize your crust (I like lite garlic) and BOOM! Deliciousness

Had honestly never heard of the brand and just went because it was close. So, for what matters on inexpensive pizza they top out as the best I've had. It's flavorful, consistent, on time. The deals are decent, the website is ... close to top notch for ordering, and the prices are good.

Now, for a masterpiece, you're probably best looking elsewhere, but man their crust is amazing.

Weekly deals putting enough pizza for two in at like $8 yup, I'll get that.
WDT18nI5VIOI1b7z1G7_LQ:
Update: I got an apology from this location and they offered to help make it right on my next order.  I stopped by tonight, and it was crowded as usual. I placed my order. Everyone was friendly. The wait takes a little while but once you're at the window your meal is always ready. Tonight my order was perfect. I hope my last trip I just came on an off day. 


I LOVE chick fil a and was so excited for this location to open up. Most of my visited to this location have been fine, but my last visit and lack of follow up were disappointing. I entered the drive thru lane around 11:10ish am and an employee was in the lane yelling at other employees about the kale salad (something like superfood is out kale crunch is in). It continued on beyond just and update, and was interfering with ordering. I ordered a grilled chicken sandwich meal with soup. Was told they have no soup, so I asked for fries instead. Towards the end I was not asked about my drink choice and inquired and she said she didn't put one in my order, but could add it. I got my meal, with no drink so I had to pay cash at the window to get a lemonade. I looked at my receipt and it was close to $14 before the drink! I was charged for TWO sandwiches even though I ordered one, and only one of those was the way I ordered it. I was charged for a side of fries and not a meal. Then I was charged for my drink in cash as mentioned, so not part of the $. I called the location to tell them incase their system was double ringing up, but my call was not answered or returned (it's been 48 hours). I understand the shouting about the kale may have thrown off the flow and a simple accident happened, but an apology would have been nice.
So grateful to have this in our neighborhood!!! We love Chick fil-a. Like other stores it's busy but everyone is always friendly and consistently provides good service and food.
Some girl sprinted to my car with my food. It was hot in my mouth and I thank her for that. Also I always get a plethora of sauces and that's yummy delicious.
IsJrxG7Du7IKLlYsNl6Z9Q: Went here on a very rainy night and got great customer service in the drive through. The employee even had a dog biscuit for my puppy who was riding passenger seat. I appreciate that kind of thoughtfulness! Fries and chicken fingers were great, too.
Today I went there and it was good. My review is mainly about how nice the workers were. Tracy was the drive thru worker. 10/10 human.

Soooo, it's a Burger King, not a swanky spot, but I have gone to the drive thru for an impossible burger (with cheese!) at least a handful of times, and they are always quick and efficiently polite.  

I always make some adjustments to my order and they have gotten it eight every time.  

And I know impossible burgers are not necessarily "healthy" but it's nice to get that chargrilled burger taste and texture without contributing to the meat processing industry. :)

I normally would not review a fast food spot but with all the negative reviews I wanted to chime in with my positive experiences.
_Du_HSOzrp4j6o-d8czhbw:
Okay, I am giving them the benefit of the doubt with this 4 star rating. The food was good, not great just good. I hear for delivery they are fantastic, however I went in. I wish I hadn't. The wallpaper was ripping everywhere, it was very dark inside and when you go in you can see stains where little kids have been touching the wall. Also when you first go in it smells musty and it is damp in there. I'll be honest I almost turned around and went home, however I got it to go, no amount of money will get me to eat in there! 
It took about 15 min to get the food. We got home and opened it up and our order was accurate, hot and plentiful, it was a ton of food and it was good. I had spicy chicken and my wife had sesame chicken fried rice and the legendary hot and sour soup. The soup isn't that good, it was l little on the bland side. The reason I gave them 4 stars is because the waitress was very friendly and the food was good. I mean it is cheap Chinese. I do wish however, they would either move or redo there dining room!
2TfLC6yAtwXYlqOOlKSPNw: Can't say enough good things about this place! Not only were my kiddos, who are chicken finger connoisseurs, satisfied, but the flavor combos and options available made for extraordinarily happy parents as well! I took the recommendation of the very helpful woman at the counter and had a chicken & cauliflower cone with jam sauce, grits and the rosemary agave. It was AMAZING! The service and atmosphere are also top notch. 5 stars!

I went by Coneheads for the first time this weekend and couldn't wait to write this review! This was by far the most delicious meal that I've had in Nashville. The customer service was amazing! The gentleman working the front was so patient in explaining to us how the menu worked. I questioned my order at first, but he reassured me that I couldn't go wrong. My taste buds have never been more satisfied! I ordered the waffle cone filled with fried chicken, cauliflower, and grits topped with chipotle ranch. The waffle wasn't too sweet and perfectly complimented the savory components of the dish. The chicken was seasoned to perfection, and the grits were the best I've EVER had! So so so delicious!! The food was so amazing that I went searching for extra cash to tip with in my car and was so upset that I didn't have any extra on me. Nevertheless, I can't wait to return... with cash next time!

Absolutely incredible food, amazing service, friendly faces!! The best mac & cheese in town, hands down. I got a chicken cone with a mac & cheese cap and a side of pineapple coleslaw. Unreal. Next time I'll be trying their shrimp and grits cone! We live about 5 minutes away so I'm genuinely concerned I might not be able to stop myself from eating here every night  Even if you don't live in the neighborhood, it is WELL worth any drive. Highly recommend!!
zRgFle8xIP66TzUHjN4RDQ: Great pizza and wings. Always excellent customer service. My pizza delivery place from now on
This place was INCREDIBLE! A true treat for anyone who likes pizza and mediterranian food. The atmosphere is nice and fancy. The staff was super friendly and nice. They uber eats!

This place is very good!  My husband an I stopped there on a whim. We got a pizza and some hot wings after reading the google reviews. The hot wings are killer! So good. But the mild is really more of a medium or hot for me. That definitely didn't stop me as I went back and got more wings for dinner. 

The pizza is very cheesy and good and made with fresh ingredients. You can't beat the price either, as you can get a large 2 topping for 8.99!

The guys working there are very helpful, he came around from the kitchen to explain how things are made and even told us the best deals and showed us the pricing.

You should definitely try it at least once.
HWXcIT04QykuEKQURj6Hcg: This place is awesome! The Brisket is the best in town, and their sides were no joke either. The Mac N Cheese was super cheesy, and the baked beans were really good too! Do yourself a favor and keep driving past Martin's and check out this place.

I really enjoyed this local bbq restaurant.  The owners were very nice and even gave us some bread pudding on the house.  I went healthy (hard to do at a BBQ restaurant) and ordered smoked chicken and green beans and the owner threw in a few chips for free to try.  The chicken was delicious and very moist.  The green beans were good for a vegetable... What can I say, I'm on a diet and have to try to stay on it, otherwise the broccoli casserole looked amazing.  The food was great and even though it's right up the road from the ever popular Martin's BBQ, it's a great option for food and a great competitor.
cute little hole in the wall bbq place! food is amazing & the staff went above & beyond our expectations. they're also open on sundays!!!
Tf7c38yepZzZvPG9wxNIqw:
Great food! They have everything from apps to salads to full dinner meals, the bartender Jenn (Not the owner) was awesome!! I told her how great it was and the best part of my job is supporting local businesses like this one, she was so happy to hear (so if you have the chance tell her hello!) They've only been open for a few months and have a great selection! I got a local vodka soda, the CauliPoppers and a lemon curd cheesecake (to go)!! 
Will continue to stop in when passing through.

Excellent food, creative drinks, and wonderful service at Jasper's! My family came to Jasper's at the end of my graduation weekend and the restaurant was very accommodating of our group of ten. We started out with some fun frozen drinks and appetizers. I had the "cold fashioned" which was delicious, such a fun take on a classic drink. My cousin had the frose and enjoyed it as well. We shared the caulipoppers, redrum roasted wings, and chorizo chili cheese fries. The fries were my personal favorite. My cousin had the lonestar cowboy chicken and I had the potsicker salad. We both really liked our entrees. The potsticker salad is packed with flavor and was filling and delicious! The chef was very accommodating of my cousin's peanut allergy and prepared all the dishes without peanuts. 

After our meals we tried all three desserts on the menu: a passionfruit cheesecake, a chocolate cake, and an ice cream sandwich. My favorite was the ice cream sandwich. It was a super unique flavor combination - strawberry sorbet and snickerdoodle. I was initially skeptical, but it ended up being my favorite one! The flavors went together so well.

Jasper's is fantastic! Deb Paquette has knocked it out of the park once again. Plus they have a cute socially distanced outdoor patio available if you're not quite up to indoor dining yet. Highly recommend the masa dog enchiladas (decadent) and red headed salmon (perfectly cooked on top of a fresh, crisp set bursting with flavor). Their margarita (jasperita) is also fantastic. The service was quick and friendly as well. Don't miss this spot, it's a keeper!
SeSALQNK5hiI0CT2XTLbrw: Nice versatile chill spot you can watch the game, grab good drinks and some hookah or Just grab some good food in general. I had their catfish and grits and it was cooked to perfection definitely would recommend this entrée sorry I didn't get a chance to get any pics was so hungry and the first bite wasn't a stopping point before I smashed everything definitely worth checking out.  I'm going back will snap some photos.

I our experience was amazing we went during brunch time and I will say that by far has been the best brunch experience I've ever had . Our bartender Nate was awesome he was well informed about the drinks and also so nice & kind to me and my fiancé  and we appreciate that the vide was great music choice. If you want a good place with great food and good music to listen to here's your place

Food was great, young crowd with upbeat music. They would have got 5 stars but they added an extra 20 percent tip to my bill which I did not add. They automatically add a 18 percent tip on the bill. But when I checked my account there was a different amount than the one I signed for. You can go but pay with cash.
M6aVJzABRTTc6qSHQgrOaw: Really great sports bar. One of the most approachable and cool spots in Wedgewood Houston. Reasonably priced food and drinks. This place is probably going to be a staple of the neighborhood.
Come here for the best hotdogs, drinks and vibes in town. Great service and food. Fantastic spot.
What a great place to come out and watch some games! These guys did a great job with this place.
luS7HvNPlO3lyLkG40C_eA: This is a well hidden burger spot. Have been to many but this is far most my favorite. Those fries are amazing!!!
Best burgers and fries are amazing!! Hidden gem tucked in Germantown/Salemtown.  Highly recommended!

Secondary location apart from Riverside location. 

Came in here for a quick bite. The ordering process is easy and the interior is clean and new. A BIG PLUS is that you can order from the window and stay outside with your pupper doggo. 

Burgers will be made medium unless otherwise specified. They even ask you while you order so pay attention. Staff are friendly and service is quick. I was encouraged to try the "Spicy ketchup" and was not disappointed. It tasted like ketchup mixed with Crystal sauce or some other variant. Really went well with the fries. 

Really optimal location with street parking.
wzkLnKruqRCLS6vf_Nnr_g: saw this spot on social media and decided to stop by! Super cute for photo ops, but definitely not what we expected. The bar is located inside a house, and overall was nothing like it was advertised. The staff were all super friendly and the live music was great!

Some of the best drinks, best vibe, and hands down best service I've had in nashville in SO LONG. The food, the drinks, everything about this place is great. Jennifer is the best! Made us feel so at home. 10/10.

GREAT ATMOSPHERE!! It appears to be a cute quaint little East nashville house on the outside, and on the inside it's such a surprise! A feel good happy environment. They are renting the space out for the daytime. This would maybe be good for musicians looking to hold writing sessions or have band practice bc it appears they have a stage and microphones and sound equipment. Also has a big backyard that would be good for yoga classes, etc! Night time is when it comes alive though! All the lights and cool art on the walls makes for a fun and unique experience. The owner was extremely hospitable and nice! Overall a great experience. Can't wait to see more!
ur-WI1EGbaI7zScdXSod4w: The ambiance is awesome. It's half coffee bar, half bar with great vibes and lots of cool decor. We came mid week and happened to be happy hour, score! The food and drinks were amazing. We'll definitely be back next trip to Nashville!
Great service. Recommend the tacos and drinks during happy hour. Parking is a little funky but there is a lot that charges 5$ for an hour or so.

We love this place just like the other Goat locations.  This one has fantastic bar food and drinks, it's all really fresh.  Also someone sunk a lot of money into this place and it reflects in the industrial setting.  This area of Nashville is still coming of age so this Goat is easy to get in and out of.
Y8djj3w2NPUkR5PoXwM4cA: Wow! This place is exceptional! From the starter salads to the flavorful mussels-- I could hardly wait for the main course. :) I enjoyed the blackened salmon, my husband got the signature fish n' chips, and my 2 year old son got the linguini with marinara and meatballs. We were in HEAVEN! Everything came out piping hot and was perfectly cooked. It was a wonderful way to end Mother's Day! Boston Commons, we cannot wait to come back!
One of the best fish and chips I had in town! I highly recommend the British style. The staff is super nice.

Lucky to find this spot, was intending to goto the Treehouse but it was too busy. Thank god. I had the best lobster roll and the Guinness was amazing too! Fries which I shouldn't eat were awesome. My wife had the Salmon and she loved it. Must stop. The Bar staff was fantastic! Great atmosphere.

One last thought get the Boston Cream Pie!! Holy cow!
gehpdMAI1yDrtg4uTUba7A: Got take out from here. Really good place for great indian vegetarian fare. The paneer 65 and veg manchurian were really good.

This was me and my sisters first time trying this place. We both were very excited by the vegetarian menu that gave us lots of options to choose from. We ordered 4 different things from the menu and non of them disappointed. The food was so fresh and flavorful. The service was great! Meeg, one of the employees, was so helpful in helping us read the menu and find foods we liked. He also threw in a free sample of an authentic Indian desert. Overall great place and great people!

We ate at the Honest Indian Restaurant yesterday evening and it was a blast.  The taste were authentic, really good, good portion size and reasonably priced.  We ate Daal Baati Churma, Undhiya Puri, their famous Punjabi Vegetable, their famous Garlic Naan and Dry Veg Manchurian.  We also ate Baigan Bartha.  They are really passionate about making really tasty high quality food.  They also have a Mithai store and be bought fresh Khopra Paak.

THIS IS AN AWESOME ALL VEGETARIAN PLACE.
2u5OFr5W2X09hibu1Orhgg: Delicious pizza, amazing service and staff. Will definitely go back to try more menu items and support a fantastic new local business!

Have had take-out here several times, everything we've tried is so good! Fresh, delicious and ready when we got there to pick up. So glad to have great pizza that isn't a chain in the neighborhood! Pesto pasta is amazing. As is the Caesar salad. And the cheese sticks. And the pizza... A must try restaurant!!
Excellent pizza spot with outstanding quality. definitely best in Nolensville area. I've worked in the pizza business for years and this is up there with the best of them.
6fesJ0Wayr-ICIBL-2Xh1Q: Went on a busy Saturday while touring the city. Had to wait for about 30 minutes after ordering. But it was well worth it!!! We love chicken sandwiches and get them everywhere. We had chicken sandwiches and tenders and fries. Sandwich in medium and x-hot were fantastic and tenders in medium as well. Fries were soooo good.  Amazingly crispy and delicious!

I can't believe there was no line when we came here, the hot chicken sandwich was insanely good. Best chicken sandwich I've ever had. The portions are small for the side dishes but who cares because they're delicious. Potato salad, cole slaw and fries all so good. I can't speak to lines or service since we didn't have to wait but very, very good food.

We came here around 3pm on a Saturday. Service was very slow and the workers weren't very friendly. You could tell that they were working as fast and hard as they could, but the customer service needed some improvement. It took about an hour from start to finish for this afternoon snack. The food, however, was incredible and our chicken was cooked to perfection. It was so juicy and tender and very tasty. We would probably try another location next time.
w6ugJ4_9uhA3bAH3YFFrtQ: I absolutely love crepes so being able to make my own custom one was great! It was so fresh and I just loved it! I made my own custom crepe with Nutella, banana, and strawberry and it was incredible! Can't go wrong with Nutella! Can't wait to come back
Delicious! Tried a banana, Nutella, & whipped cream crepe. Everything tasted fresh & so so good. Had a cappuccino. Perfectly sweet - not too much just right.

I had the ocean crepe and OMG there are no words. I was filled such glee just eating this dish. I loved everything about this place. And I enjoyed watching the preparation while waiting...if I lived closer I would frequent this establishment often...probably daily. Love it....
qSglpos4Kuk6VqDjEOwslA: Great little deli right near Belmont! Both times I have been, the food and service was excellent. A bit small, so can get crowded at peak times, but definitely worth the wait. Outdoor seating is nice, and menu is extensive, with hot and cold sandwiches, tacos, burgers, and more. I wish this had been there when I went to Belmont, I would have gone all the time!
My husband and I discovered this deli when we were out walking around Belmont. Really great quality sandwiches, bagels and coffee. We loved it so much we came back the next day, too! 10/10!

This place opened recently, I think, and it's really good. Salads are good and dressing seem like they're home-made. Good wraps, sandwiches (the Milanesa in particular, which is a breaded chicken cutlet sandwich that's excellent).
p5wWFVQbdQ_QAQKlw_NKvA:
So excited I finally made my way to the Assembly Food Hall. Man the smokin chicken is an instant win! The herb chicken was cooked to perfection! It's not dry the herb sauce was good, not spicy at all. It was moist and juicy! The only complaint was the rice was undercooked. I ordered a side of peri-peri sauce it's $.50 an extra sauce. I wasn't impressed with the peri-peri sauce it was spicy I guess I was used to a more vinegar base peri-peri sauce. The herb-roasted chicken though was the bomb! I ate all of the 1/2 chicken it was so good! The side salad was nice and refreshing. 

The drinks are $3 and free refill throughout. The staff was friendly it was a slow night when I went the rotisserie was already off and the staff was just hanging out on their phone. They greeted me right away when I went to the counter. They are super friendly and walked me through the menu. 

Tip: get the 1/2 chicken for $2 more!

This fast food restaurant is in the food court of 5th & Broadway. It's menu isn't big but the service is very fast and the food is great. 

I had the chicken peri peri pita. A bunch of pulled chicken, aioli, peri peri sauce and cold slaw. It was yummy and I'd certainly do this again!

As Arnold said, 'I'll be back'

Great food and excellent GF options.  The fries were great.  In order to make the fries GF, just remove the Sriracha aïoli.  The half chicken was juicy and very good.

We'd eat here again.
dbLKt5WOnxeN8qUL16OouA:
Came to this place a few nights ago and was IMMEDIATELY greeted by the owner. He was super friendly and engaging, with warm and welcoming energy. 

Our server was Kaitlyn (not sure I'm spelling it right) and she was the absolute best. Great smile, the best perfume (yeah I was crushing a bit) but still knew her menu inside and out. She even helped those Philly boy out with a chocolate Yuengling (but I'll stick to the regular)

This place has a great local bar feel, the food was amazeballs and the check was shockingly low (I'd been paying downtown Nashville prices all week)

They also have a Texas Hold Em league!!!

The TLDR: Good people, tasty food, and low prices. Why haven't you gone there yet?

Had lunch with my daughter here last week. The food was super good the zucchini appetizer was one of my faves and the lintel soup was super delicious!! Can't wait to go back. The service was great and the restaurant is nice and clean.

I am extremely picky about gyros. I lived in Germany and came to love Döner Kebaps. The gyros here is the best one I have ever had this side of the Atlantic. It was served in traditional Kurdish Samoon which I have never gotten before in Nashville. As soon as it was served and I saw the Samoon I knew it would be amazing. The lamb was perfect and all of the other ingredients were very fresh.

Not only was the food absolutely phenomenal, so was the service. The owner, Sekvan, served us and what a delight it was!! He was so friendly, personable and extremely thoughtful. You have to visit to really appreciate the quality of the service because words cannot effectively describe my experience. One thing I can say is that I will be returning, frequently. 

Thank you so much to Sekvan and his amazing staff!!
oW8dGLAvYu7ZK4OC9qdQUg:
After a long time coming, this location opened in August . My first visit was Friday, October 22, 2020, as a friend wanted chicken for lunch. We used the drive-thru.  I ordered the chicken sandwich and my friend ordered the two piece meal. My chicken sandwich was fresh and prepared as requested, with extra mayo. The chicken was quite large, had a great taste, and was very filling. My friend ate his food in his home so I can't comment on his. 

The young man that was taking money at the window at 11:12am was GREAT!  Did a great job of customer service and was a great example of someone I would love to encounter again at this business and other restaurants I frequent.

Great sandwich. Thx for working today in the Ice. I was super hungry. But I always try to post about French fries. Adding crunchy krinkles doesn't cook the fries longer. Just hides the mushy grease interior. Reprogram the fryers for 45 more seconds and we can finally consume these taters. I wanna bring back the French fry war. I grew up loving fries. All the big chains claim the best fries. But these days you all take them out early and the grease doesn't get cooked away. Not just this store. But you can the one that begins the first battle. These crispy juicy noodles are nasty.

Popeyes used to be here and then they closed and then they rebuilt it and after a Longggg time it opened up
During Covid. Tried to go first
Few weeks - long drive through line.
Finally
Made it. Great deals
On 5 dollar boxes - twisty shrimp
And surf and turf. 
Also tried a sandwich- almost a tie between here and the chicken place down the street. The twisty shrimp were twisty and tasty but a lot
Of breading yet flavorful. 
Service was pleasant. Would like To try their spicy sandwich and will Figure Out my favorite
Chicken sandwich on Charlotte pike.
VMRuh0kasOiGAkZ98EFdlA: Incredible.  Service was was easily the best I've had in years, maybe ever.  The restaurant is easily one of the best in Nashville right now.  The tomahawk was prepared to perfection.

We were fortunate enough to be in town for Carne Mare's opening night on 11/13/21. The ambiance was dim and full of excitement. The music was complimented several times throughout the night and very diverse; everything from Lauryn Hill to Salsa classics. The show kitchen window was quite literally a show. Watch the wood fire grill flames hit the beef and better yet witnessing the synchronization and meticulation of the back of the house in live action was a highlight.

The front of the house was just as impressive. Their attentiveness, attention to detail, and knowledge were all outstanding.

We did not have drinks on this particular occasion so I cannot comment on the cocktails other than that they looked great!

Two thirds of our party were pescatarian so we went very seafood heavy with our order.

The Shrimp cocktail with the lump crab remoulade dip was one of my favorite bites of the evening. The mozzarella sticks with caviar were a hit among the table. My girlfriend's favorite bite was the spicy crab lettuce cups. 

The Sicilian Caesar salad had a wow factor at our table.  Perfect execution on that dish. The other personal favorite of mine for the night was the Pappardelle with the lamb bolognese and ricotta. If I had to pick one dish that was slightly underwhelming it would be the Salt baked sea bass. The tableaide presentation was great but it seemed slightly underseasoned and slightly overcooked. That may be a personal preference and I am needing to nitpick because overall the dinner was amazing!

We finished with the baked spumoni was also had a great tableside presentation. The layers of Gelato were a great way to finish the meal. It could easily be shared by a table of 6 the portion is very generous.

Carne Mare met our extremely high expectations and we look forward to returning. Bravo Chef Andrew Carmellini

I'm really glad Carne Mare is in the Gulch and was excited to come in for a celebratory dinner not too long after they opened. The space is beautiful and I especially love the bar design and vibe. It is dark and moody; a perfect date night spot. 
I started with the alpino cocktail with vodka and blackberry. It was really nice and refreshing without being too sweet. After enjoying the bar for a bit we moved to our table for dinner. We had to try the mozzarella and caviar appetizer and it was exactly what it sounds like. Not really worth it for the price TBH. The complementary bread however was unbelievably good. It was slightly salty with rich butter... absolutely delicious! We also sampled two dozen oysters which were fresh and also delicious. We had a vegetarian with us and brought this up at the beginning of the meal for recommendations. So when we ordered the table side farmers salad for the group to share, we didn't realize it was prepared with pepperoni until we had been served. Luckily she was able to pick out the pepperoni and still ate it even though it wasn't her preference, but this was $15 per person and we didn't get comped for the miss even though we made our server aware. For entrees we ordered the  lobster, prime rib, 12 oz Smoke-Roasted Beet Steak, and Pappardelle. We also had the potatoes and mushroom Marsala as sides. The roasted beet had a great presentation table side which was a really nice touch for the vegetarian. I enjoyed the lobster, but should have ordered the lamb instead which was my first choice. The mushrooms were also a highlight. We had to try dessert and the baked spumoni was very nice and the perfect end to the meal. I look forward to coming back again for my next date night!
uoQdQjVL9wNKd3cJZO33Hw: Food was great, owners were really nice and welcoming. Highly recommend trying the vegetable soup w/ fufu. Plantains were cooked perfectly.

This is the only place in Metro-Nashville I have found to serve authentic West African cuisine. The owners are Nigerian immigrants who offer an authentic experience of culture. If you are expecting an "American-style" polished restaurant, you may have to relax your expectation. However if you want a authentic taste of Nigeria this place will feel like home.

After coming here from the " youtube Fufu and the egusi craze" I decided to come back a for the third time because I just love that soup and their fried plantains!!! I always get their pounded yams, egusi soup (no meat) with a side of plantains! So declious! I will be returning tomorrow (:
f4eJRUZUimlV39x1RK257A:
I stopped by the new Popeyes in my area. This was their second opening day. I was impressed with the staffs customer service and swift pace to get orders out. I decided to dine in and the dinning area was very clean. 
My chicken came out hot and fresh. My red beans and rice was very good. The gentleman in the gray shirt was very attentive to customers. I didn't catch his name, but he may have been a Manager or GM. The staff did a great job for their second day. Keep up the good work and continue to grow! I'll definitely be back again!
22Zhfb78DHpyDlAAnfU1LQ:
This place is really great! Your typical Texas style BBQ. It's cafeteria style serving. You grab a tray and move down the line to pick your selections. The food was great! I would say, perhaps a little cold, but I was also talking a lot so that could have been on me. 
There were many TVs and a bar inside to watch the game. It was too cold to sit outside, but there's a large space set up with tables, games and a bar.
