10/10! Such a great large outdoor space and a little bit of indoor seating too. It's cafeteria style so you walk through with a tray and grab what you want. Absolutely great selection of meats, sides, bread and even dessert. They have a full bar outside too if you want a beverage. 

Everything on the menu here is absolutely amazing. I personally always get the pulled pork, Mac and cheese and banana pudding. My husband tried the brisket and it melts in your mouth. Great potato salad, it's not super heavy with the dressing which i like. We also tried the chocolate cream pie which was almost as great as my grandmothers. 

Good spot for a weekend lunch trip to watch some football.

First time here & as you walk in - you get a warm welcome! It's cafeteria style, so grab a tray and saddle up to the bar (I mean the food bar). 

Started off with Beans and Mac & Cheese, plus some potato salad! The sides are probably the best sides I have had in awhile at a BBQ restaurant.

Then you have to go for the "meats!" Great choices! We had ribs, jalapeño sausage, brisket (lean and fatty and pastrami (lean). The sausage was awesome - not spicy and delicious! The fatty brisket was amazing and the lean was okay. The ribs were a bit dry for me, so that was a bummer (maybe we got there too early?). The pastrami was good but I really should have gone with a fatty cut for that. The sauces on the side were fantastic!

The tea was awesome and they have a full bar outside! The staff there is just fantastic and really customer service focused - staff gets 5 Stars ! 

Plus outdoor and indoor option is fantastic! Indoor is great but their outdoor space has plenty of TVs and seating, plus some space for kids to run around. 

Definitely will be back!
4nqjjCDlieY1Gd5drnD_9w:
This place nails it!  Gonna be a Nashville topspot!  The Sushi rules 2 !!!  

Also Order everything it's fire!

Okay let me start by stating the obvious-they have a killer name. The combination of sushi and tapas is my favorite. The location in Sylvan supply is spot on and their aesthetic is perfect for a quick casual dinner or some late night drinks with friends. Favorite dishes were the karaage chicken, Korean waffle fries and PW wedge (that blue cheese miso dressing is amazeee). 10/10 recommend adding the Russian roulette to your sushi roll. One of the pieces will have a super hot pepper (I think ours was ghost pepper?) and whoever gets that piece has to take the accompanying shot. It was me. No regrets. Can't wait to try the rest of the menu!

Loved everything about this place. The vibe is great, love the smaller cozy space. Our server was really friendly and helpful with the menu options, and checked in on us often. I loved the drinks as well, fun cocktails that were strong and flavorful. I'm really glad to have a restaurant like this in the neighborhood, will definitely be back!
GexpeoTKEG8XabWcHGjywQ: As a Mexican from California, believe me when I say this place is just as authentic as a taco truck you'd find in LA. Their carne asada is delicious in every form. I've had it in the tacos and quesadilla and the chicken is just as good. Oh, and don't sleep on the churros. They might be filled with cream (I'm a churro purist) but they're still the best churros I've found in nashville. Please go here so they stay in business and my favorite taco spot sticks around.
Stopped in for lunch. Great patio and tacos!  I had pork and Barbacoa.  Plus chips and guacamole. I'll go back.

I recently started working in the neighborhood and discovered this place when a coworker suggested it for lunch. I have now been here three times in the past 3 weeks. I've tried the quesadilla and a few of the tacos and would say the quesadilla is a better choice for both the portion size and the flavor. One of the workers suggested I try it with carnitas and it was great! It also comes with a generous portion of sour cream and guac on the side. The food comes out very quick and the service is great, everyone that works here is very friendly. Definitely a go to for a quick and easy lunch.
4YoVUmkpUBNtdYa804Wzvg: There really isn't much to add. The drinks are amazing; creative and beautifully presented. The bartenders are masters of their craft and make great suggestions. The server on my first trip changed my mind about gin. I've tried the donuts and they are like little balls of buttery heaven. Its dark and quiet in the bar and I like that you have to wait to be seated; it keeps the bar from getting to crowded. I also love valet parking. I will go here as much as my bank account allows. Its worth every penny!

The Patterson House's execution of the decidedly Southern approach to the speakeasy is simply masterful.  After passing the unadorned entrance multiple times, we entered the hallway replete with period paintings and bookcases lined with outdated legal research material.  The inside of the bar was shrouded by a simple blue curtain so we waited in the foyer.  We perused the "rules" which basically comprised of not taking photos and being polite.  The Patterson House's clientele consisted mostly of individuals in the 27-35 range with some excited young women in tight fitting yet sophisticated dresses for some appreciated eye candy for the admittedly lecherous tastes of myself and Viper.  There were also some older couples as well as some college aged girls whose sartorial choices appeared to be patterned after their grandmothers.  

We finally made it past the vaunted curtain and entered the bar.  Dimly lit, it probably best serves as a date place.  However, I was in the mood for a higher end experience regardless of company.  I started with the Bacon Old Fashioned.  While my experience did not border on the subliminal, this drink was certainly well crafted by our knowledgeable bartender.  I tried a delicious seasonal Sidecar, the Black & White, and a number of other creations that brought a novel approach to traditional drinks.  I ended with Patterson's House approach to the hotty toddy which was nothing short of amazing.  

The Patterson House's reputation is not simply built on its libations but also on its culinary offerings.  The tater tots tasted like McDonald's hashbrowns and were perhaps a bit too salty for my tastes.  Having said that, they were tasty enough and the light aioli was a decent complement to the dish.  More impressive was a delectable fried goat cheese offering served with a gorgeous red pepper sauce.  The crowning achievement, however, was the hallowed Elvis Panini; they use Benton's bacon, peanut butter, and banana to provide this updated approach to the legendary musician's favorite treat.  

For those looking for a classy, quiet night out with a sophisticated, "Old South" clientele, The Patterson House is a great start in Nashville.

I love a good cocktail, you know the kind that are made with small batch liquors and have ingredients in them you've never heard of before & you want to ask the bartender what they are but everybody else looks as if they know exactly what it is so you don't ask, I like those kind of cocktails! That's what I found at The Patterson house.Think hipster meets booshie and throw in a pinch of speakeasy-that's this place. It's hard to find from the street because they don't have a sign out front, the only thing they have is on the door, and visually it's basically covered up by another restaurants sign so you're a bit confused on if you're walking into the right place. Once you walk in there is a small lounge area and then a velvet curtain. Behind this curtain is the bar where you need to be seated by the host who wore a green velvet smoking jacket and black skinny jeans. I gave it 3 stars because the price of the drinks was a bit hefty- $12 a piece & the service wasn't friendly. The cocktails we had though were delicious and I'd go back.
_4A9jOgyQGrkI9me_TmRmw:
Holy cacao!!!
This place is a chocolate lover's dream. 

We came here on a cold December morning in search of a delicious cup of hot chocolate. And boy, did we come to the right place!!

I ordered the drinking chocolate. It was sin in liquid form. Thick, smooth, rich, and had a perfect balance of chocolate and sugar. I highly recommend. 

The regular hot chocolate with hazelnut was good also, though for me, it didn't compare to that drinking chocolate!!

Very neat little place. Must check it out for brunch, and the chocolate/whiskey pairings !!
Had coffee and chocolate- absolutely fell in love with the tastes and also the atmosphere.  This place is so cute and fun- I can't wait to come back!  Also- best coffee I have had in a long time!

Why is this place not packed every minute?!? The line across the street was long, yet we were seated immediately at Tempered and treated to the BEST drinking chocolate I've ever had, and the savory brie Belgian waffles with dark chocolate and elderberry jam? Game changing. If you like chocolate, you should visit. If you LOVE chocolate, you should move in.
4SRl5od0lY33oK1GMbA23A:
Husk was an interesting  experience. Here is what I loved about this restaurant...
- Awesome venue, it is an old Tennessee mansion. With the updates, unique light fixtures, decor and artwork it's is definitely a visually pleasing spot.
- our server was very friendly and very informed on the menu and wine list. She also greeted us with a glass of champagne to celebrate our anniversary.
- The wine list was vast and organized by soil type! This is something I have never seen before, it was really neat. The prices were very reasonable and very tasty! 
- we ordered the charcuterie plate, which is done in house. It was to die for! Loved it!
- I love the farm to table concept and how this chef stays true to his roots by only using items from the south
Things I did not love about Husk
- obviously this chef is a phenomenal chef and can make miracles with the staples he can use but the menu was limited. 
- The farm raised beef was a beef shoulder prepared like a filet and it just didn't quite work.
- They only offered 3 sides, cornbread being one and they ran out. So again just a very limited menu.
- as the meal came to an end we ordered a cappuccino with baileys they couldn't prepare because they weren't able to use espresso and they weren't allowed to use Baileys. She always had a suggestion of something similar but it just didn't compare.

We are happy to have gone and enjoyed our experience. I feel like this chef is really staying true to his concept and vision. Husk is definitely and experience but maybe not for everyone.

The dry rubbed hot wings are the best anywhere in the WORLD!!!

The dishes here are very original and super tasty!
They also have an great wine list!

We went there for brunch.  They were able to seat us before our reservation.  We started with the deviled eggs which were perfectly seasoned but not too strong. Next we ordered the country ham and biscuit.  The ham was perfectly salty and melted in my mouth.  Finally I ordered the coddled egg with boutin and pickled tomatoes.  This was my favorite thing I ate. The egg cooked in the griddle after it was stirred which allowed me to have one soft boiled and one sunny side up. My husband had the shrimp and grits which must have been delicious because he finished it before I could try a bite.
oIH5YWPy_g61YXM6R900Wg: Great experience...food was delicious and came out super fast, waiter was friendly. All around a good place.

This was our first time here. 

I went with a group of couple of friends. 

The service was amazing. 

This place seemed like a popular hit because it was also packed all night. 

We had several drinks. Everything we ate was also amazing. 

The chicken breast was so juicy and moist. 
Baked beans was amazing. 
I also had a bite of my friends black bean burger and the cherry smoked hot wings were also delicious. 

Theres also live music. 

Everything was LITTTTT. 

Definitely a place to come next time Im back in town. 

I definitely recommend it. 

I even bought a couple of shirts and hats before I left.

We stopped in here for a quick dinner between conference sessions. We ordered the fried green tomatoes, Memphis BBQ ribs, and the shrimp n' grits. The food was well seasoned and flavorful. The service was okay. It was challenging to have a conversation with the loud music playing in the background but then again I need to remind myself I was in Nashville where music is in the culture and played everywhere. I wouldn't mind stopping back in and trying other items on the menu. Although you can't go wrong with the ribs and shrimp n' grits!
YD9-VUxnobct_Jv4E9SAvA:
I was tenuous about our relationship at first, but Dose has worked its way into my heart and earned its place as my go-to coffee shop.

Why? Dose clearly cares about serving high quality foods and beverages. They make their syrups and chai concentrate in house -- you can see the actual vanilla bean resting in the vanilla syrup on the shelf! They sell Counter Culture coffee and Askinosie small batch bean-to-bar chocolate. They carry almond milk (the superior milk alternative). They'll brew you an individual cup of coffee with your roast of choice. They feature a generous selection of house-made pastries and each one is extremely tasty. Some are even gluten-free!

What really pleases me about Dose is that the staff manages to make quality their priority without being douches about it. No one ever makes me feel like I don't know enough about coffee, or that I'm ridiculous for asking for an extravagant amount of almond milk in my cup. Honestly, I almost always order like I'm Meg Ryan in "When Harry Met Sally" (minus the orgasm), and no one even so much as blinks. In fact, they often act as if my special requests are *good* ideas.

The atmosphere can be a bit sterile, and the prices a bit high. But hey, high quality ingredients cost money. And while the breakfast/lunch options are innovative and tasty, the sandwiches are always dry. Seriously, I go to special lengths to order some kind of mayonnaise on every sandwich I buy, and that's often not enough to combat the dryness. If you're reading this, Dose, you guys should work on that... 

Still, these flaws are minor, and I honestly can't think of a coffee shop in Nashville that I'd prefer to frequent.
Great coffee and bagels and it's only block from our hotel. Convenient and delish!

Taste and quality of coffee is very good. They actually implement the pour over method, which I understand it takes more time to brew. But for people who are on the run and just want some quick coffee, you shouldn't go here if you don't have the patience. Otherwise, definitely try this place out! Wait is a little longer for coffee but it's quite the experience.
3iUCCf1FWmjlFbGYvBgf9w: While in Nashville for the weekend, finding a BBQ stop was a must.. There was a long line out the door, but luckily we were encouraged to head upstairs with no wait.  The atmosphere was the best part! Loved the rooftop setup, very casual with live music.  Food was good, I tried the brisket, potato salad, and mac n cheese.  Would definitely recommend for the experience!

This place was awesome. Bar-b-que was delicious and affordable. I thoroughly enjoyed the brisket sandwich and we all split the ribs. You can see them smoking the pigs on the roof/outdoor area. Sauce was delicious and vinegar based which I enjoyed on both. Atmosphere was awesome. We hung out in the upstairs bar/outdoor area, and played darts and shuffleboard as well we watched Sunday football. Beer prices were pretty cheap and mixies came strong and also affordable. 

We ate here twice on a 4 day trip so that should speak volumes about what we thought of this place. We also had to buy a few souvenirs.

We figured we had to try BBQ while in Nashville and came here on a friends recommendation. This BBQ did not disappoint. We ordered one of the sampler platters and the brisket was my favorite, tender and juicy. However, I wish they had more of a selection of BBQ sauces, only one was kept on the table which was good and tangy but quite liquidy. The sides were all top notch. The broccoli salad with bacon was my favorite side. The coleslaw and mac and cheese were also great. The sides that came with the sampler that was suppose to be for 3-4 people were very small, more of individual portions. Overall, this BBQ was A-mazing. Would come back next time I'm in town.
sjuU4XWZl1Ed14s676hDQQ: Really well stocked Asian market! They have fresh kimchi and the deli sells Bahn Mi. Cash only!
I love their banh mi! I only stopped by Interasian when I'm in the area and pick up a dozen of the sandwiches. Its literary the best banh mi!

i stopped by interasian market for a banh mi and some shopping today and was pleasantly surprised. it wasn't the best banh mi i've ever had (new york is my point of comparison, and this is the only place i've tried to get one in nashville), but it was fresh, substantial, and a terrific deal. while i waited for my sandwich, i was able to pick up some thai and japanese eggplant, which i've never seen at my local kroger, for a good price. i also grabbed a bag of frozen dumplings to try out. the store is a bit cluttered/overwhelming, but i will definitely be going back for sandwiches and groceries! they just carry so much stuff you can't find at standard grocery stores in the area. downside: cash only!
pNIXTOP7tQm029PakX7LyQ: Great hot dogs with some interesting toppings. Good spot for a quick casual bite to eat if it's nice outside. To-go if it's not.
Awsome place with fun patio/open layout, really great place to hangout with friends. Dog was good, not to pricey, and friendly staff. 5 out of 5 stars

I just happed to drive by and thought I would try it out. Really glad I did. The hot dogs and chili tots were very good.  I had the music city dog. I will have to remember this place and try some of the other dogs they have.
781EsVHOR92qSMkjS39epQ: This new little spot is awesome. They have an arcade area with free games. Everything from Ms Pacman, to original Nintendo, and shuffleboard to connect 4. In addition, this place has a solid draft beer selection, as well as a formidable whiskey selection. The food was too notch, I had a vegan meatloaf. I was skeptical, but the waitress supported my decision and I'm glad she did because it was awesome. Highly recommended.
Excellent service from Tyler! Food was awesome and happy hour was great! Will certainly be back !!!

I would not call Two Bits as much as  bar as an emporium of all that is good in life. Walking into this garage style emporium of all that is good, I immediately saw two grown men playing Jumbo Jenga- the classic sign of good times ahead. I think my head nearly exploded when I walked into this place. I've heard great things about Two bits... but really, you cannot capture how incredible this place is (well I will because Im THAT good).  Two Bits transported me back to a better time...a time when trouble shooting your video game meant blowing on the cartridge. It was like being a 10 year old kid again.. but better yet.. this ecstatic 10 year old kid could play Galaga while drinking a Tennessee Brew Works IPA-legally. Life don't get better then that folks. TKO. Foreal.

I think I almost died when I saw their console selection. Super Nintendo, N64, Xbox... blah blah the new ones that I could care less about. (I bet they even have that complete failure of a gaming system- Sega Dreamcast). Anyway, let me tell you about the games. They have your classics- Mario Bros, mario kart, smash brothers, andddd GOLDEN EYE. AND ITS FREE. I came with a few guy friends and they choose to play shuffle board.. but everyone in the fun emporium is super friendly so I had no problem scooting up to some strangers in a booth and starting up a game of Mario Kart with them. (Of note, Two Bits has an interesting mash up of characters. There is a mix of young professionals with your occasional college student types. But as any place that carries classic video game consoles... well, lets just say I had some run ins where you can imagine the scenario would be captured by NATURE or National Geographic... First Encounters of the Female Kind).  Anyway, back on track (if you are still reading)...

Games: Classic Arcade- Galaga, Pacman, Hunter 2, NFL Blitz, WHERE IS TEKKEN?!?!?!, A bunch of board games 

Games Needed: Skee ball, TEKKEN

Beer: Such a solid solid collection. All your classic TN brews: Yazoo, Mayday, calfkiller, TN brew works... Happy hour specials from 2-7 where you get $1 off drafts or get $3 Coors Banquet/Miller Light. I have to do a particular acknowledgment at how awesome their Bar team is. I met CAS tonight and he was so great. We talked quite a bit about the brew scene in Nashville and he was so helpful in regards to educating me more about brews.

Events: Tuesday night is BINGO. I cannot possibly be more excited about that (I am completely being honest). Wednesday night is trivia hosted by Nerdy Talk who I hear is a hilarious woman.  I thought that meant trivia was all nerd trivia-- its not..but if it were.. I would win. 


BEER CLUB--- This is a new program that they are rolling out at Two Bits. Essentially you get a passport of 39 beers (physical book where you get stamps). If you try all 39 (no more then 3 stamps per visit) you can attain ETERNAL GLORY on the Two bits wall with your name and a personalized Stein. I have always identified myself as a high achiever and ETERNAL GLORY is something I strive for.. so clearly you WILL see Chalupa Batman on that wall.

Overall, I will be back. As a regular.
qGsdi_PFuqwzN8RqzeWq_w: Super fun place. Met many fun people there and danced the night away. Drinks are kind of expensive but other than that, if you are in your 20's you will have a blast here.

Here for CMA Fest and came for lunch; 20 minute wait so we checked out the upstairs bar. Very cool venue, drinks were great. Was impressed with the live band playing. 

For lunch we sat on the main floor and had the friend chicken sandwich with hot sauce and bacon. Absolutely hands down the best chicken sandwich to the point that I'd like to go back to get it again during our stay.

The menu does not do the food justice. All four of us LOVED our meal and I'm not even sure love is a strong enough adjective. We ate off each other's plates and everything was amazing. Props to the kitchen! We had the fish tacos (to die for), mac n cheese (amazing) the FGL burger and the flatbread. All highly recommend!! Will definitely be back!
ooUI-JC2tu0J-vavmOHZYQ: My Vietnamese friend told us this was the best place in Nashville to grab Vietnamese food and he was right! Went on a Sunday night and it was packed. It's a tiny spot so you just kind of wait by the door for others to get up. Luckily we didn't have to hover for long. Husband had the pho and I had the soft egg noodle dish along with some spring rolls. Everything was soooooo good! Tried my friends spicy beef broth and decided I need to try that next time. Love this little local gem!

So far, this is the best pho I've had in Nashville! I've been around to the other places around Charlotte and in terms of flavor and the sweet scent of pho on your clothes when you leave, this place reeks of authenticity. The place was also filled with other Vietnamese people, so that was a good sign. The variety in pho wasn't there so much- there's only the beef variety, so no luck for those who want chicken. But it was certainly tasty, with good portions and fair price. The soybean milk was a little sweet. But otherwise, it was definitely good. I'm looking forward to trying the other items on the menu. While it isn't the best I've had, it's the best I've had here and that definitely counts for something.

I have become a regular here. Family owned and staffed by a "band of brothers", friends that you can tell like each other. Husband and wife and three sons all help out and made this enterprise successful with simply good food and good service. If you like authentic Vietnamese cuisine you've come to the right place.
6Pu5MKIWbn6FO-oebSSrYQ:
After popular reviews on Nashville Guru and Yelp, we decided to wait an hour and 15 minutes for a table at The Southern.  If you are considering, reservations would be the way to go.  Main room is high energy and lots of action where you can see the kitchen.  Hostess employees could definitely be more friendly and we were a little bummed to be sat in the back room away from the action.  Another item worth noting, just so you can be prepared, is you sink down in the leather chairs when you sit.  I heard several people mentioning as they sat. They probably need to get new chairs soon.  

I'm giving four stars because of the hostess, sitting in the back room, and the lousy chairs. The service and the meal were excellent, though.  If you go, make a reservation in advance, ask to be seated in the main room, and enjoy!

This is one of the best restaurants I've ever been to for a couple reasons. It has a great lay out from outdoor seating , three private party rooms, indoor dinning . The menu has several unique items on just as the decor in the place is unique. They have a good wine, beer and whiskey offering.
I suggest reservations because we tried walking in Friday night and there was an hour wait. We went back Saturday and we so glad we did.
Great service and food! If you like oysters then this is the place to be. They have a huge selection of costal oysters and a great drink selection! Top Nashville location!
EuP-3_cOfA8MaHPimNeJ-g: I think they deserve 5 stars! Food is delicious, and quite reasonable in price. I think the ambiance is great as well. It's my favorite take-out place right now!
Best Turkish food in Nashville. So cheap for what you get, too. Tabbouleh is amazing, as are the lamb Kuzy and yogurt soup.

A great little Turkish restaurant. You're not going to get anything too fancy here, and the service was not necessarily the best, but the food is delicious and the staff was very friendly. There seem to be a lot of Turkish restaurants in this area, and I woefully cannot claim to have explored many of them; so this may or may not be the one to check out. But, what I do feel confident saying is that if you do choose this place you'll likely leave satisfied!
O_l8AIlR2RJvNPq4CDMbtQ: We absolutely loved our time here enjoying the evening while listening to talented musicians! We had just walked around the Broadway Street and we were overwhelmed by crowded streets of people, so it was so nice to walk to Printers Alley where things were a lot quieter. Ten dollar cover for entrance was totally worth it! The musicians and the crowd all seemed to be really enjoying their time together. If we are back in Nashville another time, we are definitely coming back here!

Our Uber driver told us about this little bar.  It was so much more than we expected.  It is personal, deep, bluesy.  The music is out of this world.  Soulful, rich, invigorating.  It was wonderful blues music!  
We also enjoyed the food.  We started with alligator bites.  They were plentiful and delicious.   We left, walked around a bit and decided to come back to finish our night.  
On our second visit we ate dinner.  It was much busier during the evening.  It still had that personal bluesy feel though.  I had the shrimp Po Boy.  I have to say, I've had shrimp Po Boy's in NOLA and this was the best shrimp Po Boy I have ever had.  I cannot even describe how delicious the shrimp were.  They were breaded and fried to perfection. 
My bf had the voodoo burger sliders.  He said they were outstanding.  He was still talking about them the next day. 
We are always looking for new bars in Nashville and this is certainly on our list.  If you like great blues, a good atmosphere and tasty food, this is THE place to go.  We cannot wait to go back!!

I miss this place!! You enter, pay the cover, and -WHAM- there you are, right smack in the middle of the show! There is a high stage on your right, seating to the left, and if you cross to the other side of the stage, you'll find the stairs leading to the balcony. Your seats are so close to the stage & the band, it's great! 

I never ordered the food here or sat downstairs, but the drinks & the balcony are great. Never heard a band here that I didn't love! Aww I miss Nashville!
EV2vO0cs_zQE5bkcfEi0qA:
My favorite coffee spot in Nashville. It may not stack up with what some other cities have to offer, but it is the best we have around here. It fits in perfectly with the neighborhood. The vibe is just really right for this kind of place. The drinks are good, the breakfast is delicious, and you can't help feeling like you are part of something when you are there. They have rotating displays of local artists on the walls. They have a comfortable inside and a great deck. The staff is as laid back as they come. My favorite drink on a hot day is the Icy Samoan!

It is truly refreshing to see a cafe that doesn't take itself too seriously.
Good coffee, a bit pricey, slow internet, but a great porch area for nice fall afternoons!

A quaint, hidden gem that native Nashvillans love, Bongo Java and Fido has luscious coffees and a delectable menu.

My favorite (and very simply) menu item is the Bowl o' Hummus, as it is flavorful, simple, and blends beautifully with a light roast on their patio on a breezy spring day. My kids will eat their own plate, and refuse to share with me - which is a good testament to just how yummy it is! 

This easy-going, laid-back, cafe is just what the doctor ordered for the simple pleasures in life.
shbDs7N86yTsfZLh4Nie_A:
I have it 5 stars because I always give 5 stars unless a business really pisses me off. I love Chicago Deep Dish pizza. This isn't one; it only looks like one; crust has no taste & if the ingredients on the Signature are on this pizza, they must have been liquified in a blender & mixed into the sauce. Maybe other menu items are good....???
On a more positive note, The waiter is nice and the air conditioner feels great!

"Why is this place always so empty?" you ask. I have a theory.

The service is real bad. You'd think you were in a skate shop until they bring out a delicious pizza. Your server will probably not be very professional or helpful, but they will get you drinks and a pizza. There are not a lot of deep dish options in Nashville, and I'm a fan of this place. Plus, it's fairly inexpensive. $15 for a pizza that feeds two people? Can't beat it.

If you highly value customer service or if you can only eat deep dish in chicago, you won't like this place. If you DO like average, inexpensive deep dish pizza with a casual atmosphere in a hoppin' part of Nashville, you WILL like it.
The pizza is good, I would not consider the deep dish, true deep dish. It is more so pan pizza. Still very flavorful! Quick service!
dB7-TdEG4llXz9HwpMAZPg: Found this little bar just off Broadway. It has a great locals feel to it, the music was great... great performer but not so loud you can't still carry on conversation. Dennis the bartender/manager was phenomenal, and had a great personality. The menu is a "bar food" menu but the wings we amazing, the best tater tots I've ever eaten, and really great burgers and pork (we had a big group). The food came out really quickly as well. I would definitely return!!

Was here over the weekend and had the time of my life! So much fun we actually spent most of the weekend there! The bartender Kasi was such a delight, even shared her personal stash of candy with me! Amazing service, yummy food and the music was on point!! Def going back there a lot!!!

Good environment with a great band playing the day we went. Less crowded than some of the other restaurants on Broadway. As far as gluten free, not a huge selection but managed to get a burger with no bun.
fzLzJGsGMN-Qb_UDDvsDVA:
After a Pampered Chef party, a friend and I decided to come here for a quick drink and a low-key night. 

Well, the low-key night turned into an early Sunday morning,  All I can say is you have to come here.  When we walked in, karaoke was going on.  We ordered a drink and started having fun.  We  both became friends with the tables around us and had a great night.

Our server, (unfortunately, we didn't get her name), was the BOMB!!

If you're a cheesestick fan, you should definitely have those.  Also, you'll get about 10 of them.

You should definitely check this place out!

My friend and I ate here last night. There was a Packers game on so it was pretty crowded but we walked right in and got a seat. We ordered fried pickles but I think our server forgot about them (it happens, not mad). I ended up eating a chicken Caesar salad which was pretty good. My friend got a huge burger that looked really great. She thought it was fantastic. Good bar food. Would definitely go back for the atmosphere and maybe a burger next time.

I LOVE THIS PLACE. WE GO THERE EVERY WEEKEND. THE OWNER AND I HAVE BECOME GREAT FRIENDS. The staff is amazing. The cook is amazing and comes up with new meals weekly. It's very clean as well. They play all the sports there and have great deals on drinks and food. This is the only place we go.
Cs0adaMTxJWTDSzuQ3vheg: The Listening Room Cafe was one of the best parts of my trip to Nashville. I have been following their Instagram for a few years wanting to see a show in person. My party of four secured "tickets" to the Monday night Song Suffragettes show and we were not disappointed. The ladies had an amazing amount of talent and I cannot wait to see how they shape the music industry in the years to come. The venue was spacious, clean and relaxed. Our party tried the fried picked and green tomatoes. We also had three meals we shared. While the food had good flavor, it was a bit cold. The waitresses were busy seating and serving so we didn't want to disturb them. That was our only complaint. The experience definitely made this a memorable night. I will visit again in the future as I know this is a newer location and they are working out the kinks still.
Really good venue for listening to music. Only had drinks, so I can't give a food review, but the staff and bartenders were very attentive & friendly.
Best live music performance I've ever seen, period!!! We did both the 6:00 and 8:30 shows!!!
QJzp26mYQuLtLhF5heEHrQ: This place is definitely the highlight of the broadway strip. ASK FOR THE BARTENDER JENNIFER ON THE FIRST FLOOR! She is literally an angel sent down to earth! She is beautiful, sweet, funny and intelligent. She came to check on our party of 3 multiple times and made some hilarious jokes along the way. She's definitely the highlight of the staff here. This place is super busy so getting the bartenders attention can be hard sometimes but Jennifer always came and checked on us in a timely manner, she was so great. I'd come here anytime I'm in town, no questions asked, just to see Jennifer. Such a gem. 10/10 recommend this place!
Great spot to listen to music and grab a bucket or three of beer.  Longest bar I've ever scene.
I was only in for one daytime beer but the band was rocking and the service was great.  I hope to go back before I leave.
6TUOsIU1vNNQcoauKrZFCw:
From the moment you step into 417 Union you are catapulted back in time to the 1940's. This eatery is presented in the style of a diner around the end of World War II. But upon arriving for breakfast, the decor isn't what dropped my jaw. It was the scintillating menu offerings. 

Cinnamon Apple Pancakes, Portabella and Roasted Red Pepper Hash and an awesomely stuffed Blue Ribbon Omlette (sliced prime rib, mushrooms, onions & gorgonzola cheese) all shouted "eat me!" from the menu. 

What won the shouting contest, while contemplating the Lox and Bagel breakfast, was the Smoked Salmon Hash; smoked Alaskan salmon grilled with onions and seasoned potatoes then topped with three over-medium eggs. Served with sliced tomatoes and toast.

This was a heavenly selection, unlike anything I've ever tasted before.  Nice big chunks of homestyle cut potatoes and the salmon's rich flavor mixed in with every bite.  The tomatoes were tender, fresh from the local farmer's market and the toast was thick-cut and served with blackberry jam.  I wanted to get up and do a little dance - but that would've meant pausing the fork, and the mouth was having none of that pausing business.

About halfway through, I sat back and actually took in the sights, which will be a loving time trip for those who love the era.  Aided by piped-in music featuring lots of big band, swing and crooners of the period, this was absolutely one of the bests breakfast visits I've had in years.  This would be a definite re-visit next time coming to Nashville.

Great brunch spot in a very cool old house turned restaurant.  Had several breakfast cocktails on the menu and a good variety of options to choose from for breakfast. The chicken and waffles was amazing. They used boneless chicken which is much appreciated. They make their own syrup which was the perfect compliment to the chicken and waffles!

Went here for breakfast and this place was delicious! The service was great and a very friendly staff! The biscuits were soft and fresh and the gravy was good and very light. I got two eggs, toast, bacon, and grits and it was amazing. My mother got the southwestern scrambled eggs (I think that is what it is called) with potatoes and gorillas. Had a fast of her dish and it was very good! I highly recommend this place to anyone who lives in Nashville or is visiting!
fEfGvg0vKsKel9nvUdBTMQ:
A Nashville staple, I just have to give my two cents on Rotier's.  Looking for a great burger?  You don't need to go any further!  I love the cheeseburger on a regular bun, though they are famous for their french bread burgers.  They are good also, I'm just a tad more traditional.

Everything here is a la carte...in the summertime, I always order a side of sliced tomatoes and fries (fries are only so-so but I still eat them of course!).  

It's a pretty small cramped place, and pretty dark--I usually don't like going on a really pretty day out, but that's just me.  Great place to go for a hangover helper, or just a casual lunch.  Check it out!
Nothing fancy, but the best burgers in town. I love the grilled cheeseburger and chocolate milkshakes. The service is mediocre, but always the same friendly faces. A great Nashville original.

I admit I went here just for the burger I've heard so much about. And for only $6.99, it was an amazing burger. I also dabbled in the shrimp basket but didn't care much for then or the cocktail sauce. Especially for $7.99. Nope. Stick to the burger. The closest thing I can compare it to is Gabby's. With bentons bacon it was almost the sane burger, but the French bread took it a step further and made it better. Don't get me wrong I love Gabby's but rotiers bread didn't fall apart and was not as messy to eat. It was enjoyable due to the easiness.
oNj7x0P4BywJBnekNjt3UA:
My favorite mexican joint in Nashville.

I hear so much about other mexican restaurants that just usually underperform for me, but La Terraza has never hit a sour note.  The service is ALWAYS friendly and the margaritas are good.  If I had one complaint it's that the hotness/spiciness of the queso and salsa isn't always consistent.  Twice in the many times we've been there, it was so hot we couldn't eat it... although we are admittedly gringos.  

Try the "tacos authenticos" ala carte.  Ask for them with onion and cilantro.  It's the best "taco truck" taco I've ever had.  I'll frequently go there when I'm craving taco truck tacos, and I get chips and salsa and margaritas to boot!

This is by far our favorite Mexican restaurant in the Nashville area.  The staff is welcoming, especially the main manager who has worked there for at least the past 7 years.  They always bring fresh, warm chips plus salsa as soon as you sit down, so you have something to enjoy while waiting on your main course.  I'd highly recommend the tacos authenticos and the fajitas, and my wife recommends the gringa.  Kids will find lots of options as well.  No Mexican meal is complete without a margarita, and La Ta makes some great ones.

On the weekends, there's occasionally some live Mexican music, so keep that in mind when picking which side of the restaurant you want to sit on.

We highly recommend La Terrazza.
Food and service are excellent! Despite how busy they were, our waitress kept checking on to see if we were okay. I ordered Nachos fajitas and it was delicious and very flavorful!
KAR-LpoXQc-awbriXyoxPQ: It's a pretty darn big mall if you are into malls (: My Son wanted to buy some Nike _ Nashville T shirts. I waisted some time in the Massage chairs in the middle of the mall. Well worth a couple bucks when your sore. If your in the area and want to mall shop it's a good choice.

Personally, I love everything about Opryland mills. The food court serves everything from Chinese food to frozen yogurt, there and new and not so basic food places. For instance it's not just a McDonald's and other types of fast food. There's Greek food , sandwich places, Starbucks , etc. there are many stores, and each store offers varieties of things. There are original stores also, such as one of my favorites , earthbound, and a lot of stores are priced reasonably.

This is definitely one of the places I frequent when I visit Nashville. Since I live close to Gurnee Mills (in Illinois) and get sick of it there, I'm sure I wouldn't love Opry Mills as much as I do if I lived in Nashville. In saying that, I always leave the mall with multiple pairs of shoes. There's plenty of places to use as incentives if the kids behave, like a stingray feeding place, Dave N Busters, carousels, etc. There's also a few quaint shops that carry unusual items. The outdoorsy store (can't remember the name) is also very cool with aquariums and carnival-like shooting range. So if you like to shop and need to keep the kids entertained as well, this is the place. Just make sure you don't go overboard like I always do and have more luggage than you'd like to travel home with.
T3Z5p2_2tJrFdD3ohUE5Jg: I work very close to Cafe LeCrumbs and I eat here often becasue it is so close, but the food is awesome!  I am always looking for healthy fresh options and this is the perfect place.  The homemade Chicken Salad Sandwich is one of my favorites as well as the Veggie Sandwich.  I am usually a meat girl but this sandwich is amazing.  The prices are a little more expensive but you get what you pay for!  It is well worth the extra $2.00.  I would also like to comment on the cafe itself.  It is very clean, laid back, and nice place to escape from the normal hussle of downtown.  I have known the owners for the past 4 years from just eating here but they are always friendly and always stop to say Hello.

So here's the story: I've been using Yelp for a couple of years and our experience at Cafe Le Crumbs convinced me to add my first review on Yelp.

My wife and I were just spending 1 night in Nashville and the guy at the hotel recommended this place.

It was 5 PM and were looking for a coffee to relax after our touristic day.

The place seemed closed already for the day when we showed up, so we were about to leave...when someone - who turned out to be the owner- opened and warmly welcomed us inside and took our order right away. Turned out, her and her husband are the nicest people you'll ever meet and we stayed there with them for an hour chatting having a great time. Forgot to say that we ate the most delicious chocolate cupcake together with our Nespresso coffee. 
Cannot recommand this place enough! They have great coffee, nice food and cakes. 

Really but Really nice and cool people

This is the best lunch in downtown. There's not a bad option on the menu. The food is always fresh and delicious. Please don't leave without trying the carrot cake. It's the best I've ever tasted. The owners are warm and welcoming.
nq0EG2UwTuYV84hbUYteWg: Shrimp Tacos and the quesadilla are amazing and the beer selection is great and always changing. Awesome bar tenders too! They keep up to date with the changing selection and know their stuff! Great addition to the Nations!

Great  place just visited on lunch. Sat at the bar and received great service and great recommendations. 

I had the salmon blt  minus the bacon. It was so tasty.

I will be back. Sorry no pic.

Made a quick pit stop for some music stuff and just ran into this precious gem! A fairly new restaurant, but the service and food are top notch! Trendy decor. A "must try" place. Draft selection is enviable!
zewTzLyiEQA8LxzNkpAa3Q:
I was fully expecting to be disappointed at NY Pie: it had a big corporate-looking sign, it was built into the Nashville West shopping center, and I hadn't heard a thing about it from anyone.  All this spoke to me that this was going to be a fraud and a betrayal of the New York style that haunted my memory from living in the Empire State.

Well, shoot: judging a book by it's cover can sometimes be a real kick in the nuts because this is actually pretty darn good.  It translated the New York style in my memories right down to that one little slightly burned corner and the extra dimension of flavor it added to the experience.  The cheese was right, the sauce was right, and the outer crust was really tasty.  When I lived in New York, I wondered why some people didn't eat the crust: when I left New York, I realized why.  It's often to big, too doughy, and flavorless.  A good New York pie doesn't have that problem.

The owner must have thought I looked lonely, so while he worked, he talked to me about how he makes his pizzas, where he came from, and the various menu items.

The place approximates the little Italian pizza joint as best as it can, and the prices are pretty reasonable.   This is a good place for a slice if your deep in the West Side.
Awesome New York style pizza and there is enough food for two people If you get something else besides a slice.

We were driving through Tennessee and decided to get some pizza. Stopped here and do NOT regret it! Delicious! Got the Sriracha pizza. I personally recommend getting at least a 10" over a slice. Go to NY Pizza if you can!
eF4YPHVUOlsGBRfIH-fOmQ: The Basement East has ping pong tables and tournaments. The stage has a great sound system. The bands seem to always be pretty good, with a variety of musical choices. There's also a nice outdoor patio on the back of the building that serves food as well. All in all it's a pretty sweet place.

This place is quickly becoming my new hang. Small but very solid menus. Try anything with the habanero sauce. The portobello press is awesome!  Good rotating beer selection as well. Parking across the street in the fire house lot or down the street near the atm if the lot is full for a show. 
Also the venue, if you are considering going sounds good and is quite large.   Check out a show sometime!

Awesome place with great vibes! The entire staff was great and the stage setup was perfect! Parking wasn't the best but wasn't bad either! The setup and stage really made everyone kind of feel like family. The sound and lighting was perfect, unlike a lot of smaller venues that tend to over do it. Overall one of the best small venues I've been to & I'll definitely be back! 

If you enjoy good people, great drinks, and awesome live music this is the place for you!!
yK5WnMiXZe5hmNFx0xXb-w: The young workers were great at midnight feeding us. They were very considerate. The food was made to order. A bit greasy but expected. The amount was small but the price was too. Their milkshakes are delicious! You have to try one!!!

First time here.  This is a cool, different QSR to all of the other chains out there.  The burgers & fries tasted good, and the shakes were awesome (banana pudding is the one we had).  

My wife had the 1/4 lb burger, and I had the double that was on the combo list.  The burgers weren't huge by any means, nor were they remarkable, but they were good (and different) for the price.  The fries weren't greasy and had a nice crisp to them.  We had to try the shakes, but we ended up getting the same one.  Banana pudding was probably one of the best "fast-food" shakes I've had.  There were chunks of banana & cookies in it, especially at the bottom.  We had to use a spoon!  We each had a combo of a burger, fries (double order), and a shake, all for $13 and change.  I thought the price was good for what we received.

The place was packed at 2pm!  I think most of it was due to a lot of Belmont students being there for some reason, but the place was still busy even after they left.  There was something a little screwy with their drive-thru area.  They had an attendant outside asking every person that came through if they were going through the drive-thru or parking.  There were cones set up to divide the two lines (drive-thru & inside dining).  As soon as you pulled into the parking lot, you had to stop to talk with the attendant first.  If more than 2 people happened to turn in at the same time, it'd back up into the street.  I'm not sure if the person is out there every day, but it kind of looked like it.  Maybe, it was only during their rush hour.  Parking was definitely tight.

The cashiers weren't rude, but they weren't overly nice either.  They had their "script" of what to say to people, but I didn't notice any of them even cracking a smile.  A mumbled "have a good day" or "my pleasure" is worse than not saying it at all.  The best part about our experience was a guy whose name I found out is DJ.  He was the busboy/custodian (?).  He was practically running around the place non-stop cleaning tables, sweeping, and refilling lids, straws, napkins, etc.  I've never seen a restaurant so busy at 2pm, and I've never (never!) seen someone work as hard as he was in a fast-food place.  It wasn't 10 seconds after someone left a table before he was there cleaning it.  The icing on the cake was that he would briefly stop sweeping or cleaning each time someone passed him to leave, look them in the eye, and say "have a good day/have a nice 4th of July", and he said it with a genuine smile!  A real smile!  These things are like spotting Bigfoot in fast-food restaurants, or even dine-in restaurants for that matter.  The cashiers at the front could definitely learn something from this guy.  Here was DJ, a guy that simply took pride in his work and genuinely seemed to wish people a good day.  That is few and far between these days.  

If anyone from the Cook Out company is reading this, DJ needs not only a promotion, but he should be recognized for his work ethic.  Of course, I say the next thing not knowing his complete skillset, but I'd rather see him behind the register or even as a manager rather than the people that were there.

If your looking for a good and CHEAP drive through besides the standard McDonald's, Dairy Queen then this if the place to go! My husband and I were on a random drive and had planned to go through somewhere else when we saw this place and turned back around and boy are we glad we did!

We drove through the drive through although you can go into eat it does look small but none the less the menu was full and a tad confusing but we quickly decided on two double burgers with bacon may and cheese a hot dog as well for the hubby and a shake for him because how can you say no to a shake with a burger?

Pulled up food was fresh and ready, first for my husband was the hotdog and it was delicious, had a slight bbq sauce flavor to it....yum yum! Burgers were excellent fresh bacon none of that microwave crap as my husband noted huge plus for him! 

His peanut butter fudge shake was so good that we drove through once we were done eating in the car for me to get a chocolate shake!

The best pay for all that food and drinks we walked away spending roughly $22 we will be back!
uvhX5n6AiIEx9lSiyCKxlg:
Easily 5 stars if you want a great double cheeseburger, fries, and a milkshake. That's what this place is...a great burger joint. You'll want to come back for their burgers as often as your cardiologist allows.

The Slugburger, BBQ sandwich, and chili fries in no way distinguish themselves as things you'd want to come back for. If you order any of those items, don't discount this place as meh....just get a burger next time. 

Do yourself a favor: don't go "deep tracks" on the menu. Order a delicious double cheeseburger and regular fries. This isn't a place where you'll find a hidden gem.  Boo chili cheese fries.
This place is pretty awesome. There's an antique kind of feel, and the food is amazing.
Delicious cheeseburger and fries ..drive thru is fast and convenient. Kind of reminds me of whataburger .. thumbs up .. I will be back!
d0xKQW6u1ij9qpg4AJO1Cg: Ah. New Year's Day delivery... great for the night in (after an epic New Year's Eve WSMFP show). Food was delicious and hit the spot. My husband loves his dish. Stuffed and happy. And we got free garlic knots- and all the food was piping hot when it was delivered.

I had a great delivery experience with Madeline Pizza. My pizza and salad came within 30 minutes, and the pizza was hot and fresh. My order was correct even though I had asked for some modifications. The pizza had lots of cheese and toppings. My garden salad was surprisingly large and fresh, with bright green lettuce and delicious dressing. My order seemed a little pricey to me, but for the quality and amount of the food, I would say it's worth it! Much fresher food than a large chain. I'm glad we have this option in the neighborhood.

Food: Pasta and Meat Sauce & Pizza Lover 
Really good food! Everything was super fresh.
Service: Everyone was super friendly
aO-9aTRxa3ftXn9HYfuCRg: I love this place! And great menu with lots of selections and cool names... Food came fast, fresh and tasty  and even impressed my mother-in-law... Who is super picky. I ordered the BLT with avocado and a side of mac & cheese... I also had the green salad with buffalo chicken... Both were absolutely delicious. They make their own pies and they have specialty ice cream as well... Worth the trip.

This is the SPOT if you are feeling cruddy from an evening full of boozy indulgence. My galpal and I hit up Pied Piper today and nearly covered our table with hangover helpers!

The onion rings, fried pickles, and a platter full of side items that included mashed potatoes, french fries, and a side salad helped this broad feel a tad better. 

Prices are fair and the low-key vibe are always welcoming!

TIP: A fabulous vegetarian menu is offered. Try the Bonnaroo Burger!

GREAT first impression on this yelper!  Ok so maybe I shouldn't have eaten there, I fear that my new house is too close to this little greasy spoon jewel!  In fact I am thinking about having my new house moved a few additional blocks away so when I walk there i can burn a few more calories.... the portions are Generous!

I had the Buffalo chicken sandwich and a side of fries (yea not exactly Weight Watchers material, but I think I might be forgiven)  It was amazing, I visited without reading the other reviews, and now it appears I have many other reasons to return (they have a lot on their menu!)

PS - if service with a smile is what you are looking for then this place delivers!  Keep on smilin'
D9s9S6zfP1VI7pGeLioqQQ: I am a native of Nashville and have eaten at Boscos over several years.  The food has improved. I was very impressed the last time I dined at the restaurant when visiting Nashville with my family. I enjoyed the late night menu.  The spinach artichoke and shrimp appetizers are excellent - well seasoned, not too salty, and excellent taste. The ingredients for the pizza are fresh and the romano cheese is freshly grated as well.  The wait staff is courteous and knowledge about the menu.  I recommend others try the restaurant to enjoy good food and ambience.

I live near Boscos so I need to find good places near me. I went one night and sat near the bar. The waiter was friendly and I'm sure he was not too happy when my party of three only ordered waters. He still was attentive to us. I ordered the Margherita pizza and it was very good. It was six slices which was plenty for me to eat there and take two pieces home with me. I will say Boscos is a little more than I would want to spend on just a hang out spot but I really liked the food. I was looking at getting a drink but it seemed like most cost about $10 and that's more than I wanted to spend on one drink. I will definitely be back though.

After revisiting this place after 5 years it still put a smile on my face when I came back. Nice decor, not sports barish but more like I'd envision what a euro pub would be. The brews never dissapoint, the food is good, especially the pizzas (but if you are looking for traditional pizzas I'd look elsewhere) and the service has always been on point. This is a good place for acasual date for that chick that likes brews but doesn't wanna do dive bars, or to get your lunch brew on then go back and tackle that project that issuch a pain in the ass. If u like Sundays, mos def try the brunch, and your parents might actually ride with you for the jazz and the mimosas... Who can hate on a good place that can give you Quality Time with the 'rents?
I81Ua5IltIBphFKnHmKwlg: Great little Asian/Vietnamese restaurant with some updated twists on the recipes. Love the lime soda and the squid appetizer. Several good options for Pho as well. Interior space is a little plain and the menu is kind of small.

Hidden gem in East Nashville! 

I always start with the fresh Vietnamese spring rolls, they are incredible. The authentic Pho soups are delicious especially on a cold winters day. I would also recommend the lemongrass chicken stir fry.

I have to start with the fact that I love pho and have been lumbering over to the west side of town when the hankering hits which is a pain so I'm soooooo excited to have this. I've been eating pho for a few years now (i'm no expert) but I've tried all the places in town so I know what i like.

This one is very small and quaint and the inside is way fancier than the inside of the other Vietnamese places if tried around town so I think you could pull this off for a really reasonably priced date night.  Of course I tried the pho which was a dollar or two higher than other places around town but the bowl was ginormous and I had entire meal worth of left overs afterwards.   I also had the spring rolls with the peanut sauce which again tasted quite good.

Here is where i'm taking a star away.  For one, there are 100's of combination's of pho so only offering one kind  disappointed me.  Secondly, with pho, you normally get a lovely plate of fresh basil, lime, chili's, bean sprouts, and a cool variety of sauces for your doctoring pleasure.  Creating your own steaming concoction of herbally, limey, spicy, crunchy soup is some of the funnest part of experiencing pho.  They delivered a small plate of basil and sprouts so I found myself frustrated with my lack of doctoring materials and it definitely left me wanting, grasping for more.   I'll go back though.
vQ4UetgOn-JaokAVlKC3lA: Once again I can't say how great this place is and how welcoming the staff was when we went there on Sunday for brunch. We sat at the bar and enjoyed to great entrees and had the chef special appetizer which was seared scallops that were cooked to perfection. We started some where else and then realized the hood would not do is wrong! Mad props to this spot  as when we left hugs were given and taken for a great time!!!!

We walked in late on Friday night and they accommodated us within 20 minutes.  Our server Emily was fantastic.  We ordered 3 of the chef's specials, calamari, fish and a vegetable plate.  All were unique and delicious.  We will definitely be back.  This is must when you are in Nashville.

I went for the first time on a Saturday night with some friends who had been there before. 

I ordered to start the bread basket. It came with a couple of kinds of bread, butter and dipping oil. It was all warm and good but not great. I also ordered the cheeseburger with battered brussels (battered and fried brussel sprouts).  I asked for the burger well done and it arrived medium so I had to send it back. Once I got it back and ate it I loved it. The cheese, tomato, peppers and whatever else they put on it went great when the burger. The bun was also really good. The brussel sprouts came with a side of ranch and were delicious though the breading a bit much. Someone else ordered crab cake and it was so good! I would order that. Out of the other 4 people at the table all but one was happy with their food. 

Our table ordered all of the desserts and was most impressed with the apple one and coconut banana cream pie. Both were interesting takes on classics and really flavorful. 

Service was okay. Our server was really nice and helpful, but disappeared a lot, or would leave the table so fast no one had a chance to ask for something. It was a busy night and maybe she had a lot of other tables. 

Overall the food was really good and I would go back. It's nice to have good dessert and good dinner in the same place. My bill before tip was about $32 for the bread basket, my entree, 1 dessert, and a coffee.
HF4Oh0KDTYo6xvf_JCoybQ: We decided to try out th Aquarium Restaurant while near the Grand Ole Opry and visiting with friends. I agree with the 3 1/2 star average rating overall. Good food and service. Costs are higher than usual based on the theme based experience, similar to Rainforest Cafe or T Rex Cafe (Florida). Good fish and chips and actually decent lobster as well.

Our server was nice, the food came out quick enough. I got coconut shrimp instead of the fried shrimp I ordered but didn't send it back bc we gearing up to head to a concert across the street. Their food is ridiculously over priced. My boyfriend got a chicken ceasar salad and it was $18. He said it was okay not as good as a $20 salad should be. Not enough dressing bland chicken. My meal was alright, but nothing different than you'd get at any other place that serves shrimp and fries. I know they jack up the price bc it is an aquarium and I heard they have 5 marine biologist on staff but good lord, it's cool to do one time but we will not be back. The fish tank is something like 200,000 gallons and sits in the middle of the place so the seating is all around it.

Very nice restaurant where you can sit and enjoy the fish in the aquarium. We got to watch a couple of scuba divers clean out the tank while we enjoy our meal. The restaurant was clean, the food was good and the staff were very friendly. Great atmosphere!
LxKrVb6A9vyGdx2gywpUXg: So thankful to finally have our favorite pizza on our side of town.  Although the original location has a certain charm, we like the larger area with more seating in the new restaurant.  Best pizza in Nashville, hands down.   Salads and garlic knots are also great!

We love this place.  My wife and I have been looking for a place similar to our favorite pizza place in St Petersburg, Florida (Flippers) since we moved to Nashville.  We found it here.  They have the right amount of cheese and sauce.  Not a blob of cheese like some other places.  The restaurant is very nice and very cool.  Service was excellent too.  Our server seemed to be legit in loving his job.  Good first impression for us and we will be back.

There's practically zero reason to the East Nashville location now that Five Points West is here. It's the same delicious Five Points pizza, except you can actually park here for free and get seated immediately instead of having to wait 2 hours! For those who are new to Five Points. If you just want a slice or two, get the slice of the day. I am a man of habit. Especially when it comes to Pizza. But every time I eat here I get a slice of both the meat and veggie SOTD, and I haven't been let down once. I used to have an attitude about Five Points just be East Nashville annoys me, but every time I eat here it gets harder and harder to deny that it's the best pizza in town (though I might turn to Desanos for a Margherita). Btw I was born in NYC to an Italian-American family so I'm automatically right.
ySxj2pv7BKZ1tMZS1C0wAA: I was delighted when I walked into Cafe Nonna, as it made me feel like I was back home in Sonoma. It's a small restaurant, tiny actually, with a patio area and inside tables that are cozy.  Our waiter gave us plenty of time to look everything over, and had advice on the best wines and dishes. My friend and I had some Italian red wine (of course I forgot the name), quite good, and bruschetta to start. I had the almond crusted trout on top of a bed of creamy risotto and veggies. Yum! I tasted my buddy's chicken, which was so juicy and tender, it was perfect. Unfortunately, I had no room to try dessert, but if it's anywhere near the rest of the food, bravo!

This place is a gem. The food is delicious every single time. It's a very small place so, you will be close to those sitting around you - I happen to enjoy that. Cozy would be a good description. Staff is attentive and polite. Knowledgeable of the menu. 
Good wine list. You can go casual or a little dressy. 
Would recommend reservations.

Neighborhood Italian restaurant that seems as though it has been there forever. 

Food was satisfactory. I mean, it was good, but now that I'm a few weeks out from eating there I cannot remember what I had. So take that as you will. I do remember that I had a smooth, chill white wine with my meal and that the server was delightful in helping me choose what would go best. 

We ate on the front porch and I really recommend it. Hot and breezy - it fit the meal of good food with a cold glass of wine. And although it will sound cheesy, it really helped to create the sense that you were in a small European town as you ate your meal.

It amuses me to see that they spell cafe with two f's. Extra fun, extra fresh, or extra fine? -- They all fit to describe the place. RECOMMEND.
snDwb7FRwpYzwu5GOTCq-Q: Impressive despite lunch during a soft opening. Grand opening is tomorrow (10/27). Had their signature hot chicken (boneless). 6 pieces was good lunch size, seasoned shoestring fries were great. I got medium which was perfect for me (about a 7 on a 1-10 on spicy), if you are sensitive to hot, go with the mild. Great, friendly, and attentive service. With other options including salads, great bar menu, and outdoor patio, this place has great potential. Excited to see the full lunch. Definitely worth giving it a try.

I wanted to love everything about it... I really did... and to be honest, I really did enjoy my evening in spite of a few hiccups.
Positives: The staff is extremely friendly and welcoming. The atmosphere is chill and super laid back. This is a great place to watch a game since there is a tv within every line of sight. It's Black Owned. That may not mean something to you, but it means a lot to my friends and I. It has great specials regularly & amazing looking choices on the menu.
The bar is placed well and they seem to have a great variety of beers on tap and mixed drinks. 

Constructive Criticism: It takes way too long to get the food. Way too long. If there is a large party, like 1 large party, expect to wait 30+ minutes to get some loaded fries. Hopefully you're good with some drinks and the company you brought with you... because it feels like forever if you're not. 
We ordered the pork (bacon) sliders first and I think the cook was rushed because the buns were cold and not grilled, and the bacon was undercooked. No worries, we ordered junkyard fries and the cheeseburger sliders also and those were delivered 10 minutes or so later and done very well... super tasty. My friend doesn't like processed cheese so she was a little meh about the fries, but it's supposed to be nasty good and that it was, delicious & nasty good.
Lastly, and I say this as humbly as I can, as friendly as our server was, she misheard our order on 2 separate occasions and brought us things we didn't ask for. Of course we were gracious and she was apologetic, but when a restaurant is loud and busy, it's just always a wise idea to repeat orders back to the customer so that we can have a chance to say "yes! Thanks so much." Or "no, I'd like iced tea, not Hi-c." Easy as pie
Alright y'all, I did my best to be positive and add my experience so that perhaps some adjustments could be made so folks can have a better experience in the future and this place can stick around for the long haul. We (Nashville) need it to stick around for the long haul
Great burgers, great services the waitresses are so nice and constantly check up on you. I love it
xL0oeV9PSTC8HKZILwPKfg:
Great beer selection and above average pub food. The remodeling that took place really eliminated crowding. However, the main gathering are does get crowded. They could of done so much more with this place. 

I am upset they got rid of the Golden Tee machine. I don't visit often for this simple reason. But would recommend anyone looking for a beer near sylvan park stop here.

Neighbors is the best place on the west side of town and maybe all of Nashville. I love it. Best waitresses too. Large outdoor patio. I'm hesitant to write this because I don't want a bunch of people going to "my place" but it's just awesome. Cheap drinks and incredible BBQ pulled right off the smoker in the parking lot.
Delicious food and laid back. If you want a neighborhood bar with the dive bar feel but welcoming atmosphere of a 'Cheers', you've found your place.
jl38yx7zzMRbg-kOK8NLDw: Great food, great atmosphere.  Stopped here on way through Nashville and was very happy with the food and the service.  The fried green tomatoes were excellent!

We have been wanting to try this place for a while and were very pleased with our experience. This could become a favorite of ours.  The entire menu is appealing to is.  We had the onion soup and escargot for starters which were both excellent.  I had the plum pork and my wife had the crab cakes with mustard tartar sauce.  The apple pie and cinnamon ice cream are to die for. We will definitely be back!

I've been to Germantown cafe a few times as I live down the street.  They are on the slightly more casual end of a fancier restaurant.  It's a business lunch or brunch type place.  Everything I have had has been good including their plum pork.  I recommend a visit.
_P8v_APY3AIXWWAiE4o3UQ: My wife and I were headed to Ernest Tubbs to see the Wild Feathers free show I said that this would be the perfect time to check out this new/old spot. We walked in and were greeted at the door and we proceeded to the record stacks to check out the vinyl. You could not beat the selection although the area was a bit small. I went to the bar and ordered 2 wines as we soaked in the vibe. I will say that the old Family Wash is nearly unrecognizable which  is sad but ok at the same time. The usual East Nashville hipster what do you want service that I guess that I am use to especially as I get older and they get younger. The wife and I really did enjoy going through all the old vinyl and looking at my favorites like Cheap Trick, Cher, Journey, and Rush 2112... I must say we waited a long time for this spot to open and we were not disappointed. We will be back as we didn't get a chance to try the grub.

Cool new record store / bar in East Nashville.  Great selection of new and used vinyl, very well curated and prices in line w/ other Nashville record stores.  The bar has a solid selection of local craft brews and a nice albiet limited selection of food.  The vibe is laid back & casual.  Ample room to sit with a drink around the bar, ice cold AC and a stage for performances.  Nice addition to the East side.
The perfect place for me. Good local beers and vinyl, can't go wrong with this place.
I8w9Jy7L7_V0gr0ieriPVQ: I contacted Don with a large last minute order, he was very helpful.  He offered me a better deal than I expected, the whole crew was friendly and my order prepared when I arrived!

I feel bad for previous yelp reviews of this papa john's location. I picked up an extra large pizza after work tonight and was surprised when I opened the box. Usually pizza chains ex: Pizza Hut, Papa John's, Little Caesars, Jets, etc have the greasiest pizzas known to man kind. However, this pizza was actually one of the best ones I've had from a Papa John's. Crust to crust pepperonis, and really not a drop of "unwanted" grease. Notice the parentheses, yea I know pizza is supposed to be  have grease but too much takes away from the real taste of the sauce and cheese. The service was excellent and I will definitely be making a stop here after work to pick more orders.

This is a good Papa John's on the way to greatness. That's a polite way of saying, do better service. Papa John's is a excellent brand, and the people working at the location need to match the quality 

I can name a few times going in, where I sense a hiccup in serving the counter. I believe that success is spelled in having a cooperating team that's ready for customer expectation.
NWKnmL4AByKg0Mc2UoqMhQ:
Food decent.  Service was semi-decent.

Love that this place is a Nashville icon.

Love to visit this place when in Nashville!  Their side salads are enough for a meal, and most always have plenty to take home.  Another favorite is their squash casserole, and my husband, a meat and potatoes guy, says it is so nice to find places that actually have REAL mashed potatoes!  Good prices as well.

This is a thankfully overlooked brunch spot. I say thankfully because so many other places are crawling with people on Sunday afternoons. 

The 2 times we've gone over the past few months--both during Sunday brunch--we've had a great experience. 

The place was bustling but we still got seated right away. I ordered the omelet which comes with potatoes, muffin, and a fruit cup for $9! Hubs got the chicken finger dinner which seemed a bit pricey for what it was--chicken fingers with fries and a house salad. My 17-month old got a variety of offerings from both of our meals! He loved the tortilla strips on top of the salad!

Our server was fine, though quite flustered. He spent more energy on bigger parties, and we had to be patient and / or find someone else when we needed something. For example, hubs got fries when he should have gotten onion rings but, by the time our server checked on us, he was almost finished. Our server still brought the rings but we ended up just taking most of them home.

Our meals were plentiful enough that our son had plenty to eat off our plates! Plus, they have balloons for kids which is always a bonus.

This has become a solid, go to place for us on Sunday afternoons. I hope it remains busy but not too popular because it's great to not get lost in the crowd.
ALK-7tTvDKAzxAtIiNyh2A: Trendy restaurant in Germantown. Lots of brick and tall windows. Sat at communal table where we eyed our neighbors' interesting  choices. We shared two small plates which were unique and savory. Each of us ordered a different entree but we sampled and enjoyed all of them. Our server was great and provided excellent suggestions.

Hip spot with modern decor! The menu is simple yet intricate and unique. We had the:

mafalde, maitake mushroom alfredo-this looked like tripe, and tasted delicious and creamy!
heritage pork: fatty pork and soft ribs that fell right off the bone
sourdough, seaweed butter: liked the green butter, bread's standard
I didn't have the pate or chicken liver, but my friends loved 'em!

RAD is a Top notch upscale casual whisky bar with great food. Every bite of food was delicious. We shared the sour dough bread with seaweed butter. Um, what is seaweed butter and how can I get more of it? It was airy and uniquely yummy. 
We also had the Swiss chard and ricotta cassoncini, lardo. They were bite size canapés that were confusing looking but tasted good. I went over board on chicken and was pretty happy with myself. I adored the ample portion of creamy chicken liver with currant mustard, red cress and toast. My excellent server boasted about the pastured chicken  with preserved lemon and garlic confit. so I went for it! 
I will say it was in my top 5 best chicken dishes from a restaurant. I loved the super chicken flavor with fresh herbs of tarragon and flat parsley accents. Chicken was served with no sided and it was so good. Just all juicy flattened chicken. My dinner partner tried the heritage pork sauerkraut gala apple with spaetzle. Sadly he was pretty stingy with sharing. The plate offered a few cuts of pork and the sauerkraut spaetzle. I tried a few bites when he was not looking and enjoyed my bites. The others at the table had the Garganelli verde heritage pork ragout, and sarvecchio (their favorite) I tasted it and boy are they on to something... It boasts Gooey, yummy bites of comfort food with style. We also shared a bowl of the squid ink Campanella, fl squid,shrimp, nduja (what ever that is?), basil, breadcrumbs... It was dramatic and also comforting. It did not photograph well but it tasted great. 
We also shared all three of the deserts and ordered secret desert cocktails. The lime curd, Carmel corn and ginger was odd looking but omg was it a delicious blend of treats for the mouth. We liked the beet and farmer cheese flan but it was not a highlight. The last desert was also unique and yummy but again the lime curd with popcorn took the prize. I was "forced" to get a desert cocktail called "Don't meletti me down" which was a mixture of Graham's port, meletti, smith & cross rum, Saigon cinnamon and while egg... Talk about a rich glass of yummy booze. 
I enjoyed a great meal at Rolf And Daughters for sure. It was a warm casual jeans feel too. Obviously I look forward to going back.
uam_mfkUT8YOXTFQZSSmUg: I would love to give 5 stars for the amazing show and extraordinary talent, but my server  was less than amazing. I practically had to be him to bring me and my husband another round. The musicians where absolutely wonderful!!! I had a awesome night and a lot of laughs.

This is a good time.. a novelty, if you will. If you need mindless entertainment for an out-of-towner, Big Bang is a solid choice. However, the bar isn't spectacular and you are going more for the "show" than the drinks or crowd (typically tourists and bridal parties).

Kenny the bartender was great.  The piano players were also amazing!! However your waiter was not so great. It's wasn't even busy when we went in. It was a Thursday night. Not friendly at all. Not on top of getting your drinks. I walked up many times to the bartender just to get another drink. When the waiter did come over he expected a tip. You had maybe 20 people in the bar dude. Wouldn't want to see you on a busy night.
8C-G2bwZJLyThpUL5hzg9w: Chicken tawook is tasty. The chicken was overcooked (dry), but the dish overall was good. The most impressive thing about the dish was that it was so healthy yet so tasty. Def going back!

I love Epice. Authentic high end Lebanese food made with delicious spice blends and fresh produce. The wine selection, including many Lebanese options, is outstanding. Have taken family from all over the country here (Californian vegetarians to meat-loving upstate New Yorkers), and no one has ever been disappointed. They also serve one of the best (and arguably one of the only edible) fish dishes in Nashville - Sayadeya. Desserts are excellent, especially Roz be-haleeb if you like rice pudding.

Place is wonderful and great atmosphere. My friend and I walked in without reservations and was welcomed immediately.
