Even though we sat at the bar (preference since we were there only to get drinks and appetizers) our bartender was so attentive and awesome! The drinks were amazing (I ordered the Fayrooz FYI) and so was the appetizers. I can say this is the best hummus I've ever had. So creamy and full of flavor. Can't say enough about this place. Thank you for your southern hospitality! Can't wait to go back.
ZHe0EmniM0IYEaNQdpfkdQ: This is a great hidden gem in East Nash, a hole in the wall and super tiny place, but totally worth your time, which you need to have in order to eat here because it's just a couple running the place by themselves but it is totally worth the wait. The Kentucky roll is one of my favorites!

Loved everything we ordered! They have some interesting specialty rolls I'd recommend (e.g., Nashville roll, which has seared salmon). Nice fresh fish. Super friendly people. We'll definitely be returning.
Amazingly fresh and tasty sushi!...some of the highest quality in Nashville for sure.  The owners are really great too!!
i9d__NEIlcNx2V55_HMelg: I've never been a big fan of the whole hibachi, communal dining scene, but Kobe Steaks changed my mind. The best part of this restaurant are the private rooms that each grill is located within creating a very intimate dining experience. Great place to go with friends, drink some sake and have a good time.

As someone that loves Japanese food, I am always anxious to try hibachi restaurants. I have been to the some of the best, and worst. I can say, from experience, that Kobe's is an excellent hibachi restaurant. Fantastic, plentiful soup and salad comes with your meal. All meals also come with a grilled shrimp appetizer which was well seasoned and prepared. I had the ribeye with garlic, it was absolutely delicious; very high quality, perfectly seasoned and prepared. The fried rice costs extra and is not that special  here to be honest. Delicious sauces; be sure to order their "white shrimp sauce" it is my favorite Japanese condiment and is only available by request. My chef was very entertaining, but did lack some of the flare that I have seen other chefs demonstrate. Overall I highly recommend this restaurant as one of the best hibachi grills in the Nashville area.

Kobe's Steakhouse is an all time favorite for my family! This is where we went to celebrate birthdays,  straight A's and everything in between growing up and even now as an adult. With a young child we only go every couple of years now so I was a little nervous with all of the negative reviews. Glad I ignored those and we went anyways! Everything was wonderful! The service, the quality and quantity of food, the wait and the half price bottle of wine! I do think that they used to put on more of a show and we missed the onion volcano, the flying shrimp and egg juggling on the spatula but everything was still outstanding! Although it did not negatively  impact our visit they are long overdue for an update to the decor, carpet and bathrooms.
zmYpN67Tw2gixqkaO67sLQ: I remember seeing this episode on shark tank! I'm not vegan but I am lactose intolerant. While I was willing to take the digestive hit, i was THRILLED when I saw vegan cheese on their menu. I LOVED my crunchy garlic chicken with vegan cheese, dipped in tomato soup. We ordered a s'mores donut sandwich to-go and I don't doubt for a second it'll be stupendous!

If you like cheese and meat between slices of bread, you will be able to find something at Tom + Chee. They have Soups of the Day if you're not a tomato soup fan. If you're out with friends who are gluten free or otherwise dieting there have salads and gluten free bread too. Try it once. You will likely return.

I really love this concept. Grilled cheese is a favorite guilty pleasure. We must get this in Memphis. There are so many grilled cheese options one could eat here daily without duplicating. 
I decided to choose a somewhat healthy option. Creamy tomato soup. The regular tomato soup is even healthier, but I didn't feel like being that good. It was good and the bowl was a good size. One can also get a cup or a dipper for dipping your sandwich in. 
I was hoping the spouse would choose one of the decadent grilled cheeses, but he stayed classic with the basic sandwich and tomato soup. He even let me taste the yummy grilled cheese. 
He was briefly tempted by one of the grilled cheese donut, but then his dinner hit him and he declined. 
The also have salads with grilled cheese croutons, natch. Healthy, but a small cheat.   I was surprised that they offer beer, too. 
A good, quick road stop.
gYeqbVXu13UcMZcp6cS-Bw: Pretty good fusion cuisine.  I liked their beer selection the best though. They had Chimay Rouge a great Trappist Ale.  Valet parking. Nice artwork on wall. Great place for a date or business dinner.

Love everything about this place! When you first walk into this establishment, you know you are some place special! We were walked upstairs to the second floor where we were seated at a table in a cozy corner. Great atmosphere to enjoy some delicious food and great company. 

Our waiter was so nice and helpful with ordering. The best recommendation was the mac and cheese! We were pleasantly surprised by the portion of this "side" dish. Next time, I'd just order the mac and cheese for my entree! It may be the best mac and cheese I've ever eaten. All the other food was absolutely divine. We practically licked our plates cleaned!

Happy Hour is where it's at!!
My roommate and I first went to Park Café in July for the happy hour specials.  We split the fried pickles, which were pretty good, but the sauce that came with it (comeback sauce, I think they call it) gave it the kick that it needed.  I got the tuna tartare w/an avocado-watermelon salad and fried toast points... out of this world.  It was light, but still filled me up - for $10!  My roomie got a goat cheese flatbread (that tasted DIVINE!) for $5.... it was enough for two meals, easy.  The sunset margarita was amazing, and $5 at happy hour price.  I spent under $30, and got 2 drinks, a meal, and 1/2 an appetizer, PLUS 20% tip to the waiter.

The service was great.  They didn't rush us, but our waiter was always available when needed.  The outside patio is sooo nice, and we were the only people on it during our dining experience -- probably due to the early-dinner time.  

They also have great weekly-night specials:
Monday: $1 Oysters
Tuesday: $7 burger + fries 
Wednesday: $6  Hot Chicken + Waffles
Thursday: $10 Steak + frites.

The burger is a great deal, and also soo delicious! Can't wait to give the other specials a try:)
aRnTDcpJcuLogQdOsycebg: Yes, this place is occasionally open for business.  Don't let the mysterious operating hours discourage you: this simple place is family run and offers  a straight-forward buffet of authentic Southern fare.  The food is fresh, homemade, but not amazing.  However, the great location in the heart of Five Points, plus the history and local character of the place, make it an above-average experience.

The Edgefield Restaurant is a sleepy little meat and two right at the five points intersection in East Nashville, next door to the infamous and most snobby Bongo Java in town.  I would recommend coming to the Edgefield for some cheap home style food. You won't get any frustrated hipster attitude here at the Edgefield, it's a pretty typical meat and two with the classic items on the menu, meatloaf and potatoes, with green beans, chicken, macaroni, and cornbread.  None of it is gourmet, but you don't go to this kind of place for gourmet, you go here for comfort food.  

I would recommend the fried chicken! It is very...comforting, in the sense that after you have finished eating all you can worry about is where you are going to lie down to take a nap.

I have two very Southern, sweet-as-pie grandmothers.  One of them is a phenomenal cook; the other is a good (but certainly not amazing) cook.  Edgefield Restaurant reminds me of the latter grandmother.  It's perfectly tasty...not bad one bit.  But it's just not as spectacular as I know it could be.

Edgefield stands out a bit in the Five Points district.  That isn't to say we don't love it--in fact, as far as food goes, it's one of my favorite spots down there.  It's the meat (pun intended) in the middle of a hip sandwich.  The Groove and Bongo Java would be the bread, of course.  The crowd, however, is refreshingly diverse.  You might catch a gaggle of old Southern women in their Sunday hats or even a tattooed young musician fending off a hangover.

One of my favorite aspects of Edgefield is its price: it's remarkably cheap.  Just today I payed less than $6 for the meatloaf and two sides.  Most sides are typical (mashed potatoes, green beans, fried apples, et al), but they don't stand out much.  Just a bit bland.  The fried chicken is pretty good, but nothing is especially memorable.

Edgefield is really small and not exactly the cleanest meat & two you'll come across.  The owner (at least I think she's the owner/operator...she's the only one always there) is sweet but seems perpetually exhausted.  The mood is simple and pleasant, but I like taking my orders to go so that I can enjoy them somewhere a touch more upbeat.  Edgefield is worth a try, but it probably won't outdo your grandmother's cooking.
cdTH26q7oWepyPQqN_yDoA:
I love the vibe of this place! 60's inspired decor and menu. 

The cocktails are very interesting and delicious. The small food menu is modern and tasty. Try the beet salad! It's sweet, vinegary, earthy, and creamy at the same time!

The service was very friendly and our bartender Yohan was a cool dude.  I was only visiting Nashville and this lounge made me wish I had somewhere like it back home.

Great place to listen to good live music and enjoy a solid drink. Honestly, not too crazy about the food options available or the prices... but this is still a great place. Oh, and the pink lighting outside is super cool :)

Okay so I LOVE this place and the chicken is the best in Nashville, but I did not have a positive experience there tonight unfortunately, even though we come so often. We were served by, what seemed to be, a new waitress. She was not very accommodating and seemed bothered by the fact she had to take our order. But totally fine... We kept on. And our food was amazing per usual. They were out of the tots, so she offered plain fries. We placed 3 orders. 
But we got up to pay at the front and our waitress was getting our checks together. She charged all of us $8 each for the plain fries. A side of PLAIN FRIES. $8 - the same as the poutine on their menu!!! But just the Fries. Doesn't seem right since the tots are only $5. It doesn't add up at all. We asked her and she was short and rude and made no effort to accommodate our concerns. I got a side of olives and a side of Fries and it was $15.
2QD1Lv4bKTqOhijz07xTeA: Great coconut based ice cream. Sampled all the flavors and all were delicious. Cute area of neighborhood. The next door coffee cafe shop had really cool people too.

Kokos is not only the BEST ice cream, but the experience and environment surrounding them is truly special. They put so much love and genuine quality into their ice cream and their shop and it shows! Kokos is truly a Nashville gem! All the stars

Best Vegan ice cream I've ever had in my life! Smooth, creamy, deliciously sweet. My meat/ dairy eating friend loved it. Wouldn't know it was vegan if it wasn't labeled. The owners were super friendly and personable too. I hope I'll be able to find their brand in stores back home in Texas one day!
YMxOjiZfWyN94L8XkZGPRQ: Great bibimbap!!! Plus you can't beat the service -- nicest owners ever. Highly recommend!!!

This place is a small tucked away restaurant that I hope you don't miss! While they have breakfast on the menu, the real stars are the bibimbap and the fried rice! I had the spicy pork fried rice and it was so tasty. Great flavors and the pork was so delicious! I can't wait to go back. They also had a tasty selection of canned/bottled drinks, and the man working there is a jack of all trades...cashier, chef, carpenter, story teller!

An easy short walk from the Hermitage Hotel. Incredible food! Had to Bibimbap and will be going back tomorrow to explore the rest of the menu! Seriously, if you are staying anywhere near the Capitol in Nashville, you need to stop here for some legit Korean fare.
EwFc_BRT3XZOJr5pQZAbkw:
Bruegger's gets 5 stars from me! Their cream cheese section alone is amazing. If I was purchasing cream cheese for like a party or big breakfast, I would get it from here.

Never once have they messed up my order and the service is quick! I love the egg and cheese on the Asiago bagel with the jalapeño cream cheese. CHEESE CHEESE CHEESE! This place is perfect to grab breakfast on your way to work or whatever. But it's also good enough that I would sit down with someone and just enjoy. Highly recommend!

Gosh darn you Bruegger's Bagel. You hit the spot! 

Having one of those morning's recently where that super healthy breakfast just wouldn't get me through my day, I accidentally came across this location. Although, bagels by nature, aren't really unhealthy; you must stop by here and try one of their delicious bagels topped with cheese. 

I was very surprised that when I let one foot hit the floor that the woman behind the counter enthusiastically greeted me. That was a nice start to the morning! Customer service here is equally as amazing as is the quality of the food. 

They have a bunch of different cream cheeses that you can match to your bagel. The variety and freshness of the bagels is at rockstar status. 

Although not near where I live...I will be sure to make this a stop on my way anywhere near the shop.

I've liked this place ever since I was a kid. There's also a location nearby, by the Harding/HWY 100 split. You can't go wrong with the bacon, egg, and cheese bagel, but lately I've been loving that promotional bagel with the Canadian bacon on it. The sun dried tomato bagel is to die for. This is my favorite place to go for breakfast :)
xq7UnFYQ4ZvLV6j1UKzEzQ: This was a first time visit and it was very good. The service was top notch. Felicia was very attentive and on top of it. I had a cheeseburger on a pretzel roll and sweet potato fries. The fries come with this sauce that's out of this world. I put it on my burger as well as the fries. I asked for my burger to be medium. It was done, but juicy and flavored nicely so I ignored the doneness. If you want well done, you may get a hockey puck. Just saying. Will be back!

Great burger, and atmosphere! The mural on the wall adds great character to the place and comes with a great story!
The sweet potato fries are tops!
Best beer & burger spot in Antioch
A6_LPH-NIunsga44vhPdbQ: Great place for any meal but the last time I was in town I was there at lunch.  I had a couple of wonderful Bloody Mary's and the Fish Taco's for lunch.  They were very good and the favor just right.  To finish off  I had a piece of Key Lime Pie.  Will be back.
Wonderful menu, and even better food. I love bricktop's. I love it so much i try not to go often because I want to lose love for it. You know?

In Nashville visiting family and found this across the street from the Holiday Inn we were staying at. So glad we found it! It was extremely busy on a Wednesday night so the service was a tad slow but well worth the wait. We meant to stop back by before leaving town but ran out of time. The deviled eggs are a must try and the shrimp and grits. The cocktail was a little too sweet and almost no alcohol but the food made up for it with the exception of the kids meal... I'm pretty sure it was boxed mac n cheese but my boys ate it. All in all, I would definitely recommend and will definitely eat here again!
to6rjQskEIczYUxCSGPh6g: This is one of my top favorite restaurants in Nashville. Their pizza is number 1 on my list of favorite pizzas. It is brick oven style and so thin you have to eat it with a fork and knife. The flavor of the pizza sauce is outstanding. I have never gone when it was extremely crowded. It is a smaller intimate place. Perfect for date night.
Great service, solid pizza.  I don't know about delivery, I always do carry out.  Crust is excellent and portions are spot on.

I often visit Nashville, and this is one of my favorite local restaurants. The best pizza in town, in my opinion. Really nice authentic brick oven crust. Good wine selection. Friendly service. Nice atmosphere with a few outdoor tables for warm summer evenings. I'll be back!
SLwKTGRk4obtmK_WPjwlGQ: It's a nice quiet & classic bar. It was only for gentlemen. Love the Jack Daniel single barrel Wisley. And they service very nice bar snacks with drinks. By the way there are happy hour, too.

The best thing I ate on my whole trip was the Fried Green Tomatoes at The Oak Bar. Amazing!  I also had the Roast Beef sandwich w/ Fries. Very good. A great quiet place to have a late dinner away from the hustle and bustle of Broadway.

Ate here with my visiting parents tonight. Being vegan, I ordered the farm salad without cheese. I really wanted some of the Brussel sprouts side so I ordered it. The lovely waitress asked if I was vegan - perhaps because I asked to hold the cheese on my salad - and said she'd ask the cook to hold the butter they cooked the sprouts in. I thought that was quite nice of her to do. 

All in all the salad was very fresh and tasted amazing. 

My mom had the chocolate creme brûlée. She wouldn't recommend it - says it taste a bit like chocolate pudding.
tGCB7vICVh5pELR99RkXfg:
OK lets start with the good. Good food, good portions, and good variety.

Now for the bad. Service is hit or miss and the menu looks so goofy. Dolphins all over the thing and menu layout is nuts. Don't forget the Spain atmosphere in a Mexican restaurant :). 

Overall good so far after 4 visits.

My luck with their delivery has been great. They arrive before their ETA and I've been quite happy with their food. The tacos are small but pretty tasty with fresh cilantro and added guac and salsa. My most recent dish was Caldo de Siete Mares (crab legs, shrimp, fish, mussel, scallops, and octopus,served with corn tortillas and rice, and lime). Generous portions of seafood in a lightly spiced red soup. I only poured enough as a sauce over rice and added the onions/cilantro mix for a tasty dish. I also found that sprinkling of lime adds a surprisingly nice zing as opposed to lemon on fish.  It's a dish I would order again, though I'm sorry no photo.

Bottom Line: I've heard folks in Bellevue complain there aren't any restaurants. False.

First, the salsa. It's fresh like it was just blended together. While heat isn't really here, that's ok. There's a fine balance of fresh veggies and cilantro. I'm serious. It's good salsa.

Next, along with the salsa, they deliver with the chips a bottle of Jalepeno avacodo sauce. That's where the heat comes in. Good alone or in tandem with the salsa, the pre-meal chip experience is a good one here.

I had for my meal the Texas Burrito with Al Pastor pork. Yes. A delicious, cheesy, full burrito with everything you need and nothing you don't. I didn't know how much I loved pastor until I had it for lunch...and dinner. The burrito was large enough to enjoy for two meals (or one big one.) excellent quality and quantity for the price. 

My buddy had the ground beef taco salad. He seemed very satisfied with his choice. It looked tasty. 

Daneli served us. She was terrific! Good server and very friendly. Thanks Daneli! We'll be back.

Atmosphere is very comfortable and pleasant. 

 I'll drive from Green Hills to get my Guac. Bellevue has a gem. Don't spoil it.
sk421Epi6awFkgsXRfjVUQ: I've ordered delivery from them twice. The food is always delivered faster than expected and very warm. First time I've ever ordered lo mein and it was delicious.  Great crab rangoon as well. The vegetables look fresh and nothing is soggy. The rice is nothing special, but when is rice ever special? I like the option to order less food for less money too so that I'm not having to eat leftovers for the entire weekend.

The food is on point and I have yet to dislike what I have ordered.  The sweet and sour chicken and hot and sour soup are my favorites. Yum!  The delivery is always on time, mostly even earlier than expected.
Probably the best Chinese delivery I've had outside of New York City. Food was hot and the egg drop soup was tasty with no need for salt or pepper. I highly recommend it!
n3xTK_jqAwZB-C3sUDXEvA:
An unlikely spot for such tasty southern comfort food, but that's indeed what we found at Jack Daniel's. 

The half chicken entree with mashed potatoes and mac & cheese was a healthy amount of food and all delicious. The chicken was plump, the potatoes were fresh and rustically mashed, and the mac n cheese was lovely with its crunchy breadcrumb topping and hearty flavor. 

While not award winning, the corn muffins and honey butter to start were on point given how hungry we were. Crumbly and special with that great cornmeal taste. 

Iced teas are made in house and served in nearly quart size mason jars, so that was delicious and adorable.

The service is where they really had us feeling that southern vibe. Frankie was very personable and attentive to our large party and kept us both well-served and entertained.

Though the restaurant is in the hotel, the meal and experience at Jack Daniel's would have you believe otherwise.

Went in for a few drinks and had to try some food as it looked and smelled good.  First for the beer, had the Kentucky Bourbon barrel Ale which was really good!  Then we ordered the shrimp & Grits which exceeded expectations, it was tasty and well presented.  In addition the bar had live music going on the county singer was really good.  Finally our server was informative and friendly! It was so good we went back the next day!

Very tasty catfish.  The side grits were excellent as were the fried pickles. 
Great staff and nice decor.  The honey whiskey peach drink cooled things off on a hot afternoon.   I will be back.   4 ppl at our table with no complaints.
TO7iRzQu7IXiSGC0lypZaQ: Fresh tables are coming soon from Mexico, but the real Mexican food continues. A fresh remodel after a fire, and some new menu items help keep this one-restaurant tradition interesting. New molcajete with meat, cactus, banana peppers and goat cheese, and new quesadillas are nice additions. La Hacienda Taqueria stands alone - back like a phoenix!

If there is one thing Nolensville Road is known for, it's being packed with dozens of authentic Mexican and Tex-Mex style restaurants.  And of all the choices Nolensville has to offer, this one is undoubtedly my favorite.  The servers are polite and quick, the food is delicious and cheap, and the margaritas are oh so tasty.

Honestly, La Hacienda isn't exactly big on atmosphere.  The whole restaurant is basically one large cafeteria style room, the floor is tiled and the mural behind the cash register isn't exactly expertly painted (although I like it).  But despite the sub par atmosphere, the food is some of the best that can be found in the area for the price.

The staff at La Hacienda is very sweet, and I enjoy my meal every time I'm there.  It's a pretty great spot to go with a large group of friends (especially if you're looking to have a margarita or two).  I know that Nashville is packed with authentic (and allegedly authentic) Mexican joints, but this one is honestly in my top five...and that's saying something.
Menudo is always on point. I would have given them 5 stars if the Barbacoa was Texas style, the barbacoa they sell here has to much basil IMO.
81nf-wIABwWLPhN7zl6FwQ: Ordered the Brussels sprouts special for lunch. It was delicious. Oven roasted Brussels, small chopped bacon and shredded chicken. My husband enjoyed his sandwich. Great homemade lunches. The only complaint was that there isn't much seating and the long line forms infringe of some of the tables. So go a little early to beat the rush.

The Food Company is great for a tasty, fresh, light lunch on a hot afternoon.

Tried the special my first time around - a quesadilla with peaches, goat cheese, arugula and prosciutto. Sounds delicious, right? It was just okay. These ingredients could be great together, but you have to help them marry. The peaches really should have been warmed before going in and the goat cheese should have been increased. It wouldn't hurt to wilt that arugula. Still, my first lunch wasn't bad, so I gave it a second shot with my husband. I had the tea room salad. It was quite delicious, but I skipped the dressing because it looked like pepto bismol. I threw back a fresh cookie with the whole thing and felt pretty good about the meal.

Nice space, nice lunch, not bad.
Prices were a little high but the food quality was worth it. Parking is difficult and it's crazy busy at lunch!
tOfaMOiMrXSmTDuPjLOCuw:
I visited the Arcade during a First Saturday Art Crawl and absolutely fell in love with the gallery spaces there. Every storefront and studio had a different vibe to suit the tastes of the artist showcased. From dimly lit, sage smelling vintage stores to exposed-brick minimalist palaces, the Arcade has a gallery or a store for everyone. I'm excited to check out the establishments which frequent the building on a daily basis, but it's quite fun to mill around the whirlwind of maze-like studios during art crawls.

I have tried only one restaurant on the base floor, Jimbo's Sushi & Korean Food. With limited seating and small plates, it would have been the perfect snack location after a busy day at work. Unfortunately, it did not please my weekend palate or appetite that afternoon. However, the Arcade offers plenty of other fare, including a large Ethiopian restaurant I would be eager to try next time I find myself there.

Enjoyable, open space with a ton of history. I definitely recommend locals and newcomers alike to explore the numerous small storefronts inside.
Awesome Downtown Nashville find. Lots of cute little restaurants and a few little shops. We ate at a place called Oriental Lunch and satisified our craving for greasy Chinese food for cheap.

We visited the Arcade as part of the First Saturday Art Crawl and I can't believe it took us this long to visit.  This place has two of my favorite things: art and food!  There are numerous galleries and several eateries and many stay open late as a part of the Art Crawl.  We didn't get to try any of the food (yet) because we ate prior to the event, but we will be sure to try some of the offerings in the future.  We did get to try our first ever 'bubble tea' here though.  

As an added bonus, you can meet most of the artists who are present at their galleries during the Art Crawl.

I'm not sure what the Arcade is like in daylight but it really comes alive during the First Saturday Art Crawl.
Vw6obVp7BBFfoP0PUA8Ykw:
This is my favorite restaurant for take out on my way to the Percy Warner park. 

My (and my kids) favorite snack is the black bean dip (smooth and spicy) with extra jalapenos.
The Lucayan salad with their Boija muffins is sweet, light, and perfect for a hot summer's day in the shade of a tree!
Their jerk is yummy, but their rotisserie chicken (the day I had it) was dry.

The ambiance is a little on the dirty side, eclectic interior with lax wait staff, but the food and price does make up for it... hence, a great take-out joint!

I am madly in love with the Black Bean and Rice Salad!  And, the best restaurant to buy it "to go" is the Harding location as their BBQ dressing containers are the biggest!  Tell 'em to go easy on the lettuce though...  sometimes it gets a bit overwhelming.

Calypso is a consistently great, cheap spot for lunch or dinner. There are locations all over town; the newest one in East Nashville. 

I usually get the black bean nachos with chicken and rice. Or, if I am feeling healthy I'll get the black bean salad which is still a lot like the nachos. Either way, it's usually enough to either share or take some home.

My vegetarian friend loves the place, and it's always fast and friendly.
TbIm5iOl6G0gXfDpPDeYTg: Breakfast was great and the waitress were awesome. The best salt cure country ham I've had in Nashville shame this is there last day. Farewell and you will be missed.

Solid, quiet. Better than the Biscuit House for sure. 

The first few times I went, I was not in love, but I kinda am now. The breakfast is cheap and tasty. The burger is enormous and cheap and tasty. The staff are friendly and accommodating, and they'll let me hang out and eat a cheese burger and read for a while, but I only do that when it isn't busy. 

Fun fact: they now have a full bar featuring several local craft brews on draught that are also cheap. And tasty. 

And I never have to wait for a table and the windows look original.

Went there last night for a meal and some music.  Service was fast and friendly and the food was very good.  Prices were great and they have several local craft brews on tap.  We didn't have to wait long for our food and they kept up with us and 4 other tables no problem.  We'll go back there for sure.
uiTjhohw9sCx4eEws6piaA: The food and service is good. All you can eat sushi for $22,however the fish slices are very thin and you end up with a lot of rice.

Love Sushi 88, perfect for all  to enjoy. So many options to try and share. For the money, it makes sense to get the all you can eat option. I went with friends, they ordered one item and a drink, it came to almost the same price as the all you can eat that the rest of us got. Everyone left happy that we chose Sushi 88. Even my son, who's a vegan, went away full and satisfied. The wait staff are kind and don't try to rush you. They give you the time you need to enjoy dinner and time with friends and family. I would recommend the for lunch or dinner. The only downside is the parking, make sure you bring change for the meters, if you're lucky enough to get one!!

For an all-you-can-eat place, they have some really great sushi! The service was amazing and I enjoyed the decor that wasn't explicitly Japanese (rollerskates as floating shelves). 

Pro-tip (because I just witnessed this in front of me): they don't (and won't and shouldn't) box your leftover food so don't be a little b***h by ordering 7 sushi to bring home and not expect to pay extra. If I were management, I'd limit the amount of sushi one can order and lower it with each round of orders. (e.g. 4 orders of food per person for the first round, and then 2 orders of food per person for any subsequent rounds).
sD5AomoXYaoS7RrzCUumPg: Any time a new pizza place opens I'm all over it, plus we were already in the area, so there was no reason not to stop in. I really liked their style: wood fired, fresh ingredients, great house meatballs, and a simple tangy crushed tomato sauce. My only suggestion to them would be to go a little lighter on the sauce, as good as it is, it left the interior a little limp. That being said, the flavor was absolutely there. We'll be back to try their expanded menu once they've had some time to settle in. I can see  them becoming the go to spot for pizza in the downtown/broadway area.
Amazing owner and staff for letting us use their business for the 1st annual Gobble Gobble Give - feeding the homeless thanksgiving day event! Thank you so much!

Cool looking restaurant- very trendy and clean. Neat venue next door too (12th & Porter)!

I have been multiple times to dine and have attended an event at the venue attached! 

The food is great, the pizza crust is amazing. The salads are very tasty too. Everything  has fresh ingredients.

Good service and friendly staff. 

Overall, great experiences!

Yummy!
tewtTBXF-_vNp9Fed97B8w: Had a good time.Took the Easter brunch ride. It would've been nice had it not been so cold and rainy. Food was alright. Plain, but edible. Drinks were good, a little pricey, but they were good. Loved going to the upper decks and sitting at the tables and just enjoying the ride. Wish we could've just done that rather than paying for the show and food. It is a nice boat and a neat experience though.

My mom, sister and I visited Nashville for the first time from California.  The General Jackson Showboat was such a pleasant experience.  The boat was in very good condition.  The staff was nicely dressed and polite.  The decks were well lit toward the evening time.  The restrooms were clean and stocked with all the necessities. The dinner area at floor level (where we sat) had round tables setup that sat about 9 people to a table.  The table settings were finely arranged. The two servers at our table provided excellent service!  I don't think anyone at our table had to ask for anything. The dinner was a chicken breast stuffed with spinach and cheese and had two sides to go along with it.  Dessert was a chocolate cheesecake.  The food was very good and so was the tea and coffee.  
The show was wonderful!  The performers were talented and gifted.   The performers outfits were flashy and glitzy.  The band did a great job too!  The show had a variety of country music that gave tribute to both the older and newer musicians. The weather was perfect with clear skies and the river was like glass.  It was about a 3-hour ride.  We would book this show again!

The captain and the staff of the General Jackson were extremely accommodating to our party.  The food was very good, but more importantly, the entertainment was fabulous.  We never expected the show to be so good.  We spent $135. for each Captain's Table ticket, but it was well worth it.  You get VIP treatment during the entire cruise, which includes first to board and meeting the captain for a little history of the ship, front row seating on the top deck in front of the band, a free cocktail, escorted to your table for dinner by your own waiter, another private tour by the captain, which includes the bridge, and a free General Jackson tee-shirt.  A must see while in Nashville.
WY7Umhuv9jcQ6fOoOFa4lQ: Great home style seafood house. Better come hungry- you sure won't leave that way. Staff could not be nicer, endless fixings- hush puppies, pickles and onion, cole slaw, fries. Had the fried catfish, nicely done in a cornmeal breading. Highly recommended.

We went Uncle Buds our last night in Nashville. The food was great for what it was. Fried catfish and awesome fried chicken. My son had the alligator tail and really liked it. It looked like little breaded chicken nuggets. I tried one and it was pretty good. The "fixins" as they call them were a nice addition- white beans, hush puppies, pickle spears and sliced onion. The atmosphere is very casual and crowded. Be ready for a wait. Seems to be a popular place for families. Our server was great and very sweet. And speaking of sweet, the sweet tea is so good.
Great catfish and gumbo! This place is real southern food with fair prices. The service is also excellent!
x_b2wweMFSJ3i6SoNtbtDQ: I really enjoyed the great food and service. They definitely give quality food and quantity. My Kung Pao (Pow) Chicken, Spring Rolls, and Dumplings were excellent.

Mmm lettuce wraps!!!! My sister talked me into it. We were in town for a show and wanted to get something to eat before going. I had never eaten a lettuce wrap but she swore by them. And she was right!!! Service was great, the staff really nice, the food was excellent and overall, a great experience.

I had a good experience here...our waiter was very knowledgeable and nice and the food was excellent.  The restaurant was very clean and the manager on duty gave me a $10 gift card since it was my first time at the restaurant.  I'll definitely be coming back when I visit Nashville in the future and highly recommend this establishment.
Lw_8MDDspsumhB37n74nNw:
Childhood place I have always loved. Ribs and pulled pork is fantastic. BBQ nachos were a huge portion I couldn't slay on my own. Many different sauces on the table that are all fantastic and the prices are great! 

My 7 year old son got an order of their smoked chicken wings and smiled the whole time he polished the wings to the bone. 

Will be back every time I'm home in Nashville!

food is amazing and was looking forward to the banana pudding. my husband got takeout on my birthday last Thursday and low and behold they forgot my banana pudding I was so upset! they also forgot my husbands side. we called the manager and he proceeded to tell us that they have a "24 hour policy" which is ludicrous!! 24 hour policy to get my food if you guys mess up the order? my husband also had to wait for about ten minutes in the drive thru after he paid so it's not like they were rushed. I am only giving 4 stars because their food is so good and I really really want to try the banana pudding....

Very good BBQ, not the best, but very good. If you're a BBQ snob or certified judge, just pass on by because you're only like what you've been told to like anyway. This is a pork place. If you expect good beef, go to Texas.

Sides are all good, ribs are good also.
jSYu2apG1uYq3eNzSA-OHw:
Okay, as a very huge Johnny cash fan, I FREAKED when I saw this on a trip over the summer. I immediately dropped by. I walked in to a rather small giftshop and looked around a bit. They have everything. Huge collection of shirts, CDS, books and even signed memorabilia and baby onesies you can buy. 

I walked up and purchased my ticket which was fairly priced and went inside. This collection of his memorabilia is UNBELIEVABLE! Not only do they have all his gold and platinum records, stage outfits, wedding outfit, personal fan club items, etc. but they also have a lot of June Carter's stuff as well. They even had a little heart shaped box of chocolate John wrote a love note on in sharpie on display. 

The coolest little place I've been to in Nashville. I recommended for anyone who likes Johnny.

This museum was fantastic! I don't know why Lonely Planet makes it sound so poor. Prices were relatively high (15$) but it was worth it. 
It is a very modern, interactive museum with an amazing array of personal items, e.g. letters, clothes, furniture and instruments. 
It's a good mix of his personal life and his career as a musician and also as an actor. 
Johnny Cash fans will probably need at least two hours.

LOVED this museum! I thought it was a little pricey for being so small but still worth it! Loved all the artifacts and readings on them. Really loved the station where I could hear one of Cash's songs in different types of taping- amazing how the sound is so different. Loved the staff there! They would chime in and add to things to what you were reading or saying. One even started dancing with me haha!
UtABCTNWNw19ilPS5USErw: Love Jack in the Box. You have no idea what your missing. From Chattanooga and we don't have them. Always stop when I am driving through

I haven't had a bad burger here. The best selling burger is the Classic Buttery Jack. If you pair this with the curly fries, drink, and throw in an egg roll... just because you can... you have enough food to have a filling lunch or dinner.

It's one of the only fast food joints close to the hotels in the area.  But no matter.  Jack in the Box has a large selection and even serves breakfast all day.  Everything is made to order, which definitely requires that you wait a bit longer than other fast food restaurants, but the food is worth the wait!
mfuO-dtZqVfLG19MRJLvzQ: Food was hot. Burgers were great. Season fries were ok. Service was excellent. We ordered from the walk up window and didn't wait long. Cars pulled up and someone came out to take the order of the cars not at the window. The outside of the building could use some TLC.

Worked in Nashville during the summer of 2012 and found this place online.   Got a delicious burger, fries and shake the first time.  Stopped a few more times last summer.  Stopped by tonight on a follow up visit to Nashville and was reminded just how good a burger this place makes.   

$7.50 for a big burger, fries and soda.  Great deal!

I heart Fat Mo's. This is the place that started it all for me. Went here first at the urging of a friend, and fell in love with their burgers. Frequented the Murfreesboro location so many times I can't see straight, but this is where it started. Makes my mouth water thinking about getting one of their seasoned burgers on my palate. Must go again soon!
s2OLYBa-s3crujHbzO3Wcg: Everything its reputation presents itself to be. Jason was knowledgable and enthusiastic which I usually hate because I care about the food. But I appreciate his appreciation for the food. Lobster mac, 11oz filet, Brussels, shoestring fries. Delicious.

I really don't like chain restaurants and only went because my boyfriend loves steak. However, I was pleasantly surprised by Ruth's Chris. The steaks came out perfectly, green beans and cream corn were top notch. The banana pudding was out of this world. We also really liked our server.
BBQ Shrimp. Lobster Bisque. Filet. Bread Pudding. These are my favorites! Don't overlook the rasberry sorbet, it is a very close second! Oh and did I mention the lobster bisque??? It is the best!!
UYO_d_iqHp4trXNxVOVR1w: Lovely place! Food- Best!! Aura-Best!! In total, loved the place. Will go again definitely. We had Jack of no trades, Heineken and Budweiser, then the nachos- I've always been a lover of nachos and this place aroused more love for the nachos in me. They were so yummy. The price of beer is comparatively low. Food is cooked well with the perfect garnishing and dressing. What else one needs? The bill? Ah! It was too reliable. We came out happily with our stomachs full of pleasant-tasting food.
If you come in here make sure to have Blake Lanham make you a Ramos Gin Fizz. It's the best one I've ever had!!!!! Have one or five! You won't regret it.
The nachos were the best we've ever had! We also had a daily special drink that was delicious. We loved the atmosphere at Bastion!
OLa-9kI4kWN37vh74lbYwg: I frequently come with a friend on holidays when the entrees are half price. We went on Labor Day and had a fine meal; both of us had beef. The wine was half price by the bottle as well.  Our server was attentive, but not too much so. My usual Manhattan was large and fixed correctly. We both enjoyed the kidney bean salad on the salad bar and of course the Green Goddess dressing truly turns back the pages of time. My favorite dessert is the peppermint ice cream with chocolate sauce. It's a bit on the expensive side, but these special deals for holidays always make me come back.

My favorite restaurant in Nashville. Great place to celebrate as well. I've had nothing but exceptional service each and every time I go. Has a vibe of old steakhouse and English pub. Salad bar? Couldn't comment since I've never used it- I prefer the wedge with bleu cheese and bacon. My strip, although no better than I can cook, was definitely a great steak. Get the tableside bananas foster or the bread pudding to end. I like the half price wine as well. I'll be back as many times as I'm able. A must have- martini with the bleu cheese olives. And that smell when you walk in? Don't sweat it- it's just the smell of old, rich, white people.

My favorite  restaurant, food is always prepared with care,  beautiful  fresh simple flavors. For our 30th anniversary we dined on delicious  Crab cakes loaded with lump meat,  perfectly  grilled lamb chops,  sweet, &  savory mango & sausage mushrooms and table-side bananas foster,  so memorable,  I can't wait to go back!
EEYl4Na1gn2yz3IhZ1mScg: I was a little skeptical at first. I'm not a really big snow cone person. I love Korean snow cones, so when I saw that they had ice cream in their snow cones I thought I'd give it a try. It was very much worth it. I had the tres leches and it was awesome!!  It had great rich flacked of Carmel and the ice cream was vanilla bean. A really nice blend. I can't wait to try other flavors. It is definitely worth a try if you want an experience full of flavor.

My family and I found this food truck because it was next to the Grilled Cheeserie. Fortune smiled at us this day. After a wonderful lunch, their sno-cones were a fitting ending. 

I was expecting a traditional sno-cone with grainy ice. This ice was so soft. I had the piña colada. It was light and refreshing.  I wish there was something like this on my hometown.

I have been CRAVING a good, New Orleans style snowball since the weather heated up and had sadly resigned myself to the fact that I would have to wait until my next trip to the Crescent City to indulge in my favorite summer time treat... Alas, all is not lost! Retro Sno is delicious and is becoming one of my favorite food trucks in Nashville! Their raspberry wedding cake snowball is not to be passed up (and don't skimp on the condensed milk either!!). The only reason they come short of all 5 stars for me is because I wish their menu was a little bit more expansive and a lot more sugary and less all natural!! Still, I have a feeling that I'll be chasing these folks around Nashville for the rest of the summer.
lTe6ItmfcPlA4-Q4CTzpfw: Best Brunch in the Nations hands down! Great staff that are knowledgeable and give great suggestions! If you don't see anyone to seat you just walk to the bar and they'll get you in! The food and the cocktails are both amazing as well!!

With a very comfortable, casual and cozy atmosphere, this is the kind of place where you want to stay awhile. The food is outstanding and the talented chef hones in on a menu that feels perfect for the season. We enjoyed several small plates and each one was exceptional, especially the pork, veal and duck meatballs; outstanding! The porcini pappardelle made with shitake and oyster mushrooms was a creative, simple but fantastic pasta dish that we thoroughly enjoyed. Dessert was done right as well, the chocolate orange tart with sweet potato ice cream was perfection. Highly recommend this restaurant.

I was presented with a lovely chef's tasting. I was invited in compliments of the chef and manager after a shaky pop up experience. I only mention this as a back story to the dichotomy of how organized and flavorful this new experience was. I was impressed. All courses were beautifully seasoned and wonderfully presented. Standouts for me were the tortelli and sausage. Whatever nutty seasoning was in the tomato salad was fantastic. The desserts were wonderful and had a great balance - not too sweet. 

Furthermore, I appreciate that the chef and owners cared enough to invite us in to try the menu in a kitchen environment he had more control in. The menu is a bit before its time in sophistication for the neighborhood but will likely catch up as the city grows. It's much more of something you would expect in Germantown or Lincoln Park Chicago. (Which isn't a bad thing if you've eaten in either neighborhood)

Additionally, after Instagram stalking food photos from the chef and other diners; I'm pretty interested in trying the whole fish (red snapper looks to die for) and the pork belly if it returns to the menu (didn't see it on tonight's full menu). The cheese and dried meats looked pretty dope as we peaked at what was coming out to other tables.
V7dXBEowSa53mOVaDtnj_A: As good as it gets!!!! Service was great!! Food was great!!! Company goal reached and celebrated royally at Topgolf!!! Thanks Rashawn!!

Made a Fathers Day reservation about 3 weeks out!  Arrived early on the day of and were met with very cordial staff who were eager to get us drinks and food.  The venue is modern and clean. 

The wait to get food was long and we never got our silverware we requested so we had to get them ourselves. It was understandably busy so I'm still giving 5 stars because our orders came out correctly.  

In the bay (3rd floor)the seating was comfortable but unfortunately not too durable as our seat's seams had holes in them. There is plenty of seating although you are limited for 6 per bay.  We brought along our 5 year old who had plenty of room also. There are two bar stools and a table at the back of each bay. 

Food was good and decently priced ($$). Reservation was also decently priced and we had a good time.  Venue also offered large Father's Day frames to use as props for pictures.  All in all, very satisfied and will return.

Completely positive experience with Top Golf in Nashville. Food was great and the service was excellent. Finding your own parking may be tough if you go during peak hours. They have valet parking and for $5 I'd do it every time. Our server came around regularly and was very polite and helpful.
TvT6zNBiIzSZRPDN66Mh5w: Absolutely love this restaurant! Will be back again! My little boys love pirates and loved the live music and got up front and danced- super fun place to eat! Probably the best place in all of Nashville!

We just moved here in October 2016.  We love bars on the water so we thought we'd check it out.  First off the service is great, everyone is very friendly, food is fantastic and they have THEE best Bushwackers!  During the day there is no other place that beats being on the water.  We will be checking out the night scene there soon for live music.

Great little out of the way joint in the marina.  I suspect a pretty crowded place peak season.  Had some of their home made soups on a cold day and they were very good.  Very friendly place.  good view.  Nice to be on the water...
TpeIZYevmpyenHeCTknqMQ: Man I have gained so much wait eating here over the years! Great place and I love coming in there to chat with Mrs. Brenda. Great breakfast sandwiches, too. A very good home-made burger for less than 3 dollars! I do miss the meat and three, spicy turnip greens and meat loaf where my favorite. This is the kind of place we have to support to keep in business!!!! This is a music row hidden gem. Give this place a shot!

I popped in here today to grab a plate lunch to go. I was hoping for some good home-cooked veggies, but the options were limited to barbecue type stuff (pork, smoked chicken, burgers). I was very pleased with barbecue, baked beans and potato salad. The lady serving up the goods was awesome. She asked if wanted big girl sauce or little girl sauce, hee. While it wasn't very spicy, the big girl sauce was tasty.

Virginia's is a Music Row institution. You're not anybody on the row until you've been here. It's right on the corner of 18th and Division, and some ppl use their lot to cross between the two. It's usually fairly busy, but not too packed. It's a convenient store/meat-n-three. 

I've only ever got the cheeseburger from the meat-n-three in the back, and I remember it being pretty good. The menu back there changes regularly and there isn't any seating on the premises. Up front they have homemade sandwiches (the turkey is pretty good) and desserts (I'm a fan of the cookies). 

As for the convenient store part, you've got all the regular stuff, Coke and Pepsi products, Sweat tea, fountain drinks, chips (baked, regular and sun). They ask that you not pay with credit card for purchases under $10 though, which I totally understand.
_xRo-2ynfc9HQitXKbsfQQ: Gotta love this place! Located in a shopping center with a bright blue (painted?) glass door their food is authentic and delicious! Much to my surprise, they also have a stage area, and have concerts and events there. A popular latin american band is playing there later this month, and they advertise soccer games being shown. The maracuya (passionfruit) juice was incredible and fresh, and only $2! Their tortillas were fresh and delicious. Waitress working there was very friendly, and attentive to children. Prices were quite low. My friends from Hondouras took me there and enjoyed teaching me about the country, and showing me places on the large school-sized wall map. It was a great meal!

I have only eaten here once, but based on that single experience the food was outstanding. The place looked generally clean, the wait staff was very friendly and the service was fast and efficient. The dishes I had were very nicely balanced with unique flavors and attention to detail. I will be going back.

The food was delicious but the staff wasn't that great when I arrived. She warmed up and chatted with me until I received my food which only took about 5 or so minutes. She was also helpful in making suggestions when I informed her that I'd never eaten there. Definitely go for lunch. The portions are smaller but its only about $6. I'd go back for lunch any time
wBFmEtHy7M3IuyY7asQu4w: Good pizza, friendly service.  Definitely more of a delivery/carry out shop than a dine in.

I discovered this restaurant when my husband & I moved to Nashville. Such a great pace to get some really good authentic Italian Pizza. Sicilian pizza is extreme & Fresh. Very reasonable pricing. there delivery is fast as hell. They told me it me my order was going to be 30min & they where there about 20min. there very professional & got my order right every time. I must say, they are the best pizza I've had in Nashville.

We were staying at a hotel near Nashville for the weekend and rather than go out and few with the cold, we opted to order in. We ordered two Italian subs and a cheese stick. The subs were huge! Enough for two people to share. Loaded with meat and veggies. Bread was super fresh too! The bread was homemade and you could tell. The cheese sticks were your typical pizza cheese sticks. We ordered a medium but would have had plenty with a small. We had lots left over and luckily our hotel has a fridge and microwave. The guy were delivered was very nice and I loved the 2 liter was cold so we did not have to leave room to get ice. You pay with your card when they come to deliver. Great option only waited about 40 mins for delivery on a Friday night. Lots of people are in town for the Music City bowl so 40 mins was quick to me!
9LQKDG_fAkAYdIYhzeOBsA: Good sandwiches. Good alternative to most other spots in the area that seem overcrowded at lunch. This place seems less busy than most for some reason.

Great inexpensive place to eat they gave my food to another customer yesterday even thou I raised my hand and they have no wifi but I will have to say the value for the price is excellent and the food is way better than the other guys sandwich shops in nashville tn
We come here often. Fresh hot subs, made to order, choose-your-flavor fountain machine, & a large selection of hot sauces to add extra spice to your selection. Service is always quick & friendly.
L-ZB0dlt1XsT0d9t1yl7qA: Enjoyed a very nice meal at Whitfield's last week. This was my second visit, and it was a nice as the first.  I ordered the scallops, which were well-prepared and nicely presented. I found the peach chutney to be slightly thick and a wee too sweet, but the scallops were perfectly cooked, as was the broccolini side. The white wine suggested by the server was a excellent choice. Gorgeous night to dine out on the patio, and both owner's graciously stopped by the table to ensure the meal and drink was to my liking. Finished up with the recommended banana beignets and an irish coffee. All delightful; cost was about $80 for two glasses of wine, tuna appetizer, scallops, dessert and after-dinner coffee. Food was delightful; the only disappointment was the full 5-minute wait at the hostess stand before being greeted and seated; apparently, she was outside seated at a table and chatting with the owners....

Taking advantage of a special offer, my girlfriend and I set out for a nice dinner and some drinks at this quaint little spot. The atmosphere was amazing. I made a reservation for Friday night on Thursday and we only waited about 10 minutes once we walked in the door. Seated by a very apologetic host even though we hardly waited at all. There was a piano bar that night, which made the meal that much more enjoyable. 

Our waiter was fantastic. Prompt with bread and water. Took our drink order immediately. I am a whisky drinker and he was extremely knowledgeable about their selection and tastes associated with each brand.

The food was great. She had the chicken and I had the trout. The trout was really good. Skin lightly crispy and very light yet flavorful. She definitely ordered better though. It was the best cooked chicken that I have had in a long time! So moist...

All in all, I will DEFINITELY be back. Pricey, but the great food and ridiculously nice, courteous staff providing impeccable service definitely made it worth paying the price.

The 4 stars are for the Roasted Chicken entree.  Best roasted chicken I've ever had (I normally wouldn't order chicken...bor-ing) but the sides that accompanied this dish enticed me to pursue it.  

The large portion justified the $21 price tag.  Most tender, perfectly cooked chicken ever along with baked mac & cheese that was anything but ordinary.  I loved the brussel sprouts -- it all went together perfectly.  

The service was okay -- I'd have to return again in order to solidify my opinion on this.  Unique, relaxed atmosphere with a one or two man band playing in the corner (not too loud).
ILcH8Zu2JiTij1hzlFuvQw: Great crispy yummy chicken. Chicken gyro was great. Wish we would have tried hummus. Will be back!

I've been very impressed with the quality of food and service from this eatery. Fresh ingredients, fast service, good prices. Yes, it's a hole in the wall, but those are usually the best. I'm happy to have found this foodie gem. I chuckle when I see the people walking into the Subway next door. They don't know what they're missing.

I come here a lot because the food is always good and the gentlemen who own and operate this place are always very friendly. 

They even offered to come to my house and jump my car when it was stalled. 

I am a big guy but the mixed plate always fills me up without making me feel guilty.
iryqT-RFwl7erA4MDAuLyg: We loved Funk Seoul Brother! We ordered the Crack Rice and Bulgogi Tacos- both of which were spicy and delicious. The man who took our order was super friendly too!

I can't say enough great things about this place! I am addicted to their sushi burritos... I highly recommend this place to anyone that is thinking about going!

Josh silver
Fantastic Korean tacos! Made fresh. Great flavors. The flavors, heat level, and to cook on the beef were perfect. Good price.
mP8gKzr8NKvbTtm9XePvOQ: Great chicken fingers! Love the fries! The servers are helpful, friendly and spunky. I have been in twice now and I enjoyed my food both times. I think everyone's still figuring out the environment but overall I hope this one sticks.

We had a really good experience here last night. We went on a Tuesday and it was quiet. We sat on the patio. Its a GREAT place for doggie lovers. Dog are welcome on the patio and they even have dog treats on the menu. Ha! 
The food and service was GREAT. The view of Nashville was awesome too. Parking was free and close! LOVED it.

Always looking for new places to play trivia around Nashville and this might be my new favorite. Laid back and comfortable environment, great food, and a large beer selection with decent prices. I got the BLAT (BLT with avocado) and absolutely loved it. The service was very good too. I will be back for sure.
BryAECgZkOzgPxbRlincsA: Traveling through and it was close to our hotel, and the Yelp reviews were good so we tried it.  The reviews were on target -- food here is excellent.  Spicy pork burrito was extremely tasty; refried beans were great.  Salsa was a bit spicy.  Cheese quesadilla was very tasty. Ambience extremely basic; service was excellent.  Clearly popular with the locals.  Strongly recommend it.

I'm a fan, for sure.  

The short:  It's the real deal.

More verbose, without being too self-aggrandizing.    I was concerned when I pulled into an empty lot at 11:30 to meet a friend for lunch, but that didn't last long.  (the empty lot, that is) We both had tacos (beef and chicken), as well as some beans and rice.  I found the tacos to be fresh in every way, full of subtle, complex flavors, and as yummy as can be.  Beans and rice was beany and ric-ey. Tough to go wrong there.  

Bottom line:  I'll definitely be back.  

Negative: Whether or not it's intentional, I don't care - but the staff has an amazing lack of understanding of English.  Don't go here if you're hung up on ambiance.

I love just about everything on the menu. The charcoal chicken is always fresh and flavorful. They didn't give me enough rice and beans though. The Steak Tostadas are great too and come piled high with toppings. This is my go to for Mexican food. Reasonable prices too!
woF39W2-DHBbqKSabbCCCQ: I met some friends here last night and I really enjoyed my experience.  The atmosphere is really nice and the service was wonderful.  I'm vegan, so I really appreciated the fact that they have several vegetarian dishes that can be veganized by leaving off the cheese.  I enjoyed my salad and my friends all enjoyed their food.  I just wish they had some house wines that are less than $10, but the food is moderately priced.  I will return again for sure!

Feast has a great bar atmosphere and reasonable prices on their food and drinks. There were a couple of missteps on my visit, so I'll get those out of the way first. 

One, Old-Fashioneds are not made with dry vermouth. No amount of tasty Blanton's bourbon can save that mistake. Two, if you let someone order their burger to temperature (ie medium) don't send it out well done. It was juicy, house-made, and well-seasoned, but still brown throughout (not medium).

Now, the positives. The space is awesome. I love the decor, this minimalist feel, and of course, the view of downtown! In the summertime I imagine their patio will be a great place for an evening out. Parking is easy and free in the well-lit garage below the building. Food came out of the kitchen quickly and attractively presented. Did I mention the prices are great? Even the non happy-hour food and drink prices were reasonable. If I was headed downtown for an evening out, Feast would be a great place to go eat delicious food and not break the bank. 

The service is attentive and professional, but in the words of Stephen A. Smith, "Come on, man!" Get the Old-Fashioned and the burger temperatures right.

My husband and I have been to Feast on two separate occasions.  We like sitting at the bar for several reasons.  Mostly because service is great and the view is spectacular!  Feast is a place where you can enjoy a casual meal without being left with an empty pocket.  My favorite item menu is the Chicken Salad.  It is a traditional, mayo- based chicken salad that doesn't swim in the Hellmanns.  Served on a bed of romaine and accompanied by toast points, this salad pairs really great with their sauvignon blanc by the glass.  Of course, a couple of basil hadens afterwards doesn't hurt either.  We have not been to happy hour yet, but it is on our list.  The app and drink specials look great!  With plenty of free underground parking, Feast is especially convenient for rainy days.  An open floor plan and outdoor seating with a beautiful view of the city and sunsets offers quite a Feastive feel!!
SgPJgt78i8v94UlDXk-nrg:
This review is for the actual Goozy store that is not in the Green Hills Mall. I was stunned to see that this place averaged 3-stars, until I realized that the store location closed!

Goozy is a cool place that serves sandwiches, wraps, soups, etc. But I'm pretty sure most people come here for the dessert. My friend and I walked in on a weekday since we were both craving something sweet. I ordered the Nutella Goozy Cake - dark chocolate molten cake with Nutella center, Amaretto gelato, Amarena cherry sauce, topped with a chocolate-dipped pretzel. The Chili's Molten Lava Cake is one of my favorite desserts of all-time, but this Nutella Goozy Cake outdid the Molten Lava Cake by an inch. At first, it felt like I was having an affair with the Molten Lava Cake, but three bites later I realized that maybe Nutella was my true love. The Nutella dominated my taste buds (ain't nothin' wrong with that), but the gelato and cherry sauce provided a nice balance of warm, gooey, and cool all at the same time.

This dessert cost under $7 for me after taxes.

Sad to hear that the store closed. Based on the reviews of the mall location, step your game up, Goozy!
Really inventive food. Sad to hear about the actual store location shut down. Hope they keep making their deserts though!

Super cool little place in the green hills mall. There is very few food options here but this place picks up the slack! The staff was super friendly and the food was very unique. My woman and I both got "walking tacos" which is pretty much the coolest take on a fruit pie I have ever seen. They open a snack size bag of Fritos and put chili, sour cream, cheese, and chives in the inside and you eat out of the bag with a fork! Super fun little snack and well priced. We also tasted the espresso gelato which was very flavorful and a little would go a long way! We will definitely be back for another walking taco when the weather cools off!
kqILsuvTSSW6v82S-_9jRg: The restaurant is small and intimate, but very clean. The miso soup was pretty delicious, full of flavor and hot. I didn't even need to add salt and/or pepper, something that I generally need to do when having miso. As for the sushi, both rolls had a nice fresh flavor. The shrimp dumplings were also quite good. The prices were acceptable. I'd come back!
Always fresh and tasty. The restaurant is always impeccably clean. There isn't a huge selection but what they have they do right. Every plate looks like a work of art and is almost too pretty to eat.
As everyone has mentioned (ad nauseum) the decor is spartan but honestly who cares?  The sushi was excellent and the service was great!  I'll definitely come back.
vM4NTqp7akWhNVxZglPTxQ:
Love this place. It helps that it's only a block from my office and therefore perfect for weekday lunches, but then again they're only open for weekday lunches, so I guess it's kismet. The managers/owners have been very supportive and clear about letting me know the beans, rice, and guac are all vegan, so this is an ideal place for a vegan-friendly burrito, bowl, or salad. And speaking of salads, that's what I eat when I'm eating raw, and there are plenty of veggies to add (along with guacamole, of course!) to make it a substantial meal. 

Their guacamole, by the way, is different from most others in town: it's heavy on cilantro and lime, I think, and that gives it a really refreshing flavor. I'm a fan, but if you don't like cilantro and lime all that much... well, what are you doing eating at a Mexican place anyway?

My only complaint is that I wish they were open for dinner and on the weekends, since I sometimes work late enough to want to grab dinner nearby, and I live near enough that this would be a great weekend option.
i love this place. the food is delicious. definitely get the chips and queso! the service is very fast. the employees are kind and friendly.
Great consistent food. ...they get so busy sometimes that they over charge by accident. So make sure to check ur bill. Other than that, they are great.
DKUCYmrxjNHkaQowwKcOQw: Such a great addition to Donelson! I've tried many different flavors, and they have all been delicious! I just had coconut cream for the first time today, and it was heavenly! I also love the Italian wedding cake and carrot cake flavors. You really can't go wrong! Also, they serve Bongo Java coffee, which is awesome! Everyone is always so friendly and welcoming too. A lady named Lisa helped us today, and she was great!

Mignon Francois and her family make wonderful and affordable cupcakes. From their home in Germantown, this new Cupcake Collection outpost fills a cupcake sweet tooth need for Donelson. I don't know that this neighborhood is hip in an East-Nashvile way, l but the local pep squad can justifiably state that the neighborhood really is more "hip Donelson" since Cupcake Collection arrived. When I bite into one of their Sweet Potato cupcakes it feels like the time having Carolina sweet potato pie, with icing on top. The Italian Wedding Cake cupcake is worth the extra 50 cents -- most are $2. Red Velvet is my second favorite and is topped with mild creme cheese frosting. You can now get Francois family cupppycakes in Donelson most of the time Monday through Friday 10-4. The hours may expand when they finish remodeling this old Becker's site. Becker's was a great Nashville cake tradition and now Cupcake Collection is an award-winning Nashville cupcake tradition. Tradition continues, thank goodness.

Excellent cupcakes and great variety!  The strawberry cupcake is my favorite but really all of them are good.  I also enjoy the carrot cake cupcake and have tried the marble cupcake and sweet potato cupcake.  You really can't go wrong!
5DFbSl-ACNHFUnc8C8_Jaw: The pizza is always delicious! I order delivery from Roma's from time to time, and the delivery staff has always been friendly. I typically order a pizza with chicken, tomato, and spinach. The pizza comes with that good kind of garlic sauce! It is also great the next morning for breakfast, and still tastes super delicious if I can't make it to the microwave on break.
I have to just brag. This is my neighhood Roma's and this pizza is the best looking and best tasting pizza I have ever had from there. Thank you for a yummy pizza!!!

Not sure why all the bad complaints on here but I have never had a issue with ordering anything from this place.. definitely the best salads around!! I will always continue to do business with this place.
YI28lz_EtAATtHGyXp8Xsg: I'm so excited to have them in the neighborhood! awesome beer selection, and eats are simple and on point. looking forward to many fun evenings here!

Comfortable bar with laid back feel. Great selection (that changes constantly) of craft beers served by friendly, knowledgeable staff. Parking can be a challenge during the busiest hours, but definitely worth the effort!

Great local/craft beer selection, outdoor seating, beer cheese.  What else do you need??

The pretzel balls and beer cheese were spectacular... I had to have more of that beer cheese, so enter the Porter Road Butcher dog on a pretzel bun with beer cheese and bacon.  Not health food by any means, but a fantastic splurge if you need a cheat day.  There are no fries, everything is served with chips. No burgers on the menu either, but let's be honest, you'd be next door at Flip Burger if that's what you were craving anyway.  My husband had the nachos with PRB chili and they were great, too.  I love that they use PRB meats!

Our server was very knowledgeable about the beers on tap and didn't mind our questions about the more obscure options. Growler filling seems to be pretty popular there - there was a steady stream of people walking in and out with growlers the whole time we were there. 

The shared lot for that strip isn't huge, so parking might be a challenge during peak times, but we had no issues.
YY1MUBZ0WTAAQnxEWKRoCw:
MMMMMMMM Fish and chips!

Huge fillets of white fish with a pile of chips!  They were cooked crispy to perfection and tasty not bland.  We had ordered the potato chips as an appetizer so I was stuffed by the time I was done.  The cheese that came with the potatoes was just spicy enough to get you wanting more.  

The waitress was exceptionally attentive and sweet!  
Love coming here with my family!
Thick, meaty and juicy Baby back ribs. Hot tasty rolls. Great service. Newly renovated and comfortable. Definately will eat here again!

As an eastern United States staple, it's great to have O'Charley's in the Nashville airport.  This Nashville-based restaurant has its corporate headquarters not too far away; so, you can expect a pretty good experience.  The food is safe for all tastes and the service is good (I always get the same waiter, it seems).  You'll need AT LEAST 45 minutes in your layover to eat here, so if you're limited, you may want to head to the food court instead.  Musts:  loaded potato soup and the three cheese bacon burger.
S8cEYPmeIpb2q6AWF9Tn5g:
I'm a fan.  And I like this location.  Mainly because it's closer to me, but it's also roomy without being too big, and great for a business breakfast or lunch without all the crowds getting in the way.  

I love their cook-to-order breakfast omelets in the mornings, and always treat myself to an iced sugar cookie in whatever shape of the season they might have.  Makes me feel like a kid again!  I'm not a huge fan of the chicken salad, but then I'm really picky when it comes to chicken salad.  It's good, but I just prefer my own.  

When I used to buy breakfast for clients, they put together a great tray of bagels and muffins--complete with plates and utensils, as well as several kinds of cream cheeses.  You don't have to do a thing but pick it up ad deliver it!  

All in all, Bread & Company is a great local establishment, and I'll continue to support it!

One of my favorite places to eat in Nashville.  While bread & company is a little on the pricey side, the food is fantastic.  Sandwhich as always fresh and I love how you can pick & choose what you want in your food.  Their bread is absolutely delicious my absolute favorite is  the cranberry walnut bread YUM.  Fruit Tea is also a very unique drink which I've never had before moving to Nashville.   When I had gone home to New York, I wanted to bring a little Nashville back so I bought some cinniman rugalache back for my brother, in which he polished within 30 mins.  He loves those things so much that when he came here, he bought 2 bags & finished them within a day, and before he had to leave he bought 5 more bags to bring back to NY some to eat & some to freeze.  This is one place where I always crave their food.

One of my must eats when I visit home!  I go for the pre-made sandwiches (they're already stellar) because I'm usually in a pinch.  
Roast beef with horseradish mayo, thinly sliced red onions and lettuce is perfect on some gooooood bread.
"the boulder veggie" is also one of my favorites!  Slices of all sorts of fresh veggies with an artichoke spread! Yum!

Great pastries and desserts, prepared foods and my favorite... FRUIT TEA (aka Tea Punch).  Can't find it out in California, so I must get my fix in Nashville.

Oh yeah, great bread too, but that goes without saying!
U4FaNiJ7rOgZT04cQDrk5w:
Oh Oktoberfest - my friend and foe. 

Every year in October I look forward to this event. Pull out the beer steins (dust them off) and get ready for a day filled with music, beer and brats. This year we were fortunate enough to have VIP tickets. If I were rating the VIP tickets, Id give a 3, maybe even a 2 star. They 'supposdely' order full liter stiens but got half liter stiens for the event. The food was mediocre at best - under cook "german" potato salad (just because you put mustard on potatoes doesnt mean you call call it warm german potato salad people!) and watery brats. It was just ok. It was good and redeeming because you were able to get out of the rain. If it was super hot (like last year) I would have probably liked it better. But honestly, it wasnt worth the money - it would be better spent on beers & brats. Anyways, the festival is filled with different vendors selling things (and giving samples) along with a few beer gardens/sellers among other things. We normally fill our stiens, walk around to the little shops and enjoy the afternoon. All in all its an amazing time.

A large block party with a German twist.  Beer Gardens, 3 stages with live bands, tons of hipsters, and a rescue cat bus.  I thought the event was well-organized, drawing people out even though the weather did not cooperate and it was cold and windy.

I found free parking within a block of the festival and traffic was not as bad as I feared.
A wonderful event! Fun by all. So many vendors and music...nice evening!
FFQeh4p0s5HKriZZajJpfw: I've been dining at The Mad Platter for several years now and it is a wonderful place to take my clients.  I feel compelled to write a review after the 5 star service I received at lunch the other day.  I was waited on a dapper gentleman with glasses who has waited on me several times before.  My client and I were very impressed with his service and knowledgeable suggestions for our lunch.  The food was fantastic and atmosphere was quite cozy but a little kitschy.  I had the chicken salad which I always get and it was amazing!  I would love to see the Brown Butler served every day however I know this is quite the indulgence.  All in all this is the best place in Nashville with local, sustainable food with a wonderful wait staff.  I would always suggest this restaurant as this is truly my favorite!

We had a decent meal here on a week night and were just about the only ones in the restaurant. We all enjoyed our meals. I wouldn't say its particularly memorable, but you won't be disappointed. Enjoyed the vegetable risotto the most. would have liked to see more homemade deserts like pies.

I have been trying to find the best american cuisine in Nashville for several months now and I was hoping I would find it at the Mad Platter. The building is quaint ad we were informed it was once a meat packing facility by our waiter. The place was empty when we arrived (on a Wednesday night at around 7:30) so there was no real ambiance to speak of. The waiters were very attentive and knowledgeable about the menu which changes daily to reflect the fresh local ingredients. I selected the 5 course meal which is an additional charge of $20 on top of the main course and I could not have been happier. 

I started with the Shrimp and Andouille gumbo which was slightly different than the traditional dish. It was an amazing and tasty take on a Cajun classic. That was followed by a macaroni and cheese made with gnocchi and beef that could have been my favorite take on mac and cheese to date. The third course was a delicious Caprese salad made with local tomatoes. My main course was the duck served on a bed of Arugula. I enjoyed the duck but to be honest it was probably the weakest of the main courses I tasted over the course of the evening (my friend had the rack of lamb which was simply amazing). The final course was the Elvis Chocolate Torte. This is a must get if you enjoy chocolate. It was the perfect end to the meal.

To date, The Mad Hatter has been the best dining experience in Nashville Tennessee. If you are looking for an amazing deal on a delectable dinner keep this place in mind.
7hJvSejJvI-ESVyH2gMO2Q:
I had a great dinner here a couple of months ago- I would recommend going with friends like I did so that you can sample as much as possible. My favorite part of the meal was the paella, which was overflowing with bountiful seafood- a lot of times when you order paella you get maybe one or two shrimp, but not this time! I had scallops, chorizo, clams, shtrimp, fish, and a lot of it! Empanadas and mojitos were also good, as well as the cuban. 

Service can be a little funny- it's very much a family owned restaurant, so sometimes you might be served by the owner, and sometimes you might get the owner's teenaged relatives, but generally speaking any kinks just add to the charm of the place.

I like the mix of Latin cuisines - while the menu leans to Cuban cuisine, you can find some South American (e.g. Venezuelan, Colombian, and Peruvian) dishes as well. We started with a sampler appetizer consisting of yuca with garlic, yuca fries, two styles of plantains, and roasted peruvian potatoes. It was a very satisfying start.
