Next up, we had a seafood gumbo and arroz con pollo. The soup had a delicious broth and good selection of seafood. The arroz con pollo was satisfying comfort food, with a nice fresh salad and sweet plantains on the side. There was no room for dessert, but room for cafe Cubano. In fact, the cafe Cubana makes me want to return for a Sunday brunch in the near future.

The service was friendly, attentive, and helpful. The prices are reasonable.
Great latin food and wonderful, friendly service. I'm a sucker for sweet plantains. Looking forward to visiting again!
9Elafazm4QcFRL5VCspPrw: Owners are sweet and food is pretty good! The first time I had the pho, I wasn't a fan because of how greasy the broth was. The second time I came my friends ordered it and I tried theirs just for comparison and it was more flavorful and not as greasy. There may be some inconsistency with that. I'll update it whenever I order pho again the next time. Their rice pork plate was so damn good. It was marinated with lemongrass. Their bun mam was very rich and bold in flavors. Their spring rolls and egg rolls were just ok.

Pho T&N has really great pho with a menu that provides many options. I got the spring rolls as an appetizer and my friend god the egg rolls, both of which were amazing. If you appreciate good pho, I would definitely recommend trying theirs. The atmosphere is also very clean and welcoming.

Pho done properly.  Broth cooked for 24 hours was flavorful.  The owner recommended which veggies to add for our desired flavor.  Food came quickly and our server was attentive without hovering.  Not much atmosphere but pleasant.  Beef stew was delicious, made with short ribs and served with rice noodles.  Ate my fill and had enough for a large lunch the next day.  I tried the strawberry bottled soda but won't get it again although we'll definitely go back for pho.
Xi5SU4VW3CYi-2o4UDxBcA: This is a great little sushi place in the heart of Hillsboro Village...Their rolls and bento boxes are excellent! If you are a fan of avocado their Eva roll is unbeatable...not to metion that you get free soup and salad with every meal!

I love the ambiance of this place. The soup and salad are both pretty good, and the sushi is a pretty safe bet. It's very good sushi. I love the dinosaur in the fish tank, the Woodstock poster, the dark room, the quirky staff and the quirky people who frequent this fun joint.

But everything else is soso risky. Once I got a chicken teriyaki bowl for lunch and couldn't go back for months because I was scared. Don't go here unless you want to snack on sushi and talk with friends.

But tip well. And buy fortune cookies. Because I love the people who own this place. And fortune cookies just make life better. Eva roll is very good, by the way. Note that they're open pretty late but always close in the afternoon, like most Asian restaurants.

EDIT: Maybe Ken's has better other stuff, but the sushi is heads and tails above Ken's. The one time I went to Ken's, it is so much more expensive than Taste of Tokyo and the sushi is dry.
rQ5a0LlEYyH5ld3k9V9NmQ: I of course had to stop in here since it is named after me!!!  I thought it was a cute little place and I wish I had more time to try the food, but I did check out the candy and fudge!!

Does anyone else feel like breaking into Willy Wonka songs as they enter this place.  Very old time soda and ice cream shop.  

This is an excellent place to stop and rest while walking through downtown.  Excellent ice cream and sundaes.  The sodas have free refills and if you get a float they will refill the soda for free.

Makes me feel like I stepped back in time for a few!
Hey, pretty good. Nice atmosphere. The shakes are big and good. The tuna melt I had for lunch was good. It was kind of small for the price, but quality.
CejoKfjo6U4_wzhwWQerWg: I would love to give 5 stars but the dining room needs clean tables. There wasn't more than 3...it wasn't too busy for them to grab a rag and clean a table. Even crazier was that we visited during shift change and neither manager seemed to notice the filthy dining room!
I also would like to give 5 stars, but their hash browns are always soggy, never crispy. I eat breakfast here most weekends because I like the Crossandwich's, but the browns need work.

9pm visit after a long flight from Las Vegas. Used the drive-thru. Order taker, female, was very welcoming and courteous. Similar service was provided by the male that took my money and presented my food. Food was served hot and correct. What more can you ask for! Thanks!
Qpe1sNkcU8y4ZrpH5Pe-mA:
I showed up at Donk's with a party of 10 after driving for 7 hours from Columbus OH. We walked in the door and were greeted by at least 3 total strangers warmly welcoming us. I immediately knew we were in for a treat. Juicy took care of us and brought us delicious fries and 5 different types of wings. I particularly enjoyed the fried pickles. 

This place is not Hooters. It's low key and classy. The food is good and the atmosphere is excellent. There is pool, sports and karaoke. Also excellent Happy Hour specials. I will absolutely be returning in the future. Thanks Juicy!
Donks was honking awesome! We showed up with 11 dudes and got white boy waisted. Great food! Great service! Its like hooters but opposite and better food!

Great sports bar in Nashville .I have been for lunch and late night when It turns into a club.

Donks is  an urban version of a hooters .Girls shorts say DONKS on the back

Wings are great as is the fish.Next time I go I may have to get a turkey leg

Great place to go on a Sunday .NFL package!
zyHP-oXgDkANEyQbJVKf8g: Love their juices of course, but if you haven't tried their sandwiches - you must!  The Deh'ai or whatever it is called is by far my favorite sandwich I've ever had.  SO GOOD.  Love this place and the staff is always very nice and helpful.

Urban juicer is one of the best juice spots in Nashville.  They have a lot of options, smoothies and healthy bites as well.  I like that you can see how much produce they are using in your juice right in front of you.  Staff is nice and quick!
By far my favorite Juice!!! I love that they have already pressed juices AND you can make your own.  It is always really clean- friendly staff- and quick.  love this place.
6ko58lgD6RjhINuNcuP6bA: The gluten free chocolate truffle cake was one of the highlights of our wedding. I'm serious! I'm jealous of a cake :-)  And they're super nice and efficient, too, more than any of the other cake makers we were in touch with. Sadly we don't live in Nashville, but it would be worth moving there just to be closer to Dessert Designs (every weekend, ideally).  Thank you a million for your amazing work, it really made our day.

I've never actually been to the store (so 4 stars seems appropriate?), but I'm rating this because Firefly Grille serves one of their cakes that is ridiculously delicious. I'm a total cake snob, and this is a good chocolate caramel cake.

My husband and I could not have been happier with our wedding cake from Dessert Designs. It was absolutely delicious - so many guests told me, "I don't usually eat the cake at weddings, but this is amazing!" It was super-moist and very flavorful. This was true not only of the cake at our wedding, but also of the 9 cakes we tried at our tasting and the cake she made for our friend's wedding a month later. Our cake also looked beautiful. I showed Leland a rustic, twig-like design I found online, and she pulled it off perfectly. I can't wait to break out the top tier for our 1st anniversary!
N8SofTPv3MYseRutayDoJg: AMAZING!  Great wine list ...staff is very knowledgeable and friendly!!  Recommend it ....esp the brunch!!!!

Visited Louie's Wine Dive last week while visiting Nashville.  Yelpers seem to really like this place!  I was staying on West End and this spot is located around West End and the interstate on 17th Ave S.  Signage was a little tricky, but once I located the restaurant and went inside I really liked the decor/layout.  Service was good and Nick was pleasant and informative.  My buddy and I met for happy hour(HH).  We tried the HH mules (bourbon) which were good.  Also sampled 1 red and 1 white that was on special.  The appetizers we sampled included calamari, duck lettuce wraps, and the scb (hamburger) sliders.  All appetizers were $7 or $8 which seemed fair - each was tasty.  My favorite app was the duck lettuce wraps.  Although the interior is nice, there is also a patio which would be nice if it's not too hot.  Parking is available at tower nearby.  I'd like to revisit when back in Nashville.  Next time I'll try a wine flight.

This place just opened right beside my apartment building. The atmosphere is rustic and cozy. We had their brunch and it was amazinggggg. We started with the salmon toast and carne asada tacos. Both entrees were flavorful and delicious!  This place will definitely be one of our go tos.  Excited to try the rest of the menu !!!
HF8829JvM0ISYsuJH_aeSQ:
A couple of friends and I were cruising the strip late one day and we were looking for a place to sit down have a snack and a drink.  We were pleasantly surprised when we popped into Pearl.  The décor is very hip and trendy inside.  It is a Japanese fusion restaurant with really good Sushi rolls and steaks too.

I had the Budda Role, which consisted of avocado, cucumber, egg, and crab.  The flavor was robust and the crab tasted pretty fresh.  I had the big eye tuna sashimi, which was okay.  

I will come back soon.  The prices are not too bad either.

Had dinner there with a few friends and we were very impressed.  I had the filet mignon and it was the best steak I have ever had.  The staff were courteous and friendly, management seemed to have a genuine interest in their customers and the food was excellent.  I plan to hit this place at least a couple of times a month.
89j5KREEXM5E2cK8CwPoRA: Thank you for your exceptional service and delicious food I had the Spin Salad with Chicken and took the Thai Chicken to go... People were wonderful and super helpful thanks you guys

I'm your newest fan!  Your salad was the best I've had in quite some time.  I ordered the chicken Caesar yesterday... part of a group order from the office.  The salad was a good size and proportionate to the cost.  The chicken and other toppings were plentiful. I was quite impressed with the amount of shaved parmesan!  The dressing was amazing --- also plenty of it, served on the side.  Cheers to your continued success!

I had heard of this place before so I thought I would order from their delievery menu and check it out. There are lots of healthy options. Yes, there is pizza, but the entrees range from salmon to vegeterian bowl with poached eggs. I was definitely suprised to see the amount of options of on the menu. Also, there were gluten free pizza options. 
I chose a chicken caeser salad and a vegeterian rice bowl. I also chose to have some Omega 3 energy bites. I placed the order and promptly recieved a call from the restaurant stating they were out of the energy balls, but could recieve any dessert for the same cost. The service was friendly and efficient. I would come here again to try the other menu items.
N7puTVZcmKjK6UQtI0ybJQ:
Hesitant to write this review and let the secret out. This is one of the best dive bars in Nashville. 

Wings are on point. Burger is superb. Beer is cheap. Servers are friendly. What more can you ask for? 

Also a great spot to watch a game. It's a bit smoky but its worth it to step into a time capsule of 1985. It's Nashville's Cheers.

I have no idea why Edgefield has gotten such mediocre reviews.  I'm not a sports guy (at all), and I love the place.  The food is good and affordable, their drinks are plentiful, and the atmosphere is surprisingly bearable.  My buddy and I popped into Edgefield last night for dinner, and I was quite pleased with my experience.

First of all, the moment I noticed the cook had an enormous handlebar mustache, I knew the food would be delicious.  Anyone with a mustache that substantial has to be good at what they do.  It's a wisdom thing.  Anyhow, my suspicions were correct--my Reuben was out of this world, and my buddy said his burger was equally delicious.

I first thought Edgefield was going to be a real jock bar.  You know...totally full of knuckleheads.  But the crowd was sort of random, and they had "Dancing with the Stars" on the big screen--not exactly meathead programming.  While we were there, someone put The White Stripes, James Brown and several other soul and rock and roll tunes on the jukebox; I was very pleasantly surprised.

Our servers were pretty good and quick, though they weren't all that attentive.  The drinks were affordable but not cheap, and the atmosphere was very laid back.  Like I've said time and time again, sports bars really aren't my scene.  But the food alone makes this one is totally worth checking out.

I suppose this is considered a "dive" bar.. but it is clean so that's a positive in my eyes.. I don't know the tall dark haired bartenders name but she was nice, and made me great margaritas, even though they aren't technically on the menu there.. they have things like beer, bourbon, and whisky... They have lots of board games, and maybe 5 or 6 dart lanes. It was my first time playing darts.. which I actually really like! You can smoke here, which I'm not a smoker but yeah.. nice atmosphere. cool music and people.. we had a good time here!
m1kM87opn4tR6lt3v9AiWw:
Not bad, for a franchise restaurant!

Slipped away from an art exhibit and decided to have a quick dinner here.
Like everyone else - 1) fast  2) cheap  3) fresh  4) convenient.

It's not as salty as PF Chang's.
It's not expensive like PF Chang's.
It's not overly crowded with long wait lines, like PF Chang's..

good.

What it is, is fast, cheap, freshly made food.
I had the Spicy Korean plate with fried rice.
Plentiful, not real salty, good for the hunger pangs, easy on the wallet, decent on the pallet.
I want to return and try a couple other dishes that sounded good.
I appreciate how the manager runs the restaurant.  He understands Repeat Customer Satisfaction.

There are many restaurants in this town that don't!
I will return and try more dishes.

Pei Wei is PF Chang's cheaper, faster concept restaurant.  They definitely got the concept right.  It's fast, fairly priced and their food is really quite good.  It's not fast food cheap (i.e. $5 combos), but for $8-10 you get a large portion of higher-than-fast-food quality food (or a bit more if you get an appetizer).

I'm not a fan of the inside of the restaurant, because I don't care for the communal seating arrangement and even the stand-alone tables are really packed in there.  But I'm perfectly content getting it to go or coming during off-peak times.  

Anyway, as far as their food goes, I like their Mongolian Beef -- I think it works well with the nutty flavor of brown rice.  I also liked both the Thai Blazing Noodles and the Dan Dan Noodles.  I appreciate how they have the Chili Oil containers so you can liberally apply it to spice up your meal.  Add some to the Blazing Noodles and I'm set!

Anyway, for a quick and reasonably-priced, satisfying meal, Pei Wei delivers in my opinion.

I usually go to the Pei Wei in Brentwood, so this review is based on my 1 trip to this location.  Also - this was my husband's first visit to any Pei Wei.

Things I love about Pei Wei:

-they offer brown rice
-the portions are HUGE so you can get 2 meals out of 1 portion
-they offer Tofu in pretty much all entrees
-the food is pretty cheap
-you get your food really fast

I tend to always get the Mandarin Kung Pao with Shrimp, or sometimes with Tofu.  We ordered it with shrimp this time & it was really good. I really like the crunchy-fresh sugar snap peas & the sticky-spicy peanuts.  Yum!  We also had the wonton soup which was quite tasty - the first time I'd tried it - love the fresh Thai basil.

I have to say we will be returning to Pei Wei.  I have ordered some things in this past that are pretty bland - Spicy Korean, Lo Mein, Japanese Teriyaki - so now I just don't order them anymore.  The Mandarin Kung Pao and Thai Dynamite are my 2 faves - very flavorful.
UcpWBeicBU71JVyul_ThTQ: Great food and amazing drinks!!!!! Can't go wrong with the 2for 1 after ten everyday . The food was pretty good i ordered the godfather sub and was really surprised .

Jonathan's is really great. Came in before a movie, sat at the bar. It was crowded cause they do specials, only 2 bar tenders but service was awesome. Very attentive and prompt. We had just left the new Chilis across the street after having a terrible experience there and Jonathan's was amazing in contrast. Got cold brews and cashed out with plenty of time to make the movie.
We've been here for games and always have a great time with good service.

We love the Buffalo wings, French Dip, hamburgers etc. We'll definitely come back and try more dishes.
Update: We came back so many times, I still love the BBQ Wings, the Pork Burger and sweet potato fries are really good as well.
KyEQ6yfl3EmesWJqmoSqVA: real mexican food... sorry las palmas, mazatlan, etc.  Happy to have a great place in Nashville.

I tell you I've had authentic tacos while stationed in San Antonio Texas and these are definitely similar they are delicious and moist and tasty I'll be back! Small street tacos most definitely but the taste makes up for the size

This is a nice little hole in the wall restaurant with lots of taco choices. The menu is in Spanish and the employees only speak Spanish, so brush up on your reading and speaking skills or bring a translator friend with you when you come. Definitely worth it though!
QdKLyqac0Smpg1n7JTnrGg:
This place is amazing.

I've had lunch there a few times and each time I go it just gets better and better. The shrimp and grits are fantastic - perfectly cooked, the peach bbq sauce is great and not too sweet (and I'm picky about bbq sauce), the grits are tasty, and it's a perfectly sized portion. Did I mention it was covered in a handful of fresh corn? I had the herban chicken today (I always love a good pun). It was also delicious and I love that they have a low-carb/gluten-free option. I could eat here every day. And fun fact about the mint/lemon tea - free refills!

Also the ladies that run this place are super nice and obviously care a ton about the work that they're doing. I eat lunch at the farmers market a LOT, and this is by far the best food I've had there.

OMG! Seriously as I just finished my last bite of the Herban from their stand at the Farmers Market today I had the thought.....I NEED TO LEAVE THESE PEOPLE A YELP REVIEW. I am so passionate, these ladies know what they are doing. Wow. The textures, complex flavor profiles...delicious, HEALTHY, low carb, satisfying. I will be back and eat any vegetable dish they prepare for me.

I have HIGH standards and this place is AMAZING!
I got the Hot Chicken Rolls (which comes with a side of bacon mac and cheese for $10.92) and it was the best decision I made all week. 
They made everything right there in front of us (they even fried the chicken right then). 
The food was delicious! Mac and cheese included. 

Only con: seemed a bit pricey, you get two chicken rolls about the length of your index finger. This was enough food for me, but it may not be for everyone. 

All in all, I would definitely recommend a visit!
H2AmlQZXIdLh94sNC3wrLQ: I am just absolutely in love with this place! My husband and I have our date nights there as much as we can. But don't be fooled, kids are definitely welcome! We took our daughter there for Sunday Brunch and the place was full of families! The food is outstanding and we have always had great service. The atmosphere lends itself well to everyone, whether you want a casual dinner or are celebrating a special occasion! This restaurant is exatly what we have been looking for in this rapidly growing part of town!

When a restaurant is within walking distance to your house (i.e. u can drink as many martinis as you want), you gotta love it.

Went there last weekend with a fellow yelper and sat at the bar.  Jenny was our bartender.  She is the mother-in-law of the chef that introduced him to her daughter...(got that?)  She's a hoot.

Ordered the apple-banana martini - whoa, jenny sure does make em strong!!!   I was giggling by the first course!  Our appetizer was the truffled white bean dip with bread - DEElish.  I got the fish special corvina.  There was quite a discussion about what it was like - all I know is it was lick-the-plate-good.  He got the tuna - a little overdone but still tasty.  

With some great wine choices we ended the night with a great dessert (or did we....I can't remember...Jenny made em SO strong!  But, again, I walked home!

Great place - especially if your in the Nolensville area!!

Just an update... my husband and I visited there this past weekend, and Sofie's Bistro is no longer. The new establishment is called Southside Grill. Although I had not visited Sofie's Bistro under the original management, I am guessing that the decor wasn't changed much. I ordered the Smoked Gouda and Truffle Oil Hamburger, and it lived up to it's name. Juicy, flavorful, awesome. Sauteed spinach side was perfect. The service was excellent, and the pricing was great for the budget.
a52ldK1JA8qtZOm17PVNMQ:
Calzone was amazing! 
but our server was friendlier and more attentive than the servers at the renaissance hotel around the corner!

My fiancé and I were recommended this place by our hotel. We were in town from Oregon. We ordered delivery online and it got to us about 30 min later. I ordered a veggie calzone and it was delicious! The crust was soft, the veggies were plenty, and the small is more than enough for 2 people. My fiancé ordered garlic Parmesan wings and those were delicious as well. Perfectly seasoned and they also arrived hot! I like hot food! I'm not sure why this place only has 3 stars but then I've only tried two things on the menu. Going to bed fat and happy thanks to Caesars!

I had trepidation with ordering food from here based on many reviews, but it seems pizza is scarce around the area of Union and 7th St. Went ahead and ordered thru yelp. We walked down at the time it was supost to be ready. The inside looked like a basic pizza place, didn't really look but at a glance It looked clean. The food was ready on time and they made sure the order was filled correctly. They bagged our drinks and we went back to our hotel. Pizza was pretty good, cheese bread sticks were great. If we lived in the area we would order from them again.

 I did do pay cash at pick up because of another review about not getting pizza and being charged for it on their card.
YK-pF0TjZBu9q6esscCvrw: if you are a vegetarian and cringe at the thought of a meatball place not having something for you besides salad (even though their side salad is amazing) think again!!! the vegetarian meatballs are delicious and very filling!!! my husband had the regular meatballs and loved them. the portions are huge so you can have dinner takeout then have a meal for lunch the next day. another tip, please try the cupcakes. I tried the chocolate and vanilla cupcakes and can't get them off my mind since eating them! my husband got the red velvet and tiramisu and said that they were the best he's ever had. everyone gives this place a try, it has something for everyone, huge portions, and did I mention? cupcakes! it's about time a good restaurant is in this area and since I live near by I will be back again soon, maybe tonight....

I had a Groupon for Jeanne's and stopped in just 5 days after they opened.  I'm so glad that they chose to come to Antioch and wish more businesses would follow.  The area needs more places like Jeanne's. 

I had a Groupon for 2 dishes, 2 salads, 2 drinks and 2 cupcakes for $12.  What a deal!  I had turkey meatballs with marinara on rice and my boyfriend had spicy pork meatballs with teriyaki gravy on mashed potatoes.  Delicious!  The meatballs have a great flavor.  The portions were so large that neither of us could finish.  The salads were great with nuts, cranberries and different kinds of lettuce.  The drinks do come in cans, but I don't mind.  

There is limited seating, but their Facebook page says they are primarily for pick up orders.  It was busy when we arrived, but cleared out quickly since most people were just picking up and taking the food home.  There are a few tables though, both inside and out.

Parking will never be an issue since they are in a strip mall type area with a Kroger and Babies R Us next door.

I plan on returning.

The meatballs are delicious. They're really nice too. I'd definitely recommend it. We found out about this place via their Groupon. The location isn't the best, but the food is great. The prices are very reasonable.  The portion sizes are great. We got the meal with three meatballs and we couldn't finish it. It's a locally owned family business as well, which I like.
jtWtWs-QrG978Mu4-bXAUQ:
the hostess was SUPER nice and accomodating.  totally got us a taxi and asked them to wait just a bit as we finished our meal...i didn't get to see a whole lot of nashville when i was in town, but was glad that i had a great dining experience when i could get out (stayed at the gaylord opryland).

btw - when you are here - get the scallops - they are delish!

What is there not to like about this spot?!?! I loved everything about this spot. Well, I must confess, my friends and I happen to luck out. When we go to the restaurant, the upstair patio was closed due to a private birthday party; when this occurs, the restaurant prepares several tables on the front to accomodate people like us. =] The evening was perfect because 1) the weather was perfect, not too hot or humid, perfect spring evening, 2) the live jazz band accomodated by the birthday part was fantastic! 3) all the dishes were just yummy in my tummy. We had the shrimp w/ risotto, pan seared fillet, hallibut, ceviche (*drool~), and it was all served w/ J. Lohr Cabernet.

I know all the unusual events made this dining experience a great one, but the food speaks for itself! It's definitely one of my fav restaurants of Nashville.

Delish!!! I was a late addition to a dinner party where my friends ordered family-style, so I have no idea what all I ate, but I can tell you that it was superb. The ceviche was just right; the sweet potatoes were perfect, the wine was up to par (and that says a lot coming from a girl who hails from Sonoma). My only complaints were that the steak and grilled shrimp were overcooked. 
There was a party going on up on the roof with a live jazz band, which really made the evening perfect. We sat outside on the ground level, apparently they don't normally have tables out there. Or the band. So maybe it was just my lucky night, but I loved the ambiance and the class of this establishment.
o-UAGRwBITO0e4yOB7nYhw: I went there for lunch today with two friends and I tried the Viiza salad:  "Dried cranberry, dried blueberry, dried figs, dried peach, green apple mixed greens" with honey mustard dressing.  The mixed greens were a good variety and fresh and the salad tasted wonderful.  The salad was more than reasonably priced (as are the cones).  I also tried the Sparkling Green Mango/ Lychee to drink and I can't wait to try the others on their menu.  The staff were all welcoming, friendly and engaging while we ordered and waited for our meals.  One of my friends order the Lobster Cone with the spicy garlic sauce and raved about how good it was also. This will definitely be a place that I will visit regularly.

I like the pizzas here because I can choose what I want. And I like the drinks also, especially the special cups. The staffs here are friendly and the services are good. Recommend it to people who want to try something new.
What an amazing find. A bit off Broadway but worth the walk. Great concept for pizza on the go.
CcaYQDslcBH_PA0wbwZtKQ: This location is wonderful. The food is, always Fresh the, staff are always Professional. I only use the drive thru unknown about the inside

I don't normally write reviews on restaurants because I don't feel the need.. but this specific location deserves some recognition or atleast the two African ladies who work 3rd shift on the weekends...
Normally (even if they have 24 hour drive thru hours) the workers are really bland and unfriendly which is understandable when your working at midnight but these ladies are pretty amazing... you pull up and she says "welcome to McDonald's blah blah " then it's " thank you for choosing McDonald's " other locations say "what is it... pull ahead" 
And ALSO they don't shut down their ice cream machines at 9 o'clock like every restaurant is known for 
I'm not giving them 5 stars because the lines at midnight can get pretty heavy on the wait but that's only because it's these two ladies working and they have to make everything... 
my last visit is what made me want to write this review because I ordered a 10 piece nugget and they were out so she had to make some more .. I pull up to the window and she's already joking with me and she politely asks me to pull up to wait , which is fine I know how it is... mind you it's pouring raining .. so she comes out side and brings me a food and still manages to apologize for my wait (it was midnight so I expected a wait) I get home and they gave me a free strawberry pie which is my favorite !
This location of McDonald's might not be the best but the two ladies who work 3rd shift on the weekends have me in awe .. I really hope someone sees this who matters.
Qomfa_ZfUnsGt4Stch6rVg:
So, I gave the pizza joint I had heard so many good things about a second chance - this time, in the actual restaurant.  I can confidently say that almost all of the problems I had with it the first time around were a result of having it out on delivery for too long.  I was very impressed.

Service was the most polite that I could have asked for, yet still casual and friendly.  Every employee wanted to make sure that we were doing ok, whether it was our server or not.  Anything we wanted came out promptly, hot, and fresh.

I tried a sausage with green peppers and onions, and I cannot express how good it was.  The sausage is shaved, not crumbled, which adds just a little bit of extra flare to it.  So good.  I'm still not a huge fan of the crust, but that's mostly just a personal preference.  I was told the thin crust might be more to my liking, so I'll have to try that next time.

Thumbs up, Pie in the Sky!

Our main reason for eating here was that it was right outside of our hotel (Courtyard Marriott) so it was quick and easy to access.

The time frame on waiting on these wasn't too bad considering that they were made to order. We were pleased with what we got. We tried the Mexicali and the White Pie. Super tasty!  Again, we waited a little longer for these than we might have waited for a meal at another place, but we did not mind.  The pizzas were well worth it.

They are a tad bit on the pricey side. We had an appetizer, 2 small pizzas, and 2 beers for about $60. For two people at a pizza place that's a little much. 

The service was spot on as well.  Would certainly recommend this to anyone in the area.
I decided after a really hard day at work that I wanted pizza and beer... This really hit the spot & the sporty atmosphere.
xdpMd5zgtnigfU02w6Fs_w: Just a scone and coffee for the first visit, but the scone is one of the best I've had. Ever. They even had Almond milk for my latte. Staff were accommodating, helping me get in and out quickly in my morning rush. I look forward to going back and trying more food. I'm only not giving all 5 starts because I will re-assess when I go back for a sit down meal.
Love the veggie sandwich.  Fillings are delicious and distinct, and combined with the crispy chewy texture of the homemade fry bread,  outstanding.  Came back for another try, yum!

What a refreshingly different brunch! Yes, you can get poached eggs, scrambled eggs, biscuits, home fries, and your other usual brunch suspects. But here you can the poached eggs on a bed of crispy mustard greens and potato latkes or braised pork or eggs scrambled with goat cheese. Even their selection of desserts and lattes are a tad more creative than the average brunch joint, e.g. bacon beignets, Elvis latte. As suggested in another review, Perl provides a great alternative to Café Marché.

PROS: A warm ambiance created by a collection of colorful mismatched upcycled wooden furniture, friendly waitstaff, and most importantly--delightfully creative, fresh, well-executed foodstuffs. If the poached eggs that we ordered are at all representative of all the other menu items, the chef knows what s/he's doing. Just reading the menu is in itself a thrilling part of the dining experience at Perl.

CONS (that have PROS potential): Breakfast portions were a tad smaller than what I expected (but maybe that's for the better). The bacon beignets with coffee sabayon were delicious, but I think they could've been even better served dry in a basket with some kind of maple-y sauce on the side instead. Not a huge fan of the kind of random assortment of art on the walls, but that's just me being an art douche.

I would definitely return to Perl...if not just for the sheer experience of reading their menu.
AAurpgemg4l0r9RDxaXdVg: Great homemade food. Excellent prices. Staff was awesome and it's in a great local Latino with a ton of other local shops and restaurants

Decided to come here for the first time and I'm not disappointed. The only thing that holds me back from a full 5 stars is that the staff isn't the friendliest but hey that's ok. I got a chicken salad sandwich and fries and drink for $8.67 which is ok considering the food was decent enough. Definitely will visit again.
What a gem! I had the chicken salad on a croissant and the fresh frozen fruit. The fruit was awesome! Such a nice surprise to randomly stop in.
LIVL3r0LDrKl2RwbQ6TY8w: Great place to grab a quick lunch. The rolls are unique and tasty. It is not a traditional Japanese restaurant, but more modern/fusion style. I love the self ordering screen and the rollers just roll! My fave is the commodore roll, the flavor blends well and the ingredients are so fresh. One of these days I will try their ramen but I can't stop ordering the rolls!

It's definitely not traditional sushi, bu delicious. People who cannot eat spicy food, be careful. I ordered a commodore roll stating that "please don't make it spicy", but it is still a little bit spicy.

I Love This Sushi Shop!!!! Get Your Sushi and Go or Have a Seat and enjoy.... Although I Tried sitting and eating but Ended up ordering 2 more rolls! The Sushi is SOOOOO good! I Recommend the Create Your Own Roll... Also I'm a new fan of the Soy wrap over the Seaweed wrap! Try it Out!
lMqs-c1Cwzm1Nym-dZNAQQ: So this is the grand opening what a fantastic space in the cool side of cool side of town. This place is going to be very successful located just a block from Cosair which is a new location that I did not know that was operational as well just consider looks to be very successful the cider is great and they also offer additional local beers by the tap if you'd like at some point you could get a tour today is their first day of opening.

Diskin Cider is HANDS DOWN AMAZING. The only thing that could possibly make it better is if it was located in St. Louis so I could go there all the time!
We came here this past Friday for a bachelorette party and they were more than accommodating! The entire staff was so much fun and they made certain we were having more than a good time! :) 
The food was excellent and I can easily say the deviled eggs are to die for. I absolutely loved the day dream cider but there was not a single one on the menu that we didn't like. 
The atmosphere is lots of fun, open and inviting. They also have tables outside as well as hammocks. The little games they had around were also a nice touch. 
I will definitely make certain to go to Diskin again on my next trip to Nashville.

Awesome place! Super friendly and helpful staff! Dog friendly patio and plenty of places to sit. All of the Cider options are tasty and they have several local beers on tap too. Welcome to Nashville Diskin!
t0FzBzAoqVpK5fNIGOPv-g: Heather was our bartender for my bachelorette party, she handled our group of ten like a pro! She was SO NICE. I really appreciate her making our group feel welcome. And also, your bar is AWESOME!

I can't speak highly enough about the service and the atmosphere. I loved the old structure mixed with the new sounds.  Food wise, my group split a lot of shareable items.  The fried pickles had the perfect about of "fry" and sauce.  The artichoke dip with the garlic pita had the perfect amount of seasoning. The meatball slider could have used a bit more spice and the fritters could have used a bit more shrimp, but we had just eaten a most amazing Tennessee Hot slider. I must say though, my favorite item was the side of grits. Maybe it's more nostalgic than anything, but I've never tried grits as good!
Great place all around! Enjoyed the food and the drinks did the job! One of our favorite spots so far this weekend! Caroline on the 2nd floor was awesome as well...great service!
oM-HxIaYiUKRCGF2iHraqA: Outstanding!!!!  My husband and I were staying in the area to catch a flight the next day.  I almost didn't want to dine here due to some of the reviews.   So heres the skinny,  my husband and I eat Mexican very often at several different places.  Let's start with the chips and salsa.  Fresh cooked Chips serve still warm and hands down the best salsa.  The Guacamole was exceptional no joke amazing!  The meals were generous portions for the very fair prices and were serve quickly and hot.  They were delicious.  The service was great.  Attentive, yet not bothersome.  I do not understand why so many poor reviews.
Went here last night for dinner.. Had a deluxe burrito and it was excellent..  Our waiter was great also..  Whenever I'm back in Nashville I will definitely be back..

Amazing food! Staff seemed very busy and didnt come around often. We were sat by the door, so it was chilly as people were coming and going. We ordered the burrito and the shrimp chimi. Honestly some of the best mexican food we have had...and we try alot of places. Chips were great..salsa was spicy. Rice and beans taste great. Freshly grated cheese..none of that pre-shredded stuff.
Tbb7lwvdxSBKz9d7-j333A: I go here about once or twice a month, very good eats,. The tabouli is always fresh everyday, the best in town!

Friendly service, good food, generous portions and great pricing--what more do you need?  Here on work from Atlanta, and second time I've eaten here--both times very good.  Try it if your looking for a good middle eastern experience.
Excellent affordable and authentic food. I ordered the gyro platter to go and it was a big platter very affordable and fast. I will definitely come here next time I come to Nashville
xg2v-Lf39AVEo4we0dnbtA:
Delicious food and very friend staff and owner.  They made us feel comfortable and went out of their way to ensure we had a great meal.  The portions are huge and flavorful.  There were 2 in our party, we had pot roast and meatloaf.

We will be back time and time again.

North Nashville, from Inglewood to Hendersonville, is mostly, with a couple of exceptions, a good food wasteland, so when a decent restaurant opens up in these here parts, I always get excited. When a wonderful restaurant opens up, I just about lose it.

And Richard's is wonderful. It's solid homemade meat and three with an unpretentious slightly gourmet twist. All the ingredients are top quality, and you can taste it. There's no bright orange Velveeta or cheap margerine-tasting mashed potatoes (as is so often the case with oft-touted by out of town critics meat and threes - I don't think they have really eaten there). 

Nope, at Richard's, you must try the pot roast - meaty and delicious with carrots and, so Richard tells me, radishes. The greens are perfectly spicy without a need for hot sauce, and the broccoli salad and coleslaw are creamy and yummy. My man swears by the fried chicken, and I can't argue. 

There's a different menu every day, but a few staples remain. Right now, he is just doing lunch, but all that changes in January when he expands to a dinner menu too. I know I'll be stopping by after work to grab dinner on busy nights. 

On Sundays, there's a made to order breakfast that's insanely good. Don't over-order because the portions are massive! 

The interior is a surprise (pleasant one) after the mundane industrial park strip exterior, with bright colors and Nashville memorabilia. I am planning to spread the word and drag my friends in one by one.

Come to Richards!  As the Jennifer with two n's said, the food is delicious and they are very welcoming.  I like to get  2 or 3 meat and two's and share the sides and meat choices among the family and guests.  Today I had the fried chicken - juicy, tender and flavorful so I don't miss the skin that I'm trying not to eat.  The jalapeno cornbread is a sweet-spicy treat.  I have to go back and get the pecan pie.  We need a meat and three like this in Madison. 

Oh, and my 6 year old daughter loves the food.  It's a relief to get a quick meal that's not fast food, and know I don't have to cook Sunday dinner.

Since my fingers are greasing up my keyboard, I'll stop, but I will be back to try breakfast next and give a review.  The atmosphere with its reds, whites and blacks invited me to sit down instead of taking to go, so I will.
aaOA5W4wRMLKrkIwWnGTPg:
Certainly the best quesadillas and burritos downtown. Ingredients are fresh and the location is so close to the center of downtown. East Nashville location closing was sad but glad they were able to maintain the downtown locale. Not sure how you could have a bad meal here. Yes it's part of a larger lunch area, but there's plenty of seating and free parking without having to use a "complimentary" valet like most places. 

Have to recommend the 90210! And now they even have an amazing Chipotle hummus. This place just keeps getting better and better.
Awesome food as always! Love this place! The original menu! The Ques-ideas and the new hummus is Delish!!! Great owners and staff as well

Wow!  What a find!  I stumbled on this place while driving around downtown.  In my opinion, they are in line to have the best burrito in town. They heat the burrito which helps the burrito stay together!  I need this because I don't always carry around a bib.  The customer service is awesome as well.  Check it out sometime!
u41uezgA8pp1wrNrpP1HvQ: Affordable lunch options and allows you to add-on either a crunchy shrimp roll or California roll for about $3.00! Great food and friendly service...happy customer!

This place is AWESOME! Just left there, and couldn't wait to write our review. Service was really great, friendly staff and very accommodating to my picky taste. Prompt service, delicious food and our chef was as sweet as he could be. My new favorite hibachi. Will definitely be back! Don't pass this one up.
Yum!! We ate hibachi style. The restaurant was quiet. The food was delicious. The chef was awesome! I'm not sure why more people don't know about this place. Check them out!!
kOdCszON4PL0JNFV-QIFPA: First timers at this O' Charlies.  Our waiter, Chaz, was very friendly and it was clear he cared.  We only wish he had talked about the happy hour when we ordered our drinks.  It seems we, as unknowing customers, have to actually ASK for happy hour prices in the restaurant.  Just comes off as not customer friendly and I felt over-charged.  On to the food....We ordered the Prime Rib for Two.  The Prime Rib was a very good hardy cut, nice and rare with little fat.  The sides, Mac & Cheese (creamy), Collards (smoky with ham bits), Baked Potato and Rolls were yummy. The Artichoke dip was OK.  Bathrooms (which to me, are a good indication of overall restaurant cleanliness) were fairly clean but the ladies room seemed really small and a bit outdated, with one faucet not working properly, pressure too much and splashed me with water!  All in all a good meal for the price and we'll be back with a 20% off coupon we received in the mail, to try other items.

Oh my goodness, so good! The rolls were so yummy my table ate two baskets! I ordered the Peach Chutney Chicken with sweet potatoes mash and broccoli. It was good to the last bite, meaning I ate the whole plate which rarely happens! I would definitely recommend that for a good filling meal. I of course had to try something from the dessert menu. I had the best pie I've ever tried - the Ooey Gooey Carmel Pie...to die for! Very decadent and rich! Would definitely recommend both of those!

We saw the 2.5 stars and almost missed a great meal.  We looked at the Thursday special on their website. Catfish is our weakness. We didn't even order seconds. We eat out a lot, and we know a good place to eat.
AefQvASk_wyVDDXIlVoWKQ: This is my favorite bakery in Nashville. All of their food is delicious and is gluten free. Not only is the food delicious but the people that work there are the nicest people you'll ever meet. They truly care about what they are making and you can tell they put a lot of passion in to their work. My moms birthday was a couple of weeks ago and she is a regular customer there. So I went in and asked what they thought she would like, they gave me suggestions and helped me so much! They said the cake would be ready by noon on Saturday but I got an email saying it was ready early. Definitely check this place out if you are interested in a delicious treat! You will leave with a happy belly and a smile on your face! I have posted a picture of the cake- cinnamon walnut with buttercream icing! (I added the colorful candles :))
Wasn't what I was expecting! It's pretty small and they don't have a ton of options, but they take custom orders, they are dedicated gluten free, and have yummy items!
Best gluten free and most wholesome delicious muffin on the planet. Antoinette is so friendly too!!! Definitely worth a visit!
vXPUbNZqSIWevt2oslzbHA:
Zackie's Hot Dogs is a new hot dog shop in the heart of  Germantown.  The hot dogs cost a little more here than getting them from a street vendor, but Zackie's does have a place where you can sit down inside while you eat, so that is a plus. The shop is new and sparkling clean they have their '99' health inspection rating hanging on the clean walls.

 You can get a side of fries with your regular hot dog, or polish sausage. You can order any and every side you can think of including sauerkraut, jalapenos, and good old ketchup and mustard, all of it at no extra charge. I only took one star off because I like to have my hot dogs a lot spicier, but the polish sausage was pretty tasty, and you can order it on a whole wheat bun so it's a little on the healthier side. 
Zackie's also has Turkey dogs, and veggies dogs on the menu.

I'm so bummed they've closed. It was Mike's great dogs and awesome hospitality that helped build my restaurant SILO. When we needed a welder for our grease duct-Mike, When we needed a quick bite and a good story Mike and Co were there.  When it was 109*F and we had no A/C yet, Zackie's was our source for cold lemonade.  

You will be missed. Thanks for 5 great years!
-Clay

I'm not a big fan of chain restaurants, but for a pescatarian (who prefers to eat mostly vegetarian) looking for fast healthy lunch options, Freshii is a godsend. 

I haven't tried any of their menu selections because the make-your-own menu is just too much for me to resist! You can choose a salad, wrap, soup or noodle/rice bowl and customize it to your hearts desire. It's like a salad bar on crack. 

For vegetarians and vegans, you can mix and match plenty of protein options - pay extra for some tasty tofu, or get black beans and chickpeas (they're even in the 'free' add-on category). 

Since I occasionally eat fish and had a gift certificate to blow, I tried the most expensive meat/protein add-on: Grilled salmon. I received a generous filet of fresh salmon on my salad, and by the time they were done loading it up with the multitude of free add-on veggies I had selected, my salad was so huge that an employee dubbed it the "bigger than your friend's salad" salad. Um, thanks?

The only downsides are that the ordering process is a little confusing (head down to the far end of the store to place your order), and $10-12 for a salad just isn't in my everyday budget. The salads, wraps and bowls on the menu are a little more reasonable at $7-10. 

You can also order online or map out your order online to see how many calories your meal contains.

Qc0Vyf84Rf57ZbIvclXdPg: This is truly authentic home cooking.  Three people in our party had the smoked neck bones, two had the pork chops and one the whiting fish. Talking about a plenty. Ate here today for the first time. This is an old hole in the wall type establishment. You know just like growing up and everybody standing in the kitchen waiting their turn to fix their plate. Act like you home here. I read a comment of somebody complaining about somebody attitude. Boy ain't nobody got time for that. They open 12 hours in a hot ass kitchen cooking soulful food with blessed hands. Can you cook like that? Please! You probably went expecting to be treated like you at a 4-star. No boo you ain't getting that here. You mama didn't do it... They ain't either. So next time you come check your "attitude" at the door. 

I'll be back around new years. Loved it!

I am a fan.

On my birthday I didn't want to go anywhere fancy, I wanted to go some place country ass. I wanted real soul food. B & C is family owned and has definitely been there since all those trendy joints opened up on mcgavok and riverside. The parking is a shambles, you're likely to get blocked in as people are coming and going, B & C is mostly a to-go joint. 

I think the reason Bailey and Cato has a bad rap on yelp is that they don't cater to the folks on yelp looking for that trendy mom & pop joint secret spot. They've been around so long they don't need to please trendoids looking for a speed record and the best fried chicken in the country. They make what they want, how they want, and it pleases their long time foundation of customers without needing more. For anyone looking for something cute and sparkling this is not the place to go. B & C is as rundown and greasy as you're going to get. They need to seriously scrub the windows and walls, and the place feels like it might fall over at any second. As a family restaurant they've got the way they like doing things down. I'd come with cash as they don't take $10 or less on cards, and I'd be prepared to wait in line as they aren't some corporate speed machine giant behind the counter. They won't take orders if you're on a cell phone and you're probably not going to get a smile out of anyone. You want their food you have to wait and fit into the way they do it.

We did. 

I ordered the pulled pork, green beans and mac & cheese. My boy ordered the catfish with yams and greens. They fry the cat fish in big batches on a rotating schedule every 15 minutes or so, so he had to wait for his, but it soon came and we dug in. Now I have to say everything was very good and felt like family made recipes. As in don't expect some ridiculous remix on soul food here. Like I said, there are original recipes gran makes but it's not your gran making them. The yams were stellar, I have no idea what they put in them but I don't think I've tried yams like that before. My pork was perfectly tender and juicy, and I think the sauce they gave me had a hint of either soy or teriyaki don't quote me on that. The catfish was crunchy and moist inside and great with hotsauce. The hush puppies were excellent, sort of like less sweet donuts. 

Now I'm giving Bailey and Cato only 4 stars because yes I have had soul food that I like more, based on preference...no one has Bludso's in Compton beat yet. But I appreciate B & C, although they could stand to smile at least. I hope they last a long time, and I hope they continue to do it their way.

As a southern California native, I can easily say this is the best beef pho in Nashville hands down, no competition. I'm glad I'm not the only person from soCal who thinks so too. If you compare their pho to the typical pho found on Bolsa (Little Saigon in soCal), it would rate 3/5 stars (huge compliment). In Nashville, it's 5/5 stars.

I discovered this place only a few months ago when I was doing surgeries at Southern Hills Hospital on Nolensville. When assessing beef pho, it's always important to LOOK at the broth first. It needs to have enough floating glistening patches on the surface to suggest that the broth has been cooking with beef bones and marrow for a long duration. It needs to be relatively transparent, just barely enough darkness to suggest the right amount of herbs such as star anise, but too much star anise would make the broth dark and bitter (cough Kien Giang cough). 

They also let you select which cuts of beef to include in your bowl. This is purely personal preference, but I generally get brisket and rare (cooked in the bowl).  Avoid the seafood pho. It's not originally a northern Vietnamese tradition to have anything other than beef in pho. Some poor areas in central Vietnam as well as neighboring southeastern Asian countries tend to use seafood instead of beef. Southern Vietnamese tradition tends to use chicken in pho (still haven't found a good chicken pho restaurant in Nashville yet).

Even though V & V Seafood markets itself as some sort of Cajun restaurant, the owners, their families, and their friends all know that the best dish they serve is their beef pho. What surprises me most is that the best pho place in Nashville is owned and run by a Laos family.

I always order their beef pho and it's their beef pho alone that I'm giving them 5/5 stars. In my book there's no reason to order anything else here (quite surprising for me since I'm usually very open at trying different items from the same location). 

Regular size bowl is $7.00 (average size, comparable to other pho restaurants). Large size bowl is $8.50 (HUGE, I mean HUGE bowl, larger than any bowl at every pho restaurant I've eaten in my life).

I need to try the pho here. I hear it's good from my Korean and Laotian friends. 

The problem is... I like the Cajun Boils so much that I only ever get that. I like to get the black tiger shrimp, mussels or crab legs. All are great with their boils. They have a choice of 1 thru 5 in spiciness. I usually go with 3 so I can enjoy the seafood without too much heat distracting me from the other tastes. They also offer white shrimp, blue crab and crawfish, but say any seafood can be boiled in the Cajun seasoning if you'd like. Also, all the shrimp are peeled and de-veined already. 

If you need to feed a small group, they offer a V&V seafood platter that gives you 1lb of white shrimp, 1lb of snow crab, 2lbs of mussels and 2lbs of crawfish for $39.99! Perfect for the Super Bowl coming up! 

They have a full Po Boy sandwich offering, along with other fried fish platters. They also have Pad Thai, fried rice, lo mein, lard na and crispy noodles. I'd like to try them all, but I can't seem to get past the boils!
best pho in town,not including miss saigon of course.all the right flavors,servers were horrible,ladies you need some practice on refillin drinks etc.other than that,food was great.
