2J_aeR1qu6s01mwisF0YrQ:
Plaza Mariachi, where have you been all my life? In just one visit, this has become one of my favorite spots in Nashville to visit and take out-of-town guests. The atmosphere was unlike anything I've experienced in Nashville.

For indecisive foodies like me, the culinary options at Plaza Mariachi can be overwhelming when you first visit. I opted for the first place I saw: Taquería Hidalgo (they had me at burrito). And I was not disappointed.

I was in the mood for chicken, so I ordered a pollo taco and a pollo burrito. The burrito was covered in (delicious) queso and was stuffed with black beans, lettuce and pico de gallo. The chicken in both the burrito and the taco was great. I also loved the corn tortilla used in the pollo taco.  I highly recommend trying some of the self-serve crema and hot sauces located where you pick up your order.

The price I paid for the meal was very reasonable and the amount of food was generous to say the least. Although I hope to eventually try all of the eateries at Plaza Mariachi, I'll definitely have to return to Taquería Hidalgo to try more of the menu items.

Taqueria Hidaglo, you had me at queso smothered burrito. I'm so tired of going to places that charge extra to put that liquid gold all over my food. And then they never put enough on. Not Taqueria Hidalgo. They spoke right to my food soul.

Taqueria Hidalgo is located in Plaza Mariachi. We came here on the 4th of July so it was pretty busy, but not annoyingly busy. There are several options in the food court but as soon as I saw their burrito description, I was hooked. There was a short line but it went pretty quickly. We ordered 2 barbacoa burritos, paid, and then gave them our number so they could text us when the food was ready. We grabbed a seat and enjoyed the entertainment (a live mariachi band). We got a text about 10 minutes later and our burritos were hot and ready. Not a long wait considering it was a holiday. These burritos are a thing of beauty. Queso is poured over top and not in a skimpy way. They also come with some salsas on the side to add a little kick. The barbacoa was juicy and flavorful. The veggies were fresh. It was all the perfect combination of goodness. The burrito was big but I was able to almost finish the whole thing. My boyfriend had to finish the last few bites while I finished licking up the rest of the queso. I was full. I was really full. For the price point and flavor, this place is hard to beat. It would be the perfect place to grab a quick lunch. Or dinner. Or whatever. Don't be fooled by the fact that it's in a "food court". It's not your typical noisy dirty mall food court. It's beautiful, clean, and delicious! I can't wait to go back and try more things on the menu...with queso.

Everything was amazing! 
The Carne Asada taco was my favorite thing followed closely by the Tostadas, but literally everything we tried was delicious. 

Located inside Plaza Mariachi - staff was semi friendly- they delivered everyone else food, but not ours which was kinda weird.
RMUWSMGs31CgF41QY6KiVw:
I saw Nooley's at the Farmer's Market and, since I am always open to trying Cajun food when I find it, decided to check it out.

I got the special, which was a bowl of red beans and rice and another bowl of shrimp gumbo, along with a drink, for $8.49 plus tax.

I wasn't sure how big of a portion I would get-I thought it would be kind of small, but it was two regular bowl-sized portions.  The red beans/rice was spiced just right, with a kick that was medium.  I could feel it, but I've had hotter.  For anyone that is not used to spicy food, this will be hot enough for you.

The shrimp gumbo also had rice in it, and it was delicious.  The hearty roux gave the dish a full, rich taste and it contained the requisite okra.

I will have to come back to try a muffaleta.  I saw one in the window, but my stomach was already set on the gumbo.

Dude. Visiting from the bay area (San Francisco/Daly City) and had a hard time deciding where to eat in the farmers market. Came upon this spot tucked away on the side. Trying to decide between the Fried Bologna Po Boy or the catfish...decided to try both! DEFINITELY NOT DISAPPOINTED! I thought the Fried bologna was going to be like Oscar Mayer skinny bologna but it was actually the thicker type. Reminded me of spam but with bologna flavor! Delicious! The brad was toasted just right....even the edges of the white bread were toasted! The catfish had a good fry on it. Man. My mouth is watering but...I just ate it! Haha also got the chicken and sausage gumbo. Warning...It's still spicy even though it's the least spicy! All in all...loved it

I can't speak to the authenticity of NOLA food but I can speak to the tasty hot roast beef po'boy. I grew up in Philly and love hot roast beef sandwiches. They were a staple at our house growing up as well as every first communion and graduation party. Having moved to Nashville, great sandwiches were something I missed. I miss Amoroso rolls. Even though the Po'Boy roll was no hoagie roll, it was delicious and helped to satisfy my sandwich craving. Don't know how authentic it was but it was delicious.
QKB21Alitgj-m6oEoFZFBw:
DUDE.

this place is truly next level. perhaps it's the honeymoon period... perhaps it's the plate of beans and pickled cabbage salad... perhaps this place really is that fucking good? honestly, it doesn't matter. this place (for now) has wrestled away the distinction of "best mexican joint" from los arcos and doesn't look to give it back anytime soon.

i just returned home from my second visit to el mirador and as i reflect with a bulging stomach (and elevated insulin levels), i keep thinking back to the little plate of beans + pickled cabbage that's presented with the chips and salsa before you place your order. i'm telling you... once you get a mouf full of this stuff, you'll know what you're in for. the chips are clearly homemade and perfect for the salsa and other goodies. i had some sort of enchiladas (forgot the proper name of them) that had an orange colored sauce, topped with cheese and flanked by beans and rice. so. damn. tasty. for real. i had enchiladas suizas the first time i went and they were good, but... you know... these killed them.

check the place out, don't let the part of town scare/intimidate you, because i know it will for the less adventurous types. some of the best food on nolensville awaits you. in a word:

superlative.

eric b

Let me describe to you the best part of this restaurant..A little old lady in the back cooking the food.

I had the chicken mole which was rich and delicate...it rivals some of the best I've had...looking forward to trying out the rest of the menu...


El Mirador just opened recently and is located next to K & S World Market on Nolensville...

El Mirador is definitely a cut above the other Mexican places I've tried in Nashville (La Hacienda Taqueria, Las Palmas, Los Arcos, La Paz, El Torero -- which is actually out of Atlanta -- and a few others I can't recall at the moment).  

In general, everything tasted very fresh in comparison.  The shredded chicken was not dry, the lettuce was not wilted/browning, the tomatoes tasted fresh, and so forth.  As others have said, the beans and cabbage salad to start are a good touch.

Liked:
 * Freshly made corn tortillas.  
 * The salsa and corn chips
 * The pollo a la diabla (had a nice kick).

Nothing I flat out disliked, but caveats:
 * The mole didn't speak to me.  It was too subtle and one dimensional.  Maybe my hopes were too high given that everything else was quite nice.  
 * They don't (yet?) have a liquor license, so no margaritas.  Not a problem for me, but others were disappointed.  The visit was mid Nov. 2009, so maybe this has changed already.

I can see this becoming my go-to Mexican place (although the people I come with might want to go to a place they can get margaritas).
NIhKpS_hNot964ct4bKx1Q:
We just had Riddim n Spice for the first time today!  So, currently, the truck is parked at the site which will be their brick and mortar store will be (East Nashville, right next to Bongo Java).  

Oh.  My.  Goodness.  

Those of you who grew up 7th Day Adventist will understand this.  Those of you who didn't will not.  It's like you died and went to haystack heaven.  For those of you who don't know what a haystack is, it's a bowl of rice, cheese, beans, and everything you could think of to put on top of said rice, cheese, and beans.  And it's usually eaten after church at potluck.  Though I didn't stay at the church, haystacks and the kindness of the Adventists will always be in my heart.

So, you choose your chicken.  I got the BBQ jerk, which was barely spicy (as I wanted) with a hint of sweet BBQ.  I couldn't choose which rice to get, so the owner (his name escapes me, but he was a doll--so friendly) gave me both rice flavors--yellow indian rice and rice with peas.  They were both so good that I'll ask for both next time.  Then you choose your toppings.  He suggested I get all of the toppings.  YES.  I got all of them.  GET ALL OF THE TOPPINGS--grilled onion, black beans, cheese, red onion, tomato, the list goes on.  

It's been an entire 4 hours since we've eaten and I'm already thinking about going back.

I caught the Riddim n Spice truck right outside the Porter Flea Christmas Market last weekend. I got an Island Bowl with smoked jerk chicken and all the fixings (tomatoes, corn salsa, queso fresco, assorted sauces). While the price was a bit steep, I did get a ton of food, enough for two servings that day. However, I was disappointed at the lack of spice. Even though I specified to make the bowl "extra spicy," the seasoning left me wondering where the spice went. My friend ended up grabbing me extra hot sauce from another truck in the vicinity. Other than that, the chicken was juicy and the Portuguese rice quite fluffy. All the bowl needed was a healthy kick of seasoning.

Service was wonderfully speedy, and the staff at the counter always responded politely. I especially was impressed at how the service person at the front stopped to refill utensils and give new toppings to these two incredibly picky women who ordered after me. Compared to the other trucks, this one in particular served so quickly, people only waited in a short line.

Try this food truck for hearty food, but be sure to emphasize the spiciness you want for full satisfaction.
Food is amazing. I'm originally from south Florida. This restaurant is authentic as it comes. Ambience is super laid back. Perfect for lunch, dinner, or a date. Favorite restaurant in the city
pZl5U8VR7bmXG5VzdrYHgQ:
We liked the ambiance, the food was good. It is described as french and as french we liked the items on the menu. I had the frog legs (trying to live up to our reputation...) and they were very good. The grilled salmon and the steak frites with pepper sauce was very good also. The creme brulee with nutella was delicicous too...
It is resonnably priced which is definitely a plus!!

My French husband is a tough critic when it comes to French cuisine, but this place was fantastic! We got 2 appetizers, entrees and a bottle of wine for $100. The lamb was one of the best I'd ever had. A true hidden gem!

I've gone here for lunch three times. The pasta are great - full of flavor and interesting ingredients. You may also get lucky and find a good special lunch (lamb chops :)). The lunch prices are half the cost of the dinner prices, though, so I'll be sticking to lunch here. I would also like more variety in the deserts - something citrus or slightly lighter would be nice.
eyJMXJ5HPLjxC86MfNRWwA: Hot and delicious! Gas station isn't generally appealing, but this little gem shines brightly inside the Lebanon Rd Shell station. Went for lunch last week and Katie hooked me up with one of the best burgers I've had in ages. I will definitely be returning,  as often as possible!
Scratch biscuits and polish sausage- oh heck yes! Went back for sausage, egg and cheese biscuit and it was awesome. Fresh cooked eggs make a huge difference too! Thank you!

This has been rebranded as the "Sunrise Cafe". I had a ham biscuit this morning and it was pretty good. Big chunks of city ham on a soft biscuit, I wanted salty country ham- but my heart thanks you I'm sure. 90% sure that the biscuit was from a can, but it was still pretty awesome. Fresh and fairly warm, I would have preferred it hotter, but still tasty. 5 stars if it were hotter and scratch biscuits. 4 stars because I will be back, great location, and perfect little breakfast at a great price point. $1.75
rHaM3niWhOvUGBDmClWHVA: If you like hot dogs you'll love this place. $8.00 for two dogs, chips, and a drink. You can mix and match your dogs. Tuesday they feature a special dog. They had a taco dog, also buffalo chicken dog. Loved both. They have a large selection which keeps it interesting.  I like the Rueben dog. There is something for everyone.
Great chili dog. Lots of options on the menu at decent prices. We liked the chili. Quick service and easy to eat on the go.

Some of the best hot dogs I have ever had.  I mean the dog itself.  Great beef flavor, not overwhelming, blends great with the toppings.  I have had almost half the menu and I have yet to have anything other than fantastic dogs.  The price is also a great selling point.  I just wish they (as well as others in the arcade) were open for dinner!
ULd_1GLuli_b_xMaHkGA3g: Delicious food and super friendly staff! Pork was very tender and juicy and the sauce was mild and added just the right amount of flavor. The bun was fresh and soft. Prices are very reasonable with pork sandwiches $1 off on Wednesdays. I'll be back next time I'm in Nashville.

When visiting Tennessee, one must try hot chicken and bbq. We started with bbq. The Whitt's location which we visited was a walk-up or drive thru only. This was a little disconcerting because it was raining and our local who suggested it, left out that important fact. However, the food was delicious. Great portions and extra tasty sides. The only unusual item for me was their bread. I describe it as a mix between corn bread and pound cake. Again, delicious, just unusual. We visited 2 other recommended stops which I'll review as well. Whitt's gets a thumbs up from me.

They had a great turkey plate with macaroni and cheese and potatoes salad. They even were generous with the pickles; they don't even know how much of a plus that was. It was so good, and I was so hungry, that I forgot to snap a picture. The menu is not vast, and I don't do pork, but what they do; they do well!
RACl0Np7ZqK3FDmh7dbX2g: Not bad burrito...the smoked chicken was the most flavorful. If you wanted something similar to Chipotle and you close this could do...they toast their's

A great stop for lunch. Quick, line style, with the owners assembling everything for you. Conveniently located near Arcade Aly with parking available across the street. A short walk from Broadway and has an outside credenza for sunny days. Highly recommend!
They guy taking the order was really funny and cracked jokes with us. The chicken on the taco salad was tender and juicy. The queso on top was delicious as well. Definitely recommend
AsGK6pT8QyNTUiHxZR6sgQ:
I am a repeat customer. I had the stuffed potato and my friend has a pork butt sandwich.  Everything was fresh and delicious.  The restaurant was very clean and the staff was great! 
I have previously had the smoked sausage,  ribs, smoked bologna, and the smoked brisket. 
I have never been disappointed and always left with a full belly.
They have closed and gone out of business. Sylvan Park restaurant is moving in and opening soon. Sad another restaurant closes.

We ate here for lunch and really liked it! No, it's not the best BBQ in the world but the food was really good, especially their side items! We ordered  1lb. of pork butt, baked beans and mac 'n cheese and it was delicious. The hot sauce was pretty good but the mild was kinda meh. And plenty of food for leftovers. They also deliver for lunch through GrubHub. The delivery minimum is kinda high ($25) but I understand it costs more to deliver than go to the restaurant. We'll definitely go again!
vqd34rzizu7ZT_R0JgyyRA: Can't really complain it's a buffet! Good cheap food got to love it. Staff is good also.

Wasn't really looking forward to eating here and then we met, Clifton!!! Clifton made it ALL WORTH IT, his welcoming smile,and spirit made for a wonderful dining experience...His coffee was A1!!! Stop by and check him out!!!!
I had the buffet and thought it was awesome. The cheesy potatoes were fantastic. I'd stay away from the french toast sticks but then again they come with the buffet.
vihxHVuaNtbrQ_MxjfqIvw: I never eat at Jack In the Box, so this review only pertains to the churros that I recently tried. That being said, I would absolutely recommend that if you are craving something sweet and indulgent you share, and I emphasize share, an order of their churros. Not only are they dusted with cinnamon and sugar, but they also have an over the top cinnamon and sugar filling that is unreal. They come in an order of five or ten and seem to be prepared fresh to order as they were piping hot. I was pleasantly surprised that these were way better than I expected based on the disappointing experiences I have had at Jack In the Box in the past. I will probably still not consider Jack In the Box for a meal option, but for a rare treat these would bring me back.

Two words: Ultimate. Cheeseburger. Soooo freaking good.

How can you not love a place where you can get a cheeseburger, chicken teriyaki, a taco, and all-day breakfast? Maybe not at the same time, but just the variety is amazing. And everything I've ever tried is great. Plus they have seasoned curly fries, which are the gods of french fries and frankly every fast food place should have these.

This was my first time at a Jack In The Box and I went there three times during my stay in Nashville since it was next door to the Lowes hotel.

Loved the spicy chicken breakfast biscut.  Staff was wonderful and the place was very clean.  My onlycomplaint was that when we went back late night they were out of the mint Oreo shakes.
uT3Eb1PqAqFF1OecPouqwQ:
OMG! So I had a taste for some nachos and since I work downtown I decided to go to La Playa's for lunch. I've been there for their burritos before (which are quite awesome) but their chicken nachos are GREAT! 
Seasoned just right, served hot with crispy warm chips...I wish I had another serving right now & I just ate them! 
So go to the Arcade in downtown Nashville & eat up!

Such a cute little restaurant in the arcade downtown! You can sit down for a delicious lunch or take your food to go. The first time I went, we sat and I love the atmosphere! The second story has 10-15 tables & booths. It wasn't crowded at all, and the waiter was attentive and quick! I ordered chicken nachos, and they were so so good! It's very good food for a great deal!

The last time I reviewed this place the menu wasa woefully lacking a lot for vegetarian options.  Walking by it today at lunch I noticed signs on the window saying there were new menu options.  I went inside, checked out their "new menu" and sure enough they added Vege-Fajitas, Veg-Fajita Quesadillas, Vege-Grilled Salad and Vegi-Nachos supremos.  I tried the vege-fajita quesadilla and was pleasantly surprised.  I would rate it about a 7.5 out of 10.  Not fantastic but ok for a quick lunch meal.  The rest of the menu looks like it has been expanded.  

Bottom line, if you haven't been thrilled with their limited menu options before, you may want to give them a second try.
9dTnpsb_tO25h44IAoeRjQ: I have been a regular breakfast customer at this McDonalds for years. So much so that I know the names of the employees that have been there for many years. I am not the easiest customer to satisfy especially when it comes to my eggs. But they have always cooked them the way that I request. They are a very friendly hardworking staff. Admittedly, they have staffing issues, but so do most fast food restaurants. But when they have a team that "stick and stay" and works together, they do a great job. If they run out of napkins, etc. its not the employees fault, nor is it their responsibility to go to Walmart to purchase those items. That responsibility lies on the franchise owner. The employees are doing the best that they can. And to me, they do a fantastic job!!

Their sweet tea makes me horny. The sweet sensational herbal substance that you put in this tea makes my little legs shake and my bladder orgasm. Thank you for having an amazing tea McDonald's ( only this location has the best tea, any other McDonald's sucks )

It is McDonald's food... So for quality I give 1/5 stars. If you want healthy gourmet food go somewhere else. 

But comparing this McDonalds to others, this is a great location. Comfy seats. Free wifi. Very nice staff. And very clean. 

I come here to do some work in the morning. It can get a bit loud, but the coffee is fresh and the air is nice in the summer.
2mdQ6nhqreMnkFkSJuY6NA:
If I give 4 stars it's earned! 

I walked in to order a veggie salad but noticed an item being prepared for the customer ahead of me and I said, "Oh my gosh, what is that?!" She explained and told me that it was the veggie flatizza... and added that it was delicious and her favorite!! I watched the employee prepare it and I couldn't resist!  Of course I did what any foodie would do. You guessed it. Uh, yeah... I eagerly ordered! 

The customer insisted I let the female employee that was working prepare it because she insisted, "No one makes them like her." I don't question fate when it comes to food so hey, excuse me ma'am... yes you. One of those please and prepared by you... and thank you! lol!
She laughed and so did the male employee. 

I waited in anticipation and when I got in my car, I could wait no longer. I devoured it like a vulture!  The jalapeño peppers added the perfect blend of spice and yummyness.  The blended of flavor smothered in melted mozzarella was mouth watering. My taste buds and my heart was so happy.  Everything that could go right in my life, in that moment, did.

I am still happy. Not only were the staff extremely nice and friendly, but the great eating experience matched!

Now, I'll say this- there was a note on the register with contact info stating we should call if we didn't receive a receipt. Guess who didn't receive a receipt (employee said the machine was broken)... 

At any rate, I didn't call. Hopefully things balance out when I check my bank account. If not, who knows... this meal experience was so amazing, I might donate 100% of the cost to Subway.

Eat fresh people! 

I got 99 problems, finding good food ain't one. ;)

Subways are always very hit or miss, but I have always had a great experience at this location! I will drive out of my way to stop at this one instead of the others in the area. Always clean, as well!

When it comes to subs (and Nippers Corner eateries), I've never gotten into the Jersey Mike's fad and ordering at Schlotszky's can be a nightmare. Enter Subway, my... "hero." (Get it? Okay, I digress.)

Of all the Subways I've been to in South Nash, this one is by FAR the cleanest and has by FAR the friendliest staff. Yes, the old sandwich artist is slowish... and the family who operates this location are not US born. But, this is a crappy economy. Don't we all need jobs?

As for lack of ingredients on-hand... what can I say? I'm a creature of habit. I like what I like and without fail, I always order a Veggie Delight on Wheat with Provolone: lettuce, tomatoes, cucumbers, green peppers, black olives, oil & vinegar with a dash of oregano. They're never out of any of those. /shrug

They always oblige when I ask for extra black olives and if I catch the owner at checkout, he almost always gives me free cookies. Three stars for a good ol' neighborhood sub shop and an extra one for the haters. :)
g6v8_P70AB2MULIY8zCQFw:
I'm still kinda confused about The Breeze. I remember when a Kalamatas was located here, then it was gone. Next was Greek Islands, which I'm told is the same thing as The Breeze. It hurts my head, I try not to think about it. 

I do know I like the food. I've had several things and was generally satisfied each time. The first thing (and my favorite) was the day's special, which consisted of Shawarma, rice and small salad.  It also came with a few pitas and this great dipping sauce, though I forget what it is. Next I just got the greek salad and was pretty disappointed. The lettuce was shredded and there wasn't much substance to it. Next I just came for a quick snack and got their hummus that was to die for! I think I got their baklava once too and didn't care for it, but I can't remember. It's a great little place to go for lunch off the Row though.

Things must have changed at The Breeze since Robert and Kyla reviewed the place. Greek is my absolute FAVORITE cuisine, slightly edging out Thai food. By the end of 2008, I had dined at every Greek restaurant in Nashville, except one.

The Breeze was the only remaining restaurant on my conquest, and my parents were in town (both of whom are chefs). It was a Sunday afternoon and there were so many establishments in town that had proved themselves. But the folks wanted Greek, and The Breeze is just down the street from my church. So I risked everything and took the parents to a complete unknown. Wow! We were not disappointed.

Athens Family Restaurant on Franklin Road was the reigning champion in Nashville before I walked into The Breeze. I think Athens is probably still the champion, but there were certain dishes at The Breeze that were better. We ordered 6 entrees between the three of us and tried just about everything. Athens is a 9.3 out of 10 in my family's opinion, and we think The Breeze is probably a 9.2. Extremely close!

Authentic Greek cooking! Not the champion in music city, but as close as it gets!!! But OHHHHH that lamb that Chef Adel Elostta fixes at Athens!

I tried this restaurant last night (though the door actually says Greek Islands, not the Breeze).  We showed up around 8:30, at the same time as another party.  Apparently they were supposed to close at that time (I just can't understand how restaurants in Nashville close down so early!), but our waitress sat us anyway.  I really have to credit her (I forgot her name, but she was Greek and very friendly) for giving us wonderful service and being very gracious even though we showed up at closing time . . .I have had several experiences with other restaurants around here either refusing to serve us (even 15-20 minutes before closing time) or letting us know in no uncertain terms how displeased they were at our late presence.  

The food itself was really good.  We had the Mezza for 2 platter, where we got to choose 5 of their appetizers (we chose hummus, falafel, spinach pie, grape leaves, and some sort of greek salad made with white beans).  We also ordered a Greek salad, and that was plenty of food for the 2 of us.  Everything was tasty, the grape leaves were really fragrant with the addition (I think) of cinnamon.  The tzaziki-type dressing with the Greek salad had a nice sour acidity to it, more flavorful than most.  Even the pita bread was better than most, warm and toasty.  We finished off with the Baklava, which our server gave us for no charge because it was the last piece.  It was not remarkable, but then again, I love all Baklava!  We will definitely be back, both for the food and the service
B9yv414n2oi4-M0A450aEQ: My friends and I came to a Business Boutique Conference and there were a number of food trucks to choose from. We decided we felt like tacos, so we hopped in line for Yayo's O.M.G. The line was moving surprisingly fast compared to the rest of the trucks, so we got to the front of the line fairly quickly. I orders the MM Quesadilla con Chorizo porque me enchanta chorizo mucho. My friend ordered the tacos. Once we get our food we sit down in the grass and dig in. I love everything spicy so I think the hot sauce could have had more kick to it. The chorizo was delicious and went very well with the cheese. It was very heavy and oily (to be expected) so I didn't finish it all but it delicious and so were my friends tacos so she claims. It was a good feed!

We ate at the food truck at Centennial Park & who doesn't crave tacos at a festival?? I got the mahi-mahi taco & shrimp taco. Both very good. They had the right simplicity, but I wish they were bigger, or that I had ordered more (and yes, I know that street tacos are small, I just really liked them). Bother the fish & shrimp were lightly fried and had cilantro, slaw and a little sauce on them. The mahi-mahi also has a sprinkle of cheese. My hubs & friends also got the legend taco which has chorizo and skirt steak - it was my least favorite, but I'm also not big on chorizo so I feel like my opinion is biased. I would definitely eat at this truck again.

Wow! What a experience.  We ate here when they were at Elmington Park in Nashville.  I got the shrimp tacos and my wife got the original and a mahi mahi taco.  The flavor and portion size was off the hook!  Neat concept to have a food van that serves great food. They take credit cards and the whole meal was $23 with tip.
hJbp9tcJlH2_tXrHZfQp3A: The Donk's staff could not possibly have been more welcoming and accommodating for my birthday celebration. I brought in about 20 gay guys who had never been there before (I hadn't either), none of whom give a damn about a woman's...donk. Everyone was fun and lovely, the service was great, I've gotten compliments for planning something outside of our usual bar comfort zone for months since, and that's because everyone at the establishment was so cool.

Glad Donks has reopened.It was always my spot when I come thru Nashville.Those Wings are always great .I got the whiting fish basket and the Mississippi wings.Both were great .Ill be there next time I'm in town.

The service was great also.Everyone is super friendly
Had the best time for brunch at this establishment! Service was great, staff was extremely friendly, and the prices were good too! Can't beat $2 mimosas and $3 Sangrias. Catfish is bomb!
j6muHG0zR4G6z1KBt92KtQ:
Most of my friends can't believe I love The Muse because it's a dump. But I do love it -- and I don't care who knows it!

As mentioned elsewhere, dark & dingy is the theme du jour here...It looks like crap on the outside, looks like crap when you get to the bar and guess what? It looks like crap when you get into the main room, too. This isn't the Ryman. It's not even the Exit/In. But when the lights go down and I am swigging on a $3 beer, I quickly realize it doesn't matter.

I have only attended metal shows at the Muse, so I can only speak for these crowds. I am getting old enough (37) that I can't get into a mosh pit anymore -- I might break my hip or spill my beer. So I stand back and watch everyone let their freak flags fly! And it's GLORIOUS!!! Just pure fun and contentment on everyone's face as they do whatever comes naturally. Every time I go I am taken back to my days of sneaking into the Exit/In and just having a blast. It's truly fun to watch...

While small and basic (three walls, a tiny stage, and a floor) the main room is a great spot to watch bands play. And man, do a lot of bands play...I went a week or so ago and saw 5 bands for $9. The house gets half the money and the bands split whatever's left. Ended up being about $15 per musician this night as best I could guess. I was floored at the effort these guys were putting into their music -- ten times the effort of lazy established acts while getting a mere fraction of the money. These bands aren't exactly blazing up the charts, but they sure as hell give it 150%.  

Most nights, parking is easy in their paved lot. But when the shows sell out, it's a little tricky. Go on their website and click on the "parking" link so you can see the handy color-coded map where they literally show you the tow away zones. The signs around the building are not always clear and apparently one of the businesses over there loves to tow people who park in their lot after hours.

Screw these people, The Muse is THE best venue in Nashville!

The Muse is a dirty, dingy, little venue; but what's not to love about that? It's full of character. The Muse isn't all about heavy metal and punk bands, I've been to several electronic dance music events here, too! They also have rap and hip hop events weekly here. Their range in music is as diverse as it gets!

If you're new to throwing your own parties/concerts (like me), The Muse is a great place to start out. Their rates for shows are very reasonable! 

The price at the door ranges from show to show, anywhere from $5 to $20. Order a slice of pizza, too! Their pizza is made upstairs at the 21+ full service bar, Undertones!

Parking is decent, just get there early enough to park around the building. After 7pm or so, you can park in the paint store's parking lot. Don't park in the porn store, you will get towed!

Honestly... The Muse is my home away from home. I love this place, and after you attend a show here, you'll quickly find out why it's the only place in Nashville I'll be throwing parties!

My feeling is that it's been a long time since The Muse has been reviewed here on Yelp...the dates seem indicative of that as well.  Just wrapped up a tour with my band, and the Muse was the first out-of-town spot of the week, and I have nothing but good things to say.

Some complaints from other reviewers are things that have aparently been taken care of (ie beer for sale, free water for playing bands, air conditioning, etc).  The on-stage mix was one of the best we've ever dealt with, and the staff was beyond gracious to us--an out of town band.  Unfortunately, there wasn't a big turn out, but a quick walk around Nashville and it's obvious why: you'd have a harder time finding any building with a door NOT playing music than you would have finding a band you love.  That is to say the competition is stiff.

The Muse is located a bit off the beaten path, but they make up for that by being located right next to a porn store (seriously), and with easy access to the highway which is particularly helpful when having to drive upwards of 13 hours starting after the show. 

The only complaint I have is that the bands are generally forced to have their merchandise for sale outside of the playing room, and no one wants to be inconvenienced if they want to buy something...but I blame the general music "fan" more than I do the venue for that little jab.

A great place to play a show, and I would imagine, a great place to see one too.
25tI2s_AmToGGE2fNQYY7w: Tried the new pumpkin spice latte this morning and I am loving it lol! It was a lot better than I expected and has a different taste than Starbucks. I may even like it better. Yummy yummy!

Went through the drive thru with a car full of kids. The woman taking my order was incredibly kind and let us order something from breakfast and lunch since we were there 5 min before they changed over. The establishment is beautiful. The staff appeared well dressed, clean and quite happy to be there. Our food was done quickly and arrived in our vehicle hot. Everyone was very nice and prompt, we would happily go to this location again.

pIvX_Kw4f82baMUhRO6WxQ:
This is our FAVORITE Nashville spot.

I definitely recommend visiting and sitting for a while. We visited on a Friday night & they had a live band. I think it was the best sounding band in downtown that night. I wish I could remember their name.. but, they kept everything going and were very upbeat with tons of variety. Because of them, we stayed longer than we wanted but in a good way - we didn't want to leave.

The stage set up is awesome. I loved how it's built into one of the walls and it's at the beginning of the building when you walk in. The entertainers can use stairs on both sides to go from the upstairs to downstairs while performing. This set up keeps everyone's eyes on them. The entire place is designed so that you can see the stage NO matter where you sit or stand.

We didn't have any food on our visit. But we hung out on the second floor. Bottles of bud light were $6 each - the only downfall.. but not a deal breaker. We weren't ID'd at the door or bar.. but about 30mins & two beers later.. security came up to us and asked for our ID's. It was bothersome but we understood and showed him, he apologized and went on his merry way.
Awesome spot!  It was a blast. Great bands and atmosphere. Good beer and drink selection. They were out of Badass Lager on the weekend which was disappointing but many other beers to choose from.

Came here with 3 other people on a Friday night. First floor was packed, second and third floors were empty so we opted to sit on the roof top with around 45-50 other people. Band played good music with asking the crowd for requests multiple times. We got a round of drinks immediate and ordered the potatoes skins. Received them under 10 min later. Nothing special, basic bar food. Overall no complaints and a nice new place.

From now on when out of town guests are in town we will take them to the Music City Smokehouse!

I eat a this place a couple of times a week and it is delicious.  They recently changed their menu but it still includes the same delicious standard that are sooo delicious.  Visit the website for the latest southern comfort food.

I don't know my way around Nashville too well, but they say this place is only 1 mile from downtown, and has a big (free) parking lot.

HARLEY DAVIDSONS! It's definitely a Hog-friendly spot, but don't let that scare you: these bikers are typically successful, fun-loving types who just love to ride, meet new folks & enjoy it all (gotta get me a bike!)
wPWLlhAeBJcw2N-7MGUSxw: I literally JUST had the best bacon burger. It was so delicious...and I'm not even sure why. It's 1 in the morning lol. I never expect much this late at night. I'm very impressed. This shake is amazing as well, and they didn't pull a McDonald's with (Our machines down) lol.

It's 2:30am & I ordered a jumbo breakfast platter w/sausage, jumbo breakfast platter w/bacon, supreme croissant, & a sausage croissant and I must say that I'm pleasantly surprised that all 4 items were actually okay/good.  

My 1st time from here & 1st time ordering breakfast items from JNTB so didn't expect much being this late at night & from JNTB but yeah, it was good.  Breakfast platters actually had real scrambled eggs.  They weren't hard, cold, microwaved to death eggs either.  I was expecting that weird square processed egg thingy you normally get from fast food restaurants.  

The mini/coin pancakes on the platter were good too.  They were not hard or stale like you would get from overly microwaved bread items.  The croissants were equally good as the bread was "fresh" for a fast food joint & the egg & meats were fresh & not old or stale.  

I'll give them another go when I'm hungry & it's late & nothing much is open.  They earned a 4 on this round.  It's by no means 4-star restaurant food but the overall service & quality of food presented for this time of night earned a 4.
Very satisfied with our service and food! Ordered online at 3:30 am and everything was fresh and delicious! First time eating jack in the box and will definitely be back.

ADDICTIVE BISCUITS. I want to go back to Nashville just for more biscuits from Maggie's.

This California girl is in heaven in Nashville! They custom made me a beat juice shot and I am eating avocado toast on a gluten-free English muffin!!!!  You have to come here. Urban Juicer rocks my world! These people and this food are absolutely amazing. No place better to eat healthy and tasty in Nashville. JFmMg_BSbNZIY29fPkh8xg:
http://www.lovejustine.com/social-studies/the-pavillion-kitchen-bar

Where Red Rooster on Demonbreun used to stand, now stands a swankier get up called The Pavillion, and I must say I was delightfully impressed with it.  Nashville needs more spots like this.  I mean, not just because it's swanky(er), but because the food was delicious, too!

The owner is actually the same, they just gave their spot, as well as their menu, a major facelift.  It's like when Michael Jackson went from Black to White.

So, I met up with a friend for drinks, and actually she is the first person I ever knew in Nashville.  She moved away for a bit, but thankfully she recently returned as well.  And now we can have these awesome rendezvous.

There were a lot of tempting items on the menu.  The Croquet looked like a Cajun ball of delicious madness.  However, I decided to go with the Pulled Pork, because the description noted that it was "pork butt."  Not pork belly, not pork shoulder, not pork whatever...but pork butt.  The immature child in me could not resist ordering pork butt.  So I did.  And it was a FABULOUS decision.  It really was.  

If you've read some of my recent food related posts, or if you are familiar with southern cooking, you'll know that one of my biggest complaints is the tendency for restaurants here to over flavor everything--whether it's with salt or sugar.  And although I only had one item from the menu, I will say that my Pulled Pork at The Pavillion did not fall victim to flavor overkill.  It was lovely.  And the sweet, tangy purple cabbage and apple slaw was a nice pairing.

My friend ordered the Margherita Flatbread, which was not her first time ordering it, so you know it's good.  They drizzle balsamic vinaigrette on it which gives it a nice kick.  Her hubby ordered the chicken and biscuits, which apparently is his favorite item on the menu, and southern boys know their chicken and biscuits, so that's all I have to say about that.

So yeah, Nashvillians...especially those of you on the Row...need a new lunch spot?  Try The Pavillion.  I think you'll enjoy it :).
d9CdYq7FUzk7p7hYGIFcfQ: Love this place! Amazing atmosphere and drink deals. And the pool table is a plus. Definitely a must go on the east side.

It's more than a dive, but not too ritzy like a lot of Nashville have become. No marble countertops, no brightly-lit Instagram photo-ops. Yes some art and plants, some cozy booths, yes a pool table. It's just a bar.

That serves good drinks. They're not making all their own bitters and lighting things on fire for you, but they're not just serving Jack & Coke. It's the perfect middle. My favorite thing is that they wrote their brief cocktail menu with a range of prices ($6 to $11), so you can drink and socialize without breaking the bank. My current favorite is the Ouzo Lemonade - a refreshing tart lemonade (house-made, I think) with a cooling anise flavor from the Ouzo. They also sometimes have killer specials (hello $5 bees knees!). I hope they write some new cocktails for winter. They also have a beer list (bottles and cans) that my partner often orders from. But if you're looking for taps, you are in the wrong spot (and you should check out Vinyl & Taps on the other side of Gallatin!).

I don't come here to eat, so I can't speak to that. It's bar fare - mostly a couple hot dog options, which is just not my bag. On Sundays, they have special food for brunch that I've heard is a unique and yummy experience, but haven't tried it myself. So my rating is based on the cocktails and atmosphere only.
Great neighborhood bar on the East side. Drinks are well-crafted and snacks are tasty!
8BzO6LkXvM5pH5XN0G9oYQ:
So excited to have Hathorne in the neighborhood!!! We've been eagerly awaiting it's opening and we weren't disappointed. The chard sourdough is soooo good and paired with the fried goat cheese-YUM!! 

We brought our 3 kids with us and I was concerned that my son (who's a picky eater) wouldn't find anything to eat, but we ordered the steak and chicken and he loved both. We all did!!!

This place is da bomb! Toasty atmosphere for a cold winters night (also beautiful patio which I'm sure will be hopping in spring/summer) with delicious and reasonably priced food and drink. Excellent service and cute bar area would make for nice little date spot without breaking the bank. Party room in the back if you're having a soirée looks like a sophisticated good time.  Happy hour and late night (they refer to it as joyful hour) happy hour are unique and fun! Michter's rye got me going, fried chicken kept me there, don't sleep on the beets. That's a-one-a spicy meat-a-ball! Highly recommend.

Excellent pancakes and French omelet. Most of the staff have been excellent. Had a bit of an issue with the hostess one time when went for Sunday brunch. We figured that it would likely get crowded over the next bit of time and were fine sitting at the bar -- excellent service and chats there. However, it did not fill up. When we left over an hour or more later, the restaurant still had about half the seats opened. That made us question the veracity of what we were told by the hostess. We've not rushed back but haven't totally closed it as a possibility.
d59JUaCVMZj-ZEtlWcOKZw: Whatever else happens, it's a good day if you eat at Red Perch. They serve the best fish and chips in Nashville, possibly the entire country. What a cute place in the Arcade. Love the Fresh seafood options. I had the shrimp bah mi and the fish (cod) and chips. Crispy and delicious!  Everything was fried to perfection without being to heavy. Will come back next time I'm in town.

I feel bad for not giving this place five stars as apparently everyone else does. Not a knock; this place is a strong four. I thoroughly enjoyed it. yRVRqGIjfIwEICeRWDfxtg: I DO LOVE ROTI! Ate here at Yazoo brewery for the first time and it was awesome! Perfect with our brew! Will definitely eat here again

A quality option when craving poke.  

The staff was really nice and inviting.  They showed me a tasty option that I didn't even consider originally - the tako salad which was colorful and really good texture.

I consider it a decent value for poke places with a set bowl being $9-12.  I like the friendly service and plenty of parking within the strip mall. 


I just wish they played a track of music or something as it was dead quiet with the sound of the fridge.  

I saw multiple web orders come in so must be the default for many people ordering here.

A_lsaliw6jk7DQV6rTfY_A: Everyone was kind and hospitable, from the hosts when we walked in to our waiters when we got to the table. The only feedback I would give was the food came out very quickly so we got full pretty fast, otherwise we would have ordered more for sure. I guess we could have ordered at a slower pace as well. But we'll be back. Delicious and awesome new spot.

My husband and I celebrated our 20th Anniversary at Sunda. It was absolutely wonderful from start to finish. We had the bao buns with pork belly and Korean fried chicken....the pork belly was delicious! I don't know what kind of bread that is, but it was great! The sushi with bacon on top was ok....not my fave. We also tried the Gambler....I'm so glad my husband got the piece with the ghost pepper! It was good, but a little spicier than what I prefer. For dessert we were treated with a coconut cheesecake. I'm not a huge fan of coconut, but oh. my. goodness! This was heavenly!!!! We were also treated with a glass of champagne with our dessert since it was our anniversary. Our waitress was a young girl with long brown hair. She was so sweet and attentive to us. We will definitely go back next time we're in Nashville!

4.5 stars! Sunda - Nashville welcomes you! I have this rule to wait a few months before trying new restaurants incase there were any "adjustments" that need to be made, but we were in the area waiting for an appointment, they had open seating, and why the heck not. And we sure are glad we did. We went at an odd time - 4:45 PM on a Thursday evening so we got sat right away with no reservations. The restaurant itself is gorgeous, beautifully decorated, but still welcoming. As well as the staff, every single one of them greeted us and was extremely helpful, including our server. 

The menu was filled with seafood and Southeast Asian cuisines! We couldn't decided on what to order, we wanted everything. But we eventually agreed on 5 dishes:

Tuna Poke - one of the best poke we've had, the best part was the fried plantains! The tuna was marinated just right and tasted fresh. 

Korean Fried Chicken steamed buns - the buns were incredibly soft and fluffy, the chicken was moist and had good flavor, but could've used more spice. 

Sea bass - another BEST! It was definitely the favorite dish! Perfectly cooked hearty piece of Chilean sea bass with a crispy thin skin over tender vegetables. Delicious!

Tuna truffle pizza - this was just ok, the truffle tare made it a bit too sweet and a little too rich. And the roti prate is a little too soft for a "pizza" crust. 
 
Yellowtail jalapeno - I enjoyed it, but my husband did not like the Japanese salsa, he thought it was too sour. I thought it was refreshing! 

Everything came out steaming hot that was supposed to be and surprisingly fast, although we were there before the dinner rush. Even though it was a bit pricey, it was definitely worth the experience. We can't wait to try out the rest of the menu!
1VnjvQKmWNY9uzJwLAmX1Q: We ate at King Siam recently and were very impressed with the fresh Thai fare. We split parang curry and pad Thai.  Both were very nicely done and tasted even better.  Can't wait to try more dishes!!
Enjoyed the second visit to King Siam Thai as much as the first. Great service and food. I recommend highly.
I'm part Thai myself and their authenticity is amazing. Great service! Highly recommend!
raot7eGR74kljf3yI3qRCg: Superior sushi and a pleasant atmosphere. The service was fast and accurate, but the server wasn't well educated in the ways of sushi.

Great neighborhood place. I definitely recommend Yellow Tail Lover roll. Mrs. Stiglitz had the Stone Pot Bibimbap which was good as well.

__YGMQexDIqJKtYynxh5gg: I can't say enough good things about Lou. My boyfriend and I went recently and the entire experience from the ambience and service to the incredible food was beyond amazing! The mimosa was unique and definitely worth more than one  We split a side of the leafy greens with fermented lemon and they were dressed perfectly. I had two eggs, fennel bacon, with wheat bread that was accompanied by a whipped honey and herb butter. It was delicious! My boyfriend had the wild mushroom and steak tartin and loved it. It was one of the most satisfying meals we've had out in a while! We will definitely be back!

Came for brunch with a friend and truly enjoyed everything. We split the fruit and honey, broccolini and soft scrambled eggs sandwich, baked eggs with mole sauce and a side of fennel bacon. This is the BEST brunch I have ever had. The food is unique but super tasty. I am a vegetarian and I don't like spicy. I had the baked eggs with mole sauce. R9CkIswjh9tRaI-oChUBGA: Heard this place opened only a few days ago and decided to check it for a late lunch. The best way to describe the experience is a Mediterranean Chipotle. There are so many options and I honestly don't think you can go wrong with any of them. Unfortunately they were out of Taziki sauce and some other things because they've had so many customers come through, but I was still satisfied with the grain bowl I had. Huge portions for a good price. Definitely recommend you try it out!

This place was amazing. Kinda a mix between Chop't and a Mediterranean Chipotle. Serving size was HUGE, too much for me to eat in one sitting. Reasonable prices. Even with a big line, it moved rather quickly.

I've probably eaten here 6 or 7 times since the opening last month. I could eat here every day and never get tired of this place. Everything is super fresh, the portions are huge and the flavors are incredible. It's not only delicious, but you truly feel like you are doing something to benefit your health.  Excellent value for what you get. Can't wait to go again!!
foA6A0aV-Kvz1nGLei8SrQ: I was on my lunch break and thought I would try out the ceviche because I thought it is fried fish not knowing it is RAW FISH.  So I stood in line... got my food and opened my brown bag in my car...and said what?  This is raw!  I sat there and decided I don't have time to go back because I am on my lunch break.  I prayed Lord please don't let me get food poisoning because I have no choice but to eat this.  That was the main fear of eating raw fish.  The Lord answered my prayers and no food poisoning.  This is the best seasoned meat PERIOD.  It is soaked in lime with onions, lettuce and other seasoning.  Now that they have a restaurant...I will go and visit.  Since then I've had other ceviches but this the absolute best.
They catered the food at a workshop I went to. It was delicious! I had a chicken stew over protestors with rice. It also had a jammy soft boiled egg on the stew. It was fantastic!

I tried their alfajores, ceviche, and quinoa burger. So so delicious. I've spent some time in Peru, and this brings back all the memories of falling in love with the cuisine. There's a reason Peru (particularly Lima) is known as the food capital of South America ;) this truck is a great representation. Also, I'm so glad more and more food from other countries are being represented here at the Farmers Market in Nashville. We needed it.
FNDBaSryH9pPjQ95MUo1dQ:
We've been to the new Five Guys on White Bridge twice now since it opened. I like Five Guys because the employees have always been friendly and the food is consistent. I usually order a cheeseburger with bacon, onions and mustard and they have never screwed it up. Always cooked as it should be and juicy. And of course I have to have some peanuts (a freebie) before my burger comes. 

Usually I don't expect much out of a new store but they were "on point;" if you live in the Belle Meade or Green Hills areas give them a try!

Service was very mixed, the two female employees were incredibly helpful and polite but the one male employee was very rude. The food was fresh and tasty! The prices for drinks and little burgers are way too high. But the fries are worth the price for sure! Next time my husband and I are going to pass on soda and order a Bacon Cheeseburger and Little fry to split, it's a much better value. Would have been five stars if prices and that one rude employee were better.

Ordered on phone came to pick up. Still had to Wait. Lots and lots of people working. Not sure if they will  have that many workers 2 weeks from now. Ordered over the phone and like I said still had to wait. We had a Milk Shake too, so they made that on the spot. Was a very good Milk Shake, should have taken photos. 
All were kind servers, you could tell the newbie's aka as High School students learning their first job. An example to my kids on why it is important to get a job when allowable. 
Food. Hamburger had 2 patties. The wimpy burger has 1. Fries good. They had great toppings and place was clean. Miss Bruegers, but 5 guys is a good addition to the West Side. Will definitely be back. Managers are top notch and patient with the newbies.
I1jkXzkuNXkeuFUPOGUdoA: I was a little weary of trying this place because of the reviews but I'm glad I tried it. I got the E combo which included a lobster tail, shrimp, snow crab, corn , and potatoes. The food was very well seasoned contrary to other reviews. My money was well spent. I had food left for later. My server Quisha was great and I liked the overall feel of the place. I like how they supply you with all the necessities to throw down. Only suggestion is maybe change up the combos a little to include some fried options.
This is my favorite seafood restaurant in Nashville. I found this gem last month and ever since I pamper myself every Saturday to their shrimp, crab legs and lobster tail boil. Soooo goood!!!

They just opened 6-7-2019.
I was well pleased with the food. Atmosphere is family friendly. Staff are very professional and courteous. I would definitely recommend this restaurant to a friend.
-WAOPYXWLomXvcX5Jtbz0A:
Used the Yelp waiting list...suggested by the host when we called ahead...Great Idea that worked well!!
Busy Friday night, but we checked in and the host was honest with us and said "hey people are staying longer than normal"  - Okay!
Got seated within 15 min - Clean table.  Waited upon immediately, drinks out quickly, appetizer served was fresh (guacamole) order taken and served within reason (15-20 min)  and it was hot and delicious!
Excellent!!
You'll wait a while on weekend evenings but it's worth it. The food is a notch up from your standard Mexican restaurants. Service is excellent.

So good.  Been there twice since they've opened.  The staff is great with plenty of servers moving around. The food is delicious and came out pretty quickly.  Tito's margarita is amazing!  Managers were everywhere. They checked with us 3 times while we were there just to ask if everything was good, as well as being highly visible in the bar area, dining areas, and in/out of the kitchen.
DqQpeXvXJBS34E5TYC5p2A: We always get great service. Our orders are always correct and delivery quick. They make them pretty well, especially the gluten free pizza.

It's Good and it's cheap and it's a chain.  But the pizza quality is consistently fresh and well made and the service is excellent.  They once slightly singed my order and tossed it out and made me a fresh one without asking.  They are located near our home so we like getting it still hot.

I have no idea what the other reviewers are talking about. I have been ordering pizza from this location for the past 6 months and I had no complain whatsoever. Through the app I determine when I am going to pick up my pizza and my pizza always ready when I got there. Sometime I arrive early, but they are more than willing to accommodate. Thumbs up from me. Keep up the great work!
jErkkAPXwb_iT9y0uQWWjA: Wow! The place didn't look like much but the food was amazing! Will definitely return when in town! Food was perfectly cooked and seasoned. And much more than expected.

We went to Hooks on the Opening night and Loved it.   My Wife and I both had the Shrimp Boil at Hooks and our Son had the Fried Shrimp.

Everything was great....

You can tell they aren't fully done with the place, definitely needs some finishing touches. Hopefully a bar coming soon. The staff was friendly and helpful, the fried shrimp  was very good. Glad they came to Bellevue because it's definitely needed. I definitely will go back.
AZ9qlYAKwwNvC4HOgyEoXQ: We haven't eaten here before and we're very particular with Chinese food! We grabbed takeout tonight and so far I've taken two bites of hot and sour soup and steamed dumplings and they are both some of the best I've ever tasted!
YUMJ268M44ji78PXFM3GYg: Legit TX BBQ with sides that hold up to the meats. Pumped I can get my brisket fix. All I need is a Lone Star!

Little speedy, but clear quality. Not sure how you can ever return to mission bbq once you eat here. My only complaints are no french fries and not open on Tuesday, Wednesday. Otherwise good friendly vibes and moist and deliciously smoked meats!

Willie has the meats, all the meats...and he knows what he's doing with them. No 15 ingredient seasoning blends or 12 different varieties of sauce...salt, pepper, smoke and proper preparation. If you know real bbq, then you know that the meat should be able to stand alone and at Shotgun Willie's it is the absolute star. They are well known throughout Nashville for their brisket, which is amazing, but the rest of the meats are killer as well. I haven't had a chance to try the ribs, I'll be sure to update when I do...it won't be long, that's for sure. I'm not into mayo based anything, so I can only comment on the beans and Mac and Cheese. The beans were very well balanced: sweet, spicy and a touch of vinegar. I really like that they use a combination of different beans as well, these are definitely not your average baked beans, they're much more interesting. As far as the Mac and Cheese goes, it's hard to put into words. Upon first bite, I heard a knock under the table. It's the kind of Mac and Cheese that I'd want to serve my family at the holidays, the kind I wish I could recreate. I'll admit, I didn't use much sauce(wasn't really needed), but what they offer is a great compliment. It's not overly sweet or overly vinegary, it's just right. There weren't any desserts available right away, but I'll be back to try all of those as soon as they're available(Bourbon Banana Pudding anyone?) Over all, we had a great experience. Great people and amazing food. Get there early, be patient, bring a mask, leave happy.
pWlZTKoNYfJ4eXJrABIIpg: My server, Taylor, was absolutely amazing. I been to many restaurants and Taylor provided one of the best experiences I've had. His explanations on many dishes on their menu were great since it was my girlfriend and I's first time there. We got oysters and shrimp a la plancha as appetizers. For our meals, she got the skatewing and I initially decided on getting the mahi mahi. She liked her skatewing, while I didn't quite like my mahi as much. I usually never complain about any dishes I receive, but Taylor was great in telling me that it's okay and helped me choose another dish. I ended up getting the Alaskan halibut. I believe it was the  manager, Travis, that helped me tweak the halibut as well to my liking. I said I wanted something with more umami and saltiness and Travis executed it perfectly. My experience was amazing and I would definitely go back and ask for Taylor to be my server again.

WOW! 5 stars truly isn't enough recognition for this establishment or the service we recently received. The 4 of us went out to dinner for the first time in months and we all had been very interested in trying The Optimist and it did not disappoint. The atmosphere was chic and just had an overall cool factor - great for a nice night with friends or an intimate date. On top of some of the most delicious dishes some of us had ever tasted, our server was OUT OF THIS WORLD. Zech was knowledgeable from the wine to how the dishes were prepared to what they tasted like, his suggestions were all on point. He also has a very charming and comforting personality which really brought the experience full circle! We cannot wait to go back.

I am a foodie who is a stickler for good customer service
The Optimist did NOT disappoint in any way!
This was my first dine in (sat outside) experience, since 'Rona and everything was absolutely perfect. The halibut  was soooo light and scrumptious. All the flavors blended together perfectly accompanied by the green beans OHHH they were amazing and the hushpuppiesThen I topped all that goodness off with the dawggawn decadent & light chocolate pudding cake. MY GAWD! 
And the service was as good as the food! All of them engaged in conversation without getting on my nerves. You know how some servers can do too much & turn you off. This was not the case. My night at The Optimist was the perfect dining experience. Katie was my server She enjoyed my eating as much as I did! 
The location, atmosphere and vibe are all on point & I'm so glad they finally came to Nashville. 10 stars!
Oh snap! I almost forgot to tell you about the ROLLS!!! I swear they were baked by God!!!!!
JL5egb_kGXq5H52bj7_oVQ: The burger was pretty good but they are woefully slow. I'd say they need to hire more people

I was on my way back to the hotel and my shuttle driver James was telling me about this burger mom and pop spot I should try since I was expressing how starving I was lol. He took me to this place called mr burgers and it's just a place where you order burgers fries and wings and shakes via a window and go around and pick it up in another. I must say that the burger was unbelievable!!! The meat was handmade, and the cheese was melted just right where it dropped on the sides of the burger. The fries was nice and soft but yet with a slight crisp to it and I couldn't be happier with the order. This place does not have review le and the people that run it or amazingly nice and sweet. If you come to Nashville and do not feel like spending a lot of money and your staying by division st at home 2 suites please give mr. burgers a try, you will not be disappointed.
Such a good place to go, I've been going here since I first started middle school right next  door. I hope it never closes!!
WANxU_W3VV6RW8DCcG_pWQ:
This location has a drive through for mobile orders which makes the pick up efficient and quick. 
The food was pretty tasty

In these tough times, I tried using the app and the new concept started by Chipotlé called 'Chipotlane'. Whereby you place an order using their mobile app and then simply drive to the location and pick it up. When you pull up to the location there is no speaker or employee to take your order you must use the app. I ordered through the app and used Apple Pay. I arrived a little bit early so I had to pull into a parking spot and wait until the assigned time. When I had about a few minutes left I went ahead and got in line because there was a huge line of cars already. I seem to time it just right now because when I pulled up to the window it was exactly 12 o'clock. 

The process was pretty easy and seamless. I would recommend if you work in the area and want to pick up food quick. Download their app.

I had the best experience here today. The women working the front counter were super friendly and went out of their way to make sure I got fresh ingredients and exactly what I wanted with my order. I had originally gone to the window, not realizing it wasn't a drive through, and the employee was very polite when telling me about the process for ordering on the app/website. I know this location has been SUPER slammed since opening, as I've seen since I live across the street. That can be difficult to deal with at a new location. So thank you for the friendly interaction and the great service!
bN1UOtAXiPFebuVqfIbSuA: Staff is very nice.  Food is pretty good compared to the other restaurants locally.  I will have to give it to them for their drink presentation.  They are amazing.  It will take some time to make a drink, but it is going to look awesome.  They were also open when every other place in Bellevue was closed because of snow.
Tequila's has great customer service. The food is delicious and always comes out fast and hot! The margaritas here are delicious!

A rare 5 star rating from me! This is a traditional Mexican American restaurant however their chicken, steak, shrimp chorizo fajita or commonly called Texas fajita is the best I've ever had. It will be your new favorite. The environment and atmosphere is 5 star as well. This place used to be a breakfast restaurant with very low standards. This place is incredibly friendly. The manager made me a free Rivera Maya tequila that was almost too beautiful to drink!
ZgwFfEH5VKBxvMJF4guPMQ: First visit to The Dutch for a Saturday dinner.  Beautiful space with a cool vibe, and not too loud even with a full house. Roaring fireplace in the main dining area was a nice surprise on a chilly night. Service was friendly, helpful and efficient. Cocktails were excellent. Supper menu has a wide variety with many appealing choices. We settled on the corn bread, butternut salad and roasted beets to start, and the scallops and the halibut over grits mains - all dishes were well-prepared and served, tasty and very good to excellent overall. Lime pie dessert was outstanding.  We look forward to coming back soon (and often) to further explore the menu, which we hope will evolve over time with the seasons!  The Dutch is a great addition to the thriving and competitive Gulch neighborhood food scene!

I've been excited about the restaurants that are opening in the W hotel and took the opportunity to stop in for breakfast this weekend. The space is open and beautiful with an earthy, welcoming feel. They have a large bar and there were plenty of tables open when we stopped in on a Saturday morning. They have a nice breakfast menu, and a breakfast buffet set up which was a pretty typical spread at a hotel. And at $30 per person, we decided to order off the menu. I had black coffee and my bf had an iced almond milk latte. The drinks were good but I enjoy a large cup of coffee in the mornings and the coffee cup was smaller than I prefer and also $5 for straight black coffee. I guess it's the gulch and a nice hotel so par for the course, but that is steep for black coffee. For entrees we had the squash frittata with herbed goat cheese and sun dried tomatoes and the avocado toast. Whoever invents an easy way to eat avocado toast will be a genius because there just isn't a non-messy way to do it. But the flavors were very good and I enjoyed the green salad on the side. I love a salad at breakfast! My frittata was good, but I wish they had evenly distributed the cheese and tomato instead of having it separate. Overall it was a healthy breakfast and good, but I'm more excited to try dinner!

New restaurant that opened up in the W hotel with immaculate interior design. I think this will be a perfect brunch/lunch place vs dinner. 
We stopped for a weekday dinner and were lucky to be able to snag a table without a reservation, although the tables did fill up quickly.  

Despite the dim intimate lighting, the atmosphere was louder than we were expecting and the tables were all very close together. If we spoke normally to each other, we could not hear a thing. The entire dinner we were half-shouting at each other to be heard. I noticed that other patrons next to us had the same issue. Seems like a popular date night spot for early 30's and up. Diverse menu pulling influences from all over the world and extensive wine menu as well. 

Food: Nothing was bad but nothing blew us out of the water either. 

Chipotle- scallion Cornbread w/ whipped honey butter- I think this fell a bit flat. We're both a huge fan of cornbread and ecstatic to see it on the menu but this just wasn't it. Between the two of us, we only finished half. The whipped honey butter didn't have a very strong honey taste and the cornbread was dry. For $6, not too bad but will not order again. 

Broccolini- Delicious and generously portioned side item! Tender yet still crunchy, and wonderfully seasoned. 

Hanger Steak with Kimchi Rice- The steak was cooked perfectly medium pink and the Kimchi was forefront and quite delicious. 

Pecan Duck w/Dirty Rice - Duck was tender but the dirty rice was only okay. 

Service was great and prompt. The kitchen/staff still need to sort out some kinks because they brought the wrong dishes to our table.

Overal, a nice addition to the gulch.
aRZV74C9w723hu9A-C20gA: Today my family and I tried Ruby Sunshine for the first time. I enjoyed the atmosphere as well as all the variety of food options. They have a little something for everyone. The reason I only gave it 4 stars is because my food came out cold, the wait was a little lengthy and parking was terrible. Despite these grievances, I am going to give them another try one day.

This spot was great. The atmosphere was great and the food very tasty. I had the peacemaker with the bayou shrimp and chicken n gravy. I wasn't the biggest fan of the red sauce on the shrimp but it was still tasty. Chicken was great. My friend had the southern breakfast with can't bacon she ate it all with nothing to spare lol. My mom had the sliders and she loved her food. Our server was super polite and helpful. I throughly enjoyed my time at Ruby Sunshine and I will definitely be going back!!

Valentine's breakfast date and baby it was packed! Parking is okay, but for Sunday brunch I advise you to get there early. We didn't pay for parking so don't hurt me if you don't, my man car didn't get towed though! I'm not sure if it was because we were getting bad weather or because it was on a Sunday. Anyway, they have outdoor seating. I would love to go when it's warm. I advise you to put your name on the waitlist while you head there and once you arrive go check in to get seated! Don't forget to check in seriously. It's not that big of a place at all. The food was cool, nothing to die over. We weren't a fan of the burger or chicken sausage. The French toast and chicken was a pretty cool meal, despite how hard and crunchy the French toast is, my goodness I did not like that at all. Baby had their ruby mimosa and I had the peach Bellini it was delicious! The drinks are like two mimosas in one, great for the price. I'm probably not going to be back unless one of my friends want to go, I can go without. My waiter was great though! It was knats in there which was very disturbing.
oZ02krhS39cMHLJZokZK8Q:
Hard to find a good breakfast place 
This one resently opened 
For 5.50 you get eggs, your choice of meat
Biscuit and grits been here 2 times now 
Very impressive staff and always polite 
I appreciate this restaurant being open
The Country ham is very good and the 
Biscuit is like they just made it .awesomeness
uPsFg_EG4sgrkimoRrBNOA: This place made me want to hip-hop dance, and I half-turned into a werewolf. It was that good. Really loved the carnitas and barbacoa. The quesadillas are unlike any I've ever tried. The horchata was wonderfully refreshing. And the staff couldn't have been more friendly and welcoming. Highly recommend!
If you want delicious, authentic tacos and kind people, this is your place. So glad to have them in the neighborhood!
The best burrito spot I have found in Nashville so far. Super friendly owners and great service.
rliUw3TlqpvPF1b4Hpn-GQ: Excellent customer service! The host and everyone else I came across were sweet and awesome. The hot chicken sandwich was great. Definitely coming back here.

Well worth the line because .....they all know how
Good it is.  Loved the black eyed pea salad !!!

Pretty daMn delicious!! Wimpy me could only eat the mild flavored chicken with the Chix'n waffles and it was huge portions! We also got potato salad, baked beans & fried okra ALL were tasty & with excellent flavor. Do Happi we stumbled across this place & plan to dine again while we are still in town!
7T8_htSMHuSzKqiwjSt3iA: Good luck finding a rock bar in Nashville that isn't this place. Great atmosphere, staff, and food. I can't stop recommending this place.

Me and a few friends stopped by a Bowie's when were in Nashville this June, it was fully open and everyone was wearing masks and gloves. The food was fantastic as were the drinks! They have a really cool stage setup that would definitely be my go to place for some off broadway music. Make sure that you put this place on your list to visit the next time you're in Nashville.

Proximity to our hotel was a plus, but the entertainment got us in the door - really great food was a plus but the exceptional service ( I'm looking at you, Peyton ) is what brought us back more than once!
bnvSozbwKDf8xnsbfe8ZzA:
Speaking as someone who grew up in New York, this bagel place makes them the way I ate them as a kid. Just the right amount of dough with a nice chewy crust and the perfect amount of seasonings on top! 

They are also super nice people to deal with. If you haven't been there, get there. If you've been there like me you know you're definitely going to be going back.

This one's a winner and a keeper.

Who would have thought that you could get a great bagel and lox along with other great toppings for a super bagel sandwich right in downtown Nashville.
Right next door to the Fairlane Hotel. Great spot to sit relax and enjoy your coffee.  Also to go... but get your bagels before they sell out.  Awesome staff.  
Frank P.

LOOOOOOVE Bagelshop. Not only are their bagels flipping delicious, but their pastries are just as good. Valet let me park outside so I didn't have to pay for parking to grab my pickup order the other day. The different flavors of cream cheese are definitely my favorite option there. Highly recommend the sundried tomato or garlic and feta schmear.
M8vKFvhRr_O31F5R9UGQWA: Went here as a birthday dinner. Great selection, kidda pricey, but it was a fun experience. If you're not seated next to the conveyor belt, veggies are hard to get to :(
So much fun! Wonderful dining experience! Great for adventurous couples looking for a fun date night dining experience out of the norm!

Good food, great service! they were very accommodating for our large party and even sent a singing robot to our tables! Selection of hot pot meat was small but since it's all you can eat, we definitely went back a lot. Good selection of sauces, would've loved to have eggs though!
_Sa_9OJ1GMW72ZFUR4zYYA: I had the most beautiful meal of my life at Audrey. The staff was knowledgeable and so friendly. The service made the meal feel very comfortable and laid back, while the care and details of the meal were a carefully curated experience. Chef Sean Brock has a knack for creating an utterly new dining experience that somehow also feels nostalgic. With a fixed menu shifting daily to use only the best seasonal ingredients, we're so excited to come back soon and have different experience of the same caliber.

Highly recommend if you are able to get a reservation. Sean Brock shows why he is the best chef in the south. The menu was perfectly curated. The grits might have been the best I've ever had. The 60 day dry aged beef was delicious. The desserts are always top knotch. Anyone who wants to serve 3 desserts with your meal, knows how to showcase a meal. Every dish we had was better than the next. Can't wait to come back as the seasons change and the menu grows with the seasons. 10 out of 10.
Exceptional lovely dinner, wow, what a beautiful evening. The food is divine and the atmosphere is perfect. Could not be more amazing, we loved every aspect.
j1PX2dXNogvGQimscKWERA: This gem of a restaurant at the historic Bellemeade Mansion is like stepping into a secret garden and having a great meal as a bonus. Yes, the food & service are great. Yet the five star aspect of this is the beautiful private park experience that is transformative both on the way in and out of a delicious lunch.

Not sure why the reviews aren't great here. The food is really good. I had pulled pork, macaroni and cheese, and black eyed peas. My wife had smoked chicken, green beans and macaroni and cheese. The food was great but the highlight of it all was the 2 young men working behind the counter. They "waved" us in (it was slow), explained the menu and even gave recommendations. Definitely didn't steer us wrong. They were extremely polite. It's awesome to see young men work this hard and act like they enjoy it!
Loved the new restaurant at Belle Meade!  Very quick, friendly service.  Food was presented well and top notch delicious!   Well done!
1wbIkxqILqNjp6aNe5mr6A: Just delicious. I had the Tennessee Tonkatsu. The broth had so much flavor and was packed with umami from all the high quality ingredients. I also got a few extra soy eggs for later (a smart decision!). I'll be back soon. So excited to have legit ramen on the West Side.

Oh my goodness! Absolutely delicious. Every bite. Perfectly prepared. Perfectly packaged for take away. Don't forget to order the chili crisps on the side. And the hibiscus strawberry boba. Oh, and the TFC. And the hot chicken bao buns. Basically, everything!

We can't wait to order again. Pretty pricey, but worth it!

Consistently great.
Best veggie ramen in town and the Dill Tofu is enough reason to keep coming back!
Service is prompt and friendly and the prices are very good for the quality.
