I've eaten ramen and dumplings here a dozen times and have never been disappointed.
8alwxME7voyqwwRaqtwB9g: Crab bag was fire . Literally one of the best things I've ordered in Nashville. I got the garlic butter with medium spice and dang it was so good!!! My fiancé and i already are making plans to go back
I loved this place. It was definitely juicy!  The spices was right on for my seafood bag!  The ambiance was fun and clean!

Last night, my family visited the Nolensville location and had a great experience. The service was great as they went out of their way to serve us. The seafood was seasoned so well, it was perfectly delicious. 

My family all enjoyed their different dishes including the crab legs, shrimp, and Cajun fries.

They also created distance between tables, were health conscious, and wore their masks.

The building is new, spacious, and clean. 

We really loved this place and can't wait to visit again!
t_n6iMoQh3tLIULRTDfzYA:
The only coffee spot in the D terminal I could find, but they certainly did not disappoint! 

I was here on a Sunday and it was super crowded. I probably waited in line for around 15-20 minutes, but that was to be expected! I picked up a bottle of water and an oat milk latte and the total was about 9$ before tip. 

The workers were super friendly and efficient. My latte was literally ready by the time I paid so I was ready to go. People who were ordering food had a bit longer of a wait. 

The latte itself was ok, the oat milk was pretty sweet (not my favorite, personally) so if I go back I would probably just get a black coffee!
Staff were very friendly. Had the hummus and an americano, both were delicious. The place was clean and had a lot of food choices. The coffee is pretty hot, which I love.

Staff was super nice, helpful, and attentive. Coffee was good and they had the only vegan option in the terminal, hummus + veg. Definitely not the same as the brick and mortar locations, but it was great having a local coffee available and not have to resort to Starbucks.
jhIHECFpt2_EXLlcaNGCFQ: Bills Sandwich Palace has some of the best sandwiches you could ask for. The bread is always toasted the perfect amount and all the ingredients and sandwich workings taste super fresh. Though the hours are only on the weekend, it's worth the wait for Saturday and Sunday each time.

Bill's Sandwich Palace is a weekend restaurant pop-up in the TKO building.  While you'll need to place your order several hours in advance of pickup, it is well worth it.  Bill's menu offerings change frequently, but everything here is sandwich perfection.  My favorite is the Caesar club, which is served on a delicious toast and has a fine blend of slaw, veggies, and dressing.  We've also tried the fried chicken sandwich.  Bill's also has great sides, including housemade cheese puffs, broccoli salad, and a fantastic  deli-style potato salad.  Yazoo Brewery is close by and a great place to enjoy Bill's best sandwich concoctions!
Best sandwich place in Nashville hands down. Absolute perfection. Looking forward to returning soon.
IzoMcpZMIwn7IVf8v_8GnA:
I have been to this Popeyes three times and honestly all times have been a great experience. The management is efficient and works like clockwork. 

And we finally got to try the rage- the popeyes chicken sandwich. It was delicious, really big and filling, and total bang for the buck! 

The popeyes crunchy crispy coated chicken breast sandwiched between two soft and slightly sweet brioche buns is amazingly juicy and warm. We had the spicy sandwich, so the sauce has a pretty good kick. You know they say less is more, and the pickles and sauce just make the sandwich perfect. 

I like this popeyes establishment, it is pretty busy but the station delivers!
Leaving BNA now but wanted to give a shoutout to the hardworking people behind the counter. Super efficient and hardworking.
BgrvT165fB_wCsZazonMtA:
Man why does this place have bad reviews?  We had a totally pleasant experience. 

Heading back to our hotel after a night walk around Nashville, we were seeking food located in an area not crowded by party goers. This place seemed quiet and offered pizza by the slice. We ordered a small pepperoni and gave a name. They called out our order politely and we took our order back to our hotel. Everyone we interacted with was very professional and kind.

The pizza was great! We loved the soft and chewy dough and the ingredients were top tier.

Open late and reasonably priced.
We had a great experience! Good service, yummy food and very quick! We got the cheese ravioli, Italian style pizza and garlic cheese bread! We definitely recommend
Very good Cajun chicken salad. Chicken wings were on the small side, they were cooked and well sauced though. I would definitely order here again. A clean, friendly and fast environment. Thank you!
FU0qA0c17JpA482ZpOs9Kw: I'm a regular at e+rose and I am always so happy with the service and my smoothie bowls! Ordering through the app is a lifesaver on my way to work bc my food is always ready by the time i get there. Also the girls behind the counter are so nice :)
One of my favorites for morning coffee and tea! Love the blue bae bowl, I like to order with out coconut! Wonderful iced green / jasmine tea

Not sure why there are several bad reviews because my husband and I LOVED this place. I am an açaí bowl junkie and very picky about them. We were visiting staying walking distance from here and stumbled upon this place while on a run and seeing a few guys outside having delicious looking açaí bowls. We had just had not so good disappointing ones earlier that day from another juice bar and I wish I had found this place sooner. We went here the next morning for breakfast and it was amazing! Super fresh and very generous portions also the girl working the counter was very friendly. I highly recommend and will definitely be going back next time I'm in town!
hXIE9418_-sT3_ivKKgDfQ: this place is great!!! excellent service and great food!! i had the shrimp fajitas and they were fantastic, along with the happy hour margaritas! will definitely come back!!
Wow... Bellevue needed a new great Mexican place! The street tacos are fantastic and the spicy salsa (that you have to ask for special) is incredible. Try the beans! Service so good.

Hubby has always wandered what potatoes would taste like with chicken fajitas....WAS SO DELICIOUS we went back the very next night. They were smothered in cheese as well. I had the steak fajitas on first night and enchiladas the next night. Service was fantastic...food was quick,hot and delicious. Margaritas great.
yX0P0_JV1imBE46I1ZMEaA:
I am addicted to Panera's BBQ Chopped Chicken Salad.  I am attracted to anything that has corn and multi-colored tortilla strips because it reminds me of food in Santa Fe (NM not Santa "Feeeee", TN).  My wife especially likes the vegetable soup with pesto sauce, so much that she knows the days when it is served.  I know where she will request to eat, if I invite her on those days.

I have eaten at a lot of Panera's around the country.  They are all good.  They are uniform and consistent.  You know what to expect and you get the same high quality food.  I especially like the 100 Oaks (Powell Ave.) restaurant best because it has the best people.  They are helpful and friendly.  It is a pleasure to spend my money there!

Be sure to get a frequent eater card, because you will be eating there so often, the points and free food will add up fast.

The only thing they need to do is to add real fruit tea to the menu.  Their acai-flavored iced tea is a disappointment.

The best Panera around!

Being in the middle of a work district (100 oaks, Vanderbilt offices, all the industries in Sidco), you would expect this Panera to be tangled in lines every weekday.

While they are extremely busy, I'm in an out of there in less than 6-8 minutes every time!

Not only is this Panera a well-oiled machine in terms of speed, but they always get my order right, not matter how large. There is a manager standing on the to go line who's only job is to check the orders. This is much appreciated!

All in all this is a great Panera... Always clean, always friendly, and always fast!!

2013/015

Despite frequenting the Panera on West End for the past year, I have never been there for breakfast.

I came in at this location's Panera, expecting to have an early lunch. Instead, I was greeted with the breakfast savory souffles that I had to try.

The spinach and bacon one was incredibly good. I think the airiness of the souffle tricks me into thinking it contains fewer calories. But I know it doesn't.

I arrived at 9:45am on a Saturday, and there was plenty of seating. By the time I finished eating at 10:00am, the place was packed and there was already a long line.

Staff was friendly (but a friend I was meeting said one staff member completely forgot to make her order).
2Yrf_aECZVHgTBVyw1r2kA:
Warning: this review IS slightly biased, because I do know the people who run this, but I am trying to review this as unbiasedly as possible. 

The family that opened this up is great at cooking. Trust me, I've known them for a long while. The food is fresh and delicious, and they have the upper hand with that, because they also own the grocery store next to it. So it is easy for them to get fresh ingredients at a good price, passing the savings to us!

If you haven't had the fresh spring rolls with tofu, please do. I'm not a fan of tofu, but these rolls were delicious.

The setting is intimate, perfect for a quick lunch, and the staff is extremely friendly and quick. Parking is also not an issue. And the decor is simple but yet very contemporary. 

Try it out; you won't be disappointed!

I am visiting Nashville for a long weekend.  My wife and I stopped here after visiting the zoo.  It is nearby and had good reviews.  We were not disappointed,  the server was wonderful and the food was also better than I was expecting.  I wish my town in NY had a restaurant this good.  The noodles were fresh and sauce was spiced perfectly.

This is the Thai restaurant that I've been waiting for in Nashville. I've been scouring the streets for a place down south that does pad thai like the west coast and finally, someone has got it right. This place is tucked away down Thompson Lane and I would have never found it without my bf's Thai restaurant radar. This place has easy parking, great casual atmosphere and lively waitstaff ready to bring you delicious food. The pad thai is perfect- not too much or too little sauce. The tom kha gai soup is absolutely amazing. Generally, they are on the spicy side, so if you don't like spicy, make sure you tell them. I'm looking forward to trying the pho- while it's hard to beat my mom's, this place has done so well with other dishes that I'd be willing to bet they can get it right. So excited this place opened just down the road from me
yZHiutcbym-xLGkmFhP-9A: I went early on a Sunday morning which was an excellent time to go. Not too crowded yet and still plenty if spots to sit. I got the California which was basically a combination of everything I loved, cooked to perfection. Eggs, sprouts, avocado and salsa. It was served with sourdough toast but I prefer wheat so I got that and it was excellent toast. My boyfriend got an architect omelette and was also pleased with his meal. Being from St. Louis we were excited to see they served kaldi's coffee. I ordered a cappuccino and it was pretty good. I would have liked a bit more foam. But I appreciated that the barista made a design when pouring the milk! We also wanted to try the apricot mango scone so we got one to go. It was very rich and delicious, I could only have a bite! The atmosphere was also nice and they had plenty of seating. The outdoor patio was really nice and a great place to sit and enjoy the morning. Next time I'm in Nashville I will be back.

I just went to frothy monkey and it was super amazing. I really love the vibe at this joint. I can imagine coming here and doing some causal work with my colleague. I got the fruit ice tea and it was pretty good. I just hate there is no parking, but that won't stop me from coming back.

My first visit to the Frothy Monkey earned its 4 stars - nice atmosphere, the menu offered a variety of drinks, and I ordered a great chai latte. But now I must give it five stars after my second visit on Friday. I ordered a Julie (bagel sandwich with a farm egg, feta, basil, and tomato) and an iced banana coffee. Bagel, delicious as all get out (I love anything with basil), but the banana coffee was remarkable. I would definitely go back again just for that drink, but the food definitely bumped up that extra star. How nice that Nashville's coffee shops can also whip up a fantastic meal!
1b5mnK8bMnnju_cvU65GqQ: the bonuts & yogurt were both amazing! there was a line, but once we were seated the food arrived immediately. highly recommend!
Great friendly service. Delicious food. Cute atmosphere. Bonuts are wonderful. I also had the sausage gravy biscuit which was great.

Love is definitely the right word for this place. We went on a Sunday morning, around 10. It was busy but the flow of the counter service and the seating was perfect, no one was ever waiting around for a seat, tables were never empty for more than a few minutes. I was kind of mystified by the efficiency of it all.  The table started with the Bonuts, they were amazing, like a savory shortcake covered in sugar and filled with a delicious cheesey creamy goodness. You have to eat these. Our main orders came very quickly as well and we started in on our meals. I ordered the SEC, a friend egg and ham sausage on a biscuit.  The only thing I could have used was a side of gravy, but I think most things could use a side of gravy so choose for yourself.  Everyone else also loved what they ordered. 

I drank a bloody mary, completely awesome! 

This place is totally worth trying and if I lived in Nashville, I would totally make this a regular spot.
4bOqibZuKDbnOa-K0kM4Sg:
I had not yet been to the Flying Saucer, though some of my friends had.  As I am a big beer drinker I was immediately impressed with the place.  The selection of beers was huge and it was an impressive to see all the taps lining the wall. There were two really great features for beer drinkers:

1. Each day there is a "fire sale" beer that goes for $3 a pint.  If you like the beer of the day, you're in luck for good beer at a great price.

2. You can build your own beer flight, so for a great price you can try 5 beers.  This was especially great for me as they had several regional beers I had yet to try.  

The lunch I had was forgettable, but several of us split a couple orders of the hot pretzels.  Obviously pretzels and beer pair well - but the real winner here was the varied dipping sauces.  Two kinds of cheese and two kinds of mustard.  

When we were there late night on Friday it was REALLY crowded - which I don't mind - but it led our server to be in a less than pleasant mood.  Otherwise I would say my experience was awesome.

Because we had such a great time there on Friday, we changed our plans and held our Fantasy Football draft there on Saturday - something I definitely recommend for anyone looking for a spot.

There's not much more to say than what everyone else has already said, but if you haven't been here yet, what are you waiting for? 

I used to regular this place in Texas- even have my own plate on the wall. Here in Nashville, I'm not so much a regular, and only due to the fact you have to pay to park or catch a cab here. I love the brew selection and the fare. It's a fun place to be. It's kinda like a candy store for the grown ups. 

The place is pretty big and super comfy year round.

I'd give them a five star if the Friday and Saturday night scene there wasn't so bad.

Don't get me wrong, I'm a member of their club, and am about 70-80 beers in to the 200 required to get a "plate" on their wall.  I love their pretzels, and especially love many of the beers there.  If you go when they aren't packed, you can generally expect a calm, comfy atmosphere.

The waitresses are a mixed bunch.  Some, frankly, aren't great.  Some, however, are awesome and very knowledgeable.

The only problem I have is, going on a Friday or Saturday night will mean fighting for a seat.  Now, prior to becoming a member, this also seemed to mean slow, generally bad service.  Since my friends and I are all members, though, and generally tip well, I feel that that has changed.  To a first time visitor, a Friday or Saturday night will usually entail searching for a spot to sit, waiting a long time to get served, and they'll likely be out of their more common sales that night.

Go on a weeknight.
8FE4V7Y5HliQ2bP-szn_sA: In town for a Bachelorette Party and decided to grab lunch... great food, great service, great atmosphere. You should probably go here.

The visit started "pourly" but the manager quickly righted the ship. She stepped in and took our drink orders and made our experience much better than it could have been.  

The fried pickles were good. 

The nachos were not the normal nachos. They had great amount of variety and substance 

Both types of chicken sandwiches and the philly cheese steak were good and the onion rings were OK. 

The street corn had great flavor. 

The drinks were fantastic!

In the end, this afternoon meal turned into a great time with friends due to the fantastic manager. 

Thanks
I gave it 5 stars to up the average. This place should get 4 stars for the bourbon selection alone. Great burger too!
x3h0A6n3zIVcnrC0q4bGKg:
So many good reviews here already! For how new this place is, I think that says alot to the good already. For my own two cents I will say this bar has quickly became my and the wife's go to bar for some good conversation and well crafted drinks. The bartenders all seem to truly enjoy working there and bought into the bar's success. This may spring from many of them being part owners or friends with the owners. The food while not groundbreaking is very well executed. The brown bag special after the kitchen closes is a brilliant idea that can be a life saver if your the type of person that goes out late and closes down five points. The drinks on the menu are solid 3-4 ingredient creations, and best of all, the bartenders seem to be skilled enough to add their own twists with regular success. The bar its self is dark and quite like a  wolf's den. Music and lighting are kept low. There is a great round booth that could sit 10 likley and would be great for a big group but i've yet to see that many folks here yet. 
 So far we have not seen the place extremely crowded and i think that's a good thing (although the owners may disagree) The entire staff has been friendly and parking has not been an issue.  

My only problem is that the prices are very high and there are few options for saving a buck or two. Food starts at 7 bucks and the drinks menu is 10. That's putting it in the realm of some really high end places and I just don't feel that this place wants to be a premium drinking spot. Or at least I think it would be better if it was not. It would really put this place over the moon in my opinion if they had a few good 5 dollar drinks and some cheap beer specials. We love the drinks as is and they should be kept! We will always be up for ordering a few. But thats the rub we will always be ordering just a few.  We are folks that like to spend a night out, not just an hour or two and with the entire drink menu at 10 bucks flat a long night of drinking can quickly tap out your wallet. For now we usually find ourselves either starting or finishing the night here with one or two drinks but spending the majority of our night at the nearby bars with a broader selection of priced drinks. Still if you have not yet drop by and try this new addition to our neighborhood.

This place is my home, home on the range! You walk in and are greeted by some killer decor, handcrafted wallpaper and Jeremiah Johnson-worthy taxidermy. There's a pool table felted in svelte black and a classic photo booth, $4 for a REAL developed photo strip, and no crappy sports TVs that drown out all civilized conversation. So, this is a great place to bring your friends, have a party, or just come by yourself for the whiskey and the burgers! 

Seriously, the Wolf burger is an incredible amount of meat and manhood! Chips instead of fries, but I'm not complaining. They also offer great HANDMADE sausages. I looked at the previous reviews just to double check the stats, and there were some complaints about the odor, but all I could smell was the allure of the burger! 

This place isn't for prissy cocktails, these guys know what they're doing. Try the Moonshine concoctions, Ratchet Rose was my fav and my friend loved the Grizzly Adams. The bartenders are great and always friendly, even when the place is wild. 

They also have great DJs cameo here and even QDP hosted a crazy Halloween party that ended up in wily bar-top dancing! Porch for smokers that has heaters and TONS of parking. TONS.

My coworker claimed that this place has the best burger in town, so of course I had to check it out. I came with 3 friends and we ordered four things and split them.

The burgers:
The Wolf. 
The All American Wolf. 
The Alpine Wolf. 

The sandwich:
The Yardbird ... recommended by chef Greg Dickson himself. 

The food was fantastic. The Yardbird is incredible. The bacon maple kale was divine and the chicken was so flavorful. 

Hands down the best burgers in Nashville.
K59I711q4mFXw7ZBxdwBTg: Great beer, food, and excellent staff!! Got a Groupon for beer tasting and an appetizer for 2. I loved the experience, they have good beer selection and the Nachos we ordered were good. I personally liked the way the place was decorated, it just felt artsy and cozy. The place had a couple of TVs in case you want to watch it instead of talking to each other lol. It also had outdoor seating with a smoking and a non smoking area. I went during the day and parking was ok but I don't know how it can get at night. Overall I really enjoyed my time there, like I said, staff was great and beer was even better!
Belcourt Taps is one of my favorite places to eat! The parking isn't bad and the location is great! If you've never tried their loaded fries then you are seriously missing out!
Loved the pork platter. Great live music and chill place to hang out and have some drinks! My foreign exchange friends loved it too!!
wRzZQ0rNNl0Ss9dHNM1S1A: This place rocks! They have a decent beer selection on draft and the pizza is outstanding. Definitely my new favorite place. The best part about this place is that there are soooo many topping choices. My favorite is their beef pepperoni. I can't eat pork, so their beef pepperoni option was definitely a huge bonus. Since you also create your own pizza, I was able to try two different sauces. One half red and the other half white. Both delicious. I'd recommend ordering the Huskey size. It's just enough to fill you up, but not so much to where you're stuffed. Absolutely a must try! I can't wait to come back!

It was noonish on a Tuesday, and the line was completely tolerable. Staff is pleasant and helpful, and the pizza is tops! Excellent crust - crispy, yet soft in all the right places. Great selection of beers on tap (including Mantra's Saffron IPA!).

Jonathan
Awesome.  Just awesome.  Unique & classic choices with just the highest level of quality ingredients & perfectly made.  Nice job guys!!!
QxUqFqyEeO0xOlp8H3vHXQ:
I like Rumba for the patio.  And for the chicken and steak satay skewers.  And for the mango mojito.  BEST mojitos in town, hands down.

I've never actually ordered a full entree--they have plenty of good tapas to choose from, so I get 2 or 3 of those...and plenty of drinks of course.  Great place to take a group--they do take reservations.  As a bar, I would say it's a place to hang out right after dinner, but then move on elsewhere unless you want to end your night early.  It's not exactly a kickin' hotspot.  

I haven't been on a Sunday in a while, but they used to have 1/2 off bottles of wine.  It's worth checking out to finish off the weekend and see if they still offer that deal!  Sit on the patio--right on West End, so it can get a bit noisy, but I just like to be outside if it's nice.  Service is pretty decent too.
Awesome food, great service, unique and tasty drinks combined with a cool ambiance. Gonna keep this one short and sweet- if you're in the area (or not)- get down!

I'm not from Nashville, and I only visit on occassion.  On this particular occassion, it was a weekend full of a Bob Seger concert, so nothing could really go wrong.  I still think about this weekend, and the steak sandwich I had at Rumba.  Mmmmmm.  My stomach is growling just thinking about it.  
The beef was thicker than a normal steak sandwich, and the spicy sauce on the side was to die for.  I remember the spice of the beef being different than anything I ever had.  Very unique, very memorable.  
The bar was also really cool.  They had TONS of drinks to choose from - caprinhas, local brews, etc.  I went with a local brew, but the mojitos that my dining mates ordered looked quite tasty.  I'd definitely go back to try more of the menu here at Rumba, and try more of the fancy drinks.
1KpNlu8VM8ukw3uOzmMFPA: We were in town for a convention and needed a break. Had the best time and the band, sorry can't recall their name, were more fun than we expected. Had me doing a cameo or Mustang Sally!  If we make it back to the Nashville this will be on our must do list! Service was great, food average.

My dad and I came here back in the first part of Spring(better late review than never). After driving around downtown three times trying to find a parking spot, we finally found a parking garage right down the street from here. We decided to come here because we like live music. There was a great band playing. Food and drinks were good. Service was decent, not great but not bad. Bathroom could use some more upkeep but otherwise no complaints. I'd come back here next time we're down South.

I got the shrimp and fish platter and it was amazing! You get 8 shrimp and 1 catfish filet. The filet is breaded fantastic! It's so light and crunchy! Some of the best catfish I have had. The shrimp are always breaded great and very crunchy! I'm a big seafood person and what I tasted was GREAT seafood. I also had some of the dry rubbed wings and they tasted great as well. Really love the rub on the wings.
r70eml0T6aVe8z51IvydRg: What a hidden gem! SomeWhere SOS is hidden just behind the SATCO on 21st - and for some crazy reason it's NOT overrun with undergrads from Vanderbilt (still can't figure out why)! It's a chill bar - a great place to hang out and watch a game! It's covered (so even in the summer, it's not an unbearably hot place to watch a game), and has a fire pit (so it's not too cold in the winter either)! They have a table set up for beer pong. A few beers on draft (many more in bottles and cans) and a full bar. Their staff is great - we spent quite a bit of time chatting up the bartender when they weren't busy! He was very friendly and had a drink with us! He mentioned that they have food as well - bar fare. The theme (beachy, Hawaiian) is great, and the decor is charming and low-key. SomeWhere SOS definitely isn't trying too hard (like so many places in Nashville now) - and it's kitschy-ness completely appealed to me! All in all, it's a great, casual place to grab a bit to eat and drink a few drinks or hang out for a few hours to watch a game! Definitely on my list of places to go back to!

This colorful little spot is tucked behind SATCO. It goes unnoticed, but it's worth a stop. There's always beachy music floating out of the open deck design of this place, and they do a good job of creating a laid-back beach vibe. The food was very tasty, the bartender friendly and attentive, and they have drink specials.

They're open from 10 AM until 2 AM daily, and even serve breakfast! It's a fun and welcome addition to the Vanderbilt area, perfect for lunch, dinner, or late night drinks. Their Nashville hot chicken IS hot, and delicious. 

Parking-The metered parking spots on either side of the street are free after 5:30 and on weekends, and there are more along 19th. Inside the parking garage across the street, you can park on the bottom couple of levels for a couple of bucks. There are ticket machines.
Great service and a relaxed atmosphere and the manager makes sure to that you have a great time!
mpPwLhTbVwlWXy5atD6TXw:
My cousin introduced me to Couser's Southern Restaurant this afternoon. The first thing I noticed as we approached the building (which was nice enough) was the lack of signage. Had I not been with someone who knew the place was there, I would never have noticed the unassuming white sided Southern house looking establishment. 
As we parked right in front of the door, and walked up the sidewalk to enter, it began to feel more like a restaurant. Inside, the decor was sparse, but considerably better than overdone, as so many family owned businesses tend to be. I did notice two lovely large portraits on the left going inside, an older gentleman, and a pleasant, sweet looking older woman, in beautiful frames. I had to get a closer look, and was intrigued with them. I am not a local, only visiting the city, so I had no clue as to who the people were, or why their portraits would be hanging inside the restaurant. As I glanced around the dining room, I noticed a nice looking more mature couple dining at a corner table, then we were led to our table by one of the nicest waitresses I have ever encountered. We made our selection from the ample menu, and decided to take a picture of the two of us for a keepsake of my visit to Nashville. There were several Nashville themed posters around, all nice, and we stood to include one next to our table in the picture between us. Another sweet waitress ran over and offered to snap our picture for us. We were most grateful, since selfies are difficult most times, especially when you want to have a large poster in the picture with two faces. We thanked her and as we sat back down, our food was arriving. We were rather surprised since we had not been there very long. We were both very happy with the appearance of our food, and began to eat. I ordered a side of Mac & Cheese, which looked amazing. One bite and I knew all other Mac & Cheese I had ever eaten in my 58 years was slightly superior to gruel. This was a taste bud sensation I had never experienced. Fantastic! I ordered another side of the amazing cheese-laden delight. Pure bliss. Put my own mother's Mac & Cheese to shame, and that woman could do no wrong. My cousin got Salmon Croquettes, which she claimed to be the best she had ever eaten. I had the chicken tenders, also, which were perfectly prepared, and probably up to par with any restaurants tenders. Of course we had other items, all of which were very good, but the Mac & Cheese and Salmon Croquettes were beyond all expectation for any Southern style restaurant.
Afterward, we went to pay (incredibly pleased with the charge), and I inquired about the portraits, and was told that was Mr. & Mrs. Couser, then I asked if the older couple dining were that couple, and my suspicion was confirmed. 
One thing I know is Couser's will be on my list of places to visit every time I come to Nashville. Thank you, Mr. & Mrs. Couser for a wonderful dining experience. I will see you again. ASAP

Couser's is a true gem in Nashville that I've been going to for years. You can't go wrong with anything on the menu, but their mashed potatoes and their cornbread are total heaven!

Sit in Frances section! She loves her customers and she's the nicest server you will ever meet. She will remember you the next time you come in too!
Awesome southern meat and three. We've eaten here several times and have never even disappointed. The food has always been fresh and delicious, and the service has always been friendly and prompt.
vC2Yt0Cdm5x0K-a78vQU8g: What a great experience.  I was thrilled with the unique venue and the quality displayed in every aspect.  Highly recommend!

Went here for lunch with my sister-in-law.  She had 12:30 reservations.  We walked in and the hostess said our table wasn't ready yet and invited us to wait at the bar and have a drink.  Very odd, as this exact scenario happened at Kayne Prime the night before!!!!  Is this a new Nashville MO?  I looked around and the restaurant was not even half full, all tables set . . . REALLY!?!?!?!?!?!  Even though I didn't make the reservation, I piped up that we didn't want a drink at the bar and I didn't see why the table wouldn't be ready.  They sat us immediately.  Funny how that worked, huh!?!  Walking by the seafood bar, the shrimp looked huge and amazing.  Needless to say, we got shrimp cocktail; delicious!  Their burger was also very good.  Our server was particularly engaging and delightful.  The lunch prices were not too outrageous.  The décor was a throwback to the speakeasies of 20's or 30's, opulent.  Will be back to check out the evening music and drinks.

From start to finish-- impeccable!!
Amazing! Our number one steak house!!! A MUST if you're in Nashville!!
RWpMHiUl5YPaw8X7OT4vgg: Very underrated. This location is almost like a hidden gem. Not many people visit this one. Food is great. Super quick. Could be a little more friendlier but to be expected most of the staff is high school aged or college students in for the summer.

Think Subway, but for pizza. You pick from four crusts (including a gluten-free option), different types of sauces, different cheese (including vegan cheese), and multiple toppings. Each pizza is a "personal" size and they cook it while you check out.

I went just a couple of days after this location opened. Food was good and the place was nice, but they still seemed to be working out the kinks on their serving line and, in my opinion, didn't have the best people on it. If I had a place like this, I'd get people who could speak up and seemed glad to be there. None of the employees were rude and one was very nice, but I can't decode mumbling.

GREAT PIZZA PIE !!   Wife and stopped into Pie Five in Melrose and loved the food and the whole vibe of the place.   
I had the Five star and she had a custom pie with olive oil and chicken and veggies.   We had our pizza in our hands in no time and it was delicious.    Staff was nice and were eager to please.   We will be back.   I can see this place being a "once a week stop" for us.   Keep it up.  
Glad a nice pizza place like this is in the neighborhood !!
lndVYqfCgFfclrmo2BN6LA: This was my first time visiting this location and I already wish I lived close by to it. We came at 6:30am and they have been non stop busy since we got here (its now 7:30). They keep the line going fast and have a great routine going on behind the counter and get the drinks out fast. I'm impressed and again, really wish this was the Starbucks in my neighbrhood or even my town. Deborah is very kind and makes a point to make conversation with customers while working very obviously hard behind the counter. Andrew is also nice as we sit at the counter he makes sure to make conversation with us and others while working. James took our orders and was conversational, kind, and made a point to get our names and call us by our names. This is a great starbucks location with great workers!

Love this Starbucks!
Definitely one of my favorite locations.
The Customer Service is great.
Employees are very friendly, super fast & efficient, even the manager too. 
Sometimes I'd have to wait in long lines.
Depending on what time of day I go.
But it was definitely worth the wait.
They take time & care when taking my order.
It's been almost a year since I've been by there.
I'll definitely be going back there for sure.

This Starbucks is probably my favorite.  Even though they don't have a drive thru like the West End one, there is always parking available and the line is never too long.  Plus, they have some cool seating with bar tables, short tables, armchairs, and booths.  Its a nice place to sit and talk or work.
uXKygoHoGly8OqINLCFbgQ: Now being from Chicago, we have pretty darn good restaurants!! Most cities don't come close....UNIL we went to Demo's in Nashville! My husband had the sirloin and I had a rib eye. These were the best steaks we have ever had. Pasta and broccoli sides were fresh and delicious. I hope we get a chance to go back to Demo's before we have to head home.

Great Italian food. Reasonable food prices. Drinks were a little pricey. Server was extremely friendly. Highly recommend the lasagna, chicken fettuccini and fettuccini imperial. Rolls are to die for. We could not believe how fast the food came out. This is a must visit in Nashville.

Visiting from Chicago with some girlfriends for memorial day weekend, we made sure to hit demos for an early dinner one evening. I was very impressed with the food- I had the seafood linguine with a salad and the house made Greek dressing. It was fantastic and the bread was amazing. They sure don't hold back on the amount of crab and shrimp in the dish!  I'd go back in a heart beat. Best part was getting my bill and it was less than $11, great value, great place!
xwjUyOwIQuFNaBCxhedwLA:
I've been here a few different times and have yet to be disappointed!  I've had the brisket tacos, pulled pork sandwich,  and chicken sandwich,  and would gladly recommend any of those! 

Every time I've been,  I always miss lunch.  And they have certain items on their menu that are only offered during lunch. Like the Tuck Special. I wanted that sandwich since the first time I laid eyes on it on the menu.  

And FINALLY. The last time I went. ..I made it just before lunch was over! I was finally able to order the Tuck special. 

It was beautiful. Actually it was really messy with egg and pimento cheese  dripping everywhere and pork falling off the buns. It was probably like 1000 calories,  but worth every bit of it. I still dream about that sandwich.

Wow, best brisket ever!!  Seriously I lived in KC for 9 years and had great BBQ, and I am from NC where we had good BBQ as well, but the brisket here is awesome!  Pork is also great, and the sides are fantastic as well.  You MUST give this a try!
I've eaten here twice in the last week and it was fantastic both times.  So far it is my favorite BBQ in the Nashville area.  Generous portions of fantastic BBQ.  You won't be disappointed!
x4p11p1v5cXJ4waelkHeFg: Great burger joint with a full bar!  Loved the craft beer options.  El Diablo burger set my mouth on fire and I wanted more!

The atmosphere is very appropriate for the setting. Being a college town Hopdoddy Burger Bar, is a festive place for great burgers and craft beers. We stopped in with our son, coming home from vacation. We were greeted right away and assigned a table. Burgers and drinks were ordered and brought to our table while the food was hot. I had the classic burger and my wife ordered the, Goodnight Good Cause burger! Bother we're very tasty and with a side order of Sweet Potatoes fries we were comfortably filled. I ordered the Bearded Iris beer, and found this beer to be refreshing and a perfect mate for the burger.

Stopped for beers with a friend. A hip joint to stop for a bit to eat in a nice part of town. Good selection of local beers in a challace. It's like the Buffalo Wild Wings of burgers but likely without the stomach cramps in the middle of the night. Wish I could have tried their burgers and sauces maybe next time I'm in Nashville area on vacay.
6ecp3KLH7IRUC7muZih05g: Friendly, fast, BIG tacos. Yucca fries and sauce were excellent. Basil lemonade: yes!

So went with coworkers because we'd been wanting to try it and we will go back. I ordered the Discada, the Pollo Asada and the Carne Asada with a side of black beans and got an extra side because I wanted to try the rice. My rice got left out of my to go order so I didn't get to try that but the black beans were great. The portion size of the tacos is awesome and beats any place around. The Discada Taco is AMAZING! I might get three of just that one next time. I was warned about the Pollo Asado and the Carne Asada needing lots of sauce and that was really accurate. They both need some sort of maybe mayo based sauce with some flavor because they are really lacking and I put as much lime juice and salsa verde on it as I could just couldn't fix it. My boss had the Mahi Mahi and Grilled veggie tacos and loved them.

I was visiting Nashville. Looking for a local highly recommend spot for lunch. This place was spot one. The tacos were excellent; I got the Mahi Mahi. The sauces were as hot or mild based on what you like. A very small joint that I would highly recommend.
VAeEXLbEcI9Emt9KGYq9aA: The burger was amazing! Very nice beer garden outside with a great selection. The line for us wasn't super long, will definitely be going back.

We came to check out The Pharmacy after the Nashville Marathon and waited 1 hr 30 minutes to be seated. Realistically, we knew we were going to have to wait in line but 1 hr and 30minutes? HOT DAMN. If our Uber driver hadn't recommended this place, we would not have come. The goal was to find a burger joint that offered whiskey battered french fries. BUT once I took bite of The Farm Burger and sweet potato fries time became irrelevant, countless, meaningless to me. The best way to describe the feeling I had when eating this burger is when you come home from work, walk into the kitchen and smell your mom's spices, steak, and bountiful Spanish rice. The aroma and homecooked meal makes you forget about everything else. The Farm Burger had that affect on me. 
I would wait 1.5 hours anytime for those burgers and sweet potato fries. 

Side Note:The patio is softly charming. They have picnic tables for large groups and intimate seating near the bar. There was only one bartender at the patio bar and we had to wait 20 minutes for a drink. No bueno!
Surprisingly good burgers! Juicy patties in yummy buns. The place is so popular. I've been there twice, but each time we had to wait about an hour. But, the food was definitely worth the wait.
ugqdONU8skxNOIUeuh7eNw:
Breakfast. Diner. Eggs. Plastic Cafeteria style plates. 

You can not beat these prices for breakfast!! 2 eggs, bacon, hashbrowns, toast $5.00. This place is old, but clean. The chairs are worn, but the food is good. There are old 8 x 10 country stars photos on the walls and the same old ladies working there. Everyone is super southern and I'm okay with that. I got an veggie omlet and grits and OJ and my total was $10. Can't beat it and filled me up all day. 

If you're just a plain and simple traditional diner style breakfast with a okay food go here. 

Breakfast is weekdays from 8:30am -11am, then 11a-2p is lunch. I think on the weekends breakfast last longer. Call em. They do take credit card. 

I'll be back, I love a good plain jane old school diner. This one has been there forever, lets keep it alive! eat local

This unpretentious little gem is not your going to be on your gourmet list. It is going to be a much better alternative to mc d's or ihop and better than waiting in line somewhere for a waitress with an attitude. It's nice people, fast service and biscuits so fluffy it'll make you wanna slap yo momma.

Mmmm, a hearty breakfast. We are from central Illinois but live here in Nashville, and this restaurant made us feel at home! Since being in Nashville, we've yet to find a laid back diner where you can get biscuits and gravy with your meat and eggs!

Extremely affordable for those on a budget. This place is not "trendy," but it's a warm, inviting breakfast stop. We experienced warm and friendly service as well. Highly recommend!
gHtOMNxliyEIqXQMoDLxww:
My daughter and I drove from Southern California this past Summer to Boston. As we were relocating, my wife chose to fly ahead and set up our new home, while my 8 year old daughter and l would have the ultimate adventure. Well, our 11 day drive took us to many hotels, most were average, but then there is the Renaissance in Nashville. This was far from "average" and resided more in the area of "true excellence." First, the location is approximately a quarter mile from the legendary Broadway Avenue, and walking down toward it you walk directly pass the old Ryman Auditorium. 

The location was one thing, but if you didn't choose to be a tourist and simply not leave the hotel, that would also be fine. The service was out of this world. We did not eat in any of the hotel restaurants but they certainly looked very inviting. One night we ordered room service, and honesly I have never had better. The food was actually hot, and the bellman apologized several times that it took longer than 30 minutes. The reality was the food arrived in 35 minutes. Yes, I timed it as the hotel was on such a roll, I wanted to see how quick they were with the room service thing. There is a very nice pool and fitness center that we enjoyed a couple of times as well. The pool is kind of small, but that was mostly my daughter's complaint. This hotel is not cheap, but when you get a quality product the negatives about cost kind of don't matter when you are having fun. I would definitely stay here again when I find myself in Music City.

One of my favorite hotel experiences I've had in a while. Considering the experience of Nashville itself, I was highly impressed and pleased with everything this place had to offer. 
Despite the fact this trip was for business, our package, which I highly recommend, made our stay even more pleasant. We paid the valet parking fee. Good thing because it rained all weekend. When we were leaving, we called valet and our car was ready with the offering hands of valet workers packing my bag. Arriving and departing. A+
When we arrived our first day, my body wash spilt every where in my bag. Keep in mind- I only brought one outfit. I hand rinsed everything in the hotel sink. If you have ever dealt with bubbles, you know it feels never ending. I called housekeeping to ask to dry my clothing. They picked up and return my outfit. Killer. 
That night it began to rain and didn't bring my umbrella for our walk down to Margaritaville. As my women instinct of complaining kicked in, the valet man outside asked if we would like a ride to our location. Complimentary bus trips?  Not sure but this guy did and it was much appreciated. 
The hotel itself of modernized and chic. Our beds and the most importantly the pillows were to die for. Everything and everywhere clean. The rooms even came with a charging port for phones, etc. 
Walking distance from the bars. An easy walk at that.
The hotel was nice.  Great location to restaurants, bars, and sporting events!  Construction is happening next to the property and work starts early during the week so NO sleeping in.
qY4YoWlEn8zB1Ho-DOTQVw: Inexpensive, friendly service, great and cozy environment, plus amazingly delicious chicken equals a totally awesome place to eat. We were close by and wanted to check it out and we were sure glad we did. Little tip: they are super busy at lunch time, so be sure to plan your visit appropriately.

(1) FOOD: Great chicken tenders and great sauces for either dipping and/or tossing. I really like the Honey Bee and Ole Smokey sauces. The fries are also great, which is key! YUM!

(2) AMBIANCE: Americana and local school/sports flare dominate the decor. In some ways eating at McDougals feels like a picnic, which is fitting. Great casual place to grab a bite with friends.

(3) PRICE/VALUE: It feels a bit pricey, but the tenders are TOO GOOD. So of course the price is worth it. Thus, the overall value is: GOOD.

(4) SERVICE: Sometimes this place can feel a bit rushed and impersonal during peak hours, but otherwise the service is great and the workers quite friendly.

OVERALL: YOU MUST EAT HERE OMG

If you think that chicken fingers are just for kids, think again! McDougal's serves up pieces of chicken so good that people of all ages flock here to get in on the poultry action.

I think the secret to McDougal's is that when it comes to the fingers, they use 100% breast meat, nothing that's been reconstituted. Whether you get your fingers fried or grilled, you are guaranteed to get tasty, fresh and juicy chicken that will have you craving more. There are also plenty of sauces available for you to gussy up your meal, from ranch and blue cheese, to chipotle barbeque and hotter buffalo-style hot sauces as well. They're all good, though I admit a personal preference for the ranch and honey-mustard myself. Get yourself a combo basket (three fingers are plenty! The portions are really huge!) and your chicken will come with fresh-cut shoestring potato fries and a slice of Texas toast as well. Another thing I like to indulge in occasion when I can resist the siren song of straight-up chicken fingers is the chicken finger sandwich, which is really nice because it comes on the grilled Texas toast and can be topped with tomatoes, lettuce, mayo and mustard (and any combination thereof). 

To top everything off, every meal comes with a free cup of soft-serve ice cream at the end. The portion isn't huge, but after filling your belly with all the delicious chicken, you won't have room for much anyway! Plus, it's free, so what are you complaining about?!?

The Coop is no-frills eating at its best. Probably the only thing to complain about is that parking on Belcourt Ave is generally a bit of a nightmare, but I think there is a lot around back. The atmosphere is super chilled out, and this place is truly at its peak during the summer months when you can dine al fresco on their big wooden deck and enjoy the weather. This is a fun place where the simple, delicious food really shines. If you're craving fried chicken in Nashville, few places will do it better than this.
cAAtoYUKC_Z8AmSF95mjcg: Loooove to get my authentic New York pizza right here at home!! Staff is excellent and the pizza ROCKS!!!
Best pizza I've had in Nashville. Loved the Kitty and the vegan pizza. The meatball sliders are also delicious. Quality ingredients and friendly service.
Late night stop after a night on the town. Pizza was yummy, not super memorable. Maybe it's cause I drank too much.  Cool place though near our hotel.
k7o9D7XQ0eGuz3nmKB1wXg:
Well, I came here for new year's so this place was packed! It was really hard to move around, but I can't blame that on the bar itself. 
What I really liked was the coat check by the front door. Most places don't have that and since it's winter right now, I'm definitely wearing one outside. However, I hate having to keep track of it inside, especially if I'm having a few too many drinks. 
The dance area is not too big, but decent size. The DJ was good the night I went there, and definitely had the crowd going. 
I will stop by again when I'm in Nashville.

Although located in Tourist Trap Nashville, Mcfaddens has really been stepping it up and as a Nashville local, it deserves mention. There are three parts to McFadden's in my eye and it caters to all types. The promoting at McFadden's is great and they actually do what they say unlike some other clubs in which I have given a negative mention.

By this I mean, if they say Tucker Max from I Hope They Serve  Beer In Hell is there to sign books, he is there to sign books. They have multiple celebrities in and out and if you connect with one of the promotors on facebook, your cover is FREE. If you are interested in what is going on, look for Seth Alexander on facebook and he will hook you up. 

The general manager, Randy, is awesome and will do anything to make his customers happy down to a cheeseburger not being cooked to the customer's liking or to a drink not tasting right. His spirit has def. perserved this place and kept it apart from many other clubs.

I usually try avoid 2nd street all together as it's usually infested by 18 year olds fighting to get into Graham Central Station or by cougars circling the area around Coyote Ugly, but alas a friend's phone call lured me to McFadden's. Though I would say that this is usually not the place that I would pick out for a night out, I actually had a great time. I was there with a bunch of friends, and did the Cupid's Shuffle with a bunch of strangers. Though I could do without the obnoxious men trolling for drunk girls, I dug the DJs picks and the crazy people dancing on the bar. It's a decent sized place, and I think a good choice if you are looking for a place to dance and drink. Check out their happy hour parties (if you bring pre arrange a group coming in you get drink specials before 11pm).
QL4C7sd9PFUOGGpHwe1dlQ: We had a show at Bridgestone on a Friday night.  It was an hour and half before start so we needed something close. The Palms, which is next door, had a ridiculous wait(for no reservations).  So we decided to go here since it didn't look to busy.  The restaurant itself had a great atmosphere and was overall a really nice restaurant.  We sat outside on the patio, which was nice.  We ordered the fried calamari and tuna tartare for our appetizers, both were very delicious.  There was also a tasting of eggplant courtesy of the chef.  it was paired with one piece of bread of your choice, which I thought that was odd. Anywho, for our entrees, we kept it simple, a couple of pizzas and the mushroom razioli. All were good, but not amazing.  Especially for the cost, which was a little overpriced.  But I would go again and maybe order something other than pizza, which you can get anywhere.  Service was good, food and ticket took just a little bit long as it was just 4 of us. But overall, good experience.

Delicious food, gracious service. I liked the lunch special which allowed you to get a salad, soup, or an appetizer. My sea bass with creamy polenta entree was great. This restaurant establishes my respect for the foodie scene in Nashville!
Amazing.  Spent time in Italy this summer and the food here is just like what we found in Florence. Great service. Highly recommend.
Ju25XEGy_NWi9heJX3FuZw: Usually went here for special occasions and was never disappointed. When Slow Bar was across the street we went to Margot for pre-show drinks. The menu constantly changes with fresh ideas which never let you down...

What a great little place! I should start by saying our waitress was very knowledgeable of the drinks and menu she was able to answer every question we had and made great suggestions. I could tell she was a pro! 

The menu changes a lot which is a great idea you always have to try something new. After a long look at the amazing choices on the menu I decided on the duck breast which was prepared to perfection. Perfectly seared! I had a dream about it last night:) can't wait to get back and try something else. Do yourself a favor and come to Margot!

This place was perfect--everything we wanted while visiting Nashville and celebrating our anniversary.  The ambiance was lovely...nice and dark with romantic decor (small fine china plates, bud vase with a flower, etc).  The meal started with warm bread.  They make foccacia in house and it was super tasty.  We ordered a bottle of wine and then I got the seafood pasta and my husband got the pork tenderlion with brussel sprouts and a sweet corn hash.  We cleaned our plates.  Everything was delicious and we'll be back next time we're in town.
EfqMKBHZ3f1Fetrf9KjY3g:
This is a great BBQ joint, and I love their atmosphere. 

As a mom of 5 kids, this is definitely a family joint. Whenever we come in, the wait staff and host staff always are sure to high-five the kiddos and, on the hotter days, it has not been unheard of for them to bring the kids ice water while we wait outside. 

Although the place is always hopping, we have yet to be passed over in our place in line to seat others more readily (which has often  happened as a family of 7). I mean, I know it's easier to seat two 4-tops and hack  off your list, but really??? We have never EVER had this problem at Jim N Nicks. 

The staff is always bubbly and ready to do whatever they can to make your visit memorable. I mean, I truly doubt anyone who works there has a 'bad day'.

Ok, food talk: 
We love the onion rings (light, crunchy, and sweet). with a mix of ranch and bbq.
beef brisket BBQ sandwich - YUM! It is sweet, smokey, heavy, and filling. My hubby and I always split this.
Our kids go bonkers for the chicken tenders, and understandably so as they are thick and juicy

Finally, the cheese biscuits and lemonade are one-of-a-kind and always available to sate your appetite (and keep you refreshed) until your meal arrives. Man, I am missing summer now!

The family and I meet here after getting a recommendation from the hotel.  The staff were very friendly and we got seated right away, although it was on a Monday. The biscuits were awesome, and I wish I had brought some of the mix to take home.  I had the brisket and hot links.  The brisket was very good and had a great flavor, the hot links were also good, but were over cooked.  I tasted my sons Turkey and it was also very good and tender with great seasoning.  We really enjoyed our meal, and the pricing was reasonable

I've been to the one in Smryna and honestly thought it was a local chain. I'm breaking my own rule of limiting chain restaurants to three stars no matter how good. I honestly had no idea it was a chain-it seemed like just a local BBQ spot with a few locations in the Nashville area. My gf and I were the first to arrive for dinner and since we were both starving ordered the queso and chips which were good-I only mention it because it's usually a throwaway item on most menus but this one was good. I ordered the 2 meat combo with the brisket and pulled pork with the mac and cheese and got the fried green tomato (the veggie of the day.) I've had the brisket before which is probably some of the best I've had and the pulled pork was also phenomenal. You know what stuck out the most though? The mac and cheese, it was so unbelievably good. The FGT though was also nothing to sneeze at, a great authentic side dish. 

My gf got the 4 bones ribs plate and she also got the mac and cheese and the mashed potatoes. She ate through the ribs-before she got full but ate all the M&C since she thought it was so good too and ate all but 1 of the ribs. No sauce necessary just a nicely smoked, slow cooked piece of rib. It may not have been the best place to introduce here to authentic TN bbq but it was an tasty one.
d4GRmLdkPIsJBeOYE7fuqA:
You know, I think the last time I went to Panera was around a 1.5 years ago... maybe longer? There are just so many great spots in town to get a sammich, salad, and soup so I put 'ol Panera on the backburner. 

However, thanks to our chilly and stormy weather of the last 2 days, I was feeling a bit apprehensive about which of the following:

A. Leaving my bed
B. Getting out of my car to face the cold wet precipitation 
C. Wearing head to to flannel (i.e. I was wearing my damn pajamas outside of my home - but I had on a long winter coat!)
D. All of the above

If you said, you're likely a brilliant mind and have earned the spot of being my one call if I was ever in a pinch during my appearance as a contestant on a gameshow that allows me to do so. Just sayin'. 

I felt a bit guilty since I'm hypersensitive about supporting local businesses year round and we're right in the middle of this Yelp Shops Local campaign, but this broad was craving som comforting soup and a little carbs. I was bored with what was in my pantry and I happened to be nearby at the Post Office to use the drive-up mailbox since apparently my mail carrier hasn't grasped the concept of picking up my mail but instead shoves my magazines and other parcel on top... sorry, that is a whole other Yelp review. 

So. I gave in. I pulled up in the drive-thru line as apparently I shared the same sentiment as everyone else in Nashville and I waited. Anyone, who knows me will laugh at this because I become a complete brat when it comes to lines and am overall, I'm not exactly known for my patience.... 

Question 2: So what do I suck up and do it for? 

Answer: A Mediterranean Veggie Sandwich, Iced Green Tea, and a cup of theBlack Bean Soup (along with a bag of chips and chunk of bread that came with my order)

Question 3: And, you know what? 

Answer: It was damn tasty! I enjoyed each and every last bite. 

The experience as a whole was very positive. From the drive-thru line moving efficiently to the extremely friendly (but not in the annoying way) drive-thru service, this location is very good at what they do. I'm appreciative of that and also due to the fact that I didn't even have to change out of my PJs to get a healthy and delicious meal. 

While I won't be a regular here or at any other chains, if I do get the urge, I'll be sure to come back to this location.
The restaurant had friendly service and good food. Free WiFi was a plus also, location is a big bonus.

I love that this location has a drive-thru. I can swing through & grab fresh delicious food.  Nothing like wholesome options on days when I don't have time to cook... Considering the other reviews, the drive-thru might be the best option because I don't have to deal with parking... :-)
KGYQFUSqOFMj1T65cBcGgA: Absolute best experience I have ever had eating out in my life!  I DEFINITELY recommend this place out of anywhere!  Their food is phenomenal, service was awesome, prices are great, and they have the best management I have ever dealt with!

i love chuy's, a lot. the service, the atmoshphere, the food. we were there president's day weekend, and thought they were super packed, we still had very friendly service. the jalapeano cream/ranch type dip is amazing, ask for it! i always get the southwestern enchilada, and it's always amazing. the salsa is amazing, the chips thin, and wonderful (i hate when salsa is amazing, but the chips are thick, and the chip takes up more space in your mouth then the yummy salsa). we were able to step back into the mall to look at the nearby shops during our 45 minute wait which made it less painful.  overall, i'm a fan of chuys, every location i've been too has been great.
Greatest date night place. Mary Beth is amazing!! The best service ever. We hadn't been in a while. She still remembered us and provided the same great service.
BxedsINk4Kp8Hu7EoolOfQ: Good place for lunch - sandwiches, etc..  smoking and non-smoking sections on separate floors
Divey and like a good old neighborhood sports bar. Great prices and pretty fair food too.

*****This Place Is Now Closed*****

Bought a Living Social deal for this place and when we arrived we were told they had no food. This was odd to here as its a sports bar. We were told the night that we came, was the last night this place would be open so they had gotten rid of all the food and had just made sandwiches which were free. This was a real bummer as I was really looking forward to some unhealthy greasy bar food. Apparently the owner was closing due to health issue and they would not be relocating. I still give this place a 5 star though my visit was extremely brief. I left my phone here and a patron was nice enough to answer my phone later after I had realized this and gave it to an employee. I went back and staff was very nice and we even got a tour of the upstairs which was no longer in use. The people here were very nice and it's a shame to see a place like this go out of business. This was a real bar. Not clean but not dirty, dim lit and people just drinking and talking. I will miss that place. The last day/night it was open was 10/15/14.
Fd-CY5oUjewSTawi-O5Bqw: I can honestly say probably the best Chinese I have ever had.  The crab Rangoon, omg .  I usually get that the lo me in.  Always delicious and always a ton of food and they deliver!!

I've eaten here twice. I am thought my choices were very tasty. Shrimp lo mein was delicious. Fried rice was great. Delivery both times was great and very quick. Egg rolls were average. My only complaint is that you have to request condiments by selecting it on the menu. I didn't do that the first time and got 1 soy sauce. Fortunately I had a stash from another restaurant. That's no it a deal breaker though. I plan to order regularly when I am too busy to go out. Better than having to give up my cheat day because of having pizza!

I love this place. We live and Brentioch and order from here all the time. They are always so happy to hear from us. I think it's a sweet family run business. 

They're delivery is fast and friendly, but we are always happy to pick up because we just enjoy the people at the restaurant. 

I have always recommended this place and I'll continue to do so. 

Thanks a lot guys!
nlOC7iDnF8me-BT-SpA86w:
We had a party of 4 that luckily snagged reservations on their first night. I absolutely love the interior. It's gorgeous, modern, and left me feeling like I was in a NY or Vegas restaurant (the light fixtures..omg). Plus, I am SO glad they aren't doing the farm to table thing and there wasn't a flannel shirt in sight. Yes!

We had a very tough time deciding on what to order, but started small with the cocktail list. I had the honey and thyme with gin and it was absolutely delicious. The jimberly and southern belle were hits at the table as well. However, be prepared that they are $13.00 each, but also very well made. 

Our server was sure to tell us about the appetizers and sold us on what he called "an appetizer platter" (whatever you do, DON'T order this, but more later) which included short ribs, quail, octopus, and sausage. I also had to try the spicy lobster gnocchi. The appetizers were all good minus the octopus. No one liked it; it was cold and rubbery and we didn't even finish it all. But the quail...wow. Probably my favorite from the appetizers. 

They were out of the mushrooms and trout for the evening, so we all ended up ordering red meat; steak and the lamb osso bucco. All of them were great, but the sides were really outstanding. The crispy sweet potatoes I couldn't stop eating and I didn't even order them! But I can speak highly of the foie gras home fries with garlic mayo and grilled brussels were extremely good as well. All around a spectacular meal. 

With one big problem: 

Remember that appetizer platter that was sold to us at the beginning of the meal? They charged us for each individual item, and the sausage alone was $25. That's right. the cost of an entree. The grand total for our appetizer platter of 4 items (one of which we didn't eat) SEVENTY dollars. You read that correctly $70.00! OUCH. We felt completely duped. In fact, we didn't stop talking about it the rest of the evening. We talked to our waiter and he apologized but then made the fantastic comment: "We were told not to say the price." Baffled, we asked for the octopus to be taken off. He took them off without any push back, so that was nice, but we still were in shock about our bill. Ridiculous. 

So. Prima is great, but whatever you do, don't order the appetizer "platter" and make sure you know the price before ordering!

Boys night out, hard to believe this place just opened as they are in a groove already.  Great location in Da' Gulch; if you live in the building thank whoever brought you Prima!

Appetizers
Lobster gnocchi was a nice play on mac n cheese. Just enough lobster to make ya happy. The gnocchi is really a pasta shaped like gnocchi so don't expect the potato texture to melt in your mouth; it's semolina based made inhouse.

Octopus was cooked to perfection with just enough kick to make you notice it but not so much that it destroyed the plate. I could have ate the other seven tentacles.

Main Course
We both ordered the lamb osso bucco. If you're Italian you know it's either HIT or MISS; there is no in between. This plate was a lil' bingo. The waiter had me worried when he brought out the knives; thankfully we didn't need them. If it was Nonna's house I'd be sucking the marrow out of the bone but this ain't the place. Spinach side was perfect. 

Dessert
I had a macchiato and the espresso was very good. They use coffee from Crema also downtown. We whacked up a pistachio thingy with white chocolate, vanilla mousse, and a cookie bottom. Great with the coffee. 

Wine List
I bought my own because the list wasn't available online but they have some fairly priced selections from all over the globe that will certainly continue to grow. 

Service
Spectacular and they are open a week. Very knowledgeable staff who were in total sync. Black or White napkins sir? Complimentary bottled water? First impressions! Some delays and gaps in the timing but that was expected for a new restaurant and were minor. Kudos to my paisane Angelo who is the GM we remembered from Kayne Prime. This guy is a pro.

Cost  was under $70 a person without drinks (soup to nuts) which is more than reasonable for this caliber of dining experience. - YOU GET WHAT YOU PAY FOR. The only negative - the decor is well done but the high ceilings remove some of the ambiance. Sinatra sounds like he's singing in Hoboken. The huge chandelier helps. The bar should be closer to the front door. Just sayin'

Observation not directed at Prima specifically
Maybe I eat out too much but the menus are becoming repetitive in Nashville.The menu choices are very limited and basically the same in every restaurant but with the 'chef's take'. I miss the list of 'specials' in New Jersey restaurants. If you visit a Nashville restaurant 3-4 times you basically ran the menu. Now what? Start eating the same thing over and over or look for a new restaurant?  Perhaps it's all geared towards tourists? I dunno. Hence no 5 star ratings yet.

Dined again with the same group as the 1st visit and we all agreed food was top notch. Service is a little clunky, not horrendous, but needs tweaking.
There are no table clothes and our drinks were served without napkins so we end up having a wet table, we wiped down with our napkins but this shouldn't be the case.
Appetizer / share courses were served without additionl plates, one dish had crusty bread, you either have to use the plate the appetizer came on or drop your crumbs on the table.
Desert came out well before the coffee / cappuccino that was ordered, someone has to get on top of these details but from previous reviews seems like it is getting better.
WjmczOJVjnU6zIrCvdqptA: We ate here the other night and had the cheese fritters and the fil a ! It was incredible! Cozy little spot too! The asparagus and au gratin potatoes were to die for!!! We will be frequent flyers here!

The Yellow Porch has been a trusty "go-to" for years and countless meals. I never leave disappointed or feeling mediocre about the experience. Consistent, delicious & amazing service. One of Nashville's best, intimate dinner spots in my opinion.

I ate here several month ago on a low-key date night. Nothing stood out about this place as superb, but I think sometimes that's nice. Everything about the night was nice, with no flair to get in the way.

The atmosphere is nice and works for anything. On one side our table was a woman enjoying a book and a glass of wine by herself, on the other was a group of eight, and all of us got exactly what we needed. The service was great, food was good (though I really don't remember what I had). We had gift card from some friends, but the prices were such that I would have been more than happy to pay for the meal myself.

So when I say "nothing stood out about this place," it's not because of anything negative. It's just the opposite - it's because nothing got in the way of a great night. It's now the kind of restaurant I recommend anytime I drive by with someone, though I really don't have anything specific to say about it.
in9VTpryqIEUxBsNehMpJA: Simply amazing. From amazing food to wonderful service to an incredibly comfortable atmosphere, our first visit to Best of India was something worth sharing. Best Indian food we have ever had. We left more than happy and with big ole smiles on our faces. Thank you! Definitely our new go to!

Have been coming to this place for years. The food is always fresh and delicious. They have changed the decor and it looks really nice and traditional. Really has the Indian vibe to it. The wait staff is very nice and accommodating. They are always on the lookout to see if our glasses need to be filled. I usually come for the buffet during my lunch break and it is hands down the best Indian buffet in town. Every dish is very flavorful and delicious!

We stumbled upon this place on accident and stayed based on the positive Yelp reviews. We love Indian food so we gave it a try. The service was a bit slow, but they were very very friendly. The food was DELICIOUS. We had the meat samosas and a lamb and rice dish. I highly recommended!
sBREjOvYJ0xI259WU-j5CA: The fox is officially my new favorite bar! I have now been twice on different evenings, one quiet evening and one very busy Friday. On both nights the staff were so lovely, and the drinks were just as delicious even when the bartenders were very busy. Also the selection of vegan cheeses on the charcuterie platters made me so happy. Both the kitchen and bar staff really know their stuff.

This place is amazing. The vibe is great, it's small with just enough seating. The staff is super helpful and friendly and the drinks are so delicious. I definitely recommend stopping by this joint :) 

Also the menu is super rad.

great place. great vibe. great hangs.
owners are awesome and super nice
can't go wrong with mickeys and the fox
Uekr58xgdLusfSRGiTk-PQ: Great food, takeout may take a while - among many anxious eaters.. Best to call in first,  but they usually get the "specific" orders right!

Three words: YaYa's Chocolate Cake.  I shared a piece of cake with some girlfriends.  There was no polite leaving the last bite...more of a stabbing you with my fork so I can get more before you can.  In fact, we had to buy a second piece because one simply was not enough.  The cake is light and moist but full of perfect chocolately flavor.  And the frosting!  Plus, they make it fresh every day, so we happened to catch a warm piece coming out of the oven.  Heaven.  I wanted to give YaYa a big thank you hug after eating that cake.  

I didn't try anything else on the menu, but they have a healthful menu with fresh ingredients at wallet friendly prices.  Ah, did I mention YaYa's cake?  You should probably get a piece...or a whole sheet pan.

Always a classic go-to for when I'm in the area. Food is always consistent and the service is very friendly. I usually go during lunch hour and the food comes out exceptionally fast. I usually get the chicken skewers and Greek salad. I highly recommend it if you are looking for quick and healthy meal.
SUL_XDzGnnccUgHqnix1Ng: Honestly wasn't really hungry when we went here but they gave us the options for a la carte sliders for $4 so of course I had to get it. I got the Boursin mushroom bacon slider and it was so delicious I almost wished I got 4 sliders. Even their bloody Mary's had a slider. Good prices and fulfilling food. Two thumbs up.

This may just be the best burger I've had in Nashville.  The Slider House would receive a perfect 5 stars if they weren't out of so many beers after only being open for 4 days and their beer prices weren't so high ($6 for a very average can of beer), but as far as burgers go, it doesn't get any better than this.

I have to say when this place first opened I thought, how will this work? Just sliders? Won't Soulshine take all the business mostly? ummmm how I was wrong. This place is seriously good!!! The inside is really nicely spaced, plenty of tables and waitress service and  very very clean. There is also a bar if you want to cozy up there. 

I have eaten here mid day and late night and it always hits my craving. In fact I love to just grab some drinks and sit out on there patio during warmer weather, even if I"m not in the slider mood. Sure beats trying to find patio space at Red Door when all the college kids crowd it. 

But onto the Sliders cause they are seriously good. Whoever thought of this menu did a great job because it has something for everyone! Beef lovers, chicken lovers, Turkey, pork, veggie/non beef lovers etc. Regular fries, and sweet potato fries my favorite! You can even order a side salad to make you feel less guilty. At least you had some greens :) (that's what I tell myself)

My favorites are hands down The Southern Comfort (chicken), fried chicken with a siracha honey glaze. Yeah tell me that won't help cure any ailments from the night before or help coat it for the night ahead?! I get it almost every time. I also love the Muchacho and Old Hickory, even tried the special Jack FM one this past Saturday (at not so early an hour). All are really good and full of flavor. If you are in the mood for burgers and fries and a great beer list all served in cans (so fun), don't go to your average run of the mill joint...go to slider house. They even let you mix and match so you can try them all!
zyPz0xvE5XkeCVH2J7g2gw: So glad this place popped up right beside First Tennessee Park. It was very much needed. Really enjoyed the atmosphere, food & beer. Derrick took great care of us at the bar!!
Solid atmosphere and great food. The wait staff is very knowledgeable about their food. Authentic German beer hall atmosphere is a trendy area.

First time here. Asked them to put the Cubs game on tv for me and they gladly accommodated me. The service is great...very friendly staff. As a Nashville beer buyer myself I'd have a few suggestions for them, but overall the food we have had and the beer we have tried is great. Bring in New Heights and Diskin Cider, please!
D4UMA28VYhKUU59KXZTiQQ:
We came here after seeing it featured on Anthony Bourdain's Parts Unknown that aired on Oct 2.  Came on Thursday, we got Barbequed Ribs and Fried Pork Chops with turnip greens, green beans, mac n cheese, stuffing and corn bread. All were delicious, including the peach cobbler for dessert and the lemonades (regular and strawberry).  

It's clearly a local place when we got there around 11:30am, with lots of working men in the area. Got busy with a line out the door right around noon, with some tourists in the mix. It never got so crowded though that you couldn't find a table and the line went  fast.
Cafeteria style set up. Homemade good food. Mostly locals. Inexpensive. Don't expect gourmet, expect your Mama's cookin'
This place is better than Swetts. I had the Mac and cheese, collard greens, cornbread and ribs. Tons of food. Won't go away hungry and great service.  Recommend this place to all.
h7B28RLjcr9AcSMfqBuvHQ: Fried Green Tomatoes and PBJ!  Freaking awesome!  Late lunch and didn't have a wait.  Friendly service!

House is a charming little bar/restaurant that was conveniently located by our hotel in west Nashville. For starters, I don't know if a "social eatery" is the best way to describe this place. We were expecting more family style seating, but the layout was kinda just like a normal restaurant. Regardless, the atmosphere was inviting with nice decor.

We ordered the fried green tomatoes, pulled pork flatbread, and chicken tenders/fries. The first two meals definitely could've used more salsa. Overall, for such a unique setting, the food was pretty standard- not bad, not great, just okay.

I visited House with a friend this Saturday. I have been wanting to go for so long, so I was very excited!
