I looked at the menu online prior to going, and though it was relatively limited, it still looked good. Parking was easy on the street at noon, and I experienced no wait for a table. The ambiance was so much fun with high tables, high booths, a couple of different rooms, and then standard tables and a decent looking bar. 

I was thrilled to see that brunch was being served (thought it was only on Sundays). I am a huge breakfast person. I ended up ordering an omelette and my friend ordered the BELT. My one disappointment was with timing. Our waiter seemed to have forgotten about us, and it was probably a half hour before he returned to our table after an initial visit, to take our order. Both of our food orders were very simple, but I don't think food took less than 40 minutes come out to us. When food finally did come out, I was informed that another server had served my dish, so I would have to wait some more. Our server made it up to us though by letting me know that he would make me a salad while I waited and comped both of our meals. My dish was delicious when it finally arrived which was only a few minutes later (again, I ordered eggs), but the point is that a negative experience was made right by our server. I will definitely return, even after a relatively rough start. Keep up the good work!
mLWsfI_NGEFyEEDyzwBfxw: We just had Newk's for lunch at our office staff meeting and it was phenomenal!  My order was ready on time, and much to my surprise, they even supplied cutlery, plates, serving utensils.  Everyone was raving about the food- it was fresh and delicious!  We will be ordering from here again!

This Newks is consistently clean and orderly.  Staff are friendly and proficient.  The food is the Newks menu of course: salads, sandwiches, pizza; good tea and sodas (Coke, etc.) on tap.  This store also serves wines and beers.

Parking lot out front is a bit small at times.   Find a place in back or nearby; it's worth it.
Better than Panerra.  I had a nice salad. I like the bar with extras like hot sauce, bread sticks, jalapeños, crackers.  Nice place!!
7NTAlTr6io7t6iMwJbgsVw: Great atmosphere and wonderful hot dogs! Very fun - cornhole on the front patio, places to sit, good wait staff. Very laid back and a fun place to come for a beer. They also have $2 domestics every day from 11-7!

It is a bar; so the food is bar food.  However,  the burgers, tots, fries and the rest are tasty and cooked to perfection.  Whats continues to set them above is their service.  Excellent Servers, Bartenders, and Security   if you are visiting Nashville - it is a must attend!  Great music and atmosphere.
Great drinks, great appetizers, super friendly bartender and very prompt service. There wasn't a band playing while we there but they obviously have it since they have a stage.
Ui9C_egCZ2_IfptxU4tAiQ: Woodbine is an absolute must try. Not only did I have the best cup of coffee I have ever tasted, it was accompanied by extremely warm service (thanks Eddie)!

Yes please. The nolensville corridor really needed a relaxed space to connect. Coffee and pastries are some of the best quality in nashville.  This is becoming my go to spot for early morning meetings and Saturday afternoon caffeine pick me ups.

Drinking a double Americano right now ($2.25). Better than Barista Parlor, without all the hype, insane pricing, or 15 minute wait for a cup of coffee.
Also, their espresso is roasted locally at Humphreys Street Coffee Co. Not sure about the other coffee.
My girlfriend got an iced chai the other day here...they make their own chai concentrate. It hasn't been strained really well, and she didn't like the sediment in the drink. I didn't really mind it. 
This place is simple, reasonably priced, and in a no BS part of town. Love it.
5quqExjLtFNhykec_na8pg: My go-to Indian place for the western KY/northern TN area. They have a good buffet for under $10 (I'm not sure if it's just for lunch or not). Lots of Indians come here, and the atmosphere is cheerful. Fabulous naan and mango lassis. The buffet could use a few more selections, but there's always at least one entree I gobble up. Great for both vegetarians and omnivores. Pay at the desk.

Incredibly delicious.  We had a very enjoyable, late dinner on Saturday night.  The service was great.  The vegetarian mango dish, with just a bit of paneer but full of potatoes, peas, carrots, and broccoli in a slightly sweet sauce, was everything I want in an entree.  I had eaten myself into a food coma during lunch and wasn't even that hungry that evening, but it tasted so good I kept eating.  My boyfriend loved his chicken korma.  

My only criticism is that the naan was a little crispy and didn't seem the freshest.  It is possible I caught it on an off night.

I would definitely go back.

I had to order from Bombay Palace when my standby delivery Indian restaurant closed recently.  The food was definitely good at Bombay Palace but nowhere near the best Indian I've ever had.  By far the highlight was the ease and speed of the OrderUp delivery service.  I ordered online and had food delivered to my apartment door in under half an hour!  It doesn't get better than that.  I'll definitely be ordering again for delivery.
-RQonzDENNng8tXzukUUww: Stopped in while on vacation. Great gyro wraps.... flavorful, juicy and fast service.
Best spanakopita I've had that's as close to what my Yiayia makes. 5/5 The only authentic Greek food I've had here in Nashville.

Simple, small place with good, basic Greek food. Got a combo platter with gyro & chicken, both were tasty and I liked the potatoes they included with them. Only 8 bucks for the plate (which also includes salad). Worth a try if you like Greek.
I9WcjQfUvKAJJI5dTDtezg:
Just as good as their place on Monroe and 4th in Nashville.
Only issue we had: pizza was not sliced completely and had to use a knife to separate the pizza slices.
Other than that, we will be back!
Had the pie been sliced all the way thru, I would have given it 5 stars!

Sooo good.  What's not to like about a Chicago style deep dish pizza right here in Nashville?  The sausage pizza is what we shared and between the four of us we still had two slices left to bring home so you certainly get your money worth on this beast! It was delicious - thick layer of sausage, lotsa sauce and tonsa cheese!  We also all shared the appetizer sampler - breadsticks were great, slightly sweet and paired well with marinara. The potato skins had a spicy sauce that really was the highlight. Handmade mozzarella sticks were served at a perfect temp and really you can't go wrong with fried cheese.  The spot itself is really nice. Simple and relaxed with ample seating and a nice looking bar with a great beer selection at good prices. Glad this spot is opened now in this area! Nothing else like it in this particular part of the city. Looking forward to coming back again soon. Manager mentioned they are all set for catering too. My office is in SoBro so I'll be sure to suggest it.

4.5 stars. This is a great little neighborhood place. I love that it's low key and not too trendy, good food at a good price and there's always seating. There are also plenty of tvs around the place so would be a great place to watch a game. I've had the deep dish and the thin crust and thought both were great. The thin crust wasn't too thin and had plenty of toppings and sauce and the deep dish will definitely fill you up. You can tell they make everything fresh to order. The hot dogs and roast beef sandwich were good as well. As others have said there are some growing pains with any new restaurant but I think this place is overall a great place to meet friends, grab a beer and pizza, and watch a game and hang out.
M83ABiFSIE2Zw_ylFRgThA: The funny thing about Green Hills is there aren't too many great restaurants there. The Perch sits among the few places in Green Hills that I'll actually eat. It's a simple spot that is clean and offers great value on well prepared breakfast fare. I had the Sedona scramble and it was excellent. The sun dried tomatoes complemented the rosemary and cheese well. I also had their basic lemon crepe so that I could judge the quality of the crepe itself. I'm happy to report the crepe was excellent if a bit heavy. They also offer decaf espresso and there aren't too many small shops that do so kudos to The Perch for that offering too. I'm going back, what else do I need to say?

I've been here twice now and have tried a sweet crepe and a savory one. I definitely like the sweet crepes better, but that's personal preference. 

Can't go wrong with any crepe that involves Nutella!! When I went for the second time, I got the chorizo crepe with eggs, manchego cheese, and chorizo. It was just okay.. probably won't order again next time. The Cinaroll latte was so good!

Very compact space, but outdoor seating is available, as well. 

Service is fine - nothing super impressive. 

Would recommend if you're looking for a decent crepe and quick 30-minute meal!
Went for brunch.  Omelettes were outstanding and coffee was excellent.  Only disappointment was no mimosas.  Would come back again and again.
NvMN6SymLONl2N8zm-n4LA:
I was a little apprehensive about going to this restaurant based on the previous reviewers. I have family in town and they are staying at the Embassy Suites (where Five Odd Fellows is located), and after peaking inside and looking at the menus we decided to give them a chance. I'm glad we did.

This isn't what a person would think of when it comes to a hotel restaurant. They have obvously cultivated an image and personality with the logo and the look of the space itself. We were seated rather promptly in a cushy booth. My only complaint about the space was the fact that the seats in some of the high tops in the middle of the restaurant were at very different heights, so some of my group seemed like they were in high chairs, and others seemed like they should have been.

Service was friendly, the beer list was apt and had a large selection of locals, and the women in the group enjoyed their cocktails. I found them a little sweet, but they seem more geared to the ladies.

The food menu was very different from what you see on a lot of other menus in Midtown. It is a welcome change from all the pubs. Duckfat popcorn, duck burger, captain crunch cereal used as breading on chicken, coconut milk in their whipped cream - little things that made their food out of the ordinary in fun ways. Everything was fresh and well prepared, and we left very full (thanks to pie we didn't need, but got anyway - we had to try that whipped cream, and we were glad we did).

I often disregard restaurants that are in hotels because, let's face it, they aren't usually the greatest. This place was a pleasant surprise, and I'm sure I'll be back. They just need to find a way to get an entrance on to the street. Walking through the lobby is a weird thing for people who aren't staying at the hotel.

Great staff and service here. Spent a week at a conference this past week and received the best service from Sierra, Cassandra, and Shelly every night after our sessions. Keep up the good work ladies, I will be back to visit you all.
The food was very good. Very nice atmosphere and staff! Would go back to this place. The Restuarant is located inside embassy suites.
tdF2pzXYwZw5uvhITLpQXQ: Some of the best food I've ever eaten - and from a food truck? I was blown away. The lamb was sublime and the falafel was delightful.

Super delicious!!!!  Great service and super yummy!!  This food truck was parked outside the downtown DMV and it was the perfect lunch after I got my new plates.  They suggested I eat it before I drive which was a good tip since the Chicken Pita is a bit messy and they serve it with a side salad.  Fresh and delicious

This is always my favorie food truck!!  I have tried others only because the line was so long at KT's but then regretted my decision when the food isn't as good at another truck.  They are informative (when I didn't know what something on the menu was), quick and the food is DELICIOUS!!  If I can stalk them, I will!!  :)
ApK4HtiTz873y_9wjTF0_w:
I have been here 3 times and will continue to go back. 

The layout is so much better than the Cafe and it looks more upscale than it had. As does Nordstrom is general - got a bit of a face lift. The bar was also a great addition - makes eating there while shopping alone not look as pathetic. haha. 

I love the pickled vegetables that they give you when you sit down! So delish. 
The first time I went I got the goat cheese and artichoke salad and that was okay. I probably wouldn't get it again. When I went back the second time I got the Wedge Salad and the Reuben. Both of which were delicious. I have also had the french dip and that was delicious as well!! 

I did have one bad experience, well I didn't but both my mom and sister got salads and both were pretty pathetic. However, the manager was very nice and helpful. She gave us our meal for free and encouraged us to come back in again. I'm not sure they have or that they will but hey, it's not for everyone.

The good news is that if you can't afford a single article of clothing in Nordstrom's, you can absolutely afford to get a great lunch there.  They are going for a 1940s diner vibe with subway tiles up the wall, a 'soda counter' and then ample tables and booths.  While the layout is cute, it is also loud, but this is not for fine dining.
Made with high quality ingredients, menu standouts include amazing shrimp salads, sweet potato fries to die for and panko coated onion rings.  I got a french dip with a fabulous baguette that was super yum.
While its lots of great things, I would say its not great for kids.  There were some harried moms there with the under 5 set, and there is no where for them to roam which made for lots of screaming babies and mom's sucking back Valium.  
You can also expect a wait if you show up around lunch time on a weekend, but the service is relatively fast and everything we had was delicious.
Incredibley friendly staff! I highly recommend the pan fried chicken breast dish it is great!
dNmqRPHBys4dWH4nReO7MA:
I really like this place.  Great vibe; great atmosphere, great patio; great Asian food.  Prices reasonable for Nashville sushi ($50 for 2 glasses of wine, edamame, and 4 rolls).  My only critique is that sometimes the clientele is a little young because it's so close to Vandy and Belmont.

PM is a triple threat:  good food, good service, good atmosphere, good prices.   Alright, that's 4 things - go eat here!!

VERY GOOD FOOD and Excellent service!  If you are an older pervy stalker type, perfect location right next to a college campus.  But really, good food!  Fairly large portions too.  I think they accept bruins bucks as well...(if you are a Belmont student)

Oh and our waiter had a kick ass name - Pyro.

next time visiting my college student son, i'll visit again!

GREAT VIBE! Seriously, the vibe alone gives this place a fourth star. 

I love that they have small plates as well. It is like an Asian inspired tapas menu. 

Speaking of the menu, it is really very good, but far from exceptional. The prices are high, but not outrageous. The drinks are mixed well. 

I was pleasantly surprised with both my calamari and tofu being cooked perfectly. They so often go wrong. 

While I love the concept of outdoor eating, doing it here is difficult. There were a half a million flies all around this place. I don't know where they all come from, but they will not leave you alone while you are trying to eat. We all had to take turns eating our food so that one person could be flailing their arms wildly about the table to try and discourage the flies from enjoying our meal as much as we were trying to do. This would have bumped the star rating down to three but it wasn't their fault and we chose to sit outside. I would say the outside area is better to get drinks, but save the food for when you are on the inside. 

It is also right next to Bongo Java, which makes a great place to go after a nice meal.
NZIq01-TuQ-eyy06WPTB7A: Try the mulitas, they're awesome!! We had the asada, carnitas, and pollo and all were good, the asada was my favorite. (Mulitas are like mini quesadillas - 2 corn tortillas with cheese, meat, grilled onions and lettuce in between, grilled til it all melts together. Yum.) The salsas that came with them were spicy and fresh. I loved the big fresh pickled carrots and thick grilled onion slices. The corn tortillas were freshly made and tasty. The white cheese they use is perfect, especially the crispy parts that got a little burned on the grill... I will be hitting this truck up again for sure. It's next to a super mercado now and I think the full name on the truck is taqueria la mulita express #2. Cheap prices ($2 mulita $1.25 taco) and well seasoned food that definitely exceeded my expectations.

Hmm...not bad. Not bad at all. Grabbed a few steak tacos and they were pretty tasty. The peppers it came with are HOT! I didn't eat them but my husband did and it was the funniest thing ever! Automatic hiccups in 3 seconds flat. Lol I'll be back to try the burritos and quesadillas.

My 40 year search for AUTHENTIC DELICIOUS Mexican food in Tennessee is finally over! 
We had a family get together last week. Our daughter heard about this truck from a friend. She ordered a few of each item so we could sample the menu in case we wanted to return. 
The first bite confirmed that my eternal search is FINALLY OVER! 
Home made tortillas, grilled chicken, vegetables and beef, authentic Mexican rice- all delicious! 
Not only was the food incredible but we bought a lot of it for less than $45.00! 
Yes it's a food truck, who cares? They're located on Charlotte Pike permanently. 
The green sauce ( served on the side in little containers) is very hot. 
Great food! The search is over...
w7p9y00FxTVAHjIZmnt-zw:
I've been here twice.  Pretty decent wraps.  Nice folks in my experience.  And staff is nice in my experience and pretty speedy (I stole this from Carol F, but its true).  

Like Robin S. one of the wraps I had was the Jamaican...a little different than Jerk Chickens of the past for me, but interesting none-the-less.

I don't think we ever had to wait more than 3 or so minutes in line or 3 minutes for the sub.

This is my favorite lunch spot downtown.  I usually take my lunch for work, but enjoy the times when I forget so I can eat here.  The food is always fresh and the wraps or salads are put together very well.  You will never have a bad meal.  I am struggling to figure out how this has gotten bad reviews!    There will be a line during weekday lunch.  And try the pesto pasta.  Ummm.

City Wraps Café is just awesome!  They can make a seriously tasty wrap loaded with tons of fresh veggies.  It can get uber-packed from 11:45 until 1:00, so watch out of you are time conscious.  The day I went there was a long line, but it moved rather quickly.

The most positive attribute about this cool little café is its great price.  I left spending only $6.68 for my wrap, which is pretty competitive for most of the places downtown!  

I usually go with the chicken wrap with mayo and lots of tomato!  Boring, I know.

The people there are really nice, too!
nygJ4mY0glpdWGv_Hmp6Dg: If you ever want to try something amazing, stop by and try the " Great Balls of Fire".  Cream cheese, jalapeño, crab meat, all coated in a deep fry breading . These are to die for.

What a great place for great food and great service.  If you want delicious food and a fun atmosphere this is a must stop,  we ordered the family meal and we were stuffed. Might not have to eat for a couple days.

I'm not a big fan of Joe's, but met some friends there before a concert. I had the fish and chips and they were good. We had about 10 people at our table and our server was really good. If you are a big fan of crab this is the place for you!
DjiBIx1d8USQoSQMELQE6g: This is a fun, lively bar on lower Broadway. They have a huge beer selection and the chips and salsa were quite good. Great spot to hang out for a while and enjoy quintessential Nashville.

great bear selection and micro brews
pretty decent food choices
nice atmosphere
we had a group of 12 and they accomondated us well

I have already said plenty about the Brewhouse Midtown, one of my favorite haunts in all of Nashville, but its downtown cousin is a good time as well.  Same great food, same large beer selection, but much bigger and with more TVs and a lot more people.  It's not as chill as in Midtown, but its still a good time.  Mostly its the same Brewhouse but with tourists and a lot of people coming in pre or post Titans/Predators games for libations and appetizers.  The service here is a bit slower because of all the people but still good.  Again, I won't repeat myself as I've already talked about my preferred Brewhouse location but this place is still a fun downtown hang with all the perks of its smaller and more intimate Midtown locale with the wilder downtown feel.
fqkEU1N-Q4Yxa1wbXRGpEw:
Quick dinner at a mom & pop Thai place. 

Hubby and I had the Panang Curry (me tofu; he chicken).  It was very good! Didn't look like much but it filled us up and hit the spot. 

The tofu pieces were large. The coconut cream sauce was just right of thickness and flavor. 

Server did not ask what heat level we wanted. It had a bit of a bite to it but I still found it palatable. 

The Thai tea was perfectly blended and not super sweet either. Yum! 

I recommend; this is a hidden gem.

Driving around on this rainy holiday Monday and came across this gem.  Fantastic Penang Curry with chicken.  Paired perfectly with the Thai Tea.  

If you're looking for an authentic local Thai restaurant this is the place. Very highly recommend.
Tiger tear salad and curry are this establishments specialty. The food tends to be milder than most thai joints unless you specify.
SVsA6EBzavimXSw8S41YLg: This place feels very authentic and cozy. The food is great too!

in the back streets of Nashville there are many hidden treasures, and Vito's truly is one of these.
The food is Delicious, and if you do not at least try a Pizza you are missing out on a real treat!
The only thing missing is some nice music. We sat outside, and while it was quiet and tranquil some relaxing European lounge music would have been very fitting.
The wine list is a little shy, and could do with some better Italian choices to liven up the selection, but the food was incredible
we shall return!
I bought a Sharing Spree for Vito's, and I had an AMAZING experience! The staff was excellent, the atmosphere was quiet but romantic, and I would highly recommend the Chicken Piccata!
_1aCRm_YRJ1x9RagRHltLg:
Yet more delicious selections at Bergamot Market today :)
Curried coconut eggplant soup. Yum!!
Chicken croquettes with mashed potatoes and gravy and beautiful Blueberry macaroons. No need to eat anywhere else
What a delightful, casual place with family smiles all around.  The food is great too.

This is my kind of lunch food. Gourmet fresh salads, sandwiches, soups and interesting sides and specials. Super tiny and hard to get in and out of the parking lot giving its location on White Bridge. There is very limited seating and the bathroom was out of order since the water was shut off, but the food was good. I got mine to go. It did take awhile waiting on the food, but it was delicious. Loved the small salad option and scoop of chicken salad. Salmon corn chowder was fresh tasting, but not my favorite. I will be back to try more. Loved the menu and changing specials! Prices reasonable, too.
OS3PZwj_KyP1iD2Js8Jpig:
Hello new brunch place! I don't remember the name of what I got, but I do remember how difficult it was to choose. It was delicious. Runny eggs and hot sauce and beans... oh man. It wasn't crowded on the Sunday afternoon that my boyfriend and I stopped by, but I think it was during Vandy's holiday break. I didn't imbibe in any alcoholy drinks (although $15 bottomless mimosas? Def will come back for those) but I DID have the Mexican coffee and that was delicious. 

Boca Loca also recently catered an event at my apartment building and I got to try some carnitas tacos, pickled veggies, guac,and some taco soup (I like to load up at buffets, ok!?) and everything was delicious. I really liked their homemade tortillas! 

I'LL BE BACK.

I'm curious to see if they can keep up the good work, but so far I'm a fan.

Don't come here if you want Tex Mex or cheese slathered all over everything (not knockin either of those things, this is just different). Do come here if you want to try some Mexican flavors that you maybe haven't had before- or you could just head down to Nolensville Rd :P

The food was fresh and delicious. My boyfriend and I each had the 3 tacos and a side deal. We were surprised by how full we were, as at first glance the tacos don't look very big, but full we were indeed. I don't remember exactly which ones we ordered, but I do remember loving my fish taco way more than the other ones, and way more than I expected! Service was quick but a little wonky- we ran into some interesting personalities, but there was nothing to complain about. 

I will definitely be coming again (maybe for brunch next time) and look forward to updating my review!

Really pleasantly surprised with this find (added bonus - close to where I live and work)! I went with someone on a Saturday for lunch and boy were we in for a great surprise. We honestly had no idea that there was even a brunch menu with bottomless mimosas! The selection, although understandably limited was great. We ended up choosing drinks off the regular menu including their spicy/jalapeño margarita and a coffee/chocolate flavored spiked drink. Both were absolutely amazing and tasty. We got the huevos rancheros and a selection of steak and chicken tacos with a side of their corn. The food was delicious, and I have to say that I could probably eat a large bowl of that corn for a meal, it was that good! The only negative part of the experience is that the tacos are small - smaller than the usual taco at places like mas tacos etc. Two tacos just really won't cut it, especially for a full meal. Perhaps they should consider a taco meal option (three tacos and a side for x dollars) to cut the cost down a little for such a small amount of food. Otherwise, the scene here is great as well (huge windows that open will be great for the summer!)
hS8z5yNV0QJQcKo1aV2KHA: You can go for the pizza or the beer. Beer is awesome with lots of variety. The Pizza could easily be its own destination. I love the meat lovers but you can't go wrong with any. We went to celebrate the 4th with their fireworks show and it was amazing. We will definitely be back.

Go here! Im so glad we found this place, the service is fantastic. Great beers and its huge on the inside. Live music and they have food too. We were hesitant to try it at first because it kind of looked like high school gym from the outside but its great! Cant wait to go back and try more of the beers

Just visited Tailgate Brewery for the first time and had such a GREAT time!!  Staff was so courteous and attentive, pizza was really really good, beer was fantastic, and the board games made for hours of fun!  We took our son and never once felt out of place having a 10 year old there - lots of other kids and families around.  We sat inside tonight, but vowed to come back and give the outdoor patio seating a try.
55yVwtNPAHIYp2t6stJKHA: Great coffee shop!  Chill environment, good coffee, very nice staff, and decent food.  I liked that they actually had breakfast type food that was not just muffins and sweets.  I wouldn't necessarily go here for a big breakfast, if that's what your looking for, but if you are looking to just grab something small and good, this is your place.  Good location too!  I am in Nashville for the weekend and wanted to do a little work, and found this place to be absolutely perfect to buckle down for an hour.  Can't say enough good things, and I would definitely recommend stopping in!

Revelator gets a lot of hate in Nashville, which I don't understand.  It is one of only a handful (a very small handful) of shops in town that would be successful in Seattle, Portland, San Francisco, etc.  

The shop is very clean, the space is beautiful, and the coffee is expertly roasted and flavorful.  It is as good as Barista Parlor and it costs less significantly less.

My favorite cappuccino I've had in Nashville so far. The espresso was smooth and chocolatey, perfectly balanced. BEAUTIFULLY designed shop, clean, bright, and calming. This is the perfect place both to come read a book on a Saturday afternoon or hunker down for a long writing grind. Would highly recommend!
3GvPXj5n0oig1ebnvwie0g:
Yep! Chocolate Razz coffee, authentic chai tea, home made toffee, fresh hand made paninis, vegetarian options. I had the chicken panini with an americano. Simple, fresh, delish.
They have singer-songwriters on Saturday from 11 to about 1ish. Today the talent was awesome we came away with CDs and a private performance for our table.
Really lovin me some East Nashville these days, wish I live there. There are some really cool eateries and shops.
I would probably spend my working afternoons with a cup of joe, and my iPad at this place.
It really puts the stir in hipster, get it, okay I'll keep my day job

I love this place.  Really good smoothies and coffee drinks, breakfast and lunch options, the owner and the staff are all super friendly and it has a calm, friendly atmosphere and a kids' corner with a play kitchen and books. Every time I go in there are only a few other people there, so I hope more people start stopping in and checking it out!  Free parking in the RIte Aid garage.

Super awesome service! I walked up 15 minutes before opening and they glad my let me hangout inside! Had an everything bagel and butter with some coffee. 

They have an adorable set up wth a few larger tables, and also a jewelry stand. Eclectic art on the walls, feels very artsy. 

First trip this morning as I flew into Nashville isn't turning half bad!
e2RBDxidD3rNQuGvJLHmpw:
Bangin' food, friendly staff and "laid back" atmosphere! 
Our server ( unfortunately I didn't get his name) was very attentive to my tables' needs and wants. I highly recommend to anyone in the area!

This is my favorite middle eastern food spot in Nashville. The favors are authentic and addictive and the fresh juices are made to order and customizable just like you would find at any fancy juice bar. The beef shwarma platter is my favorite dish. The decor is cute and has tables with cushions so you can sit on the floor. The service is slow so if you're in a hurry order for carry out.

Their new location in Hillsboro Village is quite the experience! We sat on floor pillows and ate the most delicious falafel. The staff are so sweet and the manager Rami (whom I think is the manager) treated us well. We are excited for the next time we're in the mood for falafel!
vDVPHRTFKMszIadeI-Hceg: I used to work at the Publix near this Moe's and had lunch here once a week or more. Everyone sems to love Baja Fresh more, but I just love Moe's (better selection of toppings). Their salsa bar is really nice as well - there's always at least three or four different salsas to choose from.

I've been coming to this Moe's since I started at Nashville State community college 3 years ago.
The manager Andre is a very funny guy, and he leads the other employees well and with laughter.
All the employees I've been in contact with or talked to have been kind, funny, and very attentive. They recognize I've been coming for a while and almost know my complicated order by heart.
I like this one much more than the one on west end.
Good service, clean, and fast. Arriba, Arriba! However, the atmosphere is well...just not there. But if you want/need practical mexican...this is the place.
LKVdxqd_-FThU0JVHIMveg:
Food was good. We just picked up to go orders, it's definitely nothing special. Typical bar food.

Best part is that they serve their sandwiches and burgers with homemade chips which are actually very good.

After a friends birthday dinner, a group of us came here for a night cap or two. I know its a chain and there are several of them, but this spot is pretty darn nice.
While the decor of the place isn't much. several tv's on every wall give the sports minded the ability to glance at the game and still keep up with conversation.
We sat down after having had several mass produced magaritas and/or generic beers with dinner, so we all wanted something better. Happily, Corner Pub came through.
The beer selection here is really nice, well beyond the typical fare one finds at a suburban bar, and the bar itself is very well stocked, again far above and beyond what one would expect to find in 'burbia. I opted for a Murphy's Stout, which was delicious.
The food is standard bar food - fried cheese, fried pickles (well, standard for Nashville), sandwiches salads, etc. None amazing, but all were good.

For a good drink in the burbs, its a good place to go.

I find myself eating here at least once a month. I also enjoy hanging out watching a ball game now and then with my buds. My regular order (and all out favorite without question) is the Hot Chicken Chunks and fries. I don't eat fried food much at all, but when I do I enjoy the lightly breaded and perfectly spicy chunks of white chicken breast meat chased with a cold mug of beer!! Getting hungry just thinking about it. See ya there.
R-w5jNoaHSMlq2V9gHPAlQ: Great lunch place downtown.  Huge menu with something for everyone.  Very good selection of salads with a wide range of toppings.  Portions are big and the prices are reasonable for the amount of food you get.  If you feel like treating yourself and getting away from the office grind then this place is highly recommended.

I order from here almost every week, and cannot go wrong with their pizzas. I usually get the white pizza, SOOO good. and we order from my work there every other week, and everyone always loves it because there is so much to choose from, and such a wide variety of foods for everyone's liking. Def recommend. Delivery time is always slow during lunch time, that is why we order around 10 am (online) before they even open, and it is usually here by 12:30. Sounds ridiculous, but its the only way to get your food on time, and correct. Esp if you are doing it from online.

Tazza. A menu in size 6 font with 175+ items on it. Salads, wraps, burgers, Mexican, fruit, fries, steaks, pastas, seafood or soup. I am not kidding.  

If you do not want to leave your house or put pants on... and want some food brought to you like jalapeno poppers and lets say grilled shrimp and an orange juice. They will bring it. And quickly. 

The food tastes like cafeteria food, ok, filling but you know its been frozen, you know its going to be bland, why, because I'm telling you. Look it a  restaurant to have 145+ menu items its not going to be super fresh but its good. It gets the job done. 

If you're staying at hotel downtown this is the best delivery service and restaurant. 

I would not recommend eating at this location because its dim, and dark, and usually empty. 

Tazza has helped me cure a hang-over, some strange food craving, brought me food when I was sick and allowed me to be a goraphob. I've always felt better afterwards.
V-snSOHDO_mrWOBtJHtg4w:
It's a Domino's. A clean and fast one.

All locations have been remodeled, and seem to now be kept to a higher standard than 10 years ago. Pretty good, considering it's a capitalist-consumerist-homogenized fast food delivery chain. Again; IT'S A DOMINO'S. What do you expect? Sometimes it is necessary to dive headfirst into modern, processed convenience.

It had been a long time since I had eaten at Domino's Pizza, and I did not think their food was very good.  The other day my cousin told me they had changed and was really good now.  So I decided to give them a try on Monday.  I ordered the Buffalo Chicken sandwich, and the chocolate molten cakes (2 in a box).  It was extremely delicious, in fact it was so good I ordered the same thing last night.  Well I really have to thank my cousin when I see her, so tonight I decided to order the Buffalo Chicken pizza, and yes I am hooked on those cute little chocolate molten cakes.  They taste like a wonderful crispy Brownie with a wonderful filling inside.  Everything has arrives hot and delicious.  I don't mind tipping the driver when everything is so good.  It is so worth it to not have to cook or leave home.  Thank goodness for Domino's Pizza and their employees!

I know Domino's is usually just known for how good their pizza is but let's talk about how amazing the service I got just now was. His name was Josh, and I place a delivery order with him. I don't know if it's the southern hospitality, but he was extremely patient, informative, and genuinely friendly! He explained in the nicest way that they are understaffed for Monday so that my delivery might take a little bit longer than normal, But I still ordered anyway. I asked like five or six questions just because I didn't honestly look at the menu before I called but he answered every question and he was probably smiling on the other end. He was so kind and that's a great reflection on the company. My pizza got here around the time he said it would take which was about an hour but they're slammed right now so I totally understand. It was a meat lovers pizza, because I tried the new cookie brownie, and had a Coke. The food was amazing and Josh made experience that much better. I'll let you in on a little secret, I was the first try the new cookie brownie dessert!  I LOVE DOMINOS AND JOSH! (And cookie brownies) :)
8JEcsyj5jqsSqmb0Xvwnbw: Several of my friends and I attended Hopsmith Tavern's grand opening last night. The hostess was extremely welcoming and instantly set the tone for an enjoyable experience. My party of 8 was seated inside on the second level at one of their unique booths. These booths comfortably fit 6 or 7, so 8 was a bit of a squeeze, but we were able to make it work. The booth was shaped like a bay window with everyone facing the bar on the opposite wall. At each booth, they have two taps that you can choose as a group to purchase (priced per ounce of beer your table pours). The atmosphere inside was fantastic - the music playlist was full of great selections, the volume wasn't too loud, and the surrounding noise from other patrons didn't infiltrate our conversations. As for the food and drink, my friends and I appreciated the large selection of beer and the various pour options (16 oz, 22oz, etc.). The food was really great as well. I had the fish tacos which were perfectly seasoned. I really can't wait to come back on a Tuesday night for Taco Tuesday ($2-3 tacos and $3 Mexican beers). My friends mainly stuck to burgers or sandwiches, and each one had good things to say. Our waitress was super personable and gave us the perfect amount of attention. Overall, my experience at Hopsmith Tavern was quite enjoyable, and I look forward to returning for one of the weeknight specials.
Surprised at how good the food is here. Expected typical bar food, but the Beer Can Chicken Sandwich is fantastic! Service is friendly & fast.

Decided to come here during a work trip to Nashville (was staying in Midtown). From previous reviews learned this was actually a chain from Chicago. Decided to sit outside as the inside was little too A/C heavy for my preference. Service was quick as it was busier than I thought it might be for a Tuesday night. I got the citrus kale salad as a wrap and upgraded the fries to Mac and cheese. I was very pleasantly surprised with the low upcharge price ($1). Also if you come on Tuesdays it's $5 fat pours aka a lot of craft beer for a cheap price. Very random but I was really digging the music they were playing that night; some solid 2000s throwbacks.
4MHUWIRaIPgheiD96UCtmw: Fast service, love the food here. Price was same as most Mexican restaurants. Right off the highway was a plus for us

You know how everyone has their favorite cheap Mexican place and no other place can touch it? Well mine is El Porton in Roswell, GA, and I'm still trying to find a place that lives up to that. 

In Nashville, Cinco de Mayo is as close as I've come to finding my favorite Americanized Mexican. It's inexpensive, friendly, and downright tasty. Also, they have some KILLER drink specials. 

If you too are looking to fill the Mexican void, Cinco de Mayo will fit the bill.

Friendly, fast, and the food is good.

Plus, they send me coupons for $5 off or buy one meal get one free on a regular basis.

This is our favorite Mexican restaurant.
8080Craxe6OuGh6YHH8D1A:
I start my work day earlier than most, and I work a block away from Varallo's. Every time I need a quick breakfast, I always swing by. The staff is incredibly friendly, and I've always seen them greet their regulars by name. I get the chicken biscuit and side of hasbrowns... $4.33 for breakfast downtown? Can't beat that! I recommend! 

Also, if you're in an extreme pinch for time, they accept call in orders, as well as online orders!

What a great place!  We had breakfast food and it was perfect!  We were looking for some home style cooking before the Music City Bowl and decided on this based on Yelp reviews. Once again yelp steered us to the right place. Fourth generation Varallo was working. I asked if I could have my eggs cooked scrambled on my breakfast sandwich his response was... Honey they are your eggs.  You can have them any way you would like!  Gotta love it!
Varallo's is known for their famous chili, but they also have the best catfish in downtown Nashville.
pV_bmLMZs0R0WgfBsnUAVQ:
4 stars for NOT being a honky tonk or a coyote-hooters. This place has a good beer selection and the food is great! I had the Lobster Pizza and it was surprisingly spicy. The bartender here is my hero! We were here late one night and over the course of the evening 3 different crack-heads stumbled in shirtless and oblivious to their behavior, the bartender pulled off some sort of superman move flying over the bar and escorted the crazies out before they could cause trouble. Now THAT is my kind of bartender. 
I will definitely return.

Always a great staff. Good atmosphere.  Amazing food. seriously, the food is fire and the potato salad is so unique and just amazing. They have darts, skiball and few other games. No drink specials at the moment but they appear to have some decent specials available at other times. Cool decor. Highly recommend.

My latest find in the downtown Nashville area is Riverfront Tavern.  It is a really fun sports bar at the very end of Church Street, on the river.  Well, not right on the river, it is across the street from the river.

I have only been here for lunch, but this place would be fun for dinner.  Inside it has your typical sports bar decor with large flat screens and dark wood booths and chairs.  It was kind of dark inside, but there is ample seating near big windows.  

I was taken a back at how unexpectedly good the food was.  We started with the spinach and artichoke dip which was really good and creamy.  The highlight of the meal was their chicken pesto french bread pizza.  The texture was unbelievable, nice and crispy on the outside and soft on the inside.  Melts in your Mouth!

They have Wi-Fi also, kind of strange for a place that is for people 21 and up only!
30vbjnJEGGpTisVOVU2tBg: Good beers and all kinds of desserts I could live here. Pretty sure I'd pick this place for a "last meal". Only thing that would make this place better would be larger portions, open 24/7 and maybe be married to whoever makes this stuff

Noticed that the former Christie Cookie company location is now Goozy. My husband and I were curious so we checked it out.  Little did we know how unique and tasty this place had become!  They sell desserts and craft beer and liquor galore!  And they have creative concoctions blending the different flavors and delights!  They also sell sandwiches too. 

I wanted to try everything but had eaten dinner prior.  We had the chocolate chip cookie in a skillet, and it came with vanilla gelato.  It was nice and gooey by the time it came out - so good!

The place also has an obscure collection of rock albums too so you almost feel like you're in a Hard Rock Cafe but much more quaint and smaller and secluded....

We definitely give this place a thumbs up on creativity and for satisfying our taste buds!

Great spot! I love the sandwiches, even if I'm just using them as an excuse to eat dessert for lunch. Everything is quite decadent, and you'll want it all-- do don't be surprised when your bill is $25 just for lunch! The nutella goozy cake tasted more like black cherry to me, but you don't hear me complaining. Still haven't decided on my fav grilled cheese yet.
P9TGN9z_cCPDJAX7zkFKoA:
I can't believe I am writing ANOTHER update but I HAVE to, the place keeps changing.  Literally every trip in the past 6 months has had me on my toes, what are they going to do next?  Ok it wasn't that serious but it crossed my mind.  Well, they are done people, the bartender assured me.  Not that I don't like change, I liked Watanabe before BUT I like it even better now! The new decor is hot.  They scooted the booths up behind the bar making the dining area a bit more private.  The tables have cute little red lamps, it's cozy and sexy!!  The menu has been updated with some Asian fusion hot dishes but they still have the sushi on the back.  This is what I come for and I won't deviate.  Last night I had the green goddess roll, so. yummy.   I dipped it in some spicy mayo and I was happy as a clam. We also tried the crab rangoon and it was great!  I'm still trying to get used to the change in the salad, I miss the trusty ole bowl of lettuce with ginger dressing!  I also really miss the tempura fried ice cream.  I'm not willing to let that go, I will ask for it every time I go in and maybe, hopefully, one day they will bring it back.  Come on Watanabe, it was the best!!!

Other changes:

Happy hour is all day Tuesday-Ohhhhh Yeahhhh!!!
Happy hour options have expanded!
They have a berry sake
New plates and presentation on dishes

So to sum up the place it's now an Asian fusion, speakeasy blend.  Weird huh?  I promise you it's actually pretty cool.  Ch-Ch-Check it out!

Bingo! 
Really happy with the quality & presentation of a selection of sushi. Quality decor and enthusiastic waitstaff too. Worth going to.

Watanabe is a wonderful asset to the area of Riverside Village. The parking can be a bit challenging but well worth the risk.

Although not open for lunch, Wantanabe serves up a nice variety of rolls. The fish has always been fresh when I go. The seating is fun as there are booths that are halfmoon shaped, a bar area, and tables/booths.

The rolls that are covered with several types of fish and avacado are my favs. They have realllllly good ginger here and the wasabi is fairly mild compared to other spots.

The only thing that I think could be impvoed here is the timiness of getting your food. Having to wait for nearly 30 minutes of five rolls when a quarter of the resturant is full is a bit long. 

I personally like the Dragon, Rainbow, Red Tiger and the Pad Thai rolls! Honestly, two full rolls is a lot of food. 

The also have a nice variety of sake. So, what are you waiting for East Nashville. SUSHI!
oAfFq0g38WnW_ETrw7oO8A:
Went there for brunch after church . Family of 4 . We ordered the double burger , blueberry pancakes , queso with meat and some type of pancake , bacon pig in a blanket thing ( this was really great) 

1) decor needs some help and the poor guy they have waiting tables who has never waited tables before . Maybe he is family ?

2) the food was great , really great . The hamburger was huge , double patties . Hot and juicy and if it wasn't a hand patties burger I couldn't tell.

3) the queso had meat in it and we didn't expect that for the price . 

4) blueberry pancakes were large , fluffy and full of blueberry . Awesome. We ordered a side of sausage and it was like being in a New York dinner .

5)  the bacon in a blanket was cute and different . Great for a kid . 

Side note.......the damn hash browns are awesome . Me and the hubby were fighting over then with a fork 

The bill was under $40 , THATS with DRINKS 

I will be back . This place is great , especially for the price .  I have been OTHER places in the area and our bill was way more than what we paid today

Picked up a take out order recently.  It was fantastic!  I ordered the stuffed peppers and they were stuffed with a mushroom risotto.  The only negative is that the prices are a bit on the high side for what you get.  The owner is super nice and helpful.

I'm going to give them the benefit of doubt on this one because my friend's experiences wasn't that great and she told me the restrooms weren't complete. My experienced started with our friendly server Nick aka Portland Maine Nicholas. (Inside story but he is from there) He told he also the musician there and judging by the stage this place must change at night. We were there for lunch. Clean, a little dull, full bar. Open kitchen (somewhat) I could see him and he could see me but I couldn't see his hands or the food for that matter. (Trust) Apparently this place has a New England twist (although I had to read into that a bit ) I asked about the chowdah (my friends soup was really runny last time) my serving was thick and pretty tasty. I also got NE fish and chips, breaded in panko and served with a nice coleslaw (not sweet, thank you) and fries. This place isn't far from work so we'll try it again but so far so good, but of course the best part was saving $2 for checking in on yelp! Woo hoo
YpJTxfEiXfH3iC9md0zIDg: Wow. A little piece of hipster heaven a walk from my Airbnb. Breakfasts - yum. Lunch - yum. Coffees - yum.
Always nice service, smiles all around and the scene is awesome. Got myself a burger with an egg on top and fries. The server with the pineapple tattoo on her forearm with blue eyes did a great job.

Oh my! This place. I love it. 

It's modern, girly vibe (considering the name features the favorite white girl drink) is swoon worthy. And will garner a ton of Instagram pictures. 

I went with the Avocado Hummus toast and it was EPIC. I know it doesn't seem hard to level up on avo toast... but dang this was good. The sourdough was thick and chewy. And the avo Hummus was slathered on ... definitely did not skimp. Creamy, smooth with the right amount of seasoning. The sprouts were a good addition. Definitely add the Bentons Bacon on the side, great to scoop up the rest of the avo hummus off the plate. 

I'll definitely be back to try the rest of the menu!
arQfMJal1tl67Z96ROgPFg: Came here while visiting my brother for the holidays. I ordered the steak and shrimp with a baked sweet potato and it was delicious :) Service was ok, the waiter seemed like she was new/didn't know the menu very well.

This is our 6th visit. Typically we have great food and sketchy service."We" are a party of three always seated at the bar. Today was the most enjoyable experience we have EVER had because of our bartender...Big Daddy.  His attitude... he is sociable, interactive and happy, a very happy person that wants to make customers happy. This man was fantastic. Will be phoning ahead to make  certain we plan our visit when he is there. Food great because he went out of his way.

Excellent service and food!
I ordered the Steak Chili Baked Potato and my husband ordered the Top Sirloin. His was cooked to order, tender and delicious. It came with a side Cesar salad and side dish. A fresh loaf of sourdough bread was served. It was almost better than the sourdough we have in San Francisco!

Appetizer: Fried Lemon Zucchini..it was awesome and way more than any 2 people could eat. I don't know how they fry it and it's not greasy at all!

Our server was attentive and friendly.

Would definitely go back!
tix-1KNJUzjyRerstEHw5Q: Awesome! Half the price of most tacos at only a buck each. Just as delicious. Only downside is it might be tough to get a spot to park when it's busy and cash only.

This taco truck has the best chicken tacos I've ever tasted. With the hot sauce and lime, you literally cannot stop eating tacos. I think we've hit the spot every week all summer b/c we literally cannot stop eating tacos. I recently went rogue and got the chicken burrito. Guess what? It was delicious too. Now I literally cannot stop eating chicken burritos. My wife is a fan of the mulitas as well.

But seriously, go here for the tacos. You will never be sad with 5 or 6 tacos in hand!
I have tried about every taco truck in and around Nashville and this one reigns supreme!!
PxVFM9j9IbjBMlMqvisqnA:
I love Sonic for drinks!!! They have the best Happy Hour from 6am to 10am and then again 2pm to 4pm..$1 for large drinks...tea, sodas and slushies...any flavor...and I am mad crazy over the Sweetened or unsweetened Iced tea with Blackberry flavoring!!!  LOVE IT!!!

When I am not on a diet, I  like their Corn Dogs for $1 (every day all day)...but NOT a good idea when I am dieting. 
When my daughter visits she loves the Slushies....Great Car Hop...some on rollerskates at certain locations...but not this one!!!!

Hooray for the Franklin Pike Sonic! They've saved me from many a hungry evening. The carhops are always really nice, especially when, sigh, they know you are a regular. I used to have an iffy feeling going late at night, but they have a cop who patrols the parking lot, which makes me feel safe. Sometimes I just need my Reese's Sonic Blast.

This is the first sonic I've been to that actually gave me chili cheese fries when I ordered chili cheese fries!!!, number 3 plain..... it's actually plain..... kudos to the cooks and communication these employees have. The one on Murfreesboro pike needs y'all to train them. Cuz that's a sorry excuse for a sonic over there. This location is where I'm going to come from now on
NfOnxD8NT4UQmSfBY6iqng: I visited the roof top bar for the first time on Saturday and I was quite impressed.  It was 90 plus degrees outside and I wanted to go somewhere to watch the Tennessee/BG game. The restaurant/bar area looked very nice although I did not eat at the restaurant part nor did I visit the museum.  I was going to see the view of the roof top bar.  The guys that were playing were decent and played some classic country music and the biggest thing that impressed me were the water misters and fans which were an AWESOME touch since it was so hot out.  I had one drink and the male bartender was very friendly and then ordered the chicken tenders which were good.  The menu is limited for the bar area but it is a bar.  I was also impressed with the staff that cleaned up.  It started to rain and everyone moved to the covered part of the bar and staff were immediately moving tables together, bringing in more chairs for us to sit.  Then when the rain cleared out there was a gentleman drying off all the wet chairs and bar stools, and this just really impressed me.  I liked that it was not a smelly dive bar and the view was awesome.  I will definitely be back and hopefully get great service again compared to the other reviews.
Great displays, though some of the listening stations are not functional. A shrine for any George Jones fan.

Came here early afternoon for lunch. The place was packed but we managed to find a table at the rooftop outdoor bar. Service was good, did their best despite the big crowds. We split the veggie and hummus board, smothered brisket fries and wings. The hummus board with veggies was he best choice to balance the rest of the fried foods we ordered. The smothered brisket fries got everyone around us talking since it looked and smelled and tasted delicious. The brisket was so soft and tender. Definitely recommend!! The wings were good, didn't find it really unique from other places but it was good.
mbIe1LarJRddU6MnWNbp9Q: Not being able to compare it to other vietnamese restaurants in Nashville - I thought this place was great!  It is very casual and unassuming, the perfect place for a nice relaxed dinner.  I tried the Pho and also a rice noodle soup with seafood.  Both were delicious and I will definitely be back!

This was my first experience with Vietnamese food, and I rather enjoyed it.  I've been twice and gotten the Pho Tau Hu (pho with tofu instead of beef) both times.  It' flavorful and filling.

Very reasonable pricing, decent service.  It also helps that it's just down the street from work, so it's a good place for me to pop in for lunch.

this is my favorite restaurant in Nashville. hands down, the best pho in town! i try and come to pho yen hoa once a week. its cheap, fast and SO GOOD. i dont understand why no one is ever at this place when i am here...i was however glad to see like 4 couples in the restaurant last time i was in (which was yesterday). if you like good Vietnamese food, check this place out. i have never been disappointed in this place (except when i got the tripe in my pho, but i guess that was my fault for ordering tripe!!) my favorite restaurant in town
.
lI7_d8mNLLKpx9jRbv2Dew: Excellent sweet potatoes!! The bbq ribs were delicious!  We drove 2.5hrs for some good soul food and got it here!  Would come again

So good it's insane! Every single bite was delicious. Between my wife any myself we had: ribs, fried chicken, mashed potatoes and gravy, greens, pinto beans, potato casserole, yams, fried apples and German chocolate cake and pecan pie. Can't express how good adequately express how good everything was. Very friendly staff. First time here; we will be back!

This soul food restaurant is so good. Home cooked and seasoned just right! While visiting I had the fried chicken, creamed corn and cabbage and collard green mix. Mr. Swett was there this day. He is the sweetest man and even recommended the Swett vinegar hott sauce. For my cabbage and greens mix. He even had a detox type of fruit water. On this choice day it had apple, lemon and oranges in it. I highly recommend this establishment for soul food lovers.
otHYLRH2uGN2mc7MaWON-w: This was my first time in Nashville and my youth group and I were craving some pizza. We decided to stop by Luigi's City Pizza, and I was very pleased with my experience. Fast service with good, fresh food. Friendly staff as well, I would definitely stop by again if I ever have the chance!
Friendly staff, cute atmosphere and pizza was hot, fresh and ready by the slice .... definitely and easy quick spot for food.

We live in Ohio and a new luigi's was opened at Polaris, same great food as in Nashville. These places are also attached to Wanna B's karaoke and Tequila Cowboys. Spent the evening for dinner, then went to watch a good country band then finished up the evening singing some karaoke. Good night with our friends.
m-GAMtteFJGUDrIb2yN-bw:
I took a friend of mine here to enjoy some music and have a few drinks. It was our first time being there, after remodeling into Revelery & Anthem of course.  As I walked up to the front entrance of Revelery I immediately noticed the old school boom boxes layered on the wall just behind the counter where the girl was checking ID's. It was pretty sweet to see them utilize them for that.  It immediately gave me the impression, along with the girl at the front door, that this is a trendy place now.  
We walked in and immediately I noticed the difference in layout from the previous business that had took residence in there.  The bar is much more pleasant to look at and sits in the space on the lower floor just across from where they built a dj booth that overlooks the floor.  It was nice.  Much nicer then I had expected.  We immediately went to the bar for drinks and after I had ordered I heard the bartender explaining that there were drink specials for this night involving jack daniels and vodka drinks.  Luckily I already ordered a whiskey sour so my face lit up realizing I was getting a lot more for my buck.  I checked the place out a bit, they even have an outside smoking area that was built onto the side so you no longer have to walk all the way out the front any more.  Brilliant idea if you ask me.  I found a spot at the railing bar on the upper level overlooking the dance floor and bar area to sit back and enjoy my drink.  The dj's there were playing for their first night as Represent Anthem Vol.001.  Which is a drum n bass music night.  If you enjoy the music genre I would recommend catching the next night when they're there.  I noticed during my time there that they even serve food as a friend of mine was eating something they had brought out to him on the bar.  So I may need to check out their menu on my next venture here.  I highly recommend this place for a good spot to hang with friends while listening to some good music.

I have only been to Anthem to attend Yelp events but both times I have been I have enjoyed myself immensely.  The staff are incredible.  The drinks and unique and tasty and reasonably priced.  The bar bites are always yummy.  They have an excellent happy hour.  And this is one of the rare clubs in Nashville, so as the night gets on it gets rather dancey in here.  There's plenty of parking and lots of space to explore with friends.  I need to come here on a random weekend night to really experience this place but what I have seen so far has been really awesome and different for the Nashville landscape.
I've never been treated better at any club in town. The amenities, the shows and the decor are second to none when it comes to dance clubs.
c5vToRxz_e8DNV2oD3xzog:
El Palenque is back!  I have frequented this restaurant for 20 years and thought it was closed because of the construction this summer.  Apparently the owners were required to remodel in order to renew their lease.  So happy to know they are still open!!  I actually miss the old decor.

This is a solid authentic Mexican restaurant (not Tex-Mex) and one of my go-to places in the Green Hills area.  The salsa is my favorite in town and they fry their own chips so they are always fresh, warm and super crispy.  We also love the guacamole, pollo fundido, taquitos and margaritas.  

They have outdoor seating which is nice on a pretty day even though it overlooks the parking lot.

This is a great, old school, Americanized Mexican restaurant. Total comfort food. I had a bean and cheese burrito, a regular, crunchy taco and a cheese enchilada. It was everything I was hoping for. Guacamole is good. I wasn't crazy about the Queso starter. Loved it. I'm sure I'll become a regular!

This restaurant deserves five stars for being exactly what it is presented to be - a neighborhood Mexican restaurant that is fast, good, and cheap.  As a group of four on New Year's Eve around lunch hour, we were seated right away.  Our service was extremely quick, food was great, margaritas were strong, and the bill was inexpensive.
ASUthJLYb9KRBXy-hn70vA: I came here with a friend who suggested Calypso as an option for me and my unique diet I'm currently on. It was great suggestion. There are many homemade and healthy options for meat and plant eaters alike. I got a chicken salad with a side of the greens. The greens were a little too acidic for my taste but I enjoyed the salad and the vinaigrette  I asked for since I couldn't eat the ranch.  Chicken was plentiful on the salad. Overall a nice option for those of us trying to eat healthy.

This was really good food. Great healthy choices. The price was low, the flavors were original, the service was fast. Just what we were looking for when passing through Nashville and needing some dinner.
I always make a point to stop in when in town. One of my all time favorite places. Food is amazing and price is very reasonable!the best greens in town and the Curry sauce is to die for!
SYaTEGlLmJZ6Jgx_DK_wZw: My family had lunch there today.  The food comes slow, but that is how food cooked to order should be. Everything was delicious.  We had breakfast for lunch and my picky 4 year old had in his words "the best grilled cheese" ever.  Brunch usually has a crazy line everywhere in East Nashville, but at the Meridian Cafe it was nice.

I enjoyed the food at the Meridian Cafe.  The cornbread was great, and I really liked the sweet potatoes and the green beans.  The fried chicken was unfortunately a bit dry.  It is VERY reasonably priced and the portions are big.  The staff is friendly.

I'm a bigger fan now, 4 years after being the first reviewer, now as a breakfast customer. I'm thankful they're still here so I could re-assess. The salmon cakes are crispy outside, soft inside, and delicately flavored. Two pancakes will fill you up. The hash browns are an interesting reformatting of tater tots, seasoned well and crumbled. 

This is a spot where people seem to come often, if not daily, though I don't feel excluded.

My only warning: You will walk out with your clothes smelling like something fried. It's a great reminder of what you had, but be sure you're not going to a formal event after.
7p8DY8LPwBSdF0gzJTqIAQ: My favorite BBQ in Nashville. Good staff, good prices and portions, and good deals for returning customers. Definitely try the Alabama White Sauce.

Wow is all I can say!! Fastest delivery food hot and taste great!! The meatloaf is the BEST . They were out of the Brisket I ordered and they were nice to call and let me know threw in an extra side. This was my second order with them and was not let down...
Excellent. The brisket was as good as my favorite Rudy's in Texas. I'll be back. I even had enough to take home. Thanks for a great meal.
c32mGy62lMCp-ltl0Rcc5g: Hole in the wall next to hotel that we walked by several times.  Decided to grab a bite before leaving town.  So glad we did.  We both had the philly cheesesteak and fries.  Awesome.   Highly recommend.

This was my first visit at Phillyman as a customer.  I've visited before with co-workers but reluctant to order.  I ordered the whiting fish sandwich and I will say it was GREAT!  Cooked fresh and not one flaw to my sandwich.  I am definitely a fan and will return soon for the chicken livers!

The home made tater chips are great. A very small place with LARGE TASTE .
The fried chicken is moist but not greasy .

Philly Cheese Steak one of the top 3 I have ever had . 

The service is fast and the staff are extremely friendly
H0MScRPcaB_TuFNEhWvJQg: It's so nice to have a local coffee shop close to home. Me and my husband visit Bowtie at least 3 times a week. Colby and his staff are so amazing and they always remember our drink order. We have nothing but great things to say about this new addition to Donelson.

I visited The Bowtie Barista for the first time yesterday and fell in love with it. I got a latte and muffin, and both were delicious! The latte was made with macadamia nut milk which I ended up enjoying a lot. The place has a simple, homey atmosphere with several tables and chairs. I was able to sit a get some good reading in without it getting too noisy or crowded which was wonderful. I'll definitely be back!

Great spot to stop and get coffee. They just opened and are in the process of expanding their menu to juice and food. Great coffee and service so far can't wait to see this business grow. They also have milk and milk alternatives.
4zDTd12kFeqC_4KtwmnLsQ:
I just had a wonderful experience today at the Juice Bar in Berry Hill. First, I was greeted ever so politely by the staff who didn't rush me at all while I pondered the menu board of what to get. They had lots of yummy options to choose from- juices, to smoothies, to make your own, granola bars, on the go options, salads, and more! 

While I waited for my delicious Green smoothie to be made, the staff chatted with me and made me feel right at home. I also got the cranberry quinoa kale salad which was also delicious. 


I will definitely be making this my go to juice bar since they were super quick making my smoothie! Awesome experience - if your in the area I recommend you try this place! :)

We stopped in for breakfast. Smoothies were the perfect breakfast option to prepare for a day of football watching and beer drinking. The service was really friendly and we enjoyed chatting with the two employees while they made our smoothies. The inside of the building is cozy and they have a nice patio as well. It has a local feel, but turns out, they have several other locations. I'd stop in again if I saw one somewhere else! 

The green smoothie is delicious! I forgot to ask if they added sugar, so I'm not sure, but ignorance is bliss I guess. It's pretty sweet but very tasty! My husband had less luck with his. He ordered a chocolate banana smoothie and said it was okay. I wish they had more food options. The smoothie was enough for me, but he got a locally made pumpkin peanut protein bar. Hate to say, it wasn't very good. But their other food options, although limited, looked promising. Hummus, quinoa salad, vegan truffles, etc.
Tried the acai bowl. So good! I'm excited to know there's a juice bar so near to me. I will definitely be back.
yHxABIXZn9MVfrjloKd7bw: Had a wonderful dinner and drinks.  I would recommend the Blackened Fish Tacos.  If you like Mules, I would recommend the Dragonberry Mule.  Great atmosphere for the family or if you're dining alone.  Service was superb as well.  My bartenders name was Robert and he was amazing!

Love to bring my family here. This place is definately unique in its own right.  They have fake animals there such as gorillas, elephants, tigers, lions, etc, and every 15 mins or so they move and make some noises.  Every 30 mins there is lightning and all the animals move and make noise giving you the feel of being in a jungle or as they call themselves, the rainforest.  It is supers in that aspect.  Keep in mind that some young children might get scared of the noises, but most seem to enjoy it.

As far as service, it is a hit and miss.  I have had experience with waiters that do a lousy job. But the majority of the times, i have gotten a decent server.  Last i went was 1 week ago and he was extraordinarily nice. Even heard that it was my birthday and decided to suprise me with a free very delicious cupcake to our amazement.  It was an overall great experience

As far as prices, they are pretty high, especially for their salads.  I do find them a bit too ridiculesly high.  But their food is great.  Our favorite is their coconut shrimp.
If you also want to out a smile on your family faces you can order their VOLCANO. It is a big chocolate cake with ice cream and sparkles.  It is expensive but feeds a lot of people.  Sadly i am allergic to chocolate ao i just get to watch, but it looks amazing.

This restaurant has an atmosphere like no other...this is the number one reason I enjoy coming. Life like rainforest decor and animal robots that go off every so often make this an awesome place to bring your kids and show them something educational, and grab something decent to eat. The prices are a tad steep but you do get large portions. Definitely recommend this to families!!! Your kids will love it.
27eH5ZI6JvEIW66QadM8EA: Chipotle is a go to for fast food as it is the best quality at reasonable prices. They are inclusive in menu selections offering both meat and vegan friendly plant based options.   I appreciate that immensely.
I love Chipotle, but this one really stands out. The staff is always friendly, professional, and the taste is consistent. I recently ordered catering and it was superb.

Food is fresh and usually very good. Quality has deteriorated since my last visit.  Lettuce was scarce, and rusted.  Chicken was gristly.  I ordered a salad and received less than a cup of lettuce.  Who skimps on lettuce?  Maybe it is an off day.  Noticed their guacamole was discolored last time.  Will give them one more try, before giving up.   

Food was excellent on follow up visit.  Ample lettuce,  fresh guacamole.  Glad I gave them another try.
D3NEVZaEIOR1nFCPnHNUzw: Friendly service and tasty gumbo. First time to visit. I ordered the shrimp gumbo to go. It was a large portion, hot and had plenty of shrimp with just enough spice to keep my nose running. Highly recommend.

Fun.  Friendly staff.   Interesting decor & music--provides a little respite and change from your office or usual eatery.  Decent portions and reasonably tasty.  One bonus for non-hot food eaters--you get to decide how spicy your dish is (although obviously some of the spice is cooked in).  Not sure this will actually help you if you don't like any spicy, but is helpful for me who stays on the low to moderate spice scale generally.  Oh...also they allow you to taste dishes before you purchase.

I don't eat cajun much, but I thought the food was heavy on rice and light on meat.  I had the jambalya, so that may be par for the course.  

For folks that eat downtown on a regular basis, this is probably a nice alternative to the usual fare. 

I will be probably be back to try something with chicken.  (3.75)
This place is great! So happy it's close to where I am working a temp job. The etouffee is top notch as is the peach cobbler! Yum! Very affordable and delicious.
jugVvW4yAJu92lzv3jun0w:
We are in town visiting Nashville for the first time.  We didn't look up any reviews or any research.  We just drove around and stumbled onto this place.  I have mixed feelings about this place.  

Walking in it seemed to be more of a restaurant,  but it's very informal.  So no host or sign saying to seat yourself.  So you are on your own there. We came on a Sunday night so it wasn't that busy, even though it was trivia night. 

For food - you go up to the counter and they bring it over.  The food is all hormone free and organically grown.  It was pretty tasty and unique. I would never have put shrimp,  rice,  marinara, pita and mixed veggies together.  They did and it works on the Flame Grilled Shrimp Platter. It was reasonably priced for around $16 including a soda. 

For Brews - I thought it was pretty impressive, most of them are local brewa.  Again,  you need to go up to the counter to order and its at the same place you order your food.  

Overall I would definitely come back,  now that I know how things work.  No waitresses!

After returning to Kay Bob's a second time, I've decided that I like it. This time, I had the sandwich filled with a bunch of veggies and goat cheese (Dancing Desiree, I think), and it tasted fresh and delicious. And it was filling! I went with the spicy mango as my sauce for the sandwich, and I loved it! Perfect amount of sweet and spicy for a sandwich. I loved the sweet potato chips as the side, although I found them to be a little on the salty side. All in all, it was a pleasant lunch (and the Scoutmob deal made it that much better!). I think what makes Kay-Bob win is the fact that the concept is fairly unique in the area, even though the sandwiches themselves may not be the best around. I like the garage door being open too as it has gotten warmer out - great lunch spot in the Vandy area!

I think I like Kay Bob's more and more every time I go! 4 stars are no longer enough. The vegetarian quesadilla-like Dancin' Desiree with sweet potato chips and spicy mango sauce is DA BOMB. It's so good I'm bringing back terminology from 1997. 

The dancin' desiree is the best in my opinion because it has black beans, caramelized onions, roasted red peppers, butternut squash and goat cheese. Seriously, it's dope. All that and a bag (read: side) of (sweet potato) chips.

And now that it's warm enough to open those garage doors but not so hot that you stick to the seats? I'm down. Seriously, at this point if anyone asks where I want to eat for lunch I'mma request getting jiggy with Kay Bob's.
yKSQMO9mUYP9y5OvFGKRLg: Had a good Sunday lunch, service was very good. Green sauce on the Bandera burrito was tasty. Asked for the hot sauce, was different and tasty.  Will come back.

One of my top favorite Mexican restaurants in east Nashville.  I like how it has a large open area in the middle that makes it very bright.  The booths surrounding the center square are darker, so it's a very unique Mexican environment compared to other Mexican restaurants. I like it on rainy or too-hot days where I wish I could sit outside, but instead, I enjoy the brightness and AC inside.  I love the lunch mushroom quesadilla and I always add spinach.  Food is fresh and service has been great every time!
Great food not as busy as it usually is because came in early. Good was hot and fresh . The salsa was really good. Finished off with the fried ice cream which was amazing !!!
igpAM0YJ6Eoa7wYfF2FRqg: I've lived in Hillsboro Village for the past 4 years, and I'm THRILLED that's there's finally a place for nice cocktails. Love the vibe of this place too, and hope that it doesn't become overrun with hipsters in the next months.
Carmelo (is that how you spell it?) was the best bartender ever and made recommendations when asked that were perfect. Also super fun to talk too!

Honestly, I have not wanted to review this place at all. Granted, I'm not one to believe my reviews have an insane impact, but regardless, I have been scared of giving up Nashville's best bar to the rest of the world. When you get to the actual space, the speakeasy vibe of comfort, casual, yet appealing, it seeps into your bloodstream. People enjoy themselves, but it's never crazy crowded. More than anything, that is why I have loved it. It is a safe haven of great drinks, friendly bartenders, and good times.

It's called Amari, plural for the alcoholic spirit, amaro. I had to ask about that, but I am glad I did. My favorite cocktail and shot of a spirit involve an amaro. The Black Manhattan cocktail, and fernet are my favorite things here, but there is a crazy extensive menu, and the bartenders are there to walk you through it.

Look, I'm probably not doing the best job on this review, considering I have had a drink or two already, but you have to check this place out if you are into the description I am giving of this fine establishment!
8tsgnMFsMHuMIgrdjpEHLg: Best milk tea I've had in a long time. Very glad we saw this place and went in. Definitely going to be my spot to go to in Nashville for my bubble tea fix.
Has new owners and the place has renovated really nicely. The milk teas are the best and most authentic in Nashville. Refreshing and many varieties to choose from. Will definitely be back for more!

Came to Nashville hoping to find a boba spot. This was the first place that showed up and was just down the street from where I'm staying. Super friendly and welcoming staff. Ordered just a milk tea, they asked how I liked it and made it exactly how I wanted. I'm from Southern California and know what good boba is. Seems like they're just testing right now to get the right taste. Im mos def coming back for more throughout my stay out here.
9xa_JD-_Vqbnusmx0k6i9w:
We came here because other place had 1 hour wait on a Saturday.  They said we'll seat you now. Ok so maybe it wasn't our first choice but, oh boy, this place was good. It's a burger place. You want a good burger then go. You won't be disappointed.  Go drinks too. Great Atmosphere.  Go. Take a chance. You'll like it.

Live music sometimes.
Amazing! Rolled into Nashville after 8:00, made it here before last call for food at 9:00 and enjoyed delicious burgers and 2 for 1 drafts. We'll be back.
DELICIOUS!! Super reasonable prices and a lot of food. Literally in a back alley. It has lots of character and everyone was ver nice. Definitely coming back!
