oI_0uod6jeq8fJJu8ySFSA: My favorite Chinese place! Eat here many a bit too often. Never had a thing I didn't like. The chicken with garlic sauce and lo mein lunch special is the best. Usually turns into 2 meals for me. Also love the general tsos and honey chicken. Service is fast and super friendly!

Been here twice now. Ordered online with beyondmenu. Food is ready and waiting for me when I walk in, yet still piping hot.  Good portions for the price. Fresh. Yummy. I've been to at least half a dozen Chinese places since moving here in October and this one is the best I have found. A+
Love the food and the owners. Best Kung Pao Shrimp in town. My husband loves the honey chicken. Would recommend to all.
GmXBlRwiwHSaq3ekhvIaXw: We love subs. We're from out of town and have been seeing jersey mike commercials on tv for who knows how long. We noticed there was one near our hotel so stopped in for lunch and they subs are amazing. So much meat. It was fast even though we got their during the lunch rush. Granted this is a chain and I've never been to another location but I know we'll eat here again before heading home.

"It's sure no Subway"???  Tart was certainly correct about that.  Instead of piling pre-packaged cold cuts onto measly little bricks of bread, Jersey Mike's slices all their meat right in front of you.  All of their items are fresh, from the bread to the tomatoes, and their sub sizes are 7" and 14" rather than Subway's puny 6" and 12".

Ok...sorry to get so worked up.  I worked at a J. Mike's in high school.  Yes, I'm a bit biased, but this is genuinely one of the best sub chains out there.  Unfortunately, Jersey Mike's aren't as ubiquitous as places like Subway, but of their many locations, this is one of my favorite.  When I attended Lipscomb, this was one of my go-to food spots .

The staff at this location certainly isn't talkative; they don't seem quite as accommodating as the folks at the Elliston Jersey Mike's.  However, their subs are always fresh, and the shop makes a pretty good spot to have lunch.  Parking is typically plentiful, and, aside from Baja Burrito, you're probably not going to find a better spot to eat in the immediate area.

I had never eaten at jersey Mike's. I was always a subway girl. But WOW! I was definitely missing out. Everything tasted much more fresh. The staff has always been friendly as ive been so frequently now i might be a regular now. I would recommend checking them out.
d9OUbLtz0F5buaBBmIVArg: Best Thai I've had in a while. Wonderful service with great ambiance and even better food. Please do yourself a favor and dine here soon.

Excellent food. Panang Curry, tomkha soup, sesame chicken, and spring rolls were all perfect. 
We ordered to go and had prompt service. They even gave us some extra spring rolls. The food is really a huge step above every other Asian spot in the area.
I have eaten here several times in the past few months. Great food, great value. This is the first place I consider when I want good Asian food
NRd01eXx85MPnfSz6YInvA:
Until two months ago I didn't know this place existed.  Friday, I finally got to try it out.  When I first walked in the Cashier and I recognized each other from another Mexican Restaurant.  I explained I didn't go there anymore and she immediately understood why.  She began telling me how great this place was and was so excited to share that the flour tortillas were made in house.

First I was surprised when asked if I wanted an American Coke or a Mexico Coke.  Being no fool I ordered the Mexican variety because it is so much better.  They brought me three choices for Salsa.  I recognized two as the beyond hot versions they serve at the Mexican trucks.  They are more that hot, but the third had just the right amount of heat and was quite tasty.

I ordered a Chili Relleno and was asked what kind of tortillas I wanted with them.  I was a little confused because I had never gotten tortillas anywhere else.  I opted for the made in house flour tortillas.

FIRST, the Chili Relleno has been seeded which made me HAPPY, HAPPY, HAPPY  The cheese they used had a high melt temp and didn't ease out which made it more enjoyable.  The sauce they used was light and fresh with a wonderful flavor to it..  The rice was also enjoyable.

I can't wait to go back and try other things on their menu.

I think I scared a couple of people that work there as I photographed the food so I could share it on Social Media.  I don't know if this will show up but here is the link to that photo:  https://www.facebook.com/photo.php?fbid=10201743402491597&set=gm.237188099817533&type=1&theater
The food here is very close to food in Mexico, I'm from Mexico and this places is my favorite Mexican restaurant :)

We stopped by here on a Sunday around 11:30am. The place was packed. The restaurant is located on between the Lowes and the strip mall that has Big Lots in it. We entered to find a slight wait, like I said, the place was packed. A lot of people with a Hispanic background were eating here, which is a good sign. We were sat at a table and given the chips and salsa and menus. We took a little while to find what we wanted. The server came by two times within 5 minutes to check on us, then we didn't see her. A man came up to us, I think he was either the owner or the manger. He greeted us and took our order. I ordered the quesadilla de camaron. It comes with a ton of shrimp and a handmade tortilla. 

The chips and salsa were pretty good. I loved the salsa, it's got a nice heat to it. They also bring a little green bottle of some sauce, it's hot as well. It's kind of peppery in flavor. We got our food within ten minutes. The guy who took our order came back and asked how we were, the also said that we was going to bring back some of their fresh water. He brought us some rice water and mango water. Rice water, or horchata is a creamy drink that has nutmeg and cinnamon. It was great. The mango water was very refreshing. I was very nice that he let us try the different waters.

The quesadilla I had was huge, it was also $13.99. I was only able to eat half of it and had to take the other half home. It came with rice and beans or salad. I picked the rice and beans. They were okay, they both came on the plate cold, both Phillip and I received them cold. The quesadilla was loaded with shrimp. The shrimp come with the tails, which is kind of hard to deal with when in a quesadilla. Also, the quesadilla is very hot when it's served, which makes since, but it also cooks the shrimp more as they sit inside the quesadilla. The shrimp end up being a little tough. Nothing incredibly bad, but not as tender as I'd like.

Overall I really did enjoy the meal. The chips and salsa were great. I thought the guy that helped us was very nice and you could tell he cared about the place. The servers weren't that attentive and the girl that cashed me out at the front counter wasn't the friendliest. I enjoyed the shrimp quesadilla but wished the tails were on the shrimp. We will be back to try more.
_3WAkg_nfdRoOvadFs9teA:
Yes. 
I know that I normally zip around town like a mad woman but sometimes this broad is feelin' LAZY. What do I do when I want a healthy meal on the go without having to leave the comfort of my driver's side seat?

I zip right on through this Subway location's drive-thru as it's the only one in the area that I know of that has this luxury? 

I order the usual. Veggie Patty with all the veggies, sweet onion sauce, and toast it on wheat. 

I dig it.
Always fast and great customer service. This location is definitely one of the best Subways I've been to. The staff on the drive-thru are always fast my order has never been incorrect.

I came by to pick up a sub for a family member. Was quite delighted to see they have a drive-thru. Quick, convenient, and easy. She ate her sub with no complaints, so it appears the order was correct and tasty. It's directly across from Zanie's. I initially passed by, so I hope the landmark helps.
2LSVbGkyFSVflCbjWyVrhg: Such a chill and relaxing place to hang with cats and have a fantastic cup of coffee. The reservations were sold out today but the owners were still kind enough to let me spend some time there. I got a cup of bongo java coffee that was perfectly brewed, and I'm super picky about my coffee! Love it there and I'll be back.

So fun! A big group of us came for a work outing on a Friday, right when they opened. The cats all have cute names, based on singers (Tim McClaw, for example) and are VERY well taken care of. Made me wish I was a cat. I expected the cafe to have a distinct "cat aroma" but it smelled great. They really keep the place clean. The two ladies working (Kate and oh darn - I can't remember the other ladies name :( were so sweet. You could tell they really cared about the cats. They were super friendly and answered all of our questions. (The ladies, not the cats). I'll definitely be back. Oh, and one of my coworkers adopted one of the cats!

This place is purr-fect for cat lovers, tourists and locals looking for something new to do. 

There is plenty of space to hang- couches and luscious floor cushions. Wand toys are provided so you can play with the cats. There are plenty of photo taking opportunities- the place is music themed with drums repurposed for cat lounging. The decor is so, so cute. Have your cameras ready!

The best part is that the cats are up for adoption and they are well cared for. There is a lot of space for the cats up high so that they can get away from humans if they wish. 

I highly, highly recommend this place. Great cause and lots of fun!
ev9PTrWNm6gX2KCIW04QwQ: I love the Finding hidden gems when I am out of town and this is one of those places. My husband recommended I stop here after reading the reviews and I was not disappointed. Strong coffee, good food, and great service! Everyone was friendly and the atmosphere nostalgic. I highly recommend you give it a try if you are in the area. I will be back some day.

You can't go wrong at Wendell Smith's.  In my opinion, it's one of the top 3 meat & three's in Nashville.  I love their baked chicken.  Very, very fast service.

Good ol' Southern cooking.....

The other meat & three's I like are:
Arnold's
Swett's

Some people have written that it's cheap.  I wouldn't say it's cheap.  Cheap to me is $5.  I usually spend $10 (with sweet tea & meal).  Not expensive....and not cheap.  I don't mind what I spend there.  It's become a lunchbreak staple for me.

We made a last minute decision to switch from Hattie Bs (which we always go to when we are in Nashville) to this spot as I loved the real nostalgic look of it and the reviews were favorable.  

The food gets 3 stars and the value gets 5.  The "meat" part of my meat (beef tips on noodles) and 3 was really good as was the baked chicken everyone else got.  However, none of the sides were very good.  I think they all come from a can and while edible, I would rather they charge a couple more dollars, offer a few less of them on the menu and make them all from scratch.  I would probably not come back as Hattie Bs is across the street but I don't regret the decision to to try it out.
GQCAdpC230swTKfIJLkbHA: What a cool little cigar bar hidden in downtown Nashville.  Bar menu with gourmet sandwiches and cheese trays.  Great selection of cigars.  Must buy cigars on site or pay $10 cover charge.

I'm very glad I found this place.  They've been in a soft open for the past two months (they're grand opening is in October).  There's lots of room with very comfortable sofas and arm chairs with additional seating in the loft area (with power outlets).  The bar is great and so is the service.  I liked it so much I moved our herf get together from another spot to here later this week.  This will definitely be a go to place when I'm in the downtown Nashville area.

Very nice bar with welcoming owner and staff. Nice selection with good service both in bar and humidor. Nice selection of cigars at a reasonable price. 

Supportive of military and law enforcement
NWNMw4t-N-l353aNl1ON6Q: New to the area, having tried a bunch of bars in East Nashville nothing comes close to this. Hands down the best vibe I've found in Nashville. Friendly up to the point where you are still allowed to remain anonymous. Cheap beer. No liquor... Which works for me, but maybe not for you.

Betty's Grill feels like the inside of a Nascar trailer.  You can count the menu items on one hand and the beer list on the other.  Corona is the most exotic item you're going to find here.

The bartender/waiter (the only one in the establishment) had a quaint manner of of speaking and walking, despite looking like a biker type.  He seemed to go across the floor like he was on a moving walkway and talked the same way.  He also introduced every item on the menu with "the best in town" in a sort of monotone.

There were half a dozen people dawdling over the bar or the single pool table, but it was rather quiet and peaceful.  I had a cold Miller Lite and some hot Chicken Tenders, both satisfying.  Next time I will try the Wings or this thing they call a "Cheeseburger."

The BG is conveniently located on 49th Avenue, between Charlotte and the no-man's land that exists near I-40.  A true dive bar.

UPDATE:  I've revisited another day after sundown, and they do in fact have a jukebox, so it's not as peaceful as previously described.

Like Cheers...only with more NASCAR paraphernalia!  Way, way more!  I genuinely like this place, even if it isn't exactly my scene.  I was on Charlotte today with a buddy of mine, and we just decided to duck into the Betty's and check it out.

First of all, Betty's is full of 40- to 60-year-old men who I can only assume are devout regulars.  A couple of them have undergone tracheotomies (probably because of all the smoke in Betty's), and they all look like they spend most of their days there.  Betty herself seems very sweet, and all of her customers call her by name.  Betty has the beers you'd expect (Miller Lite, Budweiser, Natural Light, et cetera), and they're $2 a piece--cheap even by dive bar standards.

I ordered a cheeseburger, which only cost me $4.50, and Betty made it herself (pretty quickly).  The cheeseburger was genuinely the best I've had in weeks, and I've had one at Bobbie's Dairy Dip recently, so that's really saying something.  There is a virtual bowling game and a pool table, not to mention dozens of posters and pictures with Dale Earnhardt, Jr.  Trust me...this place is the real deal.  It may be a dingy, dim, old dive full of people twice my age, but there's just something about it I like.  It's like a Dino's for the West Side.
2MAQeAqmD8enCT2ZYqUgIQ:
The Melting Pot is a Fondue restaurant that serves a full dinner and desert menu.  I would recommend this place for a fun dessert spot. Anytime I think of Fondue I remember the scene in "Lost in Translation" where Bob remarks, "What kind of restaurant makes you cook your own food!?".... but funny remarks aside, the atmosphere is romantic and the crowd is always dressed well.  This is a nice date spot, just because the restaurant is really intimate and vibey. 

The desert tray comes with a Fondue Pot of hot sweet chocolate, and you get a tray of fruits, chocolates, marshmallows, and cheesecake to dip and cover with the chocolate. It is all pretty tasty, and fun. You can always pull one of those moves where you feed your significant other from across the table. 

The price is pretty steep considering you cook everything yourself, and the desert requires no cooking at all...but that is the phenomenon of Fondue for you, don't try to understand, just dip!
Great food.  Wonderful ambiance.  Terrific place for a super fun dinner.

This is as good as it gets! We celebrated my 40th birthday here! As we were seated, the waiter had placed a red rose at my seat and discreatly wished me Happy Birthday. The meal was amazing and the staff very attentive. The manager even stopped by to wish me Happy Birthday as well.
FSrRTDWwJlK9ZHU7aIIsxg:
Jim's Nachos is a perfect little Mexican joint right across from Lipscomb University.  That's why I take away one star--sometimes overloaded with college kids.  But, food is great!

I usually enjoy a lunch special--ultra cheap, plenty of food, and quick!  My favorite is the Speedy Gonzalez--taco (chicken or beef, regular or soft) and enchilada, with choice of rice or beans.  Oh and also includes chips and salsa!  Not the best chips I've had, but the salsa is spicy and tasty.

Open for lunch and dinner--till 9pm which is a plus for this late eater.  They will also deliver and cater--while I was in there today, they were discussing a catering order for over 100 people for Cinco de Mayo--I'll have to keep them in mind!  

If you want cheap, good Mexican food in a nice neighborhood setting, try Jim's.  He won't disappoint!

My wife and I just ordered dinner for delivery from Jim's Nachos.  This was our first experience with them, and we were very pleased.  We ordered a double order of their 'Fajitas Mixta' - beef, chicken and shrimp fajitas.  They were excellent, and the portions were way more than we could even come close to eating.  We'll be eating it for lunch tomorrow as well.  The shrimp was real, not that frozen mess you usually expect from an inland restaurant.  The fajita side fillings were also good.  We also ordered chips, salsa and queso dip.  All were fresh and tasty.  Delivery took only about 20 minutes (we live pretty close to them).  One small complaint, they didn't give us enough tortillas for the massiive amount of fajita fixin's, so you might want to ask for a few extra.

Had Jim's deliver dinner for my wife and I on Friday night and it was perfect. The food was very well cooked and the sides were perfectly prepared.

I had Chili Colorado and it was excellent to say the least and the cleaned plate was an excellent reminder.

Will we order or eat there again? Heck yeah we will. Less than a mile a way, a no brainer.
y7AvDp_0WZlyjoAeWULJMg:
Ah, Dan McGuiness. Irish food, beer, bands - what's not to love? 

I have to admit, I'm more than a little bit nostalgic about this place. My friend used to play in the cover band on Friday nights and I guess that's how it all got started.

I'll give you the bad news first: Anytime anything cool happens (i.e. St. Pats, Halloween) there's going to be a hefty cover. The service is so-so depending on the night.

What I love: The food. I love the fish 'n' chips and certain days of the week (last I remember it was Thursday) the do have specials. I suggest the wee order; it's more than enough. I also like the mahi mahi sandwich. Go off on a limb and try the potato salad instead of the fries - you will not be disappointed.

The beer selection is formidable and good times are usually had by all. They also host a writer's night. One of my favorite memories of Danny Mc's was watching the world cup shoulder to shoulder with everyone cheering for the USA. It was hot, it was sweaty, it was hard to get a beer, but it was worth it.
love the fish and chips special on thurs. great place to sit outside and drink a beer. great fried pickles and chicken tenders.

This is one of my favorite (reasonably priced) restaurants in Nashville and over the years, I've tried many of their dishes including bangers & mash (very good), shepherd's pie (very good) & their cheeseburger (huge but not particularly memorable). Try the Scotch eggs sometime when you aren't on a date. They're delicious! However, I love their fish & chips which is what I had today. It was their $6 special. I'm a sucker for fish & chips and consider myself a connoisseur. In my estimation, this is the best in Tennessee. The light batter is perfect and not particularly greasy. The cod is tasty and perfectly cooked. The lunch portion was perfect though I have had trouble with the dinner portion feeling guilty over-stuffing myself. 

The ambiance is basically what an American thinks an Irish pub looks like. Soccer on the flat screens. High backed leather booths. Comfortable but not even close to authentic. They have a full bar stocked with dark beer and Irish whiskey. I have started and ended evenings there and always have a good time but today's meal was nonalcoholic. The service has usually been good even when they're busy and today was no exception. The attractive waitress was efficient but not annoying. 

I notice someone didn't like the patio. I don't either and won't eat there if the restaurant's full. They present live music or a DJ on the patio in the summer but I don't go there for that. Frankly, there are better music venues down the street and whoever books this place doesn't have a clue.
1QBbJ27hYLxAwo9zP8436Q: Good burger but for the price it should be dynamite In-N-Out or 5 Guys level. All of their ingredients are all-natural and that comes with a $14.83 price tag for a double burger, regular fries, and a large soft drink. Thank you to the staff for your great hospitality on your opening day and thank you for the t-shirt.

Went here, had a burger, fries, and iced tea. As a fast food restaurant(which it really isn't since they are painfully slow) the food is very good albeit expensive for what it is. If you are looking for a top notch hamburger, there are a multitude of places around town that put out a better burger. I'm sure I will enjoy the shakes though. Not much else I can say. Definitely didn't live up to the hype in my opinion.

Nashville finally gets a Shake Shack! What! One of my fave burger chains hands down. Rivaling up against In-n-Out. I've been to other Shake Shack locations, and I must say the Nashville location is one of the best. I enjoyed the Nashville exclusive burger. Makes me sad that Nash gets all the cool stuff AFTER I move. But hey..when visiting home I have many options! Shake Shack I'll be back....to play foosball.
EqOZwljhql-e1JthBQG7UQ: Went here for the first time today- was really impressed by the Kogi taco (more than the fish taco).  Looking forward to going back to try other options, including kimchi quesadilla and the burritos that were only advertised by a handwritten sign next to the main menu.

5 stars alone just for the fact that Peter is bold enough to try this West Coast phenomenon in Nashville.  How much better of a concept is there than Korean food infused with Mexican?  

The menu here is very small; you'll find spicy pork, kogi (short ribs), chicken, and fish tacos.  They also have a spicy kimchi quesadilla, and a steak quesadilla.  We ordered the spicy pork--a really nice flavor, even though it lacked a bit of heat.  The kogi was super tender, nicely cooked, and had a slightly sweet marinade.  Both tacos were topped with a combination of lettuce, paper thin cucumber, red onions, and dressed in a semi sweet dressing.  I would've preferred kimchi on the tacos, but the flavor of the meat made up for the toppings.  The tortillas were stacked and tasted like it was a mixture of corn and flour, surprisingly delicious.  I couldn't pick out exactly what the accompanying sauce was, it tasted like a mild Korean chili paste with a sweet finish, and had a very faint orange color.

We ate our tacos at a table outside, and when we finished Peter personally visited with us.  It was a nice touch that is unexpected from a "taco stand."  He also brought us complimentary bottled waters.

4 tacos and a drink for under $10.

Peter's is about a 5-10 minute drive north on Rosa Park, located on the very left corner of Rosa Parks & Clarksville Pike.

We tried the kim chi quesadilla, the spicy pork taco, korean bbq with kim chi taco and the kogi (korean bbq) burrito. Overall everything was good. The spicy pork was super delicious and actually better in my opinion compared to the kogi taco. The kim chi quesadilla was pretty good too but needs some meat. 

We just went through the drive through which wasn't super fast but the food was worth the wait.

I would definite come back for the spicy pork tacos. For $2 a piece, it's a steal!
BhT3qX7-EndTIpSXgospBQ: Dude. This bar is awesome. Never too crowded because it's a good sized building. The drinks are amazing. The food is phenomenal. The staff is fantastic. They have darts, as well add some other games. I really don't have a single bad thing to say. It's new, it's shiny, and I like it.

Cool bar with great draft beer selection. Very inviting with a restaurant and bar vibe. The bar tender was awesome! Gave me a bunch of places to check out in Nashville.
Good spacious vibe. It's a cross between sports bar and American restaurant with a divey vibe. Good prices and seem to have a great brunch option.
Good spot. The owner is a good guy and is very accommodating. The tots and chicken wings were very good. Relaxed atmosphere and not too crowded.
V_c80hsmCNRwU3V46sB5EQ:
Still my favorite breakfast joint.
Please correct the hours on this site. I arrive shortly after 6 am and people are finishing their meals and leaving.
Keep up the good work!
Great little spot. Friendly service, good menu, cheap prices. I got an awesome breakfast of oatmeal & egg/cheese biscuit for less than $4. We'll be back!

* Basic but solid breakfast
*Normally 1-2 waitresses working
*You can order to go food
* Appeared to seat around 45 people (packed out)
* No alcohol
nIHCBYGcgBWixinvsFFXvg: If chipotle and Taco Bell had a love child, this would be it. Super tasty tex mex with lots of options for toppings. Love that they offer different burrito sizes and different tortillas! Also, the staff is super friendly which I truly appreciate! Especially when I'm with my toddler.

My new favorite burrito joint!

I ordered online, and when you pick it up it's waiting for you on a shelf so you don't have to interact with anyone, so I didn't get to see how the service was but that was really convenient! 

The food is REALLY delicious and seemed really fresh. I appreciate them having a fresh spring mix along side romaine so I can get in a little more nutrients. I filled my burrito bowl with things I normally get at other burrito joints, and when I did the online nutrition calculator the bowl was much lower in calories than the other places I go to! So now I don't feel as guilty getting a huge burrito bowl. I'll most likely go to this place much more than other burrito places. I'm hoping to try to queso soon. The variety of what you can get is great and I really enjoyed it.

The only reason this is not 5 stars is because the parking lot is very small and the slots are very narrow, and if you're able to find a spot to park it's a hit or miss with being able to fit your car in. I could barely open the car door to get out being next to two other compact cars, but thankfully I was just going in and out so no one could accidentally hit my door with theirs. But it's not a reason not to go! It's very yummy.

I had some time to kill in Green Hills with my son so we stopped in for dinner. Kids under 12 eat free on Sunday, which is nice. I got a steak taco and a chicken taco, and my son got a chicken burrito. The meats are tender and seasoned very well, better then Chipotle or Qdoba in my opinion. The chips and queso were just ok, my son was not a fan of the queso. I think they overdid the flavoring/seasoning. I did not care for the hot sauce at the table, it was different but not in a good way. Perhaps a few other flavors would be a nice touch. Two of the three sauce/salsa dispensers by the soda fountain were empty (gotta keep those full guys). Overall, it's not a bad place. I would go back, but I think there is room to fine tune some of the details to make it even better.
KsT3oaQJiM4v7hnqUU9EEA: Freaky fast delivery and your go to spot for last minute catering. Don't waste your time anywhere else!

Super fast delivery. Exceptional sandwiches every time! I live downtown and Jimmy Johns is a must at least once a week for me. I've always been super pleased with this location. I normally have specific instructions for my order, and they always get it right.
Just as delicious as the other location. Pretty consistent!! Good job JJ!!
vysyK-G875gUP08n2Qnj3g:
This bar reminded me of a bar I would find in LA, so don't go here expecting a unique Nashville experience, but it was definitely a cool vibe (being from CA I really enjoyed it but we didn't stay long since we wanted to see Nashville). 

They have great beers on tap, modern feel, a lower level that has a second bar and dance floor.

Such a great bar in midtown! It was an upscale feeling, but bartenders and staff very down to earth. Being a transplant from NYC, this bar reminded me of a typical manhattan bar. The cocktails are great, made with real ingredients not mixes and sugar.  It was a nice change of pace from other bars in Nashville.

What a great place to unwind and have a drink in the front of this establishment or dance the night away in the back. This is a classier establishment from the honkeytonk life of broadway. This is a great low key spot where tons of locals go to mingle.
BUGqVV6ziyekbMF3xVwuiA:
Cocina Grill has a great location downtown around the business district.  This is one of the only Mexican places downtown.  It is the perfect place for a quick bite during the mid-day lunch rush!  During lunch, the place can get pretty packed, but they have a lot of employees which makes for a speedy line.

The day I went, I had the taco salad which was zesty!  The taco bowl itself had a spicy/salty seasoning around the edges which I enjoyed breaking off and scooping up the generous helping of guacamole that came with the dish.  I will definitely be back during my next lunch break!

Despite the slightly-aged chain look, this place has surprisingly good / fresh food to this picky, former San Antonian.
 Bonus: they have great options for vegans / vegetarians with two kinds of veggie patties and no chicken broth or lard hidden in the assembly line of ingredients. 
 The staff is super friendly and answered my veggie questions without snarling (two admitted they don't eat meat either).
  It's a great place downtown, away from all the more plastic / touristy restaurants closer to Broadway and the water.
VlsLTCFCBL2LIdT8Ijx65Q:
I came here for lunch and this was an amazing experience.  For full disclosure, I went there for free as a reward from my work.  Nonetheless, this was a great experience.

To begin with, there were excellent fresh homemade tortilla chips.  After I ordered my Coke, it was never empty, the waitress always made sure there was another full glass long before the previous reached the bottom.  As our party's members showed up, the waitress appeared in seconds to take their drink orders.  This lady was on the ball!

The menu looked excellent, with a wide variety, but a placard on the card announced a lunch special, the Mucho Lunch: unlimited burritos, regular tacos, and enchiladas for $7.49.  And it was well worth it.  Although I didn't pay, I still consider this to be an unbelievable deal.  I do enjoy a good quesadilla, but the burritos and tacos were so good, I don't think I would need anything else.

I also need to say that I am not a fan of guacamole, but this is the only place I have ever seen guac made right at the table.  They mashed up the avocados, added the onions, tomatoes, and other items.  If the guac were any fresher, your wife would have to slap it.  

El Chico also has the richest dessert I have ever seen.  Take an enormous brownie, cover it with a generous helping of vanilla ice cream.  Now take a huge amount of their "brandy butter sauce", and pour so much over it that the brownie is swimming in it.  So much sugar and fat, people have had heart attacks looking at it.  So rich, billionaires hold signs up in front of it.  Enough dessert for the whole family to share.

This was a great place for Tex-Mex.  When I came in, the ample parking lot was packed, so I should have known I was in for something good.

If you want good Tex-Mex, and anywhere near Murfreesboro Rd., this is the place to go.  Great service at good prices!

I haven't been here in yrs. Normally we just drive right by this place but decided to stop in. The food and service was really good. The refried beans were great. Not your normal pile of mush that you get at some Mexican restaurants. We will be back
Lunch specials are awesome and reasonably priced. Cannot go wrong with the old fashioned tacos. The tomatoes and avocados that come with the dishes are always super fresh.
Ybb1739zfXNm3ZZwa8c6Xg:
Can you believe I have not been to a burger king in my adult life?  Having no knowledge that they served veggie burgers there and not really digging their fries I ended up there last night for what had to be the quickest transaction in drive through history.

At the end of my meal all I could think was that man that was a tasty veggie burger and wondering if Marcellus Wallace looked like anything.

It probably helped that my breakfast was half a serving of noodles, no lunch, and a handful of appetizers from 1808.
I don't typically go inside. This location is near my office. Lunch service is super fast through the drive thru! Food is always fresh and yummie.
4vJu5cMzarFHxEIuOgxlog: While I loved coming here for delicious Nashville Hot Chicken, the business appears closed permanently.

This place has a specialized menu and they do that menu its justice. The customer service was fabulous and the chicken was delicious. The french fries were the crispiest I've ever had and I'd love to know their secret. Would be 5 stars but their carpet was incredibly sticky.

It was my first time at this place and it really was good. The counter clerk took my order properly and she was very professional. I would come again. I will say the sticky carpet needs to be cleaned deeply or replaced. Other than that I really like the food. Hopefully they will get there menue posted on online soon.
uIRSTdXj549AZe_UQ2lvlw: I'm really shocked no one has added a review for this lovely restaurant! Walk in and pick up a cute little shabby chic nick back for your house, then get in line and have. A wonderful salad or divine dinner.  Quick and full of flavor!!!
Stopped here for lunch because this place had great reviews. We were definitely not disappointed! I had the Salmon poke salad and it was excellent! Eat here for sure!

I'm also surprised Chef's doesn't have many reviews! ( however, on local wedding sites they have all 5 star reviews )

Chef's Market is a great little restaurant that I've supported since the day they opened many years ago. 

Chef's has a daily buffet using the freshest ingredients for original, delicious recipes beautifully displayed. Besides the buffet, you can chose wraps, salads and sandwiches made while you wait. 

Chef's has dessert cases filled with an amazing assortment of homemade cakes 
And desserts that make other bakeries look like amateurs. 

Chef's also has the best catering service you could hope for. Jim Hagy and his wife, Cheryl have filled a void in this town. 

I've used them for past events and have them booked for an event this coming October. 

Once I knew Chef's Catering was available, I sighed a breath of relief knowing everything would go smoothly. 

Jim and his staff will take care of the food, presentation and service in the most professional way. They also have a talent for transforming any venue into a magical event better than you could have imagined. 

With the connections they have, they can also help with any other vendor or service you may need. 

Having a special event or party? Use Chef's Market and Catering! They're second to no one else. 

Would you like to have a great meal different from anything else offered around here? 
Treat yourself to lunch or dinner at the restaurant. 

Too tired to cook? Call them, place an order and take it home. You'll be very happy you did. 

Check out their reviews on weddingwire.com or any other site that uses caterers often. They are the best!
rgcbGLaBL3DPQLV-JFR2ww: Always hits the spot, and does so spot on! Super quick, always tasty with the best got sauce and horchata around. Try the lengua and carnitas and prepare yourself for a mouthful of heartfelt song.

This place is a delightful Taco Truck meal. Super affordable- less than $6 for 4x tacos. I can't say enough about the tacos.  Simple and delicious. And cheap. If we could get the screaming child off the actual grill, my score would be perfect.

This is a taco truck parked in the lot of the shell gas station. Very good food and fast service. A great place to grad something quick or to buy a meal for the whole family. Most of the lunch crowd are blue-collar workers of Hispanic origin, but even if you do not fit this profile this is a great place to visit. Two thumbs up!
3YBUj9va9KbN1kkNcDQPug: Awesome place!  It took me a while to realize how great their food is as well as the atmoshphere.  So stop, have a drink, have a smoke, and knaw on some good eats!

Oh, Corner Pub. 
Let's be honest, my friends and I have been a rowdy mess there so many times, I'm genuinely surprised our pictures aren't posted in a back room somewhere. Yup, we're usually THAT table. 

This spot is a favorite for Friday night (night starts at 4pm, right?) happy hour due to the location and 2 for 1's. 

The service is usually pretty good, but on busy Friday nights there have been a few less than stellar experiences. meh. I forgive them. Just keep the beer and cheese beans coming. 

They allow smoking inside so beware if you are sensitive to that sort of thing. Or just have a few beers and forget about it. 

They accommodate large groups - the side room you can reserve holds quite a few people. 

If you're looking for a quiet, classy after-dinner drink or a gourmet meal, look elsewhere. This is just a place to forget about the work week and have one too many beers and one too many fried foods while staying in your budget.

Ended up here on recommendation of the Chuy's staff when we showed up after their kitchen already closed.  We were half way through a holiday Sunday night bar crawl and needed something to soak up the alcohol.

This place has a great diner feel, but still serves drinks.  We didn't order any drinks, because it was definitely time for a break.  Our food was gobbled up ridiculously quickly!  Cheese fries are literally my favorite food on the planet, and these were about a 6 on a 1 to 10 scale.  I was not a fan of the nacho cheese sauce that made the fried mushy.  Prices on all food items were pretty standard for a diner.

Service was very quick, though admittedly the place was pretty empty.  Location is great, right on the strip between a lot of other places.  It was clean and decor was typical.  

It was a great place to make a pit stop between bars!
ELgiifL3QLqzMaCo1frbdg:
I was a huge fan of Waffle House when I was in college - cheap food any time of the day and, you know, waffles! But in recent years my trips to the WH were disappointing enough that I had almost given up. Then I gave this one - at the I-40 Bellevue exit - a chance and my love of the place has returned. Waffles, eggs, hash browns - all at the quality I had come to love in college. The service has been great and everyone is super friendly. 

Oh yeah, the biscuits, because I always have to try the biscuits. They have new cheddar jalapeno biscuits that are great. If you order a biscuit breakfast sandwich, they grill the biscuits first, split open and face down, so that they heat up nicely and taste fresh baked. Yum!!
Your classic Waffle House experience. No complaints. I've spent many a late night here with the friendly staff and All Star Breakfasts
It's a solid Waffle House.  Pleasant employees and signature, pretty delicious food because it comes from their well used grill.

Found our server Amanda to be pleasant and attentive. 4/6 of our party got the spaghetti and meatballs ($26) 1 got the 3 course special (pear salad, spaghetti bolognese, tiramisu for $32) and I got the lobster/crab cannelloni ($31)- everyone cleared their plates! UNzgGakdjDgdwSNSdhhV1w: O......to the MG! This was goood! Service was attentive. Amuse bouche was great. Bread basket was fantastic. The beet salad was nothing to write home about, but the pork.....THE PORK!!!!!! So. Freakin. Good. The creme brûlée was also great. Truly, I enjoyed every aspect of this meal. Cheers!

East Nashville and I are slowly becoming friends.  I know they have great food and cool hangouts but it's still, even after years in Nashville, not an area I am as familiar with, and so any opportunity to try someplace over there is an opportunity I go for.  I had never even heard of this place before.  I had to check it out here in Yelp to see what it was, and from the menu I was intrigued.  And so it was onwards to dinner with bubbling excitement over experiencing a new place.

Small, off the beaten East Nash path, and very cozy, Fundamental had their shiz together.  Its a small restaurant, maybe 15 tables with a nice bar (I recommend a reservation on weekends) and very attentive and friendly staff.  And the food was amazing.  According to their website this cafe strives to be like a small French bistro with southern influence, and that was obvious and charming, and it worked.  I have to say, I loved their stacked salad.  Very flavorful and fun.  Their dishes are beautiful and simple without sacrificing flavor.  I also highly recommend their custard or chocolate brulee for dessert.

I will absolutely be back here to try their brunch and their "bonne nuit" Tuesdays.

It was with great anticipation (and just a little trepidation) that we tried Chef Jamie Watson's new restaurant, Café Fundamental on its first night of dinner service. The venue, the former location of long departed Zavos across from Family Wash, has been transformed into a cozy but classy locale with black table clothes, a small bar (not zinc), two rows of tables, and a booth. The decorations include black and white art photographs on the wall, a wooden sculpture, white painted stamped metal ceiling, and a wait staff decked out in black and white to match.

After a drought of French restaurants in the 1990s, Nashville is now seeing a flowering of Gallic influence that is moving to a new generation of restaurant  beyond F. Scott begetting Margot's Café and Marché, then Table 3 and so on and so forth. (It is not all that strange because Nashville has made it to France for country American-themed restaurants http://www.oncle-scott.com/oncle-scott-restaurants-story.php ). It is a delight to find so many places that move beyond the clichés to discover finer bistro without the formality of the finer old-style French establishments.  

While the lunch and breakfast menus bill the restaurant as a French-styled, Southern inflected patisserie and brasserie, the dinner menu is more that of an upscale bistro with two meat  entrees (in the American sense) cooked  SousVide with other choices including fish and duck. Starters ranged from consommé to pate. We opted for the Duck Liver Mousse and Stacked Salade followed by Poulet Roti Gran Mer and Sweet Corn Angnolotti with the option of scallops. The meal came with a pleasant complementary toasted crisp of bread topped with house made bacon pate, a quail egg, and a touch of yellow tomato preserve. For the starters, the two quenelle-shaped scoops of duck mousse were pleasantly light, served with a roasted grape tomato and four toasted rounds of French bread, although a couple of more rounds  would have extended the pleasure. The stacked salad was about five or six inches in diameter and several inches high packed with layers of lettuce, tomato, chopped eggs and dressing; we found it perfect for sharing and simple but elegant in presentation.

The Angolotti, filled with mascarpone and topped with a combination of corn and corn milk was delectable and the two huge scallops seared on the outside and pleasantly rare on the inside. This left the one who ordered it in blissful enjoyment. The chicken came with just a few of the pommes cocotte--two small butter sautéed potatoes--once again shaped like small quenelles--crispy lardons (bacon slivers) and the promised pearl onions replaced with a side of sautéed oyster mushrooms. The bacon was smokier than that used in more tradition French restaurants, Southern style, but the chicken breast and wing drum quarter was simple but flavorful. Our only wish was, again for some bread (perhaps slice of crispy baguette) to sop up the sauces. The price points for the mains ranged from about $17 for the chicken and pasta ($8 extra for the scallops) to the mid-$30's for the intriguing tenderloin sandwich that we hope to try in the future.

Desserts include cheese selections apparently from Bloomy Rind-PRB, while the prepared desserts were listed by taste: chocolaty, citrusy, and fruity. We opted for the two latter. The lemon crème brulee and a cushiony upside-down hollowed-out muffin came filled with diced pears and apples with a caramel sauce on the side. One came with a lovely herbal shortbread and blood orange sherbet and the other with vanilla ice cream. The one discordant note was that the price and description  listed the desert as being served with sherbet and ice cream while the tab added an a la mode fee for each desert. While we did not make a fuss about this on their opening note, we assume that this difference between menu and computer will be clarified in the future.

Beverages included a full bar stocked with top-label local spirits (we saw Corsair) but also a solid selection of French. The wines available by the glass were the standard size pour and price at about $10+, which is becoming the norm at more upscale dining spots. The one peculiarity was the charge of $11 for the Daron Calvados, which seemed a bit high (without knowing the pour size) for nice everyday spirits that is not top shelf (clearly a markup of 10x over retail for this is excessive).

As for service, while it was opening night as demonstrated by the zealousness to please and the feeling that some of the staff were still learning their paces, our server was totally charming. With time, will come the smooth unobtrusiveness of well -practiced service.

In sum, this is an ambitious effort expanding the upscale options on the Eastland-Porter Road-Inglewood corridor. The food is refined and elegant without being pretentious. This is an enjoyable and most welcome addition to the 'hood.

(If half stars were possible, we would give this 4 1./2).
0dFeimiXpwXAC445r9xsVQ: I've eaten here 3x in the last week. So fresh, quick & easy. Staff is great. Good portions. Love it!

I really enjoy this spot for lunch. They have a ton of options to customize your poke bowl and everything tastes good and fresh. The ordering process is quick and it's nice and modern inside the restaurant.

The only con is that it's pretty pricey (though poke usually is). I'll be back!

This place is amazing!!! I couldn't be happier that this place landed here, a 3 minute walk from my apartment! My girlfriend and I went to Hawaii earlier this year and we had some poke there that we loved and we haven't been able to find anything here that measures up until now! Please go visit this place and try it! Even if you don't like raw fish, they have shrimp! Even if you don't like seafood at all, you can get tofu!
4AbGfjbcyrTvP8eDTMSzeA:
I am a bit of a BBQ snob and I must say that the pulled pork was much better than I was expecting when I walked in. It was a little greasy but that's not a huge detractor. I will be going back to try the ribs in the near future.

I really liked the atmosphere, maybe because it brings back memories from my childhood, and the staff was very cool. I can see why it was chosen to shoot some scenes for the show Nashville.

+1 to the previous review! I work nearby and BoondoxXx has become our BBQ Friday's spot. The pulled pork has been great, and the green chile mac & cheese is fantastic. They also do a great 28 oz Ribeye that is perfectly prepared (seasoned, smoked, then cooked to order). Our entire team (10 people) are obsessed with the BBQ, and it's quickly become a tradition in our office.

On the downside, I think they are still figuring out their volume, as the wait is lengthy and they are often out of items on the menu. That said, I've tried almost everything they offer and have loved all of it.

Is Nashville the BBQ capital of the World, no. It will never be. But be fair, BoondoxXx is a quality addition to the Nashville BBQ circuit for sure.

Also get the cookie, fresh BIG chocolate chip and oatmeal.  Our bill was $15 but we left $20, we felt his prices are way to cheap for what you get.

Better off coming here than the fast food chains around.

The band box was a lot of fun with food and drinks that didn't disappoint! Our group sat at the round tables and were promptly greeted by friendly servers. Overall great atmosphere providing an elevated ballpark experience.
There was a broad selection of craft and domestic beers, as well as wine and mixed drink options. The whiskey and coke icee was delicious!  
Food was served fast and hot; ranging from ballpark hot dogs to quinoa salads. The hot dogs and hamburgers were packed with flavor, and are both sourced locally. Our group also tried the nachos and tater tots. The tots were crisp and not too greasy, SO good. 
Overall, would definitely recommend eating dinner/snacks here while catching a ball game.
hojhzuA9hiTHk_NtdaWWpw: The lamb gyro is quite tasty! The sauce adds a nice unique flavor.

I've only been here once, but from what I tried, it was damn good.  I had the falafel pocket.  It was a pita filled with falafel, hummus, tabouleh, and tahini.  It was cheap and awesome.  The server was very courteous.  It was overall a good experience.

I had lunch at med cuisine, as I have many times before, but it has been a while and since I just moved back to Nashville I was ready to form a new opinion.  Surprisingly I still really like it. I have heard a slew of bad comments and how people think its not very good, but overall, I am normally very satisfied with my experience. The service can sometimes be poor and slow yet today I sat down at 1230 and was leaving by 1 with a good meal in my stomach.  I think that their hummus is great, they serve the real lamb gyro meat, which is a big plus considering so many mediterranean places have switched over to the frozen pre-sliced bullshit meat. I tried my friend's falafel and it was crispy and delicious.  I had the hummus platter with the gyro meat and it was a little oily. I dont know if it was olive oil drizzled on top or the grease from the meat, but it was still tasty none the less. They also have hookah's there which is a plus and even if the service was bad, im still more concerned with food than anything else so I probably would not change my vote.  The price is also reasonable for what you are getting so that keeps me satisfied as well. Will definitely go back for more.
TeDC6CPQS2AsgEk7TFi5ag: This is the best hookah bar I have been to in Nashville. It is clean and comfortable and the family who operates it were very nice. I was surprised they served food and more surprised when I tasted it. I had the chicken curry and it was awesome!!  Very high quality food and delicious. We were there from 6-8pm on a Friday so we got great happy hour deals and it was not loud or crowded, just the opposite- very relaxed.

Every time a new hookah spot opens my husband and I have to try it,since we consider ourselves Hookah Connoisseurs. We went after they had been newly opened to celebrate my husbands birthday. It was on the weekend but quite and sort of dead but I expected that. Hookah was great, cuisine was interesting. We had never had indian and hardly knew what to order, so our party ordered a little of everything. 

Lollipop Chicken was pretty good, the atmopsher was expected since its a hooka lounge,service was alright the waitress was new and learning the drinks and menu so  i'll give it a pass. Since no other patrons were inside they allowed us to hookup our ipod and play music, nice touch.There is limited park behind the location,so be prepared. Drinks were good,def was a great night.

Overall I liked it and hope to go back soon

The manager Chante is great.....even had to go out to store to get extra mixer for my drink. It's my birthday and celebrating a promotion. This is definitely a great spot sad I'm leaving soon and this is my first time here.
S7cQSq_l1lNk31FddfpAGA: The staff was friendly. The food was delicious! There was a lot going on. Was neat to be there during U.S. Vs Mexico soccer match. After the game was over there was a live band. It was a fun atmosphere! On vacation and we were pleased with our dinner here! :)

Friend of mine told me about this place. His dad goes here and they are true Mexican family that knows where to go for great food.
This place is located behind Hooters off Harding Place near I-24. It's tucked in the back in a small little shopping center next to a bakery which I was told I also need to check out.
Maybe another day!

It looks like any hole in the wall place with plenty of seating. Groups, family, friends, they have the room!

I will say that as always, when tasting salsa, it lets me know if the place is good. I've always gone by that standard and never fails me.
Their salsa is AWESOME! You can tell it's home made and is chunky with the ingredient in front of your eyes. I love cilantro and every salsa has to have it. This one does!

I ordered the Fajitas with Shrimp! Coming out hot and steaming! I will say the tortillas are also great and made right there as well. Not typical corn tortilla's. If they are store bought, well you've fooled me cause they looked like they were made to order.
Great flavors of course and for that dish, $10. Great deal. I've been into places where same food can cost $15-17. 

They have a lot to choose from.
Parking is small so not a lot of room.
Don't worry, you don't have to know Spanish to order. This area is very pact with the Spanish community but servers are bilingual, at least the girl we got. I was with friends who spoke both English and Spanish. They ordered in Spanish when ordering.

I will be back. Taste buds are everything to me and this food hits all my senses!
I dislike bland foods, this place is full of flavor and I love their salsa!

OH LET ME MENTION! They have amazing Guacamole. It's little peppery but has a spicy kick. I've never had guac. that way before. Something to try! And another thing, they have a great cream light green sauce that you can put on the meats and it's also awesome with a spicy kick.

While visiting Nashville, Tennessee recently for a concert, my husband and I decided to try this Mexican restaurant. It was kind of hidden and run down looking but had really good authentic Mexican food. It all was a little pricey and the only complaint is that none of the employees spoke English very well but after being there for a long while, we noticed not many other races were coming in so maybe it was for only Mexican or Spanish people? Even if so, they were very nice and attentive to our needs. It seemed like it took longer for our food to come out versus other people who had arrived after us.
_IjQro4Dt9E7XnUC3gPG6Q: I love this place. Best burgers in town. They have a lot of sizes to choose from. Baby mos to there really big moes. They have regular and seasoned fries. It's all made when u order so it takes just a little bit longer than a regular fast food place but it's so fresh and hot every time I go. If your looking for good big portions of food that are a little bit Greasy than this is your place to eat.

Definitely a fork and knife burger joint.  A great place to gorge yourself with beef until you pass out.  The combos come with fries, but after eating the burger I've never been able to finish the fries.

This is definitely a once a month treat for me but what a burger! Their deluxe burger and super deluxe burger are truly to die for. Spicy fries as well. I love their sauce and jalapeño. I'm extremely surprise it's anything less than five stars. It's extremely greasy and messy, but that's the way it's supposed to be. Definitely the best 'dirty' burger place, anywhere. And you know it's good because people line up to get a burger out of a trailer.
nwCr13LeZQ4LuQH7ZrQlmg:
Very nice people. Great food. They are open at 10am, which was surprising to me. They even serve a breakfast dish. It's in a dive of a spot, right behind the gas station. Very good stuff, worth the visit.

Also, get the raspberry drink. It's actually very delicious.

Muy buen servicio y la comida muy rica auténtica comida salvadoreña!! Se los recomiendo a todos!! El caldo de gallina excelente y las empanadas de carne super rikisimas.. opss no pude sacar foto xq de tan rico q estaba no lo comimos todo.. lol..

I recently moved to Nashville from Buffalo, where there was absolutely no Salvadorian food. There was barely any Mexican food. Anyway I'm so excited to see that so many Salvadorian places are open here. 

I came to this restaurant a coupe of days ago. I was told to go by a lady at the Latino Mercado down the road. 

I ordered two pupusas, bean and cheese / loroco j cheese. They were so big! I was so happy! They were also super stuffed with cheese. Just the way I like it. 

The curtido could have used a couple more spices (maybe a couple of jalapeños thrown in there) but it was good none the less. 

I was super hungry so I got the Pollo Frito. It was good. It wasn't as great as the pupusas but it was delicious. 

I can say, as a native, Salvadorian-born and raised that this is some authentic shit. You gotta try this place if you want real authentic Salvadorian food. 

The horchata was bomb diggity too.
-7CqBmK0zxwyjEqa3LBVSw:
Dairy Queen is one of those places where I've seen their national commercials (the one with the big lips?), yet have never actually seen one in my former corner of the US. I just checked, and there are about 5 in the Bay Area -- but the closest to my old address was about 15 miles away. I had no expectations of how the experience would be, except to at least try their famed blizzards.

I had a cheeseburger/fries/drink combo for $4.69, subbed onion rings for the fries for an extra $.69. The cheeseburger was just okay. I have since tried the bacon cheeseburger, which included fresh sliced onions, tomatoes, lettuce, pickles, and mayo. The onion rings were fantastic. Very crunchy, just the way I like it. One day someone is going to invent an onion that will not come out in one long strip when you bite into an onion ring.

The Mud Pie Blizzard was outstanding. Given the heat that's afflicted this region, it hit the spot. I love the crunch of the cookie and soft melt of the ice cream.

Service was slower than I anticipated. But again, I didn't know what to expect. I had no idea if this was a fast food place with food lying around under heat lamps, or if the food was cooked to order.  There was a bit of miscommunication on their end: I was asked by the male cashier if I wanted the Blizzard at the same time as the meal, or afterwards. I asked for it to come with the meal. When nothing happened by the time I finished my burger and rings, I went up to the female cashier. She knew immediately what I wanted, but she explained that she was not told that I wanted it concurrently. She honestly thought that I wanted it after the meal, which seemed to be a common request.

I also think that I was overcharged. Again, this could be my miscommunication. I thought I ordered a small blizzard, but with the heat, my stomach could have very well made my mouth request a medium and my brain did not register it. I will just assume that I misspoke.

I try to stray away from fatty, I mean fast food, as much as I can, but this location is just too damn convenient for lunch and the food is just hits the spot... for awhile at least.  Normally, I would try not review a fast food chain, but this particular Dairy Queen has really nice and friendly people that work there.  I actually consider it more of a restaurant than a fast food chain, because the service is so good and the food comes out looking like something that you would order in a restaurant and tastes like it too (food comes in a basket with perfectly cooked fries every time).  I have never had a bad burger there.  

Obviously, it is not the finest burger place in Nashville, but it certainly is the best fast food style restaurant.

I love this dairy queen,  the staff are always professional and always get your order correct I miss the one by my home but I'm glad these restaurants still exist this location was very clean as well.
g2bomqGwXyMJiYFIaYnk4g: Food really good. Service... Still very new so I'm going to factor that in. But I've been there 3 times and every time service has been painfully slow.
I've eaten here a few times now and consistently the food has been very good. Tasty bar food at a reasonable price.

What a great brunch experience. Dave our waiter was super! Right there ensuring our drinks were filled and we were satisfied with our meals. 6 of us were in town for a mini vacation and wanted a nice brunch before heading out. We came across the Goat on yelp after we were not going to be seated at another restaurant for about 30 minutes. We arrived as they opened and was greeted warmly by Dave. We were seated in this huge comfy half booth and drinks were ordered. The decor at the Goat is phenomenal- industrial farmhouse is what we decided to name it. Coffees, bloody Mary's, and mimosas were all on at our table. No complaints just compliments.  The table received complimentary biscuits with homemade strawberry preserves.  So good.  Hot brown, the classic, and the French toast all tasted epic. Will definitely go back to this joint on our return to Nashville. There lunch/dinner items look great. Love the atmosphere here. Very cool place to hang and have drinks. Had TVs throughout the joint so you can watch the games as well.
jwJXTp4gm3woU93lxj8UdQ: Everything here is good ! Great for breakfast , their gyros are great and last week I had Mediterranean pasta which was FABULOUS!! I wish I had the recipe . Very reasonably priced . The Italian place down the street is $$$$ more and their pasta is not anywhere near as good !!

Very good customer service and food. The wait was not long with a very pleasant vibe . Had the traditional breakfast with pancakes on the side - Perfect
First time but definitely will not be the last..

Stopped in before the movies. I usually check yelp before but I didn't this time and I assumed it was a regular American diner. I was pleasantly surprised to find out it is a Greek diner. I had the chicken soulvaki. It wasn't bad. Nothing to scream about. The fries were good. My fiancé had the chicken club and she really enjoyed it. The chicken soup is awesome. Overall it was a pretty solid visit. The prices aren't too bad and the service was good. Nothing spectacular but a nice sit down restaurant with unique options. I don't know if we will go back because it's kind of out of the way but I would recommend checking it out if you want something different than the typical pizza and burger fare.
MuRKOZju_EejwEh92xnEgQ: I love this place, great vegan/vegetarian options and fresh ingredients. I love how they don't skimp out on their servings. ALL of their sauces are amazing!

We were just walking through the Nashville Farmers Market trying to decide a place for lunch. The smells coming out of Caracasville pulled me in. The beef and the chicken arepas did not disappoint. A great little place.

I spent a summer in Venezuela, and have missed Venezuelan street food ever since. I was really excited when I saw that Caracasville would be in the Farmer's Market, and I was not disappointed. This is really good, authentic food. I highly recommend their arepas -- make sure and try them with guasacaca sauce!
OGixx7xJps9yYZbkoOhIpw: Service here is always good. While in general this Taco Bell is just like any of the others, I really prefer to come to this one. The only complaint I have its that they sometimes fall behind paying attention to the dining room (i.e. it's difficult to find a clean table to sit at), but other than that, worth every bit of 4 stars.

This is by far the best Taco Bell I have ever been too! They were polite, fast, and very friendly! Normally, I expect to get in and get the heck out but this place was not even close to the typical Taco Bell. All the employees were extremely friendly. Everything I ordered was correct and they even gave me extra sauce packets. Most Taco Bells that I've been to are very limited with their napkins and sauce packets. I would rather travel a little further to this Taco Bell then the one near near Antioch!!!
One of the nicest taco bells I've ever been to. Kell & Quesha were so nice to me. The food was fast and fresh. The chessiest cheese roll up I've ever had. Loved it!
-GvA6iekqOACGodgLYiMKA: This place was awesome. Great food and the watermelon Margarita was Delicious we went there with my cousin she's been wanting to try it for a while so we figured why not good choice we all enjoyed it

My family and I have been frequent customers of Taco Mama since it opened in Nashville. We love it! My favorite is The Sizzler tacos, but their quesadillas are always a pleasant surprise too. Fast service, friendly people, and delicious, fresh food makes for a 100% enjoyable experience every time I visit.

If anyone rates this place any less than 5 they have some screws loose. By far the best fastening food I've had in Nashville. Service is great. They serve liquor and the food was out of this world!!!! Great quality ingredients.......
-CLHHZzNmv1DYqyNQmlNtA: Portland Brew is one of my favorite coffee places in Nashville, not just because the atmosphere is great, but because the coffee is really good.  The atmosphere at Portland Brew is really conducive for relaxing and good for studying too.  They have free wifi and great booths to sit at.  In addition to the booths, there are also really nice tables outside where you can sit back and reflect on the day or prepare for the day ahead.  One thing that irks me about coffee places is when they have mediocre pastries or the like.  However, Portland Brew is home to some of the tastiest bagels in Nashville, I am not lying!  It is also nice that they are in a pretty convent location right off of West End.

Honestly, this is my coffee shop. It's a stop every morning on my way to work for some great coffee. At least four blends are available every morning (well, 3 and one decaf version). The baristas are very friendly. There's also a frequent-buyer card...be sure to get one. Mine seem to fill up very, very quickly. And even though you get a stamp for just buying regular coffee, you can use them for any kind or size of free drink you want -- you know, like a nice, large, three shot latte. (The large usually comes with 4 shots, so I get them to tone it down a bit.) You can also use the card when you buy coffee beans.

Some good (if a bit pricey) baked goods -- try the zucchini cake -- and bagels. I just wish they had the breakfast sandwiches they used to have. I think they may have started serving them on the weekends, but I'm not sure. The Gulliver was my favorite -- ham, swiss cheese, and egg on a bagel. Good stuff.

There are booths for you to sit and enjoy some coffee, some really comfy looking armchairs in the back, tables out front to enjoy a smoke with your coffee, or some tables by the windows for you to people watch as well as the requisite free wi-fi. Portland Brew has three locations around in town. Its a great, local business I'm happy to support.

I'm not a big coffee drinker but I crave a cup of hot chocolate on cold winter days. Two years of grad school and an inability to not be distracted by just about everything often lead to seek refuge at Portland Brew. I really dig the atmosphere, but dislike the fact that on more crowded days you have to fight for your own space (I like to shake my fist at the people who lay claims to larger booths for hours at a time). Either way, the wireless internet helped me buckle down while the local paintings for sale where enough to quench my attention deficit disorder. The one coffee beverage that I had (a mocha) was pretty yummy but a little pricey. In the end, however, I was willing to forfeit the extra dollar for a study space with a great vibe. Also on Saturday afternoons, when school work is often on the back burner, Portland Brew hosts wine tasting for the Grand Cru Wine and Spirits store next door.
3Pln4LmZY8LvNDhuJS5g_A:
Wanting to go sit out on a patio for an early dinner this past Sunday to enjoy the beautiful fall weather, a friend suggested we try this new place Steve's.  Guess it's not technically new as it's been open since April, but I hadn't heard a peep about it.  

The sign for Steve's is on Gallatin, but the actual entrance is on the opposite side of the building.  Located upstairs, you walk right onto the large covered patio which boasts views of downtown.  Plenty of four tops all the way up to more than ten tops are scattered throughout with lots of space in between tables so it doesn't feel crowded.  There's also a plasma screen outside showing sports.  

The waitress who greeted us was extremely friendly, and later was the indoor bartender.  She recited the list of draft beers ($2 for happy hour) and since most of them are not your typical draft, she even brought out samples to try beforehand.  We both tried and ordered the Sweaty Betty which tastes even better with a lemon wedge.  

She went over almost the entire menu with us, giving us her opinions and favorites and customer favorites, and we settled on the Asiago artichoke crab dip for an appetizer.  It was delicious and served with warm garlic bread portions.  Only complaint was that the portion was pretty small.  We then ordered the rib combo to split, which along with ribs comes with a choice of a half roasted chicken, shrimp, or wings.  We opted for the chicken, with fries and a caesar salad for the two sides.  Both the chicken and ribs had excellent flavor.  The ribs weren't the best most authentic bbq in the world, but were still tasty.  I loved the shoestring fries and the salad was great too.  

Didn't spend any time inside other than to check it out, but it's clean sleek with modern decor.

Overall, I thought this was a great place that seems to be undiscovered by most for now.  It doesn't have the character of some of the more popular East Nashville hangouts in nearby 5 Points, but to escape the crowds, have some good food and beer, and enjoy the patio and maybe a game, it's an excellent choice.

Steve's delivers what it says it will: classic American food.  What it does, it does well. . .  think a classy Applebee's.  The type of food that it serves is something that I normally am not a fan of, but I don't think I was expecting much and was pleasantly surprised.  

I didn't partake of the bar, but it looked like a good selection of draft beer and good prices.  

The decor of the restaurant is really nice.  Warm word and local art panels the wall.  The outdoor patio has a great view of the city.

My one major complaint is the building it is in.  Where the in world do you park?  And how do you get up to the restaurant without going all the way around the mulberry bush?  

Give it a try!  It's a great place for families and taking the parents to when they are in town.  I'll definitely go back, most likely to partake of the happy hour.

OK, so the entrance is in the back of the building.  No problem.   Easy, free parking just across the street.  We had a great time.  Cold happy hour beer on a hot summer day.  Nice decor and quiet environment for pleasant conversation.  The ribs are very good and I enjoyed the cole slaw and baked sweet potato as sides.  My friend enjoyed the rib platter with mushrooms.  She also like the chips and salsa.  We had no complaints and would highly recommend this restaurant .
CFQy8pmXcEJ-g9Iwf6tG1A:
Oh, wow.  This was a pleasant surprise.  I ordered the special of the day, which sounded basic enough:  roasted chicken, fried potatoes, and spring mix salad.  In my indecision, I also got a cup of crab/mussel bisque, knowing I couldn't eat it all.  

During prep, Chef Patrick gave me an extra piece of chicken, possibly because it was already after 1 pm and the lunch crowd was thinning, or possibly because their cash register was malfunctioning.  Regardless, I appreciated the gesture.  Even before that, the staff at C'Bon were incredibly gracious.  My bill also totaled less than the sum of the menu prices.  Whether that was intentional or not, I left feeling like I'd stolen an awesome experience.

My meal was outstanding.  It's a bit surreal eating food of this quality with plastic utensils in a market setting.  I'm not a white tablecloth kind of guy, but I would easily visualize this food in that setting.  The food appeared to be almost "plated,"  except the portions were generous.  Such a bargain.  See my photo for reference.

I don't like to give five stars after one visit, but my experience could not have been better.  Highly recommended!  C'Bon is raising the bar (and making my decision more excruciating!  NFM is a food gauntlet!) at my favorite Nashville lunch destination.

If you haven't been to the Farmers Market in a while, now would be a great time to give it another whirl. Out of the choices there, C'Bon was a pleasant surprise to find nestled in the middle of the food court at the demonstration kitchen. As I scoped out what my next lunch would be, I was drawn to C'Bon by the wonderful smells that were wafting from their setup. When I approached the counter I was greeted with nothing but friendly smiles and pleasant conversation right off the bat. I think that Jennifer was the name of the nice lady that greeted me, and she is great at being warm and welcoming while answering any questions you may have about their offerings for the day. 

After checking out their menu and peering over the counter for a visual, I was ready to order. I chose the crepe with andouille sausage, garden greens salad with mustard vinaigrette, and couscous. Even though I caught the tail end of lunch, the food was still surprisingly fresh and tasty. My salad was generous, extremely fresh, and included a mix of greens, fresh peas, and asparagus, all topped with the vinaigrette and a nice creamy dollop of creme fraiche to top it off. The couscous was not dried out, or worse, mushy, even though I was one of the latecomers. My crepe was still soft and perfectly fluffy, while the ingredients inside were hot and fresh. The kicker for this delicious crepe was the creamy and decadent bechamel sauce with balsamic drizzle that sent it over the top, I'm sure not only flavor-wise, but calorie-wise as well. Meh. Who cares because it was worth every dairy-laden bite!

Was the price a little higher than what I would normally spend on lunch (around $12 bucks)? Absolutely. But was the quality more than I would normally receive for lunch? Absolutely. The only disappointment that I would mention is something that they can't even be faulted for, and that was to have a nice, big glass of wine to go with my wonderful food. Otherwise, I can't say enough about the friendly, approachable service, the quality of the food, and the wonderful flavors that were paired on one plate. So, give C'Bon a try the next time you want something a little more decadent than what you might normally have for lunch. Trust me, you will be glad you indulged yourself!

I hate confessing to the awesomeness of this place because I'm dreading the line that I will have to face when I come here again.  And I WILL come again ... and again.  I don't have the delightfully witty commentary and nuanced descriptions that other Yelpers possess so I will only say that the food makes my tongue dance for joy in my mouth.  I do have to warn you that the decadence of the flavors comes with its dark side: calories for miles.  If the French can stay thin on this kind of food it must be because they exercise a lot.
UPDATE 07/25/2012: The owners say they will now be open Monday-Friday.
xXZpW7-Ca4aXITh6yq089A: Great pizza. Staff wad great with our kids. Just needs a little updating. It seemed really old

I haven't had Pizza Hut dine in in forever. These peeps workin hard here to provide good service and take care of you. They care about what they're doin and are trying hard. And even though I don't eat the hut that often cause it's heavy, it's still the best tasting chain pizza. Give Papa Johns a rest and get some Pizza Hut.
XryDxAK_p61fPsR2whP_nQ:
Good prices,  delicious sandwiches,  Arabian stuff,  European stuff,  Persian stuff.,  and very much nice people.  Simply the best place for the middetrean food :) 

Also they sell hookahs and flavored tobacco
Have been going there for years. Love it. Miss getting the gyro ground meat in the meat case as they run out.

Great little middle eastern grocery. I was craving some grape leaves (dolmas) but wanted to make them myself along with some other Greek and Persian dishes. This grocery market had everything I needed and was very friendly. I will definitely be back again.
1xBNndHICRMXyZ-5itRq5g: I never thought I would see some one complain about a burger overflowing from the bun. The taste of Hugh Babys goes all the way back 50 plus years when meat had real taste and substance. It's cooked well done and crispy around the edges on a proper grill, greasy spoon style,  there is no "rare" here. If you like your burgers super pretentious, filled with odd flavors, served on brioche buns to big to hold, with a wine menu on the side, this is not your place!

I love this place!  The burgers are delicious! My favorite is the BBQ Burger, but the double cheeseburger is equally terrific. The burgers are flatter than normal ones and crispy around the edges and they taste so fresh. They also have good, friendly and fast customer service. The only thing I don't love is the fries. I've been there 3 times so far and ordered fries each time. The first time I ordered chili cheese fries and they weren't so good. The second time I ordered just cheese fries and they were a little better but there wasn't enough cheese and it seemed like they just melted a slice on top as opposed to having a pan of hot melted cheese ready to ladle on top. The last time I had regular plain fries and they very good and very crispy. So....Get the plain. Overall terrific.

This place is flying in under the radar right now, it opened 4 months ago and its literally one of the best cheese burgers I've eaten. I tried this place three days ago and I've been back every day since. Highly suggest you check this place out. Great service and clean!
wtEfdCyFWS7pv2g-NmlRBw: Good pizza. Never had a problem with our food. We order almost every Saturday from this place and they are always fast and friendly. We definitely will continue eating here.

I've always had great service and food from this location. I love the garlic butter! I do wish they'd make the toppings get a little closer to the edge, seems to be too much crust sometimes. Alls well, it's more to dip in the butter.
Food was great a few mins late, price was reasonable will definitely eat there again!!
daJi3RqUR6N0xWDDlZxtaA: There's not much to say about this.  Honestly, it's just another Arby's.  No better or worse than any other Arby's.  However, I love Arby's, so I could never give one less than four stars unless they screwed up my order really bad.
