I'm giving this Arby's 5 stars not because of the food (because really it's the same Arby's food you can get at any other Arby's) but because of the workers. We live in this area of town and are used to getting lazy and/or rude workers at various fast food joints around here. 

Not the case at this Arby's. There hasn't been one time that we've been by there and the workers haven't been anything but the nicest people. It's such a refreshing change from other fast food workers in the area and they are all so nice.
This is one of the friendliest Arby's that I have been too. Thank you the great food and service.
C7jw0oQLb1t3VkPqpteRbQ:
I know Panera is a chain, but it's so good I had to review it!  I had never actually been to a Panera before I visited this one (usually I try to go to places a little off the beaten path).  

But, this location is really nice.  It is brand new and right downtown!  They have approximately 6 tables outside (only two under a parasol) and ample room.  The facade is all marble and the restaurant sits on the bottom floor of a large office building.  The weather was nice and I genuinely enjoyed sitting outside on the luxurious patio.  

The food was good too!  I had the smoke turkey breast sandwich with chips, for only $5.86! a great value for all the food you get.
This is a great place to eat if you are coming to downtown Nashville to shop or tour. Parking was $10, which is normal cost for parking downtown. Yummy food great atmosphere and free wifi!

This is your pretty standard Panera. We got a quick breakfast here before a Segway tour one day, I just got a bagel. They don't have salt bagels and I'm not sure if that's just a northern thing or what, they gave me a crazy look when I asked for one, lol.

This was quick and easy and I would come back if I was in the area.
hg2mEJmz0bGUqRb3CgkokQ: Four of us went to this lovely Ethiopian restaurant. We were served a huge platter of food. We had been told in advance to order the food for 1 less people than we had (so we ordered the platter for 3). There was so much good food on the platter. Spicy chicken, stewed beef, lentils, green beans, chickpeas and several other items I don't quite remember. All of this is eaten with your hands using injera (Ethiopian spongy flatbread). The injera was plentiful and so was the food. I would highly recommend. As long as you don't mind eating with friends off the same HUGE platter, you won't mind this place. I'm sure if you preferred, they would bring you a knife and fork. But why ruin the authentic experience!

Favorite Ethiopian place in town. I have had the coffee ceremony which is fabulous. I really love the veggies here - greens, green beans, etc. Four people can go and easily split the "combo for 3 people" and be out well under $10 a person, and so full you all will think you will never eat again. I also prefer the injera here to everywhere else in town.

For anyone who hasn't tried Ethiopian, it is very different and some of the dishes can be very spicy. Right up my alley :)

Tried Goha last week---like the previous reviewer, husband & I were delighted to find an Ethiopian restaurant in this neighborhood.  I can't say that I thought the food was spicier than other Ethiopian restaurants; but I can say that I enjoyed it!  The injera was a tad more sour than I've had before, which was a nice additional flavor.  We shared a combo platter, and I REALLY. LIKED. the beets that were included.  Even husband enjoyed them---and he doesn't like beets.  We were interested to note that the menu includes breakfast---that's definitely on our list to try in the future!  The place is a bit sparse on the decor, but really, who cares?  The food is good, the people are nice.
dAKoLEVOT0gAU3ot8sFB_g:
I may become a fan of Arbys all over again!

I hadn't been to Arby's in years!!! but decided to eat something a little lighter instead of a burger. So i tried the Roast Beef and Cheddar sandwich and Mann it was good sonnnnn!! of course I added some barbecue sauce to it ...I'M FROM TX SO DON'T LAUGH! but to me barbecue sauce makes all meat products better! :)

ANYWAY, my food was hot, the drive thru girl was very friendly and even engaged in some conversations about the weather with my wife and I. 

Will defiantly be back!!

This review is in regards to the service. It's absolutely excellent. The cashier lady was very friendly and welcoming. After my friend and I were done eating, she took our trays for us. 

As for the the food, tastes like any other Arby's restaurant.

My husband and I just had the best fast food experience of our lives and we can't stop talking about it! We went inside to order because we weren't sure what we wanted and John was over the top accommodating, patient and kind!! The cooks in the back were very friendly as well, offering a to-go cup for a sauce they were out of and apologizing, but ensuring it would be on the truck tomorrow! We were just blown away by the genuine, kind, attentive service! We will be back! The food was great and the place looks newly renovated... has a very cool rustic "Nashville" vibe!!
9QHxpGxshyJXh3JnE2VHIg: Tribe is a fantatic bar modern and SUPER friendly staff. Right next door to play dance bar. If you are looking for a classy joint to grab a drink with friends or meet new friends, this is the place to check out! :D

First off..anyone can go to tribe. Its just a great place for a cocktail. The place is a large video bar. They have a smoking bar and a non smoking bar. The bartenders are usually lame and the drinks arent that strong unless you get the right bartender. They have really cool events and its a great place to go out if you dont feel like getting all sweaty...on the dance floor that is. Have fun kids.

Typical gay bar. Good music, they were cool with letting us move tables to create small dance floor. The food is pretty good, drinks regular. No cover charge, next door at play they charge $8 and keep your id
wuqsyYfgpg1xSh2Xf3Ld5A: Quick and fresh is all I need from a fast food drive-thru and this Wendy's delivers.  Don't be discouraged if the line is long because they are really fast at lunch to serve customers.
I really like the management team. This place was clean and the manager treated his new employees with respect and patience while handleing my customer concern politely. Great Job, great food

Great food, Extra friendly staff, but very slow for fast food.  The friendly team made up for the lack of speed.  Plus a free frosty for kids wearing their team sports uniform.  Just a nice perk, since my son had his baseball uniform on
pdtLdjKYw_wxzLb6JHNDAw: Mac and cheese was on POINT. Fresh bread, happy people, and most importantly amazing Mac and cheese. Woo!

This panera bread is located at the green hills mall, and it is pretty busy because of the amount traffic in the mall. There service is super fast and there food is nice and warm. I ordered corn soup and a chicken chipotle sandwich and it really did fill my tummy up more then I expected. The quantity was plentiful for 2 people and it was perfect because my mom just wanted to nibble. This location has a nice bathroom also just in case you want to just come in and just use the restroom.

Talk about a whole new Panera. This Panera seems like the Hilton as you compare it to the Panera on 21st Avenue at Vanderbilt.

The staff are always friendly. There is always ample sitting area (both indoors and outdoors). Generally when I am there, I have an empty table next to me.
UzRMlnXQKlWa16_spVHHXw:
Don't be scared away by the more large formal menu. The roasted tomato salsa was unique and delicious. I had the chicken chimichanga and it was superb. My friend had the chicken enchiladas with green chili sauce. The green chili was fresh and extremely tasty.
Will definitely return if I'm in the area again.

Stopped into a place that we haven't eaten at before. Very friendly staff. We were seated quickly. I had a the chicken chimichanga. Well, let me tell you the vegetables were fresh, the chips were still warm and the queso was awesome. I will definitely done here again and maybe try the oysters!

OMG I am not willing to eat out at most places in Nashville. Food in this town sucks when you have traveled as much as I have. However this place is amazing. If I could give it 10 stars I would. If you don't go here than you hate food and eating and good things. In fact you are just wrong if you don't like this place. Our Bartender Geo was fast, friendly, and really helpful. The restaurant and bar is clean, open and refreshing. The food is out of this world and made from scratch. If you don't go her and love it there is something wrong with you!
vjlMxdeqRKQCo7ZJ7BKxPw: All I can say is AWESOME!  Everyone is a rocker. One of the best bartenders in Nashville works here, Faith. With the other two bartenders, Lauren and Mijo, makes it a team to beat! Food and drinks are wonderful. Always a great place to be.

Considering I live in Nashville, I'm not very motivated to get a "Hard Rock Nashville" shirt, but I have ventured in here once or twice.

This is a smaller sized Hard Rock store than some of the other ones that I've been to  (London, Paris, Venice...) but it still has all of the normal merchandise. One of the highlights in here for me is the autographed guitar from Josh Farro (former lead guitarist of Paramore) and autographed orange-taped microphone from Hayley Williams (lead singer of Paramore). Josh played this guitar when they filmed their 'crushcrushcrush' music video and Hayley used this microphone to sing at Hard Rock Calling in Orlando back in '06.

It's been a while since I've been in here, so I can't remember what kind of music they play, but I'm pretty sure it included country (surprise, surprise).

If you're coming to Nashville on a vacation and want the HR Nashville shirt, obviously go in here and get one.

Let me say I never want to eat at a chain restaurant when on a holiday. But Nashville was a little crowed with the Stanley cup. So the wait was long. So Hard  Rock was the choice let me say the place was awesome the steak fajitas was perfect. Birthday for the girl she got a sundae. 

Good place for food in Nashville.
5_i3f4vdoj4Rq_CHsiG31Q: Friendly fun people. Better then Larry's kareoke, bring ur friends n have a ball. Some talented people tonight. The owner is personable. Fun fun fun.

RIP Betty 
Good place to go after you've been on the lake all day

Very low key good food, music and drinks confidently right by the lake!!
Pulled in after a long day on the motorcycles. Bartender and owner were more than hospitable. Music was five star. Also the burgers are incredible. Highly recommend.
iC1_RgJ7u0qhEPPBEIVYIg: The buffet at this location is awesome and the service is wonderful. Very friendly and accomidating staff.

I love this Pizza Hut.  I never go to any other.  One of my best friends lives right across the street in the Knolls Apartments.  It's our tradition that whenever I visit, we get pizza from Pizza Hut.  It's a really convenient location, right by the gas station, Kroger, and more, so we always just pick it up there.  The staff is very friendly.  They greet us by name whenever we come in.  They're fast and punctual and always get our food to us ASAP.  I've never eaten in the restaurant, but the seating looks nice too.  There's also a lunch buffet open around noonish, but I've never been there early enough to catch it.  I've heard it's a good deal though.
The best service I received while staying in Nashville TN.  Hot, Fresh served with a smile. Highly recommend.
NB_tZBmmMwHREmYedfqrJA:
Recently moved to the south side of Nashville and I must say, of the three Taco Trucks i've had near Nolensville this one takes the cake for me. 

The tacos and meat are very delicious and CHEAP! Only $1.25 per taco and $5 for a Torta. They provide you with Red and Green Salsa, the Green which is amazing.


I tried the Chicken, Spicy Pork, and Barbacoa and was impressed by each, the grilled veggies on top were great and you definitely get the full authentic taste at Taqueria Aguilar!
I've been eating here for about 2 years now - CONSISTENTLY DELICIOUS AFFORDABLE food.. 2 steak mulitas with a steak torta!  so good. Customer for life!

Only had a couple of tacos, de asada and al pastor. They were very tasty, as good as any other food truck, and only a dollar. I plan on trying their plates, such as their shrimp plates that look really good.
_DGULHeb-Pk40QAywiG62w:
#awesomeness place everytime I go it's always on point 
The staff are always professional and friendly the food is out of this world not only do the Vanderbilt staff come here but people like me
Q3_Z2oFu9D67QVB1uJTXyA: I don't normally rate a Mickey D's but he workers here were just amazing and friendly and went the extra mile.
this new manager working the lunch shift today is amazing! the best customer service I've ever seen at a McDonald's. I was very impressed and I will be back again.

One of the dirtiest, most rundown McDonald's I've ever been to. I don't ask much from fast food restaurants, but c'mon guys. The fountain drinks were behind the cashier counter toowhich was annoying. 

Update: They've since renovated the building - interior and exterior. Much nicer!
B54j53k-x8Ej-bgfOF1lsg:
This little restaurant is well worth a trip down Nolensville Road.  The bakery also serves a good lunch of Turkish food--I had a ground lamb pide and hot tea, both of which were excellent.  

The young couple who run Ephesus are friendly and prepare everything to order.  They have a good selection of Turkish baked goods, including a simit, which is a type of Turkish bagel.

Love this little place. Been going about once a week now for a month or so. Really small, simple and plain but clean place. Nice and quite too.

I LOVE the -diced- lamb pide. Turkish coffee is wonderful and I always take home a few baked goods as well.

The info on here says they accept credit cards... i cannot vouch for that as i always pay cash, but I do not recall seeing a machine. You might want to bring cash along to be safe at least the first time.

UPDATE... I noticed since I wrote this that they do indeed have a card reader 
machine, so you should be ok if only have plastic on you.

UPDATE! As of May, 2013, the place is shutdown :( 

I think they are moving elsewhere, but I have no idea where. They have a facebook page, but there is no news about it. PLEASE COME BACK SOMEWHERE!

The pita bread is delicious, fresh, fluffy, and $1.25 for a huge amont to take home. The "Turkish pizza" is also a great deal and yummy. We got those pretty sesame bagels and some other treats but they tasted like they were made yesterday--kinda stale.  The baklava was good, but really when is baklava not good? Filo, nuts, spices, and honey, that's ALWAYS delicious. 

My new go-to party dish is going to be to buy a bunch of pitas and some hummus. Everyone is going to think I'm fancy but haha, joke is on them!
GHoJVISeWPxgDdB-yJ3fXQ: Very good pizza and such a reasonable price.  Papa Johns is my go to place when I'm in the mood to veg on the couch & watch movies.

This is my local Papa Johns. We order here about once every other month & it's almost always correct. We order online & the pizzas and sticks always come out as they should. 

One time however I had to wait 30 minutes at the store because their computer system was down. It was so chaotic that I thought I should've gotten a coupon or something but didn't. The frazzled manager gave a "sorry" & that was it.

We have been using this Papa Johns in Donelson for almost 30 yrs. The few times something was wrong, one of the great managers were always there to help. I am sitting here waiting for the delivery. I gave Jacob my order over the phone. Very sweet boy but it seemed to be his first day. I really don't know but it took me having to spell every word out for him and still had problems getting the info.Not a good idea if he is new to have him taking orders on rush hour Friday evening. I was also going to have to pay for the 2 garlic butters for 2 people eating the pizza. Please tell me that was just a mistake to charge me for the garlic butter. I had Jacob take them off of my bill. The reason I am reviewing this Papa Johns is something for corporate to help me understand. My pizza came good and hot! Just the way we like it. My total was $16.11 with me using my $12.00 coupon.I have never not tipped the drivers and I tipped again just now but someone needs to explain to me why the store gets $2.75 for "delivery service". These delivery people have always been kind and responsible. They drive their own cars and pay for their own gas and also upkeep on the car that is running all over the area delivering pizza. Why do they not get the $2.75 per delivery? Please help me to understand this. You have wonderful drivers. They represent your business. Is $2.75 too much to lose? I have always known it is the employees that make or break a business. Thank you for not letting us down on tonight's dinner!
2mnIQtBR4ofuXYe5Zc7TFg: This place was really awesome and so much fun. They had 4.00 mimosa on Sunday and all the games were free except the basketball. Hubby and I stayed for at least 2/3 hours just enjoying the atmosphere and the entertainment. If your looking for fun times here you go

My boyfriend and I come here often since it's a neighborhood bar. We always sit outside. Never eaten there until last night, we got the chicken tender basket. The verdict, best fries ever. The chicken was perfectly fried, crispy outside and soft inside (or whatever you call perfection) the only thing, the chicken was bland, it tasted like it was seasoned with nothing by salt. It was so good though. I took the left over, and I added the Nashville hot oil that I happened to have in the fridge. I poured it in the chicken and I went to bed. I woke up in the morning and I had a bite, it was heaven. Even better than Hattie B's chicken that I love. I also ate the fries. I never eat old fries, but they were that good. We are talking about going back tonight and see if we can get the tenders or fried chicken in ninja Nashville hot. I saw they had it as a sandwich and tacos but not just chicken. If we can get it that way, I'd be happy. Ok, I'm done.

Update: we did come back for food. We had the basket of tenders, this time I asked for Nashville hot oil in the side, it was amazing. We also had cheeseburger egg roll, also amazing. The guy in the kitchen is super nice. Can't believe I waited this long to try food here.
qmBYuem4NQv-5zWiVI3YLg: As Subway's go, this is a good one. Clean but small, staffed by a guy who's a bit hard to understand, but he makes a good sandwich. Has same specials as free standing stores.

It's a chain! And you choose your food! I don't understand how people can be so picky on a build your own sandwich!

The service was pleasant and if you want use the Subway app! You can get your order as you want and win points!

I'm not sure the reason for the lower ratings, the location is primed for tourist, so time is everything. My visits usually is afternoon and there is no wait!

As mentioned you can always pre prefer. 

I normally don't review fast-food chains. People need to understand the expectations.
8xiinKmMW_vR_kxlg3QC7Q: My daughter and I have been here a few times.She is a little finicky when it comes to places to eat.Well, she liked this place and so did I.Great salad bar. Plenty of entrees to choose from.Hey this isn't The Palm or  anything but for what it is it's a great value.Neat and clean . Our waitress was always friendly. It even has 110v plug at your booth to charge your phone while you eat.The soups have been good. Plenty of entrees to chose from.They have a special each day.I would recommend this place!

Great food and staff. This place is inside the TA Truck stop, they have a very good inexpensive all u can eat soup and salad bar. For $6.99 I  had a fresh salad and fruit, along with vegan and beef vegetable soup also cheeseburger soup. Delicious and homemade. Their menu prices are cheap as well. Great find!

this is only for the Country Pride portion of the TA truck stop here in Antioch Tennessee the full review can be found to somewhere once I get to it
still one of the better ones for the most part in the TA Country Pride chain opens from  5 to 11 7 days a weekone of the ones opens earlier than the other ones.. many of the other ta Country Pride on one's open at 6.
I've always liked this in my almost 30 years of trucking back when was the 76 to Nashville south Interstate 24. wow still have horse has to Popeye's and the Burger King this Country Pride restaurant still brings out fresh salad bar all the time and for the most part the food has been very consistent for the last few years very good..for example tonight I had a nice healthy salad bar that I had the grilled chicken breast nice good size plump one little Heinz 57 on top with the baked potato and the garlic toast very good..
_although for my truck and friends you definitely want to get here early this parking lot fills up fast and always has and still does and what the new elog rules it still fills up quite a bit_ you get a lot of General travelers in here to that enjoy eating it's still a really good one kept very clean quality is very good just a good place to have a regular old-fashioned American diner kind of meal and you can't get it to go.
1mEABmQ9223ZYxSbbkRAKw: Thin crust pepperoni is a great cheap pizza. People are always super friendly and helpful.
Wow the deep dish and the extra pep one is amazing. So I was hesistant at first because of the reviews but gave it a chance and NO REGRETS! So YUMMY and good price also.
Qoo-G03zTkgTv63sMHorNg:
Everything you have heard about Tee's is totally dead on.  Its great.  Its terrible and its all things in between. 
The food is great and the location is really good and a breakfast/lunch diner place is something Bellevue is sorely in need of.  On their good days, they rock.  The staff is attentive, the food is good and it is totally fairly priced.  On their bad days, you wish you could have just headed over to Dunkin Donuts.  
Even on their bad "I think I'm going to go into a diabetic coma if you don't serve me in the next day" moments, the food is the only thing that is consistent about this otherwise schizophrenic little restaurant.  
We have never failed to have a good breakfast.  Its just that sometimes it arrives around lunch time.  My husband is sure the owner is a great cook and rotten business man who has hired all his family and forgot to teach them how to wait tables.
Bottom line: if you look in and it's busy, run.  If not, hang out.....it will be worth it.  Going on those guidelines, I'll toss in my 4-stars and hope the owner takes notice of his dreadful staff and staffing issues before this place goes under.

I love Tee's! It's convenient to where I live so I get to-go orders often. I love their quesadilla. It's filled with fresh veggies, cheese, chicken, and lots of flavor. The chicken strips are also outstanding. It's a simple place with a simple menu but it's YUMMY! I have yet to have a bad experience. The staff is always very helpful and willing to go above and beyond to deliver a good experience.

I love your location!  I just recently found out about your restaurant from Sharing Spree!  What a great deal I got to try out a new place!  I was so thankful because I love this new find!  The food is great and at a wonderful price!  Thanks for working with Sharing Spree to help our local charities too!
PC7155v05U9Mg412MDQEKg: Walk by this beautiful space, and you won't be able to help but step inside for a closer look! Gorgeous, light and bright decor create a European atmosphere and an extremely welcoming feel. We arrived for Sunday brunch right as the doors opened and were immediately greeted by the friendly staff. The bar manager Taylor made sure we felt right at home before being seated in the main dining room. Our server Kelly greeted us and shared some of her favorites from the menu (all of which sounded amazing). We took Kelly's suggestion to start with a cinnamon roll, and we were glad we did. I want the icing recipe so badly! The coffee (which one server told us was from Panther) is fantastic, and the espresso drinks were amazing as well. Service was friendly, attentive, and patient, and it's clear that the staff cares about the restaurant and work together seamlessly to provide an amazing experience. Oh! The tea service is beautiful and something I can't wait to have on their picturesque, street-side patio. For entrees, the waffle madame, chicken and waffles, and pancakes were all delicious as well, and I imagine they'll keep getting better as this establishment really settles into their rhythm. I'm so glad to have such an amazing spot to frequent, and I can't wait to return and try more!

Stumbled into here before a Titans game. Staff was friendly and knowledgeable about the menu. Food was great. Had the grilled romaine Caesar salad, French onion soup, biscuits and grits, and a few cocktails. Food and drinks were fantastic. Decor is immaculate and clean. Outdoor seating looks perfect for brunch, drinks, or dinner on a nice night or before a show at Ascend (or anything downtown for that matter). They've walked the line between drinks and snacks with friends and nice dinner with the Mrs. making it perfect for either. Will definitely be coming back to explore the rest of the food menu and the custom frozen cocktails they've got spinning in the machine behind the bar.

Great addition to Nashville food scene. Beautiful decor and awesome decor. 

Special thanks to our server Kyle; exceptionally knowledgeable and kind. 

We'll be back soon.
-Wd5KqFszREg0XdXfSv5tg:
Groundside next to the security exit on the A side, this Starbucks is perfect if you're waiting to pick up an arriving guest. And like so many spots here in Music City, there is a stage set up for musicians. How awesome is that?

Standard Starbucks layout with pastries and coffees. Remember that you can't bring your coffee with you through security. There is another one on the other side.

No free wifi here.

This Starbucks is located between Terminal A and Terminal B.

Stopped by on my way to Terminal A for a 6am flight. This location opens super early (around 4:15am). Quick service and polite staff. They had 2 cashiers and 2 baristas. You gotta appreciate that! This was a great stop before heading to the A gates because there were no great food options in that terminal.

The lines may be long but it's worth it. When I ordered she was so nice. Greeted me with a smile and said "Good morning and how can I help you? " I placed my order but forgot I was hugging a water bottle to purchase. I received my coffee and cinnamon blueberry bagel, toasted of course and this lady too greeted me with a smile. Then I realized I still had the water and had not paid. So I quickly put it back and told the lady and we laughed. She offered me a cup of water and assured me it was filtered and triple filtered. Haha They're busy but not too busy for to give a smile.
nvaGtrwp24HMgervrmMKwQ: Oh so good! Stopped in at Rise to grab a little breakfast and I definitely wasn't disappointed. I had the bacon egg and cheese and was shocked to find a REAL, not reconstituted,  egg on my sandwich - that's a rare find for fast breakfast spots these days. The hashpuppies were amazing, I just wish a few more came in the order for the price. I also tried a maple bacon donut which was the perfect combo of sweet with a kick of salt, and even though I'm not really a coffee drinker, I had a cup of coffee just to round off my visit and it was very good, not at all strong and burned tasting like a lot of the coffee found elsewhere. All in all, the food is great, the staff is super friendly and I'd gladly stand in line for some breakfast here any day, however I do think the price structure is a tad expensive for what you get on most of the items. Having Rise within walking distance is sure to be a test in willpower!
SO GOOD!!! Biscuits and chicken were amazing. Donuts almost put us into a sugar coma. Would definitely come back here when I'm back in town!
Super friendly and fast service. Delicious southern chicken biscuit and hashpuppies to begin our day of exploring
VGzkEiZz7gqK_SYW86GlaA:
I'm from Houston which has some pretty good Asian food, but this place was objectively good (despite being in the middle of Nashville)! I had the Chong Qing noodles which were legit, had a marinated tea egg with it as well. The soup was very well made, with strong meat flavor, and the noodles were perfectly cooked. Also got the peach oolong tea with milk cap, which was super refreshing and sweet! 

Ambiance is interesting: sticky notes on the wall from past patrons and rubix cubes/cards for people to play with if they're so inclined. More restaurants should do that.

Waitresses were really friendly and helpful, suggesting that I drink the tea with no straw to get the full flavor. I appreciated that! Keep doing your thing.

So happy a Chinese noodle place opened! Came in yesterday and loved it! Got the pickled veggie/Swai noodle soup, and the broth tasted just like my mom's. Will definitely be back to try their dumplings and other noodles! 

They have the option to create your own noodle bowl as well.
One of my staple places to eat in Nashville. As authentic as it gets, and good portions. Always fast and friendly. We are big fans and recommend to everyone!
3NogWpnW5gjivjEZ-wL9Wg: This Arby's simply has the nicest staff that I have ever met at a fast food restaurant. If you are dining-in, don't forget to ring the bell on your way out!

Nothing more then a regular Arby's but clean and friendly

I found this place from a coupon wanted to try it and oh my lady ga ga the guys here are awesome, the service was great and the food was hot and delish so needless to say this is my place for now on
VX6HrL0ZryetyzHyNlxhzg:
In town for business from St. Louis.  Stopped by last night and picked up the half slab for dinner.  Lady at the register suggested to get half the ribs with dry and the other half wet.  Some of the best BBQ I've had.  

I'm back tonight For a full slab of the Baby Back Ribs!
Big fan of Martin's! Great menu selection, they have everything you would possible want! The cornbread hoe cakes are fire.

This place is great. We love BBQ and they got it!
Pulled pork, bake beans, mac n cheese, coleslaw, 
and strawberry cake that was super yummy!
tAYDA1RuZfG88zF6Pzp8cw: Truly amazing food!  Overwhelmed with the kind Service we received.  The Owner was generous with accommodating our party by giving us a few dishes to try just for fun!  The atmosphere is very "Nashville" - artsy and cool!  615 Chutney is a must for all Indian food Lovers!   Thank you for a great eating experience tonight.

Today was my first visit! Everything I had was made to order and super fresh. Reasonably priced, generous with portions (and chutney!), great ambiance, and super nice owner. I plan on having lunches here often :-)

Love this place.  So nice to have Indian food on the Eastside.  We have made three trips now and they appear to have worked out the service kinks.  Everything we've had had been great and the Irachi Ishtu is a fantastic dish.
6ej2f00IC9-IF6yfSVTzrA: We were nearby for a meeting with our contractor. She lives in Madison and recommended Fonda El Cubilete as an authentic restaurant nearby. And I'm glad she recommended it. We started with a pineapple agua fresca and an horchata. My husband got the barbacoa, which was delicious: tender and braised in a really good, rich sauce. I had the poblano chicken, which was a some pounded chicken with cheese and poblano slices and onion and a creamy (and a little spicy) sauce. It was good. The frijoles were tasty. Quick, tasty, affordable. Not a bad meal! I'd be happy to go back when I'm next in Madison!

I live down the road from this place. I wasn't really sure what to expect, but the ONE review posted a lot of really interesting pictures. I had the green enchiladas and a steak taco, with a side of guacamole - let me say that this place isn't winning for ambiance, but the food is pretty incredible. Everything tasted fresh and homemade, and the food was hands down better than any of the other Mexican places I've been to in Nashville. As much as I love the crazy crack cheese dip I'm used to gorging with unlimited chip baskets, this is not the place for that. While I wish they also had margaritas, they definitely have a few decent beers to choose from, and while you won't get any chips, you will be getting an awesome meal. I will definitely be going back.

El Cubilete IS our favorite Hispanic restaurant. My wife and I eat there 2 to 3 times a week. It is one of the best restaurants in Madison. If you're looking for a taste of Mexico by all means we highly recommend. The tacos are FRESH and made with REAL tortillas not the store bought things these are hand made I recommend tacos the carnitas excellent. We recommend any and everything on the menu from Flautas to the Soupa El Rey. Soup of the king Beef with vegetables even the loaded Fajitas. WoW and the chorizo is by far the best I have ever had.  A wonderful lil taste of Mexico. It's usually FULL because the food is great
0uL-FXY1M2UC12eZcFlTuQ: Six of us enjoyed a great dinner on the 2nd floor. Service was excellent. Afterwards, we headed downstairs to the bar area for a few more drinks. Service was horrible, and had to get our check from another server; otherwise, I'd give this place 5 stars.

Went here for my birthday brunch & it was incredible! Absolutely gorgeous restaurant and DELICIOUS food & great cocktail (get it in the clamshell, you won't regret it). Our server Ammo was awesome too. My fiancé got the chicken stack (fried chicken and French toast) and it was SO good! I got the Hampton Mac & Cheese and it was everything I needed. And then they brought me out a yummy little chocolate cake for a birthday treat! Can't wait to go back!

Hampton social is now one of my favorite places in Nashville! We did our holiday party there and it was so much fun. Between Brandon the manager, Leona the event coordinator and our server, it was an all around an amazing experience. The food was great and the drinks were strong so we all left super happy! I will definitely be returning and bringing friends when they come into town!
pzfHUtL1Z6e17IlTtuBMUw: Walked up the street for breakfast in the morning. The coffee, scrambled eggs and Brussel sprouts were perfectly cooked and delicious. I specifically asked for a side of vegetables instead of bread.
I wish there were more stars. The fingerling potatoes..... get out of town. Then everything stop. Jesse was awesome. Go eat there!

Visiting from New York and blown away by how delicious everything was.  The sourdough bread and thyme infused butter was scrumptious. Next up the Brussel Cesar and French Onion fingerlings can't be missed.  Moving on to the Pork Chop and Shrimp and Grits. Both were incredibly flavorful and delicious. I can't believe we had the ability to order to dessert but we did and it was the best part of the meal. The Lemon Butter cake cannot be missed. Heaven on a plate.
zJmp_J8SUNosLxk6borpFg: Some of the best vegan food I've ever had. Ate here twice during our 4 night trip and would've gone for a 3rd time had they not been closed by the time we got around to dinner. I don't think you can go wrong with anything on the menu. I had the tempeh buffalo grinder with a side of pineapple cole slaw on the first visit which were both amazing. Our second visit we shared an order of nachos as an app. I don't know what's in these nachos but I'm going to go w/ crack as they are all I can think about now. I have never had such amazing nachos IN MY LIFE. It was embarrassing how quickly they were gone. For dinner I went with the tempeh reuben (as good as I'd hoped) and a side of the garlicky kale (which I wasn't crazy about but that was probably more of a personal preference as opposed to anything wrong with it). The restaurant itself is adorable (small though, be prepared to wait if there's a crowd. totally worth it), and the wait staff attentive and charming. Am seriously considering a move to Nashville and I would say 50% of that desire is to be closer to this place. It's that good.

This place is great!! Food was delicious, vibe is cool, service was fantastic and the area around is trendy and fun. Between the 5 of us we had a little sampling of everything from buffalo tempeh app to taco salad to Reuben sandwich to Asian inspired bowl to the daily fried avocado taco special and I would go back and order everyone one of them again. Wish I had a place like this in my town......very cool!

Great vegetarian options, but almost always a wait. I know good vegetarian when I eat it, and this place delivers. Really wish there was a south Nashville location as it's difficult for me to make it here as often as I'd like.
uhqarcdeS3x3QNne2isZBQ: Today I met Kerry. Fast, courteous and professional. I drove to this location after a knucklehead on the window didn't know what she was doing. I'm reading the menu, and her dispute was to tell me that what's posted is not what it meant. I graduated grad school with a 3.8, but can't understand a menu?. I see why they're ratings are low, plus their entrance doors are dirty. Bad attitudes, dirty doors, is the food ok?. I'll never know! Give Kerry a good raise, shes holding it down on Clarksville Hwy.

Today I visited this location I was greeted and served by Donna. Excellent, Professional Service, with a beautiful smile. Great customer service and great food. I hope the corporate office sees this and be sure to give Donna a pay increase because she's holding it down in Bordeaux

I always go to KFC is not far from where i live and for years the customer service sucks. The ppl are mean and rude but i went last week and just from the gentleman talking to me taking my order i could tell he was a way nicer. I pull up to get my food. The gentleman smiled and talked friendly  the whole time, he asked if i could do a review. Its been at least 4 days and i still remember that visit to KFC. Thanks Terry!
WyV5R02-feXJUTIs53Cm-Q: Five stars for bringing authentic Italian in an easy grab-and-go fashion to the Mall at Green Hills! But if you'd like to eat at the kiosk, there are 6 high top bar stools, plus plenty of nearby table seating, as well as couch seating. Several items are already prepped... like wraps and salads. Custom pizzas are ready in 2 minutes. I had the mushroom pizza, which was topped with yummy gooey cheese and fresh roasted mushrooms.  I am going to grab sone custom cannolis to take home for my guests this evening! Yummers. You can choose a shell, a cream filling and a garnish. They were Amply staffed and very friendly. Venture towards the Dillard's entrance and you will find them at the escalators!

My wife and I shared the Chicken Pepper Melt Panini.  It was excellent,  very flavorful.  They also have some wonderful looking desserts,  Tiramisu, Cannoli, Ice cream in espresso.   I can't wait to come back to sample all of them.  This is a much needed addition to the Green Hills Mall.
rCa_2d4960Lg7vP6xMV10A: Cool little spot. We walked in and the entire staff greeted us and they were super friendly. There are so many amazing and healthy options (nothing unhealthy here).  Almost everything is organic with the exception of a few items (maybe 4-5). They have bottled juice in the fridge that you can purchase for later consumption. I was able to sample a variety of juices from the fridge. I'm glad I did because that allowed me to purchase exactly what I wanted and I know I'll be enjoying those juices later. I will definitely return. Thanks for treating us so well :)

Stopped here by chance after a workout. The team member who helped me was SO friendly and told me about their juices and smoothies on the menu. They're flexible and can make you almost anything you want! I got "The Recovery One" and it was PERFECT! I think plant based protein is nasty so I love that Clean Juice offers Whey... and the smoothie had good fresh flavor without being too sweet, and the 16oz smoothie was roughly $8 with tax which was a much welcome break from the $11 ones I've sipped from other juice/smoothie joints. Job well done Clean Juice! You've earned a new regular customer!
Refreshing and delicious smoothie. Can't remember the name. It was yellow with mangos I believe. I will be back to try more!!
Arj_cxegXo_rs9LtWq-aTQ: First of all let me say, don't judge a  book by it's cover. You can not tell from the outside but inside the restaurant is really nice any very laid back with dim lights at the booth we sat at. I really want to compliment our server name Jane. She was very attentive, granted there were two more tables beside me and my friend. She truely understands the word customer service. As sweet as a person can get. Anywho i ordered the fried rice (chicken and tofu) also a  roll of sushi with thai tea. May i say, that all was delicious. Everything was presented quite beautifully i will have to come back soon to try  the Japanese menu. Its a four star for the food and one more for the excellent service.

I had a suprizingly fantastic dinner here! 3 friends & myself had dinner, one had the Pat thai shrimp & the shrimp was huge & flavorful another friend had the red curry w/ chicken which she LOVED! My date & I shared a sushil entree which for only 14.00 had a callie roll & 8 nigri( & added a Toyko roll )which I was amazed @ the quality of the fish for the price! we shared a bottle of decent chardonnay(nicely priced) my friends had Kirin & Sapporo beer OH...plus the spring rolls w/ the peanut butter sauce ....the friendly service, EVERYTHING was REALLY great & for that price in Green Hill's? "OUTSTANDING!" :) We'll be back!
RluwogZeEmXzqEeJOho99A: B1281 is the coolest new find in Nashville! It is such a good restaurant to go to for pacific rim/ Hawaiian food! Pictured is their ramen bowl! So delicious.

I get a real gitty feeling in the pit of my stomach every time I see a brand new, unreviewed yelp business. I found out about these guys the old fashion way. Word of mouth. A coworker of mine *would not* shut up about this place. (In a Boston accent)It's so cool. We gotta go. Tom Brady would love this place. Blah blah blah." I checked on yelp, and nothing. No reviews. Very few pictures. Just opened yesterday. I'm in. I walked up to what looked like a construction zone. In the back, hidden up some stairs, through the woods, over the bridge, and halfway to grandmas house you will find a 1960s Florida looking building with a bar and food truck outside, and it's amazing. 

I got a poke bowl ($13.50) that came with tuna, rice, 2 sides (broccoli & corn).

So good. They had drink specials for $6. Such a great deal for a Friday night in Nashville.

In 3 months this will absolutely be the place to go in Nashville. Big fan.
Great food, super cool vibe, and great service!! The kalbi bowl and ahi Poke are great!!! The shave ice was fantastic with passion fruit whipped. The bar looks great too! We really enjoyed it.
Ny4ycAePXKD7_wQXL_Z7DA:
I've only enjoyed cocktails and haven't had the opportunity to stay as a guest. We went opening weekend and the entire team did an excellent job with prepping the drinks. 

Happy hour had numerous drinks at $8 and were delicious. The crowd was great and the decor is absolutely beautiful. 

Enjoy!

We stopped by last night to check this place out and have a few cocktails.  This review is for the bar area, not the hotel.  They are located right at Five Points in East Nashville.  We arrived about 9pm and it wasn't very crowded which was nice.  There is a comfy couch area, a large outdoor patio/area in the back and then the bar.  The decor is mid century modern and very well done.  We got a seat at the bar.  The bartenders were great, drinks were really good and prices were on par w/ Nashville bars ($12-$15 per specialty cocktail).  There was a DJ spinning in the bar area (which I think is on weekends) so it was pretty loud - a mix of new and old music.  By the time we left there were a bunch of people in there and a good mixed crowd.  We will definitely go back and sit outside when the weather is not as hot.

Loved staying in the Rum Room at the Vandyke for my birthday weekend! I'm obsessed with boutique style hotels and this one for me takes the cake. Each room is styled around a different form of liquor- rum, tequila, vodka, etc. The Rum Room is perfect for 1-2 people (personally) and is small & cozy. I was beyond obsessed with the bright room, vibrant colors, and cute decor. When we arrived I will say our room was freezing and with the floors being concrete, we were a tad confused why the heat wasn't turned on upon arrival of a guest stay when it's winter and freezing outside. So the first hour was very cold but it warmed up and was fine the rest of the stay!

Our bed was legit the comfiest bed I've slept on in some time, I didn't even need the pillows. We both slept heavenly! The room was equipped with nice towels, a great shower to use, little treats you could eat/drink for an extra price, and they have a bar at the hotel as well. They also have a super cute outdoor area to hang and drink during warmed months! Looks like it would be a great spot. 

We visited the bar inside and WOW! Loved it! The atmosphere was so moody and unique, we had two drinks that we both ordered twice since they were so good; the Trashmopolitan & the Cream Soda Old Fashion. We will definitely go back and stay in another room to see what it's all about!
1yeVKNQRYPCeQtADajZ9Sw: Super intimate and a great place to conversation or do some work! The iced tea was so yummy. the ladies behind the counter were awesome and gave me samples of the tea and coffee because I had mentioned it was my first time there.
Yummy breakfast croissant, delicious blueberry tea, amazing service and kindness. I'll be back often!

So I love trying me some coffee shops and so glad I finally got to The Bean Bag..really nice shop with even better coffee..The lady introduced me to all the coffee & teas they offered and provided awesome customer service. This will be a regular place for me now.
w0Y0bgERBoo8-zWmudS_Hw: A fantastic dive bar. Locals are friendly and energetic. The staff is very attentive. I got a burger it was delicious. It's a perfect dive bar.. Good food cheap drinks nothing fancy at all. My kinda joint
Friendly bar with a great bartender (and her mom), warm regular patrons, and delicious fried foods to munch on. Great place to de-pressurize and have a good drink or 10. Will definitely be back.
Went there this Wednesday.  Super chill, locals only, but very welcoming.  Pool table outside, music on the inside. We will definitely be making this a regular stop!
NpylMQP84RBc0PiixPYaMw: It's a new space. Service was a bit slow for my liking but very friendly. Valet was easy enough. The food through I thought was delicious. It has a diner feel. Definitely get the corn bread! Really good vibe. Could do without the live music.
Yum!  What an experience!  The food was fantastic!  Service is awesome!  Great atmosphere!  I will definitely be back!

Thanksgiving dinner... 
We had a wonderful and relaxing holiday meal. The prime rib was out standing, the roasted turkey was very unique. It came on top of shredded leg/thigh meat which was  a perfectly unique way to serve the dark meat. All of the sides were very good and we all left as full as if we had cooked at home. We also have leftovers!!!  Their service was great. We had the entire serving staff (including the director of food and beverage for the entire hotel) making it a great dining experience. We will definitely be back for another meal.
T_Qj3xR1CrZZhIo3eH92Mw:
Craft beer. Great food. Great atmosphere.
Hunter's Station, overall, is a great place for any group or any one person looking for lots of good options for food and a place to hang out. The Picnic Tap has a great food selection and an even better selection of craft drinks. You can snag food from the bar or another restaurant, order your favorite beer on tap, and hang out to watch a game or a movie or play some games.

This establishment, with a constantly fresh and rotating beer selection, is a joy to visit. The staff is knowledgeable, and the owner Eric, is a nearly constant presence. With food, board games, four t.v.'s, and a selection of craft cocktails, there is something for everyone. The ability to take your beverages around the market is also a plus. Nice work.
This is a great place! Love the rotating beer selection and food! The bartenders are knowledgeable and attentive
f4ouae_IyCaqGo7zERBXqQ: Decent sandwiches. 10x better than Subway in my opinion, maybe not as wholesome as Penn Station nor as packed with meat as Jersey Mikes (but not as expensive either). Tasty Breadless lettuce-wrap option for low-carb diners. Drink options are not too plentiful-2 sizes of cups and a small coke fountain at this location. Also there's not a lot of room to eat in, so you get the feeling it primarily caters to the carryout crowd. But overall a fairly healthy, tasty chain sandwich store with good value and easy parking.

YALL this Jimmy Johns location is me and my boyfriend's new favorite super cheap "I want a sandwich" date. 

I hadn't been to Jimmy John's in a w h i l e because a while back, my favorite menu item (#6 combo) jumped from $8-$10. Fast forward to a few weeks ago when my friends and I were in the plaza and starving, we decide to head over to JJ's. We walk in and I see a miracle: ~lower prices!~ The veggie sub is now only $5, and they also have GIANT subs, that are great for splitting, or if you're dying for a giant sub. 

They also just got kickin ranch, which was a delicious, flavorful but not spicy ranch for dipping your sub in! It's about $0.50 extra, but sooo worth it if you're a ranch fan. 

I've been to this location twice now, and usually I wouldn't give such a huge chain 5 stars, but the people who work here give it that extra star. They are all super kind and friendly, even when we come in late at night. They make this location awesome! 

As a long time fan of a good JJ sandwich, but also someone who hates spending a ton on sandwiches out to eat, these new, affordable prices plus the kind workers make me excited to come back for a quick, delicious meal.
Service is fast and friendly. My sandwich is always made correctly, and the restaurant is always clean. Jimmy John's has become my go-to when I'm on that side of town.
NNwAUflppzAqYNU4-pmTOw: It's a Burger King in a mall. Just like any other Burger King in a mall. They lost one star because I was their only customer at the time I ordered and still they were all oh so slow.

It was slow day in the mall
And i was looking for a burger 

Manager toni was on the store
She offered me to make my whopper fresh of the broiler 
And my fries was fresh off the fryer 

Great day at the mall for me
K3ggSZca7bq4O3ir9hp--g:
Give it a chance. After many bad reviews I had low expectations- but this place was super cool. It's kind of an oxymoron with decor- it has chandeliers and then 1950s diner chairs. Oh, and then there's a disco ball. 

Food was good- gumbo had a dark roux and a hush puppy that was delicious. We ordered a everything on the menu and the catfish was our favorite.

Crab cake- good, Gumbo- good, chicken tenders and truffle fries- good, Scallop dinner- really good, Brussels sprouts side- good, smoked salmon dinner- good. Service good and attentive. Nice neighborhood find. We will be back !
OMG- just had the hush puppies. D-Lish

New little spot that opened up in 12 South neighborhood. Cute, quaint, and charismatic. 

Server Josh was friendly, attentive, and knowledgeable about the menu. 
Head Chef Jeremy came out and talked with us for several minutes about the dishes and ensured we were enjoying not only the food but also the experience. 

We went in for a quick bite midday. 
Bacon wrapped scallops with a corn purée was my fave. Also a must is the smoked salmon plate, the hush puppies, and the crab cakes. 

Selective but local craft beers on tap. Loads of wine  options as well as bourbon. Drink prices are fair and they have lots of small plate food options which I loved.. 

Great little spot for a ladies brunch, date night out, or a unique little spot to pop in for a drink while you're touring the neighborhood. 

Also, they have $1 oysters for happy hour!! Yes please! 

Looking forward to going again!
G1nix5cIWx3Jla8xZG-xAA: It was my first time in Nashville and to my surprise there was a Blanco restaurant opening and I had to find out when they opened. The outside of the restaurant looks amazing, upon walking up you're greeted with a warm and friendly staff. We got sat and I believe we were the first ones to get seated at the restaurant. We were greeted by Joshua with such a positive and energetic attitude. He walked us through the menu and gave us some of his favorites from the menu. His trainer Logan was there to support him in every way. She was just as attentive and very friendly. Nicole also stooped by and made great conversation and took the time to talk to our table and made sure that we were having a great experience.  Overall it was a magnificent experience and when I visit Nashville I definitely want to come back to a place that feels like home. The food was amazing the Carne asada Fajitas were delicious and not to mention the cocktails, they were gone and round 2 was a definite. Dessert, I don't even know what to say because as soon as they hit the table they were gone. It was a great experience with such a warm and friendly staff. Great job everyone. Knocked it out of the ball park. Cheers.

Such amazing flavors
The shrimp fajita are out of this world yummy
The margaritas are amazing as well. Spices and full of flavor

I can't say enough about Blanco. The staff was friendly, protocols made us feel so safe, and BAR NONE Josh is the best server we have had through travels to many cities. Would recommend to anyone visiting Nashville looking for an incredible experience, amazing food, and delicious drink.
LNMi7I89I9NI3bQz8LPetA:
E3 was an exceptional experience for the following reasons:

I visited here celebrating a birthday and also because it is a new restaurant in an area of town I frequently visit. We arrived approximately an hour before our reservation and was greeted by the valet to park ($7), he then led us inside to the lower level of E3. It was appropriately decorated for the holidays and the hostess and bartended warmly welcomed us and wished me a happy birthday.

Before we went upstair to our table we decided to get a drink at the bar downstairs and chat with the bartended Lauren. She was a phenomenal bartender who creatively made us cocktails off the top of her head. I would highly recommend stoping by her if you can.

After the pre-dinner drinks we headed up the staircase to our table where our waiter promptly explained the menu and gave us some suggestions on what to drink/eat. 

The food while on the expensive side is delicious. The sides we chose (Pimento Mac and Cheese & Gruyere, Gouda, and Garlic Mashed Potatoes) are shareable and could easily feed 2-4 people depending on the portions. I also order the 8oz filet and it came out perfectly cooked to  order. On top of the remarkable food, I was surprised by the waiter with a gift basket that contained steak rub, homemade beef jerked and a desert pastry because I was celebrating my birthday. On top of that he dropped by with a flight of creme brûlée that was literally the icing on the cake to the main course. 

After dinner we ventured upstairs and sat by the fire and chatted with another bartender about the restaurant and how 10% of the net profits go towards non-profit projects in the Greater Nashville Area, as well as international projects.

Overall, I would love to visit again soon and thank you to E3 for making a memorable birthday experience. 

*The only thing I could possibly complain about would be that they do not validate your valet for some reason? I do not  really understand that arrangement but it is small detractor on the night as a whole.

Last night the husband and I went in to check this new steakhouse out for apps and cocktails. Is is absolutely gorgeous and we had the most amazing server Rebecca! She took care of our every need. Oyster app was to die for. We will definitely be back to have the full dining experience and will for sure ask for Rebecca!
Fantastic place for drinks and dinner.  Quality top to bottom. The 3rd floor roof bar was an excellent find. Will be back often. Oh...and they valet.
IDp8PurNbbIyeqTWbf6SSA: I've been here a few times.  The food is great, and they are one of the few places that stay open late.  My wife loved the $5 catfish sandwich, but they removed it from the menu! :(   The wings, fries , pizza and sandwiches are good too.  Nice guys.  Wish them success.  Keep up the good work.

Best wings in Nashville! My husband and I order from there at least twice a week. Flavors are amazing, but I'm not too sure about the "lettuce" under the wings. Overall great place for a great an even better price.
o59FKrkVKag4bzAjyH64SA: The matcha latte is the way to go. A little on the pricey side but delicious. The hotel lobby also makes for a cozy sitting area with cute couches and fun chairs.
We stayed at the Graduate while visiting and grabbed coffee each morning. The employees are so friendly and the beverages are wonderful!

We stayed at the The Graduate, which is the hotel the houses this restaurant/Cafe . The Breaskfast sandwich was excellent! I'm a lover of breakfast it's my favorite meal . I've ownEd restaurants and my husband and I cook all the time this was a very pleasant surprise.
mwwDJ2ZgfhFUvFtKXa9BtQ: I finally, FINALLY tried a Cuban sandwich from Soy Cubano. And let me tell y'all, it is the first good Cuban sandwich I've ever had in Nashville. I've been searching for years! Having grown up in Tampa, Fl, yeah, I might be what you would consider a Cuban sandwich snob. I can't help it. If you know, you know. And if you don't know, well, maybe you're the lucky one who isn't constantly chasing down the unicorn that is a legit Cuban sandwich. Anyhow, Soy Cubano is popping up all over the place, so you have plenty of opportunities to track them down and treat yourself to some wonderful food. I personally had my first taste at the farmer's market downtown this past weekend, and can't wait to try more of their offerings in the near future! It's the attention to detail that really speaks with this food. Great bread (it really, really matters!), thinly sliced pickles, the perfect amount of condiments... I wouldn't have changed a thing! Thanks, y'all! I'll be back!
dope food by cool people. i recently had my first (delicious) taste of soy cubano at never never, which also rules. looking forward to more goodness soon!

My husband and I both got the Arroz Con Pollo and it was DELICIOUS!! The elote and black beans with rice were also fabulous. Everything was super flavorful! The chef was so personable, as well! We will definitely be back!!
JxV7r92Upwo3PyysqFW51Q: Best wings in the city! Great vibe! Owners are amazing and work with you on your orders. Located in the heart of Nashville. I don't know what could be done better about this place!
The wings are great but the service... was waiting and the women at the front just went off to smoke hookah. Come on man. Like I don't do that at my job, wish I could. I just wanted some good wings.

I came to Nashville a few weeks ago and one of my friends introduced me to Pluckerz; long story short, I've been hooked ever since. I would suggest the fried cauliflower and fried pickles to any newcomers. The staff is very accommodating and they try their best to satisfy customer needs as quickly as possible. 

At this point, the cooks know my order and make amazing recommendations based off of what I usually get. If you're not a fan of the lounge feel, try going during the day for lunch or stop by in the afternoon for the 50 cent wing happy hour. 

Honestly, just try it out and leave the judgment at the bottom of the stairs, the food will meet your expectations.
7P7nix455SqZ8cDhna9rBQ: Great burger. Patty crisp compared to Freddy's but thicker. If you're a big eater, get a double. Single perfect for me. I'm not a burger person but this was a great burger.

If you're looking for a quick place to grab a bite to eat near Broadway - Shake Shack is it! When we arrived, the line was long, but it moved quick. After ordering, it took about ten minutes to receive our food. I didn't think this was bad at all considering how busy the place was. Plenty of seating indoor and outdoor. Overall, a fairly good experience.

Never had shake shack before & it was my first meal "downtown" in a year due to the pandemic. Beautiful sunny Sunday afternoon after visiting the newly opened NMAAM next door. 

Impressive burger to say the least. I got the online recommended double shake shack & bacon cheese fries. I guess I understand why people love this place. 

Will be back!
ak5cTOoVvZLaRcyqg05K4w: Staff is best ever, you never leave with a bad experience at all. There's a Sonic closer to our house..but we always say "Let's go to the good Sonic".
uAvZ_rPgyP3MQLtUtM0BsQ: Greatest food of all time. No better fish in the world. Tacos are incredible, but really can't go wrong with any of the main dishes.

Absolutely amazing! One of the best, if not the best meal we've had while in Nashville. Bob is super nice, and the man can fry fish! We had the fish dinner, regular and Nashville hot. Side of beans, hush puppies, and a special salad of sorts that blew my mind! I cannot say enough about this place! Awesome!!
Excellent - had the loaded fish tacos.   quality products!!
-qyzTT2NndkDNhDYBIBtXQ: The food and hospitality is wonderful! I love their catfish, and macaroni and cheese. The owners make the experience worth while coming!

Great service & great food.

Turkey Leg, Cornbread, Potatoes w/ Gravy, & Cabbage was good. 

Banana Pudding was great as well.
This is a really good restaurant everything is fair there's a lot of bad comments on here from white people trying to make it look bad. I enjoyed the food. #BLM
vW2ArBLifE9EW-30sVoIZA: Pretty Good stuff. Loved the Jalapeño mac n cheese. Got the opportunity to try them at the Food Truck Festival in the East (East Nashville). Check 'em out
The chicken sandwich here is amazing. Spice level is spicy but manageable, fries beyond good and fair prices. Recommend hands down. (Located at Fat Bottom brewery)
I've tried three different dishes from Mac Shack and they're all great. The buffalo is my favorite, but the barbecue is amazing too!
c3QxX3toWdqJnKQmmIliRQ: A delightful establishment.  Great service, food was five stars, and a worthy crowd.  The male and female servers could be models in Brazil.

What an amazing experience we had at this establishment!! All employees wore a welcoming smile!!  @Austin's Rose was a fabulous couple and serenaded us through our delicious meals!!  Highly recommend this place!!

I'm an out of towner but have visited every spot on Broadway and dozens outside of it. 

The place is aesthetically well put together, more importantly our food and drinks were wonderful, and more importantly than that, the staff was kind. 
We had a group of 5, ordered some apps and tried each other's food. 

Things happen with restaurants, and a lot of folks leave negative reviews that deal with instances outside of anything the establishment could be expected to control. 

I think the reviews for this place may get beat up a little bit since it's right on Broadway, and naturally you'll have some folks wander in who've had a couple too many Bushwackers.  They do have a stage. There was not a band playing when we had lunch, but I can definitely see the place having a different atmosphere depending on no band vs acoustic set vs full band with drums and bass. 

I think if they can control the noise level and vibe during certain times of day, like acoustic sets earlier in the day, then it'll be fine. 



I'll admit, this place is going to look better on social media than in person, but only for the reason that it's not going to be as large as you might expect. 

Real estate ain't cheap on Broadway.
GFrT38hSaIZ2xO6WCV3rbg: They were so nice to me and gave me cookies even though I was pretty chunky already aka. michelin man. The lady is so nice and helped me prepare a sandwich for kings and not only did it fill me up for the rest of the night but it also gave me hope in America. The reviews are legit. The pupusas are legit. This is a place you should check out -- without a doubt.

I am hardly ever in the Tusculum area. In fact, until recently, I didn't even realize that it was a part of Nashville proper. I suppose as more and more people move into the city, the boundaries will stretch and get re-established. Looking at a heat map, there's a lot of home building in Glencliff and Paragon Mills. In 2013, mentioning both of those neighborhoods would have earned a response of 'what?' So, it should come as no surprise that expansion is reaching southward.

It definitely doesn't come as a surprise to the people and communities that have made this section of town home for years. Driving down Nolensville, I kept thinking 'Where is this place?' It's a good thing I had on my navigation system because if I hadn't, I would have missed it. PSR is small. It's a small blue building with a tight dining room and small seating addition. This isn't a place to come with a large crowd. I was the only non-Latino inside (and, outside; a bunch of guys were hanging out) but the woman at the counter is fully bilingual (and, very nice). She addressed me in Spanish and I replied. I thought about going further down that road but she switched it up after I explained that it wasn't my native tongue. She also gave me tips and patience to get through the menu. 

Before going crazy at a new restaurant, I like to start with the standards. If those are up to snuff, then I expand on the options. I tried three pupusas: pollo y queso, chicharron y frijoles and chicharron y queso. It took, about, 20 minutes for these nicely sized-pupusas to be made to order. And, at $2.50 a pop, I was out there for under $10. The pupusas came with curtido, green sauce and tomato salsa. Y'all, I'm definitely coming back. These pupusas were the best I've had in Nashville -- and I thought I'd had the best pupusas in Nashville. I couldn't understand how these discs were packed with so many ingredients and with so much flavor. Each pupusa was distinct in flavor (sometimes the masa can overwhelm the dish) and each bite was packed with something to enjoy (again, not just masa). I'm giving PSR 4.5 stars since I didn't order anything other than pupusas but I, easily, rounded up because if the pupusas were this good, the rest of the menu has to be fantastic. Due to the size of the place, I'll probably stick to carry out but there will be many more trips down Nolensville to eat of this goodness. Thanks be to Yelp for putting this on my radar and in my life. If you're a fan of Salvadoran cuisine, you owe it to yourself to come here. The stars don't lie.

This is the most authentic Salvadoran food I have had, not just in Nashville, but the whole U.S., since my time spent in El Salvador.  The Pupusas are delicious and the homemade Tortillas are incredible!  The family that runs the restaurant is very friendly as well.  DO NOT MISS OUT ON THIS PLACE!!
2jwUldEzfMAjPQVCgljLCg: I picked up a couple dozen doughnuts for my office.  They were a hit!  The doughnuts are fluffy on the inside and a light crisp on the outside.  So many options!  I'll certainly be back!

Actually the best donuts I've ever had. They are not traditional donuts, more flakey like a sweet croissant. Great people work there and the donuts are one of a kind. I might fly back to Nashville just for another one of these magnificent treats.

I work in Nashville right around the corner from this shop, and since I was at the office last week, I decided to pick up a dozen for my family. 

The day after I brought them home,  my husband asked where I got them. I asked are they good (I hadn't actually had one yet). His response was just a long moan of ecstasy lol. I tried one after that (the raspberry pistachio), and oh my God they're amazing. 

They were so amazing we picked up another dozen today!
eGey-aARfLgElykoA9YdsQ: If you can appreciate execution Czann's is going to quickly become your favorite local brewery.   This place is the Sierra Nevada of Nashville.

Easy 5 star based on the beers, the brewery owner and brewer Ken, and the beautiful newly renovated tap room!

This is the same Czann's as the old downtown location - but bigger and soon to be more food options to enjoy with Ken's great beers.  

There is an outdoor patio - dog friendly and a newly remodeled interior (which I am told was formally a church).  

Full line up of beer tbd - but you really can't go wrong with Czann's given Ken's long tenure in brewing in Nashville.  

See you there ... rumor is that the opening date of this new brewery location  is Friday - March 12, 2021 ... which just so happens to be today!!!
Amazing new brewery in the Nations!  Amazing service, very clean, and the service is friendly & helpful...oh and the beer....EPIC.  Guess I found my new happy hour.

00FlwcHcngPcmMHzUnXetA: LOVED coming across this wine bar. Such a fun lounge/speakeasy/artistic vibe and solid wine selection. I will definitely be visiting this neighborhood spot many more times!
Great wine bar with a wide selection. If you're looking for a speakeasy this place is great if you're trying to avoid all the tourists and bachelorettes.

I'm so glad I was finally able to visit The Authentique! It's tucked away in a nondescript strip mall on Gallatin Road. Once inside you are transported to France. I've never been to France, but it certainly gives off that vibe. They really paid attention to all of the details. The walls, ceiling, tables, lights, bar, etc. were carefully designed. They were playing a mix of music but I do remember Manu Chao was in the rotation. 

There were plenty of wine options and snacks. We tried a brut rose, two different reds, and a white. All were smooth and had great tasting notes. Price wise too, it was exactly where I wanted it to be. They also carried a bunch of bottles at different price pints. The snacks offered were charcuterie, fois gras, sturgeon caviar, a few desserts, and more. I can't comment on the food, we stopped in for drinks only. This place is perfect for a date night or to catch up with friends. I highly recommend!

I get that that one uber driver had one bad experience, and that delivery is a tough gig where time matters, but that's no reason to say they don't deserve to be in business, nor make their only review seem like they're overall a bad pick. Yelp reviews have real world consequences for people who are just trying to make it. It's not a place to vent your spleen about having to wait a few more minutes. People are way too comfortable attacking someone's livelihood on this app. (Yes, one star reviews that put someone's small restaurant at the bottom of search results do indeed count as attacking someone's livelihood. Think twice before you do it.)
